text:
Booked Morimoto for my boyfriend's birthday celebration dinner and was extremely disappointed. Before ordering, I had asked our server at length about the Chef's tasting menu and if it had diverse options (i.e. apps, noodles, seafood, meats, etc). He REALLY SOLD IT TO US and said it did -- but when the courses arrived, they were basically ALL seafood and not very good. He described it to us as a "best of the best" at Morimoto (dishes that made them famous), but it really just seemed like food they were trying to sell/get rid of. 

At one point, he did ask if we were pleased with the dishes. I was very polite but told him we were unhappy, saying that I understood this is a Japanese restaurant with many of those types of dishes, but since I had explicitly asked, I thought he would have done a better job of communicating the fact that this would be 99% seafood. It just seemed like he really wanted us to spend our money on it, and he didn't care much if we liked it or not. 

What I also found a bit strange was that he kept TELLING us how good the dishes looked and smelled when he put them down on the table, so he "KNEW we would like them" -- it was a bit odd and assuming. Also after I explained my displeasure with the menu, he told me "dessert would more than make up for it." Really? After dessert (which also was't very good), he said: "when you come back, I know you will have a better experience." I said: "IF I come back..." His approach and tone to the whole thing was really uncomfortable.

In addition:
- The service was very slow. Our dishes and empty drinks sat at the table, and we had to ask to get service. 
- He sold us hard on the tasting menu, but wasn't accountable/did nothing to help after we didn't like it. This was not cheap.
- When I made the reservation, I noted that it was my boyfriend's birthday and asked if they could bring out a little candle/something extra for him with dessert. They never did.

We love and appreciate food and the dining out experience. I was very excited about Morimoto - but I was really upset about the whole experience from start to finish.

I would like to preface this by saying I have never had an issue during the day. The staff seems upbeat and personable, traditional McDonald's staff. Also, I am pregnant and want nothing more than McChicken's half the time. I know I know, it isn't great, but it's that or no food at all, so here I am housing these terrible sandwiches.

So far this week here is what has happened at this McDonald's. 

Saturday Night: husband and I stop here after a wedding we were attending, around 11:35pm. I watched the woman working as we drove into the lot and up to the drive-thru. She looked physically pained by the fact that there was a car approaching. When we get to the telecom box her greeting is "Sorry I'm not taking orders right now." Ummm, okay. You just don't want to make the McChickens you know my husband is going to inevitably want. (The week before, upon handing him the McChicken's she scathingly remarked "Sorry it took so long, I had to make them fresh." GEE THANKS FOR MY HOT SANDWICH THAT IT IS YOUR JOB TO MAKE!)

Same Night: my best friend and her boyfriend go and order whatever they wanted, another scathing and rude interaction due to the fact she had to make something fresh and to order. 

Last Night: This is slightly less offensive to me, mistakes happen, but I'm distraught because, like I said, I am pregnant and also super emotional... Husband orders three McChickens, two plain, one normal, of course he gets back and all three are plain.

I have had other instances here involving the same issues (all being plain or none being plain, general rudeness) but felt the need after the horrid past two weeks worth of experiences here to actually let the people know! I plan on contacting corporate at this point, as well.

This review would be one star if not for the pleasant daytime staff. I am a WONDERFUL person to serve/wait on and I think the people during the day appreciate that, but the night lady/ladies is/are a jerk(s) no matter what.
Recently had take-out lunch special...Gen Tso's Chicken  was very tasty, but portion was very small...web pricing AND menu pricing are  not correct. I was told they are two years out of date.

Our complaint is that the walk-in vaccination clinics only do a certain number
of animals. (No sign-up slots and it's a mystery guess as to the magic number). Today we were sent away after a brief stand in line with many other people.  This is not advertised and should be.  We took time off work-
unpaid of course!

The lobster rolls were perfect. Sat at the outside bar and ordered a couple martinis. They had live music on Saturday afternoon, I think they have live music everyday but I could be wrong. Good atmosphere at the bar.

Sadly, we had a pretty not great experience at Alma. We visited on Sunday, Dec. 12, 2021 around 1:30pm for a late lunch after shopping. It was a long wait at the other restaurants near Alma but Alma was only 30 minutes, so we got a drink at the bar and waited for our table, which technically only took about 20 minutes. Sadly, after we were seated, no one ever came.... For over an hour! We actually got impatient and went back up to the bar for another round, but still, no one ever came to serve us. We had menus and everything so I'm not sure why we were never served. We saw the reviews online were mediocre so literally while we were still waiting for a server at Alma, I walked over to Whiskey Cake, waited 20 minutes for a table there, and we just left. Sadly, we never got to try the food at Alma because no one wanted to bring us anything, but the drinks and bar service were good. Wouldn't go back, just seemed a little chaotic and unprofessional.

Wow. This bar is amazing! The owner and the bartender Steve...just a perfect team. I legit cannot find a single thing to complain about. Being here watching a playoff game repping the Warriors I felt at home in my neighborhood bar. The food. The service. The drinks and the customers just makes this a go to place. Recommend this spot to anyone.

This was my second visit to a Ruth Chris location. The Chattanooga location was excellent in every way. The Nashville location was less to be desired. The lead server was not attentive. She apparently was having a bad night. The food was good and manager did stop by and ask 
Our opinion of the meal. All the other servers were excellent.

If you want some BBH but you don't wanna drive over to philly to cafe diem, this is a great alternative. It's in a small strip mall with plenty parking out front. I came here with my family after wedding venue shopping I normally don't get bbh unless it at cafe diem but my hairdresser recommended it and I went for it. I'm so glad I did because it was so delicious. I might even say it's better than cafe diems  

The interior is not cute but your standard pho place. They sell a bunch of lime trees, ripe limes and Serrano peppers. Random but interesting. The pricing aligns with the surround pho places in the suburbs. I would say it's slight cheaper with bigger portions. For example usually when u order summer rolls you get 2 for like 5 bucks, you get three here. My family all loved their food and they got a mix of pho, chicken n rice and bbh. If I was ever in the area I would deff stop over here!

This is one of our favorite local restaurants to take company when they visit us.  The local brew is award winning and is quintessential "Tampa".

There's something for everyone, and even Rover is welcome.

The outdoor patio is first come first serve so it helps to have someone scout out that area for tables if there's a wait inside.  They don't take reservations but it's worth the wait. 

Tampa Bay Brewing Company hosts regular beer festivals and events, but it is perfect for any night of the week as a last minute dinner option.

The Hillsborough Avenue location is convenient for both Hillsborough and Pinellas county residents.

The Airline Chicken is always a fan favorite, but my two favorite dishes are the Bad Ass Burger and the Drunken Shrimp. 

If a calzone is your thing, order it and be amazed at the sheer size of it as it lands on your table. It's more like a regulation sized football!

Enjoy a selection of locally brewed beer and take home your favorite to enjoy on the sofa. 

While TBBC is not on the water, the sunset views are spectacular and the cafe lights on the patio create a beautiful evening in an otherwise casual environment. 

Don't let a full parking lot discourage you. They have plenty of parking behind the building that is not visible from the road. 

This is sure to become on of your favorites if it's not already.

I usually try to avoid going here because I'm addicted to their coffee but today is a reminder that I need to focus on my diet as well as my health.  Halfway through my iced coffee I saw a floating black speck... I thought it was some coffee grounds at first but coffee grounds do not have legs!!! Please see pic of baby roach in my drink.  Never again!

I took my concern to the BBB in an effort to get Harpeth Hills to acknowledge their improper treatment of my Dog. Their response was arrogance and complete denial of any responsibility for the mistreatment of my 9 month old Boxer. 
If you take your pet to these people you are placing your pet in danger. 
These people should not be in business!!!
This place doesn't deserve the 1 star I gave, the owner is very mean, the food is outdated and bland, place smells and price is not affordable!!!!

Dr. Wertel is a great Chiropractor! Yes, I said great! I have been seeing chiropractors for some 40 + years and Dr. Wertel has been the best one to date. He is very upfront with me and with what I need to do to get better. I was having severe pain in my neck, arms and hands that kept me up at night or woke me from a sound sleep. Some Drs. thought it was carpal tunnel syndrome and wanted to do surgery although I do have some signs of carpal tunnel I really didn't believe that was my problem and neither did Dr. Wertel. After a few adjustments he knew that I needed something other than what he was doing. He took some x-rays of my neck, found out what the real problem was and recommended therapy and suggested that I see an orthopedic doctor if the pain continued. He knew right away that simple adjustments weren't going to help. Long story short, I had to have surgery to remove and replace a plug between disc 4 and 5, I am now pain free. Dr. Wertel doesn't take unnecessary x-rays, he knows when they are needed, nor does he tell you that you have to come back if he doesn't find it necessary and believe me he knows, he leaves the decision up to you. When I feel I need to see him, I make an appointment. Dr. Wertel is there to help relieve your pain and to help make you feel better. I would recommend Dr. Wertel to any and everyone. I even take my grandsons, who are just 8 and 10 years old. They tell me how good they feel after Dr. Wertel gives them an adjustment, it even helps their attitude because when your in pain your not always a nice person, trust me I know. I trust Dr. Wertel explicitly.

If you want to feel safe during COVID this is NOT the place. I've been reluctant to even try to go out to dine and quickly regretted my decision. Not one employee was wearing a mask. No social distancing whatsoever. Every table close together, nothing taped off. I understand it's a pain but think of others please.

We ate here twice during a visit to New Orleans. It was close to our hotel and had breakfast and a late dinner. Gotta give it a thumbs up for really good meats such as ham, chicken and sausage. Place gets crowded and lines form if you go there during normal eating hours. Go early or late is my advice.

I've only been here twice so not sure how my judgment would be if I was a regular. Before going, my roommate warned me about Nathan because she said he was rude to her. I took the risk anyways because I had a good experience the first time and enjoyed my nails. However, when I got there they couldn't do the design I wanted because they didn't have the embellishment materials. I decided to just get the basic nails I wanted (nude/white ombré). They also didn't have any nude powder so he presented me a light pink. He did the nail and asked me how I liked it and I was a little unsure so I started asking a few simple questions like what other powders they had. It was evident he was getting agitated with me even though I was just trying to see what I would like. When he tried a different powder he told me I better choose because he's not going to go back and fix it. I felt pressured to use the powder I asked to see because I didn't want him to get more mad, even though the previous powder looked nicer. I just sat their quietly and let him do whatever even though I wanted to get up and walk out. When he finished the nails, the ombré was uneven on some nails and just looked like he rushed and was sloppy. Pretty disappointed with the service and overall experience because they couldn't do the design/color powder I wanted. I've never had an experience like that before and will never be coming back. Nathan is rude and makes you feel bad about changing your mind.

I come here every other week. It's a godsend for people who want to eat late but don't want po-boys or burgers. The service is slow but the food comes out fast. The Chinese food isn't the greatest but it is definitely above your local fast food delivery. The sushi is good and fresh - if you order in season. And it's a nice relaxing atmosphere to unwind after a long day of work. Also, sit at the bar or in the bar area when George is working. He's awesome and very, very cool.

I was so excited to try this place. Whiskey AND doughnuts?! C'mon!!!!

Given, I did go in and order mule at a whiskey bar but they were completely out of vodka... BUT the bartender was very friendly and offered to make me a mule with orange vodka because that is all he had left (and let me tell you it tasted like an orange dream-sicle). So the service, the environment, and the drinks are great. And if you are into whiskey, they have a HUGE list.

The doughnuts however weren't amazing. They are very basic and were very dry. For a place advertised as a whiskey/donut place I expected more. But overall I was happy with my experience.

My husband and I bought our wedding rings  there and the staff were really helpful. We loved their selection and constantly went back for Pandora charms. I believe Shirley helped us and has always been delightful to work with. She wasn't pushy and had good advice on selections. They also offer cleanings (free) for our rings at any location, this is helpful because my husband and I both are active duty and move a bit.

Every time I try to eat here there is a problem. Most recently over the weekend... After staying at PCI for 2 nights- our group wanted to eat breakfast here before checkout. The Hostess was not very helpful at all and continued to say that they did not have enough servers and they were sending one home... That it would be 30 minutes. The restaurant was basically empty! They also had plenty of people waiting to be seated- but we're sending people home??!! I did not want to go here in the first place because I've had so many issues in the past, but everybody else really wanted to and then when we walked out- agreed with me. For having such an awesome establishment in general, this restaurant is lacking so much.

We did not have reservations and made a last minute decision to dine at Ava. At 7pm I called to see if we could get a table and was told not until 9:45. We decided to stop in anyway and check out the bar... There were tables in the bar area inside and out!  The host should have told us that on the phone!  We ordered drinks and food.  We ordered a salad, scallops and a pizza. The salad was good but the scallops and pizza were served cool to luke warm.  Not good.  Service was ok.  We had to ask a coupe of times for refills on our water.  I enjoyed the atmosphere and cocktails.  I would go back for drinks but not sure about the food.  We will give it a second chance before we make that decision!
I saw Cat and got a great flattering, haircut. She even gave me some cute waves and curls since I was going to an event that night! Great experience!
Awesome good food and drinks!  Even better ambiance! My husband and I will definitely be back for more.
Excellent attention to detail - washer installed properly and carefully - on time - excellent customer service
Nothing is fresh. Fried food is a disaster. I came for a buffet. Apart from lomein everything else was not at all fresh.

We went to Saj on a Friday and it was not too crowded and good service.

Pros: great appetizers. We had the baked feta and grilled halloumi. Baked feta was perfectly sweet with a layer of sugar and under a caramel iced some of honey...delish

Cons: only used vegetable oil (soybean oil) and could not accommodate for an allergy even though some items on the menu said they were cooked in olive oil.

BYOB but a store down the street that sells wine.

I'm here on Saturday March 13 2021. The new place is horrible. So loud. Lacks the cozy fun Mexican feeling. 
Sadly I think they're understaffed that's why I asked for napkins three times and still don't have any. Salad served with no utensils.

Dropped in here on a whim while out running errands for work today.
 
I ordered the spicy pork Korean soft tacos and the "Kim Cheese" burger I have heard Chesterfieldians rave about. I ordered to go and it wasn't particularly "fast" but it wasn't slow either. Out in less than ten. The counter lady was kind and even made some suggestions.

The spicy Korean pork tacos were bona fide awesome. If I would have had them by themselves this review would have been an easy 4-5 stars up front. They were simply a perfect flavor combination and if everything on their menu were that good it might be the best fast food I would have had for a long while (and I don't like fast food). And it would easily trumped Seoul Taco (the city favorite). The lime, cilantro, corn, pico, and Korean BBQ combo is simply a flavorful explosion I nary have had. The tortillas were homemade as well. Well done!

The Kim Cheese burger was a bit of a stretch as the Korean flavors (ginger, sesame, etc) do not come out as clearly being dominated by mayonnaise, yellow cheese, and ketchup. My bun was also a bit dry and crusty when it was something of a potato bun (I believe) so it should have been soft and pillowy. It was good overall, but I don't get the hype. I'd be open to trying another but I found it better than "meh" but less than 4 stars good. 

Overall Kim Cheese is earning 4 stars this time however. The people were very kind and knowledgable (especially for a fast food place) and they even hand draw their own to-go bags. Those pork tacos were hella awesome but the burger was something I think they invented asking themselves "Hey, how can we get West County hoosiers up on Korean fusion? I know! We'll drown it in cheese, ketchup, and mayonnaise like every other fast food place but keep the Korean meat on it so they venture out into new territory and try something really good instead or if they get open-minded." 

Can't wait to go back. Recommended. And hella cheap! Do it!

This place is horrible. I had to return my car to them 3 times. It still isn't fixed rite.I took it there for vibration and wanted my belts and hoses replaced due to a long road trip I was taking. They had to do my timing belt twice. It was screaming the 1st time they returned my car and there was oil all over the engine. Like someone dumped a bucket of oil. I've never seen anything like it. It was all over my new belts, hoses, wiring harnesses, cv boots, battery, even all over the underside of the hood and my windshield!!!  It was dripping in more than one place on my driveway. They had to retention the timing belt and make sure it wasn't covered in oil. They had to replace drive belt and pully 10 miles after the 2nd visit because they messed up a pully and it messed up the belt . The 3rd time my car was still vibrating they ended up replacing 2 more engine mounts. Now my car is leaking coolant. The all lights are flickering. According to another shop. My alternator is probably junk due to the oil bath it got. My headlight is burned out. Not exactly safe for a road trip. I asked them to use all OEM Honda parts. I don't know if they did or not. They said they had the old parts but never returned them to me. I called the Honda dealer and they said all the rubber components that got the oil bath will fail prematurely. So basically everything I had replaced plus all the other engine parts that got the bath are subject to fail. I spent $1519 at this place. Have a car that is in worse shape than before I took it in. Lost many nights sleep over the long drive coming up. Will have to spend at least that if not more to get this car back in order. I am completely and totally disgusted. I will say the owner was nice but what good does that do me. Is she gonna pick me up when I'm 1000 miles from home. They have a huge sign that says the are a BBB accredited buisness. THEY ARE NOT!!! I will be filing a complaint with the BBB and taking legal action against this place. Thank God I documented everything and took pictures of the condition I got my car back. SERIOUSLY PEOPLE GO ANYWHERE ELSE!!!!

A table of 10 was easily handled by the wait staff and cooks. Try the delicious crab cakes when you order. Friendly staff and the chef greeted us upon food arrival. Great atmosphere and would recommend to anyone looking for a local flavor in Harahan and River Ridge.  Stop bye and check it out.

Just like all the other reviews, they promise shipping within weeks, it is more like months, and still using COVID as an excuse.  Ordered my cover in February, received an email in June they finally started production.  Delivery was originally slated for middle of June, then middle of July, now end of July possibly beginning of August.  If it wouldn't cost $150 to cancel, would have.

DO NOT ORDER from them, horrible customer service with the same rehearsed lines call after call.  They are quick to run your credit card while you wait for months for your cover.  Here's hoping the cover eventually comes without issue.

This is the best Cuban I've ever had.  Get there early so you don't have too long a wait, though food this good is worth waiting for.  The line moves quickly and the folks working there are very professional so things move along pretty quickly.  There are quite a few sandwich places down town, but I count the Bodega as one of my favorites.

Went here one time in the winter with some work friends when our office lost power. it was amazing! The staff was so friendly and nice! With a party of 8 they were very quick and kind. Coffee and food were great with good prices. We will be back! 

Only downer is parking.. but that can't really be helped.

I went in for a physical and PPD shot. The physical was fine, doctor was friendly and the staff was pleasant. The wait was not at all long. I was told by the nurse that I HAD to return there to have the test read in two-three days, but the doctor said I was able to have it read elsewhere. The nurse would not give me the paperwork therefore I had to return on thanksgiving (I was leaving for vacation that night) and drive 2.5 hours out of my way to wait 50 minutes to have the test read. The doctor told me I should have been given a copy and had the test read elsewhere. This is my biggest complaint about this place. It should have been communicated to the nurse that I was able to have it read elsewhere, it was the biggest inconvenience!

OUT OF THEIR OWN FOOD...seriously? We specifically went there for dinner because i wanted to have the carne seca. But their monotone unpleasant waiter Ruben informed us they were out of carne seca. That has to be one of my biggest pet peeves for an establishment to be out of their own food. To top it off he didn't offer another suggestion or discount for the inconvenience. 

Their food is good but the place is dated, dark, quiet and gloomy with a bunch of catholic stuff on the walls, not very neutral for everyone else who isn't catholic. The only reason we went to Speedway location was that it was closest to our next destination.

Needless to say we left. I called the location on Campbell and asked if they had carne seca and the lady was super sweet and and they had it. She sounded confused as to why I'm asking if they have their own food, I'm assuming they don't run out of food like Speedway. The Campbell location is lively, with colorful traditional Mexican decorations with friendly and courteous staff. I will give them a 5 star next.

Just took a trip to NOLA and we came here to take an airboat tour. We took the 2pm tour with Captain Kelly!!! We cruised through canals finding gators all around from Midnight, Air Jordon, Big Black & Benny were the gators we saw. This was an epic moment on the trip the staff was very friendly, but our favorite person was Caption Kelly! There were five of us on this tour 2 people from The Neverland, a woman from San Jose and my friend and I from Sacramento. Cap Kelly answered all our questions, took all the photos we wanted and traveled to different spots trying to find all the gators we could find. He was an awesome tour guide and shared his passion with all of us. My favorite thing about him was I could tell he really cared about the gators and I believed that those gators were his friends- it was very sweet. You have to come take a tour here and ask for Captain Kelly!!

I Really really want to like this place. It's a great new location and the patio isn't too bad.. but the food is absolutely awful. It's all fried and frozen.. you can tell it comes in plastic freezer bags. 

There is a full bar and it's great for sports games.. if you're not hungry.
Very nice rooms , beautiful golf course, very good food and excellent staff.  Great place to stay.

I was also there on Saturday night 9/24/11 (see Michael T's comment).  They were out of lamb only as far as I know.  They were short-handed - just the husband and wife owners were there to cook and serve.  The place was full and people were even eating on the patio outside.  We try any Mediterranean/Middle Eastern restaurant we can find in our area and have the same experience in each one: great food, poor service.  The Mediterreanean Cafe was the exception.  Even with as busy as they were our service from both the husband and wife was excellent.  The food was excellent also.  We finally found a restaurant we want to go back to.  Loved it.

I have eaten their twice.

Loved the food. Swamped both times. Great service once.  Not so great the other.  Its a brand new restaurant so lets give em a chance to get it worked out.

BTW when I visited another pizza place close by and there was not anyone there - it took twice as long to get Pizza that was even close to Amicos or Pie In The Sky.

In a word....ATROCIOUS. 

No offense to the other reviewers...but what were you smoking when you wrote those reviews?  Please be advised that the following review is long, but detailed. 

My girlfriend and I just visited Sono Bana this evening because she was in the mood for Japanese. We are from California, so we are accustomed to a variety of Japanese cuisine, both good and bad.  Needless to say, when we went to this establishment, our hopes weren't too high. 

My girlfriend ordered the combination plate of chicken teriyaki  and tempura, and I ordered the appetizer of sesame chicken in addition to the chicken teriyaki  with rice I ordered. 

The miso soup that both of our meals came with was VERY bland, almost water with a bit of tofu and green onions. The salad was iceberg lettuce with a bit of shredded carrot, which would have been alright if the dressing that came with it did not resemble the texture and taste of baby food. 

Our food arrived a few minutes later. The aroma of the chicken was similar to old dog food that had been sitting out for a day or two, and the taste wasn't much better. It brings to mind the old songs of where Asian restaurants would use stray dogs or cats in place of real meat. The sesame chicken also tasted old. Not only were the dishes not hot or even warm, the sesame chicken tasted like the 3 day old oil it was cooked in, and the tempura was only slightly better. 

My girlfriend and I both had one bite of each type of chicken, and left the rest untouched. My girlfriend ate the tempura and a bit of rice because she felt bad, but needless to say the food we ordered did not even come close to edible. 

The server came over to see how everything was, and when I told her it was awful, she seemed apathetic, then  halfheartedly  offered to get us something else. I politely declined and asked for the check. The waitress was less than friendly when she brought the check, and to my disgust she had not even discounted any of the items. After standing there for 10 minutes in the empty restaurant, I walked up to the counter to where the THREE waitresses had been standing, and asked what they could do for me. She took my dinner off of the bill, but said that was the best she could do. 

I paid the bill, and we left. It was by FAR the worst Japanese that we have ever had, and one of the worst restaurant experiences I have ever had. 

In all fairness, neither of us tried the sushi. This is just a cautionary tale of where NOT to go to get even halfway decent teriyaki. Do your self a favor....go to Jack in the Box and order their teriyaki bowl...it tastes MUCH better and is half the price.
Delicious food! Great vegan options. Nice atmosphere, high-end feel. Although a little cramped on a Friday or Saturday night. Portions are small and prices are high.
Great food, great price, and you get a lot of food. I'd recommend the place to anyone in the Tucson area.

Was referred to Dr. Sten Ericson by the the much beloved Dr. Michael Thompson and couldn't be any happier with the quality and professionalism of the whole of Sten and his staff. The friendly pros behind the counter (secretaries and assistants/hygienists) coupled with the modern and comfortable suite are relaxing as well as accommodating. 

I had out-of-house root canals with the awesome Dr. Lawaty, whom I was referred to by Dr. Ericson, and finished the crowns with Ericson and couldn't be happier; they did an outstanding job no pain whatsoever and no complications!
This restaurant does anything and everything to make sure the customers are satisfied. I loved the vegan nachos and will be going back for more delish food.

I was so lucky to have lived next to this place. They really have the best donuts I have ever had. Great selection and good coffee. Can't find a shop like this anywhere else. The owners are the best!!! Always smiling and always taking care of their customers. 

Great service  :)
The food is very underwhelming.  I was told that they have the best roast beef sandwiches.  No way.  Not good at all.  The beef was rubbery, the fries were good but the salt made the inedible.

we went to have a few drinks and listen to the band on a saturday evening. we went ahead and set up a tab for my husband and i and so did our friends..all seperate tabs. we ordered 20 wings and i had 4 drinks and my husband had 5...our total bill was 98.00!!! they are way over priced on their drinks! One of our friends ordered a mich lite bottle and they charged 4.75!!  and then to top it all off we asked for a glass of ice water but they were only allowed to sell bottled water for 2.00 a bottle!!  the womens bathroom was dirty and musky and the vanity along the floor and wall had water damage and it looked like mold. the one stall i used did not even lock. On top of that, they also charge a cover charge! I will never go back to this place again!!!
A hole in the wall bar smelly, sticky floor,1970 tv s. Skunky taps oh yes UFC or no UFC it's a gamble if there is. I give staff 4 stars any day of the week

We arrived at 7:35... it is 9:05 and we are still waiting for chicken Alfredo and a pork chop. I would not recommend. 

Update- 9:10 the food arrived. My pasta was glued together- as to be expected if the food was not cooked at the same time.

We received three different stories-
1.) they didn't like how it looked after it was prepared
2.) it was not being cooked all of the way through 
3.) the ticket was lost

FYI- the owner has not come to see us. He did offer us a free drink.

Please save yourself a trip.

Took friends here last night for dinner and was incredibly disappointed! The wait staff service was so rude and unprofessional I was embarrassed that I talked my friends into coming. We ordered burgers, which were good and is the only reason a second star was given. The woman manager would smile to our face but didn't even wait until she was fully turned before making a rude face to her staff about us. Our waiter spilled a beer over my friend, and while the dining area was only 1/4 full, and we still had our plates in front of us, was hinting we should go! We were a group of 4 parent having my a kid free dinner and this was how we were treated?! I have never been so disrespected by a server ever. At one point, he even sat down at the table next to us and said he can stay there as long as we stayed. Which is uncomfortable as hell when you and your friends are just talking while finishing  our meals. It was only 7-8 in the evening. I went here often 10 years ago when I was in college. I'm pretty sure last night will be the last time I go there if that is how the staff AND THE MANAGER wishes to treat customers!

Lady was rude and rushed my nail job. I have never gotten gel polished removed and redone in less than 30 minutes in my life. No soaking, no cuticle cutting, rush polish job. She seemed very annoyed and impatient the minute I walked in the door and absolutely nobody was in there at the time. Never going back. Oh. And they are not very sanitary either. No towels were used during my session. Hand and arms on the dirty table the entire time. She didn't wash her hands or use gloves either. Gross.

I had a great visit - I was seen right away, the staff was friendly, took XRays and addressed my injury as well as being proactive about recommending follow up treatment. This will be my go-to clinic from now on.

They ok man.

The one thing I want to point out to Yelpers, was the 3 dozen, small ass, clean and raw oysters we had.  3 dozens for $23.97.  Not bad, you can get them daily or whenever as long as Hooters is open.  Sometimes I come here just for the oysters.  The food is ok because Ive had salads and the wings.  Decent enough.  People come in there with kids too so it's fine.

Service is on par, parking is pretty decent size lot, and sometimes the lots get full but I feel as if that is due to the sheer amount of men who cant seem to get enough of  their wings.  There is a full size bar.  Watch out for the dirty old men in there checking out fresh 18 year olds.

Most of us know that size of oysters vary according to the season we've had and sadly, we have had a bad season.

One time we complained about the amount of oysters we received because of size of them---the manager came by personally to  apologize and deliver more raw oysters to us.  That's what you call great service.  Now if only some of the bourgie ass restaurants in town could be deliver service like this.

There is bingo night which falls on the night where I think it is all you can eat wings.  When we came in we saw some sheet of paper on each table, turns out it's the rules for the all you can eat wings.  I didnt want any wings but one thing that caught my eye was that they only brought out a certain amt of rings each  time -- perhaps 15 at a time. Dont quote me.  Next time Im coming for bingo!!!

The place was vibing to some throwback pop jams, and I felt the excitement surging in me the moment I stepped in. It's been a minute since I had rolled ice cream. However, that feeling quickly dropped when I realized they didn't have mochi. When asked if they had gummy bears instead, they said they only had sour gummy worms. Why write gummy bears then? And it was interesting they were out of those on a Monday morning. 

Nonetheless, we ordered the Bangkok with choice of 3 add-on toppings ($7). You can watch them make it through the window, and it comes out beautifully presented. 

The Thai ice cream was subtle. It was hard to taste the flavor, maybe because of the coldness, but I think it was just also pretty bland in general. The flavor could have been more rich. The ice cream also fell like shavings, as it wasn't very creamy/ice cream-like. Overall, not the best I had, and it is unfortunate they ran out of my go-to toppings so quickly but I appreciate the offer of this dessert option in the area.

I walk by this place nearly every day and had noticed they were now offering breakfast. I do love breakfast, so today I finally decided to give it a try. Unfortunately, I was very disappointed. We ordered the "huevos rancheros", a Grecian omelet, and pancakes.

The "huevos rancheros" were absolutely NOT huevos rancheros.  I'm sorry but we are in the southwest, we are in Tucson...do not try to pass off a tomato, pepper and onion scramble served with a side of salsa and half a pita as huevos rancheros. If you don't include beans (as stated on the menu) or a tortilla they aren't huevos rancheros.  We also asked if we could have the eggs over easy instead of scrambled, and the server said  no...really? you won't make eggs to order?

The Grecian omelet was nothing more than a scrambled egg folded over nearly an entire can of sliced black olives with a few chopped, roasted red peppers...the menu said it came with feta cheese, but it didn't have any in it.

Pancakes were meh, seemingly "just add water" and toss on the flat top.

The server actually stuck her finger up and said to "just wait a minute" as she walked away when I asked for a condiment.

I won't be returning.

Honestly should've listened to the reviews but I didn't. Not a very good experience in my opinion. I got the burrito with beef. It was not edible the flavor was off on everything. Had it a month ago but remember not even being able to eat it and grabbing something else not long after. Not sure what happened but the reviews are definitely true to taste.
Expensive!!! Wanting to charge 800.00 for a ceiling fan installation on existing electrical....stay away!

This was by far our favorite bar on our trip. Me and my husband sat upstairs and rocked  out while drinking with an amazing bartender. She was friendly and funny She entertained us the whole time.If you are ever in the French Quarter, go visit the redhead bartending upstairs. You will not regret it. She really was the best bartender we met the entire time we were in New Orleans. She told us a little bit about the city, a little bit about herself, and we talked a whole lot about rock 'n' roll shows we've been to. I can't wait to go back!

Sorry, I used to live in SB and loved breakfast here. We met a friend for lunch and the gal recommended the tri tip sandwich which was more than terrible on a dry, cold bun with no spread and a few pieces of dry, over cooked tri tip. The bill for three beers and two lunch items was over $40. Skip lunch here and go to Brophy Bros or the Boathouse at Hendry's Beach!!!!

entered 5:30 and asked the two hostesses about 'happy hour'..they looked at each other quizzically, confessed ignorance and said to each other 'guess we should know that'...anyway it extends 'til 7 and all starters (a big choice) are half off, a good deal...whole place is bloody noisy, with a share of drunk regulars being greeted by an abundant staff...ahi tuna was excellent, quesadillas were packed with chicken, wings were disappointing and not helped by a poorly chosen 'maple' sauce...nice selection of micros scrawled on a board, six bucks a piece...they graciously allowed us to take out a fourth 'starter' at the same half price...the whole (limited to burgers, sandwiches, etc, nothing fancy here) menu was most reasonably priced, everyday and easily found pub grub you never feel particularly good after eating...it serves it's purpose but 50/50 i'll pull in next time passing...
Drinks amazing! Atmosphere was very relaxed! I ordered salmon with broccoli and potatoes, potatoes were gross but the rest was amazing!!
Worst pork chop I have had in a decade. So overdone that I could not even chew it. Do not order under any circumstance. Absolutely awful!!
So they kick out people who wre having a good time regardless of alcohol or not... You can dance so bye bye

Went here for some beers on a friday, my friends beer tasted really odd, little funky. my beer was ok, my friend had the pretzel strips, meh, pretzel strips. Finished our beers and bounced. Probably won't be back.

If you've ever traveled the roads on any of the islands in Paradise, then you surely have had plate lunch - the staple mid-day meal that consists of two scoops of rice, a scoop of macaroni salad and a choice of meat. Often served at a roadside stand or more likely from a food truck, it's the island go-to for Kam'aina (local or resident; aka child of the land).

L&L is the real deal and it was quite a surprise to find a coupon advertising it in a local coupon mag. We made a bee-line for the franchise and found it to be just like the ones we visited on Oahu and Kauai. Affordable lunch served by congenial wait staff.

The Kalua pork was tender and moist--relatively bland contrasted to Tennessee barbecue; the generous portion proved to be enough for two meals (or to share). The Chicken Katsu was spot on: golden brown, crispy coating on a tender chicken breast. Be sure to dip in the accompanying Katsu sauce...but don't count the calories. They're pretty high.

A big disappointment was the Poke. I am a Poke aficionado and I've tasted everything from commercially made Poke to (my) Hawaiian family's recipe--literally prepared on the beach with fresh-speared fish. Both the Shoyu (bland) and the Spicy (ugh mayo-like dressing hiding any true flavor) fell far short of the mark. The Spicy did have a mild and lingering heat, but that's the extent of it. Where's the Limu, bruddah?

I am anxious to return and try some of the other offerings. The Spam Musubi (which we have made at home) and the Loco Moco looked good. (For some people anything covered in brown sauce is good.) My guess is, they probably are. For now...(3.5) 4 stars for an inexpensive plate lunch.

Finally we have an Iron Hill Brewery in Center City, and with such a gorgeous location here at the sprawling new East Market project!

This is a big restaurant, as it should be for such a high-profile, bustling, Center City location. The wall of windows that surround Iron Hill's perimeter are breath-taking, and showcase great views of the Reading Terminal Market and the vibrant sidewalk crowds. There is an outdoor dining area just along the side of this new building, for those nicer weather times. There is music in the air, but at no point was it overwhelming to drown out great conversation with others.

Came here with a friend for lunch, and we both pretty much ordered the same entree - buffalo wings. Iron Hill gives you 10 for $13; they are juicy, and they are a pretty decent size too. You can order them crispy, which both of us did, or not. I've already been to other Iron Hills to have experience with other menu options, such as the delicious char-grilled burgers and sweet potato fries; but I'll wait to update this review on those once I come back to have them here.

And the on-site brewed beers are always a highlight when you hit an Iron Hill. Today I went for the White Iron Wit - because I'm a Belgian/wheat beer kinda guy. This particular beer will satisfy those of us in the Blue Moon crowd, with a perfect amount of orange to compliment this light-styled brew. If there ever was a better Blue Moon beer than Blue Moon itself, then this is would be it!

Our waitress just couldn't have been nicer or more attentive, and I couldn't help but be happy to reward her with a generous tip for being so fantastic.

I don't make a frequent habit of coming to these kind of pricey establishments when I fly solo, but Iron Hill proves its value when you are on a date or meeting up with friends. Iron Hill has always been a great experience for me, and I couldn't be more thrilled to now have it here in Center City!

I wanted to like this place but unfortunately it didn't live up to the hype. I went on a Saturday night with the Bf. Let's starts with the Cons: 
Parking:  There is only street parking. 
Seating:  The place is tiny so if you try to go on a weekend. Good luck with that. 
Food:  I was not impressed with the chicken whatsoever. This was sad considering this is what they are known for. The greens were horrible. They were sweet. I've never had sweet greens in my life. They were Vegan style which I somewhat appreciate but I did not come to a southern/chicken restaurant for vegan greens Throw some pork in that shit!  They also ran out of Macaroni and Cheese. The server informed us that this typically happens on busier nights Iike weekends. Ok that's no excuse!  I did not come to a FOOD establishment for them to tell me they ran out of food. Sounds like it's something that happens frequently. Well then do something to prevent it!
I also had the mashed potatoes with gravy. Everything tastes flavorless. I had to put tons of salt on my mashed potatoes despite them having gravy on then. My chicken was tasteless. They give you two sauces for your chicken. One is a weird sweet yogurt concoction. The other was hot sauce. The yogurt one was very sweet. I found it to be a very weird thing to offer as a sauce for chicken. The other was hot sauce. Probably some store bought brand transferred into a clear bottle they are trying to pass off as their own. The server recommended mixing the two and that was a great idea. I enjoyed the sauce concoction better than the chicken. That's Not good!
Menu:  Lots of items I wanted to try where no longer on the menu. Not sure whose fault that Is but if you are looking at the yelp menu they don't have a lot of the items listed. Speaking of menus even the ones inside the restaurant were dated and tacky. Items were crossed out with marker and the menu was literally a piece of paper taped to cardboard. 
Pros:  
Seating: They have the no wait app so that helps out with the limited seating. 
Staff:  The staff was really nice. Several different ones helped out by filling our drinks and running our food out. 
Food:  The Pimento cheese was good and filling. This was the only food item that I enjoyed. That's not good considering it's an appetizer. 
Drinks:  The flavored sodas are amazing. I had the Mango. I wanted to try the toasted almond one but they no longer serve it despite it being listed on the menu. (See cons. Section A)
Decor:  The decor is pretty neat and different. 

Overall I won't be returning especially since Sweetie Pies is right around the corner.

It was Sunday around 8:30 when I landed at this place. The place looked in decent part of town but something did not feel like a traditional eatery. Well I settled down and the waitress (very young) who was awesome in service and smiled all the  time attended me. I looked at wine menu and ordered La Crema Pinot Nior. She came back and informed me they are out of it and she selected the wine for me. I don't want to criticize the place but here are some suggestions if they do want to thrive from someone who has owned dozen Indian restaurants in last 30 years.

1) Don't cook or advertise it as tandoori if you don't even have tandoor and you going to advertise it as tandoori dishes. I was so offended that I came in order the tandoori chicken, keema naan and glass of La Crema and they were out of wine and chicken was not fully cooked inside. 

Rest do keep the girl who served me otherwise I would not even taken my food to go and pay and leave. By the way my tab was $37 and I still tipped $5 and paid and left like gentlemen. 


Work on improving or you will be another one in flames. From someone who knows your business too well and been doing it since you 1986.

A very nice Publix.  Had it been here before I started shopping at the Kroger less than a mile away, this might be my local grocer. 

Trying to park in the front lot can be a comedy though. In my most recent visit I thought I was on a hidden camera show! Every time I spied a spot, it was gone by the time I got to it!  Save yourself the frustration and park in the covered lot nearby.

If you are looking for a local grocery store, don't hesitate to give this Publix a try.

I've been going here for a year and a half and I love it!!! Front desk is super friendly and have a variety of equipment so I get to change my workouts based on what I want to do such as cardio, lifting, etc. Only downside is that they don't have a variety of exercise bands compared to the other Crunch facilities I go to like the one in Tampa Palms. BUT One of the front desk associates let me borrow her own personal one which I really appreciated 

It tends to get reaalllllly busy during certain times mostly the evening and early morning. To the point where I actually have to schedule what workouts I do based on what time I go. but I say afternoon is the best if you have gymtimidation and want to workout in peace.

Hadn't heard of Chad's before. Dining highlights included excellent cheese biscuits (we asked for seconds) and a good salad for a steakhouse (homemade dressings and they offer feta or blue cheese crumbles). Angus prime rib was very thick and tasty, however, part of it was overcooked (which in my case wasn't a detraction). Babyback ribs were fall-off-the-bone and the BBQ sauce declicious. Service was excellent and the waitress very attentive, although we went on a slow night. Decor has an outdated, tacky Western theme...wish atmosphere was was more updated for the price we paid. Entrees are a little more pricey than other steakhouses such as Silver Saddle and El Corral/Pinnacle Peak. Might go again....

Can anyone say old school? Cuz that's what this casino reminds me of. Maybe I'm just biased because I'm so used to Las Vegas fancy lights and high tech casinos. I was not impressed with this casino when I first saw it. The sign was so colorful that it looks like a kid had design the sign. Casino itself is small and not a lot of machines to play at. The only cool part was definitely the oyster bar. It is a bridge over the freeway so you can sit there all day long getting some oyster shot and watching traffic go by. Advice, don't try the oyster shots combo. Not a pretty sight mixing tequila, vodka and bloody mary on an empty stomach. Do experience this place, but it's not the same at all like LV.

My experience at McCrossen's was rather disappointing.  I dined with a friend before attending a preview at the Phila. Museum of Art.  Lunch was Bouilliabaise   $22.00 for a bowl of mostly emply shells and a broth that was seasoned with liquid smoke.  Come on guys,  $22 bucks for this.  The sweet potato fries were smothered with salt; couldn't taste the potato.   Our server who was a young Asian/American woman shouted at us as though we were both hearing impaired.   The place has a very warm ambience but the service and food were substandard.   We will not return.

Treadmills and ellipticals always broken. It's the exception instead of the rule to see someone wipe down a machine when finished. Canceled my membership and continued to be charged for another 4 months. Had to cancel the card all together to have the bills stop..

The tip of the day for this place is come early. We've been here numerous times and its always busy. Menu is unique with allot of breakfast options. I always get the Greek Fritata and have never been disappointed. Prices are a bit high for breakfast but they do have a self serve coffee station for while you wait.

This is probably my least favorite place to eat.... The menu consist of overly breaded, and salted fried chicken. There were a few sides, and three sandwiches to choose from. One of the appetizers on the menu was corn nuts...Yes you read correctly, I did in fact say corn nuts. The menu was just two sided and the other side was full of every beer you can think of...from the cheap piss-water beer to the foreign, and also some local beers as well (the beer menu was quite impressive I must say. The fresh squeezed lemonade was delicious, and the atmosphere was nice. The outside seating area was roomy and open with umbrellas covering a few of the tables. Seating on the patio is all shared/community tables...you may be the first to your table but others may join you.
The staff was polite and attentive. Refilling drinks without us having to ask, and checking in every so often making sure we didn't need anything else. 
Will I go back? Yes, for drinks and only drinks!!!

Me and my boyfriend came here after doing a little Black Friday shopping. The first thing I noticed was the unique shape of the bar which seemed to offer a ton of seating! We sat down and ordered some wings which truth be told were pretty great. I would definitely come back here The Mile's wings. My boyfriend ordered a sandwich and I ordered the Born to Run wrap which was huge! The only issue I had with the wrap was that it was a bit dry and would have been great with an actual side of salsa. The fries that went along with it were good and they had a great beer selection. We'll definitely be coming back here!

Great store to grab things you need! Quick coffee to go or grab a breakfast sandwich.  It can get very busy in this store.  I went there once and they were limiting how many people could enter so there was a line down the street.  Otherwise very quick and easy convenience store.

I eat a lot of Thai food and lived there for several years, so I bring a different perspective than the average "farang". I gave this three stars based on our first visit and will certainly be back to try other dishes. Prices seemed a bit high, but I'm willing to pay them if they use quality ingredients and prepare them well consistently. Suggestion: The duck curry is a small (and expensive) dish which cools quickly. You could probably afford to include a bit more meat, but PLEASE warm that big square plate before placing the food on it! I dislike choosing between savoring a good meal and rushing so it doesn't get cold.

Service was good and friendly, decor is simple but clean, which is fine with me. Looking forward to my next visit.

Update:

Been here three times now and probably won't be back.  No better than average for Thai places and priced higher.  I had a seafood soup dish, meal-size (not just a bowl as we think of soup).  It had exactly three small shrimp, four muscles, some curls of squid, and a few small bits of other fish and veg.  The broth was clear and nearly tasteless, and there were virtually no spices despite asking for "level 5".  That meal didn't deserve even one star for taste or value.  Other dishes have ranged from below average to OK, and there are too many options to settle for that.

Sorry guys, but I'm moving on.

Pay attention to the Stars people!  This is not a happy place.  Too many disgruntled employees.  After you put your name in for a seat, listen carefully because they seem to whisper the names and will forget you in a minute.  Not a pleasant experience!  Still waiting for food.  Been here an hour.  Blah!  Denny's home office pay attention!  This place will put you down if you give them the chance.

Had a burger with jalapeño poppers on the side. Tasted pretty good but took forever and made me sick. Ended up being a pricey, wasted meal. The service is poor and many of my friends wanted leave midway through the meal. Hindsight being 20/20 we should have eat elsewhere.

PHENOMENAL pizza!  It's not typical pizza ... it's an experience!  We ordered the  Great White ... Oh. My. Heavens.  Garlic white sauce, artichoke hearts, chicken, sun-dried tomatoes, caramelized onions (omg!!!), basil, garlic - To. Die. For.  The dough/crust isn't typical pizza dough - it's like ... an airy, crispy FRESH slice of heaven.  Will forever come here for an amazing meal! ALSO - hidden treasures - their FRESH BREAD ... you are missing out!!!  Do not go here and leave without a loaf of their fresh AMAZING bread. . . It takes your breath away.  It's like a mix between a baguette and sourdough but it is airy and lite and CRUNCHY crust but a fluffy cloud inside - you have to try it to understand!

GO HERE!!!!!!  I am beyond impressed!!!!!

I love the Fox Theatre.  I have been here many times over the years for musicals and a concert.  It is beautiful and the sound is always awesome.  I would love to give it a 5/5 however the most recent time I was here they made me a little mad.
I came with Kate B and a few other friends to see Book of Mormon.  The show was awesome but what pissed me off was all the people moving around.  The staff did not do anything to stop it.  For the first 10-15 minutes we had people constantly still coming into the theater.  This is simply unacceptable.  I hate to gripe but whatever.  I will still give you a 4/5 but I really wish I was giving you a full 5/5.

Would NOT recommend this place. 0 stars if possible. I ordered curbside pickup at 6:18 p.m. scheduled for 7:00. During the order they collected vehicle make model and tag and advice to park to the side in curbside pickup area. Arrived at 6: 55, waited till 7:15 because there had been no communication I called the check the status of my order (I had to look up the number as new number was provided in the order info). Someone answered the phone collected my name and vehicle color and said "aight" and then immediately hung up. I waited another 15 minutes, called again and no one is answering the phone now. I go in and they laughed about hanging up on me and then got pissy and said I had an attitude. They refuse to refund my order unless I waited in a busy restaurant for a manager. At this point I've already waited 45 minutes on them and I'm not interested in them wasting more of my time, as they didn't seem to even care my food was late or missing and they had hung up on me, ignored my calls and then laughed about it. I WILL NOT return to this restaurant. AVOID this place, there's much better food in the area with much better customer service. I can understand if my order is late, especially due to staffing issues etc, but not the lack of communication and outright rudeness of the staff.

Well my son got a used car from this place  the sales department was good. But the salesman did not really have a clue. We asked if the car he was buying had a spare key. The salesmen stated that he could not check if there was a spare key for the car we bought because the department was closed locked up. 
So after several emails to the salesman who sold us the car. Because he never did get back to us about the spare key. He did tell us that we could buy one for $250.00. The salesman stated that because this was not a certified car they did not have one. We were never advised of this that we would have to buy a spare key. So be aware of this place if you are looking for a used car. Make sure they have a spare key if not you will have to pay alot of money for one.

Prepare yourself for $6.50 local bottled beer (that's $165 a case) and no other customers to talk to.. this place needs some thought given to their pricing..  They offer 2 for 1's but the base price is so far off base for the loud, sterile atmosphere, it's not worth it..

Been going to The Keg in Phoenix for years. Decided to go out while in Tucson (Oro Valley) for our Anniversary. Service as always at The Keg was great. Food was delicious and reasonably priced. Very attentive staff to details, such as the fact it was our Anniversary. Seated us with a card signed by the staff and the manager came over at the end of our meal and provided us a complimentary champagne toast. 
Stopped at the bar before dinner and took advantage of a glass of wine at happy hour price! 
Thanks for a great night!
Seated much quicker than anticipated according to the online waitlist. However, our meal was fair at best.. Prefer quality over quantity.
We absolutely love this place!!!!!!!! Great beer, staff and music!!!!! Can't wait to go back!!!

Disclaimer: this is only for the sandwich I had. The cheese selection seemed impressive but I didn't study it closely or buy any.

I ordered the Hook's Cheddar (grilled) and it was a very good sandwich, but I can't see myself returning here just for that. If I need a hard to find cheese I may return, but otherwise I'm not sure I'd come back just for another sandwich. 

Service was fine, no issues there. Again, I have no complaints, I just didn't love the place for the price.

New owners and apparently they don't accept gift certificates from previous owner regime...even though it was just purchased for my birthday in September.  Really, $10 is gonna break you? More importantly- a measly $10 is gonna lose me and my friends as customers. Good luck! See ya uncle fats when another (smarter business person) takes over.

The views. THE VIEWS. The views. 

My first trip to R2L happened at a particularly good time; my group arrived at the start of happy hour and we caught a snow squall. That's pretty damn cool at 37 floors up. 

We were able to relax in the lounge facing west with table service and all. I didn't eat anything, but I was perfectly happy with my Sunset Punch and Malbec to sip on. 

Views with happy hour? Yeah, I'm pretty much sold.

Wow! We chose the white chocolate raspberry bundt cake and it was amazing. The cake is extremely moist and flavorful and pairs perfectly with the cream cheese frosting. Although all the flavors I've tried were delicious, this might actually top them all. The customer service  and cleanliness of the environment is excellent. I've already put an order in for my next cake. Definitely give this place a try. You won't regret it.

Our daughter wanted ice cream after a few hours running all around the City Museum. This place one of the closer options for ice cream so we tried it out. A few of the frozen yogurt machines were not working correctly, the yogurt coming out of them was all liquidy, not frozen. We also had to wait in a line to pay for so long, since the people before us kept changing their minds and adding more yogurts and toppings. We weren't allowed to get a spoon until after we paid, since you weigh your bowl to pay based on the weight, and by the time we had paid and gotten a spoon my daughters frozen yogurt was all melted. Paid 7 bucks for a bowl of melted frozen yogurt with a couple sprinkles. Not worth it.

This store was incredibly rude, my 7 year old niece had to use the bathroom so bad and they made us leave the store and go somewhere else. We also had a newborn in the car and ended up adding but 15 minutes to the trip. I can't believe they would treat a little kid like that. She almost wet herself, and wAs so embarrassed. Won't be coming back here with that kind of service.

Best pho in Chinatown. Cheapest too. And probably best decor if that's your thing. Dac biet's got a little of everything (as long as your not opposed to a little offal). Decent service. Quick. Family affair. Just keep in mind $10 min for credit.

4 stars . Could have been five, but bad attitude with the reception area entering the ER. Other than that the hospital is gorgeous. My first time here so I am wondering are the doctors any good. The Interim  Louisiana  Hospital has moved here. I was kind of sad because I got used to the raged dy hospital with  bad attitude workers and long wait time. I was expecting that from that place, but now you have this amazing  built Hospital  and they just transferred all the attitudes workers here along withe the waiting time. Lol. Hopefully the doctors are nice and can diagnosed me.
*****************************************
      Okay I had arrived here at 10 pm. You have to get a parking pass when entering into the garage but I later found out I can have it signed and you won't have to pay as long as it is validated. This place is nice and big. I totally was getting lost but that's ok too because there were signs and so I eventually just followed the signs. There's the hospital side and then there's the ER.  I had to go to the Er. I thought I had strep throat and so I wanted to make sure I was safe considering the fact in have a One year old. Ok upon entering the facility,  there's the reception area and the employees were Not really as attentive to customers.  I guess they figured we should put two and two together and use the Kiosk to enter our  information.  So I was standing in line , one lady was on the phone and the other lady was directing us to use the kiosk. By the way, you will receive a number/alphabet  and it will show on the screen as well as what room to go to. Now here's where the bamboozled happen.  I go into a room tell the nurse what's wrong she gave me my bracelet with all my information and a set of stickers with my information.  Ok surprise you guys!!! Stage 2 waiting room. It's  1045 am and it's two other people waiting. That's it.  I literally took a nap for an hour and some change until they called my name.  Then also they had no kleenex to blow my nose i had to use tissue. Im like you mean to tell ke this beautiful place do not have any tissues?There was a television and nice cozy waiting  room. It just took forever to be seen by a doctor . So at around  12:20 they called woke me up from my sleep. I'm so tired everything they did aggrevated me but I managed to  still be cordial. These nurses and doctors were nicer.  The staff seem to know what they were doing. I liked how one of the nurses actually engaged in conversations with me and it made me feel more comfortable. Once I was diagnosed  and everything  I waited again for about an hour just to get discharged. I left the garage at 3:15. Let me drop this bomb on yall. There were hardly no one in there and they took their precious  time.  But I can't get angry because  it is the ER after all and that's just the way of the World I guess!
*******************************************
    Let me tell yah like this....If you are going to come to the ER clear your schedule  have yourself a meal and bring you some water because Aiant no telling how the cookie may crumble. This place is beautiful  and we'll decorated. I think the staff at the reception  counter when you enter the ER could have been more friendlier and also the waiting time was just crazy. I now know. OTHER than that This place is amazing and the doctors seemed to know what they were doing. Very clean by the way!

~ Shame on you, Ventimiglia's! Tony's is an Alton landmark restaurant that you have shamed because a family member could not keep it in his pants! Family or not, this woman should have been paid MUCH more than a measly $75k. Shame on you!

http://blogs.riverfronttimes.com/dailyrft/2010/11/dry-humping_manager_costs_alton_restaurant_75k.php
better than Geno's, but still just an overpriced tourist trap. you can get a better steak at most pizza joints and lunch carts.

Did someone say sandwiches? Well, if sandwiches are what you want then this is the place to come for Breakfast and Lunch . The service is quick and fast. Don't be fooled by what you see on the outside! these are the best sandwiches you'll get... from anywhere. I visit Tampa for work from New Jersey and I always have to stop in for a sandwich you also get to enjoy of other different types of dishes.

They are always behind even if there is 10 people working and 3 customers. They always take the phone off the hook. The last 2 times I went in the pretended they didn't know and the other time told me it was dead and they didn't know. Quality of food has dropped drastically in the last few months
This place is awesome!  We got seated quickly and the service was excellent.  I had the Feel Good juice, delicious and the German Omelet, also delicious.  We will be back!

Disappointed last night.  Took forever to place order and receive food.  Appetizers came out piece meal.  I ordered sides and they came out 10 minutes before the meal.  I questioned the waiter about it and he said he thought we wanted them as appetizers. I said, OK but we have no plates or side dishes as you cleared them. LAME.  Meal was average.

My dad, his girlfriend, my sons and myself went to Friendly's last night around 5 for dinner. When we sat down and our waitress finally came over my dad's GF lets her know she has a groupon that she would like to use. The waitress says ok and proceeds to ask us our order. We get our food and now its time for ice cream she takes all of our orders and asks my dad's GF what she would like, she says vanilla with strawberry topping, the waitress says what topping? and my dad's GF say strawberry she says ok you get another topping so his GF says wet walnuts. I asked for a lid for my son's honey mustard. She comes out about 20 minutes later with our ice cream and my dad's GF has strawberry ice cream with wet walnuts -_-, so she gets corrected and says she will bring out the correct one. After all is said and done we have ate our ice cream and its time to pay the check, my dad's GF says again that she has the groupon, the waitress said ok I will be right back. She sends the manager over and the manager tells us that we can not use it there and we can only use it at the outlets....Um why didn't she say this when she was told about thew groupon before? The groupon was for $30.00 our bill was about 56 the manager comes over with a $5 off coupon?? Seriously? your waitress didn't listen to us the whole time we were there and you bring this over? Not sure if I will return...maybe we will go to the outlets so we can use our groupon.
I put a pizza box on the $250 table pad and now I have a big square on it. So, no it does not withstand heat. Their solution was to buy another one! Horrible customer service!

Excellent ambiance, food is decent. This place really needs to work on its service. 

We were four for dinner. I think there were only two waiters for the evening, and even with the four or so tables that were occupied, they had a hard time.

The open kitchen had one lonely chef running all over the place. There should have been at least four more people back there. 

Our food came very late, it was good, but the lack of service put a damper on the experience.

I will give it another try because I liked the food and the decor is very trendy/unique.

It has potential to be a good spot, just hire more staff!

All the 5 star reviews here are right! These meatballs are amazing. I've been to this truck a few times now and they are always consistent. You know you are going to get a great meatball no matter what. They are beyond fresh and you can taste it. The ones that are stuffed with cheese have the perfect amount in it that it doesn't take away from the meat itself. The other flavors are also on point. Seasoned with just the right amount of ingredients and they blend well together.

I first had them at the Manayunk Streat food festival and have been hooked ever since. Service is amazing and pricing is right. I can come, get the 3 meatball sandwich and walk away feeling full for a great price. Trust me on this....don't split it with someone. You will regret!

Overall: I will keep coming back as long as they keep up with the quality and service they provide! AMAZING!

I wish it had zero stars cause that's what I would give. Called up there twice today to ask what time they close. Girl said 6. Walk in about 5:30 and was told they were closed already. I told the girl well I called up here and was told 6. She looked at me like I know it was me with a smirk and said we're closed. Then there was a gentleman working behind the counter that wanted to throw his rude comments in. Horribly ran. Definitely need to weed out your employees. Very RUDE STAFf

I was happy to have finally found a place that does threading so I went there. Ends up the lady threaded around my eyebrow and then proceeded to pluck my actual eyebrows for 10 mins. If I knew she was going to shape them by plucking, I would have just gone to the waxing place instead.
Best tri tip dip sandwich I've ever had! Meat is so tender. Love this little place! Will be the place to go for Virginia City trips! And no fries but potato salad was great!!!!!

We always head out to BWW on Tuesdays or Thursday's fir their traditional Tuesdays or boneless Thursdays. 

Food is always good. Prices are decent. Large variety of sauces. I like the jammin jalapeño, Parmesan garlic, mild, or honey BBQ.

Service is hit or miss here. The best waitress I've ever had is Gabby. When she is working we only like to sit in her section. If she isn't there we sit at the bar. 

Check this place out
Staff was extremely rude. Didn't want to give me my side of peppers after I paid for it. And they use frozen "steakums". Very poor quality sandwiches.

I read the reviews on Yelp regarding the Cuban's tasty prowess. I went there and got two but was sadly disappointed. Traditional Cubans have tender chunks of fresh roast pork, ham and Swiss with pickles. El Punto's had ham, bacon, charred shards of what I think was roast pork and something that resembled mortadella.  Not what I was expecting.  A disappointment.

Overall this place had a lot of character and was an okay stop for my group on Frenchman Street. There was a one drink minimum per person and a live band playing when we walked in. Hurricane drinks were $12 and beer a little less than that. The band was nothing special but a little pushy about getting tips and came back to our table twice in about 45 min shaking their tip jar despite some of our group throwing in a dollar or two the first time. 

I was in a large group looking to eat and we were able to be seated in the back all together which was nice. The server, Josh, was great and made sure to correct my order when it didn't come out right the first time (kitchens mistake). I would sick to the po boy sandwiches if I was eating there again as the burgers all came out well done and were dry. The rest of my group seemed to enjoy their po boys though. 

Women's bathrooms were what you would expect for a bar but usable.

We stopped by Mike's to cool off after sitting in the sun at LP Field all afternoon. It was the perfect post-game spot. Surprisingly, we didn't have trouble getting a table, and the line to get ice cream wasn't very long. It got longer while we were sitting there, but seemed to move quickly. At $2.99 a scoop it isn't cheap, but it is a generous scoop (the scoop pretty much fills an entire cup). I got the Red Velvet Cake which was perfect... it even had bits of red velvet cake swirled in it.  The people who work here are very friendly (I'd even say flirty) which makes it fun also.

I love that the flavors are either homemade or locally produced.  There were a lot of interesting flavors like maple bacon, which I unfortunately was not daring enough to try.  I'm sure that everything is good, and I only wish I lived closer so that I could go back and try them all!

Very confused about all of the high ratings.  Perhaps my visit was a fluke...

Firstly, the food is really good.  I had the lunch special with sweet-n-sour soup, spring role, and a mushroom-stuffed tofu skin dish.  The price is really good too - about $7-$8 for the lunch special.

But man, was it a bad idea to come here during the weekday lunch rush.  They only had a *single* waiter for the *whole* restaurant, one table runner, and one host (who also was cleaning tables).  15-20 min passed and no one came to our table after being seated, so we had to flag down the waiter.  Overall, it was about an hour before we even got food, which is really not doable for a work lunch.

After scanning other reviewers, I can only guess that perhaps they came at off hours since I notice many folks are from out-of-town.

Would perhaps consider take out, but certainly not eating there again.

YUMMY!  I have been here a few times and have meant to post a review. This is a staple for rittenhouse area. The pizza is delicious and consistent, The rotolo is amazing, and they have a great beer selection that rotates appropriately. 
I have never made it to dessert here but have witnessed others enjoying it. 
I have had the Caesar, the shaved cauliflower, the arugula and the roasted vegetable salads and all are unique and delicious. 
I recently had an out of town visitor that commented she couldn't wait to return to Philly for the rotolo.....this place is that good...you can't stop thinking about going back. I'm glad it's "in my backyard".

It was amazing, everything from appetizers to the main course.
Please try the Mac and Cheese with Bacon, you are going to want more!
I had the Coconut Curry Salmon, it melts in your mouth, a friend had the Pork with Plum sauce, I stole a bit and it was delicious as well. What makes me think you can't go wrong with what you choose on their menu!

Eat up and Enjoy!!

I ordered the mushroom sticky rice & the honey cashew chicken through Caviar. 
The sticky rice was so overwhelmed by the truffle oil that the addition of the mushrooms was simply ornamental. The chicken dish was not served with rice & it was not clear that the extra rice that could be ordered was not extra at all. 
Had I been in the restaurant I would have returned both items & asked for suggestions. And despite the fact that I ate very little I have spent much of this morning close to the loo!
As it is I shall contact Caviar to let them know how substandard this restaurant's offerings are.

I had a great experience with Kevin and First Class Blinds and Shutters.  I was shopping for 21 windows in a new home.  I got a few quotes from Lowes and Home Depot under the Bali and Hunter Douglas lines.  After choking on the quoted prices - I decided to check out some small businesses.  Kevin quoted ALTA blinds with the features and materials I chose at 40% less than my other quotes.  The ALTA warranty is comforting, but the initial quality suggests I won't need to make a warranty claim.  Kevin was more than accommodating with evening appointments for quotes and install.  Communication was effortless.  He was very professional throughout the process and checked in twice AFTER install to make sure we were satisfied.  Very happy with the experience and most importantly (for me) the price!

Came here for the hibachi though my daughter had the all you can eat sushi. Luckily we had reservations. It got crowded fast. The food was quite good and the price was very reasonable. You get soup, salad, hibachi and ice cream. The performance by the hibachi chefs were great as usual. For my daughter's sushi, she got an appetizer and all the sushi she could order from the menu. She was charged the kids price (17.95) though she ate like an adult. We will definitely be back next time we're in Reno.

My husband and I went to Vetri last night to celebrate my birthday. The atmosphere was cozy and refreshingly low-key for such a refined meal. 

We were greeted with glasses of Prosecco and asked our food allergies and aversions. We informed the servers (I say servers because we were asked for this information multiple times which seemed like a good sign) no shellfish and no beef so we can share everything.
