I was served TWO shellfish courses that ignored this request. I am mildly allergic, but it could have poisoned my husband if he tried to snag a bite. And a dead spouse makes for a terrible birthday present...unless you hate them. Then maybe it's just what you always wanted. But, I am digressing. 

The first time it happened I just let it go and shuffled the food around. The second time a server was happy to replace it. But really, when a meal costs upwards of $400 dollars including tax and tip, this shouldn't happen. You should be able to trust what you are served. I think this can be a consequence of a serving style where everyone pitches in. Things get lost in whisper down the lane. This seems to have happened to other reviewers as well, so I'm not alone. 

That said, everything we could eat was absolutely delicious. So to be fair, I'll split the difference in my rating between the very good and the very bad.

If service wasn't so slow and the bartender completely unknowledgeable of how to make pretty standard shots, they may have gotten themselves a fourth star based solely upon atmosphere and the delicious Pujols Salad I had.

I and some co-workers came to Pujols 5 for a happy hour celebration on a Wednesday night.  They arrived much earlier than I, so I missed out on the apps, but reports on my arrival sounded promising.  My mixed drink and martini (Italian Apple) were both good.  The shots, as stated above were sub-par.  The bartender didn't know how to make many of them, and instead of saying she didn't, she asked around and kind of fudged them.  The Royal Flush did not really taste like a Royal Flush.  Service was also pretty slow, even when the crowd started dying down.

The saving grace for not so great service - the Pujols Salad.  I highly recommend it and would even consider coming back just for it given the chance.  Spring mixed greens, chicken, strawberries, grapes, blue cheese, and green beans in a in house dressing.  There were also spicy pecans, which I personally wasn't a fan of, but that's just me - I'm not a fan of spicy.  I'd just do without next time.  The salad was a taste of spring on a chilly early March night, and was welcomed indeed.  The ingredients were fresh and well prepared.  A little on the pricey side, but I guess you'll have that in a Westport sports bar.  The portion also made up for some of the sticker shock.

This is the salon I've been looking for my whole life! Steph has been doing my hair for years now, and she always does a great job. She listens, and gives me the cut and color I ask for. Plus I always have a great time when I'm there!

came here to celebrate our anniversary and i don't think we could have spent it anywhere else! from the second we walked in until we left we were congratulated about our anniversary and greeted warmly!

customer service was truly a 20/10. our waiter was phenomenal and they even called me the next day to make sure we had a great experience

i do have to say the ambience is a little strange. it's a nice restaurant but then they were playing almost dancey bar music? not that i don't like it but it didn't really fit the vibe of the restaurant, but might just be me.

now for the food... 
we started with east coast oysters and calamari/shrimp. the oysters were great but the calamari and shrimp i've definitely had better. i did appreciate the array of sauced and jalapenos

we both got the boneless rib eye, au gratin potatoes, mushrooms and brussel sprouts. i'm not gonna lie i wasn't wow'd. definitely not the best steak meal i've ever had.
the rib eye was a little bit chewy and wasn't melt in my mouth butter steak which i'm used to. the brussel sprouts were a little underdone 
i did like the potatoes and mushrooms but so many mushrooms were in these tiny little shredded pieces. 

overall i'd give it a 4/5. customer service was amazing, no other steakhouse compares, but i've definitely had better steak in the city.

Cute little spot great decor. I only had beer it was good. The man that looked like simon cowell was super friendly and advised me that they do paint nite and yoga etc   the only downfall is i found i couldnt easily hear the people i was talking with  ..not sure if the noise just bounced off walls or the busy traffic in front (oh thats also a bonus free street parking in front) :)

Tried Zesto for the first time. We had to have second pizza sent back because all the cheese had slid to one side upon delivery. The second pizza that arrived had way too much cheese on it and again had shifted. I can't blame Zestos for the delivery, but overall experience was not good. The pizza probably needs to slightly cool before being run out the door.

Finally, South Tampa is no longer an Asian food desert. Prior to Noodle Kitchen, Before-NK(B.N.K.)South Tampanians like me had to venture north of Kennedy Blvd to find good mostly authentic Asian food. Imagine the horror. Now that is no longer the case. I ordered the egg noodle bowl with tofu and with all of the included vegetables on it except for bok choy. I also took some pineapple chili sauce and thai chili powder from their complimentary sauces and spices bar. All of the sauces and spices are created in the restaurant. However I recommend the pineapple chili sauce because the other sauces mainly sambal chili and anchovy vinegar are extremely spicy. The noodles were delicious and so were the tofu and vegetables. However they do not make their own tofu and I'm pretty sure that they do not make their own noodles from scratch. The price of course is around 1.5 to 2 times the price you would pay for similar Asian food north of Kennedy, however, considering it is located in the trendy SoHo district, the prices are not too bad. The ambiance and design of the place is reminiscent of a hipster Asian-American takeout joint that is frequented by upper-middle class millennials. One of my major complaints about the Noodle Kitchen is the lack of tofu in my noodle bowl. Only six pieces of tofu in a $14 noodle bowl. That is just atrocious! At Thinh An Kitchen and Tofu I can get a much larger noodle bowl with a lot more tofu for only $8.00. The service, however, is quick just like at Thinh An Kitchen & Tofu. Parking is often very scarce especially on weekends, but that applies to almost any place in the SoHo district. Overall, the best place to get good authentic Asian food south of Kennedy Blvd.

Love the beer selection. Drinking Gumball Head on the patio!!! #favoritebeer pizza is awesome...try the Stockman or Philly Cheesesteak....hog wings are a good way to start the meal. We have had Chris as a server and he is on top of everything....

Saying this place is amazing is an understatement. They have quite possibly some of the freshest and most delicious food I've ever had (we got the Giant Pretzel and the Impossible Burger). The staff was incredibly friendly and knowledgeable and even went out of their way to accommodate our dietary restrictions. They have definitely gained a regular!

The food was ok, but a bit bland. We love Indian food, so we have experience with which to compare this. Here, medium spicy seems to mean bland. I will compliment them on the rice, as that is so often a weak point. 
The real problem was the service. We were only the second table when we arrived, and the other was occupied by just one person. The woman server seemed nice enough, if not warm and friendly. But, the man was just rude. He doesn't even seem to know how to place dishes when they arrive; he just handed the platters/bowls to us and we had to set them down. His entire attitude was one of being irritated - by the presence of customers!  As we were leaving, he rushed up to a table of two women who arrived after we did. He snapped at them that they had to pay cash, because his card processor was down or something.  They were both startled and neither had enough cash on them. I don't know how that worked out. I'll just reiterate that this man, apparently the owner, is unpleasant. None of us at dinner - 3 of whom live nearby - will go back.
You got be kidding me. The food is trash. One of the two stars I gave is for the drink I got, which is actually good.

Went there today for father's day early dinner (i.e. the place was not that busy). Some of the food was very good, some not so much. I ordered their bacon bbq burger, two double patties. The bacon look old and unappetizing and the burgers were overcooked, flavorless and the uniformity of frozen beef. For $19.50 I would expect a fantastic burger. Also service was just OK. After we finished up, got some to go boxes after had to wait to get the bills and once given, after waiting about 10 minutes, had to go to the bar and get a manger to finish the transaction. Overall not a thrilling experience.

On Tuesdays they have Salsa Dancing Lessons for $5, before the actual dance party begins...pretty good teachers too and some serious dancers.  I was there on a Tuesday with some friends, I wasn't able to dance, but I had so much fun watching!

I didn't have any food, but did have a drink...a Pisco Sour, truthfully it wasn't that great, but I'll try something else next time.

Seems to always be an issue here. Black mold runs around in some apartments. They tell you to sign a non disclosure agreement instead and then you move having to pay for new furniture. Mold tech has been out several times for this issue. Stay away.

No matter what service, or upgrade I pay for, it always comes out looking the same. My advice, get the basic.  They are so fast that they do a poor job, I like that they are fast don't get me wrong, but you should get what you pay for (it's not cheap)! They always say up front that they do a "light dust" on the inside ... I'm calling bs on that one for sure.  Go cheap... do it yourself or pay for the $8 outside wash they offer. Never upgrade!!!!

I was very happy to be turned onto this downtown to-go restaurant. They specialize in fried chicken wings and legs but also offer a full menu with catfish, burgers, day/weekend specials, etc. The ordering process was seamless - I texted my order and we finalized back and forth. Once complete they texted a link to pay and said it would be ready in a bout 30 minutes.....and the food was delivered to my car.

The chicken was great - crispy but not greasy. I ordered our food on a Sunday around 4 and unfortunately they were already out of a lot of the side and other food options but we were about to try the spiced sweet potatoes, and smothered cabbage, which was wonderful. 

The fruit punch was also a real surprise with fruit in the bottom of the cup! It's not something I would have normally ordered it came with my meal and the kids loved it.

I think chef Donna's restaurant fills a nice void in downtown, as I'm not aware of anywhere else you can get affordable and delicious fried chicken and soul food. Kudos to her!!

The worst hotel ever. The hotel amenities listed a bar and restuarant but that was false. It also listed a refrigerator and microwave in each room, again false. We had requested a double with 2 queen sized beds but both beds were full sized.The room was very small. The bathroom was dirty. The towels were old and thin. We had to go to the front desk for additional towels and toilet paper. The free breakfast was horrible. The coffee was lukewarm and the microwave was by the ice machine down the hall. The elevator got stuck several times. NEVER STAY AT THIS HOTEL!!!!

At the request of the two ladies who helped me, I must say I had a damn good Banh Mi. Maybe I'll stop at Saigon Bakery on the way home to be sure of which is better. Can't wait to be back to try the recommended styles.

MSY is a quaint little airport on the Bayou - lots of cool restaurants inside - and a long line for the bathroom.   Otherwise - it takes a little getting used to as they are changing some things up and don't have all the signage up in the right places yet.   Not sure if it is the TSA or airport, but they need to do TSA PRE CHECK the same as in the other airports - it can be a bit confusing...
This stuff is true Thai food cuisine. My grandparents use to live in Thailand and my grandfather gave his approval. Yummy food, good service and it really quaint and homey.

Great stop for a souvenir for that cigar lover in your life.. the old guys are rolling them right in front of you here. ( see pic)
We were celebrating so my husband was happy

Worth the calories? Yes! Si! Oui! Ja!

Found this place on Yelp, and with the awesome reviews it got; decided to try it! 

Thank you fellow Yelpers you have not failed me once again! 

I have a very bad habit when it comes to unique food places that have so much variety! I want to try it all, every last bit of everything! Haha. 
So we decided to share two burgers. Jumping on the band wagon; we had to try the PBJ burger, and then picked out the Sweet Hot burger as well. Got a side of fries with cheese sauce, cookies and cream milk shake and limoncella adult beverage. Feeling like we covered our bases we waited. 

Super excited when we saw our food come out. Well... Everything was "mini me" version. The burgers were small and so was the milkshake. Oh well, size doesn't matter right? ;-) In this case... Nope! The burgers were beyond delicious!  Such interesting combinations that were perfect, so amazing. I love goat cheese, so the PBJ (NOT a peanut butter and jelly burger... that also sounds good though..) was naturally amazing. And the Sweet Hot was surprisingly delicious as well. The fries, sauce and milkshake were good. The lemoncella adult lemonade I got was great, and strong. The potions left me satisfied, not still hungry and not overly stuffed cause I couldn't control myself with all that goodness!. Thanks :-)

Service was ok. Guy was stressed out. But did his job adequately. 

And the decor was diner/industrial/shabby chic. The ambiance defiantly indicated that Baileys Range was "the spot" for hipsters, business professionals, family's.. In fact everyone. Great vibe!

My complaint; that there isn't one in downtown San Diego!

Beautiful atmosphere...loved the prime rib sandwich at Harry & Izzys and thought I was getting the same thing here....noooo...no great slices of beef.just one hunk of fatty meat...sorry Lounge..know you're new...keep trying...

I used Mark Hill and Tandem Realty for six years to rent out two of my properties.  Initially, I was pleased and recommended them to a friend.  He had issues with them early on and left.  It took me a while to catch on.  I could never get anyone on the phone, they didn't do a good job of screening applicants, they used handyman companies that I could have called and found on Google, and some of the repair work was really bad and there was no quality control.

I was invited by some family to eat here.  I enjoyed my chicken fajitas. It had a lot of flavor and it was light.  My husbands neice let me try her turkey wrap it was delicious.  Our waitress gave excellent service. I would like to return to this restaurant.

A relative gave us a generous gift certificate for the holidays.  We used it a few months back and I have to say we had a very good experience.
--The place is beautiful and we appreciated the piano player to set a mood.  Next time we will request the bar area to hear even better.  We had reservations and were seated promptly even though they were very busy..
--Our waiter came right away, explained about the restaurant and was available to answer questions.  He was only one of many that tried to make our dining experience a good one.  I do agree that it did make it a little confusing for them and us at times.  We saw that they were prepping a large table very close to us, so I suggested that we get started with our order.  (been in the business)
--We had cocktails, Garlic Chicken Flatbread, Stuffed Mushrooms & Baby Spinach Salad.  For our entrees he had Grilled Chicken w/tamarind sauce, and I had the grilled lamb chops.  We had to try a wonderful mini indulgence.  --and of course a few to go boxes taking a little of everything home!  Everything was very good, the ambiance and service were good.  We splurged because we could!  (gift cert) & had a lunch to go!
--I so agree w/Jim T. and I'm proud to have Seasons 52 here in Indy because of what they stand for.  I love their nutritional chart and their portions make eating there a no brainer to enjoy your food without worries.   
We plan to go back and take a relative that will be coming in town for the holiday.  This is her cup of tea!

Enthusiastic service is about all I can say that was positive. The buffet is small, and the dinner menu overpriced. My malai kofta was exactly as spicy as I wanted it, but it was also salty as hell and not at all creamy. Husband's chana masala was good, but watery. 

Bombay Palace has ruined me for any other Indian restaurant. :/

I enjoy the uniqueness of their menu. I love Mexican food but get bored by the sameness offered at most restaurants. I usually get takeout and the food easily makes it to my house. I love their street corn as a side. The food is made fresh as ordered. Service is quick even during busy times.

We LOVE This Place.  Best Pizza in all of Tampa!  So excited they are close to our neighborhood, so they deliver.  They care of about their quality of food and services and it's apparent. Their delivery services, is one of the best.  Besides, their Pizza we have also, had the calzone, super cheesy, YUM and the Chicken Carbonera, and Beef Lasagna, is really good.
I love going to this place. The waitresses are always so attentive, and the food is absolutely great!

An okay place to grab lunch if you are at St. Joseph's (or in the area) and craving Asian food. Otherwise, I wouldn't really come here.  The pork vermicelli was okay and I could have done without the artificial tasting thai bubble tea.
The food is amazing and so is the customer service. Breakfast is absolutely incredible. I love Sabrina's!

I have shopped at Whole Foods quite a few times.  I am not sold on the whole "organic" food movement. That said however,I think what is sold here tends to be of a higher grade than other grocery stores..and it should be at these prices. Overall I'd rate Whole Foods high..but my low rating today is caused by another customer.  The reason I gave them 2 stars is because the cashier was being TOO nice to the guy and it led to my poor experience.  I don't blame her..it stems from the touchy feely mindset that is Whole Foods but sometimes,you just gotta be an asshole.
  So, I was in the neighborhood and I had been having a little issue while relieving myself of all that food I eat in order to make reviews(Or was it my fried bologna sandwiches?).  I suspected my GI flora(bacteria) was a little out of kilter.  While things seemed to have normalized I thought it wouldn't hurt to give them a little boost.  So in I run to get some Kefir.  Kefir is basically a drinkable yogart.  What's great about Whole Foods is they have a bunch of flavors, including Vanilla, which I really dig.  So I grab that and go to get some Italian Soda.  Normally I get the orange kind(though it's been a while) but it seemed they didnt have that one.  They did have a Cranberry one which I decided to snag a liter of.  I wisk over to the express check out.  Upon arrival I notice the guy who I think is paying that is wearing a Party Patrol shirt and then the guy in front of me who seems to have about 10 items on the counter in addition to the 5 items in his basket still..in the 10 ITEM or less lane.  After a while I glance at the customer screen and notice that the total is over 200 bucks and I realize that the stuff on the counter isn't for the guy in front of me..HE is waiting on the MORON in the Party Patrol Shirt.  This guy apparently had well over 25 items.  Being the rude asshole that I am..I have a tendency to tell people when they are being STUPID rude assholes.  (I have told off quite a few customers for cashiers and wait staff over the years for  being total prick.) I figure Mr Self Centered Party Patrol is nearly done..so I won't make a scene as he is about to pay.  He then pulls out his money to pay for his $232 bill.  After a couple of minutes I think what the HELL is taking so long and pay attention again.  Apparently, this idiot that saw fit to bring WAY too many items to the express checkout had decided to pay with $1s and $5s and YES,his bill WAS $232 dollars.  I start commenting to the guy in front of me about how some people don't understand what 10 items is.  I point out to the people behind me how rude he is paying for his $232 bill at the checkout   I do this NOT quietly.  I am doing the passive aggressive thing first as I am weighing fully crawling in to his rectum and tearing him a new one. I am actually in a great mood and I make the assessment that this guy will relish the confrontation and all I will do is cause a scene which will delay myself and everyone else in the growing line so, I refrain.  The folks behind me bail for the coffee check out.  I point out Mr A-hole to the girl behind me.
She tells me "OH,he works in the quarter.He is one of the guys that goes around calling people in to bars." Well at this point he finishes and we all start to check out.  I talk to the cashier about it in a friendly manner and she mentions to me that she didn't want a confrontation,though I suspect if she had known he was going to pay her with over 100 $1s and $5s(she told me) she would have sent him to away. So here I give whole foods their first 2...because their overly friendly customer oriented mindset is part of what allowed this.. I figure this won't hurt their overall rating as they do a great job.
  Touching on this Party patrol fellow.He was 6 ft with blonde wavy hair that was short looking like it was just starting to want to get long. I researched the New Orleans Party Patrol and apparently they are a tourist scam. They walk up to people saying, "you can't do that" wearing their official looking uniform. People ask what? and they go in to some spiel about not having fun or walking too much. One of the things they do is they hand people party hats...then when the people accept them they ask for money for them. They also ask for a charitable donation to some needy cause..I won't go on too much here..but I MIGHT write THEM a review as he WAS wearing his PP shirt.  But with knowing that this guy is basically a predatorial scam artist that makes a living by confronting people..I read him totally right about how the confrontation would have ensued.  I think I might make it a point to head to the quarter and find him and ruin his scam for a night..
  But back on to whole foods..my Kefir went down smooth and yummy. It's not very sweet and has a slight sour bite to it but is rather good.   Later I drank my Cranberry Italian Soda and I must say..it's by far the best. I think I will get another today and write a good review.

I so much wanted to love this l place. I love biscuits and gravy and I followed them on Instagram.  When I saw they had a place in Reno I had to make it a point to stop on our annual trip. Vibe was good but I didnt  like other patrons hoovering over us for our table. I felt rushed to eat and free up space.

Our table ordered a variety of items. Biscuits and gravy, hashbrowns, chicken biscuit sandwich and a breakfast sandwich.  My biscuit and gravy with hashbrowns where a disappointment.  They were covered in paprika and hashbrowns are on the greasy side. Other people in my party enjoyed their biscuit sandwiches.

We always like this theater because it is close. Because of Covid, the line is long because you have to select your seat. 
It was great to see a movie after all this time!

I'm a native New Yorker. Among other things, that means I'm VERY particular about bagels. Don't get me started on the "round bread" that has long passed for bagels in these parts. 

Ben and crew at Crieve Hall Bagel are doing it right. I started buying from him when he had a cottage business delivering bagels that were ordered via Social Media. Once he got the brick and mortar store going, I couldn't wait to stand in line. Neither could about a ka-jillion other bagel lovers, it seemed; I always drove up just as they put up the "sold out" sign. 

I messaged Ben on Instagram, and he took care of me by setting aside an order of 18 bagels and a lb of chive cream cheese for me. I got 1/2 dozen cheddar, 1/2 dozen cheddar/jalapeño, and 1/2 dozen onion. I also got an onion with chive schmear and a cup of coffee while standing in line. 

Bagels to go-check. Baker's dozen, to boot!
Bagel to eat-check. Crisp on the outside, chewy on the inside, with just a hint of sourdough bite.
Coffee-hot, fresh, delicious. As a New Yorker, I can't stand "so-so" bagel shop coffee; Crieve Hall Bagels serves a solid cup. 

Highest recommendation for bagels in Nashville/Northern Williamson County.

My order came quickly which was the only good thing about ordering from here. I had to pay $50 CAD for duties once it arrived (which I understand isn't really their fault). I ordered a set of hair extensions and when I opened the package the color was not the color I ordered. Thankfully I was able to make them blend and they look like they match my hair (but not as well as the color I usually wear.) I would of been very annoying if I had to send them back and wait for a new pair. It just shows that the company is not very thorough with their processes. I will probably order from here again ONLY because they are this is the only place I have been able to find the hair extensions I wear (rose de nuit seven piece remy). But if you plan on ordering something like hair extensions from this place I would CALL and actually talk to someone instead of just ordering online and make sure you are sent the right color. Just an overall annoying experience.

Very well put-together hot dogs. They have a Mac and cheese dog any kid or teen would love. As far as the buns go, not my favorite but ok. 

If you're a dog person I'd recommend a stop-in for sure.
I want this supermarket in my state.  Friendly staff, decent prices, excellent prepared food section.
Awesome southern breakfast and brunch spot! I loved the Migas breakfast plate. They also have great southern favorites like shrimp & grits as well as fresh biscuits.

Go someplace else or dont go.There is no maid service, but they charge you for maid service any way.  There is no valet parking so you may have to walk a distance in the heat to get to casino.  They will email you ahead of time with a phone number to call if you have any questions before staying....that has been discounted.  Says you can register on line the day before, not true.  Try to find a manager.  I dont think there is one.  No room service until 6 pm.  Buffet is $33 per person which is outrageous.  Cant find an email address.

It's Taco Bell.
The only negative review I can give is that there's been times where my burrito is cold. Which if you have ever bitten into a cold Taco Bell burrito, ... You know it's gross. 
Other than that, it's typically busy, but there's this one employee who can hold two different conversations at the drive through like a pro. She keeps the line moving very well.

Definatelt the best sushi, salad, soup, lo mein and pad Thai I've ever had in my life. Great prices, fantastic food, it's never crowded (can't figure out why!) Staff is friendly....nothing bad to say. This place is awesome!!!
The staff makes an effort to remember the drinks of regulars.  Sometimes its the little things like that which make the biggest difference.

Shrimp Culichi was good but it's not something I would crave for. The worst horchata drink I've ever had. I didn't even think that's possible for a Mexican restaurant. I had to exchange it for a coke.

I was really looking forward to trying this place but didn't love their gyros.  I appreciated the fact that they griddled their pita but I found several issues with the sandwich overall:

1. Their interpretation of tzatziki was a bit on the bland side and appeared devoid of cucumber...a signature component of a good tzatziki.  Also, they went a bit light on the sauce.

2. The meat was carved from a spit (big plus!) but was one of the off-brands that come out of the Chicago restaurant supply houses.  If you're charging eight bucks for a gyros it should damn well be Kronos or Olympia supplying your cones.  Also, the slices of meat would have benefitted from having spent a little more time on the cone or run over the flat top to give them the sear that really brings out the flavors.

3. Red onions instead of white seemed a little out of place.

Service was good.

Folks, there really are some good places to get gyros in the STL but unfortunately this doesn't turn out to be among the best.

This dessert place is a refreshing addition to Chinatown as a sit down place with really nice decor. I had the green tea crepe cake and tofu cheesecake; they both come with a scoop of ice cream that really makes for a satisfying dessert set (both were around $8-9 each).

We ordered the tofu cheesecake for the novelty of it, and it was pretty airy and light without being overly sweet (the name also makes the dish feel pseudo healthy, which is a plus when you're wolfing down desserts). The espresso / chocolate / peanut butter ice cream that came with it was surprisingly really good as well, and the rich flavors complemented the relatively light flavors of the cheesecake very well.

The mall was- an average mall, nothing spectacular but not boring either. Better than some malls I've been too. I think the Starbucks is in a great location- next to the ice rink, but it should be bigger- as the lines were long and waiting took forever but that's Starbucks in itself and not the mall's fault. It's location is great- right off of 19, but there are many things right off of 19 in  that area. The tattoo shop inside- was a plus, got some ink there by Carolyn- awesome. Over all good memories were made. :)

The novelty of this place is 5 stars. I'm really glad I went and would encourage everyone to go once as it's a very interesting and unique place. I was a bit disappointed with the flavor of my ice cream in the end especially because it is pricey, but it's a great spectacle!

I was in town for business and I found this great restaurant.  The place has a great atmosphere and I liked it from the first step. My dinner was delicious and I had enough for lunch the next day.  I would definitely recommend it.
Love this place! I go there every time I'm in the neighborhood for the Tropikal, Ceasar salad and one of those blueberry almond protein bars.  Delicious and oh so healthy!  Thank you!

So much miscommunication!! Do your employees even know how the wellness plan works? If so then why would you schedule a visit under the wellness plan that isn't covered and then call me 30 minutes ahead to say oh by the way this will be out of pocket. This has not been the only time this has happened. Please learn your products for the health of the hundreds of pets served here.

My Family and I made reservations for Relish on NYE. The decor, atmosphere and ambience was very nice. The food on the other hand did not quite live up to my expectations. I pictured upscale soul food cuisine and it was not! The fried chicken was undercooked and under seasoned and they could have allowed the chicken to be on a paper towel or dabbed the grease before playing. The macaroni and cheese was reheated quite a few times throughout the day( everyone knows what overcooked / reheated macncheese looks and tastes like and that was it. I didn't want to take any pictures on my snap because the food even looked disappointing. The mashed yams were pretty good and the cabbage was really good. The best thing I had was the SangriaHenny but the waitress did not bring our drinks until after we had finished eating our meal( there were problems with the ordering devices). Driving from Syracuse NY to enjoy an evening in Philly did not begin well with the food we ate here. We won't be returning unless it was on the house and they have a different cook for the evening as we were not impressed to say the least.

Wonder ambiance, easy location on Magazine, good service with a food that is good although not mind blowing.

No on the sweetbreads, having it fried takes away so much from such a succulent organ. 

The butter squash cavatelli was a very pleasant experience, do try it.

Both the Duck and the Red snapper were okay. 

A good date place.

I know this location is new so they've probably got some kinks to work out.
I'll even give a bonus star for Mango Habenero sauce, 

But the bottom line is they fucked up my Superbowl.  

I'm not driving all the way to Spring Hill.  I waited and waited for this place to open up and they missed the entire fucking football season.  They FINALLY opened up in time for the SuperBowl and they fucked it up.  

Ordered my shit a day in advance.

Waited over an hour in line to pick up a pre-ordered TAKE OUT order.  

Finally said fuck it and just took home the remainder of the wings at WalMart's deli.  

These fuckers actually had the gall to call me AFTER HALFTIME.... I didn't bother to answer... I'll save my expletives for Yelp.  Hooters never fucked me on a SuperBowl

Let's talk about hair. 1. I am always terrified trying out someone new in fear of getting my princess locks chopped like salad. That's a joke, but seriously, I am. 

When I booked my appointment with Apryl I was a bit reluctant since I would be doing a cut and color and both Styles were huge changes. I wanted to cut a couple inches off as well as try an ombré color. 

Apryl made it easy for me to contact her (even when I got lost in the hotel- yes it's in the Sands Regency but don't let that fool you or make you think the caliber of the salon is any less than amazing!) she was kind and made sure to fit me in on super short notice which I very much appreciated!

She worked with me when I got there and truly made sure to understand exactly what I wanted. She have me suggestions and advice and we even worked out what would be most cost effective for me (helloooo someone who actually cares!) she offered me the groupon price even though I didn't get one and let me know how much extra it would be. 

I went for it and all along the way she made sure to ask me if I liked it or wanted this or that way. It was refreshing to actually be talked to during the service instead of sit there drowning in fear and anxiety as to what is actually happening ! 

All in all Apryl did an amazing cut and color and I couldn't have asked for more!! I can't wait to come back and get a touch up from her when the time comes! Go see Apryl you won't be disappointed!!

First, I must say the women here were very nice and friendly and had every intention of being helpful. However, the fit specialist measured me and guessed 28D, which is just crazy. I've always worn 32B, and no one in their right mind would look at me and think a D would be a better fit. I suspended judgement, as I've often heard that a lot of women wear the wrong size. So I tried on what she suggested, and it totally did not fit. The right size? Yep - 32B, just as I thought. 

I was looking for a strapless bra to wear under a wedding dress. She brought a corset-style bra, which sounded like a good idea. When I tried it on under my dress, the ribs in it totally showed through. The ladies assured me that it "wasn't bad" and that I could just make some small cuts and remove the ribs. In retrospect, I really question the wisdom of spending $68 on a bra I would have to perform surgery on. Still, I bought the bra, and when I got it home and tried it on again, I realized not just the ribs, but the whole shape, was going to show through my dress. I also realized I would never wear it with anything else, that I could not get into it by myself, etc.

I went to Nordstrom and found a much better choice for the same price. I've heard good things about Ann's for those who need a size most stores don't stock, but I didn't find it very helpful, despite the very kind staff.

Terrible experience, we will not be returning.

We put it in an order at 3:30pm to be picked up at 8:30. We arrived at 8:35 and were informed that after our order was confirmed, our entire order was cancelled because they ran out of the desert we included in our order. They made no effort to call us and let us know that none of our food was going to be made. Immediately frustrated that we were not informed, they offered us no apology, compensation, or an alternate option for our meal. 

Sticking to our guns we had them put in our order to go and they said it would be 20 minutes. 30 minutes later they gave us our order and still offered no apology or appreciation for our patience. 

After getting home to have our long desired meal we found our orange chicken to be soggy/mushy. Our beef and broccoli was closer to wet beef jerky and broccoli. Our lettuce wraps were okay. Our desert was the lava cake which just ended up being small and dry chocolate cake with some fruit. 

54 dollars and an extra half hour later, we ended up with less than desired customer service and less than Panda Express quality food... We are left with no desire nor intention to return.
They have the best Wor Wonton soup! The price is reasonable as well. Staff is fast and friendly. It is one of my favorite place to eat in Reno.

Nobu! I love you so much! Your originality always intrigues me and the delicious tempuras you can always provide brings joy into my life. It feels you are the only Japanese sushi restaurant who truly knows what they are doing - unlike those cheap posers who think they know sushi. You know sushi, you know your customers - GANBATTE!

A tiny place with terrific, talented people. 
Absolutely a credit to the community. 
I took two pair of shorts for a somewhat complicated repair. 
Fantastic results. Actually much better than I'd asked for. Couldn't be happier.

Did not act like Masters in this trade.  After reading the reviews I had high expectations.  I brought a bridesmaid dress in that needed alterations and the tailor immediately said no that he prefers to do menswear.  I then asked him to shorten a shirt dress from h&m (very simple) and he once again insisted on only men's clothing.  He sent me away to take my business elsewhere.

We had a great time! The view was great. Our chef was very entertaining, which says alot when you are entertaining 5 teens who are used to having their faces in their phones. Everyone enjoyed their food. Combined we had salmon, steak, chicken and shrimp. Dessert was equally delicious! We had the fried icecream and it was nothing like we expected it to be which was great because it was so much better! Overall we had a great time and all of the teens said they enjoyed themselves so it was a win!

I went with my dear friend and daughter. The employees were super friendly and nice. But not sure if I will return.
I went with the 45 pedicure and gel manicure. The pedicure was great. The manicure.... not so much. I got my gel manicure done on Friday and ita already peeling and chipping and its only Tuesday. I refuse to believe the pricey manicure will only last 4 days.

The other issue I had was I feel they are bot upfront about their fees before beginning services. My daughter has acrylic overlay with gel polish.  They did not mention that there was a $5 charge to remove the gel polish. If she knew that, ahe could have easily peeled the polish off herself because it had begun peeling. Then her pedicure and manicure (same deal, gel polish over acrylic overlay) came to $101! When we asked the man why was it so much he would not give us a reason. Just told us it was 5 dollars to take gel off. Hindsight, I would have made him write down the bill to find put how much he was charging for the services.
The atmosphere was great and everyone was friendly.  I just dont know if I will rerurn. If I did, it strictly be for a pedicure.

We needed a break from Bourbon street and I really wanted to enjoy live music, so we went to Frenchmen Street and were drawn in by the blues music played here. It was exactly what we were looking for. 

It does get pretty crowded inside but there's also plenty of seating and if you can get a table, the servers are quick to ask if you need anything and serve you right away. Just be careful not to stand in the way of service or you'll be elbowed around. Drinks were fine and prices were average. Don't forget to tip the awesome band if you enjoy the music!

I had a coupon for a 2 for 1 deal for lunch.  Usually we don't eat lunch out but I had a bit of shopping to do at Safeway so it seemed like a good opportunity to get the deal.  I must say the system they have is really good.  The manager is the one who took our order.  I have seen him many times before and he has always been very pleasant.  It came within 5 min. and was tasty.  I had the lox, cream cheese with fresh capers, red onion on a toasted bagel.  Hubby had the turkey, bacon, mayo. Both were good.  For me the  prices are high for a lunch.  It would have been over 15 bucks for lunch but with the coupon we both enjoyed our lunch for 7 bucks.  Can't beat this deal.  Was so full, we had no dinner that night.

Service was not so good. The food was mediocre at best. They brought my friend the wrong dish and then it took so long to get the correct food that we ended up canceling it because everyone else at the table had finished. No manager ever came over to apologize. Poor customer service! I will not be back!!

Order somewhere else please, it's not worth the risk of having to call in sick the next day. We ordered two burritos last night, one carnitas and one chicken. They were not appetizing at all, and we both had instant regret. What a waste of money. Even worse, now I feel sick to my stomach. I hate to leave bad reviews, but have to with how ill I feel after eating that mess. This pace seemed to have a good rating on here. I should have known to throw it away after I took the first bite - the chicken tasted like wet dog and the tortilla was slimy and translucent. I figured it was just the flavor of the chicken that I wasn't into and ate around it. Wish I had followed my instincts.

Bus #1 was broken down before the trip even started and led to a 1.5 hour delay.

Bus #2 was so run down that the air duct cover fell off and hit a couple passengers in the head, and then broke down 1 hour outside our destination. We waited another 1.5 hours in 100 degree weather without AC.

Bus #3 although in better condition, the keys to bus #2 were not working properly so all 50 passengers had to leave their baggage behind, because they could not get the doors open.

They told us that it would be 24 hours before our baggage would arrive at our destination, which is unacceptable considering we are 1 hour from our destination and many passengers have medications and valuables in their luggage.

Fun fact: Customer service has a catchy phrase," My manager will not be able to offer you any additional services." They then proceed to tell you to email inquiry@megabus.com and hang up. 

I don't know if I'm more angry at the lack of compassion for the passengers with luggage baking in the sun for the next 24hrs or the unacceptable and unprofessional customer service. 

Dear Megabus, You are a charter company, with many busses, it is worth the time and resources to deliver 50 people's luggage in a timely manner. You are lucky to have a driver like Tracy who really worked her best to help us all out

I would stop by here all the time for lunch. A great place with great food. It's a good switch from all the pizza and wing joints nearby. All of the wrap sandwiches are great. The fries are also some of the best I've ever had. Get them with feta and rosemary.

As frequent diner at the Mexican restaurant across the street, I have gazed questioningly at the crowds at Los Caballitos.  Even laughing, as a bike messenger screams "Hail Satan" as he bikes past.

So this past Sunday, with a craving for Mexican and margaritas, a friend and I convinced my boyfriend to change our usual routine and try Cantina instead of El Zarape.  I had a large nachos with chicken (when really I only needed a small, even for an entree portion).  The nachos had a good balance of cheese, beans, cream and meat.  Good flavors.  The BF did not enjoy the goat burrito even though he said it was fine, but my friend did rave about the goat entree.

Cantina is great if you want Mexican and margaritas, but if you're only craving Mexican food, I would suggest going somewhere else to get great food at a better price.  But if atmosphere is important to you, join the crowds at Los Caballitos.

i just called to see if they had an item in stock, and in the middle of asking a question, the employee on the phone cut me off and "ma'am i have another call coming in that i have to answer, have a good day" and HUNG UP ON ME. i didn't get her name but the times i've gone in the store have been just as bad as the phone call i just had.

Here's a typical car dealership where the sales team's goal is to make as much money off customers as possible. I bought a "certified preowned" vehicle from here... Before I could even cross the 30,000 mile mark with my new car, I had to take it back to them 3-4 times...

The main issues were pretty serious ranging from the car shutting down during a drive and smoking through the vents to the whole engine actually shaking when it was revved. The car also had 2 recalls in the one year that I owned it.. Now all my repairs here were covered by warranty and the service department was good with all repairs and transactions.. The service department was able to work with me and around my schedule.. 

HOWEVER, this review is a two star because of the SALES team.. They were unprofessional with me from the start.. Very pushy, I should have listened to my instincts turned away after my first interaction with one of their salesmen when I first came for a test drive.. His tone and demeanor completely changed when I said I simply wanted to drive the vehicle because I've never driven a fusion before.. He actually took a smoke break while I waited at his desk.. A week later, because I was in a rush to buy a new car l, I came back and was able to negotiate with another salesman who was did not budge on the price, the sticker price was decent and I bought the "certified preowned"... After a year of use I realized there were so many issues with the car and there was no way it would survive long term.. I brought it back asking for a deal on any of their other vehicles because the first one they sold me could possible be a lemon in a few years... The sales team was still trying to make a major profit off me and did not negotiate on any of the prices.. Note: I was planning on keeping my first car long term but all the repairs in one year of ownership were big red flags for long term ownership.. I could have written a letter to the manufacturer but I decided yelpers need to hear about this..

Long story short...If this is the "highest rated ford dealership in the area".. I WILL NEVER BUY A FORD AGAIN mainly because of the dealer's inability to work with their existing customers to make sure they are happy with their vehicle... . 
I now own a BMW, and I honestly still think the Ford Fusion titanium is an excellent vehicle but don't buy one from these guys..

This is the most disappointing experience that I've ever had at a seafood restaurant. I had a high expectation reading all the good reviews on yelp about this place and our experience was completely opposite. We started our lunch with diluted, watered down mojito; but when I complained, they charged me $2.00 extra for remaking my drink. The Greek octopus was mushy, it obviously is not fresh. The sauce that came with it was buttery and bitter. The grouper sandwich was doused in mayonnaise and tasted slightly better than fillet-o-fish. On top of that , the potato salad was very sour, practically inedible. I leave 2 stars because the crab cake was good was the only good item in our whole meal.

How fun is this place? They bring you a scorching hot "stone" that looks like a paver of sorts.  Then they have your selection of meat on the stone. You slice off what you'd like to eat, and lay the meat on the stone as long as you'd like until it is cooked to your preference. I had the filet mignon, and it was wonderful, especially w the compote butter. It's a unique spot with delicious sides, steaks and salads. Be warned the "wedge" isn't iceberg lettuce but romaine, so if that is important to you order something else. Nice date place if you don't mind TV's hanging around here and there. Not too fancy, jeans or shorts are fine, but prices are a bit upscale.

So my issue with PO'B's is that it is just too much of a tourist trap.  I hate hurricanes.  I'm not crazy about Hawaiian Punch or Kool-Aid, either.  If the weather is perfect, sitting outside can be great.  It's a beautiful courtyard.  The people who work here are as talented and professional as they come.  The food is very good, the drinks are decent, the service is excellent, the location is good.  There's nothing wrong with this place other than the fact that it is just so touristy you feel like you're in Florida and not NOLA.  It's overpriced as well, but it's not their fault the visitors from out of town will pay anything for a souvenir glass full of booze and sugar water.

Seriously, it's a nice place and everything here is excellent.  It's just too commercial for my taste but don't let that dissuade you from going there!

If you look in google maps, you may not find this. It's part of 8 or 9 food stalls that are part of a market. Excellent food. We ordered all the vegetarian food on offer and without exception each dish was excellent. The dosas are outstanding, the sambar was pretty authentic. The Dabheli was excellent.

All in all a must try if you are in New Orleans.

Great king cake. Originally Lawrence Bakery recipes of filled cakes makes it special. Cakes though are not consistent, and can be overly sweet. If you need a king cake and live in the gentilly area they are the neighborhood best. Donuts and other bakery items a tad over priced.

The drinks and food were very good. 

The service was laughable. 

I took my mom here for her birthday and really wanted to like it since its close and has a great patio. I can definitely forgive some mistakes in terms of service, but we were virtually ignored for the majority of our meal. I won't be going back.
Great atmosphere that anyone can love even if you are not a parrot-head. HUGE QUANTITIES (nachos) the servers are great and funny. They have a very funny balloon maker that made it much better.

I live out of town and needed someone to fix my hair and makeup prior to my destination wedding in Mandeville. Melissa fixed my hair in a partial updo and it was absolutely gorgeous! I received so many compliments. My makeup was done by Gabi. It looked good but was considerably much more makeup than I have ever worn. It was painted on with makeup brushes and then sprayed to have "stay power."  Just for my own personal preference, I blotted off some of the heavy makeup and applied a little blot powder, then it was perfect.  It stayed in place all night. 
Very nice, upscale salon! Cost for hair was $65 and makeup was $50 plus tip.

Went for a late lunch with my in laws. We were seated promptly at a great table right on the water. It was a gorgeous February day. 3 of us got fish and chips. The food was very good. Our server was great but slow. I'm guessing she had too many tables. The biggest reason for 4 versus 5 stars was that smoking is allowed at any of the tables outside. So we got to "enjoy" our meal with 2 tables nearby smoking.

Great service, positive experience. Kyle arrived at the last minute call because the company that was scheduled to complete inspection cancelled the last second. He did a thorough job, polite, very professional. And the best thing, he was available to discuss his findings over the phone later and addresses all questions we had as buyers.

The walk is closed from 7.30 am to 5.30 pm Monday to Friday (I believe it is absolutely unnecessary, because there are only a few cars allowed to go up). You can see the people there from 4 am walking up  To walk up early definitely helps, as during the day it is a bit hot in the summer (smile).
There is ample parking along the street.

So happy we stopped in here after leaving the courthouse. Great people and the restaurant is set up beautiful. They also had vegan options, yes options more than one, with homemade hummus! Stop in and try it out so many different options, but I always listen to what the cook suggests and I'm glad I did! Food was incredibly delicious.

I like this place, from the moment you pull into the parking area you feel you are entering a place with a bit of class, unlike the rest of Lansdale.  The formal rear facing entrance is very inviting, especially if you are a first timer, and you are greeted by one of the charming staffers.  The dining room, although a bit dated, is arranged so almost every table seems like a private nook.  You should bring a bottle of wine with you, but if you don't they will give you a complimentary glass which I thought was a top shelf move on their part.  The menu is traditional Italian, with all of your favorite offerings in place.  Kudos for the available lobster stuffed raviolis, one of my favorite all time dishes.  The portions are on the generous side but do not include a salad, which I found a bit unusual for an establishment of this scale.  Their bread is hot, fresh and right out of the oven served with what seemed to be an olive oil/garlic/pepper pesto.  The  high lite of the meal is their gravy, which is on the sweeter side and is one of the most enjoyable sauces I have ever encountered.  Bravo..  I enjoyed my  meal and am happy to recommend La Campagnola, I will return.

All the flavor of Prince's with the convenience of online ordering, a Gallatin Road location, and no hours-long wait time.  Tip: order chicken tenders, so you can eat with utensils and avoid touching the spices.  (Your mucous membranes will thank you.)  Sides are fantastic, especially the red potato salad.
Great pizza. Great sauce. Great crust. Just to think people pick dominos over this place...sheesh.

I am consistently disappointed in the service at this Scotty's location. It's always slow or non-existent in my experience. My most recent disappointment was submitting an order for pick-up and waiting over 45 minutes after the designated pick-up time to receive the order.

The car dealership was very helpful when it came to getting us to buy a car but as soon as we said ok we would like to buy the car - customer service went out the window.  Paper work that should have taken an hour took 3 and no one showed any desire to help us out as they all "putzed" around the dealership.  Honda is a great car - but this dealership has no problem wasting your time.

When you are in Philadelphia, eat Philly Cheesesteak! I had heard about this place on a number of different occasions so finally got to try it with a couple of friends. The space is pretty large with yellow walls, brown chairs and booths, as well as light pink accents. It wasn't too busy when we arrived and we were able to grab booth seating by the window. 

I decided to try the Ishka Special Cheesesteak, which included mushroom, sweet peppers, onions, pizza sauce, and American cheese. I am not a fan of American cheese, so I asked if that could be substituted with Provolone, and they were able to do so without any problems. 

The wait time to get the Philly Cheesesteak was minimal and it came open faced, piping hot, wonderfully cheesy, filled with plenty of steak, tons of veggies, with a tantalizing smell. The steak was tender, flavorful with plenty of tasty seasoning. The veggies were nicely crisp and fresh, while the pizza sauce added a lovely layer of yumminess without being overpowering. The Provolone cheese was delicious. All the ingredients complemented one another and the bread was soft. I liked that the sandwich was not overly greasy. Also, the sandwich was quite large and definitely makes you full. 

I also ordered their signature drink: Gremlin, which is a mix between grape soda and lemonade. This was absolutely delightful, refreshing and not overly sweet. There was a strong taste of lemonade, which blended perfectly with the grape and I enjoyed the bubbly, sparkling effect of the beverage. 

I enjoyed the cheesesteak here and am glad I tried it. I would possibly come back, but there are so many other cheesesteak places in Philly that I would want to try those eateries first.
Great service. It gets fairly busy, so a call ahead is suggested.  The bartender is wonderful!

The best Nachos ever!  That's what the waitress said, and she was absolutely right.  The chips are unbelievable!  

The fries are fresh and "real", but not too real that my picky 10 year old wouldn't eat them .  We also shared a wonderfully fresh and tasty rib eye.  

The service and patrons (older crowd) were all super nice.  They have TVs for games, but it's not for the rowdy loud crowd.  

I've heard they're breakfast is phenomenal!

Saw a popular movie on a Saturday night. The movie started at 10:45 but we were not allowed into the theater until 10:40 because of cleaning. As a result, I stood in a line in the lobby for nearly 40 minutes. And, because people were walking up to the ticket checker, getting their tickets scanned, and then getting in line, many people without tickets were easily able to get into them movie.  

The lobby and bathrooms were incredibly dirty. There was popcorn and trash all over the ground with no visible attempts to clean it up. The bathroom reeked of urine and there were multiple toilet paper rolls strewn across the ground. That said, the theater itself was quite clean and I enjoyed the movie once I was finally able to get in and sit down.

At around $4.50 / lb for boiled crawfish, they are way too expensive, espcially considering the place is filthy and you can't even get any napkins or paper towels if you eat in. $4.50 for boiled crawfish is insanely expensive. Fresh LA crawfish tails are only $15/lb at Rouses ... $4.50/lb with shells on comes to about $25/lb. Ridiculous.

That said the boiled seafood is quite good and very spicy. If you want to eat in be sure to bring a roll of paper towels, newspaper and something to clean the table with.

I can handle a forced 20% gratuity charge on two people if they give us something to give 20% about. Everything is self serve -- you order at the counter from a guy who seems to care less that you're trying to place an order, you get your own drinks, utensils, condiments, etc. You sit. The server brings your food. And that's it. 20 percent? I don't think so. 

That being said, I still had high hopes that the food would be good. Consider my high hopes crushed. I ordered the Bifana sandwich (which, apparently, is pronounced "bihana"), with pork, sweet mustard, and charred red onions. We also ordered fries to share. The sandwich was nearly tasteless and odd. It also had a horseradishy burn that I thought was strange, considering the lack of horseradish. I was hungry and didn't even want to touch the second half of my sandwich. So I didn't. Sad.

I also had a bite of the Albany style pierogies (with kimchi and soy butter) and they tasted.. like nothing. They literally had zero flavor. How is this possible? My Elderflower fizz was flat (sad) but tasted okay. The fries were the only saving grace.

Never thought I'd say this, but I really miss Drinker's. At least their food was good.

I really like this place! The parsley potatoes are on point. I would give this place 5 stars, but I felt the ribs were not as tender as they should be, in my opinion. Prices are great. The food comes almost immediately, service is great too. For dessert, I got the apple cake, absolutely delicious! I'm a fan!

Not sure what to say. We went for a special evening having heard so much about the place from others. We had a nice dining experience but my expectations must have been too high. My wife and I were not overly impressed. 

My meal, the Gamberi Capellini, was very nice but hers was disappointing by all standards. She chose the nightly special in the $25 range and found it overly salted and very bitter. It was ravioli. That's not how ravioli should be especially at that price. Appetizer was fine, dessert was fine. We were hoping for more than fine. 

The personalized greeting at the door by the owner was fun, both upon entrance and exit, but it was tough to get past the strip mall feel. Not sure if we will be back.
Rude and unprofessional operator.  I asked if they serviced Glenside.and he told.me to call DE county and.hung up on me when I.said that makes no sense.  Excuse the periods on a crappy phone.
Sooo bad.  Awful and not cheap.  Why is there no Chipotle's here?  Seriously?

First off I could I would give them a -5.  These people are incompetent of communicating with you when you ask them anything.  I used to bring my dogs here to them for over 5 years, every time it was a new person that we saw and they never looked as us other than $$.  Yesterday 11/08/17 my dog was brought in without my knowledge and the person that brought him in said that he was aggressive and needed to be put down.  I had asked them in the past to put a note on both of the dogs charts that anyone that brings the dog(s) other than me they are to immediately call me and verify this with me, this request has been put with them number of times and it never got updated.  Well... long story short my dog was put down last night without my knowledge.  I went to the facility today to speak to 'upper management' which are dumber than a box of rocks, to see what happened because the conversation I had with them over the phone they wouldn't tell me anything as to why and what had happened.  But Sarah, who I spoke to on the phone and wish to never speak again to, told me over the phone that the dog is 'not a human being or a living thing' and that b cause of HIPPA she can't disclose what happened because she is not sure if I am the actual owner.  I role her if you are thinking that way, you should not be doing what you are doing and since he is a 'thing' according to her HIPPA doesn't apply then, since it applies to living breathing creatures. Then she proceeded to tell me that the dog is property.  All in all, nothing is going to bring my dog back but be aware and stay away from this place.  They will never treat you like a person, they will always look at you as a chart and $$$

Walked in around 710pm and it was loud due to a live band. That's all fine. Sat at a table and waited for a server. And waited. And waited. And waited. Another couple sat down after me. I waited. Server shows up and takes their drink order and goes to the back. I left. No acknowledgement.

I've been living here since 2009. I've seen a lot of changes in management, like 4 new managers..
We had good doorman service... Haven't had them since October 2016, and yet that is in our contract..
The elevator call box (if you're stuck in the elevator - which happens often) is located in what used to be the doorman's office, now it's the package room. With no doorman service, you're stuck in the elevator and of you don't have Verizon, no other phones work in the elevator, you're screwed. I found an old man that had been stuck in the freight elevator for an hour. He was lucky I realized he was tapping out SOS on the alarm bells and called the fire department for him.
They painted the outside of the building - and the painters did a lousy job. They missed a huge spot on the side of the balcony next to my place. It used to drive me crazy when I would go out for a cigarette and see this. I quit smoking, so I don't see it now.
They painted the balconies that used to be brown for the Kyoto and green for the Toledo. Now both buildings are the same color. Good luck describing to your visitors which building you live in now. The green part on the balcony is plastic, the painters painted that. It's only a matter of a couple of years and the paint will start chipping and look like shit.
Outside gym behind the Toledo building... I only see children playing on it. Used to be a blacktop oval where people would walk around.. When installing the gym, which is nothing but pipes, the installer cut through a wire going to the lights into the parking lot. More money wasted to fix that screw up. The blacktop in the outside gym was painted. This was a couple of years ago, now it looks like shit.
New unnecessary signage - We can tell it's a swimming pool and a tennis court, we don't need signs stating such.. Oh, those signs for the pool and tennis court.. right in the middle where the kids USED to play soccer.
Flag poles - more wasted money. After the last hurricane the poles are tilted and look like shit. More money wasted on ginger bread.
They painted the fire stairs - and didn't stencil the floor numbers. That was great when there was a fire on the first floor and after coming down from the 7th floor, I couldn't tell what floor I was on to tell the fire department...Fire code violation 
The dog park.. more wasted money. In the summer you can really smell the dog piss because there is no drainage in the dog park. Great job!
Carpeting in the elevator.. Great when someone's dog pisses in the elevator. Used to be rubber which was easy to clean up, not anymore... Btw, the dogs piss in those elevators because the freight elevator wasn't working... Again...
Paved the parking lot. Looks really nice, except where the dumpster is. There's a slope that meets and water pools. In the winter it's a skating rink. But it looks nice.
Rent increase every year, and yet we don't have doormen anymore, no more shuttle to Philadelphia, the package room you have to get your own packages and sometimes they go missing.. 
Someone stole two of my neighbors motorcycles .. Right from the parking lot, just loaded them onto a truck and drove off. Their security camera is of such shit quality it was useless. Had we had our doorman, especially Mike Renzi, that man always kept an eye on things and caught people trying to break into cars, even though that wasn't in his job description... Miss Mike and our other doormen... And yet we are still paying for them, our rent hasn't decreased but the services have. 
This morning, 1/6/2017, ice in the parking lot. There sits the snow plow and salt spreader, right by the back door... Not putting salt down. Damn near broke my ass walking from my car. Had to avoid the skating rink by walking across the grass. 
Two weeks ago a plumbing leak was repaired by a contracted plumber. The hole in the wall hasn't been fixed. I see this every day.. 
Not to mention the mold in the bathrooms, tiny kitchen with cabinets that go back to the 1970's...
I don't know where people think the walls are paper thin.. They are concrete block walls. You have to use a hammer drill to hang something. I always hear the new people that move in trying to hammer a nail into the wall... Just banging away and getting nowhere. 

In 2010 we had new windows installed, new toilets, new compact fluorescent lighting, new boilers to heat the building... Everything to save them money. It's been downhill ever since. We need updated elevators, not dog parks and paint jobs. The building is 50 years old. Congrats on getting rid of the Orthodox Jewish by getting rid of the doormen. On the Sabbath, they cannot operate the electric front or rear door. They all moved out. Shame, they were very nice people. I miss some of my neighbors, especially the woman with the 3 children. They were nice. I hope they are doing well and happier than they were here.

We have done business with Teak and Hardwood floors for nearly 20 years. They have refinished hard wood floors in my home, installed laminate and hardwood floors in my home and installed laminate and refinished hardwood floors in my rentals. They have always been very professional and courteous. They go out of their way to keep your home clean which is a difficult job when you are refinishing a hardwood floor. Jeff the owner is very knowledgeable, and more than willing to explain all of your options with hardwood floors and laminate. I find their rates fair and reasonable for the quality of work they do.

Ok food, very confusing. Cute decor, quick service. Salsa bar is awesome. It would have been nice to have had someone tell us how things work. We were ignored. Had to search for napkins. I didn't feel welcome, and wouldn't return.

Been here a couple of times and found their empanadas to be tasty, but not something I would eat very often.

The fried treats are done nicely and the oil has never tasted rancid. They come out crispy and not too heavy or doughy. I prefer the savory ones more than the sweet ones. The prices are low, $2.50 per empanada.

I prefer to take out as I found the dining area not well kept after others have eaten at the tables. They could improve on this aspect.

My husband and I went to RT Weilers on Sept 27.  I had the combo 1 top pizza and bbq sliders for 8.99.    

My husband got the blackened chicken and fries.

They were out of fries because they were closing in two days. 

Which is sad, because they had great food for the prices.

Lemongrass resutant
Start with summar roll
Entire Mi Quanq
Our server Katie (the owners daughter) was great.  She walked us through the whole experience.  The summer roll was very refreshing and with the peanut sauce was a wonder blend of refreshness and an umami.  The Mi Quang was a great surprise.  The flavors blended into this flavors bomb of sweet, spice, meaty, freshness.  The peanuts gave a nice crunch and the rice crackers provided a beautiful crispiness. Pork belly and the shrimp was like a Vietnamese surf and turf it was wonderful.
You have to try this gem. It is new and we don't want to loss this one.  Really you need to try it, I will be back to try the whole menu.  It will be an adventure.

The manager is a liar!  I upgraded my phone and paid the $48 tax and $75 upgrade fee plus ave them my old phone.  Now my new Bill also has a $49.99 and $30 upgrade fee too?  Wtf.   Smooth talking liar who didn't tell me there would be additional charges. I wish I could switch carriers.  Called ATT and they said the rep at the store should have explained everything.  Well, he didn't!

The three stars are strictly for the atmosphere. The food is absolutely horrible. We both ordered the chicken Cobb salad which was presented to us by our waiter as having delicious fried chicken on top.. it came with deli sized, cold, cubed fried chicken. It was disgusting. We both said we would've been better off getting a Wendy's salad because at least a Wendy's salad isn't $13 and it tastes better. Then we ordered the Hot Chick sandwich which sounds so good on the menu. Again, majorly disappointed. It was a thin, small chicken patty that gave me flashbacks to the middle school cafeteria. The bread was falling apart as if it was stale. Again, we wished we had gotten fast food because then at least the price and food quality would've matched up. Anyone who says they enjoy the food is lying.

This place blew me away. Sometimes I find the newer, trendier restaurants in New Orleans to be wildly disappointing. However, Seaworthy exceeded my expectations. The drum was expertly cooked, the oysters were excellent, caviar pairings were unique and the dessert topped everything off nicely! I will definitely be back very soon. Happy hour is on my list of things to try!

This is a great sandwich/salad place.  The coffee is good, though I wish they were a little more authentic to Italian sizes/consistencies (hence only 4 stars).  But like I said, this is a gread food place.  The ingredients are amazingly fresh, and the breads are to die for.  I've had salad, panino, various baked goods, and the bean/hummus crostini which was to die for.  I love the Italian cafe atmosphere, and if you order champagne, it comes in an ice bucket made from an old olive oil container.  I do wish they had room for outdoor tables.
Great selection of food for breakfast or lunch.  Servers are great and bussers are very efficient.  Great to support local businesses and restaurateurs like Pete.

I would have given this restaurant 4 stars, but there were qualifications which I would like to explain. The food is very good. We arrived at the restaurant having heard about it from several friends as a must! Although we knew it was a BYOB, my husband and I really don't drink enough to warrant bringing an entire bottle. The hostess offered a complimentary glass of wine is we would like since we didn't bring our own. When our server asked if we had brought wine and we did not, she immediately removed the wine glasses from the table-not offering us what the hostess had promised. We mentioned this and she said, "oh, okay". That wasn't the best start to the night. It also seemed they were understaffed as our server had tables totally across the room so there were great lags of attention.  That said, we started with a wonderful and I mean delectable french onion soup that rates 5 stars. Next, we shared a pate that is also a 5 star item. Then we waited, and waited, and waited for our main courses. Noone came over to say how long or why there was a delay. By the time it came about 30 minutes after the previous course, we practically were falling asleep and the restaurant by that point was not crowded at all. We would normally have wanted to try the dessert but the long delay kept us from doing so. While the server was quite friendly and knowledgeable of the menu items, it was spotty in attentiveness. We also had to wait a while for our check while our server spoke with another table for quite some time. I would love to come back, but now I'm not sure I would try again as it's quite pricey considering the circumstances.

First, I will be back to try again. I'm thinking this was just an issue with my order since I'm not seeing any other reviews on it. I got the fried tomatoes sandwich with rosemary chips. I COULD NOT eat the chips they had SOOOOOOO much salt on them. I Wish I was exaggerating but they were inedible. I decided to wipe the salt off some of the chips and I literally had a pile of salt in my lap. The sandwich had GREAT flavor but was lacking in the amount of fried green tomatoes; I wish it had more. I was desperately searching to have that ingredient since that is main ingredient.
Such a great restaurant. Wonderful staff, beautiful setting, and everything was so delicious. We got the scalibut, bluefin and sizzling oysters...all were amazing. A definite must!
Best cheesesteaks on either side of the Mississippi. Highly highly recommend. Bring cash and go at an odd hour to secure a spot at the bar.

We went here last Saturday for lunch. First red flag was the place was almost empty. We saw a commercial on tv and decided to go. It reminds me of Joe's crab shack. I have not found king crab legs around. I asked what was market price and the server said $60.00 and  I was expecting that. I ordered the garlic butter sauce with noodles that were extra $$$. Meal came and it's 1 leg only for $60.00!! The menu or food server do not mention that. Total ripoff, if you just be warned. The bag thing is odd, but the food tasted good. Might go back, but I doubt it.

I've written to the better business bureau and the attorney general because this location should be audited ASAP. They sell defective vehicles, hire a team to deal with title and registrations that do not give accurate information...I could go on but please do your research before going here.

Honey! When I am in Philly I am going to a Strip Joint was my statement as I was about to leave on a business trip to Philly.  My wife without batting an eyelash said "Enjoy you bloody steak"...knowing full well that for this male human the word strip is almost always beef related (no pun intended!) to my favorite cut.

The Strip Joint used to be something else before this, but I am glad the owners saw it fit to create a steak place with almost every cut under $20! Quaint old fashioned setting inside with dark wood and splashes of red evoking an era of the old, all well accompanied by well made cocktails. On this occasion I decided to dine at the bar and found the bartender to be a very knowledgeable and friendly chap who made me a perfect old fashioned while I waited for my steak tartar.  This was a really well made tartar with the beef hand cut into tiny cubes and seasoned with dijon, shallots and capers and was almost a refreshing dish.  I of course ordered the NY strips which I had to sling back the first time because it was overcooked, but came out perfectly med rare after the fix. The fries were ridiculously good, crispy tender and really worked well with the tender meaty bites of the strip.  They also threw in some mushrooms which added the touch of umami to the whole proceedings.

They even comped my a glass of wine since they screwed up the steak and I noticed that they had a nice and varied wine list, again which did not have horrific markups.

All in all a very enjoyable place that I could easily see myself walking into everyday if I lived in the neighborhood.  (I made another trip before leaving, and everyone remembered me, including the cut of steak I liked, and how I liked it cooked!..not bad service I say.)
Great atmosphere. Clean and nice setting of the Bay. Fun gathering place for family and friends. I just wish if there staff is more training well about the service and how to talk to their customer

I was in a wheelchair  so, he called 911 due to my breathing.  They couldn't  transport the wheelchair.  So i had to sit outside  90 degrees  for another hour to go home.

They will kill someone.  This wasnt the regular  dr. So. I don't know if that was the problem.
