They did nothing.

I've been here several times before it closed down.  The food was decent for what you expected and paid.  However, I did unfortunately get a chance to see the owner on occasion and I can see why this place closed down.  He's in a word a "nut".  A very classic example of an old school Italian tough guy who gets worked up over anything he perceives to be an insult in the slightest.  What a wacko!  Unfortunately for the business the manager/nephew of the owner who has a stable and steady personality wasn't able to get in the last say so time and time again I've witnessed the owner blowing his top while dining there.  No surprise the place is RIP, never had a chance.

I love Brophy Brothers. I love the view of the harbor, I love the fried fish, and I love the service. It's a tried and true Santa Barbara staple. It's always just so darn crowded! Today we had the yummy Bloody Mary, a little spicy but very good indeed. We started with a cup of thick New England clam chowder served with a tiny packet of oyster crackers. Chowder was thick and full of clams with just the right amount of potatoes. We had fish and chips with coleslaw and crisp French fries and an appetizer size fried clams. Fish is always flavorful and portions are large. Come hungry and leave full!

Bethany did my hair and make-up for my May 30, 2015 wedding and I couldn't have been happier! I got married in Malibu however she came do my hotel room in Santa Monica to get myself and three bridesmaids dolled up. I was worried at first that she wouldn't have enough time to do everyone however she got everyone's hair and make-up done ahead of schedule, with the help of an assistant (I'm blanking on her name!). At my trial I opted to purchase clip in extensions to add volume and length and I'm so happy that I did. Bethany clipped these in for me and my hair was still looking great the next day! I would highly recommend Bethany and TEAM to any bride-to-be for their wedding day. You won't be disappointed!

The service was sooo poor, I feel like writing a long explanation of why is giving this place too much attention. We seen our server when she took our order and when she refilled one drink. Then she pulled ninja magic and we didn't see her again until we flagged her down for the check. I ordered a Pepsi...20mins later I was forced to drink water as the ice from my Pepsi melted. Never did get a refill. Food wasn't great, but not worth the money or the terrible wait.

The service was not terrible, the servers are quick and friendly. However, my problem with this particular location would never bring me back no matter how good the service.

The fruit in this place all tastes gross and is not ripe enough. I can't remember ever having a decent piece of fruit here. The rest of the food is good, nothing to write home about.

THE KICKER - last time I was here I watched who I assume to be the manager in the back, a lady in a black shirt when everyone else was wearing blue or red. She was having a conversation with someone while clearing some half eaten plates, and she was eating people's scraps. I thought this was so disgusting, I couldn't help but watch her do it for several minutes. It so happened that from my seat I could see into the back room and she wasn't even trying to hide it.

This grossed me out so much that I am done with that location, we have been going to Tutti Frutti's instead and everything there is far superior to Cora's. Get some new management, or at least pay them enough to eat at home.

A friend and I decided to go on Thursday night and we are SO glad we did!! The restaurant is decorated beautifully with so much attention to detail, the service was absolutely perfect, drinks were so good and the food was to die for!! We ordered 3 appetizers, 2 entrees and 2 desserts. We split everything and didn't feel gross after eating everything. The food was so flavorful and fresh! Needless to say we have already planned our next visit!

This was, most unfortunately, a very disappointing experience. With four people we paid 169.00$ for one glass of wine, three ice teas, two Happy Hour appetizers and four dinners. We had some fond memories of dining here in the1990s and thought it was time to give it another try, even though we live in Oro Valley. Shrimp were improperly defrosted and over cooked, scallops were small, a trout dish was unimaginatively prepared, over cooked and fishy and bland. Pastas were all very well prepared. What was most upsetting however was an up charge for anchovies, after an already up-charge for Caesar over the house salad and another up-charge for blue cheese crumbles on blue cheese dressing. We were asked if we would like these things with no mention of additional cost. That kind of practice, in my opinion went out with chariots. Sadly, this family will stick with Oro Valley, or downtown with its many fine establishments. By the way, the trout at Culinary Dropout is outstanding

We loved every single thing we ordered at Público! We had the tamale to start then the guacamole arepas, smoked oysters, brisket taco, carnitas taco and finally the flan. Our favorites were the oysters, arepas, carnitas taco and the flan. The service was excellent across the board. If I didn't live in Chicago I'd be back but hopefully on our next trip to St. Louis we can stop in again. Excellent place!!

Due to my boyfriend's sometimes heart breaking spontaneity, we didn't have anywhere to stay on Valentine's night, after we had driven 3+ hours to get away from home for the evening. We were parked at a police station searching for cell service, practicing my patience with him and searching Yelp! for hotels nearby. After calling upwards of 10 places and SURPRISE, all of them being booked for the weekend, I landed on Hotel Indigo. They had a room at a decent price and they were a few minute walk from anything we could have wanted or needed for 24 hours. 
 The hotel was extremely cute. And by cute, I mean quaint. There was definitely no room for excess. No excess baggage or movements. No biggie. They had gifted us with a complimentary bottle of wine, which we thoroughly enjoyed. We freshened up and hit the pavement. 
 It ended up being the perfect spot for a quick night away. We ate, we drank, we walked. Lovely. 

My only complaint: the room was icebox cold when we first arrived. We ended up sweating all night because the air didn't kick on not one time when we got back. Even the house keepers couldn't get it working for us. We learned this morning that the front desk controls it! Would've been nice to know. 

I doubt we will stay here again. We will plan better next time, but it was cute and it worked!

Ate there at lunch with friends for the first time today. I am gonna start doing my reviews in a slightly more orderly fashion so let me give this a try.

Atmosphere
From the outside I was expecting very simple furnishings and bare bones interior. I must say I was very surprised with the little details they have in there. The interior is a blend of middle eastern and egyptian themes with basic tables and chairs. There is modern middle eastern music playing. It does  feel slightly cramped and on a busy night I would wager you would hear the conversations of all your neighbors. The tables have the glass topper with some advertisements posted underneath.

Service
It was not very busy but our server was very attentive and helpful. She kept our glasses full and checked in on us the proper amount. She was not there too ofter but I wasn't left wondering when she would arrive. The food arrived a little later then I expected but it was fresh hot and well presented.

Food
I ordered the Lamb Shawarmas with hummus and a salad. The menu had a variety of good options as well as a large drink selection. I would almost say there were a few too many options as I was slightly overwhelmed at the number of selections but this is a mild complaint. 

The Lamb was delicious, tender, perfectly cooked, and seasoned. The Hummus was not the best I have had but was tasty and was a nice compliment to the meat. I didn't really enjoy the salad as the leaves were bitter I  ended up picking around them and eating the cucumber and tomato.  The dressing was flavorful and aside from the bitter leaves I had a stellar meal here. The Pita that came with my meal was very good and had a nice light flavor.

Ok...so, I have found an all time low!  I don't know if this is a chain, or a roadside one off...but, it is truly the worst Mexican food attempt I have ever had. Granted, when you get off the turnpike, and pull into the Azteca next to the best western, your expectations are not going to be high...but holy crap...this is horrific.   The plate of whatever we had was covered in stone cold, watered down cheese wiz...it could have been almost anything under there.  The token rice and beans were also freezing cold...and the lard hasn't been changed since twinkles were popular. 
My partner ordered a drink...more watered down than a pitcher of cool aide. I am not sure how it tasted, or what was in it...but it smelled like cat pee. She had two sips and was done. 
The waitress was very nice and did a great job.

Come to Speedway Dennys where the call in customers are fed first today on Christmas before the sit in customers.  A lot of people are sitting in waiting over an hour just for their food to be cooked but sat down with drinks to keep them full.
I realize I'm in the minority here but I've had better in gulf shores Alabama. Dry, cold, blah.! Not fresh. Disappointed.

Pushy vendors, knock off clothing and perfume, men in sarongs selling brass knuckles, and the most dismal rodeo of all time.  Welcome to the Cowtown.  This Sale, County staple is pure dystopia.  Where else can you buy a switch blade and a Brazilian she-male porn for under ten bucks?  Well maybe in South America, but you get my point.  Anyhow, if you take this place for anything more than a really fun novelty you WILL be disappointed.  Cowtown is sketchy, it's reasonably ghetto, but that's part of what makes it so fun.  There are both permanent shops (indoor) and garage sale style vending (outdoor).  The latter will appeal more to people looking for collectibles, appliances, and all sorts of random oddities.  The former will appeal more to those looking for a cheap pair of Dickie's shorts. Everybody haggles, so it's definitely a plus if you're good at that; don't be shy.    Wow.  Writing this review is really making me realize that I'm overdue for a visit.  Very nostalgic.

Fun fact: Steve-O from Jackass got his start here working as a rodeo clown, high on cocaine throughout the day.

When we came in they were very rude and told us to walk outside to wait to be seated.We tried to eat here with a group but they would not offer any split checks. One check one card.  So we left. Not accommodating.
I'm very relieved to find them here. We are headed out of town and swung  in. Dr here is fantastic and perfect adjuster!!

If you're in Pass-A-Grille this is a great place to stop in and grab a bite or drink. Inside is a bar setting, outside on the balcony is awesome. There's not many restaurants with views of the ocean like this in the area. The menu is typical and the drinks are strong (no complaints there). Service staff is super friendly. Be sure to try the Monkey Balls!!!! They are amazing. Overall I would give this place a 3.5.

I was here several years ago... and I was abused. I had bruises all over me when I woke up after being unconscious. When I woke up, I was in the ICU, and disoriented. I didn't know where I was. And instead of comforting me, or at least being civil. She yelled at me. I was in tears. She forcefully put a heart monitor back in place so hard that she bruised me chest. At the time I was young and not the type to complain. But this has stuck with me for years and I felt the need flit actually leave a review. I did tell the next shift what happened/ but they blew me off. I work in health care and I know you don't brush off complaints like this. I'm still haunted by my stay here, if you or your loved ones need a hospital... please look elsewhere.

Enjoy this Mexican restaurant especially on Taco Tuesday where you basically get a free soda with two tacos and a pile of nacho chips.  Service is decent, including bringing out your meal.  When they aren't super busy they are usually very attentive on refilling your beverages, unless you sit outside and then not so much.  If you run out of chips (which is rare) you can ask for more.  On Thursdays you can get a burrito with basically a free soda as that day's promotion. Their multi-sauce bar is also pretty god with a wide selection.  And the food is good beyond the taco and burrito I mentioned.  Prices are very reasonable and there is a lot of tables to sit and enjoy the couple TVs.

I was excited to try a new restaurant in the area and I was HIGHLY disappointed. We went for the weekend brunch and they do not have half of the items that they display on the menu. The food is not that good and things aren't labeled so you have no idea what is it. Staff IS NOT friendly. I would NOT recommend going.

ok. first of all. i get to this location at 9:23pm on 06/09/17. the hours of operation says its open until 10pm on the door. So we go to attempt to walk into this "wonderful" establishment, and the door is locked, with 2 employees inside making a sandwich for a customer. Upon seeing this peculiar sight, we make eye contact with one of the employees, and we instanly get dismissed. However, the customer(currently getting his sandwich made) see's us standing at he front door, and speaks with the employee on our behalf, and then "middle-man's" a response to us. His response to us, "she's closing up at 9:30pm." REALLY?? She couldn't open the door to make two sandwiches, which would have taken at most the 7 minutes left on her "time frame" of when she wanted to close the store. 

Needless to say, we will NEVER be coming back to place location. 

***AVOID LIKE THE PLAGUE***

The good: clean, prompt service. Food came fast. Local beer in cans and on tap. 

The average: food

The bad: I can't think of any!

I had the Patty melt. It came with slaw or tortilla chips!

Called two 5 star plumbers here, Mark and JB Plumbing answered and helped me out, the other couldn't return my call till later that evening.
Go with JB!
I described our ceiling leak and Mark set an appointment for the following morning. Less than a half hour later he called back saying he was in the area and could pop in. He took a look and tested some of the issues near our bath, deciding it wasn't a plumbing issue at all but some water leaking out of the tub and through some caulking.
This was a great relief and something I could take care of myself. I offered (or asked) what I could offer him for his troubles but he was not interested. Just a great guy providing great service!
Highly recommended.

The staff were very friendly and helpful but I'm not much of a fan of escape rooms. We played a game that revolves around discovering who a thief was. You get 45 minutes to solve the problem and have to work together in a team. I would recommend only going with a group of friends or people that you know because otherwise it might get challenging (and probably annoying) to be working with strangers, especially given that some people might have higher levels of motivation than others. We ended up having it where two people essentially took charge in our group while others lounged around and helped a bit. 

Again, staff were friendly though and the prices were fair so if you like escape rooms or want to try it, this could be a good place to go to as it's located centrally on Jasper Ave and has some parking and LRT stations nearby. The owner's dog was there and was fun to play with. We also got to take a post game picture.

while I wouldn't say that this is a lousy restaurant, I think that more may be raving based on it's lineage.
Mrs Hays Stuffed wings are good, but nothing of note.
The caprese salad suffered from a lackluster tomato, prolly hard to produce in this environment.
The grilled pork chop was a little too sweet and a bit chewy.
The almond tart was low on sweet (good) but also low on flavor.
Cappuccino was excellent.
But the star of the night was the glass of red that the waiter recommended.  I wish I could remember what it was, but it was excellent, inexpensive, a little spicy and oaky and not fruit forward.
Nice atmosphere and cliental.  Not too loud.

"It's a lot more fun to eat in a bar than it is to drink in a restaurant", is their motto and for those of you who are not familiar with Chickie's & Pete's, they have won the title of Best Sports Bar on the East coast by ESPN, plus countless other awards for their wings, crabs and "crab fries".Crab fries have no crab meat, but are a huge portion of crinkle cut fries seasoned with their secret spices, similar to Old Bay if you ask me and served with two sides of a thick white cheese dipping sauce. Even though they are not mind blowing, they are addictive once you start to dip those puppies and I tend to eat them like a robot until the last of the sauce is gone. They are a must have when visiting any Chickie's & Pete's. 

"Everyone's favorite crab house and sports bar" at the Parx Casino is huge with well over fifty giant screened TV's, this and some of their other locations feature Play2 where you can golf on a wall sized screen with Tiger Woods or interact playing many other games. The set up is unique with a large centered open bar on the main floor, there are actually three levels and it's cool to watch from your own private high backed booth literally being able to see all the action. 

We started with the crab fries, ordering them along with our drinks, I had a watermelontini which was okay, very well chilled just had that artificial after taste to it and hubby had a large coke. Our server, Nikki was so knowledgeable of the menu and space, she was so friendly and personable and on the ball. The food is cooked to order and does take awhile, but it comes out hot and she keeps you informed as to how your meal is progressing. She really added to our dining experience and I will not hesitate to ask for her in the future. 

We ordered Chickie's Riverboat clams which are clams casino, theirs have whole clams with spicy seasoning, red and green peppers and onions and just enough bread crumbs to bring it all together, we enjoyed these, but they came out pretty well after our meals. Bon Jovi goes to Chickie's & Pete's for one thing only and that is their "Lisa's Blonde lobster pie", so I had to order this. It is a white flatbread pizza loaded with cheese, herbs and all lobster tail meat and I do mean huge chunks of lobster tail on each slice, unless you can really eat, you will be taking some home, because it is by no means a personal sized pie, cost $20.00.and worth every penny! 

Hubby had a cheesesteak, what can I say and it was wit onions and provolone, served on a good roll and he liked it, I snitched a bite, twas good indeed. I really am so excited they have opened this location close to me, even though passing those one armed bandits is virtually impossible! I need to grab a few girlfriends and or coworkers to check out sharing the hot and spicy bowl of shrimp, they have won awards for their red or white mussels for eight straight years, the jumbo lump crab cakes, their crab flatbread with red sauce and aioli, oh I could go on, it all looks and sounds good. 

This is a crab house folks and they have crabs galore, king, snow crabs, blue crab, dungeness crab and lump crab meat.

I love their restrooms! Black wall and floor tiles, even the stalls with colorful tiles scattered here and there and their sinks are huge crab pots with matching metal strainers inserted on top. Chickie's & Pete's opened their eighth location inside the Parx Casino last Friday, but they will be celebrating their grand opening this coming Friday, August 6. Do go and check them out. Wheelchair accessible.

I strongly urge anyone looking to get top surgery to find another doctor. Dr. Rumer almost gave my partner the wrong kind of surgery but luckily he spotted the error on the forms right before the operation. The pre op appointments were hasty, rushed, and sloppy, and she dismissed all of his questions. Then, her staff brushed him off when he told them he suspected an infection in his incision site, which has led to permanent loss of sensation on one side. She is a sloppy, careless surgeon and should not be trusted with this lifelong decision!

The family and I were in town on vacation and rode by this little 'hole in the wall' pizza place. Frankly, it's the best pizza I've had outside of Italy and the Italian family at the table across from us (no joke!) agreed. There is way more seating than the other reviews would lead you to believe. A little pricey for pizza, but that's ok for a little slice of heaven. Oh, and the kale salad was delicious too.

This Starbucks is pretty darn good. Not sure anyone really cares about which way the windows face, were not hanging out at Starbucks for the view! The drinks are all properly made, service is good--same as most Starbucks, and really consistency is a main reason we go to Starbucks. Friendly folks here too. Cleanliness has never been an issue when I've been in there. Its the same as any other Starbucks--well maintained.

Are the fries suppose to be sweet? It was like they switched the sugar and salt shakers. The fries were also soggy. 
The wings were okay but I would have liked them to be more crispy. We got three flavors: original hot, cajun, and garlic parmesan​. The only flavor that actually stood out was the original hot. Cajun was too salty. 

The service was actually nice and fast. The place was crowded but we got our food at a decent time. We may come back to try the other flavors but we'll just leave out the fries next time.

It was a really chill environment.... lots of college kids... a really nice crowd.. good place to go to relax and smoke hookah.. highly recomended!!!!!!!!
not mention i didnt think it was that bad priced..... 13 for a hookah and 9 for a refil.. and they dont care how many people you go with unlike a lot of the others that have a limit

nothing great here...pretty cool place but the brisket was fatty....the ribs and pulled pork not too tasty. the chicken was good.     not the Stephen Starr restaurant I was hoping for. The smoked meats are just average and not worth the trip or price. I am a competitive bbq judge and this place does not make the grade. There are better smoked meat bbq joints in Philly.

Had the lunch buffet.  Best to worst...
- buffet: great way to get variety, and all you can eat
- spicy achar sauce (next to raita): spicy and flavorful
- lentil soup: had three (albeit small) cups.
- fried veggies: I think they were labeled "pakoras" or something, but more like American fried slices of zucchini and eggplant (and others?)--but really good.  Only got 1 on the first plate (I like at least tasting everything), but got a few more next plate.
- tarkari curry dish with potato and bamboo shoots(?): different than other (Indian/Thai/etc.) curries I've had, and good
- veggie korma: pretty good, not the most memorable/remarkable.
- dish labeled "bok choy" (but had lots else in it)
- naan: good, even after sitting in the buffet
- jap chae/chap chae: noodles are different, but not otherwise remarkable
- rice: fine as bed for curries. longer-grain white, perhaps some basmati variety.
- price (~$13.50pp incl. tax+tip): slightly higher(?) than comparable places, but maybe I'm behind the times.
- (they had 3 chicken dishes, I think bulgogi, tandoori, and tikka masala, that I didn't try)
- (they also had mango pudding and rice pudding, but baby started crying...)

Maybe I'm just subconsciously comparing with Indian food and would rather have that (at Gokul or somewhere)...

Very good, affordable and interesting food from a friendly staff.

My first visit was opening night and it was clear the Sassy Jacs team was well prepared for their opening.  I had the fried chicken special that 's been thoroughly well received by reviewers on Yelp, RFT and STLtoday.com over the last 2 months. It is an excellent plate. The deviled eggs came first, and were an outstanding choice made great by the bacon and flash fried basil in the dish. My girlfriend had the "club" sandwich,  her measuring stick for any restaurant serving one, and found the chicken breast perfect, and andouille a wonderful alternative to ham.  

Several months later, we've had 75% of the menu and are yet to be disappointed. Andrew clearly can cook much more than the limited menu currently requires of him  but I understand his motivations for limiting his operation.  This has been a troubled location as Verlin's and Phelan's; keeping it simple (and very good) while the business grows is very smart.

For a 5th star the Sassy JAC's team could improve their beer selection and expand the menu a bit.  It is short on craft brews and long on American style lagers from AB, Coors and Miller. The menu has room to expand to further showcase chef Ladlie's talents. As a Soulard resident, it is my go to place for a quick bite or a low key cocktail. I look forward to watching this business grow.

Potato salad is authentic and to die for. Have been going to Schillers since I was a little girl. (these are second owners). Food is excellent, good selection of wursts, beer and items not found anywhere else !!

We came in because we read reviews of a wonderful patio and we wanted to sit and chat. We were told they were waiting for one party to leave and nobody was in front of us. They said it would be about 20 minutes. We went to look at the courtyard patio and saw several empty tables and told them we could pull an extra chair for our party of 5. They gave some ridiculous excuses every few minutes or so before we left in an hour and a half.

If I could give ZERO stars I would! Usually love this place, however tonight we walked in and were treated so rudely! They close at 11 and we came in 45 minutes before closing time and were ignored by the host and three waiters, we stood at the door for about ten minutes without anyone giving us any attention, then finally a manager came over and said they would get a table ready for us, when he told the waiter you could see everyones annoyance,  we didnt walk in 5 minutes before close, but we certainly waited until 10:30 ... So disrespectful.. And while we continued to wait the waitress cleaned our table as slowly as possible, we ended up leaving of course..

I ignored the bad reviews because eco-friendly dry cleaning is hard to come by, but I am starting to wonder if they are "eco-friendly" because they don't clean your clothes at all. I've had the usual experiences where they return clothes that have clearly not been cleaned, but I thought I would give them another chance. This time, I picked up a dress with new grease and grime stains after "they cleaned it twice," even though one of the stains even got better by just scratching it with my fingernail.

Each time the owner has been totally nasty, for lack of a better word. I will never be back to this cleaner.

Took my cat here 2 weeks ago for a upper respiratory problems. Got antibiotics and steroids and was on her way. She was doing great up until two weeks later we had to return because her condition had gotten way worse than when I took her there to begin with (even sneezing blood out of her nose now!). Staff is friendly but don't seem to know what they're talking about, the actual doctor never steps foot out from inside the place or spoke to me personally. Gave me take home medicine and gave odd advice on how to give it to my cat who is having breathing issues (forcing a pill in her mouth and shutting it, forcing it down her throat). Not the worst but definitely not the best vet. Wouldn't recommend coming here if your pet has serious issues, they're not informative and just try to send you on your way. Don't really discuss what the problem is, they just barely explain how the medicine works. Going to try to take my cat elsewhere, wish her luck!

I'm allways amazed with Welcome Diner. Very high quality and always perfectly prepared. Everything I've ordered so far can be described this way. The service is always friendly and attentive. Since my second visit the super nice staff always makes the effort to stop by and say hi or thanks me for coming back.

The chicken breakfast biscuits, calabacitas, fried green tomoto sandwich, sister buritto... all amazing. In an effort to get bacon with breakfast for less than 10 dollars I've found plain old ala carte 1 egg, bacon, and toast to still be outstanding.

Why four instead of five stars? Two reasons. The first is the price. 

Rating every place simply on food quality and service without considering price cheats the occational better than average budget places like Robert's or Cafe Pase. Balance between good prices, high quality food and prep, and amazing customer service is imporatant. 

Here is the math: $14 to get an omlette with a vegetable. $17 to get iced tea with it. $23 with tax and a decent tip. I paid this much for a high end french breakfast at Grand Central in Manhattan. Welcome Diner delivers but might consider the community and offer an affordable option or two. A daily discounted special maybe?

The second reason I rate 4 stars is some of the entrees aren't well thought out or portioned. I love the high grade of ingredients in roasted vegs breakfast. This dish includes a centimeter of standing liquid on the plate and a soggy mess to wade through. Also, whenever someone orders an item including gravy... the rationing is real.

I'll keep coming back, just not as often as I'd prefer!

after eating here so long i'm done. you can't even order a pizza at 8:09. last friday i tried at 845. i get the covid is hard but this apathetic way will doom your business. good for lunch only these days. bye

Not so good...

Popped by for a nice lunch on a Tuesday.  It was in between lunch and dinner - around 4pm.  But about 15 other people were eating so I stopped in.  Ordered a simple, small Jambalaya and a romaine heart salad with Caesar Dressing - quick and easy.  As a local I know jambalaya.  This was NOT a good example.  The portion was laughable - barley a large spoonful in a large round bowl.  Was it meant for a cup? Even so it would not have filled it.  Then to add insult to injury it was dry and over cooked.  Not an ounce of moisture.  The rice was dried out, the sausage hard and shrimp chewy.  BAD!  This is a staple dish in New Orleans! How do you get it wrong? A store bought mix would have been exponentially better.  Then the salad....what a sad salad.  About what you would expect from a Safeway salad bar - not a marble topped Vieux Carre establishment.  Each item was $8.  With  a drink and tip it was a $21 meal not worth $4.  And the waiter - uncomfortable and peculiar acting.  Not sure if he was in a rush to go home or simply over it all.  Will not be going back and I will be sure to tell all of my out of town visitors that despite the unbelievable location - not worth it.

I had been meaning to try Bing Bing Dim Sum for a long time, so when I finally got a chance a few weeks back I was so excited. I actually didn't realize it was a fusion spot until getting there (fusion gets dangerous because it's hard to do well), but I figured I'd give it a shot since I was already there. Overall I was disappointed with the dishes. The price makes it even more disappointing. 

Ordered the
--Roast Pork Bao - a roast pork sandwich in different packaging. I would take any other bao over this one. It was just a bad combo. 
--Spicy Cucumber w/tofu - really great! Light, refreshing, and a little tangy. 
--Pork Soup Dumplings - the pork was fine but the skin was a bit too thick (making the dumpling somewhat bland). 
--Soy Sauce Pork Noodles - more expensive and a little less flavorful than ones you'd get in Chinatown.

If you like really like Chinese food, then I suggest you stick to Chinatown

I really wanted to support a small local owned business plus I love coffee so I wanted to experience their organic fresh ground/brewed coffee but when I walked in, the two young men behind the counter were NOT wearing masks.  I asked why weren't they wearing masks & they said "if there's less than 10 people, we're not required to wear them."  I told them ok, well I don't feel comfortable with you serving me in the middle of a pandemic without a mask on so I'll take my money elsewhere.  SMH!  Dunkin  got my money today!

The bartenders and other staff were very nice and the service was good every time we've been to Primebar. The atmosphere is also nice, particularly as a good place for happy hour drinks. Unfortunately, the food was pretty disappointing both times we've eaten there. I thought the first time might've been a fluke so we gave it a second try, but we both independently came to the same conclusion each time, and we're not the extremely picky type, so I'm confident we're not being unreasonable. That second time was a few weeks ago so I don't recall details well enough to mention specifically what we had and why it wasn't very good, but both of us only ate less than half of our meals and were hungry again in just a couple hours. It's actually not that the food is so extremely horrible to the point of being inedible ... we're just not used to what I call "bar food," so I think our disappointment stems from the fact that the food is priced as it would be in a decent restaurant (thus setting expectations), but it is really just bar quality food.  We'll still go back to Primebar for drinks, but we'll eat first next time.

I had dinner with a small group at Villa Barolo tonight and had a disappointing experience. While the waitress was nice, the service was very slow. It took more than 10 minutes after we sat down for her to come over and offer water and tell us about the specials. After placing our order, it took more than 15 minutes for the wine to arrive. This was on a Tuesday evening with the restaurant being nowhere close to busy. The food wasn't great with a couple of exceptions. The calamari was bland and the special eggplant appetizer had more sharp provolone cheese in it than eggplant. The seafood risotto was undercooked and the jalapeno overpowered everything else in the dish. The Mediterranean salad was okay but not a good value at $17 for a basic salad with a couple pieces of crab. The lamb chops were good though. We also ordered the Apple Tarte which apparently takes 40 minutes to make at the same time as we ordered our entrees and were excited to end our meal with it, but found out after having our plates cleared that the waitress forgot to put in that order and we'd have to wait another 40 minutes if we wanted it so we left without getting dessert.

Due to the whole covid 19 situation we ordered our dinner to be picked up curbside. The pickup was great and the young lady who brought out our dinner was professional and friendly (she's the only reason this review wasn't one star). Where things fell apart was the food. The Kung pao chicken was really bland. Chicken, peanuts and Thai peppers (that were obviously added after the chicken was cooked). Panda Express does a better job than this. The other dish we ordered "Singapore street noodles" with extra chicken was severely lacking in both flavor and protein ingredients. Not sure what we paid for with the extra chicken. We counted the chicken "chunks" and there were ten. Pathetic. The food bill was $40. Next time we'll save our money and find a local restaurant that serves food that's both flavorful and filling.

My wife had a craving for red Robinson we decided to go. Didn't stay long. 1st the hostess was also taking to-go orders over the phone causing a huge back up in the lobby of customers coming in to be seated. Then the hostess was brash and nearly rude. No greeting other than "name and how many" a couple and in and asked to sit at the bar which was empty other than 2 other tables and they were rudely told "no it's at capacity" the gentle pointed out that there was plenty of seating and was straight up told "no" to which we then decided to leave.  We will probably never be back which is a shame cause we didn't even get to try the food. Our experience with just the hostess was enough to turn us off to this location for good.

I have to say that the service itself was actually great. They were friendly and took my order right away, a d the food took a while to be prepared, but it was fresh. I also found the facility itself to be clean.  Now let's get to the food. I have always enjoyed LJS, but the food I bought here was nowhere near as high quality as I've been used to. We got the family meal with both fish and chicken, with onion rings and lots of extra hush puppies (our favorite side). I also got some cheese curds. The curds were the best part of the meal, they were just right. The fish, rings, chicken, and hush puppies were all too greasy. Keep in mind, we love fried foods, and like I said, I've been eating at LJS for decades. This was beyond great. I literally squeezed grease out of this food! Very disappointed, I will not return to this location.

Didn't even come here for an actual meal, just for cheesecake and while the cheesecake itself is fine, the speed of the service was a little much. It was difficult to tell where the line to order was with the crowd of people around the counter and after we ordered, it took 20 minutes just to receive a single slice of cheesecake (after also watching several people who ordered after us get their order before us). Wouldn't recommend if you're in a rush.

We met friends of ours that were staying in the hotel for drinks there, and at that time we liked it so much we said we'd return for dinner sometime.  Well today dinner ended up being a late lunch but we went for it anyway.  You walk into the resturant seating area through the lounge, which has a very modern feel to it.  We ordered drinks and proceeded to seat ourselves in the resturant dinning room, also decorated with very modern finishings.  The seating area also features an expansive view of the Santa Catalina mountains to the North.  We started with an order of fried calamari for an appetizer.  It came out sizzling hot with a couple of lemon wedges and a red dipping sauce.  The calamari itself was enjoyable, very tender with a light touch on the batter.  The only drawback I had with it was that the sauce was a bit to much on the ketchupy side for me, so we  just used the lemon slices instead.  I ordered the fettuccine with grilled chicken, my spouse ordered the the slow cooked rib panni sandwhich.  The fettuccine arrived with a light sauce and grilled, spiced chicken breast.  The fettuccine sauce had a nice flavor, but just a touch thiner than I prefer.  The noodles were cooked just right, slightly al dente.  The chicken breast was great, nicely spiced and very tender.  We both loved my spouses rib panni sandwhich!  The panni was very fresh, and the rib meat was very, very tender; with a subtle smoke flavor to it.  All in all, a very pleasent experiance.

Me and my girlfriend went here and the food was decent. We sat upstairs, but we were confused because we thought it was hibachi where they cook in front of you and this that and the third. When we went upstairs I ordered the chicken hibachi and it came out how you normally would get food in a restaurant. I don't know if the miscommunication was on our end or if they really do have a hibachi dinner where they cook in front of you. Also the sushi was bit very good at all.

I was craving ramen today, so I decided to check out this place! Walked in and was seated shortly. I love how simple the menu is and very easy to read. I decided to get spicy Tonkatsu ramen which is my usual order at other ramen places. When the food came out, I was amazed at how big the bowl was and how the portion was very good for the price. The ramen broth itself was delicious, but not spicy enough that I was used to. The meat, however was chewy and the egg was over cooked. Other than that, it was delicious and the servers were nice. I would definitely come back.

I've given this place two tries now and both times were extremely disappointing. With advertisement like a healthy option comparable to fresh kitchen, id say this is the opposite. I got the sweet potato noodles which were overly salty and drenched in some type of teriyaki sauce. The cauliflower sauce was dripping and too watery, making everything soggy. Perhaps the worst was seeing a kitchen employee handle transferring food pans without gloves on. This food is too expensive for what it is. I ended up having to buy another lunch next door, partly bc it was not pleasant and partly because I just couldn't stop thinking about the glove-less employee.

I wish I could give a negative review by far the worst customer service I have ever had in my life dealing with a car dealer.  The car had to be returned a half dozen times to get a repair done under warranty. I am so disgusted by the complete incompetence of Cherry hill Vw service department.  I really want to write a bunch of curse words to describe this but cant. IF your able to take your car anywhere else I mean anywhere else do tour self a favor and take it. I don't care if it is opening day at a shop near you  and the shop has never once worked on a car a day in their lives it will still be better . WORSE SERVICE EVER!!!!!!!!!!!!

Came here on an open mic night on a recent Friday, and what made the night for me was the amazing company and the fun atmosphere around performances, not the drinks unfortunately. I had high expectations especially for their boba since it's even in the name, but their boba was more like bullets which made me suspect they might not have been cooked long enough. 

The jasmine green tea I ordered was even more disappointing because it was unbelievably bitter and impossible to take more than a couple tiny sips. A couple of us asked if our jasmine green boba tea could be replaced with something else, and we each got a smoothie and specifically asked for no boba. My chocolate almond banana had such an unpleasant taste that I finished no more than 10% of it, but the chocolate coconut was a lot better. 

There are dozens of seats and the vibe is casual and alternative, but I doubt their drinks will ever draw me back to this place.

I've been going there for the past 10 years now, never disappointed!! Steven, one of the owner, is my go to person. He knows I have very high expectations about my haircut. Always takes his time and make sure everything is perfect! Highly recommend this place!!

This used to be a pizza place I can count on for whenever I crave for pizza. Sadly this time my experience was subpar. Garlic bread was mostly bread with hints of garlic and the margarita pizza had a burnt base. Everything tasted average at best. Gonna have to hunt for a new place. :(

A very cosy and whole in the wall Japanese place.A little isolated but very traditional Japanese food their wasabi is so fresh  never tasted such fresh wasabi we ordered vegetable and shrimp tempura which was good :) spicy California is awesome  spicy crab roll was ok their teriyaki chicken was delicious. They give refills of green tea  be ready to enter a lil home  in Japan that's how it looks and feels also :) def try it out if u like Japanese food . Wendy was very helpful in picking out the right dishes so special thanks to her I  have attached all the pics including their menu and prices check them out

Poor service! I asked for a 6 inch meatball marinara sub. The two guys helping me with the sandwich were asking all the appropriate questions, i.e. Vegetables? What kind of cheese? Toasted? All went ok until it was time to check out. I asked the lady if there were any deals for ordering a drink and a sub. She ignored me the first two times and than walked away from me to the soda machine. I asked again Are there any deals? She finally answered me but was a little ways away so I could not hear her. She couldn't hear me because I finally decided to get a soda anyways along with my sub (deal or no deal), I had to repeat my soda order again. It seems even with the difficulty hearing each other, she absolutely refused to take one step closer to me. And I was leaning as far as I could over the counter. They were not that busy. It seemed that I was the only customer that lunch hour on Oct 25th 2018. I thought that they could be more appreciative of the customers that you do get. Why o' why work in a customer service related job when you don't like providing a service to people. I would not order from THIS Subway ever again!
Nothing special. Heard rave reviews from others when i moved here. Meat on a shell with onions. Salsa bar. Thats it? Not for me.

Went again last night and got the last 2 community table seats. Something we noticed the 1st time but failed to mention is the restaurant has an odor, somewhere between pine sole(my wife mentioned both times) and mushrooms cooking down in bleach, has anyone else noticed this? Had the coppa (salume) appetizer, a cheek / jowl with pickled watermelon rinds, not so great. There was so little meat vs lardo that the coppa had very little flavor and seemed to come apart very easily. Pear and blue cheese salad w walnuts, excellent. The mains were black ink pasta w a squid/shrimp sauce, very good, fishy but in a good way. I had the meatballs w a dandelion gremolata in the  sunday sauce, while very good the gremolata added so much contrast to the meatballs (citrus, garlic) that I found myself wanting some bread or pasta to cut the noise, would be great if I could get 2 meatballs on some pasta. This place is very hip right now so its like going to an opening of a broadway show, hopefully it can be a regular stop once the noise abates.

So impressed with Dr. Soli  and highly recommend him as a very knowledgeable veterinarian. He spent a long time with me and my dog - we were first-timers - and through simple tests, and a solution he mixed up himself, he managed to heal my dog's chronic ear infection without antibiotics. Also, I didn't have to wait at all - they took us back immediately and They promptly called me with test results. So happy to have discovered this vet!

Solid.
Good life fitness has all sorts of equipment.
But it is not the type of gym where you want to go if you are in anyway self conscious.

I came here for a free event , and found myself thinking. I know my way around a gym, but this place could be pretty intimidating for people new to fitness.

Lots of guys who are obviously on steriods, and girls working out in full make out. Good Life seems like a great place to show off your Under Armour, and Nike sports wear.
(Don't show up in your tattered tshirt or sweats.)

I liked Good Life fitness but only gave it 3 stars.

Why it got 3 stars
Lots of ample parking
People who are working out are serious here
Lots of equipment

Why it didn't get 5 stars
Intimidating not friendly
For the same price there are better gyms.

I was told a simple install would take half an hour...it took 2. Salesman was untruthful.
Tonia

This is my second visit to this Red Lobster in a year. First time the biscuits were bland but I thought it was a fluke. Went tonight and ordered the whole steamed Maine lobster dinner and had to send it back because it was rubbery and the meat stuck to the shell, indicating overlooked. Manager offered a replacement so I accepted, but the second one was as bad as the first. The "chef" at this location has no idea how to cook a lobster. Would not recommend this location to anyone.

OMG!!!! I was beyond excited when I found out I was close to Bobbys Burger Palace. I LOVE LOVE Bobby Flay.. I had the Dallas Turkey burger,  fries and Orea milkshake.. YUMMY!!!! The turkey burger was full full of flavor and the milkshake knock your socks off!!!! The fries were over cooked and dry and that is why I only gave 4 stars but I will definitely come back while Im in Philly..

We were on vacation in Nashville. We went to Morton's for our 48th anniversary. I ordered a 12oz filet mignon and my wife ordered a 6 oz filet mignon. Both medium to medium well. Hers was very good, pink inside. Mine was butter flied and over done. Well done. Not good. The bread they brought out was covered with onions. I hate onions. I asked for something different and they said all they had were hamburger buns. We paid $194.00 for a average at best meal and drank water.  Way over priced for the quality.
Good luck if you eat at this location. I suggest you go to a local bar and get a good sandwich.
Ron from Lancaster, Ohio

I had a problem with the secretary as I told her I didn't have money to do my crowns yet Patricia schedules me for a 2 hr session I went for my follow up visit in a clean up that cost me $185 which I'm ok with that amount but because I couldn't afford the crowns at this point she had scheduled me for a 2 hr appointment and charged me $50 because I didn't want to order my crowns.. I will not return. I didn't have $50 extra otherwise I would of order my crowns but she didn't care and the Dr Beatriz never came out to resolve issue only good person there is the hygienist Suzie try to avoid

Thoroughly disappointed with the service AND the food.  Give me a break!  If you like overpriced foo-foo dishes you'll finish in 3 minutes, this is your place.  I'll never go to this place again.  Luckily for me, there's plenty of other options in the area.

What a disappointment. The last time I ate at Sketch was 2009 and  it seems to have gone downhill rapidly.

The staff seemed to be slammed the day I came and didn't really seem to care. I watched as two different tables left after not even being seen by the server.

Having a server doesn't make any sense since they just turn in a checkbox list into the cook. The service (when they did anything) was rude, short and not understanding.

On the plus side the fries were good. So was the burger. For the money and time it's not worth it, unfortunately.

Great atmosphere, fantastic food. The goat cheese tart is INCREDIBLE, and I occasionally visit just for the short ribs. Drinks are unique and delicious. Good service, and the wait is usually not terribly long. I just wish they were open for lunch!

What happened to circles? This place use to have great food. Last night, we ordered delivery. The noodles were mush, and unappetizing. The massaman curry dip Had soggy bread and barely any sauce and no chicken. The cheesesteak spring rolls were great in taste but you only got three tiny ones For $9. I won't be ordering from here again. $40 Down the drain.

I was very much anticipating my night out at Fond, but unfortunately I was very disappointed with the experience. We were sat at a table near the door and by the windows. There was a very perceptible draft coming from the windows and a freezing breeze every time the door opened. There was a space heater next to me but nothing for my husband. The wait staff kept going in and out, I think they were trying to track down a cab. It was extremely unpleasant. Everyone was perfectly nice, but we sat for a while without being greeted and the service was generally slow. The food was not great and certainly not worth the price tag. At the end of the meal, the server sensed we were dissatisfied and tried to make it up with some sparkling rose, but that didn't take away the sting of spending $130 on a sour experience. If you go in the winter either request a table far from the door or bundle up.

Great tacos and drink specials. I went the other night and they added some new items to the menu.  In my option, they should just stick to producing the best tacos in town and not try and add mussels, etc.

I recently went back to Alibabas (on another groupon), and we had a great server who knew the menu well (a first). The food came out quickly and halfway through my meal, the owner came to our table with a pitcher of water(another first), asking how our meal was. Food is amazing, as always.

The plates can get expensive but if you're going out for lunch without some groupon-like discount, then get the Gyro Sandwich! Costs 4.99$ and is a full meal. 

Rating 4/5 stars because this is the first time I've had a good server, so I will raise it to 5 stars if it is the same next time I go.

Maybe it's because I had higher expectations of this place coming in--
I just wasn't impressed. I just wasn't. I ordered the two most mentioned dish and both were mediocre at best; the service was brisk but at the expense of seeming rude.

The roti canai wasn't anything special. It's supposed to be an Indian pancake and the pancake itself was an interesting dish just because of the fact that it's crispy and flaky but chewy at the same time. The side sauce is the curry sauce, which I personally thought was uninteresting.

The mee siam was a disappointment. Maybe I have a bias because I'm not a fan of rice noodles. 

It's very reasonably priced, however so it may be worth checking out once.
It's alright. Not my fav place in IV but if I do go here, Macdaddy is my go to!

Do not use this company if you are not a celebrity or some person with a little blue check mark for status. Five star treatment is far from what I received. Employees took my personal information that were not authorized and contacted me at 730am trying to rebook my move on moving day or asking to show up immediately. I asked them to bring extra large boxes to compile a few small items and they showed up with two small boxes and set them on the floor. To top it off movers were seen DRINKING ALCOHOL on the job by a property manger and taking advantage of a free happy hour and eating food -going back for seconds, meant for local residents while leaving my personal belongings in the hallway unattended. I was made aware the drinking the following day and because they had no uniforms on staff were unaware who they were. They broke and shattered a glass frame 15 feet high in the hall. Such an embarrassment to have my belongings strewn everywhere, unattended. I had sectioned off a basket of items I planned on taking to my car. I told the movers DO NOT take these items- several times !!! Later on I found the basket sitting out in the hallways unattended. When we arrived at my new home the lead parked he truck out front blocking a thru traffic area. I had told him that it was suggested we unload in the back. The mover insisted that we park in the front which wasted an hour. One of the movers became aggressive and hostile later in the day and I watched them drop & then kick a $400 piece of electronics that I specifically asked to be padded and wrapped.I asked to speak to someone on call as things were getting so uncomfortable and uncoordinated that I started carrying my own things down the hall. I understand that moving in some of the apartment complexes can be tricky but perhaps it would be helpful if you did your research before sending people and made sure of such things. Later, The two movers were arguing with each other and the third was dragging stuff down the hall and smelled like weed. 
It starts to rain and was getting so late that we had to finish the move the next day. Then one of the movers went on to confront me in a threatening voice asking  "did you tell my supervisor we were not doing a good job?"
Hands down I should have just called the police and had them arrested as 2 of them were clearly under the influence while working and the other smelled of weed. They proceeded to lose their phones in the moving truck and sat in the parking garage area until nearly 1am looking for their phones swearing, cussing with the truck door open with my belongings for 2 hours. Covid or not -drug screen your employees -I should not have to smell weed from your employee. Get your act together- have supervision around your employees. It took them over 10 hours to move a 1 bedroom apartment across town. Moving is one of the top stressful events and your people made it way worse that it should have ever been. I'm no superstar but I'm a strong person who felt very disrespected, and down right threatened by the punks that were working. A total embarrassment- you might want to start investing in damage control because you have definitely got a bad reputation with the apartments in town.

When in for rose gold highlights ended up with orange hair! I have not colored my hair in over 18 years highly disappointed don't know what to do!!I have a mess of hair! I have been in the past 3 time for a cut and a style and never had any problems! Crystal was the beautician that did my hair! I honestly don't think she knows what she's doing I wish places like this wouldn't hire people like that! Very disappointed customer I hope there is some place that can help me with my hair! Almost $200 plus tip in hair color and product that I have spent at this place!And now I have to find somewhere else to fix my hair! And pay more money! Just sad!!

Prices were not as advertised. When we showed up in person the price was $20 more for weeknights and almost double for the weekend, than what was posted online. We had no place to stay, because our previous reservations were at a prostitute and Roach infested hotel, and booked this place in desperation 2 days before we needed it and when we found a better deal and tried to cancel our reservation they would not refund our $113 deposit, because "we did not cancel a week in advance." We can't cancel 1 week in advance if we didn't book a week in advance. They also never told us about their cancelation policy. So much for "southern hospitality".

Been here several times and had good service and great food. Went this past weekend, sat at the outside bar and was ignored. We eventually were able to order a margarita and the guacamole and the bartender never asked us if we wanted anything else. Our intention was to eat but since no one would acknowledge our existence we hunted down our bill and went to another restaurant. One could say it was because they were busy but there were empty seats at the bar so I'm not sure that's a valid excuse.
Overpriced and parking sucks. We had to leave to feed the meter 3 times during our meal. We are locals but this restaurant is clearly for tourists that they know won't be back anyway!

a damn good calzone and pizza that to me can hardly be matched anywhere else!  but my god,  a trip back to the 70's atmosphere with dirty carpets,  sticky tables.   nice staff but please,  put some money into this place and fix it up!  clean it at least!

I have to agree with some of the other reviewers---I have been looking forward to trying this restaurant for forever. Perhaps I hyped it up too much in my head, but it wasn't that spectacular. The squid ink pasta was just OK. It didn't taste better than marches handmade pasta and the pricing at this restaurant is much higher than I think it needs to be. Yes, the ambiance is great, but so is holland houses patio and lighting...at 5-6 dollars less. The waitress was nice, but her niceness became forced throughout the dinner when she realized that we only wanted to order dinner and no drinks or dessert. Overall, my stomach and I left feeling somewhat disappointed. We left to drown our sorrows at holland house.

My husband and I were given a gift certificate to go to Firebirds Restaurant for Christmas..we heard such great raves about the place and was looking forward to going..we are in our 60's and we go earlier to beat the crowds and waiting for a table...we arrived around 3:45...nobody in the place other than the bar. we were seated in the dining room area...we were the ONLY people seated in the dining room..first thing we hated was the loud music blasting in our ears.. and the unusual dark dim dining room.. hard to see.. the lights were down very low.. the waitress  greeted us right away and went into a big shpeel about the "wood fired" food, etc..it was quite lengthy and quite boring to us.. I like stuffed shrimp and nothing much is on their menu with shrimp..very disappointing..anyway, I ordered the prime rib, baked potato, and was shocked that they have the nerve to charge you 4.99 to add a salad, for their high prices , u should get that included!!!I opted to pay extra and get  shrimp on a skewer added to my order..another 6 or 7.95 extra ..my husband ordered the salmon...I had the lemonade and they boasted how good it was, but I wasnt impressed, and I hated the sugar they put on the rim of the glass, too sticky ,and the lemonade is quite sweet too.. I prefer Paneras lemonade much more..the lemonade was 3.95...at Firebirds...not worth it..we put our order in and waited and waited and waited for our dinner...in the meantime they give us 1 measley roll to split between the 2 of us!! and 1 melon ball scoop of butter for the 2 of us too???? Hellooo, how chincey is that?? I had to constantly ask for butter throughout the meal!! VERY ANNOYING!! They didnt even give me any for my baked potato!! Anyway, the worst is yet to come!!!! We waited and waited and waited for our food till well after 5pm!!!!!They finally came out with some cockamamie story that the cook had to go and cut the piece of salmon off of fish and it thats what was taking sooo long?? WTF??????? We joked and said, did he go to ALaska and fish for it?? The food FINALLY arrived and the primed rib piece I had was cold and over 60% fat!!! IT was AWFUL!!!! The shrimp on the skewer was warm to cold...no cocktail sauce, had to ask for that too..I couldnt eat the prime rib and told the waitress how terrible it was...the manager came up and said we wouldnt have to pay for the meals except the for the shrimp, since we had waited so long and the prime rib sucked so bad!!! Then the waitress asked me if I wanted to get something different,  I wasnt sure what she meant, I thought that they were just gonna replace  the lousey prime rib for something else, but I didnt know they were gonna charge me!! I ordered the NY strip!.. Well when that came it was like shoe leather ,no flavor, and most of it was FAT TOO? WTFFFFFFF????  This food is quite expensive, the prime rib was around 33.00 and this was NOT NY Strip, what NY strip has tons of fat..and it was 28.99!!!WHAT A TOTAL RIPOFFFF!!! We hated this restaurant to say the least!!!! WE were exhausted with the whole experience and so fed up and tired, we couldnt wait to get  out of there!! My husband said his salmon was ok, but a little too dry..and certainly not worth the 24.00 they charged tho!! The waitress was very nice, but too talkative for us, but we would rather have somebody personable than some ignorant unattentive one like we had at "Bucks", earlier in the week...she was the worst we ever had anywhere!! We would never go back to Firebirds,  quite frankly, it was THE WORST restaurant experience we ever had at any restaurant ever!!!! The food was AWFUL and waaaay overpriced for what you got!! We dont get how anybody can pay this much for this terrible food.. We still ended up having to pay 39.00 for the shrimp skewer and the NY strip..so nothing was really free for putting up with this awful experience!! We do not recommend this restaurant at allll!! A total rip off!! Steer clear!!! Overpriced garbage!!!

Stopped in after spending some time walking down Bourbon. My first time in NOLA so we had to stop at the place of the original Hurricane! Total for two hurricanes with Souvenir glasses came out to be around $25. Not too bad in my opinion. Worth it!

Lots of seating when you go further into the establishment. Wasn't too crowded.

This was one of the worst meals I've had in a long time!  The appetizers were okay though.  The main courses were horrible!! Service was very inadequate.  It took a long time to be served anything.  They gave us free desserts but really the whole meal
Should have been on them!  It's a shame bc it's a beautiful venue but just horrible!!

We'd been to this Italian place for a salad once and I wanted to try their Chicken Marsala. The salad had been good and I didn't have any suspicion that the other dishes might not be good, so it seemed pretty safe.

It was horrible.

The chicken was rubbery and had no flavor.

The marsala sauce clearly came from a can - probably right out of the can because it was very nearly cold.

The mashed potatoes were from a box.

The only thing on the plate really worth eating were the vegetables - those were fresh.

C. R. E. A. M.
(Chinese Rules Everything Around Me)
(Chinese food, that is)

Red's is that FIRE.
Straight up.
Unapologetically aggressive.
Unwaveringly unique. 
Unabashedly bold. 

Constantly impressed by the culinary technique and style.
Continuously blurring the lines between East, West and NOLA. 

So when you see that red square lit up on St Claude.
Know it's all over.
Tobias will have you seeing pink hearts, yellow moons, orange stars and green clovers.

These guys are always professional and spot on with their recommendations. I do a fair amount on my own and never get fictitious info from them. I dropped my car off before a business trip and returned their call late upon my return (10:30pm) expecting to leave a voicemail and they answered. Excellent, trustworthy service.
Oh how DD has fallen. Donuts and bagels are always stale especially at this location, Kugler Rd. Very unfortunate for such a well known franchise to now be so bad.

This really is a beautiful space, but the purpose is a winery. As much as I wanted to love it, I was completely underwhelmed. The wine is just not good. I can find a wine that I enjoy just about anywhere I go, but that was not the case here. I hope the wine gets better with age because I would love to continue visiting the lovely property.

Tacos are out of this world! Great local, hole in the wall spot in East Nash. Chicken taco was my favorite followed by the pork, fried tilapia and ground beef (spiciest). All of which were at least 8/10. Plantains were fantastic, Street corn was excellent too. Great vibe, great food. Will be back.

Came here with the family after browsing through all the positive reviews. This place is located right by Clearwater beach. You order inside and then take your food outside to a nicely shaded patio.

Most of us opted for the fish tacos. I thought they were great. The siricha sauce had the perfect amount of heat to it, and if you aren't a fan of siricha, they have about 4 other unique sauces to try at your table.

My wife had the cluck-it tacos (chicken) and they were equally as good.

Each taco plate came with some very addicting chips/salsa, I only wish they gave you another handful!

The service was fantastic. We let them know our daughter has a peanut allergy and the chef  personally walked out and assured us our food options were fine; it was a really nice gesture.

I would definitely recommend this place, especially for a quick bite to eat after spending a few hours on Clearwater beach.

Worst. McDonald's. Ever. 

It's so bad that I have to write about it.

The staff here is so unbelievably incompetent and rude.

I was here last week for a quick breakfast with my family. It was close to 10:30am, but the cashier insisted we still had time to order breakfast. Just as we were finishing our order, the menu changes from breakfast to lunch. She then rushed us to finish our order then tells us she may not even be able to do it because the menu changed. Thankfully, she did anyway.

The lines to pick up orders were extremely long and hardly moved. While waiting for our food, I was watching other people pick up their orders and any interactions between staff and customers. The worker whose job it was to call out order numbers and hand out food was the rudest of them all. I watched him call the order number of an older gentleman who said "I'm here" once or twice to pick up his food. The worker RUDELY yelled at him, "I heard you!" I also saw him be rude to this girl (maybe 12 years old or so) who did not speak a lot of English and was waiting to pick her food up. He started getting an attitude about not understanding her and didn't do anything to let her know her food was on its way or anything. He just walked away from her when she handed him her receipt.

Finally it's time for me to pick up my family's food. They completely jacked up the order. They gave us things we didn't even order and didn't give us enough drinks. My dad and brother ordered coffees and asked for a total of 3 sugars and 3 creamers. The rude employee gave us ZERO sugars, ZERO creamers, ZERO napkins, and ZERO straws. When I went up to ask for the sugars and creamers, the guy said, "I'm not allowed to give you more than 2 per order." Ummm... then why do they ask how many you want when you order then? Then, the guy took for-freaking-ever to give us napkins and straws. I'm sorry, but if I have 2 OJs and 5 people eating, we need more than 1 straw and 2 napkins. 

Oh yeah... they also messed up everyone else's orders. I imagine that if you're located on a busy street in a busy city, you should probably get it together and learn how to handle the morning rush. And it isn't exactly comforting to see 5 or 6 cops inside... not eating but posted in several locations of the restaurant. 

I'd give this place a -5 stars if I could.

Was there Sunday afternoon 11/8--Where do I start?? First, this has to be the only diner on the planet that does not offer full breakfast all day (only eggs). Also the only restaurant I've ever been to that does not take credit cards. But they do have an ATM machine in the lobby- so if you don't walk in the door with cash already in your pocket you effectively have to pay a surcharge to eat here. Is the owner that cheap that he or she cannot afford the small bank fee (which they can write off anyway)? Also the only diner I've ever been to that did not offer soup and/or salad and bread with a full dinner. Can you say CANNED green beans?? Lastly, the server seemed like she would rather be working elsewhere- barely spoke except to take our order.

we came here just for the banana foster! its quite a formal restaurant, but as visitors, it didnt keep us away from going there dressed in jeans and tank tops! i saw this on the food channel once and figured it was a nawlins thing that we had to experience. they actually prepare the banana foster right there in front of you, the flame, the bananas, the brown sugar, the rum.....Mmmmmm.  i believe we paid about $7 for it, which was totally worth it.

the staff could be a little snooty but i guess thats expected for a place like this.

Yikes! This place has gone down hill. First off, the menu has been reduced and all of my favorite things are gone. No more NJ burger with blue cheese and fried onions. No more buffalo burger. NO MORE FRIED PICKLES. Everything on the menu seems super similar now. I ordered the club with crispy chicken, Brussels sprouts, and my fiancé got the barbecue bacon burger. We waited about 40 minutes to get our food. The burgers seemed about half the size they used to be and honestly could not be more boring or less flavorful. The Brussels sprouts were not seasoned. We were so incredibly disappointed. I don't see us going back here in the future.
Love their coffee. Their whiskey coffee is awesome!!! Also they have CBD coffee and a wide selection of other unique coffees. Super nice, friendly service. I highly recommend!!!!
Don't be like me if you get your tires done and have a ton of loose change in your ashtray ‍ but someone might of needed it more than me ‍ great work and easy breezy experience!

Why do they make you pull up to the stop sign to get your food?? Seems like it would take longer for them to come outside and hand everything to everyone. And what if they forget something? I don't want to be rude and have them go all the way back inside for it, so I'm usually the one who wastes my time going back through the line, or just leaving without whatever was missed... 
It would make sense to have longer orders pull up so they can serve the customers who just have drinks/etc., but they literally have everyone pull up, and then they have their employees (sometimes younger girls) come outside, day or night, and walk over the curb with rocks all over... it's just not efficient in my opinion, and a workers comp claim waiting to happen... 
Also, a secret shopper needs to come and check out their chili. It's basically just lumpy red grease.

First things first: The French onion soup was superb! I love French onion soup, and therefore, have tasted a lot of different versions. I can't remember tasting anything better than this - does that mean it's the best I've ever had? Or does that just mean I have a faulty memory? Perhaps the latter...but nonetheless, it was freaking delicious! It was so rich and flavorful. The crouton, instead of disintegrating into the broth, stayed intact and delicious - the crouton definitely pushed this soup into the upper echelon of French Onion Soup-dom.

I've read reviews that the service is really poor - I did not experience this. Even with the busy weekday-lunch crowd, the service was not bad. Our water was refilled promptly, our food and check were both brought within a reasonable timeframe. 

I have nothing bad to say! I will definitely go back when there's room in my tummy for dessert!

I am torn between giving this place 2 or 3 stars. 

I've been meaning to visit this place for months as I keep hearing about it and we finally decided to stop by on a lazy Saturday morning.

Food: The donuts were pretty good with the exception of the regular cake donut I had. It was pretty dry like it had been sitting around for awhile. The red velvet donut though made up for it. It was exceptionally good! I also had a lemon donut that reminded me of a Hubig's pie. YUM!

Decor: Meh. Kinda bland and dingy on the inside though its in a cute little house.

Service: This is where they are severely lacking. Maybe it's just me and my expectations but I would think that employees at a donut shop would be a little more cheerful and happy to see you. Mostly I felt like they could have cared less that we were there. And they weren't busy. Literally had four customers in the 40 minute span that we were there. We got a curt "What can I get for you?" upon entry and were basically ignored while we perused the offerings to decide. I think the lady who rang us up may have said 10 words to us total. They weren't really rude more just a sense that they couldn't be bothered and didn't really care that we were there.

All of that being said I may give this place one more try just to see if it was a fluke or not.

I should have written this long ago and spared people some misery. The equipment is outdated and the xrays were painful. I don't know why they did them, since I had to tell the dentist where my fillings were. The worst was some technician named Victor who jabbed me so hard with the probe that my gums were bleeding extensively (plus it hurt). He said I was all '6's and needed a scale that I had done somewhere else the year before. Also, when I went to a real dentist a couple years later, I found out that I had a huge cavity that wasn't diagnosed. I had to get a crown.

Sadly, the one word that best describes my experience is mediocre. The hostess was lovely, the bartender was friendly, food was average, but the staff needs training in hospitality and they need a better understanding of the menu.

Absolutely the WORST experience purchasing a vehicle ever. They realized they mispriced a vehicle and refused to sell it to me. Kevin, the used car manager, sold the car out from under me even after I submitted my CC for a deposit to a sales associate.  Horrible communication and the worst customer service I've ever experienced. Do yourself a favor and avoid at all costs, no matter the deal you think you'll get. They are greedy and will play you hard. I have never felt this way about buying a vehicle, let alone a 15year old used truck!! Their only concern is their bottom line. Happy to have a conversation with anyone that thinks differently.

Original list price was $16,497. The web price was modified to $18,997, presumably due to interest. So what price did it actually sell for??  I have screen shots of the web prices. When I initially called the vehicle was available. When I left my card for a deposit, it was available. When I called Kevin and Reese, it was still available. They had multiple opportunities to make the right decision but chose to make a few more bucks instead. I'll gladly provide text conversations that refute the dealer's reply and their story.

My husband and I both love the food at Teddy's Burger Joint. We usually get a fried mushrooms appetizer with ranch dressing. They are delicious, fresh, and hot. The burgers are reminiscent of burgers off of the grill at home... which I love. I usually get the plain Teddy Burger with bacon and ketchup. My husband is a bit more adventurous, but I know he's enjoyed every sandwich that he's tried there. I will say that he wasn't a big fan of their tenderloin, but it's worth a try if you are into tenderloins. The fries are fries, but tasty enough too.

The decor is kinda industrial meets chain restaurant meets warehouse/garage. The place can be a bit too loud sometimes, depending on when you go...  and they do live music on certain nights, which kind of annoys me because it makes the loud restaurant even more loud. The last few times I've been here, I've noticed that there are a lot of unwieldy children/parents that frequent Teddy's. I realise it's a more casual/family environment, but I want to be able to enjoy my food without a kid smashing into my chair every 2 minutes.

The staff is friendly, though service can sometimes be a bit slow... especially when they get busy/backed up.  So far as I can tell, they make the food fresh (including pounding out your tenderloin when you order it), so I don't mind a bit of a wait for good food.

TL;DR Good food. Crappy dining experience. Get carry out.  :)

We came here for a wine tasting during our yelp road trip. It was pretty early probably like 10:30  in the morning so my husband and I were the only ones there.  The place is very spacious and decorated very nice. Our host was very friendly and knowledgeable about all the wines. We were given a sample of 8 different wines and she explained each wine in great detail as she poured it.

I liked that they divided their wines into different categories on the chart such as dry red, dry white, and sweet wine and included a description of each one. That way you have a better idea of what you're getting. I tried to pick at least a few different wines from my husband so that way we can maximize all the flavors, but there was a few that we both picked on both of our lists that looked too good to share.

I tend to like wines that are sweet, but there was one dry red wine called the red standard that had a Smokey black cherry flavor that we both really enjoyed. Every wine we tasted was very good and we ended up buying 4 bottles to take home. I got 2 bottles of Niagra which is a sweet white wine, one bottle of the red which was a sweet red grape tasting wine, and we got a bottle of the red standard. We received the in store offer for 10% off 3 bottles or more, but I didn't realize we had a yelp offer for 15% off until after we left, oh well. 

We enjoyed our time here and would like to get to the actual Chaddsford location one day maybe. I have friends that have been there and recommended it to us.
