When in New Orleans, go to Erin Rose to get a frozen Irish coffee. It doesn't matter the time of day, they'll have it and you'll be in good company because there will be other people that made the pilgrimage to get it who are there also. Splurge for the souvenir cup because it's bigger (and the drink is delicious) and it just so happens that their CC minimum ($10) is just $1 more than that of two smalls ($9), so buying the souvenir cup portion will save you from buying an extra drink you don't need...and your stomach prob can't handle more than one frozen Irish coffee.

As a bar outside of the frozen Irish coffee, it's a'ight.

Here's my feedback regarding the recent 2 night stay at this resort.

1) I had called your hotel on 4/19/2020 and spoke with someone (did not get the person's name, but it was male) about whether your pool was going to be open, he specifically said that the pool at the hotel is open. This is mis-information that was given to customers, as upon arrival, I was informed that the pool had been closed for 1.5 months. I chose your hotel because I thought the pool would be open, but in fact, it was closed. What a disappointment.
2) Upon check in on 4/28/2020 morning, Michael assisted us with finding an ocean view room. When we arrived at room 30X, the ocean view is really NOT an ocean view because right outside our balcony - is the largest tree ever that blocks our entire view of the ocean. Please also note that when we arrived to our room, the floor was covered with at least 5-6 pieces of dog kibble. Makes me feel like the room was unkept and unclean. Is this how a 4-star hotel should be?
3) I had to contact the front desk and spoke with Anna to request a room change. This was not an easy request to satisfy either, because she gave me some options such as moving over the to Eucalyptus building 2nd floor, and would not provide a specific room number. I had to personally walk over to the 2nd floor of that building, and look out at the view and realized that there were again multiple trees blocking the ocean. I had to go meet her at the front desk to request for another 3rd floor room near room 30X, and she gave me some excuses such as your hotel doesn't allow people to move rooms during their stay, and that she can't find any other rooms available on the 3rd floor, etc. Honestly, it's just a bunch of excuses and you guys are making it MORE DIFFICULT THAN IT SHOULD BE. Customers are here to enjoy, relax and spend time in a soothing environment, but all I get is a room with a partial ocean view, and NOT what I had paid for. I informed Anna to please note in my reservation that for the next morning, I would like the front desk to find me a room near 30X that doesn't have an obstructed view. I was hoping that I could at least have 1 day where my view was truly unobstructed.
One hour later, Anna calls me back and offers Room 30Y, which she said is open and available now. I had to go downstairs and get the keys from her so that I can FINALLY move into a true ocean view room around 4 pm. What makes this situation so odd is that Michael told me that you guys are at 20% capacity, so how can there be no rooms for me to move into with a proper ocean view room?!
4) On 4/29/2020 morning, I am told by the front desk lady (and also Michael said the same thing at check-in) that I am able to redeem my free wine for a juice / milk at the Java corner. The Hispanic lady at the java corner (did not catch her name) refused to give me a simple milk carton, because she said she can only do "coffee". Then she proceeded to say that I did not have a mask on and cannot serve me. I told her that I only needed her to point out where the milk was located, and that I am NOT needing any "serving" by her. She was quite curt, rude, and just very poor service. I finally retrieved the milk carton on my own, and placed the wine card at the counter. This leaves a distaste in my mouth after interacting with her. Why does every interaction with your staff have to be so difficult?
5) I am currently a Gold member with Hilton Honors, but because I used Capital One points to book this reservation, I am not awarded any Hilton Honors points, and I don't get credit for staying here for 2 nights, which don't count towards my Gold status nights accrued. This is rather disappointing. I also offered to NOT have my room serviced during my 2 nights, so that I can earn 1,000 Hilton Honors points for being "eco-friendly" but was told by Anna that I am not eligible. Again, a disappointment. I feel no sense of appreciation from Hilton, even though I have a Gold member status. What a joke.

I really wanted to enjoy this hotel and gave it a chance, even though your reviews on Yelp are so low. But now I feel like I understand why the reviews are only 2.5 stars overall on Yelp. 

Some things I did enjoy about your hotel are:
- 2 hour bike rental, but this was basically covered by my resort fee
- the fact that your location is across from the beach with easy access, but your hotel does not offer any beach umbrellas
- there are charging ports for electric vehicles, which is essentially paid for by parking fees

Very disappointed with Noah's. We ordered a Santa Fe salad which is supposed to come with corn, salsa, cheddar cheese, tomatoes, and tortilla chips. We received a small sandwich size container with just ice burg lettuce and grilled chicken. None of the other toppings! The cheese was a congealed nacho cheese sauce on the side. Worse than Taco Bell cheese whizz. And the "salsa" was marinara sauce! Laughable! Also, we ordered chicken fingers which melted through the styrofoam container and had a nice side of soggy fries. And, when we went to pick up the food the credit card machine was down. Unprofessional and irresponsible food establishment. Bummer because we live nearby and were excited about this new place close by. Won't be ordering from Noah's again.

From the early days to today, United Cycle has the most comprehensive selection of, yes bikes, and sports gear and wear in the city.

I remember the first time I went into United Cycle, ahem, many moons ago, and was very impressed, if not overwhelmed, with the selection available. You name it, they got it: hockey, soccer, skating, curling, racket sports, snowboarding, volleyball, rugby, ringette, etc. But let us not forget bikes as that's where they started.

You'll get high end, high quality equipment along with experienced and knowledgeable sales people who are obviously involved with sports to assist you. With this you'll get the high end price. However, you will get what you pay for.

Part of the experience is the atmosphere of the building knowing the original part of the store was old building material from a local church. United Cycle is also a good corporate citizen as it gives back to the community in various ways - like promoting healthful living and sponsoring kids sports. Makes me feel better about buying gear here.

Charming little place with a French theme. Had a pastry and coffee that was good quality. Not so warm atmosphere with employees working there - the girl at the register seemed like she could care less and just took the order like it was a cattle call "we're out of this, we're out of that, here you go..."
Generally my neighborhood sushi spot, however, this time was a completely different experience. Earwig in my sake and hair in my salad - needless to say I didn't wait for my sushi.

I've had one of the most disappointing meals ever here. Everything was way overcooked, and dry. I ordered a medium steak and got completely well done. My husband had ordered the special of the day- and the pork was as dry as jerky. Its pretty bad when the 'special' of the day is totally messed up. With that said, the server was amazing!

We went during restaurant week... Not sure if that means it could get better or worse..?
 If had better at NJ diners to be honest. But in fairness it was better than some of the reviews i read here....stuffed mushrooms were rubbery, mussels ok, pasta ok, red sauce ok, shrimp seemed fresh....
..the decor inside much different from what i imagined from the outside and their website...very outdated 1980ish...
.the help was friendly but don't expect anymore than adaquate...waitress obviously had someone uncork our wine bottle in the kitchen (at least I hope...ahem) and yelled "i said caaaaaaannoli" so loud I almost dropped my teeensy wine glass. meh...typical so. philly fare!

This is a contemporary burger spot. Everything is fresh and high quality, but don't expect your standard hole in the wall mom and pop burger and fries (which are my favorite) here. 

Had the Kobe burger with Truffle fries and a soda. I really enjoyed the meal. 4 Stars because the bill before tip was close to $30 bucks. Seems a little high to me.

I love Korean BBQ.  At glance walking up to the restaurant it looks really nice and clean. Just need to pickup some of the things that were out front. Once inside.. I looked the very inviting feel it gave. The customer service was awesome!  The servers were great and made sure to answer all questions and made suggestions.  I had the Sushi and it came out in a timely manner and was Delicious!!!!! We had BBQ for two and it was just enough and filling.  It was also delicious.   The price was good for the amount of food we ordered. I recommend this place and would go again.

We ordered a tire to put on the rim and they did call us when the tire came in. Then we dropped off the bad tire  so they could put the rim on the good one. They told us to come back in an hour and an hour turned into 2+. I would suggest just staying at the place if you have the time or else you will be skipped. Also if they can call you when yoir oder comes in then they can call you when they get backed up on work so customers don't have to change their schedule around them. It looks like the store has a large selection and they speak English and spanish.

Nothing exceptional here and nothing awful. However, I did have one great experience here once after coming for almost 5 years. Other times it's just normal, a great server can make all the difference
Went there for dinner and it was terrible.  Food arrived cold and low quality.  The food is just trash, don't go here.
Be careful with this lender and Jason Hunter.   They promise big lender credits which disappear at closing.

Worse ever!  Don't go here.  It's a waste of money and TIME!!!  Unfortunately, Noodle City was closed so I had no choice but to go there.  The pho is horrible!  The beef brisket is way hard and the broth is lukewarm.  Sure, they were generous with the broth but what's pho if the broth is not piping hot??  Icing on the cake?  Almost halfway through eating, found a piece of clear plastic in my soup!  Informed the waitress and she was nice enough to replace it.  That should've been on the house!  Got the lunch special which came with dessert.  Waited 20 mins additional just for two freakin pieces of fried banana!  Overall, just not worth it!!!!  It's a miracle that place is still in business.  BTW, if i could give it 0 stars, I would.

Dunkin' Donuts... well I've been to this DD a few times before mainly in the early morning and never really a problem. But I went a couple of times in the afternoon/early evening and it's totally unacceptable. I should have gotten the hint by the empty parking spaces directly in front of the store. But Today I decided to stop in because it's a little chilly out and wanted a simple Dark Roast coffee and 2 ice coffees with a egg croissant. I get the ice coffees pretty quick and after about 6 mins or so i get the sandwich (in which he had to make himself) so I asked about the Dark Roast and he says "yeah I'm heating it up"?!?!.. I asked him what do you mean heating it up? He says to me he has it in a carafe on top of the brewer literally heating up the coffee on the burner. Who knows how long it been sitting there. He then tells me I can make a fresh pot but it's going to take a bit... REALLY?!?.. I honestly believe it's THE MANAGEMENTS FAULT because they only have 2 employees working so the one who takes the order has to prepare the sandwiches get coffees etc etc. The same goes for the one operating the drive-thru. Needless to say I just walked out with a reg coffee that was way too light which went straight in the garbage. It's a shame because it's a fairly new DD in a prime location. But there are many other DD on 301 so it's time to move on and give them a try.
WAKE UP MANAGEMENT!!

Being new to this restaurant I was excited to try the ever famous steaks that it was known for. 
While the steak (tenderloin) that I had was scrumptious, I could hardly eat it due to the saltiness of the outside part of it, I had to ask for some lemon to neutralize the taste! My husband had the NY cut and my brother in law had the rib eye cut, and they had the same problem.
The sides were very good though, and the corn bread was awesome!!
Looking forward for an improved taste in the future!

We ordered online for an office lunch meeting. The order was accurate and packaged well for delivery. They had added extra sauces which was nice. Excellent service and we will order our office lunches from them again.
Waited over an hour. Got wrong food. No manager on premises. Remade order. Wrong food again. No resolution tired of waiting left with wrong food. Wtf dunkin?

$5 dollars for a bottle of domestic beer!! $5!!!!! This is Nashville and you should be ashamed of yourself Lime!  I ordered a Miller Light because the mojito you made for me was a nasty color, tasted like sweet tarts, and conatined no rum only to find out that you would try and rip me off in the "cheap" beer department also. The bar constantly smells like bleach because the glass washing thing is right in your face, and the girl bartender can't make a drink. I've been back 3 times to give it a fair shot and every time she effs it up and the guy bartender has to remake it for me. He normally just shakes his head at her and looks at me sheepishly.

I've never had the food, but it looks overpriced . The design is cool, except that there is not a lot of dancing space and this girl salsa'd her stilettos right into the top of my foot which was bruised for weeks.

I like new, fusiony, trendy places, but they have to be able to deliver. Lime does not deliver for me; not in the prices, or the drinks, or the layout of the space. I will give it an extra star for being open and airy and full of pretty people, but the whole thing just screams rip-off to me. Sorry Lime.

Yesterday was my 1st visit to Chung Dam, I've been to Kingspa in Jersey before and both places are similar and different in different ways but that's a review for a another time.  The facility is very clean, the staff is helpful, but the place is small, not to a fault small but I do wish the actual men's section was bigger.  I'm no pro to Korean spa life but I'm no amateur either...that being said lets get down to what your probably here on yelp to find out

1. Location is convenient, way closer option than driving to jersey...30 mins compared to 2 hrs!

2. Price is right! $20 for a day pass that includes all access to spa areas, saunas, and the fitness area...also if you decide to get any massages that entry fee is added to the total cost of what ever massage you get...eg. body scrub is $50, but you only actually pay $30

3. Yes folks...nudity is a must, so if your self conscience or uneasy about it then this isn't for you!  I will say this though, its not as bad as you would think...if your willing to give it a chance go on a day you think isn't going to be crowded, I went on a Thursday evening after work got there like 6pm left like 9:30 and in total coming and going my whole time there I counted 8-10 ppl within that time frame, most all at one time was 6 including myself but were all spaced out throughout the area.  Showers are open air but you do have privacy glass on both sides and 3 behind you and they do have the traditional sit down area also.  Sucky part about that is the shower attachments don't work so if you use it you'll have to scrub there and still use the standing showers to rinse.  

4. Soap, shower gels, 1 large and 1 small towel are provided but I suggest you bring your own everything..I used their stuff then used my own, I brought 3 towels and used 2, they sell scrubbers and water and snacks and all that good stuff also but I'll be better prepared next time...this was just the initial visit

5. Staff was helpful, its a guy back there to show you around and give you the lay of the land, tells you about showering before you enter the pools and what areas you need to get dressed for so that was cool...his English was good enough for you to get the point

6. 3 pools...cold, warm, and warmer (lol) because it wasn't hot hot to me, because in jersey they have a pool so hot its playfully called the lobster pot! (when the Koreans and others of lighter skin complexion get out the lower half of them is red!) its also a steam room and a dry sauna, both have nice herbal woodsy smells.  The body scrub area is located in the back and is pretty private so that's a major plus, the one in Jersey is open air...they also have a shower that sprays water at you from all sides...be careful, the middle dial is temperature and the top dial turns it on!

All in all I had a good 1st experience, its small but not claustrophobic...I would say its adequate for the price point you pay.  Its not super modern but the showers were cool, locker room was very upscale to me, bathrooms were clean, facility was clean, staff was nice, uniforms were ok, variety of things to do was limited but again for $20 why complain! Once you get over the nudity your fine, just like any gym or anyplace like that you may get your trolls/ gawkers but I didn't really feel much of that kind of vibe.  The Koreans will pay you no attention, its the Americans that usually make these experiences awkward.  I had maybe 1 or 2 guys that kinda gave me the "I'm checking you out vibe" but nobody engaged me, nobody came trying to "talk" to me or be in my face, it was a respectful distance if that was the case.  I did think one guy was trying to flag me down but over time I just think he was very expressive with his hands because every time I thought I saw it, he was talking to somebody else....but I wasn't creeped out like its perv city or anything,I've been more uncomfortable at beach than at this place,  if you get the vibe just be aware and cover up and that's for anyplace!  I'd visit again, and soon...my skin is soft, I smelled good, I relaxed and I slept so friggin good that night!!!

I ate dinner at around 6pm on a Wednesday evening at Stir Crazy. My first thought was wow, I don't think I've ever been here when there were so few people. This place used to be jammed. I guess things change and time moves on. I read the menu and nothing was overly appealing so I went with the choose your own stir fry. I got shrimp, brown rice, and chose my own vegetables from the "market bar." There was no line so my bowl went straight to the wok guy.  It was good but not amazing. While I ate it occurred to me why they weren't mobbed anymore. When this first opened it was unique to choose your own vegetables but now it's everywhere and not that exciting from a culinary standpoint. Also if you spend half your time choosing your vegetables and standing in front of the wok having your food cooked then you have missed half the time at the table with your dinner companions who ordered off the menu. It's more of a workaurant concept than a restaurant. I'm sure I'll be back but I'm not going out of my way.

Having been to the original Lonnie's downtown a few times, I was interested to see what the Lonnie's oddly situated on West End had to offer. With its debaucherous Karaoke ambiance it actually ended up being a great time. Though I have always liked Lonnie's downtown, I have not once attempted to sing there. If you have ever been to the downtown Lonnie's you will understand why. Karaoke usually reserved for the tone deaf or inebriated, at the Lonnie's downtown is a showcase for local talent trying to make it big. Though it's impressive it's not really encouraging to those that are not so musically inclined.

Lonnie's on West End, however, not only encouraged my presence on the stage but also demanded it. With a whopping rendition of Sir Mix a Lots "I like big butts", I felt that I definitely did Lonnie's justice.  It's not the kind of place that I would wander into by myself (not usually a huge crowd), but accompanied by my own motley we managed to take over for the night.

man, i REALLY want to give these guys more stars, what with their recent renovations and their friendly owner, but i just can't. :(

this place is just up the block from me but we've only been going once or twice a year for the past 5 years. it's excellent for the "american-chinese" palette, and it always seems to have great wok-hei when i walk by, but suffers from same-brown-sauce-itis...and no chow fun! at least the lo mein isn't half bad.

on a recent visit we went for the live music on a sunday evening. if the atmosphere hadn't been so nice (the live music was very enjoyable), this place would get one less star from me. we ordered a well vodka soda water and a grey goose martini and the bill was a smidge over $16 (WTF?!?). it took ten minutes for someone to wait on us after we were seated, and the drinks took an additional 20 minutes to come out. (i listened to 4 songs and changed my baby's diaper in that time). when they came out the martini was warm and served in a gimlet glass. my vodka soda water was in a small rocks glass and consisted mostly of ice. honestly, i left feeling a little violated.

in the past we have walked here with neighbors and gotten the meal-deals: one dish with side of rice, soup and i can't remember if there's an eggroll or not. i guess it's reasonably priced. also, i've had their take out which took a little longer than i wanted to wait for. the recent renovations are excellent--nice patio, great street appeal with a tidy little pond, but i can't recommend the food if you're looking for authenticity. oh, and i can't recommend the service, either. lastly, if you are non-asian, you should feel at home with the clientele.

I have stopped  in here a few times & it just seems that I can never find exactly  what  I  am looking for. It is a fairly nice store and is full of products. I don't know if maybe they cater to more of the body builders, than the nutrition novices.
I always have a list of a few items I want to try & they never have them. Granted what I may be looking for in any given visit, may be somewhat obscure, like cats claw, but it does exit.
It is a nice store to look around in. The staff is very knowledgeable,  friendly & helpful. I personally, just have never found anything that I just had to purchase at this store, that's  just me.
What's a girl to do? Keep going to obscure places, looking for obscure  items & you are sure to find many hidden gems.

We went to Karaoke. When we entered the restaurant, waiter/waitress just looked at us. Took about 3min to get hosted to Karaoke. We had fun. However, when we were about to pay for the Karaoke, they charged two times more than we did. They didn't keep the time. They added one more hours to our bill. Gladly we didn't pay for what they billed, but be careful if you want to go for Karaoke.

I love this place and eat here once a week. 

For the first time, I have a terrible pick up experience . Pics attached , terrible experience

Just had a pretty bad ham and cheese hoagie. Took quite a while to make this underwhelming sandwich. Very little meat, tons of shredded lettuce = very little flavor. Such an easy menu item. They need to order a few hoagies from other local spots and take some notes. Have not had the pizza, but I'm not sure I'll make it back to try.

I use to love this place and would frequently eat here. Something about the food not tasting as good has changed that. The last time I was there, I ordered fish tacos. When I received my order, the fish did not seem done. I sent it back and they returned two more tacos on the same plate to me. It seemed kinda tacky, I wrote an email and they sent me a free meal. The food isn't terrible and I still really like the BLTA. The quinoa salad is very good, also.

Went with my ladies for some dinner and drinks! Inside is simple but gorgeous... love the windows! Our server was on pointe. He was very knowledgeable and made everything sound like I had to try. Wish I would have remembered his name! ‍ I order the Rosè Sangria... 2 actually! We had the Shrimp with some spicy-ish mustard for an appetizer. I had the New York strip and shared the potatoes and Brussels sprouts, all were equally delicious. I will say it was all worth it but be prepared to spend some cheese!
Good parking. Fairly clean restrooms.  Lots of fuel pumps. But to overpriced for the food and products. Fairly easy on and off the interstate when there is no construction.

I like Chase bank.
Clean and clear to the point.
No easy way to earn interest.
I do enjoy their credit cards. Their online banking is excellent.

I literally stood in line for 10 plus minutes waiting to fax  some papers. There were 2 other associates standing around talking the entire time doing absolutely nothing. The woman helping the customers in front of me were talking and talking and talking about personal things while myself and 2 other people were standing in line. The other guy talking walks up and joins there conversation asking about the customer's last name because it sounded familiar with someone in his family. That went on and on. The fact that I had enough time to take a picture of the customer experience email address and phone number is completely bad service. I'm pregnant and had to use the restroom as well. Your associates need to learn how to work while at work and multi task. Never once was myself or anyone else greeted or acknowledged for standing in line 10 mins while your associates held on their personal conversations. Complete horrible experience. The one associate that was actually working and not running his mouth was Rasheed B. 

Store: 2308 West End Ave Nashville, Tn 37203 
         06/01/2019 @ approximately 11:21 am

I love the neighborhood feel of this place. I love the way they take care of you when you eat in, adds a bit of charm.

Although I think their prices are great, they need to update the menu shown here.

I wish I could give this place a negative star. So I dropped my brand new phone one day after buying it cracked screen and everything. He told me he had to order the part and will need a $30 deposit. Fine. I paid the deposit and bought screen protectors and a case off of Amazon ($20 in total and non-refundable) to make sure it doesn't happen again. My part came in and I went to get my phone fixed. He fixed the screen but my phone did not work! I'm trying to get out the store I've been there for 2 hours and know I'm at least an hour late for work. Oh yeah and I like rap music and all but someone can walk in with children and hear music blasted with Btch this and nga that, it's unprofessional as ever!! He proceeds to give me a used busted up phone saying I don't want to leave you with nothing and unable to communicate, telling me when it's fixed he'll email me. Problem is it's been almost a week and I haven't heard anything. I had to reach out to him. Avoid avoid avoid

You really have to be terrible for me to give a restaurant one star, because as a Chef I know anybody can have a bad meal due to various situations (hence two stars) but the food here was just not good. 

Now I know this place has been a local favorite for years (and the place really looks it) but retro decor aside I just don't see the attraction. 

My Manhattan clam chowder looked like it literally took the trip down the turnpike to our table, tasteless and "full" of potatoes. The steamed clams were gritty and dry. 
My entree left much to be desired as my fries were way over cooked and my coleslaw was warm. At least the butterfly shrimp were good. 
My guest had flounder which served up a decent portion but otherwise Ehh!
Service was fair, but if you ask me they would do well to give the place a real face lift both in and out. 
I knew there had to be a reason I held on to this gift certificate so long. Sorry but never again.

Ahhhhh.... Hemingway's..... we love this spot inside of Armature Works. One of our favorite places. The food is always so good. Sometimes they're sold out of stuff but that's only because the food is so delish that everyone wants it!! The crew is always friendly and greets you with a smile. We've met the manager a few times and he always has great convo with us. Definitely a staple for us !

3.5 stars overall. 

This place kinda makes me think of McAllister's and Panera, but a bit cheaper. Sandwiches, salads, Mac and cheese, and pizza options + tea!

The best part of it is the small bar (Newk's Roundtable) on the side with extra toppings for your meal - oregono, pickles, garlic, cheese, oil & vinegar, and more. When I went here in Dec or so, the pickles were delicious. Now they are the bread and butter pickles (disgusting) so be warned! 

My pesto & chicken sandwich was good - and better when I visited the Newk's Roundtable and added lots of oregono, cheese, oil & vinegar. The 5-cheese Mac and cheese was good too. 

It was all "good" but not great. And the portion sizes were SO small. I got the 1/2 pesto sandwich and 1/2 Mac and cheese for $9.29 and it was tiiiiny. So I don't feel good about my bank account after eating here - it seemed like not a great use of money for quantity and quality. 

Also, they do have cake, and that is pretty good. So, overall I don't hate this place but won't come often.

Sadly, the prices have eclipsed the quality of the food. It's been a few months since we last visited, and this was always a good local option. On our visit today, I was surprised at the prices. Additionally, the servings are smaller. I'm afraid it just doesn't make sense, economically or gastronomically, anymore for me.
A very special place. Some of the most beautiful and delicious pastries in Edmonton.

Overall, tonight was a great experience and I'll be back again. Beer was fantastic, great selection. Food was good, but restaurant was busy and unfortunately food came a little cold and burger was well done (even though medium well requested). Still, we usually don't complain and the food was still tasty. Husband had the mac and cheese burger and I had the burger on sourdough bread. Great menu, awesome beer, and a fun atmosphere. Will be back.

We got a delivery today for lunch and got the lunch specials it was nothing special I never had General Tso's chicken with walnuts on it. The pad thai noodles were bland. The specials came with wonton soup and there were none of the Chow Mein noodles with it nor duck sauce or soy sauce. Was disappointed with the food and will probably not order from them again and they especially forgot the fortune cookies.

This was my second time here. My first experience was awesome. This time not so much. The positives are everyone is very friendly to the front desk and check out girl to the woman who takes your blood pressure and weight to the xray tech. Very professional and nice. I was taken back fairly quick as well.

This time I saw a PA for shoulder pain. I felt like she wasn't really listening to what I was saying. She typed her notes in and said and you should go to physical therapy. She told me she would be right back in. She came back and said here is the number to physical therapy call and make sure your insurance covers it and gave me a muscle relaxer. She didn't even tell me a diagnosis. I had to then ask questions about what my diagnosis was which was very vague. I felt like my visit was a waste of time and a waste of a 50$ copay. I was very disappointed. I feel like I still have no answers.

They changed their wings!  I have been a loyal patron (once a week) since I moved here two years ago but recently they changed the size of their wings to normal size. I have to say the beer is great, but this is the reason my family loves this restaurant  the most and go back every time. Very upset. I have challenged others from out of town and when they visit they can attest--these are the best wings ever. Not anymore. I would pay more if these large wings aren't profitable. But the fact that they sold out makes me feel like they are not in touch with their loyal patrons.  Used to be five stars and now I would say three. Sorry.

Pizza ordered at 6:24pm on Tues, 3/17.  Expected delivery time at 6:54.  pizza arrived at 7:23pm, lukewarm at best.  Crust had cold, uncooked spots.  Delivery driver could care less if I left ANY tip.  We order pizza once a week usually. If you are loking at pizza, look at Jet's at the corner of Concord and Nolensville, or Pie in the Sky in Lenox village. The old saying of "ya get what ya pay for" seems to ring true.  Just for reference on pizza delivery time, we live exactly 3.5mi from store. 59 minutes for a lukewarm pizza...I think I could have made one in that time frame.

Went here with my extended family. They accommodated the large party easily. We started with a few appetizers, of which the chaats were the best, though everything else was tasty as well. The entrees were even better. The malai kofta is one of the best I've ever had. Makes sense it is chef recommended. I'll be back for sure.

Due to its smaller parking lot, it's a lot harder to find a parking spot here after 8:00 AM if you are a commuter who needs to get into Philly before 9:00 AM.  If you get turned away, your next bet is to run over to Ferry Station. Much bigger lot. 

During rush hour in the morning, trains tend to stop every 8 minutes or so.

No bathrooms, similar to other PATCO stations.

A bit closer to Philadelphia, so a quick 12 minute hop
They screw up my order every single time. Never coming here again I gave them too many chances...

What can I say about this Broadripple Staple?  Dingy, Dark, Loud and full of 20-somethings doing shots.  What else do you expect in Broadripple.

No specials really, and food is mediocre as you'd expect in a college type bar.  For a place named Old Pro's Tables, you'd think more pool tables would be available, but there aren't.  Good luck ever getting a table here. :(

Of all the options in Broadripple to grab a drink, this does not rank high up on my list.  I feel that most people come here out of the nostalgia from when they were younger coming to Broadripple as well as the younger crowd trying the place out to do a shot and then moving on.

Just wanted to add I called and they were amazing with customer service, what probably happened was the sever probably swiped my card on the old ticket first on accident but then cancelled it so it showed up in the pending charges. It never did stick, but such genuine concern for trying to get it solved was so appreciated. Raising a star for great management and customer service.
Food is good, but staff is crummy. Really can't beat their steak sandwich lunch special.
this pizzeria is very good.  try the cheese steak pizza ...it is delicious.  have tried a few other special pizzas and were all good.  best pizza in Riverview .

F this place. I wanted to make a reservation  for 10 and the person was so rude. I asked for a table in the back room and he said no then I  had to ask if it was closed for a private party and after he said yes he said "We will have to put you where we put you." WTF is that? Asshole.
Victor was a great help. Very courteous as he tested and replaced my dead battery for free.

This coffee place is nice. Plenty of sitting places. It's also in a nice location by the river. My father and I ordered a latte and a chai latte. My chai was delicious and served in a cute mason jar. We ended up getting our drinks and walking along the river. Customer service was great as well.

This is in the shopping plaza by Kroger. Ask for the foot massage for an hour and they do your whole body.
The rooms are in the back and very dark. The music is very calming and the mood is very relaxing. 
They come and lay you down on a table and place your feet in hot water. They will take care of your head, ears, face, neck, shoulders, arms, legs, back, feet, and massage them all 
They use some sort of oil that is very mild. I have very sensitive skin and am allergic to many products but this was fine for my skin. You start on your back for half the massage and then they dry your feet and after they finish your foot massage, flip you in your front to do your back side.
Anytime I need a relaxing massage to wind down I come here. At the end of the massage I feel great and so relaxed. I almost don't want the massage to end. I've come very close to falling asleep because it was so relaxing. Make sure you wear comfortable clothing, like shorts and a loose t-shirt. I definitely recommend coming here to get a massage. I'm

Prices are reasonable for food, only complaint is the rice and clam chowder were WAYYYYYY to salty.  Salmon and grouper bites were very fresh, bartender/staff in bar area are very friendly.  The girl taking names was a bit unwelcoming did not look like she enjoyed her job.

Oops, did we close the bar ?

Wanting a night out with friends, this was actually my backup. It turned out to be a fabulous spot. 

This fairly new wine bar is located to the right of Simon's in a strip mall. Plenty of parking. A large patio that I can't wait to sip on next summer. Several seating choices inside. Plus..........a large selection of reds on display and a huge walk in fridge for whites and bubbles. Not a wine drinker?  Why not ? Are you crazy?  
If not, there are two large coolers with canned and bottled beer selections.  The snack menu offers a nice nibble selection. Pâté, cheese board, charcuterie, salmon and some dipped to enjoy with your wine. 

I didn't notice any background noice. Once we were sipping and talking, it felt like we were the only ones there. But all the tables were full. 

I thought the red choices were pretty good and varied. I was surprised to see a couple from the Languedoc area of France, a Greek wine and many Italians. Also present were several familiar Californians. The prices were great !!  Check all around the bar before making a decision. There were a couple of mix and match sales that came to $10 a bottle when purchasing just 3 bottles. 

 Excited to see a wine bar back in this neighborhood location. Will be be very soon.

What a wonderful way to start a beautiful day in Santa Barbra , my family and I stopped in to our favorite lil bakery cafe on state street . Always excited about their exquisite menu , after trying to figure out what to eat we enjoyed the social climate and beautiful  ambience. The staff was super helpful and nice perfectly sweet to my dog & welcomed an  LGBTrans family Perfect . I will always come back when visiting family .

The burgers = excellent
The coney dogs = excellent
The wait = not ideal, but not surprising
The staff comprised of people all under the age of 19 = not so excellent (they're slow and often overwhelmed)
The abundance of screaming children = opposite of excellent

Still, the proximity of Bub's to my house, the prices and the great food make it worth the wait and the irritation.

ETA: The last two times we've eaten here, my husband and I have both had "stomach monkeys" to contend with afterwards. I guess going here again will mean having Immodium on hand for the aftershocks.

I hate leaving bad reviews, but for $65.00, we are disappointed with the meal. We ordered takeout last night... I had the prime rib... the meat was tender but it just lacked flavor. My husband ordered the NY strip well done and it was medium.  The blooming onion was soggy (which we kind of expected because it was packed in plastic so the steam was trapped inside),  and the baked potatoes were a bit under cooked (almost like al dente if it were pasta). On the positive, we placed our order at 2:30 for a 4:45 pick up and it was ready by 4:40 and the customer service was great!
These guys rock! They did the job the very next day at a great price and went above and beyond by cleaning up our grounds without my asking. We were beyond impressed and highly recommend them.

I headed over here for lunch yesterday pretty jazzed about the idea of having a gourmet burger to go. The place was bustling, but I was able to order my meal in about 5 minutes. While waiting for number to be called, one of the staff members came up to ask how I was doing, what I ordered, etc. He was very friendly and I liked that they were willing to go the extra step to check on their customers. 
I ordered a medium burger with provolone cheese and mushrooms with a side of truffle fries. I could smell the fries through the bag as I walked back to my office and couldn't wait to dig in. 
Honestly, I wasn't all that impressed. The fries were pretty tasty but didn't have the extra pizazz I was looking for. The burger was surprisingly tasteless; some more seasoning was definitely necessary. I was also kind of disappointed by the mushrooms. Again, not seasoned well and also, very few on my burger! 
I can see this being a great option after a night out (it's open until 3 am) but I don't think this is a place I will be craving in the future.
Great no frills Chinese food with a family atmosphere. In my opinion their egg rolls are the best around! Great lunch buffet m-f 11-2, as well as a full menu. Staff is friendly and attentive.

My friend recommended Perfectly Bare Laser to me because she had such a great experience here. I have been twice now and I really love it! I love that it's pay per session rather than buying an overpriced package deal like some other laser hair removal businesses. I was a bit nervous about doing laser hair removal because I expected it to be very painful, but it's not at all! To me it feels like if you put pop rock candy on the areas that they're doing. Lol. The staff is friendly and professional and they make you feel very comfortable. The location is a bit tucked away in what looks like a pretty barren office building but once you get to their suite it is decorated beautifully and is very clean. On their website, they have a blog that answers FAQS and explains the services that they offer. They do charge an extra fee if you pay with a credit card so make sure to either bring cash or make sure that you pay with either Venmo, cashapp, or zelle. 10/10 so far, I enjoy going here!

Although the food was good (and deserving of more stars), the service was terrible. 

Almost immediately after we received our menus, the server asked if we were ready to order. We asked for a couple of minutes and didn't see him again for at least 10 minutes. During this time, he cleared tables and ate lunch, but didn't notice us waiting until his boss pointed us out. 

The food came out very quickly, but the server managed to tilt one of the plates, allowing a portion of the food to slip off onto the table. He apologized but didn't offer to get a new dish or even clean the table off - we were just left with some noodles on the table. He also forgot to bring out water until reminded. 

We had to ask for the check and apparently the credit card machine is down now and we've been waiting in uncertainty for the past 20 minutes.

The set up of the restaurant isn't great. The tables are so close together that when trying to navigate to your table you're left with the age-old question: "As I pass, do I give you ass or crotch?" 

Maybe this place is best for take-out?

This restaurant is offering both sushi and Thai cuisine. The staff is very friendly and attentive. The quality of sushi is pretty good, and is are the Thai dishes. The bartender, who is also owner, is always willing to have a conversation with you.
Tasty little tacos and great real deal beans and rice if you make it a combo.  Bargain prices.  The green and red salsas they have are nice and hot.  Just a couple tables inside.

I'm a fan of the soup but unfortunately they lack in consistency. My biggest reason for the 1 start review is due to the employees not wearing masks. Some were wearing their masks, while others only allowing it to hang from one ear leaving their noses & mouths completely exposed. I try really hard to support small businesses especially with the pandemic impacting them greatly. I will not continue to give them my money until they can show the same support & care that their customers show them.
I went here with a client and her mom. The food was ok, the restaurant was clean and our waitress was nice.

Great country store , no matter who you are ,  if you got money or hardly make it week by week or from the city or country  , everyone is welcomed ... joe is hard working son of gun and always carry a conversation with you . I've been going there for years for lottery at first ...then it moved to lottery and food , so I can't help every time I get lottery  food has to be involved , all the employees are great especially Joe and Rae... If I hit the powerball tonight I'll give them some extra money and if I do I'll give this place 100 stars

A decent spot for tacos, burritos, and bowls just steps away from all the theatres on Broad Street / Avenue of the Arts.

If you choose a taco plate, you can mix and match certain meats, so I chose the carnitas, pastor, and barbacoa. All these meats had a good amount of flavor to them and came reasonably portioned. You can also choose from a variety of add-ons like lettuce, cheese, and sauces. 

It's sort of like Chipotle, but locally run and reasonably fresh. Seating is communal, and even though the staffers appeared to be dissing patrons right in front of them, I didn't have the energy to summon my college-era Spanish-language skills and listen closely to what they were really saying.

Although I love my ipod, and I am a sucker for downloading music off itunes, I still love CDs and am adamant about supporting my favourite bands. Blackbyrd Myoozik is where I go to find a lot of indie music that I like, and I can't leave here without at least purchasing one CD. The store is fairly small, but Blackbyrd is about quality, not quantity. You won't find a bunch of MC Hammer, Tiffany, or other forgotten artists. Instead, you'll find a lot of timeless music, and there's a large focus on the indie music scene. You'll find everything from punk, dance, techno, electro, alternative, blues, and more. Many of the CDs are used, but they are good quality and at reasonable prices. Blackbyrd also carries new and used vinyl, which I find really cool, because I really don't want vinyl to ever die. I've also sold some of my CDs here and got a fairly good price for them.

They're getting 3 stars because the people that work there are super nice, the atmosphere is ok, and they sell Jarritos and alcohol. That's pretty much it. The food is WAY overpriced for what you get. I've ordered from here 3 or 4 times and I've finally decided to let it go. It's not truly Mexican food, for starters. The chorizo is not real Mexican chorizo, and the so-called nachos were literally the kind you make yourself in a microwave. The burritos aren't bad - but there's hardly any meat in them. I guess they have good burritos if you're a vegetarian. I just can't get over the prices too - $8 for guacamole nachos, which is literally just a scoop of guac under a ton of melted shredded cheese. Most other dishes are just as expensive, and that's just for the lunch portions. The hours are also odd and inconvenient.

Great and fast. They use cotton thread which is a plus so it doesn't feel like the thread is slicing your brow. 

Unfortunately I don't remember what girl k had but she did an amazing job. Even brows :). And she was super fast which is a bonus because I don't like sitting in a chair for 10 mins holding my eyebrow. The girl I went to say in the first chair when you walk in if that helps.

In the past I have loved stopping by this place. Unfortunately recently the customer service has just been so bad! I went in today and they were out of most flavors (which is fine) and the woman working made each flavor they had sound so terrible. It almost seemed like she just wasn't in the mood to scoop so she made us not want anything. I had gone in a couple weeks ago as well and the same woman was working and she had an attitude then too. I love the owner of this place but this woman working is just not friendly and will make me think twice about coming back.

Love this place. Get through the smokey casino and you've made it to the best steak house in Reno/ Sparks. Great service, nice lighting, yummy food. Prime rib is always good and they have real horseradish. With out a doubt, it's worth a visit.

Nice variety of items. Some items didn't have tags so be sure to ask. Friendly service. A little more expensive than other big box stores, as expected, so we stuck to plants vs soil, decor, etc. Worth a trip for your next landscaping project!

Would give zero stars if I could. We went for a late lunch. Absolutely the worst service imaginable. We were seated by a male who ended up being the bartender but was arrogant and snippy. Not a single server showed up to bring us water for 10 minutes. I had to flag down a server to even get a glass of water. After waiting to finally order our food, we never even saw our server. It was the server of an adjacent table that helped us when we needed anything.

In addition to the unprofessional service, the food is greasy and overpriced. Caprese was decent but burger and sandwiches are incredibly greasy and unpleasant. 

Avoid this place like the plague.

I went to the Weavers' Guild annual sale, where gorgeous handmade wares of all kinds are available. Clothing, scarves, hats, jewelry, raw materials to do your own crafting, Christmas ornaments, bookmarks, potholders, placemats, table runners, decorations of all kinds and more.

There were some weaving demonstrations that were informative and fun to watch.

It's always a great two-day sale usually the end of October or early November. Unique wares and great gifts!

Kinda out of the way of the airport and you need to drive through a residential 25mph neighborhood if your coming in off of Essington.    The service is very friendly and they are there immediately when you arrive to drop your car off and when you call on return.   However our driver made numerous lot stops and he was in slow motion which is OK if you leave yourself plenty of time to get to the terminal.

Am I the only one who misses their old menu? I'm a big fan of all the Tria locations, but liked that this one offered more substantial dinner options vs. being limited to smaller plates and snacks found at the other Tria Cafe locations. If it was an option to, I'd prob knock it down a 1/2 star for that.

I've been here a few times since the change in menus and the Italian meats, smoked duck salad, and grilled asparagus, artichoke, and white bean salad have become my new go-tos.

I last reviewed this spot 7 years ago, but need to amend based on my latest experience.  Just checking what the current ranking is was 3.5 stars which seemed a bit low for Primo's but probably on par for this location.

While most traditional mom and pop shops on the Ridge are closed on Sunday this chain is open, which becomes one of the few available options outside of traditional pizza.  Combine those closure with a late in the day Eagles game this place is going to be downright slammed with orders and of course it showed.  Waited at the registered for 5 minutes while people rushed around to put together as many sandwiches as possible before that 4:15pm kick off.  Also was great to hear them complaining about people calling out on Saturday and how bad it was. 
Finally was greeted by some guy who just walked into the shop, that I presume was a delivery guy.  He asked my name and grabbed my bag and I was outta there.   I didn't want the ordeal of them having to ring me up for chips and/or soda or a pickle so I just left. 
On to the hoagie itself.  For a primo the meats & cheese are quality so that was there and I expected that.  As for how it was made, it looked disgusting.  The slapdash configuration was piss poor with onions strewn about and a solid block of provolne cheese cutting through bottom of the roll killing the structural integraity of the sammich.  Nothing folded or tucked or neatly; it was not packed like a hoagie should be.  Finally, onto the worse looking lettuce I've ever seen.  The diced iceberg lettuce was so soggy it looked like it had been sitting in ice water for a day.  So wilted I had to pick it out and toss it.  
Just look at the other reviews for the lack of customer service at this location.  I haven't walked in there within the past year without people waiting in line and the cashier looking flustered.  This location needs to get its act together. 
It's Primo's so I'll be back, just hope this review reaches someone and changes are made.

Always has a great stock of records coming in, both new and used.  Keith does an excellent job of cleaning up the second hand vinyl which is greatly appreciated by a collector.  The only downside would be that there's always something that I want, which will eventually put me in finical ruin.

In the past I would have given this place 3 stars. Everyone is friendly. The place is clean. The food ok!  This time around...the fish was over cooked and flavor less!  Did not help to watch the cooks drop food and try to save the food on the plate with the bare hand that was not clean and re-plate it. Not sure who that plate went to but it upset people at the table.

We stayed here using our Best Western points.... so not bad for a free stay.  View from balcony was breathtaking. ... best part of stay.  Bed was not very comfortable and pillows... or lack there of... was terrible.  Four half size... like a throw pillow on your couch... on a king size bed.  Walls are thin and could hear kids running around hallway.   Plus side did have a restaurant/bar attached so that was handy.  Front desk was helpful for the most part depending on who was working. Shuttle was convenient.

With the 4.5 rating I was expecting to be wowed. While they have a decent selection, we found them a bit too sweet. They also have coffee and a couple other pastry snacks. There's plenty of outdoor and indoor seating and the views are welcoming. Staff was also pleasant. All in all you'll get your gelato fix here but ultimately I prefer Allegro down by the UofA.
Their Mediterranean pizza is sooo yummy ugh! The customer service was great, will definitely be back!
Taste was OK. But the portion was too small. You might want to grab Big Mac on the way out.
Great organic brunch!!!  Very nice environment and tasty healthy food selection    Awesome for kids too!
The buffalo chicken crepe was soooo good. Tasted about 6 more of friends' - all delicious. Great service, nice staff. Solid!
Tried rapid pickup, when i got home two out of three items were wrong with the order. Tried to call location, no one would answer. Had to go back to get the missing items.

My night at Killer Shrimp was out of this world. I came in with a few friends and got a variety of appetizers: calamari, popcorn shrimp, and the ahi tuna poke. Every dish exceeded my expectations and I enjoyed the atmosphere. The manager ended up buying us a few of the specialty drinks: the westside which was by far the best drink on the menu! I will definitely be going back to this place!

Think Sierra Snow and Ski, but with less variety.

Prices weren't as low as I was expecting, and choices was pretty slim.

I understand in Clayton there are not many quick  and good lunch options, so for Crushed Red to open up in the Downtown clayton area, I thought I would give it a shot. After all, they promote fast pizzas, salads and appetizers. Why they don't have sandwhiches is beyond me. 

I don't know if it was the fact that the people working there were all brand new, but some of the people taking my order knew nothing about parts of the menu. They certainly could not describe the olive tapanade, or whatever it is called. 

I ended up getting two pizza's and an appetizer with my bf. The appetizer was this diced olives and pita bread. I'll say the pizza's were OK and the appetizer left something to be desired. The pita bread was floppy and not warm, it pretty much crumbled in my hands. The olives were OK, but really weird the way they were presented. I prefer my olives whole, not chopped up. The olives apparently were considered to be a spread, but was not of the consistency to be a spread. Just awful. 

It gets packed at lunch time, but not terribly so. However, trying to find a seat is a gamble and you should really go with someone so that they can grab a booth and you can order the food.

Our 6 month old puppy tore-up the carpet in the door way of my master bedroom.  I thought I would have to replace my wall to wall carpet.  I called Scott and he came to my rescue.  He seamlessly patched the carpet with some carpet from my hall closet.   He also replaced the small patch he used from the hall closet with a piece that he had.  He is truly experienced in his trade.

Scott was prompt, knowledgable and very nice to work with.  He went the extra mile and used all organic products which is wonderful since we have small children.  My carpet looks brand new and the patch job blends perfectly.    If I could give Scott 10 stars I would.  I highly recommend him and I know I will be using him from now on. It is so great finding a true professional.

Teri. Tahoe Donner Truckee, Ca

My first place to eat at during my New Orleans Trip. Staff was very helpful and friendly. Carbs galore if you're into that sorta thing.Ordered a delicious breakfast and the Beignets with Mimosas! I would recommend this place but be advised it is quite small. Does have a nice small outdoor area too.

The food and drinks here are horrible!  It's a shame because it's a great night out with a group of friends and they usually have good acts come through. But when you have to order 2 things per person at least have semi good options.

Let me first start by saying I've been getting brazilians for years and have gone to many places. I've never been made to feel so uncomfortable as I was here. Nayeli had great reviews so I went to her. My first time she nagged me the entire time about how I shouldn't have shaved in between waxes. The second time she was 15 minutes late and then shoved product at me. And I still would have came back, but the third time is what did it for me. The moment I laid down she asked me how long it had been since my last wax and I told her 6 weeks. Which is a normal growth cycle if you go anywhere else. But of course she started with saying that was too long and that now I would have more hair. Ok so what?...Then when I tried to make small talk she brought up AGAIN that I had too many baby hairs because I was in between cycles and it made it more difficult for her than it should have been. As she was finishing, she found a strip of hair that she had missed and had the nerve to say "oh it looks like you have a strip here. Since you're already here, I'll just go ahead and remove it for you"....as if she was doing me some type of favor by removing hair SHE missed in the first place. Then at the very end she made sure to reiterate how I shouldn't have gone six weeks and that that was he reason she left some baby hairs. Weird....every where else just plucks them and moves on. Then after she finished insulting me for 15 minutes she walked me out and proceeded to shove product on me as she always does. Needless to say, this is terrible customer service. Getting a Brazilian is already not the most pleasant thing to do. I don't need to be nagged and made to feel even more uncomfortable throughout the entire process. The only reason I'm giving this 2 stars is because the girl at the front is always really sweet and helpful. It might be a good idea for management to work with the estheticians on etiquette and how you treat a customer who is lying butt naked in front of you.

MehMehMexican
Only people who think Applebee's and Chili's are haute cuisine could like this place. That ssid, it is wildly popular. The Mexican plate wasn't awful. Nothing was awful. The margaritas were even good. I just don't see why anyone would wait 5 minutes for a table here. Bean dip is 80% cheese. Chips were pretty greasy. Beautiful area. Nice ambiance. Eat at Los Primos and then come here for a margarita. I have nothing against them. I simply have nothing for them. Meh.

Okay literally the worst chipotle in America! First of all their whole management team sucks, ZERO customer service skills, second their extremely disorganized the amount of times they've messed up orders is insane like it could be a company record, third there is no sense of urgency for the customer they need to be more efficient. What I would say is avoid going to this chipotle IT IS NOT WORTH IT! Go to the Voorhees one or the cherry hill one, avoid this one save the head ache and hopefully they'll do so bad they'll get shut down!

The customer service was horrible. Got to the drive through and the person taking my order was rushing me to get my order as if she had somewhere to go. Then I get to the window and ask for her to tell me my order back and she was speaking so fast I did not hear the whole order. I had four combos in my order and they stuffed all of them into 1 bag. Then I check my order and one burger was missing and one burger was incorrect. I went inside to address this issue and it took them 17 minutes to get me 2 burgers. The people behind the counter was extremely rude and was acting like I was disturbing them. I will never go back there. The level of customer service was expected to be better especially for the coat of the meal and just because you should want to provide your customers with top of the line customer service. I hope they get it right and treat their customers better in the future.

This place deserves zero stars. It's a shame because they used to have really great service and many in my family and significant other's family purchased vehicles from them. Well, not any more.
My boyfriend's truck was having trouble for a few weeks. It was a bit scary and we were driving together and it would switch gears real quick. My boyfriend gave John Kennedy Ford a call and they said to bring it in, saying we would need to drop if off for a few days. His transmission was messed up and he told them we would need a rental car if the truck needed to be there overnight. The truck is under warranty, and should not be having transmission troubles under 38K miles. He had the phone on speaker to take down any helpful information and I could hear the first person he spoke with was  a real nice older gentleman. He was told us he would need to bring the VIN # .  We found the # and called back and were placed on the phone with another staff, Jim Mullen. He told us that we would not be getting a rental car free of charge regardless if the truck was covered under warranty. We went back and a forth a bit, so my boyfriend figured he would come in as it's much easier to talk in person.

He went  in and spoke to another gentlemen at a computer. Told him the story and he was very polite and kind and said sure he would look in to it. Not a few seconds after that, Jim Mullen came charging out of his office proclaiming "No, I talked to you on the phone and told you that you cannot get a rental car"  He had absolutely no sense of customer service and I am utterly shocked that he not only works there but manages the store. He came charging out as if he was stopping a thief. When simply, we were trying to obtain a rental car for a few days while the car we had that had under 38,000 miles had a bad transmission. We were then told by another gentleman that we could get the rental car reimbursed if it was the transmission. Odd, because Jim never mentioned this once, yet he pompously raised his voice and proclaimed we would not get one.
It's not the fact that we did not receive the rental car, which we also find frustrating as the car is under warranty,  but the way Jim presented himself; an utter lack of communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. 
 Shockingly he is the supervisor as well and kept on informing him of this over and over he was saying " I am the boss. And after me is John Kennedy himself and I know him really well"  
I am not sure if Jim was having a bad day, or if he regularly treats customers this way. I have worked in the customer service industry for a long time, and rule of thumb is that customers are always correct. You work with your customers to make them happy...especially if they and their family have purchased many products from you (we were at about 6 cars in 6 years)
They've lost not only my business but my family's. ...oh and very recent business. We bought a new truck today from another Ford dealership. One that was much more accommodating and agreed the truck had transmission troubles.

Going from a Dunkin/Starbucks to this type of coffee is a revelation. I thought I knew what 'good coffee' was before, how very wrong. Some dear friends turned me on to the likes of Joe and Elixir and the 'I have to try soon', Ultimo, and what can I say. These trips have been some of my favorite experiences to a shop this year. Coffee that doesn't disappoint on flavor, bitterness being a non factor....and actually being able to drink coffee black and enjoy it without a mean look on my face, lol. Reasonable prices and a nice relaxed atmosphere ensure regular trips to this awesome place.

I know i do it. I'll admit it.

I try to pigeon hole the Barbary. I try to list its limits in my head every time I get invited there or a particular night attracts me. I think of every reason I could ever have to not go here (which sometimes works on nights where they charge a cover...covers are easy ways to talk me out of going somewhere sometimes).

but in the end, we all know we go to the Barbary to forget who we are for a couple hours on the dance floor. its way too packed to even see anyone else's moves so you know what...you're free to just throw your body around how ever you want cause no one will ever notice if you've got MJ's moves or are doing your best impression of the Mummers dance. 

they ALWAYS play good stuff here. the dj's are on their A-game. there's guaranteed to be more than one time during a night that you and all of your friends look at each other and through your arms up and give out a little "AHHHHH!" because a great, special, gem of a song has just come on. 

photo booth, an upstairs (that i have yet to see cause it's rarely open when i'm there), two bars downstairs (one is just beer i think), coat hangers (i don't trust leaving my coat ANYWHERE but if you do , there's a place for you). somehow, atleast the ladies room, is never a long wait no matter how many people are here. bartendars are generally pretty nice guys.

like i said, it gets pretty packed. and sometimes that's a mood killer for me. sometime the exact opposite, a mood maker.  if you want a nice quiet night, go before 10pm, or on a sunday karaoke night (they have a great selection of songs to belt out while you're chugging those beers by the way...i've been known to bust out Natalie Imbruglia and Dusty Springfield). but if you're ok with getting sweaty and bumping more than a few bodies during the night, this is definitely your place.

Average burgers. Overpriced. Out of all the times and people I've gone here with, maybe twice has someone used their bottomless fries, which are dry and not that great. 5 guys is much better and cheaper.

Delicious, quaint, romantic Italian restaurant located on the popular 7th Avenue in Ybor city. This is authentic Italian food. It's a very beautiful place but not too large making it more intimate. It's closed Sunday and Mondays but open for lunch and dinner Tuesday through Saturday. They have a great wine list and great variety on their menu. We started with the Caprese salad, I had the Scaloppine alla Ortolana: Veal scaloppine topped with eggplant mozzarella garnish with sweet red pepper, light tomato oregano sauce. It was delicious, the veal was nice and tender, the sauce was creamy but not overpowering. Our server combo (they worked as a team)  were wonderful, gave great recommendations for food. You can also bring your own bottle of wine for a small corkage fee. Best to sit by the window and people watch out onto 7th Avenue. This is a great little gem in the heart of Ybor. There's limited street parking but also a pay lot behind the restaurant or the garage is nearby. I'll definitely be back, there are at least 4 more things I want to try on the menu and I was too full to try their dessert this time.

Based on all the reviews and star rating, I was inclined to come here on Christmas Day (visiting from NY). First I got tricked by the many doors they had as I foolishly tried to enter the restaurant through a dummy door. Ironically, we were seated by the dummy door I tried to enter. If you get seated, avoid area by the 2 doors as you can't see anything going on (worst seats in the house).

We didn't want a lot of duck so we just got the duck boas. Duck was juicy and moist and very good. The baos was not fluffy as I've had at other places. The chicken wings were served on cracked chips?? The garlic wings were decent. The beef chow fun was ehh, no flavor. The eggplant and chicken was over cooked and sauce was too gloopy. 

Overall, food came out fast. That could possibly mean non-quality dishes. I would say the duck is the best and only thing you should get here if you manage to enter through the correct door. Everything else, hard pass.

Great popular dog park.  Street parking
great astroturf and lots of seating 
nice place to enjoy afterwork with your pup
Never had a bad experience here! They always take their time. The pricing isn't bad and you definitely get more than what you paid for! I have been a repeat customer for almost 5 years now.

The food here is actually good. Unfortunately, every time our order is incorrect and the managers severely lack customer service skills. We've been blamed for their mix up and they only off 50% discount for the wrong item because we still have the item we did NOT order (it was delivery, it's not okay to request the customer drive across town to get their refund). We've paid for their mess up for the last time. Will not be returning.

Ordered takeout, told us it would be 20 minutes and then we sat there over an hour waiting for our food. They got my order wrong, called back and after fighting with the woman on the phone she agreed to credit us back the cost of my meal. 2 months later still no credit. Rude and liars, not a good combination and we will never go back.

Came here last night we started with the wj poutine fries which were very good. We order the two slushie drinks that souned good but were nasty as well we had to choke them down.  My husband ordered the fried chicken sandwich which was good and very big and had alot of flavor.  I ordered the perminto grilled cheese sanwhich with tomato soup.  The soup came out luke warm! Who brings out luke warm soup to a table.  So asked them to heat it up, which they did.  Didn't taste that great even after they heated it up. A little bit later I was puking my guts out and it was only tomato soup so something about the soup wasn't right.

I wish I could recommend this place for the two dishes we did like and because service was good but the fact I got sick off the tomato soup here means I can not.

Sorry to say one of our favorite places has seen a steady decline in prep and quality the past few months. While staff is great and attentive, food has not been up to par- the burgers are one of the things they do best but in past few months have arrived under cooked and med well= rare and bloody. Most of the other entrees are over salted including veggies and good has arrived luke warm. We get the loud crazy atmosphere is part of the deal but when food quality and prep is falling then what is the point? Many friends are saying the same thing hopefully this will be fixed.

Across from the Pageant and in the loop, this beauty bar gave me an up-do and eye-makeup/lashes. The owner and the only everything woman here does hair, makeup, extensions, and there is someone else for nails. I think they are looking to hire others. But for now, Ley is the only person I would trust. 

I was really really please, actually more than please. I was looking for a beauty bar that can work for ethic woman in general, but Ley can just do about anyone's hair and make-up. If you know how to do a black woman's hair - you can do it all. Ley also spent her training/time in LA- so there are a lot of influences there.  Ley was really professional and also just down to earth/friendly. 

In addition, I received so many compliments for my hair/makeup that it was worth the 2 hours in the chair. This place is almost a secret to Vegaz (which truthfully I could not get an appointment to - so decided to stumble about this gem here). But- I am glad I did. 

So my experience was on-time, friendly, and professional. So - totally worth it. The services in general are really well priced, but shampooing, etc. are extra. They are a full service bar and can do it all. If you are from cities like NY, Boston, LA, etc, this place is very underpriced and cheaper than Vegaz. 

For my hair/eye make-up/extensions- I spent around $96 (which includes the discount). But they also have loyalty cards. And I will be back and try new services.

I also know they have make-up classes on Sundays. If I can fit it in, I completely need to give this a try and bring the girlfriends along.

I had 3 tacos, carne asada, chipotle chicken and a pork one. I was expecting a regular taco but the flavor and freshness of the each taco was extraordinary. I wanted to got back that same day for more. Also, it is not expensive.

The Container store is not the same business that I once liked, and was a frequent shopper. The Sales People are nasty, and unprofessional. They have no respect for older shoppers, and I try not to go there. I go on the internet for alternate businesses.

Why is such a nice-looking, well-designed downtown space empty on a Saturday afternoon? Perhaps because it is staffed by a pair of posers who sneer at dark roast and then charge $4 for a bad-tasting cup of attitude. I had brought a buddy from out of town, hoping it would help to elevate his impression of Tucson. Fail. I'll stick to Luce and Time Market.

The other handful of reviews appear to be older and are clearly outdated. My guess is that the ownership has changed because there simply isn't any quality here in any way. This is not a hidden gem. I've been there twice over the last few weeks because I'm not a fan of negative reviews after one visit barring total catastrophe. The Sandwich Machine isn't a total nightmare...it's just not special at all. I've tried a roast beef sub and a ham/turkey/salami sub. Both were as bland as can be. The seasoned fries? Certainly nothing beyond the average fast food fare. The broccoli cheese soup was watery...but surprisingly decent. I was sure that ordering that would be a mistake.

Will I return? Maybe...but only when desperate and in the area. It's not deserving of a special trip, and the owners/employees certainly won't make you feel like you've made one. It's like walking into a morgue but with less upbeat people.

As a new resident of Tampa, I was tasked with finding new health and dental providers. Jackson Dental's reviews suggested promise and quality care, but the customer service that I received was far from welcoming. As a point of information, emails with highlighted deadlines (deadlines?!) and text with all caps read as aggressive. Coupled with yet another voicemail ... too much. I'll take my business elsewhere.

Always a fun time. Tonight we celebrated a fellow foodie's 25th birthday at her favorite spot. Hotel Tango debuted their Summer Cocktail menu and it did not disappoint! The "Razzario" and the "Temptation Island" were both yummy. Both sweet and strong! They had the large barn doors open and it was the perfect, breezy summer evening. There will be many more summer nights like this to come in our near future, I'm sure!

Can't miss with this place!  Good food, nice portions, wonderful service and reasonable prices.  We  happen to be there on a Wednesday ... oysters were $5.95 a dozen.  My wife and I split the Viola Special on muffuletta bread ... meatballs topped with our red gravy and melted mozzarella cheese.  Wonderful!!  We've tried lots of other things on previous visits and they were all good, too.

I visited Gio's yesterday for a small networking get-together. I'd never been here before, so I wasn't sure what to expect. It does have a great location -- it's in the middle of downtown with easy access to sports venues, monuments and bars. I took the Metrolink from work to the 8th and Pine station and walked a block to the restaurant -- nice not to mess with parking!

Inside, the decor was light wood and many windows. On one particular side, you could dine along the wall of windows and feel like you were outside. The person at the front escorted me to a small private room toward the back where our party was meeting. I had a chance to see a bar area with several tvs.

I had to make the event a quick lunch, so I didn't order a salad or appetizer with my entree, which was fettuccine con broccollini e funghi -- pasta con broccoli with mushrooms. It was a decent portion for lunch, and I had a little bit to take home for later. It was served very hot, and the sauce was creamy and tasty. I liked that there were plenty of mushrooms, though I could have used a bit more broccoli. I'm not sure it was worth the price of $14, though -- it was good enough, but not outstanding by any means.

Our waitress had her hands full with people coming late and leaving early, so I give her mucho credit for balancing everyone's needs. Water refills were of average frequency. The only thing that took a while was settling the bill. A couple of us were ready to go at the same time and were waiting for a while to give her our cards and then to get the receipts. Still, though, she was pretty quick for all other accounts.

For the record, I didn't notice smoking in the section our group was in, but I don't know if that carried over to the section around the big corner.

I might go back again. Gio's certainly didn't offend me, but it didn't wow me, either. I'm a vegetarian, so I just can't justify spending even more for pasta con broccoli (or basic pizza) at the dinner prices.

Comically bad.

Their sushi isn't sushi.  It's a tribute to "Krab".  On one role, they painstakingly stretched little strips of Krab across the top, like a wig of Krab.  Another roll - the Volcano - is just a simple tuna role, but it's topped with a horrible pile of Krab strips mixed with warm aioli.  

The seared ahi salad was topped with a slab of tuna cooked to medium well.  Not exactly what I'd call seared ahi.  After tasting the tuna, I suppose that the chef may have felt a need to cook it that fully.

The ceviche consisted of some Rojo's pico de gallo with some frozen shrimp mixed in.  If a friend made this, I would have said "It's clever, and pretty tasty." - but this is a restaurant.
