After spending $60 on lunch for two people, we left hungry and with food on our plates.  The location is great; having Lucky Fins there is an unfortunate waste.

Despite the horrible food (clever ceviche excluded), we did enjoy our meal, as we couldn't stop laughing at what they called "seared ahi", "ceviche", and "sushi".  That said, when we got the $60 check for two of us for lunch, the joke was on us.
I was only here for a layover, it was clean and efficient.  Good enough.

This Dunkin' Donuts is a good place to pick up coffee and a breakfast sandwich. The people at the front are good with orders. The tables aren't always clean, but they still manage to get stuff done. They make your drinks right. They are always ready in a timely manner. Also, The online app is even faster and easier because your Order is always finished by the time you arrive at the store. I'd recommend you to visit here if you're feeling down & exhausted in the morning. Or when you're hungry.

Saturday afternoon 230 pm, walk into the mens room and fall on my ass because the porter has decided to wet mop the floors the only day a week  they are busy with patrons wagering. Spoke with the manager and asked why and got attitude from him about it, for the LIFE OF ME I dont understand this thinking.  It does not matter as I will never set foot into this place again and I do bet 100 to 150 per race and am known as a heavy better around the Delaware valley.

The only reason Ken didn't get a lower rating is because he came the same day that I called. We called Ken because our handyman could not get the wiring to work correctly when he built a wall to make a new room.  We needed to move an outlet and a couple light switches. 

Ken showed up and looked at the handyman's work and said he would fix it. We agreed on a hefty price and he went to work. 15 minutes later he said I might not be able to put the two lights on separate switches(which is what we called him for).

An hour later he told us that the job was complete. We walked through the outlet and switches and it was no different than when he showed up. He just wanted the $340 that he was charging us and wanted to get out. We had a disagreement and I let him know that his communication skills were definitely lacking when dealing with customers. 

I would not recommend!

I was in Philly for a business trip and decided to stay longer for a night out in town with my fiancé . He stumbled across this Cuban resturant and it was such a great time ! They sat us in the third floor I believe in a nice little area overseeing the downstairs floor . The area was dark but well lit . The staff was professionally dressed and well attentive . The food was DELICIOUS! I was very proud of my fiancé for picking this spot ! Lol . It was def worth a cheat day :)

Update : they charged my card that day , than the money came out and charged me 9 days later ... totally through me offif that confuses you pay cash !

I wish I got to see more of this place it was insanely overwhelming crowded and poorly marked for driving it was very very stressful trying to leave with so many people walking in the middle of the road. It took 30-45 minutes to get through the lines for the attractions, I wish I got to actually enjoy it but it was so packed I just had time to get donuts and leave. If you go make sure you're not an anxious driver and that you like huge crowds, it didn't feel very covid safe. It's a gorgeous area though I'd love to try another time of year

Will only go here for piercings. We had a pierced here several times that was great. However, the tattoo artist we had today seemed like she didn't know what she was doing, and I had to basically walk her through sterile technique as she was forgetting a bunch of simple steps (change gloves, clean the tattoo gun, etc). She would touch things such as the biowaste garbage with her gloved hand and then touch my friend, and even put on the plastic protective sheet barehanded. These all scream infection. The tattoos she did for my friends have blowout as well. I know this is for a good cause, but you get what you pay for. And sadly, potential disease and ink needing a redo in the future are on that list. Also, they use tape instead of plastic to cover the tattoo gun, which would not prevent blood from soaking through in the event it got on it.

My boyfriend and I ate here on New Years Eve this year (2012-2013), as we wanted to have a nice romantic and enjoyable dinner. I did research on this place before we went and was quite impressed with the history in the prohibition era and Irish back round.

I was able to make reservations with ease online and even push back our dining time as we were running late. 

We both ordered drinks, i ordered wine and he opted for beer. They decant the wine before they pour it which was pretty neat. I was informed that they are known for their corn cakes, which they bring as a starter. Warning, they are pretty addicting and can fill you up if not careful!

We both ordered steaks, my boyfriend ordered their cowboy t-bone steak and I ordered a 6 oz fillet. The steaks were great and the potato was nothing special, just a baked potato with a small cup of butter on the side.

For dessert, we decided to order one of the special dessert drinks and shared, since we were both pretty full. I ordered a "Siberian Sleigh Ride". A wonderful concoction topped with a mini candy cane.

We thought the service was great and the waiter was attentive. The food was a bit over priced, as we have eaten at many steak restaurants before and this was about average.

Stumble across this jewel from friend's face book photos.  What a great meal!  6 of us went on an off hour and ordered 2 appetizers and 5 entrees.  EVERYTHING was good.  With my large family, we may be limited to going only during the non traditional meal hours.

I love it that everything is very casual that I don't have to "dress up" and make a big production in order to enjoy some excellent food.

The quality of the ingredients are top of the line.  Very generous portion.

Parking may be a problem, make sure you enjoy the meal the way the chef intend it to be.  I wouldn't dream to ask for substitution or "tweak" the chef's skill on a dish.

Can't wait to go back.

I love supporting local business... but buyers beware !  I went to Barto and my sales person was Frank Mumphrey.. who seemed very knowledgeable and assisted me with trying to find out what I wanted.  After we looked at the items in the back, I picked out several Frigidaire appliances that I loved.  At this time Frank told me about a "rebate" that would work perfectly for me, and if I picked out 3 items I would get a $150.00 rebate!  Sealed the deal for me so I got 3 items I liked. Well I submit the paperwork for the rebate and I was told that the rebate was expired.  Well I get with Frank and he told me that it was a error and he would help me to fix the problem, I guess this was the way of blowing me off : ( 
Since November 2017 I have been "working" with Frank to help solve the problem, but since December 15, 2017 Frank has refused to return phone calls and has not responded to emails.
So just wanted to let people know PLEASE pay attention to the small print when you are told something because it appears that you will be told anything to make a sale, and customer service is not important after you make a purchase.

Nothing special at all.  Rather disappointing as a matter of fact. Fajitas were over cooked. Served with a thin slice(?) of tomato, about 5 strands of lettuce and no cheese. Asked for cheese and lettuce and was charged extra for both.  Portions were small and the place isn't very inviting. We won't be rushing back anytime soon.

Loved the old place, Emils in this location. Wanted to love this place but can't. Minus one star for "phasing out" Eggbeaters in favor of tasteless egg whites, but still keeping the one dollar up-charge. That "phases out" the most appealing two thirds of the menu for the large number of us who have to watch our cholesterol. Come on. Even Perkins offers Eggbeaters and without up-charging at that! They're easier on the cooks, who don't have to crack and scramble eggs. Minus one more star for putting the "bla" in bland. I mean really. Just a little salt and pepper would be a start. BLT breakfast wrap needed Tabasco even to rise to edible. Most exotic flavor was the mayo. Breakfast potatoes also just limp with no hint of browning or flavor. Almost like hospital food.

Went here for dinner with my fiancé and did not enjoy it. We were seated right away, but waited so long for our waitress to come by it was getting awkward. By the time she came to us we were ready to put in our drinks, appetizer and entrees. A short time later our drinks came out..and then the appetizer. The man that ran the appetizer to us gave us our plates, mine was dirty and literally covered in food. He ran back and got me a new plate without saying anything. We didn't see our waitress much. She didn't appear to be on the floor so I'm not sure what she was doing in the kitchen. The bartender walked over to bring us our second drinks. Still no sign of our entrees. We watched the tables around us, that had been seated after us, all start getting their food. Again, no sign of our waitress. Eventually she came walking up and asked us if we were interested in dessert. Clueless. We informed her we hadn't even gotten our entrees and she immediately turned around and walked back to the kitchen. Another 10-15 minutes would go by before we finally got our food. The food was nothing special. I got the stuffed shrimp, half of which were not stuffed. The entire thing was sitting in a bowl of butter basically. My side of roasted veggies was actually steamed broccoli that was cold. Disappointing experience.

Food is good - tasty with all that Cajun fried magic that'll make anyone happy and ready for a nap. Whatever you do, get the bread pudding. You won't be able to eat it all at once. Unless you have some superpower that'll make you the Ironman of bread pudding. It's filling and southern excellence. You know, grandma's house good!

Visited Poppy's for the first time, today. It did not disappoint. I had a egg and cheese biscuit with bacon. My wife had a breakfast wrap. The food is excellent. We both agreed that it is better than some restaurants in the area and half the price. The coffee was very good, as well. Located in the shopping center at the intersection of Pennell Road (Rt 452) and Dutton Mill Road. It's a small place with about  five tables. Very nice casual atmosphere. Opens at 6:00 AM.  Worth a try. We will be back.

It is always great to eat outside on the deck. The Greek salad was at least 4 stars. The rolls very very tough. The pizza was 3 stars at best. Overall, a pretty good beach pizza place, but I wouldn't go just for the pizza.

The Thai tea rolled ice cream with lychee is good to try if you're undecided on flavors. However,  I don't think this place is really all that or worth the hype. The ice cream looks cool but tastes just like hard ice cream. The rolls get all smooshed together and melts so you can't even taste any sort of special texture.

The second time I came here, there was a 30 minute wait for rolled ice cream so I opted for the shaved snow ice instead. Let me just warn you that the snow ice isn't really snow or ice. It's literally large blocks of the same milky batter mixture that they use to make the rolled ice cream. What comes out isn't icy or snowy or fluffy or what you traditionally think shaved ice should be. It tasted like froyo that got dried up for being in the freezer too long and was way too vicose and too sweet. I would not get it again. The portion size for the snow ice is big though and a better deal than the rolled ice cream.

This establishment is probably where I'll be taking friends visiting me in town for novelty's sake but not a dessert place I'll frequent.

Update: With new places like Frozen and Teassert bar right across the street, Ice Land is not even worth a visit anymore. Ingredients at Ice Land taste like coffeemate mixer compared to the REAL fresh cream used at Teassert Bar. Go there instead!

As much we love coming to this place, we've noticed the last couple times the hostess has no communication skills, we are never greeted as we walk in, they barely look at you when they walk you to your table, no acknowledgement at all... Hostess are rude AF! Other than that food is great!

I went to this location with the BF a few weeks ago and was sadly disappointed.   The food was just okay, but the service was definitely questionable.  We waited for our drinks (soda, no alcohol) for over 15 minutes.  When our food arrived, it took our waitress 25 minutes to ask if everything was okay.  When we asked for fresh parmesan cheese, she told us they don't have it.  Ummm.  Really?  Since when?  I think she was just too lazy to get it and bring it over.   Then, it took another 10 minutes to receive the check.  FAIL.

Our dishes were mediocre at best, but the lack of customer service is my deterrent for ever returning.

I've visited Los Betos enough that I feel I should write a review, its only fair. I previously moved down here from Phoenix and was used to the quality of Filiberto's being status quo for fast Mexican food but Los Betos takes it to another level. For a 24 hour drive thru, its hard to beat the quality of this food. When it comes to late night eats, or a quick burrito for lunch, Los Betos is the perfect place to go. They also make some great breakfast burritos all day long. They have a wide variety of options, even some good salads if you're looking for something more on the healthy side. But face it if you're there after midnight, you probably aren't. 

What I like most about Los Betos is they make everything quickly, and to order. If you want some weird combination of meat, cheese, tortillas, and sides, they'll make it happen. Betos also has decent sonoran dogs, rolled tacos, and they have a $12 sampler platter that could feed a family of four twice. 

Los Betos has some interesting burrito combinations like the Texas burrito which is basically a California burrito with chicken instead of carne asada, and some sort of creamy chipotle sauce? I'm not sure exactly, but its fantastic. They also do a great veggie burrito (the only veggie to speak of is lettuce) the size of my forearm. I'd also recommend the carnitas and the red/green chile pork. All well done. 

If you're looking for some fast, good quality Mexican food that's better than what you'd find in most of the rest of the country this is your place. It's no fancy sit-down, and there are better tacos in town but none of them are open 24 hours with a drive thru and this many options. At least not on the north side of town!

I went here tonight and the service was terrible..  the host sat us down in the worst area.. the waitress look like she was high.. she did not ask us if we want water or did she ask us if we want anything  else when she came to the table or when she walked by.. it took more than 30 minutes just to get two mixed drinks.. how long does it take to make a drink? The food took forever.. we asked for some sauce for the shrimp and we was damn near done with our food before the sauce came out, our shrimp was cold by then.. never again! The waitress definitely lost out on a great tip! I definitely won't be going back here.. my rate is -1 Star..

A trip to the dentist is enough to make grown men afraid to the point of putting it off for years. When I was told to seek the help of an endodontist I was afraid and worried about what they were going to do. My entire experience with Absolute Endodontics has been nothing short of wonderful and I highly recommend that anyone needing a root canal or other treatment come here. First of all they were able to see me within a week of my calling to schedule an appointment and when you are dealing with tooth pain nothing is more wonderful than a quick turnaround. I went into the appointment full of dread about this complicated procedure (root canal) with many questions and a few misconceptions. The staff was absolutely wonderful and took the time to reassure me all while explaining the procedure in easy to understand terms. 

There was no wait in the lobby before I was brought back except to fill out the required paperwork. From start to finish I estimate I was in the chair for about an hour and a half so plan accordingly. I am incredibly nervous around needles and have had an adverse reaction to traditional novocane before so that was the part I was dreading the most. The staff actually said they had an alternative and I was able to be given that instead which is something my previous general dentist never did! I opted to use nitrous oxide (laughing gas) and it was the best decision for me. My procedure was less than 24 hours ago at the time of writing this and I am already experiencing far less pain than after any cavity filling I have ever had. Compared to the pain I was in before and leading up to this procedure this is nothing! I will recommend this place to all my friends and family as I truly have never been more comfortable in a dentist office. Thanks Absolute Endodontics!

I haven't actually sat down and eaten in here, but last weekend La Chica brought me a roast pork sammich that was to die for.  She said the plan was to get me a Cuban, but everyone in line was opting for the roast pork sandwich instead.  This thing, even after a 25 minute travel to Westchase, was still warm, juicy and freaking delicious.  It's a roast pork sandwich, how can it be so good, one might ask.  So I'm here to tell you why.  This was no ordinary pernil.  This was lechón-style pork with the crunchy bits and pieces of fat and skin.  Imagine taking your favorite pernil, and sprinkling in delectable pieces of chicharron, and then slapping that on some pressed Cuban bread.  That's what this sandwich was like, and I can wait to eat it again!  A true Tampa, Florida sandwich, where Puerto Rico and Cuba meet.

Took the puppy along on a road trip down to Philly -- but what to do with him once I'm there? Take him to Petsmart, of course! 

Not all Petsmart Doggy Day camps are made the same for sure -- employees are super happy at one vs. grumpy at another -- and I was pleasantly surprised by the staff at this location. Check in was prompt and easy (I called the day before to have my local Petsmart call this one to verbally verify vaccination expiration dates), and check out was great  -- I was even updated with how many times he peed and pooed while they had him (definitely going beyond expected service). They sprayed him with some dog cologne so he smelled fresh when I got him back, and he fell asleep within minutes of driving off. Couldn't ask for more!
Last three times I've visited this place they have been out of Boba!? That's like McDonald's being out of fries? No explanation just out.

Prices go up for same food. Not worth it much any more. Just a big chain to exploit. Portions get smaller, price goes up. My heart goes out to a few of their servers. They have some good servers. May be a few who are high on themselves, especially a young short skinny one with long hair. Seen she treats other servers like crap but is sure favored by gm. Food is usually good but prices keep going up. Too bad servers get crap led on by owner or gm. He will berate the in front of customers so he can get his fat bonus in his fat pants. Chad don't seem to care how he treats them.

Pros: They have a great selection of gourmet foods and treats for dogs, and the staff is very friendly. They also have regular shot clinics with a vet onsite so you can get vaccinations and flea/heartworm prevention very easily and affordably.

Cons: I find their grooming staff lacking (they had great difficulty trimming the nails of one of my dogs but this has been a non-issue everywhere else we've taken her) and the boarding should be avoided altogether, as I will explain below.

Their boarding prices are fairly reasonable but their attention to individual dogs is abysmal, they try to up-sell you on everything, and the check-in/checkout process took WAY too long.  We usually board our dogs at another Bay area facility and have never had any issues. We decided to try Royal Pets since it's much closer to home.  The facility is very clean and, again,the staff is very friendly.  However, after only 3 days of boarding, my boxer mix came out of there with a 'hot spot' that turned into a major infection that took months to heal because they did not notice that the plastic collar they put on her caused a reaction when she was out in the heat. She also had a large scratch/open wound on her head, above her eye, that no one seemed to notice and no one could explain. Not only that, but they did not offer to discount our stay at all. My other dog was fine, but she is older and not sociable so she was not exposed to other dogs.

Bottom line: if you need a specific food that is difficult to find elsewhere in Carrollwood (as ours, California Natural is), or you want a more affordable option for annual vaccinations and/or flea/heartworm medication, I would recommend it (these are the only reason I did not give it a lower rating). But, if you want a grooming or boarding facility, look elsewhere.
I would say very beautiful complex. Went to apply on Thursday. Didn't have a good reply to my application, felt a tad bit misled and am in hopes of a call back on Tuesday from email submitted.
This is review is long overdue. 5 stars for quality of service & customer service.Thank you Nina  my cookie is nice and smooth

Haven on earth has a diverse menu of bakery items, lunch items, and breads.  I have the pleasure to come here a couple a times during this month. The first time I got lunch of pizza and a cupcake. The cupcake was dry, and the pizza dough was okay, kind of gooey and also pretty dry.   The second time I came, I got a couple of loaves of bread and pumpkin bread.  The bread was dry and it crumbled easily after only a few days open.  I loved the pumpkin bread, it was moist and tasty, lots of flavor and you can taste the spices and the pumpkin in it.  The service was slow the first time, but I breezed through the second time.  The decor is simple.  I will probably still go back since there is not many gluten free bakery around.

Not the best, but certainly not bad. Went here with my parents a while back & it took quite a bit for us to get our food. Our pizza wasn't even super hot when we got it...huh?

Since I've discovered Bellagio, I probably will stick with that instead.

If you like thick juicy burgers, this is your spot.  All burgers come with a baked potato done up how you like.  No fries here.  Menu also offers steaks but this is the ultimate meat and potato place.  Signature drink is the Monsoon.  Delicious...yes.   Though a ton of rum.  Being a seasoned drinker I mistakenly thought 3 was a good idea.  Not so much.   Fantastic vibe and a lot of locals.

Will never go here again.  Service was atrocious.  We were checking it out as a place to take family during holidays to try C.J. Hamm's cowboy burger from Tucson Foodie's best burgers in Tucson list.  We actually had to walk out due to horrible service that finally delivered cold and inedible nachos and wings. We will not be going back.  Good luck getting a glass of water with your $40 order to dine in.

This BBQ joint is grande because they have a dish that comprises bacon with pulled pork in The Thing but they have a lovely cocktail and whiskey selection that makes me feel welcome as I don't drink craft beers nowhere near like I used to. 

This spot is a lovely one, filled with fantastic fun, and I'm not just saying that because I'm buzzed from one of their Urban Fashioned cocktails. Always a fan of Old Forester 1920, so I obviously had to get this cocktail and it didn't disappoint. The Urban Fashioned brought me to a place of spiritual purity and understanding.

The Thing is a rock star of a dish that has bacon and smothered in a gouda cheese sauce on cornbread. How amazing is that with the pulled pork and fried egg? Completely!

I went all in with my order of the chocolate peanut butter pie and that was the icing on the cake of a meal that screamed pure culinary satisfaction. I applaud URBAN Brew & BBQ and would come back here just to slam back some cocktails then indulge in some brisket and pulled pork because why not? If you're going to do something, do it right.
What is there to say that hasn't already been said.  GO there, get yerself a hurricane and enjoy in the courtyard.

If you are even remotely thinking about getting a massage, stop reading this review and just go make an appointment with Eleanor! 

But if you need more convincing, here's a little more info: This is not a fancy day spa. Instead, I like to think of it as a cozy cabin tucked away in the woods. Very clean and comfortable. Eleanor herself is down to earth and easy to talk to. I know massages can feel awkward at times, but I immediately felt comfortable with her. She listened as I babbled about all my pregnancy aches and pains and I didn't feel awkward taking my time getting ready or asking her questions. 

The massage itself was just out of this world. She never had to ask if she was using enough pressure. It was like she just knew the right amount to use. At one point she discovered that my back is ticklish (who knew?) and she not only changed her speed so that it wouldn't tickle, but she also explained why it was doing that. She was so knowledgeable about the "whys and hows". 

Last but not least, Eleanor is just an amazing person. Last year, my husband bought me a gift certificate for Trinity, but before I could use it I had a miscarriage. She gave him a refund without asking any questions because she could tell in his voice that something was wrong. What business does something like that nowadays? I felt terrible having to ask for a refund from a small business owner, but I am so glad that almost a year later I was able to use her services!

Food was just okay. My bagel came out so burnt that I couldn't even eat half of it. My husbands bagel was good though. It took almost a half hour to get my kids plain, untoasted bagels because the two girls behind the counter were too busy talking and kept forgetting them.  The mocha frappe was good but the caramel macchiato was mediocre.  It's really hit and miss. I've given them 3 chances and definitely won't be giving them a fourth.  Sorry, your staff needs to do better for the high prices you charge.

We ordered two drinks, I always thought that I enjoyed matcha, however I was proven wrong this time, the coconut matcha latte was for me a bit seaweed tasting. The matcha was still slightly powdery which was unpleasant for me. Perhaps I'm not a fan of matcha but regardless this was not the drink for me.

However, the pistachio delight was soooooo good, I still dream about it. It was perfectly creamy, the pistachio wasn't completely broken up which left tiny pieces of pistachio heaven, the boba was a neat addition, the drink was so good honestly I wouldn't of noticed if the boba was gone. Definitely will go again and will try their other drinks, and maybe order a teatanic size of the pistachio.

My husband and I ate lunch at Drago's. New Orleans has been a tough food city for me as I try to make healthy selections, avoiding sauces, dairy, red meats... So, perhaps this review is only good for those with similar diets. We ordered two different salads and raw oysters (the charbroiled are drenched in cheese - not to my liking). The salads were fine. I do love oysters but I realize I am not a fan of gulf oysters - preferring blue points and other east coast varieties. They just didn't taste of much. Overall, not worth the money.
Definitely the worst McDonalds in the area. No one knows what's going on, no one knows what to do, know one knows what you ordered and no one has any manners. Avoid unless you are STARVING!

Pro
- Fresh live crawfish.  I believe they had jumbo size crawfish mixed in the bag so that was a plus!
- The crab and lobster fried rice were really good and flavorful. They gave a decent amount of crab and lobster too!
- BYOB with no corkage fee
- The staff were really friendly and helpful.

Con
- Parking is really bad around this area!

I've been to Guadalajara several times with my husband and kids. The food is good, the mariachi's are great, and the service is typically very good. However, four friends and I went there the other night and the service was worse than I have ever experienced anywhere. If you get sat in Oscar's section, I recommend you move if you don't have 3 hours for dinner. We waited 15 minutes to get greeted by our server and 45 minutes to get our check. When Oscar came out to finally give us our check after tracking him down through another server, he apologized and blamed it on being 'swamped' inside. The place was almost empty as it was now 10pm on a Monday night. He was inside watching World Cup soccer not helping other customers. Suffice it to say, if I go back it will be for the patio, atmosphere, and food and if we are put in Oscar's section we will promptly ask to be moved.

My girlfriend and I went here for restaurant week on our anniversary. Started off the dinner with two complementary glasses of champagne to celebrate so that was very nice. The restaurant week menu was built around coming in pairs where a pair would be $35 a person and would be able to choose two antipastis, one piccoli piatti, two primis, and one secondi. 

For our selections, we asked our waiter what the most popular ones were for each dish section. The most memorable dishes that we had were candele which was a savory bolognese over long pasta and that was delicious. From the piccoli piatti, the rabbit was our choice and it was delicious. The rabbit was extremely tender and soft. There was a burst of juice and flavor in your mouth when biting into it. It also came with some delicious polenta. 
For our secondi, we got the pork chop. This, along with the rabbit, were the highlights of the meal. The pork chop was so perfectly cooked that cutting it into slices and off the bone was an experience of its own. Actually eating it was just as enjoyable.
For dessert, they gave us a slightly sweetened polenta with pecan mousse with candied pecans on the side and that was a pleasant and soft dessert that wasn't too sweet to cap off the great meal. 

Our server was very kind and helpful for the night so that was definitely pleasant to have. He came really frequently to refill water which could have been toned down a bit. The only other thing is that the restaurant is very dim but those are both things that shouldn't detract from the overall joy from the food.
They don't wear masks or enforce them. It's a simple act that can potentially save many. Shameful

I'm only giving 2 stars because our waiter was very friendly.  The food was average at best.  Cornbread was dry/crumbly, meat was lacking flavor and none of us walked away feeling good about the decision to go.

Was expecting great food based upon Kat Cora's recommendation on TV
Food was so so, toast was hard to eat as the crust was so hard
Butter came in a pat that was left on the warm toast thus had melted to the point I couldn't butter but one piece of toast.
Poached egg on one of our dishes was overcooked, on another plate the artichoke spread was extremely salty
Our waiter was not very attentive as he forgot toast on one plate and we had to get up and find him twice for service ( place was not that busy)
Overall experience was a 3 out of 10
Not worth the $100 we spent (plus tip)

My boyfriend loves burgers so I was so excited to try this place out. We visited on Sunday around late lunch time which wasn't crowded at all. The seating was right by the entrance on a high table where it wasn't all that comfortable but that was acceptable. The deviled eggs and duck fat fries were good, although a small dipping sauce with the fries seemed to be overpriced. Okay, I was very looking forward to try their signature burger which is twice the price of the other ones, but I hate to say I wasn't impressed. Yeah No, not for the price. No... It was overcooked than medium rare, the toppings were very average taste. Although I live 5 minutes away from this restaurant, I won't go back. Sad.

I had a great experience at this diner from the service, food, and everything in between! I came here on a Sunday morning so it was fairly busy and loud but to be expected just like any other typical diner. We were seated immediately because they had plentiful tables. As soon as we sat down our server offered us coffee to sip on as we explored the menu. A disclaimer - the coffee was awful but thankfully everything else made up for it. 

Their menu had a plethora of breakfast options ranging from sweet to savory crepes, omelets, variations of eggs Benedict, and so much more! I ordered the spinach eggs Benedict which came with potatoes and fruit. It also came with a side of meat but as a vegetarian I substituted it with more potatoes. It was served fairly quickly. The only issue I had with my meal was that it came with literally three pieces of fruit. The menu makes it seem like it was a side portion - not a decorative piece. BUT the eggs Benedict and potatoes were absolutely phenomenal. The eggs were poached to perfection, the spinach was savory and cheesy, and the potatoes had such delicious seasoning.

On top of the food being phenomenal, the service was great too! Our servers name was Don and she checked on us regularly, was super attentive, and extremely friendly. What more can you ask from a server, am I right? 

I definitely recommend this diner to anyone who is looking for a good breakfast place.

This restaurant is close to where I work, which is the only reason I have returned there several times. Today was it -- I'm done with it! The food is mediocre, the service tends to be poor and slow. Today, I asked for a sharp knife and was told they didn't have any. Seriously? How do they cut things in the kitchen? I asked to speak to the manager. After going back to the kitchen, the wait person returned to our table and said, "He's too busy." I walked out and will not return.

As an SPG lifetime gold and platinum I stay more nights than I'd like away from home. Checked in today and waiting in the lobby for 2 hours. At 6pm, room is still not ready and told it will be 7pm. Asked for another room then and checked into a very dark, tired (scuffs on walls, missing tile in bathroom, scratched up furniture). While waiting at lobby bar for room, they never entered my order.... waited then finally asked. Asked for water twice and still never got it. Finally asked for the check. Seems like the whole operation is drained of attn to detail or service. I will say, that Evan at the front desk was super, trying to help but issues seem more a lack of management. Next time I will try the le meridian a short distance away.

Not much good to say about Swingers.  I'm giving a one star for the outdoor patio with swings and one star for the possibility of low kitchen staff and supply shortages.
The brunch menu was lackluster choices, several options were not available, server never checked on us, food took forever (especially for eggs),food was basic. Keep in mind that there were about 8 employees on the floor (not doing much and definitely in no hurry) and only a handful of guests.
Taking this one off the list.

Stay away from the coffee grinder shop. Owner is exremely rude when it comes to customer service. Asked her for 1/4 pound of coffee and because her helper did not believe there was enough for a 1/4 pound of coffee beans she refused to weigh them or sell me what was left without even looking for herself. I requested that she at least weigh them to see if it was close to a 1/4 pound but she refused to even look. A grown woman rolling her eyes at me like  13 year old that doesn't want to haer what u have to say. Saying she was to busy with other things to weigh the beans even though i was only customer in the store at the time. Very disapointing experience! The only place worthwhile going to at qmart is Fredricks meats otherwise stay clear and spend your money somewhere were the owner appreciates your business.

Memo to Green Eggs Cafe: Love the food, love the green/organic thing you have going on, but don't make me pay for the privilege of TAKING MY OWN LEFTOVERS HOME by charging me 25 cents for every container. I'd feel better about it if you just built that cost right into your meals. Don't $%^&ing sneak it in as an extra line item on my receipt, nitwits.

I have been to more Thai restaurants than I can count, and have never found a bad one. This one is special, I went for lunch and was pleasantly surprised from the moment I walked in. The place is comfortable and the tables are spaced far enough apart so that I don't have to listen to someone else's conversation. I split an order of Tom Yum soup with my lovely lunch date. It is a Thai version of hot and sour soup and was a unique combination of subtle flavors that made each mouthful an event. The entree was Padthai and was every bit as good as the King and I. The one complaint i had was that it could have been hotter but since I was given the choice of hotness, I only have my self to blame. 
I don't normally consider "service" that big of a part of my restaurant experience unless it is so bad that it was insulting but this was different. I felt that the owner and the waiter considered us guests in their home. 
All in all this was a most enjoyable experience. I will make the half hour trip to visit this place so evening to savor their dinner menu.

I hate to put down local businesses, but I will never shop in here again.  I used to live just a few doors down from here and after being recommended to the store by my boyfriend, I decided to give it a try.   The customer service is just bad, and the staff acts like their shit doesn't stink.

However i must say thank you bicycle revolutions, for recommending Bicycle Therapy to me when I needed to get my bike shipped somewhere.

Have been here several times for brunch. Very much like their sweet brunch choices especially pancakes and waffles. Not so excited about their savory selection (e.g., breakfast burrito). Usually not a very long wait and very family friendly.

I placed an order for delivery on a weekday afternoon. This was first time trying this place out. I ordered a burger, loaded mashed potatoes, and a slice of carrot cake. The burger was average. The mashed potatoes looked juvenile. The carrot cake was ALRIGHT. Super average food. It is extremely likely that I won't order from there again.
Worst restaurant experience ever. Waitress had to be on crack with her constant itching and shaking. Messed up the bill 3 times and asked me to come back the next day to receive a refund.

Haven't been since the renovated the lobby so it was fun seeing all they did to it. I didn't really think the room we were in was all that impressive for the extensive renovation they did. The lobby does look really nice though. The bar was very lively to be expected for a weekend. I ordered food to go at the bar and they accidentally gave my food away but the server realized this pretty early on so I was able to receive my food not too terribly long after. I found a bug in my room which is a no go for me :/ I informed the front desk which they didn't seem to care too much about. I even asked later in the night after I got back to my room for the evening:

It's just not that great.  I've been before and I think I won't be back.  The food is just ok! The steak fajitas taste like the meat has been cooked and cooked and recooked.  Hard to chew and what I imagine leather tastes like.  
I do love the salsa but I won't come here just for salsa.  
The hostess greets ya without a smile.  Seems very unhappy working here. 
The booth we sat in had cloth covered back rests and they are FILTHY! Looks like a food fight took place.  Can't ya clean them?!

I walked out of here with two boxes (yes, boxes) full of different soda varieties, and we've been working our way through them for the past few months. Nothing has really jumped out at us as amazing and they are priced pretty high (buying large quantities does get you a discount). It was a neat place to visit once, but it won't be going on my bookmark list.
Soups here is amazing. Full of flavor. And many options to choose from.No brainer for the colder winter months

Awesome food. I had the Bison Burger, Chestnut St. style. The pierogi is apparently the thing to get, they change the filling frequently. Fries are great too. Go for the live music on Thursday nights, they get amazing players!

Shrimp and grits
Good a little pricey 
But was real good 
Would look to get it on rice. 
The fried deviled egg. Are the boom 

Nice place to take some one special

Staff. Was nice and Fast 

This is 
better then
 a lot of the other high end place in town

Was expecting more from this place once I knew it was the oldest Korean restaurant in town.    But sadly I had only an mediocre experience.   I ordered the stone pot bibimbap, but they got it wrong and gave me the regular.    And not once did the server return to the table to see if everything was ok or if I wanted another drink.    For the service, I would have marked then lower, but the dish was tasty and I ate it all.
I ordered the moo goo gai pan which is my fave. Usually. A little too peppery for me. And the chicken tasted like it was warmed up. The accompaniments were all good. But I can't recommend.

I just saw Noah in the IMAX theater here, and it was a bad experience.  (There are non-Imax theaters here which might be really good.)

The film was shown in 720P (which is about one eighth the resolution of the advertised 4k).

There was a LOT of ghosting where one frame bleeds into the next, so any time anything moved too quickly, the picture was just a blurry mess - especially when the camera pans around.

The sound was loud (which I like), but the IMAX speakers weren't capable of playing sound clearly at those volumes.

The IMAX sound setup is 5.1 or less.  Instead of letting us hear real sound sources like the regular Dolby theaters here with the huge seats, IMAX uses awful trickery (similar to Bose systems) to make us think we're hearing sound from sources that don't exist.

If you're a casual scrub who is wowed simply by the large size of the screen, then you deserve the IMAX experience.  But if you're a purist who appreciates high quality sound and picture, stay out of the IMAX theater.

-------
UPDATE:  I gave the IMAX theater a second chance with Interstellar since the movie was filmed natively with IMAX cameras.  Again, the film was output maybe at 720P which is horrible.  Same ghosting issues/flickering.  It also seemed like the aspect ratio was squished vertically.  Sound was loud again, but the center channel was drowned out by the loud music at a critical moment.  They need to boost that center channel.

That's 0/2 for the IMAX theater.  I'll maybe give IMAX one more shot with The Hobbit 3D HFR...

Always a must after the club or bar when we visit NOLA.   Don't let the bodega/simple corner store set up fool you. The hidden gem is in the back: the chef.  Go for the shrimp or oyster po' boy....dressed (sauce, lettuce and tomato on a sesame seed hero). It's soooooo good and it's big, but not overwhelming. There's a little bit of a wait, but definitely worth it. Food is cooked to order, so eat it while it's hot. Sorry....no pics to post...I ate it too fast!!

This place is amazing, and the ever changing, and inventive flavour creations mean that no two visits will be the same.

The only way this place could get any better, is they offer some stick-less versions of their deliciousness, if for no other reason than samples.

I had the opportunity to dine at the Signature Grill al fresco with a large party here last week while staying at the JW Marriott Starr Pass for a conference. Patio dining was a beautiful experience. We got to watch the sun set and the city lights of Tucson sparkle below us. 

Our waitstaff were excellent and did a good job managing our large party. They brought out our drinks quickly with a basket of awesome breads including a cracker-type wheat bread with pecans and amazing white rolls. I ordered my drink off-menu with a prickly pear margarita, and it was refreshing, although too much on the sour side compared to the same cocktail served downstairs. 

We enjoyed the quality of the food here. My colleagues gobbled down bacon fat steak fries, and I enjoyed the ahi tuna crudo for a shared starter. People loved the short rib and the Arrachera steak salad main courses. I ordered the seafood and wild mushroom risotto, which was the highest price point on the menu at $38. The flavor was good (not the best or worst risotto I've ever had), and the scallops and shrimp were cooked perfectly (the lobster was a little too cooked). However, the portion was very small for the price point. I was a little shocked at the size for the price, especially compared to the ample "entradas" around me with some of us even taking portions back to our rooms. It would be good for the staff to let folks know the smaller portion of this dish. 

In closing, the Signature Grill patio is straight up romantic, so consider them for a date spot, even for just drinks and apps.

The restaurant is nice, but the food isn't anything special. I wasn't impressed at all. The salmon was ok, but I have to say that i can prepare a better salmon at home. On top of that, it's pricey for a restaurant of its kind. I don't think I will be going back.

Hate to give bad reviews but this place deserves it. We went today for the first time and stood around waiting to be seated (theres no sign to seat yourself) finally an employee showed us to a table and we sat for 10 minutes without drinks or service, keep in mind there were only THREE tables with people and the staff seemed to be content chatting with one another. 
The waitress managed to come take our order and was polite, I ordered a burger and my gf the lamb gyro, the burger tasted like lighter fluid and the fries were pretty bad. The gyro was great, tender lamb and fresh tomatoes. Our drinks were empty prior to getting our food and I politely waited as long as possible before I walked over to our lazy server (older lady with dark hair) and asked her if I was supposed to get refills from the fountain myself or not. She apologized and refilled the water and coke...then disappeared for 20 minutes until she gave us our check and asked if everything was ok and if I would like a refill. I was pretty pissed by this time, I could see if it was busy but there were literally 2 other tables while we ate and I was more than happy to leaver her a $1 tip
We wont be back, if I want to serve myself and be ignored ill go to a buffet or mcdonalds

FOOD TOOK AN HOUR. I don't care how busy you are it doesn't take an hour to make a salad. On top of that the tomato salad had no vegetables in it and was covered in oil. The cuban breakfast platter was good though, but I wouldn't come back, no matter how many free funky spicy cheetos they hand out to distract me from the impossible wait.

My boss and I were on assignment in Indianapolis, and decided on Mexican for dinner. I did some research on Yelp, and hoped that this place would work out, since one never wants to take their boss to a lousy dinner. Bakersfield Mass Ave did not disappoint!

Though the restaurant was busy, we were quickly seated near the bar area at a high-top table. Our server was very informative and friendly. She delivered the information we needed without going overboard, and took our drink orders promptly.

We both had house margaritas, and quickly downed them. I am a sucker for fresh lemonade and/or limeade, and the margaritas here feature a TON of fresh citrus. They have a bunch of whiskeys as well, thought we did not venture into that part of the cocktail menu.

We started with guacamole, which was fresh and very flavorful. It was a good balance of citrus and heat, and the texture was very smooth (without being like baby food). The chips are house-made and done very well, not greasy at all.

For dinner we each had an assortment of tacos, which you can order singly. For me it was pastor, fish, and chicken, and steak, pastor and fish for my boss. All of the tacos are made with house made tortillas that have good integrity and do not crumble. The tacos al pastor were subtly spicy and featured pineapple, which I love. The real highlight was the fish taco, which had perfectly fried crispy fish, a creamy sauce and nice fresh cabbage. The combination of textures was great. 

I hope to return next time I'm in Indianapolis!

Room to grow but not there yet. Killer B's could be the real deal right here in Santa Barbara but the training wheels are still on.

A lover of BBQ, I could not bass up State Streets latest opening. A late lunch in the middle of the week found 1 take out order waiting, no one on the patio and a couple friends of the boss at the bar. I took a high top up front but there are regular tables in the back. Two flat screens glowed as final touches to the recently opened joint were underway. It's looking good.

The menu boasts "Our menu is created by Certified BBQ Master Chef Robert Van Houten. Serving as our executive chef, Rob has created his own unique blend of hickory, apple and cherry wood that we use to slow smoke all of our meat daily. Everything is made fresh including our own Signature BBQ sauces and dry rubs. So please, sit back, kick  up your feet and ENJOY!!!"

Certified BBQ Master and executive are not words I would expect to see in a BBQ joint. All I want is delicious meat and interestingly spicy sauces. What I got was actually very good. I guess there's something to this certification thing.

I ordered a beer and the Sliced BBQ Beef Brisket Plate for $15.99. That's a steep price but I guess the high rent district demands it. The plate comes with two sides and I chose the baked beans and cole slaw. Other options include fries, onion rings, corn on the cob, sweet potato fries, vegetable medley or mac & cheese. More on the sides later. Now, onto the meat.

The brisket was great. Good smokey flavor and moist. Not dry at all. It did come out with an almost baked on wet rub all over the meat. Obviously applied after the cut. Please leave the sauce off and let me apply. So why the 2 stars? While I ordered the brisket, I was given brisket, with mixed in tri-tip. The pile of layered meat was intermixed with the much different texture and very dry in comparison, tri-tip. Maybe they were low on the brisket and trying to make it last but please serve what's ordered.

Onto the sauces. I tried them all and found a winning combination.
-House- house, nothing special but good. I'm sure most will use this.
-Habanero- pop goes the tongue. Look out!
-Honey Chip- this has kick like a sweet buffalo wing
-Sweet- tastes like honey. too sweet for me
The Honey Chip was my favorite and even with it's little spice, it needed to be bitched up. A quick squeeze of the habanero and we have a winner!

How about the sides you ask? I'm not a fan there. The cole slaw is dry and I'm a fan of wet. Many will like it I'm sure. Just not for me. The baked beans were average. A little spice a pair of beans and some shredded meat. It was good and I would order again. There was a thick film on the top indicating they were sitting for a bit in the bowl which brings me to service time.

BBQ is a labor of love and takes hours to prepare. It's not fast food. However, serving it should be. It's already made. remove from warmer, slice and serve. So why did it take 10 minutes to bring it out? The kitchen is behind closed doors unlike most BBQ shops I'm used to so I don't know exactly what was done. 

the bottom line is Killer B's has great potential. A good, moist brisket, great sauce selection, beer and location. I promise a follow up visit in the coming weeks and an update to my review. I want to love you but for now, I only like you. Once you're ready, Ill be back.

Oh, and thank you for the meat. At least the brisket portion.
Stay stay away from the lemon orzo soup, taste like straight pepper couldn't even finish the bowl . Disappointed

I stopped by Hurry Back on a Tuesday for $2 Tuesday. Both the tacos and beers in the fridge are $2 so if you're looking for a deal, this place is nice. There's a comfortable patio so you can enjoy a beer with some friends and unwind.
I didn't try the tacos, but they looked alright, not really substantial enough to make a meal but good for a snack. 

I'll be returning and sampling more of their selection, the craft beer was mainly local or extremely international (Belgian imports and such). If you're stopping by for a show at Exit In this could be a good place to kick it before or after a show.

I blew a tire on my way out of town on a 10 hour drive.  Luckily, Josh and the team were VERY helpful in getting me the replacement tires I needed the fastest they possibly could.  They were so helpful that I came back the following week for my alignment and got an oil change!  Great prices, very friendly, knowledgable and helpful staff.  I don't like coming to Kenner, but I certainly will for all of my car needs now!

We had a great experience at this Outback Steakhouse. The surf and turf was delicious. Loved the Bloomin Onion. Our server was Kelly C and she was super friendly! The manager stopped by and was fun and friendly.

Gianna, my 8 year old Granddaughter, wanted to go here. Atmosphere was typical sports bar...and so was the food! But, she was happy, and that's what mattered. 

We started with the Buffalo Chicken Dip and the Boom Boom Shrimpl Obviously we went spicy and all of us enjoyed it. I asked for an Indian Burger NOT Blackened, as I was Jonesin' for a bleu cheese burger. It was done right for me! The table also ordered the Chicken Quesadilla and a French Dip which were really very good. (Yeah, I had to taste everything!)

All in all, it was just fine. The Baby Girl scored her Chicken Fingers and Tator Tots and was happy with her go-to meal.

The AT&T store has come a long way ... it appears that they are trying to mimic the Apple experience ("A" for effort, but don't really see that happening).  We were politely greeted at the door by someone whose sole function it seems was to do so, to take our names and to invite us to have a seat. When we got to speak to an associate (Ivan), he was very polite, pleasant and seemed genuinely interested in helping us. When we were done with our transaction, he walked us to the door, opened it and shook our hands on the way out. Overall conclusion: while not the "Apple experience" definitely a better AT&T experience than I remember!

Was in Santa Barbara for a wedding and this was the first spot my boyfriend stopped to grab a bite! Beautiful view of the ocean! Food is OK- I had a turkey burger (meh) and my boyfriend got the chicken sandwhich ("Good, what I expected. It was fine but I had low exception. I ultimately went there because it was convenient and nearby and had a great view"). Honestly, don't expect great food. Expect passable food; a place where you can throw back a couple of beers while enjoying the beautiful view that Santa Barbara has to offer!

Beautiful ambiance. Got here for the early bird dinner. $30 for full course meal! Plus the martini specials from the lounge apply in the restaurant. They even give you a hot towel at the end of your meal. My server Cooper was cheerful and fast. I appreciate him alot.

Great place!! Came with family and my best friend while she was on leave from the Air Force, they gave a great military discount. Staff was friendly and kind. My sister lost her phone in the ball pit & staff was super nice helping us search for the phone. We eventually found it! Great facility just wish it was bigger.

Absolutely wonderful food!! My husband and I ate here for my birthday on 12/8/18. We made reservations, but because of traffic, we were over 30 minutes late. I had called to let the restaurant know that we were running late. They were still able to get us a table quickly, despite being fully booked. They also have a waiter taking drink orders specifically for guests waiting in the lobby area. The ambiance is nice and calm. I ordered the surf and turf, a wedge salad, and had a complimentary dessert for my birthday. We left full and satisfied. The steak was the best steak I've had in ages! My only complaint would be our waitress. She forgot my husband's drink and did not return (so we could tell her), for over 5 minutes. Now if a waitress is busy, I can understand, but she was standing at the table behind us talking loudly for that entire time. For the price, I expect exceptional service. After that, she was much better, though. 
Overall, we had a fantastic experience and would definitely recommend this restaurant!

So I have gone into this dealership a few times and have not been impressed, today my husband and I said ok let's go in with an open mind and give them a chance. So we made the 30 min drive over. Firstly for a high end dealership there showroom floor is rather interesting they have a desk located toward the back of the floor so you are not sure if that is for service or a reception desk for the sale department either way it made no difference since they NEVER, EVER look up to welcome you or ask if you need help. We walked around for a few mins before a salesmen came over to speak to us. I explained that I am in the process of having my current Benz totaled and I will need a new car. Generally we work with the MB of Princeton but due to a few problems with the service department I said let try and work with this dealer instead. 
Off the bat our salesmen was nice but not really helpful, we walked over to his desk which I have to say the seating arrangement was set-up more like a low budget dealer rather than a high end brand. There was only one seat in the cube so poor hubby was standing for a while before our salesmen even noticed he need a seat. All that aside let's get down to business, I explained to him what I wanted as this will be our 4th car with the brand so I have nailed down the must haves; he explained that they did not have it in stock and we discussed what was in stock and how long it would take if you had to have it custom built. The answer was 3 months to customize your car. Fine I get that but I also know that there are a few upgrades that can be added to cars at the beginning of the manufacturing process I have done this before. The green, yellow and red coding on the vehicle list will determine if I could change things. So he say hmm no there is no new productions lined up till Feb of 2019 (right). 
So we asked him to show us what he had in stock, he walks away leaving us hanging at this point I was not sure what was going on. He pulls the car up, does not offer a test drive walks us over and walks away to chat with a friend of his. I looked over the car and said to him I am not feeling this one. He says ok and walks away, my husband and I was left standing here with our jaws hitting the ground he walked over to speak to his friend and I walked over said good bye and ran. It's sad to say that in my option the salesmen felt like an order taker at a fast food restaurant.

I haven't been here in years. We used to come all the time. It wasn't very good this time. The pizza my daughter ordered was burnt and my eggplant Parmesan was burnt and cold. I don't think we will be back. The waitress was very nice

Very good burger and nice atmosphere. The philly steak sandwich was very good too. The mushroom poppers were an embarrassment to the mushroom family. Too much breading.  Otherwise a good pub food experience.

My husband and I went here for lunch while visiting New Orleans as we heard great things. We waited in a line outside the restaurant for about 15 minutes or so. We thought it must be amazing for there to be a line, but we were not too impressed to be honest. We had the fried crawfish tails as an appetizer as well as a dozen of raw oysters. We shared a peacemaker po boy which had fried oysters and shrimp on it (it was big enough for two people). I liked the Tabasco infused mayo on the sandwich. With the being said I've had much better po boys in the past. My favorite of the entire meal was the fried crawfish tails. The oysters were huge, but not briney enough for me- but It seems that is how all oysters are in New Orleans? Later, a friend had told us that Acme was known for their grilled oysters, maybe next time we will try those if we were to come back to New Orleans.

I live right close by and it took me a while to try this place out because I hadn't been entirely certain that it was actually in operation.  It is small and easy to miss.  Since giving it a try "China 1" has been my go to take out Chinese place.  I have had both the General Tso's and the Cashew Chicken and they have been magnificent.  They actually have a bit of a different take on both of these dishes and while it wasn't exactly what I was expecting it was extremely delicious.  I am a fan of their egg rolls.  My criteria is that they have to be tasty and not disgustingly greasy.  They pass my criteria.  The Crab Rangoons are pretty standard.  I would say the main draw is that they do not skimp on the filling.  Be prepared for swollen Rangoons.

The service is what I have come to expect out of strip mall Chinese diners.  The same three people are always in there running the show.  It appears to be a small family.  Be warned I think the only one that speaks English is the daughter.  I had an awkward attempt at a conversation with the older gentleman my first time in there.  Luckily she came in from the back and saved us.  

I look forward to future visits so that I can try some of their other offerings.

For starters, we had chips and salsa. The chips were a bit chewy, and the salsa tasted canned. The restroom stall didn't have a lock on it, and the toilet holder was loose so when I pulled the roll, it fell on the floor. Tables and chairs need tightening up before someone breaks a chair and falls. The restaurant is also poorly lit. Now the food: The flour tortillas were very good, and the vegetables were delicious! Unfortunately, I cannot say much about the chicken. It was bland and watery. I'm not a fan of chicken stew in my tacos; I prefer grilled chicken. In addition, I chewed on a bone in one of my tacos. I enjoyed the rice, but the refried beans were way too runny! Our server was very nice and pleasant, but I cannot give this place more than 3 stars.

I must say that I was underwhelmed by several components of this brewery. Number one, the size of the samples of beer were a little disappointing.  Number two and more irritating were the prices of the beer.  Let's say you want a 6 pack. You're buying direct from a brewery and it was more expensive than I've seen in most liquor stores.  Really?  Also, if you want to buy a draft on tap the prices were mostly in the $6 range (and the Tripel Horse was $7).  I wasn't loving this aspect, to be frank.

The tour itself was pretty good, and our tour guide was quite knowledgeable.  She was able to answer most of the questions asked (by the same person) throughout the tour.  The beer was also good.  I was pleasantly surprised by the Summer Blonde ale (I'm an IPA girl).

Just disappointing that after the tour we drank our last sample of beer and then left since we could just as well go to an actual bar and pay the same price for a beer in a cold pint glass.  Also, it would be nice if somewhere on the property you could sit outside and enjoy your beverages...

Didn't hate it but didn't love it.  If you're in the area, swing by.

We wanted something different for dinner, and I'd passed by on my way to take my daughter to an event down the road. I was told the wait was 45-50 minutes. "Damn", I though, but okay. I guess that means the food is awesome, because there were tons of people waiting, many of whom seemed to be regulars. So, my daughter and I went and ran some errands. Came back at about the 40-minute mark. Food still wasn't done. No biggie. It was an hour after I placed my order, by the time they finally called my name. We did get offered a free dessert for the inconvenience {the coconut cake was not better than mine, but still delicious}. The hubs loved his bbq chicken, my son loved his burger, and my daughter loved her ribs. I saw the mac & cheese, however, {I believe} being taken out of a microwave. Not sure how I feel about that. I guess I assumed, with all the soul food sides, that it would be baked, as mac & cheese should be. My biggest problem, though, was my scallops. They were very small. They were so salty, I couldn't eat them. Nobody could. The rest of the family tried them, and no one could finish without spitting them out. It wasn't the coating, either. It was the scallops themselves. It was a waste of $14. Not sure we'll be going there again, which is a shame, because I like to support Black businesses whenever possible.

As three starts indicate my experience was A-OK. Some of the other reviews stated that it was a chain restaurant, I did not know this. The place had more of an authentic feel then the typical american-mexican dive. Experience was good, the only reason I wouldn't return is because I did not care for their salsa. It had a chipotle flavor that I just wasn't a fan of. 

I was pleased that the food wasn't served with the typical over abundance slop of beans and rice. All of our plates had a neat serving of rice and beans that were delicious. I had the carne asada tacos, the meat was very tender, with lots of fresh cilantro. The food would have been more impressive if it was served with some fresh pico de gallo. The tacos felt like they were missing something.

In a lot of other places, Tietra would be a solid option for Italian.  However in a city where BYOs and gastropubs have pushed the bar of culinary expectations into the stratosphere, it is mediocre at best.  A few specific observations:

1. I ordered a bottle of 2009 Rioja Vega Tempranillo and the waiter brought out a 2011 Opera Prima.  He showed it to me without comment, as if it was exactly what I ordered.  When I called him out on it, he stammered for a bit and admitted that they were out of the Rioja Vega and he would give us this one for the same price.  Keep in mind, the replacement was 2 years younger and of lesser quality.  Admittedly, neither of these wines are top end, but this was not a simple case of a mistake...either he or the sommelier were clearly trying to pull a fast one on us.  I can absolutely understand being out of something, but to simply select another bottle for me without asking or even informing me is inexcusable.  At this point everyone in our group was tired and hungry so after a short argument I just went with it, assuming he would comp us something in good faith.  How naive I was!  To put the cherry on top, when he opened it he didn't even give me the option to taste...he just poured everyone full glasses immediately.

2. The Seafood Rose actually tasted better than what I was expecting, although it clearly looked like it had been under a heat lamp for awhile.

3. After the meal, we got a full-push sales pitch on their desserts (not unexpected) and their happy hour specials (unexpected and weird).

This all might be acceptable in Pennsyltucky or the Jersey Shore, but not Center City Philly.  Foodies expect better quality here in both service and taste.  I found it ironic that it is situated next door to Applebee's, as one would be correct in thinking its level of quality as an equivalent to an Olive Garden.  I get the sense that they realize their mediocrity, as the prices are relatively low...you just have to know what to expect when you go in.  With that said, there's too many other better options within just a few blocks to settle for that.

I wouldn't recommend ordering delivery from here - have no idea how dining there would be. We ordered the Vegan tofu scramble and seitan wings, and both were cold and hard.

Everything was separate in the scramble for some reason? The potatoes and tofu and peppers were all in their own corners of the delivery box. The potatoes seemed like they'd been taken from the freezer and then defrosted on the ride over.

The most edible thing was the bread we didn't realize we were getting. Have no idea how any of this was cooked, it's kind of fascinating, but also a waste of $25.

Visited this place a few days ago... The store is new, so very clean....the person that greeted me, upon finding out it was my first visit, proceeded to attack me with the menu (which is very confusing by the way, the menu that is) I studied the menu as the best I could wile being under fire from the woman body slamming my ears with her interpretation the menu. I decided to go with what I know under the stressful situation and order a steak and cheese, I asked if they had jalapenos, they where out! Man, I love a steak and cheese with jalapenos. After the transaction I asked if they had a menu that I could take, so I can be ready the next time... They did not! After waiting a long wile my sandwich showed up, and I went home. When I got to the sandwich it was still hot, I only live a few blocks away....and sad to say....it SUCKED! They use red onions,that must of been old, cause that is all i could taste! I ate half, and tossed the rest out! I also had a mac and chee... It was bland, but my son liked it.... I doubt i will ever visit that place again!

Came to visit my friend in Philadelphia .my friend took me to here and here was very crowed with long line . Looked  menu they have a lot of choose for you like hard ice cream with many favor and soft ice cream , water ice ...they have parking lot near by ...I order hard ice cream coffee with corn sugar and my friend got water ice watermelon....my ice cream just ok ...and my friend love it ... 
    Price not real bad $7.50 for small ice cream and water ice ...my friend must  take 3 days for finish it ....
  Any way we had fun and enjoy the weather ....maybe I will try other option next time when I come back in here ....just don't let me down

I recently moved to AZ and was having A/C problems.  I originally took my car to a different garage and after a few follow up visits to fix the A/C problem, I decided to try Milex Auto Service.  The employees are extremely professional and more importantly my car was fixed right the first time.  I have had several bad experiences in the past with garages around the country, so needless to say I am skeptical about most car repair shops.  I was extremely satisfied with the service, feel comfortable with the employees, and would recommend them to family and friends.  They also provide shuttle service and military discount.

I am a regular customer at Guadalajara Grill, however the owner has never once acknowledged me or my guest. Instead, when I asked about the .69 cent sur charge, he rolled his eyes in discuss at me.  That's not very professional! He has fantastic server's and the food is good, but the owners behavior is very unprofessional. This is the only restaurant that I have visited that charged a COVID 19 sur charge!

We had our daughter at Right Steps for almost 2 years and our infant son for several months. We were extremely sad when it came time to leave due to relocating out of the city. 

Unique aspects to Right Steps that weren't offered by other daycares:
- indoor playground
- on-site chef that provides breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snack!

Would absolutely recommend if you are looking for a reliable center in the city.

This is a beautiful gem nestled in Havertown. I shouldn't write a review because I am selfish and really don't want anyone else to know about it. 
Everything was exquisite.

I would have given them two stars. However, my server Dana was really nice. We had the St. Louis barbecue dinner and the lump crab fries. The ribs nor the fries were enjoyable  I wish the taste match the presentation. We also had a sangria and a root beer to drink.  Those were really good

Food is the worst Chinese I have ever had. Been eating Chinese food for over 60 years and this was the 1st time I could not finish my order due to it's lack of quality. Do yourself a favor and avoid this place. Not good!

The food is ok. But the issue I have is the management staff.  In particular, one manager.  I did a takeout a while ago.  When I got home, the food was cold. I live 2.8 miles away.  I immediately brought it back.  Not for a refund or replacement.  Just to let them know.  The managers first response to me was.  "It was  hot when it left here." I couldn't believe my ears.  We went back and forth for a minute, and then I left. 

The next day, I spoke to one of the partners. He was aware of the situation.  And said he had spoken to the manager.  But my takeaway is that this is not the first time they have heard this about this person.  

I left feeling, at least I said my peace. I've been back a couple of times. Including taking my mom there when she was here for Christmas.  But I guess you can tell that it still sits in my craw.

Called in am order last Sunday for some appetizers to take to a dinner party.  Was excited when she told me they had new ones.  I ordered the trio which would come with pretzel bites mozzarella sticks and cauliflower and an extra order of pretzels .I arrived to pick them up and they weren't ready yet so I waited for a bit no biggie but I was running a bit behind.  Got to my party only to find out I did not get what I ordered and instead got the extra order of pretzels and the tiniest container of calamari??? Not only what not what I ordered but way under what I paid for ..nowhere near comparable.  I called and talked to the girl that helped me and she asked if I wanted to come back and get my correct order..nooo I'm all the way across town at this point so she says she will refund it...took my number and the last four of my credit card and said she'd call back in an hour or more.  Two days went by and no call no refund no apology either by the way.  I decided to send an email maybe a manager will get and reply to my issue.. it's been almost a week and no word from anyone .. this is crap service and we have already had issues here and we're giving it another shot.  Pretty much out of chances at this point.  As much as I wanted to support this local business they don't seem to want it ..very sad.

Great food great service....worth the wait!!!  It was about 2 hours, but a great place to walk around and shop while waiting for a call!

The hush puppies are amazing and the drinks are good.  Great atmosphere!!

I love Wawa, but this Wawa is definitely subpar. While the flaws at this location are only highlighted when it is crowded, I would say it is crowded 90% of the time. 

The first thing one will notice when it is crowded is that there is no where to park. Then when you get inside the Wawa it feels very cramped with everyone standing around the ordering screens. This location never seems to have enough people on staff. Now I understand that when the Wawa is not crowded, it is much more tolerable; however, other locations have more parking, space, and staff to assist with the lunch and after school rush - something the Yardley Wawa has not seemed to pick up on. 

However, I will continue coming here to celebrate Hoagiefest and get one of the delicious hand-spun Milkshakes (although they have been unable to make them the last two times I was there [weekend of 6/16]) and patiently wait for another location to open up nearby.

The food was mediocre for us. We are big steak fans, frequently visit Flemings and Ruths Chris but I wouldn't say we are sneak snobs. We love a well cooked tender steak, well my queen cut prime rib was dry and tough- not the sort of mouth watering prime rib you can cut with a butter knife. Plus they had no sauteed mushrooms, I find it strange that a steakhouse would not offer mushrooms. The nachos were literally just store bought chips with a sprinkle of shredded cheese. The portions are big so props on that, and the salad bar was yummy, I especially liked the ranch and the salad toppings, but we didn't drive 40 minutes to a steakhouse and pay $80 for a salad bar, plus our server forgot the rolls and I had to keep asking him for refills.

The music was so incredibly loud that it literally gave me a headache! The band was good and live music is usually really enjoyable, but the volume was absolutely insane. 
The chips and salsa were okay. Nothing great, but not terrible. It was the same for the fajitas and veggie burrito. Just okay. There are better Mexican restaurants around, so I probably won't be back.

I wanted to give this place 4.5/5 stars, but was unable to.  Overall for the price I paid on the 16 oz hot coffee, I was satisfied.  The coffee tasted good, the owner guy was friendly and courteous and the coffee shop had a quaint decor to it, which made it a chill place to relax in.  I'd like to return in the future!

I judge cafes on their coffee and Java is not high quality from what i tasted. I asked for a latte and got an under extracted coffee topped with mostly foam. Java needs to teach its "barista's" how to make espresso/lattes etc.
I dont think Java wants to be defined by its coffee but by being a nice place to hang out which it is. I thought the staff were nice enough, the food ok without being fantastic. Head a few blocks to The Crux if you want better coffee.

The pizza was great even if the crust was a little burnt. We really enjoyed the bread basket variety as well. but the pasta portions were disappointingly small. Although they were rich, they should be about 33% bigger, especially the ravioli. Service was friendly but a little slow considering the restaurant was mostly empty. This place is a little out of the way so I wouldn't rush back, but if I return I will stick to pizza.

I've eaten here a few times before and have had pleasant experiences in the past. However, the staff is not all too friendly and the food isn't always great. This last experience was the worst however. We ordered three pounds of crawfish and they were BLAND and not even served hot. They needed a little seasoning on them, they were just too plain for me. We noticed they had to start telling customers they were out of crawfish while eating, and while we were leaving, we overhead the staff saying they had a little more crawfish left (talking about ours left over on the table).. This is unacceptable in a restaurant and will not eat here again.

Great place to have a party they have two different rooms that both hold a decent amount of people. They are pretty cheap as far as pricing for catering food and don't have a room charge which is hard to find. Staff is friendly and they were really accommodating. Will be back for sure!

My girlfriend and I came to Philly on a weekend, and found that this is one of the few bubble tea place around town. the quality of the tea is not bad, and the location was convenient. it is a great place to just chill and hang out with friends.

Wow, we had a really bad experience here! We waited for over an hour for our dinner. No extra bread was brought, no one asked to bring more drinks, we just sat. We asked the servers what was happening, they wouldn't give a clear answer, they just said, the food takes 15-20 minutes.
