When the food did finally arrive, we asked about the missing naan and were given no answer. The naan never arrived. We had to walk to the counter to get a bill and we had to explicitly ask to get the bread taken off. Then we were vastly overcharged, an oversight that was corrected but was really the icing on the cake. The food here is just fine, but this experience was unacceptable. At least they could have apologized, brought some more bread, or offered drinks. It was really disappointing.

This is the perfect Chinese buffet restaurant for someone who has never had solid foods.
Or used anything but salt, pepper and Old Bay.

Blah food. Blah I say!
I went through 4 dishes while there. Not because I ate that much, I actually went away hungry. No...it was because the dinner of the last people who ate off them was STILL ON THEM.
Seriously...you can get just as good Chinese food in a soul restaurant or a Poboy shop.

Heck, the Opelousas street grocery store does better.

I can't say enough about this restaurant, food and staff. It is amazing, phenomenal, superb. It has some of the best tasting food I've had so far.  I am sad that I am so far away from it, but so glad that my husband and I experienced it for his birthday.  Morgan was very attentive and knows what to suggest! We will go back one day! Keep up the excellent work!

I really was looking forward to having First Watch--it's definitely been a few years--and my first at this particular location. I remember liking them in the past, but after today's experience, I might choose a different breakfast spot. We ordered online for pickup.

There were so many good-sounding items to choose from, I had a hard time deciding. I opted for the chickichanga since it sounded flavorful and something different than you find on other breakfast menus. My husband ordered the pumpkin pancake breakfast. We also got a side of the million dollar bacon.

When it arrived, it looked great...except the fruit "bowl" had two blueberries and three small pieces of pineapple. The potatoes were extremely salty, and that's all you could taste. They were cooked perfectly, crispy on the outside, so it was really disappointing. The main entree was good. Nothing special enough for me to order again.

The bacon was okay. It was spicy and maple syrupy :)

But all in all, it wasn't good enough for me to choose again when we have other breakfast options in the area.

Best BBQ in Philadelphia. The buffet on mondays are unreal. Self serve yourself mouthwatering ribs, brisket, pulled pork, etc. All choice cuts & amazing quality. A little light on options for dessert but I'm usually too full to worry about that by the end of my meal. 10 out of 10 for their food & service. Never had a bad experience

We have had a membership here for close to two years, and visit frequently, (about twice a month). Both my children and I love their dinosaur exhibits, and their Outside In exhibit on the top floor which includes an area where kids can look at nature items under a huge microscope, a beehive with transparent walls to watch the busy bees work, and a sand area where kids can dig for shark teeth among many other fun activities. Their docents are friendly and seem excited to talk to my children. They also have a cafe on the first floor, where guests can enjoy foods they've brought from home or enjoy very reasonably priced options provided by 12th Street Catering. Definitely call in advance to find out when their auditorium presentations are, which are worth timing your visit around. Also be aware that special exhibits and the very cool butterfly room cost extra.

Nice environment and I was seen right away but the young lady who did my pedicure didn't really seem like she had much experience doing pedicures. I felt like I simply got my feet washed instead of scrubbed.  She did a good job painting my toenails though however I will not be returning.
Stopped in to get dessert after lunch and picked up an eclair and Napoleon.   Extremely clean shop with helpful staff.   Very nicely made desserts with a home-made taste.
A budget Chinese buffet not too far away from town. Everything you'd expect for a Chinese buffet. They did have lobsters though. :P

Me and my sister were very excited to try this restaurant. Unfortunately, even with reservations, it took 40+ minutes to receive our food, once receiving the food, the salad was fine, both plates of fries were cold and one of the burgers was cold. The cold burger was removed from the ticket, but not by our waitress. She had only seen us once to take our orders and never revisited our table. Other waiters came and helped us out once we had gotten our food. All and all and ok visit, probably won't return.

This used to be a good location but it has gone horribly down hill.  Firstly the washroom is beyond disgusting. I've been to cleaner campground outhouses. Second they got my order wrong and overcharged me twice. Third the napkin dispensers were both completely empty. Third the fries were way too salty.   I should qualify this review by saying it was completely empty when I came and a bunch of employees just standing around. 

I mentioned the washroom and napkins twice but nothing was done during the 30 minutes I was there.

I drive to the southshore from Mandeville frequently solely for my Caffe Caffe fix! The chicken salad sandwich and chicken salad are my two favorites! I've tried many other things over the years too, and I never once have been disappointed! Wish y'all would come back to the northshore,  but until then, I'll keep making my little trip to my favorite lunch spot, Caffe Caffe!

They have the best tasting food.  The owner is very friendly, and always trying new things.  The restaurant is always clean, it bright and welcoming.  The price is great for quality of food you receive.  My family loves this place!

This place is awesome! Friendly staff, relaxed vibe, and great bar (with complimentary cocktail at check-in). A tropical storm blew into town during my stay and the team went above and beyond to ensure I was comfortable. Thanks Moxy- my time in NOLA was amazing and you were the cherry on top!

This a new store that took over the old Boston Market on Nutt Road.  I decided to give it a shot based on the current $10.00 promotion.  The store itself is cramped and, on my visit, the counter was strewn with paper receipts.  No complaints on the service.  

PH wants customers to know that know that they've made improvements to their crust.  If this is an improvement, I'd hate to know what it was like in the past.  I give them credit for being fairly generous with toppings but, tastewise, it's just bland, and the crust doesn't take well to an oven reheat.   

If it's a chain pie you crave, head out of town to Papa John's in Royersford or Trooper.

Tasty good-quality ingredients prepared in small batches that come together to create the rainbow of delicious flavors characteristic of good authentic Mexican food. This restaurant was opened by a former manager of Felipe's and you can see the similarities, but Taco Loco better preserves the flavor that Felipe's has lost. Has a large and customizable menu--there's something for every pallet. I highly recommend the veggies! Or go back again and again and try the ever-changing meat specials they keep coming up with. Has an intimate mom-and-pop feel that makes it perfect for a cozy lunch or dinner with a friend, family, or date. Also has a full-service bar with delicious margaritas.

My husband got the works, I ordered the tri-fecta, and brother ordered avocado toast. My waffle was the best waffle I've ever had. All of us could not get over how good it was. But they were fast, nice, and the food was so good. I couldn't get over how good it was! Please order the waffle!!!
This Pho restaurant is okay.  Positives the portion size was HUGE!!  Okay customer service.  Variety of bowls excellent!!! The taste was average.
Wow! I just walked out from this place after no one acknowledge us! There one bartender n no server? Is this even a brewery that serve foods? Terrible service. They need to step up their game!

Not the same place i remember as a kid. Fries were cold burger was overcooked. Service was really bad she forgot to give us condiments and straws and took forever to give us the check so had to go to the front counter. This was my second bad experience here in a row and last time there. Its a shame I remember the leo burger being the best of delco years back and now its awful

I came over here to meet up with a friend and her group of people recently.  I don't know if I'll be back anytime soon.  

The bar was completely packed on a Thursday night to the point where I was getting shoved every few minutes.  (It was a snow day so a lot of people had off work that day and Friday so I'm sure people were treating this as a weekend night.)  The crowd was full of girls in heels (really?  It's actively snowing, but sure wear your 6 inch heels) and guys in tank tops (again, really?!) all loud and shoving each other.  I think I'm just a little too old for these kinds of places anymore.  

We managed to grab one stool at the bar and ordered drinks.  Unfortunately, I had been drinking wine at the place before and didn't want to switch so we ordered wine.  I knew the quality was going to be nowhere near where it was at the other bar, but I did it anyway.  My wine smelled like eggs.  I'm not kidding.  There was a strong sulfuric smell everytime I went to take a sip.  I even let my friend smell it and she agreed.  

I might, MIGHT be convinced to come back for happy hour or lunch one day to try the wings, but I don't ever see myself coming back here later in the night ever again.

Very cute little establishment in the middle of Doylestown with some great food options! I'm an avid carnivore but the vegetarian portobello wrap caught my eye and it was pretty good. The four choice barbecue sauces offered really brought the wrap to a whole other level. My sister throughly enjoyed her California avocado burger that had a bun that was to die for. I'll have to try their signature BBQ options the next time I stop in!

Do not buy your Toyota from this location. When we purchased our brand new Highlander 2013, the deal was to get the extended maintenance service at every 5000 miles for up to 50000 miles. Clarification from the salesperson Brenda of ten free oil changes. When we brought it in at 5000, they said to bring it in at 10000 and now we're here at 10000, they are claiming there s nothing in the system at all at this point and there are going though a lot of changes so there is no way to check. We love Toyotas, but do not purchase from here.

I just moved back to the area after being gone for 4+ years and during my time away, finding a good nail shop was always hit or miss and even the hits sent you home with a miss now and then. I hadn't had a Mani/pedi since late August because I've been so turned off and skeptical of new shops. Well my nails looked extra WRONG so I decided to give Red Cat a go and I have NO regrets. 

Everyone was very nice and inviting. I like my gel manicure and my pedi and the girl who did them, Elisa (I believe), was amazing! She wasn't too extra and not too mute. I'm so satisfied! Man did I miss the east coast!!!

I'll definitely be back as a regular and I definitely recommend this place!

Update: although [I think] I will still recommend this place, I had to take away a star. I got my nails done on Saturday and they started chipping Monday, just like a lot of other reviewers mentioned. I've been getting gel manis for as long as I can remember and my nails NEVER chip. If they do, it's literally at least 2 weeks in when they need redone anyway. This is very disappointing. Another thing to note is that I went in for a gel manicure but my hands didn't take a single dip in any ounce of water & my cuticles are looking low key trashy. I'm annoyed. I'll go back once again and will deduct another star if this shit happens again.

It's delicious and local! 

This is some of the best ice cream base I have had in Indy, definitely the best in the Avon area.  The Frost Bite will definitely take you back to your childhood.  If you grew up in a small town like me, you relish the summer nights when you could drive to your local ice cream shop, walk up to the window, and everyone ate in the parking lot.  This place will definitely resurface those memories.     

Their frosts (Blizzard equivalent for all you DQ fans) are fantastic.  The prices are cheap for the size.  A medium frost is a large most places.  The sundaes and milkshakes are awesome too.  Insider tip...order a cheesecake frost with your choice of fruit topping = greatness.  

Next time skip the DQ and give the Frost Bite a shot.
Great food as always. Highly recommend the shrimp wrap as an appetizer and beef pho as entree
Great food, margaritas and service! Price is just right. Really good atmosphere. Homemade salsa and chips awesome! Best Mexican food in this area.

As a long time patron of Max Brenner, it was nice to make it back after a long absence. The huggable mugs of hot chocolate are perfect in December. Our table ordered two Mac & cheese burgers that were delicious and cooked to order. The fries were, as usual, amazing (seriously, 6 stars for the fries). I had personally ordered the veggie burger which was tasty and not too heavy, but the avocado that came with it did have some brown spots that were unattractive. The main criticism of tonight was our interaction with a hostess. Our waitress had been gone a while and our small water glass had been empty for a longer than usual time. Not seeing our waitress anywhere, we flagged down the hostess who came over and just bluntly told us we'd need our waitress to fill up our water and then walked away. She didn't offer to find her for us or even just grab the pitcher herself. If it's protocol to only have wait staff fill waters, that's fine, but the hostess easily could've been more helpful. We were taken aback by her abruptness and it sadly dampened our otherwise pleasant experience.

Surprising gem in Zephyrhills! Found this place while looking for a change from my normal sushi place and I have to say this place exceeded my expectations. The menu is extensive with plenty of choices both sushi, Japanese and Thai food options. I had the French roll which was a sushi roll with krab, avocado, fish egg, rice, wrapped in seaweed and a crepe, served with eel sauce, I loved it! I also got an order of the chicken teriyaki which was a huge portion and although the sauce wasn't exactly the teriyaki flavor I'm used to I enjoyed it. My friend got the chicken Thai curry and said it was very spicy just how she likes it! She also got the spicy tuna salad and it was fantastic. Overall definitely eat here if you are in the area and looking for some quality sushi, Japanese and Thai food :))

I would love to be able to give this place more stars,  but they just don't deserve it. The staff is nice enough,  but the baked goods just aren't good. I picked up $40 of gift cards for $20 and have had four visits so far trying different things. Cookies I've had: snickerdoodles,  sugar,  chocolate chip,  oatmeal raisin. Have had the brownies,  the chocolate chip cookie sandwich  and their one bar that's topped in chocolate with a peanut butter is cake type bottom. It's not peanut butter obviously since it's nut free,  but the texture reminds me of it. 

Have had a couple other things but they just werent memorable enough to remember. I don't know if it's their base flour or what but everything I've had reminds me of what non-glutards think of gluten free baked goods. Every cookie has had a dryish cake like texture to it along with a bland overall flavor. Nothing like the gluten filled equivalent. The gluten free chocolate chip cookies from Trader Joe's are much better. The brownies from Amaranth at the Bryn Mawr farmer's market are better. They make some nice breads too,  and you can pre-order things and save some money too. 

What this bakery does is make me miss the Main Line Bakery that was at the Wynnewood train station. The original owner was overwhelmed and she sold it. The new owner was overwhelmed and had the family chip in until they couldn't do it anymore. The recipes were phenomenal. Had a chocolate cake from there for my b-day party and not one person guessed it was gluten free. And her recipes were created out of necessity due to her kids' allergies. If someone could get their hands on them, they could completely overshadow Sweet Freedom. My mom's own experiments have faired better than everything I've had from here. She's always playing with different flour combinations, and I don't mind being the Guinea pig. I've made better cookies from scratch too. 

Lastly,  they are just overpriced for what you get compared to other bakeries I have tried and again, makes me miss the Main Line bakery. My one friend went to SF  to get a pie,  and I think they mentioned it was somewhere around $40 plus for a pie. Last I checked,  the crust is the only thing different in almost all pies. The up charge compared to standard gluten pies from specialty bakeries is absurd. She was like,  I'll just make it myself. If I have to deal with these prices,  I'd rather venture into the city to PS &  Co. Every dessert I've tried there has been decadent. And I wouldn't have guessed it's gf, vegan and organic. I'll pay the higher prices when I know the ingredients warrant it. 

Maybe SF will step up their game  but I don't see much competition in the area so I don't see that happening. Have about $4 left on the gift card. Maybe I can try a standard double chocolate cookie next time. Something else they can't seem to make. Every time I go the chocolate cookie has something added... Mint,  cayenne. Ugh... But I can order them. I wouldn't order anything I  quantity unless I could taste it first. 

I'd give it 1* on the baked goods alone. But the people that work there seem nice. The place is clean and kept well. I'd try a smoothie or something else,  but everything else they carry I'd rather make  at home.

We don't go to Costa Vida too often, but when we do, it's this location as it's closer to where we live.  The food is always fresh and delicious, and the prices are reasonable.

Our family of 4 stopped in here on Monday about 45 minutes to closing, and ordered a full meal, drinks and dessert.  Admittedly we were probably annoying as customers - all of us talking, indecisive, asking a bunch of questions, just all over the place.  But the staff here rolls with it, makes sure you know your choices, explains the different between beans, answers questions, etc.  It seems like they have a lot of staff on hand (which is better than being noticeably short-staffed).

The food was delicious, they give you plenty of food, and free drink refills.  I wish some sour cream came included with the meal, but any sour cream costs extra.  Their key lime pie is REALLY good, their flan is a little less firm than I would like it to be, but it's still pretty good - both with a significant about of cream on top.  The horchata wasn't very good and I don't remember that from past visits.  It was weak/watered down, so I dumped it and got a soda instead.

Still a fan though.
Great seafood. Good oysters. Wonderful atmosphere. Great 2 for 1 deals on mixed drinks -ask server.

Easily this experience was a meh for me. We ordered two bowls here and some donuts. It seems this place is more for the bread fried chicken sandwiches which we were really not in the mood for. Our bowls were quite simplistic and did not show any depth of flavor. A kid can whip the bowls up in a second. So easily non inspiring without a doubt.

The donuts afterward took the yucky flavor out of my mouth afterward. I had tasted my hubby's bowl with the mustard type BBQ sauce eww! So the donuts helped it a lot.

I like the area where it's situated bc inside and out is a pretty cool set up. If I were to come back, I'd order from Fresh Kitchen because I love their bowls and maybe, maybe come back for donuts.

Beautiful interior and nice outdoor seating. Sadly the food is lackluster. I've had pizza a couple times and both were dry and lacking in flavor. My companions had a rubbery steak and a panini with the hardest bread ever last night and each dish left much to be desired. Honestly, the tater tots are the best thing going. The service is always outstanding and they truly employ some kind people. I enjoy them immensely. I'd like to see Binkley's up their cuisine because it's is otherwise a solid spot for dinner and drinks.

I recently decided to go go this brunch spot and by the time I got a table it was lunch but when I got there I order lemonade and it was all sugar I order some grits and they looked like burnt sponges. The only nice place was the bathroom because it was were I sat and crapped myself after this food. I was with my nephews and the waiter asked if they wanted tequila shots. This Is outrageous behavior from such a nice restaurant in a nice area

Really far away from the airport. Ghetto. Old beat-up trailer with nowhere to sit while you wait. And you will wait a long time because the customer service is slow and barely competent. Cranky old lady who doesn't want to deal with customer questions. Scuzzy. Ran out of cars and downgraded us. But didn't make a price adjustment. Whatever. We just wanted to get out of there. Obviously.

2.5 Stars.  I shop at the Publix in the same shopping center, so I decided to try Jade Garden.  The front door was propped open, and it's clearly because there is no air conditioning.  It would be way too hot to eat inside the restaurant.

They still have their COVID-19 plexiglass precautions all set up, and the women working the counter behind them was very unfriendly, to the point of being rude.  I ordered off of their "house special" section of the menu, and got the General Tso's Chicken.  It was described as chunks of chicken sautéed with broccoli in a spicy sauce.

I got home, opened the container, and actually laughed out loud.  It was these fried nuggets in a greasy orange sauce, with a total of 2 pieces of broccoli.  I know there were exactly 2, because they placed them right on top so you could see them.  For all the greasy sauce, there was zero flavor, and zero spice. This place was a one time and never again for me.
Poor layout! Needs more eating establishments/coffee stops.  Stairs are awkwardly placed and too spaced out! Not my favorite!

The food was ok, I order the crawfish, shrimp and crab, everything was so small the it was a lot of work to eat it and very little food after finish cleaning it. the potatoes In my food wasn't cooked,the service wasn't that great either, I have to ask for all the esencial to eat my food.

Love the food but the woman with the short blonde hair made me and my lunch date very uncomfortable. She kept giving us very rude stares and would just stare away each time she passed us up or sat down, not a very professional thing to do. And makes the dining experience unpleasant

I heard two great reviews about this place from people I know.  One of them decided to invite me.  

The style of Chinese restaurant made this place different.  The food was ok.  The best food item was the fried wonton strips.  I had the moo-shu chicken which had 4 Chinese pancakes (looking like flour tortilla to me).  I did not care for the sauce (maybe plum sauce).  The menu stated Chinese mushroom (unlike most of the other descriptions) so I hoped they were shiitake mushrooms, but they were portabella mushrooms.  The size of the entrée was big.  

My entree' came with a few other items.  They offer hot tea but it tasted a little watered down; I am not a fan of tea anyways.  The egg drop soup was alright but was not as yellow as I have seen them.  The rice come in a small container for the whole table.  We were given each a fortune cookie.

I tried the other dishes that the other people ordered but did not find them outstanding either.

I was in town visiting family and we were looking for an adult night out. Eventually came upon this place and it was a night to remember. Great crowd, live music, large dance floor. At the bar, Sarah was AWESOME, even helped me carry multiple shots over to our table. 
I don't see myself making the 11 hour drive to Boise very often, but I know where I'm going to get a drink when I do!

My fiancé and I (both pianists) have been in here twice and both times we had the place to ourselves and without a doubt, this is the kind of place I wish all restaurants could be.  1.) Simple menu.  I hate overly-complicated menus with 100's of variations of the same dish, all done with extreme mediocrity (think Cheesecake Factory).  Each item is unique and well executed!  We have had the Chicken Kiev, Chicken Kebabs, Cornish Hen, and Lamb Kebabs as entrees, and we've had the carrots in milk sauce, rice pilaf, Crimean potatoes, and mixed veggies as sides.  Of course, as recommended by everyone, the Napoleon dessert is to die for.  2.) The food is very good and fresh.  Nothing seems "packaged" or "frozen."  Simple ingredients, well prepared.  Honestly, it should ALWAYS be that simple but few restaurants do it.  3.) Great atmosphere!  This is not the place for loud kids.  This is the kind of place for adults who enjoy peace and quiet, and a place to actually enjoy a conversation with your date or just enjoy the live music.  4.) Gregory enjoys playing for his guests and it shows.  The last time we were there he had already left - slow night and they were closing up when we walked in - but they took us right on in and Deanna (Greg's wife) assured us that they would be happy to fire the kitchen back up for us.  We invited her to sit with us and we all had a wonderful conversation and it felt like we had all known each other for years.  Gregory later returned just to greet us before we left!  What more could you ask for!?  Diana has always had good wine recommendations for me too.  We will be driving all the way from Greenwood to come back for regular visits.  Places and people like this deserve our support!  It's everything all restaurants SHOULD be!

Let me first just say that when I enrolled my kids in jiu jitsu I had very specific goals in mind, I wanted them to avoid physical conflict if possible and learn what to do if they had to use it.  I realize after having kids in classes at Miloki Flow for the last three years that my standards were too low as they far exceeded them.  My kids routinely practice respect, manners and helping others in addition to what to do if they are disrespected.  The instructors are approachable, kind and thoughtful in what they teach and practice with the kids.  The kids think classes are fun because kid approved games and techniques are built in to each class so they have a blast.  Signing my kids up was one of the best things I could have done as a parent and we plan to continue for many years to come.
The location on the east side of Indianapolis give wings a bad name. And the service was the worst.

I've come to this establishment once before during the evening time to watch sports which is what this place has to offer. After all, it is a bar. So no complaints there. But, I recently visited for Sunday brunch and had a underwhelming experience.

Typically, I'm a brunch snob so I should have known better; however, a friend vouched for them and they offer bottomless mimosas. There were about nine of us which is a fairly large party but the place was nowhere near busy. Our food took long enough to come out to the point where we noticed. And when it did, one person's order was not complete, another's eggs were cold and my omelette wasn't fully cooked where part of the whites were still clear which I'm not a big fan of. I did eat all the breakfast potatoes which were delish. A couple of my friends ordered mimosas which they mentioned were pretty strong (again, it's a bar so I would hope so) but after the first one, they ran out of orange juice. They offer another drink called Black Velvet (Guinness and Champagne) but were out of Guinness.

All and all, it was just basic tasting. I would probably come back. Not for brunch but to drink at the bar and watch sports (there's tons of televisions).

Do not stay here! Save the money and sleep in your car. That's what we ended up doing anyway.. When we first checked in it took over 30 min. It wasn't completely the girl behind the desks fault because someone should have been there to help her. However when i tried to pay my $528 my card was being declined. So i paid $300 cash and put the rest on my card. JM who was the girl upfront told me i didn't give her $300 cash. I counted it twice but she told me it was only $287 so i gave her another $13 dollars.. Again my cards aren't going thru even though the money is on there. By this time is was because she tried to slide it 4 different times. So my sister pays for the rooms with her card. The first one went thru and the rest we're declined. For some reason we had to do each room separately even though it was all on the sane card. So by this time i asked for my money back so i could figure it out. When i finally got the money back the envelope was over by $13 you can call that an accident if you want. I asked to speak with a manager so she gives me his email. I called my bank and went in to check in. After another 20 min we finally checked in. JM shorts me on my change for a SECOND time and we go up to our rooms. They smell HORRIBLE. there are stains all over the carpet and the sink is so rusty and not even wiped down. We complained about the smell in one of the 3 rooms and again all we got was an email address. We weren't even offered to move a room. It deserves 0 stars

Very good cheap mexican food. Better tacos are hard to come by. 

Very friendly family run staff. 

There are a lot of bad mexican food places to get trapped into in Tucson, Rosas is not one of them.

We were hoping for another great NOLA meal, the line out the door showed we were not alone. Unfortunately, everyone's order had something messed up. Food alright and the service was good but it all came from the kitchen in pieces and as stated all 5 of us had one or more missing or additional sides. I enjoyed the green beans and biscuits.  Not what I was expecting from a NOLA legend. Our waiter got the rating an extra star.

I was so excited to have a cozy spa day on a recent visit to the Philladelphia but unfortunately picked the wrong spot. For a hotel gym it is great but the spa part needs to fall off of the listed amenities. It is a gym where you can also get a massage. Excited to relax I arrived an hour early for my service with visions of comfy chairs, a hot glass of tea and good magazines to thumb through. Upon arrival I checked in at the front desk. My reservation was found no problem but when the nice guy confirmed my appointment time a woman behind the desk cackled loudly " Why You Here So Early?! Now You Have To Wait an Hour!!" uh oh. This same woman was instructed to take me back to the locker room and show me the waiting room. On our way to the locker rooms she suddenly stopped? Ah...an episode of One Life to Live had caught her attention on the gym's flat screen. " Just a minute!" she says as I stare at her. Once we arrived  I felt like I was in the locker room of a nice 24 Hour Fitness. "Fine" I thought this is a gym but the spa area must feel different. No. The waiting room is a very small section of the locker room separated by an Ikea curtain. The chairs inside are stiff and remind me of something one would find on the opposite side of the desk in most middle management corporate offices. No tea to be found but they did provide a plastic Britta full of water that I could indulge. Massage was great so if my expectations were different this review would have likely been 3-4 stars.

I'm really sad to be doing this but I'm going to knock them a star. I really loved their food but the few times I've been here I have had less than average service. I know I'm still giving them 3 stars, but it's PASTARIA! They really have the ability to be a 5 star in my book so it makes me sad. Overall I think that the last couple times the service has been bad honestly. Our last server was incredibly rude and seemed like he would rather do anything else but serving us. I was also disapointed because I suggested Pastaria to a friend and her husband and they had a very similar experience. They loved the food, but the service was really lacking. I'm hoping in my future visits that it is not like that!

This is my probably my favorite makeup brand. I am trying to get all of my make up, hair and body care to be animal friendly and this brand does not test on animals.This bare minerals reminds of me a small boutique store. The layout is pretty simple but it has such a nice look to it!  I have come in the past to get my favorite lipstick but sadly they discontinued it, but on my last visit the friendly staff was able to help me pick the best foundation and setting powder. 
The products can be pricey but it's well worth it. I recommend coming here over Sephora, for the bare minerals products!

First time here. Guy that took my order seemed super overwhelmed. Maybe he was new? My 9 yo twins wanted spaghetti so I asked his opinion on portion size. He said they'd pry be better off with their own dishes, so I went with it. Their plates were HUGE (smalls). I didn't appreciate the dishonest suggestion or maybe lack of knowledge. We also ordered a medium pepperoni pizza that my 12 yo shared with me. Took almost an hour. Plus there was a lack of communication bc some of their staff didn't speak English so when I went to grab "my food" he shook his head "no" bc they called two orders at the same time....they did however have quite a bit of people come in and out while we were there and it was between 245-345...so maybe that's good? No idea. Won't be back, though! Also, they Are no affiliated with Giovanni's in Orcutt!

Fried Flavors delivers on what it is! I decided to try this new spot, which took the place of Pecks's, to see if the other reviews were warranted. I can assure you, they were. I selected the cheesesteak and wings combo, my wife went with the fried shrimp and wings, and a fish sandwich for my son. Everything was cooked perfectly, with plenty of flavor. We also went with onion rings as sides for both her and I, but unfortunately they missed with putting her order in the bag. 3/5 for the service, in missing this one. 5/5 for the food.

We used a food delivery service, and Fried Flavors may need to update the descriptions for their sandwiches, as they all have ground beef as the description, even for fish and turkey. Otherwise, a great establishment, and we can't wait to give them another try.

UPDATE: Just visited again yesterday, and the food is still good. However, I don't recommend the gyro at this time. It's made with prepackaged slices of meat vs sliced of the cone, and no tzatziki sauce. It was a bit of a letdown.

I ordered a room here from Booking.com . I asked for a non smoking room. When I got there the Manager Erin had already processed my credit card which Bookings said wouldn't happen .When I opened the door to my room the cigarette stench almost made me puke. The Manager Erin knowingly put me in a smoking room knowing I requested a non smoking room, when I asked for a refund and that I wouldn't be staying there she said " oh well take it or leave it" and because I booked in less then 24 hrs I wouldn't get my money back. I still have not received my money back and probably never will. Do not book here online they're shady as feck. I'll be reporting them to the Better Business Bureau.

6/20/19 UPDATE:  DO NO USE BIG EASY LANDSCAPING-HE IS SCAM ARTIST AND WILL TAKE YOUR MONEY! So the saga continues with Big Easy Landscaping. We still do not have our money back from them even though he promised he would.  This despite our filing a complaint with the Attorney General's office and receiving assistance from them as a mediator.  Derek was contacted by the AG's office on 6/4/19 in an effort to try and settle this matter out of court.  Derek promised the AG he would refund us our money in exchange for removing our Social media posts.  He claimed he would have the $4500 in 15 days delivered to the AG in Baton Rouge.  We agreed.  He counter asking us to remove our posts first.  We declined that and he still agreed to have the money to the AG on 6/19/19.  Well, he did not deliver.  We did not get our money yesterday nor did he call the AG with a reason as to why.  I am sure he will call in a day our two and say either his dog died again, he or a family member were in the hospital.  See, this is his normal MO.  We will be filing a complaint today with the Secretary of State regarding his licenses.  We have also been contacted by the LA Agriculture Department to investigate him AGAIN as he does not have a horticultural license in the state of LA.  
Since April, I have been contacted and am talking to 7 people who are having the same issue.  
He low balls the estimate, takes a small deposit and never comes through with his end of the contract.  Do yourself a favor, us another contractor even if the cost is higher. 
This is a man that will lie to the LA Attorney General's Office-Do ya really want to do business with him?

My friend thought that Nora's would be an excellent alternative to our Jim's cheesesteaks since she is a vegetarian. The tacos are extremely cheap and the food looked amazing. My friend asked to get a quesadilla, even though it is not an option on any of their menus, with a small side of rice and beans which is $2. With her quesadilla, she wanted cheese, lettuce, sour cream, and pico de gallo inside. After THIRTY minutes of waiting for her food, we took her food to-go, since the rest of us were eating cheesesteaks, so that we can eat at Jim's. 

Once she opened her box to eat, she realized that her quesadilla had nothing but cheese. On top of that, the cheese looked like it had been thrown on the stove for about 10 seconds. The cheese wasn't melted and the tortilla was barely cooked. My friend is not problematic so she just tried to make the best out of it and eat it anyway but since the cheese wasn't even melted, it wasn't appetizing. For $7 (price of the quesadilla), that was not acceptable. Especially when we had ordered, there had only been a couple behind us, so they were not at all busy or should have had trouble with her order, since it did take 30 minutes to make.

We decided to return so that we can work some kind of solution out since my friend had spent money on food, yet was still hungry. We politely told the woman who had took our order that the order had been messed up and asked if she could either get a refund or an entirely new one done correctly. The first thing the woman had said was, "Well, you ate half of it." She did not care AT ALL that they had initially messed up. We were very shocked that she reacted this way since we didn't want any problems at all but she had acted as if it was our fault. We continue to tell her that we would either like a refund or an entirely new one because of this and after she talked to the only other worker behind her, she said they will make her a new one and took her box. Before she walked away, I had asked her if she needed to write down what my friend had initially wanted in her quesadilla since she just took her box and was about to walk away. She then RUDELY and sarcastically said, "Yeah I know, you already told me, remember." ???? I was so confused by this since if she already knew, why wasn't it done right in the first place?!

I completely understand when people make mistakes, we are all human. However, the way this woman handled the situation was absolutely unacceptable. I don't understand why she had an attitude with us when we just wanted to fix a mistake they had made for our friend. It was so difficult without it having to be at all.

I did not get the name of the only woman we had encountered, who took our order, but she does not know anything about customer service. I'm really upset that this had happened too, since this was my first time coming here, I thought it had a lot of potential. 
I would love to give this place 5 stars, however, after this experience, I will never return here again.

This was probably the most fun we had during our weekend in Reno... We came here on a whim, just looking for a bar to chill at... when we first walked in, it seemed a little crowded at the bar but the tables were all empty... we sat in a corner table just people watching... when the bar cleared out a little we decided to sit up there which made it easier for us to order drinks... The bartenders were totally entertaining... they did tricks cocktail style throwing bottles and shakers in the air and catching them... trying to teach us to pour three different shots from stacked shakers all at once... and they just kept us entertained the whole night.  They have great deals at the bar and different themed nights as well.

It use to be a go to but this time was awful. It smelled awful as soon as u walked in. Like trash and dirty sewer water.  Disgusting. It was probably the stack of dirty dishes at the bus boy station and the dirty margarita machines with a nasty color green liquid. We didn't even eat here. Health inspectors will have a field day! Be careful.

Great breakfast place, ive been a regular for years. Great food but I'm always surprised that a place with high quality food standards would have such bitter coffee. Its hit and miss as far as servers go. There's a couple older gals that have been there for years and honestly if i had a choice I'd rather not have them.

OK so here we go, been here a couple of times in the past must have been OK cause we,re back for an Brunch.  To start I will rate this on a basis of Denny's being an avg. standard fare.
I had ordered the Hash breakfast with 2 eggs,home fries and toast, my wife ordered the french toast with peppered bacon total around $19.88 we'll call it $20. So now we're comparing apples to oranges and BJ's should be a cut above.  I'll start with the Hash. Less then avg amount of meat and the potatoes were under cooked in fact they were just shy of being crunchy. The home fries were also the same texture as the potatoes in the hash.  I did like the eggs they were cooked exactly as requested.  The Sourdough toast was good, it was definitely a good brand. Now I'm use to having marmalade jelly on my toast  and when opening it literally ran out of the container so I opened another one in fact all 3 were runny so I looked  at the packaging and they were an off brand not your normal Smuckers or Knotts Farms.  How much more could that cost?  So my meal was below a Denny's breakfast. Now my wifes french toast I  know cause my wife didn't eat all of her meal so she gave me a slice. She had notice that I only ate 1/2 of my meal. I thought that a slice of her french toast would save the morning and placed it on my toast plate and used my fork to cut a piece to eat I actually had to use a knife to slice the french toast. The bread was either old or ?  My wife also complaint to me on how tough the French toast was but she said the peppered bacon was good.  So this is why I'm giving the rating I'm giving didn't meet the Denny's avg fare.

Terrible terrible terrible. Today is mother's day. We ordered by door dash at 4:13pm due to the Coronavirus. Spent over $67! 7pm rolls around, no food. We call and the staff say "we have a lot of tickets. Is that a doordash order?" We were door dash, so no apology, just deal with it. An hour passes and we call back, there is an automated system thats answers and hangs up on us after their recorded message. We call door dash and cancel the meal. 

Future reference: never tell a customer how many tickets you have. We don't care. Apologize and offer an incentive or a refund.  We WILL NOT BE ORDERING  FROM OUTBACK AGAIN!!! 

terrible service. PERIOD.

Never order from this place.  I placed an order through GrubHub, received confirmation and my PayPal was charged.   After an hour I called GH and they tell me the order was cancelled by the restaurant because too busy.  Never received an email or message and it could take up to 6 days to credit my PayPal account.  Never use GrubHub or order from this place.
Good pizza.  I think creating your own is the way to go and it is fairly priced.  Cheese bread could use more cheese though.

This mall was dying a slow death, like several others in Tampa before it. First came International Plazza, which gave e it .more competition,  but also added the stores and restraunts that payed the higher prices in International Mall,  only about a mile away.
But, just the opposite happened,  International Mall gave Westshore Mall the shot of B-12 it had been waiting for.
Within a short a.period of time all the empty stores were full and new restaurants sprung up in abundance.
Actually I see no competition between the 2 malls, one is all high end and the other ( Westshore) is a regular town type of mall. 
I love them both for different reasons, stores and restaurants.  Instead of them being in competition, I think International mall brought Westshore mall back to life and revitalized it, and trust me it needed it.
All of the new restaurants just make it all that much better. I now bieve Westshore Mall Is here to stay gor awhile, I had my doubts, but not anymore.
What's a girl to do? High end, medium and low end stores and all have their positive points, so just shop them all!

Bell's is a neighborhood tavern/ restaurant.  Walk in to the bar area (certified Guinness taps) and then to the dining area in the back.

Good blue cheese salad, and it's great they offer half portions of pasta.  I enjoyed my tortellini.  A bit loud, but a good dinner with two friends!

Great work on putting new heels on a pair of cowboy boots. May have been a little pricey but did a great job and quick. Can't speak for others experience with JAW shoe repair but mine wasn't bad. 

My recommendations to owner would be to organize and clean the front of the store to make it more customer friendly and treat customers items with more care. Lots of shoes just thrown everywhere gives a bad image to people trying to repair/care for their shoes. Attention to customer service could be used as well. A simple "welcome to my store" or "thank you for your service come again" would be nice. Also, revisit pricing to see if your in line with other companies. 34 bucks for two new rubber heels seem a bit high but then again I don't own a shoe repair shop. 

Overall, I would recommend giving these guys a shot. Hopefully, they will listen to constructive criticism because being one of the only shoe repairs in town they could easily become the go-to place.

I ordered a 3 pcs chicken through DoorDash and he he food cane hot and chicken he delivery was fast but the chicken was TINY! It was a wing, small thigh and small drumstick!! NOT WORTH THE MONEY! I paid $13 for 3 small pieces of chicken and a large mashed potatoes and gravy! I used to go to this Popeyes a lot since I know they're all locally owned but I won't be coming back to this one!!

The food was really amazing but even more then that was the owner coming out and chatting with us about fun things to do with our daughters. Italian is my favorite and I was so happy to find this place!!! Apparently Florida doesn't have water Haha
This place is abandoned and closed. It doesn't look like it's been operational for quite a while. The inside is gutted

Horrible parking arrangement obviously. I've come 7 times during their "business hours" and door is locked every single time. Called numerous times and only got someone on the phone once. I give up. Just bad business.

I used to live by Coffee X Change and yes I did frequent this place quite a bit when I first moved to Tucson but it was because I didn't know better?  Their coffee is always super sweet and I had to make sure they heard me when I asked for non-syrupy coffee drink.  I also find their punch card system ineffective, by punch card system I mean stapling the receipts to a piece of paper nine times - no credit for those receipts that aren't attached that that useless paper.  Really?  I don't understand what is so wrong about keeping the one punch card without those receipts?  I may be making a big deal over one small thing but I suspect that they want the benefit of offer their customer free drinks but make it nearly impossible for them to get that free drink - plain ridiculous.  So, I am giving Coffee X Change a 2-star rating for not so great coffee, inefficient service, and mediocre food.

Haven't been in awhile but we were so excited to actually get them but something def has changed. The people were fine and the wait was maybe 7 minutes for two breakfast bagel Sammies but the sandwiches themselves were average at best. Maybe we were spoiled from years of amazingness but the eggs and bacon in between were non existent. Boooo!!!

Gave then 2 chances
Service: I was on time first appt in the am, told to wait by the service persons desk, he was on hold on a call. I stood there for FIVE minutes, he never looked at me, spoke to me or acknowledged me in any way. AFter five minutes I walek away and asked for someone else.
Sales: Honda customer service told me to go into the dealership as my lease was close to the end to sort our financing. I went on a saturday(wasted a nice 1/2 day) and the salesman, who was obvioucly not happy I was not there to buy told me "No we dont do that you have to call Honda" so it old him I had and they had told me to go to the dealership,SO he went to get the finance man. His attitude was "we dont like doing that; it is expensive and a lot of work for us! I commented that I had wasted my time going to the dealership... his comment was "yes you did" and walked away.
So DOBBS HONDA you get my lousy rating for both sales and service.

I stopped in the other day for a carryout on my way home from work.  For $19 I got 6 pieces of a half & half: sausage/mshroom and artichoke/prociutto.  The quality was wonderful but the pricepoint was way too high.

I've been here twice. Here's how it went:
Happy Hour: crowded. slow service. but fries were yum diddly delicious. we got every single french fry offered. cheese did the weird gross thing when it gets cold and congeals. in like 2 minutes. that sucked. 

Random Friday night at 9pm: loud-ass music could barely make conversation which is weird because there were like 10 people in the bar, clearly not there to start a dance party. One waiter. So service was slow. Waiter tried to make up for it by being good looking.

While searching for takeout one lazy afternoon, we stumbled across this rare find in a shopping plaza off of 202. We were surprised to see some of our szechuan favorites in a place in Delaware and had to try it. And to our delight, the food was amazing. We have the fish fillet in hot chili oil, mapo tofu (lunch menu), and twice cooked pork (lunch menu) and everything was spectacular. The fish was spicy and tender, the tofu was flavorful and delicate, and the pork was succulent and satisfying. The quality and flavors match anything you would find in Chinatown and really makes this place standout from any takeout place in the area. I could not recommend the place enough.

I went with this driving school after reading all the reviews.. But, that was a waste of $80.. I had 1 driving class, that lasted almost 2 hrs, with Mike.. The only thing we did was driving around.. Only right turns.... No left turns, no parking, no reverse, no parallel parking... He didn't even tell me anything about the controls like light, windshield wipers etc... Also, he was on his phone most of the time.. If you really want to learn driving, please don't choose this school.. 
I'd say Mr.Jerry from Lebie Driving School (Upper Darby)  is the absolute best teacher you can have..
Eggs, toast, potatoes and French toast. Everything was mediocre at best I've had better but I've also had way worse. This was in walking distance from our resort. French toast was amazing!

Just got a blizzard. It's watery. I understand sometimes machines fail or whatever BUT WOW. I haven't even made it home and literally 3/4 of it is liquid. I could have gotten a pint of ice cream at Wal Mart for $4. Gets 2 stars bc the flavor is great. But it's a one star dessert.

Beware! I booked the October special for facial and massage through Facebook.
When I arrived I was told I didn't have an appointment.  They said they had my information but no appointment.  Front desk not apologetic at all and said these things happen.  I won't be back

Found this place because family and friends liked it.  It didn't disappoint, especially if you like the New Orleans feel.  Not all the dishes is Cajun flavor, but they have some of the fav. like jambalaya.  I really enjoyed the atmosphere, very relax and casual, good music in the back.  I personally like jazz music so everything kind of just fit for me.  The service was excellent along with the dishes I got to tried from my plate and others.  Have to say their salad actually got me hooked, if you like salads get Bleu Cheese and Walnut salad.  I ordered it with salmon instead of chicken.  Or get the Jambalaya Pasta, I subbed it with rice instead of pasta, just personal preference.  Only reason I gave it a 4/5 because steak option isn't that big.  But everything else is awesome.
Nice local beach in Carpinteria. Less crowded than most. We walked over here from our rental. Great little beach town feel with local campgrounds, restaurants, etc. near by.

I have stayed here a couple times for work meetings. The first time I stayed here (I have never stayed at an aloft hotel) it seems more like a trendy type of hotel, like one you would stay at for leisure as opposed for a little business meeting. Rooms seemed a little small but everything inside was nice. Have a nice little cafe type thing in lobby we're you can get snacks and drinks and breakfast in the morning.

Parking lot seems small just like everything else. Not a lot of things to do near by and is about twenty minutes depending on traffic from downtown. There is a decent place to eat within walking distance called Jonathan's. 

Like I said earlier I would stay here for a leisure/pleasure trip with friends. A little to small and trendy for families.

I was not happy with their cleanliness. I went there to get a manicure. The woman grab utensils out of her drawer which were not sanitized. Who knows how many people she has used those instruments on. Will not go back again. I don't want to get a disease.

Seriously the best sandwich in town?  Some people need to get out more or the employees are reviewing the place.  The bread was fairly good, but not a whole lot of meat on the sandwich, minuscule amount of dressing w/ strange after taste.  The bonus was the guy making it, if he forgot which state he lived in he could look in the mirror, oh how I love Florida face tattoos, way to rep. your state homie.

My experience there is that they were trying to squeeze every freeeeakin' dime out of me which I generally find ANNOYING. Stop trying to get me to upgrade to larger more expensive car unless you're going to give me the upgrade for free. And don't try to sneakily get me to sign up for prepay gas and charge me an extra $50 for it. 

In general if it weren't for the one manager-type guy who was trying to ease the pain I was clearly feeling with the guy "helping" me I would have walked out. 

Also if you come here, don't use your debit card. Use your credit card unless you walk in with utility bills and a pay check stub (supposedly). I had reserved it with my debit card and they were trying to get me to do pull up all this crap until I just whipped out my credit card.

Wow. Expecting so much. Chips nothing special. Three salsas meh. Tacos are what they are known for. Carne Asada. Dried meat and missing ingredients listed on menu. Wife could not eat her quesadillas. Guess we should not have come after Alinea. If you are in a fraternity or sorority you'll love it.

We're from Los Angeles and came here because it's close to the Nashville airport.  We had a few beers and also had 3 burgers. Everything was so damn good the burger were amazing!!!!! Must try if your in the area!

From a regular customer who even tips on takeout (never again): Food is good, but customer service needs improvement. The General Tso's chicken is usually at a 4 out of 5 level. The restaurant sent the wrong item for delivery and refused to provide any compensation for the inconvenience of waiting for a remake of the correct item, even when flat out asked. "Sorry, we messed up but we won't be doing anything about it." Incredible. They even asked me to return the item that was incorrectly delivered. Are they going to resell it? Isn't that a health code violation?

Demi was the bomb server! Took the time to explain the menu to us, answer our questions, and was upbeat and knew the company's story! 

Got a couple cocktails, they were heavy poured, which is always a plus! 

Had mac n cheese app, guac and chips app - both crushed it! 

Glad we avoided the almost 2hr wait at The Cheesecake Factory, lol!

I came here because Domilise's was closed on a Sunday (Domilise's has 4 stars vs. Parkway's 4.5 stars) and locals recommend this place so it was ok. I had an alligator gumbo, shrimp po boy and an Abita (local beer). They keep it simple here and they don't skimp out on flavorful shrimp. You can get either a regular or a large. I got the regular which was still big.Po boys I've had in the past were overdressed. The gumbo here was hot and packed with good flavor - it was a bit spicy and smokey. I wish I could bring a pint with me to Vegas. You can get either a regular or a large. 

There's plenty of parking space in the back. The atmosphere's laid-back. Our server was nice and provided pretty good recommendations. Our food came pretty quick. I was not disappointed at all.

Worst hotel stay I've ever had.   Management did absolutely nothing about the noise and disruption caused by the hockey teams playing hockey in the hallways or the continuous running and screaming to the point that the tv could not be heard nor could the workout facilities be used,  A three night stay from Thursday to Sunday chaos. Numerous complaints by a numerous guests with no resolution.  Granted the parents should have has better control but when they didn't Embassy Suites has an obligation to step in to correct the issue. They did nothing
I have stayed at Hilton properties a lot but never have I been more disappointed and  will not stay at this specific hotel again and may need to consider the brand of accommodation we use as a family

I was looking to get my car detailed and I'm so shocked. I called several places. Called  The BodyWorks Auto Body Repair. They said they could do it 5/19/16. When I got there they told me it would be a full day job from $150-$250. I said I thought it would be a couple hours? Why did you tell me you could do it today? I asked if he could do the interior only then. He replied not today. The owner there, (Mel) also was not friendly at all. Well, I lucked out completely. I looked at yelp reviews and called "Keep It Clean Mobile Detail Service" at (727) 851-4349.  I'm blown away. Herb from "Keep It Clean Mobile Detail Service" told me Trust me, your car will look brand new when I'm done with it. My car was filthy from living on the beach for 6 years and never having it detailed. It's a 2006 BMW 10 years old. I was ready to just trade it in. It took him about 1 1/2 hours and I can't believe how it looks. It looks brand new. The stains on the carpet are gone and they were really bad. It's perfect!!! I'm still shocked how he did it. Plus he's the nicest guy I've met. He comes to you in this cool painted van that's fully equipped to do everything. I mean everything. I just can't explain how great he brought my car back to brand new. The cost was only $75.00. What? Are you kidding me? Plus when others at our condo seen my car they were also shocked and wanted his card and business. I was so completely satisfied I gave him a extra $ 50.00 tip. So, take my word for it, call Herb at "Keep It Clean Mobile Detail Service" at (727) 851-4349.  Plus he does more than just detail.  I'm 100% satisfied and he will do all our BMW detailing from now on.

In town for an event and looking for a cheap, decent place to stay. I suppose it met those criteria, but I can't recommend it either. A first glance the room was decent, but it was filled with flies. I killed at least 15 the first day and another 5 or 6 the next. The shower was garbage, no water pressure and it would arbitrarily go between lukewarm and super cold constantly. Hallways smelled strongly of smoke and trash. Other than that it was what you'd expect from a 70$/night motel. If you must stay here don't pay anything above that. Last thing to note, the little connector road in the front of the hotel from the main road to the parking lot is super steep and if you have a low front end on your car it will bang it up every time. But you can also leave out the back through the shopping center to avoid this.
I really like the Pad Thai here. The sushi is meh. This place is more Asian pseudo than actually Asian food. But since their Pad Thai is filling

Great spot for Vietnamese FOOD!!!Delicious rice platters ! Love the custom dessert options. Service 5 stars -highly recommend for date night . Food temp always perfect. For me -  enjoyed ALL the  rice platters & pork chops. Didn't like the BBH - pho was just ok . Service solid perfect every time. Servers are very attentive. Cleanliness full 5 stars. My 4 stars rating is base on overall experience and taste of food.

This was a "meh" at best.

We had the BBQ sampler platter for two.  The brisket was dried out, like jerky.  The chicken was so dry it was almost grainy.  Ribs were OK.  Sausage was fair.  I could almost get past all this...but then the hammer fell.  THEY CHARGED ME FOR EXTRA BBQ SAUCE.  75 cents for a scant cup of it (less than 1 oz each).  So...$1.50 for BBQ sauce to try to bring the dry meat back to life.  

We had some mussels as an appetizer...they were great.  Other than that, fast food would have been a better choice.

This one is a HARD PASS if you like to eat good food.  Find some place better.

I would give them a minus 5 stars if I could...  Took my 2004 chevy cargo van into this store on wednesday 9/13/17 around 100pm for a smoke test for emissions.  Called on Thursday afternoon to check on status.  Was told mechanic just started on it.  I was called Thursday late afternoon that  they were having trouble finding leak but would keep trying.  I went in Friday afternoon, 2 and a half days later and still no progress on van.  I told them I would just have my driver driver pick up the van later that day.  I was NOT charged for repairs which made me suspicious in the first place they really didn't do any work on it.  When my driver picked up the van later Friday afternoon, he ran into the mechanic who supposedly was working on the van and he was told that they didn't get to work on the van because they "got slammed".  Part two which solidified my feeling that they didn't work on my van at all.  Two and a half days of a delivery van wasted due to very poor customer service.  If they couldn't do the work, just tell me!  I used to have Jack Furriers do all the work and tire purchases on all our personal cars and business vehicles but they just lost my business which shouldn't bother them because they are too busy to work on my van....

The Bad News: China Cottage Restaurant is too far from my side of town to eat here as often as I would like.
The Good News: This is the best Chinese restaurant in Nashville, even if it is in Rivergate. General Tso's Chicken, Orange Beef(the best I gave ever eaten), even the Pork Lo Mein was better than at any Chinese restaurant on the west side of town. Even the potstickers have a more delicious sauce than I have had in Nashville. Plus they offer Jasmine tea as well as green tea and oolong tea!  We were in the area, and I chose this restaurant based on reviews, but it's worth a drive to eat here again.

Friendly and attentive staff! Side Caesar salad ($3.50) was a decent size but had a bit too much dressing. Creole jambalaya ($10.95) was underwhelming with not enough meat. I really enjoyed the relaxed atmosphere and would likely come back just for that.

I'm very disappointed with this location it, I come from Hawaii where it's top quality, then to this location where the pizza is always soggy/under cooked in the middle and the crust burnt and wasn't even cut through to the slice. They not only took close to 3 hours to deliver a pizza it was the wrong order. Then I said I will come and get the pizza and it was soggy/gooey again. I called the manager and he said he would give me 15% off my next purchase, when they messed my pizza up, twice and made us wait 3 hours for it. Last night was just the final breaking point where we will not waste a single dollar again at this establishment.

We went here for dinner and was seated and placed drink orders.  We then chose the salad bar (something we come here for).  My husband and I order a tea with fruit flavor and ours came with fruit in it making it hard to drink with a straw.  Our daughter order a soda.  Our meals came out and she was offered a refill but we were not.  We just thought it was a slight on the waiters part.  She was very interested in selling gift cards to the tables and maybe should have been watching all the tables for service.  We finished and ask for a to go cup of the same drinks less the mashed fruit, and we got a kid cup to go. Shameful as these drinks were $3.79 each. Also as we sat there waiting to leave, we noted that several patrons took their dirty plate back to the salad bar for seconds.  ( it is stated on the bar to use a clean plate for seconds.)  A suggestion when the salad bar is ordered, the waiter should advise folks that a clean plate is to be used each time or bring the plates to the tables as needed.  The food here was OK, but some items were pricey.  Salad bar keeps us coming back.  I see it is indicated that I am from Calif. I live in Tampa, Fl.

I wish that I had read the prior reviews before giving this place any business. It is true that the staff is friendly and eager to take your money. It is also true that they are both incompetent and disinterested in completing orders on any kind of timeline. An initial estimate of one and a half weeks turned into a waiting time of more than one month. After a months time some items still were "expected any day now" and I simply cancelled them for a refund. To their credit, they seemed very used to providing refunds, as they issued them promptly and without any argument. I say refunds plural because only after bringing my order home did I discover numerous errors within it, requiring an additional trip for additional refunds. I was relieved to find same-day service and much cheaper prices at a competitor who is used to helping fix the messes caused by the PSU nightmare. If PSU does not have something immediately in stock, go somewhere else and thank me later. Do not place any products or services on order.

I've been going to Edison on occasion over the last 3 years for lunch, corporate outings, and casual dinners. I started out being in love with the place and it's dedication to the gastropub cause, to now finding it as not worth the hype in terms of local competition and price (our party of 6 spent about $300, collectively).

As far as the ambiance and experience goes, I would honestly give it 3.5 stars. It is the definition of a pseudo-swanky Tampa private owned restaurant that is not a chain. Unfortunately, the lack of efficiency in how the restaurant operates brings it down a few stars for  me, especially after I brought a group of co-workers and friends for a birthday outing that could have been better in terms of service. The fact that we had to send food back, and didn't get an apology nor any type of patronage discount, along with painful wait times (almost 20+ minutes to get our cards and checks taken upon meal's end) made it a slightly painful situation. Let alone 2 hours taken to get in and out of the restaurant during lunch...and we were the first table seated, with reservations...and only table for 30 minutes). 

I do give Edison credit for creativity and presentation of their food. Things such as a sorbet made to compliment a seared tuna appetizer is something you cannot find at a typical restaurant. But...until the service is as above-and-beyond as their menu offerings, I may need to take my business elsewhere, especially during a time crunch.

As far back as I can remember, this was always the place to go for adult beverages. Sure, its a PA thing...you can't really buy beer in that many places, but when you find a place in which you can buy beer, it helps if they have a good selection. And they do.

Beyond the usual suspects, they will have Flying Fish, Dogfish Head, Victory...not necessarily ever last variety of such, but generally they have what I would want to buy. The walk-in cooler section doesn't have as much to offer (and they tack on a minor, additional charge for semi-cool beer), but its there in a pinch. No sixpacks or any sort of mix-n-match, so don't bother asking.

Its hard to justify 4 stars for such an establishment, especially after living outside of PA for a bit, but if you are in the area you really have no other choice. And this joint could do a lot worse. Oh yea, they sell lottery tickets and gum. And they are a short hop from Pudge's. Why live anywhere else?

The atmosphere at this restaurant was awesome. Service was also good. However, food was very bland, basic, and disappointing. Some of the dishes I ordered tasted like the restaurant just put some Costco ingredients together. The poached eggs I ordered were still sitting in water once they arrived to my table. Food was expensive for the quality.
Not an authentic natural hair care salon if you only know how to use a flat iron on natural hair.

Kampai on Speedway has been a regular Friday night stop for my husband and myself for over 5 years but the last time we went in we were in for quite a surprise. The atmosphere was totally off and the usual greeting was not there. After sitting down, we noticed the owner was not behind the sushi bar and there were a lot of new faces in the sushi chefs and servers. We had plenty of time to check everything out because for 10 minutes, after we sat down, no one even acknowledged we were there. We sat at the sushi bar and the chefs didn't even greet us. Looking around we noticed some subtle changes in the decor and decided something was terribly wrong so we decided to leave. On the way out we noticed an application for a liqueur license on the window, in a name other than the person we knew to be the owner. At that time we decided to drive up to the Kampai on Oracle and see what's up. Whew! Everyone was there. The owners, the regulars, the ambiance. Though the Speedway Kampai is within 2 miles from my house, we'll only go to Kampai on Oracle. I can't comment on the food at the Speedway restaurant because no one ever spoke to us or asked us what we'd like to eat but I love the sushi and the comfortable feel I get when I'm in the Kampai on Oracle! If you're ever at the Kampai on Oracle feeling adventuresome, order the Chirashi or the Hwe Dup Bop. I also think they make the best ginger dressing for salad in Tucson!

Ca'Dario wouldn't honor our reservation last night. We had reserved on OpenTable for a 7:00 seating on a Monday outside. They had given away all the outside tables. Explanation: Outside reservations are just a "preference," not guaranteed. Unfortunately, that's what's called in restaurant parlance a "fib." The reservation system lets you choose outside, nothing about a preference. Nor did they attempt to make any accommodation (it was fairly clear some outside tables would be done in 15 or 20 minutes.) By the way, the hostess was polite (if unhelpful), so this is clearly a management problem not a staff problem.

A restaurant is certainly entitled to set any conditions on reservations they wish. Not honoring what they set up is not okay.

Too bad, we've been to Ca'Dario before and enjoyed it. This was for a minor special occasion and they blew it.

stopped in for dinner, place was busy. happy hour beers half price-always a positive. Staff was very nice. I ordered 1/2 lb ribs, 1/2 lb brisket, broccoli salad and burnt ends sandwich then pecan pie for dessert. burnet end sandwich was not burnt ends just shavings from brisket. brisket was not ended and both had no smoke flavor at all, severly disappointed. ribs were not tender, were actually tough with silver skin still on them and again no smoke flavor. broccoli for broccoli salad was not crisp but mushy. pecan pie was the only gd thing.

I came to Llama Tooth initially expecting something fantastic and was sorely disappointed.  Stars are for the huge patio seating area, funky reggae music, and drinks, which make for potentially relaxing happy hours after work.  Sadly, the other parts of the restaurant failed to make a good impression at all.

Prices are reasonable for the portions of food and drinks.  Tried the following: mushroom truffle mac and cheese, pulled pork mac and cheese, chicken gyro, veggie burger, sweet potato fries.  What was notable was that the friends, who are normally not picky about their dinners, kept saying how little flavour everything had.  Sweet potato fries were gigantic, as if large sweet potatoes had been quartered lengthwise and fried.  Impressive only in size, not in taste or quality.  Veggie burger was probably the only standout on the menu with great seasoning and execution, though they could've held off on the large amount of raw red onion.

Service was friendly enough but extremely slow as molasses; food did come out warm but getting our order put in, fresh glasses of water, plates cleared, and receiving our check took forever.  For folks like us that tend to linger over drinks for ages and forget about leaving, the length of time between everything was noticeable.  Other tables nearby seemed to be suffering the same waits as well.

Given that this place is right across the street from Union Transfer, I'd probably be likely to return here solely for a pre-concert drink given their extensive beer list and welcoming patio seating.

I want to like this place more than I do. We live next door so it's easy on nights when we don't want to cook. Buuuut .... it's really just ok. I feel like Sen is so close to being good, but the spice never gets close to hot and the food I've had (drunken noodles, green curry) lacks that unique Thai flavor (Fish sauce! Basil! Coconut!) and just comes off as pan-Asian. Don't get me wrong, it's good food, and maybe its better when you eat at the restaurant. At least for the takeout, readjust your expectations.

We got a coupon and since I didn't have P.F in so long; I really wanted to avail it. I have had better tasting P.F Changs. Their lettuce chicken wrap starter was great and on point. 

However; their spicy sichuan beef and chang's chicken, the signature dish were small portions and the sweetest spicy mains I have ever had. Disappointing; especially compared to other franchises. 

It will take desperation or an involuntary social gathering to have me dine here. 

Just maybe, for the apps?
Beautiful rooms. Great price. Great continental breakfast, friendly staff. Close to Nashville.

Sizzling rice soup - rice cakes pan fried served with wonton soup broth that had an inch of American "homestyle" chicken noodle soup with mushrooms at the bottom of a 32 oz cup.   Not very much flavor or substance was my final verdict.

For $5... Um, I had better.

My pregnant sister took her truck there to have the A.C. checked as they said they can fix it. They gave my sister a list of parts that need to be replaced and a $720 estimate. She called my husband and he told her to go to a well known auto place that specializes in A.C. When she took her truck there the guys said that the parts that Firestone noted needed to be replaced do NOT need to be replaced. They also told my sister that the guys at Firestone must of emptied out her freon. She only spent $200 to get her A.C. fixed. Firestone on 1st needs to be investigated. Get a 2nd opinion if things don't seem right.

I really really really want to like this place! Everyone likes B.E,, right!!!?? Well, sadly I disagree. I can go to Macy's and get brand new clothes for cheaper than what I can find at B.E. Yes it's trendy. Yes it's recycled. Yes it's green. But honestly, I don't care!!! I'm not paying $25 for a 2nd hand tank top! Ain't doin' it!!

This location is somewhat better than the others. You can get some pretty cool knick-knack type objects including recycled elephant poo paper and mustache band-aids!! Who doesn't need that!?

Thank you for the attempt B.E., but for 2nd hand, I'll be sticking to Plato's Closet http://www.yelp.com/biz/platos-closet-tucson#hrid:O3zVnnbzLVUCSwiun9FoWw
fabulous food. wow. just go and try something, anything.
Taylor is the best bartender in all of the conshohocken philadelphia area

My first review, and of course it's because I was so disgusted I had to warn others.  If you like to take health risks and be ignored, then this place is for you.

I ignored the recommendations of others and decided to give this place a try.   The place is very dirty and the service/staff is awful.  I'm a huge fan of eclectic places and no-b/s service/staff, but dirty taps, mold and a don't-want-to-work-here attitude on top of bad hygiene is inexcusable.

The food?  How anyone could award this place anything had to come from friends and family.  No one with any prior pizza eating experience would ever vote for this place for any reason.

Monday night dinner for two with two bottles of wine. Just under $300.

There is a large selection of appetizers and we had several.  Tuna tatami special featured good quality tuna but sadly it was served in a puddle of what tasted suspiciously like bottled teriyaki sauce.  Salmon tartare was a complete failure and we ate only half.  Stuffed portobello was very good.  Escargot were inedible.  Really.  Finished by splitting an entree of grilled duck breast with peppercorn sauce.  Tasty but duck was seriously overcooked.

Wine list is uninspired and selection criteria unimaginable.  Didn't recognize anything.  First cab (about $60) started pruney and degenerated to sweet.  Second bottle (about $75) was much better and would have been appropriate at, say, $50.

But the waitress was very prompt, courteous and accurate.  

Every restaurant has an off night.  Maybe this was theirs.  I could be talked into eating there again - maybe for lunch with beer.  They would have to earn my dinner business.

Not sure why I'm giving them one star.  I had an ant issue, and still do.  Yes they retreated, still had ants.  

The rep that signed me up was very nice, skipped a few details like, the quarterly service includes a one month service after the initial service, you pay for.  If you cancel the service, any discounts they gave you come back, to your total bill.

I had missed appointments, no call back and did I mention ants, yea I still have them, will be calling Orkan!
