Pretty good pizza, however, some of the more exotic options get a little soggy due to the abundance of toppings. Definitely worth a shot if you are looking for a different pizza experience.

So my friends and I thought we were going for brunch as their website lists their brunch menu. We each had our dishes picked out before we even walked in the door (we love brunch). However, we were advised that they stopped serving brunch in 2019.  Yet, it's 2021 and it's STILL on their website. Ok.  So we ordered from the dinner menu as that's what they serve at 3.  Shrimp cocktail was good, cheesesteak eggrolls were just ok.  They don't compare to Double Eagle's cheesesteak dumplings at all. There was a sauce on top which was the only flavorful component of the dish. The tomato and burrata salad was fresh, bright and delicious. The catch of the day branzino was very fishy in taste, and the skin was not crispy, which is the thing that makes fish skin palatable for me, so I had to send it back.  The salmon was completely bland. The sirloin was not cooked to the requested temperature, and like the eggrolls had no flavor on its own. The truffle butter on top made it edible. We ordered a round of VIP's but they were not as promised. When they were brought to the table the color was pale so we knew they wouldn't taste like Del's D.E. which they didn't.  The truffle mac n cheese was absolutely delicious and was probably the star of our lunchtime dinner. Nice & creamy with a truffle essence and was seasoned just right.  

Our server Kayla was knowledgeable and attentive. However, it was not enough for me to ever return. Unless maybe if they bring brunch back.  Afterward, we walked over to Double Eagle and had a couple rounds of true VIP's (yellow in color, and potent pineapple flavor) which were delicious as usual.  I will definitely stick with the original restaurant for sure.

Rustica is my fave pizza supply in the city.  They'll deliver just about anywhere though it may take a while since they're popular as heck (wonder why?).  

Wasnt crazy about the eggplant topping and I think it's because they deep fry it.  The roasted garlic, though, is super great.  Heck, their tomato pie is simple joy in red.  My recommendation though is to just stop in and pick the slice you find most sexy because they look so great when they put together all the fun toppings you'd never consider all'a carte.  

Word to the wise: there's some place near the ICA in Ucity that is called rustica.  When I saw this, my heart sped up a bit and an increaseed involuntary salivation made me consciously remember to swallow more.  But to be sure, I called up Rustica (that's right, I've got em on speed dial on my cell) and they informed me that this location along with a few others are indeed knock-offs.  The Northern Liberties location is THE location for great pizza.  

Needless to say, my heart slowed back down to the near coma rate with disappointment and my mouth dried up... sigh

Take your dog anywhere else to be groomed. Today was strike three... every time I bring my dog here there's an issue. They overbook and then want to charge for things they didn't disclose. The atmosphere is chaotic and the grooming is not well run. Not going back.

We were there to catch the drag show which is only between 7:00p-8:00p on a Thursday night. I was there with a group of about 10 girls. They were super excited to see the drag show. Many had never seen a drag perfoRmance before. I had been hyping this up for some time. I made sure that all of these girls were ON TIME (which is difficult for a group of women coming to the city from west county). The drag queens were about 30 minutes late coming out on stage and then stopped performing when Rupauls Drag Race came on the screen. The three drag queens stood at the bar watching the program while my table sat twiddling our thumbs waiting for commercial breaks (as they only came back out on stage during those times). Very disappointing evening. There was nothing on the website indicating that this program would be shown on the screen. The manager stopped me at the door on the way out and encouraged us to come back on a Sunday. I'm not sure that I will be coming back. It was a waste of a $40 Uber ride for me. The positive was the food which was good. My table had stacks of $1.00 bills. If the drag queens would've performed instead of watching tv, they would've left the evening with a few more bucks in their purses.

I've wanted to try cooking Indian food for a long time, but have always procrastinated due to the number of spices involved.  Enter Patel Brothers.  I snagged bags of whole black mustard seed, whole cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, a 4 lb bag of split peas, and a 5-pack of fresh spinach thepla for under 20 bucks.  Time to get cooking!

One of the women who works here is consistently awful, no customer service skills. Any question I ask her I get a very cold, abrupt, short response. She always looks extremely stressed even when the store is basically empty. They may very well be busy behind the scenes, but I wait patiently for her help and still get rude responses. It may be a personal thing. 

Maybe I don't look like a typical rich Williams
Sonoma customer. Next time I'll wear my Burberry and do some makeup, sorry for my appearance ma'am. 

Their selection here is on the smaller side, but proportionate to the store size itself. I'll avoid this location at all costs.

FEMA Roll!  Katrina Roll!!!!!  Where else can you find rolls named after a well-known event circa 2005?!  LOL

This restaurant is and feels like a stand-by Chinese restaurant.  Super bright lights, mediocre tables, tons of staff in matching uniforms, and a very bleak wall with a huge plasma mounted.  Little Tokyo is like the fall back or back up restaurant just in case...

We had cucumber roll, crunchy garlic tuna tataki salad, rice paper roll, rock n roll, FEMA roll, gyoza, and tofu steak.

Dude, the tuna tataki salad was AWESOME!  Lightly seared tuna steak sliced thin on top of romaine greens w/ ponzu sauce and crunchy bits of garlic...yuM!  

FEMA roll was freakin awesome....all I remember is the tuna on top with snowcrab and more tuna w/ a pink sauce in a soy wrap....presentation was so pretty!  

Tofu steak came out sizzling, but the bottom of the fried tofu plus sugary teriyaki sauce on a hot plate = sticky caramelised bottoms.  Not so tasty there....

It was fun b/c the LSU/Ohio game was on and the sushi chefs were cheering loudly for LSU....I only gave the 4th star b/c of the humorously named rolls.....

Food is great!  But it is so cold and depressing in there.  Dark freezing cold, depressing music.  I dont think the owner knows how to run a business.  Too many waitresses just standing around..making no money.   The fact that they dont deliver is a huge mistake..they have like 4000 customers in a 2 mile radius in all these apartment complexes....we all are disappointed he doesnt deliver..who goes out for pizza and subs anymore?  We want delivery.

Great restaurant to go to at night. Even late at 9:20 before they close. The bar area has the live piano music. It is great if you on the business trip by yourself and seat on the piano bar. 

I have the southern style shrimp and grit. The shrimp count is at the large size. The ingredient include chorizo ham and bacon. It make the taste interesting. I would said this is average value. I like the piano music at the bar. It make the time pass quicker while you eat and listen. 

The signature margarita is average. The service of the bar is a bit slower due high volume customer during that time i. Overall is a good experience. I like the small plate menu at the happy hour. I will try that next time for the happy hour. I will be back.

The Manager of Patrick's invited our group back for a second chance.  I will say that they redeemed themselves.  Our server, Shaun, was very attentive to us despite having quite a few tables he was waiting on.  The food was better than last time, but still just average in my opinion.  I got the bison burger, which lacked a bit in flavor.  Overall, Patrick's took ownership of their poor service last time and asked us to give them another chance, which was very much appreciated.  Given their effort, I won't completely write them off the next time I'm in the Westport area.

I got a facial from Kristin yesterday & it was amazing!  She is very talented & knew exactly how to treat my skin.  She was extremely friendly & made sure I was comfortable & relaxed.  She was even nice enough to stay a few minutes later to answer my questions about skincare and various products.   Reflexions is located in a yoga studio which can be a bit confusing, but Kristin's space is private, homey and clean.  Although I have only gotten a facial here, I am excited to try other treatments and services!!!

I have tried several desserts, my favorites include the chocolate mousse tart and chocolate cupcakes. Brunch is good also, they use fresh ingredients and the dishes are creative. I enjoy the waffle with peaches, steak & eggs, and sausage omelette. I plan to try dinner which is only offered Fri. & Sat. It's byob.

My husband and I stopped in on a snowy Friday night and the place was packed!  The hostess greeted us pleasantly and sat us right away, which surprised me because it was so crowded.  It recently opened, and has been very nicely remodeled, including the restroom.

The waiter came to the table quickly, and although he wasn't full of personality he attended our needs and checked back with us several times throughout the meal. 
I ordered a BBQ chicken paninni, which was tasty.  My husband ordered the open faced roast beef and ate every morsel!  The food arrived very quickly, which again surprised me because of the crowd.

The food was good diner food.  Not great, but if I want great food, I don't go to a diner.  I would definitely go back and recommend it to others.  I'd have given it another half a star if the rating bar above allowed!

This may be long because it describes multiple issues with the Goleta CA Store 

1. 2 years ago we ordered pergo flooring thru your assistants. We also set up installation thru HD. First they filled the order with the wrong color. Second the installers were awful and we finally had to use another installer after weeks of complaints. 

2. 2 months ago we ordered more pergo thru your staff.  The day before my installer was to pick it up, I went to the customer order desk went over the order to verify it was all there yet when he showed up the order was screwed up. Supposed to have 2 boxes of t molding and 5 of trim. Only 1 box of t molding! And they did not have my guy Check off the delivery. So we had to reorder which took 2 extra weeks to get delivered. And since it was weeks after the pergo had been installed I will have to pay an extra $120 BECAUSE OF YOUR MISTAKE. 

3. This Monday I went to return product we didn't use. It took 90 minutes to do this because 3 different associates had no idea how to return product. It took a 4th associate to complete it. Btw when I was there someone called into the store and No one picked it up for at least an hour!

4. Today I went to buy a sprinkler. When I got to where they were all of the product was covered in bird shit! When I checked out I reported that health hazard to the checkout person. Their answer was "there was nothing they could do about it". What hell, clean it up!

Then I tried to call in to talk to a manager and after 45 minutes without anyone even picking up the call I gave up. 

Your management and training is among the worst I've ever experienced.

I like the Buck. I really do. But lately it seems like I've been having subpar experiences here. Maybe I'm not cool enough, but it can sometimes feel a little bit alienating and uncomfortable because the staff aren't particularly welcoming all the time. Really hit or miss for me. 

Don't get me wrong, the food here is great. I don't understand how everything is vegan because let me tell you it tastes like the real deal! Sailin' On have done a great job here- the kitchen and kitchen staff have their craft down to a science and an art for that matter. My favourite is the corndogs and fries. Super delish. 

It's dark in here and I feel it's better suited for a night out at the bar than a dinner, which to me is a real shame. The patio/balcony is quite small but it's nice when you can squeeze in there for lunch/a drink! They've got lots of space inside for when you're out on a Friday night and feel like dancing. 

The one thing that's really hit or miss is the staff. Maybe it's just really super cool to seem uninterested and bothered by everything but it doesn't feel super cool when you're a patron. I just hope that the next few times I come back I'm treated a bit more like a human being with human feelings. I try to assume the best in others but sometimes you just gotta put a smile on your face and fake it till you make it rather than sulk around and act bothered by everyone at the tables. 

I'll come back, and just cross my fingers that my experiences are better. Because this place is great, and deserves four or five stars. And they're totally capable of attaining that.

I should have read the recent reviews before dining here. Saigon is probably the worst Vietnamese restaurant I've ever eaten at. The food was bland and uninspiring and the service was slow and inattentive. There are a ton of great restaurants in Santa Barbara. Do yourself a favor and eat anyplace else.

Cabana is a good spot for bachelor/bachelorette parties. They do a nice job accommodating large groups. Food is simple and good, and the menu has something for everyone. Our waiter, Bryant, almost ruined the experience for us though. He was very rude and talked back to almost all of our table. He scolded my friend and I for saying we would like to share two plates of food. He told us we shouldn't use the word share in the restaurant, and then proceeded to get very agitated and rude as we attempted to order dinner. I would not have given him a tip, but it was already included in our bill because we had 14 people. He made the experience very uncomfortable and really put a damper on the night. I hope this isn't how all the waiters at Cabana treat their patrons, because they will begin to lose business.
Great food. Great service.  Proof in pictures. Order the crepes or the pancakes for a great experience
They are very nice and I love how big it is but the ceiling always seems to be leaking and there is a smell, a mix of musty,mildew and rotten milk when,you walk in

We went there for breakfast on a busy Sunday morning and the wait was average not too long.  The waitress was very courteous and quick in bringing out our food.  The food was ok for a diner.  My husband did not like the pancakes said that they were uncooked however my kids loved their eggs and bacon and omelets.   A lot of people seem to go for the buffet maybe we will try that next time.

Great service. Was expensive because glass had to be odered through dealer.
Not Jeff s fault.
Timely appointment and good coffee with real cream.

This restaurant is well worth the price. The menu is extensive, the service is friendly and attentive and the price is very reasonable. In addition to eating at the Jenkintown location, I have ordered delivery from the Wyncote location and the food has arrived quickly, still warm and tasting fresh out of the oven. I have tried their pizza, pasta dish, salad and sandwich - all of which were fresh and delicious.
Excellent service, cute restaurant, the food was fantastic and not over priced.  Short rib ragu was the best. Fresh baked garlic rolls.
Authentic Mediterranean food for a decent price! Friendly staff, with the option of various smoothies to complement!

Last week we headed down to Golden Eagle again for both my boyfriend to get a massive Koi on his leg, and his Mom to get a wolf sleeve. This is now our home away from home, and I'm heading in soon to get my feet done. This shop rocks. Andrew rocks. Nuf said.

Stopped by here this morning and had my coffee fix. When I travel, I love to visit coffee shops to find the best coffee shop in the area. I absolutely LOVED my coffee. 

I got the turtle latte- it's made with Ghirardelli chocolate and caramel so I expected it to be really sweet. It was actually the perfect balance of sweetness and coffee. The staff were very nice and I got to record them making my latte 

If I lived here I would come here to study and do homework all the time. They have an amazing open space with plenty of seats. It seems like it could be a tad bit dark in the evening but plenty of light during the day. The interior design is beautiful. I would love to come back again and try other lattes

Amazing food and a personable owner make up for subpar service and cramped dining conditions. Everything is delicious; from the roasted beet salad, the Palermo pizza, Chicken Gremolata and the to die for Godfather Chocolate Cake.  Just go in with lowered service expectations and you will thoroughly enjoy your meal.

Cones of meat, fresh soft pretzels the size of your head, octopus sausages, and out-of-this-world charcuterie boards. 

Seriously, the charcuterie boards are gorgeous. Think: fresh meats and cheeses, olives, savory nuts, soft bread, jams, mustards, mini peppers, and so much more. 

There's also a variety of sausages that are available and made with all types of meats. I recently learned that they make pizzas as well. Will be back for one of those! 

Located within Sun King Brewery, this is such an exciting, locally-sourced addition. (Check out their Indy other stores: Smoking Goose and Goose the Market).

I am a bbq person , I have traveled all over the US and I am used to competition BBQ. This was the most disappointing bbq I have ever had, and I only had a bbq salad with brisket. I asked if the Brisket was going to be sliced and was informed that the meat would be chopped. What I ended up getting looked and tasted like the scraps that came off the cutting board. The meat tasted old and was not eatable without sauce. BJ's was a great disappointment.

Okay, I admit with some guilt that I'm a fan of IKEA... but only want to "do" IKEA maybe once or twice a year, 'cause every visit there is a huge investment of time.  That said, it's just fun to walk around, look at stuff, eat the swedish meatballs, and even furnish an entire room with mod stuff for a couple hundred bucks.

Are you a glutton for punishment and want more details?  Okay then, here are some pros and cons.  Cheers!

Pros:  

* Amazing selection of affordable, even disposable furniture, and every year they roll out fresh new lines.

* The styling can be pretty cool.  Awesome, even.  And the way they've set up the furniture in displays really can inspire ideas.  

* I furnished my home office from here for about $500, have been very happy.  We also got a rockin' modular closet organizer that has worked out amazingly.

* I think it's fair to say that you get a bit more than you pay for here.

* They have babysitting!  Although it's first-come first-served.  

* Usually plenty of staff on hand to help, and I understand they treat their people pretty well.  That's a big plus in this era of WalMart abuse of employees.

Cons:

* Going to IKEA is a commitment: they've deliberately designed it so you can't get through it quickly, no doubt so that you find more things to buy and spend more money.

* It's nice there's a cafeteria: you actually need a break it's so big.  But the food is somehow lacking... perhaps because it's not completely unhealthy?  But it can be a bit bland and portions small.  Maybe it's a Scandanavian thing?  The Lingonberry soda is always a treat for some reason though, although it's really mostly like raspberry ginger ale.

* The checkouts are always a bit crowded, as is the store. 

* Getting back to your car with your purchases can be a pain.

* Then, when you get home, you have to put everything together!  Ya know, thinking about it, maybe it's better to just spend the extra money and get the stuff assembled.

will be short and simple...

totally uninspired food. way to expensive for what you get. distracted service, along with the feel of unclean surfaces. dislike the look and setup.

not to my liking.

Was craving ramen during a recent trip to SB and found this place thanks to Yelp.  The veggie ramen was fantastic, rich broth and chewy noodles with lots of veggies.  Large serving also.  Service was really good-helpful and friendly but not overbearing.  It doesn't have the greatest 'curb appeal', I was a little worried when we arrived at the place but it was great.  Also had some veggie rolls which were wonderful also.  Will definitely return on my next visit to beautiful Santa Barbara.
When I am in Nashville, this is one of my favorite places to go. The food is great and the staff is friendly. I like that the sushi bar is far removed from the hibachi area, as well.

2 stars is generous for this location.  

The employees, if you can find them, are really shady.  And overall can be extremely rude to the point of confrontational.  

I asked an employee in tools to ring two items up separately and he said no.   Then the price came up wrong and I pointed to it on the item, he seemed dumb as anything and still wanted to argue.  

Then at the end of the sale he dropped my receipts on the floor and walked away.
Denny's is opening up remodeled on Tuesday, June 16, 2015.  I love their Samon.  Hope they didn't change it!

This place is definitely ran by amateurs. The kitchen looks clean but the laundry room is a mess, and so does the toilets.

Thank god they have wireless, or I would have left it already.
Disrepectful and rude man who took my order. If you dont want to be there then quit. Delivery gentleman was great. Figure it out mgmt

Great place! Food is delicious- we had the biscuits and gravy, shakshuki and Dutch babies- so good! The spicy Bloody Mary deserves its great rep as well. We had amazing service as well. Our server was attentive and friendly by low key. Overall great experience.

Good food not great, pretty much run of the mill pizza place, in my option a little pricey for what you get but that is up to the individual to say, staff is friendly and capable, hope they make it out here but $17 for a calzone with couple adds and $ 27 for a pizza might be a bit much out on the beach.
Best New York strip in the city....hands down. Service was impeccable. Valet parking helps too
Service was awesome! Food was delicious! Loved nuggets! Bbq sauce amazing! Nachos were delicious! Will come back!

This was my second visit, and both visits were about the same: hooray for consistancy!

First, a word about the restaurant itself: it is nothing fancy.  Like most of the asian restaurants on south grand, most of them are nothing much to look at.  The service was friendly and mostly prompt, although we we thrown a confusing smattering of different servers and bus boys for some portions of the meal.

Now, the food: we began with the boiled chicken pot stickers.  They were cooked to the correct doneness for me, although the filling was a bit bland.  Their pot sticker sauce was a bit saltier and less sweet than I am used to, but was still good.

I went traditional and ordered the pork pad thai.  It was overly sweet in its unmodified form, and had a strange pink coloring to it.  After I tossed in some chili flakes and soy sauce though, it was much better.  The pork wasn't that great, it was somewhat tough and fatty; I would go with chicken or beef next time.

For dessert we had the fried bananas with ice cream.  Two words: yum yum.  The batter wasn't too greasy or heavy, and while the ice cream was likely out of a plastic tub, the drizzle of honey on the top worked well to give it some additional flavor.

Overall, a good experience!

I love the chicken quesadillas here, their amazing!! Being a California native who knows real Mexican food I was surprisingly impressed!!  I have not had any other food from this place so I can't speak about that, but the chicken quesadilla with jalepenos..so delicious! The one down side is they are a bit slow, hence the three starts instead of four! Go owls!!
Greenland Grill is great to satisfy your craving for Mediterranean food. The best things about this place is that everything is fresh and tasty and they deliver too. Try the gyro!
Creepily empty, good little chocolate squares and tiny desserts.

Just had the worst expirence at this market. I was wearing workout clothes. I was profiled as a thief. I paid for my items and ate them outside while being watched by 3 losers for 15 minutes. This is not the first time. I will never shop here again.

Also health warning! 

None of the hot food is at the correct temperature.

Was very excited to try this place but was a bit disappointed in the food.  Had the crab cake which came out cold and in a soup of sauce.  The French onion soup though was fantastic and then came my NY au poive...I asked where the peppercorns were...and the server told me that is has pepper on it.  Not even a peppercorn sauce!!!! Steak was cooked well and was tasty but by no means au poive...Scampi was tasty...Just ok but don't need to go back.  bummer...

Single handedly the greatest dining experience of my life. I could go into detail, but I feel like it would be the equivalent of divulging the ending of a great movie. JUST DO IT. You will not "ragret" it. garunteed.

If you can look past the shabby carpet and austere furnishings you will be rewarded with some of the best Indian food available.

Try the Lamb Marsala and Chicken Biriyani. Outrageously good.

I was given an estimate of a $700 out-of-pocket expense after a $1300 insurance claim. Dr Smith did the work and then billed me for an additional $751. After receiving the bill, I spoke to the office manager on the phone multiple times, and personally went into the office on two separate occasions to find a mutually agreeable solution. Dr. Smith refused to speak to me directly. The office was very combative and offensive when I expressed concern over an estimate off by over 100%. The manager initially placed all of the blame on me, and eventually conceded to "errors in both parties." After pursuing all reasonable efforts to pay an amount closer to the estimate, I was forced to pay the full amount in order to save my credit from going to collections.

I frequented this place a few time on my trips to New Orleans.  This time I tried something new...Tomato and Crab Fried green tomato... It was really tasty.  I didn't know what to expect but was very pleased!

Tonight we had dinner for a birthday celebration at this location. Our reservation    was for 4:00 and we could not be seated until 75% of our party arrived. This is what we we told when the reservation  was made. Some of out party would be running late so we decided to have a couple of drinks while we wait. Glad we did.

Danielle at the bar was awesome. As always we were greeted with a smile and suggestions for drinks. Guys went with the Happy Hour beer specials which were about 4.25. Others in the waiting party decided to go with martinis. Very good choice. The drinks were made perfect. You could tell the liquor was there, however they were not to strong for the ladies in our party to enjoy. Danielle  mad a special drink that had a mango taste. It was simply amazing. We called it the Danielle Special when we ordered more once we got to the table.

Now we are at the table!  This is were the 5 stars went from 5 to 3!

The two people assigned to our table was not give the same friendly vibes that we ALWAYS get when visiting  BlackRock.

The male - yes I said the male only because he never gave his name came over and said first thing is we need to decide how we are handling the checks. Wow! After explaining the check situation he advised two people in our party which one was the birthday girl "She will  take your order since she has the other table and you 4 are on the same check" .. this was over looked. 

Now time to order my food. Ordered the Tuna! It was amazing. Very fresh tuna that I had to cook in the stone. Loved the experience. Added the casino butter, and grilled it on the stone.

I advised him that I had a coupon for a free appetizer with the purchase of a meal. He asked ehats your meal. I explained to him that it would be the dynamite chicken, however I did not want it then.He advised no problem. Seconds later the manager walks over. She greeted us with a smile and also with free appetizer  cards. My party was so thankful for the cards. 

The male came back by and I explained that I would now like to place my order for my meal. I ordered the dynamite chicken without sausage, baked potato without bacon, a side of casino butter and a side of asparagus. My food arrived by a female waitress/server -----( the Male NEVER came back) with other members of the party. Dynamite chicken as ordered.... 5 minutes passed no potato, no asparagus 10 minutes nope ... 12 minutes now I have the asparagus and potato without the casino butter. Members of my party enjoyed salad and or soup. The male never confirmed which one I wanted, so guess he decided not to bring me either!  After enjoying my meal I asked the female who is now attending to us about it. She kindly asked which one I wanted and packed it to go. 

I normally sit at the bar and now I see why I do. Will I go back to BlackRock yes. Will I do dinner in the dining area no.  Would love to sit family style, however with the service today I do not see that happening anytime soon. 

Danielle thank you for your awesome service at the bar. Everyone in our party said they can not wait to come back and see you soon.!

Although I will never be found standing in line to get in, I do appreciate the nostalgia and decent food that is Mother's.

Po'Boys, Red Beans and Rice and Seafood Gumbo top my list of yummy menu items.

Very casual and very loud.

If you've never been, you sort of have to go. Just cuz.

It is never fun not getting what you ordered and finding that out when you get back to work. I read another review before I went where the person ordered chili cheese fries, but got regular fries. So when I ordered the chili cheese fries, I thought I was speaking clearly enough. But I was not. If you order the chili cheese fires, look the person taking the order in the eyes, pause and slowly say chhhilllliiiiiiii chhheeeeeeessse fries. Say fries fast so the emphases is on the part that makes the fries good.

I am not impressed by their plan fries. The hot dog I ordered came as I ordered it, mustard only. It was a good dog and I enjoyed that part a lot. Maybe now they will make sure they heard the person correctly.

Right on the pier! Popular and crowed. they recommend me the famous Strawberry cheesecake ice cream! It looks great, tastes so so, very expensive. Like $6. Would not try again. But you know, when you are on vacation, feel relax and walking on the pier, you just have the mood for an ice cream, right?

Unfortunately the restaurant continues to go downhill. Poor service and mediocre food. One of us had to send the food back because it was undercooked and he did not receive his order until the rest of us had finished eating.
Such a fun environment, and the food is delicious. They finish most dishes at your table, which is a cool touch.

Every time we have worked with Ed to get a new mattress, he has been absolutely wonderful!!! 

He's down to earth, knows his mattresses, and is very reasonable on price. 

You can't beat the ease of purchasing through him versus a big chain store. Also, he was willing to work with us on a special delivery situation. 

Highly recommend!!!!
Had the crispy chicken sandwich.  It was quite good, but $9 seemed a little steep.  Will come back to try the burger.

Probably pretty lucky that they are not closer - stumbled on it looking for a "nosh" place after a regular lunch time and found this gem.   Just go there - look at the menu, decide what you might try and then write down what to try next time.  I had a killer brisket sandwich, the fresh and amazing slaw and the brussel sprouts.   Hubby stuck with a chicken sandwich - both were amazing.  Space is small, but homey and nicely decorated - I could spend all day trying the different sauces and asking questions about BBQ.   Great staff - everything is made to order and you just want to hang out with them and eat.  And eat.

Big servings, good price, decent service, but it was all too, too sweet.  

The entire platter tasted like dessert from the BBQ chicken, sure sauce can be sweet but this was diabetic coma sweet with hardly any tang, the greens were sweet as can be, no hint of vinegar.  The yams just tasted like a box of brown sugar, and the corn bread was like the sweetest cupcake I have ever had.  The mac and cheese was nothing to write home about.
Took 2 hours to change 1 tire WITH a reservation. This is ridiculous. Worst customer service too. Never going to come back. Do not come here unless you want to waste your time.

It was a good experience. The wait time wasn't bad and the staff was friendly. Everywhere has different rules for our insurance it seems like and not that this is a huge issue because I came expecting to pay but it would be nice that before services are rendered they make it clear I'm going to have to pay $250. Maybe for future patients if the bill is going to be a little hefty let them know before you do anything.
I remember trying to come here years ago. I felt very unwelcome I was nervous to even try and eat here. I was with my dog and did not appreciate the gestures and looks they gave me.

I've been meaning to write this review for a very long time. I was in New Orleans for a wedding in June, and didn't want to deal with the struggle of styling my hair myself in the humidity! I decided to go with Buff Beauty Bar because of all of the great reviews. Yes they give you a mimosa, and the salon is cute, but the stylist I had was horrible.

When I met my stylist she wasn't too friendly. Walking up the stairs I asked her how she was and all she said was "good." She was not welcoming at all what so ever, and didn't even politely ask how I was doing in return. I showed her the pictures of the hairstyle I wanted, and she didn't seem too enthusiastic. 
 
I wanted to get a low side messy bun with some loose curls. I showed her some pictures on my phone for reference, and I have had my hair styled like this before from a previous hairdresser. She told me those hairstyles wouldn't look as good since I didn't have blonde hair! The pictures I showed her were actresses with blonde hair, but I've had my hair done like this before and this is what I was asking her to do. I am the client right? I should get what I am paying for. 

First she was going to just pull my hair back and put my hair in a clean side bun -- I could have done that myself! I stopped her, and told her I wanted a messy bun. She was reluctant to do it, but did it anyways. I told her I wanted my hair curled a little bit so the front was not tied back so tight in a pony tail. She said it would be a "waste of her time" because you wouldn't be able to tell the hair was curled. Mind you, I booked her for an up-do which is an hour long session.

My hair looked "okay" and they stylist literally took 15 minutes to do my hair -- the appointment was for an hour. My up-do was not worth the $55, and I will not be going back there if I visit NOLA again.

Gumbo was decent, beer was cold. Two major complaints: the bar tender / waitress was extremely unfriendly, unprofessional and I almost walked out. In general, the people in LA are always over friendly and very welcoming, this woman was just the opposite. Secondly, I had the blackened redfish and it was very rubbery, it was hard to cut with a knife. Not even sure it was redfish. I will not return. Hard to believe the owners of this place would allow an employee run the front of house the way this angry woman does.

I can see why this place has 5 stars. The sandwich that I tried was amazing. I couldn't believe how good the food was.The food came out quick and the guy at the counter was super friendly. If I am in the area I will stop by again.

We walked in for Sunday morning brunch, and we were lucky they were able to seat us within 20 minutes. The decor and ambiance was great, but the front staff definitely gave off a "cooler than thou" dry hipster vibe, which was disappointing considering this place could easily fit in with other classically gracious New Orleans restaurants, full of southern hospitality. However, the bar and wait staff were gracious and professional. 
The food was delicious! Our table got the blue crab beignets, which lived up to the hype. Fresh crab and not too oily batter. Other entrees we got included the waffle (delicious), famous cheeseburger (rightfully famous), BELT sandwich, which initially the kitchen forgot to put the egg on but quickly corrected it, and the pork and eggs dish. All were very good, and it was a refreshing change from your uber-typical New Orleans brunch menu. Very good coffee also. 
Overall it was very good, and I'd like to give it another maybe with a reservation.

I just received a nice message from the business manager they are sending us a gift certificate to check out the restaurant again - it's nice to know someone is committed to customer service - will keep you posted 

Today we returned to Glory days with gift certificate  - Saturday prime time lunch again - the place was packed - we ordered burgers and the fries were cooked properly this time - atmosphere was great - no servers lurking in the hallway which was a plus - so I will say they redeemed  themselves - much better this time - we will return - thanks for gift certificate corporate!

Wanted an authentic mexican dinner an did NOT get it here. Expensive place. Had taco's the size of a sand dollar, which is $4.00 each. Be careful when they give you their so called Nacho chips, as you have to pay for the tasteless salsa $4.00 an the guacamole dip is $10.!!! No mexican rice! Really?? No pina colada, margarita (slush/frozen) They had bout 5 things on the entree menu. If you sit by the stairs going down, you can smell the bathrooms!!! Paid $70 for a micky mouse meal!!! Good thing the fringe was on as we went for dessert there!!  Julio's is still the best!!

My fiance and a friend went to Brick House last night and one of the most telling parts was that there wasn't another female in sight. Yes, it is comparable to Hooters with the addition of a menu that is degrading to women. However, I have a sense of humor and it didn't bother me but some women could definitely be offended.

The beer menu is decent. Maybe 15ish on draft and many more in bottles. Also some weird combos...like crabjuice and guinness (never heard of that, yuck?). You can also get beer towers for the table to share. 

No complaints about the food, but then again we ordered simple items- chicken fingers and fries. The chicken fingers were tasty and come with 3 dipping sauces, yum! Our buddy had onion straws and chicken wings and said they were delic'!

The service was friendly & attentive. Yes, you will see alot of leg & stomach (look before you eat) but hey, at least the girls are working and making a living. 

There are TVs everywhere so I think it would be fun to come here for a game.

Overall, its a decent place especially for a large group of single guys. Girls, if your boyfriend wants to go there you don't have much to worry about...they don't sit at your table and flirt like hooters lol. I would go back again, good beer, good food and HD TVs!

Let me start off by saying that Old Time Pottery has been one of my favorite stores to browse and shop at until today! I am completely appalled by the staff and lack of supervision at this location. My fiance and I visited this store around 4:00pm this evening, and were honestly taken back at how clean and well organized this particular store is. I found an item that I wanted to purchase and we proceeded to the check out. This is where everything went completely down hill for me. 
First of all, there were about 7 cahsiers at the front. I did notice another lady who appeared to be as confused as I was on where to go since not one of them were actually at their register. They were instead running around (literally) like little children, joking and slapping each other on the butts. 
Finally someone came to a registered and motioned us to come up. I placed my item on the counter and she asks if I had a military I.D. I do not know what was so funny about that or our military, but immediately her and the women next to her started laughing uncontrollably. She then randomly pulls an item from the counter and starts reading off the back of it. I had absolutely NO idea why she was doing this and I tried asking her but she wouldnt stop so I just took my money back and started walking out. 
I couldn't just leave without saying something in hopes it would make the situation better for the next customers. I went back up to another registered and asked for a manager. The lady pointed to another lady next to her so I started telling her why I was upset, she didn't seem to care much. I then asked for the G.M's name and information which she "didn't know", but she then paged the manager as she was only a supervisor. 
The manager came up and I told him everything that happened and he asked me who helped me. I walked him over to the lady that helped me and told him who was laughing and asked her what was so funny about our military. Her excuse was that she was not laughing at that but the fact that the other girl was reminding her to "upsell". I don't believe this as she couldnt even ask me if I had a military I.D without laughing. 
Unfortunatly this will be my last trip to Old Time Pottery since they not only think our military is a joke, but also their customers. I strongly suggest that this location do some strong house cleaning, and maybe hire people that WANT to work, and take their work seriously. I don't see a problem laughing and joking and having fun, but there is a limit especially when customers are around.

Corned beef hash looked nothing like the menu, was excessively greasy a burned corned beef grease patty. I took 2 small tentative bits and was done. $11 way over priced for what it was, and it was nasty. On the up side the mgr was very kind- made some rather lame excuses but did comp my breakfast which I believe was the appropriate thing to do. The place was packed out so I'm the exception I guess. I was invited back and ask to give them another chance but it's extremely doubtful. If you can't trust the menu depiction why okay Russian roulette with your meal?

I was later challenged on my review because the restaurant was supposedly trying to fix my experience. The point of this app is that was my experience.

If I could leave one star that's what I would do. My wife and I eat at Avanti at least once a week. It is usually a good experience. Tonight my father-in-law arrived about 15 minutes before the rest of our party. After we arrived we sat there for what seemed like 15-20 minutes and had no service whatsoever. We were told several times that someone was coming. To top it off when we got up to walk out the waitress gets in an argument with my father-in-law. That was completely uncalled for and inappropriate. I hate to say this but we probably won't be back. I always support small local businesses but service like this is what makes them fail. If I provided service like that in my place of business I wouldn't be able to pay my bills. It's very disappointing.

You have to take the time to make a roux for authentic gumbo. Coop's Place, which bills itself as a Cajun/Creole restaurant doesn't. The watery broth they pass off as gumbo is a disgrace. Shame! Avoid this place.
i love their tamales - other than that its a bit non challenging enough to return very often
Never disappointed !! I have been here a few times and thoroughly enjoy their food but the brunch here is out of this world

Sat on the balcony with an amazing view. Had the Demi royale appetizer nearly orgasmic. Then finished with the fillet Oscar paired with a wonderful house made sangria. Tomas and Chris gave Service beyond our expectations. Come here if you can folks.

We stopped here for dessert on our way out of Santa Barbara - what yummy pastries!  Our family enjoyed several macaroons, an almond croissant, and a chocolate chip cookie.  The macaroons were very moist and fresh.  They had approximately one dozen different varieties to chose from.  We had the vanilla, hazelnut, and raspberry - all three were delicious.  The chocolate chip cookie was great.  The best of all was the almond croissant - one of the best we have ever had! We look forward to stopping here again when we are in the area.

ruly the most humble and honest shop to deal with .. a well of knowledge with insight and appreciation for the craft. I normally don't leave reviews however after witnessing the passion and attention to detail the staff puts into their trade at vintage julz it was the least that I can do! With so many fakes out there I feel like I've found a gem working with the guys at vintage julz for any of my eyewear needs. Keep up the great work!!

Nice shop, got my daughter a very nice longboard and was impressed by the selection of non-generic, unique and high quality boards. 
Only reason for 4 stars was it was difficult to find, no signage and the guy behind the counter didn't have much knowledge about longboards.

We stumbled upon this place late night b/c it was within walking distance of our hotel.

We found 2 spots right at the bar and ordered a bottle of wine and an appetizer. 

While both were good, the service was meh....

It looks like it's a place locals go for a nice evening out in the area, but since we were obviously not locals I feel like the bartender wasn't nearly as interested in taking care of us b/c he knew we wouldn't be back.
I would definitely send more of our clients here.  They have a track record of having great therapists. Chris Vtrainers Personal Training Studios

NOPE! Stayed at the B with some friends this past weekend. We were all excited, seeing as though the online photos looked awesome and posh, but the reality is very disappointing. 

* The lobby smells like the sewer with perfume poured in it. I don't see how the front desk staff could stand it. 
* Elevator was very slow. Seemed to be only one out of 2 working
* Rooms are small and the furniture looks chipped and cheap. 
* I shared a one bedroom with living room and there was no mirror in the living area, so it was tough for both of us ladies to get ready every day.
* Bathroom is VERY small. Hardly any room to close the door from the inside while you're in there. No counter space to put your makeup or toiletries. 
* Took the bathroom shower 10-15 minutes to warm up. 
* No iron or ironing boards. Only a steamer. No thank you!
* The room phone did not work when I tried to call about the iron situation. No dial tone or anything.
* Room TV advertises a Netflix connection option, but when you click it, it says no internet connection. Lots of the TV channels would not play because of this as well
* Hotel is a long walk to everything and is in an area that gets a little sketchy at night. I would not walk back at night if I were you. 
* The hotel lobby doors lock after a certain time, probably because of the neighborhood. The key card box didn't work one night when I came back. 

On a good note, the canal street trolley runs right by the place so it's easy to get back and forth that way. Also, the hotel staff seemed nice enough and the housekeeping came by every day and did a nice job freshening up the room. I visit NOLA a few times a year and have tried many hotels. This is the only one I would never return to.

This was a nice playground to find in Santa Barbara.

The playground is included as part of a nice park near the downtown area.
The park itself is rather pleasant; paths lined with different types of trees and some beautiful ponds in the area.

The playground itself has the odor of a hamster cage needing cleaning, likely due to the fact that wood-chips composed the grounding.  The equipment is also composed of wood, and through it's definitely a splinter trap, the equipment was plentiful with slides, bridges, ropes and climbing areas.  Lots of kids in the area, so it's easy to find play-friends.

There are picnic tables near the playground and lots of park benches scattered throughout the playground.

i love this program.  I am in the northern part of nj and have gone to so many events. Plays, comedy clubs, classic music (brought my mom who thought I was a hero) , REO Speedwagon, Bad Company, - lots of bands.  Conventions that I would not have attended if I had to pay for a ticket.  I am thinking about adding the Philly onto my membership.  LOVE IT - but you really need the app on your phone to take full advantage of the events.

NO POINTS FOR YOU - If you book it on www.opentable.com and arrive 10 minutes late.

I booked a reservation at Sterling's  when I was in town for Cyndi Lauper in 2011, and my party was just 10 minutes late, and they canceled my reservation,  What the hell man!
 
My mouth was salivating while enjoying the Fillet Mignon (any size is just too small), and the Cosmo's were top shelf (Belvedere or Chopin).  The dessert of Creme Brulee trio was amazing, each bite was ecstasy.

It would have been a 5 star review if I had received my dining points.

My only regret is that I didn't get to try more Philly Cheesesteaks. I only had time to go to one destination and Jim's was it. 

The line was out the door and around the building but it moved relatively fast. We were able to get a split sandwich (half provolone, half cheese whize) To appease both my gf and I. I would've just ordered a whole one myself, but we had just eaten lunch so this was technically just a "snack" The cheesesteak did not disappoint and I remain a huge fan of cheese whiz. The meat here is thinly sliced and so tender and flavorful. I also really liked the carmelized onions on top. *DROOL* 

No one can do it quite like Philly can when it comes to cheesesteaks. I'm going to reserve the 5 star rating until after I get to try Geno/Pat's.
Extremely rude, arrogant, unprofessional, and not friendly at all. Amazing how people people act like this and can keep a job.

A five star shrimp poor boy! Huge portion. Just got mine without tomato. 

Also got a pineapple margarita which was far too easy to drink :)

Well worth taking a street car away from the French quarter to get here.

I've been here twice for the amazing cuisine over a 4 day span!  

We tried the alligator bites (we tried both fried and blackened) which were terrific.

We traveled from Chicago just to have the Redfish Oceana. Perfectly cooked, moist, flavorful. 

Bread pudding. Amazing.
I bought another car from Royal Kia in the summer of 2018 and had another great experience with them. Juan is a great salesperson, and months later I'm still very happy with the car he sold me.
My husband and I's go-to Thai restaurant. They have a huge menu with large portions, service is fast, price is more reasonable than other Thai places, and the food always hits the spot!

This so called restraint serves substances that qualify as garden fertilizer, not food. I am currently on a vacation with a couple of friends. We all decided to eat there because of the cheap prices and decent reviews. I am here to say that the layout of their building is much like a cramped apartment, small and cheap. My friends and I sat down at one of the tables, and a waitress came to take our orders. We all decided to share a couple of large orders of spaghetti and chicken Alfredo. The waitress was very kind and set a good atmosphere for the night. The food came out and we all took a portion. Three out of five of us took our first bite and immediately spit it out into our napkins. When the waitress came by next I asked for a simple meal that you would have to try to mess it up. What she then brought back was a small plate of steamed broccoli and a slab of what looked to be chicken. I first tasted the broccoli thinking that it would taste acceptable. What flavor I got in my mouth resembled a sandal worn by a sweaty man for a couple of hours. The chicken was next, I attempted to cut it when it leaked about three cups of water out into my plate. I took a bite and it was much like a sponge in both flavor and texture. It had tasted as if they had boiled a slab of meat in the same water that they had collected from the steamed broccoli. My friends and I left the rat infested pit of a restaurant hungry and angry.

THE MOST RUDE STAFF I'VE EVER ENCOUNTERED. Woman behind counter reading her phone looks up after I walked in and asked can I help you? I said yes, she stood up and said well do you see anything you like in there? pointing to the cooler with the flowers. I was looking ignoring her rudeness when she started to yell about each set in the cooler. I asked her some questions because I know nothing about flowers, she looked incredulous and was not helpful at all. She was huffing puffing and seem to be upset that I was about to spend $55. After that another rude woman came out of the back and was equally abrupt, against my better judgment I chose A bouquet, took it to the counter and decided to just keep my mouth shut and wait for her to ring it up so I can get the heck out of there. She asked me if I wanted a receipt? I said yes, and she did not print a receipt, now she's huffing and puffing because she pushed no on receipt button by accident (she claims) and had to call three other staff members over to try and figure it out. This woman has no business serving the public, the woman from the back has no business serving the public. I hope they go out of business soon.

Okay, to be honest, I went by the Center on a bitterly cold day because I was in town to visit my boyfriend's parents, and I wanted to see the place where my bf has worked as a teenager (so romantical!) But I did check out the place as far as ice skating was concerned and here's my take.
Nice warm break areas with tables, lockers and a good elevated view of the rink, which is standard size and well-kept. The staff was sweet. The rink is outdoor, so if you are going to stay on the ice for a long time, consider the elements. Rental skates seemed fine, but I usually bring my own. They had steady bars for little kids, hooray!
Overall, fun!
I walked there, so I can't speak about parking.

Apple bee's was able to accommodate our team after along night of softball. Party of 35 at 9 pm. We were all seated promptly and served by several servers. I ordered the 2 for $24 Bourbon Street Steak and spinach and artichoke dip. The food came out fast and hot. The steak although small was well cooked medium rare. The spinach and artichoke dip was OK a lot of chips not enough dip. Service was good.

A vacation brunch (up for the weekend from LA) - was pretty delicious but as someone who brunches a lot in LA, it didn't quite live up to the hype.  Everyone's favorite was the banana kahlua french toast.  It was pleasant surprise how fast the ordering/getting seated was on a Sunday morning.  

It was a bit overpriced (again, based on going to some very delicious brunch places in LA).  Glad we tried it but don't think we have any real desire to come back - would rather try other brunch places in SB.  

The one really annoying thing was that they have the tiniest water glasses I've ever seen next to the water dispenser - they had some sign about the drought, but come on - there are better ways to deal with that (it mostly comes from agricultural watering, not people drinking water at a restaurant).  We had to get up five times during our short meal to refill water.  Some people had normal water glasses around us and we asked for that, but were just brought the tiny glasses.  So yeah, Jeannine's figure out the water situation.

Other than that, a lovely brunch spot but not the top of our list.

He's one of the few doctors in the SB area that accept medical/cencal and once my insurance ran out I choose him. BIG MISTAKE!! He has a ridiculous amount of patients and cannot give them the time and care they need. Every time my kids have had an appt. we still wait around 30 or more min. to be seen, once we're in a room it's another 30 min wait. If you want a second opinion and need a referral, he will give you the run around to avoid giving you one. Him and his father are notoriously known as the brewers, they have a bad reputation and I understand why. Avoid bringing your children here, we are switching doctors and I am filing a formal complaint against him.

i called in my order via phone and the owner asked if i had ___ when i approached the counter. I do think preparedness is a key to good customer service :)

i ordered a chicken quesadilla with a side of cheese. i couldn't substitute out the sour cream for the cheese . the owner is sometimes short but he always exchanges pleasantries. 

i personally think this cafe is pricey and cant make this a regular because $10 for lunch every day is a bit much. they are clean, cozy and convenient. it's never overly crowded. i was introduced to it by someone who lived out of town . 

my chicken was perfectly seasoned! the portion was more than enough. 

they also serve a full breakfast and it's next on my try list, although i don't see that they serve turkey meat products. They use spot check in got rewards and you can place your order over the phone. They have plenty of private front lot parking right in front of the door.

I'd been wanting to try this spot for a little while. I was so disappointed when I found out that Cafe Ponte had closed and then I found out the owner had a couple of other style restaurants in Tampa. I came here with a couple of girlfriends for brunch. We got the bruschetta as an appetizer and that was the best dish I had. It was so tasty. I also got the avocado toast that was also quite delicious. I super excited to see the flourless chocolate cake as a dessert option, this was my favorite dessert at Cafe Ponte and I've never been able to find a flourless chocolate cake that is as good as that one. This one tasted just the same as I remember, I'm so happy they decided to continue serving it in OLIVIA now. The only reason I didn't give OLIVIA 5 stars was because the service wasn't great, she forgot to bring over a couple of drinks we ordered, was a bit slow, and wasn't super attentive. But overall, she was nice and we still enjoyed our time.
Good beer and delicious food, but the wait staff could have been quicker - thought the waitress was very friendly.  We had a great time and got what we expected.

Food was good despite them getting my sandwich or whatever its called wrong, however there was a short blonde girl who talked bad about me to another co worker right in front of me for explaining that I had a shellfish allergy and the gentleman who kept making food with shrimp and then wasn't changing his gloves or the actual pans was cross contaminating my food. If people use the same rubber gloves or change them once they have reached in a pan with shrimp on their hands the pan is contaminated as well!! They really need a trainer or training shift for some of the staff. I'll go back amd give them another try before I decide to not support the business anymore.

I dropped my car off Saturday morning asking for an oil change and an estimate on the repair of my heater. Saturday night, I get a call saying the car is ready for pickup. I ask about the estimate. They only had the oil change noted, so we go through the heater symptoms again and are told they'd check it out. About 15-20 minutes later, another person calls and says the car is ready. We again ask about the heater. Again they know nothing about it. Since it's almost time for them to close, this guy says they'll take a look at it first thing Sunday morning. We agree. No phone call at all on Sunday. So I just called now on Monday morning. I'm told my oil change is finished. What about the estimate? What estimate? So 2 days and asked for an estimate 3 times and there's still not even a note that it needs to be done. I told the 4th guy nevermind. I'll take my car elsewhere to get my heater fixed.

A little over a year ago we bought a Kenmore Elite fridge, a microwave-oven combo, a dishwasher, a natural gas stove top, and a washer and dryer from Sears. They have  been in use for just 10 months because they sat in their original box while we remodeled our house. So far, only the dishwasher and the fridge have been working ok, all the other appliances broke down. I am beyond disappointed with Sears and their unqualified repair techs. The microwave-oven smells like electrical wires burning when we use it which stinks up the whole kitchen. We stopped using as soon as it started smelling - a Sears tech came, took the oven out, looked at it and without running any tests or diagnosis or even looking inside said it was ok and nothing was burning. We had to put a cup of water in it to show her the problem but she still said nothing was wrong. Now they don't want to grant the warranty because we bought the appliance over a year ago. Tonight, our washer started leaking water. I looked at the plumbing and it's fine - the machine is leaking underneath! The stove top's flame barely reaches my pans and take f-o-r-e-v-e-r to heat up the food - the tech said nothing can be done. 
I wish I had the money to throw all my Kenmore crappy appliances away and replace them with a decent brand.

this place gets a star for the two things I liked about them:
1) they were the only place in Reno that allowed us to bring in a cat.
2) they were super helpful getting us a good rate for the night, giving us a AAA discount and also waiving the pet fee.


First off, they still allow people to smoke in the sorry excuse for a casino that is attached to this place which means that the whole place smells like a giant cigarette took a dump in the lobby. Even the non-smoking floors reek of smoke. 
The room was old, and I mean OLD. the carpet doesn't look like it has ever been changed since the place opened. This place obviously used to be a holiday inn, you can still see the holiday inn logo on all the room number plates. 
Avoid the diner in the lobby at all costs, it was just about as depressing as things get and we're talking about Reno here.

honestly though I feel bad giving this place 2 stars, I wish I could give them 2.5.

Check in time was very long ( not in line, just waiting for clerk to check me in) Waiting area just adequate.  Was promptly notified of my vehicle being ready, but took 25 minutes longer than promised. Will return because of ease of scheduling and proximity.

Tried the breakfast buffet.....don't bother!  Tried it for dinner one night.  Lobster bisque was good.  Buffalo chicken dip was very tasty but the chips on the stale side.  The bucket punch was pretty good but not quite worth $12.  Waitress.....very poor.  The also sat us at a table right next to the kitchen traffic which was annoying.  Too bad this place isn't up to snuff with the hotel attached because that would be very convenient.  However, I won't eat there again...too many other better choices in Clearwater Beach.

Keep making calls until you find someone else. 

Our first verbal exchange was me asking what the Chinese symbol on the wall meant. He said, "Let's stay on track." If you don't want people asking about your art, take it down.

On my FIRST visit, he was ready to write me a prescription for Electrc Convulsion Therapy. Dude. I was joking. He was not. Yikes!

He is about 13 years behind in psychiatry, psychology and pharmacology. His attitude is more like an angry accountant than that of even a pill monkey. 

He is so painfully unfamiliar with modern medicine and so incredibly insulting and rude. He clearly hates his work. He should move on.

Hint: The way to build rapport with your new client is to not insult their old doc who is 5x more qualified than you -- or to deny the tremors your patient is obviously having because you either haven't looked at said patient in the eye ONCE or just don't want to deal with it. 

Please retire or catch up. No. Just retire.
Very clean and the decor is adorable. All the food was fresh and flavorful. Highly recommend!

Unfortunately, this company is one of the only places that is authorized to repair sub-zero appliances so I have been stuck with them a few times and they never fail to disappoint, irritate and enrage. The customer service is inconsistent and unreliable. They frequently say they ordered a part and never follow up so I have to call sometime weeks or months later to keep chasing them down. They "fix" things that break again. Sometimes bill for services and sometimes don't. It is clear that nobody involved gives a damn. They should just shut their doors and let someone else take the business who has more of an interest in providing professional, efficient and friendly service.

Getting a refund took a week and a half, at least three phone calls, 20-30 min total time on hold, and speaking to three different people (call center employee, supervisor, and an email to the director).

John Daily, the call center employee, gave me the run around for several days.  When he didn't get back to me, I had to call back for him.  Instead of speaking to him, I was put on the phone with his supervisor, Felicia Jones, who offered me a partial refund ($30 of the $80 total I paid) because 1) my parents arrived 30 minutes late to a 3 hour workshop (they had trouble finding parking) and 2) my parents didn't bring a laptop.  This last part was the most confusing.

I let Felicia Jones know that 1) my parents' lateness should not automatically disqualify them from having a positive experience and 2) there was no advanced request for participants to bring a laptop.  In fact, I showed Ms. Jones my reminder documents saying that participants can either a) bring their own laptop or b) use a Franklin Institute computer.  My parents chose to do the latter.  Felicia then tried to get me to look at the description of the event online - which I did - and it also did not specify participants should bring their own laptops.  Despite this evidence, she said my story was heresay and there was nothing more she could do for me.  I offered to have my parents call her, but she declined.  At this point I was fuming and asked to speak to someone else.

She indicated her director was away but gave me her name number to contact her at a later date.  Because I'm upset at this point and do not want to drag this ordeal out any longer so I begin drafting an email to her director, Hillary Olson.

In the midst of writing, I receive an email from Ms. Jones saying that she was able to get in touch with her "manager" (no name given) and that my refund would be issued. 

I send my email to the program director (Ms. Olson) anyway because this is a pretty ridiculous mess.  I live in the city and before this point would regularly attend exhibitions and programs at the institute (hence me thinking this workshop would be a nice Father's day present for my dad).  I haven't gotten a response yet, but I'm not optimistic I'll get one, nor do I really need one.  I'm getting my refund and I am never going to the Franklin Institute again.
Nice small place with a strong varied menu suiting addressing lots of options --- burgers, big salads, fish and steak entrees etc.,.

Dr. Lang & Dr. Lee are fantastic! Their bedside manner is top notch and they are always upfront and transparent with my dental care. The front staff is always upbeat and willing to help in any way. I will never go to any other dental office!

music is entirely too loud - completely disrupts the peace of the block. The pizza is only great if you are blackout drunk. But really, they need to stop blasting music OUTDOORS after 9pm. Do you not realize there are people that live in this area??!

GO AGAIN? Yes, wonderful zoo with the highlight seeing an orangutang "outside" way up on top of the high poles swinging along the cables connecting the poles. Most surprising. Very nice zoo. And, go have soft serve ice cream at Nana's - delicious!

Went for a manicure prices are not explained properly was told $15 for manicure and $5 extra for gel polish but when went to pay it was $35 wonder what I understood wrong.
Any ways service was ok.should have a proper menu explaining the correct rates so its not embarrassing for me to ask price for each and everything.

This is a light review as I only ordered one item from their menu. 

A few co-workers and I decided to try out this spot due to one of them suggesting it. Upon entering the entire place smelled of BBQ... this restaurant has a pretty interesting setting, gives me outdoor BBQ vibes. 

I decided to get their side of beans along with a piece of cornbread. The beans were really good, they came with chucks of briskets and for a side, I'll have to say it was a pretty decent serving. 

The service here is absolutely amazing. My main reason for this review is because of their employee and the service I received. They were beyond friendly and continued to check in on us and went out of their way to make sure we were good. The service reminded me of Chick Fil A's. Overall, good food... great service.. comforting atmosphere.
Food was so-so. Great place for sitting at the bar; however, there are plenty of better options close by. Grab a drink here and eat elswehere...

A lovely place, like a slice of autumn and summer, with little vases holding big sunflowers and specials written in calligraphy on a giant chalkboard. Great if you just want a good environment and classic selections, because they've handled this perfectly. 

While the food is well-made, the portions are disappointingly small for how much you pay for it, so if you want to be rolling in yummy food for a hungry brunch this place might not be for you.

2 stars is for the food only. This place is definitely well staffed, quantity not quality here. We grabbed a drink at the bar prior to being seated. The bartender couldn't handle serving us and the manager took over. While our drinks were being made our table became available (first indication of slow service). The second half of our first round of drinks came well after we ordered food and food came about an hour+ after it was ordered. We finally got so fed up we asked for the manager, who also took forever to come by and waited until the opportune moment of exactly when our food came out, did not introduce himself as a manager and was quick to walk away. Again, the food was fine. My friends ordered me a surprise birthday dessert. It came out without a candle and the server saying, "who is the cake for". Slow drinks continued throughout the entire meal. We needed to leave before our last drinks came out so we had to cancel them. The manager was very defensive with is saying he had to pour out the drinks (they're so slow I doubt that happened) and was not apologetic at all for the horrible service, just demanded top gratuity after finding out we were in the industry. Bottom line, the service was so bad I will be telling my friends and local family members to stay away.
Food was cold. Had to resend multiple meals back. Please do not get the Mac and cheese. It was terrible. Maybe this was a bad day but I won't be back to this particular location.
Great food, but I miss my wood-fired eggs from the breakfast menu! I went to Helena Bakery instead but nothing like that there. Lucky Penny should bring back breakfast!!

This little gem wasn't open when I moved away from Tucson, but luckily I discovered it during my last trip to the old pueblo over memorial weekend, and I've been counting down the days until I could return!!!! 

A little twinge of sadness went through me as I learned they'd soon start selling gin they distilled themselves... Starting two days after I leave town.... Silver linings and all, I guess that's just a perfect excuse for a return visit!!!!

Try the cherry limeade. As soon as I read the description on the drink menu, all I could think was, "shut up and take my money!!!"  Seriously though. You can thank me later.

I hate Dunkin Donuts burnt tasting, awful coffee, but sometimes you have to bite the bullet and take what you can get. I needed a coffee at Philly airport and this was the only game in town. 

So, not only am I miffed about having to drink what I know is going to be, at best, an average cup of coffee, but the line is ridiculously long. When I arrived to the place its at least 25 deep. And it was moving soooo slow people were grumbling about it, giving up and going to their gate. Not sure what the hold up was, it's pouring coffee and swiping a card. Yet, it was taking forever.

Lame experience aside, if you want a coffee this is probably your best (and maybe only) bet near gate A20.

I've been here a few times and didn't have a complaint, but had to write this review because I was so put off by my recent experience. Our family came to Manayunk Brewery for lunch on a Saturday. I'm not sure if our waiter was having a bad day or what, but he was absolutely MISERABLE the entire time we were there. I could understand if the place was busy, or he was overwhelmed, but that was not the case. It was relatively quiet, with our family and a few other families seated in the dining area. It seemed like because we had small children (which so did other groups) he did not want to wait on is. He rushed us to order, and at one point when we asked for extra napkins, he gave us a dirty look. The other waiters and bussers were much more pleasant, but unfortunately his attitude really put us off for coming back in the future.

Decided to eat here on a whim. We rushed to stand in line on a Saturday evening. It was a relatively long line as we waited within the last 20min of the Reading Terminal closing. I got the cheesesteak with sweet peppers, mushrooms, fried onions, and american cheese. The cashier wasn't too friendly. It's CASH ONLY, fyi. The total was $11.45. I thought the cheesesteak was good but not worth the price. Not sure why it's that pricey. It may be because it's in the reading terminal. Probably will not return again because there are other good places to eat cheesesteak in Philly at a better rate.

The wait was long, so we got our name in and went to the Carousel Bar. This place was totally worth the wait. With a James Beard award in 2015 it totally lived up to its reputation. We ordered two pies (the prosciutto and calabrese), and the lasagna for our entrees. They were amazing. Particularly the pies were great. Fresh ingredients and the dough was next level. So airy and yet crunchy. The apps we ordered were also very good. The roasted cauliflower and the barrata were so good as well. Definitely make a visit here. It is 5 stars. An added bonus is it is in the Roosevelt hotel which has a few bars and is worth touring as well.

One teller with a line to the door is just unacceptable. They should have the "oh so important" people sitting in their offices come out and take care of customer. This is ridiculous. If I was not forced to bank here, I absolutely would not.  One star for the AC on a 90 degree day.

I couldn't add on water at the window whenever I visited but usually when I order water at the drive through they always forget it. They were charging me for ranch and would only give me one for 7 items and didn't fill up the small fries or large drink ... this is beyond me. And the thing about it all is I stopped by earlier that same day when it was a different window worker and everything went completely fine.

This place has the same menu as most every fast casual Greek Gyro joints I've been to, but everything I've had so far has been executed perfectly. The gyro meat has a little char to it that other places don't do. The chicken is tender and delicious. The salad is fresh and the tabouleh is terrific.
Maybe a buck or two more per meal than the Gyro place across the road, but it's been worth it so far.
Great value and great service. Give it a try.

Favorite nail salon in town. Jenny, Miri and Tracy are by far my favorite technicians. The entire staff is very friendly and the place is clean. 
I love that they have a loyalty card- every 10th visit you a manicure. They also reward you for paying cash!

Where to start. Probably the best meal I've eaten anywhere in the world. First we started with the green tomatoes, Ceasar salad, NY strip steak cooked perfectly with mashed potatoes and spinach finished off with a liter of a Chardonnay. The desert was too die for. I only wish something like this was in Atlanta simply amazing. To eat a meal like this on my birthday was awesome.
Been here many times and I am never disappointed! Their sausage egg and cheese croissants are the best! Their donut selection is awesome! My favorite is the buttermilk or glazed! Both very good!

I hate to rate this too harshly, but I was really underwhelmed. We had dinner which cost about the same price for 2 as ordering takeout with leftovers. I like the idea of variety, but the quality of food here just didn't compare to our normal takeout place. Also, they didn't have some favorites of ours like orange chicken or plain white rice. They did, however, have the Mongolian BBQ. But, they didn't open it until 5 which I didn't know so I had already grabbed a plate of food that seemed like it had been sitting out for awhile. The BBQ is good, but I wouldn't go here as my first choice for the BBQ. I will say it was pretty busy so maybe others really like this place.

I like the new set up the Grand Buffet. The food choices were good and there was a nice variety of desserts. Jonathan was our lunch server and he did an awesome job! His level of customer service was exemplary.

Just ate at this nudy's for lunch! First time at this location, and I loved it. I ate a chicken salad club on rye toast and it was delicious. 

I ate lunch with a fellow carnivore, she ordered a blackened tuna salad, kinda weird, but she loved it and loved the tuna. 

I've had the breakfast before and it was delicious. The French toast is awesome. Waitress was great!

5 stars!

Very few restaurants open, more than half of rides were not operating, and sanitation was spotty. We took the train with two year old twins and they were operating at one way trip to the next station. We had rented a stroller from the park and were told because it doesn't collapse we would have to leave it there, and that we could do a round trip, and to just stay on the train. The next station would not allow that and only due to us pitching a fit and the fact.that they still had room were we able to take it back. I recommended in the future to have one station call ahead and tell them what was going on to which I was told, yes you should do that. I will not be back.

Best authentic tasting Laotian or Esan food within the 50 states. Guaranteed! Their ganormai had us literally fighting over who get to sip the last drop.  The authenticity of these dishes were just how our family has been making for generations.  Our stomach were full and our hearts were fulfilled. Thanks to the owner and the chef for keeping Lao food authentic and for their generosity and hospitality.   So addicting, we drove 2 1/2 hours the following day for more. It was worth it.
