I got Pinworms twice eating here.  They have to start using gloves when handling fresh uncooked food!

Coming to this place was like a dream come true.  Love the design and feel of it.  Great service along with great apparel and drinks.  Very well put together.  An awesome idea pulled together by an awesome team!

First impressions are everything. Most of the time. 

I asked people in my social networks if they've tried this place yet, but Little Korea was pretty new (the server said they just opened a few days ago. the owners are Korean, just FYI). I've also never eaten at a Korean restaurant in Louisiana. Ever. So please don't ask me to compare it to Korea House or The Wandering Buddha...

Atmosphere
The music playing is Korean music, naturally. Big windows and cool lighting fixtures make this little place hip, comfortable, and casual. I think it's a good size; you don't feel too out in the open or too close to the next table. Brown and pink is definitely not a color palette I'm used to for a restaurant; it reminds me of a bakery (Sprinkle to be exact). 

Food
We ordered korean ribs and dumplings, and some other food... I really can't remember because my friends ordered the food before I got there. However, overall, the ribs were great, but I loved the dumplings! They weren't steamed, though, but I still liked the crunchiness and flavor. The Korean ribs weren't too chewy or hard, so eating it didn't stress me out (lol). I wasn't too fond of the side dishes (each person got a plate of 3 things), but what can I say... I never really liked kimchi to begin with -- I know! it's blasphemy, but I've tried so many times to like kimchi :[ and thumbs up for the rice for not being too sticky ^_^

Service
There are a few things they missed here and there, but it's not a big deal. They were still attentive and accommodating. The bus boy refilled my water only once though :( 


Why 4 stars
Overall, I liked everything, but it has so much potential to become well-established. I look forward to dining here again. 


On another note, I feel perfectly safe dining here, but maybe that's because I was here in broad daylight where vampires can't get to me (unless it's the Cullens, then we'd really have a problem).

Went to mesh on a rainy Sunday around 2 PM ready to FEAST after a night out. My friend started with the BLT eggs, presentation was lovely, but overall she felt they were just good. 

I ordered a chocolate milk to begin and although most people aren't coming here for a chocolate milk, it was delicious! Perfect amount of creamy.

Anyways, we then dove into the restaurant's soups. I got the Tuscan Bean Soup and it was pretty good! Not the best I've had but I was satisfied. What did surprise me was that there was a lot of bacon in it which they didn't mention to me or on the menu (beware vegetarians). My friend got the Lobster Bisque and once again gave it a pretty good, but she was hoping for more and bigger lobster chunks.

For our main course we both got the mesh salad, which sounded great as we are both beet fans and are all about the goat cheese.  The salad was very average.  Barely any beets, and wished for more goat cheese/quinoa... a little upsetting but it wasn't awful by any means. I also got chicken on mine which was a wee bit dry. 

I'm from Boston so I won't be back, but my friend who is from Indy would maybe give it another try for dinner.  Overall service was about 2.5 out of 5 as it took a while for our items to come out with very few people in the restaurant. 

Overall, filled our bellies but when we got home we weren't thinking, "WOW, that was GOOD."

The fiance and I went with a friend last night to check this place out hoping all the great Yelp reviews wouldn't let us down.  They didn't!  I'm being stingy with my 5-star ratings lately, but I'd give these guys a 4.5.

Appetizer: We tried the dry rub BBQ roasted wings.  They were really delicious.  Definitely not a first date food, but totally worth trying.  Very flavorful and something different than your ordinary run of the mill wing.

Pizza: We did a half Spicy Lei and half Wolf.  One word: Fresh.  All the ingredients were really fresh, every topping flavorful, and the crush was just right.  It's a thin crust type pizza, but not too thin.  We destroyed this thing.

Dessert: Peanut Butter Silk pie was fantastic.  At this point I sipped into a food coma and had to be carted out.

Also, a fantastic beer selection.  I had two of the Saigon IPA, something I rarely see on draft anywhere.  

Highly recommend!!

I love the miso ramen soup without any pork. It's my favorite thing to get. They ladies are real nice about making adjustments and they cook right in front of you so you know if you have some dietary restrictions you are in good hands.
Decent food but terrible parking lot strays me away from coming here more often. Seems to always be looking for help. Curious what that's about.

The Queen Village Sweat Gym is not a good option for child care.  The woman who watches the children is disinterested and unengaged.  She'll either turn on youtube on her iPhone or pop in a movie.  It is a huge room full of toys.  It is completely unnecessary to turn on TV.  Don't waste your money there.

It's been too long between bowling balls and that definitely factored into my lame game and low scores but I had so much fun here I barely cared.

On one side of our lane was a large children's birthday party that was booked to be there for hours. On the other side were some serious and pretty impressive bowlers who are part of a league.

Our group was comprised of friends from high school who just like to find different things to do and new ways to have fun.

And we were all there for the same reason - to have a good time.

This business has been in Tampa for a long time but the inside is well maintained and the operation is well managed. You will feel like you have stepped onto the set of a Happy Days episode due to a definite retro vibe but it is all part of the experience.

There is a concession area serving food and drinks, and that does include adults beverages. Walls well away from the lanes sport various arcade games, plus pool tables, so there a lots of ways to keep the kiddos busy.

You will likely find yourself singing along to the songs blasting on the sound system, making conversation with your bowling buddies on either side of the lane and even finding a way to make a few pins fall in between gutter balls.

I can't guarantee you'll bowl a strike but I can promise you'll have a ball!

Totally unimpressed with this restaurant's ability to serve customers.  We waited for 5 minutes before even being acknowledged.  There were several servers standing around, avoiding eye contact.  The hostess was on the phone dealing with a reservation.  There seemed to be no plan in places as to who helps the hostess when she is busy and people need to be checked in.  

LaScala's was recommended to us by one of our co-workers, who came with us on this evening.  She apologized profusely, as she had not had any problems on her two previous visits.  We had to ask one of the three servers talking to one another near the kitchen if they could seat us.  One did offer to seat us, but one kinda shot us a dirty look for bothering them.  

Two of us received dinner menus, the third a lunch menu.  When asking our server what "ricotta saluda" meant and was included in one of the dishes, she didn't know and had to ask another server.  She was then shadowed the rest of the evening. 

When the food came, we were not offered pepper, which was not on the table.  The server did not follow up to see how the food was, but eventually the shadow server did.  

The potatoes were bland, the asparagus was good and the meatloaf, their Monday special, was overly salty.  

The interior was starkly decorated, with large flat screens tuned to ESPN.  The audio over the speakers was a distracting mix of Christmas music and snippets of television programs that were disjointed and unentertaining.  

We may go back for lunch, which our co-worker was raving on, but not for dinner.  She could not apologize enough for recommending this restaurant to us.

The sushi was fantastic, not the best in the world, but for a quick little sushi place it was nice! However the service was horrible. The lady dropping off the rolls to tables was just plain mean. She would shout a number, if no one responded she would shout again while frantically looking around. Finally she goes "I wish you all would remember your damn numbers!" After this her and a guest got in an argument about a roll and how he could not place an order because they were closed. The restaurant closes at 3, and it wasn't yet 3 o clock.  Even though the doors had already been closed and locked 11 minutes early. Her and the guest were very loud and it was really unpleasent to be around. Finally the guest did get his order but the lady was yelling and mean and was arguing with his whole group at this point. One of the guys in the group asked for the tip back that he had paid and she refused.


The food is great! The other workers are great! The short haired lady who was in charge (Maybe?) just was not, and because of that I can only rate it three stars.

However, if you want sushi and a show, I recommend going late on a Saturday night! She will probably be around waiting to scream at guests that forget their numbers, or would like to order more food before close!

Do not go here if you are hungry! We went there tonight and they screwed up our order. After waiting an unreasonable amount of time my daughter went and checked on our order. It had not even been cooked yet! When we finally got our pizza it was over cooked and a nearly burnt!  I will never again go to this Giovanni's!

Wow-- I currently live in New York, which is the epicenter of global cuisine here in the US. I've been trying to find a good Malaysian place, and have eaten at a place that costs twice as much in Manhattan, and one that costs about the same price as Penang in Chinatown, NYC.
Neither of them compares. I went here with my girlfriend, and the prices are surprisingly low. We ordered Roti Canal, Hainanese Chicken, and Nasi Lemak. All are extremely delicious. The prices came out to be around $21. That's like an equivalent of ONE okay dish in Manhattan. This is definitely a Philly gem. Will be coming back again.

Great place for a cheap lunch on state street in the Paseo Nuevo plaza. I came here with my brother since we were hungry from shopping. For a cheeseburger combo (cheeseburger + fries + drink), a wipe out (chili on fries + onion rings with cheese and bacon pieces on top) and a another drink was ~$16. The food took a while but probably because they were still cooking the wipe out. My brother also didn't get a lot of fries in his meal (which he loves French fries) but overall, I give them 4/5 stars because of their friendly staff and great quality meal. This restaurant is also one of those where you clean up yourselves by throwing out the trash/leftovers on your plates and returning the plates in a bin. Don't wanna throw their wooden plates out!

Spending time in this restaurant was the nicest experience I've ever had at PHL. The seaweed salad was solid, tuna roll was fresh, and the ramen was much better than I was expecting to find in an airport. The decor and ambience are modern and relaxing. Yes, this is an airport restaurant, so it's pricier than what you'd get otherwise, but it's reasonable for what it is.

We stopped in on a Saturday for dinner.  We asked for one of the tables for two by the front windows so we could watch the people go by.  The waitress came over with fresh bread & whipped herbal butter for to us to nibble on after she took our order.  I started dinner with the Lobster & shrimp bisque.  My wife had the fig and goat cheese salad to start.  Both were good.  For the main course I had the coconut gulf shrimp over flat noodles and green curry.  I was a little surprised they left the heads and tails on.  It made it a bit messy to eat.  Oh, well...  The taste made up for the inconvenience.  My wife had the braised short ribs.  She raved over them.  Finally, dessert came, a champagne mousse made with local Pontchatoula strawberries.  What a way to end a meal.

Service was good.  They had a simple but effective wine list.  Picking a wine for dinner wasn't a problem.  Zeenat, Sara's Mother, came over to chat with us while we were waiting for the dessert.  It was easy to find parking on the street right across from the restaurant.

My services at Mimie's House of Beauty was great my stylist is Angel she has done an excellent job on my hair. She is so professional and knows a lot about hair. I chemically damaged my hair and she actually brought it back to life. So, if you are looking for a stylist ask for Angel.

I would rather rent a car from satan himself then ever do business here again. I made a rental reservation several months ago, called ahead and they advised they would take a $500 deposit, and that would be it. Upon arriving to pick up the car they inform me I must pass a credit check, I explained that I had not been told that previously and they essentially shrugged their shoulders said I'm sorry there is nothing we can do. I asked to speak to a manager and was told they weren't present and there was no way to reach them (suspicious). 

Had they told me about the credit check I would have forgone a few actions that dinged my credit last month, this was entirely preventable. 

There was a local place down the street (atlas) that was able to get me set up and the rental was cheaper for a bigger car. Moral of the story, do do business with dollar rental at the Nashville airport. 

I did talk to all of the rental companies who were overbooked but empathetic to my plight. 

It will be a cold day in hell before I ever give dollar one of my dollars!
Fries were old, pies were from 920pm. I ordered at 121 and got my food at 205 after I watched an employee roll a blunt in the dining room.

I no longer can justify going to these places. The prices are through the roof. The drinks are bad. I have never been in establishment with so many employees that do so little. Sometimes there are more employees than customers and I still get a sub par drink and the privilege of waiting 10 minutes for it.

Oh Cabo's whatever did you do?! You have painted the once lively and colorful place grey (who wants to sit in drab grey at the beach/), raised your prices and you have changed your staff so drastically that I am not sure I even want to return to this once favorite place I frequented while living there and visiting throughout my 40 years! Debbie made this place buzz with energy and she was always greeting people with a kind and friendly smile which made up for a lot that had been lacking. The service now is forced and semi unkind. The amount of ice put in your margarita pitchers is excessive - Note to consumers - go for the medium, as there is less ice and actually more margarita! My friend and I were able to barely get two each from the large and over two each from the medium. The al pastor tacos were very good, but the carne asada lacked flavor. Where did you put the seasoning salt for the chips? The tri tip is till my go to and the chicken is still delicious. The outside table are an improvement from the old and very shaky plastic ones. And what happened to the jukebox? I just don't know if I was even in the same place! I had to keep looking around like it was a bad dream! I remember coming here back when it was Chuy's and believe me, I would take peeling paint, rough floors and the old school feel any day when there were servers like Debbie and others like her that made this place really special. Its still Cabo's and it is still Carp and that may be the only reason I would go back.

Great food. Abt omelet was tasty and generous with the bacon. Stuffed French toast banana Nutella pancakes were delicious and big enough to share with you whole table. Long wait for food, probably 40ish minutes. Inside was lively and friendly. A few flies that pestered was the only downside.

What got us to walk in was the hollographic video displayed on the sidewalk -- that's how they get you! We stood there checking out the fish swimming around and a guy came out and asked if we hang dinner already. We had not. ZING! The place looked pretty happening, but then he pointed upstairs were there were several people checking out views from the roof. We were sold. We went upstairs and the trendy rooftop bar was so cozy and inviting. We ended up ordering off the bar menu because they didn't have the regular menu upstairs. It was sufficient because I didn't feel like a full meal that evening. There was a lady playing the keyboard and singing and the weather was just perfect. Lovely views of the St. Pete pier and nice fire pits and lighting. Will be back.

Great breakfast and very filling! Although its quite pricey, the flavor of the food was amazing! I had a brunch item called if you finish this you get an A+ and it was a LOT of food! All of it was great! Eggs were wonderful, the toast was good, the house fries were cooked well and tasted good as well! Also tried the stuffed french toast which was huge and quite good as well!

Wow, this place is amazing!  Make sure you sit outside when you come here.  The garden is breathtakingly beautiful, complete with quaint umbrellas over the tables and a lovely fountain in the center.  The service was prompt and friendly.  I had the shrimp and grits for brunch, and it was one of the best meals I've ever had in New Orleans!  I must have scarfed it down in under 5 minutes!  Amelie is definitely a must-try for the French Quarter.

Last time I came here there was a very long wait in the drive thru. I thought it was a fluke and tried again. Waited 45 minutes today. There were only 4 cars in front of me. Wish I could have pulled out but it's blocked by shrubbery. Never wasting my time here again. Came here for fast food but a sit down restaurant would have been quicker.

I have been hearing good things about this (T.H.I.S.) place --hah!  I decided to make a quick stop in here between appointments to grab something to go and, also, case out the place. 

The decor is minimalistic and modern. The space is nice, albeit small. The furniture is not the most comfortable, but there is still a cozy feel to the place. 

There is definitely a "mom and pop" vibe to this place that is appealing. The customer service is great, and the people who work here are very friendly. I was told that they specialize in gourmet hot chocolate. Certainly, the hot chocolate menu was extensive with some great options like orange, spicy and almond. The hot chocolate here is not made from syrup or powder, but, rather, actual chunks of chocolate. It really is a purist's hot chocolate! Verdict: delicious. Be warned that they may not always have soy milk available. 

I have not yet had the chance to try any of the espresso-based beverages, but I'm looking forward to revisiting T.H.I.S. place to try them out. 

There is parking at the rear of the building, accessible from the alley. However, there are only 6 spots available that are usually taken from the afternoon onwards.

I don't know how this place got a such high rating.  I saw the advertisement on Wechat and the food pics looked good so I decided to come here and give it a try. 
Pros: the oyster uni spoon is good. 
Cons: 
1, super understaffed, bad service and management. We waited 20 mins for our first dish. That's fine. After finishing the first dish, we waited another 30 mins to get the second one. Wth? And waited another 15 mins to get the third. I was about to order more, but no, I'm not waiting no more. I mean, it's a Wednesday afternoon, not even weekend night. 
2, the food looks super fresh on the ad pictures. In reality it's not as fresh as it presented on the commercials. I ordered toro tray and uni, which are their signature dishes, but the toro is a letdown. 

My bf said this is the worst sushi place he had ever been. I'll give it a 3 star tho.

This is a SCAM! They charge a deposit and continuously "reschedule" your "appointment" then when you call or text them, you NEVER get a response back. And the voicemail is conveniently full. Then on the last "appointment" date, they never show up. Ron and this so called "company" are scam artists. And I truly believe that all the 5 star ratings are from buddies of theirs to help sell the scam. Don't waist your time.

Went in to Pinewood last Saturday for the first time and had a really awesome time! Danen was our server on the patio and he really went above and beyond to make sure we had a great time. He  took the time to explain the menu to us and the outdoor games. After we had had guacamole and some drinks outside he also took the time to get us a table inside and check on us as well as the server we had inside. He really made the experience a lot better and makes us want to come back

I thought I was a fan.  Went there about 2 years ago with a business group.  Loved the food and the martini's.  We started out with a grumpy waiter but by the time our dinner was over, he was smiling.  I returned to Nashville this past week and couldn't wait to to go again.  I was a bit disappointed.  Ordered salmon sushi mi and 1 very thin slice cost $4.  I can have 4 thick slices in Memphis and it cost the same.  Our waiter this time was very knowledgeable.  Don't know if I'll return.  Might have to scope out a new place in Nashville.
Very aesthetically pleasing. The people are very nice and make you feel comfortable. I highly recommend!! This place actually made me like sushi

This is a nice place to satisfy your sweet tooth craving. My favorite there is their glazed doughnut and the powdered cream. Occasionally, they make the gigantic sized donuts, but I have not been brave enough to try yet. The people behind the counter are always nice and patient with me. The apple turnovers are really good too!!
Everytime I come back home. I always stop here to get some good ole fashion Cajun food... I prefer these oysters than Dragos. It's a bit pricey but we'll worth it. Delicious that's all I can say...

My fellow Americans! Pay attention and heed my warning! This place is a freakin cesspool! I went there to get a breakfast burrito and found a thick, long black hair in my burrito! Alicia B. knows all about what goes on down at Viva Burrito, so be sure to read her review as well. I wish I saw her review before eating lunch there.

First of all, the service is terrible. It took them about 8 minutes to get me my burrito. There's also a bunch of cheap, overweight, skanky chicks with all kinds of attitude problems working there too. While waiting for my burrito, I managed to see who was working in the kitchen and caught a glimpse of, what look to be, a ghetto ass dude who looked like he was up to something. Now, being a person who usually gives people the benefit of the doubt, I went ahead and just waited for my burrito.

I got home after a long morning of classes and sat down to eat my burrito. First goddam bite and I notice a fat, heavy black hair stringing from my mouth to my burrito! At that moment, I bee lined it to the restroom and vomited like a drunk high school girl on prom night. I'm not even making this shiz up. 

I just want to get the word out to others who might be thinking of having lunch or breakfast at this place. DO NOT GO THERE! You'll end up like me with your head in the toilet or at Alicia B's hospital up the block. I'm pretty sure if I ate the rest of that burrito, I would've been laid up in Alicia B's hospital and taking meds for weeks. I don't know though, I'm not a psychic. I'm just a normal dude who wanted lunch. Anyways, stay vigilant people.

-Joey Rockafella

I feeling wast my money for this menu that couldn't eat at all
When the menu said all noodle special comes with a sushi roll and doesn't happen at all... I don't why need a menu when is black and white write. Is that mean  restaurant can be not following the menu when is one

This place is pretty affordable but it is slow as hell and you can be waiting for hours to get any little thing done. Only recommend if you have limited money and lots of extra time, bring a book and maybe a puzzle for the wait.

What happened here? I come here at least once a year and it's always been above average. Maybe today was just a huge miss...

As usual I sit at the bar and the staff is super friendly. I get what I always get, the seared scallops, pork belly, and foie gras (in that order, because the scallops and pork belly go perfect together!) Unfortunately they were out of pork belly :( so I got the carpaccio instead and it was good except they had rather large chunks of sea salt in certain areas which made me grab the water immediately. 

Next were the sea scallops; perfectly cooked, not chewy at all. It came with a light sauce that complimented the scallops through every bite. 

Finally the foie gras, saving the best for last! This was the biggest DISAPPOINTMENT!!! The foie gras itself was cooked excellent, however the pairings are just as important as the foie gras! Every single time I've got it there is always a small apple crumble or apple strudel (that you can tell was made in house) that offers the perfect texture balance for the the foie gras. Instead of the strudel, there were cooked Granny Smith apples. I can see why they chose that, because the crunchiness and tartness of them is a good balance, however there was no sweet element on the plate which the strudel took care of. The "sweet" element was the fig (I think it was fig) compote. I couldn't even tell what it was because it was so bland in flavor! Normally my foie gras plate is cleaned 100% but this time I finished half of the apples, none of the compote and forcefully ate the foie gras without the pairings. 

With such a bad experience already I was ready for dessert because that should save the night for me. I ordered the creme brûlée and I sincerely regretted it. The topping was torched for so long that the container was hot and the filling was melted! I thought I was eating ice cream. The topping was so hot that it even melted the chocolate they placed on top. 

This has always been my go to place for food in grand Sierra and after this I don't know if I'll be back. 

P.S. I'm going to post a picture of the foie gras I got tonight and the foie gras I had the last time I was here and you'll see the difference.

They are in need of customer service training. I walked into the store at 8:35 this morning. The girl behind the counter was trying to untwist cup tops. I greeted her. She didn't look up. I waited thinking she's going to say something. She turned her head to the right and spoke to a coworker but then continued to look down. After about 30 seconds or longer she looked up and rudely said, "it'll be a minute!"  I turned around and walked out. She showed no concern for me as a customer and felt no urgency to help me. All she had to do was kindly greet me as I walked in and let me know she'd help me as soon as she finished her task. Being ignored is horrible customer service. If Krispy Kreme was on the way to work, I would have swung by there. I ended up on the one on Campbell and they were very accommodating. Too bad I had to visit 2 DD for a dozen.

Horrible!  Our kids had their prescriptions for contacts/glasses done in May.  My husband noticed some additional charges on top of the exam and the rest of the fees, he questioned for "fitting" the contacts, which he was told was for showing them how to get them in the eyes.  Our oldest didn't need this done, so he complained about the fees being so high.  They offered to refund that part of the fee.   Now I went to go order more contacts online and was told that the prescription isn't  valid. I called and was told by Antoine that because they offered us a refund of that portion of the bill, they also rescinded the prescription and it's no longer any good.  If I want them to tell the supplier what the valid prescription is, I have to pay them $60 first.   No thanks, I will take my business somewhere else.  

Also, before this incident,  the contacts they provided my youngest he was having trouble putting in his eyes. He had a box of trial lenses and it just wasn't going well  The texture of the lenses was very difficult to work with, so we asked if we could have a trial pair of a different brand, the ones his brother and father both use, to see if it may be easier for him to work with.  We were told no, not without paying for another exam/fitting fee!

Avoid these people! I will never step foot in there again.

Went into this maggianos because I went to others in Florida.  Ordered and everything went find until they passed around our food to another table and tried to then give it to us. Manager remade it an chef came out and apologized.  I got the rigatoni D.  Shredded chicken on it was not great.  Food was overall mediocre. Then they tried to charge me for an extra meal.  Lol.  Oh well.  Won't be going back here.

I find gelato to be the shining example and aspiration of all ice creams everywhere...

This rich and creamy treat is a great reprieve to the Florida heat any time of the year, and Paciugo's does it right.

This shop, conveniently located a short walk from The Museum of Fine Art in St Petersburg has a large selection of freshly made flavors sure to satisfy any palate.  I tried the coffee concoction they had for the day, which included dark chocolate and espresso chunks.  That'll wake you up!

Servings are modest and a bit expensive but expected for these home made confections, and the tasting of any flavor is not only expected but encouraged.  They want you to leave happy.

Seating is limited, with some outside which is okay in the cooler months.

Good selection of vegetarian options at decent prices. Had the falafel salad and spinach pasta salad. Pasta was overcooked but dish had good flavor. Falafel salad sounded better than it tasted. The dressing it comes with is odd and leaves a bitter dryness in your mouth. Portions are large, service is friendly. I'll give it another try, but not the falafel salad.
I've heard about this restaurant for sometime but never got up this way. I am very glad I did. veal piccata  was delicious as well as their cheese pizza. I will definitely be back to try the filet!

Way way way over priced. Holy crapoly 12 pieces of clothes $116. No freakin' way should I be paying $8.94 for a silk shirt to be cleaned. I don't care how nice the place is inside and if they give me my own locker. $1.99 on 86th street in front of Sam's club does just as good a job and, well they're $7.00 less or more per garment. Stay away don't be sucked in. I think I'll open a $1.99 Cleaners in The Village of West Clay!!!

After taking a break due to vacation (yay!), I'm back to writing some reviews. I'm sure you've missed me, haha!
When moving to a new area of town, you have to go about finding new places to get things done-a new nail place was on my list! I ended up finding this one via Yelp (close to me, I could've walked!) and headed over to get my pedicure before our Disney vacation. One thing I will say about this place, make sure you aren't in a rush-they take their time and truly make it a relaxing experience. I'm used to about 45mins for a pedicure + drying time. I think when I left, I had been there an hour and 15mins and there wasn't a line. They have a diverse color selection, which not all places do- I went with silver sparkles! 
It was generally quiet here. Sometimes the nail techs would talk to each other (and of course, to their customers), but mostly, it was a quiet, relaxing atmosphere. Everything looked clean and well-taken care of. My pedicure still looks good, even a few weeks later. When it's time to get my toes done again, I will head back to the Golden Nail.

While shopping at Scheels next door and because of the reviews we stopped in for lunch.

I really liked the ambiance and the decor it is pretty cool.  The service was excellent.

The pizza did not blow my mind and it was a bit expensive.  I gave it a three for the overall experience but food wise maybe 2 3/4.  It was good but would not go out the way to try it again.

Overall: Dissapointed

Pros: affordable food, family friendly, good spot to watch sports.

Cons: busy, dark, loud, unimpressive food.

20 minute wait on Friday night, quick attention from waitress & bus boys, puddle under our wobbly table. Guacamole & chips where fine but the guac is served as a small scoop on top of a bowl full of shredded lettuce, you think you can scoop down deep but no-can-do, instead you get a lot of iceberg.  Would have loved fajitas, but there spin didn't sound so good.  Hubby & I each got okay tacos & flagged down waitress for bill to get out of there.

My husband claimed El Churro is better but I've never been.

Fork is such a vibe. If you want a laid back, cool but a fine dining experience, this is your spot. I love the dimly lit ambience with a bar in the center. The service is impeccable and the menu drool worthy. 

We started off with a beet salad with spiced cashews and ricotta. It was a good starter. Next we had the focaccia bread with onion jam. I love it because it was not dry and a good doughy texture. The onion jam consisted of caramelised onions. The octopus was probably the best dish on the menu and we almost did not order it and were so glad we did. It was charred and seasoned well and also had a hint of lime. Wild rice was our main and nothing wrong with it, but would probably not go back for it again. It was my husband's birthday so we ordered the coconut custard and the flourless chocolate cake. Loved the cake- very rich, decadent and just perfection. The coconut custard was slightly lumpy and a little underwhelming.

I only eat halal and kosher meat so was super sad that I couldn't try the burger. It sounded amazing. But overall, very happy with our experience. We had great service and had the most helpful and polite server. Five stars to him!

Once upon a time, The Royale served my favorite brunch in St Louis.

Once upon a time.

I was here a few weeks ago with some friends.  It had been a while since I had been for brunch, and the menu had changed.  Gone was the complimentary fresh squeezed orange juice and a few other items.  I ordered the biscuits and gravy, remembering their tasty, classic sausage version that I'd enjoyed many times before.

That was a mistake.  I should have read the menu more carefully, because the biscuits and gravy are now "southwestern".  (check out my photos)

Umm, what?  

They were just plain weird.  Not at all what I would have thought of as biscuits and gravy.  Although the portion size was generous, it wasn't very tasty.  The biscuits were dry enough to have the consistency of a dinner roll.  The gravy wasn't actually gravy, but a thin sauce with bits of bell pepper and corn (corn?! floating on top of biscuits and gravy?!) in it.  I stopped eating before I even got half way through because the gravy had pools of oil floating on top.  A huge turn off.  On the other hand, the scrambled eggs I had were cooked perfectly.  They were light and delicate and flavorful. 

The service was spotty.  Our waiter was a total flake, but thankfully another waiter stepped in and took care of us.  The second waiter was great.  Too bad he hadn't been with us the whole time.

I still really like the ambiance of the Royale, I just wish they'd go back to their old brunch menu.

First time there. Loved the courtyard! Excellent server named Anthony. Gnocchi is AMAZING! Just melts in your mouth. Very fluffy. Highly recommended. Stay away from the calamari. Very bland and way too many onions. We will def be returning!

My daughter came here shortly after it opened and got a full set, extra long, and coffin shaped. She went in yesterday 2/25/19 for a full set, and the nail technician did a horrible job. Went back the next day to have the owner remove and replace the set and he insisted on charging me again. Claimed he would only file and not give her the shape or length she wanted.
I would think that a new business would want to keep their customers happy...especially a NEW business in the area. It wasn't like she came in a week later....I took her back the NEXT DAY and he insisted on charging me to fix it properly.
Will never come here again, it's a shame because it's so close to my house. Not mad at the work that was done, I'm more frustrated that management wouldn't make it right.
Hope this helps anyone

I was extremely disappointed when I dined here..... Extremely. 

So for starters there was a wait. Now that's understandable, as popular places get lots of action and can cause a wait, but.... This was a week day at around 11am so I can't say I expected much of a wait. Therefore, when the guy at the door said the two young ladies standing there were before us would be seated in ten minutes, and us shortly following, I thOught, no biggie. 

Well, come to find out there was another duo outside that had dutifully signed the list as they were instructed to do, but since the guy at the door want paying attention, he sat the other group first. So out of order! So, long story shorter, we didn't get seated for about 35 minutes. Bummer. 

So. Unlike my guy who was losing patience as hunger increased, I was keeping positive. Wait time, no biggie, the food will make up for it. Well. Unfortunately it did not. He got a breakfast sandwich that was dry... Like literally, nothing on the bread, and his cheese wasn't even melted. My bananas foster French toast was decent at best, but my potatoes were BURNT... Couldn't eat em. And, my last and greatest disappointment....I asked for a side of one scrambled egg white. It came on a plate soaked in oil. Literally soaked in it. I tried using my napkin to blot off the oil, but there was so much I just cOuldn't stomach eating that stuff. 

Totally disappointed. Can't understand how so many people rave about this place, and my experience was so awful.

We went for brunch. The building, music and huge amount of space are all positives. The service is nil. We got our own water, coffee & had to bring our food upstairs ourselves. No problem but seems a bit pricey for no service. The food was good overall. The coffee was not so good, at least to me. My husband got a delicious dish that I got to share - The Hatchery - it was in a coconut-flavored sauce with fry bread. Super flavorful. The gift shop has great Nashville treats. Fun spot.

Excellent experience,being our first time. Food was very good & warm.
I would ask for napkins, chopsticks & additional cup/ plate to divide dishes up. The soup broth container had no room to add vegetables.
I would rate our experience a 4 star.

Always "understaffed" and full of ghetto dwellers. Garbage wawa. Never go here unless you want to wait an hour for gas and have employees be rude except the one girl who pumps gas. Outrageous how "understaffed" they always are. Pathetic. I lived in the midwest where things were self serve and NEVER encountered a gas station with 8 pumps closes for no reason. Garbage store. The manager jeeds to get some stones and start clamping down on these lazy turds

#Starbucks continually pushes using the app, but there systems have been down at this location for a week now.  Not only am I losing points each time but it's inconvenient!  I don't know if it's a management issue or a cost issue. But nonetheless get your act together #Starbucks.

Ordered two rolls via Waitr. For two people. Order came. Missing napkins, one set of chip sticks. When I asked the delivery person we were missing stuff his reply was that's all they gave me. Called manager who wasn't in. Left number.  No call back after  6 hours. $32 for this.  Never again
hands down the best in the city.  their prices are half that of starbucks, and the milk foam designs are twice as beautiful!   I go out of my way to go to la colombe

We walked into this place with no reservation on a Friday night and this is what I can tell you:
Sit by the bar, ask Jason to make you a drink and suggest a few dishes. You will have so much fun enjoying the amazing food and watching him making drinks and working. It is always refreshing to see someone giving his whole heart to his job and loving it. The way he makes drinks and the quality of the dishes are all a combination of art and taste coming together.

1st impressions are lasting impressions. Went in last night to get two subs and two young men working there and no hair net in sight. This doesn't typically bother me but when I get home and pull hair out of one of my sandwiches, it becomes a huge problem. Unfortunately I went right before it closed so I called to let them know I would be back the next day. 

No apology - I'm ok with you making a mistake, but to not acknowledge the mistake or to not apologize for the mistake, is a huge problem. The 'manager' didn't care nor did he say he was sorry, when he was the one who made my food the previous night. 

1 Star- Why, because you can't give 0 stars. I will not tolerate hair in my food, even if it is a mistake, but to not care about the mistake, yeah, you won't get my business anymore. 

Tighten up Jersey Mike's because your reputation is on the line. And when you have young people working in your restaurant, it may also be a good idea to have them trained in customer service and proper food handling and etiquette. It's nasty to get all the way home and find your root hair in my food.

If you like watered down ice coffee or burnt hot coffee and not getting exactly what you order food wise you'll love this Dunkin. The other one down the street Id rate at 3stars it's bigger and cleaner yet both places make Krispy Kreme's coffee taste great

This was the worst breakfast sandwich I've ever had in my life.

It was a rainy morning, so my usual food cart was not open. I figured I'd try out Devon & Blakely since there's a big sign outside that says "Now Serving Breakfast Sandwiches." To start, they apparently don't care to understand English. I walked up to the counter and asked if that's where you order breakfast sandwiches. The woman looked confused and turned away. Eventually the lady behind the register came over and told the woman that I'd like a bacon egg and cheese on a plain toasted bagel. This is the point when I realized ordering here was a mistake. The person scooped out cold, pre-made eggs and bacon, slapped them on an untoasted bagel along with a couple slices of cheese, and squished the concoction into a panini press for a minute. I took one bite into the sandwich to discover it was cold, gross, and flavorless. The sandwich was around $6.50 with tax compared to the food cart which provides excellent breakfast sandwiches made to order for $4.

Never before have I reviewed a food establishment, and I created this account specifically to warn others of this terrible spot. Take that for what it's worth.

I was thrilled to hear a proper southwestern breakfast taco joint was hitting East Nash (brought to us from the Butcher and The Bee chef, I believe). Having lived in Texas twice, totaling around 7 years, I came to know the joy that is a breakfast taco. My wife and I gave this place a try this past week (opening week) with high hopes but we were let down in many areas. Yes there was an incredibly long wait (took a little over an hour to get 7 tacos) but we expected that on opening week and with it being in east nash. Tacos that tasted and looked as if they had been sitting under a heat lamp for 30mins and being filled about halfway (I'm not lookin for a 'merican sized taco here but not a laughable, pathetic looking portion either), decor that seemed more suitable for a cafe you would find in IKEA (although we loved the diner vibes present in this place), and feeling the stress of the kitchen were some of the most notable things. I do believe these guys are still finding their footing and flow. While we were disappointed with the food, we found the servers to be total pros (we even chatted with the bartender about some of these observations and she was very chill and open minded hearing all the feedback) and potential for the vibe and overall workflow of this place to hopefully settle in. They had a guy working on building a deck for outdoor seating while we were there, which is awesome. We didn't try the margaritas but they looked pretty tasty. I'm giving this place 3 stars because it does have loads of potential and it has great Nashville food people backing it. We will try going back in a month or two and see what has changed.

Do not buy a groupon from Power of Touch Massage!  I had the same experience as others.  I booked a massage and the day before he sends me a cancellation notice with no explanation.  This guy is a scam artist!!!  I might report him to the police dept.

Food was okay, service was quick and professional. They would of gotten 5 stars if there was just a few more meatballs in my sandwich.  There was more sauce and bread then actually meatballs. Sauce was good and so was the bread which is not normal for Florida

In leaving this review, I hope to help people make an informed decision when seeking a new home. During my short stay of less than a year, I was completely dissatisfied with the attentiveness, professionalism and awareness of the staff at Warrington Crossings. In reaching out for an ongoing maintenance issue, it took over a month of calls and contacting the corporate office to get the assistance needed. After their follow up, it was deemed the apartment, which was already having water infiltration issues, also had mold. With the right people and equipment, we were able to determine the mold extended into most rooms. However, the corporate office was unwilling to remediate this and their offerings for the inconvenience were less than satisfactorily. When they realized I knew what I was speaking of and that a legal case could be coming their way, they decided to allow me out of the lease early. This came only after they entered my home without my permission numerous days and sent nasty, unprofessional emails. As the health and safety of my family is top priority, I was happy to get out and start fresh elsewhere. It was confirmed with maintenance staff that there are many buildings on the property that have water infiltration and mold issues. Stay away.

We used to love this place but in the last year or so it has gone downhill considerably. Our lunch today included watery beer cheese soup, excessively greasy fish n chips and shepards pie with a flour gravy.

Not surprisingly we did not recognize a single face from the staff, I believe they have had significant turnover which is most likely the root of the problem.

A shame, an expensive shame.

Wiz Wit! 
If I was rating this Pat's vs. Geno's, I couldn't give Pat's enough stars. Don't be fooled by your friends, the lights, or the shortness of the line, you should still go to Pat's. Ok, now that I've got that outta the way, I must say, Pat's can be very hit and miss. Occasionally, especially in winter, you get a cold one, and there is nothing on earth more disappointing. But generally, they are great. If I'm bringing someone into town for a cheesesteak, showing them "Philly", we go to Pat's, the Original, the best. Its not the best around anymore, but you can't say its bad. Cheese fries are a must, as is a Birch Beer. I've never had my meal any other way. The rolls are unparalleled in their softness and taste so no complaints there.

I had a great experience until I asked a simple question about my gel dip and the gentleman doing my nails told me very rudely to hush. Then preceded to talk badly about me to the women next to him. Also they must have raised their prices. I got a simple pedicure and a gel manicure and it was $126. It's a clean place but never again.

Adrienne nails my cut & color every time!   Need I say more?!

Be sure to PREBOOK your appointments because Adrienne runs her shop solo and is booked out 3 months in advance.

This 24 hour after my review and I get contact by the location to come and talk whit them about this

I always open to talk and make thing right 
Because everybody wrong some time
Thank you for take care my situation so fast
Annoyed that there are no prices on baked goods in the cases.

Finally got around to visiting Yats in their new location. Same great food as always in a much bigger space, which I am still undecided on whether or not I like. Gone is the intimate feeling that the other location (of which I've been visiting since the late 90's) had. Four stars only because I'm still a little sad they've moved. 

Beer and wine are available in the 21 and over bar. You'll know you're in the bar when you a) see the bar stools and b) the handwritten "21 and over only"

A kick ass picture hangs next to the cash register. I want this photo.

Parking is much easier. Meters up front.. spaces in the back. Less competition with competing businesses for parking on the far end.

I purchased a pair of glasses here. After a few weeks the lens started to crack in the corners. I came back to the store and they told me the lenses were not cut properly and replaced the lenses. A few weeks later the started to crack again. I went back to the store and I was told that it might be because I chose transition lenses with the type of frame. If it happened again that I might need to choose different frames and that maybe they were causing the cracking issue. I wore them and immediately they started to give a headache. I went back to the store assuming the wrong prescription was used. The receptionist told me the prescription was correct and there was nothing they could do. I refused to take that for an answer so I went to the glasses technician. A sales associate politely asked if they could help, bent and adjusted the frames and it worked. No more headaches. But, needless to say the lenses started cracking again. I stopped wearing the glasses and was very frustrated that I had been to the store 7 times over a pair of glasses. I should have immediately contacted the store when I noticed the cracking. I finally contacted them today (months later)and they said that they told me last time I was there that they would not fix the glasses again. I was not told that. If I had known that I would have asked for different frames to avoid having to drop off frames and later pick them up for the fourth time.  I have worn glasses for over 20 years. I have never had any of the multiple glasses I have worn in my lifetime crack. I do not recommend buying from this store at all. Total waste of money and 7 trips to the store.
The service was great.  My dish included fried chicken and shrimp.  It was delicious!  I enjoyed this restaurant and would like to visit again on another trip.

Ulta is a supermarket for beauty products. It is a more affordable Sephora. They carry a wide selection of make up from Maybelline to their own brand to Hard Candy. You can also find a wide variety of hair products, including Chi flat irons. They also have a salon in the back, although I have never let them do my hair. Ulta is a must for any fellow makeup enthusiast on a budget!
I enjoy eating from here. Their garlic knots are my favourite! It occasionally takes a while for them to fill the order but it's always delicious. The staff are always positive and approachable.

Their chicken ceasar salad cost me $5.66. Its a side salad with only lettuce, croutons and chicken and there wasn't even parmesan cheese.  Their excuse for why such a small salad at a high price was its a quality salad.  Bullshit for a $1 more I can get a regular size salad with parmesan cheese and eggs from The Works.  Thats what a real Chicken Ceasar Salad is.

Overpriced, overrated, unorganized, new restaurant establishment in Newtown. 

First I can start with overpriced house wines. My Wife asked to tried a sample of their house Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc. She didn't like either turns out it is Cran  Lake from California. Well I'm familiar with that 3 buck chuck wine that they sell at Trader Joe's for like $3 a bottle?  Better yet they charge you $8 for a 7oz pour. It's a shame that a wine bar would serve such low quality wines even as house wines.  
There is a fairly extensive menu with prices that don't seem to bad. That is until you get your portions. They come on a fairly small plate. I ordered scallops for $27 and there were only 4 on a bed of risotto. They don't serve rolls with olive oil and their meals don't come with salads. So you have to put up with the servers trying to up sell some flat bread or the harvest salad for $8 which was a small handful of spring mix. Ridiculous. 
To summarize it winds up being a expensive night out for low grade wines at a premium price and small portions. There are many alternative places where you can get a nice pour for $8 and it's not from a $3 bottle of wine! Many other restaurant choices where you can get a dinner out without being hungry when your done. 
The two hostesses that were seating people were stuck up and rude. They acted like the were doing you a favor. The wait staff was unorganized and slow.  Spent most of their efforts getting you to order apps and salads in addition to the meal. 
Although the Restaurant itself looks nice from the outside it was uncomfortably warm inside and extremely loud. 
I would recommend going to another place unless you like the taste of cheap wine, small plate entrees, rude service and an expensive check at the end of your dinner.

The nachos were good but they didn't have a full kitchen wich is expected its just a bar but they also only had wine based drinks but they had pool and darts and it wasn't very busy so all in all okay
This is the best fast pizza in town. Perfect when you're in a rush. Staff is amazing! Super clean place!

I'm not sure why so many people were raving about the Thursday dinner buffets here. It's rather overpriced given what is offered. Also, is it just me or am I the only one that consistently gets very poor service each time I dine here? It always takes at least 40 minutes to get the food, even with multiple inquiries on its status once I am able to hunt down some eye contact with the staff... Of course, that's after at least 10 minutes of waiting for the staff member to come to you after they first seat you!!! 
There have been multiple times when the food came out wrong, but after waiting a good part of your afternoon or evening, we give in and eat what the incorrectly served us. If you are part of a large party, be aware that they will serve each person at different times, so some are left eating while the others stare for a good 20-30 minutes.
As I complete this review, I believe I've decided that I won't be dining in here. Instead, I'll go with ordering take out after giving them ample time and reviewing what I am given before I leave. The things people do for good Thai food in middle America...

Well let's see before I went to Annunciation I was really excited.. I saw on their facebook page, Description  Fine Dining without feeling fussy...
Reservations recommended to avoid disappointment, especially on Friday and Saturday evenings.

Had two friends in town, one all the way from Italy so we wanted to have a good dinner, the four of us had a 9 pm reservation which is not to bad.  We arrived a few minutes early and they had us seated by 9:45 pm.

I did not remember all of the menu but I know we started with the Veal Sweetbreads with Gnocchi, Pancetta and Mushrooms, the fried oysters topped with brie and the crab claws, which were all nice.. but nothing amazing.

Then the turn downward started, the four of us wanted to try alot of things on the menu because we have heard alot of good things about Steven Manning and since he came from Clancy's we just expected it to be good.

We told the waiter that we wanted to try a few different things and to bring them in stages so we could all sample them and we ordered the, (2 ) Fried Soft Shell Crab with Crabmeat Meuniere Sauce. Crabmeat, Lobster and Shrimp Risotto
Angel Hair Pasta with fried oysters, Sauteed Black Drum with Crabmeat, Coconut Milk and Jasmine Rice, Veal Oscar, Veal Annunciation and the Slow roasted Pork Shoulder with Sweet Onion Relish. We had one more but I cannot remember what it was.

We told him we wanted to start with a nice white wine for the first dishes they would send out and it came out quickly and was nice.

A few minutes later he came out and told us they were out of soft shell crabs : (  so we just added another dish.

Five minutes later all 8 entrees came out at the SAME time.. they were nice to give us each a 6 inch plate so we could smash all the items together like at a buffet. 

The risotto was hard, a few of the dishes were cold and some just did not have much flavor, or it could have just blended into all the other dishes that were crammed onto my plate.

And of course only had that one bottle of wine, not the 3 that we were hoping to have over a nice 2 hour dinner.

I have to admit that the Maitre d and the GM were both great about the situation and I would have to guess that the waiter just was overwhelmed with the order.

So... maybe it might we worth another chance in the future... not sure : (

4 minutes ago

Everytime I go in there, I know I'll be waiting for an hour plus, even when I just need one tire replaced. 
I got my tire replaced about 3 months back and they did not tighten my lug nuts correctly and my car started making awful noises, a week later. Took it somewhere else to look and fix it, which was a $500 repair and they told me it's because when I recently got my tires done they were not put on correctly. I got that fixed. A month ago a tire they replaced 3 months back, popped. Took it in to get replaced, not a problem but ONLY 6 DAYS LATER that tire popped. They would not replace it, even though my man and myself called separately to make sure we could get it replaced under our circumstances and they said they could and set us up an appointtment to do so. I have had to replace 6 tires total because their tires have popped or lost tread very quick. 
I also have took my car in before because I heard a terrible sound coming from the back, after making me wait an hour and 1/2, not even driving my car to see, they said they saw nothing. Was seriously no help and a waste of my time.

I went to Curry Leaves with some coworkers for the lunch buffet. We love Indian buffets, so we were eager to check out Curry Leaves shortly after it opened. It isn't a huge place, but fortunately we did not have to wait for a table for the 5 of us. The service was great - everyone who worked there was extremely friendly and eager to please. Our water glasses were never empty! They were constantly making and distributing fresh naan to each person in the restaurant, which was awesome because naan does not keep well on a buffet. The food was  good...I probably would have preferred a bit more flavor/spice, but that's just me. I would definitely return, and am excited that they offer the buffet on the weekends, too!

I've been brining animals on and off to this hospital for several years now. They're one of the few places that treats guinea pigs in town and I've been so grateful for that. In the past my experiences were positive and the prices were very reasonable. 

As others have mentioned I've encountered the new doctor's preferences towards natural. I am all for treating our animals with exceptional care and providing them with the best we can. However, this practice needs to understand that its clients financial means vary greatly. Not all of their clients can afford to feed their animals organic goat's milk or organic produce on a daily basis. And frankly, this may not be how the client prefers to care for their animals and that is their choice alone.  

I found the feedback to how I cared for my animals to be condescending. I personally will not frequent this hospital again and advise you to do your research to see if your needs/values align with the current vet.

So disappointed. Restaurant is overpriced for the service and quality. The restaurant is beautiful with great artwork. This is the only good thing about it.  New Orleans is about hospitality and the service and management have neither. Snapper was salty. Two people order same entree and both came out with different portion sizes. Server tried to add automatic tip with a table of 5, but policy states table of 6, sneaky. Our water was never refreshed and we were never offered bread.

There are many restaurants in New Orleans which you don' mind paying top dollar, but this one is not one of them. It was Friday, the 13th. Unfortunately, 1st impressions are lasting impressions.

A coworker brought in some cupcakes a couple of months ago for a staff meeting, and they were so good, I had to find out where they were from. 

She told me about Phatso's in Chester.

A few weeks later, I needed a few dozen cupcakes for a baby shower, but with some special requests, including one peanut butter cupcake for the mommy to be. Not only were Rick and Co absolutely accommodating to my needs, the mother-to-be fell in love with her giant cupcake. 

This morning, I picked up two dozen assorted cupcakes to surprise my boss at lunchtime. The storefront is small, and most of the waiting customers were absolutely annoying, but the staff was wonderful. The cupcakes were a hit, as usual, and there has been a steady stream of people coming by looking for seconds for the last three hours.

Their prices are GREAT, to boot. Where other places are charging 2 or 3 bucks per cupcake, my two dozen came to $22.00 including tax.

I wonder if I can get a carrot cake or a Boston cream pie next time.

It is a 3 star hotel. I had no problems in 3 nights, but I am an easy customer. building is very old, room small. Location is amazing, central to anything you could want to see in center/old city. A bed bug or two... Staff was fine, Brian printed my boarding pass for me while complaining about the two russians sitting right in the lobby, who apparently were making a stink over $17. They indicated at check in that failing to turn in room key at checkout would be an extra fee.

If you just want to text your dinner conversation, then this is the place. Got there early and as soon as the appetizer arrived they sat a party of four next to us. We had no privacy and couldn't carry on a conversation. After we asked to be moved, we ended up with the sun blinding us! There were plenty of empty tables that they could have chosen. The ambulances with sirens blaring were a nice touch as well. Not worth the money. I can prepare better meals at home.

I remember when this theater was shiny and new. Now it is shabby and old. The fact that it sits out all by itself just east of the huge bulldozed area where Parklane Mall used to be doesn't help at all. 

It has an outdoor ticket office with is a huge negative. When you walk back towards the theaters, it seems dark and spooky. 

On the plus side there is plenty of parking. 

The last time I went, I saw a movie with a group of friends and I am glad I was not there by myself. Now I am not usually a timid person and I seldom hesitate to go places by myself, but this theater just seems a little dicey. Probably will not be back unless there is something really spectacular showing that is not available at my neighborhood Cineplex and I have friends to go with.

Spring break in Tucson: Took a group of 30 lacrosse players for tacos. We waited awhile for the large order, but worth the wait! Better than the tacos in No. Cal. One of the players chose UA over ASU based on the tacos at Boca! The Macho Taco is king.

Love this place! I came here both times I've visited NOLA. The Po'Boys in the back are so good and I really love the frozen Irish coffee. I wish the bartenders were a little friendlier, but we got what we came for!

Besides the little communication between Frank and us, the work performed was mediocre. There are lots of visible seams in the artificial turf in a high traffic walking area. We had to clean up an area from the old grass they pulled up! The workers left our rock work scraped hap hazardly so we had to replace with more river rock. We also had to add base to one side of the driveway ( where there was none ) and had to tamp down the base ( which I requested them to get) in the front of the driveway. The paver work is o.k with minimal unevenness, nothing to say, "WOW" to.  When we showed these to the project manager, his words were, "We are finished with this project." It seemed the workers had very little direction as WE had to tell them about the drains and dimensions and what style of pavers were to be put down!! Basically, we had higher expectations from this company.

Smee's has quickly become my in-laws favorite spot to eat and hang out and it's easy to see why. Don't be put-off by the fact that it's in a strip mall. Once you step inside, you feel welcomed and with the amount of regulars that eat here, it's like eating with family. 
The bar is well-stocked and well priced. The menu is extensive, but not overwhelming and you'd be hard-pressed to not find something that you'll enjoy on there. Having been a few times now, I've tried something new each time and been happy with my choice each time. Chicken wings, fish tacos, fish and chips, steak, hummus- all delicious. 
The owner, Vinita, is a well-known presence at the restaurant and will often stop by tables to chat up the patrons. She's a big reason why you will feel like family when you eat here.

Visited in the lunch time ,ambience was good, but very congested seating's. After our order taken by a staff, it took almost 45 minutes to get all our food, that was little frustrating because  customers came after us was served more faster than our order even though our order was just a Thali and some masala dosas. all the dishes were good ,some of them lacking the true flavors.
Most of the down side  of the visit was inattentive staff , we have to  waved our hands in the air several times to refill the water or  some second servings even for the check :(
If the management can rectify some of these then its a good restaurant to dine especially if you are from any of these  kollu/mallu/ tamil region

The tech was great, responsive and worked quickly in the intense heat. However, for an expensive AC/Heat system that is only just over a year old, there have been a couple major repairs needed.  Luckily it is under warranty, but the inconvenience adds up.  Again, I stress that Goettl is responsive and does send out people as soon as possible to diagnose and/or repair.

I was visiting from LA. This was a stop on the way to Andrew Lincolns Hermitage. Service was good. We sat outside. Fans help with the heat. Our server was on top of it. Food was good and tacos were good size.  Everyone got different tacos. The beef brisket taco was a let down. I should have gotten the carne asada. Everything else was good. Corn and plantains , chicken tinga, pastor, and carnitas.

Spent hours meticulously preparing and moving 25+ boxes and bags of baby items from our basement, so that they were ready for a 7am pickup this morning. Called the day of to confirm pick up. Hours go by, no pick up or notification -- after I called to follow up, was told that the driver did not want to find parking and continued to another stop.

The driver did not call even though a note was intentionally included on delivery instructions with our phone number in case of issues -- there was also ample parking on the street. We were waiting near the door all day. Customer service representative had zero solutions or recommendations and zero concern.

We now have piles of items sitting in a small backyard with no one to take them for the foreseeable future. As someone who has donated several times via Green Drop before, we will never use this service again.

I have emailed and called RPM so many times for help or some kind of solution to the continued harassment from my downstairs neighbors and have received no reply which has led me to writing this review. Yesterday I spoke with Samantha at RPM and she told me to email her at inforpmcorazon.com detailing all of the harassment from the people living below me and she said she would talk to the property manager which I'm assuming is you. No one from RPM has contacted me regarding the neighbor on all occasions that I've emailed and reported them, there have been zero offers of solutions to the harassment even when I OFFERED TO MOVE. The people below have repeatedly yelled at me, cursed at me, banged on my roof when I am getting ready for work or getting ready for bed. I don't get a phone call from Samantha or from anyone at RPM. I would really appreciate, if someone could give me a call or offer any kind of solution. The people below me feel that they can harass me because I live alone and am a young black woman and since RPM won't call me back, wont email me back and won't help me move it seems they condone this behavior. Any allegations my neighbors are making are clearly lies and I would like proof of all of the accusations that have obviously led to my apartment being inspected. I am sure that RPM was aware of how horrible the people below me are and that they are the reason the person before me moved out, if RPM was aware of their actions prior to me moving in then shame on you. I pay my bill every month on time, I have the right to feel safe in my home, this continued harassment and discrimination by my neighbors without any intervention from RPM shows that you and your company condone their behavior. They have two dogs as well that bark all times of the day the only difference is that I go to work everyday and they don't. This company doesn't care about its tenants.

Ordered a to-go goat dum biryani.
Good taste.
Nice cooked goat. Overall biryani was nice , not dry at all. Goat pieces were big and nicely cooked.
For that price though i was expecting a bit more in quantity. In that price you can get a lunch buffet elsewhere.
The front desk counter for billing charges a minimum 15% tip. For to go as well. Really ? .
You are forcing your customers to tip you.
Inside the Westin. great food. best Gooey Butter Bar I have ever tasted, the dessert was so good that i ordered it 3 times in the 2 days I was there.

One word: meh.  (Which isn't even a word, more of a guttural sound)

And my gut tells me this place is just below average. 

My $12 Mohito, was clearly watered down, lacking both simple syrup and rum. Especially lacking rum. I think they may have forgotten the rum. 

The burger, in spite of being piled with ingredients, was nearly tasteless. It's like they chose the exact opposite of fresh local ingredients. At $14, it was downright horrible.   There is a place in Detroit where I can get two craft beers and the best burger I've ever had for less than $14.

Salmon pasta was exactly alright. Not bad, just nothing to write home about, which is exactly why I'm writing about it here. Would have been happy at $10, but not $18.

If I had only tried the salmon pasta, I may have given three stars. My guess is an Olive Garden salmon pasta would have tasted much better. 

Some reviewers called this a "dive" bar. No, this is not a dive, it's just crappy. A dive bar would serve great beer and tasty burgers for cheap, in an atmosphere where sidling up to the bar, ordering a shot of Jameson's, and rambling casually with the other local characters, was the norm. A dive bar does not make stingy drinks. 

The atmosphere reminded me of a casual bar restaurant you might find in a small town somewhere. Or it reminds me of numerous touristy ripoff places in Times Square. 

Bottom line, foodies, diners with discerning taste, and pretty much anybody who has taste buds should stay away. 

* note:  the freezing cold fries were promptly replaced by our waiter with no protest. So service was pretty good.

Scared little pussies WOULD NOT COME OUT AT 1.30PM TO LOVE'S TRUCK STOP. "It's unsafe. If our driver is raped, we'd have to find you. It's not a permanent address - you don't live there." said the woman who answered the phone. Just some bullshit excuse. Called Domino's Pizza - they came out, NO PROBLEM.

We went here because our friends from Louisiana are die hard fans of this place. So I know we will be back next time we are in town. We went for Brunch during the weekday, so the place was only mildly busy. Still had customers but not packed, great for us! 

The place is a small hole in the wall. So for fans of that, this would be a great place for you.

It's not fancy, just simple tables and chairs. The cook can see you from behind a bar and you can order from the cook or waiter. This day the cook was our waiter as well. So service was a little slow. But understandable since he was the only one on deck. 

The food was ok, my potatoes were bland, but salt and ketchup dress that up quickly. We sat next to a very lively couple who kept talking to our group, so tables are close quarters and other people get in your conversations. Nothing wrong with being nice, as long as I am not hungry or starving. :P

The cook/waiter was very funny and nice.

Came here with a Groupon.  I really wasn't a huge fan.  We came in, and were not greeted at all.  Actually it didn't seem like there was anyone attending anything.  We decided to sit down and a man popped up with menus.  He never brought us drinks.  We waited for 20 minutes before he came over to take our orders.  We ordered everything at once.  He was not very personable.  People came in and waited to get seated. He asked if they were ordering out, then if they weren't just told them to sit wherever.  It probably would be better if they had a sign that said "Please Seat Yourself". 

We ordered a pork spring roll and pho, with a couple of boba teas.  The spring rolls were good. I thought there was a tad too much fish sauce.  The pho was bland and small. The boba teas were delicious and looked like they used fresh fruit for the strawberry one.

We finished eating and no one came to clear our table or ask us if we needed our check.  I got up and paid.  We left.  It wasn't the best experience. Miss Saigon is definitely better. I wouldnt go out of my way to come here. To be honest if I were in the area looking for pho, I would just go to Pho 88 or Miss Saigon.

I agree w cyn, it's not what it used to be before Katrina. I dropped my car there on the way to work for a detail. I find myself ALWAYS having to go over their job.  Imean seriously, how hard is it to clean a friggin windshield! It's what you look directly at when you hop into your car and I'm not too sure that the crew there can grasp this concept. Dusty instrument panel, half ass wipe down in the dash, wax not properly wiped off, too bad there isn't another "detail shop" uptown.

My father has been a long time customer of Stoveworks (unfortunately it's the ONLY wood burning stove store in the area) and has purchased a top of the line stove from them.  Throughout the whole process they told my father that he couldn't fit the stove he wanted into his hearth. So he measured the dimensions again and it would easily fit. After several conversation with ED, he finally came out to do the consultation.  Guess what, it fit! My father never got an apology for aggravation that they put him thru and has never had a pleasant experience with them.  I recently called the store to ask what time they were open til and instead on just giving me a time, Jan nastily asked me what I was looking for... I told her stove accessories.  She responded with a time quickly and hung up the phone before I could ask her another question.  So we got there about an hour before they closed.  I had my puppy with us so I thought it would be ok to ask if I could bring him into the store.  It was a freezing cold day and I didn't want to leave him in the car.  I was in the store for literally 3 seconds trying to find the owners when this woman swooped up on me and said "Oh no! No dogs! People are scaried to death of dogs and that's why I have to keep my dog out back!" I looked at her in amazement that she made such I huge deal about a little 5lb puppy.  She didn't even give me the chance to ask!!!!  She is so nasty.  So that is the last time I will EVER go there and I will make sure everyone who asks me knows the unfortunate experience I had.  I would like to give them 0 stars

I went here once a while ago and remembered having an enjoyable experience, so I went back. Hor. ri. ble. Coffee was ooookkkk but i could hardly drink it and ignore the potent urine aroma throughout the whole place. The only reason I stayed was because A) I was already there and B) I needed wifi. Maybe they were just having an off day, but there's other good coffee in Tampa.

Great place to stop for pizza and wings!!   I loved the thin crust pizza. Wing options - regular wings and boneless available. I ordered hot and blackened (cajin). I really liked the blackened flavored wings but not the "hot" so much. They weren't very hot and tasted a little bland.  I added some grated cheese which seemed to help a little bit.   The service was on point.. prices were average.  I will definitely be heading back the next time I am in the area.

I'm not really sure what the St. Louis style BBQ is other than the style of ribs, but the sauce on everything here is sweet.  Too sweet. The potato salad was even sweet. I gave them 2 stars because they were so friendly. Give it a try if you want because they were on the tv show bizarre foods, but you've been warned.

My mother, who is disabled and uses a service dog, was turned away from this location after being asked illegal questions about her dog (asked for "paperwork," which places of business cannot require and indeed does not exist in the U.S. for service animals). After being informed politely of the ADA law regarding questions that employees may ask to handlers of service animals in public locations, the employee then forbade my mother to make an appointment with the doctor on the grounds that "she is allergic to dogs." Now, first of all, how would she know that? But let's say she does. Why wouldn't she mention it when my mother walked in, instead of requiring phony "paperwork?" It seems retaliatory and is definitely illegal. Why does this location hate people with disabilities?

It is any way that a community can fired this company? They are bad managing our money. They think that we are an ATM and they are looking for the ways to get more and more money. I live in Talavera in Spring Hill. Rizzetta decided that the community need a new grass. So waste of money. They have plans to add stuff without asking the residents if we want or not that new stuff, but they want to increase the CDD monthly payment. So, when I moved to this community it was 598 houses, now over 700 and I cant understand what they did with all the extra money they received from those new residents. They have the water sprinklers on 3 or 4 times PER DAY, even when its raining. All that water and money wasted there is incredible. While I have to be careful at home with my expenses. 

I cant believe that I will have to move out of here just because Rizzetta doesn't know how to manage our money. I'm about to find a good lawyer and file a case against them.
