Dr. Curcio and her team are great! Dr. Curcio is one of the most highly trained physicians for cosmetics - why pay for a non-MD or a lesser trained physician to take care of the way you look?  The offices are beautiful too.

I bought a Groupon for an oil change with an expiration date of 90 days.... Called to make an appointment 10 days before the expiration date and was told that they are fully booked until two weeks from today.... I asked if the Groupon would be honored and he said no... I feel this is highly deceitful sice it wasn't my fault they can't accommodate me...and now i have to pay the full price for an oil change??? C'mon... It is an oil change that takes 20 minutes and they have my money already... Will not buy another Groupon for them or use them in the future!

Ok, so I'm rating lunch at Chahuan as the 2 star "Meh. I've experienced better." I was very excited to try the food at Chahuan because I'd read/heard lots of great things, but unfortunately my lunch experience did not deliver. The lunch did not contain enough variety, there were only 2 main dishes on the menu, both containing chicken and a cream based sauce. 

The food itself was tasty but not mind blowing and for the price I would have expected better. So a pretty ordinary chicken tiki masala/rice (which I never usually order, but there wasn't really anything else to get) for $14 for lunch was just not exciting enough for me to want to go back.  

The service was also pretty lousy, the restaurant was about half full, and the waitress couldn't even be bothered to look/speak when she dropped the check. 

Go to Woodlands or Sitar instead, it will be cheaper and better.  Maybe the dinner at Chahuan is great, but my lunch experience was enough to make me never go back.

stay away from this place!
service is HORRIBLE  for leased and car owners. 
I had a bad experience with both and it is not worth your energy.
They write nice answers to your bad review but the reality is - they do not care! therefore, nothing changes after their " nice response to your complaints." This is the type of place where they charge for something that you do not even need. Complete rip off! Stay away from this place. Save yourself the energy and money.
Solid food and very nice people - wings are pretty reliable too!

Great place to eat breakfast on a Sunday morning in SB, and easy to find.  About a 15 minute wait, but they have self serve coffee out front.  We ended up getting seated way in the back by the bathroom, at that point we might as well have been at IHOP.  It was so loud inside.  If its a beautiful day, I would recommend waiting for an outside spot, because without the beach view, the price premium is not worth it.  I overheard a local say that there are much better places to eat in SB, but they tolerate Sambo's when their friends visit.  The coffee was good, and I had the California Benedict.  It really was delicious, with fresh hollandaise.  It was pricey, about $13, but something I have not seen anywhere else, I would go back for it.  My wife was disappointed with her French Toast, and the syrup was watered down.  Home potatoes with the green onion was nice.  Service was prompt once we sat down, and the mini muffin appetizer was a great way to keep us happy while waiting for the food.  The water had a horrible aftertaste.
I would have granted Silver Peak more stars but the point is moot now. They've closed their doors forever.

I get it, you get busy. That's normal. It's how you handle it that matters. 25 minutes for a coffee is unacceptable. What's worse, when people complained to the manager, they were dismissed. The young woman taking order would say she had no idea how long it would be! You better have an idea!  You know when the orders placed, you know when the name is called. Step it up a Cville!
They suck..... Waited 40 mins for a freaking omelet when no one was there. Non courteous poll. Just cos they are  24 7 and I'm an immigrant they will I'll treat me. Morons.

I'm not aware of a S. McCarran store. I've only shopped at the Mt. Rose Ave. store near Junkee. Because it's a chain, the website doesn't even tell you where the store is, or anything else that's really pertinent to shopping there, except a 'we're a teen to twenty-something store' statement. A bad website is also a turn-off for me, but the statement is correct. If you're over 50 ( a forty-something friend of mine likes Plato's), you will NOT find very much interesting here. I can wear tight jeans, but they're almost all tight. The shirts and tops are Cute, not a word I use about myself at my age. These are only party clothes for the over 50's crowd, and you know those are never in a thrift price range for the barely middle class. As far as selling gently used clothing, it needs to be close to designer brands or don't waste your time and get looks from the staff member who says "sorry". Shop on, young ones! I wore these clothes back in the day, but not now.

The food is fine and I like skinnylicious menu but my waitress had a mask constantly below her nose which kind of drives me crazy because everyone else wears their mask properly. If Waitstaff are going to wear a mask they need to wear it properly. This is probably why Covid is spreading. Even at restaurants they are not making their employees wear a mask over their nose. This is happened to me three times in the last month at separate restaurants. Makes me not want to go.  Think I'll just eat at home for a while even though I do try to support local restaurants.

The service leaves a lot to be desired. Felt as though I was interfering with their night by asking for drinks and food.  The bartender had zero customer service skills.  No smile. No greeting.  No sense of service at all.  The drinks were inconsistent, a simple vodka tonic was served in three different type of glass each separate order with one of those being almost straight vodka. Watched the bartender dip the glass directly into the ice well and break the glass. No one dumped the well since she "got all the glass". Rookie and dangerous mistakes.  The food was okay, nothing to bring me back for more   The atmosphere is okay but the area they are is very competitive, okay will not cut it for long.

Great day food, at s good price. Let's face it, fast food is fast food, but it's convenient. It's nice to have a decent place to go, and is ready on the budget.  Nothing is worse than a great food joint that thinks they sell gourmet food.  Here is you get good food, a soda that is only inflated in size, and not price.

When we got here, we were told it would be an hour wait.  I didn't think we should wait this long to eat with so many other options around, but others in my group insisted the food was worth it.  Aaaaaand...they were right.  

First off, it took them only 15 minutes to seat us, not an hour.  The beet salad was delicious, the crab cake tasted fresh and delicate, and the shrimp and grits were borderline orgasmic.  Also, I'm a huge Moscow Mule fan, and they have several different kinds served in their proper copper mugs.  I had the grapefruit-infused one and was blown away.  

Lastly, I have to say that the service here is superb.  Our waiter made excellent drink and food recommendations and he was pleasant and inviting.  If I ever come back to New Orleans, Cafe Amelie will definitely be a stop.

We made a visit for a Sunday Brunch after reading an extensive review in the Indy Star.  It's a popular place.  Even at 1:30 there was a crowd waiting for tables in the parking lot.  Considering they close at 3 that tells you something about the popularity.
Even though we're all "seniors" we couldn't shake the desire to rub shoulders with the "hipster" crowd.

The food was, unfortunately, not up to our expectations.  The worst example was the "asparagus and palm hearts antipasto salad".  In our imagination, it was eagerly anticipated.  The reality was a small dish of lettuce, a couple of spears of asparagus, some shreds of pepperoni and no evidence of palm hearts.  At $12, the only thing that kept us from complaining was that it was Sunday and we were in a forgiving mood having just come from church.

The Belgian waffle was recommended as a specialty.  Unfortunately, the popularity must have waned in the closing hours because the waffle had obviously been waiting for a plate too long.  It might sound strange to describe a waffle as "tough' but that's the adjective that comes to mind.

The third dish was the featured combination of a baked egg with tomato sauce over bread accompanied by a lamb sausage.  That was the best of what we tried but suffered from too much moisture in the sauce that turned the bread into mush.  The egg was perfect and the tomato sauce was freshly made and was very good.  When you come to Milk Tooth you have to be ready to pay a premium.  The $17 price seemed pretty healthy for a sausage, a piece of toast, a slice of bread and tomato sauce.  

Total price with tips and soft drinks was almost $70. 

The surprising thing about Milk Tooth was the "Drinks Menu".  It hints that the real energy in the restaurant is at the bar.  The list is extensive and deep.  If you're looking for an unusual drink to accompany your meal, Milk Tooth can't be beat.  The list of craft beers would easily stump an aficionado.

Family restroom-closed. Why? Nobody knows or can explain. Changing station in bathrooms -dirty. We stopped here before-never been an issue. Trash cans-overflown. And its nit like there is no cleaning crew. They all walk around. Center is half empty
Love this place! Consistently good food and the bread basket is to die for! Whether u go for brunch or dinner you won't be disappointed!

If the experience once you crossed the threshold was even half as nice as its exterior, Marsha Brown's would be special. We stopped in for lunch after some shopping. The place was nearly deserted. We were seated promptly, placed our order and waited. And waited. Eventually our waiter came by apologetically, saying something about a computer problem, so we started all over. When the food finally arrived, it was quite good. My husband liked the eggplant Ophelia, even if the menu description wasn't exactly accurate. My chicken Caesar salad was just fine - but really, it's only a chicken Caesar. Nobody normal ever waxed poetic about such a simple meal. Lunch prices here were much more reasonable than the dinner menu, which borders on highway robbery. Bottom line - if you are in New Hope and feel like lunch (and you're not in a hurry) stop in.
Quickest pedicure ever. . Had to point out two different places that she missed. Won't go back.

I walked into this salon Wednesday with the expectation of having my hair done (simple balayage). This salon ruined my hair not ONCE but TWICE! Charged me full price for a cut and color when they didn't even finish my hair cut, told me they didn't have "time" to style it and I walked out balling my eyes and on top of it they told me to come back 5 days later to fix it!!! When they should've offered to come in early and fix it right away the very next day! I went back to receive $220 refund because that's the cost of going somewhere else to fix it. Keep in mind I was charged full price $265 for services I did NOT receive and I tipped them $50 even though she completely ruined my hair. The owner argued with me stating that she's not going to give me a refund because I didn't allow them to fix it, BUT I did! I had them fix it a second time BUT I was told by the owner I have to physically walk out of the building and come back for it to be considered a redo. Even though they processed my hair twice with bleach!!!! They told me since I'm a first time client that I need to give them another opportunity because they don't know me and they don't know my expectations. Which is literally the worst excuse I've ever heard. It's okay to mess up someone's hair twice because they've never met you before?! These owners need to be put on blast. Please watch out for fake 5 star salons with 1 star results!!!! This place is Bexley Salon located in Brandon, Florida. 
Kendal is the girl who did my hair and while she was doing the balayage I said I don't think this is right I've never had a balayage where they dyed the entire half of my hair blonde with no natural hair out to create dimension she told me to not worry she knows what she's doing. I later find out the owner overheard the conversation and told me I should've asked her to stop, but if you are the owner of a salon shouldn't you check to see what your stylist is doing if a client says I think this is wrong?!? 
They gave me half my money back after I sat outside telling them how awful they were handling this situation. As I'm leaving she said you need to drive back to fort myers and have your stylist do your hair because no one here will achieve my expectations. I can NOT believe these people are in business!

This is a small Mozzi's pizza shop and is carry out or delivery only. Their pizza's are very similar to Pizza King, and if I recall they used to be a part of the PK family maybe 20 years ago, then changed the name but kept similar pizza's, haha. 

The staff seems okay here, not that I talk to them much being carry out / delivery, but they get the pizza's made, and usually everything is made correctly, the pizza's look very good and taste pretty good, but not the best. And they are more on the expensive end of pizza's, when their pizzas are more of a middle range taste, haha. 

They do have another large location on hwy 40 that has a pizza buffet during lunch hours and it is pretty good, if you're looking for an eat-in location. 

I recommend this place because the pizza is good. Try BBQ sauce instead of standard sauce for an extra kick of flavor.

This place is so steeped in history that you almost can't help but get caught up in the nostalgia. I had no interest in going here but my wife insisted. She really got this one right. 
This building is over 100 years old and anybody who's somebody has played here or wished they had. The list of singers, actors, shows, etc. who have occupied the stage is amazing. The Grand Ole Opy is only one of many who called this the ultimate stage.
The acoustics here are incredible. The only thing I have seen like it is the Hollywood Bowl. You can barely see the person and yet you hear them very well. A photographer was barking instructions to her subjects at stage front and we could hear her clear as a bell walking above the balcony seats.
We tried to book a show after our tour of the theater, but they were all sold out. So book ahead and rest assured it will be a treat!

One of my favorite places in Nashville.  My boyfriend and I dressed up as Jay Gatsby and Daisy Buchanan for Halloween so we figured this would be a sweet place to have a couple drink.  We only waited around 10 minutes and then we were seated at the bar.  The atmosphere was dark, music was perfect and everyone was great.

They have a menu that's divided by base liquors and the drinks are listed below.  We both started with a whiskey cocktail and loved watching the bartender craft our drinks. Most drinks were around $12 each so a little pricy but so worth it.  They were great so we had a second round.

I would definitely recommend!

Entering the place I was greeted and the staff was friendly but once I got my food it was literally all celery. I ordered the combination plate it came with a good amount of noodles per say but I found only little tiny pieces of meet. Like 4 pieces and all celery very disappointed I mean It was only 6.41 with tax but still I wish I would have picked a much better Chinese take out place then this one.

Went yesterday because I was craving a philly cheesesteak and it was amazing! It was long, extra cheesy, and not too expensive with the special. The manager was extremely nice and friendly as well. They'll be adding vegan options soon to their already expansive menu! 

The name has changed to Violas so don't be confused when you pull up.

A hidden gem in the city of Tampa, the closest place to get away from noisy, dirty, and a busy real life. There was a dog playground along with a fishing spot and a small pier. Some trails are worth discovering. I do not think the beach here is good for people, though. An excellent place for relaxation on my own or having a BBQ party with friends. Very windy, too.

We came in at rush lunch hour day after thanksgiving. We we're seated immediately despite how busy it was to a filthy table. I had to push away 2 food covered forks to put my feet down. Our server was nice, overly friendly. We order the top 2 most popular appetizers. The crab fries were amazing and the fish spread was average. The apps came out efficiently timely and the food took almost 20min. My Diet Coke was flat. The entrees were gross! Like I can't believe that we paid $50 for lunch with the grossness of our food. Both were cold from sitting in the kitchen too long and were low quality. Would I eat here again? Only if my husband really insisted. Your paying for the atmosphere hon.

I wish I could give this 0 stars! This place is a racist piece of garbage. My partner and I came because their menu had a lot of breakfast options. When we got into the restaurant there were maybe 5 tables seated which was not a lot as their were several open tables and booths. The host sat us at the back by the bathrooms, even though as I just mentioned there were several open tables that were distanced from others. But we were still just happy to finally get breakfast. However, we sat at our table for 10 minutes without anyone coming to us asking if we wanted/needed anything (even though three servers walked past our table several times). The final straw was that a table of about 8 people came in at our 7 minute mark of waiting, we're sat, and within a minute a server approached their table and took the drink orders for all 8 people! We immediately got up, informed the host of the piss poor service and how racist they were acting (and yes the way we were serviced was racist, consciously or unconsciously), and then left. We will not be returning and I will not be recommending this place to anyone. And now I have to search for another place to eat.

My main experience is around lunch time and the pretzels are always warm and fresh.  It seems like they are making them all day when you are in the store.  I like that every time I go in, there is not really much of a line and you are in and out very quickly.

The best! I go here at least twice a week and send everyone I know this way!

As my best friend says, we don't go to Miguelito's to eat. We feast! Order one of each, mix and match and literally stuff our faces. The chicken tacos and al pastor are the best! Everything is authentic and tortillas home made (you can see her pressing them after you place your order!!). Service is slow since everything is made fresh to order but oh sooooo worth it. The lady is very attentive and generous. Not one of those places that charges their clients for sighing. Cheap yummy eats!

Staff is nice and facility is nice, however my first time here on Monday I got a chemical peel and now my face is burned under my left eye and on the corners of my brows.  I expected peeling, which didn't occur (which is fine) but the burns leave discoloration and is not the 'fresh glowy' look I was going for.  It's 6 days after my peel treatment and scabs are still very visible :(. I have gotten several peels at other places and not been burned... This is an unfortunate first.  I'll see if there's a way to post pics, it's def not pretty.

After babysitting my grand babies all day I decided to get my hair cut and went to Fantastic Sams in Wentzville, MO. I typically have my hair done close to home in Illinois. From the moment I walked in I should have turned and walked out. The only stylist there was upset that another employee had not came in to work. It was about 6pm and they were supposed to be open until 9. Constant phone calls from the missing employee "making sure Sa.... wasn't upset". She would say "NO", then complain when she hung up. Calls to and from the manager were abundant too. My turn, I show her in a book what I want. She says my hair is too short, but it isn't, it is curly and looks short when not blow dried. I do not frequent these type of salon's, but there was no water applied, no shampoo, not asked if I wanted that, which I did. She cut my hair dry while on the phone 80% of the time. I was turned away from the mirror and she used a razor to thin my hair, which makes my type of hair frizz. I've worked for years on the length of my hair and she cut it in layers!!  She explained to me she is only working there until she gets into a high dollar salon. Nice. Whoever hires her at a high dollar salon better teach her etiquette, manners, customer service, a listening ear and training for different types of hair!!
I hate this, I never write bad reviews. I have stewed over this for close to two weeks, hoping I would figure out how to live with this cut over the Holidays. Not happening.

This has to be the most unprofessional place I have ever worked. My very first day of training the whole entire  staff quit. They make you sign a bogus contract. And they don't pay you for six weeks . I had no real training, I came in with experience and to me that place is a joke. The owner came in one day, Looking every bit of a train wreck. With a coked out manger telling her new staff it was ok to do drugs before clocking in. I was hired for one location and then try to force me to work at further location from where I was originally hired. This place is horrible! I was only there for a good two weeks and all saw was a circus show

So lets just start with the good stuff: classy environment, great company, extremely friendly staff (from bouncers to bartenders), and coat check was pretty cheap.

However, they do a lot of false advertising! We paid $100+ for tickets that included complementary champagne toast, open PREMIUM bar, and food.

1) There was no champagne toast
2) They missed the countdown (like ... how?)
3) There was no premium liquor being used, and every drink tasted like soda (unless you tipped the bartender every time, and we noticed one telling the other not to use so much liquor but she said we tipped so its fine... lol)
4) There was no food whatsoever.

I live nowhere near this place but I stop there whenever possible.  Perfect for stopping by after the airport as it's a quick stop near I95's Academy Rd exit.
As a diner, it is above average, and as a place to pick up a cake for an event, it is amazing.  Prices are good and the cakes are fantastic.  My favorite is the chocolate layer cheesecake, but I've tried them all in my time.

I was a long time customer of Joes, all the way back to the 80s. I eat Mexican food 2-3 times a week and this is by far the worst Mexican food I've ever had. The quality of food is worse than a fast food place. The only good thing is the staff, they are very polite. It did seem clean. It makes me sad to see an Indy favorite so downgraded. I will not be returning.

First time was okay but the second time back was a different story. The bagel sandwiches are pretty on point; however, the drinks were a disappointment. My girlfriend got the iced coffee and I got the iced latte, 1/4 of a way through our drinks we started noticing a weird taste. The drinks started to taste like rubbing alcohol or so we think that's what they tasted like. We threw the rest of the drinks away. I recommend they check their equipment or their entire drink making process, something was fucking the drinks up.

Used to order from here all the time   I really like the mozzeralla and tomato hoagie with a side of tomato sauce for dipping. My friend loved their pasta dishes. 

Then they started going downhill. Would take forever for the order to come. Then twice it never came at all! We called and they had already closed and just never showed up. I actually can't believe I ordered again after the first incident. I learned my lesson after that

Last mouth eat here when I traveled here. Really terrible place to eat and stay. If could give zero star, I will give zero. The short-haired waitress's attitude is like eating . I don't want to give any tips to her, but how crazy, she not allow me to leave until I leave the tips on the table. Never come here again. Ps: different attitude to different people.

Got a keg from SB Ice for my birthday. They have a great selection of beer - possibly the largest selection of keg beer in Santa Barbara. People there were really helpful and nice, and the beer was great!

This place might have a higher rating if id gone as a spectator but my band played and the people running the club basically smoked weed all day and didn't feed us ect...(though it was in the contract) because they were so busy getting stoned. But I really liked the club otherwise and the security guards were nice. Not brutes like some can be. All in all a really fun club to play and the crowd was super awesome! Would definitely play again an if I lived in the area, would see shows here.

Incredibly slow service, over an hour and still no food! This has to be one of the only times I have left a restaurant before my order came! 75 minutes after ordering still no food! Miso soup (the only thing we ordered and got) was unimpressive and somewhat flavorless.

I ordered $700 worth of blinds from the Riverview home on Bloomingdale Rd.   They promised me that they would be and in 2 weeks when they actually took about 6 weeks to come in. I had one special order that was correct the remainder of the blinds were supposed to be cut to size.  They had the measurements and when I came in to get them the 1st time..  They gave me uncut  blinds.  I did not know they were the  wrong size until their installer told  me so.  I had to haul all of them back.
Fun restaurant spot in Franklin.  Great food. Cool atmosphere.  And great music and entertainment. Service very good too.  Loved the smoked meatloaf.

If you ask me, finding Thai in Indianapolis that was spot on was a task....until we stumbled upon Thai Spice. We started with the crab ragoon, which was out of this world! They were huge and delicious. We then ventured onto the Pineapple Fried Rice....... STOP right there.....those are the only 2 items that you need to know about, go order and then thank them profusely for giving these gifts to Indiana..... YUM

Mission Taqueria has one of the best happy hours I have been to. It gets crowded extremely quickly, so make sure you get there early! The only downside to the happy hour is that you can only get the happy hour deals at the bar area, not if you get seated. I have been here multiple times, some for happy hour and some not. All of my experiences have been wonderful. The chips and guacamole, tacos, and margaritas are all fresh, delicious and flavorful. I highly recommend Mission Taqueria for happy hour or dinner, just make sure you're prepared to spend full price if you can't get a seat in the bar area for happy hour.

I stopped here with my parents for lunch, the food was good and our waitress Liz was tentative and pleasant. While eating one of the hosts walked over and yelled at the wait staff in front of the 4 tables that were trying to enjoy their meals.  Instead of quietly approaching them she stood and demanded they get off their cell phones.  Shortly after she went to sit down at the counter with another employee who was also on his phone and she said nothing!  She actually was lookin at his phone with him!  Due to her power trip disgraceful behavior I was completely turned off and will NOT revisit this restaurant!

This place is great. I love how close it is to Reno. Literally a 20 minute drive max from Reno. There's never any traffic on Mt Rose Hwy...makes the exhausting drive after a ski trip less exhausting. Also, the road to the resort isn't too bad, not too many winding roads.

There's a bundle of blue and black runs, with their infamous "chutes" run. There's a couple lifts for beginners, but if I could recommend anything, I'd recommend some more green runs. Overall, I think they have a great variety of runs for beginners to experts. 

Customer service is always great---the staff there always have a smile on their face and are willing to assist you. 

A lot of locals come here because not a lot of people know about this ski resort--so it doesn't get too crowded!

Get your tickets off liptopia to save a few bucks!

There is a lot to like about the cactus club. It has a wonderful ambience. The lighting is a dimmed intimate allowing for a cool lighting effect on the walls. The servers are friendly, courteous and helpful. It's also attracts a very classy clientele that gets dolled up to go out for dinner. It seems like Earls on a whiskful dose of steroids. 

And you will pay for the atmosphere. I had the butternut squash ravioli with prawns. And while it was good I wish it was bigger because for the 24.50$ price tag I expected more.  It wasn't bad, but the way it was placed and served on the plate you'd figure is was godly food. It made me think, is this a metaphor for my experience here?

Is it a bad place? No, in fact it's kind of nice. I just don't think it's for me. However, I would recommend coming here to try for yourself. It was nice.

I have not been to Nashville in a while and decided to go back to my old favorite sushi restaurant and get my Sushiyobi fix! They have the best sushi in Nashville! Hands down. The BEST! Taste the rice at any sushi place and compare it to Sushiyobi. Sushiyobi wins. The sushiya explained to me the difference in quality of rice that they use. The rice is not dry like most other sushi places. It's moist and has a hint of sweetness to it. Also, the fish is always really fresh here. You can actually taste the fish. Most places hide the fish with mayo and other sauces. Sushiyobi makes great rolls. The prices are pretty good too. My favorites are: Crunchy Mochi(probably the most popular), Spicy Scallop Nigiri, Rocky Mountain and you can't go wrong with the Rainbow or the Soft Shell Crab!

Katie gave us an awesome tour of the complex. The view from the rooftop gym, pool and shared social space is gorgeous. We found a two bedroom apartment layout that is ideal for us. I especially enjoyed meeting the dog residents, all well behaved and friendly! Hope to see you again very soon!

What I love about Thai food is the balance of bold flavors: spicy, sour, sweet, bitter, and salty. There were hints of each flavor in the food, but in the end, everything was a bit muddled and muted. Proteins had the flavor and texture of being previously frozen. 

The only bright point was the lab neua. These flavors were well-balanced and fresh. If the rest of the food was anywhere close to this dish, the overall meal (and score) would be much better.

Why has no one opened competition for them yet? Their snowballs aren't that great, prices are too high, and the service is atrocious. The line isn't long because it's good-the line is long because they are slow as hell and there aren't any other snowball stands in the neighborhood.  And someone is going to get killed on the corner of Carrollton and dumaine because their line is so out of control.

This company came to our home. Most contractors who come to your home are respectful of the house and it's furnishings. When we returned after they left we found that they had pushed multiple chairs away 2-3 feet from the walk and left them there. One bar stool was left in the middle of the kitchen. They left insulation that fell onto the floor from the attic without sweeping it. They left lights on that we had not turned on, and perhaps the worst is that they closed our previously open chimney flu without telling us. We discovered this after starting our gas fireplace and letting it burn for a time with the flu closed.

I agree with the another posting that it isn't BBQ from the south, but to be so far from home and get a great BBQ sandwich....you won't hear any complaining here! The staff are very friendly and accommodating, the food is very good and the patio on a sunny afternoon is fantastic! Add to that a nice glass of wine or a beer with dinner and it's a great meal! Not sure what to make of the baked beans other than they seemed more like chili than southern baked beans but not bad....the BBQ pork sandwich was excellent though next time I may get the slaw and sauce on the side since I think I wore more of it home than I ate! LOL The bun was awesome and the fries were very tasty also! Two thumbs up!

I just finished eating here and couldn't finish my meal. My daughter wanted some of the green pancakes she had been seeing on tv. She only ate two bites. My wife's omelette had a brown chunk of avocado on top. That was an extra $1.25. Yuck. I could not eat my eggs or hash browns. So I just spent $39 on a meal that we wouldn't eat. Also after my wife took a few sips of her tea you could see the dirty ring around her glass. 

Nope! Nope! Nope! No more IHOP for us.

Was staying at the aloft, right around the corner and decided to get a takeout breakfast.  Service was fast, although not very knowledgeable about menu items (could not tell me if pepper and eggs on a long roll was Green peppers or just black pepper). Got a sausage egg and cheese on a hard roll.  The eggs were cooked perfectly fluffy and moist; the sausage was two links splint lengthwise so they covered the entire roll; decent amount of cheese; the roll was a bit stale and although toasted (briefly) on the grill could have benefitted from more toasting to overcome its ripe old age.

This diner was a pleasant surprise. I went there to meet with some relative. They wanted to try it and it was good. The menu was a small novel. They have everything for everyone! The onion soup was superb. The cheese was melted to a golden brown and the broth was hearty with onions. I had a staple of diners, the hot open roast beef. The roast beef was tender and flavorful, and the brown gravy was perfect. Others in the party had the open turkey which was equally as good. They also had the broiled trio of fish with crab stuffing, shrimpcake, and a shrimp stuffed mushroom. All the meals where great and the prices where even better. For a party of five it was about $75. Not too shabby!

I love Arby's, so I was looking forward to eating here.  Unfortunately, I could tell immediately upon entering the restaurant that I didn't want to spend my money at this location.  It was dingy, and the employees seemed to not care.  I left without ordering anything.

Service was good. The server was knowledgeable of the menu and beer. Food was not good for the price. Food came out luke warm. The basque mussels( tiny) also had big chunks of mud in the broth. Didn't feel like cleaning the mussels apparently.

Incredibly fresh Poke bowls with a lot of variety! Truly one of the best I've had in the area. Ordered off a delivery app at work and did not know what to expect. All of the ingredients were very fresh and blended well together. Planning on stopping into their physical location soon and ordering again at work.

When we got to the Restaurant the first sign we saw was for marina parking only.  So needless to say the parking is limited so get here early. 
The restaurant is definitely catered on the bar scene with good food rather then a restaurant with a bar. They also have live "loud" music inside, so keep that in mind if you are wanting conversation rather then a dance party. We sat out side so it did not bother us. 
There is a nicely listed drink menu, I had the sea hags brew, it was very tasty. The first one went down real easy as a sweet drink, however after starting my second the alcohol started to kick it and it hit me how strong the drink is. 
The food was all very good, they have a special you can add 2 hush puppies to any order for $1. The out side is a little to crispy but the inside was sweet and tender so it balanced out. 
The Chicken fajita quesadilla was well prepared, the only thing I questioned is why the tomatoes and scallions were on the side rather then in the quesadilla itself. 
The fish tacos. The whole chicken  shrimp bucket and the cuban all of it taste great we enjoyed the huge servings and everyone had to take left overs home. Great food for a great price.
food was very good,specially the grilled Mackarel,im a big fan now...cheers and more power...

Not an all you can eat restaurant.  The fish is better than the all the all you can eat sushi places here in town, but still nothing compare to what we've had in California.  You do get more, bigger fish slices here.  Service is not bad but not that friendly either.  The only time someone was super friendly/has good customer service was the first time we tried the place... I think he might be the owner or manager.  By the way, we tried their green tea milk drink, yuck.

After having limped along for two years, with a jimmy-rigged, obsolete HVAC system we decided, in May 2019, to have a new system installed. 

Of the three companies we decided to call for an estimate, we chose Calloway, owing in large part to other customers' reviews.

Can't say the lead up to the actual job wasn't bumpy. But they promptly addressed most of my concerns and completed the install in a day. Installers Josh, JR, and Rob worked diligently and in daunting temperatures to complete the job.  

And days afterward, when a minor problem developed (a tripping float switch) they immediately sent someone out to fix it.  

Though I must say I was somewhat disappointed with Trane's lack of product documentation and literature (i.e. owner's manual, etc.)  I guess I expected more from a company with Trane's reputation.

Otherwise, Calloway's service has, to date, been great, and I've no complaints so far with this new system.

This place sucked!!  Got 6 oysters and sent them back. 4 of them where so small and at $3 each no way was I eating them. 4 barley the size of a nickel. Also had the kale salad. It was OK but nothing to write home about. Had a tuna platter that I had to get after they told me that they were out of the crusted tuna. Forgot the name but it was also nothing to write home about. Was still hungry so I ordered a $16.50 burger. I have to tell you Applebees burgers are ten times better. I normally don't write bad reviews but this place was not good at all. I will never go back to this place. The only good thing about this place was the drinks and our server was very nice. I'm only giving this one star because of the drinks and the server.

Ah-sy?  Ah-sy-ee?  A-see-a?

Well, now we know that it's supposed to be pronounced Ah-zee and is French for something like "in Asia" or "of Asia".  But who cares as long as the food is good.  And it was!

We started with a couple of their house craft cocktails.  They were good, but nothing particularly memorable.  For food, the two of us went with 4 small plates and an entrée, all for sharing.  The "Sexi-Mexi Maki Roll" was good.  The "Golden Brussel Sprouts" and the "Spicy Garlic Belly Edamame" were delicious.  Really superior was the "Gyaw-zah", as good as the best we ever had in Japan.  The sauce on them was so delicious that we saved it.  For the entrée we had the "Tumeric Rice" which was the weakest item we had.  The grilled beef on it was good, but the rice itself had a muddy flavor.  We poured on the sauce from the "Gyaw-zah" and it helped a lot.

The restaurant is comfortable and the servers were very pleasant and helpful.

I would happily return.

The overall experience was alright. We had been waiting for weeks to try this place! We love pasta, pizza and the location is very close to our apartment.

We were seated immediately (were only 2 other tables with people) and the waitress was nice. Before we got our food, there was already drama. Apparently something happened with the table behind us and they got into with the manager. The manager was visibly and verbally angry. A lot of raised voices and back and forth. He did apologize to us.

The food was sadly kind of bland. I was torn between the carbonara and the Alfred and the waitress told me to get the carbonara. The sauce tasted a little powdery and kind of like chicken top ramen. It was suppose to have pancetta bits but they were very small and almost unnoticeable. My boyfriends pizza crust was really good and it was cheesy but the sausage didn't have a lot of taste.

We ended with the caramel gelato which stole the show.

I don't think we will be back for food but maybe for the gelato.

I've been to other Outback's and I enjoy their food occasionally. This one is just the pits. The service is nearly always really slow, frequently disinterested servers, and I'm often sending back my food as it's been cooked wrong. As I'm writing this I'm waiting in the parking lot for curbside takeaway. I'd been here for 10 minutes and had to call since no one had come out (why bother with the camera?) and it's been 10 more since the last I spoke to "let them know" and still no one has come out. Guess I'll be heading in in my pajamas. BTW, it's 4pm, so it's NOT a busy time for them. Why do I keep coming back? About once every two years I decide to try again, hoping they'll get their act in gear. The answer is clearly, no, not yet. 

When I went in it turned out my order was ready and just sitting there. I asked if the video camera wasn't working, the hostess didn't know but she said there was currently one server for the whole restaurant AND takeaway -- so I left a tip when otherwise I wouldn't for this level of non-service. Unacceptable, franchisee!

This is probably the poorest excuse for a pizzeria ever let alone a "NYC style pizza" place. The burned my calzone and then they made a new one when I got home to eat it I was disgusted with how salty their ham is. I might as well drink a cup of salt water next time. The pizza was terrible. Their cheese is mediocre and their sauce comes from a can from who knows where. Even the lady said she wasn't sure if she was even allowed to tell us that they buy their horrible sauce. The pizza was atrocious and it stuck to the box (I'll post a picture above). Never will I go back to this place. I'm from Jersey just moved down here Oct 2016 and I can tell you that this place doesn't touch city pizza or Jersey pizza. The only thing NYC about this place are the plaques and pictures he brought down from a corner store in times square but even those might be fake! I'll never eat here again. I'd rather drive home over night. Pay the $15 toll to get into the city and go eat a $1 slice from papaya dog than to ever eat here again. Stay away from here. Trust me, not worth it.

We shared the volcano nachos and they were pretty tasty. My son got wings and he enjoyed them. We had to sit upstairs and it kinda took away from the scene. I would have rather been downstairs with the live music. I enjoyed the Margarita flight and it did not disappoint.

Good food and questionable service seems like an odd ways to describe this particular Chick-Fil-A. Twice I've had orders screwed up. Once with a lost sandwich. The other with a free sandwich card that they failed to credit at payment time. Complaining will get results. They apologize and take care of their customers. My guess is the sheer volume of customers lead to inevitable problems with a young staff.  Even a well trained staff. Otherwise the quality of the food is good, really good when you compare to their competition. I will continue to go back. Recommend.
The music is TOO LOUD here. Food is okay. Servers are friendly. The cafe is not very clean. Could use some attention.

I like the yoga studio, some teachers are excellent but some are really inexperienced recent grads of the studios teacher training program so it can be hit or miss. Now the owners are really pushing an essential oil MLM scheme super hard on their students and it's honestly so disappointing and frustrating.

I needed help finding a universal remote control that could handle at least 3 devices and the clerk found one right away and told me it was easy to install.  If it was difficult, I could get help by calling the store.  First thing, I had to run back to the store to get the battery case opened.  Then I went home and tried to install it but it wouldn't work.  I returned to the store and This time, I was sent to another clerk who told me I had to set it up on my computer.  So I tried that but got no where.  I made another trip to Best Buy and the clerk told me I needed the Geek Squad at $145  for a $40 part.  I was shocked, but had no one else to turn  to.  So I took them up on it.  Payment was due, in full, when I made the appointment.  I've used the Geek Squad before, and though expensive, they do a great job and are fast and courteous... just expensive.  What would we do without TV?  Read a book?  Hah.

International Plaza & Bay Street is one of my favorite places to visit in Tampa. It is filled with upscale shopping, as well as great eateries. You can really spend an entire day walking around. My favorite visits: Nordstrom, J. Crew, Zara, William Sonoma, Sephora...and the recently added H&M is a treat as well!

Pretty standard Americanized Chinese take out.  I tried a dish that I haven't had before: Pork with eggplant.  It was good, I might order it again, but it could use a bit more heat to it.  

Wonton soup was fine, not the best I've had, there weren't shreds of pork in the broth and very few scallions.  

Service was quick enough for pick up.  

All in all, "meh, I've experienced better."  It's not something that I would seek out, but it's fine for what it is.  I don't think that it deserves all the praise that it is receiving here, it seems like the high reviews are just because it is close to people's houses.

Beautiful place. Overpriced and a little hoity toity. Went with five women for a birthday dinner. One received wrong order, one had an undercooked steak had to send it back. Two were chastised by the maitre d for wearing jeans but he did let us stay. Would probably not go again.  The praline parfait was amazing.

Typically not a fan of chain restaurants, but I had a gift card so my daughter and I went for lunch.  Our server, Kevin, was great.  The soup, salad, and breadsticks were better than both of our lunches, so maybe stick with what they do best.

My daughter tried the chicken parm sandwich and declared the breadstick bun, "not ready for the public."  :-)

Kevin was the star here and made our lunch very enjoyable.

Found it one day, and came back three more than in same week.  It was gooood.  So glad there is finally a really authentic chinese food in the area.  

Thanks yelp

If I'm honest this is officially our favorite Indian restaurant in the Area and Philadelphia. D.P. Is easily one of the most attentive waiters, always greets you with a smile and always refilling the water. The food is absolutely prepared to order and always comes hot to the table. I haven't been able to find a better Indian restaurant and I won't even try. Can't wait to return!

I have to say, this place was great and far exceeded my expectations for an inexpensive hotel room.  We went down pretty last minute, booked the room a day ahead for less than $80 over the weekend.  The Best Western in Santa Barbara was almost twice that.  

Sure, you're 10 min. away from Downtown Santa Barbara and it's close to the freeway and not very close to the beach but it is clean, inexpensive, good freeway access, excellent customer service, parking, etc.  Remember, it is a Best Western, we're not talking Four Seasons.   But for the price it is a great place and I wouldn't hesitate to stay again.

Average Chinese food.
Very Informal.

Not much of a dining area, I noted as I came in for carry out.

The rice was good. 
I had Orange Chicken and it was average. There was more fried gunk wrapped around the chicken chunks then chicken as most average Chinese food places. 

If you are hungry or have a craving this will do but it won't bowl you over in any sense.

Note: There is limited parking in this strip mall. There are many restaurants in this small strip mall so when it is lunch or dinner time you might be hard pressed to find a spot.  Plan your time accordingly.
15 minutes late and charged us an extra day and LDW extra day! Horrible customer service too!! Would highly not recommend!! @Hertz

Fish and chips, pizza and bloody Mary were good.  Waiter dumping a Coke on my son and never saying"sorry" was pretty poor service.  Forgot bar order and generally delivered poor service.  Normally we're above average tippers, not today.

This Waffle House is top notch! I have been to a lot of waffle houses in my days and the staff and unit manager were on point. The food was great and the service was even better. August the unit manager knows how to
Keep customer happy and coming back. There were many regulars in here and the staff new a lot of people by first name. I give this place a 5 star for service and food.

it was my first time at this theater.  They had recently gotten new seating that had better padding, per my brother.  Unfortunately, this was still the most uncomfortable theater I have ever visited.

I came here to see Black Panther.  Fortunately, the movie was great and was just enough to salvage my experience at the theater.  I had no issues with the service or personnel.  But this venue has so many factors that make this not worth a visit.  

I have found sitting in church to be more ergonomic than the seats in this theater.  Yes, they are new and padded.  However, they are very upright (even more so than church pews!) and they do not flex back one bit!  the seats are on the narrow side and do not give you much arm space if you sat with another person.  I found myself struggling throughout the entire movie to get comfortable in my seat, which would not happen.  

The showroom itself took away the feel of movie magic.  It was long and narrow room with a limited sound system.  The screen was clear (and small) and the volume was fine, but what lacked was the experience owed when paying to see a movie.  You need to big wide screen and a sound system that covers you in every direction with bass and multi-channel sound.  This place had a small screen (less than half the diameter of typical modern theater sizes) located in an elephant trunk of a room.  I felt like I was in kindergarten where we watched Disney movies on a 32 inch tube television from the back of the classroom.  You feel like you don't see anything.  The room itself was not inclined and so you really look up at the screen unless you are situated in the back.

The ticket prices were $13 for a ticket I believe.  I managed to get a whole dollar off with a student ID, so I paid $12.

Overall, the theater is conveniently situated right in the middle of the city and is easily accessible for those that live there and those without cars.  It was also clean.  However, I do not see a reason to come back here except when I want to see a movie with my Moviepass card that I would never pay money to actually see.  It was truly uncomfortable and lacking in the theater experience.

Love this place; style, service, ambiance, yummy food.  A little pricey but if you know what to order it's totally worth it.  The octopus was amazing and the pitcher of drinks we ordered was delicious and a fun way to enjoy cocktails as a group!

Ehhhh, I'm not sure how I feel about Haydel's. I've had their cakes, king cakes, and eclairs and they never truly hit the spot for me. Today, I ordered the pineapple/ strawberry wedding cake slice and the cake itself was really dry! The flavor was good but the was just dry. I expected more for the price i paid! I ordered two slices and it came up to $12.....  VERY EXPENSIVE for a slice of cake. The service was great! The enforced the rule of wearing a mask and they greeted you at the door. The bakery is cute but very tiny on Magazine St.
Used to be one of my favorite places. NOT ANYMORE! just spent $25 on food that tasted horrible. My fav was the Vegan Farmer but now the sandwich is something that belongs in a school lunch box.

A little pricey but worth it.  The burrito bowl is amazing and the salad is great too because they have an amazing dressing.  They also have something called the "quesorito" which is not on the menu but if you ask they WILL make it.  It's the same thing as a burrito but instead of a regular flour tortilla, the burrito is wrapped in a cheese quesadilla....AMAZING!

I bought heavy cream and when I poured it, it already curdled. The expiration date is about 28 days away. So the next day I went to customer service expecting a refund. Instead of apology and refund I was told that the store did not accept return. I was saving my breath from arguing. If it's Trader Joe's I bet they will refund me. I would be better off going to Whole Foods across the street or Wawa around the corner.

I have never written a review before, but unfortunately feel I have to write one about Springfield Diner that I went to today for the first time.  Got our food quickly, and then the bill right after we got our food.  I finished my tea, and asked if I could have a Diet Coke and she gave me the Diet Coke in a "to go" cup (I did not ask for it to go).   We sat and visited as I was having  breakfast with a friend I don't see that often.  While we sat there, the waitress gave us nasty looks and mumbled under her breath "their still here" as she went by.  There were plenty of booths open, and no one waiting to sit down so there was no reason to rush us out the door.  Had there been people waiting, I would have certainly gave up our seat.  The food was "ok", but the server awful.  We left a tip, but felt we were not treated nicely, and I doubt I will go back.  I understand servers need to turn over their tables for tips, but had we been treated nicer, I would have left a much larger tip since we were there over an hour.

I can barely begin to articulate the horrific events that we just experienced at this place. Let's just say, we spent an hour and a half there and the "night" ended with our group never getting served food and witnessing our waitress screaming/cursing at other customers in response to their frustration with the lack of service. This place will only go down the drain so long as the long haired brunette chick that looks like she is on crack continues working here. Never got to try the wings but $2 Bud Select specials seem nice..if you can talk your waitress into taking your order! I am being a little generous with an extra star due to the amount of TVs present...betting it's awesome during football season and you play fantasy :).

However, in the words of my roommate, "this place should be called f*ck it".

Not good enough...

Stopped in for dinner on-the-run last night around 630.
Store was empty.
Staff was friendly and helpfull enough.
Decide upon a Big Mary combo with Tater poutine and a bottle of water. Total was just shy of $14.
Sandwich was smaller than I expected and mediocre at best. Coleslaw side was pretty bland. Poutine...
Well let me tell you...
I enjoy a good poutine...
And this was FAR from good.
I ate a 1/4 of the dish at best. Couldn't be bothered to make a bigger dent...even after the smaller than expected sandwich.

I really doubt I will be back.

This establishment maybe got too famous for their own good. The service and the whole system was just too laid back for us. We were waiting for an hour just to get seated. And after that wait for another twenty minutes to get something to drink. While they messed up our colleagues order. The chicken were indeed flavourful，but we were wondering whether it was all justified and I am strongly against it. Would not recommend this on a hot summer day. Maybe in the winter time :)

This place has changed hands since my last review. They now use a different coffee bean provider, i liked the old coffee better. 

Still a good little local coffee shop.
TERRIBLE SERVICE. Great food!!! I give the food 5 stars. Came here twice, both waitresses sucked the life out of my delicious meal.

I paid like 13 dollars for a 2 meat platter with 2 sides. Order chicken and brisket, when I opened the box they were five small pieces of each. Each piece was smaller than the corn bread. It wasn't worth the money. The food was good, just not a lot of it.
Pizzas are awesome, especially the deepdish. Make sure to ask for it well done though!
Sushi was really good and great for the price. One of the better places in philly for sushi delivery. If your hesitant you should do it.

Unreal service. And I don't mean that in a good way. We were here for a convention. After waiting an hour we spotted seats at the bar. Great! We were on the wait list so we figured we could eat and grab a drink. As I told the hostess to take our name off the list I was told in a rude tone, sorry but we don't serve food at the bar now. What? (Also, after I told her I was checking in on the nowait app, she was like well there's a lot of people in front of you, see, and scrolled thru a list of names?? Yes, I'm aware how this works. Rude from the beginning.) Literally everyone had food at the bar. Sure enough the bartender told us after we sat down and after a hour wait sorry no food service here anymore. So, we left and we're now eating as I speak at THE BAR at a close by restaurant. Too bad. I wanted to try their beer.

One spring night I was incredibly stir crazy at 10 pm. Since there is nothing to do in this area after 10 pm except go to trashy jersey bars, we went exploring. Located in a random neighborhood area, I never knew this existed even though I've lived in South Jersey for 8 years. 

Thankfully yelp reviewers clued me in to this spot, which was (Amazingly) open til 11 pm! We pulled in and the retro drive-in sign and asymmetrical angled building called to me like a beacon in the night! 

I Got a flurry with chocolate custard and Reese's Peanut Butter Cups. The boyfriend got a Pepsi float. Both were good, but they were too big and too sweet for either of us to finish them. We didn't mind since the prices were really low. I wish they just had regular ice cream (instead of Custard), but I guess that's just the weird area we live in! I will say that for the price, the hours, and the taste this place has got it going on!

The whole process was easy. They did the estimate to re-coat my roof and came when scheduled. The quote was detailed and addressed my concerns. Other quotes didn't address those concerns, so be sure to examine quotes carefully, I say. Since they don't do full roof replacements (or at least they didn't in the past), I feel they will only recommend work you need.

Stopped by today as they're super close to my work.  Staff is amazing and beyond helpful. My egg salad sandwich is on point!  Prices are a bit more than the other shops around here. But they definitely make up for it in quality. 
I'll be trying one of their top sellers next time, as everything smelled so amazing!  They even make their own in house chips! Salt and vinegar was great, definitely gotta get some of those too!  Clean and happy energy atmosphere, would honestly take my kids here for a sammie, and they serve pizza!!??!?!! So good. Don't let this hidden gem go unnoticed, they deserve all five of the stars I'm giving them! 
One word: YUM!!!!!

i like woods. its not very flashy and could use a face lift but all the key elements are in place. nice grass tees with sufficient targets and a 18 hole par 3 course that i love during the week. i sneak out of work early some days to pay the $14 and walk the course. a great little course to work on wedges and irons. leave the driver in the car because you don't need it. you can walk 18 in about 2 hours if you hit straight and don't have beginners in front of you.

Yeah.... the restaurant itself is nice, but the pizza is surprisingly not! I've eaten here twice now and it was pretty bland and flavorless both times. The crust was a little overdone and although the servers are nice, it's not a place I'll be spending money at again.

Decided to try it out and I am not impressed service was sub par with our waitress Brittany ignoring us, beer was warm but at least the free tacos were good. They tried to play the music loud but the speakers sucked and i all I heard was static. Good place for a quick beer other than that you can have it.

They have rows and rows of shoes.  From trendy to sexy to classy!  The staff is friendly and helpful.  

Don't forget to check out their clearance racks in the back.  They usually have good deals there!  If you find shoes you like at your size, you better grab them quick because they won't last.
Not bad for 13th street fare. Have to say that the byo tequila margaritas top the meal which was a bit too salty. Something to consider in the future.

Love this pace. Food: their Margaritas are the bomb. The Don Julio fajitas were delicious. It come with tortillas, rice and beans. The guacamole was nasty. I wouldn't even call it guac, tasted like some fake stuff you would get at the gas station. Just skip the guac. The chips and salsa were ok. 
Service: our server was sweet and very friendly. Environment: the ambience was chill. I appreciate the social distancing guidelines given the current times.

Went here with people from work an.it's not bad. The place had a decent bottle and draft selection. You do not have to break the bank there since it has cheap brews to expensive craft beer. The food is your basic bar food and nothing to write home about. The burger was okay and I did like the herb Russian mayo dressing. If you want a laid back hangout with good brews, then come here.

Terrible in every way. Service was lacking but the food was horrendous.  Two appetizers, two salads, a pizza and the "clam" pasta all were equally offensive. The table cloth had large stains and crumbs all over it so I put napkins down under my plates. I left hungry and angry.
This place is great! Super cool vibe, server was awesome and I had the best scallops I've had in a long time. They oysters are on point with perfection as well! Highly recommend!

This review is for the food only (I'd go back for happy hour), and while I admittedly have a small selection of experience, I am thoroughly disappointed and do not intend to eat there again unless my friends force me to.

The burgers. I have literally zero idea.how everyone thinks they're so good. They're not juicy, the toppings are not great, and the brioche bun is so big and adds nothing but more mass to your dry bite.

The fries are fine but the cheese fries suck. The whiz is just.not good and they always sprinkle it with their red sprinkle stuff (some sort of seasoning that I don't like on my cheese fries...never remember to tell them not to put it on. But it shouldn't be there if it doesn't taste good, and it doesn't. Its edible on the plain fries.)
-
I've had a couple other things including the nachos. My friend loves them. I think Chipotle's are significantly better.

And finally brunch. What a disappointment. They just started brunch maybe a month or two ago. And really I could do so much better in my own kitchen. I ordered an omelet with fried onions, cheese, and Ham. The omelet was a plain omelet and folded in the middle was the onions, cheese, and ham. And the ham was literally one large piece of ham. Not cut up into little pieces. So frustrating to eat. Same with my friends cheddar and bacon omelet. It was eggs and folded in the middle was the cheese and a couple of strips of bacon. Strips of bacon...That's not an omelet!!! The only saving grace was the home fries which were actually good. Thin potatoes with good seasoning and I ordered them with onions which made them even better.

My friend ordered the French toast and said it was weirdly gooey like it was probably undercooked. Others were unimpressed as well, except the one who ordered the Burgers Benedict (with a turkey burger) and the one who ordered eggs and bacon with home fries. They liked theirs.

Worst of all, on this most recent visit for brunch, the food took a good half hour to get to us. And we were literally the only people in the place. Not cool. Plus the food was cold/lukewarm when it got to us, and I found a jalapeno in my home fries that wasn't supposed to be there.

Fortunately its not horribly expensive. Entrees ranged from like 7-13$.

I had the great opportunity to go here after our Yelp event for a meeting with my boss. 
It was a business breakfast of sorts. 

The service here is wonderful. The staff really go the extra mile to make sure you are satisfied with your food and drink. I ordered the bacon and avocado sandwich...It is one of the best breakfast sandwiches I have ever had. I am a HUGE fan of breakfast sandwiches! It has egg, bacon, avocado, and pepper jack cheese! It had me at bacon and egg but the avocado and pepper jack are what make this sandwich the king of breakfast sandwiches. 

Now, maybe the king of breakfast sandwiches is a bit extreme.  I have yet to try the other options and need to give them a fair shot at the title.  I must come back and experiment with them. I did not try any coffee but I did get hot tea. They served it to you with the little pot (so cute). 

This seems like a great place to go for lunch. I need to come back for every meal! I saw a sign that they do wine & cheese tastings, yummm....If they keep up with what they have going, this place is going to be very popular.

Maybe it's that we came in the last hour they were open, but I have to say that I had higher expectations for the muffaletta based on Yelp reviews.  Didn't realize they were pre-made or how long they had been sitting.  I guess I was just expecting something warm...These weren't the most fresh things in the world and super oily.  Maybe an off day?

My partner and i have used Upack twice now. I wouldn't use anyone else.
The first time was cross country. We had another co. All set up, our house packed and ready and the other company didn't show up when promised. They kept saying they'll be there..they never showed on time and we had to be out..so last minute i called Upack. They were so kind and professional and showed up within hours. We used them again recently and once again they were great! Awesome service. Couteous driver.

Basic, inexpensive diner food. It's quite good if you keep your expectations at 'reasonable'. That being said, I would go here again. 

I went here in the morning while all the drunkards were passed out. It was very relaxing.

The space itself is pretty nice. A good close feeling to the stage even if you're not able to get right up front. That being said, that's about the only thing going for the TLA in my opinion. Was there for a concert this past weekend and all of the staff members I communicated with were extremely rude and unhelpful. I will not be attending another show at this venue.

My friend and I went to Jackson's while she was in town. We arrived for dinner at 8 PM and were seated outside. The restaurant view was gorgeous of Tampa and great for someone who is coming from out of town. We ordered the crab ravioli and 4 different sushi rolls with sake. The crab ravioli was delicious and the corn emulsion was tasty. Our rolls were delicious, the Tampa roll with grouper was amazing and the green onion gave a little kick. The slammin salmon was very flavorful as well. For dessert, I had the s'more bar and my friend had the peanut butter torte. As much as I love s'mores, the torte was delicious.

Make sure you come on the weekend only, we came for our second visit on a Monday night - TERRIBLE! Owner and/or manager must not be in on weekdays. Neither the pot roast my husband had nor the chicken I ordered was seasoned. Our first visit was on a Friday night and it was phenomenal, completely different experience

Thai Lotus in Reno has been in our top 3 for over a decade. We have watched them grow and always enjoy the staff, food.

We snuck in at opening for dinner, 4:30PM.  Probably under ten minutes and everything was out. The Pumpkin Curry with chicken is my favorite, along with HOT HOT HOT combo fried rice w/chicken.

Spring rolls always on point, along with Pad Thai, Pad Ka Mao. One of the few places that bats 1000% almost every visit.

Staff and service are always top notch.
If you like heat, ask for it on the Thai scale, they will deliver the HEAT!

" Nothing Special "

We went to have a drink and to have a breakfast at Blake's On Poydras. We started our meal off with 2 Bloody Mary's which were very good. I had The Breakfast Bowl with Fried Green Tomatoes, Fried Eggs, Bacon, Feta Cheese on top of Grits. Sounds like it would have a lot of flavor but it did not have any flavors. My wife had the Crab Benedict with a side of Grits. She said it was delicious.

Avoid at all costs.
Made the mistake or eating lunch here.
Got the "salmon combo"
The rolls were poorly made. Most just a lump of rice with salmon on top. Had to specifically ask for napkins?! Avocado was turning brown.

Won't ever eat here again. Front counter lady was not friendly at all. Like she was annoyed to have to serve people.

I loved the environment when I first walked in. The staff was so nice and helpful. My brother tried almost every flavor because of his indecisiveness haha but the staff was pleased to help him find the right flavor for him. 

This place has the best ice team Ive ever had. The cones were delicious and the service was quick.
I love the silver taste testing spoons.
I bought furniture off Kittles and the furniture ripped and they came out here and fixed it and it ripped two weeks later I have a five year warranty and they refused to replace it t

Toys, Toys and more toys and that's not all! Great stuff for boys and girls - and some fun games and puzzles for us "older" folks.  A fun place to shop without the hectic big box crowds. Owners are local and very helpful.  I found what i was looking for and more. Great experience, will definitely be back!

The food is wonderful but comes in small portions considering the bloated prices.  

I had the okra and collard green sides, both of which tasted really good.  The fried chicken was very good as well but for $20 I got a tiny drum stick and smaller than average thigh piece.  

The chicken pieces were so small they were more like quail pieces or perhaps hummingbird pieces.  The drum stick was more like a dwarf stick.  I'm exaggerating but you get the idea.  That is my gripe, give me more chicken in my main entrée.  

The service was okay albeit a little disorganized.  My food took forever to arrive.  

Very modern and well appointed interior.  The restaurant is clearly going for a cutting edge hip hop ambiance and succeeds to that end. 

 Ms. Tootsie's serves up tasty food but much sure you bring a full wallet if you want a full stomach.

Fun busy restaurant with a variety of seafood options and good classic Cajun food on the menu. I got oysters on the half shell and the oysters du jour which were fried and topped with a crab salad. I always prefer raw oysters and this was no different. The fried oysters were good but the crab salad was overpowered by the condiments and the crab didn't shine through. The turtle soup was ok, I never had turtle soup before so I don't have anything to compare it with but I didn't expect it to be so much like a stew. The hush puppies were awesome and the sweet potato fries are dynamite! The service was really nice as well. They don't split checks but you can put down multiple cards or pay cash. While it's not the prettiest restaurant it is tasty!

This location is not worth a try, yet my wife keeps giving them a chance. 

We had been hung up on when a couple people were arguing, we were given the wrong pizza twice. 

Tonight was funny! This is how they cut the pizza. To be honest, it tasted fine... but come on now!!!

I kinda had the same unfortunate experience as Aileen S. who wrote on Yelp in April. 
I decided to meet some girlfriends for brunch this morning (Sunday) around 11:30am. I walked into the restaurant only to be standing there for about 10 minutes with waitresses coming and going next to me, without even acknowledging me (there was a sign that asked to wait to be seated). I was finally told that there was a 15-20 min wait, yet there was a bunch of empty tables (and the restaurant is not that big so it's not like the waitresses had many tables in their sections). I took a seat at the bar... meanwhile I saw 3 couples leave the restaurant after just entering because they were told 20-25 min, yet a bunch of tables were still available.. I mean hello! Sit us down, explain to us that we can have coffee or a mimosa, but service will be delayed because the kitchen is swamped.. most ppl would understand... So 20 minutes pass (and my friends were running late) so I finally get to sit down at a table... I waited forever to be able to get my hot chocolate (3$ for a cup btw) and there were no utensils on the table (I was hoping for a spoon for the whip cream on my hot chocolate)... ANYWAYS, so my friends finally joined... must have been another 10 minutes before they got asked if they wanted to drink anything. I ordered a small glass of juice and some tea which came to me with a dirty cup... gross... 
The food didn't take too long to get to our table, and it was amazing, but we had to shout every time my girlfriends needed a coffee refil... 

The moral of this story, go somewhere else where they actually want your business and where they will take care of you. This restaurant has the shitiest service that I've had in a long time...  very poorly managed.... I hope the restaurant owners know how much revenue they are losing by having these morons as waitresses who are turning away customers and who are ensuring that the current ones will never return.

I asked for a toasted lox! It was over priced paid 12.95 for coffe and bagel! They didn't toast my bagel and cream cheese wasn't even spread right! How can someone mess up so bad on just a bagel! Not coming back! Not worth it

If you crave a Cake donut with Chocolate Icing, then this is the place for you! A few years back a coworker brought donuts to work from here and I was hooked on the cake one. It's not too dense, moist, not too sweet and a hint of butter in the mix. Balanced chocolate flavor, not too rich.  I'll say, I can't co-sign for their other donuts. I only get the cake.

This store is usually quite friendly and efficient. However, yesterday at the Deli Counter proved to be an exception. 
I asked for one and one half pounds of a particular cheese, and pointed directly to a large, full, unopened block on display. The counter lady instead pulled out a small piece of cheese encased in Saran Wrap. She told me she could get one half pound from this small piece. 
I asked her again for one and one half pounds. She again implored that she could get one half pound from the small piece of cheese in her hand. 
Finally, I asked why I could not have one and one half pounds, using the large block on display. 
She replied that it would take more time, as the large block was unopened. 
I finally gave up and left, cheeseless. 
This is a deli where the staff is too lazy to fill your order. 
I will continue to shop at the store--but definitely not the deli.

Um...no.

This is essentially Panera in disguise but no quality control. Food was over salted, over peppered, over seasoned everything. Bummer as we had high hopes.

I came here 5 1/2 years ago right before the Dolphin Tale movie was released. It was an
average experience then - one of the highlights in the lower level was a fish and sea life viewing area. That area alone Had enough to keep my 2 year old granddaughter entertained for quite a while. Hundreds of different fish.

Flash forward to today. All of the fish I mentioned were gone for the list part except a few nurse shark. The ray tanks had a third of what they had 5 years ago. Literally besides Winter and Hope theres maybe a dozen creatures. 

They did amazing work with Winter but to hype the whole experience and add such an extremely disproportionate amount of window dressing around the place is shameful. They should have added to the experience other than hype instead of taking away what was there before. My granddaughter, now older, wondered if the aquarium was built around what happened in the movie. At least they had a small documentary to watch but you have to wonder what will happen to this place when the hype around Winter fades - it's not like the movie was a blockbuster!

For $3 more a ticket you can go to the Florida Aquarium!

The Oregon Coast Aquarium did it right. The NEVER hyped Keiko being at the aquarium and after he was gone didn't make it a shrine. Much more there for only $1.95 more a ticket!

We stopped in here around 8:30 pm for a few drinks and to catch some of the football game ( (Clemson - LSU). We were able to be seated quickly without a reservation (not sure if they take reservations). There is a decent whiskey/ scotch selection and food gastro-pub food. The ahi tuna, Thai shrimp, and nachos are good table snacks.

I do not understand the hype here.  The hosts here hate their job, understandably so. The waitlist app is really helpful and really does considerably shorten your wait.  Server was friendly.  The food was just ok. Bananas foster pain Perdue was inedibly sweet with overly mushy spots and also dry hard spots. Limp, sad bacon to accompany. The Migas egg scramble was good but nothing special.

Well I drove 14 miles to your bank on a Saturday morning to chat about your thoughtless error and to my surprise, your lobby was closed. Who knew?
So now you want to fix the problem?
Are you going to give me something for my gas expense there and back just to recieve the same checks i had? Or is this just an attempt to look good?
You know, Its very easy to see why i got the same checks, because nothing had to be done on your end. No type setting needed to be changed, no re-printing, etc. just pure profit.
The thing was, i had run low on checks and needed to pay a few bills. Sorry Hughes, but i didnt have another 10 days to wait. I would have been past due on those bills.
Furthermore, I asked for an ADDRESS CHANGE ON MY CHECKS AND NO CARBON COPY.
I was just sent the same thing. The laziest possible response to my request.
So disgusted in what youve become since this stupid pandemic started. I dont feel like a customer anymore, i feel like im under scrutiny when i walk in or like youre being told "ONLY OUR DRIVE THRU IS OPEN" we might all get a runny nose and die ahhh. Not to mention all these wierd looks your tellers give out.
Im just chalking it up as the messed up millinial world today.
As a business in which you run tv ads claiming you're so great of a bank, you should have gotten it right the first time.

This is a small and cozy restaurant. Prices are very reasonable, service is good and fast and food is amazing. Last time we went there for lunch we ordered thai dumplings, tulip dumplings, drunken noodles and mussaman curry chicken. I don't tolerate spicy food too well but my dish had the right amount of spiciness (*). My husband, on the other hand, loves spicy food and ordered drunken noodles (***) that made him sweat but he loved it.

KC's Pastries reminds me of childhood.  My mom would pick up pastries from KC's and come home with a white box full of pork buns, sponge cakes (chocolate and coffee flavored), coconut custard buns. and lemon curd rolls.
