We used to pick the pastries behind the counter, but now it's more self-service.  You'll find many of the buns in glass bins around the store.  Take a tray and use the tongs to pick what you want, then head to the counter to ask for some additional pastries behind the counter before paying for your goodies.

Strange experience    Went through drive through, hands me the bag of food like its a football, bunched up from on its side. Meanwhile a man is standing on the flip side of the window as they apparently accepting walk up orders, vs letting someone in.    No napkins placed in bag not sauce offered.    All around bad drive up employee.

I was reluctant to go in this place for a myriad of reasons including the reviews on here. I'll preface this review with a "when Mananas falls, East Falls falls." With the dismal entrepreneurial climate in this city at the moment, East Falls needs to show some support and give some positive pointers to the operators of the restaurant.

Alright, from the street I had recently noticed the effort to keep the place spotless. Super clean windows, outdoor seating, attentive staff always moving. On my walk over to the taproom, I said "hey lets give Mananas a shot ..." 

We ordered Margaritas (the high point of the meal for sure! EXCELLENT quality) chicken burrito, beef tacos, and the standard chips and dip. Chips were good, salsa was good, chicken burrito seemed to be super fresh and quality, and beef tacos were average and need some work. Drinks were excellent. I would definitely go back here for drinks, chips, and a game.

I found this place to be better than most dive style Mexican and above chain (chillis etc) quality. However, Philadelphia has a tough Mexican market and the plethora of unique food trucks, Garces,Steve Starr restaurants and the local spots too.

Not sure what could give this place the edge, a name change? New Chef?
I'm also wondering why my meal was so much more delicious than everyone on here?

Libertine may be my new favorite cocktail spot on Mass Ave. I had never heard of this place or been to a speak easy in Indy until a friend suggested we go over the weekend. I am a fan of Mass Ave downtown and was definitely on board to try this place out. Situated in the basement of Pizzology, it has a nice, cozy, 1930s prohibition vibe. You walk down some stairs and are greeted with the brick walls, dim lighting, and bartenders decked in suspenders (love it). 

We had to wait a few minutes to enter because it was at capacity. They let two of our party of four go in. My sister and I found our way to an opening at the bar and were instantly impressed with their liquor selection and cocktail list. Looking around we didn't notice it to be overly packed and I instantly became grateful that management there cares about the experience enough to not let it get wall to wall full of people- so cool! 

I wanted to see these bartenders skills so I told him what I like and had him create me a drink. TO DIE FOR. All I know is it was vodka, with a cucumber and I believe mint on top. Not sure of the other ingredients but it was mixed to perfection. All of my friends enjoyed great craft cocktails as well. We we able to snag a table after only a few minutes and retreated there the rest of the night. There is a screen set up that is playing what I think is live footage of downtown indy- once again, SO COOL. 

This space is unique and a great place to grab a cocktail with friends. Definitely more upscale than $2 you call its- so be prepared to fork over a few dollars for drinks.
I usually get the food to go and it is always consistent and the beer is cold. The turkey is the best around.
The gyoza is amazing, the hibachi is authentic and delicious. I only wish they were located closer to my house!!!

Terrible. No flavor at all in the Alfredo pasta. Very watered down. My Southwestern Caesar salad was ok but the spinach chimichanga was over cooked and very bland. Our bang bang shrimp was way over cooked also. Our waiter was super nice and good at his job. Just wish the food was worth the cost. It's really not.

Food and atmosphere is kid-friendly and I have kids so we've been here a few times.  Service is hit-or-miss.  Some of the wait staff are friendly and attentive, some are indifferent.  Today we had a very very poor experience.  Our server was in a foul mood and had no problem sharing it.  I don't think I'll be back for a while because it left a bad taste in my mouth.  There are many choices out there...  I'll go somewhere where my family ad I will be treated with courtesy.

Chips from a bag, chunky tasteless salsa, margaritas (more like gnargaritas) and toxic blue salt on the rim.  

We tried desperately to get a seat on the patio but our efforts failed even though there were many open tables!

Philly has a plethora of excellent Mexican restaurants.  Ditch this place and try somewhere new.

I've lived in this area for about 6 years and this place has just gone downhill. It used to be Egg and I, a great restaurant, but must have been bought out. The service has become so much slower when the place isn't even busy, the glasses are always dirty and look like they haven't been washed, and the food is alright but not good enough to by-pass the other problems. Most of their "healthy" options have a large amount of sugar and they've never gotten my order right when I've asked for replacement sides or for them to leave off an item of the meal. Overall - bring back Egg and I

While visiting Giant this Subway is located in I asked again about "Daily Special ". Staff said yes it was available. They were all very nice and made me a great sandwich. Told me at times when daily specials would be overridden by aSubway "special". Never heard of such a thing. Good meatball sub.

This place is great. Lot of variety on the menu. Good clean decor and has a small bar and a good all you can eat salsa bar which I find amazing. Food came out hot and fresh tasting. Prices are normal to slightly high but the quantity of food you get is good.

I"m not sure if they are going out of business or what.  But they appeared open with only a few pallets of anything inside.  There were people inside, but it didn't look welcoming.  I left as soon as I walked in the door.  It DID NOT look like the photos.

My first visit to a Cora was a few years ago in Calgary and it was an AWFUL experience.  Not really their fault though.  We waited forever to get in, then sat down and looked at the menu - My husband at the time is allergic to nuts and doesn't like eggs and I am allergic to most raw fruit.  So... we found it quite tricky to find things we would enjoy.

Fast forward a few years... and move your sights to Edmonton.  My aunt from out of town came in and this was the only place she wanted to go for breakfast.

Sigh.  Fine.  

As expected, the wait was long, and the line up was cramped into the front entrance, feeling very much like cattle.  My aunt is in a wheelchair, so when she met up with me (i was halfway through the line) she waited outside, rather than trying to maneuver the stuffy line up.  When it was our turn to be seated, the host (maybe manager? he looked like he might have been in charge), happily opened the exit door for my aunt to come in without having to make the whole line move.  Okay... that was very nice of him.  My opinion was changing.

Sat down... service was quite slow and inattentive.  I looked over the menu and found something that I actually thought I would enjoy!  When the waiter finally came to take our order, I asked if it would be possible to get ONLY strawberries and bananas on my plate, as I am allergic to almost everything else.  He said YES!  i was very happy.  My meal came (i don't remember what it was called... Peggy's something... it was basically a poached egg on toast with fruit).  it was delicious and i was SO happy to be eating fruit that I was able to.

So... why only 3 stars?  My mom and I went back a couple weeks later because I was craving it.  And again, I asked for only strawberries and bananas.  The waitress said YES!  but there's a charge.... Last time we went, my aunt paid and i never saw the bill.  And the waiter didn't tell me that there was a charge.. 
I don't like finding out AFTER the fact that I'm being charged extra.

Great food - no question. Super martinis. But our waitress was clueless. She didn't know the drinks well enough to pour them in the right glass for the right person. Somehow I think the S'more martini would've been better if she went through the effort to bring it garnished with a toasted marshmallow as described and dumped it in the glass with chocolate drizzle instead of pouring it in my dinner companions used glass which had a citrus martini. I mean wow - for $8 get it right we were her only table as they had just opened.

Authentic chinese noodles.  We got the lots of spice hotness level and could not finish our bowls!! very numbing peppercorns.  The pop corn chicken was also good. They were very busy and we waited a long time for our build your own bowl orders.  Other than that very good food.

Surprisingly fresh and delicious lunch time sushi at the mall. Always friendly, if not frighteningly prompt, the staff aims to please. The salmon is particularly tasty, and it's great for a quick bite to eat for lunch.

I had my Tragus pierced and my fiancé had the air Jordan jump man put on his calf WE LOVED OUR EXPERIENCE  we will be back one day because we are from n.j. but will drop in again for sure!!!!10-1-2015

First of all there was no one at the check in desk! We finally found someone to seat us! We were here for breakfast and they had a breakfast bar so we ordered it.  Very inconvenient you had to fill out a piece of paper to order an omelet & could only have 3 items give it to your server and then wait - meanwhile food getting cold - ordered 2 eggs over medium. Food on bar was slightly warm & not well prepared and probably the worst thing was the poor service - the server would fill my coffee cup after I flagged her down & ask but not fill my husband's cup & we had to flag her down again!  I would not recommend this restaurant!

Our server, Sara/h, was really sweet and just the right amount of attentive. The food was good, and reasonably priced.  However I'm confused. Really confused.

The place is called Pub 'n Grub, however they call themselves a bar and ristorante. There is nothing italian about this place.

The menus are paper and look like take out menus, yet they have cloth napkins.

I think they need to decide which direction they want to go in, because its just complicated at this point.

I wouldn't seek pub 'n grub out, but I wouldn't refuse it if the opportunity presented itself to go again.

This is going to sound nuts, but I had the best mushroom Swiss burger at this pancake place today. My coworker wanted breakfast, but I was craving a burger. Fortunately, the giant menu at Christina's had us both covered. Unfortunately, I left my phone at the office so you'll have to take my word for it, as I have no photographic evidence of my well-portioned burger. The platter came with fries or Mac-n-cheese and soup. I didn't want the soup, so asked for a side salad instead. No dice, unless I wanted to pay extra for it. That side salad would have likely bumped this review to 5 stars. Anywhoooo... our server Brenda was great. Very attentive. Quick on the drink refills without hovering. She really seemed to enjoy her job. The decor is homey without being kitschy and the environment was clean. I told my coworker we should put this back in the regular rotation.

I use to go to Great Clips until I found that although my hair was shorter, I still looked the same. Then I found the Handsomizer. Not only do I feel cooler, I am Handsome. Can you put a price on this? No, but luckily there's a menu of services to help guide you. Thank you Handsomizer for making me look and feel handsome!

indy definitely has a more interesting and better-focused night life than most small cities.  loved the slippery noodle.  could have had something to do with my old buddy Biscuit (and his band, "the mix) headlining.  nice venue.  good sight lines, friendly staff.  not your typical "gruff" blues club experience, but still quite authentic and definitely rockin.
I came back and they have a new staff and chef. Its back to what it use to be. Enjoy myself  there for the first time in s log time.

don't waste your money - hole through the cigar after barely getting a few puffs - brought it back for them to see - and was told... that's how they are rolled... right - I said it is rolled incorrectly... got into an argument and told I didn't know what I was talking about. Um no my friend - you don't roll a cigar so it has a HOLE in it!

don't waste your money - there are better cigar stores in New Orleans with QUALITY merchandise - this stuff is just crap

This review pertains to the "Corn Creepers" Haunted Maze only and not the farm as a whole. We only had tickets for this and did not have time to do the other attractions such as the regular corn maze, "Zombie Paintball", and the pumpkin patch.

We pre paid for our tickets online and I highly recommend that you do as well. The line just to buy tickets was insanely long. Also keep in mind that parking is not only free, but well organized as well.

We made our way to the line for "Corn Creepers" and got our tickets transferred from digital to hard copy (that's something you need to do BEFORE getting in line). The line probably took around 30 minutes to get through (a "skip the line" upgrade would've been great). 

The maze itself was ok. It was a lot longer than I thought it would be so that's a plus. However, they didn't do a great job at spacing everybody out. There was a bottleneck of people throughout the whole thing and due to this, my party saw most of the "scares" coming a mile away. They did manage to get me a couple times, though. 

There was a solid effort on their part and the scare actors were definitely into their roles. I think with some additions and tweaks, this could be an excellent haunt for the Reno area. Space parties out better, create better hiding spots for the actors, etc. We've already decided that we'll be back next year, so hopefully there will be some improvements done.
Great service! Delicious food! Beautiful views from the dining room. I am never disappointed with Vivace.
There can be some real characters in here, but the convenience of this Walgreen's can't be beat.  Except maybe by the Schnuck's across the street.

Extremely disappointed in this restaurant, and they have lost a longstanding customer. I've been ordering from this restaurant for over a year now and I don't take lightly to being duped out of my hard earned money, especially when your prices were high to begin with. The food was good enough (although the wings are small) so if the prices went up, no problem.. I'd pay our don't order.  But either your menu needs clarity, or your employees need to know what they're doing. As long as the young man was preparing my orders, I had no problems. All of a sudden, my last 3 orders were incomplete. Not happy and never ordering from you all again. I've dealt with it out of convenience, but no more.  I will also be sure to pass on my experience so people will know.

I've been here 3 times now.  Pleasant people, reasonable prices, and an education in the "world of beers".  I have discovered some new favorites thanks to this place. All in all, a good addition to the rejuvenation of downtown. Yayyyyyyy!
First time trying this restaurant. Surprised us how good the food was! Delivery was prompt as well. Will definitely order again.

We recently dined at Brick and Brew after getting rave reviews from friends. First let me say this location in Media is perfect. Downtown vibe in the burbs, it's trendy and the decor is nice. I have a few issues though with our experience.

First is the service. We were seated almost immediately inside by the open kitchen. It took roughly 10 minutes before we were greeted. Our server did apologize because he didn't know it was his table. He walked by us at least 15 times though. Anyway, let's get to the food.

We started with the mussels. Easily forgettable. First off, half of them were still closed. Which means, they were not ready. Secondly, the sauce fell flat. There wasn't much flavor to it, there were barely any Fresno's and no corn at all.

My wife really enjoyed her salad. She loved the vinaigrette. The fruit was nicely grilled and everything was fresh. I went with the burger. The best part of it was the fries. There is nothing like a hand cut fry. They were perfect. The burger again was forgettable. It's not on the menu and was a special. The burger was overcooked, the bun fell apart. The bacon was good and the burger blend was nice. I didn't care for the sauce. I also would have liked some tomato and lettuce to cut through the heaviness. At $16 it really wasn't worth it. I would be willing to give it another shot, maybe for the pizza? 2 stars
Too cool for people who can't drink caffeine. Screw nursing mothers and anyone else. "We currently don't have a decaf grinder". Now, or ever? "Ever."

The cafe lattes here are delicious. I usually get flavored lattes which they don't have but they made me a "warm and cozy" which was sweetened with cinnamon and honey and was delicious.  The chai latte is more floral than spicy which could be good or bad depending on your preference.

Be extremely careful when purchasing a car from this dealer who claims to offer exemplary customer service and a 50-year family tradition.  Longevity in the business does not necessarily mean good faith and honesty.  Buying a classic car is risky for various reasons but the customers should not be cheated or misled in the process, especially from dealers with half a century of experience.  My advice is to start by decoding the VIN number and the data on the metal plate to assess the originality of the car and what has been modified that is not disclosed by Daniel Schmitt.  Pay close attention to the words they choose to describe the condition of the car as they can be very deceptive and intentionally inaccurate (impeccable, immaculate).  Even if you are an experienced mechanic, always check the car IN PERSON and look at the actual condition versus the condition described online.  Look at the exterior and interior in detail, look for basic flaws with the engine (sounds, leaks, brakes, etc.).  If the description states fully restored, request all records of the restoration (who/where/when/what). This will help you decide whether the car is truly what you are expecting and what you are willing to pay top money for.  Incredibly, you will find that in all likelihood Daniel Schmitt will sell the car to someone else after they realize you are an informed customer or one they cannot easily sell to.  Remember, it's always a gamble but your job is to avoid being scammed due to negligence and lack of follow through on your part.  Do your homework and Good Luck!

We came here pre concert for Jimmy Buffet and what a great time! Erick treated as well and encouraged us to get tequila shots (bittersweet moment). Great service overall and one of my favorite bars downtown!
Spot on customer service. You can tell they enjoy what they do and it makes a big difference. 6 year old and 12 year old enjoyed the ride. Definitely recommend.

I visited here twice. The first time, I would give it 5 stars because my partner and I had good service and food. As for the second time, I would give it zero stars if I could. I made a reservation for 10 and they happily took it... but failed to tell us that they had run out of food. I came in and was completely ignored. After waiting for a while, I walked over to inquire about our table and was promptly placed in the back. They had my table reserved, the manager just forgot to acknowledge customers because he assumed we were all walk ins. Once we sat down and realized they literally ran out of rice. They said it had been a really busy week. Everything took forever to come out. We basically only got skewers because they ran out of everything else. They didn't even have beef skewers. They took down some orders and 40 minutes later told us they were sold out of them. We had to wait 40 minutes to find that out? If you didn't have the help or FOOD, don't take the reservation or at least let us know ahead of time. We spent 2 hours here and then went home to eat leftovers from our fridge. We were so hungry and couldn't wait to get the little food that they did have in stock. We waited so long for everything I was so excited for the check so I could go somewhere else to find food. It was my family's worst dining experience ever.

The staff were nice enough, but the manager/boss was the worst. The restaurant was so busy and crowded but all he did was make angry faces at the staff. He could have greeted guests or apologized for the wait or neglect or lack of food. He could at least have acknowledged us. Instead, he simply bossed the the waiters around and refused to make eye contact with anyone else. It was such a tense and horrible atmosphere!

The cooks were packing food for takeout and delivery the whole time. The manager approved all the take out and reservations even though they didn't have the manpower or food for it. I blame poor management for the poor experience. (The "manager" I speak of is the white man who usually sits at the counter making angry faces at the waiter instead of helping.) The waiter was nice despite how busy and overworked he was.

Whatever food we were able to get was good, but quite sweet/one-noted and lacked umami.

This is my go-to salon, and I almost don't want to write a raving review because I don't want everyone to start coming here and overcrowding it. 

I came here after I was kicked out of my old usual nail salon because a bridal party booked the place. Thank goodness for that bridal party, because I found this gem.

You absolutely can't beat $48 for a quality gel mani and regular pedi, which is what I always get. Best prices for good quality in the city. Only reason I'm giving it 4 stars instead of 5 is because, service wise, there are other nail salons where I've gotten amazing additional services such as massages and foot shaving.

Can't believe it took me over a year to get back over here. SHAME. 

My pops and I went this past Saturday, and again a handful of folks were on the waiting list, but fortunately for us, there were two seats at the bar, AGAIN! 

Peggy was our wonderful waitress, who was extremely attentive and very pleasant. 

I totally should've checked my review because I ordered the same thing as before, the brie and prosciutto crepe, and again, it was perfectly savory and filled to the max with prosciutto! We did get the half order of Aebelskivers, delicious balls of doughy goodness. And no meal is complete without an order of their bacon, quite possibly the best side of bacon in town. 

No booze, but again, I'm okay with this considering their food is so great, the service is wonderful, and the restaurant itself is so dang cute. 

If you are with a bigger group, expect a bit of a wait, but it's worth it. And if you're like me, and come with your pops, check to see if you can belly up to the bar!

This is my absolute new favorite veggie burger in town! The sweet potato fries were quite literally the BEST I've ever had. Thai Coconut Soup and Corn Fritters are, also, quite good. I would recommend this place to EVERYONE!

Disappointing brunch.  
Limited menu at brunch.  No lemon ricottta pancakes.  French toast came with mushy bananas , not carmelized and no walnut crumble.  
Highly overrated.

Stopped here several times for lunch, including a late lunch today.  Very dark inside, dark wood, dark floors, dark ceilings, and all the windowblinds closed.  But hey, it's supposed to be a cozy pub, right?
Typical pub/bar fare, not that great.  Had the traditional pub tenderloin, this one looked like it was fresh out of the deep freeze from the wholesaler.  No hand breaded tenderloin here, just some institutional stuff like you'd get at the high school cafeteria.  The bun looked like something I'd have leftover in a bag of buns smashed down in my bread drawer for a week.  Cole slaw was most likely from a tub as well.   But it was edible, not horrid, and the place was clean.  Must be the hangout for chain smokers, because the place smelled horribly of stale smoke, despite the exhaust fans running like crazy.  Came out with my clothing saturated with smoke smell, even though I only saw one person smoking on the far side of the room.

I decided to take myself on a little vacation and picked Summerland as my destination.  Unfortunately, I didn't think about food.  I googled a few places, saw that Stacky's Seaside was literally right down the street so I decided to give it a shot.

I can't say I had the best experience.  I was the only customer there and yet the girl at the cash register made me feel like I was a nuisance.  I ordered 2 fish tacos at almost $3 each, a Casesar salad with "unreal crab" and a diet coke.  This came out to almost $20.  Wow.  This better be really good food.

I have to admit the fish tacos were pretty good (once I scraped off the tartar sauce) but I was annoyed because I asked for no tartar sauce.  When I got back to my inn, I realized the cashier didn't convey my request.  

I was really excited to dig into my caesar salad because of the crab.  When I saw that it was "UNREAL CRAB", I was thinking "Wow!!!  It's going to blow my mind!"....and then I realized with the first bite that it was imitation crab.  It was "unreal" alright.  What a disappointment.  

Another reason why I'm only giving them 2 stars?  I had to ask the cashier to please put a lid on my sode after I had already told her this was a to-go order.  I also had to look and make sure I had utensils for my salad.

I'm sure I'll be back in Summerland again and I'm also sure that I won't be going back to Stacky's.

Terrible New Orleans cuisine and the staff appeared to have a bit of chip on their shoulder. The shrimp and grits was a watery bland mess and way over priced. I am giving two stars because the bread pudding was very good.

As a golfer I had a great time as the range was intriguing.  The colors that came off the golf range were mind blowing.  To get your score high you have to shoot into their traps and get points.  Fun.  The food was not all that bad but would not take my wife there for a super awesome dinner experience.  I would rate the food as bar food.  I get it, it's a bar.  I went to a yelp event and we rate the food experience.  Again the food was not bad but the complete package is great.  I will take my wife here for the fun.  The service provided by the waiters was incredible.  The waiters were by my side to provide me with drinks as needed.

Craig and his staff were phenomenal! Craig was respectful, honest, and on top of everything from the moment we called him for our wedding. 
Craig and his team safely shuttled our closest family and friends from the hotel to our wedding venue in Goleta. The buses were great - clean and most importantly, on time! Not to mention he might be the last vendor in the Santa Barbara area not gouging his customers. He offers a reliable service with great staff and at an affordable price. I highly recommend them!

I have outrageously expensive testing equipment; all of the equipment I have is $45K as well as pre-market prototype items that the manufacturer is keeping under wraps before they make it widely available to the public, so it's up to us to keep everything safe, or I'm sure my future grandchildren will be paying for it too.

After I checked in and hauled my suitcase up to my room, I went back down and got my Pelican Case, then after prepping myself for a nap after driving for 2 days in a row with the only rest being 3 hours of sleep at a truck stop, the room phone rings. The front desk is calling me because a guest alleged that I brought a firearm into the room; firearms aren't allowed on the premises, and if I do have any, I have to at least keep it in my car or somewhere off-premises (which is stupid considering most violent crimes against unknown persons are committed using stolen guns, and I don't want to be part of that statistic), or leave and go elsewhere with no refunds... Most SUVs (including mine) don't have a locking trunk that can readily secure things (you can smash the rear window vs. needing a crowbar), so it's ripe for the taking.

Best part of this? You know the $45K of equipment I mentioned? That was what was in the Pelican Case; I didn't have a gun at the time! Apparently either some guest decided to make a wild assumption and get everybody worked up over nothing, or they're trying to make an imaginary guest to offset their liability like the classic phrase "asking for a friend" (lots of things don't add up, and even if I did have a gun, there was nothing on its website or Expedia detailing this so I know to not part with my hard-earned pay for anti-2A hotels)!
Even though they were busy, the food came quickly. The roast beef sandwich was amazing and what I expected. We all enjoyed our food and service.

Very tasty Chicken Parmesan and reasonably priced. House bottle wines a bit pricey considering home brand. One of our 6 guests had 2 of 3 ingredients of her entree uncooked and had to send back (no we did not demand free meal). Manager provided 2 large portion deserts for table as apology- not expected but appreciated. 
If nice outside, do the patio if able!
Would be 4 to 5 stars if entree was cooked right the 1st time and wine prices a bit more in line.

This place is pricey. We came during their lunch special $11.99 for a steak, beans and side. Just two lunch specials with water came out to be around $25 dollars. You can go to Texas Roadhouse and get two specials with drinks for that price. Also, their steak was very bland and their French fry's which looked very appetizing tasted like fry's you'd get from mc Donald's. Very disappointed with this place. Not worth the price.

I got the grilled shrimp poboy but I wasn't a fan of the flavors. The shrimp had a great juiciness to it but it was overly garlic-y? I don't really know if it was garlic but there was something overpowering about it that didn't mesh well with the prickliness of the carrots and radishes. I'm curious about their pork belly poboy though.

My husband and I used to visit the El Maguey on Olive Blvd in St Louis, and were excited when it moved to our hometown.  We have eaten here many times and are never disappointed. Most of the time we have no complaints, but occasionally we have had a slow server.

I decided to go here because I was throwing up to the point of passing out, so they put me in a small cramped a** janitor closet basically with a tiny bed and a tv to match for well over 7 hours. The nurses were all short with me and rude as you expect from a hospital, but the worst part comes in the fact that the doctor rolled in and said that he thinks I have stomach cancer or Crohn's disease, then he hands me a fat stack of papers which you may think had a good deal of info on it but instead was just a safety Manuel for how to not get COVID. After all that the very next day I get a fat bill, I talked to my insurance and they said they would cover even more because of how much was charged so I get in contact with this hospital to tell them what I was told, with screenshots of a statement my insurance sent me in the mail. Customer service as you'd expect from a company that takes from the dying to line their own pockets told me to completely f*** off. TLDR: come here if you want to get treated like human trash by staff or instead you could just go to your job and get that same treatment but getting paid instead.
Nice menu. Recommend the fig salad. Stay away from the house wines - the reds are served cold. Not friendly service. Came on our way to the Trenton airport. Not impressed.

I made the mistake of ordering oysters, what I got should have closed them down forever.  The second order, was full of sand, yuk.  The peel and eat shrimp were not fresh.  Do not spend your time or money here, want good seafood, go to gulf blvd in tampa bay.  This was truly disgusting.  I did talk to the manager about it, and the very very young man did not even seem to care.  I will NEVER go back there again.

I arrived in New Orleans late and this was the only place still serving food.
Little did I know, I was about to have the WORST DINING EXPERIENCE OF MY LIFE.

How do you manage to make shrimp in creole sauce and red beans, sausage and rice bland? Even the jambalaya was disgusting- the closest comparison I can come up with is dog food. The bread pudding fell short as well and was served in the kind of bowl you might find by a college cafeteria soft-serve ice cream machine. 

Now here's the kicker- I was at a table of eight, all students, some of us acquaintances meeting for the first time that day. Most of us only have cards, no cash. And we weren't allowed to split the check. There was no possible way of us paying on one card. So, the manager came over to "help." He said that they could split it, but it was the owner's policy not to. This policy was displayed on the hostess stand on an index card about a foot from the ground, but, somehow, this fact made the manager feel he had no responsibility to help us.

He disappeared and the waitress returned with the check, a calculator, and a pencil and told me I could split up our check manually. Seriously. 
So I did- I wrote tiny checks on a ripped up napkin, complete with sales tax and their 16% added gratuity for groups. 

There was not even an apology from the manager or the waitress, even though more than one person at the table found their meal to be inedible. We would've been better off eating bar food. 

I have no idea how a place this awful stays open in a city like New Orleans. If you value your sanity and want to have a good experience in a city known for its food, stay away. Stay far, far away.

I dropped my vehicle off after 5pm on Monday, June 15. After receiving no phone call, I finally called on Tuesday to see the status of my car. The person on the phone advised that my service person (Michael) didn't come in until after 4 and to call back. I called back and left a message with the receptionist since Michael was not available. I received no phone call. I called again on Wednesday. Michael was not available, I left a message, and no phone call back. I called Thursday morning and spoke with the Assistant Service Manager, Chad. He told me my car had been ready since Monday evening and he couldn't explain why I was never called. I went to pick up my car, spoke with Chad since Michael was not available, and he was zero help. I had asked when I dropped my car off to get the oil changed with synthetic oil. That did not happen. I asked why I was never contacted and Chad tried to argue with me that they had. I told him I would pull out my phone and prove there were not missed calls from them. For a manager to be arguing with the customer really shows a lot. I know this dealership normally has terrible customer service, but this is by far the worst I have ever experienced. I normally don't take the phone or email surveys, but I will be this time. From now on, I will drive out of my way to go to another dealership. I will never return here or refer this location to anyone I know.

This restaurant was a small, cozy place that I highly recommend. I loved that it was more of a small shared plates style. Everything we ordered was so yummy, I couldn't stop eating if though I was full. My boyfriend and I ordered the bacon wrapped blue cheese dates, the mac n cheese, the pork belly, and then the bibimbap rice bowl. Each dish was delicious! We also ordered the pecan pie. The pie was exactly how I like it - simple, homemade, and not too rich. 

If you'd like to take a break from the Cajun/creole style food, I suggest going here!

This restaurant is a good place to go for a simple breakfast. Not welcoming (rude actually) to newcomers which well,  prevent their return. They have vegan options which is a plus, and experimented with unique flavors. However, they fell short in setting themselves apart from other breakfast places.

Oh P.F. Chang's, where to begin. I myself was an employee at this restaurant for multiple years before I finally tired of the degrading and hostile workplace environment that I encountered on a daily basis. To base this review solely on the food just wouldn't do the restaurant justice and I would like everyone to know exactly what goes on before they eat there again.
If you're looking for a mediocre faux Chinese meal, this is the place for you. There is nothing especially exciting about the menu and you could get a far better meal at a number of local family Chinese restaurants. That being said, lets move on to the treatment that employees recieve. If you thought that the service was subpar while dining at this restuarant, the reason is likely the extraordinarly high turnover. This is likely due to the fact that employees are treated like dirt by the management. First, if you work here, don't even consider being sick. Ever. If you call in sick, you will promptly be fired. This fact leads many employees to come in to work and handle food while extremely ill. There were times that I had been throwing up in the bathroom and trying to do my job five minutes later. The fact that any restuarant not only encourages sick employees to come in, but fires them if they don't, is astounding. Second, I personally have never worked for a more demeaning group of people. The management treats employees like they are complete and total idiots. Employees have been told that managers don't need any input from "$2.83 per hour people". Everyday you come to work you live in fear that one small mistake or any misstep will result being screamed at and humiliated in front of the entire staff. I have never worked at a place where so many people ended up crying at some point in the shift. 
I suppose that this review is more to let people considering eating at this establishment decide for themselves if this is the kind of restaurant you want to support. Because I know that I wouldn't want to line the pockets of the type of people who run this place.

Thanks to Nothing Bundt Cakes, my Mom now has an addiction to bundt cake, and she doesn't even like cake.

It doesn't seem like it's going to be all that special, but holy cow, this is a good cake. I always get extra frosting because it's so yummy. I love the white chocolate raspberry flavor the best. I've tried several other flavors and they are fine, but that one is the best one. They do also have seasonal flavors, and I plan on trying the Peppermint Chocolate very soon. 

I like that I can order online for pickup. I do not love that they are closed on Sundays :/ Regardless, ordering and getting a bundt is easy as...cake.

If I could give zero stars I would. This is an update to breaking family heirlooms, dishonesty, and marring furniture and not packing carefully but just throwing into boxes. 
Adam's told me to submit damage report which they provided. However, they have not replied to my damage report submission. 
Stay away from Adam's Moving and Hauling if you value your household items. They will destroy and not take respond to your needs.
Very rude on phone and defensive; very unprofessional. Choose anyone but Adam's Moving and Hauling!!

My experience.. Expedia had a deal with this hotel for $103, I called the hotel directly before I booked maybe they had a better deal well it was 123. I said that's fine but I wanted to do late check out 5pm, the man said it would be 40-50 dollars. I went through Expedia cause of late check out. We get to the hotel exactly at midnight, their was another guest complaining about cockroaches in her room and stormed out. The older lady at the front desk lacked customer service skills she seemed annoyed didn't even great us with a smile. She advise us of the additional 40.00 fee they have to charge us. This wasn't even posted on Expedia nor the man I spoke to that works at that hotel. Hotel itself is eh and the room was clean but could be cleaner. I did find a dead cockroach outside the hall. It's okay to stay at if your looking to just sleep and shower that's it. The breakfast wasn't worth to be added in package so stay at hotel without breakfast so many restaurants surrounding the hotel. Also late checkout was 25.00 up to 2pm

Oysters don't get any fresher than this.  Very talented shuckers can lay a dozen oysters in front of you faster than you can take a swig of your Dixie beer.  Add to that, a solid menu of friend/grilled/blackened seafood and you have a "must stop" in New Orleans!

We often take the train down from Birmigham to New Orleans and this is the FIRST stop, EVERY trip!

I was issued this card by a temporary job I worked for. The process of adding my personal banks routing number and everything wasn't hard but I definitely could not figure it out on their website. I had to call comdata and be walked through how to add my bank to transfer funds. Ok, ten business days and it should become available for transferring. I waited...

Funds were held hostage for 3 WEEKS as I was waiting for my personal bank to be approved to move funds from the comdata card to my personal bank. The transfer limit is $2500. When I tried to move funds over with a customer representative, she said no, for safety reasons you must go through the automatic system, then she transferred my call.

The automatic system asked for the amount. I put in 25 cents to test it out. Guess what - funds did not transfer to my personal account - they were taken from my personal bank and deposited to comdata! Oh! What a headache. And I was charged a fee on the first transaction! :*(

I called back comdata and asked for the fee to be removed and they said ok, as just a one time courtesy. I also said I used the automatic system to transfer funds but it worked the opposite way.. Funds were deposited into comdata - not my personal account. She said yes. I asked can it be moved to my personal account? She said yes I'll transfer you to the automatic system :O

I'm headed out now to one of their ATMs. Going to pull funds in the most manual way I can and walk it over to my bank. Too bad the ATM limit is only $200/day. But better than nothing. I have bills to be paid and who has time for all of this??? $200 today, $200 tomorrow, etc..

My husband believes this is a scam and the longer they hold money, the more money they make. Not sure what that means but I just need enough for now to get the bills paid by Jan 2!
Recommended by a coworker of mine. Delicious and soooo much food for the price! The burritos are massive and the street style tacos are yummy, too.
Good place to meet for a business breakfast.   Food is good, service attentive and friendly.

I traveled from Tahoe to Honey Bakery because of the rave reviews I read and a craving for Asian pork buns.  

My first impression - I was in awe of the inexpensive prices! 

Then I noticed a sign saying that the Honey Buns on display were just that, and the ones you order are kept in a warming rack behind the counter so you know they are fresh. 

I had to ordered one of almost everything!

When you bite into the buns they are sticky, warm, sweet and soft. Each one had a distinctively different flavor. The BBQ Pork Buns, despite their artificial color, had a great, sweet pork and onion flavor. The mushroom flavor in the Mushroom Chicken was front and center. And the Curry Chicken Buns are definitely my favorite.

The Custard Tarts, again- despite the artificial yellow coloring, had a pleasing silky texture. And the Wife Cakes, although a bit dry, had an unusually tasty sesame paste filling. 

Everything was so good we came back the next day! But, what I was really craving were steamed buns. To our delight, they had frozen BBQ Pork Buns available to take home and steam, without having to order ahead of time. Yay! 

I going to call ahead and make sure they have frozen Curry Chicken Buns for me next time I'm in Reno. 

It may not be your fancy Asian bakery from San Fransisco but I give it an A+
Amazing Italian sandwiches- our first time here and it was a home run. Great menu, super friendly service.

Usually when we're in East Nashville we head to Rose Pepper, Nomzilla, or the Pharmacy. Last night it was late leaving our appointment and we spotted the ever delicious Popeyes across the way. Our dream of delicious, quick chicken turned into a COMPLETE disaster!

We waited over 30 minutes inside the restaurant waiting for a our order to be completed. The simple system of take the order, hanger the ticket, fill the order was fair too complicates for their crew. Their shift lead and manager were about to have melt downs as the children they had working with them couldn't follow simple instructions. They kept losing tickets, filling tickets over again, running out of food, screwing up orders. We thought about recording it just because it was so unbelievably how awful they were running. 

The place was a complete disaster! The floor in front of the Coke machine was soaking wet, so much lack of gloves on the line, no napkins- just a mess!

When we finally got our food there no apology for our wait. We had to tell them to add a missing item.

My first job 18 years ago was Popeyes- literally the easiest job EVER! Working up front- Take order, hang order, fill order, smile and say thank you. Working in back or on the line- stock things and clean as you go! 2+2 = 4 kind of simple

Just avoid this place- it's not worth it... Unless you want a show

We came here with our friend who is local. We only had to wait 5 minutes for a table and the place was packed! They do not have a huge menu, but it seems like everything on there is good! I had the half roast beef po boy and a cup of gumbo. The po boy was good, but the gumbo was amazing!! It had the right amount of spice. The chicken in it was shredded up and the sausage was tender. I wish I would of gotten the bowl! My friend had the Reuben. He said he never gets Reubens, except for here. This is because we are from where the Reuben was invented- Nebraska! So it must be a good one if he only gets it here outside of home! My husband had the house special BBQ shrimp. He said it was really good. The thing is it isn't a regular bbq sauce, it more like a garlic and butter sauce. Unfortunately for my husband not much after we left he said he didn't feel good and then yelled for our friend to stop the car. Well the all bbq shrimp came back up... We are prettier sure that he was just not used to all the butter. Besides that we had a great time!

This place is a complete dive.  When I went, it was almost completely empty and the band I wanted to see didn't start at their scheduled time, but rather 2 hours later.  There's ratty couches to sit on and some chairs scattered in the back.  There's a cheaply made bar that doesn't look clean.  They only accept cash, but there's an ATM in there.  This place has the feel of watching a band of untalented middle school students practice in a garage that they decorated with black lights.

My mother and I have been shopping here for over a year and LOVE this place! They advertise 70% to 90% off retail, but their constant sales boost this percentage to about 95% a lot of the time! We have gotten some amazing deals here. 

More recently I bought a Lafayette 148 NY sweater for $0.01 (not a typo), a Kay Unger dress for $6.30, and a BCBG romper for $15. That's $21.31 versus about $1,000. Where else can you find that kind of deal? 

We got so hooked on Clothes Mentor that we can no longer shop at a regular store. Why pay full price for a piece of clothing when you can get a bag of clothes for the same amount?

Best of all, you get quality clothing, sometimes even with tags.

Clothes Mentor combines the benefits of "fast fashion" (perpetually changing inventory and low prices) with the benefits of designer clothing (style and quality) . . . and it's basically recycling, right? 

Not to mention how wonderful the staff is. They remember your name and will help you find something specific if you ask. They will put the clothing you pick out into a basket so that you don't have to carry it around while you shop. They even have a free personal shopper program which I plan to try soon!

If I could give this place 6 stars, I would!

This place is awesome! We were visiting and looking for a fun Santa Barbara experience. This is the place for exceptional burgers! Staff is super friendly, place is clean and food is delicious!!  Easy to get to with free parking.
We came here for dinner on a Saturday night, was seated promptly and our server Brad was outstanding!

So I'm in Philly with family and, since I went to college here 10 years ago, they asked whether I could recommend a good cheesesteak place that was off the beaten track.  I'd been here once before - again, 10 years ago - and had good memories, so I recommended this place and we went.  The cheesesteaks were fine - not the greatest I've ever had, but certainly very solid and better than the cheesesteaks in Richmond.  Unfortunately, getting the cheesesteaks was a hassle. There was a line out the door, but it was pure madness inside - no real line, just people taking orders, other people milling around waiting for their cheesesteaks, and a general sense of anarchy.  My sister had to step out because she found it too claustrophobic, and I would've followed her if I didn't have to wait for the cheesesteaks.   All in all, it took us somewhere between 30-40 minutes to get the food. This was at about 2 PM on a Saturday, so not even during the lunch rush.  I'd come back again for the food, but you should probably come at a less busy time if you value your sanity and want your food in a reasonable amount of time - if my memories from college still serve, random Tuesday nights in January seem to be a good time.

Love this place. I wish they would expand though. It's too good to keep small. Always good food, great variety, great service. 
I love that it's a "center city" worthy spot that's right in the NE. You definitely need reservations though. This place is almost always full.

Spent 4 days at a hotel next to this location. I like the atmosphere and food, But this location sorry to say sucks!. Friday night there were empty tables and nobody at the wait stand. Many staff passed us and would never acknowledge us. We finally seated ourselves, and waited more. (dates 1/10/17-1/12/17)
  While waiting I sat and watched of duty servers running around drinking yelling and making a total ass of themselves. We did flag someone down who reluctantly waited on us, We had the same type of wait for the bill.
We tried again Saturday with the same results this time we didn't bother, we left and went to another nearby restaurant. I ate every meal out  for 4 days only 1 at this Hooters location because it is a joke.. Spent the night in Macon Ga on the way home and went to Hooters on I believe Riverside dr. It was what you wold expect, Great food, Hot, the server was fast and attentive.. Hooters would do themselves a favor and close the Rivergate store as it gives the company a bad name.

Dave

When I found out there was another VIP location opening I screeched so loudly that my husband ran into the room in terror. I mean like I was frickin excited. 

My excitement was worth it! We walked into VIP at 7pm on a weekend night and were seated RIGHT. AWAY. IYKYK. 

Granted, we had to sit at a high top in the bar area but that was fine by me! The bar is full service and the tables even get table service. The service time was super slow for us, I do believe that was related to short staffing at the bar and in the kitchen. 

The food is darn good at VIP. It does seem like they maybe cut down on some portion sizes? I don't love that, tex mex is supposed to be absurd. That's what makes it so special. Any-who that aside I can joyfully proclaim a new VIP location is the best thing to happen in Indian Rocks this year (possibly). 

Pro tips: the Mexican pizza is great, the quesadilla from the app menu is similar and can easily be a full meal, the fajitas are soooo good and great to share, and the house margs are strong so order a skinny one instead if you're looking to survive 2 drinks .

Visited Han Dynasty with great expectations and it turned to be just abive average. Dont get me wrong the food is v good but guess expected something to blow my mind. We ordered the famous Dan dan noodles and their spicy dumplings. Dan Dan noodles with great and taste it also good however it wasn't worth the hype, the dumplings were very good it was spicy level 7 but you really enjoyed it. The next thing we ordered was there a dry hot pot with fish as a choice of meat this was supposed to be spicy level 10 and I was scared that we won't be able to handle it but I think we did a very good job and really enjoyed it. Actually spicy level 10 could have been a little bit more spicier are probably something more to offer spicy crazy people like me. I had a dogfish beer but they didn't have the one they had on my note and I had to switch to Dogfish Ale which was good because it was Dogfish my wife order they're famous cocktail punch and she really liked it though it was on sweeter note. Videoder there Mochi ice cream for dessert, it comes with 3 different flavors and we tried by pistachio,  dark chocolate and coffee express. The dessert was good. We requested to do a take out for the leftovers and they put everything into cheap plastic boxes inside a plastic bag just like a regular take out OMG they could have done better than that. Also I would have expected the restrooms to be a bit more cleaner. Overall a good place to dine in and that's it

I loved that I was able to get a Saturday morning appointment with little notice, but felt like I was being upsold the entire time.  Amy did a great job listening to what I wanted and had great ideas.  I really liked her!  A bit about the upsell....she conditioned my hair and a special mixture....I didn't realize it was a conditioning treatment that cost extra.  When I went to pay, they started ringing up all of the products (Kerastase...great line, but I should be asked if I want to buy $40 shampoo...especially when I already told her I've used the line before and wasn't impressed) they used and I had to ask them to take them off my bill.  I asked for a receipt and it seemed like it a big deal to get an itemized receipt...that's when I discovered the conditioning treatment was $25.  They did a nice job, the salon is little too industrial and large for my liking, but the shady upselling will be the reason I won't go back.

The atmosphere/decor is great if you like sports bars, which I do.  There are TVs everyone, including at each booth.  The service & food is good, though the prices could be a little cheaper.  Seating areas for both family and "bar".

I was so excited to try this place so I ordered many different things with my partner. We got the yuzu chicken, fried chicken bao, two impossible baos with fries, matcha boba, and matcha sesame cookies. Unfortunately I was really disappointed with everything I had.
I can't speak on impossible bao because those were for my boyfriend. He said they were okay, but nothing impressive. The fries were fine. Not the crispiest.
The yuzu chicken had no flavor. I'm not really sure where the yuzu actually came in. It definitely needed some sort of dipping sauce otherwise it's just plain chicken.
The fried chicken bao was really difficult to eat. The chicken was overcooked making it tough to take bites.
The matcha boba was OK. I didn't expect the boba to not actually be tapioca pearls but some sort of jellies and the size of the bottle was tiny for how much it cost. The actual matcha was nice, but not anything impressive.
Lastly, the cookies. They taste like plain shortbread cookies. No matcha or sesame flavor whatsoever. I don't like shortbread cookies. 

Overall, just completely bland food. I'd skip this place.

Decent rolls but nothing to write home about. The edamami was very good as was the green tea. We enjoyed the birthday roll although it was rather small and the shoya roll. There are many other places that are less expensive and tastier. Overall impression a bit overpriced for the quality and quantity.

My son was burried here in 2006. I was appalled at the service I recieved. The director was horrible and not at all comforting (I'm sure they see death and funerals there every day but I was burrying my 6 day old son. It was the first funeral I had ever been to. He could have been polite) My sons funeral was supposed to be open casket and he left it closed. We payed 8k for a funeral of that $1k was charged for "transportation" they used a white van to drive his casket across the street! I would have much rather had the honor of carrying his little casket across the street. But they took advantage of our grief and price gouged us. I asked the director for a few minutes alone with my son  while everyone walked to their cars and he refused it. His exact words to me were "it wouldnt matter how much time I gave you it would never be Enough" in my grief I didnt have it in me to fight so I walked. This has turned into one of my biggest regrets. I wasnt asking for forever, I was asking for a few moments. People hadnt even made it to their cars yet. That was the last time I would ever see my son and he couldn't let me have my private goodbyes. For the amount I payed for his services I should have been entitled to as much time as I wanted. But I was pushed out to watch the dirt be flung on my child because that man wanted to get on with his day. Not to mention the fact that everything I have ever put on his grave has disappeared. I put fake flowers, they magically disappear. I put a porcelain cross, it disappeared. I put a hotwheel car, it disappeared. I cant even have a sacred place to be near my sons grave. The only good thing I can say about this place is its got pretty scenery.

I brought my 1-year old here for a possible ear infection.  I was told by the employee at the desk that my deductible had not been met and I would need to pay a portion of it ($125).  I was pretty confident that my deductible had been met, but they would not see my child unless I paid this.  I made the payment and was seen.  I called my insurance company the next day to find out my deductible HAD been met.  I called billing before the claim was submitted, but they would not issue a refund until it went through insurance.  I called 4 weeks later and they are issuing a refund for overpayment, but told me I would not have been refunded for the overpayment unless I had reached out to them.  This is very disappointing considering the payment I made was a mistake on their end because they "couldn't see" the family deductible status on the site they use.

This place isn't bad. I really loved the atmosphere, it was very calm and with a warm welcoming feel. Chairs are a little uncomfortable but overall was a comfortable place to be. I ordered a drink, which was a lemon and whiskey mixture. Not bad, but super strong so if that's what you like you'll be happy with it. We definitely over ordered, the small bites are very filling. I loved the fried chicken bao, but the pork belly bao was so sweet I couldn't finish it. Also tried the chicken wings, and those were a positive highlight. We had the curry and bread flats, which I cannot remember the name of now and that was delicious. Biggest downfall was I ordered some pad thai and asked for no shrimp but keep the chicken and they served no chicken and extra shrimp. I ended up taking it off the bill and not having them remake it because it was too late at that point and I was pretty full. Disappointment though because it didn't look bad, I just cant stand shrimp. All in all the restaraunt was a little anti climactic, we were excited to try it and everything kind of fell short. But it wasnt horrible! You may like it.

I discovered Mark's about 3 months ago.  It is one of the few auto-shops that will work on foreign cars.  I drive a 12 year-old extremely temperamental Audi, and both Marks' really helped me out.  One of them even drove to a junk yard to find a used part.  I really can't express how much Mark has helped me.  This auto-shop is honest, fair-priced, and truly a gem in this city.  I'm in a business ALL about referrals, and I recommend this place to everyone!!!  PLEASE start supporting mom 'n pop places vs. Firestone and other such companies that will RIP YOU OFF!

Love the style of the office. Sooooo Saint Mary's in general makes me CRAZY!  The way they have the phone systems, makes me want to loose my mind. You have to call an 800 number to get connected to your own stinkin DR.  I hate that!  However, my DR, Jessica Curti is AMAZING!. and an alternative medicine doctor.. so I put up with the St Marys stuff because of it.  The staff is absolutely GREAT here. I go in for B12 shots and they always remember me, my name, what I was up to the last time I came in AND they way I like my shots! 

I took one star off because of the communication challenges AND... because they have had a broken AC unit on the left hand side of the building for a few years now.  I wish someone would read this review and offer to go in and fix the darn thing! LOL

So....as DR offices go... This one is a good one :)

This is my fav brew house! Scarlet Lane is about 5 minutes from my house so I go there often. I love that they change the beers regularly. They offer some sweet infusions, knowledgeable beer keeps, and a rad selection of games. The only downside is at this time they don't offer a lot of food options, but I am told they are working on that. That is another great part about Scarlet Lane, you become family and the team there want to tell you all of the things that are coming up. 
Speaking of coming up... They are hosting events weekly. They have trivia night, there was a Clueless pizza party, they opened the patio and have cornhole! This place offers something for everyone. The atmosphere of Scarlet is nice. Check out the mural when you come in.
I would recommend this place to those who enjoy good craft beer. Once you walk in, its the vibe of hanging in your basement.

This place is a rip-off, plain and simple. We ordered margaritas, that should have been called fruit punch (0.0 ABV). The food felt like amuse bouche. Tiny portions (octopus tostada, tuna ceviche) for the price. With Dos Segundos down the street being both affordable and delicious, Anejo's pricing doesn't make sense. I looked up their other locations, and they seem to be a NY establishment. This is Philly, the rents are not as high as Tribeca, bring your prices down. Not going back.

This place has gone downhill. Started as a good place, buffet/a la carte, but of late, with this new management and the experience we had recently with one of the staff ( if you want to be rude...dont work in the hospitality industry).  We have been going here for over a decade....not anymore
Always completed on time, always completed with quality. Friendly counter help and often willing to go above and beyond.

Sinbad's is a casual place to lounge about if you have the time for it. It is quiet and relaxing but the service is not the greatest. It takes them a while to refill your water and when they are busy your food will take quite a while. When it is light, the speed picks up slightly. It is pretty expensive for a college student budget but when I came in a group once a gratuity of 25% was added.
The food is pretty good. I've gotten the shwarma and the shish kabob in the past. The entrees come with hummus which is a bit oily but tastes good. The rice was good this time but a few times it was a bit undercooked. The meat is more tender on the shish kabob than the shwarma. The portions are just enough to feel full. I do enjoy the pita and their tzaziki sauce. The experience is enjoyable with less people in a more loungey mood and the food is above average most of the time.

Very happy to have this business open in our neck of the woods at this time!  The selection of baby clothes is great and they also buy gently used baby clothes. May the circle be unbroken! The folks who run the place are exceedingly nice and helpful too! As stated by another reviewer, this is a great destination when you pack up the stroller for a weekend adventure down 4th Ave!

I wish I'd looked on Yelp before buying a dishwasher at Goedekers.  
I am not a happy customer.
The delivery of for my dishwasher was quoted at 8 to 10 business days but actually took 20 business days.  This was 29 calendar days over the holidays that we had to spend without a dishwasher, washing all the dinner party dishes in the sink.
When the dishwasher finally arrived, it was somehow wet inside.  The manuals were soaked inside their plastic bags, and the drain hose had water inside.  How does this even happen?  A dishwasher is waterproof unless the door is open and a hose is pointed at the opening.  Was this a used dishwasher?
The styrofoam base the dishwasher was on was shattered into many pieces, the cardboard box ripped and torn.
I had NO updates on the delivery time until a day before it was to arrive.  No email communication and their website was useless.
After all that I missed out on a $50 manufacturer's rebate from Bosch because it expired before the dishwasher arrived.

Bottom line:  Pay the extra money to buy local.  Don't trust thier delivery quotations.

The hotel is not that bad it's located in an area where there is good eats shopping gas stations. The pillows are very small they do need to improve in purchasing better pillows the housekeeping is great they do clean the room every day the housekeeping ladies are wonderful. There is a young kid at the front desk his name is Zofiel Not very helpful I walked in was sneezing and coughing all over my room keys and the paperwork I needed to sign the last thing I need to get his catch the flu. The day I checked and there was a lady and one of the entrance of the hotel with her dog her dog got a hold of my jeans and was trying to bite me I did complain about this to the front desk Zofiel Promise to fix the problem and give me a call back and never did get that call. Then I asked him that my room did not have a shower curtain and he had the audacity to say if you see a housekeeping personnel let them now. That kid should not be working at this hotel that is my only complaint about this place.

This is where I go to pick up my ingredients to make my famous Bibimbap.    
The store has a good variety of fresh greens and meat selection.  The aisles are just a tad small, but that's the case with most Asian grocers.

Great little diner with huge portions! We both got the Eggs Benedict with locks and both took half back to the condo for the next day's breakfast. Service was great and manager was great. It's a bit of a locals place it seems and we were befriended by an older couple who told us about all of their favorite spots. This is definitely one of them!

Foods good however employees walked in and out with no face covering and allowed patrons to walk in and around the inside with no face coverings. Employee was heard entering with another employee and when the other had no covering on he said "you know the fucking rules just put your shirt over your mouth for now". Nearly each time an employee came out from the back, no face covering. Doesn't make you feel too comfortable they're wearing masks in the kitchen where you can distinctly hear them yelling, likely spraying their spit everywhere. Can do better

Breakfast Yum tucked away in the middle of Lamp & Lantern Village.  Ended up here because Einstein's was ridiculous - who waits in that kind of line for a bagel?! Its a bagel people, get a grip!  Anyway, I digress...

Reasonably priced breakfast items and lots of combo's to choose from.  No hash browns, which is always a let down for Teen 1 and I.  That said, their home fries are better than most.  Bacon was crispy and generous portions were served (YAY - lots of bacon!)   Everything else was good.

The server was really nice and had a really laid-back, sort of surfer dude vibe.  Wasn't sure how that was going to go but he did a great job and also made some recommendations that we liked.

The real standout here for me was the coffee.  I asked if they had iced coffee drinks; he said no but suggested that we try their regular coffee over ice because he really likes it.  Okay.  This was the best thing I had there; Teen 1 liked mine so well that she also ordered one.  I can't remember the brand of coffee, but it was nothing I had heard of before.  I sucked down two of them and so did Teen 1.  Oh, and the best part was that he just charged us for regular coffee.  This would have been $20 worth of coffee at some places. 

Will definitely be back when I'm hungry for a big breakfast with some delicious coffee.

Been here a few times and it's always solid.  I really like the venue itself, it's a cool building.

They have a great selection of burgers, but the only downside is that they are fairly small (palm-size).  Nonetheless, they are delicious!

Just wish they were either larger or cheaper.

I am finding  Franky & Johnny's going down on each visit.  I am worried.  I love this place, I love the parking, I love the basement.  I am finding though the food is now spotty.  I don't know whether there is a new chef, cook, or new recipes.  I love their daily specials, their crawfish was always REAL spicy, and their bean recipes could not be beat.  They need to kick it up a notch though because some of the dishes are not the same.

Food was okay, but nothing special. The breakfast burrito was was you'd expect, but nothing unique or overly flavorful. The salsa they give you with it is bland. 

Great outdoor seating area across from the beach, which is nice. 

Blood Orange Margarita is incredible. That item itself gets 5 stars.

Great atmosphere, good food. Long wait. Just a precaution: ~30 min in line, 30 min waiting for food. My boyfriend asked about the time to make the beignets: 5 minutes. Overall, good food, but needs more efficient service.

Ordered 3 milkshakes for dine-in and Waited over an 1 1/2 hours for them to be served after we paid for them.  No sense of urgency  on behalf of the staff to get shakes served.  They had plenty of staff members as I counted 8 staff members standing around the ice cream bins.  

Their cashier was telling people that the wait was around 1 1/2 hours.  The shakes were good but in my opinion not worth the wait, nor price MOL $13.99 a shake.
My parents went there last weekend and ordered the cornbeef special . The meat was tough and chewy , generally it is good. The staff are always nasty and not polite. Very expensive!!

I cruised into Creative Ventures randomly on the search for a white elephant gift. I think the budget was ten dollars. When I walked in a little bit of anxiety set in as I was standing in the entrance with no idea where to begin. The store is much larger than I anticipated and stocks even more goods that I could have possibly imagined. If you're in white elephant fix or searching or a quirky gift or two then this spot has got you covered from Mexican decor to beads, mugs, posters, and Cactus shaped back scratchers, which is what I purchased for my white elephant exchange and it was exactly $9.99. How did I find that little gem among the mounds and bins filled with goodies as far as the eye could see? I asked the owner for help! Otherwise I would've been in there at least an hour. The back scratched was his first recommendation. When I saw it, I burst into laughter and said "I'll take it." by the way, the gift was a hit and was claimed three times during the exchange. A winning choice indeed. I'll definitely be back to peruse the rest of the collection and pick up a quirky holiday gift or two. Our local shops really are the best

Pampa is great for any meat loving person.
Drinks are expensive but otherwise, it's a set price for dinner.  I've been several times and the last time took my very picky Dad who only eats meat.... perfect place for him.  He loved the premise - the waiters come around and offer you various meats when your tab is turned to green.  When you're full, you flip it to red.  
We leave full and happy!

Co-worker & I took a stroll down to City Bistro for some after work drinks & dinner. 

The place is contemporary looking and was clean. 

After some cocktails we ordered some food. I ordered the roast beef and my friend ordered a salad. 

The salad looked good and she said it was very good. I ordered roast beef sandwich which was pretty moist. 

Our waitress Tiffany was pleasant and very attentive to our needs. I would definitely return here. Our total bill was $33.56 but I used my Taste Cards & got $10 back! :) 

Only reason I'm giving them 4 star is because parking somewhat sucks.

I cannot express how disappointed I am in the lack of customer service since we have purchased our 2018 Laredo Altitude. First they had the wrong address for our plates so had to spend 6 hours at DMV to get new ones and status original plates sent stolen. Not to mention it cost us extra $ to get that done. Then the electronics on the vehicle developed a mind of their own and the service area individual was very arrogant and not really helpful. In hind sight if I had to do this over again we would have gone to another dealer and taken our business else where.

One of the best authentic Mexican food restaurants out in Tampa! Staff was super friendly and the food was great. My favorites are the quesobirria tacos, breakfast tazón, and the agua de sandía (sweetened just right)!Music selection was top notch too :)
Good basic diner. Eggs cooked right, regular pancakes, crispy bacon. Normal american diner coffee. Nothing fancy, nothing bad.
Old rooms, smoky casinos...jacked up prices over holidays. Disappointing. Showed up at midnight on a holiday weekend and waited over an hour to check in. No thanks.

Here's the deal.  The cocktails are really exceptional and works of art.  The quality of the ingredients are evident.  Now..... let's talk about the rest of the establishment.  The hostess' are typically rude and make sure they project a "I don't care if you come to drink here or not - you're bothering me"-attitude.  This is the schtick - You are a nobody unless you happen to be some regular or in you throw your money around to get special... well actually normal .... treatment.  On one level it works because for the wannabe's that want to "be all that" they like the sense of getting in a place that is exclusive.  To people who travel and have experience many lounges and VIP clubs... it's a joke.... their attitude.  

Nonetheless, it's pricy and pretentious when it could be exclusive and charming.  My sensibilities become fatigued here very fast and become an amazing bore....  I will need to try the Holland House which is similar in it's cocktails and concoctions.  I just hope they don't have the snottiness.
Great food and excellent service. Best place in town. Not best for watching sports but definitely has that sports bar food.

Today, February 28, 2020, I went to Maya's for lunch with the girls but the host/server (I believe his name was Joshua) greeted us with a very bad attitude. We asked for separate checks and while we understand some places do not do separate checks, the way he told us no was very rude. We still sat down at a table after this and then we heard him cursing at us from the back of the restaurant in Spanish. We understand Spanish and as soon as we heard that we decided to leave. As we left, he told us he kept insulting us, in front of other clientele. I've been to Maya's before this and thought the food was mediocre without a real focus on one Latin American area. Despite this, I decided to return but never again. If you like mediocre food with even worse service then I recommend this place, otherwise, stay away!!!!!

Obviously if one is writing a review for a pet crematory then one is writing with a broken heart. However, Steve and Elaine as well as their staff are some of the most compassionate and understanding people you will ever meet. They help make one of the most difficult times in someone's life just a little bit easier. I have used their services for all my departed pets and will continue to do so. I highly recommend them to anyone needing this service. 
I unexpectedly lost my cat, Carmie, last night. I called their 24 hour phone line and reached Diane. I explained I was at Animal Emergency Center and would be euthanizing my cat. I wanted to arrange for her to be picked up for after care. Diane was wonderful, and so sympathetic and comforting. She said she would contact someone. Within ten minutes Elaine called me to arrange aftercare. They are closed on the weekends so pick-up was not available until Monday. I indicated I would be taking Carmie with me so Elaine arranged for me to drop her off today (Sunday) at the crematory as Steve accepts drop offs from 2pm-4pm. 
Steve was wonderful as I picked out an urn, arranged for the private cremation, and a paw print. They keep a metal tag with an identification number on it with your pet so you know you are getting your pet's ashes back. If you are still unsure, they will allow you to be present for any part of the cremation process. The turn around time is usually within 24 hours as they feel strongly about pets returning home to their owners and pets not being kept in a freezer. Steve told me today that he has had staff there on Christmas Day to perform cremations so pets could return home. They are so dedicated to the owners who entrust them with the aftercare of their precious pets. Communal cremation is also available (ashes are spread with the ashes of other pets in a beautiful location - never a landfill). Their prices are fair and they have a beautiful selection of urns and memorials to choose from. Urns for private cremations are returned in blue velvet bags embroidered in gold with "Until We Meet Again at the Rainbow Bridge". A certificate of cremation, the Rainbow Bridge poem, and a packet of wildflower seeds that can be planted in your pet's honor are also included. Though this subject is so hard to talk about it is comforting to know they are there to care for our beloved departed pets.

Good taste, good service, huge portions!

Very surprising given that our expectations of a chain restaurant weren't high, we both left there planning to return.

The overall massage was great! However, when my therapist told me my 90 minutes was up I thought that couldn't be right. I was right! My hands on massage was about 65 minutes. I checked my watch when I was told to undress and checked it again a few minutes after my massage was over. I was in the room a total of 70-75 minutes and I paid for a 90 minute massage. I spoke to them  about it and was discounted the price of my aromatherapy, $10.  This is the second time this has happened at this company. I don't think I'll be back again.

Good little dive for a Mexican restaurant.  Chips and salsa were delicious. It was very quiet and could have used some music to authenticate the experience.  The food was a little greasy yet very tasty. I'm on the fence whether I would go back.

NEVER SUPPORT THIS PLACE!  IF I COULD GIVE ZERO STARS I WOULD!
