First of all let me say I live a block away and have been in here several times as a repeat customer.   Last night, I was treated the absolute worst as a customer I have ever been treated anywhere in my life.  I ordered two slices of pizza.  Apparently that was a huge mistake.  I sat down to wait for my pizza, and a few minutes passed until they called me up and gave me my total: $7.45.     

This was quite confusing to me, as a slice from Uncle Nicks is usually $2.50, so I replied to the server questioning the total, who had a difficult time speaking English.   I felt for the guy, but we were having trouble communicating so I bent down and tried to check out the screen to see the breakdown and figure out how my total was so much, but I don't have very good vision so it was difficult.

At this time the owner chimed in.  

Owner:  "What's the problem?"
Me: "How is it $7.50 for only two slices?"
Owner: "what do you mean? What's the problem?! Is that a lot?!"    *getting a slightly irritated tone with me
Me: "um... Well yeah, how is two slices that much money?"

It's at this point that he told me I ordered three slices, which i contested and told him i only ordered two, which he argued back and forth with me three to four times, as if I didn't know how many slices I ordered when I clearly only ever wanted two slices, getting increasingly rude with me, then started all out SCREAMING at me to get "take my fucking money and get the fuck out of his store", getting up in my face in a rather violent manner, which I can only assume to be the behavior of someone who does a lot of cocaine or other hard drugs.  I even tried to shake his hand and apologize and tell him I wasn't trying to be a dick or anything like that, but he wasn't having it.

As I was leaving I even heard him call me a "fucking faggot" on my way out.   Pathetic.

Absolutely INSANE response to a simple miscommunication and quite frankly COMPLETELY inexcusable behavior for ANY worker to treat a paying customer, especially the OWNER of the restaurant.

As if this isn't bad enough, I told my friend who lives in the neighborhood about what happened and he told me one time he tried to order delivery, it took an excessively long time to get there so he called in to ask how it was taking so long when he only lives 4 blocks away.   The owners response?  "Oh you only live floor blocks away?  Walk."    And then hung up on him.

People like this should not even have a job let alone be allowed to run a business.

Got a small combo platter.  It was alright for the time & price.  I guess the best way to describe halal guys is that it is like a subway or chipotle-- but with Mediterranean food.  A bowl was 1/4 rice, 1/4 salad, 1/2 chicken & lamb.  The lamb and chicken was diced in order to fit in those metal bins.  The sauce was really good.  Glad that I said yes when they offered it....

Cinco de Mayo is the perfect pregame location before a show at the Cannery or Mercy. It is literally right down the street, the prices are fantastic, and the food is A-Ok. It's not the best in town but it's not bad. It's typical large-scale Mexican food. They also have tamales which are hard to find. We went through a Cannery/Mercy hot streak and ended up eating there before the show almost every time. Also, you can't hate their happy hour. The beers are gigantic. Ginormous. The size of your head. In the words of Donald Trump, UUGE.
Great deal on my new Navigator. When they get a better staff to clean the vehicles after you spend your money they'll get a fifth star

If I could give no stars to this place I would. It is filthy, falling apart and just down right gross. The employee who checked us in had no idea what he was doing. The two bedroom "suites" have no air conditioning in one of the rooms, barely working in the other. We were told that is how all of there suites are set up. Miserable is the only way I can describe our stay.

I have used wright's for take out lunch many times. I have not had a sandwich or salad that I have not liked there. The fruit and cheese platter is really nice for a group. Also they are known for their cakes. You can get an entire cake or just a slice. Either way it's AMAZING!

Bartenders shouldn't talk politics with their customers, period, especially when they rely on the interwebz for their facts.

Politics aside and mediocre service aside, one can always count on Vallarta's to be average at best.  It's almost mind-boggling to think that this place has been in business for so long with all the great Mexican food in town, but it is what it is.  This is just a place to go for meeting friends, decent drink specials and large food portions.  Only plus about this joint is that you can smoke a cigar at the bar after stuffing your face.

Nothing I've ever eaten here has been memorable enough to mention by name, so I'll refrain.

I've just now become a big fan of this shop. Thomas did a great job. He explained the problems in a very simple ways, and the most importantly, he tells us what problems are of urgency or not. Dealers and some repair shops usually recommend us to replace many parts, which is definitely overkilling. He is really fair. I recommend this shop.
Sunday Brunch . Great Spot . Music is Awesome . I recommend anyone coming and celebrate birthday or even happy hour. Very nice . Knowledgeable staff . I definitely will come back. Food was amazing .

Very nice dealership they took the time to show me the car that I wanted explained financial options even though my credit wasn't so hot and came up with a solution so I can get my dream truck. Really had a great experience here

Went in to get at least 8 inches cut off my daughters hair and it was hacked. We wanted it short, but I could have done a better job if I would have cut it myself. 

I'm going to have to pay double to get it fixed. Just be careful! If you're going to go for a major cut for your kid, consider taking them to a real salon.

The owner messaged me and they have since increased their portion size and offered me free breakfast.  The chef most likely used a wrong bowl size.  Thanks great full gardens you are awesome!!!!!!!! Myself and family love your food & service!

They get my husband's order wrong 100% of the time, and that's not an exaggeration. The previous time we were here, my husband orders his sandwich with lettuce only and gets it with lettuce and mayo. He tells the waiter he said lettuce only and the waiter goes "so, no chicken?" And my husband says no. I want lettuce chicken and bread that's it. It comes out with tomato on it. Today, he ordered lettuce only and they gave it to him with only chicken. And it took 20 minutes for him to have his order taken.

I don't understand why this place is still open! The food here is so unappealing, bland, and blah, and there's so much better you can get for your money in New Orleans! I would assume that you're paying for the atmosphere and decore of the restaurant, but with food tasting like a glorified portion of frozen dinner, why bother?

I really wanted to give this place a higher rating for a number really great things they did well....like
1) the host/sommelier was extremely nice upon on our arrival, helped us in picking a good wine that wasn't incredibly over priced and helped in our menu selections also 
2) our waitress was so cute and attentive eventhough I'm fairly sure she had a few too many tables 
3) the other waitstaff (bread, removal of items, pouring wine) were jovial and seemed to really enjoy their jobs
4) the non-salted butter and sea salt with bread, is a perfect presentation (in my opinion)
5) my sweetbreads appetizer was one of the best preparations of sweetbreads I've had in New Orleans (including R'evolution)

BUT there was one thing that, during the meal I thought not that big of a deal, but essentially ruined everything else in retrospect.

My main dish, the duck confit was meant for horses...in that it tastes like a salt lick!!  I'm not someone to shy away from salt (to my bloated dismay many mornings) but this was inedible.  The beautiful crisp skin was lost completely and even after trying the duck under the skin, I realized that they must have used too much salt in a brine solution because you could not get around it anywhere.

Luckily I was able to snatch a few bites of my date's dish, the short rib.  I was happy to have tasted this dish because eventhough I dispise coconut, it only added a slight sweet flavor to the Moroccan spiced meat.

With so many wonderful restaurants in New Orleans, I doubt I'll be back to Iris...unfortunately.
Food is good, it's more tex mex then mexican for who thinks it's authentic mexican, but would recommend.
First time trying Korean food and the food was awesome.. I wasn't sure if the server was the cook also from how long he took to take our order and pretty much never came back
terrible.  London Grill???  Exactly what does this place do that is british?  Unless British equals lousy service, dirty and a menu which looks like it was created by someone with no sense of taste.

This is one of my favorite places so far. If you're on vacation stop at this restaurant. They have really good reviews. I'll tell you I am so happy we did this. The food is good and people are very nice.. The atmosphere is very cozy and kid friendly.
Great selection of beers from some of the best local breweries, helpful bartenders, and a cool outdoor patio. It's great having this place on the south end of town!

I typically don't write reviews on yelp, usually on OpenTable, but I am compelled to warn potential guest from dining at Giuseppe's on Cowpath Rd.  First the food was good and if we didn't have the issues we had, iwe would probably go back. Service was not great, but it wasn't terrible either, just really slow.  We live fairly far from this restaurant, over 30 miles so this was a first time for us here. A few days later I received a phone call from my bank's fraud department about some recent activity on my card and turns out that someone had my credit card information and just happened to use it across the street at RiteAid in Lansdale for over a $1000 dollars! Conscience? maybe, but I think there is some shading business going on there and wanted to warn anyone to watch out.

It is what it is... and you get what you pay for

At first I thought I was going to hate it. It took us something like 5 minutes to get any service and it's not like it was busy. I counted 8 total staff members walking around, doing a whole lot of nothing. But hey what are you gonna do when they're mostly all college students..

Wednesday Special: 1/2 off burgers -- making most of them $3.50 a piece. Worth it? WORTH IT. I mean burgers is something that's hard to make wrong. And for 3.50, it is well worth it. Toppings of cheddar/bacon are only 99 cents on top, so I'm a fan.

Spanish fries: are spicy, but good. In all honesty it's just frozen fries + canned jalapenos + onions. They're good, but nothing special :p 

Margaritas : something like $8.25 during non happy hour. But they give you the whole shaker, and it poured me a total of 3-4 margaritas, so I can't complain. They're not THAT strong though, really sugary. 

I'd be back, strictly on a Wednesday if I was in the area craving burgers. There are no other reasons to ever go there

Great local spot for Chi-Mek (chicken and beer) and Karaoke. If the staff is extra nice, they will let you order from their sister restaurants next door. The karaoke rooms offer current k-pop and American music.They recently added grapefruit soju to their inventory!

Delicious foods with interesting flavor combinations that will tickle your taste buds! The French dip was very good.  The cheeseburger with their strawberry and thyme cocktail was wonderful. Definitely going to this restaurant again!

Absolutely the best brunch I had. Drinks are amazing and food is delicious. Fair warning no kid friendly music here, but best adult get away/ girls day spot. Energy, vibe, and food are 100% worth the wait and you can get a great drink while you wait. The GM Katie was personable and amazing. Definitely go get Drunk Bitch and have a Thick bitch. You won't be disappointed! Emotions was my favorite drink.

My cuticles were in terrible shape, they brought my skin and nails back to life. Dropped in on a Sunday morning and was warmly welcomed by staff members. Price points are better than a lot of places in town. It's nice to have a good nail spot in the North Valleys. I will be going back, I didn't get the women's name who did my nails, but I would trust the others, they all were very meticulous with the care they took with their customers. Thank you for the great experience!
Amazing pizza and hospitality. Everyone working was happy to be there and cranking out some amazing pizza. I will definitely be back soon!

I'm not going back! The door said they close by 11:30 on a Sunday. We arrived around 9. Our greeting was: you have 5 minutes to order before the kitchen closes... I wanted to get out, but I wanted tacos. We ordered what the server recommended, was good flavor, but it was cold... Like very cold and the server never came back to ask how was it. We got the ticket and left with the promise of never coming back. Idk if rudeness is their theme, like dicks in Vegas, if so, the staff did a terrific job! Just one positive thing, the bar area is nice and pretty.

Went on Saturday before "dinner time" and the buffet was empty. Only 1 other table in the entire restaurant!! All the food was old and had been sitting. All the food was cold. Not many options to pick from either. The lunch food was the same as the dinner food, nothing new came out. Lunch is $8.49, kids are $5 ish and dinner is $14 ish. The food is gross and I will not be back. Workers were nice but that's it.

Needed to find a hotel/motel that accepted dogs quickly around the location I was in at the time. I came across this place and decided to check in because of the cheap rates. Sadly, you get what you pay for. This motel is for if you can not find anything else.

It's "working girls" everywhere, so much that it's a sign or something to tell with room is "working." This motel is full of roaches, one of the rooms they gave me was infested with roaches. The furniture is very old, stink inside, and falls apart with one touch.

The receptionists are rude, forgetful and annoying. One woman receptionist called my room everyday asking when I was paying when I paid for a week already. Another woman receptionist flirted with ny husband everyday since the first day. She hated seeing me around my own husband. The housekeepers are also rude, forgetful and annoying. They take a loooong time just to bring fresh linen and towels.


The structure of the motel is not stable, it's on the verge of collapsing. The pool never look clean to get in. One very small grill on the premises. 

I do not recommended this motel!

This is an Bavarian food, not a German restaurant and it's not very good. I'm German, and I cook waaaay better than this. Bad beer, bad wurst, and barely passable schnitzel. And no rouladen. Also, NO brotchen for the bratwurst! I went here when it first opened, and swore I'd not be back. But some friends wanted to go, so this summer (2020) I went back. Nope, still bad, not sure how they're still open.

This is not fast food. There were five cars ahead of me. 

I was in line for twenty five minutes.  Once you are in line you are stuck. 

Management needs to fix this issue. People sat at the window for over five minutes waiting for their order.

Once I got to the windows, I realized the person giving out food was also filling drinks and doing other tasks. 

Time for management to access this situation.

I ordered and was charged for an item that was never shipped to me.  I emailed the company several times and never heard back.  Do not buy anything from this company!  I also called and left a voicemail.  Please refund my order #116412!

This place used to be AMAZING but the past year, year and a half the food quality and taste has really dropped off. It's a really shame it used to be so awesome l. Seems like once they added the brewery part the food quality really dropped off

I'm a fan of Seoul Garden if you want the cooktop table experience.  I've eaten at most of the Korean spots in town.  Read my previous review from 2014 for my original experience; since then I've been back a handful of times.  I've increased the rating to 4 stars because I think it's more than just okay, it's good, authentic Korean fare.  I just wish the service were a little better, that it cost a little less, and that the portions were bigger.  It's hard to compare it to spots in LA and NY when you can seriously have all you can eat for the same prices you pay here.

I still prefer Korea House and Hai Woon Dai.

I bought two bedroom suites, living room and dining room suites. Had to pay extra for my boxsprings and mattresses. I was happy with the prompt delivery. But they just dropped it and left. Nothing was removed from any of the boxes, it was left for me to do. I had to leave for a few weeks because of death in my family. When I arrived back with my daughter to help me. I sat on the couch and the springs let go on one end. I called and talked to a gentleman who said he was sorry but there was nothing they could do because I waited too long. Yes got it in May but didn't get to use any of it till July. So I now have a couch that I fell is just useless to sit on and I tried to fix but wasn't able to. So I am looking elsewhere for a couch.

I'm by no means a frequenter of Taco Bell, but when I want tasty junk food, I'm not above throwing my digestive inhibitions to the sky and diving in to some crunchy taco supremes.

My fiance and I came through the drive thru around 8 p.m. on a Monday. We were the only car; soon after someone pulled up behind us. We ordered five taco supremes and two chalupas. It took an unusually long time to get our food. I thought 'Sweet, they must be making the stuff fresh if we're waiting this long.' Nope! All three of my tacos were cold with soggy bottoms that fell apart.

It's Taco Bell, so how high are your expectations? But this was a miss.

We arrived at our reservation time, and the hostess was seating us at the upper level. My husband asked if we could sit on the lower level. She said those seats were only for those who requested them... there was no option when making reservations online, and there was only one couple we could see on the lower level. So, here we are, 'requesting' seats.? So she said she would see what she could do. 

We were seated and for the first 20 minutes was slightly nauseous which kind of put a damper on the whole thing.

The menu is small and seemed lackluster. The food was ok, but not really worth the price. A glass of my favorite restaurant staple wine was $4 more expensive than at pricier places I've been. 

We both had 1 drink, a salad, and I had an appetizer and he an entree. When leaving, we said it was like we paid $40 for the food and $80 for the view and neither was really worth it. 

They seem to bank on the idea that you're there for the novelty but the novelty doesn't quite make up for the rest.

I went to Blaze for 7 years and truly loved it, however I absolutely noticed a decline in service when they moved to their space on Church Street. I had trouble booking appointments with my stylist and eventually had to switch to someone else. Although that went well for a little while, over time the attention to detail declined as well. I have bangs which of course requires frequent appointments - my appointment times were rarely honored (sadly, even when a bang trim only takes five minutes), and my bangs were botched more than once. Sometimes my stylist wouldn't even take my ponytail out to comb all the bangs down for a proper cut. I would come home and realize half the bangs didn't even get trimmed. And after my trims, I would leave with pieces of hair all over my face and shirt, looking like a mess. One day I had to ask her for a lint roller, and I actually had to run home on my lunch break one day to change because I was wearing my bangs after my mid day appointment! Last July, the day before I was attending a wedding, she truly botched my bangs - it was like she cut my side bang backwards and then cut the center choppy. It was shorter on the side and longer in the middle of my face! It took nearly five months to grow them out and work up the courage to go elsewhere. 

Speak up when you go to Blaze and ask questions - I regret not confronting my stylist on how terribly she cut my bangs, and I regret not contacting management about it.

I was looking forward to the Big Cat Rescue as a self proclaimed "crazy cat lady". It turned out to be the closest I have ever been to a tiger (along with some other usual cat varieties). 

Our tour guide Nicole was very familiar with the Big Cat Rescue's history and their individual animals. I also liked learning that the volunteers are selected over time to give tours. Her knowledge and expertise showed her dedication to the organization.

I was not impressed with the facility. I had expected the animals to have larger living spaces. The facility was more like a zoo than a sanctuary. The cats seemed lethargic. (It might have been the time of the day.) 

The history of the organization also appears to be in a little hypocritical. The original owner acquired a large breed cat(s) prior to the discovering they don't belong in peoples' homes. She was the same kind of person the organization currently condemns. Overall worth seeing when in the area (but it wasn't a life changing experience).

Joey B's On The Hill, situated in the South Hampton neighborhood of St. Louis City, offers diners a taste of both Italian & American fare, while also catering to the sports-bar crowd - complete with a lengthy bar, multiple HD televisions, and a broad selection of adult beverages.

What's not to love? Quite a bit, in fact.

Let me begin by clarifying that Joey B's is not "bad" by any means. In each of my multiple visits, their food has been both relatively appetizing and consistent. Yet, at this bustling and energetic establishment, there seems to be that genuine lack of authenticity that entitles The Hill as one of the best culinary neighborhoods in the city of St. Louis. In addition, the restaurant regularly fills to capacity (with waiting patrons packed like sardines near the front of the house), making a weeknight dinner a dreaded, angst-filled event. To Joey B's misfortune, there seems to be an overwhelming sense of "franchisement" that weaves its way into both the restaurant's ambiance and its food - with an atmosphere, flavors & portions akin to those American chain restaurants that us foodies have adamantly damned to culinary hell.

While good-intentioned, Joey B's should not be your party's first choice when dining on The Hill. With countless original restaurants and delis spanning in every which direction, Joey B's simply fails to live up to this famous Italian neighborhood's tall & historic standards.

Horrible Staff. I felt I was being herded through like cattle. If you stop and stand anywhere a bouncer will tell you to keep moving. There are these large open spaces on the 1st floor now where seating used to be. Apparently you are not allowed to stand there unless you are grinding on someone. After my brother-in-law and I were yelled at to keep moving when we were in line for the bathroom I had had enough! If you want a nightclub where you are forced to keep wandering around or dancing on the dance floor at risk of being thrown out by a power hungry bouncer then have at it. There are many other clubs I would gladly give my money!

This is definitely one of the better hooter's locations I've been to. Love that it's on the water and the outdoor seating. Our server was Jessica and she was absolutely fantastic. We shared some delicious fried pickles to start. She kept my parents' beers topped off and our waters full. We had original breaded and naked wings which were delicious as well. Overall the food was great and the service was even better!

Great New Orleans tradition.  Don't miss it.  Oysters on the half-shell are oysters on the half-shell.  Grilled oysters are much better at Drago's and a little better at Acme on Veterans Blvd. in Metairie.

Food was very delicious! I had the chicken tinga tacos, and their cochata, coffee and orchata, I was very curious, interesting flavor, something I wanted to try definitely not my favorite, nothing like an original orchata and tacos, the place is very neat but when you're making the line is hard to order because you have people waiting behind you seeing the menu last minute doesn't really help, it'll be nice to have the menus at the end of the where the line starts, thank you seis kitchen for your interesting flavors!

All - I am never one to involve myself in drama but I am compelled to speak up about the injustice that I've received from School of Dance Arts, located at 47 E. High Street, Philadelphia, Pa 19144:
1. Upon enrollment, the director and her business partner (or assistant) daughter strived to force parents to provide their social security numbers so that they can count it against your credit report if you try to leave the school before the end of the school year. Families are given the initial 2 classes to decide if they want to stay and then are responsible to pay for the entire school year thereafter. There is no leaving WITHOUT paying for the entire school year if you happen to feel disrespected by the rude manner that the director speaks to many of the parents or the unacceptable switch in teachers that occurred in my child's class. Thankfully, I always paid my bill on time and in full and never had an issue, however this is a predatory business practice.
2. The director's non-degreed/ non- credentialed daughter served as the dance teacher of my child's 5 yo ballet and tap class. After 5 months of teaching, she disappeared from teaching the class and the director replaced her with a 15 yo child as the teacher for the remaining 4 months of the school year but continued to charge us parents full tuition of $65/month for one class per week.
3. I have always spoken respectfully to the director and her daughter despite the director's rudeness but when they learned of my low-rated FB review I posted, they banned me from bringing my 5 yo to receive her trophy from the school after all her hard work in preparing for the recital and her performance in the recital on Sunday, June 4, 2017. I have spent a total of $1,045 at this dance school and am being dismissed unceremoniously simply for posting a review that they don't like. I'm warning everyone I can to please think twice about enrolling at this school. I wish someone had warned me.

I spent:
$65 per month
$85 costume fee
$75 dance photo package
$300 for performance package (parents were muscled into buying large blocks of seats for the recital to get a cheaper price per ticket of $25 per ticket if you bough 10 tickets as opposed to $45 per ticket if you bought less than 5 tickets.) Package also included a full page black and white ad in the recital program, bouquet of flowers and a DVD of the recital.
If you can get past some old wallpaper and furniture, this is a great clean hotel, a block from bourbon street. Beds we're comfortable, and staff gave us a fridge when we asked for one.
Sat for over an hour no food, no manager, wrong items on the menus, skunked tap, got up and left

Amazing!!!  The food is complex and delicious, really well-prepared. Service was friendly and efficient. Decor simple and bold, pictures of Angela Davis, Einstein, Lincoln, the guy in front of the tank at Tiananmen Square. And that was lunch! The dinner menu looks even better.
I had the shrimp in garlic sauce with white rice and it was disgusting. My boyfriend tried the general toast with shrimp fried rice and he did not like his food either.
Amazing! Very friendly and great food! Joe helped us and was fantastic. Definitely a go to place :)

First time I had these cookies, they were DELICIOUS!!! Tried too many to even list: chocolate chip, sugar cookie, white chocolate macadamia, peanut butter, strawberry cheesecake, and a few others. So many flavors. They arrived warm, fresh, and amazingly tasty. Didn't even know nola had a Cookie place; SO impressed with the quality!!!! I will be ordering again

Loved their customer service. And their service was fast.  Food was delicious. I had the Gion Udon Soup (veggie) and it it the spot.  And the Imperial roll was even more delicious! If you're in the America Airlines terminal make sure to stop by.

This place is the best. I love the vibe--laid-back, welcoming, smart, hip (but not in an annoying way). The beer selection is fantastic and always changing. These guys know what they're doing, and they don't take themselves too seriously (read the beer descriptions). The beer is fresh and always refreshing AF. Love this place.

so amazing!  the gruyere sammy is beyond words, i wish i ordered two!  the salad that accompanies it is so good!  we also shared a cheese platter with 5 cheeses and were so pleased and shocked at the generous portions of cheese they gave!  would absolutely go here again next time we are in nola!
A place that has an all you can eat wings item cannot do anything worse than 3 stars even if the wings are crappy... which they are not here they are 4 star wings... everything else is crappy though.

We really enjoyed the French decor and atmosphere. Great place for date night. Excellent bread to start any meal. We had the escargot for our appetizer and it was excellent. My wife had the scallops and I had the white sausage special. They were both good. Excellent coffee and port to finish our meal. Over all one of our favorite places to dine in the central west end area.
Love the chicken here!! Super crispy and tender and the sauce is awesome! Love their honey garlic and soy garlic and their spicy is not too spicy which makes it pleasant to eat!

Was here for $2 Tuesday and holy moly was it busy! We got there at 5:30 and it was already a 20 minute wait. $2 domestics, sangria and tacos. The tacos are mini versions of their bowls on tortillas and they're great! Each one is so unique it is culinary gold. Each $2 taco is a trip to another country. Would highly recommend it on $2 Tuesday. Have been here on a "regular" night and though the food is good, you have to commit to one type. So I'd recommend anyone go on Tuesday and figure out which style that you prefer then go back when you know the one for you.

Nice place to visit with friends for halloween.. 4 attractions for $25.. if you want to do both edgewood and trepidation please buy the combo tickets online first only.. if you want to buy edgewood tickets on the spot that is another $20 more.. the folks were nice and I dont get scared of such haunted homes but my friends and parents enjoyed getting scared and screaming their lungs out.. by the end of it all we had a few of them with a sore throat cos of all the screaming and shouting and yelling inside.. would recommend for a nice tome with friends..

We had a bad experience at their old location right before it closed down and they moved to Swansea. To their credit, the owner reached out to us and offered a meal on the house at the new location. It was meh. The breakfast skillet had zero flavor. We gave it a try a few weeks later and still meh. Service was slow the second time as well. You can get way better breakfast for the same or lower prices at 3 other nearby restaurants.

I was already in the pedicure chair when I checked in and read the reviews! Of course that freaked me out a bit. The place is clean and fancy. No one is friendly but that is ok with me. The pedicure looks great and I'm happy with it.

Dirty, disorganized, poor service, did I say dirty? Felt like we were at a homeless soup kitchen. The space sucks, the help was over worked and inattentive, the food took forever, and the experience was a disaster. I'm usually on the side of the restaurant because people are too quick to the trigger to become a food critic. But..... with all that Olive Street has to offer in terms of Asian ethnic cuisine, Wei Hong falls off the charts. The bathrooms were filthy, tables and chairs stacked in corners, carpets stained, .....just bizarre. Would never go back.

I liked the sushi- but be warned if you are gluten free and trying to get hibachi! what stinks is the sauce and soy sauce are both filled with gluten so they prepared my dish separately in the kitchen (which was really great and nice). so the rice was needless to say lacking flavor. the salmon they cooked with it was excellent though! i went with my family as it was my dad's birthday request and our bill came out to well over $100. the chef doing hibachi was really fun though and made for a good family atmosphere.

Food wasn't bad but not overly impressive. Small menu. Service was really good and nice clean atmosphere. Wouldn't eat here again unless u are staying at the hotel but if u are staying here it's worth a meal or two just for convenience.

I was so happy to find a dress to wear to a wedding this weekend. I went everywhere from Nordie's to Macy. I did not even think this place until I walk in I found all types of selection and great service. I loved the dress I pick out and it looked great, not so easy for a curvy gal like me. I will most definitely shop here again.

Before moving to Tampa this was on Diners, Drive-ins and Dives so we wanted to try. Now that we reside in Seminole Heights we're here once a week. Favorites are the barbacoa beef tostadas, beef tongue tacos and fish tacos. Being from SoCal, this is the best it gets here for Mexican and that's not saying much. 

Best to get the food to go as it's not that clean of a spot.

These guys are awesome! We were in from out of town and forgot the key to our bike rack. They were super friendly, helpful, and very reasonably priced. They had us back on the road within ten minutes! Thanks guys!

Stopped at Franklin Fountain on a Thursday night with my roommate. I ended up getting a small mint ice cream on a waffle cone with sprinkles. It was AMAZING. The waffle cone is $2 extra but WORTH IT. It had a vanilla taste to it that I loved. Not usually a fan of waffle cones but this was an exception. I'm so glad I got it. The ice cream was super creamy and delicious. Would give 5 stars but it was $8 for an ice cream cone. I'd probably go back but not often for that price #collegestudentprobs
I like the food, I had a diker and it was cooked well. Service was good as well. Will be back in the future.

Excellence Happy Hour with some neighbors.  Very tasty and price Is right! Will try dinner next time.  Seated at the patio for some light.  Michelle our server was very attentive...my first time here so very impressed and parking is validated so I got it for free.

Don't break your apple watch. 
For some reason they can't swap them out here like every other apple care product. they have to send them off to a depot for someone to make sure it's really broken. The screen that popped out isn't indicative of a break. So the most personal device they have ever created, that they want you to wear consistently you have to wait a week to get it if you break something.
Love this place. Many GF options and BEST coffee! Also a great place to bring the kiddos as they have a kids corner where they can play while dining. We visit a lot.

Don't know why this place is still liked. I used to love their paella. I recently went again. The paella sucked. Not flavorful. And rice was undercooked. For the price I expected something that would blow other places with paella out of the water! Like before when I used to come.The service was incredibly horrible. On the good note the bread pudding was delish . BUT I am hopping it was just a bad night

my experience dus far from Martinez to Reno was awesome! now my return is less desireable only because of the attendent behind the counter..#1 He was not busy, so when I arrived a 1/2 hour early he said window closed. will not check my bags as I go to leave he then decideds to assist a lady to check her bags and a stroller..as I stated no one in line he was not busy and to dismiss me was highly unprofessional,so Amtrak if you hire people to be the face of a company please let them know about the first impressions! can impact a business

This is the first time visiting this place and my first impression is that this is an indoor flea market with many stalls selling goods and food. I only saw two stalls selling fruits and vegetables.  There was also several Amish stalls selling food. Overall, it was an interesting visit. Open only on Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays.

I have only been here to drink so I don't know anything about the restaurant part.  I was here on an idle sunday funday afternoon. My group of 3 friends and myself were the only few people at the bar.  We all enjoyed the absinth "experience."  The bartender was very knowledgeable and friendly.  He knew everything there was to know about cocktails, alcohols and their history.  It was a great time!

Literally just got home from there (was in a hurry to take my Bf to work) anyways I ordered a cheesy gordita crunch with a nacho shell, but it came with the bland one. Also ordered a Doritos locos taco and received a Fritos burrito. And the beefy 5 layer had old beans(I say old because it wasn't hot) it was kinda okay but I'm just disappointed

First off... Walked into this place and plenty of spots at the bar were vacated but not cleaned up ...food baskets and tabs littering the bar. When we finally got a place at the bar to sit it took quite a while to be waited on. I had to ask for the bar to be wiped up. 
We ordered burgers. I had ordered a burger with the substitute chicken option... Huge mistake!  I expect the chicken to be grilled as you are substituting it for a burger patty.  Instead, what I got was fried chicken fingers on a bun.  My sandwich was a 12 year old's dream dinner. Heart burn city!  The actual beef patty burger was okay... Nothing to get too excited over.  It all felt like I was eating fast food though. So, food was below average to terrible depending on what you order. 
When we closed the tab they automatically charged it to my credit card. I don't understand why you need to take and keep my credit card to begin with when I'm eating food at the bar... Where am I going to run off to?  Anyhow I had to have that fixed so that I could pay in cash. 
This place is brick and cement all around with an unfinished ceiling... So in other words it's loud as hell even with only a hand full of people in there... Not to mention the terrible selection of music blasting.  I'll never get why bars in Nashville have to blare recorded music so loudly that you can't even talk to the person next to you. 
Cool looking place if they could just improve the food and service. Oh and no smoking inside which is good considering almost everywhere else in the area has smoking inside (it's like walking into 1980 all over again).
Nice place with some pretty good grub--great service. The coconut mojito and cucumber mojito were delicious. This place is definitely a three and a half. Please, Yelp, add half stars!

Food was spectacular so fresh made to order like 10 different soups to choose from it was amazing cost wasn't their daily special was wonderful the people were so nice to give you samples of the soup I will be going back
Today I had beef stroganoff soup so yummy they're only open from 11 to 2 weekdays
I can't wait to go back

I have eaten a lot of mediterranean food in my life and coming from the bay area I have had quite a selection and naan n kabob definitely is on my top 3...when it comes to taste and portion size I was very pleased...this is man food...meat rice and veggies...I shared the family plate with one other person and I am a huge eater and I still couldn't finish it...I was very pleased to find this gem in reno!! The service was great I always love when the chef personally comes to each table and asks how the meal was and offers discounts for future visits :-)

I have gone to Soho more times than I can count and I don't plan to stop!
Their sushi is fresh, tasty and generally you get a good plate presentation. 
The servers have always been wonderful, even if someone is relatively new or in shift change, they always make sure you have what you need. 
The price's are great for the spot on quality received!

I was pleasantly surprised how easy the whole process was, especially considering that we came in on a whim with some time to kill. Darrien helped us narrow down what we were looking for, and he did a good job of giving us enough info & choices without being too pushy. I was shocked that when we made our purchase on Saturday afternoon they got it delivered on Monday. We've had our new bed a week now and are quite happy with it!

This is a typical diner type restaurant which has multiple locations.   The breakfast menu is extensive.   This Sunday, service was not very good.   My coffee cup had chips on both sides by the rim, but couldn't catch our server  to get a new one.   I ordered a Philly cheesesteak omelet with home fries and a biscuit.   The order came out fairly quickly, but I was disappointed.   The home fries were nice and hot and tasty.   The omelet was extremely dry as it was over cooked.   The biscuit was flat as a pancake, not fluffy like biscuits should be.

We really enjoy the food here.  The pizza, wings, and grinders are all really good.  They also have some great salads.  All of the servers are friendly and the service is consistently good.  We are there almost every Monday night for the 50 cent wings.  They also have songwriters nights as well as karaoke for those that can sing.  The place is always clean and our food always arrives hot.

You guys are fantastic! I would love to get a job here! Fantastic environment to work in. I even joined in welcoming ppl to zoup!! The tastes of the soup helped a lot when decided what I wanted! Love you guys!! Thanks for the Zoup!

This was my first visit.  I had heard how great the food was and I wanted to try something new for my birthday.  We ordered lettuce wraps and pan fried wontons as appetizers and they were very good but before we could finish enjoying those our entrees were already out. It felt very rushed. Unfortunately my entree had no flavor.  I had my husband try it and he said the same. His dish on the other hand was very good. We did have dessert and it was very good as well but we had to ask for dessert. It was not offered. Don't know that I would go again.

One thing that really bothers me is when I walk into an empty restaurant on a Sunday with my 2 year old and get placed in the BACK of the restaurant where no one else is seated. If you can guess, that's what happened when my daughter and I went there last week. 

Since we were in a section of the restaurant that was completely barren, the service was just terrible. 

But the food was decent and my strawberry margarita was fantastic.

This place is great! I went here with a group of my friends for my best friend's birthday. After telling the restaurant before hand that it was her birthday they had a card waiting for her. 

We all ordered bottomless brunch and the drinks came quickly and weren't watered down. Our waiter was Casey, he was very attentive and never made us feel rushed to leave. The food was delicious and the portions were huge. We will most definitely be returning!!

What's up with the crazy pushy Asian servers at the restaurants in Philly?   (I know, I know- this is an Italian restaurant- I know what I am reviewing- You can see my recent sushi review for the other annoying service).

I wanted to have a moment to breathe without the server coming back to our table every five seconds.  You would have thought she would have heard most of our conversation which centered around my friend's father's recent death.  My friend would be telling me about a particularly poignant part of saying goodbye to her father and the server would be there asking if she could clear some half eaten items.  Attentive service is great, but pushy is NOT good.

The food was very good- too hot to eat anything heavy- we stayed with salads and appetizers.  Loved the concept.

I will return and try to avoid getting that particular server.

Ordered the Spicey Ramen for pickup. FYI get your pickup in back.  I couldn't see the big red sign from the front. As for the ramen the favor was delicious and a spicy as I was thinking it would be prior to read a review.  It WAS spicy in my opinion. My mouth and lips were tingling before I even finished.  The separate (cold) non liquid food came in a  separate bowl to mix together at home. So even though the liquid came hot, when mixed together it was damn cold. Since I'm in a hotel with out a microwave I sat there and ate cold ass $20 ramen. Thankfully,  it was at least  tasty cold ass soup.

If I could give this hotel more stars I would. Lovely experience, lovely staff. We were only in Nola for less than 48 hours and this hotel made our vacation. No matter what time of day we always interacted with polite, kind, professional and funny staff members. We checked in late and left late and the entire experience was wonderful and relaxing. Rooms are simple, yet gorgeous. Bar and restaurant are lovely. Centrally located and beautifully designed. Less than a 15 minute walk from the French quarter, but also plenty of things to do and eat in the area. Will definitely be staying here again.

Excellent spot for lunch. Meatballs were fantastic and the roasted potatoes were an excellent suggestion from our server. The blackened chicken salad is also a refreshing lunch option if you don't want something as heavy as meatballs can be.

Fantastic ribs! Serious porkification.  Tender, peppery ribs.  Sausage was solid. Only had the 2 items with sides. Solid coleslaw and baked beans. $20 real good price.  Felt like I was in TX. Wish I had room for brisket....judging off ribs and dry rub,  I'd guess brisket would be solid. Good service. Nothing fancy, just very good  BBQ

Our experience with Ashley Furniture was a nightmare! Our salesman was Douglas Diedrich and probably couldn't spell customer service.  I have called him twice, sent 3 emails and even went to the store wherein he promised a phone call within a week....that was a month and 1/2 ago. We purchased over 4k worth of furniture and the deliveries continued to get pushed out week after week. I get that it is not Doug's fault however, he promised us that they did not do business that way. That if it took longer than quoted he/they would step in. He sat on his hands, did nothing and could not even reply to an email. I still have not received a call from Ashley Furniture- I have always had to call them to check the status and the hold time is anywhere from 15-30 minutes. Our chairs were set to be delivered in 2029-but still no follow up from the store. By looking at the recent reviews, it's a miracle they are still in business. Again Doug- a reply email would have sufficed. Treating your customers like you actually care about the situation would go a long way-Doug.

This is by far the best Vietnamese food I've ever had. Between 4 adults and 3 toddlers, we had the curry chicken, fried rice, summer rolls, BBQ sandwich, beef noodle soup, chicken noodle soup and fresh bread rolls. The food was phenomenal! The curry sauce was so good I poured it over the fried rice. I also enjoyed a Mango Boba slushie after all that.

We LOVE Urban Comfort. So when we found Urban Deli by complete mistake yesterday we were thrilled. We were wrong. Let me give you the good first.

The server. Our server was the nicest, sweetest, coolest, cutest chick EVER! Her and my guy are both from NC., she was super friendly, and she really sold the place well. 

The other good, the homemade ice cream sandwich. But you only get half a sandwich. So we each had a quarter sandwich - or what we figured out was 1 cookie cut in half, ice cream in the middle. Cut in two quarter pieces. It was amazing.

The bread. No review needed. Bread is amazing. Plain and simple.

The bad: we spent like $120 on food to take home. We got 1/2 pound of bacon, pork, pastrami and roast beef.

The bacon is good, would get it again. Pork too. But the pastrami and roast beef were awful. Pastrami should have fat. It's a basic fundamental. And it should taste like pastrami. Not smoke and pepper. The roast beef the same. They were actually so much the same, that when I went to make French dip sandwiches tonight, we couldn't determine which was which, aside from the fact that pastrami should be cut smaller then roast beef. I made a wonderful meal and it all tasted of pepper and liquid smoke from the meat. Bleh.

Then they had a wonderful beer deal. $8 six packs of craft beer. They failed to tell us it was likely because one of their coolers went down and the beer was skunked. Which, it's hard to skunk craft beer. But they managed.

Their provisions made in house are good, the pickles appear to be made elsewhere, and they suck. Buy a jar and it says only good for two days, but they were mush just 16 hours after purchase. The pickle juice was amazing.

Long story, long, I will be back at Urban comfort. Might even be back to urban deli for the server because she rocked. Sad my bank account is over a hundred dollars lighter then before I walked in. None of it was worth writing home about. And we are craft beer enthusiasts (my guy brews craft beer for a living) and love spending money on good food and I wouldn't do it again there.

Oldish feel to the hotel and it's more in the commecial area of downtown, rather than being smack in the middle of downtown.  But I'd say it's one of the better midscale hotels in the area.

Fitness room is a really good size with plenty of equipment.  Good for people on business.  Free airport shuttle too.  But other than that, nothing stood out.

Well this is my first none yelp visit and unfortunately it was just ok. There were some highlights but a few missteps. .

First thing the service from Adam was amazing . He was very attentative and on top of things. He kept our drinks full and checked on us multiple times  to be sure everything was ok. 


We started off with the boudin balls which I wasn't a big fan of and the corn bread that was amazing. I think the corn bread may have been one of the best things I've eaten.  Next were our entrees, shrimp and grits and chicken and waffles.  The chicken and waffles weren't very good, the chicken and the waffle was very over cooked and tough. As for the shrimp and grits, the grits were creamy and the shrimp had really good flavor. 

I will definitely be back and update my review, they may have just had an off day with some of the food.
My wife and I love Pepperfire! Quality food, great employees and great spot!! Perez-order and you're in and out!
Place is incredible! The best local Italian specialty shop. Definitely worth going to. Well priced, super friendly and delicious food.

WOW.  I don't have to drive to Oro Valley for chiropractic care anymore.  I'm so relieved to have found such a wonderful chiropractor, quick, painless and competent.  You don't find that in Tucson with any regularity.  THANKS Dr Tory.

I am in Tampa quite a bit and I tend to stay away from Starbucks just because it can be insane, however, I was one of the designated coffee-getters for an event and Starbucks seemed the most classic. We came in with a 21-drink order and the baristas handled it like champs. They messed up on a few things but made up for it with amazing attitudes in the face of our Starbucks swimming pool challenge. The atmosphere is typical earthy tones and people sitting on their laptops. Comfy... if you have a Mac. (;
Stand in line for 30 mins while the manager gets his head out of his ass, just to told only room for leaguers. Don't waste your time, you probably won't get a lane. This place really sucks!

Ehhh sorry I will have to side with the negative reviews here on this establishment. I was starving and wanted to love this for sure. I will highlight some good and bad. 
The restaurant is nothing fancy but nothing too bad either. The service was nothing to talk about but I mean I got the buffet so I wouldn't be too critical on that. 
Let's get to the food (why we go right?) I too, experienced old food an hour before the buffet was ending with empty/nearly empty dishes never being refilled. The tandoori chicken was good though for it not being fresh. They offer naan bread when you walk in. Garlic and naan. The garlic was fantastic However I feel it overpowered some of the food I ate it with. The chicken tikka was average, not bad but not the best I've ever had. It's creamy with not too much in flavors. I wasn't a fan of the rice pudding and disappointed they didn't have the gulab that every traditional buffet I've been to has had. 
Also, the most expensive Indian buffet I've been to. $11? I'd have to pass on returning

Definitely one of the biggest museums I've ever been to!  Pretty much a must-see for anybody that's into airplanes.  There are hundreds of aircraft here... definitely a little bit of everything!  The collections of passenger planes, cargo planes, and bombers were particularly strong.  Particularly of note, you can see an actual SR-71 here! 

Since there are so many aircraft, and so many of them are so ginormous, the museum is actually made up of several different hangars and a *huge* dirt lot filled with airplanes and helicopters.  The aircraft inside the hangars have been done up pretty well, but the ones left outside are not given their due by the museum.  For the hundreds of aircraft outside of the hangars, there's no map or cheat-sheet that tells you about an airplane's history or specifications, and almost all of them could use some more TLC (otherwise this place could be 5-stars!).  There is a tram that will quickly loop around the huge dirt lot so you can see everything without the death march through the Arizona desert, but if you do decide to hike out into the heat, you can get up close and personal, walking all around, in between, underneath the various planes.

Definitely a fun experience, and probably one of the best things to see in Tucson!

TIPS: Do not visit here during warm weather!!  Plan to do a lot of walking, and a good part of it outside.  Make sure to bring sunglasses!

My first trip and only got the chicken soup.  It was not really hot but hot enough to enjoy. You do get a lot for the money good value on this. I enjoyed it but with all soup would really like to know the sodium content. Alot less it seems than the commerical soup out there but still would like to know.  I would get this again will go again. I will try the chicken. It was a nice place seemed clean. I will agree service was fast and friendly. Will up date when I have the chicken.

Pizza slices are good, very filling.  We have been visiting here for over a year, and my husband really likes their "stuffed pizza" product.  We were disappointed recently when they stopped giving free refills for fountain soft drinks ... who doesn't do that these days?  They have since changed their stance and we are back visiting them with frequent regularity. 

You must try their vegetarian (not vegan) pizzas -- lots of veggies on it and very filling.  Great place to grab a slice before/after seeing a movie in the theater right next door.

Zhou's is conveniently located near my house so we usually do take-out with a snack of egg rolls which are nice and big. My favorite is the chow mein it has a white sauce on it not like other places that are just fried. The food is pretty basic but I'm never disappointed. The servers are nice. The place is clean, I've never seen anyone in the restaurant, I think most people do take out.

Worst experience with this theater everrrr. Me and my
grandma have been coming to this theater for a long time and since they been under new management, it has turned into a shit storm. Also, my boyfriend left his wallet there on accident a month ago. Called the SAME night and said they had it. They said they would hold it until it was picked up and didn't have it there when i went to pick it up (we live 2 hours away) Also when he called and asked if there was cash (supposed to be $400) they said there was cash in it when it was found but it was no longer there. This company has turned into nothing but crooks and unprofessional employees. Stop hiring ignorant high schoolers.

Horrible experience last night.  Waited longer than estimated which wasn't a big deal until we realized it was because of another couples resistance to sit at another table suitable for a couple.  The waitress was horrible as well. We waited 30-45 min for her to come back so we could ask her if she could split our check.  The only nice thing I have to say is the margarita was strong and numbed some of the pain.

Really great French onion soup, really good prime rib French dip, really crappy service, under cooked fish, and long wait times, on a Tuesday night!
Had to flag our server down, every time, and then wait at length for responses. 
When fish was extremely undercooked server took to manger who then offered to re-cook even though at that point we didn't even want it, waited 45 minutes again, for the next plate, didn't even bother to add discount or adjust bill. Heck the manager didn't even come out at all. Totally not worth it, and FYI much older crowd this restaurant is definitely aging, no wonder no one I know comes here any more. Sad.

Love love love. Not a big fan of country in general, but this place is fun and set up very well. Enjoyable atmosphere, freindly, staff and good music. I've had a great time everytime I've been here. Definitely recommend to Nashville natives, transplants, and visitors.

My experience here was just okay. It was nothing spectacular and I have to say I was really disappointed in the way Shelby handled my service. At FIRST, everything was amazing. Great conversations and she really knows what she's doing with the shears. I got there 4 minutes late & for some reason at the end of my haircut I wasn't able to be blow dried. I tipped her $10 bucks and was really taken aback by the lack of customer service in that sense. I had to sit under the hooded drier for a few minutes so I at least didn't leave soaking wet. I graduated from Paul Mitchell & the one thing that was taught & apart of our culture was to never let the guest leave without styling their hair. (Whether that be natural with the blow drier @ no extra cost, or iron works if they consulted that with you beforehand) she told me she didn't have time and it was extremely disappointing to have drove all that way and take a day for myself just to leave with damp untamed  hair, I was unable to see if I loved the cut or not all while having paid her already. Like I said it wasn't terrible but unprofessional to say the least. I'd never do that to any of my clients! I don't think I'll be returning, and I wanted so badly to love this place.

Previous to my visit I read all the glowing reviews and was excited to try out Lilibeth to make my hair lighter. I used the Axxess card in order to get 50% off my first salon service. I made sure to show her a couple pictures of what I liked and wanted, also informing her that I was somewhat new to this process. She took one glance and quoted above average pricing in my limited experience, but I was willing to try with the Axxess card. Without really making sure we were clear on our common goal, she began working diligently with the foils. It was a long process and the end result looked NOTHING like the photos I showed her! On top of that, I borderline felt like she was about to tell me I was done without drying or styling my hair. I felt like I had to ask for her to blow dry it. When I said I didn't like how dark the lowlights were, she insisted they were like the photo I showed her. I disagreed (because I looked barely even dirty blonde at this point) and finally she agreed to see me another time to fix it at no charge (due to another client waiting). I receive a text day of the new appointment that it needed to be changed to a different day. Fine, I can do that. However what I found unprofessional is that she changed pricing on me to fix the color because I "changed my mind". I refused the extra charge as I had tipped her generously for the first visit on good faith she would hold her word on the no charge second visit. This even though I didn't like it. She finally reverted to the original agreement. At that point I decided to avoid the risk and take my business elsewhere to a salon that charged the same amount (for an amazing/time consuming color correction, haircut, AND tip) that Lilibeth did for her chunky highlights and lowlights at "half price". I added a second star due to the fact that she eventually said no charge to fix her own mistake, though she was not willing to admit it didn't look like the picture. Wish I could get part, if not a full refund for the unsatisfactory services provided. Maybe it was just me, but make sure you're very clear on what exactly you'd like before she begins.

Great food and waitresses but why are the bartenders always so miserable?! They don't even crack a smile or ask how you're doing when you sit down. My glass stays empty unless I ask for another by getting their attention. I don't know, this used to be one of my favorite places. What's happened?!

The difference in one week was tremendous. The service/food last week was great. Tonight, terrible. My FO soup was made wrong; had to ask for regular butter 2x and even told her before not to bring out the regular butter. The coffee was basically luke warm water and I asked for the pie heated up and it was served cold. I won't be going back.

First time here, passing through on the way to Winterthur. Lunchtime, New Year's Eve, steady flow of customers, but not overwhelming amount of people. Seated quickly, server was pleasant. The specials board out front mentioned choice of soup or salad and half sandwich. It was also listed on the insert in the menu. The half sandwich was a grilled cheese with bacon which sounded wonderful for a cold day. Ordered with the half salad. After placing my order, the waitress came back and said it comes with soup also. I was excited but I made sure to ask if that was for real since the sign and menu both said otherwise. My food came out, no sandwich in sight. I was told that it was soup or salad or sandwich. The waitress then proceeded to say that the hostess must have written it wrong on the board. I then watched two servers go outside to reread the menu board. The waitress came back and said she spoke with the manager and it would be "addressed". No one changed the board and I didn't hear the whirl of a printer whipping up new inserts for the menu. It all sounded a little odd but it definitely seems like our waitress doesn't familiarize herself with the daily specials. My advice: speak slowly when ordering or even a illustration on the napkin may ensure your order is correct.

This place has the best atmosphere, the classic arcade games, great drinks, even possible for gluten free people to eat and drink. Staff is friendly, and there is a spot to put you drink next to every game! Love this place!

My Review : 

Upon entrance to Somerset, one is taken back .... 

"ah hem" ; 

as to where am I ? A contemporary wonderland ? Inception level 2 aesthetics ? oh do proceed ....

Perusing the drink menu leaves one in a state of tickle ; as amusing & unusual concoctions dot the pages ....

Shall we acquiesce to a "duck fat-washed bourbon" or dabble in "apple 3 ways" ? yes & well, yes .... 

Matrix-esque servers fill water @ sublime levels, while tending to frothy libations, which ultimately leads you to the question : "Will I Return ?"

Some say it is so ......

I LOVE everything about this place. The staff is so friendly and ensure that my pizza is taken care of since it needs to be both gluten and dairy free. The food is so delicious. I usually take it home to eat but there has been a time or two where I've just sat and ate alone in the dining room because I liked the vibe. All of the workers seem to be happy working there and I hope that's true because they are so nice!
Best Italian food I have ever ate. The chief and server was the same person. He was amazing. Would recommend this place to all my family and friends

Have gotten delivery from here many times. We like to get the lamb samosas, chicken tikka masala, chicken vindaloo or chicken curry. All white meat and spice levels can be adjusted-we like it pretty spicy. The onion and spinach naan are really tasty and arrive warm and fresh. Price points are reasonable. My only criticism would be I am not a fan of the biryani.  Definitely give the a shot. I believe the do a buffet at lunch.

Honestly, where so I begin? When I walked in I was greeted by a man that made us pay then led us to a table. On our way to the table I already noticed food, puddles, and utensils on the floor. Long story short the place was a mess. I'm fine with a small mess if it is very busy but there were probably 15 families there when I was, making me think that hey don't maintain the place very well. 

Food was extremely cold and not just the sushi, and if we are talking about sushi the rice was most definitely old because of how stale and hard it was.

The ice cream bar had one ice cream scoop in this hole in the counter top with water in it. The water wasn't constantly running so the water was mucky making me not want to eat ice cream. Instead I settled for soup which was a huge mistake cause it just tastes like salt water.

Since I was still hungry I decided I would eat some fruit to get the bang for my buck. One word; unripe. Every single fruit at the fruit bar far unripe, not to mention the fruit was next to cocktail shrimp and raw clams and oysters, which I find pretty weird.

Finally as I'm ready to go (no full by the way) I see a guy behind a wall, where most of the workers were when they had nothing to do, in a biohazard mask. I don't know about you, but I don't want anyone in a restaurant wearing a biohazard mask.

Loved the relaxed vibe, tasty, upscale yet affordable
2.14.14

Zesty. Crunchy. Tasty.
Caesar salad with warm Seared chicken breast (Romain lettuce, warm sliced marinated chicken breast, garlic croutons, Parmesan Chris, Caesar dressing $10.75) my 13-year-old son nearly licked his bowl

Chicken & waffles
Comfort food with a flavor infused flare
(Bacon cornmeal waffle, crisp battered chicken, time infused maple syrup, with butter, $13.25) my wife thoroughly enjoyed this and took home half for lunch tomorrow


Horseradish crusted salmon: 
Zip. Crunch. Flare.
(fresh grated horseradish and panko breadcrumb crusted salmon, sautéed spinach, roasted garlic smashed potatoes, red wine natural jus $17.95) winner!

Although the menu is thin, I think that contributes to quality entrées

I  had been a loyal customer for years up until today. My husband I ordered pulled pork sandwiches as we have many times before and for some reason the bbq was SUPER sweet. It tasted  like someone had dumped a five pou bag of sugar in the sauce and the meat was clearly a cheaper cut, dry and string. We couldn't eat our food and threw it in the trash. Seriously, is it worth losing your loyal customer base by serving up unhealthy garbage?

Visiting from Monterey Peninsula in California on business, and was looking for good food/wine for a solo meal on a Monday night. Kinda tall order. Annata didn't disappoint. Ate at the bar. Bartender Aaron was fabulous and made excellent suggestions. Enjoyed meeting locals and discussing St. Pete. Had beet/farro salad and tuna tartare. Both excellent. Sampled 4 wines per Aaron's suggestion and enjoyed all. Might go back tomorrow. Recommend!

I live in Midtown Village, so I'm no tourist, but my family forced us to go. $19pp (now $14, which is a little better than when I came) is ridiculous. You have to be high if you think that's okay, like more than 57 stories high! Even the things in the gift shop (again, I was forced) were way overpriced ($25 for small "Bear Franklin," $40 for a big eagle, etc). Don't waste your time unless you wanna go at sunset.

Ok I'm going to say it and I always stress I do not play favorites or know anything about this place other then I found it on google and will drive 30 minutes out of the way to pick up bagels here. This isn't your typical flat south jersey bagel that's either to crispy or to soft. This reminded me of being back in system island on Bay Street snapping into a crispy bagel with those beautiful little air bubbles from the oven and then hitting a warm and soft core. Their cream cheeses were so fresh and didn't taste like a glob of whipped butter and seasonings. I got an everything bagel with veggie cream cheese, and a onion bagel toasted with garlic herb cream cheese (so subtle but so perfect.) I'm a north jersey guy so I suggest asking for extra cream cheese but honestly these were the best bagels I have had since moving to south jersey. Family run buisness in the  Haddon shops next to hair cuttery. This literally was such a good start to a Sunday afternoon. Highly suggest this!!!

Nicki's is hit or miss. I came in at lunch one day and was looking dead in the eyes of the regulars. They look at young people like you're the plague. I loved the food, though it wasn't exactly in overabundance. The price was reasonable.

I went the second time for breakfast with some family who came to visit. I got a 10 dollar breakfast and it had so much food my stomach was about to explode. I drank probably 5 cups of coffee and it was delightful. The only problem was the number of people. Good lord. If you go in the morning, get ready to club someone over the head for a table.

Overall, good food, good service and at a healthy price.

this was like moving back in time, into the 1970's or something.  but, it's a family type establishment, so i sort of get it.

people raved about the fried ravioli, which didn't really do it for me.  i dunno, they seemed sort of hard and dried out.  i didn't think they were that exciting and wouldn't get them again.  

i will see that my seafood linguini was packed with fresh seafood.  mongo scallops, and several shrimp.  the clams were all chopped up in the sauce and was a little hard to distinguish.  the dish was huge and wasn't bad.  the portions were so large that i couldn't take it all out.  (maybe i should have skipped the ravioli.)  not outstanding flavors, but okay.

staff was friendly and efficient.   

really, this is somewhere around 2 1/2 stars.  i might have been a little crankypants when i visited.

Okay HGI.

Room was decent size and clean although the bathroom was a little beat up.

Fitness center had enough equipment but it was way too warm in their both days I stayed. 

Breakfast offering was standard and the servers were all pleasant.

417 Union has been serving up grub since 1939 in the current space although it has underwent a few face-lifts since it's inception as a tea house for ladies. The current aesthetic is a homage to the 1940's with the throwback decor and interesting artifacts displayed throughout.
We made our first visit this past weekend for breakfast and although we had around a 10 minute wait, we instantly felt comfy here. 

Our waiter was a little rushed and not super friendly at first but when our orders were taking longer than expected, he stepped up his customer service. There wasn't much that I could order on the breakfast menu that would work with my vegan diet but I can't deny that I longed for the Caramelized Banana Pancakes ($7.29) which consisted of pancakes topped with rum and maple glazed bananas. Um, wow.
I opted for the Veggie Reuben ($8.99) from the lunch menu instead that was a pretty bland grilled portabella mushroom with sautéed vegetables, lettuce, tomato, and Swiss (which I excluded) on grilled rye. It was teamed up with crinkle french fries which got the job done of fulfilling my hunger but wasn't anything that I couldn't make myself at home. Need I remind you that I am no chef....

Overall, I would certainly consider coming back to 417 Union but it just wouldn't be a first choice as a vegan.

TIP: Daily specials are offered Monday - Friday.

They are rude and very unprofessional. I stopped by tonight at 9:50pm and the worker behind the counter said they were closed when the sign on the door says it's open until 11pm. She laughed and said "we're closed" three times as I looked at my watch. I will never go back to this establishment when the employees are rude to customers without explanation on why it was closing earlier. The woman needs to be fired and there were 3 other customers inside the Popeyes that were getting their food.

OK OK- yes there does tend to be panhandlers here, which is a little annoying, BUT it is still a decent store. I sort of think of them as "taking the hit" for other shops in the area as pandhandlers tend to gravitate here instead of CVS or other c-stores. It has a great beverage and protein bar selection (my focus) and for old city, it is generally the best pricing you will get on both.

I had heard that there was a weeklong promo for tenderloin sandwiches. FYI this is the hallmark sandwich of Indiana. They were offering the sandwich for $5. Thats a pretty good deal. So took my family to eat here. We were greeted and seated quickly. I was then greeted by our vey nice waitress. I wanted a good beer and they have an ok selection. Some of the tap selections were out but they had Yuengling which is good. Drink prices were quite reasonable here. Back to food I of course got the tenderloin with a side salad. My wife got a small steak and baked potatoe. They have pretty good kids meals and luckily Sunday is free kids meals. My daughter got the cheese pizza. 

Food came out quick. The tenderloin is great. It was hand breaded and not too thin. Large enough to cut in half and eat one side for dinner and one side for lunch later. It was great. Had some of the leftover steak which tasted great too. Service was awesome. FYI there menu has a few items you just won't find probably anywhere else like a fried balony sandwich. Looks like they offer items sourced from local places like Fischer farms, XXX root beer etc. They offer a bison burger that sounded good too.

Will be returning again.

This was my first time back to this location in almost 20 years.  The food and ice cream were good, but the dining area was filthy.  All of the booths are dated with holes in the fabric.  The floors were disgusting ( a broom and filled dust pan greeted us when we came in, and sat idle an hour later when we left).  Drive through only from now on.

This is a horrible company. We had our son take Santa pictures and paid for the highest package. We get home and we are missing pictures and keychains. We called customer service and they offer to mail the photos that were not given to us, however, it is during the holidays and they cannot guarantee when they will get delivered in time. We opt to get the photos retaken and drive all the way back to see Santa so that we can get the pictures in time. The customer service representative refuses to give me a voucher for the missing pictures and instead gives me a voucher for a less expensive package with fewer wallets and no keychains. I ask for a voucher for these pictures and keychains and she refuses and says she will mail us the additional wallets and keychains and hangs up on me without getting my address. I proceed to call back and try to speak to a supervisor and the person answering the phone instead transfers me back to the SAME customer service representative (after getting her name) who proceeds to tell me she is NOT doing anything else for me and she will not take my address to send me the pictures and keychains I paid for, nor will she give me a voucher to retake the pictures and get the additional pictures. She also tells me to call back the next day or speak with someone else if I was unhappy with her answer. She proceeds to HANG UP on me again. Please do not use this company. I have never been treated so poorly by a customer service representative before. It is unclear what kind of company this is as the receptionist clearly transferred me back to the same customer service representative on purpose. We paid or merchandise we did not receive and still will not receive. I will never deal with this company again.

Man I used to love this place but I guess time marches on. Hadn't been then in 10 yrs or better I would say. Used to be an older couple but now I only saw the wife. Guy before me has 4 little boys-gets 5 comes-asks her to make them small so they won't be messy-she obliges and says "just give me 6 bucks". 

Next up -niece gets a single scoop I get a double-no fancy cones or dips or sprinkles or anything (the Mouse is tipping the scales at like 112 lbs she passes) anyway bill is 11 bucks. Am I in some kind of Obama or Bernie universe where I pay for the guy before me?

Walk a few blocks to the south to Larry's or 50 yards north to Twistee Treat. Take a zero on this joint.

In Sept 2017, I ordered a ceiling kitchen light fixture by telephone from Home Depot, Fairless Hills, PA. On HD's recommendation, I hired "Mr. Electric" company of Warminster, PA., to install it. Three months later, it came down.
    Fortunately, it did not hit me on the head. If it had, if I had been sitting a little to the right, at the worst, I would have been hospitalized; at best, there would have been no need for hospitalization, because I would not have survived. Fearing that it would fall down, I had to hold it up for over 30 minutes until help arrived, for a cost of $300. Another scenario suggests that, had no one been home at the time, the fixture - 15-20 lbs, 1  1/2' x 4' - might have crashed to the floor, resulting in Incalculable mess, damage and expense. Although my insurance company might have covered the expense, I would still have been responsible for a $500 deductible.
    In a responding letter, "Mr. Electric" disagreed with the person who came to re-install the fixture, which meant that I had to choose whom to believe - the company that had so incompetently installed it, or the extraordinarily capable  "handyman" who has helped me for years. An easy decision. They also said that they could only take "my word" that the fixture had come down, so I sent them pictures to substantiate that.
   As of this writing, "Mr. Electric" has refunded to me only the money that it cost to install the fixture, minus the tax. I have requested that they also repay me what it cost to re-install it, since I chose not to call them again. If however, a correct check is received, I shall send another post.
   I do not recommend this company for any electrical work, and I have so informed Home Depot, which recommended them in the first place..

Ordered a couple of slices today, and I must say, the pizza was good. However, 1/2 way through my second slice, was a long piece of hair. Four people working in the back only 3 have masks and contain there hair. Absolutely disgusting!!

We grabbed a seat at a bar table for dinner. Our server was slow and we never got a bread basket (not sure if the bread is something you pay for but for their prices it should be included for free). Our dinner seemed to take a long while to come out (40+ minutes).
