We had two steaks, two drinks, lobster Mac n cheese ($10 extra for little to no lobster) and our bill was $130 before tip. 

The steaks were cooked perfectly and were high quality and full of flavor but our $20 "lobster" mac was not worth it's $20 price tag.

I would say the steaks are in line with the pricing / a good value compared with what the other resort restaurants are charging for the less fancy food they are serving. Just don't expect 5 star service along with it (at least in the bar area).

Me encanta esta pizzeria, diría que es una de mis favoritas en todo el vecindario, usan real queso mozzarella, los mejores ingredientes y todo súper fresco, definitivamente es una pizzería que recomendaría a todos probar. 

I love this pizzeria, I would say that it is one of my favorites in the whole neighborhood, they use real mozzarella cheese, the best ingredients and everything super fresh, it is definitely a pizzeria that I would recommend everyone to try.

It was super fancy and the service was very nice. The bar area was old school and elegant.The employees there if you ask will let you peek into the famous mens art deco bathroom. I would definitely recommend this place to everybody!
I wish I could give this place stars. They take advantage of people in an emergency. I'll suffer until my dentist opens next time!
Nice hotel. Ac works. There's a bathroom. Be sure to buy the hat. The best hat ever. Almost fit my head. Only 15 bucks. And they may know the price when you call the front desk.

Order oysters -- only oysters. We ordered both raw and baked oysters and enjoyed every bite. We also ordered the mixed seafood app, but should have just ordered more oysters instead. 

The line at this restaurant tends to be long, but it's worth it.

We waited way too long to indulge this spot! I haven't been to this restaurant since it was the previous restaurant and Pelago did a pop up. Cute smaller restaurant but still enough room between tables where you won't be in peoples conversations.... unless you really tried to be. Could not stop looking at the mural closer to the entrance with the different Cry Baby Logos. Okay okay enough of the decor.. onto the food.

Ordered the garlic butter on toast because why wouldn't you have garlic bread at the table to dip into the sauce of your pasta. Charred just enough to not be burnt and that's just how I like it.

Entrees: I ordered the Ricotta Cavatelli and my bf ordered the Rigatoni. Beautiful presentation, portions were just right where we were full but not falling asleep at the table from getting the itis. Satisfied and finished every little crumb. We will be back!

We stopped here based on Yelp reviews. We each had burgers, fries, pie and drinks and the total bill was under $20  The food was good and the other diners were interesting. An old gentleman met us at the door and said he had been waiting for us to get out butts in there. We were not disappointed.

I hate to have to write this but this store is horrible with customer service. I shop at Michaels twice a month at the least. Every week at the most. I buy a lot of Swarovski Crystals for the work I do. Well the stones are usually under lock and key so you have to request the help of an employee. I pressed the button to request help and the employee comes over and I am picking my stones and placing them in the hand held basket. Once I am done picking them she says I have to bring them to the register. I then went on to advise her that I spoke with someone at the corporate office (because of another incident) and was advised that policy is no longer valid. Well she gets really upset and gets her manager and they both  come over and start to antagonize me and pulled my handheld basket out of my hands. I called corporate and have yet to get a response. If I did not need these stones and paint. I would not go in this store at all. I think I am going to search for suppliers online and sever ties with Michaels once and for all. These stores are becoming horrible with customer service. And calling corporate is not resolving the issue. SHOP AT YOUR OWN RISK!!!

I came here for lunch with a coworker on Friday, it was quite a popular place. I love the atmosphere here, it's very laid back and homey. They got bar seating and booth seating area. Everyone seems to know everyone, this is a place full of regular customers. 

Anyways, besides the atmosphere, the food is also on point. I ordered garlic bread cheesesteak sandwich with a side of fries. I love the cheesesteak and the price is very reasonable as well. I would definitely come back and try different things. I highly recommend this place to anyone who is thinking about coming here. You won't regret it.

Working with Heartland Outdoor stands as the smoothest home project I got done this year. 

When fishing for quotes, Larry was the most responsive of all the others I was speaking to.

When throwing out ideas and alternatives, Larry was EXTREMELY transparent about costs, estimates and timeline. 

I'm a proponent of the idea that good customer service isn't just when things go right, but how things get managed when things go wrong. In this case, when the first option I was looking to do involved parts that were not going to be readily available - Larry was great about providing me with variations and alternatives, always accounting for cost so that I could decide if a change was worth it or not. That empathetic frankness made choosing them to complete the work easy. 

The completed work itself looks amazing. With money saved from not doing the first option, we added making changes to the all the doors that lead to my backyard. I'm thrilled about the work they did, how clean everything was left and am looking forward to working with them again on future home projects.

My fiancé and I checked this place out during devour week. We were promptly seated for our reservation and were offered the full menu and the restaurant week menu; we opted for restaurant week so we could check out multiple items (plus save some money)!

Appetizers: hamachi roll and bao buns. Easily the best part of our entire meal! Both were superb in their own ways. The hamachi had a great balance of fresh and a slight kick at the end. The bao buns had great texture with creamy coleslaw. I would come back for these appetizers alone. 

Entree: tamarind noodles and sirloin. My fiancé asked for medium rare, and his came out more medium well. However, the waiter was gracious to order a replacement, which came out medium rare. The noodles had huge shrimp in the dish and was very well proportioned to the noodles. 

Desserts: yuzu bar and strawberry soufflé cheesecake. They were just okay. Certainly not bad, but forgettable. 

Overall: good experience. A little slow of service, but with it being restaurant week, I'm sure they have their hands full. We cannot wait to try other items (did we mention the appetizers?)

The rooms are clean and spacious.  This property is located away from the hustle and bustle of the quarter but still close to everything you might want to do.  It is adjacent to the New Orleans Convention Center and about half way between the quarter and magazine street.  

I was not impressed with the staff at the bar/restaurant but in the overall picture it was a little thing.

No steve it has not been worked out. Your staff is pretending it has been fixed but it has not.

Discuss with them, they seem to have ALL of the answers.
The service was absolutely impeccable. Food delicious!  Fried green tomato, oysters, red fish!!!!

Great coffee and awesome staff.  It would be such a cool place if it was in a different location.  It is in the middle of a concrete jungle.  No personality until you walk through the doors then it has character. This place would be a better fit in St. Pete than downtown Tampa.

We took at family vacation to Florida and spent 1.5 days at Busch Gardens. Overall, the experience was disorganized and many rides were not opened. 

Falcon Fury was supposed to open earlier this year & it has yet to open. The wooden roller coaster was closed both days we went to the park. 

When rides were open, the organization was lacking. Workers would let you through the entrance and this is where the organization ended. Riders went into whichever rows they felt like and people had to figure out how to manage riding with family members in different lines. If workers didn't allow so many people through at once, the system would work much better. 

My favorite ride was the Cheetah. It did not make one queasy but had quick bursts or speed with a few loops. Another exhilarating ride was the Sheikra. This ride takes you up, hangs you off the side, then drops you at 90 degrees!

The management group that took over the Nugget from John Ascuaga has ruined what was the nicest place in town. There used to be 7 outstanding restaurants and since the changeover, I have eaten at 3 of them and will not return.  The quality has dropped significantly and as far as I am concerned there is no reason to go back.

The marshmallow fruit salad was spoijled. When we complained they got rude about it and ignored us the rest of the evening once we requested our money back. We will not be returning to this restaurant ever again!
Don't believe the rating. This place is not good. The cheesesteak is bland and not seasoned and the fries are very basic.

Small, hidden gem for great food and I love the cook!  It's small so I'd probably recommend take-out.  I had the vegetarian plate and it was so good!  The rice and peas were amazing and she asks how spicy you want it - really spicy!  I didn't like the cabbage it came with but I don't like cabbage.  I can't remember what drink the cook talked me into drinking but it was really good!  Fresh cane juice?  Mmm, can't wait to come back!

:( 

 I was really disappointed. bland bland bland. I tried the chile relleno con crema, super rica especial,#2  tacos , #8 and even though the tortillas were tasty, everything else was really, yes, bland. Oh well, at least now I can say I've been.
Food was very good and fresh! We sat outside  under a tee and it was lovely. The pizza with arugula and prosciutto was delicious. The tiramisu was also very good. I highly recommend it.

I really liked staying here and will stay here on my next trip to Boise as I found the rooms well maintained and the price was reasonable. The front desk staff was awesome, I worked out in the gym room (no complaints) and soaked my feet in that jacuzzi. So recommend. Only draw back is though its close enough to town, not much is there in the immediate surrounding the hotel area. Very close to the Boise Airport (but given the size of Boise almost anyplace in Boise is close to that Airport too).

Went there last Saturday bc my regular nail salon was closed. The guy didn't understand well and couldn't explain how the gel process work and if I could get in the pool with it. He cut three cuticles too deep and I had dried blood around my nail. Was sore naturally for two days. The cheap polish was so thick I had to go two rounds under the dryer and it still wasn't dry. Three days later, it began chipping and cracking due to the ungodly thickness. Never again!!! They really get zero stars!!!!!!!

Jones is hit or miss when it comes to the food. I've had things that were overly salty or under-seasoned. From salads to home classics...at best, it is average. The decor and vibe of the place are what's fun. You feel like you stepped right into The Brady Bunch.

Okay.... I had no idea this place existed until my buddy suggested we check it out last week. Great Scott.... Manfred's is my new favorite spot.

I ordered the same thing my buddy got, this cheese infused crape topped with a cabbage mix and a vegan unicorn meat. It was so good that I nearly passed out after the first bite.

Very fair prices, extremely friendly staff (especially the owner). It is a very small space, which I guess you could see as a drawback, but you can make and order and they will bring it over to either Woodland Empire or Doc's.

Give this place a try... the parking is a bit tight (I got a $25.00 ticket for reasons unknown to me).

We stayed at le Meridian for 2 nights over Memorial Day weekend 2017, while attending our brother's wedding. We are a family of 4: 2 adults, 2 kids, one on the way. 

A few days before our arrival we got an email from the hotel staff, asking if we had any special requests. We thought that was great. We asked for a hypoallergenic room (explained that I am allergic to feathers), and some extra towels since there would be 4 of us. We also asked that our room would be as close as possible to the elevator, since we had 2 small kids, a stroller, and were expecting to be in and out of the room a lot.

When we arrived at the hotel, there was a huge line to check in. After a long drive, with 2 exhausted kids, that's not how you want to be greeted. If the hotel was expecting so many guests, they could have had extra staff. 

The bell man didn't bother to take our stuff to our room but just left us stranded with the cart with 2 kids and a stroller. 

We bumped into the parents of the bride, who were asked to wait in the lobby bar, cuz their room wasn't ready by check-in time...

We were taken to our room. Feather pillows on all beds, as I found out after I started to get a severe allergic reaction. No extra towels. No crib. The room was 753, the farthest away from the elevator on our floor. It took us another 2 hours and several calls to the front desk go get the pillows replaced with hypoallergenic ones (after my eyes were all red and swollen in preparation for the wedding), and to get a crib for the baby.

We had breakfast at the hotel. It was very expensive, and most things from the buffet were already gone. We were charged the full rate nevertheless. Drinks at the bar were really expensive for nothing special. There is only 1 bathroom in the lobby, which was always dirty, occupied, had a wobbly changing table, and had no paper towels. 

Parking is expensive, too, and all they have is the valet option. 

The following day I asked for room service to come before our son's nap time. They were in no rush. I had to call them again before it was too late, and remind them before they came.

On the day we were checking out, and rushing to get to the family brunch after the wedding, the bellman took a while to come upstairs, because there were too many people checking out and because someone else grabbed him while he was on his way to our room... we were 1/2 hour late to the event. 

Additionally, the lights in our hallway were out, the toilet barely flushed, and the air in the room was very stale. But overall, the accommodations were decent, the service - awful. 

I recommend staying somewhere else. For the money we spent for two nights between the room rate, taxes, bar, parking, breakfast, we could have stayed in a top notch 5-star hotel. We would have gotten much better service.

I love this Bonchon. It's a really chill, modern set restaurant down a little alley in Chinatown.  If you don't know, Bonchon is a chain that's known for their Korean chicken wings which they do a great job of.  However, I like to come here with my white co-workers and watch k-pop videos while scarfing down wings.  What better way to decompress from a stressful day of work then to watch k-pop videos with colleagues.  It's funny to see them try to articulate their thoughts in the most politically correct way possible and try not to show any semblance of yellow fever.  I'm Asian and all, but even I have to admit, they really all do look alike.

Ordered for pickup and tipped 20% in advance as I normally do for any restaurant. Order was ready 10 mins late. When I got home, I saw a container labeled "ran out of tots" and found they gave me a "side salad" (just lettuce, no other vegetables in sight) as a substitute for tater tots! Really?! And they didn't even tell me in advance so I could just not pay for a side. Mac and cheese was very bland. I called to complain and they said they'd refund me money. A week later and still no refund. I'm not surprised they failed at that, too.

I live in Creve Coeur and this is  really one of the go-to places for quick and healthy food, be it breakfast, lunch or dinner. I love bagels and they have a great assortment of fresh bagels which I get bread sliced. Their coffee is good too. I like that they have a lot of vegetarian options. I like they sandwiches, salads, pasta in pesto sauce. My favorite is black bean soup in a bread bowl. 
the missing star is because the tables are not always clean.

The first few times I've ordered pickup from Ra was pretty great but the past few times I've ordered to pick up I've missed half of my order once or my miso soup a few times. I work in the mall so sometimes I don't have time to go back when I'm on my lunch time. Today I was pretty disappointed and had to go back in. I ordered the salmon poke bowl with rice but I got the lunch poke bowl version. So I kindly went back in and asked if it was the lunch menu salmon poke bowl and the girl in the front said that the menu had changed and I showed her the picture from the online menu of what I ordered and it was missing the sauces and green onions and the portion was a little smaller. And I've ordered the regular salmon poke bowl with rice and it did not look like this. I just felt like i was ripped off. And I think the online menu needs to be updated if it did change.

Some buddies and I were pumped about Fogo coming to the Big Easy.  So we booked a table for 6 on a Friday night and did a guys night.  

We had a reservation for 7pm, but it took Fogo a while before the table was ready.  The place wasn't packed by any stretch, but they said they were thin on wait staff...or whatever.  This was disappointing.  I've eaten out before.  I get it.  Not everyone gets seated exactly at 7pm for a 7 resi, but this was more like 7:30 or 7:40.  Too long to wait for all you can eat steak.

Then, we were seated in the back of the restaurant on the 2nd floor.  I do not think there was a worst table in the house, unless there is one inside of the men's bathroom.  There were so many available tables, and they put us in the back corner.  Whatever.  We were determined to make the best of this night.  Our servers were really nice and the gauchos were doing their best, but our service was subpar.  They kept throwing the less desirable cuts at us.  Yeah right Fogo.  I didn't come to dinner for pork sausage.  We continually asked any gaucho or waitress in the vicinity to send us our requested pieces of meat.  It was kind of a battle.  And I think the table position put us in a blind spot of the staff or something.  

In the end, we had our fill.  It took a while, but eventually, we all left stuffed.  Frankly, we weren't going to be denied.  We had been looking forward to steakfest for weeks.  Me and my buddies have been to plenty of Brazilian steak houses including some other Fogo's.  We thought this was quite a weak showing.  I hope to repeat Fogo de Chao.  I want them to be better next time.

I was really excited to get my frozen yogurt fix when I walked into U-Swirl. The place looked really nice and had a lot of sitting areas. I also noticed that they have more flavors and a lot more toppings to choose from. 
What I didn't LOVE was that most of the fruit flavors were not all that creamy. The texture was more like a sorbet. All the flavors are really good though, and my all time favorite is the original/tart.
The service is really good, they offer as many samples as you want. Thaw reason why they didn't get more stars is because of the texture, but it is a nice place to sit down and enjoy good frozen yogurt.

The food and service were excellent but something else wasn't quite right and I couldn't place it until my ever perceptive boyfriend pointed it out - the music selections.  I see on the details listed about this restaurant that there is a JUKE BOX.   Why not some modern Mexican music?  Classical guitar, huarache band, Latin jazz - but definitely NOT pop music.  It was definitely the one sour note of the experience.

We did the tasting menu.  Usually I would say that $55 on food alone to be pricey but it was a good experience.  The lamb chops and guacamole with lump crab meat were amazing.  We would like to try the scallops on another visit although they are only offered as an entree and not a small plate.  The interior is dominated by the color pink and made me feel as though I was sitting inside a giant pinata.  Very cute decor.

This is one of the worst brunch experiences I have had in a long time.  I was with a large group of people and our food took two hours to arrive to our table.   Our server told us the food was pre ordered by the organizer of our group,which were appetizers.   The server misinterpreted her order, and we ended up having to wait a long time for our drinks and to take our individual orders for our food. The server did not do a good time organizing our orders and maintaining time between our orders, but the restaurant itself was not prepared for a busy day on memorial Day.   We arrived to the restaurant on 11:00 am and we did not get our food until 1:00 pm.  We did get the appetizers before hand while we were waiting, but it was not enough to fill us up.   Finally,after two hours, the GM jessie came out and explained the situation and apologized to the remaining members of our group. Several people left after one hour wait.   Jessie did provide us some free drinks and food for us and said he was going to discount our food, but we still ended up paying the $15 price for unlimited brunch, so none of us received a discount on our bill.   All in all, this was not a good experience for me at this restaurant.  It will be quite a long time before I come back here for brunch or dinner.  If I do decide to come back here, it will be with a small group of people.   Peter, the server, was a nice guy and tried his best, but today was not a good day for him as a server, and he could have stepped it up a bit to accommodate our group better.   Do not go to this restaurant with a large group of people.  You will be waiting a while for your food.
Oh no! And no one here to fix it... no paper towels near sink... Called and left message hopefully someone comes soon

I want to give four stars for the quality of food but the awful service prohibits me from doing so. This is my second visit here and I am still holding out hope they will hold charm school classes along with their already scheduled pie school before my return.

Yelp suggests that two stars means, "Meh. I've experienced better.'  That was my strong feeling about the Mud House, so that's why I'm giving it two stars.  I had an egg dish and the other two friends with me had different breakfast/egg dishes.  We all had similar experiences--one thought it was average, one didn't even finish her dish, because it just wasn't that good.  We are all transplanted New Yorkers and we've eaten all kinds of good food.  Nothing here was bad, but nothing was even close to being outstanding.  One of my friends did say she had a very good cappucino.

I seriously debated before leaving this review, because I love Brugge's food. I always leave waiters 20% tips even when I get bad service because I figure my pay doesn't get docked if I have an off day, so why should waiters? I finally decided to leave this because this place could SO EASILY be a five star place if they would just work on their service. Their food is amazing, and that's why I keep going back-- I've gone a few times per year since they opened, and service has been a consistent problem for them. I'd go more often if the service was even just okay, but it's always bad. Their servers seem like they mostly work the kitchen or bar and just kind of show up to tables in between when they can. They usually are borderline rude. I'm not one of those people that expects or cares about "service with a smile," but these guys seem vaguely annoyed every time you ask for anything. One even cut me off with a "Yeah yeah yeah I know" when I started to ask for a water refill! Every time I go, there is at least 1 thing (usually more-- last time it was 3) I have to ask for multiple times. You basically have to tackle a waiter to get one water, let alone a refill. This kind of service would make sense at a place that was a little lower budget and casual, but Brugge's prices and ambience make you expect something nicer. And what is truly troubling is that the food is five star food! The mussels and frites are AMAZING. But seriously, whenever we go there, I remind everyone with me, "just remember we come here for the food." It's kind of a joke amongst my circle at this point. Brugge could easily be the classy nice restaurant in Broad Ripple if they just worked on this one thing.
They need to have the food ready in the chipotle lane if it's your time slot. If not ready, have people pull around.

We made reservations for the patio on Friday evening. Being new to the area we had no idea what to expect. Parking is kinda limited but we got a spot. Let's start with the reservation, patio seating was a must. We got just that. The art deco was unique and a must see. The drinks were very good... Go with the suggestions of the staff. We are fried pickles, fried green tomatoes both were delicious & potatoes rolls something like that... I wasn't a fan. The wings were great too. The chicken pasta dish was good. I will go again for sure. We added it to our date night rotation.

I can't speak to the sushi, but their pho is fantastic! Pho is one of our favorite foods, and we have eaten it all over the western US. Their broth at Fresh Sushi Pho is the most flavorful I have had in a long time, and prices are very reasonable. Really wonderful and we will definitely be back!

I have used their services several times and they are consistently amazing! This time I brought in my SUV that is used to transport a small human that consumes lots of food in it daily (long school commute=breakfast and snacks). Anthony was super kind and non-judgey;  now Rosa the car looks and smells like new.

Just okay. The sushi makers on a Saturday night dinner are somewhat rude, and make the rolls so big with rice. Good strategy but come on! There's so many sushi places in town so they have to step it up a little bit. 

Yes I am comparing it to hiroba and the joint. But I guess it gets the job done.

This place sucks. I was attending a conference here and had to run back to my hotel room to grab something. First of all, this place is massive and so poorly labeled. I missed the first lecture I wanted to attend because I couldn't find it. There were digital maps scattered around that were not helpful at all, half of them didn't work. Second, the parking. Parking is $32. On their website, this is listed as $32/day with NO fine print stating you have to be a guest. It's not $32 a day. It's $32 with NO INS OR OUTS. I had to run back to my hotel room in between lectures to grab something and was not allowed back in to park unless I wanted to pay another $32. This is not listed on the ticket or their website. The only way you know is if you made the mistake I did. What an absolute rip off. $32 a day should be $32 a day for everyone. My only option now is to park somewhere else and walk in the 50% humidity. I also stepped into the gift shop to grab a few things. A bottle of water and hair clips cost me $10. After this conference, I won't step foot into this place again nor will I recommend it. This place should be ashamed.

I used to really like Saki when they first opened, enough that I would go by myself. Now, that is no longer an option.

I really loved Saki because they are the only place I can think of with unlimited salmon roe sushi and short ribs. Of course, I will order a salad, appetizer, some nigiri as well but, really, I'm just there for the roe and ribs!

Unfortunately, the past couple times I've been, I've always left feeling sick. At first, I thought... Hey, maybe I got too excited and ate too much. The last time, I had only had about two orders of the short ribs and 4 pieces of salmon roe sushi before I started feeling sick again. I don't know what happened, but I can't keep going and leave feeling nauseated.

Let me start by saying this review should have been done quite some time ago so maybe they have straightened up their act a little. This is a place that had a great view and can be a romantic location for a date or a night out. I wish I could rate it higher but the food was just ok and the service was terrible. We got the wrong wine however they managed to get the brand right, the bread guy had been missing in action for the first 15 mins we were there. And the guy sitting next to us with his two lady friends that looked like they were being paid to be there having inappropriate conversations made it hard to hear my wife. Definitely a sour night for what could be a great place to dine.

I like to stop in for the smoked salmon bagels, or sometimes a bagel with cream cheese. Honestly, I think the coffee is terrible though. 
Sometimes they have cups of sliced strawberries with strawberry cream cheese and chocolate sauce, if you are early enough- they are a great treat.
If you are hoping to pop in between classes I recommend waiting, the lines can be very very long. I waited about 30 minutes in line once.
The workers are not very friendly, but they are usually in a rush I guess because of the crowds.
PS if you like salmon, do NOT try the salmon spread for the bagels stick with an actual smoked salmon bagel sandwich, because the spread is really off-putting it tastes like salty hot dog smear blegh!!

I was looking forward to enjoying this place. I didn't even know it existed until i saw they had a groupon. The groupon said $20 value for towards two pho dishes and I didn't even get a chance to use it for the visit because he said I had to order pho. Instead of putting dishes on the groupon they should of put pho noodle soup. Its misleading because I thought we could of used it on any food dishes. I guess I misread it on my end. Besides the whole groupon ordeal, we sat ourselves at the booth and the waitress came and dropped off menus. We didnt see her until 10 mins passed. And when she took our orders took her another 10 mins to bring water with no ice. Food took a while and when the food finally came the man asked if we wanted ice. Its like goodness took you 15 mins to realize we had no ice. I am ok, I will enjoy my room temperature water.  I usually order pho when going to a Vietnamese restaurant, but I didnt feel like eating noodle soup today so i opt for the vermicelli noodles. It was just okay. I really had better and the fried rice was pretty generic. It was like getting fried rice at panda express.  Overall service was bad. I get they just opened and looking to hire more staffs but they are going to lose lots of customers at the rate they are going. I don't think I will be back because service took wayyy too long. I have had my share of Vietnamese restaurants from here to California but at least the food made up for the lack of service like most of how the Vietnamese restaurants are. But not so great food and horrible service definitely wont have me back.

What happened to this place? Restaurants have gone to serving crap. (See photo). Called casino host to book a room. He wanted to charge $149 per night.  The SL website was $90.  Hotels.com $68. Some host!?  When the sports book was sold to William Hill I was stiffed of nearly $500 in accumulated comps, even though they promised they would roll over.  Blatant lies. 
I was even told to contact the MGM to redeem the lost comps.  Sharon Cox must have thought I would not, because when I did they had NOTHING to do with it.  But thanks for the Wild goose chase.   Now 6-5 on blackjack instead of 3-2.  Nice touch. Very little reason for players to play at SL.
I feel bad for the owners watching my former favorite casino go straight downhill.

I'm breaking up with you Minato.  And it's not me, it's you.

We were regulars at the old location and frequently and insistently recommended them (If you are local, you know there is a plethora of sushi places and everyone has their favorites they champion).  We loved the menu, the service, we were even comfortable with the small space.  

With their move to the new location it's like they are a completely different restaurant.  What happened?  They added new items to the menu and took off some old ones.  A lot of the new rolls are similar with the imitation krab mix and lots of sweet sauce, not very innovative. I can't really fault them for that since they are probably very popular so they are filling a demand.  They also have "special" nigiri they offer (limit one order per person) but everytime I went they were out so I never had the opportunity to try.  

The main reason however that we are done is the service.  Back when they were in a smaller space they staffed 2 or 3 girls on the floor, now that they are in a higher capacity they didn't raise the number of floor staff, although it appears they have an entirely new staff.  The familiar faces are gone.  

Our waitress was flailing and I'm guessing very inexperienced?  She stopped frequently to ask how things were and then walked away while we were answering. She dropped of items and was puzzled when we would ask what it was and floated from table to table trying to find a home for dishes she didn't seem to know who ordered. She never once picked up our order sheet, we had to flag down the other staff to submit it.  We never received requested condiments or refill on drinks which was bad because dinner spanned over 2.5 hours due to her slowness and confusion. They were not busy that night.  Our waitress had 2 other tables aside from us. a 2 top and a table of 3.   The other 2 girls were very attentive despite not being our waitress (I think they were actually bussers). We were patient throughout until we asked for our check from the waitress and waited.  20 mins and no check, although she came to the table next to us 3 times to check on them.  My kids were getting restless so I packed them up while my husband waited.  On my way out I reminded the waitress that we were still waiting for our check and that my husband would be paying (I didn't want her to think we were leaving without paying). He sat and waited at our table.  I took the kids out to the parking lot to walk around and look at the mini golf course until my husband came back out.  10 minutes goes by. 20 minutes goes by.  We've paced the entire parking lot like 10 times, so I go back to peek inside and my husband is still patiently waiting.  I go in to see what's taking so long.  It's been 40 minutes now since we've requested our check and my husband has reminded her an additional 3 times whenever she stopped by the table.  Finally he had to get up and walk over to the cash register where one of the bussers had to check with the manager if it was okay for her to close out our check so we can pay.  We spent almost 4 hours trying to eat dinner and pay.  We normally are out of there in an hour or less when we eat sushi.  We didn't eat more than we usually do, we just spent about 75% of the time waiting and waiting and waiting.  It was a very frustrating experience.

I'm trying Pizzeria Bianco tonight, sitting at the bar I started with a pear pecan goat cheese salad and a light dressing which was really good. A glass of la Valentina wine paired with my lasagna...  Which is only made on select nights Friday thru Monday depending on the chef.....Made with bechamel.  The best lasagna I've ever had, and that's a huge compliment with my mom being Italian. Wow.  Jason the bartender made  suggestions and he was spot on. They do sell their own tomato sauce on a jar $8 and tomatoes in can $6. I'm in love.

We enjoyed the Fettuccini with Miso Alfredo and Roasted Cauliflower Steaks dinner entrees. The fettuccini was creamy and the sauce on the cauliflower steaks made the dish. The restaurant is well lit, clean and has fun veggie art all around. Service was great, our server was polite and able to answer all our questions. We were greeted after dinner by a member of the staff who asked about our experience and kitchen staff gave us a smile and wave on the way out.

Came here for lunch with my Dad. As I've done so many times in the past, tried to order a lunch combo on the weekend even though it's only a weekday promotion. You'd think I would learn, but you'd also think that Applebee's would NOT HAND OUT THAT MENU INSERT ON THE WEEKENDS. It's just annoying, but I'm not saying I'm not at fault.

Ended up ordering the chicken fajita rollup in a snap decision. The same thing I always get. It tasted the same as always. The service was fine, not exactly friendly, best described as matter-of-fact.

All told, same ol' Applebee's. To paraphrase Ron Burgundy "we've been going to the same party for 12 years and in no way is that depressing"

We had to cancel our wedding due to covid-related issues in April of this year. Love City was on our list for a reception space initially - and when we contacted them in the beginning of May, I was so impressed by how quickly they were able to help us. 

Phoebe was our main point of contact throughout the planning process and she was WONDERFUL. She was so responsive and friendly in our emails, it really made the process easier. Day of (8/13!), the manager Mike was so pleasant and helpful. The staff working the outdoor bar were all so sweet and nice.

Almost every one of our guests mentioned something they liked about the beer, the space, or the staff. The tent made the heat and sun bearable and everyone working helped make our day special and perfect. Thank you so much!!

If you ever want a really cool, out of the way spot to host an event, Love City delivers, 10/10 recommend.
Great place!!  Very much like the beer halls in Germany.  Lots of good beers on tap, lots of seating.

Small rooms.  Room literature says pool open at 6:00 a.m., but actually opens at 7:00 a.m. which is simply too late if you are here on business.

Staff is friendly and helpful (this has greatly improved since I have been staying here)

Required valet parking of $25!

Excellent for business at the State Capital, a little far for walking to downtown areas.  They do have an excellent shuttle.
2 girls ripped me off for $40 and then they didn't even give me a dance! Place fucking sucks
Outstanding value at this AMC. Under $5 for weekend Matinees before 6 PM. Even new releases. Plus they had a self-serve soda and icee machines.

Happiest place in the city!! Everyone is so friendly!!! I got my hair done two times this week while visiting Philly. The service was great!! They were on time and worked fast. My hair looked great! I will come back again the next time I am in town.

When you first look at it from the outside & inside you think typical dive bar. It is an old bar but the food is excellent, never had anything I didn't like! The outdoor space is awesome & I think all the bartenders are nice & quick to get you whatever you need. I've been there multiple times & I will continue to go back! Plus for all you huge beer fans, they have a giant selection.

Saw the response - not sure how you can say there was an outage in the area at the time I called, when you have no idea what time I called. I never mentioned it. I also live in the area, and there was no outage I was aware of that day. 

Strange.

Per other experiences I would still generally rate this as an establishment to only get carry out from, although you should double check the order is accurate before heading home. Also for some reason food from here only ever shows up cold when ordered through doordash. Tried twice. It's warm if you pick it up yourself, so I'm not really sure what that's about.

Came here for my birthday with a group of 6, we contacted the restaurant about three days in advance for a reservation and would up with a 9pm reservation on a Friday night. Corkage fee is $10 for wine and $1 for each beer you bring. We brought some IPA's and lager which was great with the food. You can buy alcohol in the Old City location for cheap.

Atmosphere is a bit chaotic on a weekend, and it can get a but loud in there. Food was awesome we were able to try a bunch of things, most dishes are able to be shared family style. My favorite things we  shared were Dan Dan Noodles, Chili Dumplings, Cumin Style Chicken. I was not intimidated by the spice level of the foods because the heat was complex not just burning.

Prices were reasonable, and it was absolutely delicious.

This is a budget airline and you definitely get what you pay for. 

The flight can be cheap but you will literally pay for any and everything else. Picking your seat, Snack/Drink, bags, etc are all an additional charge. Plan accordingly; I filled a water bottle in the airport and had trail mix in case I got hungry. 

I had 2 people next to me that thought they would be slick and bring carry on size wheeled bags as a personal item. (I heard someone else say they always charge for wheeled bags so keep that in mind when packing if you don't prepay!) 
Needless to say, the gate agent wasn't having that and told them $65 for their bags. Card only, so they wer screwed since they only had cash. The one guy literally threw his luggage away so he didn't have to pay. 

Seats are a little snug but doable. I'm only 5'6" and there was just enough leg room. They did let the girl sitting in the middle seat move to a less crowded row since there were empty seats which I thought was nice.
Great food.  Really friendly service!  Would recommend as a good neighborhood brunch spot.

Would go lower if I could.  Service was horrible and food was terrible.  Very disappointed since we paid so much.  Sriracha shrimp was cold, Wasabi Tuna was overcooked as was the lobster tails.  Only good thing was the beer, can't screw up a bottled beer.  Oh, and the lemon in my water had the sticker still on it.

The dental hygienist was friendly and through, but the dentist tried to peddle some necessary oral cancer screening for extra $$. Given that I am under 30 and do not smoke, this just seemed like something I would expect a used car salesman to do. 

They also were not exactly gentle during some fillings I got done- the upper and lower part of my cheek was cut by the end of the procedure and I was full of canker sores for the next week. 

Lastly, I was double charged (over $300) for a visit because of issues with their credit card system. They admitted the double charge occurred, but instead of just writing me a check they made me file a dispute with the bank. I didn't get my money back for over a week. 

I have been to better dentists. I would not recommend.

These donuts were the shit! Could not believe how light and fluffy and flavorful. We had the lemon and classic donuts and omg they were so good we took them home too and they lasted no problem. Only sad thing is they were basically sold out of everything when we got there and it was hours before closing. Literally so delicious though and I don't even like donuts!

I was scalloping..but on land on a Sunday. And as challenging as scalloping is for real-it might be even harder on land on a Sunday.

Attempt #1 took me to Ryan's. My first time here. I am underwhelmed by the selection of their prepared foods, which was actually a relief. Because avoiding temptation like that is sometimes just easier. 

I was mostly impressed by their meats. Go figure. I'm not a big red meat eater but dang their selection, quality and price were game changers! 

Unfortunately, no fresh scallops. I could have called first, sure, but I did like the adventure. 

Staff wasn't overly attentive. So I don't have a gauge on how educated they are on the product if you come in with questions. Seems like you might need to do a little google consulting. Overall a great little spot.

I would have given this place a 4 star rating but after having a nice meal and enjoying our wine, the two managers on the floor stood by our table  after we finished our meal to "encourage" us to leave. I think I was still chewing my last bite  and I wasn't finished with my wine. I know it was busy because it was Valentine's Day but we were not sitting there for an extended period of time. The food was good (not great), cocktail menu was whiskey and bourbon heavy, decent wine selection...but to feel rushed like that is bad customer service and at the end of the day that is what matters most.

The bar was a great find while my boyfriend and I were in Philadelphia for a quick weekend trip. It was our last night in town and we wanted to find a bar that was worth the evening. Can't say enough that we were very happy with our whole experience there! 

For a Saturday evening, the place didn't get really crowded (except for when a college bus dropped off a load of kids for an hour). There were enough seats and tables along with a long bar. They had a wide range of beers to choose from and other liquors. Their food menu was nice and they had a good little section for tater tots! We just ordered plain tots and boy did they give you a lot! 

We really enjoyed our time there and we stayed at the bar for a few hours just taking in the game on TV and the great music they were playing. The bartender and door attendant/bouncer were really nice, too!  The bar was a great place and they treated us very well! If we are ever back in the area, this will definitely be a place that we're coming back to!
I ordered the duck leg and it was so delicious, and more fancy than we anticipated. Fast and friendly service. Definitely recommend the restaurant.

Nothing too impressive but would give it another shot

I came here to meet two friends on a Thursday night and the service was slow even though the place was dead. I had a glass of white wine which came in a miniature bottle which was different. The free chips and salsa were ok but the chips could've been warmed up first. My friends wanted to order dishes that came with rice but they were out. of. rice??? I ordered a taco salad but it was really heavy on the lettuce and they put huge SLICES of tomato on it like, wth? I've never had a taco salad that didn't have chopped tomatoes like you wouldn't disgrace a taco with slices so why would you do such a thing to my taco salad? Overall I wasn't impressed but I would give it another chance. I think there are better options in the area.

took the two teenage boys for their first Lou's experience. Each of use destroyed a garlic bread cheesesteak sandwich for lunch. I haven't had anything else here before. Only been a few times and any place that mixes garlic bread and a cheese steak - well that's all i will order.

I love Chatham Tap. The food is always good. Especially the portobello fries. They are fire. Or stupendous if you prefer. My friend and I were there tonight. We had the most amazing service. Our server was also the manager, which is always a good thing. But he was attentive, funny, engaging, and helpful. I always had everything I needed. He even got me things that I didn't necessarily need. I

I always love Chatham Tap on Mass Ave and in Fishers. But the service I had at Fishers has changed me into a regular. Thanks for the stellar service and the delicious food.

Spent the past weekend here and the place was pretty nice.  I don't know why some reviewers said parking was free... it was $10.  It's alright... it was a vacation anyways.  This place is right in front of the beach and not too far away from the pier, which was great.  

Reserved a room with 2 queen beds but eventually upgraded to a partial ocean view. It was nice and the room was very well decorated. We enjoyed our stay here.  Checking in was a breeze and the manager was very professional and pleasant.  No real complaints.  The price was a bit high but, oh well... it was right across the beach and the hotel was very well maintained.

We also ate at Eladio's.  Food was great albeit a bit pricey.  Guests received a 10% discount.  Free wifi!!

I would recommend this place!  

Just an FYI - they offer a 10% for AAA members! Don't forget to use it!

I ordered vegetable fried rice and told on the phone no meat specifically. The person who answered the call did not have patience to listen and she immediately hung up after getting my phone number when i was stressing i want NO meat. When i got the food, i was shocked to see bits of meat in my order. I threw it out and did not like it this time. When your customer requests something and you cannot make it don't commit. Atleast wash the freakin dishes you cook with. I am not going there anymore.

I purchased a Creative Loafing deal for Dale 1891 and normally, I have a great experience anywhere I go with these type of deals. However, my experience was less than stellar. The restaurant was dark and dingy and the air conditioner was on such a low temperature that I was shivering. The nachos appetizer came out cold and the beer cheese on them were not pleasant at all. I ordered a burger and my boyfriend ordered a philly cheese. He didn't even want to finish his meal and I opted to not even take my leftovers home. I will say, however, that the service was fantastic, but maybe that was because we were the only people in there...

James is a great employee. After a lot of research, trying to see where to complete section 2 of the I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification form (as I am a remote employee), I found out that they will complete the form for you there as an Authorized Representative for $2.50. James was fast, efficient, and helpful.

Very convenient - right across the street from the Holiday Inn Express on Haley! GF beer & cider on the menu. Food is just so-so; nothing to brag about but Standard pub fare. Service was also so-so during happy hour; staff are more interested in charting up the regulars rather than serving the tourists who may never return again (which may be understandable but I'm a "regular visitor" - I may be too old to be your typical brewery patron but I'm here every 3-4 weeks on business so don't be too judgemental - and business customers generally tip generously - if the service is good!

CLOSED!!!!!!!!!
Had coupon & needed pet supplies.
Alas it is out of business! No good for anyone.
What's a girl to do?
Go back online?

This rating is more of a 3.5 than a 4. It hurts my heart because I really wanted to love this place. It's close to my house and is a beautiful environment/establishment to eat breakfast or brunch. The biscuits in my opinion just are not great and the biscuits should be great at a biscuit house. They are very heavy/dense and do not have a lot of flavor by themselves. 

I got the Chicken. Set. Go. biscuit with pimento cheese, fried chicken and added a fried egg. It was pretty good but the biscuit didn't do anything for me. The cold brew, however, was amazing. My husband got the bacon egg and cheese biscuit and it was also ok. We got the strawberry and dash to share and that was actually incredible. The dense biscuit works as a shortcake situation with the strawberries whipped cream and cream cheese. It added the sweetened and flavor the biscuits were missing. 

My lack of enthusiasm for these biscuits could just be my personal preference so check this place out for yourself. It really is an awesome environment with room for groups, see attached pictures. I will say the staff here was awesome, friendly and efficient. 

I would probably eat here again but I still sat biscuit love has my loyalty for a go to biscuit joint. 

There is free parking behind and in side of building. Also check out the murals inside and out, they are amazing.

I would comment on the shopping experience, except I didn't have one. I'm recovering from a broken leg and ankle, therefore I need to use one of those motorized scooters when I shop. I usually shop at the Meridian or Fairview Winco's, which both have many, many scooters which are all kept at the doors and are always fully charged. Kudos to them. But the Myrtle Street store doesn't keep their scooters by the door. They are in customer service which is nowhere near the front doors. And when you send your scooter experienced almost 12 yr old granddaughter to fetch one for you... they say "No minors". So apparently I'm supposed to walk down to customer service and get it myself. Do they not realize people use scooters because they can't walk (or can't walk long distances)? And yes, I asked an employee at the U-scans. She said she would have someone bring one to me. Five minutes later I was still waiting and no scooter in sight. So we left, and went to the Fairview Winco. I'll never return to the Myrtle store. They get a big fat "F" in the handicap-friendly category.

Some days you need a midday pick-me-up! I stopped in Dunkin' today to get a decaf coffee and a croissant. I have been on an iced coffee kick and keep that at home for daily use, but fully caffeinated coffee at 2:30 PM doesn't bode well for me getting to sleep at night, so I opted to order a decaf coffee from Dunkin'. It's always risky to get coffee mid-afternoon because chances are fairly good that the coffee may have been sitting there for awhile! I placed my order on the app and walked in to pick it up. When I walked to the pickup counter, I was greeted by an employee asking if I had ordered the decaf. When I confirmed, she informed me that she just put on a fresh pot. I am always happy to wait a few extra minutes to get fresh coffee. I won't go in detail on the coffee quality, I like a mild/light roast coffee, so Dunkin' is one of my favorites. I admit, I had low expectations for the croissant. I expected it to be average. I may have just been really hungry, but I thought it was really good. I had picked up on the go and when I got home I put it in the microwave aside a cup of water for 20 seconds and it was soft, flakey, buttery, and delicious. 

The service and friendliness of the staff was top notch! I would highly recommend this location.

Hungry hungry... not happy. It's not even 11:30 am yet and they are down to 2 kinds of bagels. 

Check out the pic. This is what they call adding cream cheese. I think they used 1 tsp on the whole thing. At the first bite I could tell these were not done. I love chewy, but not chewy and doughy. 

Sorry Bruegger's, you lost a customer
It was not the best ramen I have had ..... it missed something I can not describe ... service was not great and there was a fly next to me all the time

I wish there was a little more flavor to the chicken. This place is cool on the inside. The Mac and Cheese is okay. Would have loved the use of less creamy cheese. Wouldn't confuse it with southern mac and cheese but it could hold you over in a pinch. All my opinions but, wasn't really as good as the hype.

I was craving a good burrito while my boyfriend and I were visiting New Orleans. We read good reviews about it, so we thought we'd give it a try.

My boyfriend ordered the Kamehameha nachos and I got the Western burrito. My boyfriend was very disappointed in the nachos. The flavors mixed together just did not taste right to him. The burrito was pretty mediocre to me. I would've been more satisfied with a Chipotle burrito unfortunately. I don't think we'll be coming back.

Went and got a ticket to have my license renewed, put cell number in to receive text when my turn was close. Never received text, got second ticket that said estimated wait time 0 minutes, waited 45 minutes until I was called.

This is the worse bus trip!!! there is no ac on this bus!!!! the bus is blowing out warm air!!!! This is a miserable bus ride from new york to dc on fathers day!!!!! it is 87° outside. i ask the bus driver to turn the air up please. and she said that she can not control the air, then i called customer service and she said that she could not help me, she wanted me to email my complaint and someone would get back to me. which means that i have to ride this hot bus for  5 hours!!!!!

Not a pleasant experience. I do NOT recommend this place at all. We had hot pot, and they didn't even cut off the root of the spinach! I don't know about other dishes, but for hot pot the price is not appropriate to the portion size at all. They claimed on the menu the base was bone soup, but when we asked to add soup they said they only got hot water to add. What? The waiter was very inexperienced and he didn't add any soup at the end. He didn't even clean up the empty plates. Btw, for my taste, all sauces have been added water to it. The sesame sauce does not taste like traditional Chinese sesame sauce at all. The beef in the picture is about 2/3 of the portion ($6)

They calls themselves "Best of Yelp" on Groupon which I think is hilarious. This is more like a kid that wants to make his own schedule, but if something better comes up he skips his appointments.

 I bought two groupons, one for my boyfriend and one for myseld. He was a no call no show for my boyfriends first appointment. We called to reschedule and he did the job. Not the best detail I have ever gotten and we still had dirt to clean after he left.. Then he was a no call no show for my appointment even when I texted to confirm the night before. I have left three voice mails and two texts since then, which was two weeks ago. He never responded and I had to call Groupon to get my refund. 

DO NOT waste your time with this kid! He is more trouble than he's worth and you can find professional legitimate companies that will keep your appointments and do a much better job.

Very frustrating!

Very friendly staff and great fresh food, will definitely be going again. I wish more people would give it a chance did not look very busy while some of the chain places that serve microwaved food looked full.

Great vibe, the shrimp is amazing, and down-to earth staff. I had a great, romantic evening with my wife here. Cocktails were fantastic and the shareable plates were a terrific idea -- all while listening to an amazing singer and a terrific piano player.

I stayed last night with co workers, we were all in separate rooms on different floors and had the same in room experience. The night of check in there was no hot water to shower and called front desk to ask if there were any issues with the plumbing.  The front desk sent maintenance to the room but he left saying he had to check the main water system agreeing there was no hot water in the room.  We went on with our night hearing nothing assuming the issue was being taken care of until I tried to shower this morning, still no hot water.   When my co workers and I  met up in the lobby we realized we all had the same complaint of no hot water and that our bathroom sink didn't drain causing one person to flood their bathroom after the front desk told them to keep the water running and it would eventually get hot.  The front desk had no apologies or even gave acknowledge there was an issue with 6 different rooms standing in front of them with the same complaint.  Poor Service!

I went to this place to grab a beer and eat dinner. No body at the front to greet and I had to wait for 2-3 minutes. I ordered a beer but didn't have the ID so I was refused that I understand. The manager came and sat on the opposite side. Bad attitude!! 
Secondly, she explained me about the policy that they check IDs for people below the age of 30-40 years. Really shocking!! The policies should be same for all and how does she knows if a person is over 30 or over 40 years of age. At the end, she said if you show me your ID once, I would not have to bring my ID again as she would remember it. She also said if you would like to go out and dine somewhere else you're most welcome to do. That pissed me off.

The law is that person taking the order must request for your ID and checks if it is a photo ID and also checks your age whether it is 21 years or not. (legal drinking age in U.S) 
No straw with the first drink, had to remind server to get it.  

Simple solution was to politely say no- and tell me that I do not have a ID and I cannot be served alcohol. Secondly, she should not have sat opposite me disrespecting me as the guest. Really, I had to remind the server to get a straw on a slow night? This took place at Uno, Providence.
A small restaurant with outside seating - cool atmosphere.  Great tacos! Fast & friendly service!

Wonderful little place to get your sweet and coffee fix on! Homage Bakery offers up fresh and tasty goods daily. Whether your looking for a fresh, flaky crossiant, chewy and soft cookie, or maybe something more exotic such as creme brulee, chocolate bombe, or peanut butter pie, you'll find it here. Their coffee and chai are very tasty and they also offer lunch entrees, which I haven't tried yet, but have heard they are awesome by numerous friends and co-workers. They are a bit pricey but their treats are unique and never a let down! They are located in a cute little house with a relaxing outdoor seating area with lights to create a cozy atmosphere.Parking can be a bit tricky, but that's the only downfall. Come in and check them out whether it be by yourself or to share a treat and drink with family and friends!!
I've been here a few times but always forget to write a review. Even though I love wings, the waiting time was pretty long for a not-so-busy restaurant at the time.

I've been to NXNW a handful of times but every time I've been here it's been interesting. The crowd is very diverse and definitely all ages. It's a great spot to see a band or to hang out and either watch people dance or get down on the dance floor if that's your thing. They have a mix of musical styles including jazz, Latin, blues and all kinds of world music. A few times that I've been there they have had dancers on the floor that were definitely professionally trained but don't be shy because I've also spotted older dancers that remind me of my Dad trying to bust a move... 

Overall, it's a fun place with plenty of music and dancing to keep you entertained for an entire evening.

Today is my 3rd time visiting since it changed its name and I must say I have enjoyed my self each time...this time I ordered the 1/2 rack platter...it was delicious served with two sides...the mac and cheese side was excellent...decent beer selection too...this place has what it takes to help revitalize downtown...I will be back!

I would give Homey 3.8 rating. Pretty good hotpot.

I will be lenient with this place due to the fact that they are still operating during these times. 

I really enjoyed my hotpot experience. I love that the buffet is still open and that you were able to grab as you pleased. Most places have staff members serve the buffet line, which takes forever. 

I decided to do the hot pot and buffet experience. I quickly chose a few items from the buffet and sushi bar. I was not really impressed with the buffet line up for the cooked or sushi items. The sushi rice was mostly dry and hard. Sort of like grocery store sushi. The cooked items also appeared to have been sitting out for awhile. -- regardless I came for the hotpot and not the buffet. 

They have a vast assortment of toppings for your soup. Be sure to look for the beef before you fill yourself with all of the fish balls and vegetables. 

The sauce bar was great too. I did a combo of peanut sauce, chili oil, red fermented bean curd and white fermented bean curd and cilantro and scallions. 

The tables are designed so everyone can have their own soups. Staff was friendly. I wish they turned on more lights, the pendant lighting above the tables were off. 

Desserts at the buffet were just okay. Typical Chinese buffet desserts: jello, pudding, generic cakes, and fruit.

Would come here again, just for the hotpot. They might have an all you can eat sushi only option, but again, I think their specialty is the hotpot.

I was surprised how good it was.Didn't look so impressive on the outside but the price was cheap and the food was yummy.I liked how they didn't salt heavily on everything.I am so glad we found a local breakfast place we can go to on the weekends finally.

We eat sushi weekly here! The green goddess and the woodland roll are our fav! Edamame is always the starter. Kids love the miso soup and burger and fries.  Of any sushi restaurant in Nashville, this is the best!

Front desk girl was super nice offered a discount for writing review. Building had bad service. Asked management for wifi pass, she said she doesnt know, and if i want to finish the review, to leave the business and come back once i review it. the special was a good idea, but telling someone to leave ur business is not exactly the best way to get a promo across. Message to management: create a 2nd wifi channel for guests and have a pass for customers ready. Its 2015 . Ill change to 4 stars once this is done and 5 when i get an apology for being told to leave by management as thats just kinda odd.

DON'T USE THIS COMPANY IN Philadelphia!!!! When we dropped off our car the staff assured us that someone will pick us up, we had concerns because we had a late flight arrival. We asked the staff what we needed to do, since we had a groupon and we were told to just have our reservation number and the ticket that was handed to us. The staff said to call Wally Park when we get our luggage and someone will pick us up in 20 minutes, we were told the shuttle runs every 20 minutes after midnight. For a 1  1/2 hour we kept on calling Wally Park and was told someone would be coming, I got fed up and paid a taxi to take me to Wally Park. After getting my car I had to keep honking my horn to wake up the attendant at the booth, which she seem annoyed. The groupon did not work at this point I was exhausted and wanted to get home I paid $82 to park here. I don't believe there was a person driving the shuttle, the only person at the site was the sleeping attendant. STAY AWAY FROM THIS PLACE!!!!
Excellent pizza and a good atmosphere! Definitely worth visiting if you like Real New York style pizza.
We love this place for our dog! The grooming always turns out great, and we know that she's in good hands when we leave her for the day.

I was thoroughly impressed with Frusco's customer service. First, I reached their call center when I called after business hours, and they returned my call quite promptly even though there was no emergency. They confirmed my appointment and address before arriving. The repair men were courteous and honest - not trying to sell me anything I didn't need. They also wore covers over their shoes. They showed up close to the time they scheduled. They also took credit cards. I'm calling them again today for a different issue.
HORRIBLE. HORRIBLE. TERRIBLE. HORRIBLE. TERRIBLE. Waited 15-20 minutes in the drive thru, order was completely wrong and cold. I would like a refund. Please do not go to this KFC/Taco Bell.

This was a recommendation from a Philly native.  Such a disappointment. Just moved here and was looking for a good Philly cheesesteak.  They should start seasoning the meat before they cook it.  The onions are flavorless and the bread is nasty too.
Butchered by Saad!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! That guy has no clue how to cut hair. Sorry! Dee had to fix it! Stay away from that joint!

The bulls eye brisket is sooooo good. 
First time here but this place is awesome.
Secret bar in back and very nice staff.
Would recommend to anyone in area.
Awesome food.

CDB's Southside is sort of off the beaten path - very South Westshore Blvd. It was a pretty quiet early Monday evening and I had my choice of upstairs or downstairs. The bar is up and all high top tables so I elected downstairs (short legs were not made for high top tables :)  I had the cosmo orange, stuffed mushroom app, and gnocchi bolognese special with a house salad. It was good, better than some, not as good as others. The entree was gigantic - not a good thing when it comes to gnocchi, which is very filling to begin with. The bolognese was pretty good but just a bit lacking in flavor. It would be fine if I lived in the area but would probably not make a special trip to go back. Server Molly D was great!

It's the definition of dirty mexican food. You walk in and have a 50/50 chance of the place smelling like garbage. If it weren't for the nights that I feel like cutting some years off of my life by indulging in some extremely tasty mexican food, I wouldn't love this place. Sad but true. 

I have never had anything I didn't like, though there are some inconsistencies. My carne asada burrito has gobs of guac one time, barely any the next. I mean, you really can't expect gourmet at this type of restaurant. I go here once every three weeks or so, and occasionally at 3a :-).
I love the food, the service is wonderful, and the atmosphere is great for every and all ages. Great place!

So I've been going to this place since I was a kid. The food is always great but after my last few experiences there I will no longer be returning. The prices on the menu have not only gone up but the portions of food have gone down. I sometimes get the chicken fajitas for two and I swear there couldn't be more than 1.5 chicken breasts on the platter. And when you mention that you feel like you didn't get a fair portion suddenly the waiter/ waitress doesn't understand. 
This place just isn't the same.

There's almost no parking and it is not your typical Crawfish Boil place. It's kind of a bar mixed with a mom and pop shop mixed with a deli. They were cooking the Crawfish in a big pot outside. A bit expensive for what was offered.

We have been taking our dog here for years but as of late the service as been horrible. The straw that broke the camels back was when we picked up our Bichon/Poodle mix, she was complete shaved down to her skin. It was HORRIBLE!!! We believe they did this because they did not want to take the time to scissor cut her. And just wanted to close shop early. They told us she was gonna have "to be cut short" but did NOT say they were going to completely buzz her to the bone. This was actually during the winter time and she's only 10 pounds and she was freezing. We called and they could careless. They just offered excuses. Also I think they have treated my dog rough, her paw was hurting her for some time after. She is the worlds sweetest dog and she's older, she's 9! NEVER GOING BACK! I tell all dog owners I meet to STAY AWAY!!!!

Well pleased !  Our drains plugged on a Saturday and it was Sunday late that another drain service came out unprepared with a long enough cable.  Called Beth at Absolute Drain.  Service guys came out same day and quickly solved our problem.  Highly recommend.

Once again, I need that half star that Yelp does not allow us to have. 
The food here is a solid 4 Stars. The service is 3 Stars, so this rating is actually a 3 1/2 Stars. 

My go to's here are the Baby Spinach Salad, Grilled Salmon Filet and the Grilled Shrimp. The BSS is topped with crumbled Goat Cheese, Candied Walnuts and tossed in a Wild Berry Vinaigrette. I'm typically not a fan of walnuts and will pick them out, but not here. They have given me an appreciation of walnuts that I did not know I had. The dressing is also one of those restaurant vinaigrettes that I wish was sold in the bottle. Being a fan of Goat Cheese, I am pleased that they are generous with their crumbles. 

The Grilled Shrimp is on the menu as an appetizer, but I order it as an entree. After my Baby Spinach Salad, these beautifully grilled shrimp are just enough to satisfy my hunger. I'd say in my humble opinion that they are better then some seafood restaurants. 

My other favorite entree is the Grilled Salmon which is topped with a dill sauce. The filet is a large portion and is served with vegetable rice. I'd prefer a side of steamed vegetables, but the rice is still an except-able side dish. Again, it is a quality that you'd expect from a seafood restaurant. 

The service can be hit or miss here. To be honest, if you have a male server, it is a hit. If your being served by a female, it is definitely going to be a miss. The male servers here are more friendly, attentive to details and make it a pleasant dining experience. The lady servers are less friendly, are slow, forget things and are more interested in turning the table then creating an enjoyable dining experience. I've been here enough, to be able to make this comparison. 

Will I return? - Yes, with hopes of a male server

When: December 19, 2017
Why: Christmas with friends

Initially, I felt disheartened that there may be any good Vietnamese food here in Tucson, or that it won't fair well in comparison given that I was born and raised in the Little Saigon, CA area my whole life--where there is a plethora of Vietnamese cuisine in every corner. I WAS WRONG! The bun thit nuong tau hu ky was pretty bomb. The meat was marinated well and had a charred flavor. My two girl friends also loved their dishes as well. We came in during the lunch rush around 1:30pm, so there was about a ten minute wait, but noticed there were many free tables when we left an hour later. The servers were polite and got our orders in on a timely manner. I would definitely revisit again and again!

This is the best nail place in St. Louis, without question. They stay busy, so appointments are a good idea. Super reasonably priced and the friendliest staff ever. They do excellent work- highly recommend!

If I could give zero stars, I would! For starters, I made an appointment 2 days ago and on my way in i for a call & I was asked to arrive 15 minutes later because they were running behind- so what's the point! 

I arrived and was taken to a pedicure bowl where the water was cold and staff appeared disorganized although there were only 4 guests including myself inside! 

As I'm waiting for my service to begin a STATE INSPECTOR arrives and begins demanding licensure and identification for employees. A couple of them scattered and the employees seemed dumbfounded. The inspector began removing drains from the pedicure bowls, and taking pictures- at this point I'm sweating and I want to leave but I'm so curious. The inspector was checking everything. Customers were left mid manicure while employees scattered. An employee began passing out $20 gift cards for us to come back! One lady only had on tips and had to leave.

The guy removed my gel toe polish and cut 3 of my toes in the process & I actually bled! 

I would never return or recommend this place. It's so sad because their work with designs and crystals appeared to be good. This is what happens when you cheat on your regular salon

The big knock on STL's mass transit (or at least the light rail portion) is that it's limited in the number of areas that it serves.  While that is true, if you're near one of the stops you should take advantage of it.  It's fast, clean, new and not that expensive.

During my last trip out of town, I choose to use MetroLink to get home from the Airport.  This significally saved on expense since I didn't have to pay for airport parking.  I live in the Webster area and MetroLink only goes as close as Maplewood.  But for the $4 ticket I was able to make a free transfer onto one of the Metro Buses to complete my ride.  It took about 45 minutes to get home, but I didn't have to deal with rush hour traffic, and my hands were free to catch up on email & social media.  

It's somewhat confusing to use the bus service.  Take time to plan your route ahead of time via their website.  They have a trip planner that will give you the best route option based on your starting and ending address.

I really really wanted to love this place. Looked amazing from the outside and smelled amazing when we were finally seated.

Honestly though my Fusilli Delizia (shrimp) was kind of bland. To give them credit though the other dish we ordered was spectacular (Fusilli with chicken)

Guess I'll give them another shot and return on another occasion. 

The service was impeccable and the place itself has a nice feel. I've just had better, and definitely had better in Philly.

Great bar food and great patio! 
I've come here often and recommend the chicken tenders anytime you're here for lunch or dinner!!! With honey mustard! 
For breakfast, the  breakfast pizza is my #1 choice, scrambler is #2 choice. 
I've had great service here and I've had terrible service here. I still always enjoy it. It's a good place to go for groups, day drinking, live music, or any fried, midwestern food cravings :)

Cool ambiance, ok food, but this service is awful. They won't seat your party unless everyone is there which I understand is a policy that the hostess has to enforce but they have no tact when explaining that to guests. Everyone was rude and acted as if we were a bother rather than customers. It's a bummer. I've been to plenty of cool patios, it's not enough draw to bring me back after this experience.
It is very sad how downhill this mall went. Many vacant stores. There isn't many stores that people want to go to here... I'm shocked that the place isn't completely closed

I come here every weekend to relieve stress and do some line dancing. This place a has great atmosphere and welcomes all ages. If you're looking to have some fun and drinks I'd stop in and check their line dance classes every Friday night at 8:30pm dancing starts at 7pm until closing!

I've actually ordered something three times and the food is fantastic, the down side is service. First time they burnt my food, told me they'll fix it better next time. Second time I waited two and a half hours while dancing before getting my meal. The last time I ordered I had to hunt down a waiter and they forgot my fork and didn't bring me the check at the end of the night. Still though, I love to dance so I show up for that and have a great time!

Very dirty hotel.  The carpet and the skuffs in hallway are extremely gross. Cumon a little touch up paint goes along way. Then I get to the 1st room they gave me and it was unacceptable. Gross partied on a million times couch and the weird layout of room was very odd and very used. To say this hotel needs a face lift is an understatement! They charge u a crazy amount to park even more to valet. Then they charge for wifi. 12 dollars for 24hrs. 

So the first room was extremely dirty. Carpet, walls, curtains, vent, bathroom, all dirty. Clean the room, its your job!. I've stayed at many Sheratons and never seen a property so in disarray. So they give me another room. A little better but by no means what I'd expect from Sheraton. The long walk to the second room was more of the same. Trash, puke stains, spills everywhere.  So gross!

So I'm 60+ dollars into parking and WiFi, unsatisfactory room dirty windows on the inside. I mean forehead prints on the windows. It just seems like nobody cares. The first night it was pretty cold outside and so was the room. The heater barley works. It didn't turn off all night and it never reaches the temp I set it at. Which was only set to 76 degrees.  I really feel like I got bent over and it keeps getting worse. 

So the second nite. There was a fight or some extreme violence in the room close to mine. I tried to stay out of it. But after 45 mins I couldn't ignore it anymore. So I walk down to convey my concern and the front desk attendant says "oh this happens all the time" I will send security over there. Obviously I didn't want to get involved,  police did come and it quieted down around 2am
