 I got 1 more night here and I would do anything to get out of it. But I fear that this is probably a little better than the other old hotels nearby.  So since I'm already paid I have to stay. But I will never stay here again. And it makes me a little leary of Sheraton altogether.  Up until now I've never seen a hotel this nasty. Dust, filth, stains everywhere. Believe it or not I could say more but I think I've said enough. The ratty couch and dusty cushions seem so uninviting.  The trash up and down the hallway, numerous stains, drug bags or what looks like paraphernalia everywhere. The violence and the front desk non caring attitude was a real bummer. I plan on saying something to someone @ hotel. But I really needed to vent. And I hope this might inspire some changes. So thanks for checking out my review. I don't dwell on negative so this is probably my first negative review. But I had to get it off my chest.

This spot blew my mind, for brunch! I got the skillet with a side of avocado and eggs over easy! Gluten free  just the way I like it! You could tell the ingredients were so fresh and delicious. I would definitely head back there!

I like this place a lot.

Simple enough, if you don't read anymore of this review just know that the food is still great.

So I'd been hearing all sorts of great things about this little family run seafood place fighting change in the 'burg.  The kind of place where you always need a reservation and the lot is too small for the volume of business so they keep valets there all the time... Well, I'm not sure who the owners are now (hey, they may all be family too. Just not the original family!) but there was no need for a valet last night, I'll tell you that much. I sincerely hope there are reasons like, "Everyone is at the Shore", or, "Sunday is not a great night to go out", to explain why we were the only two people there the entire meal. I hope there isn't some  silly prejudice against the new owners, because I know old timers are like that. If that is the case then they would be missing out on great food and doing this business a great injustice. John Henry's is still great!

Spent almost $30 for a large tip and hot links.THE HOT LINKS THEY SELL ARE SCOTT PETERSON POLISHES!!! When I asked for a refund they refused saying that they were in fact hot links. Being that im from Chicago I know the difference between a hot link and a polish sausage. The ribs were awful; tender but unseasoned. Sauce was awful. The food gets no stars from me and I will not be returning due to bad customer service from the cashier and the cook.

Food was excellent once they got my order right. The place was a little dirty and the waitstaff was helpful but snooty and did not divulge the policy of their entertainment coupon discounts and daily specials. My coupon says "buy one get one free of equal or less value dinner". The menu said "kids eat free with purchase of one adult dinner". It did not say no sandwiches or apps or mention that it is only good for entrees for adults and children. I presented my coupon and the waitress said nothing when I ordered. She gave me my check and said "oh yeah, I can't give ya the discount, because you ordered burgers." WHAT???? It is an 11 dollar burger so isn't that the same price as a friggin entree? Read this fine print chickie...It is a shame this happened because the food was good. The meals are huge and tasty. I order the gyro and they forgot the Tzatziki sauce which put a damper on things because like I said the meat was really good. The waitress went to grab my plate and I shrieked with horror of the thought of a chef getting scolded for forgetting Tzatziki sauce and dumping a load of jizz on my gyro. I asked for a cup of sauce and I got about 1 tablespoon of sauce. YAY! Now that made it taste like a Gyro...NOT!!! The onion rings were off the hook and my friend's burger looked delish. It's just a shame that "little thing" get in the way and ruin a good time...and ruin a good review for great food as well. MEH.

Ordered from here twice via Uber Eats last month. First time I was quite content as the portions were generous (or rather, was the norm with hawaiian bbq places) and the price reasonable. I ordered the pulled pork plate, which has the pork, rice, macaroni salad, and a Hawaiian bread roll. Admittedly, the amount of carbs put me into a semi-food coma, but the taste was decent and they provided the sauce in a separate container which I appreciated. 

However, the second time I ordered was a week or two later, and the portions were smaller--and if they weren't, it FELT smaller as it came in a smaller container with less separation between each component. Maybe it's more eco friendly and maybe I was hungrier the second time, but I certainly felt less full after the meal even if the taste was the same. Wish I took comparison pics for reference.

For the prices the food was mediocre. I have been before for drinks and games but never to sit and eat. The drinks this time we're so weak and cost 10$. 
I got a veggie sandwich, which was good but nothing to rave about. 
Service was fine. 
Come here for the games but not for the food or drinks off their menu.

I found this place in one of the back corners in West Edmonton Mall.  They were having this huge sale and I was interested to see what they had considering I've never even heard of this store before.  Personally I don't think it's in the best location of the mall.  Anyways, I have to warn you that if you have any large bags or backpacks you may be asked to store them behind their counter; I guess they've had a lot of theft, which is really too bad.  

The look of this store was pretty cool.  They have most of their clothing items on clothing racks against the wall, but they also had a huge amount of stuff neatly folded on low stylish tables.  I really liked the stores appearance; it was very classy or stylish/modern. 

They have a huge selection of jeans for both men and women.  They have different styles and cuts and jeans with different designs on the back pocket.  If you are looking for skinny jeans this is the place to come to.  They have a whole slew of different styles, most of them I've never heard of.  They were having a sale on some of their jeans, about 30% - 60% off, but I do have to warn you that their jeans are pretty pricey.  The ones that I checked out were in the $100's or $200's range!  I was really surprised to find that they cost that much, considering some their styles were not totally unique.  Oh well, if you want cool expensive jeans you can find them at High Grade Clothing Store.

I guess the name of the store, High Grade Clothing Store, really reflects their clothes.  Their clothes are high grade at a high price.  I guess if you really want to look cool or sexy there is a price to pay.

The staff there is very friendly and nice.  They were busy helping people carry their items to the change rooms.  The guy behind the counter was also very nice and happy.

If you like to look at what the latest styles are but don't have the money to pick them up you can window-shop at High Grade Clothing Store.
Delicious tacos. Yummy margaritas. We had the chicken mole and pork cochon tacos with the habinero hot sauce. Super spicy but delicious. The bar is super chill. Great bartender. Highly recommended.

This is not authentic or well seasoned food as some have mentioned. If you have been to Greece or have been to some of the better Greek (or even mediocre) Greek restaurants in the US, you would find the food bland by comparison. They do use quality meats and fish, but if you are looking for tasty dishes, not so much. I was excited to have Greek restaurant within a few miles of my home, but I cannot recommend this one. It is expensive to boot which wouldn't bother me if the food was good. I'd love to be able to get a great Greek meal even at a higher cost. But the quality does not warrant the price.

This place is awesome! It's definitely on par with some of the better sushi places around town and you can't beat all you can eat for $19.99 (during the week). I recommend the rainbow roll and the spicy girl. This is my go to sushi place now.

Keep in mind that we are still in Indiana folks. Trying to compare this place to somewhere on the coast is a bit extreme. Nevertheless, it's some pretty decent sushi for the good ole hoosier state.

I went out to dinner it was a little busy and didn't mind waiting, but it took 40 minutes from when we sat down to get appetizers. So then 20 minutes later we get our entrees which the sandwich part was delicious I was highly disappointed by the French fries. They were the softest excuse for fries ever, like barely attempting to make them suitable, no salt, no crunch, or crispiness to them at all. We literally couldn't eat them, as seen In the pics. I assume they were understaffed or just giving lousy service that night because we were only seen like three times about drinks and order. I hope you see this review and improve your service experience and revamp how you make your fires because it really ruined the whole experience.
I go every 3rd blue moon  for  biscuits and gravy. Though they're not 5STAR biscuit & gravy and serve it on the weekends it'll do if your not wanting to venture far.

This place is ADORABLE. And the people are way nice. The ice cream is homemade and the texture is BEAUTIFUL. HOWEVER. The only reason why this is 3 stars instead of 4 is because of the actual flavors. They are all very sweet and quite similar. I tried three different chocolate flavors and they tasted IDENTICAL. There is not much depth, just a creamy sweetness with quite a bite of vanilla in every kind of flavor. I like a dark chocolate ice cream with depth, like a Belgian-type flavor, and some of the chocolate tasted like vanilla, it was so light. The red velvet cake flavored ice cream was GOOD though. And did I mention that the actual place is so freakin' cute?? If you like mild, sweet, and creamy ice cream, definitely go. If you are a chocoholic like me and you want something with more actual FLAVOR and depth, your best bet is to go somewhere else.

Long awaited restaurant after both hearing first hand and reading rave reviews. The restaurant itself is tiny- downstairs is just the bar with only about 10-12 tables upstairs. Very nice ambiance- dimly lit with a very European vibe. Service was impeccable. 4.5 stars for the food- started with oysters and shrimp cocktail- only critique here is the lack of a mignonette; the only thing that came with the oysters was a lemon wedge and horseradish (which was frozen as it had been sitting on ice with the oysters).  The table then shared the pork pasta, roast chicken, roasted potato gnocchi and the fish special (which happened to be halibut), and the collard greens. Because the kitchen forgot the collard greens, we were also given  the fried artichokes while we waited. The pastas were fantastic, particularly the pork pasta was very rich and filling. The chicken- OMG the chicken- so flavorful and juicy; would DEFINITELY recommend.  The collard greens, however, were underwhelming at best; very little flavor and  undercooked/tough in my opinion. 

Overall a great meal in a great spot- will be back again!

I can agree with my friend's review that this buffet should experience a serious refurbish, especially with the way they serve food in this joint. Sometimes they do have food out there that would just sit there for hours on end without replacement, which is troublesome because of food bourne illnesses.
Secondly, some of the clientele that attend this establishment border on the questionable. One day, I passed by this tall older man whose right ear was half missing and the rest of it had some sort of mossy growth on the rest of it. Now, I am a Christian and its not my place to judge, but I was praying to the virgin Mary that the rest of his ear was not in the salar bar. Also, this place does get very packed to the point where I sometimes worry that the fire department codes may be violated.
This establishment does try though. Their servers give great customer service for the most part and I love, love, love the chocolate fountain in the dessert section. They also offer military discounts and during Veteran's day, their buffet is free to veteran's. But, I don't see myself as being a regular patron.

Came to this place expecting a hidden gem but was disappointed. Coffee had grinds all in it, which is gross to drink through floating grinds. My eggs benedict came out chilled, not just luke warm but chilled. When I asked the server if it was supposed to be that way, I was being genuine because I've not had that experience before. He took it back, (politely) but did not apologize and just brought me a new one that was still not even warm much less hot. Odd. The table next to us had the same experience so it was not just a one off.

Okay, where to start.... It was just a horrible meal. The chicken we ordered was a greyish purple color ... It was also very tasteless... But we took it to go to see if my roommate would Think differently because I doubted myself and thought maybe I was just sick and that's why I couldn't taste it. BUT NO! My roommate took one bite and asked me why the chicken was an "off color". The weirdest chicken I've ever had. BY FAR! Two table in front of us also complained about their food. Overall, the food was very poorly made and just so weird... I don't know how else to explain it. 

The server was very nice, but understood and brought us two of the same order. He seemed super out of breath and frantic even though the restaurant was pretty empty and there were plenty of servers to go around. 

The atmosphere was super weird. Bright yellow walls and out of place decor. We got there at 6pm, but as soon as we walked in it felt like it was early afternoon because the lights were so bright. 

The drink was good though! Good bar! Also, two tables in front of us had bad experiences as well.

As a native of New Orleans, born and raised, I will tell you that I did not bother to try the food at  Hardshell Cafe. Not one place in the STL has ever really served Cajun cuisine the way its done in the mothership of Cajun cuisine New Orleans. However, this doesn't mean I didn't enjoy myself at 1860 Saloon. In fact, I liked it so much I plan on going back.

Let's start with their drink prices being ridic affordable. A Jameson shot under $10? Wha wha what??? The new game room has a pool table (wish there was at least two), darts and a cool bowling game that I am convinced someone is goig to break the screen when hitting it to make the ball travel down the lane. It also has a satellite bar with limited selections but the main bar is not far from there so its easy enough to go there for a wider variety of refreshments and bring it back down to the game room. 

Live music was playing on a Tuesday night. Good live music. Not stank cover band crap. I hope to go back soon and take in more of the 1860 Saloon then report back with an updated review.

I went to Dental Medical Arts back when they were Dental Healthcare Group and always had a great and easy experience. Last fall, it seems they sold the practice and moved all the patients over automatically. I had one cleaning there last fall, and another in the spring. Last fall it cost me $0, this spring it cost me $355.

When I called to ask what was going on, they told me it was all very simple- the previous doctor I had was in network, the new one I had was not. This was never communicated to me, and in fact I had several different doctors in the years I was with the practice, so having another new person doing my cleaning and guilting me about flossing was nothing new, and I didn't question it at the time. It was only later when the bill came in that I found out that my new doctor wasn't in network.

Why wouldn't you tell your patients that their doctor is out of network? Why would I have ever gone somewhere for a $350 teeth cleaning? 

I now see that other people have left similar reviews of the same thing happening to them, so how dare they act as if it's all some misunderstanding. They told me they "thought they had been clear about it with everyone", but obviously I and several others who reviewed this practice in the past few months have had similar experiences.

Please, if you don't want to get screwed over by shady business practices and crappy excuses, don't book an appointment with Dental Medical Arts.

I had my order taken to my vehicle. They were prompt, friendly, and masked. I enjoyed my iced mocha; it was perfect. The croissant breakfast sandwich was tasty and done just right. We also got a variety of sweets to share and enjoy midday.  The tiramisu is traditional and, an average helping/price, and taste is on point - same with their cannoli. I took one star off because of the cookies. We got the sugar and the chocolate chip. Sad to say that they were very dry, like in-the-box-grocery-store-cookies dry. Some people may like a dry cookie but I expect a bakery cookie to be fresh and moist. However, they don't claim that their cookies are moist. Perhaps I should have asked. All the menu says is, "Large Marble Chocolate Chip Cookie" and "Large Sugar Cookie."  I'd go back for more specialty coffee drinks and to try other menu items like the NY cheesecake. Quite a few things were out because it's just a few days before Xmas.

The only thing this place has going for them is the location.  There are no other ranges in the city.  

Things that suck here:
No head shots on target.  Preventable maintenance reasons.  
Can't push target back past 7 yards without proving marksmanship (which is easy).
Parking lot is crappy.

This place is acceptable if: 
You live in the city
You dont bring friend (high rates for sharing lane)
Don't bring gf (filthy)

It was my first time in here and I didn't know what to expect. My expectations were surpassed. The ladies who work here  are amazing. Helpful, nice, warm, and what an inviting place to shop, with great clothes and discounts. I highly recommend this store to sell your clothes to, as well as buy great fashion from! I'll be back for sure!

I joined my mom here for lunch and I'm sad to say it was very average at best. Our started with some cocktails of the day. I don't remember the exact names but they were acceptable. We didn't order seconds but we didn't send them back. They were fine. We shared a soup and salad. The salad was recommended by the waitress. Apparently this is their best seller. It was something I could have made at home. Canned olives, canned hearts of palm, giant chunks of tomatoes out of portion to the rest of the salad. Just very mediocre honestly. The soup of the day was good but I'm struggling to remember exactly what it was so obviously not that memorable. I almost gave them a two star review for that depressing salad but the rest of the meal was at least average so I settled with a three star...barely.

I don't know...I don't see why everyone loves this place so much. 

I grew up in a Chinese-Vietnamese household so I know what good Chinese food is. I think this restaurant has decent food at a cheap price. But at the end of the day, their food is Chinese-American food. It's not authentic Chinese food (which is fine) but their food, in general, is so greasy and have the same aromatic flavors. 

This place is fine for and cheap and quick bite, but please don't say this is the best Chinese food you've ever had because Chinese food is so much more than this. Chinese food has fresh vegetables that balance out the savory umami flavors for the meat dishes. Some dishes are refreshing, others are creamy and sweet. They use beef that taste and feel like real beef. Go to Chinatown in Philly to eat real Chinese food. It's worth the experience. 

If you do go here, I do like their egg drop soup. You should get that.

I have been going to this car wash for a good 8 years now and have watched it progressively get WORSE and worse.  It's at a low point right now. I'm not a angry customer or anything - I'm just over paying $14 for a terrible job. It's basically the machine they have does all the work and then they wipe wet towels around afterward. No care shown for your car at all and the management is terrible - speak broken english - rely on their son to talk. If there is a problem, they just get defensive. Failure of a location, honestly.

I was very happy to see someone open up a new pizza business here because it's right in my neighborhood off of N Himes Ave. I 've been here 3 times now & while I do hope they are successful, I feel that if they don't make a couple of changes, they will not be around for very long.

1st off, their pizza is REALLY good but both times I got their pizza, it was a 14" cheese pizza, and I feel they are a bit light handed on the sauce & cheese which is a shame because both have great flavor. It costs almost $13 for a 14" cheese pizza while nearby competitor's are less expensive & not so light on the cheese & sauce. This place only has two sizes of pizza...
14" & 18" pizzas. Their 18" pizza prices are more in line with their competitors but the 14" pizzas are not. Note to Hanks....if you are going to charge more you should give more.

If I was basing my rating on the pizza alone it would be 4 stars but I feel it could be 5. What makes this place a 2 star place, at best, is because their sub was probably one of THE WORST I have ever had at a pizza place. The sub roll, or I mean hot dog roll, was about 8 to 10" long and an Italian sub was close to $10!!!! Total rip off!!! That sub roll might work for your BBQ joint but if you are going to make subs right, get a different sub roll because I did like that you DIDN'T skimp on the meats on the sub, there just was't room for anything else after you put the meats on the roll. If other places in this area can get Amoroso rolls perhaps you should look into replacing the extra large hot rolls you are using. 

Lastly, this place is super clean & they have cable TV's to watch sports or whatever. I really hope they will make some changes that could make a big difference between success or failure. BTW Hanks, if you want to charge & get top dollar for your subs, build 'em & make them like Laspadas in the Orlando area does.

I think the people on here love them selves. All morning its all about them. I cant believe Mike J. Keeps his job. Apparently they think hes great. Him and his facial expressions. Please. He always walks into the center of a story. Hes gotta be number 1. I hate watching this channel, they dont tell the news. Again its all about Mike. I think hes an azz. They are supposed to tell the news, they are not entertainers

The restaurant is quite expensive which is fine however, it's not so fine when the entrées are very small and not tasty. We had a pasta with marinara sauce and added chicken (made off the menu), a vegetarian risotto, a vegetarian antipasto and a kids meal pasta. Both of the entrées were just no good, lacked flavor and there was nothing different to remember it by. I already mentioned that the quantity of the entrées was small, in fact the kids pasta is the same size and adults pasta. The complementary bread was quite good, but all other items just didn't have anything special to remember them by.  The ambiance is lovely, everything was very clean and polished, the seating chairs are big and comfortable made of white leather, the waiters are dessed in suits and the bar is thriving. But since the food itself was not satisfying we regretted coming to Armani's.

A few weeks back, I drove up to Santa Barbara to go wine tasting with a group of friends. I should correct that our destination was Solvang. We left Los Angeles a little later than we wanted to and arrived at Santa Barbara a little after noon. Our group stopped in on Santa Barbara's State Street for some lunch. We wandered around looking for the perfect place to dig and after negotiating our way through the tourist, shops, restaurants and after much debate, we found the Pierre Lafond Wine Bistro. We all decided to stroll in. 

First thing I ordered - coffee! I have to say that the portion size for the coffee was quite large. But very good. It was a really delicious cup of coffee. 

Having missed having a breakfast. I noticed that they were still serving brunch/breakfast food. I decided to go with the Bistro Benedict. I'm a fan of poached eggs, English muffins, ham (or pretty much any pig product). It was delicious.....hollindaise sauce was rich, creamy. It was perfect. Went well with the coffee and was the perfect breakfast for a lunch in Santa Barbara.

The service was good. I recall our server being very attentive and friendly, to a bunch of tourists like us. Cause, yeah - we had tourism written all over us. I also liked, not just the decor - but the furniture. We (there's six of us) were seated at a thick glass table. The decor was modern and clean. Yet, I felt very comfortable eating there.  

And yes, I see the irony reader - oddly enough.....none of us got wine, seeing that this a wine bistro. But in our defense - we were heading to Solvang for wine tasting.

Sandbar owner/staff and food, especially "Happy Hour" specials, make this place an exceptional place to hang out in New Hope. Outdoor seating (when weather permits) and a cool round bar in the center of the restaurant are highlights along with a pool table upstairs. Try to catch your own lobster dinner in the lobster tank ($2 for the try and if you catch it, the staff will cook and serve it to you for free). Each bartender has their own specialty but have always found the mojitos to be superb. Most food is delicious here but appetizers and burgers are top notch. See you there! Enjoy

A bunch of us visited a week ago.  The ambience and the food is fabulous.  It is pricey and the portions are small but it is well worth it once and a while.  The coconut and lime sorbet was so good.  I can't wait to go back before it's gone.  The toffee also brings me back again and again!

Of all the Starbucks I visit to work on music promotion, I am at this one the most. It is close to the Musician's Rehearsal Center on Dunn Circle. 
Its a new building. Seems clean. Bathrooms are okay.

It can get a little crowded certain times of the day. 

The staff that I interacted with, were friendly.
Orders were served in a timely manner.

This a great place to go to enjoy the French inspired patio    Although I've been a few times and the food never really shines. The portions are small. Most of the time my items have been overlooked or under seasoned. My least favorite of this restaurant group.
This is a great place for sushi. Its small and family owned. It does take awhile to get your sushi, but so well worth the wait! I will be coming here more often.

August 31st I walked into Mattress firm and presented my situation to the John the salesman.  He seemed to totally understand my immediate need for a mattress.  My husband was coming home with a broken hip and needed a bed to sleep in.  I found the right bed for a good price and the only problem was that delivery would take 9 days or September 9th. I was told it was due to the high volume during labor day sales.  Well Irma decided to hit Florida on that weekend.  The delay was excusable for a reschedule on Se ptember14th .  Now the next call on the 13th with the same excuse of the hurricane delaying delivery until September 19th.  Not happy here I continue to explain that my handicapped husband is sleeping in a recliner waiting for this bed that has already been paid for.  Then her comes the 18th and another call blaming the hurricane for the delay and delivery would be the 23rd.  After calling the salesman John I received numerous reasons why the bed was not delivered,  it was Serta's fault because the mattresses were twin xl and then it was the bed frame company Mantua's fault and a delay in manufacturing.  I went on line and found that my Mantua frame was in stock in many places and ready for delivery.  John the salesman continue to say that there was nothing he could do or no one he could call.  As I write this I'm waiting for the 22nd and another cancellation.  If this happens I will be cancelling my order and going elsewhere.  I can not explain the pain my husband has gone through waiting for this bed.  I would never go back to mattress firm and have advice family, friends and coworkers to look elsewhere for honest reliable service.  Mattress Firm gets the booby award for unhappy customer.

Went in to get a Bluetooth deck. Made a appointment at 9:00am the next day. He said it shouldn't take long to install a deck, and it would be done in the afternoon. Time passes starting to get worried,  call them around 3:00pm earlier to see what's happening, said car wasn't finished. Called them around 6:30 still not done, said they would call me when it's done, Turns out one of his installers got sick and called in, and they had a huge job to do. Thinking he made it up I said fine fine, starting to get aggrovated. So it's 8:30 at nite and they closed at 7 they call and I finally pick my car up. IMO they should of installed mine first then do the big job and have him wait past 8.  I should have trusted the other reviews. Unreliable.

Stopped by on Thanksgiving Eve, it was crowded but not packed with college kids looking for cheap swill (a good sign).  I had been in before but this time the beer menu was really cooking.  

Anyhow, go to this place, plain and simple.  Its not the best location, but once you get there the food and beer are fantastic.  

Stan can usually be found talking to folks when he's not cooking, but you want him to be cooking.  I'm not sure what polish grandmother's estate he robbed for the recipe for his perogies recipe but man - are they a must order.  The armored fries are also a delicious take on poutine.  From there, you have a great take on bar food, but done a little nicer than a normal pub.  The wings, burgers and sandwiches are all really good.  And things are priced well.  

Alright, now, you also should take an uber and prepare to drink a ton of very very good beers.  You're not going to find the trendiest new take on a wheat IPA that's popular this month.  You're going to find beers made to taste the way they are meant to be taste.  They just tapped the King George - that tastes like an english ale right out of the tap.  The Burton's best is also delicious.  I talked to the bartender who told me that they invited the community to bring their home grown hops then made beer out of it.  Such a cool idea and a way to involve your neighbors.  

Alright, I'm done.  Go here, eat and drink, best in the area.

We have actually been avoiding this place because the reviews said the service wasn't great/takes forever but we decided to stop on a Monday late afternoon. We thought the tacos were great! Fresh tacos, are they full blown authentic..mostly not but they were good fresh tacos and good margaritas. We've been looking for a place downtown st Pete with fresh tacos and margaritas and are all a bust. I think this is a great addition to the area and will definitely be a regular stop after a beach day so I hope service continues to keep up. My only negative thing to say is that our chips were mostly over cooked with the queso. Half were chewy chips but not big enough of a deal to not come back. Winston was our server and was very attentive, checked on us just enough but not too much. He was great :)

The cleaning job was mediocre.  They are much more expensive than other services I have used.  I bought a 5 session voucher and they came 3 times and now say they used up all the 5 sessions because they stayed longer on two occasions.  NOT TRUE.  Stay away from this company.  They want $120 for a bi-weekly cleaning of my 1,300 sq ft house with 1 1/2 tiny bathrooms and 2 bedrooms!  Another company who does better work is asking $75 for bi-weekly.

After undergoing renovations, this location is simply not the same.

The new staff seem to be more engaged in their own conversations instead of helping patrons... 

Also, the availability of stock isn't the best. The salmon spread is almost always out of stock whenever I'm around, even at 8 in the morning. How can this be?

Dave gave me a great haircut at fair price. I used to go downtown to Groom, but after reading the reviews for Dave's here on Yelp decided to give this neighborhood barbershop a shot. I'm so happy with his work I've already scheduled my next appointment. Dave's shop is a welcome environment complete with cold drinks in the fridge. He went above and beyond to accomodate me in order to get me in for my first appointment after - life - happened. He was able to breeze through my difficult (straight, pointy) hair and shape it into the classic style I need for work. I'm very glad to welcome Dave to the Wissahickon (Manayunk, Roxborough) area and plan to do my part to keep him here. If you need a haircut near the Wissahickon Valley, you definitely should schedule an appointment at Dave's.

Come to Mother's restaurant to get some real home-cooked Southern dishes! After watching Adam Richman from Man vs. Food pay a visit and eat a juicy Po-Boy - I just had to try it! YES - there is ALWAYS a line and its always LONG. But suck it up - because it goes pretty fast. I'd say we had at least 30 people in front of us and we were in within 15 min. Why? The restaurant inside is HUGE (self-seating) and the line to order your food inside is basically the only bottle neck.

--Famous Fredi's Special ($11.75)--
MUST TRY! This is what Mother's is known for! This sandwich is basically a huge pile of meat all placed between a french roll (which is also dipped in meat juice)! SO hearty and satisfying! What kinds of meat you ask? A variety: black ham, thick slices of roast beef, and of course the DEBRIS! When they described it on Man vs. Food - I was a little disturbed. The bits and pieces of left over meat - how can that be good? IT IS! TRUST! Its like a juicier version of pulled pork - soft and slow cooked! Make sure you get a stack of napkins - because this sandwich can get MESSY. With the meat juice, shredded cabbage, globs of mayo + mustard, gravy, and of course the layers of meat, you will have to wipe with each bite. All the meat went really well with the tangy and yet creamy sauces - I went to carnivore heaven!

--Jerry's Omelete ($11)--
After feeling a little sickly from all the heavy meals in NOLA - I ordered an egg white omelete in hopes of giving my poor stomach a break. The omelete was filled with bell pepper, turkey, red onions, and mushrooms. This was the blandest plate of food ever! I had to salt + pepper with each bite! Not only that, the topping to egg proportion was too little and the entire plate was covered in grease! I did enjoy the taste of grits - which I'm not used to! Felt like home in my belly! :) I also got to try Mother's famous jam with my toast.. but all in all, this is a dish to avoid.

--Combination Platter ($19)--
Yes we're tourists and we want to taste everything. So what better way than to order the combo platter which comes with Jerry's Jambalaya, etouffee, red beans, vegetables, and potato salad!? This plate is a blessing because you do get to try so many dishes - but after tasting these same dishes elsewhere in NOLA, I can tell you this combination platter may not be worth it. A lot of the food @ Mother's just tasted really mushy, goopy, and a little bland. The etouffee was one shining star on the dish - which was very flavorful. I think one downfall of this dish is that all these dishes are plopped onto the same tiny plate and the flavors get lost in the mix!

--Mae's Gumbo Cup ($6)--
A small little cup of gumbo - which tasted homey and less salty than other places. But I honestly had a hard time differentiating the different gumbos at the variety of NOLA restaurants. Meh - nothing special.

Tips
1) Self seating area is much bigger in Mother's next door
2) Soft drink ($2.25) - unlimited refills
3) FREDI'S SPECIAL!
Excellent steak and the bird sanctuary is relaxing  to watch while dining near the windows. Service was excellent. Recommend

Great food great service!! Food comes fast.  Wings are incredible.  Meatballs are great. Pizza isn't my style but still everyone i was with enjoyed it. 
Overall fantastic place with fantastic value.

I love this place- it's right on the beach, it's colorful and lively. The drinks are good and the food is fresh. The few times I've been here the service is also very good. 

Beautiful beach view, it's on the louder side but it really makes you feel like your on vacation- highly recommend!

I pretty much wasted an entire day waiting specifically for services at my home especially because I needed the inside sprayed. According to the email I recieved from green shield stating services between 12:30-2:30pm. I then finally call the office around 320pm...green shield said that my scheduled services had been canceled "because a technician had an emergency". This is strike 2 for them emergency calls. I never recieved notifications regarding this matter. One more strike and I'm terminating my services, to my knowledge I pay monthly, no contract. Ridiculous having to wait and waste a day. Not even a courtesy call!

Somehow I keep coming back to this place, because it's so darn convenient--I walk by it on my way to work.  And then I somehow end up inside. I blame it on the electric projectors I teach with, which project my fingers, GIANT, in front of the class when I point to a line in a handout. And then walking home...sigh. Today was the most recent time I was suckered in.

Relaxing? Not so much. The last few times I've been here, they've been blasting "Keeping up with the Kardashians," in the main area, so I've had to listen to the Kardashians fighting with each other. I think today's argument (very dramatic) was about one sister not wanting a mayonnaise mask. 

All the people that work here are very sweet, however. There's one older woman in particular who is very kind.

You get a (small) discount on a pedicure if you have a Cat Card, so $20 total, pre-tip.  Today I stopped in just for polish on my toes, and was charged $10.

Make sure to bring cash, because they charge you extra for using a credit card.

Traditional northern China I believe cuisine. Definitely different from anything else I've tried like this. Very good portions and the steamed meat buns are super tasty. The pot stickers are great but I recommend getting them in the soup as they needed a sauce. I didn't get the soup. I also had the spicy ground pork dish. Again a bit dry but I added soy sauce and chili oil to compensate. Truly a unique experience for me and the food was fresh and flavorful. If you're looking for a new flavor profile and are interested in actually tasty Chinese food not the delivery type or food court stuff I recommend this place. And it also speaks volumes when you walk in and it's all full of people from China. We were the only 2 Caucasians in the restaurant and all of the other customers were really happy to give us their favorite dish recommendations. This place is really popular. There were people waiting for tables for up to 30 minutes and  no one seemed to mind. The food speaks for itself.

Nothin' fancy, fer sure. But, it's a place to meet with friends, have a nice, cold beer, maybe some wings and enjoy the boats that cruise along the river. 

Service was quick tonight, even though it was very busy. My beer was cold. I was relaxed with friends.

Mission accomplished.

My wife and had dinner for the first time here. She had the filet and I had the ribeye. She enjoyed her dinner, but my ribeye was a little lacking. It was not very thick, but for the price it should have been. The restaurant was clean and a bit small. They were busy so service was slow, no big deal since we were not in a rush. The food server was pleasant and so was the rest of the staff. I had dessert, apple crisp. It was OK, but the coffee was terrible. For the price we paid we felt the overall quality should have been better. We will try the lunch service an re-evaluate later.

I had been anticipating trying Nada since it had opened just a short while ago - I'm a sucker for Mexican food, the endless instagrams and yelp pics of the queso had my mouth-watering, and it always seems poppin' on a weekend night. 

I'm not gonna lie, after a long day trip to Kentucky and back, in the rain, I was NOT feeling like doing but being in my bed, but after a few more looks at the Yelp pics of Nada, I convinced myself to make it to Nada in time for my dinner reservation. I was not disappointed. 

On 8:30 on a Saturday night, the place was packed, with all the tables and booths filled, and people crowding around the bar waiting for drinks. Nada has a very young + lively feel to it, and seems to be a great place to celebrate a birthday, or before a night out on the town. 

Despite the business, we were promptly seated at our reservation time. Our server, Cayman (sp.), was an A+ employee. Throughout the evening he was attentive, constantly refilling our drinks and checking up on us, and making loads of friendly conversation. He was very knowledgeable of the menu and was happy to answer any questions we had. 

We started off with two cocktails to start: 

NADARITA // The house margarita, which was very refreshing. 
It tasted fresh, without any of that syrupy-mixer taste to it, and they were pretty strong. After my 2nd one I was definitely feeling it. 

CHILE-MANGO // A fun play on the margarita! This 'rita had habanero and mango flavors, and a chili salted rim. I didn't care for it (I don't like mango), but the boyfriend really enjoyed this.

To start, of course we had to get the queso - it's basically why I insisted we go here. It was just as glorious as I expected it to be. The QUESO GRINGO was brought to our table in a copper pot, and poured into the serving bowl over a mixture of black bean puree, pico de gallo, a habanero salsa, and some peppers. The queso is not made with traditional Mexican cheese, but instead a smoked gouda - very unique!! The mixture at the bottom of the bowl added texture to an otherwise one-dimensional dish, and tons of flavor from the peppers. We mixed it all up in the bowl, and each bite was full of flavors - the smokey and creamy gouda, the spicy peppers, and the black bean puree. The chips were thin and crispy fried tortilla chips, and were the perfect salty accompaniment to the queso. GET THE QUESO. I repeat, get the queso. And don't be embarrassed to take a boomerang of that cheeeeesy pour.

After the queso, we each decided on two tacos (2 baja fish, carne asada, pork belly), and then two sides to share. Cayman told us that the elote was really good, but that the cracked potatoes were heavenly, their best seller, and being referred to as "nada fries". He could not have supported these potatoes more.

The tacos are small, like many other reviewers are saying, but not too small I don't think. The perfect size to try many things. The tortillas were good, and we really loved the carne asada tacos & and the baja fish tacos.

BAJA FISH // Beer-battered mahi-mahi with cabbage, pickled onions, pico de gallo, and a chipotle crema. The fish was perfectly fried and crispy, and the crema added a smooth component to the dish, while the tomatoes and onions added acidity. Great taco.

MU SHU PORK BELLY // This was our least favorite taco. While the pork belly had good flavor from the hoison sauce, it was way overcooked. This taco wasn't even finished. 

CARNE ASADA // I don't normally order steak tacos, but I was feeling kooky, and I'm glad I went rogue on this one. The slices of skirt steak were topped with a tomato salsa and a crema, and were delicious!!! I loved this taco.

WOOD-GRILLED CORN ELOTE // Our favorite of the two sides. The corn is served on a layer of chili mayo and topped with cotija cheese. The corn has a nice grilled flavor, and the mayo is easy to mix around into the bowl to give every bite that fun punch! Licked this bowl clean.

SWEET CHILI-GLAZED CRACKED POTATOES // These potatoes were alright. I had the highest of expectations for these after hearing our server talk about them like Garth Brooks had harvested the potatoes himself. The flavor was definitely there. The sweet chili-glaze was everything I wanted, and was complimented well by the cooling crema. But the potato pieces were too big, leaving crispy exteriors, but loads of potatoey interiors. The best parts were the little pieces in the bowl that were crispy throughout. These would be a lot better if the potatoes were cut smaller, to get rid of that middle-of-a-potato bland texture and taste. 

Will I go back? Yes. Nada is a fun, lively spot for happy hours or weekend dinner plans. The food is all really good, and so are the drinks. A great spot to go on a date or with girlfriends.
Great service and very reasonable prices. Never waited for a table. #9 with an iced green tea.

I totally forgot how overpriced Bay area food was.  The portions here are probably twice as large for the same price, and the food is pretty good (though the scrambled eggs were kinda neglected and came out tasting a little more like fried eggs).  They do use some amazing cream with their fruit pancakes though.

Very friendly service as well, and kind of a fun bear theme.  Was a nice solid breakfast.

On ANOTHER occasion, "The Bowl" from Nama gave me food poisoning!!! This is the FOURTH time this has happened... NOT acceptable. Nama looks nice from the outside but the food is terrible, the service is terrible, and the management is rude.
We picked up soup from Win Wah tonight. It is pretty good. As others mentions staff of not all the friendly, but for quick take out the place is fine.

Closed for a private event. If they'd picked up their phones or had a prerecorded message when we called (or had it posted online - it is 2018 after all) it would have saved us a bunch of hassle when we showed up and couldn't get in.
Good sized portions. Not to big or to small. That's a wide variety of flavors on the menu.

We both had tasty Reuben sandwiches, which we ordered at the counter.  A waiter brought our order to the table.

These sandwiches were really thick, and the Kosher meats were quite lean.  So much healthier than the greasy fat laden Kosher style we've been experiencing here in Clearwater.

You can help yourselves to free soft serve ice cream after your meal.  That is a true bonus.

Enjoy!

I truly hate writing sad reports.  First couldn't find parking. Parking is very limited.  Second was told we needed reservations. Ah. There was plenty of seating. Probably because of no parking.  Received menus and water quickly. Ordered food. Waited 90 minutes for food. Had to ask for bread. Was getting ready to leave and food finally arrived after asking why the delay.  Food was good. Large portions.  Don't think I will return. Just not impressed.

If I could give this place zero stars I would. I will from this point on boycott this BWW location. I don't know about any other BWW location but what I do know about this location is that they are racist. 
I, a black woman went last night to this location with my white male friend. Red flags immediately went up when we walked in and they seated us in the worst possible place in the "house". The table and two of the chairs were pushed up against the window and they expected us to just sit there. The service was extremely slow and when it came time to get our food, they did NOT give us any plates to eat off of. We had noticed everyone else seemed to be in a better seating arrangement and had plates (there were better and open seats). 
We realized we were being discriminated against so we left (I asked for boxes). Later when I went to eat the wings, I noticed the quality of of the food was terrible. Barely any sauces were applied and the flavors we requested weren't even. I also found a long white hair in my food!
This was the most blatant form of racism I have experienced from any restuarant.

Monday Tuesday 8.99 pizza pie deaa are great. Best pizza in Lutz! (after pizza sumprema and amici piza in Wesley chapel)  definitely try this small family business shop. Much better than your franchise shops. Lot of dining area too. Check this place out. Love the i95 sign.

A shout out to Glenda who has helped my husband and I a number of times. She is very helpful , very friendly and should be acknowledged by her superiors. 
Thanks Glenda!  We think you're the best!

The last couple of times Ive been to this location Ive experienced poor management skills and understaff unhappy employees. As well as a very limited choice of chips. The taste of the sub I always get has also change somewhat with little or no explanation? Still good food and the best sub around, but this store location needs to straighten up or they will soon be out of business too.
The corn beef Reuben n the potato leek soup is good!
Best fresh squeezed OJ.  Everything family ordered was delicious!  One of the best breakfast places in Reno.

Do not depend on this taxi service. I scheduled the taxi 3 days prior, plus called to confirm 3 hours prior to pick up. All attempts to have this service to be on time has proved to be useless.  I have now missed my appt. because of their poor service practices.

I have visited this Panera by Vanderbilt several times because it is convenient. Never again. If you can not do better about your customer service and how you handle loyal customers, then that's fine. I was disappointed by the response of the cashier not willing to correct the incident only to say "the manager did it". Come on now, just fix the problem. Panera you have got to do better!! What you served today was not worth the container it came in,

I was running out of time to get a bridesmaid dress altered and decided to come here instead of my usual tailer because it is more convenient.  The dress only needed to be hemmed and that was fine.  A little more expensive than I anticipated ($55) but fine. Except that when I put the dress on for the wedding, I noticed a small hole towards the bottom that had been singed--almost like a cigarette burn. It didn't smell like cigarettes,  so my guess is that she cut it a little and then singed it to keep it from fraying. The fact that she never mentioned this to me when I picked up the dress feels dishonest to me. So next time I will be sure to leave plenty of time to go to my usual tailer.

Unfortunately I got food poisoning after eating an impossible croissanwich this morning. About an hour after eating I became really ill and started having stomach cramps maybe they didnt heat everything thoroughly enough but I suffered for whatever food safety precautions they didn't follow.

Wanted to put a better review.. the staff was nice but it took over 2 hours to do a oil change with no line.... that's just insane and when I went to complain the guy just had my paperwork sitting there

Food is always good, but the service could be a lot better. I always feel rushed. The girl making my food crushed my tortilla into a ball and wrapped it in foil. The girl at the register threw all my shit in a bag and didn't even smile. The over all atmosphere sucks.

This was my second venture to this Germantown hotspot. If I was disappointed the first time then the second solidified my opinion. Look, I get it, it is a very cool spot. Big old warehouse with high ceilings and cool plants and super trendy people everywhere and a really cool patio. But if I'm going to pay a nice price for my food I expect it to impress. 

For our appetizers, we had bacon-wrapped quail and sausage-cheddar biscuits. The biscuits were good, but nobody needs to pay ten bucks for four Sister Schubert sized biscuits. The quail was less good than your standard Buffalo Wild Wings chicken wing. 

I had a cup of white bean soup and the duck'am for my main course. I was rather excited as I rarely have lobster or duck. Needless to say, they were a let-down. The duck was rubbery and the local ham that it was wrapped in left much to be desired. The soup was quite lacking in flavor which is hard for me to understand when it has lobster in it. The lobster was either of such poor quality or so overpowered by whatever else was in the soup that you never really got to enjoy the meat. 

Hey, maybe the third time's the charm. But I doubt it.

May I give this amazing restaurant 10 stars?!?!?! The original Columbia restaurant in Ybor historic area of Tampa made us feel like we were in Spain. The service was helpful and perfect all the way around. We started with Sangria (not too sweet, perfect) and ended with a Cuban coffee. We had their specialty salad, shrimp and scallops which were all amazing. Don't miss this wonderful opportunity and historic Tampa.

Gross abounds. Totally average rooms, bathroom looks a little tacky - the extra toilet paper is front and center in the open counter storage cubbies. The towels are big though. The pool area is right beside the parking lot, which is open to the reception area. It's a really weird set up, and hard to describe.

No mini bar, no room service, and it's close enough to Bourbon that you can smell the wafts of urine. The pictures online are not so true to life. In short, spare yourself the disappointment.

Great lunch spot for a slice, coke and fries.  I have been known to go here a couple of days a month for a slice.  Service is always great and they make sure everything is OK a few times while you're there (even when you just get a slice).  Great job, guys.  Keep it up!

Cafe Poca Cosa is unique all around. The food is magnificently delicious. The owner, Susana, is the older sister of the Lil' Cafe Poca Cosa owner, and she also greets you with a warm and welcoming hug. 

The food is considered "nuevo Mexico" not as in New Mexico the state but literally a newer version of traditional Mexican entrees. The dessert is also one of a kind and they have vegan options as well. 

They are open Tuesday-Saturday for lunch and dinner. Lunch often requires no reservations but for dinner it is a MUST. They are often booked a week in advance as they are busy most evenings, regardless of the season. Speaking of seasons, they are often closed for Christmas, New Years and Easter for extended days (7+ days). 

The ambiance is soft and gentle with Latin music as background noise. The bathrooms are immaculate and have one of those nice sinks where the water seductively flows down the horizontal drain. The decor throughout the restaurant is unique in that it is colored with deep reds, bronze, and other early tones. The lighting is semi-romantic and put anyone in a relaxed, pleasant mood.

We were greater with fantastic service... the atmosphere was fantastic,   The servers and hosts polite and effective;   Everything for the people on the floor was quite enjoyable.   What bothered us, and the main reason we won't be back, was the way our food was selected and cooked.   The filet side of our tiny-filleted porterhouse was less than desirable, and well overcooked what was requested.   Once the server noticed we were displeased, she must have figured that she lost her tip and started to avoid us... oh well because we were quite happy with her...

Great place. A FOOD restaurant. Clean place and great staff. Could have better drink specials for what it is. Other than that love this place. We go here every other Sunday. And have never had a bad time.

Blah blah blah blah.  Casino casino.  Same old same old.
Casinos always have people in them that look like they haven't slept in weeks and are smoking cigarettes that have three inches of ash on them.
THOUGH,
my mom did win $1500.00 here tonight on a penny machine.
I guess that's worth two stars.
If you don't mind taking money from the devil.

I went in with a blood pressure spike, back and jaw pain and a history of DVT and PE.  Everything was quick and efficient getting checked in, but then it all came to a stop.  I was there six hours and never saw a Dr. until the end.  Heck, I barely saw a nurse.  I did have bloodwork, an ekg, a chest xray (all done in the first 30 minutes), and thanks to the nurse, a CT scan to rule out a PE.  After five hours, and me asking where the Dr. was, I finally saw a D.O. who said everything was great and as long as my BP looked good, I could go.  It took another hour to get a nurse to take my BP.  It was not good...168/91, but the Dr. said I could go anyway and to go see my Dr. the next day.  Instead, I looked up my bloodwork on the hospital portal and saw four tests were not ok and all of them pointed to DEHYDRATION!  I drank five bottles of water and by that evening, my blood pressure was back to 112/68 which is normal for me.  All it took was Google and water.  If they had hung a bag of saline, it would have been fixed.  I already had a needle in my arm.   If they can't figure this out, what are they doing with life threatening emergencies?

Update:  Largo Med billed almost 18K for this worthless visit.  Thank God I have Medicare.  This is why we need single payer in this country!  It's obviously working in countries like Canada, England, Sweden, etc.  So why can't we do it?  Oh that's right...Republicans.

Grits gross
Bacon gross 
Sausage gross 
The potatoes were ok 
The water was the best thing I had, don't waste your money !

When I first moved to downtown Indianapolis I was looking for a dry cleaner and found Deering. The first few years were good. The were friendly and a fair price.  The last 6 or so months the service here has declined. 
First issue with the recent service has been the lack of oversight to make sure services are performed correctly. I ask that my flat front dress pant be done with no creases. On several occasions they have creased the pants. I have taken them back to have them corrected and they do press the pants over instead of laundering  again and the crease marks remain. 
The second strike is they have lost a brand new shirt. For a week they have promised to locate the shirt and have failed. On several occasions they promised to call with updates and do not follow through. Still waiting for the issue to be resolved and no contact from the manager who is always away or not available. Her promises to return calls are also not fulfilled. 
Be warned this cleaners has taken a turn for the worst. Find another cleaner in downtown Indianapolis before taking anything to Deering.

My mom and I went around 1:30pm on a Tuesday and waited about 40 minutes, not too bad because you can get drinks at the bar and wait out on the benches against the water. The piña colada drink is rly good (I think it's called their punch?) and when we got seated we ordered the lobster mac n cheese and grilled tofu with mango sticky rice and coconut curry (mom's vegetarian). The lobster Mac and cheese was truly amazing, so much lobster and truffle flavor. The view was amazing especially at that time (we tried going for sunset night before and it was too cloudy to see). The waitress put in the wrong order for us so she waived the tofu and gave us fries while waiting, so they have great service as well!!

So I'm still agreeing with my first review, but I just have to add something. The parking over the year was horrific. It wasn't the fact that parking was a block away. That was annoying but not a big deal. It was the fact that I pay for a spot, with a number, that says reserved, that says if someone else parks there they'll get booted or towed, and literally 25% of the time there was someone was parked in my spot. And you know what happens to them? Nothing. Not a thing. I would have people parked in my spot for a week, and no one, not the garage and not OPOP, would do anything. Nor would they refund my money that I paid for the parking spot. It was bad enough to negate all the positives about the place. I wouldn't suggest to anyone moving here unless you're completely ok with people going to Schnucks, the game, their friend's house, parking and leaving you parkingless, and no one giving a ... about it.

The ole bait and switch.... Don't be surprised if the jar you open when you get home has drastically sub par buds to what is on display. Gave away my first time customer free 1/8 and dealt with smoking the other 2 strains I purchased, which all were early girls and smelled/tasted like popcorn and hay. Boo to you Reef. You have the pleasant staff and great atmosphere to make it happen, just knock it off with the substandard product. Quality control possibly. We are medical patients expecting quality for our dollar, not dumb school kids spending the lunch money our parents gave us.

Decided to try Ruby Tuesday's before going to movies.  Just looking for a burger with the salad bar.  Food was as I expected but I had a problem ordering beer.  I was there during happy hour.  My wife and I were sitting in a booth 10 feet from the bar...the empty bar I might add.  When our waitress came to take our drink order, I asked her what they had going for happy hour.  The waitress said that happy hour is only for drinks ordered at the bar.  So I looked at her, pointed to the bar and said that maybe I'll order my beer at the bar and bring it to my table.  The waitress said that can't be done.  I'd have to drink it at the bar.  I ordered an ice tea, had my burger & salad but I  resolved that no more Ruby Tuesday's for me.  What a ridiculous policy.  Happy Hour is happy hour and should apply to the drink regardless of where it is ordered!

My family was very excited to have our Easter dinner at the William Penn Inn but were extremely disappointed. Each person was charged over $50 for a mid level hotel buffet style dinner only to find tasteless everything including old mini deserts.  The space was packed in and the noise level uncomfortable.

But the real problem was the service - obviously they, the owners/management, had booked big time so the wait staff they had to hire were untrained and very unprofessional temps who preferred to talk in small groups and ignore our attempts to get their attention to the point where we had to walk across the large room to order drinks from the service bar and remove our own dirty plates.

Really disappointed.

Great spot!  Nice to have an Indy classic nestled in quaint Villages of West Clay. Gives it a homey feel. Food awesome as Sahm's standard is known for. Service sweet. Not too busy so very comfortable for dining and visiting with friends. Hope Sahm's sticks around at this location.
Excellent tacos for a great price. Salsa needs some improvement for 5 stars

I wanna give it more stars because the Strawberry Limeade is always on point.  We went to Sonic looking for a burger I realize its a fast food burger but what I was served was absolutely atrocious lets start with the meat that was probably sitting on a table somewhere because it was nowhere near warm, because it had no warm my cheese slices were seriously like straight out the package placed on the burger  and lastly the bacon.  Listen y'all if they had cooked this bacon any further it would have been cremated.    I don't mind a bit of crunch to my bacon but come on.   So half way through my sandwich something tastes off not sure if its the meat that had been sitting after being cooked or the almost cremated bacon but it was awful.  I ended up making myself a Peanut butter and Banana sandwich that was 5 stars compared to what that sonic called a bacon cheeseburger.

So, I ended my last review of Sandpiper with "I would stay here again". And, I did! This time we had a family suite, as Gramma was along. I felt safe, the place was spotless, and the front desk staff was friendly and accommodating. Good work, Sandpiper!

We have been a customer of Carinos for years and even had our wedding rehearsal here but after tonight's experience they've lost our business. We waited over an hour for a table, normal for a weekend evening. However, once they called us in we waited for 10 minutes at the hostess station because the host staff had no idea what was going on. Not only did we wait but we also weren't greeted or even acknowledged. Once they took us to our table when we had said 3 times prior we needed a high chair, they did not give us one. Once we tracked someone down, they realized the table we were at was not compatible with a high chair but they had nowhere else to put us. We waited awkwardly in the aisle while being tripped over by a waiter. 

Finally they find us a table. Our son has celiac disease and it's not easy to find somewhere he can eat where he doesn't have to get grill chicken or a burger. They advertise on their menu their gluten free menu options. We go to order to find out that they're no longer ordering gluten free options but refuse to remove it from their menu. At this point I was nearly in tears after waiting with two children for over an hour for my son to have only one option, grilled chicken... 

We spoke with the manager who decided to give us a free appetizer or dessert, also not gluten free so this was not a help to us since we had nothing to offer our son, who we were celebrating with going out to eat. 

Our waitress was very sweet but extremely overwhelmed with her other table and kept forgetting things because she was too slammed. 

Above and beyond the terrible service, the fact that they've removed the gluten free options for whatever reasons they have, and don't care to list the change on their menu is so disheartening to a family like ours who struggled to have our son be able to go out to eat and enjoy his meal. 

If you have a gluten allergy or celiac, do not waste your time here!

They have the worst dentist! I went in telling them I thought my tooth was chipped and my tooth was hurting.

This middle eastern woman who was going to take the x-ray took a dental tool and started hitting my tooth saying is that the one that hurts? 

I told her to stop then she walked out of the room and the dentist came. She was Middle Eastern also.

I told her what she did and the dentist did the same thing!

I told her I'm not going to have you work on my tooth. 

I left I told them I'm not paying the bill!  you can't go around hitting sensitive teeth with metal tool.

 How stupid is that!

The burgers are fantastic! The food came out hot and fresh! Our waitress was really nice and prompt. The restaurant is an ideal place for watching anything because the place is filled with TV's. They have a full bar. I got the American Burger and it was cooked to perfection. The fries were good as well. Overall I will return whenever I am in the area.
bad and never come in again. Food is cool and restaurant is dirty. In addition, food does not look fresh.

I'm gonna give Paul 2 stars instead of 1 because what he did do.... Came out great. He was easy to get ahold of, also, good about getting back to me. I've never used yelp till now... Thought I'd share about this experience. Paul just may fall into the category of what you call, "shyster"..... I'm totally aware that I could go to local hardware store and rent a tile and grout steam cleaner for about $50 per few hours. Thing is I'm a busy guy... Had a lot going on at the time like always and my SUV was full of stuff, I was in the middle of moving so I figured, hell, I'll call somebody, make my life a little easier and let someone make a couple bucks. I don't knock the hustle... Love to see people doing their thing. Thing about Paul is.... When we spoke on phone I let him know I was aware of going rate but when he came and gave estimate that rate went up. I reminded him about our conversation then the rate went back down a little. I still felt it was a little high being how he wasn't going to have to move anything really but I let it go. The day of the job he was here earlier than scheduled. I like that usually but he came inside when he saw me throwing a couple floor mats out front...before I was ready and then attempted to help move my Ottoman for me which happen to have 2 good sized mugs of coffee on it. How he didn't notice the coffee on the Ottoman, I don't know but it inevitably spilled. He said leave it cause he'll clean it up but had a weird little attitude about him. I pick up on these things. The guy was in his feelings about the coffee he just spilled all over my stuff, then disappeared outside. I left the house (small condo actually) to take my daughter to school with a bad taste in my mouth. I'll admit Sometimes I'm not a morning person. My daughter hadn't cleared her floor all the way the night before so I wasn't the happiest and I don't like grown men that spill my coffee and then get a little bitchy attitude with no apology.   The job was so small...it took him less than an hour, literally. I was just down the street when he called so arrived in minutes. Floor looked great from what I could see. I was very pleased.... While in middle of my inspection he let me know he had a busy schedule that day and needed to get going. He Was already packed up and trying to leave so I went ahead to square up with him. All of the sudden the rate went back up. I said something about it as he's starting his truck and trying to leave. Shows me some piece of paper that had numbers on it I've never seen that's supposed to mean something to me and was different from the numbers we spoke about. I already know what time it is. I let it go and gave him the money so he'd just leave. I had to because little did this prissy attitude having guy know, I felt like giving him a slap for trying me but I can't go that route these days and.. naturally he doesn't even have change.. so I really need this man to leave before something bad happens. Go back inside to finish looking things over.... Guess what's still on the other side of my ottoman, couch and floor going all underneath and behind my couch...... coffee. Thanks Paul. Paul is not what he seems. And if he's reading this... He knows where to find me. You guys... Go to home Depot and spend a fraction of the price that Paul charges. If you can't do that...Don't let anyone charge you more than 35¢ a square ft unless they're moving some serious furniture around to do your floor. I'm telling you... Stick to what I just told you. 35¢ per square ft at the most.. Even if your floor is real dirty. If they have a rebuttal to that, tell em that you'll go to Home Depot and spend 50 bucks then see what they say. I thought car salesman cornered the shyster market

One of the most amazing customer service experiences I've had.  Owning a small business I always appreciate when people go out of their way to help customers solve problems.  I can't thank you enough for finding a way for my son to get the contacts he needed.  Being from out of town and needing emergency contacts was a long shot, but you made it work.  Marina is the best and I can't thank you enough for going the extra mile.

Not sure if this place is hit and miss because of all the reviews but it took about 10mins to order drinks. About 5mins to get them. Then another 15min to get asked "are you ready to order?" We just got the all you can eat shrimp, chicken tenders, riblets. I thought I would start with the riblets and my wife the chicken tenders. We are still waiting and it's been around 30minutes and we are still waiting. Should I have ordered 2 all I can eat items? How long will it take to get the 2bd item? There's a decent amount of people here, a ton of workers but I have no idea what's taking so long.

My boyfriend and I were looking for a new place to try.  We reached to some friends who had tried it and they said it was good. 
Fat Dan's is in the heart of BR, small restaurant, with good service and great food.  I had the burger (fantastic) and my BF had the italian RB combo (w/italian sausage) ("Good").  The fries were home made and nice and crispy, w/a great seasoning.  The prices were a bit high (7.95 for burger + 3.95 for fries and drink), for what it was, however I would pay again for the burger :)

Overall--it was a nice find and I will go back.

best citywide in town
very fun decor
nice neighborhood bar feeling with a twist
can be hard to flag down a busy bartender

Love this place and have been going here for decades (couple changes in ownership, but food remained the same). I'd give it 5 stars, but the prices are a bit steep. However, that really doesn't stop us from going. The matzo ball soup is the best I've ever had and the mushroom barley is delicious too (but I never get it because I can't pass up the matzo ball soup). They have great deli sandwiches and reubens. And they have this outstanding concoction- corned beef, pastrami, swiss, cole slaw and russian dressing served on two potato pancakes. Omelettes, waffles, muffins and rye bread - all great. 
For lunch/dinner they give you a bowl of 2 varieties of pickles while you wait for your food, which I've always enjoyed. Also, it's BYO.
When you need a quick bite to eat after shopping n aramingo ave this is the spot to come by and eat some good Chinese food .

Sylvester's Western Wear probably has to be one of the last places in town where you can buy authentic western wear and cowboy boots.  There are a couple of places up Gonzales way, if you're into traveling I-10.  Sylvester's has been around since before the Urban Cowboy days.  Nice selection of shirts, boots, hats, belts, whatever you're into.  I tend towards Panhandle Slims for their cut (slim) and longer arm lengths, but they do have shirts for those stereotyped cowboys that can hide their oversized belt buckles.  For the ladies, there is a boutique inside the store for more fashionable clothes and jewelry.  The store is a survivor.

For the price this is one of the better fine dinning experiences I've encountered.  If you are interested in trying delicious food, multiple courses and artistic plating then look no further in Nashville.  

The cuisson was absolutely on point, I couldn't find much to fault during the meal - outside of subtle balancing of some courses.  The atmosphere and intimacy with the crew working on your food was what I savored most.  Essentially the entire dinning room is sitting at what is traditional considered 'the chef's table.'  They are excited to talk to you about their food, inspiration and most anything else.  We felt so incredible welcomed in this warm environment - a special shout out to Will for taking time during the meal to make a special 'suckling pork skin sandwich' for us between dessert courses, as he easily picked up on my gushing excitement to be eating such a delicious meat.  The pairings were mostly well done as well.

If we are ever back in Nashville you better believe we will be making another stop here.  Really delightful and absolutely on par with 1 star michelin food I have tasted in Chicago, NY or Europe.

Stopped in at 7:00 pm for a hot n ready thin crust pepperoni pizza. The place was packed with people waiting while a guy was doing chin ups. I ordered and he said they didnt have any ready so I turned to leave and he loudly and smart ellicly says "bye" and waves at me in front of everyone. Which was embarrassing and unnecessary.  And to top it all off the place was dirty.

The doctors here are great, they are attentive and have gone out of their way to make me feel comfortable. But, the office staff are incompetent. They BS with each other instead of checking in patients and are incredibly slow. They have screwed up my appt time on more than one occasion and make the wait unnecessarily long. It's almost enough to make you want to switch practices.
The service is just SO BAD. Every time. Doesn't matter when you go or where you sit. It's brutal.

Visited Grandin last week and had a classic option, cod and chips. The atmosphere here was really refreshing and despite most items being deep fried, the restaurant didn't have that greasy smell or feel to it that some do. Decor was really well done, clean bathrooms and I love the bar area at the front for overflow seating. Parking in this area can be hit or miss, but we lucked out for this visit (something that almost deterred us from going there in the first place). Huge portions and really reasonable prices for a speciality place that isn't fast-foody. The only complaints I have would be the draught offerings, not much variety, as well as the fries and fish having not enough flavouring for my taste. Batter was nice and not overly bready which is always a plus. Lots of fish options depending on your taste. I would try this again for sure.

I was unimpressed with this bar. I added it to my "bar crawl" while I was visiting NOLA due to the reviews on Yelp. The bar was not very welcoming and the service wasn't that great. The hurricane was very sweet and very strong (worth the money if that's what you're into). There is a nice courtyard in the back where I wish we would've sat, because the bar experience was just meh.

I've been to many Maggiano's and this was by far the worst experience. It took over an hour to get our food. We had to try very hard to flag the waitress down after paying to get a to go container (no offering to wrap it or bring a box ahead of time). Then for the take home pasta we were supposed to get baked ziti and received noodles and meat sauce. The waitress then argued with us saying it was the baked ziti... we've taken it home many times and it never looked like this. And to top it off we found out this Maggiano's is not part of the LettuceEats rewards program so we were unable to use rewards or get points for the meal. Very disappointed.

I'd rate this a 3.5 if I could. 

We went for dinner around 8:15pm. There was a band playing on a Friday night that was asking for a $5 cover. Since it was our first time, we didn't want to pay a cover just to try out the food, so we said no thanks and walked out. A few seconds later, the door man came back out and said the manager told him to go ahead and let us in with no cover charge.  We took a seat at a back booth since the band (a blues band, that were actually pretty great) were pretty loud. We ordered a beer and looked over the menu. It was small in , but had plenty of BBQ options! I chose the brisket with beans and slaw. My buddy chose the pork steak and the same sides. We shared, of course. I found the brisket to have great texture. Not too soft, but it still was tender. It had a dry rub and came with a side of sauce. The sauce was really unique-a little sweet with a little spice! They mentioned they have a great hotter version but we didn't ask for any. The pork steak fell apart with a fork! It was also a dry rub with sauce on the side. It had a good smoke flavor as well. 

So, the food was good for the price, the ambiance??? Well, it's a restaurant connected to the Econolodge... so it appeared to have seen better days. But all in all it was in decent shape and the staff was very friendly. 

Would I go back? I think so. But it would not be my first choice for BBQ in the Lou.

Never really been a huge fan of Aquariums but I had the CityPass so may as well use it. It was your typical aquarium. Nothing spectacular but we had a good time with the kids and that's all that matters. The girls seemed to really enjoy themselves. The aquarium is located in Tampa Bay downtown area and that CityPass allowed us to bypass the lines and go right up to the front entrance. The place is cool inside and very close to other attractions like the arena and the convention center. You can take the trolley and get off near the aquarium as well. But if you drive, there is no parking at the aquarium but there is a garage directly across the street.
