There is a little cafe inside of the aquarium as well and although we didn't eat there, it did smell pretty good. If I'm ever in the area again I will definitely take advantage of that CityPass and visit again.

It's a car wash.  Like another review states, it gets a bit crowded on nice days and the small lot can become tough to navigate.  They offer 3 main grades of wash.  Middle grade was $18 for exterior wash and wax, undercarriage wash, and interior vacuum. Don't bother trying to get anything "a la carte" because the attendant will look at you like you have 2 heads.  Can't stay in the car for this one.  Staff is not particularly friendly but they get the job done.

Our first time here just ordered the virgin margerita. Sour! Decor is very nice and festive. Husband had chicken enchiladas and I had mancho nachos. His choice was basic or soso sauce tasted like chef boy r d' s sauce but clean in taste and texture. My dish was delish with every vote. Must try!

Dr Brad helped me so much with both of my cats. Not only was he responsive but he texted to check in on Myrtle Mae to see how she was doing. While he must be busy giving all the families and animals attention, he always made my household feel considered and cared for. One of the best resources in philly!

I really love longhorn, but this location has gone all wrong I had to wait almost an 1hour 30 mins just for a table for two, our waiter was really nice. The food came out pretty quick , food was all cold my boyfriends steak was completely undercooked, mash potato was cold still had hard pieces of potato the Mac & cheese was no good and cold the food was just very disappointing for how long we waited it just should've been perfect! Only thing that was perfect was our bread !

Went for my first time and received incredible service from the owner, Monica. Helpful and hardworking, she allowed us to order off menu since we wanted curry and naan together. When a drink got spilled she helped us move to a different table and the spill was quickly dealt with. Once of the curries was spicier than expected, so a complementary raita (yogurt) was provided, and when it wasn't able to calm down the spice enough, we were given a whole new curry with naan for free. Since the Indian fudge wasn't available that day, an extra Gulab Jamun was given. The lamb butter curry was a nice creamy flavour, and the naan was a good texture. Fries were seasoned with a zesty spice mix. Will come back again when craving Indian food.

Inconsistency is the major reason for my low review. 
I have yet to have both good service and good food in the same visit here. The issues with this most recent visit were:
-no record of our reservation that we called twice to confirm.
-2 hour dinner for only 6 of us due to being forgotten about many times. 
-never got a refill of water for any of us nor did they ask if we wanted another beverage. 
-food that was good: ribs, burger, mixed cocktails, dessert
-their special was really bad, striped seabass. Waitress suggested it and then it was not good at all, she did remove it from my bill without me even asking which was really nice.

Jacques-Imo's Cafe is the best and most quintessential of New Orleans. 

We ordered the grilled oyster special and shrimp and alligator cheesecake to start - the cheesecake was halfway between a cheesecake and a quiche - an interesting texture with well-balanced flavor. The grilled oysters were absolutely delicious - the best oysters we ate in New Orleans.

For entrées, we tried the redfish, the fried chicken, the crab cakes, and the venison. The fried chicken portion was gigantic, the outside was well-seasoned and crunchy while the inside was moist and juicy. The redfish was also very good - a more understated dish with a creamy and somewhat sweet sauce that perfectly complimented the delicate redfish. The venison was delicious as well, but it tasted like something you could order at any good restaurant in the US.

The selection of beers was great, and the fresh watermelon mojito was my favorite drink of New Orleans. The free cornbread was garlicky and amazing, and the sesame oil-dressed salad served with a fried oyster was absolutely delicious. Despite tipsily saying some pretty questionable things to our waiter, he humored us, and our waitress was absolutely lovely. She even gave us a free dessert - crème brulée and bread pudding, both of which were excellent. Also, the decor was folksy and artistic, adding to the perfectly laid-back atmosphere.

I was so impressed with the meal that I actually texted people during the meal to tell them that they had to come to Jacques-Imo's. With fantastic food and prompt and friendly service, this restaurant is a must-go.

Had only one mixed experience here.   Took our adventuresome grown daughter, and she decided to try an unknown dish that involved noodles and an egg in an icewater bath.   She had questioned the server who spoke some English but did not really get a valid description of what she got, which was watery, tasteless and strange.  

Meanwhile I was filling up on excellent bul go gi and kimchi, and my wife had some other tasty selection, and we all had very good egg rolls.  I know & love kimchi so it was a good experience for me....not so much for our daughter.  But not all culinary  adventures are successful. History remembers the successful explorers like Columbus & Heyerdahl,  it forgets those who disappeared in a watery grave...with or without noodles.
The flavorful food is only overshadowed by charming and attentive staff! Additionally, I was happy to hear that they are big supporters of the Pour House charity, which helps the homeless community.

Disclaimer: My review is based solely on the cake. I purchased a slice from Kroger, which receives daily deliveries from the bakery. 

I've heard a lot of great buzz about this shop and have seen the Pinterest worthy photos of the storefront. When I splurged on one slice (~$11) for Valentine's Day, I had high expectations. I selected one of my favorite flavors, red velvet, which looked like a piece of art with the beautiful, glittery roses on the side. 

Unfortunately, I was disappointed. Perhaps it's due to my excessively sweet (and not necessarily sophisticated) palette, but I thought it was really bland. The cake itself was not memorable and slightly dry. The roses made out of frosting had a creamy, fluffy texture, which was fine - but it was flavorless and tasted just like ordinary whipped cream. The only sweet component was the buttercream frosting in between the layers of the cake. 

Next time I am craving for red velvet, I will go elsewhere. Sorry Cake Bake, you are so pretty, but you just didn't do it for me!

Congress has a disease. On one hand, congress has restaurants with great interior decoration, nice service, environmentally-forward policies on straws and napkins, easy parking options relative to other metropolitan areas, though basically everything great about these restaurants disappears in the back of house. The Hub restaurant is one of these places. 

The menu is confused. I'm quite certain that only kids places or childrens menus are supposed to have food from varying cuisines--and, in my defense, usually that's okay when it's a fusion place or the flavors make sense. But this is an establishment that has all-American burgers w tater tots, but also pho, and ontop of that, fancy European braised pork belly on the same menu. My memory doesn't serve me well, so there's likely more contradiction in this menu, but at our table, the conflict gave off a really amateur vibe of the head chef. Is this all he can think of?

With that said, the food we did order was really well made. The pork belly was pretty tender, the burger and sandwiches were constructed solidly, the lasagna... was lasagna, the pho tasted like pho, though the mac n' cheese tasted like it had a Kraft single ontop... which it might have. Our table was inconclusive over whether it did or didn't. But that's kind of a problem. Lasagna can really only be so good. It's egg pasta, tomato sauce, cheese, and meat. There's not a lot of creative input there. This isn't a luxury burger place, these are just normal burgers. Pho can be super interesting, but it's played safe here. All of these things are pretty well done... for dishes that don't actually require an expertise to make or think of. 

With that said, this could be a great restaurant, the ice cream is a really good foot above other places. But we've gotta cut the TV's in the bar, sportsbar isn't a good look when the entirety of your restaurant is geared to look contemporary and modern. Service was nice and attentive, shout out to Robert, I think.

I can't say that I was incredibly impressed with this place. I didn't like their tempura batter. It tasted like French fries. They offer a lot of different rolls which is something I look for in a sushi place, but they don't carry mochi ice cream! It's close to where I live which is convenient, but I can't say that I would return unless someone is bringing me. I had a volcano roll that didn't have any rice; the tempura shrimp was soggy and cold. Yuck. I like that the establishment is pretty big so IF I had a large party they could definitely accommodate. I always order 2 piece nigiri sake before delving into rolls. Gotta say I've had better, it wasn't terrible. I would say if you live in the area this place is worth giving a shot. I definitely suggest getting all you can eat because two rolls pretty much cost the same. 
As far as service goes it was okay as well. My water was never empty, but I did have to get up and ask for some rolls. For lunch hour the place was pretty empty So there's really no excuse for that. 
There's not really much in the means of parking especially if the surrounding businesses are busy and they're tiny. I watched somebody ding somebody else's car. Yikes!

Could not recommend them any less. It's the day is my move, the only day I am available for the next month, family flew in from Texas to help unpack, family drove in from Chicago, and they just called to inform me someone "fell off the truck" at another move and they can't move me anymore. They will either move me Wednesday when I work. Or refund my deposit. Phone service was great. I thought this would be a "family" like they market themselves. Don't waste your money. Spend money with a better company.

This place is shit and so is their workers. F*ck this place. We went in to get a pizza and apparently they had a sign where the had no pizza's and I was like and this rude ass worker told us if we can read, like dummy shit up and do your work instead of sitting down and not not doing shit. Never again going here f* ck these workers in there. just straight up lazy.
Well maintained and clean, service was prompt , and kept on top of our needs. Food and selection was good, prices average for this type of lunch.  I would return again.

The experience is worth it. Ice cream was delicious and creamy! We had vanilla with chocolate chips, whipped cream, and raspberries. They did go light on the raspberries but there was still a lot to eat:)

I used to get my Brazilian's done by Kim when she was at Better Health & Tanning Spa. I stopped going to her for a while and found her again at this salon. Long story short? She burned my vag twice with wax that was too hot, and the second time couldn't be  bothered to answer the phone despite multiple attempts to contact her. 
DO NOT GO TO HER FOR YOUR BRAZILIAN. YOU WILL FEEL LIKE YOU HAD INTERCOURSE WITH A CACTUS IF YOU DO.

This is the best thai restaurant in Newark. I would take my whole family here again and again. They have awesome kickin' lemongrass chicken curry, pad thai and heavenly smelling chicken satay. Come here if you want to fill your belly and soul. You will find that they have alot of options, i've become a regular and never found one dish that isnt tasty.

I will make this review short and sweet. I decided to check this place out because it was free. So I showed up and saw the general lee and hazzard county tow truck out front. Pretty cool. I walked through the whole place in about 20 minutes. Could have taken a little more time but I was by myself and I was fine. There were some cool things in here, and quite a few things to buy all over which is why it's free I think. Stop by if you have a few minutes, but don't make a special trip.

Kind of overrated in my opinion. The taste was basic and service was decent but nothing stood out that amazed me to the point where I would come back. It's just a sports bar that sells seafood. Nothing fancy. The best thing I ate was the New England Clam Chowder. That was worth the $6. I think it should be more but not complaining. Now the crab fries are a waste of money. $5.95 for crinkle cut fries with Old bay seasoning is not "a hit" to me. I ordered them thinking I was going to get crab meat on top or something. That was the biggest disappointment to me. As for the sand which, I had the meatball parm. It was bland and didn't have any flavor. Tasted like turkey meatballs with ragu sauce on them and cheap parmesan cheese flakes on top. 

Overall wasn't bad place to hang but food is basic and minimal. They didn't leave me a very good first impression here. I would go back but just for the sports bar part and some clam chowder. Everything else I would hold off on especially the high priced "crab fries". 

B- in my book.

Deanos makes some great pizza.  I just remember it being thin, greesy and LOTS of flavor.  Nothing too complicated, but they have a very dark, nice atmosphere and would be great for a date or with the kids.  If I'm not going to Super Cucas down the street past Albertsons, I'd be going here.

Very good lunch here today. Tacos with chorizo, al pastor and asada were all very tasty. I liked the add-your-own toppings bar so we could tweak the ingredients to our own liking. Horchata was very good. 

Will definitely return!

Mt. Laurel New Jersey 

Good products, a notch above SAMs , they literally have everything. They're meat department is awesome. The rotisserie chickens unreal, juicy, for 5 dollars ,can't beat it. I also have insurance thru them, Ameriprise which is excellent.

Michelle and her team made me feel so comfortable during their plus size WToo event. I found the perfect dress for my big day this October. The dress selection is modern, and up to date in style. The prices are very good as well. Would definitely recommend for anyone in Reno!

I went to this store this past weekend and i must admit that they have a large quanity of items to sell for any individual needs.  The bad side is that NO-ONE was around to assist us with our questions since this was our first time looking for kayaks.  They need more people on the floor and less people behind the register.

THANK GOODNESS FOR NEW YORK BAGEL BAKERY!

When we relocated to the Philly suburbs, we began our mission to find real bagels in the area. After finding many bagels that were just okay, and a lot that were comparable to Thomas' (NOT ACCEPTABLE!), we finally tried New York Bagel Bakery and were over the moon. They are the real deal! 

To note: They are closed on Saturdays and on very busy days they will not put cream cheese on for you- you'll need to buy it on the side. Do not let either of these things deter you- this is the best bagel you will find in the Philly area. 

Getting one of these still warm is the closest you'll get to heaven on City Ave. Soft and airy on the inside, crisp and chewy on the outside. These folks know that they are doing.

PLEASE DON'T CHANGE A THING GUYS! Thank you for bringing delicious bagels to all of us! You make these native Brooklynites very, very happy.

I walked in for lunch today at 11:50 a.m.  I ordered the #22 lunch special and a Diet Coke.  

40 minutes later, I noticed a woman at a table not far from mine staring at her plate of food -- which had the same things I had ordered on it -- and trying to flag a waitress down.  The waitress eventually went over, picked up her plate, and came to my table saying "this is yours".

I told her I didn't want a plate that had been sitting on someone else's table for ten minutes, so the waitress went back to have the cook do another one.

By 12:50, with no food delivered, I walked out.

The owners should fire the chef (40 minutes to get a lunch special out, really?) and the waitress and start over.

This great company has delivered some of the best arts entertainment and education to the Reno community.  I've never been underwhelmed with any of their shows.  REEFER MADNESS THE MUSICAL was especially hilarious, and they presented one of the best renditions of THE ROCKY HORROR SHOW ever.  I follow them on Facebook, and I'm looking forward to catching their reenactments of late-night TV shows the next time I'm in town.

This store is a mix of overpriced, horrible condition for the price cabinets and some random well priced finds.  

It really depends on what your looking for.  You might find it, it might be worth paying a bit more for new, you could score.  

I'd check again if looking for something, but I wouldn't have high hopes of finding it.
Had a great blackened Amber Jack fresh catch of the day sandwich. Also highly recommend the Nitro Merchant Porter and the Jurasmat Baltic Porter.

Went here for dinner before a Cardinals game. Sat at the bar. It was early on a weeknight so there weren't that many people. Somehow the fact it was early didn't seem to stop the guy next to us from getting slightly pissed (drunk) and insisting we order the tenderloin tips... We resisted. 

We split a burger/fries. Burger was decent... Good size bun... average tasting burger. Had good char. It was obvious that even though we said no onions, one had been put on and then removed. Because the lettuce tasted oniony. That disturbed me. I'm SO not picky about food, but I allow myself to hate one thing. Onions. 

The fries? Good Heavens they were magnificent! The skins still on, some crispy, some soft, loaded with seasoning and salt... I want to go back and eat a whole basket. 

The bartender alternated between being friendly and looking irritated by our presence. Hmmph.

Solid choice for dinner at the Atlantis. It's on the second floor so a bit out of the way of all the casino noise and smoke. Nice menu and solid wine list. I especially thought the featured vineyard wines were a good value. My 12 glass of Pinot Noir was huge.

Food was good. My lamb shank was fall apart delicious and my husband's pork chop was huge, though a bit over cooked. Unfortunately the vegetables were really undercooked, carrots were basically raw. But overall a nice meal.

My husband had the crème brûlée and I had a truffle and port pairing that was really nice.

The place does a lot of happy hour and that's probably a good choice too. 

Service was not as good as I expected, felt a little rushed for as nice a place, and not super friendly, except for our bus guy, Mark Anthony, who was excellent.

***3.5 stars rounded down***
I have mixed feelings about this place. While the food is pretty good, things are just so hectic and disorganized.

I came here with a few friends for brunch and got the seafood benedict. This dish came with crab/crawfish cakes, poached eggs, and a spicy hollandaise sauce. If also came with a side of hash potatoes. The hollandaise sauce was really flavorful and creamy. The crab/crawfish cake was tender and savory. I enjoyed this dish and I was glad I chose it out of everything on the menu! I also got to try the crawfish étouffée which was good but didn't seem fresh.

As for drinks, I got the strawberry mimosa which was just ok. They told us they ran out of flutes?? So it came in a water cup which was disappointing because I ordered it after I saw someone's flute of mimosa. I got over the chute but the taste didn't make up for it. It tasted watered down and just really weird. 

Everyone in our group got their food on time except for my one friend. Her food got lost somewhere or they forgot it so they brought it out after everyone in our group had finished their food. They also brought out duplicate orders of two or our other dishes at the end of our meal (talk about disorganized). The waitress said that she'll take my friend's dish off the bill, but forgot, so we had to trace her down and remind her. Overall, the whole meal was wildly hectic and just took a lot of time from start to finish. Although the food is descent, the lack of organization gave us a headache.
I had a BLT while waiting for my flight.  Very friendly and efficient staff.  Too bad the food was just OK.
The food was good. The spacing was ample but the beer was just okay. The location was convenient to where we stayed.

My nails turned out absolutely awful. They are bumpy, not straight, and the edges are awful. I kept saying that it was off but he didn't do much. The other guy there had to help straighten them. Only gave one start because they were all nice. Do NOT go here and get coffin nails. 
Yes these are supposed to be coffin... 
they also made my friends very skinny and ours look COMPLETELY different.

My bf and I ordered the "couples" special, 20 wings, a cheesesteak, fries, and soda for about $20. We did a split order for the wings, half teriyaki and half cajun. I also got the cajun cheesesteak, cus I looooove cajun spices.

I loved everything, the wings had a great crispy skin and though the sauces weren't as thick as we usually like them, they had great flavor and tasted really good. I can't say that I'm an expert on cheesesteaks but my sandwhich was delicious, lots of meat that was juicy and flavorful. The fries were pretty good too. My bfs only complaint was that he thought the wings were on the smaller side, he likes them with a little more meat on the bone. But he also thought they were tasty and we both agreed we're getting food from them again in the future.

Looking at the menu, the name definitely fits. They had so many choices and I'm sure everything tastes good. This place is worth a try if your craving something filling and delicious for reasonable prices.

Wow. I get to be the first to review this hidden gem of a beach get-away?

Miramar Resort isn't as big and fancy as the other beach front properties. It is a single story, renovated, beach motel which was built in the 1950's as two separate, but similar, properties, which provided drive up parking to your door. However, all that has changed because the old parking area is now a courtyard with two shuffle board paying areas, several covered picnic spots and two open air grills for bbq'ing and two open pools.

The inside units are small but cute and provide you with a place to cook, each unit has a kitchenette, bathroom/shower, bed, fold away bed, tv, and free internet. You should be spending your time on the beach, out in the courtyard, sight seeing, or visiting one of the many infamous restaurants or shops nearby, not staying indoors!

The beach is amazing with white sand and has quiet gentle rolling waves. Often you can see dolphins jumping out near the sand bars, which you can walk out to, plus plenty of sting rays flying out of the water trying to catch fish, but be alert and shuffle you feet when walking to avoid stepping on one! Don't sweat it, they don't bite!

Nearby there are plenty of sights including the Clearwater Aquarium which has the infamous dolphin from the movies, "Dolphin Tale" 1 and 2. There is fishing, skiing, sight seeing flights, a bus tram to take you around town, the Salvador Dali museum and gallery, the Pier, sailing, shopping, the glass blowing demonstrations, and much more.

The cost is inexpensive, only $100 for a room that can sleep 4, but they have vav few 3/2's which will cost you more.

Very clean rooms. Very nice employees. Perfect for a weekend getaway. I highly rate it. Sorry no pets. Parking is also limited.

Shhhh, don't tell any one!

I had high hopes coming into Harvest Bowl because I love Fresh Kitchen so much and there is nothing like it in SSeminole Heights. Well there still isn't many thing like Fresh Kitchen inn Seminole Heights. It was quick, but the  food lacked in any flavor. I got the steak bowl and the best thing about it was the jasmine rice. Will try again, but very disappointed vs expectations.

No complaints though I thought the food would have been better. I had the "La Completa Cubana," which offers a taste of a lot of Cuban dishes. Pork was good though I thought the Empanada de Picadillo was overcooked. I love the black beans, yellow rice, and plantains. But I could have done without the yucca. I had my first mojito. It was recommended by my server but it was nothing great. Stick to margaritas. We also had the Mahi Mahi "Veradero." I thought it looked OK if you like meatier types of fish. Also had Florida's Cigar City IPA. OK for beer in a can. No dessert. The view at the top of The Pier in St. Petersburg. was very nice.

After all the hype and great reviews, we expected more.  Pricey and short wine pours, measured next to a sample pour kept on the service bar; overcooked crust on tomato pie; cold fried chicken and mashed potatoes, sent back to be heated and still cold. But mushroom side was delicious, interesting assortment. Bartenders Storm & Carolyn were a big plus; friendly and informative. Hostess was also very welcoming and outgoing.

Looking for a steak dinner and was referred here. Steak was great and the service was attentive.  Enjoyed the potato skins and green chilis appetizers and I got the blue cheese salad and ribeye, which was med well done and it melted in my mouth. GREAT place to visit and eat dinner.

This place is good but not great. There are not many restaurants (if any) in the Ft. Washington area aside from a few fast food chains and pizza places.  One thing that I would recommend from Zakes  - vegetarian soups that are freshly made (usually they have a vegetarian chili that's pretty good).  I would not recommend their salads (just okay).  The thai salmon is usually overcooked so the salmon turns out very dry, same with the shrimp, not a fan.  Unfortunately, you are very limited in this area but if you are craving a non fast food place, I would check out Zakes.
No, just no. Not good, not authentic.  And what "mexican" cuisine doesnt offer a qusadilla on there menu for kids? Never again.

Been wanting to try this place because there were so many great reviews.

Atmosphere: hipster, modern 

Menu is different. I ordered the build your own breakfast: toast, 3 slices of bacon, 2 over medium eggs and avocado. Was pretty good. Also ordered hot cocoa with whipped cream and their homemade marshmallows. It was okay, didn't finish my cup. The homemade marshmallows were pretty good though. 

Husband ordered the build your own breakfast: 1 eggs over medium, arugula salad, avocado, poblano peppers and toast. He enjoyed it. He also ordered their dirty chai latte. 

Location is near downtown. Noticed that there were a lot of homeless people right outside of the building in the grass area.

I would say this place is living on its reputation and longevity. I'll make this simple: 

Reasons to go here: shrimp cocktail (more specifically the cocktail sauce), the old prohibition feel and the tomato juice that you can sneak some vodka in. 

Reasons not to go here: steaks are average and overpriced, drinks are much of the same.  Also, the seating is horrible and packed in like sardines.

So on Labor Day I opted to get out the house for once since my fiance and I actually were home together.  I seen this Groupon Deal and took my honey out for the afternoon to Sharrott Winery and it was great.

Me personally, I enjoy small crowds and quiet time during the day, so going on a holiday was ideal (there were 4 other families/ couples present). We walked in the small lobby/checkout/standing bar, but it was very welcoming. The hostess/bartender was great. She was friendly and social and of course knew her wine AND inventory which to me is important.  I imagine this goes with all the staff there.

With our groupon we recieved cheese and crackers, two wine glasses, and a bottle of our choice... Winter Blends was just amazing first warm wine ( I had it cold) i ever heard of! 

We enjoyed a glass on their covered patio before heading home. Although its smaller than I imagined, my first winery experience was great, I only wish I could have done a tour. 

My final word... Visit!

Atrocious Comcast-level customer service. DO YOUR RESEARCH BEFORE JOINING THIS GYM. Please read the other reviews and you will clearly see the countless people that have been negatively affected by this gym during a global pandemic.

The way they have handled Covid-19 is extremely telling of this business. QTNM overcharges for gym membership as it is. They may have good equipment, however, it doesn't outweigh the way they treat members. It is not worth it to get locked into paying the amount they charge and the struggles in dealing with their shady customer service. 

I was given completely inaccurate information. Offered a 3-month freeze only for it to be frozen for a month and charges to resume. They purposely take longer than advertised to reply to emails. They word things in their favor to effectively swindle you if you're not careful. There is no way this gym is struggling during this time, they have been banking all the exorbitant membership fees while every other gym in Nashville has shown empathy during this time. It truly is not worth the trouble to go here.

Great new BYoB for Nor Lib! Next level, super-fresh food and presentation at really decent prices in a cozy, comfortable atmosphere. Went with some friends and it was a great night. Love the approach chef Kevin and Michael have taken, a chalkboard menu that will change with whatever fresh foods are available to them daily/weekly they said. Nice. The place is simple and cozy but the food is anything but. My fiancé and I have been to a TON of BYoB's all over Philly, you will not find one serving such high quality food, with 5 star presentation and plating execution, not for the $ you pay here anyway. I had the skirt steak n potatoes, (never had potatoes like that), my fiancé the chicken n 14hr cabbage, and a couple of our friends had Brussels sprout tortellini, pork and everyone loved their food, raved about it actually. The polenta app was REDIC, for real. Get it. Squid app was so good, and the mussels app was killer. Dessert was real good too and I usually don't do dessert. They really just take simple fresh food, then take it to another level. Look, go check it out, you'll thank me. We'll be back soon.

The smell and the name of course lure you into the cookie cabin. The portions are huge and definitely enough to share amongst 4-5 people. The cookies smell amazing but the taste was nothing too special. The cobbler though was really good especially with the vanilla ice cream. The prices though are a bit too much for what you're getting but I guess while you're up there, there isn't too much to choose from. My suggestion is to just share so you can get a taste of it and say you've been.
I had a goblet here it was real amazing. The bacon Bloody Mary is very good as well!! N

Expected excellent based on word of mouth recommendation and the yelp reviews.  The prices are very reasonable, and the bresaola app was amazing.  The lasagna was pretty good, but the noodles were way overcooked.  The eggplant was better, but it's still nothing to write home about as it wasn't cooked to temp.  This is definitely 'neighborhood gem' material, but I wouldn't come here expecting to be blown away.
Dr Kerry & Dr Chappa are amazing. Happy to report that I am pain free! These guys saved my well being and my career!  If you have pain you need to make an appt to see them!

While the work that Bright Now Dental has done with my teeth has been exemplary, and the staff are incredibly friendly, their customer service regarding wait times and cost is horrible. During my first appointment, the hygienist disappeared at the end of my appointment for at least 20 minutes without telling me where she went. Prior to my root canal, I was advised of one price for the root canal and crown. When I returned for the temporary crown, which I was told would take one hour, I was charged an additional amount for the crown. At this time it was too late so I had to pay. Granted they changed my loan to same as cash but I was there for almost TWO hours and had to rush back to work. Today I arrived 15 minutes early in hopes to get done quickly and sat unacknowledged in the waiting room for almost an hour. Unfortunately I have paid for my crown so I have to go back to get it completed. I called and spoke with the manager, who said he'd return my call right away...that was over 30 minutes ago. So while everyone is nice and they do good dental work, you had better schedule at least 3 hours to get your appointment completed.

I returned Kokopelli for a second meal and am sad to say it will most likely be my last. The food is still great, but unfortunately the suffocating, and at times perplexing, service was enough of a turn-off to not want to go back.

Upon arriving, my date and I were seated directly next to another twosome. Not something that would bother me, except for the fact that the entire restaurant was empty, and at max one group came throughout the meal. I was RIGHT next to another person, close enough to accidentally touch their table or arm. Why would the hostess EVER seat us there?

The waitresses (who was even our waitress? who knows. so many random people constantly coming and going it was impossible to tell) were overbearing and way too talkative. They pressured us to order more, constantly asked if we wanted more to eat, and really did nothing positive. 

We brought a $50 food certificate that we had purchased online for $25. I'm sure they sell a lot of these (several hundred at least), but when I went to use it to pay I was greeted with what only could be called rudeness. The waitress seemed confused and, honestly, annoyed. I was still planning on tipping on the full amount, as I always do with these sorts of certificates, so there really is NO reason for her to be disrespectful about my using it. It took forever for her to return with the check, which came with yet another passive-aggressive rude statement about using the certificate

In the end, I really am sad at how horrendous the entire service experience was. Still, the food is great. I can only hope that the chef/owner figures out how awful their hired help is fix it fast, before it leads to the demise of the restaurant.

Not what I was expecting.
I called ahead to ask if the staff could help me with selecting a house plant. They said sure, but when I got there I was ignored. No greetings, nothing.
After a while someone did eventually ask if I needed help. It was awkward. I will go back for herbs in the spring. It's a nice place, but I recommend bringing your phone in with you and if you see a plant you like research it yourself so you'll know if it's a good fit for your lighting and space.

This is a bad carpet cleaning company. I recently purchased a home in meridian. The carpet was in great condition but there was a strong cat odor inside. I contacted ultimate carpet cleaning and scheduled a technician to come out. My realtor met the worker at my house. The technician called me on the phone and I voiced my concerns. I asked for a deep pad cleaning and he agreed. He also suggested that I do specific areas where the odor was strongest. I also agreed to that. He said it would be $450 total. I was a little discouraged by the price but I agreed anyway. One week later my property manager was showing the house and still noticed an extremely strong cat odor that was inside the whole entire residence. I called ultimate carpet cleaning and spoke with Justin who without my knowledge recorded our whole conversation. Justin advised me the technician did an surface cleaning only and did some spot treatments but if they had to return to the property it would be an additional price. 

I was already upset that they charged me close to $500 and the job was not done correctly not to mention the technician did not do what I asked. I specifically asked for a DEEP PAD CLEANING, when I mentioned this to Justin he's had nothing to say. 

The owner Jeremy refused a refund and I was not about to have this company come back out and charge me more. 

Unfortunately people like me get taken advantage of like this and left with little options and bad customer service. 

I called a different carpet company I found here on yelp. They did the job correctly and fixed the problem for 1/4 of the price. 

Please don't get taken advantage of. Save your money and time and look elsewhere. This company is horrible and has other bad reviews.

Let me begin by saying that Cajun Cafe when it comes to food deserves four stars.  With that being said one cant ignore that the prices are a little steep, which takes them down one star.  I like the food here, very authentic.  I love their étouffée, the fried green tomatoes, they have great poboy's, and their whiskey bread pudding is great.  As I said I think their food is very good and their staff is friendly.  My guess to their high prices is that they probably know that there is no competition around.  If you want Cajun food in this area this is the only place to go.  We still eat here about once a month but if it was more affordable I would definitely put it in the weekly rotation.  I recommend that if you have the cravings for this delicious type of food you try it out, but come prepare to spend lots of your hard earned money.

This place gets an extra star from me just because our server, Savannah, was so absolutely incredible! She took the time to get to know us, and the other diners around us, and was attentive and kind. More about her in a minute. 
Ravello is a nice restaurant - and ridiculously expensive for what you get. 
We started out with the caprese salad as an appetizer and that was delicious. We both loved that. I mean, the tomatoes could have been better but this time of year they may be hard to find.
For the main, my husband and I both got pizzas and neither of them was great. He said his (the "carne") was just ok. Mine, the white pizza, was a great idea in theory but in reality it was so dry and bland that I had to ask for salt. And even then it was still just ok. 
All of this adds up fast so we decided not to get dessert. Well, Savannah had other plans. She brought us tiramisu and a rose on a plate that had "congrats" written on it in chocolate syrup. It was so incredibly sweet! When she was asking where we were from, we had told her this was our first vacation in five years - we hadn't even gotten a honeymoon due to finances - and that it was our last night at the resort. She remembered that tidbit from the beginning of our time there and at the end brought us something just to say congrats. It was so sweet it just blew us away. It made our whole time there better! Ravello gets four stars just because of Savannah! (If I was rating just Savannah and not the whole place she'd get a million stars!)
(Also - the tiramisu was just ok - it was the thoughtfulness that went into the gesture that made it great!)

Good service, decent food. Nothing memorable though. Had the pulled pork sandwich. I've had better...

If you go for drinks in the evening, grab a table outside. That'll save your experience.

This place was so tasteful! The chicken burrito and chicken enchiladas were absolutely delicious! My friends and I had a great casual dinner and enjoyed the strawberry margaritas! The service was fast! Would definitely recommends this place for an enjoyable, fast, casual dinner!

This location is closing in early January. Nice size store with plenty of product left. It's located basically in a strip mall. My visit was rushed as I was on a 30 minute break from work and my commute was longer than anticipated... thanks Waze. 

Came here to get last minute material in an attempt to make a tree skirt last minute because paying over $50 for one was not in my holiday budget. The material I choose was 50% off. I purchased 2 yards of 2 different fabrics for under $10. The lady who helped me was very knowledgeable and engaging. She explained to me the one of the yards she had purchased about 4 years prior and made a friend curtains and the material was 5x the price I was paying.

If your in the area give it a whirl...

If you like craft beer and creative food this is a godsend for the area. Great environment and great service!

As far as food, the consensus seems to be that their sweet potato fries are good and tasty. You can pair them with a Buttermilk Fried Chicken/Seitan Sandwich, or a strong and meaty Bison Burger and more. They also have a tantalizing Italian Grilled Cheese and some Mussel dishes!

As far as seating, you have three choices; sidewalk seating, restaurant seating, and bar seating. I love the environment, it reminds me of a tiny castle. I mean this place was on the boob-tube partially because it's a nice and cozy place to film, I'm sure. They got that aged wood look going on; candles, antique lights, gutted pianos. 

*Tues open mic   *Wed quizzo 

*Only criticism would be the curry dipping sauce (it's apparently Tabasco dipping sauce but tastes like curry). I love Indian food, and I love this sauce, but it doesn't go with errrything. Another dipping sauce option would be a huge slam-dunkaroo.

The body shop was great - Carmen was a peach.  However the repair folks were the pits.  They were a bit surly to deal with but agreed to do a couple of minor things while the car was at *their* body shop.  The body work took a day longer than planned, not a problem, but when I arrived to pick up my car I was told that they did not have time to do the repair work.  

So based on their attitude and the fact that they did not do what was promised I do not think that I will go back.

Best sushi in Nashville if you're looking for the real stuff like nigiri, handrolls, simple cut rolls, etc!!! High quality fish every time. I ask myself why I don't come more often every time I eat there. Thank you, Mr. Lee!

I vowed also to write a positive review for this place because I feel like it deserved one.

It is a little far and off the beaten path but since Justin is a die-hard fan/St. Louis ex-pat, we were determined to make our way over here.

Love the plenitude of TV's.  Love the toasted ravioli (which apparently is either a St. Louis or general Missouri specialty, from my experience).  Their wings are decent as well.  And it's not greasy or sticky the way other wing places are known to be.

We've gone here twice now...and will keep coming back.

The reviews are right. This place is massive. Yet there are only two windows opened at a time. They don't distinguish between passport or mail which is really irritating when all you need to do is drop off a package. Half the staff is cranky/slow. When you've been waiting in line for a half hour, attitude is the last thing you need. Wish there was another one closer to my office. Avoid if possible.

The atmosphere, experience, and food were all delicious. Granted, there's little to compare it to, but excellent nonetheless. Get the Sambusa for an appetizer. Go ahead and order 2. Great value and friendly service.

Very interesting menu! We had the ultimate omelet (4.5 stars) and the cream cheese crab omelet (1 star for being overly fishy tasting. I will say that the crab was obviously real crab meat so that's a plus but this being our first seafood omelette - overly fishy made for not a big fan. Beignets were great & we rated 4 stars! Coffee was delish. They have outside dining which in this pandemic time is a big plus. They did remove the crab omelette from the bill because we did not finish/like it. This was most gracious of them. Their brunch menu looks very interesting so we will most definitely give this place another try!

It's OK. Some people can't stop talking about how great this place is but that just shows an extreme lack of intelligence. The chicken salad is mediocre. Nothing compared to homemade. The mac and cheese had little to no flavor.
One of the best BBQ joints in all the US! They don't just make great BBQ in Texas and Alabama only!

I ordered the scrambled eggs with alligator and it was really good. The crawfish was also delicious. Overall the food is great here. The customer service is mediocre and slow. I would come again for the food but know not to expect much attention from the wait staff.

We stopped in here for a drink after Sunday brunch.  I've been here a million times, but that was years ago.  Nothing has changed here...a bunch of South Tampa bros hitting on the 3 bartenders working there.  We did a one and done because it definitely wasn't our scene.

This restaurant totally blew me away! I have never been to such a high quality vegan restaurant, it was an amazing experience! The ambiance was absolutely beautiful with the beautiful bar, and outdoor trees with hanging lights. The bartender Billy was awesome, as well as all the staff with amazing service. I cannot say anything bad about this place - really if you are from out of town and want the best vegan dining experience with the best food you have to go to Oliver's.

Okay so I generally used to love this salon. My usual nail tech is amazing. However, she had been unavailable for a while and I just do not like when anyone else does my nails because there is always something that goes wrong. Before today, I had this place at 5 stars. Today it drops down to 3. Here's why; I like to give my nails a break from acrylics so I decided to take them off today. From what I understand, the best and most gentle way to remove these is to soak in acetone and gently scrub away the acrylic with a tool. Well, today my acrylics were ripped off my real nails. It made a mess! I had acrylic pieces in my hair and on my clothes. Also, instead of ripping off the fake nail, the nail tech almost ripped off my actual nail. It hurt so much. Once everything was ripped off, she filed my natural nails down. Omg what a nightmare. None of my nails were filed evenly, some were longer and shorter than others, some were round, and some were square. I actually ended up going to target on my way home. I bought a nail file and fixed it myself. I shouldn't have to fix something I ended up paying for at the salon. My boyfriend even told me my nails looked ugly when I came home and that's how I knew it was not worth the money. I could have ripped the nails off myself and filed them better at home for free. The only reason this is still at 3 stars and not 1 star is because I love my usual nail tech and I'll be going back when she's available.
Very good breakfast, priced good. I will go back. Service excellent. They only serve breakfast and lunch though.

I'll never stay at the omni hotel. I've heard that they hire contractors and subcontractors that don't pay their employees for the work they do! For a hotel that charges up to $300 a night for a room, this doesn't seem like a fair way to treat employees!

This place is great to hang with friends to grab a quick beer and watch the game. Friendly bar tenders manage the three bar areas [2 indoor, one outdoor (weather/season permitting)], and there is also a dining room for dinner/family dinner. Multiple TVs cover the walls with all varieties of sports games. Themed nights: open-mic opportunities, trivia nights, etc., as well as nightly food/drink specials Never over crowded (unless its a playoff Phillies/Eagles game), great prices for food and drinks, as well as very delicious food and drinks. Surplus parking, quiet neighborhood, great atmosphere!

When our planned cocktail location for the evening was unexpectedly closed for the evening (grumble grumble), we turned to Yelp to help us solve our conundrum. 

The musts: it had to be within easy walking distance, it had to be decently rated, and we had to be able to get a drank there.

Louie's Wine Dive came up in the search and covered all three musts, so we headed on over. It was early in the evening on a Monday, so it wasn't even remotely busy around 5pm.
The space is lovely. Clean and bright. The tall wine rack behind the bar is visually appealing, that's for sure!

We seated ourselves at the bar and began perusing the menu. I only wish I had looked a little closer because they had some pretty decent happy hour deals! But they were listed on the back and neither the servers nor the bartender made it known that on Mondays...happy hour is all day long! I probably would have made some changes to my ordering to get more bang for my buck.
Some of the wines by the glass are more expensive than I am willing to shell out. I ended up ordering the rose listed on the specials marquee. It was not my favorite, but I figured I would get wine at a wine bar! But I sort of wish I had gone with my original desire of the evening and ordered a cocktail. They have some and they looked pretty tasty!

We also saw brussels sprouts on the menu and knew immediately that we would be ordering them to share. And they...were...DELICIOUS!! So good we almost ordered a SECOND serving! So so good.
The service was pretty great, the not mentioning happy hour deals aside. Our waters were never empty, we never had to flag anyone down for refills or to order food or request the check. I enjoyed our visit and wouldn't be against returning of visiting another location. If the by-the-glass prices were a little more approachable, this would absolutely be my kind of place at which to be a regular!

Dined here on a recent Saturday and was not disappointed. The weather was beautiful so we took advantage of their ample outdoor seating. We ordered one appetizer, a salad with chicken and a pizza. The food tasted good and we were definitely satisfied by the meal. I would go back again if up on 202 for any reason. I hope that they are doing enough business to weather these challenging times.

Wow! So far quite happy. My order was perfect. What made this a great experience was the ability to order online and delivered timely. I received exactly what was ordered with nothing missing. 

I will never go back to Cheer King in Irvington. Fire up your smartphone and download the app.

We had our kitchen remodeled by Til Mar Design after a successful bathroom renovation in 2015 and did not receive the same quality as we previously did. 1. Our under the cabinet lighting is of the stick on variety that was purchased at Home Depot, not the type that is attached to the cabinets. In places where the cabinets dip and where the microwave is, the lights are attached with tape! If a bulb goes out the entire light strip would need to be replaced. 2. The countertop fabricators arrived to measure on the same day that grout had been installed to the floor. We now have grout that is permanently dirty where they walked on the wet fresh grout. 3. Wood filler pieces for our cabinets were ordered incorrectly (or the wrong pieces were used in one place, leaving another place without the correct piece.) This caused a major delay in the completion of the project and the pieces that were eventually ordered are a different shade of wood than the other cabinets. 4. The porcelain tile floor has so many uneven tiles that we can actually stub our toes on the high corners of the tiles. Even with these problems, we did not ask for any consideration or any items to be changed. We did not complain or ask if anything could be done to correct the above problems. What we did ask is that someone come back to our house to adjust our cabinets so the doors line up and the drawers lay evenly with the fronts of the other drawers and cabinets. We offered to pay for this service but the work has not been scheduled and our messages have been ignored.
We would not recommend TilMar Design.

This is actually my first Yelp review, but I thought it was important to warn visitors and hopefully get the Sheraton management to fix things.

I stayed at the Sheraton the week of October 20 for 4 nights.  The room itself was fine overall, what you would expect from a Sheraton- comfortable bed with nice linens, large flat screen tv, etc...  The bathroom could have been a little bit cleaner, but I always bring Lysol wipes anyway to get the door knobs, light switches, remotes and other items that are always on the "dirtiest items lists".

During my 4 nights at this hotel I had only one hot shower, 4 lukewarm showers, and 2 cold ones.  After asking the front desk to look into this on my second day, I was told they came up to my room to fix things.  That night I took the most frigid shower I've every taken in my life!  I believe that the hotel simply does not have the equipment needed to support hot water for all the rooms.  I stay at about 15-25 hotels a year and have never experienced anything like this before.

My other complaint is the excruciatingly slow internet.  I even paid extra for the fast service.  Forget about getting any work done in the evening, the internet is so bad that it could not even load a full webpage without timing out.  

So if you are okay with no hot water and unusable internet, this is the hotel for you!

I was in last night for the last half of the colts game. I hadn't been in for a few month's since there was a management change for the worse! The waiter was more interested in cleaning off empty tables instead of taking care of customers that were still there. I Use to go at least once a week and always had a good experience. I would take my family there as well, but not anymore I gave it another chance after the management change. The restroom was in desperate need of some cleaning!

Very disappointed our experience here.  We were told they were closed on Nov. 23 at 11:58 PM.  There were people in the bar and all doors were open  Hours on their website showed they were open until midnight. Reviewed them on Facebook and the manager pulled the wrong day and time and called us liars and that he didn't want it business anyway. I wish I had read the negative reviews before choosing to eat there.  I've donated items to Villa Lobos, walked dogs and fine the tour.  The shop is also cool.  Still a fan, just will help the rescue in other ways.

I looove the pizza for sure but it is extremely hard to put an order by phone!!!! There was one time when the girl put me on hold and said she would pick up the phone soon, but then I was held for more than 15 mins with a sudden hangup. Just now I called them and they wanted to put me on hold longer after 1 min because they wanted to serve the patrons in store first... I mean why don't you have a person taking care of the phone alone? I'm sorry I just cannot wait!

Food is okay, service is indifferent. BLT my favorite. Their shakes are horrible-the only shake I've ever thrown away without finishing. They're the only option in the complex, and it shows as they don't value customer service. If you're in the building and limited on time, stop by and you'll get fed. Otherwise, pass on this place.

Unfortunately there isn't an option for 0 stars so 1 star will suffice. Like several families today, we were not allowed to watch the PG-13 movie we happily paid $60+ for since this was being viewed in the "Adult theatre" at 1:40 PM the day after Christmas. I understand the need to provide an "Adult only movie experience" and can respect that, but to have very poor advertising explaining that no one under the age of 18 can watch movies in the "adult theatre" regardless of time of day or even if it was rated PG is ludicrous.  

We watched close to 20 people get turned away who came from over 90 minutes away to spend their holiday watching a nice movie at the AMC Painters Crossing. Not once did the manager apologize or even help find a reasonable solution.  Buyer beware, the tickets nor do the website explain this 18 and over rule. 

AMC needs MUCH better advertising and more importantly better customer service and management. I will not be returning to this place.

Been twice sushi is on point, but service is hit and miss one 5 star experience and one 1 star experience.  There are tons of AYCE in Reno and ijji is right in line with prices of others.  The service when the place wasn't busy was slow, and items ordered with are party of 4 never came (this is common when sitting down) but looking at what didn't come it was the obvious more expensive items.  REALLY ijji?  The 5 star visit was during a time when the place was packed and we (3) sat at the bar.  Incredible chef nailed down our taste and gave recommendations, glass never empty.
My advice, sit at the bar.

I have been there twice and each time was better then the previous. The avocado toast is a must and if lunch is in order get the tomato basil soup. 
Staff is alway friendly and helpful  while the atmosphere warm and inviting. Making the best of these times for a meal  includes a trio to Marathon.

Delicious Mexican food. Friendly staff. Located in a fun area of Soulard. I would not recommend ordering food at the bar. Wait for a table - it's worth it. Build your own burrito is the way to go. They are huge.

Was a little nervous but had to eat in house, so here I am very pleasantly surprised. The food is very good. My burger is perfectly cooked and delicious coleslaw side. My Mom says her smoked chicken quesadilla is the best she ever had. Gloria, our server, is a delight and very efficient. 4 stars folks, come on over.

Delicious food and beer. 
 I highly recommend. 
Definitely get the pretzel and cheese dip
The Macaroni and cheese is awesome too. 
I had the pork belly with succotash, amazing! So much flavor.
The cheese crisps were good too. 

The waiter was not super familiar with the beer selection, but he was friendly. 

My boyfriend and I will definitely go back.

We had seen this restaurant on a food tv show and I wanted to try it just for the grilled cheese. So, that's what I got. The bread was great. The cheese mixture could have been better. Just not what I was looking for. And the tomato bisque wasn't as good as I expected. Not much flavor. But we had the pretzel with cheese as an appetizer and it was awesome! The cheese was really good. We went late lunch on a Saturday and it was not crowded at all so you think the service would have been a little better. Atmosphere was really neat though. Prices were right too. It was ok but we probably won't go back.

Went for $1.50 taco Tuesday.  Tacos were small and all the meats were over cooked.  Flavors were still somewhat there but not good.  Staff wasn't efficient with taking orders and fulfilling orders.  I waited 30 mins for food and I guess my food was sitting in a box for 20 of those minutes as they were unorganized.  I will not go back, save your time and just go to Lolies.

Food is great but I won't be back.   I was there with my son and it was crowded.   We took the only open table which could seat six.    There were three young people waiting for a table.   The hostess was incredibly rude and asked us to sit at the bar, which had three open seats, so they could have the table.   They were more important than us how????   I'll never go back!!

The space is the clear winner! An excellent way to be the neighborhood coffee shop is to provide space for all your clients. Shaded patio in back was fantastic, small area for kiddos (snack cups were fab too). Agree service could be quicker but the $1 refills on coffee encourage you to settle in and relax. They aren't promising quick so if you can be patient and relax and read or chat the wait is no biggie. Everything salmon bagel was bland and the vanilla Latte ran a bit sweet. Staff were friendly. Decor was eclectic.

My dirty little secret? I like Panera Bread. The Sierra Turkey and the Smokehouse Turkey are my favorite sandwiches. A couple of the items are seasonal (Tuscan Chicken Sandwich) so don't get too attached to one item on the menu. The salads are pretty good too. If I can't make up my mind I usually get the "you pick 2" so I get soup or a salad with a sandwich. 

Why only three stars? The parking. OMG the horrible parking lot that I must circle 3-4 times to then park across the street at 717.

Free wi-fi, great sandwiches and coffee, and best of all - they have a rewards card!

Shoe Shine in the St Louis airport would be a 1-star if it weren't for the witty convo.  I forgot his name, but he was always there, even with no foot traffic, and he'd talk about the Cardinals, about his family, about the women passing as he shouted out to nearly all of them.  On the 3 connections with Southwest through STL, he was there, and I always get a shoe shine in the airport if I have time.  Every time the shoe shine was the same, and the chit chat was the same.

Giving 5 stars for quick service, HUGE portions and prices. I'm not comparing this place to other Mediterranean places I've been too. I'm looking at it in terms of value and it taste good. I got the chunky chicken salad.  Comes with pita and hummus and I had enough to take home. Don't need a lot of dressing since variety of flavors in veggies.  My man got the Tex Mex wrap and it was huge and looked exactly like the picture. Well done! The only negative was the smell of Fabalouso but glad the restaurant is clean!
Enormous, delicious ribs! Long line to sit in, better to take out for me. Excellent sides, great service. I ALWAYS stop here when I'm in St.Louis!

Went in ordered and paid for our pizza's.   Alexis was very friendly and told us it would be 15-20mins before it would be ready.   We came back about 20mins later and Rachel was on the register.   She was very rude to other guests and we had to wait still, our pizza's were not ready.  Finally when we got our pizza's. Rachel said it was going to be about 3mins for the breadsticks.   I ask why everything wasn't ready at the same time and she said she gave our breadsticks to someone else.   We had been standing there waiting the last 10mins!  Rachel just walked away with no concern no apology nothing.   When I asked for refund so I could just go somewhere else, after wait 45mins all together for my family's dinner, she didn't seem to care.  Still no apology!
Horrible experience...cold food both times I've gone.  My husband and I won't be back to this location.

Finally, after driving by everyday for about 2 years on-and-off from/to work, decided to try Back To Cuba. My first Cuban culinary experience was earlier this year in Miami, so I was hooked. Going in, I already knew not to compare to Miami, I kept an open-mind and this review is solely based on Back to Cuba. The place is small, 8 small tables, plus some bistro seating outdoors. Its in a small shopping strip attached to a gas station. Getting parking and going in-and-out can be challenging. We went in around 11:30, luckily not too busy. The menu is displayed on their wall, and can be a little confusing/overwhelming. We just asked what they recommended and we went with that. I had the pork roast plate and my husband has the cuban sandwich with fries. We also tried their chicken empanada. The roast pork could have used a bit more flavor, and maybe some spice/acidic. I really wished I could've substitute the yuca for plantains. The rice and beans were just average. The cubano was extra crispy. And the pork was chewy and dry, I couldn't even eat it. Also lacked some spice/acidic. Fries were good, and the empanada was as well. I definitely want to give it another try, perhaps try something different. Service was good, food came out fast.

I was visiting from Ontario and looked up a place where I can purchase pipes. Now I got the usual  head shops and such. But this shop came up on several searches and I am glad it did. I was looking for 3 matching pipes in the 75 dollar range and in most shops that would be impossible, but not here. The staff were great pointing me to the pipes which were in my price range and suggeting the ones they had doubles and possibly triples of. I felt like I was at home and well informed. The selection is as good as the shops I visit in Nashville, Montreal, Toronto and many others around North America. When I move to Alberta I will be shopping here often.

An authentic Korean Restaurant in Greenwood?!

I was shocked (in a good way) that a Korean Restaurant appeared in my google search so close to home. When I moved down to the south side I left behind the few Korean restaurants on the north and east side of Indy. I was CRAVING Korean food today so I stopped by after work.     

The three ajumma's (respectful word for women) run this restaurant called Unnineh (sister's). They renovated and recently opened Sister's on Feb 12th.  

When I entered I was greeted and sat at my table. First thing on my table was a nice warm glass of tea. Following the tea healthy poritions of bonchon (side dishes) took over my table. The bonchon was all very tasty and they would stop by and ask if I wanted more of a certain kind which was nice. I ordered the Yukgaejang (spicy beef and vegetable soup) which came boiling in a clay pot! If you get a soup that comes this way, be careful as it is extremely hot. I would recommend letting it simmer down and indulge in some more bonchon! The Yukgaejang was cooked just right, just like grandma makes it in Korea. 

The experience was excellent, the ajumma's are all very nice and accommodating, and the food was authentically delicious. This is definitely a must try for south siders. I will definitely be coming here often! 

If you aren't into Korean food or haven't tried it before I would recommend the following to get you started:
Jjajangmyun - black bean noodles YUM!
Bibimbap - rice, veggies, and meat that you mix up!
Bulgogi - broiled beef, can't go wrong with that!
Kalbi - korean staple. It's main ingredients are soy sauce and brown sugar which makes it a very sweet and tender short rib!
The food tastes good. There is a good atmosphere. There is a wide selection of beers. Always a good place to stop.

We arrived by boat. The weather was perfect. It was sunny.  We sat outside.  We drank beer. We ate fish dip for appetizers. We enjoyed the atmosphere and the views were great on this perfect day in March.  I ordered an oyster po boy sandwich and oh my golly it was delicious. The oysters were perfectly fried and the bread oh the bread it was so delicious and for sure a NOLA recipe.  My mouth is watering just thinking about it.  This was a perfect day on the boat with friends. This was a perfect place for lunch and I can't wait to return when I'm in the St Pete area.  The service was great and we loved every minute of our time especially the food and the view.  A must if you are in the area.

Spend your time and money elsewhere because this place is not equipped to handle reservations or groups of any size.  The drinks are watered down and the atmosphere is cramped and dirty. 

Bachelorette parties flock to this place for a fun night or singing and dancing so I made a reservation to include open bar for our group for 2 hours, a round of shots, a table near the pianos, bachelorette decorations on the table and a roast where the guest of honor would be called up onto stage for a little fun.  We arrived on a Saturday night right on time for our 10:00pm reservation and the hostess was completely caught off guard, as if she wasn't expecting our group.  She happily collected our money and fastened colored bracelets to our wrists and tried to send us on our merry way.  When I inquired about our table, she looked at me as if I had three eyes and scurried off to improvise.  And improvise, she did.  She seated us at two dirty tabled that she quickly pulled together with no decorations in sight.  When I inquired, she grabbed the manager who informed me that our decorations were left out and as a result, were ruined.  He then offered to give me a few bucks back.  When I began to express my dissatisfaction, he hastily offered a free bottle of cheap champagne for the table.  In effort to keep the night positive, I took the money and accepted the bottle of champagne.  

By 11:55pm, we had not received our round of shots and our guest of honor had not yet been brought onto stage for her roast.  Once again, I had to seek out the hostess about both and she ran up on stage to inform the pianist.  At that point, everyone in the bar was hammered and our group was losing interest in the cheesy show-tune performances and it was clear that her roast was an after-thought.  

My advice to bridal parties trying to plan a fun, hassle free night out with the girls: Don't waste your time or money at Jolly's.  The entertainment is mediocre at best, the drinks are severely watered down and the sticky tables/floors will ruin those carefully selected heels.

This hotel is a Phoenix and far exceeded my expectations.  I am not sure what it was before but now, the remodeled part is nice, clean and modern. They just opened a couple of weeks ago. Upon check in, I was warmly greeted by everyone - even security. 
My room was on the back side and I had to enter from a side door but felt safe. My room looked like it had been furnished by West Elm! I wasn't expecting a full galley kitchen with an eat at bar, but it was there. What I really liked was the K-cup coffee maker. Unfortunately,  all the coffee was decaf. When I tried the front desk the phone didn't work but I used my cell. I was told that ? Would be right there. I was in my PJs so I asked if they would leave it outside the door. She explained that the delivery was by a robot. It was the cutest R2D2 looking thing! The moment I opened the door, his little top popped open and there were the K-Cups - along with a few complimentary items. I only took out the K-Cups but was instructed that all items in there were mine! 
The bath soap is truly boutique worthy. My room overlooked a Zen Garden that had a paved path and a waterfall.  

They have wi-fi, complimentary breakfast, and a work out center. This will be my go to place for my Tampa business as it is close to where I need to be and there are so many great places to eat nearby. Do yourself a favor. Stay here (in the nicely remodeled rooms!) The employees, Staff and management are awesome.

Nice place with a good selection of Peruvian treats!

Not as good in my option as Don Pedro Peruvian restaurant was ! (Just sayin)  

Prices are the first thing that gets your attention in this place (high) but who knows don't have the food yet?

Well once the food arrived from our maybe 18 year old very in experienced waiter, I felt yes I was correct on the value vs food thing! 

Also service quality vs price point is off!

The food was very tasty and of good quality just not much of it and everything more is of extra price!

Overall not a bad place but not gonna have me running back soon!

Just OK!

Nice atmosphere, we sat for 20 minutes before our Waiter took our order and 45 minutes to get our food. Chips and salsa were good! Prices are high in comparison to other favorites! Chicken chimichanga was good size, chicken over boiled no flavor, you get tiny portion of rice and beans which were ok. Service was poor waiter was not attentive. I have been to this location before where service was better and food tasty! My experience with this establishment is that each visit is inconsistent as far as food flavor? This visit was not pleasing.
The Pizza was wonderful and the place was super clean.  A win win all around.

I found a bug at the shower and I could barely sleep in my hotel room due to the high heat because hot rooms are uncomfortable for me. I really hope my family doesn't stay in this hotel again. I prefer the Wyndham in Trenton. Also, the noises that the people were making were loud and hotel staff aren't there to tell them to stop. So, I'll try my best to not come back to the hotel. What's bad is that this is near Trenton and Trenton isn't the best place to be in for that matter. Though the breakfast was good and the beds aren't too terrible. Just people making noises and it being dirty is the problem.

We both ordered a standard cheese steak with provolone...one with and one without onions.  

They were OK but for all the hype in these reviews we were pretty disappointed. They were flavorful but greasy.  

They have some pretty great bread there, which probably saved the sandwich, but the meat to bread ratio was kind of low.  

Also, the dining room is pretty bad. Don't bring a date here, just take out if you can get to it quickly.  The sandwiches are going to get pretty soggy if you wait.

We might go back there someday, but there's nothing really exciting us.

My waitress was friendly and sweet.  However, I hate to be asked "how is everything" literally before I even bite into it. Anyway, the food presentation was great, looked delicious but it wasn't even hot. The food was barely warm but I could tell the potato wedges were fresh. The restaurant itself appeared very dirty and messy.  A table near mine was stabilized by about 6 wet naps so the leg didn't wobble. The floors were disgusting and needed vacuumed badly.  When I sat down I put my arm on  the table to scoot down the booth and something saturated my shirt sleeve. It really grossed me out and nobody knew what it was. I absolutely will not return to this location and don't recommend it purely due to cleanliness or lack thereof.

Been there once and had delivery once. Ordered the Turkey Club, Chicago Dog and Lamb Gyro. The turkey Club was OK but the mixed greens were partially rotten (black and slimy). Chicago Dog was delivered as was the Gyro. Both were room temperature. I was no more than 2 miles away. Once again the mixed greens on the gyro were partially rotten. Won't be going back ever.

There are two lines -- one for your meal, and one for add-ons such as fries and drinks, which I think does cut down the length of time you have to wait in line. 

I ordered the chicken tenders sandwich and was disappointed that it was basically a long loaf of bread with dry breaded chicken and some melted cheese. The bread was overwhelming the chicken. Adding tomatoes, lettuce, and several different kinds of sauce would have improved the experience because it was so dry! Try eating it without a soda or water--nope! The fries were good but not amazing, which is the erroneous impression I got. The fries were also served on baking paper, so they spilled everywhere. I mean 500 paper plates cost around $4 or something at the grocery store. Really, baking paper?

What I noticed is that the employees were all tough-looking macho men with tattoos and hairy arms (where are the female employees?). Regardless, they're friendly and attentive, which is a plus.

This is definitely a place where local employees go to grab lunch--it was certainly busy. And what I love about Philly, and this place too, is the diversity. It's great to sit down and eat a meal (albeit subpar) and see a range of shapes, sizes, colors, and ethnicities. 

Editors, designers, and English-language mavens might find this place's menu jarring--inconsistent capitalization, poor syntax, misplaced punctuation, haphazard color scheme, and extraneous italics.

Any trip to Philadelphia would not be complete without at least eating one cheesesteak sandwich so on this occasion had a few hours before my flight I decided why not try both at once. 

I went with the cheese steak with onions, mushrooms and provolone cheese and have to say that Pats compared to Gino's had the upper hand on multiple levels and I felt as if while Geno's was more friendly and looked nicer on the block the food was not comparable.

Starting with steak I felt as if it really had no flavor to it and it almost seemed as if it was like gyro meat cut from a pole and wasn't cooked on the grill didn't have all the flavor. The onions were very undercooked it seemed as if they were just kind of thrown into the mix the mushrooms same exact thing and actually we're not even warm and felt as if they just came out of the can. The bread was rather soggy and really lacking for a cheese steak.

The positive side, the people at Gino's were very friendly and the store looks a lot nicer not to mention the line was shorter but the good quality was not worth returning for what I ordered. Oh, and they charged me an extra $.50 for the same item I got across the street

Next time in Philly I will do the same thing and see If I get better results, but for now its really only worth 2 stars...

Art with a personal touch. Lots of classes to take, lots of variety of well-made local art on the walls, jewelry and smaller items, and gorgeous handcrafted furnishings. If you don't see anything you like, you can talk to the owner, she will actually help you track down the right piece of art for you made locally. Support local, support the arts, support the newly revitalizing downtown core.

Simply some of the best bbq j have had. This is based on my experience with some elite bbq joints (Memphis, Kansas City and Nashville). The ribs are my personal favorite, and the sides are all good (although I'm sure most love the sweet potato fries as the number one side). The staff is no nonsense and gets hour food with lightning speed. This is done almost out of necessity given the size of the line that is usually stretched out of the door

I was here for a baby shower on a Saturday, and although the entire group, including myself, was really happy with the food, I couldn't help but feel very disappointed with the customer service. 

A private room was booked for the shower, and the menu was preselected. For my primi, I chose to get the lobster bisque which was fantastic, to say the least. It was rich and creamy, and loaded with lobster. I really enjoyed every last spoonful!

For my entree, I selected the grilled salmon Fresca which the menu said came with pesto vinaigrette, feta, diced tomatoes, and balsamic glaze. What also came with the dish, but was not mentioned on the menu, were sweet potatoes and asparagus. Not sure why it was left off the menu, but no complaints on my end, because I was happy to see them on my plate! The diced sweet potatoes were, by far, my favorite part about the entire dish. My friend who got the salmon as well said the same thing!

Unlike the food, the customer service was a huge letdown. The staff assigned to our party were extremely disorganized, and even seemed inattentive and negligent at times. There were many instances when they didn't ask people for drink orders or refills, and I can note a couple times when they brought out the wrong drinks and dishes to the table. 

Since this was an organized party that had been planned in advance, it was also disappointing that the entire lunch ran late. The room had been booked for two hours, but the event ended up going almost three hours because of how long it took for the food to be served. We ended up not having time to play one of the games we had planned, and many of the attendees had to leave before the event actually ended due to it running late. 

While Brio impressed me with the food, I would really be hesitant about organizing a party here myself.

The absolute worst customer service I've ever experienced. After signing in they told me there was 1 person ahead of me, there were 4 employees & only 1 other customer besides me, the one employee helped the other customer while the other 3 employees stood around & did nothing.
