After waiting a half hour I asked how much longer I'd have to wait & I was told the same thing, there's 1 person ahead of you, I asked why I couldn't be helped by one of the other 3 employees and I was told they were busy when they were clearly doing nothing. In the end I waited 45 minutes for nothing and was treated very rudely during my wait. I will never step foot back in that store again & am ending my contract with Verizon immediately.

I wish I could pack this place up and take it home with me. Great drinks, great food, and great staff. Atmosphere is kick-ass too! The food is basically modern interpretations of southern comfort food (and vegetarian friendly for my fellow veggies out there). Though it's a secret I kind of want to keep to myself, I can't give this place enough love.

We were pleasantly surprised at how nice it was. The exterior is plain, but it has a pretty high-ceilinged lobby and pool area. Our room was very spacious and included a sink, microwave, fridge, and small dining table and sitting area. The king bed was really comfortable with nice pillows and sheets that felt like much better quality than most motels/hotels in the same price range. The rate included free full breakfast. We would stay here again.

The daiquiris are good. It was packed the night I went so good took about an hour to come out! It's not all the way authentic either. Overall a fun place to go to with co workers after work or just to get some cheap margaritas

We have been using C&L for nearly 20 years and are very statisfied with their preformance and will be using them in the future to maintain our equipment.   We are glad to see Mitch carrying on the family tradition.

My family and I have been going here ONLY in the AM, better  Dim sum than others, we did try the other one down the street, not as good, if you are looking for typical Chinese food, there are others. The siomai, the chicken feet (yes!) and new dish: the calamari here is okay, eggplant is good,  our kids love it here and the nice fish in tank for .. Well, you know, lunch  you know what I mean-
Food is good, however the service is horrible. We went this past week and after not being waited on for quite a while, we got up, walked out and walked over to Petrini's and had a great meal.

Their pizza is good but their service is HORRIBLE! I've never been treated so rudely, for no reason at all. I called in an order to eat at the restaurant because I had 2 small children and knew they wouldn't sit long enough to place an order and then eat.
Once I got there they refused to seat us in the dining room, told us we had to pay in advance for our pizza at the counter and would not give us full service. So we found a table, sat down and sure enough they refused to wait on us and were very rude about it. We had to beg for plates and silverware.
I'll be taking my business down the road to Amici.
I only had the pizza. Nothing special, see-through pepperoni, and you get about 1.5 slices less than a large round pizza for the same price elsewhere.

The travel store is decent other than classic PA Turnpike ripoff prices on everything.  But the BK restaurant is a nightmare! Slow, rude, inferior service. But the food was average which I guess was the absolute best thing about it. Starbucks seemed pretty good though, the coffees were flavored properly and the staff seemed to be fairly knowledgeable.

Don't go if you are in a hurry.  But go if you want one of the best meals in town in a truly historic and beautiful setting.  The Oak Bar is also a refined setting for a nice drink after work. Surprisingly good take out food, too, especially the blue cheese burger. Friendly service.

The place is nice but I didn't like the chairs. I'm short and my feet couldn't reach the water if I sat back to to feel the massage. Also I wanted hot stone but no one even asked me for an extras. I felt rushed to pick a color and sit down. For the price the foot massage a joke, also I'm use to getting a shoulder massage with my gel manicure and I barely received a hand massage. I will not be going back.

poor service, would not use, not thorough even when I provided a lot of details etc.  does not seem to be a well rated BMW repair shop in the area.  Fortunately moved to Az. where there are a lot of good independent shops around that are way more competent.

Would not buy a BMW in Reno knowing that service is suspect, but I assume they are somewhat better if you buy a new one from them, still very poor experience compared to most other cities I have lived in.

Excellent food, something different! Have been here several times and have had great food and good service. I recently found out that they sell their house dressing, it sells out quickly though. Pleasant atmosphere. 
Next time I will take some food pictures to share.

DO NOT USE THIS AVIS.... whatever you do !!! First I get there in the 4th and I waited 45 min to see somebody then when I saw someone they said my car wouldn't b ready for 2 MORE hrs.. so they offered me an Ford Expedition at 10 extra a day fine I took (I had no choice) so I bring the car back with the fuel a little under full and then I get an extra charge for 130.00 dollars the guy in the phone said I brought the car in under half a tank !!! They are LIARS !!! They credited me back a couple dollars which means they KNOW THEY ARE crooks... if you want a nice experience with efficient service do yourself a favor and AVOID THIS PLACE AT ALL COSTS!!!

Went for the lunch buffet on Sept 5.  Only $8.99 per person + cost for drinks.  For two, the bill was $20 with an on-line coupon.  A true value.
Asian Buffet has an extensive set of offerings including many chicken and seafood dishes as well as sushi.  I am a chicken lover and had 6 different chicken dish samples.  I was totally satisfied.
They also have all of the traditional offerings. - lo mein, chow mein, veggies such as string bean and mushroom dishes, and sweet and sour dishes, etc.
They serve both spring rolls and veggie egg rolls.
There is a variety of desserts including 8 different self serve ice creams.  One was even sugar free.
The place is clean, the food is fresh and constantly replaced as eaten.  The staff is Chinese and have accents, but that makes the whole meal more authentic.  They were attentive, refilling drinks and clearing plates.
There was a steady flow of customers but the place was not crowded or noisy.  We stayed for two hours; no one rushed us.
Overall I recommend Asian Buffet if you want a very good Chinese meal.

I've been once for lunch before so I thought I'd get some take out this time for lunch: the food is good but the service is lacking. As I walked up to the serving table the waitress acknowledged the person that entered right after me. The owner came out and asked what I wanted. Without being told about the choices I picked the first one with various pasta shapes and was told it's the most popular. He offered me "salad" which is just lettuce with Italian dressing. I had to ask for the bread before I left or I wouldn't have gotten any. The waitress asked me if I was paying card without even telling me the total owed.  After I paid I thanked them but wasn't acknowledged once payment was received. Hopefully their dinner service makes up for the crappy lunch service.
I think I've been to this store twice or thrice! I came yesterday because they are closing out & wanted to check things! I did buy a couple of items, the most I got was stuff at 80%off!

My food was fair.  I ordered a fried shrimp and oyster platter.  It was filling and tasty, though nothing special.  My friend ordered an etouffee.  He's polite and doesn't like to complain about food in public. But he only took two bites of his seemingly soupy plate of rice and decided he was done.  Our waiter, a young man with dreadlocks, acted like he didn't want to be there.  It felt a bit rushed and he didn't make himself approachable for questions or even desert requests.  I wish I hadn't given him the low tip I gave him because he was nasty.  

There are other fabulous restaurants to dine at that where the food is good and the service just as good.  Check out Huck Finns on Magazine Street.  Stay away from Gumbo Pot!

It's a bell...some of the staff were super friendly, others barely nodded at you. The information in the center is nice, as I didn't really know anything about the bell.....it's also far smaller than I thought it was going to be. Go when there isn't a line though, it's definitely not worth waiting for a huge line!

My bike been in crossroads now for 2. Months in every time i call to see if its fix the mechanic guy name pete tell me my bike is fu#k up and it needs a ton of stuff..very unprofessional and till this day my bike still never got fix..it really mess my summer up..will not recommend no one take there bike there if this continue
The worst publix deli Experience I've ever had. They are so slow and they skimp with the veggies. I hate when I don't pick up my lunch in Brandon before coming to work.

Still not sure about this place after three pies. The flavors are good, no doubt...  But we feel like the pizza could be a little more cooked.. It was soggy. The slices are very limp, on every pie we had. I guess we just don't like it limp.

Be prepared for a big crowd and a wait.  The place is also warm and tables tightly packed.  It's louder than most.  Service is efficient. We had an omelet and crab Benedict.  Both were good as were the home fries.  Known for brunch.  I'd go again.

My daughters and I had dinner at Rawr last night. I had never been to a raw restaurant, and wasn't sure what to expect. I was so impressed with the chef and his creations. Everything he prepared was flavorful, had interesting spices and sauces, and was beautifully presented. The beet ravioli and chocolate mousse were especially good. The chef is friendly and obviously really enjoys what he is doing. 
   However, the reason for only three stars is because of his assistant who apparently has not had any training in food handling. She was a young woman with very long, dark hair which she kept hanging down the front of her. It was not tied back, as is protocol for someone working with food. When she leaned over the counter to serve us, her hair would lay on the clean dishes there. When standing next to the chef who was preparing our dinner, she played with her hair frequently. She was the one who made our desserts and I was concerned I might find her hair in the bowl. Also, I observed her several times eating from the food preparation area, including when she was preparing our desserts. She cut up peaches, then popped some of the leftovers on the cutting board in her mouth right in front of us. She also licked a spoon. Really tacky and unappetizing. Finally, she was wearing one large ring on each hand - not appropriate if one is preparing food.
  Note - I saw none of this kind of behavior by the chef. He seemed very professional. 
   Clearly, this woman needs to be appropriately trained for working at a restaurant. She also handled the cash and credit cards, and I wondered if she knew to wash her hands after handling transactions. The food at Rawr is great and I would go back, but only if everyone in the kitchen adheres to cleanliness standards.

I had a fish taco for lunch. I would have gotten two, but was, alas, two dollars short. No matter: one taco was still tasty, and delicious. I was a little leary of the sweet potato taco and instead went for the fish taco--and was v. glad that I did.

Made to order, it was lightly grilled, moist, and delicious. The fresh guacamole and corn salsa was a great accompaniment, and I didn't even think that I liked unpopped corn---it added a nice contrast of texture, heat, and taste when you bit into a cool, slightly sweet kernel with the steaming hot fish and a drizzle of hot sauce atop it all.

I ate the taco in about five bites on the walk from the truck back to the trolley stop, kicking myself that I hadn't had the extra $2 that would've netted me another one!

Giving this place 5 stars because I've had good experiences each time I've been here. This location has quick service and gets our order right each time. I think if you're looking for a quick sandwich this place beats out chains like Subway and Jimmy Johns. Be sure to try a cookie, they are wonderful after dinner treat!

We had a great time the other night at La Cucina! The class was great! My girlfriend and I learned how to do something we hadn't done before - make pasta from scratch. The space is big, open, and clean, and being able to bring a bottle of wine was fantastic. I would definitely recommend the class - everyone seemed to have a great time and the food we made was delicious. Thanks Anna!

Delicious food, pretty little place, friendly and courteous staff. This will be one of the best Thai food you'll have. So if you're in town be here. Don't make excuses!

When you're at The Smiling Elephant you order Pad Thai. That's what you do. If you want to save 15% or more on car insurance you switch to geico, that's.. Wait. You get the point. We also ordered Gai Yang (chicken cilantro dish) which was pretty kick ass. The honey lime tea (not really a tea) was refreshing. And the best part, when you order the ice cream you get two scoops( try the coconut lycee)!  Everything tastes good here. Service is nice and quick as well. Totally recommended.

Secret tip : This place gets crowded. Parking lot isn't the largest real estate in the country either. Get here around 8:30pm and you'll beat the rush hour. You're welcome.

Okay, so my one bad review probably won't steer you away, but we were seriously disappointed.  We actually went in 2003, so this review may be outdated, but I'm simply not going to ever go back again.

I guess that I expected more from such a celebrated and highly rated restaurant.

1.  The waitstaff while attentive was quite "bored" with us.
2.  They listed a 2001 Beaujolais that we ordered.  Out came a 2002.  I didn't want a 2002, I wanted the 2001 that was listed and ordered.  Funny thing is that they didn't even say  "Sorry we don't have the 2001" they just presented it to us like it was acceptable.  And I know that this is an old fashioned place and southern manners run deep, but you present the wine to the person who ordered it, NOT HER HUSBAND SIMPLY BECAUSE HE IS THE MAN.  Sorry.  Had to get that off my chest. That really bugged me.  He doesn't even drink red wine.  Anyway, not having been ever put in this situation, and not being presented with perhaps another alternative - any waiter knows to offer a suggestion when the customer isn't going to get what they want - and feeling pressured into just "take the wine lady, it's only $40", we  accepted it.  And didn't really like it.
3.  My crab was waaaaaaaay too salty.  My husbands whatever was waaayy to bland.  I didn't even know what to say at this point.  The bored waitstaff seemed to glare off any objections that I may have raised at this point.
4.  Bread pudding was good, but wrongly gooey in the bottom middle.  Like it had previously been frozen.  What?  In the Commander's Palace?  The nerve!  I'm not saying that it was, I'm just saying that it seemed like it was.
5.  It felt like we were being dismissed at the end of the meal.

So I guess that if someone else where buying, I'd give it another try only because they can only improve on this fiasco.  It WAS 2003, so perhaps times have changed.  I've also learned from this experience that I need to speak up and get what I want since I'm the one paying for it.  Just for that wisdom I should give another star, but I won't.

We were sat in their inner but outdoor area. It was a nice patio setting and had a really nice ambiance. Unfortunately when we went, they were having electrical issues in the kitchen and had a smaller menu but that wasn't an issue. We ordered the gumbo, jambalaya, Muffeleta, Sazerac cocktail with absinthe and the Pimms cocktail.

Gumbo: We were enjoying it until I noticed a piece of an opened Splenda packet in our gumbo. We had eaten about 3/4 of our gumbo already and the packet definitely affected our appetite. We notified the waiter who apologized and asked if we would like another gumbo. We declined so he took it off from our bill
Jambalaya: Flavorful, but was lukewarm at best, not hot which was disappointing.
Muffuletta: They warmed the buns, which made it really good and better than Central Deli. The filling was italian cold cuts with olives and basically the same as Central, but because of the warmed buns, I recommend Napoleon's over Central.
Sazerac cocktail with absinthe: smooth, but tasted diluted for some reason
Pimm's was refreshing and citrusy.

This was really nothing special at all--and not worth the 2 hour wait for delivery.

Since this was my first time ordering from Gianfranco's I decided to go with just a plain pie.  Like I said, it took about 2 hours to get here, so obviously the pie was downright cold by the time it got here.  The pizza itself was very chewy and somewhat soggy.  The sauce had that out of the jar type taste, and the cheese consistency just felt, well...really off.  It wasn't just because it was cold.  I feel like if the pizza had been hot, it would have been very oily to the point where the cheese would slide right off.

I won't be ordering again.  I'll stick with Slice and Robert Chiarella's.

Great place to have breakfast at!!
My friend lives a few blocks away from it, and when she's working I take her little dog for a walk at the park on Coliseum and end up having a relaxed breakfast. He usually gets a treat and some water as well. All on the house.

I have been dying to go to American Social for a year and now it is finally open! A group of 15 had lunch there today. The food, service and ambience are top notch! 

Get the $5 burger!! Everyone loved that and the $14 In & Out Specials. And let me not forget the sweet potato fries! The best I've ever had. 

My next trip will be to experience AmSo at night.  See you all soon and often!

As a result of my second visit, I had to take away a star. Hostess over looked me twice. I was maybe a foot from her. She helped the person on both sides first. Of course I called her out!! 

The damn bees at the outside bar was scary.

The visit was pleasant, this new brewery is in midtown not too far from me. We came to check out the sour beers! The on the bright side -Raspberry was my choice and pretty much nailed it for the sours! The on the bright side pineapple /guava was really good as well. The atmosphere is nice there are big enough takes inside to play some games. The outside patio is really nice as well. They have boards set up for bags and even play movies out there! I love that they have the simple board games to do something. Their selection is all around up on the brewery charts as well as the atmosphere for the indoor vs outdoor choices. I'll be going back soon!

I'm a regular at Samaria, although the owners are Greek if I had to describe this place I would call it an old fashioned diner.  The menu is huge, something for everybody from salads to sandwiches to Greek specials (the phyllo dough on the Spanakopita is very flaky and light) and they serve breakfast all day.  My favorite item on the menu are the homemade chips, they slice the potatoes thin and deep fry them and serve them to you hot out of the fryer.  If I don't know what I want for lunch Samaria is a good choice because I can always find something on the menu.  Service is friendly, the owners always greet you when you walk in, and even at a busy lunchtime you get your food and check quickly and efficiently.

The Hugest drinks I've seen... And I don't even drink. Food was great! They split the check at request. Service was a bit slow, but that could have been attributed the fact that it was a busy holiday weekend.

Tourist place, been here forever, just went for first time. Sign out front says chicken shrimp steak and catfish and that is everything on the menu. Overpriced steak was cooked right and tasted fine but it was still an overpriced steak at $23.95. Salads never arrived, lots of fried stuff, waiter tried some trick to flip cornbread out of pan and it landed on floor so he picked it up and continued the trick of trying to flip it. So yeah, if you're a tourist and here with kids, have at it, lots of decent fried stuff served fairly quickly but for any other folks, don't bother.

I have had issues with this location but thought I would give this location another try BIG MISTAKE NOTHING HAS CHANGED!!! I went in for an appt. and they wanted me to fill out pwk AGAIN even tho I have been going  frequently to another location for the same service! Owner Denise came out from the back when she heard I didn't want to fill out pwk again and she practically bit my head off! She was rude and her attitude horrific for a business owner! I will make it my goal to spread the word of this customer service at this location! This is a place you go to de-stress but instead they caused stress! I canceled my membership immed. Denise (owner) you should be ashamed of the way you talk to your customers you obviously don't care about repeat business or possible referrals! 

You left a reply that you canceled my membership when in fact I requested my own cancelation immediately due to how rude and unprofessional you were as well as not answering my questions! I rejoined in Oaks whom also confirmed this happens quite frequently at your location! So lying isn't going to get you more business! Wait,  I have an idea try being PLEASANT/PROFESSIONAL for once! 

FYI one of your employees called apparently she was embarrassed by the way you acted and apologized and said you are a bit difficult to work for! I told her thxs but I WILL never go back and I will tell EVERYONE I know as well to avoid this location!

This Kroger seems as good as any, and it's definitely better than some Krogers I've been in (but not as good as other Krogers farther north). There's a big wine and spirits section, and a lot of fresh, hot food, as well as a sushi bar. 

The Redbox is located outside the store, which is nice and convenient. 

Parking is abundant. Plus, this Kroger is in the same shopping center as a laundromat, so you get the sweet smell of detergent wafting toward you as you walk from your car to the store.

The food and wine pairings are awesome at this place. My friend and I went there last night and had a great experience. While there are only about six tables indoors, there is a private room with a few more tables and fun rocking chairs out front. We had a great bottle of Cabernet Sauvignon, a charcuterie board of delicious crunchy French baguette bread with olive oil, rice crackers, Smoked salmon, wild mushroom duck pate and an amazing creamy delicious cheese. We followed that up with their small plates. I had the short ribs over a creamy cheesy pasta that was delicious and very flavorful and my friend had the octopus with fava beans. Both were very tasty and unique in their flavor profiles. There was hummus with the octopus which was an interesting choice since the dish already had beans. This was more than enough food and drink for both of us. Highly recommend this place. We lingered to finish our wine and ended up taking the bottle home corked and put in a plastic bag ready to go. This is a fun neighborhood place that I would definitely go to again.

We just picked out dogs up from a one night stay here. They were excited to see us upon pick up as always but we were quickly worried that they didn't have enough water because of how much they panting in the car and trying to lick at our water bottles. When we stopped at a restaurant to eat and get water for the dogs, both dogs (105lb golden retriever, 40lb Australian Shepherd) were desperate for water; jumping at our glasses on the table. Our golden is currently on bowl #4 and is still going. The Aussie had 2 before stopping. I can't be certain of the water situation but I'm uncomfortable with just how desperate they were for water and will definitely be using a different facility next time we are in town.

I've never joined this gym (but have tested it out....see my positive feedback below) for two reasons: 

1. One major issue that I have in reference to how they do business:  I mean, sure your business, your rules. Still, a posted policy on the following would be most helpful to others. 

I walked into the gym last week with a coworker who was a happy member of the SouthRAC who was moving out of the area, and driving 40 miles to go to a gym would cost more in gas per month than the membership itself. She'd called beforehand, and was informed that she can do a 1 time transfer of her membership (but no one told her that it must be to an in-house family member, which if they had, would had saved her an insane amount of grief. Also, if the ENTIRE household is moving, how does doing a transfer help?) and when we came to the gym, she had to pay basically 3 months of membership to get out of her contract. Their rules....but their policies cost them two customers: She, now a former member who I don't even think would rejoin the gym even if she moved back to Reno....and myself a potential member who was more than happy to pick up her contract, wasn't allowed to, and will not join as a result (See #2)

2. I may be moving out of the area myself in less than a year, and with a contract I'd be stuck in the same situation in a few months. Paying twice the monthly amount to avoid a contract is not the way you'd want to attract business.  No Thanks SouthRAC! 

The gym itself is incredible, great facilities, great classes and great everything. It's their policies on contracts and if situations arose that prevent one from completing them that are just completely ridiculous in my opinion. I'll stick with the small (even if not updated) gyms that I have free onsite access at my place of work and at my apartment complex than deal with this. 

If you even THINK that you'll be moving in less than a year....don't go to SouthRAC and sign a contract with them.
Best Sandwiches EVER. Get the #6!!!! The service is always good and nice. I've never had a better sandwich in my entire life

Came in here Monday night after wrestling  and feel the experience warrants my first Yelp review. Sat at the bar around 9.30 and was greeted by our bartender Rella. She was very friendly and informed us that the kitchen was serving late night menu and would be closing at 10. We were starving so we put in our orders immediately. At 9.50 a manager (a short man with glasses)  stormed to the bar and angrily told the bartender that the kitchen is closed and that we would not be getting our food. Rella was visibly embarrassed and told us that she would be right back. She went to the back and came back a few minutes later and assured us that we would be eating. 
The food was delicious and Rella was absolutely wonderful, but the manager was very unprofessional . We asked what time the kitchen usually closes on a week day and no one could give us a definitive answer. Apparently it is up to the discretion of whatever manager on duty. We saw at least 5 other groups walk in after us and leave after being informed that the kitchen was closed. Rella was even kind enough to give directions to a neighboring bar that was serving food(she even called them to verify!)  
I give 5 stars to service and food, but 0 stars for management. I recommend you call ahead if you're hungry, apparently the kitchen closes whenever they feel like it. Or make sure Rella is working, she will fight to get you fed!
Just had my first haircut w Laura. Great experience. Friendly, helpful and efficient. Will definitely be back.

I reviewed this restaurant a little over 4 years ago.  I frequented the place  on a regular basis because it is conveniently located very close to work.  A while back I noticed that the ownership (or at least kitchen staff) had changed.  I did not review back then, because I didn't want to speak ill of a place that I patronized, and that was going through an obvious transition.  It has been probably over a year since I have eaten here, I was avoiding the place because the prices went up and the food quality went down.  Their meatballs went from amazing to big sponge balls.  Today I was craving eggplant, I figured that it had been long enough for them to work out the bugs and I would give them another shot.  

 My fantastic server answered my questions about the menu knowledgeably and brought my drink order right away.  I ordered the eggplant parm dinner w/ spaghetti and a side caesar salad.  First up was the garlic bread, which has always been good here, but it really was good.  My caesar was ok, but over dressed.  The eggplant, however,  was not good.  I am pretty sure it came out of a frozen package.  There was too much cheese covering it (I know, how is too much cheese a thing?  Trust me.)  I think the copious amount of cheese was to cover up the lack of flavor of the eggplant itself. The sauce tasted like they opened a can of Hunts tomato sauce and heated it up without adding any spices to it.  It was very bland and sitting on top of overcooked spaghetti.  I didn't need the to-go box to toss my leftovers out in, but my server was too nice to complain to.  It's not her fault the food was below mediocre.  If you can't serve properly cook pasta, you're not an Italian Restaurant, don't believe the sign, this place is just a Pizzeria. The highlight of my $12.99 meal was the garlic bread.  The eggplant parm is listed under the Specialty Dishes, but there was nothing special about that dish. 

The pizza looked good, if you eat here, I would stick with that.

Good pizza but we waited an hour even after we showed up on time for our reservation. Prices were not listed. Turned out to be $20 / pie for not super filling pizza. Bought 3 for 4 people. No leftovers.

First time there not a fan had a reservation and had to wait for a number of the party to show up to be seated?‍ reception staff young kids not friendly.  Ordered drink at the bar had to pay could not add on to bill?‍
I've stopped in twice. The staff are incredibly friendly and their kindness aligns with the hesrt behind the biz.

Came here on a freezing Thursday night with two friends and it did not disappoint. Perfect night for a hot pot. Service was good and the food was good as well.  Nice big selection of items. Overall nothing ground breaking but definitely nothing bad either. Very modern and in a good part of university city Penn campus.  I will probably come back the next time I'm back in Philly.

Love February at Dunkin!!! 
That means "Brownie Batter" heart shaped donuts!!! 
The best!!!
Order on the app and ready when you get there!
So glad they FINALLY got this feature!
Great latte flavors and February means they also have signature girls scout flavors thin mint and coconut swirl .
Stop by, you wont be disappointed!
The waiter, will, was extremely nice. So was the bartender (don't know his name but he had a light beard). He was very accommodating and very sweet

I have to say that my most recent visit was a major disappointment. Normally I would not rate a place so low that I have had several decent meals at. However it's a mater of how they handle themselves when they screw up is what counts. And this fateful night they screwed the pooch in a big way.

It all started with my wife and I saying hey let's go to Manolo's we haven't been there in awhile. So we get there order our food and the salads come out and all is fine. This is pretty important to us since we were very hungry to start. The bread was dry and wasted calories, but not a huge deal. I ordered the Manolo's calzone with eggplant, ham, mushrooms and onions. Sounds good to me plus it has some veggies which I always struggle finding at restaurants. 

Time goes by and the food comes out. The calzone looks amazing! Then I cut into it to find sausage, pepperoni and black olives. I don't care much for olives and was hoping for some veg but it tasted okay. I brought it to the attention of our server who quickly told me the sausage was eggplant. WTF!!! Then she preceded to blame the chef, then did not come back with an answer to why my meal is not what I ordered. After eating a little of my meal I kept feeling more and more neglected and pissed to be frank.  I confronted the server one last time and she told me the chef prepared a Manolo's pizza in the calzone which has totally different toppings/fillings than the Manolo's calzone. WTF#2. Anyway there was no offer to compensate anything and there was no apology for getting the order wrong etc, etc.

The situation could have turned out different if they had a little better customer service. I may not be a regular but I have spent a good share of money here over the past few years. Sadly I will not be spending much more.
They were great, came exactly when they said, with the fair price they quoted us, very friendly, excellent service!!!!

I really wanted to like this place. I came to this place for dinner after yoga class next door (Dig Yoga: AMAZING!). Ordered dishes I classified safe: chicken sate and green curry. The sate came out neatly presented and appealing; the taste, however, is completely sub par. If it wasn't for the grill marks on the skewers, I would definitely say they were microwaved! They take the liberty of putting the peanut sauce for you, so regardless of whether or not you want any, you're gonna have to live with it or ask for it on the side. The green curry was bland and under seasoned. The service was exceptional.
Great bagels! I ordered an egg and cheese on everything and the bagel was loaded with seeds and garlic. It was delicious. The only issue with this place is that the parking lot is tiny!

My friends and I wanted to go to a bar for a few drinks on a rainy friday night, and we decided on Mixto because none of us had been. We walked in and were impressed with the decorations; the far wall had bright, colorful paintings and the one closer to the door had traditional paintings. We were told we could not have a table if we just wanted drinks and were directed to their bar. They actually have 2 bars, one is coffee and the other alcohol, but without room we got stuck at the coffee bar. We bit the bullet and got a table and agreed to order food.
Drink menu was interesting, but expensive. Most drinks were $10-15 and they were not very large. We got the spicy margarita, which was DELICIOUS, a rum drink (ehh), and I got the champagne coctail (great). We decided to get the appetizer combo. Noticing that "shrimp cerviche" was involved, I asked the food runner where it was located. He pointed to a bowl and mentioned what everything else on the plate was. Chicken, cheese, ground beef. Took a bite of something fishy and didn't think about it, maybe it was just gross. Took a few more bites and realized my tongue was swelling. Oh there is crab in this? WHY DID YOU NOT MENTION THAT. Since they mentioned all of the other proteins, how did this get skilled? Why is shrimp mentioned, but not crab? Shellfish allergies are incredibly common! 
I had to leave quickly and go find my inhaler/drug myself with benadryl, while a friend stayed behind to pay the bill. He told me that the server came up to question why we left so quickly and he mentioned that I was allergic to crab, so the server ran away. He had a hostess come out and give him the check. No apologies! He wasn't even asking for them to comp anything- which THEY SHOULD HAVE!- but to be so readily ignored!!
So, if you aren't inclined to allergies, you should be fine. I, will not return.

We decided to give Ocean World another try after decidedly meh experiences several years ago.  The food...exemplified american-ized "entry level" sushi.  Every roll is doused in "spicy sauce" and I always find this to be a sign the fish is not very good.  We requested no sauce and I was right.  The rolls were poorly constructed and fell apart.  The fish was low quality.  It was some of the worst sushi we've had in a long time.  There are so many better choices in Indy for sushi, so we won't be back. I will say the service is quite good and they did honor our requests for removing the sauce and some other changes.

I live in New York & their hair prices are crazy so I'm going to my sisters college graduation in Florida I figured I would get my hair done for cheaper. Lizzy comes recommended, I made an appiontment 2 weeks ago for today Friday May 5 at 4pm. I show up 15 minutes early & they tell me Lizzy will be there shortly. Then 5 minutes later someone comes over & says Lizzy got the appointments mixed up & she thought it was for tomorrow. Tomorrow is my sisters graduated I couldn't come in tomorrow & desperate to get my hair done. She told me someone could do it today. I showed her a picture of a couple months ago when my hair was done I wanted it exactly like that. The person who told me about Lizzy is explaing to the girl that is going to do my what to do & what to use in Spanish. I don't speak spanish but i recognized words like balayage & toner. Then she asked to see the picture about 3 more times. So she starts to separate my hair & paint on the dye except she teases the top 2 inches of my hair. Which in the last 8 times I got balayage they have never done that. I figured there prob is more than one way to do it. Then she puts me under a heating lamp. Then she washes me & puts 3 different things in my hair. Then she goes to cut it & I told her I just wanted a cleanup up just a very little like half of inch. She didn't separate my hair. She just cut it then on the left side SHE CUT ANGLES !!!! I did not have angles in my hair nor did I want them. She didn't ask she just went ahead. Then she is on the right side & she goes high & tries to make angles again & I grabbed her hand & said I didn't want angles. So I go to cash out & the total is 250 ! 250 dollars. I could have got my hair done in New York at my normal salon & paid much less! So I paid 250 plus tip for angles on the left side of my hair & a balayage that looks nothing like the picture I showed her several times. I did not get her name, but honestly I would stay clear of this place.
The only place I go to get a slice of pizza when I'm in the city!    I've been a loyal customer and I've never been disappointed when it comes to pizza or cheesesteaks for that matter.

Great flavors, the chicken is seasoned well, and very juicy. Have to warn you that it has some kick to it. It's not that spicy. The baked beans are perfectly sweet and the fried okras were just fried the perfect way. I wasn't a big fan of the bread. Maybe if it was a Texas toast or the white bread was toasted with butter would have been perfect. The bread feels like an after thought. Overall great chicken.

In the words of my 11 yr old picky eater "we are definitely coming back here!"  Spur of the moment stop on Good Friday and so glad we did! The place was clean, staff friendly and most importantly the seafood was fantastic. Got the shrimp basket which was more than enough for two kids to share. Came with delicious fries and a ton of them. The manger was on the floor asking people how their food was, which I always appreciate. The only reason not giving a five star is they had soft shell crab on the specials menu but were out of it. Should have been taken off the board, I was super disappointed. We will definitely be back next time on that side of town. Feel free to open one in the Southshore area!

Our avocado egg roll appetizers went to the wrong table and sat there for 10-15 minutes before the waitress took it off of their table and directly onto ours.  The food was partially eaten!

Of course, the replaced the partially eaten, cold appetizer.  We then had to return a pasta dish back to the kitchen because the pasta wasn't cooked enough.  The food ended up being decent the second time.

The manager gave us 2 free cheesecake slices as an apology, but we probably won't be back anytime soon.

This is by far my best experience getting breakfast at a restaurant. I had the challah stuffed French toast, and it was amazing. I just couldn't stop eating it. 

And it doesn't stop at the food, the atmosphere is also amazing, with cool items from media's past hanging on the walls and using mason jars as their glasses. They also have live flowers at every table. 

This will definitely be somewhere I come back to. Looking to try the kielbasa sandwich. I don't have any doubt it will be just as terrific.

This little hidden coffee spot is just amazing. It's super "cute" (my girlfriends words) and is the best thing if you want to get away and be outdoors and just chill. They have everything you could want from a coffee shop. With outdoor seating, it makes it perfect for these adjusting times during covid. They have a great selection of brews, coffees and teas. Hot and cold with some small bites also available if you get hungry. During this visit we had iced drinks. I had an iced black coffee and my girlfriend ordered an iced pumpkin spiced latte. We also had a banana muffin and breakfast croissant. The staff is super nice and welcoming. They provide you with WiFi as well in case you want to connect and work. The drinks were perfect. Refreshing and just sweet enough. The croissant was delicious! It comes with eggs, bacon, spinach goat cheese and maybe an aioli sauce? It was very very delicious. The banana muffin has to be on of the best I've had. They warmed it up and the sugar on top just caramelized just perfectly.

Some dishes have a good value and make you want to come back. To remain relevant some spit and polish is needed desperately. The place looks shabby and is. Clutter on the walls and everywhere make the place dark and contribute to the  appearance of shabby and almost dirty Even though it probably isn't. Display cases are so high you can't even see the people behind it.Dining room had many tables with a un-bussed dishes. To be relevant time is now to "sandblast" the interior n  some fresh  paint and clean the place up. Doesn't necessarily have to be a lot of money. It's a shame what the place has turned into. Go for it spring cleaning!

I went here with a girlfriend for some drinks and dinner to catch up. After only a couple bites of the Ahi Tuna, I began having a reaction and developed a rash all over my entire body. Thinking that I had randomly developed a food allergy,I went to the allergist to get tested. I called the restaurant to find out what ingredients had been used so I could narrow down what I was allergic to. The staff, including the manager on duty were completely unwilling to communicate what had been used and to find out...

 Come to find out, After allergy testing, i am not allergic to anything. In fact, Brewstone was serving old fish and I had experienced food poisoning to fish called Scombroid which looks like an allergic reaction. 

Thanks Brewstone for giving me a hellish experience and causing me to waste time, money, and worrying thinking I had a food allergy and giving me food poisoning. You clearly don't know how to store and keep fish. If you can't do it properly, don't do it at all! 

Never eating here again!

Check out their weekly specials-- Chris's offers a $6 lunch special every day.

I just got back from enjoying a chicken salad club with bacon, french fries, and a drinks... with tax, a very palatable $6.50.   I've been equally happy with other $6 specials, which have included an asian tuna salad, and a fried fish sandwich.

Note that there is not live jazz during lunch, but it is a nice atmosphere and good service, if not the fastest.

If you like Chris's and are looking for good deals, you should link up with them on Facebook.  They often have happy hour freebies for their Facebook followers-- I've enjoyed some great crawdads on the house.

Meh. It was super busy! And I barely saw any of the animals. Maybe it was nap time or something... the highlight of the whole time was the caricature a dude drew for us. I feel like we got what we paid for... nothing. Yeah, it's big. Yeah, it's expansive. But the Memphis zoo has this zoo beat by a long shot. It could've been the day we were there or the time of year, but the whole family felt like it was a waste of time. We basically saw some bugs, some butterflies, and a couple of elephants. Everything else was hiding in its cave. Huge disappointment. We'll probably come back one summer to see if it's better, but this time was downright awful.

#PlanetBeauty: I'm in a bit of a rush and enter this store looking for sulphate-free shampoo for my colored hair. My hair salon only carries the $50/bottle kind-too much for soap in my opinion and figure P.Beauty will offer a range. The store is empty when I enter. I tell the sales woman what I'm looking for and she leads me to the expensive products. "No, I'd like a less expen--"  but the phone rings. She answers it without saying anything to me like "Excuse me for one moment..." I hear her chatting about what's up later this evening, obviously talking to a friend while I'm standing there waiting for direction. She hangs up & I ask her to point me to some less expensive shampoos. Then a lady walks in. She turns from me to her without saying "Excuse me..." and starts talking. Evidently, the lady wanted to make a hair appt, not with her, but she pulls out the book and they begin chatting about best days to come in, etc. 
You know the end of this story: I left. 
Can't give this retail store any stars. They may indeed have great products but I wouldn't know because the sales help didn't want to help me find them.

First thing I went in there was the restaurant was nice and clean but the service was terrible the cashier lady sounded like she didn't want to be there and some of the workers didn't wear their mask properly and some didn't even have their gloves on when it comes to gathering all the food for the order. Since we're in a pandemic still all the workers should've wear their mask properly and wear their gloves just for everyone's safety.

Pros: Good wine

Cons: Slow service, food was undercooked, had to send two out of the three dishes back

Probably will not ever go back

Food: thought the cheesesteak was average and slightly under seasoned but the bread was toasty and crispy. The truffle Parmesan fries were good, especially when dipped in mayo. The best part were the pickles on the pickle bar.

Wanting to try somewhere new from my weekly stop at Tha Joint and ended up coming to Jj Sushi. I really wanted to love this place but I liked it, don't think I will be making a return any time soon though. 
Honestly, it felt like a gamble going there and I'm very happy I didn't end up with food poisoning. 

Let's start with the positive first, the staff was friendly and quick and eager to have us sat. While I was there though, a few people got turned away due to lots of reservations for a Sunday night (we got in for the lunch time). Really loved the BOGO free on any beers on Thursdays and Sundays because who doesn't love free booze?! The muscles, they were super cute the way they're prepared. And the Red Bull was definitely the best roll there! And oh my goodness, if I don't come back for the sushi, I'll come for the free beer and the tempura dessert. So good! It was as if an ice cream was fried in funnel cake batter and it was  perfection. 

Unfortunately, the negatives outweigh the positive. Even though the muscles were presented nicely and tasted well, I bit into a tiny crab and it turned me off to my rolls with crab on them. I gave them a taste and it wasn't worth finding another grab in there. I like Tha Joint out of most places because they don't use a bunch of rice and it makes it easier to eat more for AYCE and these rolls got some weight to them, not as heavy as other places though. One of the crab rolls I tried, the fish in it was fishy, and that is not a very good indication when eating raw fish. I got a quail egg shooter and the tobiko was bad, like it tasted like bad fish breath. 

I'm not sure how well this place is with taking care of its fish but it really turned me off to having anything raw here again.

I love this gym. Maybe because it's the first gym that has been around the corner from me! Yes, it's architecturally appealing, as someone else said. And I also get my hair cut at Ground Zero South on the second floor. And the classroom on the first floor is great with a superb sound system. I love the classes there. There are always enough pieces of equipment, and I have several favorite instructors there. Yoga classes can get pretty full sometimes, but that's because they're great! Lately, I've been concentrating on the cardio, mostly, and I notice that the treadmills and ellipticals are mostly full after work hours, but I've never found myself having to wait for a machine. The only thing I'd like is a few more towels there. No, I've never thought it was a meat market, either. I go looking like crap, and I notice lots of others do, too. It really is like their ads - anyone can go, chubby, thin, yogi, musclehead, hipster, what have you. And I love their motto on the wall.

I've also been to the Old City location, and it's nice enough. The South Philly one is comparable, too, though the classroom is smaller. I'm moving to Fishtown soon, so I hope they build one up that way soon!

I was so excited to visit this place! I had seen the picture and great reviews! Being Indian I had to try this place out. First of all our waiter didn't seem to very much interested in serving us after we were shocked by the prices and needed more time to decide. Definitely expensive Especially for a Chat place! We order chai (tea). It had definitely been sitting around or burnt because I couldn't put in enough sugar for it to taste good. They did offer to replace it but at this point I just wanted our over priced food. Chai Came out Luke warm, 5 minute later food came out and our chai was cold. We ordered the Maggi, it was ok. Definitely not enough spices or flavor. The Samosas chat was delicious and warm. Small portions. The waiter was very weird towards my friend and never responded to her saying thank you. If he did it was more of "uh huh". I think he heard me offering to pay for our meal and was only polite to me after that point. If your looking for good Indian food with a great price, leftovers and great service. This isn't your place. This was definitely a disappointment. For my wallet and my belly

Mixed feelings about this place which is very disappointing as I just moved in a few blocks away and was hoping this would be my "regular" bar.  Poor customer service last night particularly at the bar, as we were denied any food from the bar menu because of the time we came in (9pm). I explained that I was interested in the bar menu because there were more vegetarian options and the bartender without compassion pointed out the whopping 3 items on other dinner menu that I could choose from (one had an egg in it, so really only 2 options for some vegs). Not vegetarian friendly at all.  I had checked the website in advance and it is not clear what time the bar menu is available. It's a shame because the drinks were good but I will not be back.

Have heard much about this place so decided to give it a try. It was ok not great not bad. On a Wednesday evening there was a wait to get in.  Everything was Ok -service, food etc.    very very loud place can't hardly hear people at your own table.  I had the grilled shrimp with fries and coleslaw. Food was not hot but not cold -tap water cost $1 -I was like what ??? A dollar for tap water ?? One of the people at table had the catfish special and fries and the other shrimp parm. Again food was Ok service was Ok

Lousy. Everyone thinks this is the "premier gentlemens club" in Philly.  Do yourself a favor - drive the extra 2 hours to New York and go to Scores. If you got money and take care of the girls, they will appreciate you a LOT more there.  At Delilahs after you spend $700 on lapdances, they will still hustle you like crazy for tips.  It's not even funny, I called the girl out right to her face and told her how pathetic she looked begging me.  Threw a bunch of ones all over the floor for her to pick up.
I recently moved in here and I love it. The management team is helpful and knowledgeable. The complex is very quiet and the apartments are extremely clean.
Great location,  cool place to go for dinner,  and drinks to avoid downtown.  Best part.... eat on floating docks,   service is great,  and fresh foods.  I recommend it!

Soooo...i went for the first time last week for my birthday. I was super stoked after reading the reviews and watching the pictures on Yelp....

Ambiance: very quaint and intimate which i loved. There are probably only 8 tables in the restaurant so i would call ahead just to get an idea about wait times. I went on a Thursday evening around 6.30pm and there was no wait time. 

Food: this was the problem for me...oh how i wish it weren't so. We had the Sake pulled pork buns (which was delicious!!!) but when it came to the ramen (Tan Tan Men)...that fell flat for me personally. I did not like the flavor...it was too bitter!!! I had no problem with the simplicity of the dish but i really couldn't get over the flavor palate. If only I could erase the flavor from my tastebuds! My husband and I were truly disappointed. I can't talk for anything else on the menu but i will surely not have that ramen ever again.

This joke of a dealership does not even deserve 1star. Horrible experience. I recently moved to Tampa from Pittsburgh. I have had my Lincoln serviced multiple times in PA with the valet service and always received a loaner for the duration. Any and all services never exceeded 7 hours. 

Parks Lincoln called me to tell me that my navigator would be done by 2 pm AT THE LATEST. 2 pm comes, no vehicle, no call. 
I get a text message around 3pm stating that my vehicle would not be returned in the next day at night. 
I immediately called and got no answer. I called again and I told them to bring my vehicle back immediately and that this was unacceptable. I was told that WALK INs come before my scheduled service. (Which I scheduled weeks in advance).

I was told my oil change and tire rotation was complete. I told them to immediately stop the detail and just bring my vehicle back to my house. I was then told my vehicle would be back BY 6pm. 
6pm comes, no vehicle and no call/text anything from valet. I call back and they are closed. I call the sales and ask them for help. The woman refused to help me and said to call back in the morning. I said that I was calling the police and reporting my vehicle stolen as it is missing. She then proceeds to help me. She calls the valet and cannot get an answer. 

15 minutes after the valet arrives. My driver seat was in strange positions. My wheel is scratched. My tires were rotated but they didn't check the psi in any of the tires. I guess they are too lazy to complete a task of that nature. I filled my tires with air as the front was so low after being with them that the sensors kept being triggered. Somehow the mileage didn't match up with how far the dealership is from my home so i have NO clue where my vehicle was even taken to. 

I DO NOT recommend this dealership. It was unprofessional from start to finish.  Horrible customer service and horrible vehicle service.

First time there so I went with the classic chocolate chip and also white chocolate macadamia nut. The plain chocolate chip was just out of the oven: hot and gooey. The white chocolate macadamia was fresh but not right from the oven.

The cookies were good but not as complex in flavor as I had hoped--something was missing. They just weren't standout. Two cookies were $3.90. 

I would have to recommend Hope's Cookies--I think they are more flavorful and an overall better value.

A cute small plant selection compared to Green Things across the way! The prices here are fair but don't expect a lot of options. There are no vegetables or compost soil, which is what I was looking for.

This is a nice little restaurant with lots of Mediterranean flair to go around. I just wish my stomach could handle it.

Boyfriend and I stopped in after a Phillies game for dinner thanks to Living Social. He ordered the lamb donor, I ordered the chicken shish kabob.

Within minutes (as in, surprisingly quickly in a confusing way), the food was on our table. They *do* have a pretty small menu, though, so perhaps they just have dishes ready in preparation for obvious orders? I don't know.

Nonetheless, I didn't feel well enough to stroll down South Street after, which was a bit of a bummer. However: The food itself was good. I'd maybe make this an end-of-the-night stop (they have hookah) instead in the future.
Cluttered takeout restaurant. Food was.ok, but greasy. Staff was friendly, though. Plenty of other options in the area.

The French restaurant in lambertville nj served up a dish of scallops for $37 that had a mere three scallops. I had to ask if I was provided with an appetizer portion? 
The waitress abruptly replied no and my table was left wondering if she ate a few while the dish was en route to the table? Overall the food is good but the portion on this dish and others was inexcusably small even for New Yorkers.

I got married on 11/2/19 in Carpinteria. I was having trouble finding an affordable florist until Julia came along!!!! Not only did she do an amazing job, but she was willing to work within my budget.  She kept in touch with me throughout the whole process and helped me find examples of what I was looking for.  Then she replicated them perfectly!  I highly recommend her for any of your floral needs. She was a pleasure to work with and just handled things for me without any stress. Love her!

I had no idea what to expect because I had never heard of this place. After some shopping on Whyte Ave we used our iphone to find out where the nearest vegetarian restaurant was and Dadeo showed up. The place was ridiculously busy and we waited around 20 minutes for a spot in the 50s style diner.

The complimentary green jello/pepper spread is disgusting but the homemade biscuits are okay. We ordered the cheddar & jalapeño fritters which were amazing. If I ever eat here again I am just going to eat the fritters (even though they are over-priced). I was ready to give this place a 5 out of 5 rating because of the fritters alone, until our main meals came. I ordered a veggie po'boy with "potato hash". The po'boy was disgusting, the bread was stale and was covered with a little bit of mayo, that's it. Inside it was spewing with bland grilled peppers and zucchini. My boyfriend had the jambalaya dish which he said he loved. For dessert we shared a key lime pie - which was as delicious as the fritters. 

I'm giving this place a 3 out of 5 because the appetizer, dessert, and service were great but my main dish was disgusting and the place was somewhat dirty.

Brandon our bartender was very attentive. Introduced himself when we walked in. Loved the music on Monday, May 14. Not sure who the band was but they were phenomenal. Everything from Merle Haggard to a Metallica. Best classic rock music on Broadway!  We had already eaten but the pizza smelled so good, we decided to order late night pizza. Pepperoni and  half chicken and pineapple.  Absolutely fantastic!

3.5 Stars. I wasn't sure what to expect from all the things I've read about this place. So, I made sure to stop by before the Army/Navy game to find out for myself. Well, first off, there's a lot going on in there and I know I didn't experience everything in one, short visit. I made my way to the Victory bar and ordered an $8 draft in a plastic cup. Eek! Well, at least it was Golden Monkey, so it was a bit of a twofer.

Conceptually, this place is cool and I'd like to explore around more when there's a local game being broadcast throughout the multiple, interior bars. For now, I'm going to play it safe and just stay somewhere in the middle.

Came here on a random whim while at the Westshore mall recently.  It was eerily quiet and we had our pick of pretty much the entire restaurant.  Since there was a Lightning game on we sat at a booth in the bar so we had a good view of the multiple TVs that were on.  The bartender was our order taker and only person we really interacted with during our visit.

Drinks came out quick and the flatbread I ordered came pretty quickly after that.  The flatbread was great, but for an app and the price it was a bit steep.  Most of their menu items were also priced pretty high and it seemed like this place wasn't very busy so I'm fearful how long it may last in this location.

My first visit here deserves 5 stars, as I received a delicious Miami sandwich (or whatever they call it) and one of the best bloody maries in town with friendly and efficient service.

The second time I came, I ordered the sliders. The wait was rather long, and when the food finally arrived, one of the sliders came completely bare (it was supposed to have an egg on top). The mini-burgers themselves were beyond well-done, overcooked to tasteless slabs of cardboard. The fries were cold. Service was okay.

So yes, not great consistency. Would probably try again at some point but definitely not preferred after my last disappointment here.

During COVID, you'd think they'd treat people nicer.  The waitress made it obvious that it was an inconvenience we didn't know what beer they had. ( we were sitting outside).  She was very rude, so we left.  Glad you're doing so well you can afford push your customers away.

It's little bit in the middle of nowhere, it doesn't have the fancy look, whole neighborhood is not my favorite place on earth etc but their iskender kebap was the best one that I had in philadelphia-delaware area. Pide was really good but overcooked(it was burned a little bit). Definitely will go again.
Never have had bad service or a bad item. Favorite items are the chicken blt sandwich and Mac and cheese on the side!

Going to Yards Brewing Company has been on my to-do list for almost 5 years.  So glad that we finally took some time to get down there.

We arrived around 3 and since there were only two of us, the hostess graciously slipped us into the last tour group which was full.  The tour was hilarious.  I'm usually not one for museums and listening to how things are made but our tour leader had us all dying laughing while still giving an informative tour.  Extra perk- free beer samples on the tour.  The whole operation is really impressive.  Cool to know that something so delicious is brewed locally.

The bar area was extremely chaotic but fun.  There's minimal seating in a pretty open area with glass doors/windows so you can see all the brewing machinery.   Service was great, especially considering how swamped they were.  Don't envy those bartenders.

Prices were really reasonable for beers here.  They had some yards brews that I haven't traditionally seen around the city- Rye IPA specifically and some other version of the Thomas Jefferson Ale.  You can also buy six packs for really cheap and pick up cases if you're in the neighborhood in an express line.

I had a blast here and would recommend it to anyone who is looking for a fun weekend afternoon.  Once you're done you can walk right over to Fishtown and NoLibs to continue the fun.

Love. This. Store. The staff is so friendly and I find all of my favorite brands for much cheaper than usual, and they're still on trend, unlike other off-price retailers. Definitely the first store you should go to!
So went here tonight because the reviews were good.   Absolutely rocked it!  Best Bloody Mary ever.  And what they said about Carlos?   Right on. Going back tomorrow night for sure!

Its cozy food that stays the same for as long as i have been going I love this place. This review is long overdue one of the best thing about furrs is its a family coming place. When you arrive it's a display of any kind of foods you love. They have a diverse mixture of salads, deserts, fish, chicken, meats, vegetables, and of course corn bread they have the best jalapeno cornbread your have to try it.

While I was here, I had the propitious opportunity to visit many of the spring training ball parks in this area, everywhere from Port Charlotte to Lakeland to Tampa.  And by far, the best-maintained, most appealing, and loveliest one of them all is Legends Field.  I was sorry there wasnt time to stop and watch a game, as I would have loved to been able to explore the park a little more in detail, as well as take part in one of my favourite pastimes, which is watch handsome, young baseball players run around, haha.  Great job Yanks!

The fries were delicious. Perfect crunch and crisp. The veggie burger with the pomegranate sauce was really good! Substituted lettuce bun for the bread. For weight watchers followers this is an awesome place with low point options! 
The Diet Coke (from New Zealand) no points!! And super good!!

We visited this location a while back on a trip to visit family.  I must say I really was hoping this would be much better, but it was not.

To start off the dinner they had a list of specials posted at the table.  When we went to order one of the specials they said it was for bar patrons only, so they could get more people to sit in the bar.  I asked the waitress to request a manager override that because there wasn't anything saying they were bar only specials.  She refused.

This interaction to start the meal put a real sour taste in my mouth.  The food wasn't nearly good enough to get that taste out either.  Unmemorable, under seasoned, and over priced.  

My advice to anyone looking to eat at Bravo is to just pass it on by and head across the street to one of the good old stand by Italian restaurants.

A Fan No Longer
I started coming here in 2013 as a new and inexperienced sushi eater, my lack of sophisticated palette had me really enthralled with the place. After venturing out and now claiming to be a sushi connoisseur (not really) I realized Azian was just ok sushi for being out in the desert. I still ate here because the desert gives you slim pickings by way of sushi and it was decent enough. Yesterday will definitely be my last time, for one the prices keep going up and the food quality keeps going down. For two, the shrimp tempura was fried to a crisp, the salmon on the casino roll was warm, and the over load the roll with rice to take away from the little bit of ingredients they are actually placing in the middle.

First and last time at Mo's. service was great, unfortunately steaks and food was not even near the price. I regrettably asked for the 8oz. Waygu... Cold, unseasoned, bland, not tender... The worst spent $73.00 bucks in my life. My husband's NY was not right temperature and lacked flavor ....starter bruschetta was horrible.. Strawberry shortcake was just ok, not worth it.  We came home feeling ripped-off, we don't mind spending money if the food is parallel... We visit steakhouses often, don't  waste your palate... Ted's, at a walking distance from Mo's has awesome cuts at less than half!

This small, charming shop has a wonderful selection, very discerning, and has a very helpful and knowledgeable staff. When my wife asked for a book that was a couple of years old and had something to do with Garfield's assassination, the bookseller knew exactly what she was talking about and located it on a spinner rack in no time flat. Could have easily spent way more time in there, but would have spent too much money.

What a yummy place for handmade icecream! Lots of flavors, 5 or 6 different cone choices, fair pricing, and a very pleasant staff. Be prepared to stand in a line, it's a full house (in a narrow space)!

Only reason I wouldn't say 5 stars is due to the location. There's no easy way to access this location unless you're already downtown. You could get lucky and find a parking spot, but with 2nd street becoming more popular, it's a bit difficult to get to.

I enjoyed a scoop of cake batter with sprinkles. It wasn't really all that, but my boyfriend got the red velvet cake, and that was to die for! ABSOLUTELY delicious!!

Staff were nice enough (despite what I read in other reviews.)Room is a small, dark, purple box without windows. I mean I have never stayed in a room so small. Yikes! AC is making very strange noises as well- I hit the wall and they stopped momentarily. Baseboard and rug have a brown stain on them.... Hmmmm...

Ok, I need to update: I did call about the AC and no one ever came to look at it so I went down and spoke to them about the AC problem and showed them a picture of the stains. To the front desks credit, I was moved to a nicer room with a window and no squeaky AC. Again, I did find the staff to be really nice (and sympathetic). :-)

It would be nice if their lounge served food - even appetizers!
I love that I found a place where a MD does the laser hair removal. Most places use estheticians.

We spent $40 bucks on two 12 inch pizzas - That's $12 for a think crust that I can easily get at Imos for a fraction of the cost and a whopping $25 for a 12 inch "double decker" with pepper slices, sausage, and onion. One of us hates mushrooms so we tried to order one half of the double decker without mushrooms and were told it would be more expensive if we try to "split halves". Really? I don't care who you are but $25 for a 12 inch pizza (It is not deep dish. I don't care what they call it, double or triple decker) is just highway robbery. I can and have dropped some serious dough on food but this just wasn't worth it.

Stopped in for a beer while walking around Philly. They have a lot of Yards on tap, which is great and their prices aren't too bad. The 2nd floor balcony is a great place to have a  drink while enjoying the weather and I'd definitely stop by again if I was in the area.

It is good German cuisine restaurant. Considering this area is a popular restaurant. During this time, it is just like October fest. Many love to sit outside like beer garden. In the good weather, you may need to come early if you want to sit outside. The restaurant serve German food and gyro. Interesting. The chef can handle two types of food. 

We got the Jagerschnitzel. It is breaded pounded pork gravy with mushroom and bacon. The side is potatoes salad. This dish is good when you are hungry. Love the coating flavor on the pork. We also get the Doener Bowl. It is shave lamb with rice and one special sauce. This dish we added a knockwurst with sauerkraut and falafel, the wurst is good and brown finish outwardly. Falafel also tasty. 

Overall the food is good. Good thing we arrived before the big crowd. They only has one waitress. With their business they may need two. Many come here for the beer.

What a cayoote pop shop!!! I left obsessed with its trendy interior and amazing pops! My friend and I stopped in here for a sweet treat despite the cold. I tried the Jasmine Tea Blueberry Pop and was so happy!! Fresh blueberries, a strong tea flavor, mixed with the creaminess from the milk used to make the popsicle - perfect combo. Unlike a lot of other popsicle shops I've been to, these pops aren't icy, but are super flavorful and just amazing. There are so many other flavors that I want to come back and try!!!

I went here for the first time for lunch.  At 11:45 they were out of chicken salad, tuna salad and avocado toast.  I ended up ordering a breakfast sandwich.  The menu said you could have it on whole grain toast or bagel for $8.00, rang up as $12.00,  when I asked they said it was $2:00 for the bagel, $2.00 for cheese.  Needless to say I wouldn't recommend.
Howl at the Moon can be a fun place. They put in a new patio and windows that open onto Georgia Street, so maybe it will be even better this summer.

We got a groupon from a friend that she couldn't use, so we decided to go for the 7pm comedy show. The place is busy and sits in a slightly unassuming strip mall. There is the regular restaurant and them theres the comedy area where you can also eat.

The space is tight. I basically was a part of the conversation of the people next to me. The comedy itself was so so. The announcer and the first act did not measure up to the main act at all. 

The menu is extensive and prices reasonable. We only ordered the fried calamari and it wasn't too great. The calamari had an almost undercooked texture.

All in all, its not great, not bad. Its a night out.

I've eaten here several times! Generally the food is really good. Lately the food has been ok. This morning we went and I ordered a white chocolate mint cold brew. When I received it it was 3/4 of the way. So when we went to leave I went to order another, of course will to pay. That was upon asking them to not put so much ice in the cup the employee said "we have to put that much ice in there for cost purposes". That is NOT something you tell a customer!

4 stars for vegetarian options!

I have twice gotten the mock sweet and sour chicken and avacado cuke rolls- both were delicious both times. Piping hot and when I called in and picked up, it was ready in exactly 15 minutes form call time. The guy at the counter was very friendly too.

However- called for takeout last week. I live about 15 in walking distance- can literally make it there in 5 minutes from the car, but I was feeling super lazy so we ordered in. I had to call them when it hit past an hour thinking they forgot about us. They likely did, but came about 20 minutes later with very hot food. Total time 1 hour and 20 minutes from call. Really decent food, great options for a vegetarian, but I would caution the delivery option.

All I can say is "it is the best hoagie in Bucks County", fresh meats, top of the line imported provolone cheese, simple and delicious. And it is prepared by Mario, the owner. Bristol could not have a more warm and generous family than the Mazzanti's.

Awesome way to celebrate a Friday night! We went to the Magic in the Making event.  The night was filled with beauty cars, art, boutiques, food and drinks!  The artists ranged from canvas to performing arts!  Delicious food and lots of drinks celebrating lots of artists!

Maybe I am too picky but I was not nearly that impressed as most reviewers! The food was good! The chips are thin and the salsa is too puréed for my tastes. The servings are small for the price! They are over-priced! I am all for small portions if it is reflected in the prices. My margarita was $9 and came in a small wine glass! Maybe 5oz of liquor after all the Ice. I'll stick with my two handed margarita at La Hacienda or Lucianas.....

Found it to be a great spot once I had a toddler. Unfortunately the first time I took my son a couple months ago I went into high ballocity without socks my mistake but took home athletes foot. Yuck and it was painful!  Decided to give it another chance at high ballocity when my son had picked at his skin on his thumb earlier that day. A week later ended up with an infected thumb red and pussing. Took him to doctor to find he had strep in his skin. A stressful week later of making him take his antibiotics he lost half his thumbnail.  He doesn't go to daycare so I'm not too sure where he got it at but with the previous experience it makes me think it's where he got it from. 

Other than that the food is good, the new arcade and go carts are fun.

These Chinese devours come from @newleafmanayunk . Their service is really great. Their team of servers are very attentive and they check back within 5 to 10 minutes to see if everything is still OK at your table. They are bathrooms are exceptionally clean; And they do offer both indoor and outdoor dining. Our food came out pretty fast which was really good. Here is their crispy calamari, along with their Tom Yum seafood soup; and their seafood delight dish that came with shrimp, scallop, salmon, snow peas, mushroom in light black bean sauce! The calamari was really good especially with the duck sauce. The Tom Yum soup was very filling and tasteful. And the seafood delight was simply amazing with the black bean sauce; I really enjoyed it. If you're ever in Manayunk and want some really good affordable Chinese cuisine please check out @newleafmanayunk and tell'em SHIMMY sent you! #food #foodie #foodphotography #foodblogger #foodstagram #philly #philadelphia #soup #tomyumsoup #shrimp #calamari #duck #scallops #water #snowpeas #salmon

Nice artisanal space with inviting interior, this restaurant has both character and some story to tell with such a name! But on a recent dinner visit, it was for business / serious talk, so didn't get to really look around much other than chow down with a bud.

Barbie Pie (Pie, 3/5, $23.98) - My friend picked this white pie and I wished that I had picked Henrietta, Forbes or Jane pie instead. I'll give it that the juxtaposition of spicy banana pepper and tangy house bbq sauce on a medium of roasted chicken was interesting... (high marks for creativity!) but overall it was not cheesy enough (despite having fontina AND mozz on it), not flavorful enough, and just too dry for me. 

Even the crust was... too thick and not crispy and fun to bite. They did have a nice apple soda that I enjoyed with the pizza, but at 2 slices I was looking for another culinary adventure. 

I'll still give it another chance - with a red pie.

Paid fora  Kona French press coffee and got watered down, tepid swill and a chipped mug. When I asked for one that wasn't chipped the guy acted like it was an inconvenience, and it took him checking three mugs before he found one that wasnt chipped. No eye contact, no apology. The coffee shop looks really nice and has pleasant atmosphere but no customer service to speak of. I would recommend avoiding this place. I wont be coming back.

COMPLETELY ADDICTED. 

I went for the first time last month with an old friend to catch up.... I've been back four (YES, 4) times since. 

Anytime it is up to me and I feel like getting out I always suggest Arabella. Also, it's located near a bunch of happening spots on St. Claude (HiHo lounge, Sibera, Kajuns karaoke bar) so, it's the perfect pregame spot! 

The atmosphere is chill and it's always the same ppl working every time I happen to go... They are both very helpful and easy to talk to. 

As for my order, I'm stuck on what hooked me in the first place 
Black pepper fettuccine, with creamy pecan pesto, and mushrooms. 
TO DIE FOR. 

I've also tried ( aka took bites from my boyfriends or friends plate ) 
The "Jim BlueCheese" HOLY FUCK, it's good.

If you've got anywhere to be after dinner, this is not the place for you. Also not the place if you show up hungry. I went to dinner here with 3 friends. Here's a timeline of our dinner: Reservation for 6:15. Sat at 6:20. Ordered about 6:30. First meal served (a salad) at 6:50. 7:00 bread requested because we were tired of waiting for food. Next two entrees served at 7:13. Bread delivered at 7:19. My pizza was served at 7:44. We then ran to our movie at the ritz to arrive only 5 minutes late for the 8:00 show.  In the entire time we were sitting there, our waitress refused to acknowledge us after she originally took our order until we called over a manager. One friend ordered a second wine, which was not served until she was finished her meal 20 minutes later, after we reminded multiple servers that we had ordered it. The rest of us never were given the opportunity to order a second round.
