When my pizza was finally served, we called the manager over to point out the fact that everyone else had finished their meals, while mine had only just arrived. The excuse we were given was, "Our pizza oven is only so big." (So I guess that's a tip - if you do bother going here, don't order the pizza.) It was also under cooked. Finally when we did get the bill, they had comped us for the pizza, but charged us for THE BREAD - the bread we'd asked for only because we weren't being served our meals. There was one competent waitress that felt bad for us and at least filled our waters throughout (because our waitress obviously wasn't doing it) and got us the to go pizza box - thanks red head waitress. These guys will go out of business as quickly as everything else that's been in that spot if they keep hiring staff based on their bra size. 

There's lots of amazing places to eat in the city with great service. My recommendation is that you go to one of those places instead of Barra.

Carmel is not lacking with the amount of Chinese restaurants. I wasn't sure about my pick so I relied on other reviews. 

I went inside to order and wait for take-out. It was a Saturday evening around 7 p.m. Not too busy inside but the employees were really rushing around. I gave my order to the lady at the register and paid. Two eggrolls, hot and sour soup, and garlic chicken for $16.  I really was looking forward to the soup. I definitely judge a Chinese restaurant by the hot and sour soup. I can remember the first time I had it(Chan's Steakery, Milwaukie, OR) so I order it every chance I get. As I sat waiting patiently for my order to be ready, I watched the people coming and going. At least one customer showed up not knowing what he was picking up and not convinced the hostess/manager knew either. She was very impatient and it was uncomfortable to watch the confusion. My wait was not too long, thank goodness because the chaotic scene made me anxious. 
I wish I could say the food was really good but it wasn't. I felt like there was way too much celery in the garlic chicken. It was overwhelming, and I really don't remember celery in any other garlic chicken dishes anywhere else. The sauce had a good flavor after getting past the greasiness of it. Egg rolls were okay. The soup was good. Not the best but good. I would go back just for soup. It was not overly spicy like I have had in other places. I like it spicy. I like to have it when I am feeling sick or have congestion because it hits the spot. This soup was good but not "healing."

Great Mom and Pop place for seafood.  Great service... nice comfy atmosphere.. and that too food was Good. Had the carb cake dinner and followed with a slice of Key lime pie. So looking for place that is not a chain place, good food and prices get to Shells   To get a 5 Star all they need to do is enlarge their TV.

Came here years ago and thought it was great, (because I had a burger and beer on a slow night) and came here a couple of weeks ago and remembered why it took me so long to return. 

Food : tapas style for the most part so they are small portions, however, they are not super delicious. Figs in a blanket was good as well as the cheese quesdilla (how can you go wrong with something that simple?) The crab or seafood mac and cheese was ok , but had a lingering fishy taste afterwards . The fried goat cheese dish was good , however , the tart sauce that came with it did NOT work well with the dish . We also ordered a potato dish as well as other quesdilla and taco-like dishes (I can't remember clearly what they were) , but they weren't worth the $12-$15 price tag per half a bite per person. 

Drinks : ordered at the table was the jalapeno margarita, which to me was not good at all (maybe I am just picky because I once bartended?) but it did not taste like it was infused in ANY jalapeno at all. Just a glass of liquor with two jalapenos and a cucumber . Also ordered was the tequila mockingbird ; a very strong drink that went down smooth , but lacked in flavor for those who like flavor in their drinks . Almost tasted like watered down liquor . 

Service : it was a busy night so I did not expect the service to be magnificent and we were also seated outside , however , I did not expect to wait over 20 minutes for a pint of beer ! The staff there who waited our table seemed to be a random pick of people : some were very professional and seemed they qualified for a Ramsey's restaurant , and some could pass for working at a Ruby Tuesdays . 

Overall , I probably won't return because to pay that much for tapas , I have had way better and unique dishes for the same price and better service . For this place to be as hype as it was , it is very overrated .
Service is friendly, but why don't they salt their fries?  I thought the first time was a fluke, but now twice.  Horrible.

service was great , very kind people , definitely wish the restaurant  was cleaner . I love Thai tea and love the one in the bottle from the Asian market but I wasn't expecting to be served bottled tea at a restaurant  . On the up side this is the only pho restaurant I've been to in Tucson with veggie broth .

It's a nature preserve, lots of turtles every where. They're so adorable! Can I just add, it's a park-ish it's not suppose to be fancy or anything... There's trails to follow, a little play area for the kids. You can go canoeing, have a picnic, and just enjoy the outdoors.

My second trip to Opal left a bad taste in my mouth.  

We were seated next to the server station and several times I heard the waitstaff making snide comments about customers.  This should definitely not be happening in front of other customers.  Period.

The bay scallop salad was fantastic!  Seriously tasty stuff.  

I honestly don't know what happened to the steak.  My first review was a mouth-watering 5 stars, but this time there were NO seasonings that I could tell.  Also, I asked for it medium-rare and portions of it were cooked into oblivion.  I could have sent it back to the kitchen, but for the cash money I was throwing down it should have been right the first time.

New to area, and very picky with what I eat. I came in and they had an awesome selection of vegan/almond milk options are were awesome. And they put on a good show also. Definitely will be returning!!!
The food and service and drinks was beyond perfect! Everything came quick! This place is a must visit while in Louisiana!!

So I went for the first time tonight and got no food. I sat at the bar with a few friends toward the end of happy hour and beyond. All I can truly speak for is the atmosphere and the quality of the bartenders. 

I had a great time for a Friday night. The bartender was highly attentive in my opinion and always asked whether I wanted another beer just as I was finishing one, which I feel is good bartender etiquette as I've also been a bartender myself. 

I know the place has 3 floors but I only saw two. Although the floor I was on had as many TVs as one would need to see from any angle. 

I would def recommend this as a hangout spot with friends. They have a great happy hour and even place a nice shamrock in the foam of Guinness. Something that's not done in many American pubs and takes great care to do. 

Fun time!

Unfortunately, the Echelon Mall has been on the decline for a decade.  About half of the stores are currently empty, or display only.  As of 2011, there are a few stores worth stopping by for.  But most of the other area malls are better.

But there is a light on the horizon.  Echelon has been transformed into "Voorhees Town Center" which is a combination of condos, the Mall, and a series of small specialty stores which mostly are set to "open in Fall 2011".  There is a good chance Echelon will be a much more fun place to visit next summer when we're back in the area.

Pretty good breakfast, laid back atmosphere, and decent prices too. I just had breakfast here with my wife for the first time ever. I've been wanting to eat here for years, but I have always ended up going somewhere else because of no parking and a VERY long line out the door. Well, today I passed by this morning and they weren't busy at all. So I had the Eggs with ham and grits. And my wife have a vegetable omelette with hash browns. Her hash browns were a tad overcooked but everything else was great. So the food was just average to slightly above average I would say (I have had a LOT worse meals than this, trust me). It was pretty good but I wouldn't call it "outstanding". But the old fashioned atmosphere had me feeling like I was transported back in time. So would I go back, yes but only if it's not really busy. As small as that place is and the lines I have seen out the door, you would easily be waiting over an hour for a table. I don't know of very many places that I would stand in line for over 30 minutes let alone an hour, I would get antsy and go somewhere else. 
PROS: Good food, GREAT service ( my waiter was awesome!!) Good atmosphere, Decent prices (I said decent, not GREAT prices), Huge Portions, they make you feel at home
CONS: NO Parking (have to park in a nearby lot or find a spot on the streets), VERY Small so there is not much seating (that's why the line gets so long when they are busy)

I was staying near this location for a month and my experiences were 50/50 right down the middle.. Everytime my order was just one drink.. the service and and drink were perfect. I had received suggestions on how to make my drink better next time I ordered it and recommendations on what might add flavor.. it was great.. 

But the other half of the time I ordered I always ordered 3 drinks and they were never correct. Ever.. if I said white mocha I got a white hot chocolate.. when I ordered my DoubleShot I received a Mocha.. whats weird is that each time it was a different persons fault.. one time it would be the cash register person writing the wrong order.. and the other time it was the barrista making the drinks.. it was really weird.. I have concluded that it is my fault for these mixups. Presumably my stunning features or my impeccable jaw-line distracting said persons to make mistakes. 

I have resigned to never attend this location again. I apologize for flaunting my essence and impeding your abilities. Good day.
Stopped in for a quick beer.  Beer was very good.  Will plan to stop again for food and to listen to the band

I ordered a burger and fries were ice old , no condiments. I asked for new fries and the condiments I ordered, waiting some more. Mind you, this place isn't packed at all. THEN, my dad orders a water and there's a lemon stuck in his stray. The server, manager Christa & Scott were so cold and their story of the lemons being next to the straws was nonsensical! I could feel the sarcasm behind their "apology". There's many dining establishments at the French Quarter, don't go here.

Went there for a late lunch/ early dinner Saturday May 18th. We sat at the bar waiting to put in our  food order, the bartender was too busy talking to his friends then having an interest in getting our food order. We had one drink and left to have food elsewhere, even paying the bill was a challenge, took him seemed like 15 minutes to cash us out. I take my girlfriend to new hope often, we've always have good service outside, due to the weather we went inside, never again.

This location is cramped!  They're doing some remodeling and everything is haphazard all over the waiting area, so it's very cramped and uncomfortable, add to that, they do not enforce mask-wearing, so if you're high risk, maybe skip this location.  

As most other spots they seemed understaffed, and it's hard right now, but this is not a quick drop and go for amazon returns or whatever.  Be prepared to wait.  I'll find another location to go to.

Went to Moontown, spur of the moment,  with our family.  Loved the building and it's history. The tables built from the large oak tree are beautiful. 
Everyone really enjoyed the variety of beers. I had the porter, absolutely delicious!  
The food  was great. 2 vegetarians in our group, plenty of things to order. Daughter had spicy bean burger, she loved it. I had the broccoli cheese soup and Cali fries. Both so good. 
Others had burgers and  hot chicken. The hot chicken is truly hot!  
We would recommend Moontown for a casual stop for a beer and as a full on dinner. 
We will be returning very soon.

It is incredible what a cool $78 million can do in terms of renovating this historic opera house. The Art Deco touches are well thought out and the building has revealed itself as an elegant and comfortable place for a show. I had the opportunity to see Green Day's "American Idiot" here and couldn't have been more pleased with the show and the venue. I sat near the front and found the acoustics and visibility to be outstanding. Since I had waited until the last minute (for me) to purchase tickets I was doubly impressed. I look forward to attending other shows here and am hopeful that the Peabody Opera House will continue to draw emerging and established performances to the area.

The doctor and nurses I have had experience with are amazing and caring. Unfortunately the front desk just ruins my experience completely. As soon as you walk in you greeted with a glare and the women's who name that vegans with a "V" is always so rude. She ruins and irritates my appointment every time. She's never nice, it seems like there's always an issue with her and I feel like she judges me. My boyfriend has noticed her attitude and how negatively she interacts with me. It's unfortunate and she may be the reason I no longer come back.

Killer foot long Coney dog. May the chili and cheese host a silent night. Onion rings are perfect, along with the pretzel sticks.

I was amazed it all fit on a small lot, I remember when that lot has a small gas station!

Employees are nice, and that is the key! Close to home and a good place to watch to crazy ass  200 yard long drive-thru line at Cane's across the street.

Two stars only for the cheap drinks. Bartender upstairs was very friendly too. But the wings were ehhh, the music is really loud and there is nothing special or unique here. 

Just cheap drinks.

Oh yeah, and the bouncer was not that pleasant to one of my companions. Not cool.

Jamila's is epically known worldwide for their excellent food they have been providing the hungry hordes for many years during Jazz Fest.  Less well known but even more delicious is what they prepare in their restaurant proper.  The Tunisian food here has influences that will bring memories of food from the Middle East and France while producing some remarkably unique and wonderful flavors all its own.  

The hospitality here is legendary and offered in abundance by the husband and wife team that run this establishment.  The husband is frequently found to be sitting at the tables jovially conversing with customers while his wife will occasionally tell him to stop bothering people and, all the while, making it seem very much like you are part of their family as they go into great detail explaining the many wonders they have on offer for your dining delight (along with the best drinks to accompany each selection).  This is a truly enjoyable place to dine, drink, enjoy wonderful food, as you experience wonderful hospitality.

Parking on street or in garage.
Beautiful interior
Loud and lively atmosphere 
Food was tasty
Calamari-good, but still doesn't even compare to Capital Grilles'.
Caesar salad-good, not great
Sano bowl-yum
Truffle pizza-yum
Portion size is sort of on the smaller side.
For my husband and I, our bill was $110. Seemed a bit steep for what we got.
I had 1 drink, he had 2. We shared the 4 dishes. 
I'm trying to imagine if we brought our 2 children our bill would be closer to $150, yikes! 
I did enjoy our night, we sat at the bar, great service and I liked the people watching.
We did not have a reservation and walked in at 6pm, it did fill up closer to 7.
None of the staff, except the bartender that took our order spoke to us. It is nice when new restaurants open and the head staff comes around yo see how you like everything. The owner was FaceTiming or doing live stream video footage (hard to tell)...

I like the restaurant but on a recent Friday night they were closed for a private party, so I was left standing in the rain looking for alternative restaurants. They don't take reservations and their website, that I had reviewed that afternoon, didn't warn me about the closure. I guess it's ok if you are a neighborhood resident, but it's a little awkward if your Uber dropped you off and now you are left wandering around in the rain and dark. I probably won't try again because it's too much trouble if they happen to be closed. I prefer restaurants that take reservations so I at least know if they are open.

Arrived roughly at 6:25. Was told food should be ready in 20 minutes. Ordered ribeye, mash potatoes, corn on cob and Queso dip with chips & salsa. I got my food at 7:10. Drove home and it was horrible! The chips were stale, the queso was cold, the steak was way over cooked. Not sure I would return! Very unhappy. I had not had this type of experience before.

After a round of golf I went into the Mainland grill for some refreshments.  I get add on's but let's makes some sense.  My friend got a soda with ice.  I got a whiskey on the rocks and the rocks were so special they charged $2 for the rocks that come free in a soda...... pay attention to your check.

This place is so freaking charming and cute from the outside I just had to stop by for a visit. Looks can clearly be deceiving. The interior seemed much less cozy and my iced mocha was a total disappointment, as evidenced by the fact that I threw out half of it. The baked good I had (some sort of bread- I think Zucchini) was dry and totally forgettable. Hmm guess I won't be lured back into this shop again..

Unfortunately, we are from Chicago and we were in town only one night.  We ate here on Saturday night because I read all of the reviews and wanted to give it a try.  Wow!!!!  I had the "chef's special" which was all sorts of different fish, shrimp and scallops with vegetables, potatoes and rice.  It was AMAZING!!!!  I couldn't finish it because there was so much food.  I had to have the waiter take it away because I couldn't stop eating it.  The fish was fresh and delicious!  My husband had the chicken and he said it was very good as well. 
I would seriously come back to Indy just to eat at this restaurant!  The service was great as well.  If you love Greek food this is the place to go!

Cupcakes are pretty but the taste is nothing special. It taste like maybe they make homemade frosting but the cake comes for a box. They're nice n much bigger than the normal cupcakes but I've had better from the girl that sale cupcakes on Frenchman. N didn't they once have ice cream??

Romeo & Juliet was well done. The lighting , stage presence from the dancers and music made a worthwhile performance. I would recommend people to visit once more to see the Black Swan or the Nutcracker.

Went for breakfast this morning-service was very, very slow. Only one cashier and she was running dining room orders as well as drive thru. A gentleman complained about the slow service, another employee laughed at him and then to the entire dining room:"you people bitch about it being slow, don't come in at 7am. Come at 7:30. This is shift change, I've been here since 11pm, you don't see me being miserable." She continued to laugh and make faces at the man behind his back. Very rude and unnecessary.

My new favorite Ramen restaurant! They even provided the bib. Albeit one slurp too late. 
We were fashionably early at 6:30 and it was wide open seating. By 7:15 there was a 20-30 min. wait.

we invited all family for dinner! And it took loooooong time to chili's prepare our food.They forgot one order!!!we were almost done when ther brought it!That was terrible!!! And i hope employees smile more!

Well. This Gabriele person is TOTALLY RUDE AND UNPROFESSIONAL!!! I recently texted the number to get a quote on a Job I wanted to get done in my new restaurant. And I took a screenshot of they way this person responded to my mss. You guys be the judge of the professionalism here

This is one of the busiest Wawas that I have seen. On many occasions, the bar with the condiments for the coffee has been totally disgusting. There seems to be only one cashier for all of the people in line waiting to pay. I have to go there since there are not many Wawas in the area.

Burgers are great, wait time is long (because burgers are great).

However.... it never fails to surprise me how many kids people have in IN (and bring to Bub's).
We just moved from out of state and my wife and I started discussing how families at Bub's probably think we have fertility problems (with just 1 kid).

Also, I once came with two gay friends and, I must say, the entire restaurant was staring at us.
I felt weird.

Anyways, welcome to Carmel IN, where the neighbors will call the cops asking them to 
"check out" a suspicious car (AC repairman in this case) parked on the street late at night (8:15pm).

Vintage Stock has one large floor almost entirely devoted to video games, most of which are retro.

I saw plenty of decent prices on games and accessories. Along with a couple of SNES games, I picked up an SMS controller for a very fair price.

I suggested to my wife that we live in this store. Sadly, she shot that idea down.

Bread Company, I really want to like you more. Whats not to like? Bread and Company are two of my favorite things, but you just fail so much on the execution.

We walked in the back door, so obviously things weren't catered towards this direction. But even when you walk in from the front door, its not clear where you are supposed to go. Use signs! Its not hard, I promise. But then, once you figure out where you are supposed to go, the ordering process is complicated (you fill out a form) but there is nothing telling you to do that. In addition to being confusing, I found the restaurant to be a little dingy and loud and my sandwich was dry. I have heard there are better bread and company locations, so maybe I will give them a try the next time I am in nashville.
Super accommodating and helpful. Workers were a bit scattered and there looked like wasn't much going on. Will be back!

Ordered the spicy chicken and steak kabobs with pita chips and Mediterranean salad to-go. The order was completed quickly, only took 15 minutes and was ready when we arrived. However, when I opened up up the box I expected to see either an entire kabob with veggies or for the kabobs to be half veggies half meat. Instead I got 2 meat kabobs and the saddest looking grape tomato, little slice of onion, and a tiny square green pepper sitting underneath the meat. That's it, those were my veggies. Really disappointed and wasn't satisfied. Didn't even eat the salad, waste of money. We immediately started researching other Mediterranean spots in the area.

I love Gourds Gone Wild from Tampa Bay Brewing Company and this was my first time visiting since they tapped the keg this season.  Gourds Gone Wild is 1000% my favorite fall beer. 

Tampa Bay Brewing Company is also fantastic outside of this perfect beer.  

We visited on a Saturday for lunch a got really nice seating outside.

One of the daily specials were these fantastic loaded french fry dish with gouda and bacon, it was delicious. 

My husband and I also split the meatloaf and they did a fantastic job of putting it onto two plates for us. It was superb as well but had a little bit too much mashed potato and not enough meatloaf.

Will be headed back to TBBC many times this fall to continue enjoying my favorite beer! 

Please do try, maybe we can get it all year!
All hype. Food tastes mediocre, nothing special. Was very extremely crowded, not worth the wait and lack of elbow space.

I used to come here all the time when I lived in the city and stopped in on a recent trip back into town for business. While still delicious, it has lost some of its luster (and my favorite menu items.) Prices may be a little bit high for poboys (considering what you can get them for at other places, that is) but these ones are definitely excellent. Large, overstuffed, and delicious - certainly a welcome change from the meager poboys that are half the size and twice the price I have to buy in DC.

As I said, this place will always hold a soft spot in my heart - I bring friends and family here when they visit, and spent countless lunches here slamming a giant sandwich into my guts to get over a hangover. It's a little pricey, not the cleanest or fastest - but it's delicious and always hits the spot. Highly recommend, will be back (often.)

Wish I had more time. My lady and I went here. Were in town for business from New York. Decided to be tourists and check the place out. I really really really wanted to see the weapons and armor section, but right when we were about to enter. THE PLACE WAS CLOSING UP. WHAT? We weren't even in the museum longer than 45 mins. and they were closing? I was VERY disapointed. I think it's pretty dumb to sell tickets considering you plan to close in less than a hour. No one can go through this whole museum in less than an hour. No way. This place is freaking HUGE. No big deal though, I got my picture taken with Rocky and ran the famous steps. Only bad part was,..... it was 14 degrees outside.... UMMMMM No thanks.

The art and artifacts are beautiful and you will drop your jaw in awwww. History right in front of your eyes. Pieces that are priceless and stuff that will make you think. If you have not been here YOU have to go. Hopefully I can finish my tour of this place next time in Philly.

Urban Brew and BBQ is an outstanding place to go to get your fix for some seriously delicious smoked meats and a wide selection of brews. Wife and I had a truly delicious meal on our first visit here.

The place is in the somewhat up and coming Grand Central district of St Pete, which mean that if you are not a hipster you might make a note that you will be invading their domain when you visit the area. As long as you have a beard or perhaps some strategically displayed tattoos you should be fine.

All kidding aside, the inside of the restaurant is cozy, basically a bar with around 10 seats and then a few high two tops along the wall... It is small and intimate, and smells absolutely terrific. Out back they have a series of picnic tables accessible through the restaurant. 

We tried a number of items, including the ribs and chicken, as well as some delicious sides consisting of Brussels sprouts and mac and cheese. Everything was truly great. The rub on the meats almost made the finishing sauce unnecessary, and the sides were very well seasoned and had a great smoky flavor. The sprouts in particular are amazing, although some may find them a bit over-seasoned. 

My only negative was although the place is small, only one person was working as server/bartender/phone answerer etc. She was fast and thorough but clearly a little frazzled as the restaurant filled up. This may have been an atypical problem, perhaps someone didn't show up to work, but she was noticeably a little stressed. 

All in all I am looking forward to a return trip not far in the future. This is an outstanding, non-chain establishment serving up excellent BBQ.

In a word: unimpressed
The food really had no flavor. From the Cesar salad (that tasted like it was just mayonnaise) to the lasagna - that didn't taste freshly made (that day) - just freshly heated up.

Chill - you need to update your online menu.  My husband and I specifically made the trip to your restaurant for the duck and the philly steak tater tots, only to find they are no longer offered.  Needless to say, we were not happy.  Please don't advertise meals you don't serve!

I want to first start out by saying I truly appreciate the work that this place does!

The reason I am giving such a low rating is because of the following.

1) We went on the marine tour which we booked before hand. The crew was great and very receptive to questions. We drove out to a spot, dropped a net then pulled up a few species of fish, crab. We drove to a small man made island and walked around for 10-15 minutes to collect shells then drove back to the dock. On the way back they walked around with some of the fish we got in the net and told us about them. There was some good information but felt the price was to high for what we got.
2) I think they should change there name from aquarium. As, they are definitely not one. We were through the saw everything in less than an hour. As others have mentioned they seemed more interested in pushing merchandise than anything else (seems to be the area they put most of the money into).
3) My biggest beef was Winter. My daughter was soooooo excited to see winter and left very disappointed. The set up is Winter has an option of two pools to swim in with an open access to both which I think is fantastic (the more room the better). My issue is on the outside they have roller doors and kept the ones by Winter closed so you could barley see her. I do not understand why as they also use barriers to keep people back from the pools. My daughter left disappointed as I had to use the zoom on my phone to show her Winter. My daughter has been talking about this for months and we are from Canada. What a let down!

So i went here for the first time in forever and i feel like they were rushing a lot , but you can't rush nails or else they'll look rushed. I had a man start doing my nails by taking my current nails off and applying tip & a women lay my acrylic and paint but in between they stopped to work on other people. I feel like that's really unprofessional. I paid $75 for a set I didn't like & i just left bc I felt like if i would have asked for them to fix my nails it would still be rushed so i fixed them myself.



Update 13 days later 7 nails are off , I'm not rough my nails last for months definitely do not come here !

Love this place. Wanted a little something different on my business trip so I went with this suite hotel. This place was brand new and the nightly rate was very reasonable. The hotel is has a contemporary ambiance and although I checked into my 1 BDRM suite a little after 12 AM, the front desk clerk was very friendly and even called 15 minutes after my check-in to see if I needed anything.  I asked for the ice machine location (there are none I learned), but the clerk arrived at my door with a full bucket in what seemed literally like 1 minute!  The bathroom was spotless; dishes, plates, pots, cups, silverware, and even a water/juice pitcher were all provided (and clean!). I was able to connect to the FREE WiFi without any hiccups. There were also 2 flat screen tv's (one in living room and one in bedroom), which was a nice touch. My only complaint was regarding the complimentary breakfast buffet. The eggs and coffee were a bit on the cold side and the bread/pastries were in that 'older to stale' range. The hotel is near numerous restaurants and shopping venues.  Overall a great experience and would recommend to anyone looking to stay at a newer, cost-effective, and clean hotel.

My husband and I went here last week for an attempt at date night. Unfortunately, We didn't have a good experience. We decided to order some food & drinks before playing pool. We ordered 2 drinks, wings, and southwest egg rolls. We got our first drinks and everything was fine. Then we ordered a pitcher of beer. Unfortunately, my husband had something in his mouth after drinking the beer, it was hard and bright blue. We talked to the server about it and she was very nonchalant about it at first explaining it was a sanitizer tablet. She offered to get us a new pitcher and we explained that we didn't want a new pitcher and just wanted it removed from the bill. She removed the pitcher from the bill and then began to apologize about the inconvenience and gave us a coupon to come back. :( 
Also, we received our wings but I do not recommend as they tasted boiled and not fried. We also never received the egg rolls we ordered. Overall, I was hungry so I ate the wings, my husband didn't even bother and we left without playing pool because he was hungry. 

I do not recommend this place for food or service.

I have been here a couple of times before, it's always been ok, but today I tried to call for an appointment and was left on hold for 10 minutes before I decided to hang up, I tried to call a few more times but it went straight to voicemail, then I decided to just go in to see what they have available. They got me in right away which was nice, the women asked me what color and if I wanted them trimmed and that was literally the last thing that she said to me. She then proceeded to trim my nails...trim them literally all the way down. There is barely any nail left. One started bleeding. She never asked what shape I wanted which doesn't matter because there was not enough nail to make a shape other than stub. All my fingers are so sore and painful whenever I touch anything. I am very disappointed

I visit Red Robin a few times per year with my family.  The most recent time was this location a couple of months ago.  I ordered a spicy burger, which had hidden in it a piece of metal from the grill scraper.  I discovered it by biting into it.  Fortunately I didn't crack a tooth.  The staff comped my dish only and offered a free dessert, which I declined.  I expected better.  Likely will not be back to this or any other Red Robin.

Had a couple of hours to kill during dinnertime and asked someone where they would recommend.  I'm SO glad they told us about Ralph's!  Cozy interior, reminded me of my Grandmothers kitchen wallpaper. The food was delicious, loved the meatballs appetizer and the veal parmigiana.  Service was superb. No worry about where to park either, they have a valet option for $10 flat fee.  Can't wait to go back!
Crasher Barrel refused to honor a reservation at 10:15 we made yesterday for 12 people. Please note they do need money for large groups Beware

This store is terribly stocked, the customer service is subpar & the photo lab sucks (blurry pics, pics missing from your order, no one is ever behind the counter). I refuse to ever come back here after today's trip.

Stopped by for brunch. Great location right on Jackson Square. Was crowded but we waited about 10 minutes for a table. Everything is ok - nothing really stood out. Tip: get the large Bloody Mary as the regular is half the size. Downside is they don't have decent coffee.

I don't get the 5* reviews. Maybe because I'm spoiled because I'm in NYC? Stanley's is simply a good breakfast/brunch place, but no real wow factor. I'm not in a hurry to go back.

This place is wonderful! Everyone here is so nice. The place is so well kept. I have never had a bad experience. These people treat you like family. One employee in particular is kenzi perryman. She is the nicest and most helpful. If you need anything shes always there to help and find help if she cant!!!!

So when i write this review, I want to make sure that I dont reflect the people there badly. The staff was great and dont have any complaints about them at all. But the place is no Dave and Busters! I expect so much more from this place. The bar was nice and looked to have a huge selection of drinks and etc. But when it comes down to it, its a good ole American bar with American Beers and Jack! Well needless to say my GF asked for 5 different drinks and they didnt have any of them. They were simple drinks, like Rasberry Vodka with Soda, or Mandrian with Soda and etc.. at the end of the 20 minute ordeal she got a well vodka with soda. I ordered a Orange Vodka and Soda, DONT HAVE IT, then a Tecate, DONT HAVE IT, Negra Modelo, DONT HAVE IT, and I finally had to result to Crown and Coke. Then we ordered shots and they didnt have what we wanted other than just your college type of shots like red headed sluts and etc. I was very sad by this! Not to mention the food was horrible and it was all fried! I mean Im all about bad and greasy food.. aslong as it taste good.. but that wasnt the case.

So I resulted to playing games for 2 hours and had a good time there. The best thing about the whole experience was that my self and my gf got 40 dollars of free games. 

So no go here.. I hate giving bad reviews.. but I couldve gone to a local bar and gotten the same service. atleast there, I wouldve known what I was getting.

Happened in on open mike night.  Pretty good talent. 
The sliders and flat bread were good too. 
Prices decent. 
Service good and friendly.  
Very comfortable.  
We will be back.

Two years ago this place seemed pretty legit; bands music, divers atmosphere. Lately it feels as if it's ending into the trashy side. The last two times there were overly drunk, arguing guests that weren't asked to leave and the band might have been good if the volume weren't making my ears ring. The two goods are outside seating and generally a fairly clean restroom.

I don't normally complain about food, because I love food! But.... 

My boyfriend ordered me food at Quik Wok in Meridian for delivery at 5:05. All that was ordered was teriyaki chicken and crab rangoons. With tip total was 20 bucks. Website says 1hr delivery time and left a phone number to contact with any issues.

6:25 rolls around and still no food, so I call the number on the screen. I asked about my order and the guy could not locate it with name or phone number. So I give him the address and order content and he puts me on hold. A minute or so later he comes back and says he found that order and address but it has a name and phone number that isn't mine or Jay's. Then he tells me it's for cash, but I paid with card. He continues with an apology and says it's starting to cook and will be there shortly.

At this point I was upset because we are close to an hour and a half and it just started to cook so I wouldn't receive it until 7 PM. I asked about cancellation and he said it take 7 business days for a refund. I asked to speak to a manger at that point...

Manager answers and I explain my frustration. She apologizes and tells me they are very backed up and that's why the website says to call for delivery time after you place it. I still had the website pulled up and read to her what it says, which was nothing of the sort. It stated that its take approximately an hour. She then tells me I'm looking at the Boise website and the Meridian one says different. So I tell her I am on the Meridian website, because it says it on the page that I just read her and she continued to argue and tell me I am wrong. I decided I wasnt going to waste the time arguing over what I clearly can read and ask about my food. She said it will get to me when its ready. 

So after two hours of waiting and rude customer service I received my food (with no fork or napkins). The crab rangoons were pretty delicious, but the chicken was gross. I was able to eat 3 pieces, because the rest was fat. I picked it off and ate some of the rice before I discovered that most of was rock hard. 

Needless to say I got over the fact they were rude, and decided to wait because I was hungry and didnt want to wait 7 days for a refund. Food and service was awful and I do not suggest ordering take out from them.

Burt food, bad service, dirty this restaurant is everything bad!! To top it of one of our servers assistants a young man, was extremely rude laughed when my husband told him how bad the service was, when my husband got up to find the manager he got in my husbands face and started yelling at him he needed two other employees to come pull him away!! Unbelievable never in my life have I ever had such a horrible experience in a restaurant!
Pizza was disappointing. It looks better than it tastes. The breadsticks were decent. I honestly wouldn't go here again.

Found this salon in my neighborhood and love that it is only a few blocks away. I do feel the people here are not that friendly and never really engage in conversation which is different from what I usually experience. The dip nail here is ok and it falls off in like 3-4 weeks which NEVER happens to me and any other place Ive gotten it - it stays for like 4weeks plus and never chips. Otherwise, an OK place and prices are kinda overpriced.

35 minutes chatting and taking her jolly time talking with one customer while two others wait. Not only that, but after that first customer, they started to have problems with the computer systems which took an extra ten minutes to figure out. Absolutely obnoxious!!! Don't come here if you need fast printing or shipping. Never coming back.

We took our cat Gypsy to the groomer and she did an amazing job!!! Perfection!! We had a not so great experience prior to brining her here and this has restored my faith for sure!! We are so happy with the result!!!!

I've been obsessed with Thai food for many, many years and this place has the best Thai food I have ever had in my LIFE. It is incredibly authentic and was absolutely mind blowing. I will never go anywhere else for Thai food. 

We ordered the curry puffs, Tom Kha, Panang curry, and Pad Thai. Every dish was absolutely incredible. Everything has an absolutely perfect flavor profile. Even though we were full, we couldn't keep our hands off every dish as we were waiting for to-go containers. 

It is a little hole in the way place that is in the same building as a gas station but, trust me, if you eat here you will not be disappointed.

Sunday night before 4th of July. We were Downtown - unbelievable the biggest City in Delaware and not one restaurant open for dinner. We asked in the hotel and they sent us down to the river front where a couple of restaurants are. The Riverfront  seems the place to be in Wilmington. The brewery was quite busy but we got a place for 4 right away - nice booth. Service was very friendly but we had to wait for a while. We started with some Hummus and the Fried Brussels Sprouts  - the sprouts were really great. As entree I had the Grilled Ahi Tuna Salat. The Tuna was cooked on the point as ordered (medium rare) but only two very small pieces of tuna - all the rest was salat and $17.5 for more or less a green salat is way overpriced. We finished with a piece of Lemon Pie which was ok. So overall the dinner was ok nothing special and somehow overpriced for the kind of restaurant - but we were glad getting a dinner on Sunday July 3rd in Wilmington, Delaware.

I was at the Continental Midtown Diner on June 12, 2015 at approximately 11:00 pm. My girlfriend and I sat at a table indoors near the car and ordered a cocktail. When she got up to se the restroom, she slipped on a depression that was in the floorboard and fell. We asked to see a manager abs relocated to the bar. When the manager, Brendan, came over, he thanked me for bringing the safety concern to his attention and moved a table over the depression. I asked for his business card and ge went to go get it. In the meantime, our server from the table, had us close out the check for the cocktails we ordered with her. While we were sitting at the bar, we waited for approximately fifteen minutes for someone to greet us, even though there were three bartenders and the bar was not full. Once greeted, they took our order and brought out our food. Unfortunately they did not provide us with any plates, napkins, or cutlery. We finishe our food and asked for the check. The server asked how our experience was and I explained that my girlfriend had fallen on the faulty floorboard and that I was STILL waiting for the manager to give me his card. We waited another twenty minutes for our second  check which the manager, Brandon, brought out. He told us he "took care" of this check except that he charged a beer to my card that I opened the tab with so that I could "take care of my server/bartender". I told him that I works be happy to "take care of my server/bartender" in cash. He offered to again void the check but at that point I had already waited long enough I and was so disgusted by the service that I wanted to leave immediately. So basically, my friend was injured at your restaurant, I paid two checks, and my card was authorized for $6.60 without my permission so that I could "take care of my servers". The ironic thing is that I worked for Starr Restaurants in 2003 and ur was a great experience for me. I know what the Starr standard is and I am truly disappointed that I even need to spend more time writing this feedback. Needless to say, I will NEVER be patronizing the Continental Midtown again and am very apprehensive to paronuze any Starr establishment.

UPDATE: I previously gave this restaurant 5 stars due to the food however I am deducting 3 stars due to our experience today. The management at this restaurant has extremely poor customer service and is very rude. We ordered a drink today and when we received our drink it was wrong. Below is what happened but long story short they were trying to charge us extra for their mistake. After going back and forth they walked away and did not even apologized. Read further if you want to know the whole story.

It was a special drink so it wasn't on the written menu. We verbally told the waiter what we wanted and we confirmed with him about the herbal jelly being the grass jelly. My sister does not like the other kinds of jelly so she made sure to verify before she ordered. He said ok and we saw him wrote something on the paper menu. When the drink came it was lychee jelly. We told them it was not what we order so the person apologized and took the drink back. Then some manager chick (I guess she's a manager as she did not introduced herself to us) and said that she will still charge us $.50 for the jelly that was incorrect. We asked her why. She said they could not resale that drink that was incorrect so they will still charge us for it. We were in shock! She showed us the slip and pointed at the handwriting on there. We told them we verbally ordered this drink and that was not our handwriting. They insisted that we ordered it and asked us who we ordered from. Seriously? I told them why don't you go ask your servers which one took out order. We were in shocked that they had the nerve to charge us for their mistake and confronted us about it! For 50 cents!?! Because you can't resale it!? Seriously people. Let's be real. My sister did not even touched the drink. It's not a case where we drank half of it then decided we don't want it. We told them right away it was incorrect. We go here all the time. For 50 cents you confronted your customers and insisted on charging us when your server screwed up. We've come here often but now we will never go again.
Gotta love the merchandise in this store.  Great prices for the awesome merchandise.  I recommend this place to everyone.

I'm kind of a corn dog snob.... so this place is by far the best corn dog I've had! The county fairs don't come close. Purchased the longer corn dog for $7. Perfectly brown and golden. Each bite had a crunchy crust... none of the partially cooked and gritty dough. The Nathan's hot dog was perfectly juicy and salty. Paired well with their ice cold lemonade. 

We also had the chicken strips. Also very crunchy and juicy and well seasoned. 

We will definitely be back for the corn dogs!

The food is absolutely fresh, hot, and delicious. Their portions are great sizes - ordered 5 plates of meats, an order of chow mein, and fried rice...fed a hungry family of 6 with leftovers. We will definitely be back. We ate inside and though it's a small restaurant, the service was great and they checked on us a few times...very courteous and friendly.

Our Thanksgiving party of 7 attended the Thanksgiving dinner at Cucina.  It was our first time there, and we all had positive expectations based on the reviews.  We are not picky eaters, and generally enjoy a wide range of tastes at restaurants.  However, I've got to say, we were sorely disappointed with the meal.  Our server was nonchalant and didn't even welcome us to the restaurant.  He poured our water and never asked us if we'd like anything else to drink. A few guests wanted wine, and it was difficult to discuss any sort of drink options with him, as he kept walking away.  Our glasses were empty for most of the meal, and our plates weren't picked up on the table, until the manager ( or I think he was), took care of us. The Thanksgiving food, honestly, was awful.  The prime rib was so tough, it was difficult to cut with a knife, and 2 of our guests likened it to trying to chew bubblegum.  It was inedible.  The turkey was dry and tasteless.  The vegetable lasagna was cold, as well as the mashed potatoes.  Gravy was tasteless too.  And the cranberry relish was so bitter, no one could consume it.  2 of our guests were sick that evening w/ stomach troubles.  While we were there, another patron was complaining to a staff member about how disappointed their party was as well.  We wanted to enjoy the meal and had good expectations, but unfortunately, our experience was so poor, we are not intending to return.  Oh yeah, and I forgot to mention, it was $52 per person!

I'm giving this restaurant a 3 because my food was good, but I didn't like the way I was treated. 

Last Saturday, I ordered two tofu tacos and beans and rice. The tofu tacos come with pineapple on them. I do not like that flavor combination and pineapple gives me a weird reaction sometimes, so I asked if I could have sweet potato instead like I have done in the past. I've never had any issue with this and I've always had a pleasant experience at this restaurant. This time, however, our waitress came back and told me that there would be a $10 dollar charge for 2 ounces of sweet potato. When I have ordered this in the past, there was a small up charge but not $10. I thought that was ridiculous and insulting, so even though I have never done this before at a restaurant, I asked to speak to the manager. When he arrived to our table, he treated me like I was asking for a free meal. He was short with me and it was obvious that he was annoyed. He told me that that he would be charging me $5 instead of $10 which is definitely better, but I feel like I shouldn't have had to go through that to get some sweet potato. When I come to a restaurant like Red Mesa Cantina, I have high expectations and expect to get what I want. I wasn't asking for anything crazy or hard because they use sweet potato daily and have it on the menu daily. 

My husband and I spent close to $300 dollars that night and tipped our waitress well, but I doubt we'll be back. I'd rather go to a restaurant that wants to please their customers and give them what they want without any hassle.

I've been here a couple of times & my visit usually follows either a trip to Costco or Strike & Spare (two main reasons I drive all the way to Nashville West!).

The last time we went was a Saturday night, and it was CRAZY INSANE.  I have to stress all caps because the wait was 2 hours!  There were people lined up everywhere, even all over the parking lot, waiting to get a table.  Insanity I tell you.  We asked the hostess if the bar was open seating, and she said yes.  On to the bar we went!

We found 2 seats at the bar, but since we had 4 in our party, we continued scoping other potential tables.  I spotted a party of 4 getting up so we immediately swooped in & grabbed the table.  This was after maybe 5 minutes.  A lot of work for a table, but it was much better than waiting 2 hours!!

Ok so the food - we ordered wings, fried pickles, a burger, and a chicken tender basket.  The fried pickles & wings were pretty good - we got the "Wild" sauce which was good but not that hot.  The chicken tenders tasted like they had been fried long ago, so that was very disappointing.  The onion rings weren't that great either.  My husband ordered a burger, and after waiting a good while we got most of our food except for his burger - they delivered the wrong entree.  Poor thing had to wait another  good while for the burger to get there, and when it did, it was so sad that he didn't even eat it.  He said it tasted very charred & burned, and he just couldn't take more than a couple of bites.  After waiting so long we just were ready to get out of there.  We ate some snacks at home.

Let me say that this isn't the worst place ever.  The wine was priced alright (I am just not a beer drinker), and the beer prices were good too.  I just won't be in too big of a hurry to come back.  The experience just isn't good enough to justify being a table vulture!

I've had the pleasure of eating at many of New Orleans' great restaurants but I would not count this as one the better ones. The service started off great but deteriorated over the course of the dinner. The food was mediocre at best. Presentation was lacking. I was looking forward to the oyster trio but was sadly disappointed. I will not return and would not recommend.

Came in yesterday for lunch before we went to the airport and I loved the ambiance. The restaurant felt upscale but not stuffy. Our server was polite and knowledgeable (I think her name was yona or Ilona something like that)... either way she was great and the service was fast! 

For lunch I ate the chicken and dumplings and I'm still dreaming about it. So delicious! I can't wait to come back for dinner the next time I'm in town! Sad I didn't see this place sooner! 

My only complaint is the hostess that was at the front of the restaurant barely greeted us when we walked in she was too busy applying makeup. The restaurant wasn't very busy at the time she told us to hold on for a minute and checked her phone before she sat us!!! Like visibly! 
 
The food and the rest of our experience made up for the beginning rudeness but that's why I give this restaurant 3 stars

Easily the best bar in midtown! Staff is amazing, and over the top better than anywhere else in town. It's not even close.  Tons of great beers to choose from and a menu that has never disappointed me in the 20 times I've ate here.   

I see a lot of reviews saying it's loud or crowded or they had to wait to long for a table...those things should be expected when you are in a BAR or a establishment with AMAZING food. Trust me when I say it's worth the wait. OR just don't show up at peak eating hours.  Hate seeing bad reviews over what I would consider petty complaints due to people over drinking and getting emotional. 

Cheers!

Every time I drive through Metry I think of this lyric from the smash hit Rainbow in Marrero: "I don't go to Metairie unless I really have to". Well guess what? I had to.

I had to because I had to mail something and this post office was on my way to work. I had to mail something, but because I rarely mail anything, I can't even remember what in the hell I was mailing. That may seem counter-intuitive and I should actually remember what it was. I don't. Come at me bro.

Anywho, back to the review. If you're commuting from the North Shore and need to mail a package, turn on 17th street, pull in, drop your mail, and leave. Why three stars? Because there isn't any easy way back to Causeway Blvd. Thanks Reagan (I'm guessing this place was built in the 80's so I hope my blame is directed at the correct president).

Not bald and lovin' it. :)

After frantically calling around town looking for a place open at a convenient hour on Sunday, I decided against going at my head with a pair of hedge clippers and finally found MOKO here on Yelp. As soon as I saw the girl with the spiky faux-hawk looking style walk in a few seconds ahead of me, I knew I was in the right place. (I was almost tempted to ask for that instead lol) She turned out to be my stylist, Rachel, who left me with a snazzy new hair cut after about an hour or so.

I was a little worried at first, as I always am with a new salon. Because many people either don't know how to handle my hair, or try to convince me to get a ridiculously priced relaxer. Thanks, but no thanks. (This salon doesn't even do relaxers or anything that uses harsh chemicals, it's all organic just like the stuff I use at home!) But I was in good hands; Rachel actually took the time to look at my hair ahead of time and asked me about what I normally did to it, so she knew as soon as I got into the chair that we needed to go for the flat iron first. Dried ends gone. Now running my fingers through my hair feels like ':D' instead of  'B|'.

My favorite thing about the salon: hands down, the atmosphere. It's clean, neat, and organized without being snooty like certain other Center City Salons-everyone is super friendly, (even the girl at the front desk) and maintains the "earthy" vibe without being overly hippie-ish. And I even got a 10% off card for my next visit!

Don't Lease here they will suck you dry take you deposit. it looks nice from the outside but it is horrible inside. i hated it all the office there are like snakes avoid it if you can. they are horrible people. parking is horrible. there normal wear tear is poor condition they WILL take your deposit they don't want pay for anything. everything is bad quality the paint and the wall will scratch easily and take you deposit all the furniture they have is of bad quality too. rooms are too small there barely 1 bedroom. the management office are very rude and very all they care about is money. and bad neighbourhood. Don't lease there they don't care about you. they care about your money.
Overall, the food was really good and at a decent price. The only downside was how busy the restaurant was

Solid middle eastern food.

The place is in the Reading Terminal, and definitely has a presence. A curated menu that packs a lot of food per item. The main draws are the platter options - either the preplanned ones or they have a make your own option. 

I ordered half grape leaf/half hummus platter and felt it did the job to satiate my craving. The grape leaves were well packed and you get a good amount of them. The platter also comes with a salad and pita bread that I used for the tabouleh and hummus. The food was good, but a bit foodcourt-y. 

They also have a nice list of smoothies that can be ordered, which I found very refreshing for a middle eastern place. I ordered their banana, strawberry, and blueberry option! There's a host of add ons that you can throw in (protein, peanut butter, etc.) but I kept it simple.

The workers were friendly and were able to explain the menu options well. There was hardly any wait to order when we went, and once adding in our order, it was quick and fresh when it came out. 

Overall - would likely go for just a smoothie if I came back here, I thought the food was alright enough, but nothing stood out that was crave inducing.

Hifalutin is just OK for me.  The food was good and was fresh, but the prices were high and service was adequate. We have had many great meals on our trip to Tucson and this is at the bottom of the list of the places that we visitied.

I ate the chicken tortilla soup, which was tasty, but was laking the pizazz that I am used to .  It was basicly spiced broth with chicken and tortilla sliceds.  There were no extras like sliced avocados to make it special.  Something that I would expect for a $6.00 bowl of soup.

I also had the salad that had apples, bluecheese and candied pecans in it.  The salad was delicious, but the portion size was nothing to be amazed about.

The restaurant billed itself as southwestern, but I would just call it a ranch style steak house.  There was very little on the menu that I would not have found in a steakhouse in my home state of Texas.  The decore was beautiful, and made me feel like I was in someones ranch home.

If I lived locally I would probably not be a regular.  Since I am from out of town, I will definately seek out other options the next time I come to Tucson.

I went on the early side for dinner and drinks with my husband and two under two girls. Yes, it's more of a well stocked wine/beer/spirits bar that serves artisan pizza than a pizza place, however, staff was friendly with the girls and even a little helpful. No high chairs or anything but a window bench did the trick.
This is an eclectic place decorated with re-purposed materials and local sculpture, interesting found objects. The bartender Joe was super friendly and funny and helped us pick out beverages with giving us a taste of things first and/or talking through brews and ryes. 
We got two pizzas. Both were good. Nit picky things... I thought the crust was a little dense and on the harder side and the tomato sauce could use a little more flavor.
This is the kind of place that you'd make "your place" to meet friends and stop by after work, be a regular...but you could also walk in from out of town and feel completely welcomed and comfortable.

They have lost their minds in Priceline. Clothing, with holes, 9.00 bucks. Yes, you might find something price right, but for the most part no. Jewelry, is over priced. You could buy new cheaper. Forget, shoes over priced. It more of a retail store.

The famous St Elmo Steak House (don't know what it famous for) so we had to treat our self to a once in-a-life time. So this is a no-bars hold dinner. Having the best steak, wine and desert this evening. A very classy, classy place so far. One the 1st round of service the chefs totally missed the mark on how my steak. I had to return my steak a 2nd time and it came back exactly the same way as the first medium rare instead of medium well.

This time got the manger involved called his attention to the old oil that my fries were cooked in as was oblivious due to the color of the fries after they were cooked. At this point it was time to call it a day. The ONLY compensation was the removal of the steak that this cooks could not get right and a apology.

Frankly, not good enough for a place that's suppose to be so renounced for its steaks--should have went to a Brazilian steakhouse they are far better that St Elmos.

I give 5-stars for the service and a 2-star for the meal as they did not screw up my wife's baked potato.

So. Much. Food. And it's delicious. The women who work here are hard working, accommodating & friendly. Ask for a sample if you are not sure which chicken to get. They are all good! The sides are also good - rice, noodles, corn. Haven't tried the plantains yet. The prices are fair, and as I said, they give you a lot of it. The area in which you can eat is a little noisy. It's clean, and lots of tables and chairs are available. If you want free wifi, log into Westshore wifi. We eat here whenever we are in the area. It's so good! And so inexpensive.

Piquant cinnamon roll. Premium pricing. Unappealing and unhygienic location. This business failed to meet my expectations in terms of service quality.
Please train your staff in above-and-beyond-customer service.

We wanted salad for lunch so decided to try this place. The older gentleman given the task of making our salads was a complete jerk. After being rushed to give him my toppings order several times, he told me he had other things to do. I responded that we could always go eat elsewhere if that was the case. He shrugged to let us know he didn't care, so out the door we went. Don't go in there if you're new to the establishment and don't know exactly what you want!

I'm updating my previous review. After speaking with Mr. Kauffman, I got an apology and a free bottle of cleaning spray. I will give them another chance when it's time to get another pair of glasses. I understand things happen and this business owned up to it.

This has been a family favorite breakfast spot for years, however, our experience today was just simply unacceptable.  My husband ordered his breakfast skillet with no peppers and onions. When the meal came out, the presentation was beautiful and fresh, but contained onions and peppers. The server said she would have it remade.  When the dish came back to the table, it didn't look as nice as the "first".  We then discovered it was the same skillet; someone had PICKED OUT most of the onions and peppers, but failed to get rid of all the evidence (as there were still a few remaining onions and peppers), and then poured more gravy on top hoping we we wouldn't notice. The thought of someone's fingers going through the entire dish is just nasty, gross, unappetizing, and quite frankly, UNACCEPTABLE.

1st time here since the renovation.
Cornbread was great. The main order of tacos did take appr. 30 mins to get to the table, even though the restaurant was pretty empty (just open for lunch). Aren't tacos easier to prep?
- Short Rib taco was dry and was mediocre.
- Pork Belly taco was not braised enough and I was disappointed.

Overall, this place needs some refinement from its service training and food quality.

Wonderful spot for a Mothers' Day breakfast. The owner and staff are welcoming and personable. The menu is creative, leveraging local ingredients and down home flavors. Great fresh baked goods too. Definitely worth a return trip next time I'm in Reno.

The service is usually pretty slow checking out, but the staff has been pretty knowledgable whenever I have asked a question.  They will run free battery diagnostics and if you purchase a battery from them, they will install it for free.  

Pro tip: If you have a smart phone, you can look on their website while in the store, sometimes the website has better deals.  I was able buy a battery on my phone while standing in the store so that I could use a 30% off internet coupon, it was "delivered" to the store within five minutes and they intalled it right there.

This place is cool, the tofu/vegetarian sliders are good, the egg biscuits are amazing, but the donuts are just...average. They look amazing but don't even taste good. The dough isn't sweet and tastes like bread, the only sweetness comes from the icing. Not sure if that's how they are supposed to be, but I'm not a big fan.staff is nice though!

$10 polish change from Emma was great! She was really sweet and gave me a little shoulder massage at the end. 
I walked in on a Thursday afternoon and the shop looked clean and smelled good. I loved looking at the plants in the window while my nails dried. 
FYI Pink Blush also had a lot of Essie and OPI colors to choose from.

I've had better experiences at the Capital Grille in Center City, but those were all dinners. I like a bustling atmosphere; this place was a little quiet at 1pm on a Tuesday in a heat wave. Our waiter was great, though he seemed a little annoyed when we asked for more time, twice. It was a business meeting after all. My biggest complaint had nothing to do with the restaurant itself but with the other patrons: a couple of Macklemore wannabes wearing backward flat-brim ball caps and tank tops on the dining floor. If these jerks can't get it together, can't the restaurant ask them to remove their caps or otherwise enforce a dress code? I'm not paying $100 for lunch so I can look at some dude's armpit hair.

I don't hate Boom Hair as a whole, but I hate the woman hairstylist with the short hair specifically. I asked her for medium hair with bangs and she literally turned me into Farquaad. I looked like Darth Vader with his helmet on. I had a reference picture for her to follow and it looked nothing like it. The bangs were thin at the bottom and somehow thick at the top; they were incredibly uneven. The hair in general was lopsided and had long strands. She ruined my hair and my self esteem. It took five months to completely grow out. The haircut took about seven minutes, which shows how little care she put into it. I asked her to fix the bangs and she continued to make it worse. If you go here for haircuts, go to the male hairstylist. If he's busy, just wait or come back. Don't make the same mistake I did.

I really like the sides this place has to offer. Corn fritters, potatoes, Mac and cheese where excellent. However I really didn't like they steak I had here. The service was not great either. That's why is place is ok not great.
My sister & I got matching cartilage piercings done here. The tech was SO nice, the place is really clean and orderly. I'd definitely recommend straight to the point to anyone and everyone!

It's been a few years since I was last here but I think i think it was still Hollywood and not Harrahs or closer to the changeover. Anyway I was really excited to go back Saturday because all you can it suits my fancy just fine.

Unfortunately this time around was a bit disappointing.
I remember when it was Harrahs the fried chicken was always fresh, crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. This time it was fresh and crispy but the breasts  (which I always eat) were very very dry. I'd lean on saying they don't brine the chicken anymore?? 
Also I thought they use to have all you can eat crab legs and sushi on Saturdays but now it's an additional 9.99 a pound and no sushi. We were told that was going to be for a different night featuring Asian cuisine. For $28 a pop I felt a bit cheated. 
The macaroni lacked flavor, the dressing was watery, the greens were too mustardy, and I guess they got tired of people asking for certain cuts of steak as the chef prepared them because they had 2 pans of steaks ready to go. One medium well and and one medium rare. My family and I got medium well and it was so tough I couldn't even cut it with my steak knife. When I decided to go savage and bite it off I kept chewing...and chewing...and chewing.
It did have a really good flavor to it at least.
Lastly the desserts looked prim and proper but were horrible. The cheesecake was disgusting and the brownie was like eating a spoon of unsweeted cocoa powder. The warm apple cobbler wasn't sweet enough but the creme brulee was good.
Even the fruit punch and lemonade tasted like prairie farms. 
Their saving graces were that the food was fresh and our waitress was very nice and managed her tables well. 
Whatever shortcuts they are taking need to be stopped.

yummy food
came for a road trip from san jose and was wanting some vegan food 
we got the street tacos and the burger- both were good
Will surely come back!

The order for pick up took one and a half hours. I get it - they were swamped and understaffed and we chose to wait. But nothing was done to make up for the wait - no free egg roll or soda. And to top it off, I ordered Moo Shoo but was given something else - no plum sauce, no pancakes just chicken and vegetables in a nondescript sauce with white rice. No mustard or soy sauce in the bag for egg rolls. No apology. On the plus side, my husband enjoyed his shrimp lo mein. I have been going here since I was a kid but it will be years before I go again.

I love that Costco sells things wholesale.  I understand the reasoning for needing a card to get in, but as someone who doesn't have a family yet, it makes no sense to get a card of my own (although, I hope one day to have one).  I usually go with my mom and we could spend hours in there looking around.  While you can usually go for certain items, there are times they will just stop carrying whatever item you are looking for with no idea when they might get it back in stock.  What I can tell you?  Don't go here on the weekend during the day.  The best times to go are during the week or evenings about an hour before they close.  However, if you are looking to sample items, then I guess you better brave the crowds over the weekend!

Frequent visitor of the Peppermill here - I have enjoyed bringing my friends and family here for the past 3 years, as it is a great/affordable seafood restaurant. It has been about 1 month since my last visit - at that time I left very satisfied and full. Last night's dinner was everything but satisfying or filling. 
I would like to start out my review with my opinion of  the new remodeling; It is absolutly beautiful - from the very impressive decorative roof and chandeliers - to the enormous televisions on each side of the restaurant - this place did a 180 with its appeal. So elegant and beautiful.
Now I would like to review their hospitality and food; we were seated at a decent table where we could see the new kitchen, (beautiful) and we're were welcomed by our server and submitted our order. Soon after my friend and I realized we had not received any water- I spent the next 15 minutes trying to flag down a waiter or bus boy, all who seemed to be ignoring me. Mind you there was give or take 6 people in the entire restaurant.  Finally I asked a young lady who was walking by if she could let our waiter know we would like some. About 15 minutes later- still not water or food, this was annoying. After about 35-40 total minutes of waiting thirsty and hungry- our water showed up and soon after our food. I mentioned to the the girl I had originally asked for water that I was upset that the wait was so long. I asked her if she would please bring me my check. About 10 minutes later a very rude manager (Miguel)  walked up to our table. He had a very aggressive and unprofessional tone- he asked me why I was upset with my service this evening- I explained to him why I was- and he began to very rudely tell me that they only offer water upon request. I told him that was understandable but that maybe they would put something on the table stating that- he said that he was not allowed to do so. I then suggested that maybe the waiter or bus boy mention that when greeting guests- he told me that was not their job. My friend mentioned that the table in front of us was brought water upon being welcomed- he insinuated that my friend was lying. This manager is no manager- he lacks everything it takes to be a manager. He was rude, arrogant, and spoke with a very condescending tone. I asked him to please bring me my check. 
I would like to state that I never once asked to speak with a manager- I asked for the check- plain and simple. If the girl would have brought my check- and I had not been subjected to that very unprofessional experience with that awful manager- I would not be writing this review. 
This 1 star review is souly courtesy of the manager of the Ocean.

Good ambiance and food. Menu has lots of gluten free options and you don't even have to ask for a separate menu they are on their main one. Service was terrible! Our waitress barely ever came over. There were 5 of us an almost there for 3 hours! Would think twice about going back because of the horrible service.

We ordered 3 sandwiches, the alcatraz was SO YUMMY and toasted perfectly. Fight Club had dry bread and the meat was really chewy but the flavors were good. The pastrami sauce was interesting but maybe just not our style. We will go back through to try different sandwiches.

I went here a while back on Open Mic night to listen to my friends' band! It's such a cute, little spot. The perfect environment to enjoy a yummy cup of coffee and listen to some music. Definitely recommend.

So I live bout a block away and the previous place was so bad I was skeptical to try. I gotta say I'm pleasantly surprised. Have only had the pizza so far and it is solid. Cornmeal crust, all that stuff. Still have to try the subs and wings, but so far so good.

My only gripe is I wish they marketed pizza/wing specials, sub/fries, etc regularly like most pizza joints work. Their menu only has a rotating "daily special". Other than that keep it up and you got a regular customer for sure!

Left me sitting for an hour and 10 mins after my apt time.. People that got there almost an hour after me were going in. No explanation until the second time I asked what the delay was..   Inconsiderate of people's time..

Worst nail experience ever. 
I paid for a premium mani - pedi with regular polish on Sun, Aug 2nd. Three hours after leaving the salon my nails were not dry and smudged. 
I called on Wed evening, 8/5 and was told to come back to have them fixed. 
The salon manager I spoke with on the phone was not there when I arrived. I had to explain the entire situation again. The nail technician was rude, defensive and combative to the point of threatening to call the police. 
The manager was called and they agreed to re-do my nails with gel polish. Less than three days later, today, Sat 8/8 while scratching my leg, the gel polish lifted. 
I am not a nail expert however, there is something sub-par at this salon in either the application or poor products that they use.
I advise any potential patron to avoid this nail salon. Your time and money can be well spent at a better performing salon.

Been getting better with greeting since my last review and I do enjoy their craft cocktails. 

We bought many bottles of bourbon for gifts.

For as many times as I have driven by this joint, it has never caught my attention or called to me in any way.  I figured it was frequented by the elderly from the old folks home across the street who come to play bridge and drink bottomless cups of coffee.  Fortunately what I found instead was a pretty solid greasy spoon serving up healthy portions for breakfast and lunch.  St. Louis is truly lacking when it comes to breakfast eateries, so I was thrilled to find a new option for Saturday and Sunday mornings.  

The staff here is very friendly, the portions are HUGE, and the food is really good and CHEAP.  I definitely recommend the skillets and the blueberry pancakes from the breakfast menu.  I haven't tried their lunch yet, but saw that they're only charging about $5-$6 for a 1/2 pound burger, so I will definitely be trying one of those on my next visit.

All in all, a great neighborhood short-order grill!

Tried to make reservations. Anne responded once but made no attempt to make the booking with us. I called twice and was told the only form of communication was email. Which was never answered after initial response. I would have been ok with a "sorry we are just too busy", but nothing.

BE CAREFUL - ER DOCS MAY NOT TAKE YOUR INSURANCE!

Had to go there twice.  Service and speed were fine, but their billing for the ER docs is a mess.  The hospital billed us right away and everything for the facility was as expected.  

But - both Physicians didn't bill until a half a year later, and the hadn't even submitted to my insurance.  So, after calling and asking for that, it turns out that the ER docs practicing there aren't on our health plan (BCBS, which is extremely widely accepted).  So it seems that this is another case of "you don't have a choice of doctor, so we'll screw you and bill you full price."  

So, be very careful and ask first (like that's an option when it's an emergency ...), you may be in for a steep price!!

I had my truck towed here because I broke down nearby on a Sunday and it seemed to be the closest reputable business to leave it overnight. Knowing what was wrong with my truck, I was expecting a somewhat reasonable estimate.  WOW, I was wrong.  For something that should have cost around 200 to fix, I got an estimate of $1,175. That's more  than my little truck is worth! I couldn't help but laugh and knew I needed to get it out of there.  I found another mechanic and tow company and was told that I was being charged $51 for them to give me an estimate!! Seriously??!! If you guys at pit stop would stop looking at $$$, and actually charge for what your customer needs, maybe you wouldn't have so many bad reviews.  I question the good one from Steve L.  Unless you have money to burn, keep your vehicle away from these crooks
