My mechanic picked it up today and just called me to let me know my bill was $260 including tow and is ready TODAY

Overpriced for the poor quality of items that they rent. Their whole staff from the manager to the delivery guys are just plainly disrespectful and have no concern or respect towards their current or future costumers. Don't waste your time. There is many other choices that are way better than this place.

Wild Garlic is tucked away so nicely I never would've known it was there if not for a friend. They have pizza by the pie and some by the slice specials that I thought was pretty cool, and they also have a special pizza of the month on top of the regular menu. Shout out to the October MacZilla! *drool* with its delicious balance of sweet and spicy with really creative toppings. Often crust on a pizza is totally hit or miss for me--but not here!. *chef's kiss* Good flavor and chew here, and don't forget to dip in the honey they provide on the tables!

Went for dinner last night with four others and had high expectations. Ordered a chicken breast panini with mozzarella, tomato and basil. $13.00. Supposed to be served with a red pepper aioli. After digging in, my sandwich tasted very dry, so I flagged down the waitress and asked for some extra aioli. She came back and informed me that they don't have any red pepper aioli. That the menu is incorrect. Hmmm. After eating a few more bites, I realized something else was missing. The basil!  So I flagged down the waitress again and told her my sandwich was missing the basil. She said that she would check, but that they probably didn't have any. And she was right! What the heck?  I was not happy. She offered to get the manager to take it off the bill, but I felt bad. I hesitantly told her that it was fine. After finishing half of the sandwich and feeling very underwhelmed, I called her back and asked her to take half off. She was very apologetic, and said she would ask the manager. She came back later with the check, and told me that they took half off. After leaving and thinking about it, I felt like this was handled totally wrong. First of all, if someone orders something and ingredients are not available, you go back to the customer and ask them if they would like something else. Most importantly, the manager should have come to our table to apologize and insist that they take the entire sandwich off my bill. I took the other half of my sandwich home, and when I went to eat it, I realized there was just a tiny piece of mozzarella on the other half, that only covered 1/2 of the chicken. So disappointing!  I guess the major problem is the outdated menu. The waitress told me that they don't ever have the aioli, yet on the menu, under panini's, it says they are all served with the red pepper aioli. If you don't want to reprint the menus, either take a marker and put a line through it, or have your staff inform your customers that significant ingredients are no longer available.

This place is maybe 3 blocks from where I just moved so I gave it a try on a Monday night (which also happens to be trivia night). The baked apple with pulled pork is AMAZING! I could eat that every night and be one seriously happy camper. The duck and blue cheese flatbread was too crispy in my opinion and not flatbready at all, the flavors were spot on though. I plan on returning... frequently!!

Oh and the desert, the carrot cake was delicious and very moist while the upside down brownie gooey cake thing was even more hip fattening delicious!

They are terrible with scheduling. Out of 5 appointment they canceled 3 of them. When I came in for my 3rd appointment the gentleman at the deck informed me that I was being charged a $50 fee for being a no show. When I explained to him that the appointment was rescheduled he informed me that he has faith in his employees and he will believe them over me. I asked him what my options were and he replied we don't give refunds for treatments not used.

After speaking with 3 others they had the same problem. One of which was charged $100 for appointments they rescheduled. THEY ARE A SCAM. The laser hair removal is 6 treatments long, that you are charged up front for. Once you pay for the package they will reschedule you than charge you for the rescheduling and if you don't pay the fee they will not reimburse you for the treatment you didn't receive. 

The 2 treatments I did received were great. If you can afford to pay $50 every time they reschedule you go for it.

Good food and lots of potential. Still having some new business issues but it's s great location and I know they will do really well. The outdoor patio has an amazing view of the sponge docks and the bar is a nice place bring people who are visiting the area   Best of luck

If you're accustomed to frequenting a true Italian bakery, then you should probably move on.... I assumed the location meant delicious cannoli's and an array of colorful cookies. I received neither. The cannoli cream tasted grainy, like sand, the shell soggy and the Danish was dry and stale. The tiramisu was a disgrace.... Yellow cake with whipped cream??? Where were the lady fingers? The mascarpone? The cocoa? The cookies were average. The only positive was that the service was quick and friendly.

I love this stand. 
It may be small. 
It may be hard to find. 
It has a Ton of flavors annd its open from 1-9. 
Score.

Family-run pizzeria with an extensive menu and local specials that benefit charities. Staff is friendly and the food is great. Selection isn't limited to just pizza, with choices including tacos, salads, sandwiches, and more. This is my go-to pizza joint whenever I'm in a pizza mood.

This place is small but the food is amazing.  I tried the cheesesteak and the lamb gyro, which both come with fries and a drink.  The cheesesteak was great and the gyro was probably the best I have had.  The prices are also very reasonable.  I would highly recommend trying this place if you're in the area.
This was wonderful!!! The food was amazing! Great atmosphere! Really impressed! Cindy did a great job!

I'd never had Provel before, so when reading about regional pizza, I was intrigued. And later when knowing we'd be spending the night in STL, I knew we had to try Imo's.  Since this location was a couple blocks away from out Airbnb, I called in a pick up order. The STL Special Box Or Whatever was a good way to try all the side nonsense like fried ravioli (chef boyardee meets alpo but still kinda good), provel bites (fried cheese cubes get cold fast), and cinnabuddies (they should probably be called that and are breadstick bites with the obvious sauce, obvi) along with a large two topping pie. 

Pie: THIN CRUST! CUT IN SQUARES! MINIMAL SAUCE! See, I hate Pizza King (MAF), but this pie was very nice. The provel is pretty much white american. Processed cheese is a little wack, but this works. Something about it just crushes. And unlike PK, Imo's uses real whole slices of pepperoni. Toppings were ample. They hooked the olives up.  

Service on the phone was whatever and it seemed like the kids in the store didn't really want to be there, and that's the lost star. Coulda been a couple bucks cheaper too. Maybe I didn't have to tip for carry out.  

I don't know when I'll be here again, but I will definitely speak highly of this delicious mutant pie.

this car wash is ok if ur just are buying a one time wash , 3 times i tried to buy a package  or buy 2 get one free ,the machine never works,  So u have to get help. Sometimes help comes to u . Usually  U have to go to them . Sometimes u get a gift card   sometimes u get a receipt thats SUPPOSE TO SCAN .  All 3 times I bought ONE I HAD TO GET HELP   EVERYTIME  I returned & tried to redeem my washes  I had to get help .  The last time i tried to buy  a buy two  get one free the machine didnt work . Nobody came out so I went to them , When I get there  they are playing hacky sack . I get treated like im bothering them . Im rushed off & handed a receipt . I ASK NO CARD ?  Told no just scan this receipt .  Obviously  Next time i return it doesnt work .When i left today   I am told next time to just come in cause this receipt  wont scan . After  this next wash ive paid for I wont be back ,  I have 2 side notes to add .  1rst I want to apologize for losing my temper to the last guy trying to help me  Ryan (i think ) . It wasnt his fault .  2nd its a shame because I would have been a life time customer . When Pelican Point  built a car wash in the next blk I felt sorry for them. They r local been here for years & an out of town corporation sets up shop right next store. I had every intention of doing all my business with them .

Always a good choice. The cookies are awesome and cupcakes are always a good choice. Not sure where all of the negative comments are coming from but we love this place. They change the specials on a regular basis and they are very creative.

Small but really well done--a relaxing and beautiful space for families and pretty much anyone!!

Restrooms are pristine, wildlife (bunnies, dragonflies, hummingbirds, etc) abound, and sculptures pique interest

They were out of pretty much everything and what they weren't out of had virtually no flavor and seemed like it had been sitting in the window for a looooooong time.  Chicken was really dry, the macaroni was crunchy, potatoes were just meh.  It wasn't the worst I've ever had in my life but it wasn't very pleasant and I wouldn't eat it again.

If your in Reno and Want the Real Deal Eat Here! 
While Driving around Reno doing some Antique shopping we saw this place tucked in a small side road off the Virgina Street Strip, saw the name and thought ...... Hmmmmmm Good Mafia Name Must be good Italian Food.... LOL 
BINGO we were Right! Several good things about this place only one bad. 
We called ahead for a reservation.. GOOD IDEA ! The place was Packed!!!!!!!! We were welcomed like Family and given our table right away. We were met with our waitress who was very friendly,knowledgeable and right on time. We were served some great Bread and Oil and Balsamic vinegar OUTSTANDING .... Some good Sparkling water, my wife had a nice glass of wine from their great wine list. We were told what the specials were and thought over the menu, ordering the Calamari Salad... OUTSTANDING ! 
I ordered the Lobster Ravioli special, and my wife had the Seafood linguine...... OUT OF THIS WORLD! 

Our Service was Good , the food excellent, we finished it off with the Tirimisu ! 

Only bad thing is the Parking ! Horrible we drove around the block several times and finaly parked a 2 blocks away.  
other than that!
We will be back to this place for sure! OUTSTANDING!
Are you kidding me? $12 for 2 single cones??  And, of course, the price isn't posted so you don't know until it's scooped & you're  at the register. And it's not quality ice cream.
It's always a pleasure getting your own French Press when ordering coffee.  Great customer service too.

I had never before been to a Red Lobster, and I am not sure that I will be back.  Mostly because of the service.  My boyfriend and I were on this side of town and starving last night, so we decided to stop in and try it since neither of us had eaten here before and the place we had set out to go to was closed.

We walked in and had to stand in the entrance way for some time while four employees standing at the bar stared at us, not wanting to be the ones to acknowledge us and thus, I assume, take responsibility for seating us.  Finally a girl comes over and takes us past tables with cleaning supplies out and seats us.  Oh, I almost forgot!  We are in an hour and fifteen minutes before they are supposed to close.

Then we wait.  And wait.  And then someone comes over for our drink order and we both get iced tea.  When she brings the drinks she asks if we were ready to order and we asked for a few more minutes.  We should have ordered dinner then.  So after a while if waiting (with my empty iced tea glass) she comes back, fills my iced tea and takes our order.  We got the sweet chili shrimp as an appetizer and I ordered fish and chips with a salad and bf ordered crab linguinie with a cup of soup.

Our biscuits came out and let me just say, those were the best thing that hit the table.  They were so yummy!  Then we waited, with my tea glass empty again.  After 5 to 10 minutes with an empty glass I found the iced tea pitcher behind me and grabbed it to refill myself and my date.  The waitress sees this and takes it away.  All I could think was "No, don't take it!  Now I will never get a refill!"  But that seemed to be her wake up call.  The first twenty minutes we were there or so, my glass stayed empty longer than it stayed full, and after she caught me refilling my own tea, she made more of an effort to come by and check on us.

Our appetizer and soup and salad came out, and they were pretty good.  I am a fierce, fierce judge of ranch dressing, and the dressing on my salad was peppery, there were fresh cucumbers, tomatoes, carrots, cabbage and more than three croutons on it (I hate when places only give you a few croutons.)  The lobster bisque was delightful, though I have nothing to compare it to and the shrimps were sweet and spicy and cooked very well.

Dinner came out, and while I was expecting more "eat with your fingers" type of fish, this came out as a huge filet.  I would have preferred smaller, more manageable pieces of fish.  My fish was a little over cooked and dry, but overall pretty tasty.  My boyfriend seemed pretty happy with his crab pasta, it did have a good amount of crab in it.  (I can't eat crab after a fateful night when I was nine and ate WAY too much crab, so I didn't try it).

The entire time, though, employees were draped around the bar on their cell phones and cleaning up tables and floors.  We definitely felt rushed and like we were an inconvenience to the staff there.  While my iced tea was empty, I made eye contact with many employees, and while some would stare, others would look away, but no one came by to ask if we needed anything.

So, I don't think I will be back.  This was a pretty lackluster experience.

My review is strictly for the desserts. I had the Creme brûlée and the Chocolate cake. Both were pretty solid. The bartender was attentive. However, he did ask if wanted ice cream with our cake which we said yes to but we didn't get. The decor was nice inside with the lights, too.

We took some clients to POD and had a fantastic meal.  We chose the Chef's tasting menu which afforded 5 of us an amazing spread of sushi, dim sum, fresh fish and meats that more than satisfied the team.  Add in a gigantic bottle of Sake and you've got all the makings for a great night.  Yes the ambiance is a little 'Tokyo 1998' with plastic and rounded edges coming at you from every angle but it works. Favorite dishes include the Sea Bass, Calamari Salad and Crab Fried Rice.

If I could give this place a zero I would but I got a pedicure and the guy burnt me with the water when I said I wanted it colder he kept going hotter and hotter and I was getting a manicure done and the guy was talking about me In his language and he didn't know that I understood his language but he messed up my shape and did dip when I said that I wanted acrylic and my nails were 80 for plain white and he was very rude when I said that I would like another person to do my nails they said I couldn't get a new person because I had already paid him‍ it's just a little sketchy

So... I love Natalie.

And she always helps me out. I have followed her to a couple different places and am soon going to have her put extensions in my hair.

Most recently she styled hair for a photoshoot I coordinated/styled... A modern day Marie Antoinette theme.

She always executes my vision perfectly.

Love her.

Ah, Imo's Pizza- the pride of St.  Louis, Missouri.  Good stuff.  I wasn't sure if I'd like it, but I most definitely did.  The sausage is very mild, and I probably would have liked it more if it had a little more fennel and other Italian seasonings.  It definitely wasn't bad- it just could be better.  The tomato sauce is of the sweeter type- not as sweet as Chicago staples like Aurelio's or D'Agostino's, but it was just sweet enough.  The crust is a cracker thin, unleavened bread, supposedly with corn syrup or molasses added to it, but I could not detect either.  I enjoyed its ultra thin cracker variety.  

However, the real star of this pizza is the one feature the vast majority of people outside the greater St.  Louis metropolitan area can't stand- the Provel cheese.  It's actually not cheese because it doesn't meet the requirements for cheese according to the U.S.  government specifications.  Instead, it's a cheese- product.  

In short, Provel cheese is a processed cheese-product.  It consists of a blend of provolone, swiss, and cheddar- no mozzarella what-so-ever.  Thus, there's no stretch or stringyness to it.  To the contrary, Provel cheese is very soft, gooey, slightly creamy, and just a little bit smokey.  It almost reminds me of a melted, soft, gooey version of Kraft Easy Cheese which is no surprise because Provel cheese is owned by Kraft.  

I enjoyed my Imo's pizza very much, especially their trademark Provel cheese.  It was good- different, but good.  It's definitely worth a try when in St.  Louis, and you have something in the area of 94 locations to choose from.  Thus, Imo's influence on the St.  Louis area is overwhelming.  We ate at the location inside the Ballpark Hilton, just around the block from Busch Stadium.  Two stars off for the server not providing plates.
Amazing! Best meal I had in NOLA and we had some good food. The po boy was so good I wanted to come back another time. Oysters are huge!!!!

Never ate at the actual location which I will one day but I have purchased my Korean bbq signature SRB short ribs from here five times on Uber eats one of the best Korean bbq's I ever tasted beyond delicious. Oh and the spicy short ribs are great to I prefer the SRB ribs.

I gave this restaurant 2 stars, only because I think the pulled pork would be good if it was warm.
Don't ask a server for a recommendation unless you want to hear, "whatever you like".

Our family of 6 are patients of Dr. Crawford and I am thrilled with both his work and the service from his staff! I give the whole team a big 5 stars! We are always greeted with personal care and timeliness on the appts. Dr Crawford is excellent with my kids(ages 10-17) and takes extra time to make sure they are comfortable and have a great experience. iPads ready to go, super comfy chairs and a beautiful clean office are just the extras that ease any "dental anxiety". I also just finished the process of getting front veneers; they are beautifully designed and look just like natural teeth! He was very meticulous and I was impressed with the technology and extra steps in making sure my teeth were just what I wanted. Thank you!!

Great, convenient location. Decent selection for a small store. That's about it. I mean, I do love that they have a little cafe section and coffee bar, but the produce is over-priced, the hours aren't great, and there isn't much for variety.

And why the heck can't they accept a phone number for the Sobey's card?!

The only reason I can give this Lobster Buffet a 1 star is that the sever was excellent. He kept our drinks full and our table clean. The lobster was like trying to eat one of my dogs chew toys. To bad!  I went several years  and the lobster was small but cooked much better.

Worst experience in the food industry of my life!
The server lost my credit card for 30 minutes and called me a lair about giving her my card as payment!
(Obviously i didn't lie if they found it in a trash can in the kitchen!!!!!)
HOW, HOW did this happen? 
I  have never been treated so poorly, I came in for my friends birthday with a party of 16 people.
We placed our orders all the food came out at the same time except my boyfriends, he is not a seafood guy but came to enjoy the party they had chicken pasta on the menu so he ordered that. Sadly by the time he received it everyone was pretty much done. They came over and apologized and comped it for him. OK , that was nice so we moved on. 
When everyone was done no one came to check on us, there where a few children now becoming fussy so the parents where standing and talking... we obviously looked ready to go. I  found the manager and asked if he could find someone to check us out and to start clearing our 2 party tables. Our waitress Keisha came, said that she was so busy she was sorry about the wait. I told her that she had earlier taken our birthday treats we brought to the refrigerator in back of I could please have it before everyone leaves. She brought it over and told us it would be a minute while she figured out how to separate all our orders. We ate the cake and chatted still no checks? 
When she comes out she starts handing them out and every one starts telling her this or that was incorrect.   (our friends don't want to pay for  items someone else recieved)
So she collected them all and made some notes and set off to try again.
Some of the party at this are getting upset we are all irritated about this wait.
1 couple gave us cash and said it would cover their bill any change give to the waitress. We said good bye. 
Another friend asked her husband to wait and pay she needed to put the kids in the car they where getting grouchy.
Now 6 of 16 have gone no checks paid yet. 
Waitress comes out and half the checks are correct  mine was correct I gave her my card along with the slip. She left to fix the last two checks and cash out the ones who where correct. She returns about another 10 minutes later with receipts and the last two checks that are now correct. 
Most people have now left I'm still waiting on my receipt she brings out the last two receipts and their cards, so I ask for mine.
SHE SAID I NEVER GAVE HER MY PAYMENT!
I told her nicely that I had it was a yellow suntrust card for 64dollars I owed.
SHE STILL INSISTED I NEVER GAVE HER MY PAYMENT!
I'm getting irritated, anyone who knows me i am very good at keeping my cool.
I told her she needed to go find my card and her manager right now!
She asked me to stand up and told me I must have dropped it!
I told her again that she has my card its not on the floor! So I stood and said "Well take a look then, but I need a manger."
She looks on the floor and under the dirty dishes that have now been here almost an hour!
She then tells me that she was sorry I lost my card but that she has never lost a customers payment and that she is very responsible. 
She empties her pockets on my table her apron her bill fold and all her tips for the night and a said see I don't have your card!
I'm so mad at this point. 
I calmly told her I need you to find my card.
She leaves again and her manager comes up and said I was told you lost your card!
I LOST MY CARD. 
I told him I gave the waitress my card that if you have cameras I want them checked!
He said that they don't have any unfortunately that he will try to find my card.
I see everyone at this point staring, other waitress have gathered and are talking and looking over at me. 
My boyfriend Is so mad i had to tell him to calm down and just let me handle it.
If he had it his way he would be causing one heck of a scene.
He has been giving death glares along with his best friend that stayed behind to make sure everything is OK while his fiance is in the car waiting with their teenager.
Then Keisha come running out holding my card she says I found it !! 
She is wiping it off trying to explain that she must have thrown it away when she was trying to take payment and clear a few dishes, that it simply got lost in the mix! She is wiping it off on her shirt and handed it to me the manager comes behind her with a sanitized wipe to clean my card that had been handed to me covered in what I assume is butter and gunk.
I took it back and and started to wipe it off they told me they would be right back with my new bill.
Only Keisha came out, not the manager? She said that they have removed one whisky sour from my bill.
I looked at her and said I need your manager because I'm not paying you.
She came back with a new bill no manager he must be tired of my boyfriend and his friends death glare and my inconvenience of a situation.

Since I was down in South Tampa, I might as well check out what the discount shopping scene was like. I spied a Marshall's I'd not yet been in, so I remedied that. 

The store is clean, neat and some what organized. However not a single employe said hello, asked me if I needed anything or made eye contact.  Lucky for me, I'm a pretty much self sufficient shopper and can do it all on my own. I found what I wanted, needed and made my way to the registers. 

I managed to find a few essential oils and other neat things, and checked out without a lot of fuss. All in all I spent just under $50 and walked out with my head held high.

So, after trying both Parkway and Domilise, the winner of best poor boy is Domilise's, hands down! I'm not sure what all the Parkway hype is about, but Domilise has better seafood, bread, sauce and selection. 

The wife and I had the Surf N' Turf and Shrimp Po-Boys at parkway. Flavor was good but not much better than any other Po-Boy in NOLA. 

At Domilise we split a Shrimp & Oyster Po-Boy because their Po-Boys are twice the size of Parkway's. It was AMAZING. Best Po-Boy, no, BEST SANDWICH EVER! My wife described the oysters best when saying that they melt-in-your-mouth. The slightly spicy sauce compliments them very well. The bread is also great and perfectly toasted. Be sure to order your Po-Boy fully dressed for extra flavor.

So I heard good things (piled high with succulent meat and huge portions) about this sandwich shop right down the block from the St. Luis convention center.  I'm always down for a nice sandwich in a new city! Oh boy, was I disappointed.  The only plus about the sandwich was that it was huge. I got the Smoked Brisket with BBQ and Swiss in the "pro" size.  Sounded awesome but frankly the Brisket was dry and kinda bland.  I wasn't a fan of the sauce either, which was flavorless.  I walked in right around closing time so maybe that was a factor.  I'm willing to give this place another try but maybe stick to the roast beef or chicken.

I have the steak and pasta this time, and it was really good. Unfortunately the service wasn't the friendliest.

Pricing is a bit high, and the menu is small. If you like Seafood, the menu seems to be geared towards that type of food.
Misappropriated the Seattle WA based Biscuit Bitch menu (word for word!) and dishes!!!  Not cool - be original folks!  Don't steal others ideas!!!!

I've loved this place every time I've been, which is about five or six as of this writing. I've done both take out and dine it. I prefer eating in, but sometimes it's good to get good food to go. 
I especially like the Pad Kee Mao, I get it about every time. I've also tried the pineapple fried rice-- it is my wife's favorite fried rice of anywhere! 
They have some bottled beer options  I wish they had some draft offerings, but I can't always have things my way. 
The service for both dine in and take out has always been good, but it is the quality and taste of the food that brings me back. I've never had the inconsistency in quality that at least one reviewer mentioned. Perhaps they DO have it figured out now.

My boyfriend was aching for a burger so I decided to stop by here since it was close to where I was shopping on the way home.  Ordering was easy due to the simple menu, and the open kitchen was fun to watch while you order your meal.  I ordered a small fry and a double bacon cheeseburger.  I was glad that there were so many choices for additions, so that your burger could be easily customized.  They also had a super fancy soda machine that could be used to create an astonishing amount of flavor combinations with your favorite sodas.  The verdict: 8/10 stars, which is quite the feat, considering everything in life for him is 'just ok'.

4 stars seem like a crazy high review for a Carrabas but bear with me. I have only gone here for the occassional lunch. The food is okay, I have never had anything to really complain about or note. Except, one time my lunch partner had something hard, like a piece of bone in his chicken soup... that was a tough one to get past.

Service has always been good; waiters have always been nice to us. Bread and olive oil never fails to be a nice touch.

But the four stars - that is all the tiramisu. Maybe everybody knows that tiramisu is great at any Carrabas; but I've only gone to this one so I could have missed that memo. I LOVE the tiramisu. It's rich and light all at the same time. Has a good flavor, the chocolate shavings are a nice touch with the variance of texture. Plus it photographs really well. Maybe you come here for just a couple drinks and apps that are average, but get yourself through that and order and savor the tiramisu. The size is good to share with one other person. Though...let's be real - I think I could eat the entire thing. #glutton

While my experience was awful, it doesn't mean yours will be too. A couple things to keep in mind.

1- Get here early. Lines are crazy for non VIP. We arrived at 9:30pm. Lines for everything are over 30 minutes.

2- Drive slow leading up the the place. The roads are bumpy and your car will thank you for it. 

3- Wear sneakers, or boots. Standing in wet grass is not fun in flip flops.

-----------

Attraction wise, it was well thought out and the acting was authentic. Less hair touching is recommended and putting foreign objects down peoples shirts. 

Like everyone says, the Ghost Hunt is not worth it. Skip it.
Very nice, new style, stadium. Not a lot to say beyond that (other than I wish the Cubs would have won).

Day Drinking on Fourth Avenue

Episode 2  (Friday, 4:30 PM)

After a couple of minor setbacks (not all establishments on 4th have a full bar), we land at Mr. Head's.  

We plunk ourselves down and settle in to watch this guy wield a blow torch while we wait for our Long Islands. Huh.  Maybe a bong?  Maybe a crack pipe?  I'm not trying to be (too) snarky; those are just the first practical uses I can think of for blown glass.  This is growing on me...I could squat here all day and zone out.

By now, I've already got a warm steady glow and am not feeling all that choosy about what I drink.  But this swill comes straight outta the pre-made jug. It's not a craft cocktail and you don't have to be a "mixologist" to throw a Long Island together.  Damn:  it's equal parts vodka, gin, rum, tequila, triple sec, lemon juice with a splash of Coke and a girlie cherry.  I think.  No, I'm not 18, and no, I'm not trying to get laid, so quit with that.  

In a day of firsts, I think this is the first time that I'm abandoning a full cocktail.

The gallery vibe is nifty...but the drinks tho.

Just picked up food at this place. 3 hour wait after being told it would be 1.5 hours. Horrible attitude, practically unapologetic and didn't discount anything or even offered me water whilst I waited.

Horrific service and lack of care for their customers.

make sure you make a good 1st impression, Apache staff are quick to not help folks they think may not be customers, little do they know this guy had over 800 credit score and their not allowing me to walk around the used rv's lot on my own showed me their true colors...
The hot cereal is a very good option here at this particular store. Sorry for the shelf that was almost bumped when the cartwheel bearing became non-operable.

Had the Carolina and it was flawless. The slaw was just right against the sweet sauce. Burger was cooked well; not a thing to say negatively about the burger. The sides are really good; be warned that the onion rings are ridiculously huge. Service was slow but nothing to really complain about; only kept the last star off the charts.

Understocking, employee discount... I got a sound system with a 20% discount on the last day of that discount for an item readily available. black friday has this item 43% off, which is more then the 20%. Item still available a day into the sale. They wouldn't give the difference because it was a employee discount buy and is called understocking. Give me a break, the item will continue to be available for eternity. If a customer (non employee) had the same situation, they would give you the difference. I understand understocking if its an item your getting before a non employee like a new xbox on day one but this item has been around forever. complete BS

Absolutely disappointed... food was ok and service was mediocre. This place may look nice at first but once you sit down you can see the difference. It seems a little judge mental and racist and that is not my cup of tea. You would think in a créole city it would be more accepting but I guess not. Not satisfied and will not return.
This is a good non chain coffee shop off of the main drag in Lambertville. Good bulk coffee lots of interesting coffee gadgets as well.... worth a visit.

Charles Chestnutt is a terrific tour guide! His style of storytelling is so immersive, we felt like we were transported back in time witnessing the Battle in New Orleans! We also love his sense of humor and the way he injects his stories with little jokes. We highly recommend the Historic Tour on board the Creole Queen to anyone planning a trip to New Orleans!

So I find the owners of this place very helpful and enjoyable. However their one employee has twice been very nasty to me. He is usually working the key machine. I asked him "hello could you make me A copy of this key?" His response was him massaging his temples and replying "sorry my psychic abilities aren't working today. Can you tell me how many copies you want?" In a nasty tone. It's not professional and I won't be going there because of him.

Never never never again!!!

Had take out yesterday in celebration of the Olympics in S Korea!

70.00 dollars for two dinners/ chicken and beef with rice and veggies and soup! 

Very very very small portions for the price... not enough to feed 2 adults and 2 children.

Extremely disappointed in the value for 70.00 dollars!  Called the manager and they said there was nothing they could do! They also forgot salad, lettuce wraps and bean paste with the meal!  I am not Korean and feel taken advantage of because of that fact!!!

I will never ever go here again... try Dolsot house in Cherry Hill or many of the other Korean Restaurants in the are.  Way better portions, prices and service!

Never again Daiswa ... you should be ashamed!!!

I really wish I had more positive things to say about this place, but unfortunately it's been a pretty consistent letdown the last several times we've visited. 

I'll make no reservations about saying that, at one point, this was hands down my favorite restaurant in Brentwood and one of the best in all of Nashville. I say that mostly because their margaritas are fantastic. The food has never been particularly amazing, though certainly not bad either. During the summer, we'd go nearly once a week. The bartenders were generally friendly and the margaritas always hit the spot. 

Over the last year, unfortunately, the service has gotten absurdly bad. Twice in the past month or two we waited 10 minutes without being greeted by a server before finally leaving. The last time, we did so only to find the entire staff out back smoking/laughing/etc. Nobody, management included, was even in the restaurant (and the hostess that seated us was back there with them, so somebody should've known we were there). After that last visit, we left and went straight to Bar Taco, which is where we will go from now on. 

The margaritas are still decent, but the food is nothing to get excited about, they've cut the portions on the queso dip in half, and the service is a joke. With better margaritas just a few more minutes away at Bar Taco, I'm sad to say I can't find many good reasons to go back to Local.

The food was pleasant and the environment was warm and friendly.   I visited this place before Katrina and recall the food being a little better.  It's a good Southern Breakfast and the place is worth a try if you are in NOLA for a few days...

For ice cream... It's the "cream" of the crop! Why 4 stars.... Will the line is fracking long yo! I would estimate I end up going somewhere else half the time cause I won't wait for crap that really isn't good for me. This is probably a good thing.. .... I must go home now, it's dinner time.

Very rude drive thru person. This guy made me wait close to 10 minutes at drive thru with no acknowledgment. I said "hello" two times because maybe they didn't hear my car's engines or malfunction with the speakers. Also when you're waiting at a drive thru, they let you know that it will take a while within two minutes of you waiting there. This location didn't let me know and the drive thru person was a huge dickhead. He told me in a very rude tone after I waited for couple minutes and said hello,  "Maam, we have a huge order ahead of you and we are busy". No apologies or anything. There was also one car in front of me too with 3 cars behind me. Me and the guy behind me left because it was ridiculous. This drive thru guy lacks social skills and shouldn't be working the drive thru. If he had told me kindly that I would have to wait, I would've waited. I have no problem with that. Socially inept people shouldn't be working the drive thru.

White people LOVE IKEA.  I guess since I'm not really white, I'm only kinda like it.  

I can tell you one thing.  Walking around the showrooms makes me WANT TO STAB EVERYONE REPEATEDLY.  

I'm just trying to get the hell out and the signs sending me all over the place and looping me over myself are just not helping me do it.

I wound up not getting everything i set out to get because I felt as if I couldn't move freely.

I do have hang ups though.

I've only eaten here once, but I would definitely make a point to go back if I was in Reno.

Funny thing, we go to Reno twice a year and stay at the Silver Legacy.  We've tried many of the restaurants in Reno, but for some reason we had never eaten here.  So we decided to try it.

The food was excellent.  I had the lamb shank special, with spinach risotto.  My companion had the mushroom ravioli, but mistakenly ordered the appetizer instead the dinner.  Considering he's a good-sized guy, I was surprised the waiter didn't point out that it was an appetizer, but maybe he thought we weren't that hungry.

Luckily my lamb shank was HUGE and absolutely wonderful, so we ended up sharing it.  It was plenty of food for both of us.  The meat absolutely fell off the bone, and the flavor was wonderful.  I don't usually order lamb shank, because I've had bad experiences with very dry lamb, but the guy next to us was having it and recommended it.  I'm glad he did.

For dessert we had a chocolate/almond torte with raspberry sorbet, which was to die for.

We'll be back!

I gave this place a try after passing by numerous times. The first thing I noticed when I sat down and looked at the menu was that it was pretty similar to Houston's menu (the previous restaurant). I was glad they kept most of the old favorites such as the House Smoked Salmon. We had the fried oysters with creamed spinach as a starter. The spinach was my favorite part because the oysters seems to be fried a little too long, shrinking the size and drying it out a bit.

For the main meal, I tried the herb rotisserie chicken and the bf had the prime rib sandwich. They were both pretty good, but I felt like the chicken was lacking something, but I couldn't figure out what. Maybe I'm just not the herb spice type.

I prefer Zeas rotisserie chicken more. 

Overall it was a good meal. I would try it again.

This was the first time visiting this place. I used to go to Pietro's in Pike Creek and a little sadden to hear they moved. 

Our waiter was really nice; checked on us when he should and left us alone when he should. Pushy or inattentive waiters are annoying. I asked him what was popular for the burgers and he said the Brewhouse so I ordered it.

I was a little disappointed with my burger. I asked for it to be cooked med (which to me means a little pink) there was no pink and it was flat, almost like a fast food burger. The bun was good, I think it was like a buttery bun. 

Their onion ring batter is different than any other onion ring I've tasted. It was almost like cakey in a way; moist even though the onion ring was crunchy on the outside; a bit greasy though. Their basil aioli is pretty good and not the typical ranch you find with onion rings.

I absolutely love this place. My only complaint at is that the beef is a little tough. Side from that everything is amazingly well cooked and delicious in fact I would venture to say this is the best hot and sour soup I've had out in the suburbs ever. Usually if I won't really get hot and sour soup I have to venture into the city find or get it for me this little family owned mom and pop restaurant was a treasure to find!
Stopped in while doing laundry. Got shrimp fried rice. Perfectly acceptable. No idea what all the fuss is about.

Worst Starbucks ever, staff ignored me when I had a problem with my order. In fact worse looked at me at the register and turned their back on me and continued to talk. All I wanted was to buy the banana nut bread I had asked for and they did not add to my order.

Let me start off by saying I had a promo that expired which I knew about but the price I paid would still be good. So before I ordered I was trying to explain that and the owner maybe didn't understand or I didn't explain it correctly but he started giving me a bit of attitude and said it was no good and contact deal chicken as they were responsible for refunds, ok finally he said he would give me the value of what I paid and I said that's all I wanted. Ok so that was solved. They brought me the chips which were fresh and tasted good but the more I ate they had an aftertaste. Not sure if it was the oil the chips were fryed in.i ordered the carne asada enchiladas and the steak was good with some nice chunks but the sauce had a wang to it. Overall the service was ok but I was a little disappointed in the food and have had better in other Reno Mexican restaurants

Stopped in for a bite on Christmas Eve and the young man behind the counter was super sweet. The girl back there was nice as well. They didn't seem to mind working this day at all and seemed to be having fun with it.  The good service was appreciated.

Where do I begin? I feel as if this business cheated me out of a poorly done service that I even paid for. From the beginning she was extremely rude and seemed as if she didn't even want to hem my curtains. After going back in forth on the original price I was quoted she finally agreed to do the job. When I came to pick up the curtains not only where they thrown into garage bags but not all of the curtains where even hemmed. From instant attitude coming in the door to being made feel as a complete inconvenience. Take your money elsewhere for better customer service and an actual job to be done.

I know that there's a Cracker Barrel somewhere in Cincinnati, OH, but I've never been to it. This is my first time at a Cracker Barrel, and although I was skeptical about it, I was won over by the time I left.

The storefront is really cool! They have everything from headache balm, to clothes, quilts, vintage candies and sodas, and candles. It reminds me of the old general stores, so I guess they nailed the concept. This location had rockers out in the front for people to chill out on, and you could buy them if you chose to. 

The breakfast menu has something like, "It's never too late for the most important meal of the day" to which I was definitely like "Amen." I got the Old Timer's Breakfast, which included: Two eggs, Grits, Sawmill Gravy, Two Homemade Buttermilk Biscuits, Hashbrown Casserole, and Bacon. Bacon was good (Nothing beats the sugar cured bacon at Clinton Street Baking Company in NYC), but I wasn't too fond of the grits (it was bland), nor the gravy. 

HOWEVER,

The biscuits are so, so, so, so, soooooooooo good. Make sure to request the apple butter, smother it on your biscuit, and revel in the taste of delicious, butter, warm-in-your-tum comfort. Oh my gosh, I wish I could just order 5 bajillion biscuits and a tub of apple butter. 

The hashbrown casserole was okay (kinda cheesy). 

I do have to say though, my boyfriend got the Chicken Fried Chicken with mashed potatoes and greenbeans, and his food was more delicious. I'm totally getting that next time. 

The woman who served us was really friendly, and it really felt like we were given a dose of friendly southern hospitality.

tl;dr southern hospitality forreals, biscuits with apple butter is a must. chicken fried chicken is amazeballs. Throwback Home on the Range storefront is pretty cool. Vintage candies!

We have been to this neighborhood bike shop three times. The owner is generous with his advice and has been more than fair every time with the charges. He steered me toward the proper way to take care of the chain, turned me on to thorn-resistant tubes (after about 20 flats) and instead of charging me for an unnecessary wheel straightening (which I had requested,) indicated the work was not needed. No charge. I also was surprised that his charge for the particular tubes we bought was less than what I found online. This shop gets our vote!
Their Pho is the best I've ever had. Service is good but can be better as they seem to have only one server doing all the work on busy weekend nights ...

Well look what happened. I have to raise my rating of Kingside Diner to three stars.  We ended up there after trying to go to another place that has stopped serving breakfast. I wasn't to happy about it because of past experiences.  What the heck. It was close by. We were very lucky to get a table outside. 

We ordered orange juice ( which was very good) and looked over the menu.  He decided on the corned beef hash with poached eggs in top and I went with my usual bacon and cheese omelet. It came with a side of hash browns and toast. DJ was our server and was prompt in everything he did.  Despite having a full crowd, our food came out in a timely fashion.  What happened here?  Our food was actually hot. Even the toast was warm. My omelet was filled to the brim with bacon and his poached eggs were perfect.  The hash browns were also good. 

Would I come back here?  I would because I can't wait to see what my next adventure here will be like.

I had never been to this restaurant.  I live in the north east and rarely go to this part of the city.  i went here for lunch with a friend who works in the area.  It was a Monday so it was not overly busy during the lunch hour.  There were maybe 4 or 5 other tables occupied at the restaurant.  

We were greeted warmly by the owner and shown our table.  Waiter service was prompt and efficient. The lunch menu had a variety of options including soups, salads, pastas and paninis as well as a small selection of entrees.   

My lunch mate had the Oliveto  salad with chicken, i went for the ricotta ravioli in a brown butter sauce.  I ordered the full size portion on the recommendation of the server, glad i did that because not only was it delicious, I was hungrier than I thought.

Since it was lunch and we were on a tight schedule, we didn't have any wine or dessert.  But based on my lunch, i would definitely like to come back one evening for a full meal with friends from the larger, more varied dinner menu.

Overall a good experience.  Recommended!!
This Brazilian hurt so bad. Great price, got the job done but man. It was sore for a day or two afterwords .

We stopped in, for our first visit, on a very busy Friday night. We ordered the 14" BBQ chicken pizza and waited about 45 minutes for it to arrive. Another table that sat down after us received their orders before we did (they had ordered Stromboli and calzones, I believe, so it wasn't as big of a deal). About 10 minutes after that, we got our pizza. No real apology for the wait. The pizza adequately served our group of four. A waitress stopped by the table who had received their orders before us and offered them complimentary garlic knots for their wait; we never received said offer. Nonetheless, we will definitely return because we agreed they make a seriously good pizza; it's one of the best pizzas I've ever eaten. They are busy for a reason!

I had two pairs of jeans hemmed here. It was a quick and easy process and they turned out great. I dropped my jeans off on a Saturday and they were able to assist me right away. There's a little changing room in the back and the tailor was quick and easy to work with. I was in and out in under 10 minutes. Picked up the completed job on the following wednesday. I felt the price of $5/leg or $10/pant was fair. Really the only reason I am not giving a 5 star review is because the automatic text message system that would have told me my pants were finished didn't work and I had to call in. Overall a good experience and I plan to use them for my future dry cleaning and alteration needs.

Sky Cafe, I'm so sad that I'm moving away and leaving you. 

I came here on Saturday with my sister and it was like being transported back to Medan, Indonesia. We were the only non-Indonesians in the place. 

You MUST get the sate ayam (chicken sate), and the kwetiau goreng (fried noodles). Both taste just like the food that I used to eat in Indonesia. Sitting in the restaurant and listening to the bahasa Indonesia and stuffing myself with Indonesian food was definitely the highlight of my weekend. 

I also love how affordable this place is. Lunch for two came to $12.
My salmon bowls are decent but the staff was sorta short and rude when I was ordering like I was bothering her that I came in. I'll still come back hopefully get a better staff member.

It's a standard home goods, not too much to write home about.  If you shop early or score a clearance item you can get a very reasonable price from some quality items... just make sure that you inspect for quality.

Normally, I enjoy my meal at this restaurant which is why I did not object to going here for my birthday brunch. I ordered the crab cake benedict after receiving an email advertising brunch options. This had to be the worst crab cake benedict I have ever had. It tasted like cornbread and lacked crab in the mixture. Then they offer unlimited blood marys and I soon found out why. It was just as bad, if not worst. Tomato juice meet vodka and no additional effort given. My brunches this weekend better make up for this terrible meal because this certainly put a damper on my birthday afternoon. The other attendees were okay with their entrees, but they all ordered fajitas. You can't really botch that up.

Food is good but service sucked! Was told to sit anywhere...fine. After 15 minutes no server. Didnt get a menu till i went to the counter again to remind him i was here...sitting at the tables across from him the whole time.  Mind you...its after lunch rush. About 10 people outside, maybe 8 inside. Food took so long i had to get it to go!  Wont be back...i was totally ignored!  I only gave 2 stars cause the food was good. Waste of my break!!

On the day of QingMingJie(Chinese culture when loved ones visit cemetery of deceased), I was looking to buy white chrysanthemums or "Mums". Call flower shops around  Mayfair/Tacony/Holmesburg, and came across Torresdale Flower Shop. It was such a relief! The lady that answer the phone were very courteous and knowledgeable. Definitely recommend this shop! 

P. S. On the same day, I told another fellow family member that TFS got the chrysanthemums, she also stops by and got some.
Yesssssss I absolutely love the Cobb salad with the lite vinaigrette!! The staff were friendly and the food was on pointtttttt !! clean restaurant and not too many people in there when I came in

I just got my hair cut here for the first time today and it's a huge improvement over the non-style I was rocking before! The staff isn't super friendly but that doesn't particularly bother me. What did kind of get me though, and what's keeping me from giving them 5 stars, is that they charged me the price of a women's haircut for what was very clearly a 15 minute "men's" cut. Still, it was considerably cheaper than a salon downtown so I'll probably be back.

BEWARE THIS GYM GENERAL MANAGER DYLAN GREEN WILL RIP YOU OFF. 

I've been a member for over a year. When I first signed up everything was great, the owner and his daughter were very pleasant (Hence the extra star) and because I am a local business owner gave me a deal on my membership. 

As of January my membership increased 200% then doubled in February. When I inquired about the problem Dylan sent me a contract that I never signed stating I agreed to the change. He never once offered to make things right or to maintain my current membership price, he said ai agreed to doubling the cost of my membership... REALLY WHO WOULD AGREE TO THAT?! 

Luckily my bank is working with me to dispute the charges. When it comes down to it the gyms pool is very dirty and the locker rooms are not well kept so it's not a huge miss on my part. I have some local friends who own gyms so I hardly even went to this one but kept paying my monthly fee to support the business, that is until they increased it without my permission.

If you want a great deal on some local gyms message me but stay away from this spot. For whatever reason Dylan Green is a sociopath who forged my contract and then told me to "have a nice weekend"

The Wheelhouse (in Clayton) is a tough place to review because the experience depends so much on when you go, and the occasion.  The food has never been a disappointment.  It's actually upscale bar food that is higher quality than most sports establishments.  The prices are a bit higher than typical sports bars, but like I said, the food is higher quality.  I consider this place a hybrid of a sports bar and a hip bar that people go to who don't go to watch sports.  My criticism would be that service can be inconsistent.  The vibe can also be weird sometimes because Clayton is a place that isn't typically busy on the weekends.  My wife and I went here a few months ago at 8PM on a Saturday night and it was just us and the staff.  However, we went here last Saturday night and the place was pretty crowded.  I would definitely recommend this for anyone looking to go watch a sporting event at a place that is classier version than most sports bars even though you can't always count on a lively atmosphere.

When we were seated, I got my butt and pants dirty because they didn't clean up crab legs off of seat benches. It was especially hard for me because I don't do seafood. My butt was all wet from crab. The server was nice tho, he tried really hard to make our experience better. 

Food was good, I was impressed with their cotton candy. Some meat (mexican section) was soo dry I couldn't eat it. Their sweets didn't  impress me either. Everything looks good but tastes different.

I was interested in going to Iceland for my birthday in October 2017.
Gate 1 Travel came recommended to me. In May 2017 I contacted Gate 1 and booked my trip for me and my husband including daily excursions.
One and half week before our trip, my healthy, never smoked or drinks husband suffered 3 consecutive strokes and was hospitalized.
I contacted Gate 1 Travel and communicated the unfortunate 
Circumstance. I suggested they credit my account for I hoped to reschedule after medical clearance was given to my husband.
Very coldly the reps answer was that I didn't purchase "their" insurance therefore I will not receive refund nor credit. My choices were to take trip (which we couldn't) or lose the money - Her words!
I am extremely disappointed in the fashion this company treats people and conducts business. No understanding, kindness or consideration was shown. I hope to visit Iceland, but will never use Gate 1 Travel. I give zero stars actually.

I had Scott make my website. He is a scientist when it comes to being number one on the search engines. Without that, you can offer free money, and if people can't find you, it doesn't even matter. He came up with a great campaign for me, and it has been uphill from there.

Not sure what is up with the bad reviews, but I notice that most of the people are commenting on something other than the sushi.  I had the Chirashi bowl for lunch and the quality of the fish was fantastic.  The MISO soup was excellent.  The only thing that was sub par was the iced green tea.  Compared to Sushi Teri, which I tried the day before, this place was heaven.

When we came here to eat dinner, they had been open for 2 weeks. They have nice decor and atmosphere. It's a seat-yourself kind of restaurant. We were greeted and given the menu which was pretty simple, and we liked that. There were 2 tables filled when we arrived at 7:30. It took them forever to make our food and they were not busy! One of my girls ordered the sliders off of the kid's menu. The slider has mini biscuits instead of a burger bun and it comes with fries. My other daughter ordered, in my opinion, a very expensive meal. This is because it's the same size as the kids, but costs more. They also forgot to give her the side that comes with the meal. We ordered Fried Green Tomatoes for appetizers and they were decent. All of their food was a OK.

So sad that this place changed to try to fit in with the status quo...the old rose chandelier was its trademark look and what made this restaurant so unique and beautiful. My favorite breakfast spot still for food but the change in look really killed the environment for me and took away all of its beautiful New Orleans flair..portion sizes have shrunk to nothing and the only thing I come back for are the calla cakes at this point! What happened to the old plantation breakfast?

New treadmill upgrades!    

I'm not sure how to feel about the replacement treadmills, I kinda liked the trivial features of the one's they had.  They are going from a Star Trac to a fleet of Woodway's.  While I've read Woodway's are better for runners, hence their "for the long run" motto.  I'm not a fan just yet.  

I dislike that there is not a built-in fan on the dashboard.  The air circulation in the gym isn't the best at keeping me cool when I'm running.  Now it's even more apparent on the Woodway machines because they don't have this fan feature I enjoy so much.  After a sweaty workout, cleaning and wiping down the machines is more difficult because the dashboard is not a flat surface, it's full of nooks and crannies.  It's also difficult to hold on to if you're walking briskly on an incline.  Then there are the treads, which feel weird to me.  The treads are slit rather than a seamless track so it's more like trail running with a little more of a bumpy ride than a smooth surface.  I just don't know if I can deal with this change.  More of a rant than a review.   

Other than that the rec center has been great.  Only a few more weeks, guess I'll just have to sweat it out until then.

Food is overpriced for mediocre at best food. Sad excuse for Italian food. The only reason I even bother coming back here is because even with two stars it's better then half the food in Deptford. This town needs to clean house and get real food. Honestly an Olive Garden is just as appetizing as this at half the price. Seriously. Just go to an Olive Garden. You won't tell the difference. 50 dollars for two apps (soggy mushrooms) a beer and a cocktail. Gtfo

Love the vibes of this restaurant. Definitely brings some west coast feelings to Indy. The patio is one of my favorites on Mass Ave. The bar is beautiful and great to sit at for a chill night out. The food is sometimes hard because the menu is small and seasonal. Not the best place to take your picky friends. But usually they have a great burger on the menu.

The food here is AWESOME! So good and you get so much of it. Our server was informative and attentive. 

Restaurant is attached to a bar and that area is more casual and then there is a more formal seating area in the back. Not sure why that bothers some people but you can't please everyone. 

We'll definitely be back..there are so many choices on the menu that I still want to check out!
I grew up eating middle eastern food, so this place was a huge comfort. Super friendly, family run, delicious food.. What more could you ask for?
Came in today had the grilled chicken tacos don't like them too spicy. Had chilli instead bland it tastes like Campbell's tomato soup with chilli powder in it yuck  I heard it was a yumy place

A place for the convention-goers to get what they will leave believing is authentic home-style Creole.  I suppose it really IS authentic, but Colonel Sanders' recipe may also have sufficed in Kentucky six decades ago.  Lines of course are ridiculous at any point in the day (though only at lunchtime peaks is it usually out the door and wrapped around the building's perimeter).  Service is predictably sassy.

The red beans and rice here are fine, but absolutely no surprises, and shrimp creole and etoufee are a watery mess.  Cafe Maspero in the Quarter has an awfully similar menu and is equally touristy, but you'd have a lot more reliable meal at a better value.  And many locals will defend Maspero, which I don't hear them doing these days about Mother's.  Don't let the signed photographs of luminaries over the past eight decades fool you; this place is resting on its laurels.  It remains completely vegetarian unfriendly: the collard greens ) are cooked in a cork base, which is as they should be, but if you are looking for meatless eating at Mother's, you're probably taking a risk even with the cornbread.

Despite the fact that they have no culinary similarities whatsoever, you actually can get a more interesting meal at Mother Clucker's down the road.  Give it a try and leave this place for the guileless convention-goers.
Great service, and frames you won't find anywhere else. A little pricey, but good and unique stuff usually costs more for most things.

I love the food here.  It's truly Authentic Mexican food.  I was raised in California and this place reminds me of the great Mexican food there.  The staff is really friendly.  The servings are massive and inexpensive.  The neighborhood isn't great but still worth the food.

Stopped here to grab a quick lunch after church today. (We had coupons!) First time at this location.

Nothing fancy or special about this Arby's -- pretty much what you would expect.  Average service (not much personality, honestly), decent food served hot.  The menu has expanded quite a bit since the last time I visited an Arby's many years ago! 

The place was clean enough, but needs a major facelift. Peeling paint, cracking booths, generally "tired" appearance.

The bathrooms were fine, and can accommodate two people at a time, which is nice.

Plenty of parking. The place was pretty dead for the hour that we were there.

No major issues or complaints.

Adele's is that girl who is accomplished, talented, attractive, and independent, yet can't get a date. There's nothing there to fuel ardor and passion. 

The ambience is great, the food is great, the service is reasonably good and yet Adele's underwhelms. I think that the establishment hasn't differentiated itself enough from the crowd ..... yet. I hope that in time Adele's finds its niche and blossoms.

The food is geared towards milder palates. No big flavor explosions in your mouth here! I recommend the chicken, JW potatoes, gnocchi, and carbonara. I've heard people rave about the burger but cant say much about it until I actually try it for myself. 

The space itself is one of the nicest in the Gulch. I believe it used to be an auto repair shop but thankfully there is no kitschy garage theme. It's bright, airy, simple and elegant.
This place consistently has good flavors and super fresh toppings. They recently got PB too - so good!

I am rating this place a 5 star for the food alone. Everything we ate here was cooked perfectly and the dishes are extremely authentic to what you would find in England. Something that is very hard to find here in the US.

For starters we tried the sausage roll and welsh rarebit. On the entrees I had the large fish and chips with cod. They also have a haddock option. Easily the best fish and chips I've had in the US. My fiance had the curry chicken salad which she also loved.

If I were to take off stars it would be for two things. The first was the slow service. It wasn't horrible, but it could have been better considering the place only had 4-5 tables with customers.

The other complaint was the lack of low tabletop seating. The majority of their seating is high tables, sans 3-4 tables off on the corner. Everyone that came in after us also wanted low top seating so I know we aren't the only ones. As a disabled person I cannot sit in a booth or high top table. Something worth considering if the place is busy.

I've eaten here lots of times. I ate here when it was Celini's. I ate here when it was Mario's. And I've eaten here now that it's Noi. And I'm really not sure why i keep going back.

Well, yes I am. The pizza. That's this place's biggest redeeming quality and it's not because the pizza is especially good (it's good but not great, and they always seem to be out of one or two of the toppings on the menu) but because everything else is usually fairly disappointing.

I wouldn't say anything here is every especially bad, either. It's just meh. The entire experience is almost always best described as 'meh,' which is exactly how yelp describes the 2 star rating. It's your basic pasta dishes, nothing special, not even really better than you can make at home if you know what you're doing. 

Maybe this place isn't so bad, and I've just been overexposed, but if you're looking for italian I wouldn't suggest it. Then again I wouldn't try to hard to talk you out of it either. It's just 'meh.'

I ordered from Interbay Meat Market only because my granddaughter loves their Cubans
Everytime we order from them there is a problem. The lasted is we received a bottled drink that was already opened. Never again.

I ordered the catfish sandwich. The catfish filet was hot and a big portion. The coleslaw was fresh and crispy. The mayo was good. The bun was delicious. Only complaint was I wish the sandwich brought more heat! The sides were equally as yummy. Overall a solid 4-4.5 establishment.

TPD makes securing a rental extremely complicated, nearly impossible.

I was searching for a rental in the Boise/Meridian area for my retired father who isn't very tech savvy. We came across a rental he really liked on Zillow, so I requested a tour. An automated system from TPD got in contact with me to help schedule the tour. First, my dad had to qualify to even view the rental. The system took about 24 hours to approve him, then I scheduled the viewing for the following day. 

He knew that he really liked the property, so he applied for it online, paid the $40 application fee plus the $20 fee for his cat, using this weird pet-approval website where we had to upload medical history for my dad's 12-year-old cat. It's ridiculous! Is the pet paying the rent?! 

The next day, were able to view the property using their lockbox system, which is rather complicated. My dad would not have been able to figure it out. You have to call a number that tells you to enter the serial code on the lockbox, then it gives you a code to enter into the lockbox for it to open. 

When we finally got in the house, my dad fell in love with it. I remember him saying, "I'll take it!" So, I immediately called TPD to tell them that he would like to make the deposit as soon as possible. Nobody answered. Left a voicemail. Then this automated system kept emailing me telling me to apply for the home. We already did that! That's the problem with using this automated system! It's redundant and impersonal.

Since TPD doesn't do business on the weekends, I called them shortly after they opened on Monday at 10 a.m. No answer. Left a voicemail saying my dad was ready to pay a deposit. Heard nothing.

It was three days from our initial inquiry when we finally heard back from them, only for them to ask for 90 days worth of bank statements, as if proof of my dad's substantial investments were not enough to pay the $1,275 per month to rent the home. They want to know exactly what you spent money on in the past three months, despite how much money you have in your bank account. Very intrusive and unnecessary. Shouldn't a credit report be enough to approve someone?  

By the time they contacted him to request that info, we had already had a fantastic experience with another landlord who quickly approved my dad's application and allowed him to submit a deposit. 

TPD, you should simplify your process. It's discriminatory to have such a complicated process that not everyone can figure out. Extend your business hours, not everyone can do business during the timeframes you're open. Step up your game.

Easy location to get to. Room was really comfortable and clean (great shower) with a great breakfast and attentive staff. The saline/ salt water pool is on the small side and 3' deep but was nice to relax in. There isn't a whirlpool or hot tub. Large fluffy towels are provided. The workout room was large with a variety of machines and exercise balls. Parking is iffy. Large trucks and people taking up 2 spaces can make it precarious.
$12.00 for a sandwich is one thing, but it needs to be the sandwich you at least ordered!  Poor at best.....will never go back or recommend

Pretty overpriced. The waiter didn't tell us there was a difference when we said "strawberry marg" that there was a $40 on the rocks and a $28 frozen. Nice surprise on the bill.  The tacos are tiny, 4-inch shells. The kitchen takes absolutely forever, although we were a party of 8.  When we actually got our food, it wasn't anything to get excited about.

I was a little confused when checking out the menu online before we went. Is this like tapas? Do you HAVE to cook your food, or are there other options? Do we each get our own pot or is there a giant one we all share? I feel like the experience could be explained a little better on the website or menu itself. 
      This is a solid three star restaurant. It is in fact a chain, so expect chain quality food. The service is great, we really enjoyed our waiter. He was conversational and playful, and he checked on us often but it wasn't obnoxious. He was a tad odd about trying to up sell us on everything. When we asked for waters to drink, he brought glasses of water to us and then informed us that the waters "were on the house". It was odd.
         Two costumers share a pot, 4 share two pots, and 6 or more share 3 pots. The only downside to this is that you have to agree with other people on all the food options (e.i. One cheese fondu per pot, one cooking oil per pot, etc).
          I loved my Apple Bacon Spinach Salad and as other reviewers have mentioned, the dessert course really is something special. I didn't care much for the entree' portion, I'm paranoid about undercooking meats, all my goodies kept falling off of my skewer into to hot oil, and the taste was just kind of mediocre. I will say, it's definitely worth it to upgrade the oil. It's a few dollars, but the extra flavor is nice. 
         While some people might complain that the 4-course option isn't enough food for the price, I definitely beg to differ. I was plenty full when I left. The portions are fair and they offer complimentary refills on all of the little extras like bread and veggies. 
       Overall, this is kind of a novelty restaurant. It was a cool one-time experience but nothing stood out enough to tempt me to come back as a regular.

You always wonder if you hire a plumber, roofer or HVAC technician if you will find someone who is honest or a conman.  Mike from Samson and Sons is as honest as they come.  Came in and diagnosed the issue I had with my HVAC system and charged a fair and reasonable rate.  I highly reccomend Samson and Sons.

I have been going to Perkins for years for breakfast lunch and dinner
We went today for a club sandwich that I have had may times before
The menu said new ultimate club there were no pictures of the 
Sandwich so when you think of a club sandwich you think of three
Slices of bread with turkey bacon lettuce and tomato what came out
Was 2 slices of bread some turkey a small piece of tomato  one slice
Of lettuce and one slice of bacon talked to manager who blamed it on 
Corporate saying it was their fault.  First I think it is false advertising
Everyone knows what a club sandwich is supposed to be if they are going to change the sandwich they at least should have a picture or someway of letting you know what you are getting. The manager handled this terribly and was indifferent to our concerns. After going here for years they just lost me as a customer but I don't think they really care.

I went here recently - The salsa was A-OK - I have had better.  They forgot about us at the table and forgot our food... luckily it was nice outside and they helped with our bill.  They ended up putting our tacos in a doggy bag because we were there for so long waiting.

The only reason I give them 3 stars is because they accidentally gave me a fish taco...BUT I LOVED IT!

Fork has taken a prime location and one of Boise's most interesting spaces, and turned it into an absolute gem of a dining experience. Elevated, unique and accessible, everything about Fork screams "we want you to eat here, and we really want you to love it!" And guess what, most everyone does both!  Also worth noting is the exceptional service at Fork. The servers are efficient, friendly, exceedingly competent and you can tell they like being there and working with one another. This creates a harmonious environment, and I always make sure to take visitors to Fork. The BAM Sandwich (not totally sure that's the correct name) is delicious, and there's a multitude of sharables I recommend. You should Definitely go to Fork. You should Definitely bring friends. And you'll most Definitely enjoy yourself from start to finish!

If you want friendly counter staff this is NOT the place to order from.  They certainly can brush up on their customer service skills.   Also, I suggest NOT getting delivery -- usually about an hour or longer to get your food and it is usually cold or luke warm if lucky.   If I had to choose a pizza place in F'TWP I am going with Carollo's (SP)!!   Delicious, always courteous and you don't have to wait forever.  The family at Carollo's is always there overseeing every detail.  Thats how a real pizzeria should be!

the girls here are very nice and do a very good job.  Its the first nail salon I have stayed with for over a year.  I do not wear fake nails, I usually get a regular manicure and a regular pedicure and I am very satisfied.  I have had the gel polish a few times and have had very good results with it.  Gel polish lasted more than 2 weeks with no lifiting.  I cant comments above with lifting.  And yes ... they are very friendly to the regulars, thats why they keep coming back
We split the pork and beef sandwich and was disappointed that there was no peppers.  They were patient with us tourist as we didnt know what and how to order.
Wow the food is delicious!  Don't judge this book by  its cover.  This is a great little family restaurant. Full bar. Eager servers. The Fajitas are awesome!

Marriott typically does a good job. Another example of a bad training of a hotel manager. 

1. Empty the trash.  This should be a no brainer.  Basic 101 for room cleaning. Because I was looking, under the beds could use a vacuum more than once a year too. 

2. The must have paid very little for the room remodel.  If you can't see, that's a poor job of taping off or prepping for paint. The speckles are a result of no drop cloth or improper cleanup.  

3. We paid $55 for valet parking. During check in attendant told us we were better off parking in a local lot...  I expected some help on unloading and loading our bags.  No way.  The last photo is the valet looking off into space.  

Marriott district manager.  You need to see your properties more often.
Stopped here before our Swamp Tour. Picked up a bucket of beer (six Coronas) for $15 (!?!!) to take on the ride. Tavern was really smokey, but the people were great! Minus two star for smokiness.

This place is awesome, everyone here is great. Mike in service is excellent, we brought our Ranger Rover in for service twice and he made the whole process simple and easy. Quality people make a business.

This market can be a bit pricey but that's common for organic foods.
Positives: Everything is fresh, Sushi on Wednesdays is fabulous, comparable prices on produce, great oven ready options for quick meals at home.
Negatives: Some things are ridiculously pricey when trying to feed a family of four (ie most of the dried foods/nuts etc), and even the things that are reasonably priced for organic foods are pricey ( so don't go in looking to save a buck), not very many locations so access is limited. 
As a newbie to eating healthier my trips there can get overwhelming, but the staff is SUPER helpful so it gets a solid 3 stars. And I mean it about the sushi! Yum!

I'm extremely particular about my haircut and in town for business.  I saw a Marine in the French Quarter asking for Toys For Tots donations and knowing they get their haircuts every week he recommended this place.  I'm glad I asked him they did such a good job with my haircut I was extremely pleased.  Super nice and attentive.  Definitely recommend also downtown New Orleans is 40$ a haircut here it was just 20$ and way better quality in cut!

I'm excited as the next local that the Theater is renovated and open again, but there are a few things that are a little less desirable, such as it's inaccessibility and signage.

I came to the Mahalia Jackson for the first time a few weeks ago two nights in a row.  First night, we drove in and parked in the the paid lot.  Second night, we walked in from Rampart.  It would have been more helpful if there were signs stating where the main entrance was for people on foot or bike.  There is no bike rack!!  

Otherwise a lovely evening.  The fountains are beautiful to watch during breaks and intermission.

I haven't been to the Ale House in years...many years...Back then...it was a dark, dirty, dungeon...Since then...its been painted, the lights have been turned on, and it seems like a much friendlier place.  The bar is mostly regulars who are very friendly with the staff...A lot of people know a lot of people's names in there...I stopped in because I knew what I wanted...a Hot Roast Beef Sandwich!  I went back for that same reason...the Hot Roast Beef Sandwich...and I'll go back again for the same reason...The Hot Roast Beef Sandwich...I've had 2 in one week now, and will try to hold out a few more days before I get my third...I'm dangerously close to  having someone in there know my name...and have a feeling that they will after a few more sandwiches!
