I ordered mine wet with american cheese...wet means they quickly dunk the sandwich in the Au Jus...the beef was cut VERY thin and layered nicely on a Kaiser roll with some american cheese with a little cup of Au Jus on the side...a bit of horseradish and I was set to dig in...I did not specify rare or medium or well done, but I have heard others order it that way...I got a wonderful combination of rare and medium sliced beef layered on the sandwich with tender loving care...I haven't come across any gristle in a sandwich yet and left absolutely NOTHING on my plate, aside from a bit of Au Jus...the lack of gristle is a huge plus...You won't have to fight this sandwich to eat it, it will go down nice and smooth!

They also have hand carved turkey breast, but as I said...the beef was the reason I was there, and the reason I'll be going back!  They have added more beers on tap and have a better selection of bottled beer as well.  

The service was solid both times I was there...friendly wait staff, and not just to the regulars!  I'm a bit mystified at the 1 star reviews(friends of the old owners?), but I have only had the roast beef sandwich so can't speak on much else, but they get 4 stars in my book!

Just tried their new in-store coffee bar. It used to be run by Jeannine's but is now another company. Not memorable. At least they still carry Peet's Coffee. Don't bother with the pastry. Greasy and over-baked. The grocery store is quality food, and some specialty items can be found there and no place else.
Pretty good Thai food! I'm impressed! Ordered pad Thai & soup. So many mediocre restaurants out there tbh. Great vibe restaurant also love the location!
I love this shop! You can browse for days and they wrap your gifts so nicely every time!  A must to check out for a variety of gifts.

Service... Poor service on a slow day. I waited 10 minutes for a smoothie. 

Taste.. . Actual smoothie is delicious. It was the one with mango and papaya. Yum! 

Overall... 3 stars

This place was on the fly. No yelp use to find this place 

Now my rating is based on the whole experience not just the food. 

With that said the food was easily 4 possibly 4+ stars. I was very impressed by my lunch portion 

Coming in on a Wednesday at lunch time the place was empty. No biggie. Sat quickly and served within a reasonable amount of time. 

I ordered the crab meat stuffed shrimp. Over an order of ratatouille. I must say... this dish was rather impressive. If you're looking for something worth trying at this establishment this is IT.  

However the service was odd. I was greeted sat down as per normal. The server came asked for my drink selection. I inquired about sweet or iced tea options. He responds that we only serve drinks with alcohol. I assume he's half joking so I just have a water and order my lunch. 

While waiting for my check I overhear the table next to me order and iced tea and receive one. So... I'm not sure if maybe I was misheard/misunderstood or that server was unaware of what drink options were available. All in all that was a weird experience.

Great rice dishes and Taiwanese favorites! The braised pork belly with tofu and the fried pork chops are some of the best quick meals around. Decent beef noodle soup as well, especially on a cold winter night. A chinatown staple that you should check out!

Enjoy!

Not half bad for fast Mexican! Had the green chicken enchilada plate and was surprised at the amount off chicken. Beans were very tasty but rice was very bland. All you can eat chips and a fairly large salsa bar. Left very satisfied.

Incredible staff, working late hours (and I mean late hours, we once spoke at midnight) to make sure their clients get the 5 stars service.
Great experience working with Donna and everyone else. They made sure to cover all the fine print to a successful transaction. Highly recommend.

Happy hour is great! 1/2 off alcoholic beverages and pizza. Mussels are amazing, pizza is phenomenal (eggplant was just delightful). But all that aside our server, Theda, was the best. Attentive, sweet, great recommendations on what to eat and awesome service! She has a winning personality and is the best server we've had in New Orleans thus far.

I'm in love with this place.

A friend took me here for lunch and now I'm hooked.

Gyro sandwich all the way, just as it comes.  Sometimes a squirt of hot sauce, sometimes not.  It's gyro perfection.  

The sandwiches are huge as are the fry portions.  Aside from the fried zucchini, I haven't strayed from the namesake.  The fried zucchini was okay, but I'm not sure I'd get them again.  Mostly because the portion sizes are just overwhelming.


I'm a fan.  A huge fan.

I really don't see what all the hype is about Geno's or Pat's.  I've had better cheesesteaks at hole in the wall pizza joints around Philly.  Maybe it's South Philly preference but I lived in the Philly area for 6 years and everytime I had Geno's I was not impressed.  Go to Gooey Looie's instead - gooey and delicious!!
Consumed a bug in my salad this afternoon. Called to try and get this resolved. They let me know that the spinach comes prepackaged and it happens. Sorry. Hoping to get my money back.

The four star rating is definitely a quantity over quality rating.  The sushi is alright and I enjoyed everything we ordered off the all you can eat menu, but it wasn't the best sushi I've ever had (compared to sea side in BC or sushi in Asia). 

The best thing about them is their all you can eat. For $30, you get to order as much as you want over a 2 hour period. It's amazing and we spent the entire two hours eating until our stomachs hurt. Definitely hit the sushi craving.  There is a lot on the all you can menu in addition to sushi or sashimi as well. 

If you love eating and sushi and aren't a huge sushi snob, you should really come take advantage if saporro's all you can eat option!

This was a great lunchtime surprise! I wasn't expecting the level of fresh and flavorful food from a corner chain on Oracle, but that's what I found at Pei Wei. Since moving to Tucson from the northwest, I've found that teriyaki shops are almost non-existent here. Pei Wei comes very close to this tradition with tangy Asian sauces, generous rice bowls, and vegetables so fresh I scarcely believed my tastebuds (the pea pods are CRUNCHING!!). My favorite is the vegetarian rice bowl, which includes a ginormous helping of said fresh and crunchy vegetables, plus grilled and sauced tofu better than most I've ever had. Pei Wei has managed to cultivate flavor and texture from the tofu without relying on a deep-fry batter, which is unique and worth note. 

The interior is like the most Zen cafeteria you can imagine. It's not a fine dining place, but it's definitely nice enough to have a quiet lunch hour and read with your meal without feeling like you're cramped or in an unpleasant space.

One star deducted for the complete and utter lack of sriracha sauce available. This, my friends, is a teriyaki crime.

I hate to give this restaurant a 2 because I am a fan of Sweet Tomatoes but this location is not so great. I prefer the Brandon location. All of the soups and pastas were bland. The pizzas were good but I don't really go to Sweet Tomatoes for the pizza. Also, the sundae bar was also a disappointment because they only had the vanilla ice cream option. Chocolate and swirl (vanilla and chocolate) were out of order. They didn't have many topping options either. 

In case you have never been to Sweet Tomatoes, when you arrive you grab a plate and tray and head down the salad bar. There are pre-made salads and build your own options. At the end of the salad bar is the register. We paid $19.95 for dinner for 2 thanks to a coupon Sweet Tomatoes sent to my email. I highly recommend you check out their site and sign up for emails. Great way to save a couple bucks. After you have paid, there are table and booth options to choose from. From there, you are free to help yourself to the soups, more salad, pizza, pasta and sundae bar.

I reserved my 2 bed room queen through bookings.com months ahead of time only to find out at check in that I now had a King bed and a pull out sofa.  Room was a bit outdated and dark but clean.  Lots of thing to do there however.

So as we all know the food is ok and the value is awesome! This location is close to my house and I used to stop by a lot. The head boss is a nice guy too. 

Lately there is a female manager that I have an issue with. She pretty much tears up her people in front of customers; pushes other clerks out of her way when she is asked for help by them (rudely and abrasively) And the worst, she ignores the customers when in the heat of the moment without so much as an excuse me. Recently I was in the drive thru and sat watching an ordeal with some kid on drugs inside where she was obviously shouting at him from behind the counter but was not really doing anything about him. the other customers were all obviously freaked out but nobody called the police that I could see and the shouting match seemed pleasant for the young druggy. This delayed my order about 15 minutes and this was after having to ask the her on the mic to repeat the order back to me, correct the mistakes she made and didn't get a new repeat after that. So yup I was concerned. Then the fun show while I waited and finally to the window where I sat and waited another 5 minutes while she wandered about gathering my order in the slowest fashion ever. The cooks weren't involved in the fight as all my food was cold when I received it so why the longer wait?? Finally she came to the window took my card and passed me my bags. i looked in to find out...drum roll please...the corrections did not stick at all and I decided to stick my finger into the tostada to find it lukewarm at best. She had an issue or needed my id or something for the card and reopened the window about that time and I gave her the bags back and said cancel my order please. For which she totally got upset and shoved my card back at me with a very rough "FINE". and closed the window. This being the 2nd occasion with her and the first reported to the store manager I decided it is time to avoid this branch for at least a while. It is unfortunate since it is close, the food isn't bad, and I like the staff otherwise. Somebody needs to put that woman out of her misery...I mean fire her or ship her off to a nice store in India r something.

Residence Inn is my favorite hotel chain. The hotel is always clean and well appointed. Unfortunately, this stay did not meet my expectations. What good are amenities if you cannot get a good nights rest. We had a room near the highway so the noise was intrusive. They had a double window to decrease sound, but once the lights are out, the volume is disturbing. Also, the a/c is loud and blew directly on the bed. I had to use a towel to cover the vent. In the morning I realized the directional control was broken so I put sugar packets to direct the air up. I recommend others ensure they have a room away from the highway; then it may be a comfortable stay.
what a joke.  paid $26 for crabcakes the size of m&ms.  waited half hour for the peach crumbler which was riddled with hard pieces of the pit.  would recommend this place only for a few beers.

I hate going to his McDonald's the manager is extremely rude and acts like if you ask for something it's automatically your fault. The service takes forever especially through the drive thru. They need to get better employees and management I won't be ba
Best airport I've ever been. Very easy to navigate and very good food and drink choices past the checkpoint especially granite city

Not bad and pleasantly surprising. 

First off, we've been to Grand Sierra on plenty of occasions to hit the driving range, play bowling, watch basketball tournaments when my youngest kid was playing and wifey playing the slot machines. We've never actually stayed here since we always stayed at Peppermill Hotel; and since wifey likes to play those machines, we decided to take advantage of their offer once and now it's part of our hotel rotation in Reno. 

OK, Reno ain't no Vegas, but it's always a nice weekend getaway drive with plenty of adventures, hidden gems, small towns, scenic spots along the way and the ever popular outlet mall shopping at Sparks. This is the one time I don't mind shopping since it involves going to my favorite sporting goods store. Yes, it's Scheels...but we're not here for that.

This hotel is effin' huge with plenty of convention space, 1,990 guest rooms, a movie theater, bowling alley, pool, fitness center, 10 restaurants, 3 coffee shops, arcade room, outdoor driving range, playland, slot machines and poker room. There are more kids and teenagers here compared to other hotels due to the types of entertainment available for them. So consider yourselves warned if you don't like kids and teenagers. 

They have different towers and different-style rooms, which has been upgraded and modernized. This is where I was pleasantly surprised with their rooms. Their average-sized rooms are larger than the average-sized rooms compared to the ones here in San Francisco. Each room has a wall-mounted large flat screen TV, large refrigerator, microwave, sofa love chair, couch, work desk and a large-sized restroom. The walk-in shower has a large shower head, which is always a plus in my book. And whichever room you're located or facing, there's always a great view of the surrounding mountain range around you. 

My one of two negative feedback is that even though they have non-smoking rooms and non-smoking floors, the smell of cigarette is still prevalent in the air even when you are in the non-smoking area. But this is Nevada where indoor smoking is still not banned so it's always going to be an issue no matter where you stay. My second feedback is that due to sheer size and number of rooms in this place, asking for amenities could take a considerable amount of time before you receive them. I would estimate the average wait time is around 30 - 40 minutes. Staff and service is also a hit and miss and not consistent; a definite area of improvement. 

The location of the resort is close to the airport off of U.S. Route 395. It's not downtown, which I prefer; but it's still close to everything of note and an easy 10 - 15 minute drive in any direction. Overall, it's solid and above average, but not spectacular.

Closes pretty early and no real place to eat it in besides the benches for people waiting for their orders. Hot and fresh with large portions. I particularly enjoyed the fried chicken necks which were very lightly battered. The jumbo wings really are jumbo sized and a meal by themselves. Crunchy with no spice if people are expecting Popeye's. Collard greens stewed with bacon and pretty tasty as well.

They also have po'boys and other things on the menu but going by their name I thought I would do the chicken in a box.

Mostly poor service, and 100% lousy food at an overpriced hotel. I travel frequently, and this place is like a high end  hotel on the Las Vegas Strip with the impressive grounds/facades-- but without the great meals, customer service, and proximity to anything (the only thing it's close to is the Opry and outlet mall). It costs $25+ to take a cab to Broadway/downtown. The hotel was pretty awful, with the exception of maybe two people. Beautiful gardens and pools, but if you want to use the sauna or steam room, you have to buy services at the grossly overpriced spa - and even then, only on the day of the service. Considering the cost of this place, you'd think they could afford not to be so chintzy. But you're gouged at absolutely every chace. There is no way to go to a drug store or a grocery unless you rent a car. Want an aspirin? Get ready to spend $12 at a gift shop. I wouldn't mind the sickening prices if the experience had been great, but it was not. One last thing: as if the place didn't have enough strikes against it, it's not dog-friendly. No wags for you, Gaylord Opryland.

We arrived during oyster happy hour. We ordered a few drinks, a dozen charbroiled oysters and fish tacos. We got our drinks and fish tacos pretty quickly. Our waitress never checked on us again. I had to flag her down to ask her where our oysters were. She acted like she wasn't our waitress, told us they were done and walked away. It was very bizarre. She wasn't very friendly or interested in being involved with us. We were able to flag her down and get another round of drinks and chips and salsa. The salsa was weird, and the chips were stale. 

The fish tacos, drinks and oysters were all very delicious. The service definitely could have been better. The music was also very enjoyable. Not too loud and it was a lot of classics.

(45 minutes later update) We were told the drinks were half off during happy hour, but when we got our bill they were still full price. When we asked about it, her explanation didn't make any sense, but at this point we had been here almost an hour and a half so we wanted to just get out. She said because they were well drinks they didn't qualify like their specialty drinks, although she didn't say there were certain drinks we had to order during happy hour. We will not be returning here when we come back to NOLA.

I discovered the Buffalo Exchange in Tucson, Arizona years ago, and never forgot it. A, it was the magnet for the beautiful people. B, the selection, the prices, and the kitsch factor was off the meter... it was amazing.

Years later, when it appeared in Philly (before it moved here), I was so thrilled. Since it's moved, The Buffalo Exchange has little for men. What they do have is picked-over, overpriced, and really just trashy. It's a cool concept... but instead of loading up on schwag here, I'm saving up to get back to Tucson, and bring back garbage bags full of the real dealski.

Ordered take out for my husband and I. One burger was the Smoke and Pepper and the other was Southern Charm. They are similar enough, but neither one was labeled even though they were packaged in white boxes with enough area/real estate on the outside to label which burger is which.  Red Robin, PLEASE label the burgers!!
 
When we finally realized which burger was mine and which burger was his, I took a bite of my Southern Charm burger only to have the burger slip right out and the bun disintegrate from SO much mayonnaise. Absolutely disgusting. 

The fries were soggy and they did not include any extra ranch and blue cheese sauces that my husband requested, even though we included a 20% tip for a take out order. 

Step up your game.

Have been wanting to try this spot out for a while now and so happy I finally did. They have a deal where you can order three of their classic tacos for $9.75 (as opposed to $5 for one), so I got the Gangnam Style, Banh Mi, and Crispy Shrimp. My favorite one was by far the crispy shrimp. The shrimp was so juicy and it went super well with the rest of the taco. Both the Gangnam Style and Banh Mi were a bit spicy but the Gangnam Style was one would be my second favorite, as the sauce was delicious. The Banh Mi sandwich was a bit underwhelming, but not horrible. The sauce that came with the chips was quite spicy and the chips weren't flavored, but overall I enjoyed the meal and it was very affordable and filling.

This place is really great and very welcoming...but only if you can find it. Firstly, the negatives: This place needs to advertise itself better. There's nothing huge or inviting to let you know that you've made it. Only a picture of Mona Lisa hanging from the overhang next to a closed white door. When you first pass by it...you'll assume its some sort of art gallery. An open door and a sign make a big difference. After being seated and waiting you'll notice that there's a lot of art hanging around, but its hard to see because the lights are so dim. Its hard to believe that it could be that dark in the middle of the day but...take a seat and you'll want to be next to the window to get a better view. However, the food is amazing! We ordered the Mardi Gras pasta and a medium specialty pizza with a side salad, shared it with my girlfriend, with drinks and was still less than $40. You can taste how authentic and original the food is, it tastes really great. Not greasy or thick, but tasty and delicious. To prove it, we were going to take a picture of the food when it came out, but only remembered after we ate everything. If they change what i stated above about lighting and atmosphere, they'll get much more business. Next time I'm in the area im going back. But i hope to see them with more customers. Also, Lori, our waitress was super frindly. Great place.

I'm new to Indianapolis and saw this place had decent reviews online, so I thought I'd check it out and ended up with the worst haircut of my life. I highly doubt the person who cut my hair was a licensed barber, since a more experienced worker had to show him how to use the trimmer (while reassuring me, "don't worry, [my stylist] is a real barber"). Strangely enough, said trimmer is the only tool they seem to use there. I saw about 6 customers go through and never saw a pair of scissors come out. In the end, I suppose it wouldn't have been terrible if my barber had actually used the 1 1/4" guard he said he would rather than the 3/4" guard he accidentally used.

In short, if your hair only requires a trimmer, and you don't care what length it ends up, this place may be for you. Otherwise, I'd look elsewhere.
Not bad for a late night meal after a long flight. Not the best steak I have had. WAY to much salt.

A major disappointment. Jazz was smooth, easy listening and uninteresting. Service was terrible. Server didn't know the wine list and didn't have a printed list, the wine I ordered was bland and overpriced. (I should have guessed from her inability to know what was being offered). My friends ordered beers, which were okay, but expensive.

The service just kept getting worse throughout the night. Our server came back every few minutes to see if she could refill our drinks, and was visibly annoyed that we were only getting one round. She was also very rude hovering over us after the band finished trying to get us to leave as we were finishing our drinks and splitting the bill.

And then, as we were standing outside waiting our friend to use the toilet, she told us to get out of the street so she could close up. Extremely rude and unpleasant! I much prefer Ortliebs, with the laid back service, good food, and nice music. It's a shame Zanzibar has closed and there are no good venues in center city any more.

This store is so unique and beautiful! I am in a good mood just being in the store itself. They always have a fun selection of birthday cards and cool kitchen gifts. They have a jewelry counter that I'm obsessed with--there was an amethyst necklace on a gold chain that I probably have to go back for because it's too magnificent to pass up. They'll also gift wrap your items, which is a major plus because I am outrageously challenged when it comes to wrapping neatly...go to Occasionette!

How hard is it to greet a customer when they walk in? Apparently its fairly hard for EVERYONE working here. I walked in and wasnt greeted at all...eyes came in my direction but not a peep. There were only about 4 or 5 customers and each had someone servicing them so i figured it wouldnt be too long. Fast forward 20 min later and still no acknowledgement of my presence. I left without getting what i came for and will not be trying them again. Horrible customer service disguised behind a very nice looking place.

This was an overall poor dining experience. The service was extremely slow and got orders incorrect at our table.

More importantly, the food was mediocre. I ordered the cajun fried chicken sandwich. The chicken was dry and slightly stringy. The side salad was quite skimpy on toppings though the dressing was nice balance of sweet, acidic, and savor.

I can't recommend this place..two stars.

I am so disappointed by how terrible the service was here! I wasted over 20 minutes in a mostly empty room to get a cup of mostly milk. I was excited to get the new macchiato, which I tried in NY while I was home for the holidays. 

 While the interior was nice and exceptionally large, that's the only positive I have about this location. Even the bathroom was dirty.

My experience started out very well, a friendly, helpful salesperson fulfilled my every request. However, once the haggling was done and we settled on a price, he called back several hours later and reneged on the deal, saying they had to get $500.00 more out of the vehicle. I was unhappy, but still went ahead with the purchase. I secured a loan for the vehicle and was ready to go when i was contacted the next day by my salesman. He stated that because the vehicle would be titled in a different state  and the dealership would have to wait up to a month for their money, he could not sell the vehicle to me. He graciously offered to finance the vehicle through their loan providers (why that would make a difference, i dont know) who also happened to offer a higher interest rate. This was the most baffling sale experience i have ever had and the ordeal wasted a great deal of my time. Every dealer i have asked about this is as confused as i am. I will be staying clear of clearwater toyota from now on.

Worst experience ever, this place is a complete waste of time , money and gas.
They charged 15$$ for parking.

There was no food available 3 food trucks and only one open (pizza) and very pricey.
We waited in line for the ride for more than a hour ... we asked for a refund and they said they can't give it to us, so we pretty much expend more than 300 $$ dollars for no fun and dissapointment and they also charged  extra for the ice skating.

I have been struggling to find a good salon in Nashville and this one is definitely the best! I was very hesitant because of all the nasty reviews that were posted, however, this is my new spot. When I called yesterday to make the appointment, I was very pleased with my customer service over the phone. The manager (Ms. T) helped me set up an appointment, and made note of the services that I was looking for. I had to reschedule my appointment for later that same day, and they were very accommodating. My nail tech did cut me a little on one of my fingers, but overall I LOVE my nails. Very clean environment and very nice nail techs. They eve stayed 30 minutes after closing and did not rush me! I was so pleased with the way my nails came out as well.

Someone needs to come in there and manage. Line was long because no one working had any sense of urgency. No one made eye contact. Stood in line for at least 20 minutes and it did not move. They ran out of food items but made no announcements to those in line. I happen to overhear them telling a customer. What a missed opportunity. As I said someone needs to provide some leadership and care.

After posting the first review I received this email through Yelp

Tom G.
Deltona, FL
 0 friends  3 reviews  0 photos
Subject: ONE STAR FOR A HIGH ESTIMATE 
"one star is for companies that got the job and did a  crap job.  are you that butthurt that you gave us one star because of a high quote?  you should be thankful we spent our gas and time to give you such a free quote.  karma will give you a one star for being a waste of a decent human being.  don't cry to the whole internet that we require both you and the mrs to be home.  if you don't like our policy just act like an adult and choose our competitor.  or you can look like a real sissy and tell the internet about your feelings."

Canceled my booking, 2 days in advance, because life happens, and they had marked me as checked in. This delayed my refund, as they had charged my card as soon as I booked my room. Thank God booking.com was there to help me out!!

Review from my visit 6/6/17. FWIW - Small zoo so adjust expectations accordingly (I.e. Limited number/size of habitats).

Pros: Quaint little zoo not far from downtown Nashville. Very friendly/helpful staff. Lots of shade for hot & humid TN summers. Very reasonable entrance fees. 

Cons: NO maps available for distribution (seriously, someone "forgot" to order maps!!!). No zoo app either (get with the the times or you'll become extinct). Parking fee!!?! The layout requires a lot of doubling back thru things you've already seen in order to move on to other exhibit areas.

My husband and I shared the fish and chips and hot wings. I enjoyed both but my husband said he wasn't impressed. I also had a local beer that was very tasty! When we walked in we were told we would have a 20 minute wait but we ended up grabbing seats at the bar and were served right away.

Appt or no Appt - doesn't matter. Had an Appt yesterday. Dropped vehicle and went and had lunch. When I returned car hadn't been moved!!  They apologized for the delay - short staffed. 2 tires & alignment took 3.5 hours!  Ridiculous. 

WORSE - the steering wheel is now 60-degrees off. 12 o'clock on wheel is now at 10 o'clock which is irritating but since so many buttons are located on steering wheel it's unacceptable . . . and then, just for fun, vehicle pulls hard to the right if going over 40 . . . So back to Firestone 2nd day in a row. 

Sam told me make sure I'm here by 4:00. I asked for 5:30 so I didn't have to leave work early. Told them that IF I must be here at 4:00 then I'd have to return to work - - made it by 4!!  AGAIN they didn't start working on the car until 5:20!!  

So why did I have to leave work early and arrive at 4!?  Why could I arrive at 5:30 as requested??! Could have worked another hour. 
Or when I arrived at 4:00 they could have said, sorry, won't get to it for 1.5 hours? 
So working on yet another 3 hour visit!!!??!!

This lady that cut my nephews haircut forgot to cut one side off....leaving one side shaved and the other unshaved. I didn't notice toll I got home because prior I was talking on the phone and when I glanced at it, it looked fine. I don't understand how you forget to shave a side? It wasn't even busy for that to happen and her to feel rushed. 20$ out the door and I will never be coming back to this place again. Only reason it gets two stars is because I got a decent laugh out of it.

Celebrated our 5 year anniversary here last minute. I appreciated (at 5 months pregnant) being able to have a good meal in shorts and sandals. Service was great. We were serve by a guy (last name Leche) who was very informative, funny and attentive so ask for him!

The menu was to the point. Different cuts of steak or lobster. The appetizers were insanely delicious. We tried the chicken fried bacon, which is as unhealthy as it sounds, but wow... so damn good. We both ordered the ribeye which was about the size of our faces (and we have big faces) and was served with a side. The meal came with garlic cheese bread and unlimited salad. I myself had about 2 salads and 2 orders of bread (because it was that good) and couldn't finish my dinner but was so happy with my meal. Ask for the truffle butter too, great compliment to the taste of the steak. 

I have nothing negative to say about this place. I can't wait for another reason to visit this place and will be open to new things next time since the chicken fried bacon was such a delicious surprise.
Very slow drive through service every single time. I will avoid stopping if I see any number of cars at the drive thru despite seeing a gaggle of people inside standing around every time.

So I love Target...I spend way too much time and money shopping at targets. I love the specific items Target has. But unfortunately this target has a serious problem with keeping items in stock, especially in the beauty and home decor sections. If you walk through the store a lot of the shelves are empty. The selection in the grocery is good, but it seems as though that has come with the cost of less selection everywhere else. What is especially frustrating is that the app will say this location has items in stock but then once I get to the store the item is no where to be found.

Staff was super friendly, we had some time to talk due to we arrived just after Fat Tuesday. We liked the History of the place and the neat courtyard. I got hiccups and they straightened that out with bitters and syrup so I could eat my Delicious Crayfish Stuffed Catfish. The Fried Green Tomatos were not spicy enough for me and the Gator Bits a big overfried. I would go back again and just specify spicy and light fry. I plan to return its a homey pub like feel, not pretentious...

Stopped in here to try something new. Ordered the rib platter and a couple tacos. The ribs were over done and dry with very little meat on them. The carnitas tacos were extremely bland. The cornbread was ok, the coleslaw was just meh. I was hoping the sweet corn would have been actual sweet corn. It was just corn with a lot of butter. We had ordered a coke and a margarita, the margarita never showed up and it took us asking about the coke once more to get that when the meal finally showed up. The place is kinda cute, our table was sticky and the chairs were dirty. I really was hoping it would have been better, but I was thoroughly unimpressed.

Walk me in here blindfolded and when you yank it off this could be ANY BAR USA.  Wait a minute; I see A Villanova banner on the wall.  I must be near Philly.  Wait a minute again; last year I was in Alaska and saw a Villanova banner so who the hell knows now days.

The bar/restaurant design folks have their act together and they know a lot of folks like this style so they give it to us.

* You got your slightly dim lighting.
* Plenty of wood just slightly beat up.
* A very long bar in this case topped with copper.
* Zillions of TVs.
* Sports and college memorabilia everywhere.
* A line of cozy booths.
* Lots of taps and lots of bottles.  Over all a very decent selection of brews.
*  A pub grub menu that won't break the bank.

Like I said, it's ANY BAR USA.  So is there anything that makes this place different?  Yes.

* Unbelievably great wings. Six will set you back $6.99 and twelve for $11.99.  Go for the dozen and try the Peanut Thai variety.  Awesome!

* Head downstairs to the Bottle Shop and peruse over 200 varieties of suds.  If you like, mix and match your own six pack.

Special note- Some folks either visit here or avoid visiting here because they think the name and logo denotes just a seafood restaurant.  Yes there is seafood here but the menu has many choices for carnivores and other folks.

I can see where a beer aficionado might go four stars here but I'm going for a solid three.

I think in this case, you get what you pay for.  My experience was average at best.  The guy that was there working (I'm guessing one of the owners) spent more time chatting (or flirting) with 2 younger girls that were in seats a few down from me, more worried about their plans for the evening and when cocktail time was, rather than helping me have a relaxing experience.  

I was less than impressed...with nail salons being a dime a dozen around Indy, I'll be going somewhere else from now on.

Where do I begin? I'm not upset with the gaming portion, as I won $100, but the entire place itself sucks. Hotel and casino was dirty, the public bathrooms were gross, as if they hadn't been cleaned all day. The $40 "surf and turf" buffet was horrible. Lobster was lukewarm at best and the butter sauce given in little plastic cups was cold and congealed. The prime rib tasted like an old man's dirty shoe and if you wanted a properly prepared steak it was another $7 on top of the $40. The mashed potatoes were basically soup and everything else was at best, sub-par. Then, to top off a mediocre evening, the ticket redemption machines all went down and we had to wait in a line for nearly two hours. And the only thing they were willing to comp was our parking, which was already free. This place was so bad, I set up this Yelp account just to warn everyone I could. If you want a better buffet, try Golden Corral. I guarantee its better. If you feel the need to gamble, buy a lottery ticket and save yourself the headache of going to The Hard Rock Casino in Tampa.

I would like to write about this place, but I wasn't able to get in...even though I showed up 10 minutes after the opening time. Fiveteen minutes after the opening time, someone came sauntering along whom I suppose was the proprietor. He passed us without any acknowledgment or apology as he walked to the rear of the building. I left deciding if they cannot open on time for business,  they must not be too concerned about business.

Don't live here. 
    It's very loud between the walls.
    The prices are exorbitantly high, and they tell you they include utilities, then send $30-100 electricity overages. They don't send you a bill for those overages until 3 months later, which is a phenomenon experienced by most everyone. 
    They tell you they have a movie theater room and pool table and swimming pool then close all of them as early as 8pm. 
    The apartments are small, and storage/counter space is nonexistent. 
    They have quarterly inspections, that they give you notice that they are coming by anytime in a certain week, then will unlock your door and walk in without direct notice, almost walking in on my friend naked. 
    They have put stickers on my packages that are adds for The Flats itself. 
    The water pressure is bad, and the toilets don't always flush correctly and build up red copper residue. 
    The bed they give you in their "furnished" apartment is a hard cot, and if you elect to replace it with your own bed, you have to store it yourself. 
    The wifi is free, however the routers are so spaced out that some rooms get no wifi at all, and their proxy walls prevent the use of your own router (without hassle)
    They give you a good feel by showing you the lobby and the gym area of the 2nd floor but the hallways are dark, creepy. 
    They are not pet friendly
    They are however close to campus and have no bugs.
Go here for happy hour...beer for $4. Can't beat that for how much you get. I say stick to the the pretzels for food choice and you are good to go.

Well, I was pretty hot under the collar when I posted about being denied home pickup because I called one minute after the deadline.   And I stand by those remarks.

However, the shuttle driver couldn't have been nicer or more professional.   The bus was clean, felt safe, and had working seatbelts.   And he went above and beyond in helping me with my luggage and chasing after me with a forgotten item.   He was, however, a substitute driver, not a regular ...

Been going here a long time, will not be back. They have been honoring a coupon I use for a long time and all of a sudden can't use it. When I filling out the credit card slip the waitress started in to tell me her opinion on the situation and I said please get away from me and she called the manager over to say I was rude, they haven't seen me be rude yet. The manager came over and told her I was not rude just told her to get away from me. They are lucky I walked away. They were about two words from me showing them what rude was all about. Too many places for me to spend my money and it won't be here

Overpriced.... but remember that it does give student discounts. I'm usually at the Westshore AMC but the occasional IMAX movie / off time gets me here. Remember to validate your chip coin and it's on 50 cents for 4 hours.

I've seen some reviews complain about customer service and price. I just want to say I came in here one morning and was greeted by three different women, all very nice and welcoming. I actually had to get the OK from my girlfriend to spend $50 on a stuffed animal and she didn't answer the phone so I said I'd be back later. I mean if they're so terrible wouldn't that be a prime opportunity to scoff at me for leaving after standing next to this damn rainbow bird for 5 minutes on my phone like a weirdo? No they were nice and said goodbye and we're all smiles. Then guess what I came back and bought that bird. Yeah $50 is a lot but this is rittenhouse square and they have some really unique stuff. They were very nice and bagged it up with a bow, gift receipt and blacked out the price. We shared a laugh and they all wished me a great day. Couldn't ask for more!

Las pupusas que pedi estaban crudas y me dio mal estar en el estómago. Estaban muy malas.

El servicio Pacimo las muchachas no atienenden rápido y la comida es muy tardado. Ya están frías las pupusas cuando me las llevaron ami acompañante le llevaron unos tacos hondureños con la tortilla quemada y un olor horrible en el plato.
Everything was just lovely....fresh produce, great presentation and shout out to the salsas

If you're down in the CBD guick healthier spot. Lots of cheeses and the wine looked great too. Didn't love the plain bread. I normally get "hooks cheddar" but I didn't this time. Try it if you want a break from the normal heavy nola foods.

When I was there in Nov, 2009, there was a sign saying No Gelato. The case was completely empty. When the business closed in December, he listed equipment failures (and the high cost of replacements) as one of the reasons. It closed without fanfare in mid-December, 2009.

I have been going there for live music since the 80s; it has sunk to new lows-Smells like piss, sound is poor, nowhere to sit, beverages are expensive- $4 for an 8oz bottle of water wtf? Jannus Live is dead to me.

This is one of those order-at-the-counter-and-find-your-own-table kind of places. A little annoying, but I guess you save on tip? The line to order was a bit long, but when we did order, the woman was totally helpful and didn't rush us or get annoyed by our questions. I ended up getting the portobella mushroom sandwich and a side salad, and my friend got the veggie burger and a side salad. I also ordered a carrot shake sans whipped cream, which turned out to be sweet and delicious. The mushroom sandwich was flavorful enough given the ultra healthy ingredients, and the side salad with lettuce, carrots, cheese, rice, and guac, was a nice departure from the norm, and the chips and salsa each sandwich came with were totally serviceable. Tasty, healthy food at a good price.

Our Airbnb host recommended  this place since its really close to the house.  Really liked their ambience, coffee and menu options. The muffins are good and so is the coffee . We also had the avocado toast which really stood out because of the chimichurri sauce over the avocado.

Mmmmm... if you go early, the bagels are crazy fresh and delish! They are busy in the morning, for sure but I've never waited long. I buy giant orders of bagels for work 2-3 times a year and though pricey, soooo much better than grocery store versions.

The customer service is what makes this Tijuana Flats stand out. It happens from the minute you walk in, where you are greeted by someone who gives you the rundown on how their menu works, to the manager coming around near the end of your meal to make sure everything is delicious and going well at your table. 

Ordering is three steps:
1) Choose your Entree
2) Choose your Meat
3) Choose your Toppings

Everything is made to order & nothing is frozen or microwaved.

There are "specials". On Tijuana Tuesdaze: 2 Tacos, Chips & a Drink. On Throwback Thursday's: A Burrito, Chips & Drink. Both specials are 5.99. Of course, there are nachos, quesadillas, salads and more to choose from. 

For my Quesadilla I chose Blackened Chicken, Chips and a Drink for $7.79. I thought the price point was a little high for what you get. You can also add rice & beans to that for more. My chicken was of good quality, but the blackening wasn't that sharp bite of heat I expect and the quesadilla itself had too little chicken. My friend had the Crispy Baja Street Fish Tacos. The fish was not up to high standards. Basically, it wasn't fresh. The manager noticed her plate wasn't touched and was very nice about it. Not only did he offer to comp her meal, when he returned her money, he also brought her a 'gratis' warm Dessert Flautas to take home. You can't get customer service any better than that.

I would go back to try other things on the menu. Perhaps the specials are the way to go next time.


76/100

Awful customer service!
I had dropped off a purse for repair and have been waiting 3 months to have it returned to me.  I was promised a telephone call for a quote for the repair which I did not receive, it was to be done under a month and I was not contacted that it had been finished.  I have called the business several times asking for a discounted price for the service due to the poor customer service and lack of communication and have received 1 telephone call since I started the dispute 2 months ago.
I will not be returning nor recommending this business to anyone.

With all of these negative reviews I wish this company would have just been shut down already. They towed my car from a parking garage in downtown Tampa after I was parked for only 1 hour. The document said "parked in front of mailbox". There was no mailbox, this is clearly a trap and they must have someone waiting to call as soon as anyone parks there. It was $205, cash only yet they give no change?? We waited at their sketchy lot for over an hour before the man could come let us in so I could retrieve my car. Unacceptable.

I go to Hopsmith with friends often and it has always been amazing. I chose this location for a business gathering of friends and clients recently and I had a mixed opinion for the first time. 

I attempted to announce how many ppl would be attempting so the staff that I love could be prepared. Getting a hold of the event planner was very challenging.  I was eventually left a message after several attempts, informing me that Hopsmith does not take reservations for brunch. 

I arrived early in preparation for the party I had planned. The hostess did the very best she good with the policy they have in place. She could only seat us for who was there. So coming early did nothing. Our party grew as expected. I truly feel if the event planner could have at least given her a heads up it would have meant a lot to her and her colleagues.  The best part is coming..

Our bartender, Brian, was beyond wonderful. He was solo in the basement area. That is where I told the establishment many times I was looking to be. I am sure none of the staff was told this. Brian was amazing, I can't stress this enough. Our large party felt catered to. The other party of 14 and parties of 6 did too. From beginning to end, Brian made everyones event special. 

This story, is just one story. I love this location. They all work hard. I would only recommend communication being an area of growth. The food is always amazing! The drinks are consistent. Please try  them.

I went to Embers in May of 2010. It was the first place after checking into a hotel and it was close. It was around noon and it just opened. There was little open at noon. 

The server was not to great.

I ordered a ordered a cheeseburger which they would only cook medium well or well, no temperature below. It was an ok burger not bad but not great.

Service:  Service was just ok and slow. 

Embers seems to take their location on Bourbon Street for granted.
By far the best pizza place in the world. Nothing can top this restaurant and you all need to go to this restaurant, and this restaurant only. Believe me. This place Fabrica needs an award.

Sat outside on the patio area which was so nice with the lights! They have heaters out which make it comfortable at any time of the day. 

Ordered the prime rib dip (French dip) with fries, peach smash (gin, ginger beer, and mint), and prickly pear cider. The food was SO DELICIOUS. I was only planning on eating half of my sandwich, but it was too good to stop eating....

The rest of my party seemed satisfied with their food and drinks, so it was overall a great experience. 

Service was a lack-luster when we went, but it seemed like there were only 2 gals running the whole patio, so I'll forgive it this time. Hopefully they usually staff more people and this was a one-off.

I would give zero if I could. My sisters and I all had appointments and two of three of us were helped right away. I waited almost 30 minutes until someone came over to the chair. My sister had someone start on her and then without saying anything just started cleaning up and then went over to her appointment to help someone else. My sister sat there for 15 minutes without anyone saying anything. No one apologized or explained the situation. They're pretending they don't speak English to get out of it and have zero responsibility for serving their customers. RUDE

Food was fairly good, & the service was amazing. The people we're accommodating, since I'm blind Steve pointed out a light switch over the table so I could see a lot better. The waiter was very nice named Ryan. You should definitely try the onion soup, rack of ribs, & burger when you go.
10/28/2021 Restaurant is closed.  I do not know why. The plywood barrier suggests that it may be awhile.

If you're looking to shop at this mall, I suggest turning around and not walking in. Not many people go here, so there are tons of stores closed down. However, they do have putt putt golf and ice skating available, with little crowds! I have been to this mall twice and I'm not sure why it is so deserted.

The service was great!  The drinks and prices are great as well. Their chips and dips were delicious and they didn't mind giving us a refill. Devin was our server/bartender and did a wonderful job checking on us and keeping our drinks and chips flowing. We found our new place to eat and drink. We will definitely be back soon.
Love their Beef and Broccoli ,crab rangoon and potstickers. Also try their egg foo young. Not too thrilled with their fried rice.

I was a happy man when entering this diner, but when I left, I was broken, down, and empty. I give charity, I help out my friends and family when in need, and I believe in caring for others. This experience at the diner ruined me and made me darker. The head receptionist is consistently rude to my friends and I. She gives an attitude about getting separate checks. She is very rude to us and it destroys my self-esteem. She makes me feel that I don't belong. What did I do to deserve this treatment? Or should I ask what should she do to be whole again? Why are we treated with such disdain? I wish her well and hope she becomes better again. Good luck, your guide.

This is how you create Chipotle fans.  So disappointed in Qdoba today.  Ordered a chicken Protein bowl.  When I got to the part of the order where they ask if you want any other toppings, I added  jalapeños. Somehow adding jalapeños to my bowl changed it from a protein bowl to changes it from a protein bowl to something else and adds about $4.00 to the price.  When asking about the price change, she said you can't alter the menu item at all.  I'd be fine with that if it was mentioned when I added something I didn't know made any difference.

This is probably one of the worst experiences I've had at a hotel starting from shuttle driver (already late) passing me at RNO airport without stopping, to bell desk having problems sending shuttle back, to hotel manager's lack of sincerity about wasting my time. Manager offered to pay for my taxi to hotel and then did not want to cover the tip until I finally got angry. My suggestion is to try somewhere else in Reno. Their elevators are not even running right.

Have eaten here many times but they have a new menu since our last visit.  the new menu. Can't wait to come back to try other things. My hubby got sautéd spinach and says it's THE BEST he's ever had. His seafood gumbo was on point - dark roux. Yum! I had the Mahi Rueben and sweet potato fries - unique and delish. Can't wait to come back. One of our favorite spots in downtown Franklin.

I ordered some food from here thru Uber Eats the Chicken & Shrimp Steak and the Tilapia platter with asparagus an Mac n Cheese. The food was very fresh an still warm considering it came from Germantown Ave to West Philly. Now as for my food the steak was lacking substance very little in my roll for $14.00 an the shrimp were small an it didn't have my mushrooms. As for the fish platter they called an said they didn't have anymore Mac n cheese bummer  settled for the rice then called back an got fries. The fish was good, asparagus alittle overcooked for me but I loved the fries very good. Will try other selections.

Extremely disappointed, went there for the crawfish and seafood. Despite what photos show , this is not their expertise . It's Chinese food and some gumbo and jambalaya, but mostly Chinese food.food looks like it was sitting their for a long time, did not look fresh .  Based on reviews in search of seafood , was excited , took a $15 uber all to be beyond greatly disappointed .

I've been here twice. First time was fine, mostly unmemorable food but nothing bad. 

Second time was last night. Seven of us had reservations, and a few of us  were late, which they were very accommodating about. Likely because their service ended up not being particularly speedy. At all.  No worries on that though, as the ambiance IS great and we were really enjoying our conversations. 

The food was...fine. My Gnocchi with mozzarella was delicious, but lukewarm. Consensus was that my friend's meals were also lukewarm, and not as delicious. 

One girl is gluten free and ordered chicken masala with a flour less sauce. The waiter said that wasn't a problem. What she got was pathetic...two thin beaten chicken breasts without sauce or seasoning. And a few sad pieces of broccoli. Suffice to say she wasn't happy, although did not complain. 

 Unfortunately, when I was just about done eating as much gnocchi as I could handle, I found a long black hair at the bottom of the plate. EWWW. The hair was straight and my hair is extremely curly so no chance it was mine. I told a waiter, who responded with a noise sembling disgust. He did nothing, so our waiter came and said there were no employees with long hair, implying it was mine. Um, no. He asked if I wanted something else. I explained I already ate most of it and was full so, no. I expected a comp or something on the bill, but we received nothing of the sort 

Ambience - 3 stars
Service - 1 star 
Food - 2 stars

 They also have a 2 dollar corking charge and other weird rules, which other reviewers touch on.

Although good for groups, find another place.

Good service, server was nice but with super duper weak drinks. Live band played great island tunes. However even when ordering top shelf, tender must have put a thimble full of spirits in it, but charged one & done prices. Will not repeat this visit nor bring anyone here with these embarrassing kool-aid drinks.

This was my second time here. I booked this place to give it another shot. It's was an overall meh experience. Star rating is based on what was promised and priced versus delivered. 

Food was merely ok. My steak had a massive tendon along the majority of the meat part and the rest was fat. When I mentioned it to the staff it was dismissed and not  acted upon. Staff is more suited to work a prison cafeteria since they are basically a bull in a china shop.

Ambiance is good but their American only bar is honestly ridiculous and lacks the normal proper liquor and beer types and brands because they "aren't made here". Leave our alcohol out of politics please and give us what we prefer. Silly to only carry alcohol along country lines and then have a meh selection to boot. I didn't even see most of the good American options and companies in there. 

This place is pretentious and fake. Very manufactured and forced. Fairly disappointing trip yet again and is my last.

Most of the other reviews opine about the lack of flavor in the food. I'd say that's a pretty accurate assessment.

My chicken Katsu was crispy, but bland. The mac salad also seemed to lack in flavor. The sauce they served with the Katsu needed to doctored up with the soy and sambal on the table. The salad that came with it was served in a small bowl that was so perilously overfilled I didn't even attempt to eat it.

My buddy's Loco Moco suffered from the same flavor deficiency. Unseasoned eggs on top of a bland burger over plain white rice. The boring brown gravy that covered it all did it no favors. 

The wings we shared were the only decent thing we had.

It's too bad about this place because it's close to work and home and the prices are reasonable. I just can't see going back here to continue to have my tastebuds bored.

We had a family emergency so our trip wasn't planned.  The guy at the counter at the airport and his manager treated us awesomely. An otherwise traumatic trip was made the better by Enterprise, thank you.

I have only been here once despite living close by and visiting Clearwater beach often. This place is right off the highway to Clearwater and has the most amazing views!! We sat at the bar as the whole place was very busy , including the patio. The beach area has a fee to enter and is a great place to hang out with friends. Would definitely visit again.

Have been there twice.  First one was for lunch.  It was fairly crowded but we were seated right away.  I had a patty melt that was okay.  It was a bit greasy-some greasiness expected with the onions but more than I like. Service as a bit spotty.
The second time was for breakfast with a friend and I had the Eggs Zorba that was 2 scrambled eggs with spinach cheddar-jack and cream cheese. It came with toast and potatoes but they graciously gave me a bowl of fruit as a substitute.  The service this time was excellent throughout the meal.  The eggs were good.  
It's not a place I'd rush back to but I know some people that love it and go often,

It's a general fast food place. They have fresh ingredients.  
I had a burrito with 1/2 carnitas and 1/2 barbacoa. Black beans, lettuce, brown rice, Monterey jack cheese, red onion, grilled pea pod(?) and green pepper, jalapeno, on a flour tortilla. I used the bottled hot sauce for some heat.
ARE THE INGREDIENTS TASTY? 
YES
WILL I GO THERE FOR GREAT MEXICAN FOOD? 
NO.
Sorry. It's a good clean fast food restaurant........ that has kind of watered down pseudo mexican food. 
If you never had a great burrito will you be satisfied? 
Yes. 
Otherwise,  find a small family owned place who uses great-abuela recipes. 
By the way, their guacamole lists several ingredients on their website, that just weren't there. This guacamole is just whipped avocado. Save your money.
Worst place I have ever been to.  The "girls" at the front desk are rude and disrespectful.  The prices are outrageous and the services are mediocre.

The guy Jim is an arrogant jerk who debates how i want my hair done  Thus far he's berated his staff and Insulted my hometown, made anti- gay jokes and anti- semetic comments about not wanting to work with Jews. His staff looks like they are out of an 80s music video.  I won't be returning..

I love that they have chilled beer glasses.  The service is always great. Can't stand the techno music.  It's not good for eating.  Makes me feel nervous.  Are we on the set of Lost in Translation. The menu is super gimmicky.  When the hipsters start to have to watch what they eat, those beef fat fries won't seem so cool.

Love the Pacifica Suites.  A little out of the way near Goleta, but lots of room for our family of 4.  Breakfast was not bad for free, nothing great.   Cooked to order eggs, fruit, pancakes, waffles, etc.  Suite gets 5 stars, -1 for location and so-so breakfast.
New to New Orleans and glad there's a Whole Foods, but man - slowest-moving lines I've ever experienced at a grocery store.  Ever.  Really bad.

Overall, I am not fond of the layout of CVS stores. For Mr scripts they offer a better price, which is why I go to them.
I don't think their sales items are that great & overall customer service is hard to find.
Personal preferences, I think is all it boils down to. I have trouble finding things, get frustrated & feel the overall prices are higher, so I leave empty handed. Just my opinion.
What's a girl to do? When in doubt, go where you feel familiar & your $$ goes further.

I've been a member for 1-2 yrs.  This gym is longing for new management.  Why, you ask?  The greeters/front desk attendants immature and unprofessional (Managers sitting to the left, once you enter).  The front desk attendants play a lot and hold conversations when they should be more attentive to the front desk when people arrive.  I've stood there waiting for them to finish playing before they acknowledged I was standing at the desk.  Management should be aware because they sit in clear view of the front.
The gym is too dark and unwelcoming. 
There are cosmetic updating/repairs that need to be done (management should be more attentive regarding interior structures).  For instance, wall paper on the wall (behind the front desk) needs to be changed.  It's peeling (it's been in this state for 1 yr at least).  Behind the treadmills, there is constantly water leaking from behind the wall.  Not sure why it hasn't been addressed (this has been going on for at least 6 months).    
The treadmill machines breakdown often and are not fixed until someone reports the issue to the attendants.
The elliptical machines are old: keys are missing coverings, can't control volume on some machines, tv networks share multiple channels, can't customize workout settings on some machines.
This gym needs a total makeover starting at the top with management.  Then, renovating the entire gym.  
The only reason I go to this location is strictly for convenience.
The only consistency I've noticed at this location is increase of membership.
Service slow and food quality has slipped. Waited a long time to get served and the food was nothing to write home about.

I went here with a friend for brunch one morning. I would say there are definitely other places were you get better food for a better price, for sure. My food was OK, but I had ordered a coffee, which was FULL of coffee grinds. I don't mean one or two; it was literally swimming with coffee grinds. When I pointed it out to the waiter, he replied, "Well, I can get you a new one, but I have to charge you for it." WHAT? He should have brought a new one and taken it off my bill completely. It was almost $4 for just a plain coffee alone! So, that was my first (and only) time there, and I won't be going back. Hope that was worth the $4 to them.

This place is absolutely fantastic. My girl and I wanted to stop somewhere around Boise for lunch because it halfway between our looong drive from our current hometown in Washington and our old hometown in Utah. We try to avoid chains, and although we wanted something quick and cheap, there was no way we were doing fast food. 
Lo and behold we found Gyro shack and it was everything we wanted and more. I got the spicy avocado bacon gyro, milady got the standard gyro and we shared a large strawberry lemonade, all for about $11. Everything was unbelievably delicious! My gyro was packed with meat, the pita was warm and soft, the tzatziki was great and my gyro was actually spicy, as it claimed to be.  The strawberry lemonade even had real strawberries in it. 

This place is a dream come true, and we look forward to returning every time we drive through Boise. The ONLY bad thing is that it doesn't appear to be open on Sundays, which makes me a sad panda because I can't get a gyro today. :(

Since my last visit, I went back this time to try their dinner. I sat outside since last time I thought it was too dark. I did like it better, and was happy I can have my dog with me.

Our service was good. We had a really nice male server who didn't mind coming outside to check on us. He was great.

I ordered the braised short rib and eggplant rollatini to share with my hubby. They were both okay to me. Braised short rib was definitely tender, but the flavor was not as vibrant as I have had at other places. Eggplant rollatini is basically another way of saying eggplant parmagiana. With that being the case, I have had better. 

Therefore, this place is just okay to me. I did see a lot of people sitting around the bar inside drinking. Maybe they are better for wine and drinks. They do have happy hour during the week with some basic $5 specials.

This place does not wear gloves! The manager does not appreciate your business and is beyond rude instead of being grateful at a time like this. Food was delivered leaking and the delivery guy did not have gloves on. This was my LAST order placed to these ungrateful "business". Be aware people!

the service is really good here and the inside is really clean.  
I would come here late nights with some people from my church and the employees were always courteous and friendly.   
i know sometimes you can get cranky mickie d employees, but it is not the case here :)

the only annoying thing about this place is turning a left out of the restaurant -_- stupid ridge pike.
The cups were not marked so we had no way of knowing which drink was what.  I had the Mocha Tuxedo and it just tasted like watered-down coffee.

My friend and i walked into this restaurant. Wasn't greeted until someone from the kitchen area came up, seated us, and gave us the menu. When I look down the table decor there were symbolism which seems to be racist and insensitive. If you have any conscience at all, think twice about where your spending your money. My friend got up and left IMMEDIATELY after seeing that!

Went for a recall but found out the recall had already been taken care of last time I was there. I was really irritated by that because otherwise I wouldn't have made the trip (dealer is about 45 min away in traffic). Colonial sent me so many email reminders to get the recall done. Why did it take so long for Corporate to update their files?? Also wish my battery registered as needing to be replaced because it wouldn't start later that day and needed a jump to get home and also a jump to go back to the dealer. Was so frustrated because JP said on the phone the night before that they would take my car right away. I wound up being there for 2 hrs which annoyed me even more. Probably will not go back to this place, nor do I plan on buying a Hyundai for my next car. The only thing good about the 2 appts was how nice Donna was.

It was raining in the city -- not a hard rain -- a soft rain that looks like tears, if the city had a heart. But it never does. The mist just wells up over the shingles until it spills onto the sidewalk, slowly washing the dreams of the desperate into the sewer. 

It was that kind of day.

"How's tricks?" I imagine she asked, as my companion and I entered. She was worth a stare. She was trouble. She led us to a table in back, illuminated only by a low-wattage wall sconce.

I came to TM for the lunch special, a chicken wrap with a side of slaw, a wrap so large I couldn't eat it all, a wrap so big I boxed half to feed the hunger that would gnaw at my stomach later that day.  

Why didn't I order the BBQ pulled pork? Some things you do, and some you don't. My business associate liked it. If it was good enough for him, then it was good enough for me. But I didn't order it, see?

Three Monkeys was a dry place, a warm place, a place where I could forget the stinging chill and the endless gray outside. A place where I could grab a bite, share a story, and know that its dimly lit walls would forgive: would see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil.

Then I had to go. Whatever life there was for me, it was waiting out there. I wasn't much for long goodbyes. So I left, I beat it, I scrammed.

-jh, after genre "film noir": not proud, not my best work

I would love to review the food or the service but sadly cannot, as we never received either.  The restaurant was not busy at all and while we were seated immediately, we were never even addressed after that.  No drinks, no greeting, nothing.  After about 15 minutes we just got up and left, and no one seemed to notice that, either.  There were plenty of servers who walked right by us and looked at us, but never bothered to ask if we needed anything or had been helped.  I had heard such good things about this place, and I was extremely disappointed.  Not to mention hungry.  We will not be back.  Having said all of that, the food on everyone else's plate looked pretty  good.

New Orleans falls short in the cheap eats category, despite a good reputation.  Most po' boys are poor quality and cost $10.  I didn't think they were supposed to make you po'...  So I'm thrilled to find such awesome tacos for $5.   I'll be here every week!

Just to affirm my love for this truck of Filipino food factions, the RFT readers voted it as the Best Food Truck in Saint Louis!  Try the tofu adobo or the Flying Pig when available.  These Asian melting pot dishes never disappoint and are full of fresh veggies and tangy sauces.

So my wife and I've been wanting to try this place and we finally did we walk in and we should have known to turn around when the guy stood behind the counter on his phone and just told us to sit anywhere we wanted to he walked up to us with his hands in his pocket should have a second reason to leave so after ordering food and waiting 30 minutes finally got my order it was lukewarm the worst food had in a long time. 2 days later I'm still sick headed to the doctors

I ate here with my boyfriend last Saturday. We both enjoyed it. We started off with large house margaritas...he got strawberry, I got Mango. They were both delicious, and there was a good amount for the price of $9. 

We split an app-Nachos w/ pico. All of their nachos come with cheese, and you can choose other toppings as well. I wish more topping came on them standard. If you want to add sour cream, that's more, guac is $2 more etc. So our nachos were just pico & american cheese.

We both got 2 burritos for our entrees. I got chicken, and he got beef. They forgot the re-fried bean on his, but he still enjoyed it. I enjoyed mine as well.
They have a TON of selections for entrees including dinner combos which allow you to try one burrito, one taco, enchilada, etc.

One complaint (as someone mentioned below) is that they nickle and dime you for everything. most of the dishes don't come with that many-or any-toppings. 
At Mad Mex, I normally get fajitas- which come with lettuce, tomatoes, sour cream, guac, shredded cheese, rice, and salsa. Here, fajitas only come with sour cream, tomatoes, and one other topping I believe...all of the rest cost extra, which can add up quickly. If you want a refill on the complimentary chips and salsa, that costs extra too. 

The checks are hand written, and not itemized,which is a little weird. 

Our total was $56( before tip). with a 20% tip, our total came to $67. That is VERY expensive compare to other local places.

The bar area is small, but there is a large TV you can watch. The booths are comfy as well. 

Overall, I would eat here again, especially for the margaritas, and to get an appetizer (not to share one, because apparently they charge you $5 to share it). As far as entrees go, I think Mad Mex has better food, better prices (if you like having all the toppings like I do), and more beer selections.
Better yet, check out Plaza Azteca in Plymouth Meeting...it's a little bit of a drive, but the food is great, chips & salsa are unlimited, and they won't charge you for lettuce!

This museum currently (I think sadly that it is temporary) a wonderful exhibit on the history of carnival/mardi gras including the balls that are famous in New Orleans at that time.  I highly recommend it for a very good depiction of the even that makes NOLA famous.  They also have a very good exhibit about Hurricane Katrina but make sure (unlike me) to go through the exhibit in the right direction.

We were pretty excited to try this place! We ordered the lobster rolls since there was so much talk about them, and were good but just tasted super buttery. That really was the only flavor we picked up and the rest of the food was just okay. Although the portion sizes are huge, so we were completely stuffed and still got containers to go.

Went for lunch with husband yesterday (6/2/16). I ordered a cheeseburger with everything, an order of seasoned fries with cheese (as there is a sign on the menu about how they can add cheese to everything), and a soda.
I received a burger without cheese or any veggies (huh?), less than 10 cold fries with 8 shreds of cheese on them, and a cup for my soda--they have those machines to make up flavored drinks, but, alas, they only have 2 diet options--Diet Coke and Diet Dr. Pepper. 
First, the drink: I got a Diet Dr. Pepper...but it wasn't diet (I am a diabetic; I tested it). I didn't bother complaining, since by the time I tested it, I'd already unsuccessfully complained twice.
Burger: not sure why I didn't get the toppings I ordered, nor the cheese I paid for. It was dry and unappetizing to be honest.
Fries: huh? I asked about why they were cold, and why the cheese wasn't melted, and was told by a not so nice staff member that there is no microwave. Okay, but I still should get warm fries with melted cheese, right?
A bad experience all around.
Husband was also not impressed.

Midtown Eats is great! I always tend to go here on dates. I like how they will not take reservations, yet offer a high quality dining experience both in food and atmosphere that would rival much more pricey places... that require reservations.

They make great drinks, have a friendly and knowledgeable staff; and an incredible atmosphere.

It's just different enough from Sup (sorry no umlaut)  to let you remember you've been there before, but it was a much different place. 

Though a little more higher up on the scale for price, I never mind bringing anyone here so they can experience such a great place here in Reno.

What a lucky find in my search engine.  
Prompt, courteous service. Thoroughly enjoyed my interaction with this company.

1) ordered food around 11am still waiting for the delivery, and it's 2pm. Was told 30 min ago that it will be there in 5-10 min.
2) Not going to review the food since I have not tried it

Woah, the Taint.

I guess I don't hate it.  Last time I went there, all the customers were disturbingly young.  Mostly Tulane/Loyola students, I'm guessing?

I remember enjoying this place years ago.  Didn't that curly-haired lady from White Zombie used to own it?  Hm.

It's right off Magazine, but it's a bitch to get to, and easy to pass up.

Heard this was a Santa Barbara must-do from multiple locals if you enjoy ice cream. I've been here twice in both my trips to Santa Barbara and I always see fresh unique flavors. My favorites are: peanut butter chocolate chip (just enough hints of chocolate chips and heavily swirled with nutty peanut butter) and churro (creamy, just enough hint of cinnamon, not too sweet). I recommend a fresh warm waffle cones to compliment your chosen ice cream flavor but it can be drippy so grab a few napkins. Can try multiple flavors for free to find your best dessert match! A few tables available to sit down.

small open kitchen produces each item fresh to order. Split an Arrugala salad, Gigli pasta and Panchetta flatbread. Everything was excellent.  The flavors stood out , but not overbearing. Chef Mario prepared as we watched, The service was great, prices reasonable. I was impressed at the simplistic yet eficient kitchen. The entire place was spotless.

Ordered four sandwiches yesterday for pickup.  It's a busy weekend and the line was long so things were a bit chaotic.  I didn't see any sign for picking up take-out orders so I got in the line.  My husband saw someone waiting on the side so he waited there.  The person in front of him picked up her order so he gave the cashier his name.  There was some confusion.  They couldn't find the order and asked where we ordered from (Yelp) and what was in the order.  The order was done and bagged already so they found it.   I liked the meat, oil and vinegar, and the spices on the italian hoagie.  Bread was awesome - super fresh.  Only one slice of cheese and I ordered extra cheese so that was disappointing.   Rueben was good.  Corned beef special was juicy and delish.  I like the meat on that one better than the rueben.  The caprese had the great bread but the inside didn't have enough to fill it.  It was like a bread sandwich.  Needs more arugula, tomato, and cheese.

While initially I was very disappointed in this location, the manager really knows customer satisfaction. 

Being Christmas Eve, I had previously contacted this location to find out their hours. We were driving through during the holidays and staying at the hotel on premises. When we arrived 30 mins prior to the closing time the doors were locked. This was obviously a big disappointment after an 8 hr drive when a beer would have hit the spot. 

I posted on Granite City's website and received a call from the manager within an hour apologizing. To make it up to us, he delivered a growler of my fav brew along with a gift card. And what a nice guy!  

Next year when passing through on our way to FL we will make it a point to patronize them. And when we return home, to our local GC.

This learning express staff is very rude, calling and asking a question is like something they do not handle well, especially when asking specifically something you may want, made me very uncomfortable and will not return to this store again!

I was mesmerized about how quick and efficient they are.  But the food quality wasn't great, not real Mexican in my mind.  I wasn't expecting gourmet.  Maybe something more along the lines of Chipotle or Qdoba.  But it just wasn't there.  However there is a wide variety to pick from.  Overall, it's somewhere I would go to again (for food only) if I was in the area but wouldn't go out of my way for.

Another issue...the bar.  This is my real issue with the place.  We had a party of 6 and I understand we are expected to wait on ourselves.  But the female bartender (white, probably in her 30's) was horrible!  She pretty much hates her job and lets everyone around her know.  She wasn't only rude to my party of people (who were eager to tip), she was rude to another gentleman at the bar while we were waiting to place our order.

I had some work done by Victor and his crew and it was some areas that needed fixing In one bedroom It was some  Staplers in the wall where my Niece have put some posters up  Why did they just paint over the staplers they could have come out It was areas where look like the wall need it to be sanded down in another bare Room it was areas where paint had never touched so I called him several times talking about Victor He finally called me backSaid that he would come and fix it he never came so I called again he never showed up then he is going to turn me to call him to remind him to come over what the h*** that is not how you do business I would not recommend this company to anyone not a great job very unsatisfied

I wished i had read all the bad reviews before ordering. I would give them negative stars if they had it. Im not surprised about the many TERRIBLE reviews. Heres another one for the books.
