My order came one hour later when the girl supervisor, told me 30 to 45 min. Then they charged me the regular price when i had discussed the special price already with her. Then she tried to deny it. The worker was also rude on the fone when i called them back. She claimed i had an attitude.REALLY. i guess so. The driver took the food back. No problem with him. Of course. I will never go back to them after bad rude experience. I just ordered from ANTONIOS pizza which is very close to them. Very friendly. Respectible and the food is delicious. Im amongst 180 people who wont b going back to nicks..hope the owner reads this so he can give them a attitude adjustment. Get a job at walmart stocking shelves if u cant b polite to people. Remember, The customer is always right !!!  U need to read the rest of the bad reviews u have gotten.just ripped up your menu and replaced with ANYONIOS. Bye bye.

To be honest I was a little underwhelmed by my whole experience. I came in on a weeknight before the dinner rush to place a to-go order. After being shown to the bar where I was told my order would be taken, I was ignored by the bartender for quite a while before a manager noticed me standing there with the take-out menu in hand. 

I ordered the red curry, chicken fried pot stickers and the 4 Kings of Thailand. Curry and the 4 kings of Thailand were good and the ingredients were very fresh tasting. The pot stickers were not great and I wouldn't order them again.

I will come back and try more of the menu primarily due to how close this restaurant is to my house.

Well it was about 6 month's since my fiancé and I stayed here. I took her up here because she has never been and I used to live with in S.B county. So I searched around and wanted something close to shopping and to the beach, the place was beautiful and the staff was very friendly. The only down fall was the restaurant didn't really give any discounts to the people that stayed there and the bar seem to be pricey. But I understand that it is going to be expensive especially in S.B., now I am sure some people will say well isn't most of this state expensive and I will have to say yes most of the beach cities can be and  no. But we still had a great time so thank you to all of the hard working staff for the pleasant hospitality.

I found this spa during a yelp search, and read all reviews and immediately  thought this place was worth it. I called ahead as I wanted to surprise my parents with a Valentine's Day couples massage. I asked for hot stones and then asked the difference between hot stones and was told Himalayan was better. As this sounded fancy I immediately chose this one. Again this was a gift so I wanted the best. The day of the appointment arrives and my parents are mid check-in when the receptionist walks outside to yell at a bystander for peeing on their sign. Studio has some nice wall decorations but cleanliness was below average. Now it could have been a bad day and my parents are still optimistic. Once they go in the back my Mother had the woman massage therapist who gave her a massage above the blanket the whole time. My mother explained that it felt like someone who was a novice practicing on her. Massage lasted not a whole hour about 30-45 minutes. Imagine 30-45 minutes of blanket rubs. As my mother calls I'm excited to wait on her review and ask about the fancy salts when she shares there was no salt, or stones or anything of that matter. At this point I'm extremely upset and I contact the shop and a really unprofessional woman answers the phone and LAUGHS the whole time and then states " we did not look at the schedule, we did Swedish instead". I'm furious due to the lack of unprofessionalism and for ruining my special gift. I made the woman on the phone aware of my review.

Very disappointed with the service and food I received.  In no hurry to return.
In other locations this has always been my favorite place to go for a quick lunch.  This location has changed my view.

If you like slow service, loud 1980s music, and cold breakfasts that are supposed to be served hot, you'll love this pedestrian and pathetic excuse of a brunch place. If you instead want to enjoy a good meal, go elsewhere.

There's no use in comparing it to the old airport, which was convenient until the TSA made it useless because of the long lines to the entrances to the gates, and the inability to move within security, between the gates. Now you have two choices, left and right, for its associated gates thru security.

The entrance is pointed inconveniently away from Indianapolis, indeed you need to go about 5mi one way farther to get to the new, mid-field terminal. There are two long concourses, and if you like to go with United, you'll take the longest walk. 

Outside security, there are some reasonably priced restaurants and kiosks; it gets more expensive inside security. You must pass thru the shops to get to your baggage when you arrive, which if United, is only 1 for 13 this year in timely arrival. One set of bags took four days to finally arrive at my home. Thank you, United. 

There is a large gap between the terminal and where you might catch a bus to outlying Indiana cities like Lafayette, Kokomo, Bloomington, etc. The gap is where a hotel might have been placed as all the other old hotels at the old terminals are, well, five miles away now. 

If you park in the economy lots, the bus won't bring you up to the terminal. You must walk the gap, you cheap so-and-so. Park closer and you'll get a nicer bus. If you park at the external lots, you have a long ways to go to get to the terminal, and the Airport Authority is fighting tooth and nail to allow other offsite parking lots along its former Ameriplex Parkway. Might be too convenient or provide meaningful competition. 

This year, I have 50,000+ air miles through this terminal. I'm reminded of its craziness, but there are good points:

1) The TSA is professional and courteous and quick. Nonetheless, always give yourself added time to park, arrive and get through security. 

2) The layout of the airline desks is reasonable. Baggage delays that I've received haven't been a local problem, rather, a problem with United's major hubs. I wonder if United can walk and chew gum some days. 

3) The parking situation isn't too awful, and there are lot of buses. 

4) You can't smoke except in your own car anywhere on the site. This means clean air. I like that. I wasn't sure that it was a good idea, as some addicts simply must have their fix, but I discovered it's reasonable. 

5) Some places stay open late, meaning you might be able to get a coffee or a snack if you arrive after 10pm. Not 100% of the shops close up like a small town, and some stay open later than others; bless them with your business.

6) There can be free WiFi, but it can be complex and difficult to use; usually, you're required to jump through some hoops to get it. Worse, it can be highly oversubscribed. Someone saw it as a revenue source, and messed it up. I wish the airport would remember it serves the public, not the MBAs. As a public edifice, it's one more reminder of tawdry management decisions.

There are better airports. If you travel, you'll like places like PVD and PDX. Certainly the big ones have their size as a problem, and lots of hands out looking for revenue. Travel shouldn't be a hassle and fraught with irrational expenses. IND as an airport terminal is quite inconvenient, and pugnacious in their attitude. They have a big bill to pay for the construction of that beast, so beware.

Looks like a hole in the wall bar at first, then it opens up to a decent dining room. For lunch, I had the seafood gumbo, crab cakes, wife had the shrimp poboy w/ fantastic fries. Best was Errol, our server. Great customer service with us and the tables around us.  Definitely a repeat in future visits here.

Won't be back. The place was empty and they still couldn't find the time to clean up the huge flooding all over the counter and floor making it an obstacle course around the counter. The 2 booths were both broken so while you bobbled one direction, your food on the table bobbled another! 
Mine was a hot veg wrap that was literally nothing but cheese ball of tightly rolled pepper ends and my friends #7 was mediocre at best. I had to toss mine.
I'm giving 2* for the chips and drink. You're better of paying Publix and getting quality boars head meat and more selection for the same $.  First and last time here.

Went looking for new Dental services  since I retired. Had a cleaning and exam under thier coupon offer. Joined thier discount program. Ended up with some periodontal diagnosis and got a really lousy root planing for $1200 came back a few months later and saw perodontist and got a qoute for about $8000. I dont have gum bleeding but do have a few problem areas. Went to a well known perodontist for second opinion and estimate was 1/3 the cost. On top of this I had root planning done over since it was never done right. 
On the positive ... I did like the staff and dentist. They are very well technically equipped. There were some other things i did not like though (requested not get floride treatment... and question UV laser treatment. Wish i could get a refund on work done since it had to be done over.

I moved here from LA not a week ago, and I was very skeptical about the 5 star rating. But I had consigned myself to what I was sure would be another multi-month hunt for that ONE stylist who can deliver the cut I want - starting with Jessica. 

Well, I'm not looking any further!  Jessica is phenomenal, she's a first rate stylist (and I've been to a LOT of stylists charging $120 per cut) and wonderful person to chat with. She takes the time to listen to you, adjusts quickly to your input, and overall works with you to make sure you go out her door loving your hair. I love the fact that she knows not to cut bangs until they're dry - this attention to detail makes all the difference. 

I'm so lucky I found her so quickly, and you won't regret putting your hair in her hands!

A gf raved about Trio to me and I was up to try it. I do like thai food in general.
Looking at the website the menu didn't have a ton of options that peaked my interest.
I ended up ordering the beggars pouches and the sweet corn fritters.

The beggars pouches didn't agree well with me after i started eating the 2nd one. Maybe it was the sauce it came with. 
I preferred the corn fritters but I couldn't tolerate that too much either- I did prefer the sauce of this though.
I think it was probably more because they were fried.

Staying away from the usual, i avoided the pad thai. Not interested in the salmon or anything with chicken. The seafood hot pot looked interested but I was trying to stay within budget.

I ended up getting the crispy tofu ginger dish. It wasn't that bad actually. I enjoyed the tofu a lot. Which it had more sauce though. I ate with the white rice it came with.  The dish was not WOW'ing in any way.

I can't say I'd go back to this place. It's not a bad place, just not noteworthy. If I lived close by i may try another item on the menu.

I stopped here on my way home from work. I live three blocks away and work down at Broadway and Olive, so one would think I'd have shown up before now, but I just hadn't gotten around to it.

I locked my bike up to the new bike rack out front, which wasn't quite finished being installed yet, but I they were doing some other construction out front (expanded patio area, I think?) so they should have the outside nailed soon.

(Edit: Not only did they not finish installing the bike rack, they removed it.)

I went back to the back to order my food: pulled pork platter (like the sandwich only open-face and with more meat). I also got the baked beans and potato salad. The baked beans were fine (hard to mess up IMO), the potato salad had too much mayo. The sandwich was amazing. There's a huge table of BBQ sauces at the back (three varieties, all made there). Make sure to try them all, as they each have a really nice taste. Even the hot one tasted quite nice, but it could have been hotter.

No one was serving at the bar, the lady had to go outside and get the bartender to come in, as he was outside smoking and shooting the shit with some other guy.

The environment was weird: classy interior, cafeteria/bbq-house counter, plastic serving baskets, mixed-drink-centric bar.

When he came in, I asked about the beer selection. He ran through the AB products and acted finished. I asked about some of the beers and he finally coughed up the fact that he had Schlafly Pale Ale under the counter. AB products are a last resort for me, especially with good BBQ. Their bar seems set up to do more of a mixed-drink thing.

Compared to the food, the beers were all too expensive. Also, not a wide enough selection was to be had. Their happy hour included an AB beer (bud?) bucket for cheap but didn't seem to include any other beers.

Honestly, the food was good, but next time I'm grabbing it to go and eating it outside the Tincan while I guzzle half-price beers from the TinCan's happy hour.

Feels like HOME! So much memorabilia for the military. Being an Army veteran, wife of an Army veteran and sister of a Marine, I have never felt so at ease. And the food is AMAZING. When I come back in town we will be back for sure!!! Until the Hoorah!
I had a wonderful experience there,the donoughts were incredible and the customer service was absolutely amazing!! I will definitely go again

Hooray for happiness! The team working the grill and counter at Spataro's Cheesesteaks (in the always popular Reading Market Terminal) were the happiest people I met in Philadelphia. They seriously seemed to love their jobs. 

I just wish I loved their cheesesteaks as much.

Oh, I'm not saying my Cheesesteak with Peppers from Spataro's was bad. It was fine, ok, and alright. It was also meaty, cheesy, filling, and pure Philly. It just wasn't lovable or the best.

Regardless, huge props to the crew at Spataro's Cheesesteaks for the happiness and joy on display behind the counter. You made my day even though the cheesesteak didn't.

I really love poke and saw that this place had 4.8 stars on DoorDash so I decided to give it a try. I Ordered the regular poke bowl with 2 scoops of fish and brown rice. The rice and Edamame was hard and the seaweed did not taste good. The best thing in that bowl was the fish and house sauce. Sorry, but I would not try this place again nor would I  recommend this place for poke.

Eh. I ordered the spaghetti pesto with chicken for lunch. The pasta dough was oddly chewy and tough for being fresh pasta. The pesto was straightforward. Nothing to write home about. The portion was small for $15+. The small piece of garlic bread was pretty tasty but just not enough of it. We also waited over 15 minutes for our meal, and considering no one else was there, it was far too long. Not missing much here.
Food was wonderful-I had the roasted lamb and my hubby gobbled his down (so whatever he had was yummy)! The staff were attentive and very helpful; we will be going back soon!

Mug N Bun has gone downhill. Ate in the Dine-In house tonight. Got our drinks and food quickly, but we were missing pickles and tartar sauce, and the hot dogs were room temperature. We asked the server for them. After waiting 15 minutes for server to bring the condiments, I went up to the main building. I asked a server there to get me the condiments. She asked who was my server, and I pointed him out since he just got around to our request. His face turned red, and he promptly turned around and left. No condiments. The other server asked the kitchen staff for them. After 5 more minutes of waiting I asked to talk to the manager. He apologized, but then he just stood there staring at me. Still no pickles or tartar sauce. I had to ask him if he was going to give them to me, and then he finally reached around the counter and gave them to me. Our original server never spoke to us again. It is clear the owners no longer manage the place. There were no experienced staff as far as I could tell. A bunch of teenagers and college students running the place. Save your money- not the same place I knew growing up.  Too bad - this was our summer go-to place.

I'm in a spot right now where I need to spend as little cash money as possible, but my already-dry hair was getting to that point where the ends were super crispy and damaged and my whole 'do was shapeless and shaggy.  No amount of ponytail-ing could hide the fray and frizz, and it was bumming me out, 'cause I wanted to look summery! professional! healthy! and instead I felt like my hairs were bringing me down.  I didn't want a lot of length taken off--I'm trying to grow it super-long again--but the short trim I got totally transformed my hair into a sleek, shapely, bouncy 'do without the all-too-familiar, in my experience, aggravation of feeling like I'd basically gotten any length progress hacked completely back.  At a couple of points my stylist was unsure about some angling, and deferred to her teacher, who in different cases gave her instruction and/or modeled the technique herself.  My hair can be really mercurial, so for me to walk out of the salon feeling 100% fancy is no small feat...and on top of that, I didn't have to stress about the cost of that hair-based confidence boost.

For our honeymoon, my husband and I went to Santa Barbara.  During our week there, we tried several of Santa Barbara's most popular restaurants (Ca Dario, Arigato Sushi, Carlito's, etc).  By far, our hands down favorite was The Palace Grill.  The staff was incredibly friendly (Sheryl was our server - she was great!) and the food was delicious!  We're definitely going back next time we're in SB!

Fiddlehead fern cafe is absolutely adorable! It's so bright and pleasant instead! The toast I got was tomato with goat cheese and it was a big piece of toast! It is a little bit on the pricier side though

BEST Pho in St. Louis! I've been here once for take out and twice for sit down. The service is a little quirky, but the food is worth it. I think it's a good sign when 80% or more of the restaurant doesn't speak English!

Expected more. Very light on the steak. For 11 bucks you would think you would get more. It felt more like a tourist destination and they were just hiking up the prices for a Philly cheese steak because they could. Staff were pushy too, kept insisting we ordered more drinks then we did. Parking was terrible, but given the tourist feel to the whole experience I guess it should have been expected.

Just left here for lunch. Ordered the stuffed jalapeños that were swimming in grease and tasted like a ball of salt. Asked the waitress if I could get something else instead. She responded that they don't let you get something else just because you don't like what you ordered. I said well it's greasy and doesn't taste good and she said, "That's the policy." Safe to say I won't be back  I used to come here all the time and get the pork vermicelli. Their not so stellar customer service has lost them a patron.
My first visit is late afternoon on a Tuesday and I am one of two tables here. It's a nice atmosphere, there is outside seating, and there are trivia cards on the table. That's a great idea.

I had the turkey sandwich with sweet potato fries. Probably one of the best sweet potato fries I've had, four stars for that! But the sandwich was boring and standard. Don't get me wrong, it still tasted delicious but it really didn't differ from a turkey sandwich, say, made from home. I do want to go back and try something more...cooked.

Me and my friends love coming here, atmosphere is great, waitresses are super nice, music is always good too. The food could be better but can't complain, I ordered a steak and it was alright, you're better off ordering the bar food. The dessert was DELICIOUS (s'more fritters) !! I would highly recommend it :)

Came in for a dinner on the first night in NOLA. The reviews were pretty great so thought.... why not?

The good: the drinks were good and the bartender and hostess were friendly. The Strawberry Blonde was delish! The BBQ shrimp was so good full of flavor and a little heavy on rosemary but I like rosemary. The atmosphere is comfortable and welcoming. 

The bad: we ordered the BBQ shrimp as an app and it basically never came. There were three of us and we ordered the pasta, bacon wrapped shrimp and the jambalaya. The meals took about 30 minutes to come out still no app in sight. The bacon wasn't cooked still pink so couldn't eat the shrimp. The rice was terribly undercooked (still crunchy) in the jambalaya. The pasta was the only okay dish, not full of flavor but edible. 

The recovery for the flub was really good by the host (she was fab!). The server was laid back (I guess) so he wasn't too concerned he eventually moseyed down to our table after the table next to us had started to chat and convene with us. We weren't sure where he disappeared to. It was relayed our app had reached another table and they gave us a free cup of gumbo and 10% off the check. All in all wouldn't return and the food was mediocre.

My brother and I phoned in for two orders of snickerdoodle pancakes to-go. When I got there, everyone greeted me and although the masks covered their mouths, you could tell they were smiling. 

First, I couldn't believe my total was $12! I asked one of the employees to double check that they rang me up for both orders and she confirmed that, yep, 2 huge pancake orders was $12. Boo-Yah!

When we got home and tried the pancakes, I ran to grab my phone (while holding the container with the pancake in it so that I could sneak bites in while I write) so that I could write this review. 

The pancakes are so good! Jesus be a respirator because I am so taken aback with how good the pancake is. Sweet, fluffy, huge, and doesn't need additional syrup. 

I can't wait to go back.

Update 06/05/20
Okay...so I've been back 4 times since I wrote this review 12 days ago...DON'T JUDGE ME! When I went today I got the eggs and snickerdoodle pancake combo. The eggs were delicious (I added cheddar jack cheese for $1 which was SO worth it) and the hash browns were good! That's really saying something because I usually don't like hash browns. I love Baja Cafe! Can't get enough!!

I give them a zero. They kept my car for three days..couldnt find anything wrong with it...even tho it was towed in...charged me a hundred bucks..

Not happy.
...
While the food was visual appealing and looked great it lacked flavor and totally missed the flavor train. Upscale Mexican that charges you way too much for mediocre food.

Love love love Rybread! Their lunch sandwiches are amazing, particularly the Napa (get it grilled and add prosciutto).  Their breakfast sandwiches are huge and a perfect hangover or post-gym meal!  The staff is so friendly too.  I hope that they someday expand or open a second location - it's a tiny little joint and they seem to be doing really well! I took off one star because service can be pretty slow. The place gets PACKED on weekends, and they occasionally run out of a particular kind of bagel and everything gets backed up.

Nice and friendly people. They offered same day delivery to me.  They even suggested what flowers to give and picked the flowers by themselves. All i did was call them and have them deliver at my place to surprise my wife.
One thing I didnt like was that they charged $7 for delivery fee. The shop is literally 2 mins from my house on the same street. I dont mind the fees, but there should have been proper disclosure of the fees up front than for me to find it directly in my credit card statement. Second thing, it was meant to be a surprise. When they could not find anyone, they called my wife even after explicitly telling them that in case of any issues call me not my wife. basically, gave away the surprise. 
Anyways, the flowers were beautiful and very well decorated. Loved it. Highly recommend this place.

Went here on a late Sunday afternoon with a friend and her little bro. First time eating at Pho King and it's like most Vietnamese places in Edmonton - small, family run, slightly grungy but cheap, good food.

I had pho a few days earlier so I decided to order something different:
- R4: Grilled lemongrass pork chop and spring rolls ($9.95) - the pork chop was tasty but a bit on the greasy side. Came with two spring rolls, rice and side of cold veggies.

My friend and her bro had:
- King's special beef noodle soup ($9.95 for king size) - she liked it but said it was bit on the salty side.
- V13: Pork spring rolls ($8.45) - you get 4 spring rolls I believe, vermicelli noodles and veggies with a separate container of peanuts.

Food hit the spot as we were hungry. Didn't even have any leftovers. Service is fast. You pay up front as is the usual case. Menu is pretty interesting/weird/random - Western breakfast options and burgers...at a Vietnamese place. Someone try these and let me know how they are. Haha. Other than that, good food and cheap. 3.5 stars.

A great place with great food and typically a friendly atmosphere. Went here a few days ago and did not get the warm welcoming. The old GM Ed made sure there was a happy vibe where everyone was welcomed since he's passed away the place doesn't seem the same. I get those are big shoes to fill for someone. There is a new manager who never says hello or walks around the restaurant to greet tables and simply maybe say thank you. The females bathroom has a smell of a nasty sewer back up. Even wearing a mask doesn't cover the smell. It's nauseating and ruins your appetite. I sure hope something gets done with that smell. A shout out to the bartender Dolores and the servers Alice and Jamie. They are alway happy and make you feel good. Hard to tell under the masks but they seem to always be smiling and enjoying working. I hope something gets done with the bathrooms and the place goes back to how it was.

Leaves of grass?

How about a pain in my ass?

If you're coming onto this bridge from the south via 95 the merge area as you get onto the bridge is totally lame.  It really forces 2-3 lanes of traffic over in a short space.  Once all the merging is completed, I guess it's not awful, but there's something about this bridge I don't like.  

Can't put my finger on it, but I don't trust it.

The only good thing about it is that when you're crossing it going east, at least you're leaving Philadelphia.
A bit over rated and pretentious.  The food was good, rich and tasted somewhat processed (in an okay way).  Glad we went but not sure I would like to return often.

These ladies are awesome! I've been going to this place for over a year and it has great vegetarian and non-vegetarian food. They also cater to dietary restrictions if you let them know and will try and come up with something for you. The portions are huge and the food is always good. Highly recommend this place.

I've been here a few times over the past year and the service continues to get worse each time. This trip we sat for 10 minutes before anyone came to talk to us, our drinks came out in waves and only 3/4 full and our server was trying to get the attention of other employees (to check on other tables) while she was taking our order. Our food also came out in waves. Some people got their burger, others got their side, no one got both. The food quality was mediocre. The only real attention we received was when it was time to pay the check. While it was lunch time, it was not crowded. Very disappointing.

Great atmosphere and very attentive waiter.  The ginger lemonade was amazing!!  We enjoyed everything we ordered and the chocolate dessert was decadent.  Definitely recommend this as a Boise destination.

Me and my friend both go here on tues..and have lunch together..most tues we do..but I absolutly love the burgers and the breadpudding...awesome awesome awesome...i haven't been there 4 anything else..so i can only speak 4 the food right now...

Stopped in here with my wife and a friend for lunch today. And let me tell you it will probably be the last time I visit this location.  I normally love QDOBA but this location down right sucks. From the ride ass customer service to the cleanliness it's just all around terrible. I've never had a person whistle at me from behind a "line" and say "excuse me what would you like to eat". You're kidding me right?

And plus look at my pics, this chick had her hair dangling down in front of her face and swaying back in forth and prepping food. ARE YOU SERIOUS? What happened to having it covered up? I won't be back to this location, to easy to get sick or catch something.

Worst pizza I ever tried in Tampa. Crust was uneven, undercooked and the cheese was soooo salty! I asked them if they rushed it and for another pie and they told me that's how they make their pizza. No attempt whatsoever to make a customer happy, even after I told them I drove 40 minutes to have it! So sad they call this place NYNy pizza. They wish!!! If this place opened in NY, they would shut them down in 5 minutes. Good thing the drunken monkeys from the surrounding clubs and bars keep them in business and  don't know any better!

UPDATE:
Got take out last week and service was great and food was amazing. They definitely redeemed themselves for that. Wish there were more options to choose from but totally understandable right now with this virus going around. Looking forward to giving them a 4 or 5 star review when we go back to dine in. Crossing my fingers for amazing service 


Came here the other night and it was by far the worst experience I've ever had at any restaurant. The one star is for the manager Bobby for going above and beyond. 

Walked in and waited about 5 minutes to get seated. We got sat at a booth in the bar area. A bus boy brought us waters about a minute after we sat down. 

10 minutes later the bartender comes over to take our drink orders and also ordered my daughters dinner. A few minutes later the bus boy brings our drinks but forgets my 3 year olds apple juice. 

About 10 minutes later the manager drops off biscuits and I let him know the bartender forgot the apple juice. He brings it back right away. 

We wait for about another 10-15 minutes for bartender to take our dinner order. We ordered appetizers and our dinner. 

So now it's about half an hour since we ordered our daughters dinner. We get our appetizer and soup before she gets her small lobster tail and fries. 

A whole 45 minutes goes by and my daughter finally gets her food. Meanwhile my husband and I are still waiting. Sitting for half an hour with no drinks, thought I had to start sucking on ice. 

We waited for 20 more minutes for our dinner. It was taking a long time but I thought maybe it was just busy. We're starting to get full off of biscuits. 

Then I look to my left and the party that got seated AFTER us were done with dinner and leaving. That means they got their food, ate it, paid and not leaving!! MINDBLOWN!!! I got furious. Found the manager and let him know my frustration. I told him I'd like to just cancel my order, pay for what we got and go home. He asked if he could check on our dinner and pack it for us to go and he'd take care of it. We agreed. 

Here's another crazy part and my theory on why our food never came....bartender forgot to fire our food!!! How do I know this? After the manager went to check on our food, my side salad came. So it got me thinking the bartender never pushed fire. 

Manager came out with our food and asks to ask for him next time we come in and he'll make sure we have a better experience. 

I normally wouldn't go back but I LOVE lobster. We may go back soon but ask not to be sat in the bar area of c

Are there negative stars?  Although the docs and nursing staff are great, don't stay there.  You will be better off in a third world country.  Heat did NOT Work at night-  the nurses passed out those heat packs that you squeeze to generate heat.  For 4 nights, I froze.  I wore a nightgown, big fluffy robe, socks, 3 blankets and fur-lined gloves I had a family member bring from home!  And I was still cold.  The nurses told me that the room next to me also had no heat-  what was the purpose of that?  To make me feel better?????   One night I thought I would take a hot shower to warm up-  and the shower did not work!!!!!!!  Never once was I offered an apology for the conditions.   I think a State Inspection is well overdue-  which they would fail.

Called to place a pick up order of 100 wings, was informed that they were going to check the chartering menu for prices. Put me on hold for 30 minutes! In the mean time I drove up there to put the order in person while I was still on hold. Showed them I was still on hold while at the restaurant for them to tell me they couldn't do my order! Terrible customer service and have no appreciate for the customers that keep them in business.

Good value on happy hour margaritas, draft beer and chips.  Upbeat atmosphere.  Second night in business, and only 3 tacos on the menu:  beef tenderloin, fried chicken and pork al pastor.  We ordered the pork al pastor and the meat was very spicy and room temperature.  Not good; meat kept and served at the wrong temperature is dangerous.  We told the waitress and she said she would notify the kitchen, but our bill wasn't adjusted.  Also, they don't validate for parking.

Brought my dog in for a wash and trim, they complained how matted the dog was. We take good care of our dogs, When we got home I noticed him biting at his leg. Looked like he was burned by the blow dryer, took him to the animal hospital and his whole leg was burned. Never going back

What a disappointing experience (surprisingly) I had at Lee How Fook.  I'm being generous giving it three stars but I once a few years ago had a decent meal there so I'm rounding up from two stars  Okay, let me explain.

It was a snowy day in Philly that was turning to rain and I had an early 6:30 theatre curtain with a friend.  I took the train into town and arrived early to meet my friend for dinner only to get a message my friend couldn't make it in time for dinner and would meet me at the theatre.  Ok. I decided to just head up to Lee How Fook on 11th Street and grab a meal there.  I walked in about 5 o'clock - no one else was inside except the staff making dumplings at a back table. They all just looked at me and didn't say a word. I said  "Are you open?"  They said yes and I sat down at a corner table.  Finally someone got up and came to me and I ordered the salt-baked squid.  No tea ever came my way - that's a first for me in Chinatown....and it was a cold snowy day outside when tea would have been warming.  The salt-baked squid arrived promptly and it was decent - but by far not the best I have ever eaten.  Unfortunately one of the condiment bottles on the table was so sticky it was actually gross and the hot chili peppers needed a refill.  I exchanged them for the condiments on the empty table next to me.  When I asked for the bill I got it with not even a thank you expressed.  I paid and promptly left and headed into the storm to the theatre where my friend had arrived safely and the play was excellent.

I don't think I will ever eat again in Lee How Fook.  Why would I when there are so many awesome restaurants in Chinatown?

I appreciate the contemporary nature of this place, but I'm sad to say that it was overpriced for what we experienced. The two ramen ordered took about 40 minutes to come out, although  the place was quite packed that day. Not your traditional creamy tonkotsu soup, but more of an oily broth, and the chicken had lost potential.

Stay away. Stay away.  If you buy a unit that doesn't work, you are screwed. They will not replace it.  Conversely, if you buy from Best Buy, Home Depot, etc, they will replace it within 30 days of purchase.  Our new dishwasher did not work immediately after installation and we are on our 5th service call and the applicance still won't work. They refuse to replace it.

The breakfast is small and lobby is dirty. Came into the room and it looked like it wasn't cleaned. No trash bags in trash cans. The tub still had water in it with hair. Plus a horrible smell coming from the room. There were stains on the sheets and elevator is broken. Only an outdoor pool that we didn't get to test cause it was to cold. The gym is a joke. Had a treadmill and 2 other machines with sheets covering them. Only stayed here because it was cheap and allowed pets.

One time eater. Ordered chicken wings. if you have high blood pressure you can eat them wings here because they add no salt and thus have no flavor. Perfect for my Alex who has salt sensitivity issues. Yeah there are places that do not cook with salt. But who knows maybe its just that they cook their wings bland.

A nice, quiet spot to stop by and grab a bite to eat. The upstairs area is a perfect place if you are looking for a place to get work done. The coffee is of good quality, smoky and rich. And the food is unique and tasty.

Nice building/location - but service is always awful.  Been bad for years now.  This morning - no line in drive through - so figured would stop for coffee.  Placed order, said pull up to 2nd window.  When got to window - they didn't have any record of my order.  Pulled right out and went to Dunkin Donuts across the street.

We stayed in a gorgeous and spacious pet friendly mountain view room on the ground floor with our own little garden.  The room was great, except for the nutbags who were staying in rooms near us.  

I slept soundly in my huge king bed for a couple hours, and then...

1:00 AM
  -  *Bang* *Bang* *Bang* - "Police - open up!"  (I swear it sounded like they were banging on my door - a very shocking way to wake up)
  -  Distraught Woman: "We've been telling them to leave for an hour, but they won't"
  -  Policeman on radio to Front Desk: "We need to know who is in room 427 immediately."
  -  Policeman to Apparently Barricaded Door: "You can come out now, or you can come out later and go to jail."

I finally fell back asleep, and then...

6:00 AM
  -  Ceiling above me:" *Thump* *thump* *thump* *thump* *thump* *CRASH*." This goes on for about 30 minutes till I feel like sicking my dog on the little shits in the room above me that are apparently playing tag while wrestling.

6:30 AM
  -  Me on Phone to Front Desk: "It sounds like there are children running around right above us - can you do something about it?"
  -  Front Desk Lady: "I'll send security right over"

Thumping stops...

7:30 AM
  -  Ceiling above me:" *Thump* *thump* *thump* *thump* *thump* *CRASH*."

7:40 AM
  -  Another call to the front desk to complain.  Does no good.

7:50 AM
  -  I pull on my clothes from the night before and stumble upstairs to track down the little bastards.  I find the door to the room above me.  I hear cartoons, and the hallway is littered with juiceboxes.

7:55 AM
  -  Me on phone to Irresponsible Mother: "Do you have a child running around in your room?"
  -  Irresponsible Mother (suspiciously): "I have two children..."
  -  Me: They've been keeping us up for quite some time - can you get them to be quiet?"
  -  Irresponsible and Possibly Deaf Mother: "Oh, I'm sorry."

Noise stops for a whole 3 minutes.

7:58 AM
  -  Me on phone to Front Desk: "This is the third time I'm calling to complain about noise coming from the room above me"
  -  Front Desk Lady: "I'll send security right over."

So security comes to my room, comes in, listens to the ceiling, I tell them I've actually spoken to the woman and the kids are still hurling themselves into furniture.  Security goes up, comes back down, says they told the demon spawn to be quiet, and the demon spawn responded my kicking the wall three times.  Security says there's nothing more they can do.  They comp me breakfast and bring my dog a rawhide.  

So, to sum up, the hotel was gorgeous, and I won't let the morons near us affect my review, because I think I just got very very unlucky.  

Drawbacks are the lack of free wireless and the price - my room came to almost $400 for one night with taxes and a pet cleaning fee.

Also, the beach is right across the street, but pet owners beware - dogs are not allowed.

Hands down my favorite little Chinese place in West County.  My wife and I used to eat at this restaurant all the time in graduate school and now that we live in the area again we are once again regulars.

The egg fu young is excellent!

10/2007
Stopped in to pick up some food on the way home with the wife and the twins.  The staff were enthralled with the boys and took such great care of us.

Well the food was good the service was horrible gave it two times and will never and would recommend this place to anybody whatsoever I am normally really bias when it comes to reviews but there is barely anyone in here on a Thursday night and 5 servers walked by our table I nearly had to trip a guy to get a refill NEVER COMING BACK!!!

So, the first time we came here it was just ok. Good steak but horrible service. Luckily the manager was very nice & made things right. 
This time we come here & the service is a lot better but the quality of the steaks has gone drastically down!! Really paper thin cuts of ribeye & no T-Bones. Not sure if its really worth the $50/couple.
The pizza is good but they keep getting our order wrong. We don't notice unnoticed we get it home so I guess there is some responsibility on our shoulders.

I have hit this Starbucks twice on my way to work during not so busy times and wow, the service is slow! The first time I came there were 4 baristas working and only one of them making drinks...taking his sweet time making them too. I came again for a second time about a week later and the service was still slow. I have always been impressed with every Starbucks I've been too & their timeliness but this one in particular was shockingly so sloooowww!
Oh the Deliciousness  that is a hot fresh  beignet!!  3 to a bag 3 bags to an order... OH... the goodness that is in those bags...

Only rating 1 star because the receptionist Joan has the worst attitude(going on 2 years now)! God forbid you have to change an appointment  for an emergency she'll make you feel guilty about it. A family friend also uses this practice and Joan has brought her to tears over a bill she didn't know she owed. We love Dr. Hornick and the nurses but steer clear of Joan if you're calling or coming in. She'll treat you like you're the scum of the earth if you need to cancel for an emergency. Joan loves to hang up on you and get extremely cocky with you if you don't answer to her satisfaction. I'd leave this practice if we could see Dr. Hornick somewhere else!!!

I placed an order for delivery two days in advance, and called several times to confirm the location and time. The woman on the phone was patient, but they were unable to find our order and repeatedly asked me to relay all the information they had already been given. I happily complied, seeing as I wanted to ensure the food was delivered to the right location at the right time. After a total of 5 phone conversations over a series of two days, they were unable to deliver the food on time and were 30 minutes late. I would NOT recommend using their delivery service if you need their catering for an important meeting because I was very disappointed.

Their food was good but I ate from the place where they sell wings and they were undercooked. I had asked for grilled wings and they were very chewy and blood inside. I am not feeling good and pretty sure I got food poisoning from here. I'm pregnant so this is a huge deal to me. I didn't finish my food and I wish I had taken pictures but I was with a huge group on a time limit. The potato salad was good but I just feel completely nauseous.
We ordered on a Friday at 7.30pm and the pizza didn't get delivered until 9.15pm... even though we called at least 2 times to check in! And on top of it, the pizza wasn't impressive..

Most of the staff members here are cranky...I feel like I have to apologize after bothering them with my order. 
Coming from a girl who is perfectly content with Dunkin, I come here when I want to splurge and enjoy a very quality cup of coffee. Very rich and bold taste that I love. 

It's crowded on weekends and you have to come early if you want an everything bagel!! But still a nice place to study (when they aren't playing angsty grunge music too loudly).

Three stars because they have some pretty good tortilla chips! 

We got the Taquitos Mexicanos, which were flavorful but really salty. They came with guacamole (which was great), sour cream and pico de gallo. 

We also got the Nachos Texanos, which are nachos with steak, chicken and shrimp, and those were really good. They were loaded with lots of stuff -- the above mentioned meat, as well as queso, grilled peppers, onions and tomatoes. Very filling and very good.

We also ordered a burrito, but they don't really have burritos (you know, those cylindrical things you can hold in your hand and eat). Yes, they have burritos on the menu, but their burritos are more like enchiladas: tortillas filled with meat and covered with sauce. They also have enchiladas on the menu, so I'm not sure how they differentiate from enchiladas and burritos. Anyway, we ordered a burrito, but we had to explain that we wanted all the fillings inside the tortilla and wrapped up, which they gladly accommodated, but it was weird that we had to explain how to make a burrito to people at a Mexican restaurant. Basically, if you're looking for a handheld burrito, you won't find one here. But if you fancy an enchilada, then they have you covered.

They also offer a full bar, but we didn't order any alcohol because we got takeout (they don't let you buy alcohol with to-go orders).

Great food!  Had eggs, hash brown, pancakes and sausage, perfectly cooked!

Great service, very friendly and hospitable.  Very attentative to our needs! 

Nice place, ceiling vents (out) ate finally cleaned and painted!  About time!  One thing, intake vent need to be cleaned on regular basis, because black dust is still visible.  That is the only thing needed to get 5 stars!


I would come back again! :)

Second time ordering and they didn't include the rice. Why wouldn't I need rice in my food that should be included and not meant to be a side. 
And why do I gotta pay for you forgetting the rice?  Please don't make the rice a side, it makes no sense opening my food box and no rice smh. Just WHY  ??! Rice and peas is not a side option it either should be Mac n cheese or vegetable that's a side.

It takes a lot for me to give a place less than 3 stars. Actually, I think this is my first 2 star review. I came for a three night stay before going for a surgical procedure which was scheduled super early. The elevator only worked if you held the door closed button for a few minutes until it opened, not a deal breaker.  As soon as I got into the room, it smelled of smoke, but there is a nonsmoking sign on the table. Then there were stains on the bed that, with my medically trained eye, I think were blood stains. Picture provided, so you make the call. If I didn't have to stay away from all secondhand smoke before my procedure, i would have asked to maybe switch rooms.  We went to another hotel close by. My partner says the front desk clerk was friendly, apologetic when we came to check out, and refunded all 3 of our nights. Unfortunately, the price for this hotel is not enough to keep us here

The food is mediocre in my experience, but a coworker of mine swears that the mussels here are wonderful. 

The main thing I remember from my visit, is that they would pour my soda out of a half empty 2 liter bottle. It tasted flat, and made me wonder what other kind of half-ass jobs they were doing back there.

My roommate and I got food poisoning here. We left to go to a show at the Troc and could barely stand and threw up every ten minutes. When her boyfriend called to give the place a heads up (in case others were effected), it seemed like they didn't care and rushed off the phone. Will not be returning.
It was good but lacking cheese, the bread was perfect, meat was tender, onions was good but the cheese was lacking, when it's called a cheese steak and lacks cheese there is a problem

Me & my boyfriend sat at the bar tonight for a quick dinner after seeing a movie. The bartender couldn't have cared less that we were there. He was away from the bar more often than he was behind it. We had to ask him if there were any specials and had to ask him about the menu. I asked him for a recommendation and was given a quick response without any actual thought or reasoning. When our food came out, we were never asked if it was to our liking and were never asked if we wanted/needed anything else. I also ended up disliking the entree that he half heartedly recommended. I work in the service industry & can be a little picky about my service, but will absolutely make someone's night if I feel my service was great. This bartender made it very obvious that he did not care at all about our meal or us being there in general.

District Tap....

The atmosphere is fun, and when the weather is nice (like today) it's great to sit on the patio. However, their "brunch" menu, that they heavy advertise, was nothing special. I ordered the veggie omelette. It was extremely dry and had no flavor. My boyfriend ordered the chicken and waffle which as delivered cold (which was $14). The service was anything but efficient. We were constantly asking for drink refills and at the end of our meal had to track down our waitress to get our bill. Overall, for the price of the brunch, the quality of the food, & the service we received, I won't be going back anytime soon.

Love this place. Yeah, the service isn't super attentive, but if you want something it's not particularly hard to get it since the place is tiny and catching a server isn't too difficult. I'm nowhere near a regular but I've chatted with a couple of the staff and they've been great, and actually remember me, which considering the not-so-frequency of my visits is pretty cool. Favorites: frittata, pancakes, the potatoes are bangin, sausage (I know it's just a side but I'm very particular about my breakfast meats heh), cheese crusted chicken sandwich...and it's one of the few places where I trust them to not screw up my steak and eggs.
The food was fast and pretty good.  The only complain I have is that there were some bones that weren't removed in the Eel Teriyaki, but everything else was good.

Very upset with my experience overall. I am from out of town and heard great things about this nail salon, so I decided to visit. Needless to say, I should've waited until I came back home. I was very excited to get my nails done, but my nail tech kept burning each one of my fingers with the drill until I bled which hurt A LOT. Not only this, but she somehow managed to miss my nail? And the drill did a full circle around my whole finger. I have scabs on all of my fingers by my cuticle. I got SNS - something I'm very familiar with and I don't believe the process was done correctly. I say this because it's been 2 - 3 days with very little activity, and not only is the gel top coat chipping, but so is the SNS. I usually can last up to a month (or more) and my SNS will not chip. It will only begin to lift after one whole month of working and typing. Chipping, cracking, lifting and gel top coat falling off started to happen on the first day. I usually would not complain on yelp but this is out of hand. My nails have never looked this horrible. $35 down the drain!! It is so bad, I'm going to have to go back and get my nails redone as soon as possible. I can't even go back because I've not been in Tampa since. Either way I wouldn't choose to get my nails done there ever again.

**Update - the nails that had cracking in the SNS have gotten worse. It is starting to fall off not even 4 days after getting them done. I have not done anything strenuous enough to make this damage to my nails reasonable.

I'm updating, and slightly upgrading, my review because I'm now starting to understand this place.  It's not fair for me to rate it as a pure Americanized Chinese food play because that's really not what they are or possibly even care to be.  I'd probably hit them with a 1 out of 5 star review if I were comparing them to this brand of Chinese I crave every Sunday evening.

They're a bakery and roastery, specifically duck and pork.  That's what your expectations should be set to, and almost literally nothing more.  But I upgraded my review because there is in fact an order to be had if you tread carefully.

From the menu, go ahead and order potstickers, the roast duck and chicken lomein...and stop there!  I see you looking around, I said stop!  Now walk over to the counter, treat yo'self to a few orders of the pork buns, let's go with as many as you can carry, and pick up a pack or two of the pork dumpling dim sum things to round out the spread.  

That compilation is the first time in 4 years of trying to make it work with this place, where at least half of our collective family order was willingly consumed.  For that order, I'd send someone their way, because those items are tasty.

I have eaten hot chicken since the '60's. As fried chicken goes, my leg quarter was okay. I ordered with Doordash, and the food was delivered in a timely manner. 
When I ordered, it never gave me a choice of heat levels. Okay. I'm thinking there is only one heat level; after all, the sign says "hot chicken" . 
But it was just plain fried chicken. This doesn't qualify as Nashville Hot Chicken!
Maybe I order it directly in the restaurant next time. I always give a restaurant a 2nd chance when their first impression is negative.

Eddie ' s gives you exactly what you expect. Tasty diner food and a comfortable atmosphere. Solid fare, not wonderful, but not bad either. You will enjoy the retro appeal with the old record albums on the wall. Good American food, great for the entire family.

The girls were great and very nice. They came to our table and talked and asked what we would like to see. That is the only reason why I say two stars instead of 1. The cocktail never came to check on us I had to go the the bar 4 times to get another drink! Then when my wife was having a dance on the floor with one of the girls. One of the bouncers came and told my wife to stop touching the girl and my wife asked the girl if she was touching to much and she was told not. Then he came to me and said he was about to throw us out! And while this is happening a guy need to us had a girl in his lap and haven't paid for a dance and was touching her the same as my wife! We were about to go upstairs and do the vip experience but after he did that I wasn't going to spend anymore money there!

Yummy large serving of a chicken sandwich. Chicken is well cooked and crunchy. I like the choices of different chicken dressings. The bread for sandwich is alright, not the most fresh. I love the music themed decor of the diner. Breakfast, lunch, dinner is served all day.

I love this place but every time I come during the day on the weekend the service is poor. Today I waited 15 min at the bar to get a drink. It's not the bartenders fault but the restaurant could have used two bartenders. They only staffed one. The lack of staff here seems to be a big problem in terms of optimizing execution. They could serve more people and make more money if they staffed appropriately. The owners should staff for the business they want not what they have. This place is continuing to grow in popularity. Hire more people and provide better service!!!!!!

One of the worst restaurants in Chinatown. To start, my Rangoon Appetizer Favorites was greasy. My Chilli Seafood dish was too salty. My Mandalay Nungyi Noodle was room temperature and the noodles were over cooked.
Love Thai Sweet Basil! Great options and amazing food. Very friendly and courteous staff.

Love everything about this place! The quality of the frozen yogurt is impeccable, organic, no artificial colors or flavored. The varieties change, but a regular is the salted carmel,so delicious. Also a couple vegan options as well. Lots of fresh fruit toppings, candies,bacon,warm Nutella,peanut butter, and maple syrup! The atmosphere is comfy and inviting, love the swings:)

My wife and I went to the Conshohocken Cafe on Sunday morning. At first we were skeptical we were the third group of people in the restaurant after it opened and two couples came in behind us. The other two groups ahead of us were already seated. We didn't realize that it wasn't a "seat yourself" kind of cafe so they told us to wait. A sign would have been nice. After we were seated and the other two couples that came in behind us were seated we got our drinks and all. However, the server waited on the other two couples before us. Not sure why, but it was mildly irritating. So, we eventually we get our food, it was amazing! I have some of the best cream chipped beef ever. Absolutely Delicious. The restaurant has some kinks to work out but it is well on its way to being awesome.

We went there with our friends. Great food! Very delicious pulled pork, and I like their sweet potato wedges. They are next to a beer shop. Bring a specialty beer to the BBQ. Great! We will go back again.
Favorite sushi spot. Elizabeth roll is delicious. Super fresh tasting and good prices.

We love Ikkyu, we eat it biweekly.  no joke. Definitely recommend. We love the 'spicy teriyaki chicken don'. 

Nos encanta Ikkyu, comemos aquí cada dos semanas. Enserio . Nos gusta mucho el 'spicy teriyaki chicken don'.

Love this spot! My go to is the Egg, Potato, and Onion breakfast sandwich on a bagel. Cheap, delicious, and keeps you full for half a day. Give the cookies a go too -- their chocolate chip is awesome!

I came in here craving some Pad Kee Mao of what I use to have from an Asian community, it was pretty good here but a little over-seasoned/salty for my likings. They have four spice levels to choose from which is interesting, I went with mild and it was what I expected. All in all, I think it was average- not amazing! Perhaps I will update after trying a few other Thai restaurants in the area.

Saturday evening, looking for a quick meal on the way to an incredibly boring fundraiser. Always happy to find parking - there appears to be adequate on-site parking.  Very nice inside, this is a new building, clean, contemporary lines, well organized.  I particularly liked the bar height seating area.   

Service was pleasant and quick.  We tried the cheeseburger, fries, turkey burger and, getting the whole experience, a shake.  This shake is $4.49 - more than any other part of the meal.  

Freezing the shaking ingredients into a shake is kind of neat but, after the fog clears, nothing but a gimmick.  The shake wasn't any better than one made conventionally anyplace else.  Fries were ok, nothing special.  I understand the cheese burger was good.  I personally had the Turkey burger.  

Let me tell you about the Turkey burger.  I have no idea how anyone would know this is turkey by taste or inspection.  It could be ground anything.  I suspect it would take a team of forensic scientists and a multiple DNA tests to determine if a turkey did more than spit on passing patty.  At best, maybe 20 or 30 processing steps ago there was a pile of turkey skin, claws and wattle.  Way to make a McChicken look like gourmet cooking.


A little over $20, no more than average...which in New Orleans means two stars.

Would not recommend. This place was recommended to me by a friend while I was visiting Philly and the food was okay, but we also ordered 2 shakes that they were not able to fulfill on our order. I'm not sure why, they just said they didn't have shakes that night but also said they cannot refund the order. 

We ordered pickup through Postmates. I've contacted postmates 4 different times via 4 different avenues and they all state it is the restaurant's responsibility to process the refund. 

It has now been nearly a month and I have been calling veganish weekly to check the status of this. Initially I met a nice employee who was trying to help and process it. Today I called and got a very rude employee who said "I can't really help you there's nothing I can do". He gave me the number to "the owner". I called that number and it is out of service. 

I am extremely stressed financially right now, and wanted to support this business while in Philly for an emergency situation and the only thing I got out of it was losing money and time for nothing. I guess go here if you want to pay for okay vegan food and also pay for items you never receive with no option for a refund...
Great great guest service and the food is wonderful too. If you love chinese they also have some authentic Chinese dishes from local catch. A hidden gem at the center of Clearwater.

grant. hurting feelings? as a company that provides a service i would hope you understand that service isn't emotional. my review isn't emotional but only an account of what i experienced as a guest of your business.  my friends and i decided that this was the perfect placed to celebrate the birthday of an "amazing person" some brunch, bottomless mimosas and freedom to enjoy laughter among friends. i now understand that this isn't the case when you offer "passport mimosas. " no ones fault really, its that people should be aware of the conservative "environment" when they are considering this event.  thank you for your reply!

Stayed here after my sister's wedding this past weekend. The place is beautiful - I will give them that. But that's where the good stops. 

With the group discount, the room was WAY higher in price than most places I've stayed in Philadelphia. I'm just not sure why...

We were placed on the 10th floor - which sounds nice to have a view, except our view was of the other side of the hotel's brick wall/it's windows. We were also placed on the same floor as their club, The Stratus Lounge - so needless to say, going to bed was not happening. I get it - it's a club, but maybe don't put a club on top of someone's hotel room?! The music didn't end til past 2 AM and then all we heard was the staff moving tables and chairs until almost 4 AM. 

Needless to say between the high cost, bad view, loud noises and everything in between - I will most likely be staying anywhere but here.
OMG! This place is delicious! I ordered the grill marinated shrimp platter! It was the best! You can't go wrong!

My boyfriend and I are not coffee house aficionados by any means, but we stopped here while we were waiting for our ceramics time at the Expressive Hand next door. The staff at the Expressive Hand gave us some coupons for a free coffee or tea, so we headed over to wait out our time.

First of all, the place is nice on the inside and quite cozy. There are bar stools overlooking the store front, and lots of small tables. Another plus is that the large bookshelf in the middle room is full of games and puzzles and even playing cards. The BF and I kept ourselves amused with a never-ending game of Uno, so we really appreciated the fact that Chapterhouse had distractions available. 
  
However, that's where the positives end, unfortunately. The baristas were pretty grumpy when we asked them what drinks we'd be eligible for with our coupons (understandable), but their attitude didn't even change after we tipped them. Rude. My tea lacked flavor and my crappy paper cup (hello, sustainability!) had a slow leak which meant I lost most of my drink to the tabletop. Blah. Lastly, on a Sunday afternoon, the place was super packed. Most people looked like they hadn't moved in eons from their tables and didn't plan on moving for another several millenniums either. If I had paid good money for a cup of coffee and atmosphere, I'd be pretty annoyed that there was no space to sit. We ended up sitting on two slightly uncomfortable plastic chairs by the bookshelves, awkwardly adjacent to a couple sprawled on the sofa. 

To sum it up - this place put me too close for comfort with some snooty hipsters and meh beverages. No thanks, I'll pass!

Ok first the corned beef dinner looks nothing like the menu picture.  Instead of nice slabs of meat it is a little shaved meat and 1/2 a cabbage, all of it seriously over cooked. Rye bread was from the grocery store not homemade or even a bakery. 
Flavor profile was ok but not exceptional. Beer selection is disappointing and mediocre.
Wait times are long. If you make an appointment, please respect our time as well.

First off, I want to be clear that my rating is for the gamblers out there. 
My experiences may be much different than visitors that come with a family for an event or whatever other reason.  To the gamblers out there, I recommend that you do not stay here to play.

I have stayed here 10-20 times since 2009.  A lot has changed here in that time,  some for the good and some for bad.  On one hand the casino has been mostly renovated.  All of the rooms have been renovated expect for floors 3-7.  I have stayed on those floors twice and they are miserable.  On my most recent stay, I was given a third floor room.  It's fairly pathetic.  There is no sound protection so you have to sleep with earplugs.  There are also no safes in the room, so it was awesome to have to carry a large wad of cash with me all weekend even when I went out and about Reno.  

My main gripe though and why I won't come back here again is the reduction in comps.  The other reviews from people that have played here for years support my experience.  The comps here aren't very good and have been reduced significantly over the years.  I receive about 10% of what I used to get per hour and I gamble with a lot more money now then previously.  I make about $12/hour playing here and about $50/hour at Peppermill.

Speaking of Peppermill, it is the premier casino in Reno, and the only place I will stay and play in the future.

The apps were good and the dinner was just okay, but after an hour and 45 mins they asked us to leave. For a $200 dinner I wouldn't return to a place like that. They knew we have never been there before and rushed us out.

Just like to say that it is refreshing to see a company that puts their customers first    Had a nest of wasps by our front door. Called MAD they came out within 2 hours and treated nest. Still had some in crawl space. They made a second trip to get rid of the ones that got in. Problem gone. Wife and myself were self employed and that's how you treat a customer. Will use again and highly recommend to anybody that needs their service. Keep up the good work.

I LOVE THIS PLACE! 

I'm always craving potatoes, and I love hash browns. If I could come every morning to order their French Toast with a side of Hash Browns I would! Compared to other Western/Hispanic breakfast restaurants here in the SB/Goleta area, this place beats them all! 

Service is always friendly. However, the decor is outdated, but after you're presented with your food you forget about it. There's also free wifi, and it's pretty fast when the place isn't too full. There's also a small parking lot in the back, which fills up quickly on most mornings!

I would definitely recommend this place to anyone who enjoys Breakfast Restaurants!

I'm not much of an ethnic foodie and have never cared much for Thai food, but this restaurant has become one of my favorite whenever I'm in Reno.  We order a variety to share, usually including a curry dish (which is served with a bowl of white rice), a fried rice dish, and a soup (the won ton soup is wonderful), plus sometimes a vegetable dish.  I don't like spicy food, but we order it medium ("3") because there is often an ingredient that counters the heat (such as having pineapple or mango in the fried rice).  It's a great place and usually busy when I've been there.  The staff is friendly, too.

Deke's is perfectly placed right off of the Jefferson line and shares a  building with Attic Brewing. With the option of indoor dining as well as food truck, Deke's does BBQ and does it well. 

The first time I ordered from here, I was next door at Attic during a "Metal Monday" and got the brisket. It came with a light drizzle of BBQ sauce and a piece of corn bread. While the cornbread was very tasty and the bbq drizzle was good (both not too sweet) I really wanted to taste the brisket and asses flavor on its own.

My second order, many weeks later, was the same. I specified not to sauce the brisket and gave the cornbread to the girlfriend-yet I was not disappointed. The brisket was very tender, and I could tell the seasonings were simple. It is very hard to execute and serve brisket, but Deke's does it well. 

As someone who has smoked their own brisket to (personal) perfection and had top-tier brisket at other restaurant locations across the country, I can safely assure Deke's does it right.

Ate here 3 times. And I guess after 3 strikes, you're out. Never completely happy with the food, though I wanted to like it. Nice atmosphere and all. My last meal, a combo with a beef burrito and chalupa, was just not good at all. Ground beef in a tortilla--very bland. I couldn't finish it. And the chalupa was just a pile of lettuce and guac on a tortilla. Yuck. Sorry, I won't be back.

It's a jersey diner. So that covers most of it. The folks working there are great and so are the waffles. I think that most of the sandwiches come open face so if you're not a fan just ask. The Reuben was my favorite. Make sure to hit it up if you are in the area.

On my way through Philadelphia International Airport, I got hungry. Airport food is almost universally from bland chains that have cornered the market for those captive audiances, sooooo when I spot something that looks local, I'll usually roll the dice on them. Friendly, nice people here, but the food comes up snake eyes....

I'm always open to giving a place a second chance as I did with this place this past weekend.

The scrambled eggs were terrible last time so this time I did over easy. They came out cold, got send back, came out slightly better. The home fries here are not good. I don't know what seasoning they use, but they need to change it!

Hubby's sausage was badly cooked, the outside was so hard he had to use a knife.

I've about given up on going out to breakfast!

This place completely depends on your mood... and age.  I went early on a Saturday night and inside there were senior citizens doing the cupid shuffle... and outside (very nice patio)  was empty.  I especially enjoy the atmosphere out there to include the hanging chairs, corn hole boards, fire lit motif, and great waiter named Berry. As my friends and I were packing up I realized this place turns into a night club... who knew.

I have never been a huge Taco Bell fan, but I hadn't tried it in a decent amount of time and I wanted to give it another try! Unfortunately, my experience was just as poor as the rest. Sigh. The meat was just very "mushy" - does that make sense? It was simply an overall bland meal and I would not recommend going here unless you are EXTREMELY intoxicated.

Had to try this place after reading all the good reviews on Yelp. I must say I was very happy with my pick! The environment is colorful, vibrant and comfortable. Mexican music playing in the background and staff is nice. Place is BYO and that alone is amazing! We brought a bottle of tequila and ordered a pitcher of their mix: thumbs up there! 
The complimentary chips and salsa (3 different kinds) were delicious! I could just eat that. We got the Crab San Lucas and guacamole for appetizers and both tasted fresh and delicious. For my entree, I got the Pollo Mexicano and it was awesome! It came with salad too. I had the mango vinaigrette dressing: 
We were a party of 5 and everyone was pleased with their meals. To top it all off, we had a complimentary dessert for being our first time there. It was churros with vanilla ice cream with caramel drizzled on top. 
Thumbs up for this place! I would definitely go back!

Was my first time having pho. I had the beef and my partner had the Ox Tail. We also ordered a Boba Tea and the Soft Spring rolls. Everything was really tasty. There was plenty of mint and bean sprouts for the two of us. The pho is a big portion in a large bowl with a lot of noodles. You should be able to take some home easily. Enjoy.

As others point out, it's a typical Froyo spot but it was perfect on a warm night. Not only that, the staff was super friendly and helpful, which made me feel like I made the right choice to pop in. I tried a number of flavors and all of them are on par or better than similar ones I've had at other places.

So I haven't been here on a long time . The last time was when they had a kitchen fire. I had a coupon that expired but the restaurant is suppose to take the value.  

They refused to take the $12 value on my groupon. Despite my conversation with the manager, she said that the aren't taking them. Her explanation was that there is a new owner and the old owner got the  $ for the Groupons. They were aware of the Groupons when they purchased so I'm not sure why they didnt get it from the former owner as part of their purchase. She continued to tell me they took the for 6 months but that's it. In addition ,the manager  told me people many customers are upset about it and to look on Yelp. I was a bit baffled and I said you are willing to lose customers over this Groupon ? Her response was yes because the business owners don't want to  take the Groupon.

So based on the inability to honor a $12 Groupon to keep customers happy  or to even come up with a compromise to keep a customer happy we left and went somewhere else. I won't be going back,  there are too many options in St. Pete that have better customer service and much better food . One positive the servers are great, very friendly , and nice.
Its hard to screw up a dominos pizza.  This place seemingly ignored my order instructions 2/3 times.  The proportion of cheese, sauce, and toppings were incorrect and very different each time.

Went to one in Franklin... pulled up and my barber was working on some shoes?? Using spray paint and everything, did not wash his hands, fingers were black. Told him I wanted a bald fade taper, went straight to clippers and cut off all my hair to the top of my head on the sides and back , straight bald, no fade at all. I knew he was messing it up, stopped him and asked if he was fading it, he said "I've been doing this for 17 years man". Walked out of the barbershop completely disappointed, not professionals at all. As soon as I left, my barber went back out in front of the shop and started working on his shoes again? Glad to see he was a professional. Rushed the whole haircut, says on website 30 minutes. He was done in 10 minutes with everything. Completely unprofessional, seemed anxious the whole time, terrible experience.

First time here and stopped in with my son who wanted to purchase something he saw the day before. Of course it had sold but for the short time I was here the store is clean, well organized and has a lot of variety.  I'll stop by again when I have time to browse or need a item for a project. Worth the visit I'd say.

My family has been eating at Mi Amigos for approx 25 years,originally there were 4 locations in Indy but all have closed down except the one on Thompson and Emerson and that location just had a change in ownership, we just had an early dinner there which was our first visit since the ownership change, the menu is the same however I am sad to say the food quality has went down.

If you love hibachi style food this is your place. I've been to the old location but this was my first time at the new location. Its more intimate than before, with the dim lit lights, and the water fountain at the entrance. Its huge inside. Much bigger than the previous location. 

I went with some co-workers and we sat at one of hibachi tables. The service was great. The cook was funny, he did all the fire and food tricks. The food was delicious. This was my first time having their hibachi food I used to only get the sushi which is yummy too. 

My favorite part about this experience were the prices. I got the steak and shrimp lunch combo for only $12.99.

Too my wedding dress to Sandy's as recommended to me from Clarices Bridal Shop.  Sandy's was reasonably priced and she didn't stop making alterations until I was satisfied with the results.  The only downfall are her hours (TR-Sat) and they are cash/check only.  She did great work though!

I should probably simmer down before reviewing, but I ordered a half cheese half caramel mix, and I received apprx 80% cheese popcorn. Caramel corn is a higher price, which is what they charge for the bag, so not only is it disappointing to ripped off ratio-wise, it sucks to pay a higher price for mostly cheaper popcorn. The only upside is that the caramel corn had a burnt flavor to it, so I guess that's a silver lining to not getting much of it. The person who completed my order answered a personal call in the middle of me trying to order so maybe it was hard to use the scoop and jib jab.

We stayed at the Fenway Hotel this past weekend and, on a rainy and slightly crazy day, we chose to stay in and eat at the hotel's restaurant. We're glad we did, and grateful we had such a good on-site option given the weather!

One tip: use the Yelp reservation system to book this restaurant. We initially tried to search it and reserve on Opentable, but that site showed no availability at all. Then we tried to call (both on the publicly listed number and using the hotel phone's quick dial for the restaurant) and kept getting a busy signal or no response. Then we noticed that Yelp reservations covered this place and it was showing plenty of availability. So we ended up making our reservation on here.

Best drink award goes to my wife's cocktail, whose name escapes me, but it was a whiskey-based drink that essentially was an Old Fashioned infused with smoke. I also had a few top-notch local beers, including a coconut porter named Hawaiian Lion.

The food is well-made, albeit not too adventurous, New American fare. The seasonal burger's apple pumpkin butter was a hit, and the brussels sprouts were perfectly crisp, salty, and toasted. We finished up the meal with the 21+ dessert option, a baba au rhum cake. We only tried that one, but we can't imagine that any of their desserts could beat it!

Very, very, very dirty.  I could've said very a couple more times, but it would've become repetitive.  Watched a homeless man open the deodorant, clean himself with it, then put it back on the shelf.  This Rite-Aid may only be good for their ice cream and closed foods/drinks.

Because when you've just scarfed down a cheesesteak from Pat's...AND Geno's...back to back, you need a shot of jager and a beer to settle your stomach and heartrate down to normal.

Nice little place that we didn't hang at for too long, but the bartender was friendly and is definitely worth a stop.

Use to be one of my favorite restaurants, but there's a new chef in town. First we ordered an anti pasta and it was much smaller than we were served before, very skimpy on the vegetables. The calamari was fine.  Two of us ordered the rib eye steak, it was just ok, very chewy.
