Our waiter was new, we wanted Will, I should have requested him, since we had a really early reservation I figured we wouldn't have any problems.
This is an awesome space with good beer. The beers can be a bit hit-or-miss, but some are really great like the double duck ipa.

This is the absolute worst sprint store on the planet. Went in for a simple purchase, applied and was approved for a sprint credit card, then the inexperienced fella behind the register decided it was a swell idea to run the credit card while their system was "having technical difficulties" and then proceeded to run the card another EIGHT times. Very dissatisfied customers; we asked the store manager for a break on the $47 liquid glass screen covers after having to pay $300 out of pocket for a smart watch that was supposed to be covered by the credit card. At least the manager knows their store and employees are unsatisfactory as we did get the free liquid glass- but that is the only positive thing I can say about this store and employees and management. This store just really sucks, and I highly advise going to any other sprint store and save yourself the headache.

For the price you pay this place is great. For about $10 I get the Cabo Wrap with fresh ingredients and a choice of side (an ample side salad or onion rings are what I recommend). The service is fast and friendly. It is not a fancy, it is a sports bar. But for $10, fresh food and quick service it easily gets 4 stars.
The food was disgusting and the waiter who I believe was Australian was a complete ass.

Among the many establishments in South Jersey doing cheesesteaks, Chick's was one of the places that came up as a recurring must-try. While I'm not the biggest fan of cheesesteaks (tasty, just not my first choice), I felt obligated to check them out due to the proximity and good reviews...I wasn't disappointed!

Not a huge place, nor an extensive menu, but what I've had has been solid. Chick's uses the SoJo favorite Liscio's for their bread. I decided to try one breakfast item and then a cheesesteak the following day. I got the favorite pork roll egg and cheese (pepper jack) on a Kaiser for breakfast. Perfect proportions for each component of the sandwich and everything was cooked perfectly. Egg was cooked just to set (not runny) and wasn't too heavy on cheese. One of the "neater" PEC sandwiches I've had.

The following day I went back to try one of their Buffalo Chicken steaks. I ordered mine with pepper jack cheese, grilled onions and no lettuce or tomato. The chicken was finely chopped but not too fine, the perfect vehicle for the melted pepper jack cheese, and what I could guess was a light dressing of ranch. I'm not a big ranch fan, but the light dressing complimented the sandwich as a whole nicely, and the bits of blue cheese within added the perfect bit of buffalo flare the sandwich needed. Other than wanting just slightly more meat, I have zero complaints about the steak!

Overall, between the solid sandwiches and friendly staff, I can safe,y say I will be back to Chick's soon. Just wish they were open more for dinner time. Pork roll egg and cheese egg and pepper jack on a Kaiser

I'm kind of the odd man out for this longest running deli in Reno. In another review, I've stated I'm not big on sandwiches, but a place should be able to do a plain-er sandwich excellently. I've had handfuls of visits over the years and nothing has ever given me a "wow" impression. My last visit, enforced my opinion of this place as an A-OK deli. This visit, I had a basic, plain turkey and provolone sandwich, granted they were out of the Dutch crunch bread, but it was just so plain and bland... plain French roll, with plain mayo and mustard. I honestly make a better sandwich with more flavor, on open face grain bread at home. The few salads I have had are okay as well. Iceberg lettuce and toppings, while larger, nothing special for me. The soups are probably the best thing here (namely a Manhattan clam chowder), but they have a rotational schedule of the same few soups (lobster bisque, chicken noodle come to mind). 

I know others rave, but this just isn't my go-to lunch spot.

We were there for drinks and a bite to eat this afternoon.  The views were great, the service was not. We were seated at the bar for 10 to 15 minutes, the bartender finally came and cleaned our area. After another 5 minutes or so of being ignored,  we walked out. The bartender never said, be right with you folks or nothing! We could tell he was busy, but not to say a single word to us when he's on the other side of the bar from us was not acceptable.

When you're uptown and in the mood for pastries and savory cafe food, La Madeline is a great option. Standouts include their chicken friande (savory chicken in puff pastry) and the yummy tomato basil soup... the sandwiches are also quite good, your basic quiches are always available and the yogurt parfait is my son's favorite. The dessert case is full of yummy cakes and pastries. Coffee here is good- not the best in the city but better than a home or office coffeemaker. Despite some reports to the contrary, they ARE open on Sundays (I am writing this review on a Sunday after we just had lunch there) as well as on every other day of the week...
Service is decent..sometimes they are overwhelmed with customers and you have to wait to get your food prepared if you order a salad or sandwich or latte or such... you order at the counter and go bring your food to the available table of your choice, then someone comes and busses the table when you are done. 
The location is great...you can watch the streetcars from the window while you eat.

I know there are plenty of places to get a great cheesesteak in Philly. You've gotta say you tried it at least once in your lifetime right? I think so. 
I liked it a bit better than you know who's . A bit less greasy and hit the spot! Peppers were yum. Wiz with  enjoy! Went by at like midnight and I was greeted and the gentleman that took care of us was super friendly (I've heard otherwise ‍)

We really wanted to like this place!
We both ordered the cone meal which comes with a sauce, side, and a drink.  

While at the counter, we were informed there was no ice for the soda... BUT it was cold! Fine, we were walking two blocks back home, we could deal.  
When we got to the sodas, they were HOT and FLAT. They weren't salvageable so we opted for drinks at home.  
Unfortunately, it didn't get better from there. 

We unwrapped the cones which was covered with the sweet cinnamon sauce. The cone fell apart as I lifted it from the foil. I dumped out all of the chicken and proceeded to try to eat that. The sauce made the breading on the chicken gummy and sticky. I opted to just finish the cone, letting my SO eat the chicken. 

The only decent quality was the Cajun fries. They had good seasoning, and were decently crispy. Unfortunately, they were cold. 

Overall, I would not go back. The novelty idea is what sells and that hasn't been perfected.

The view is great, but everything else is a bit lackluster. We sat outside for the view, but we were seated at a foldable plastic table. For the price of food/drinks, I was expecting much better. I had a bahama mama that was tasty, but still overpriced for the quality of the ingredients they used. A large group of us were meeting there after work, so we mostly ordered appetizers with a couple of people ordering entrees. Everything tasted Ok, but there weren't any dishes that really stood out. Our server was better compared to the other times we've visited, so that was a plus. Overall, I wouldn't go back even for the view.

Get the BBQ Shrimp Po Boy. 

Nothin bbq about it. sweet, buttery, peppery, garlicy sauteed shrimp in a crusty french bread hoagie.

Great!
I found this place because of YELP. I was only in town for a night, and trust me this was just what my belly needed.  The Po Boy was awesome and I wish I could stop by this place everyday!  Enjoy!

If I could give a negative score, I would.  I have been a customer for 6 months, and on average 1 time a month they do not stream a program or movie.  For example, right not they were scheduled to stream the Chic Filet A bowl (Pittsburgh vs. Michigan State) and their website says "check back latter".  Direct TV Stream is simply not dependable.

I really wanted to love this place although whenever I received my traditional gyro, there was hardly any meat on it and what was there was tough, so I showed it to my server who looked surprised that I was showing it to him. He asked what he could to to make me feel better about my meal and I said well since the lamb was so sparse and tough, may I please have the same item with chicken. He said yes of course and then brought me back a gyro with quite a bit of tough chicken yet hardly any lettuce, tomato and yogurt sauce. So again I had tough meat and, this time, a very dry gyro. And the side that was still available was green beans which were not cooked very well, so they were tough to eat. I didn't say much else because they seemed like they didn't really want to be bothered and just haven't been back since. I'm so disappointed because I was hoping to find a 24 hour restaurant in Nashville I could count on.

I visited Asuka due to another Yelper's request, and was very glad I did!

Tucked into a typical shopping center, Asuka surprised me with its ambiance. It's very cozy, but still retains a nice Japanese sushi-bar sort of feel. I like it. The booth back 

The sushi was decent. One of my favorites, the salmon skin hand roll, was absolutely huge...I'm not complaining! The only thing that slightly irked me was that their 2 for $8 "regular" rolls were not even on the dinner menu at all...not there. I thought they misprinted the menus at first, as the take home menu shows them. Just keep that in mind when considering eating dinner at Asuka!

The waitstaff were friendly and thanked me for my business, which is appreciated. They also applied their Grand Opening discount (15% until Oct. 30) automatically, without me having to be awkward and throw a coupon at them.

All in all, Asuka is a very good sushi place in the West Chester area. I will be returning, but would like to see them expand their dinner menu to include more regular rolls (even if they aren't 2 for $8).

Seriously, I'm over these kinds of places - fast food mexican or tex mex....order at the counter........

Hey on Thursday afternoon, I guess it was slow enough, we were invited by the staff to sit wherever we wanted and get table service.  That was very cool.  And they were all really, really nice.  And not so nice that you thought they were trying too hard.  Just nice folks.  That's really nice to have!

I wanted to sit outside, but then the further back into the restaurant, the additional seating inside with all the window views of outside......we sat inside against the windows real close to the HOT SAUCE BAR.

So, we had some chips and many kinds of hot sauces served with our salsa, brought over to the table by our server.  I didn't care much for the salsa itself, but add a touch of the hot sauce flavor and WOW that was really yummy.
I liked best the pineapple hot one and one of the sweet mild ones....wish I could remember their names, but I liked from one end to the other!

It took us a really long time to figure out what we wanted.
But I finally opted for the NORITA thing....with carnitas (pork).
Luckily, I was able to exchange the guac for sour cream and dug right in.
The meat was plentiful on either end of the oval shaped plate.  Not as much in the middle, but it was fine - there was enough meat overall.
The flavors of everything mixed together tasted pretty good.
However, when picking out the meat alone with nothing else, it was a little bland.

Throughout the entire meal, our server was very attentive, refilled our drinks, all that good stuff you expect to have at any restaurant, but you don't expect it as much at a fast food type spot and am happy with this locations' offerings and most of all, service.  I will be back!!

The Middle Child is one of my new favorite restaurants in town! The service, the food, the vibe is what makes this place amazing! The menu is simple but has many options for everyone! They get alot of their food from the farmers market which I love! They have a large wine and beer list here making it easy to find something to try. 

When you order you go up to the counter which makes it great when coming here with a large group of friend or family. It also allows you to ask for suggestions and makes it easier to split the bill. 

We took such a long time looking at the menu. There were so many friends that we wanted to try but just didn't know what to get. Finally after we looked at the menu at what seemed like 20 mins we ordered. We ordered the mussels, a pizza and the chicken and gritz to share so that we could all try everything! Everything came out at the same time and was fabulous!!

The mussels were fresh and had an amazing sauce to go with them. We were surprised how many we got in our order! The pizza was cooked perfectly. It had so much flavor and the crust was crispy which is the way I like it! The chicken was fried perfectly with the best golden crunch to it. The gritz paired perfectly with the chicken and had a creamy coconut taste to them. 

Overall I had an amazing experience here with great customer service, fresh delicious food, and a great atmosphere! I can't wait to come back here soon to try other things on their menu! I heard they even have happy hour too, so I'll have to go back to try that too.
Pretty great ambiance, location, food and Sunday half price specials! Nice job Indy. Will definitively be visiting again!

Very nice. Good prices. Good service, much better than most. Good coffee with consistent top offs. Very fluffy pancakes (win!). Grits are made with milk (WTF!). So yes on the pancakes and a big no on the grits. Country Boy though is a yes overall.

The super-store - but not in a Wal-Mart way - for cosmetics and toiletries.  I always enjoy going into Sephora, but if I'm scheduled to be anywhere, my best bet is not to venture into the store until afterward.  Sephora has such a plethora of items, and I have experienced a very un-fun time feeling rushed to zip through the store because I had to be somewhere!  If you know exactly what you want and where it is, go head and pop in.  But if you're like me and like to look, and try, and look some more, try lots more, then look one last time, I'd suggest shopping at Sephora when you are NOT under any time restraints.

Sephora is the one-stop-shop for mid to high-end cosmetics - Benefit and Urban Decay to Yves St. Lauren.  It's great to be able to compare a whole bunch of products next to each other.  Prices on the higher end items aren't any better or worse than anywhere else, so it's just expected that one's Sephora receipt for make-up just isn't going to compare to that of Walgreens.  Sephora gets HUGE points for supplying an endless supply of lip and lash wands and eye shadow applicators so I can take it upon myself to try on everything in the store (except don't accidentally use the "alcohol" bottle instead of the "make-up remover" bottle on your lips like I did today. Ouch!).  I rarely buy drugstore cosmetics anymore because of the forbiddenness of trying it on in the store.

Other than the opportunity to try on make-up before I buy it, I think where Sephora really wins is in their unique box sets.  They'll sell a box set of mascaras - 10 little bottles of different brands of quality mascara, for a bargain of a price.  Or they'll do the same with lip glosses, or perfumes.  It's pretty cool to take home so many different versions of something.  One can compare and contrast!

This isn't just a women's store, either.  There are colognes and nice men's products.  I haven't perused it as extensively as the make-up, but Sephora sells high-end skin care and shampoos as well as hair irons and blow dryers.   

It's nice they have a points program, too.  Always good to have the opportunity to receive free stuff!
This place might have fresh fish but the flavor of the rice was horrible. The rice tasted soapy.

So excited to see chicken and waffles on the menu and I ordered via postmates.  I got 3 over fried hard dry chicken strips on top of fluffy waffle.  Waffle was cooked properly but had no flavor.  I've walked by this place several time and it was usually closed and when I looked at the menu it seemed overpriced (but it is Magazine St).
So I'm not sure if the was better before and they are just getting lazy and don't care because they know we have limited options now.  But I'll never order from them ever again and will not go there when everything reopens.
A chicken biscuit from McDonald's would've had more flavor and way cheaper.

So disappointed that I wasted precious money on this!
This store claims to close at 1:00. We arrived at 12:54 and the employees would not even recognize that we were there. Very poor. Don't waste your time as the employees do not care.
Small overpriced portions.  I had the pork nachos and it was average at best.  I was expecting better and was left hungry and a little disappointed.

I visited here to try a seared pork BBQ bahn mi. What a treat! The sandwich was a little dry, but otherwise tasty. I love that they have such a variety of drinks and snacks to choose from if you want more than just a sandwich.

Make sure to bring cash; they do not accept credit cards.

Sushi Sake absolutely blew our mind!! We are seasoned sushi eaters and have tried hundreds of sushi restaurants including high priced a la cart places  as well as boat served destinations.. we were so impressed by the quality and freshness of each imaginitive, well created dish.. Chef Khoun is on the front line delivering his own sophisticated rolls that range from the sweet to the spicy to the savory flavor of rolls you could only hope for in this high desert setting... absolutely amazing spot to get sushi...

Oh, dear goodness, I have become a Honeygrow addict.  How can I tell?  Well, I find myself arranging my day such that I'll 'just happen' to be nearby around lunch or dinner time.  I casually drop hints about them opening closer to me that may sound to the untrained ear like pleas or demands.  Also, I think "Is it too soon to go back?" far too often.  If you have to ask, they say, you are already done for.

It's not that the place has a super deep menu. They sell fresh stir fries, salads, one kind of smoothie, one fruit dessert. That's it.  The food is all very fresh and nothing they sell has high fructose corn syrup or ingredients that sound like chemistry class.  Ordering is at the counter via a touchscreen (think Wawa).  You pay by card right at the touchscreen or hand your cash to someone behind the counter.  Their workers are upbeat, nice, and nobody stands around.

It's the freshness, the combination of flavors, the way you can mix it up any way you like, it's just, well, yum!   The fact that you know it's yum and it's also not superbad for you is liberating, too.  

So, four stars because I am definitely a FAN.  They know what they're about, and they execute on that mission pretty darn well.

If I  could, I would give this location a  a big fat Zero for Customer Service.  I figured the last time I went was fluke maybe the representative was in a bad mood. Then last week two of my coworkers and I visited this location because it was close to Home-goods.  Upon entering this location I had my phone and order ready I walked to the counter where two representatives were helping the person in front of me. 
The representatives walked away one went to play on her cell phone and the other make the drink.  So I waited, another representative came from the back and took the person next to me order, whom had just walked in. Not once did the representative ask me if I had been helped .
Ok Tori, stay calm, the one that went to check her phone finally walked up and in her most annoyed tone asked me "How  can I help you"   I gave her my order and she advised me that what I wanted wasn't on the menu. Ok,  but can you make it .  She paused and said yes. She seemed annoyed that people were walking in. 
Starbucks are everywhere,  I will not be coming back to this one.
I really wanted to like this place. It smelled strangely and there were flies and gnats. Defending you veggies from protein is not right. Service was reasonable. Food was imaginative. Can't go back.

The place was moderately busy but for whatever reason our waiter was not attentive at bringing our drinks or even asking for the order. I waited about 10 minutes for him to come over and ask what we wanted. Then it was another 5 minutes to get the beers delivered. The waitstaff seemed disorganized and because the place has an awkward boxy design, it felt like they were constantly having to maze their way around tables. 

This may be because it was a cold night but it felt drafty inside and I noticed they had a space heater. Maybe a flaw in the design of the building or perhaps something was broken? This place has never been high on my list, nothing to really draw me in. So-so tap list, hit or miss service and a generic sports bar atmosphere. Plus, the cell service is terrible on this part of 100. Save your money; there are better bars elsewhere.
Dropped by and had the best customer service possible from Emmy. Nice place and great people.
The brick and mortar burger is one of the best burgers I've had. I love the pretzel bun. 4 stars only because of the limited soda options, only canned coke or sprite. No Mountain Dew!

Gotta wait 30-45 min for a restaurant full of empty tables? That's kind of a funny joke.

Never stepping a foot in this restaurant again.

Returned for the first time since moving out of the area.  I no longer am a regular or know any of the bartenders, so my review has changed a bit.  

Suddenly I realized how expensive the drinks are here, especially when you are paying for all of them.  I also realized that the food isnt as delicious when you aren't 5 free beers deep.  

One thing that I forgot about but which has always bugged me is that Mad Mex doesn't offer free chips and salsa.  Anyone who frequents mexican restaurants will probably be surprised by having to ask for, and then being charged for chips and salsa.  

You may also be surprised that at Mad Mex that they charge for sour creme and guac...so when you order a burrito and they ask you if you want sour creme and guac, be prepared for it to show up on your bill.  These little expenses really annoy me at restaurants, especially when you arent told you will be charged for them. 

Overall, it's probably a 3 star place, but I still give it 4 stars because I have good memories of hanging out here and they usually have a pretty good beer selection. 

Also please be aware that they typically ID most individuals entering the restaurant.  I think there are times when they don't but for the most part, if you want to eat dinner you have to be 21.  I've always thought this was strange and has actually prevented us from going on occasion, even when everyone was over 21 because if someone forgets their ID or doesnt bring it since they have no intention of drinking, they will still be turned away.
Had a good meal with friends and a GREAT Waitress - Alexa you were awesome! Thanks for such good service.
Great Mexican food! Get the burrito bowl for take out! Very generous portions. Also, their tostadas are awesome too. Love that it stays open late!

For appetizers, we had the grilled calamari and the ceviche. Both dishes were bland. The calamari had a sauce which was spicy. The ceviche had too much lemon/lime on it. 

We also had the seafood paella which was tasty. I would only come back for this. 

It took over an hour for us to get our food. (Thursday night)

Had a fairly large group with us, and had a chance to sample quite a bit.  Pizza surpassed my expectations, they use tons of cheese.  Ravioli and lasagna got good reviews from my group.  Pasta's that were not stuffed were just plain.  Not bad, just plain. Nice family atmosphere and reasonable prices.  Will be back to try a few more things out.  Have a feeling this place is trying to be all things, when they can probably carve out a nice niche with their strongest dishes.  Well worth a trip, if you haven't tried it yet.

Overpriced. Def not friendly even when you are looking to buy! If you are a woman, be prepared to be ignored at first then be ripped off. Thank goodness I didn't purchase from there! Go to another local dealer at least the salesman won't say "Well I am worth something aren't I?" when you try to baragain.

For a Tuesday night after an extended St Patricks Day weekend, I suppose it wasn't a bad crowd size. We had excellent and attentive service. TuesdY is half price wi e night so always a bonus. Good selection of craft beers. We opted to split the GOAT burger, named after Michael Jordan according to our server. At $14, you would expect the exceptional Abe while perfectly delicious, not out of the ordinary for our many burger joints! Hubs couldn't taste the special sauce. The truffle fries were great, and the pomme frites in duck fat were good but slightly greasy. A nice touch was the musician playing Buffett covers and the like to the small audience. We could return when the weather warms up as they have plentiful outdoor seating ant the ubiquitous garage doors opening onto said seating.

Cell upon my arrival I was extremely excited to try lost and found as I am a huge online shopper and have shopped this location many times online. When I walked in the store was everything I imagined it would be with the exception of its lackluster customer service. The sales clerk was extremely unhelpful and seemed rather annoyed that I was even in the shop. She offered a little to no assistance more along the lines of no assistance to aide me as I searched for items within her shop. She was extremely unpleasant and the entire time I was in the shop just watched me. Never offering assistance or if I needed help finding a size or even if I was looking for something specific, which I was. This was a very disappointing visit as the main reason for my visit to New Orleans was for this location specifically. Recommendation to the owner of this location hire people who are wanting to be there and assess guest as they come in to your establishment. Very disappointing as again I came to New Orleans specifically to visit the shop.

Nuvo Buritto is very, very trendy. I hope that despite its tren-di-ness it stays around. Mostly because of the utter lack of restaurants in this area that arent owned by the guy who owns all of the other restaurants ie; Battered and Fried, Wave Sushi, Beyond the Edge....etc. etc. 

I did enjoy my burrito - I had the "Gulf Coaster" at $9 and some change I was hoping it was good. Although it was  made with what I am guessing was frozen shrimp, the serving was outrageous, but that is honestly the only authentic thing about the burritos in general. These arent burritos in the traditional sense, hence the name Nuvo Burrito - very cute guys. 

The rice was cooked perfectly, as were the beans, the cheese was just enough, the sauces, etc. It did have a bit more spinach than I would have liked, but indeed removing a bit did the trick. This burrito was fun and different and I liked it. I could only eat half of it, but heating it up in the oven later, it tasted delicious again. They do go relatively easy on the seasonings, but I must say that is okay with me, the combination of veggies, beans, rice, shrimp and pesto gave it just enough without being salty and heavy. 

It is a bit pricey, but again the serving is huge and its much healthier than a traditional bean, rice and cheese cooked in lard while albeit fantastic! is not healthy. My Fiancee had the New Orleans style burrito and was equally impressed. My Daughter had a Chicken and Cheese and the serving size and combination of chicken and cheese was perfect for my foody 7 year old. 

We will definitely be back.
The new carwash at this remodeled Chevron is fabulous. My tires shine. Spend the $15 to get the best wash there.

I worked in the neighborhood and I decided to stop into this store after handling some business at the post office that is also located inside of the Pavilion. My partner and I are planning on getting engaged so I needed to have my finger measured in order for him to know what size I would need. Immediately upon entering the shop, 3 women with a small dog were very unwelcoming. I asked if I could have my finger sized and was promptly told, "No. Goodbye," which is perfectly fine because I knew immediately that I would not support a business with no only poor customer service but also with possible racial biases.

Las Cazuelas gives dignity to Mexican food.  The appetizers include the usual suspects, but the entrees really shine.  It's become our go-to restaurant for out of town guests.  Ambiance is warm and cute.  The only consistently mediocre thing is the service.  The servers have always been very sweet and accommodating, but they lack professionalism / experience.

I've visited Las Cazuelas three times, each time with my boyfriend and out of town guests.  Each time, our party was very happy with the food and pleasantly surprised, given preconceptions of Mexican food as well as the neighborhood.  (Neighborhood is fine, but that strip of Girard is not where you're expecting to find great food.)  On the first visit, our guests sniffed at the prices for Mexican food but ended the meal gushing about how good it was and planning to return.  (We've been trained that Mexican dishes over $10 must be overpriced.)

The entrees come to the table beautifully plated.  All eyes at the table light up!  Our favorites include the Chipotle shrimp, and the Chile Relleno.  My sister is a mole aficionado  and loved their mole sauce.  My "difficult" friend ordered the Arroz con Pollo, and loved it.  On my last visit, I ordered the Pork Chop which was so impressively composed on the plate with multiple sauces and so many complementing flavors.  And it passed my test of whether I could just as easily have made the dish at home.  The only mediocre dish we've seen was the beef tips - uninspired.  This isn't five star fancy food, but it is as good as most of the good byob's in Philly.  And it's inexpensive by comparison.  Bring a bottle of Tequila and they'll make you a decent Margarita pitcher for $6.  

Regarding the service, our worst visit was a mid-week visit.  They were out of the ingredients to mix Sangria with our bottle of Red Wine and they didn't have any specials.  The server explained that the chef hadn't been in that day!  We didn't need to hear that.  All three entrees came out lovely and delicious, but there was such an uneasy feeling as we waited, wondering if we shouldn't have come that night.

Been looking for a  barbque place since I moved here from Ga and this one won't be it.  I found this place thru restaurant.com.
1st -  The ribs were tough, the cornbread was very dry and cold, and the mac and chz is just velveeta mac and chz.  
2nd - The server I had was very disrespectful. When I asked for a different table she sat me at a high top w a toddler and the bartender came along and gave me a regular table.  Then I let her know about my certificate and she insisted she see it before she could take my order despite me telling her how much it was for and that I recently got it. No problem, right? Two other families had the same thing and she didn't ask either one for verification.  What's the big deal? My grandson and I didn't look like the other families.  
I planned on checking it out first before bringing my whole family of 8 but because of her I won't take the chance of my kids being mistreated.

This place is beyond terrible!!  From the moment you walk in and stand around trying to figure out who works there and who is a customer hanging at the bar.  Someone finally approached us and lead us to our table.  Our waitress was good, and that's were things end.  The chips were beyond stale and had absolutely no salt on them whatsoever.  I was confused by the menu also and what was on printouts on the table, seemed to be the same food but different prices?  It was never explained to us.  I ordered the shredded chicken tacos, and either they got the order wrong or they have no idea what shredded chicken is.  When the food arrived, it just look beyond horrible and it was.  Everything lacked flavor, from the the re-fried beans, to the rice, to the guacamole.  I could not even eat my meal and asked for a to go container just to be polite.  But what happens next is really the reason why I will never return, not even for a drink.  I had been in there years ago for drinks and quizno was underway and a question was asked that was beyond replusive about the recent death, at the time, of Steve Irvwin by a stingray.  They did not disappoint yet again last night when the quizno question was "How may people died in the pulse nightclub Orlando shouting?"  Really???  Is that an appropriate drinking game question to be shouting over the microphone in restaurant with diners??  I will never step foot inside this place again.

Our hotel had food catered in from this place. Amazing food! Mango habanero is just what you think it would be ...spicy!! The sweet chili barbecue is perfect! Just enough spice! Jambalaya was ok...not a huge hit but not bad either! The honey butter biscuits were...well put it this way 3 later I was still thinking about them! Chicken tenders are nice and big and thick! The deliver. Hit them up I do not think you will be sorry!!

We decided to get out for a walk since it was a beautiful start to MDW. The weather was perfect. My lady wanted an appetizer and outdoor seating. I'd seen this place in passing so we thought it would be perfect for her request. Atmosphere is 5 star, for casual patio dining piano and bass player. My wife had the jambalaya and couldn't Stomach the taste. I thought she was overreacting but after tasting I thought the same, after 3 scoops max we finished our sweet teas and tipped the band. I recommend changing the jambalaya recipe totally or replacing it on the menu. It was mushy with a bad aftertaste

Got in there at 745. Wait for 15 minute to order (5 people ahead of me) and 823 the order is ready. Staff is loud to each other and yell back and forth for communication and I heard they call each other bixch. eyeroll to customer.  No table is clean. The star is for the food, better than the McDonald in kingshighway and Jefferson .

It's fast!
It's inexpensive!
It works well!
It has free vacuums!
There people who actually work there! And they're nice!
Ive never felt creeped out while cleaning my car there!

Quite honestly this store feels cluttered and messy to me. That said they do carry some pretty awesome products on the whole, but I find it difficult to shop here in part because it just feels so cluttered and this overwhelms me a bit. Every little bit of this store is filled with things for sale, which makes it a good place to look for specific gizmos and gadgets that I'm looking for but sometimes the features are hard to compare and many times features are excessive. 

I'm not going to say that I won't frequent this location, but that my hopes aren't terribly high for an experience not frustrated by the sheer volume of things to browse through before finding anything of use/interest.

This place is not worth the trouble.  I went today and tried to get take-out but it was just more than I cared to deal with.

So I went in, and asked to order take-out, I was told to go to the bar.  Maybe that's normal, but I don't like bars, so I was uncomfortable there just to begin with.  I then asked about what style pizza it was, and the girl gave no real answer.  She didn't know, and I don't know if it is really a style of pizza.  I like classifying things, so I can properly describe them, and compare with similar types.

So I order the a 8 incher with one topping.  I estimated what it would come to, and but the price they rang up was about 15% more.  I protested that that couldn't be right.  It's not possible.  The woman at the bar, accepted my argument, but had to track down the manager to get the price right.

They did fix the price, and they did give me a free soda while I waited, which was nice, but I was irritated at the whole issue.

The pizza came soon enough, but it wasn't outstanding.  It was edible, and the sausage was fine, but there wasn't much cheese or sauce.  Sorry, but I'd much rather stick with a good New York style pie.

The ambiance is lovely, but that's it. The food is mediocre. Grilled broccoli with garlic is clearly frozen microwaved broccoli. The shrimp was over cooked. The fried rice was premade packaged. It wasn't disgusting, but wasn't worth $30 per person.

My doctor has been very professional & helpful.  I don't have concerns there and perhaps that's most important..but, the mistakes that the staff makes could be as important. They gave me info regarding my procedure/prep that was inaccurate causing me to go off of my normal medications for 6 days unnecessarily (therefore causing me to go through days of being very uncomfortable for no reason.) Ironic that their mission is to deal with pain. The main office seems to be pretty together but dealing with the local office was horrible. Each time I called, they called back with the wrong information, info that had nothing to do with my original question. It wasn't just once, many times, regarding my prescription. They're moving currently to a new facility, I want to offer some understanding for that but I just don't get the attitude when they were the ones who messed up. When I brought these things up I got a blank look like it was me. I was inches from going elsewhere but I did have my treatment and it seems to be working so I'll stick with a good Dr. and hope things get better on the admin portion.
A great place to go if you hate the people you're with because after your eardrums burst, 15 seconds after you walk in the place, you'll never hear them.

-2nd time eating here
-lunch on Monday with friends in town from DC.
-falafal sandwich/cauliflower hummus/lamb kebob and a few other assortments.
-delicious taste for all of the dishes
-environment was a bit stuffy for New Orleans.  1st time I ate there it had much more relaxed vibe.  I was dressed nice and the hostess did not want to let me go pass the hostess stand.  It was as if I was going to take the restaurant hostage- doing what.. I don't know.
-would return for drinks and food at bar.  Had a bartender who had fantastic service and was very professional.  I don't know her name but she had glasses and dark, long hair.
This place sucks ass!! They make you wait forever to order and are over priced! Starbucks is a billion times better! This shit hole needs to be out of business.

There for the first time & enjoyed everything I ordered. Food was served hot & fresh, with great flavor. Service was good & so were the price points. ........ Looking forward to going back. The only down side is, they either remove the window curtains or clean them.

I tried to go here with my parents on a weekday night. We all enjoy a good cocktail and it seemed like a great pick. We walked in and a man standing at the door told us that they were above capacity and could not seat us.  Peering past his shoulder into the bar we could tel that there were clearly some seats available.  We inquired about those and the host muttered something about the bartenders not having enough time to make the drinks.  At best these people are incompetent and unable to keep up with the demand of the patrons of the small establishment and at worst they are a bunch of ageist snobs who deem themselves too hip to serve a group of folks from the midwest.  We left in disgust, likely never to return.

TACO TUESDAY REVIEW: uh...owners..heads up!  Please take off your menu. The WORST tacos ever. I ordered one chicken and one beef. 
COLD flour tortillas, BBQ tasting chicken, a tiny bit of bagged shredded lettuce, a sprinkling of red onion and tomato, along with a squirt of sour cream. The beef? Odd metallic tasting hamburger meat along with the pathetic lettuce etc... I had to ask for salsa. They were AWFUL, and I returned them..
Also ordered fried mushrooms.. a huge pile of fried , (mostly stems and pieces) very dark fry..meaning the oil needs to be changed!
The best thing about my lunch was the chardonnay..only one is the house brand..DONT know what vineyard..a $4 decent pour. At least it was the RIGHT temp.,..cold.
Stay away from the TACOS! Not worth 25 cents! Don't have to be a rocket scientist to check what is leaving the kitchen!
Service was good, considering there was only one server at 4:30.

Honestly, I was so excited to eat here with my girlfriend for our anniversary but we were both super disappointed. As a Brennens restaurant I expected way more out of the food and STAFF. To start off the crab claws were terrible, served with stale bread. I ordered the shrimp and grits which was a 5/10 and my girlfriend got the filet which was way undercooked so she asked for it to be cooked longer and our server just says "okay" and never apologized. Our server was slow and barely came to the table to refill our drinks throughout the 3 HOURS of dining. The ONLY thing we enjoyed was the creme brûlée which I called the restaurant to ask if they could write "Happy Anniversary" and they told me yes! When it came to the table it didn't say anything which really aggravated me. I asked about it and I was just told that they didn't do it bc of COVID. I'm upset the experience wasn't good bc I excepted so much more.

Always had an good experience until now. I paid $180 for an contact exam, received a trial that I was unable to wear. Follows up but was unable to because I no longer had them. They wanted to charge me another $180 for another eye exam to get another trial. Customer service got ugly with me as I tried to explain myself.

Been here many time, the place is small, waitress doesn't speak much English, but their signature dish is really the best in town!!! The broth is light and flavorful, beef slices are think and tender. Ice coffee is also a must try, can't wait to go back again!

A group of (6) went to visit this establishment on June 1st right after their Saturday Brunch rush around 3:30pm. The restaurant was very slow and there was around 4 servers not doing much. 

We asked to be sat outside in a booth. The host was hesitant to sit us because we just wanted to drink rather than eat. Most likely we would have ordered food anyways. 

As we were sat the host told us our server will be right with us. Mind you a handful kept passing by. After 10 minutes of waiting we went to the bartender and asked for a server. She said "you have to ask your host". First, rude. Second, we were sat by the host. 

After waiting another 15 minutes we decided to leave. From the moment we walked in we felt unwelcome. We should have known since the host was very hesitant to sit us since we weren't eating. 

Prior, 4 of our group members had already gone to brunch their in the morning, left and then came back when it was slower to be able to sit outside when it slowed down. 

As we left the host (not the one that originally greeted us) was apologetic, however the bouncer was defensive and asked "well were you seated by a host or did you just sit." The funny thing was he was there when we were sat by the host. 

Overall bad service, and if you're not a college kid you seem to be dismissed. Which is very sad since our group is well in our 30s, established business professional, and doctors, who would probably spend way more then the average college student that attends this location during busy hours. 

To management: you never know who's visiting your establishment, treat everyone as you'd like to be treated.

I got the fish and chips and my co-workers got the burgers and fries and then we finished off with the apple crumble dessert (Topped with ice cream of course)

Fish and Chips
- Fish was ok, the sauces were interesting and definitely a twist to your regular tar tar so i'd give it a 3.5 out of 5
- Chips - was actually sweet potato fries - the co-workers rated it best they've had but i would say i prefer Lucas Park Grille's - so 3.5 out of 5 fo rme

Burgers- coming from folks who've travelled around quite a bit to say that this is good burger - This is Good burger. 
The fries that went with the burger - i'd rate them as one of the best regular potato fries i've had so far - and i've had a fair share of those. 

Last but not least, and drum roll please - Apply crumble with ice cream - out of this world! 6 out of 5. The apple crumble was very well flavoured - not too sweet, the crust was cruchny and the inside was melty, topped with the vanilla/caramel ice cream just made it taste absolutely heavenly. 

I must say St Louis, i am very impressed with a lot of the Desserts! 

overall, i am a fan and will definitely go back here for lunch!

This location is likely super convenient for folks working in/near Cortex. For my visit, parking was a struggle. There was one spot but marked as 15 minute max so I didn't use it. After finding a spot I walked in. 

The interior has abundant light which is nice. The two baristas were friendly. I order a cafe au lait but requested a coffee mug to stay. When my order was ready, it was prepared to go and I had to ask for a mug. The barista said he would remake but I told him it was totally fine to pour it into the mug instead (no need to waste).

The WiFi is good and requires a password that they give you on a piece of paper (nice touch). There's a nice selection of pastries but I didn't get one. My coffee was alright and the atmosphere is nice. Plenty of tables to sit at and get some work done but there's no real "chill" spot inside. There's a cool chair setup but it doesn't look comfortable for drinking coffee and checking Instagram (that's what everyone does now, right)?

Overall, I'd come back and try this place again.
Went to the farmer's market  for some fresh local produce -- NOT.  Might have well just gone to Megamart & saved the hour long drive.

This place is horrible! I've had problems with the lenses and frames the past several months and have had to go back there several times to have them fixed. I was assured by the People there it was the glass company. They also are closed a lot of the  time. I can't tell you how many times I've gone there and they are closed for the day or early or in the middle of the day. So unprofessional. 

But today my husband who bought his glasses 6 months ago from this place brought them back to drop them off because he was on military leave for the weekend. They were falling apart ...the screws on the hinges that hold the temples on the frames kept coming out and they fell off his face and hit the pavement and got scratched. So when we walked in we explained to the new lady behind the counter that my husband was on leave and we wanted to leave them here and he wouldn't be taking them with him ( he was being sent over seas until May of next year and had been gone since August and this was his first leave home since August) so now that that was explained to her she automatically said she didn't think the lenses would be replaced and frames fixed. So I said to her that we just got them and it should be covered under warranty and i also explained that my glasses got scratched up within the year I had them and as long as it was in the one year I got brand new lenses. That's how our insurance works. So she then said that that's not how it works and the insurance company and lense makers will try and not replace them. So I asked her to please look up his information. So she proceeded to give us more excuses. 

So my husband who had to get ready to leave to go back to his base within the hour said to her "ma'am I really don't have time for this if you just look up the policy and my information and see that it should be covered and I'll leave them here. Don't tell me what they are going to say or do or not do ....just send them back". We were so irritated that we weren't being accommodated .....they were trying to not do any leg work to send them back and trying to find any excuse to not send them back. That's how it appeared. No if, ands or buts about it. 

Well as soon as the owner Dr. Savochka heard the lady behind the counter get annoyed with my husband not wanting a run a round and rightfully frustrated. He came over and reprimanded my husband like a child. Told him that irritating talk or raising of voices were not allowed in his store. I was like WTH?! Well that pissed us off even more. He said this is my store and my business and He won't tolerate us getting upset or angry with him or his workers. So my husband said that well sir that's not acceptable. I bought a pair of glasses from you six months ago and they are falling apart. I shouldn't be hearing excuses from you or your workers. And he also told him that his attitude was not acceptable as a business owner. Which was absolutely true. So then Dr. Savochka told my husband to leave the store or he was going to call the police. Lol Dr. S was rude and so disrespectful I couldn't believe what I was hearing  and seeing. My husband was upset and pissed off but he didn't threaten the guy, didn't touch him or even say he was or even do anything that was remotely threatening to him or his store. I was like is this guy for real?  

 So I told my husband to wait outside. This guy wasn't worth him being upset. And that I would handle it. So the doctor and I had some words and at one point he threatened to call the cops on me. I said give me a break. I said to him I can't believe you treat military service members like that and calling the cops over something like this especially on an active duty military person was ridiculous and he said so what and who cares. So this guy has zero respect for military people. I then calmly asked him a question and  guess what? He yelled at me. So i said to him let me get this straight ....you can yell and raise your voice but I can't as the customer? He said nothing. I asked him 3 more times and he didn't answer ...So I then called him a hypocrite for yelling and he said sarcastically "you're right. " I know he didn't mean it. 

I will never ever go there again to purchases glasses. And always remember those who seem so nice and accommodating ....you're not always seeing who they really are all the time. I truly saw Dr. Savochka's true colors  today and they were down right awful. I cannot stress enough that if there's a problem and you are not happy with the service or product you purchase here.... These people will give you attitude, excuses and God forbid you get upset or angry. He will call the cops on you. Totally pathetic. I give their business another year of that.

Square One Brewery is the place to be on Tuesday nights. I'm sure that many people write about Square One because of their beer... but I'm here to tell you that on Tuesday nights you should visit Square One for the burgers. 

On Tuesday nights, Square One has a build your own burger bar that you do not want to miss.

Truly, the Build-A-Burger special at Square One had great potential to be a bust. I mean, what kind of $2 burger actually tastes good? [Not Bar Louie's, that's for sure.] But, Square One knows what they are doing; probably because they didn't sacrifice quality just because the meal was cheap. 

After sitting down, we were given a strip of paper that listed all of the possible toppings for our burger. And when I say list, I don't mean some kind of puny list that says "cheese, lettuce, tomato." No, this list was formidable and unique, featuring almost 50 options, from goat cheese, to roasted red peppers, to hummus.  

After selecting your base (either beef, veggie, or game [tonight's was boar]), you are able to put any combination of toppings on your burger. Each topping costs from $0.25 to $1.50 extra--which is nice because you can make up a gourmet burger for a little extra cha-ching or you can go classic and get a burger with cheese and lettuce for barely anything!

One other cool thing was that you could sign your name on the back, leave your email address, and name your burger--then, if your burger is the best tasting burger of the week, it will be the special burger next week!  [In case you were wondering, yes I named my burger and yes I am hoping it will win next week's honors!]

So, what did I put on my burger? Well, I went a little crazy with it. I started with a beef burger, added Dubliner cheese, oven roasted tomatoes, roasted corn salsa, mixed greens, jalapeños, and chipotle mayo. I named it the Fiestalicious Burger (at my friend Sarah's suggestion) and boy oh boy was it good! 

No, I'm not bragging about my ability to accessorize my burger; I am saying that the burger itself was good. Tender, juicy, and well flavored--I was impressed! [It was the complete opposite of the Bar Louie charcoal burger that I had for their Tuesday night special!] 

Unfortunately, I don't think that it will win any major awards because it was a little too spicy for the normal person's tastes. But, the toasty buns, high quality and well cooked meat, and amazing French fries will definitely bring me back for more [that and my new quest to win the burger of the week prize].  

PLUS, the whole meal (a soda, a burger with 6 toppings, and a half order of fries) was only $8 bucks. I'm sorry, but you pay that much or more at McDonald's these days--and those burgers taste like freezer burn. 

All in all, I am greatly looking forward to trying my hand at round two of burger building (probably next Tuesday--since we decided this should be a weekly tradition). Happy eating!

Ate dinner here with a few friends last week and everyone enjoyed their meals.  The Pad Thai isn't the 'normal' Pad Thai you'd find at other Thai restaurants but the Smiling Elephant's take on the dish is still great!  Also had one of the flavored teas and it was very good with the meal.

The food was excellent but our service was horrible.  Other tables appeared to be receiving good service  so I think we just had a really bad waitress or she was having a bad day.

The best grits we have ever had.  Husband loved his fried green tomato wrap.  I had shrimp and grits and a biscuit.  The shrimp and grits were very good but one would think with "jam" in their name would include some jam and butter with the biscuit. Again I think this might be because of our bad waitress -that we never saw again after placing our order.

Giving 4 stars because I don't don't  think one bad apple should spoil the entire bushel.

I've tried some produce and it's always nice and fresh, especially the corn. I appreciate that they're open a bit later so I'm not running to target if I forgot an onion for dinner! Cute little gifts as well.

I was a loyal customer at this location since 2003.Last month and for reasons beyond my control I was not going to be able to make my monthly payment as scheduled.I called the 1 800 number for Customer Service and they asked me to call the Regional Director for Pinellas County and request an extension. I called Mr. David Friday but he never got back to me. My belongings were auctioned. That included some of great sentimental value such as: coin collection that I started with my late grandparents back in 1968 and photo albums from trips to 40+ countries. Do yourselves a favor and stay away from Public Storage. Property Managers are not empowered to make decisions and top management could not care less(did not take in consideration the $9,000.00+ that I paid in storage fees all these years). They only see the $$$ across our faces.

This place has amazing food and amazing service. It is not your comfort food breakfast joint though. Their menu is definitely for the foodies among us, but I do love their plane Jane pancakes and a side of eggs. The only downside is how loud it is and the wait... BUT... you can get on the waitlist here on yelp and go over when it's almost your turn.

Just stay away.

I only had Pets Best Insurance for a few weeks. I learned my lesson quick. I purchased the "wellness" option as I knew my puppy would need some routine vaccinations and this would reimburse at least part of this cost. My claim was denied as his vet appointment was 12 days after my policy started and I apparently needed to wait until 14 days until I could use it. The literature I got after I signed up listed that I had to wait 14 days for "illness" claims, but this was not an illness - just a routine office visit with vaccinations. Even though it was only a two day difference, they would not budge and wouldn't cover it. I basically paid for insurance I wasn't allowed to use.

The only thing good they did was refund my money. Though I would have saved more if they covered these vaccinations. 

I can't believe how unclear their policies are and how strictly they are enforced. I now have another pet insurance company for my policy and have so far been DELIGHTED with everything they've done.

Give your business to someone with a heart and cares about your dog - these people just see the dollar signs. No thank you.

December 2018 
We had a wonderful time on the Creole Queen! We went on the morning historical river cruise. We considered doing the jazz cruise but we were definitely happy we decided on the historical cruise.
The historian/tour guide (Charles I think) was absolutely amazing! He made the cruise. He was so entertaining and engaging and taught us so much about the origins of the city up to Katrina. 
The food was great and all of the staff members I interacted with were friendly. 
Definitely recommend!
All in all, pretty good. Very reasonable prices ($8 for pho), fine atmosphere, and food was pretty good. A bit disappointed that they didnt have chicken pho as an option, but I'd go back.

Check your bags before pulling out of the parking lot.   Recently multiple orders have been missing an item, and it's super frustrating.   Tonight's debacle  has us swearing off this location.     When 2 out of 3 of your children's kids meals are missing the main component (i.e.  a cheeseburger and nuggets) that is beyond ridiculous.

This is great food. The owners are very nice and the Gravy (sauce) is to die for! Whatever you order will be amazing trust me. You can tell they take there cooking from scratch seriously when they have Herbs growing around the restaurant. 
Big Portions, like Mama use to give

New Years Eve event:   We purchased tickets for the Don CeSar New Years Eve event approx 6 wks ago.  We received weekly reminders about the event including info about parking and printing tickets in advance.   We arrived at 8:30 and the staff that met us had no idea where our event was.   Not a good start.   Finally found someone who walked us to the pool area and registration table.  After waiting for the group of four staff to figure out how to work the computer we handed our tickets to the group.  No one knew anything about anyone paying in advance.  Our tickets were supposed to be VIP tickets.  Finally convinced them that we had already paid.  When we finally got in we found seats then we told that we were in the wrong seats - twice.  Moving us to the back corner.  We could barely hear the DJ.  All the other seats cost even more.  We were promised appetizers and drinks.  In 4 hours 3 people came by w fried appetizers.  For $125 per person the Don CeSar did a very poor job of executing the event.  The hotel was beautiful though, hence the 2 stars.   The bartenders were great.  They did some decent entertainment

Not sure what's going on these days, but I've been getting super unlucky with food orders from my usual favorites, most recently this one. 

My 10-piece buffalo wings looked extremely sad. First of all, they looked and tasted old (as if reused from someone else's order), but they were also tiny and chewy, as if they came from unhappy scrawny underfed teenage chickens. I almost felt bad eating them, but was mostly annoyed that I did not get my money's worth ($8.50+). 

The greek salad was the same size as usual, but the level of freshness wasn't. The tomato and lettuce pieces had brown spots all over them. I also ordered a cheeseburger, and found that although the quality was roughly the same as usual, the beef patties were super greasy and the "cheddar" that came with it was more like waxy orange American cheese than cheddar. 

The lamb kefta wrap was probably the highlight of the lot, but it was a bit small and also super oily. It was hard to tell whether the filling was beef or lamb. For $5.99, it's not super expensive, but it's also not super substantial, with only ground meat, a few strands of red onions, and some parsley. 

The weirdest thing about this order wasn't even the food, though. The delivery guy parked outside of my building and called me, saying I needed to go outside to the car and get it from him. In the past, Zuzu's Kitchen has typically delivered to my apartment, which is why I've always included a generous tip on top of the existing delivery fee. But the delivery guy this time even made a fuss about dropping off my food at the security desk in my building, and the whole point of me ordering delivery was because I was experiencing some leg muscle aches, making going outside a bit tough for me. 

Needless to say, there are too many good places for food in the city to pay extra fees to get greasy food of declining quality not get delivered to one's home. It's sad because I used to really like this place.

Had a great experience at the training course. They are really helpful and knowledgeable about guns and safety tips. I would definitely recommend this class to any new or even old gun owners that need to refresh on their skills.

Forgot two white rices from both of my entrees. All chicken wings were over cooked and dry. General tsos chicken was almost rubbery. Only ordered from here because Jade Gardens delivery guy went home early day before Thanksgiving. Wish I had just went and picked up from them instead.

We have been seeing Dr. Jeff Peppard for over 20 years.  He was so good my 3 year old son came out with a smile.  I worked in the dental profession for many, many yhears and he is so professional and goes over and above in the hygiene of his office.l  Every is sterile and immaculate.  I live in the Ventura county and the dentists there, even with my PPO dental insurance, were so bad, I had to come back to Dr. Peppard.  He is the BEST. FRANK is
the man.

I only Give this place the 3 star rating because of its competitors in the metro area.  My experience dining here was pleasant, and the food was decent.  but they did not step up to the plate compared to Chima, located downtown.   If you are not in for a trip to center city, then this is your place. To dine.  The prices are Brazilian Steakhouse appropriate, and the set up is identical to other Brazilian Houses.  overall, it was a fine night dining here. the salad bar is big, lots of choices, and clean.  The meat is cooked to perfection, with slightly less choices then competitors. The beverages are provided by the Attached ale house, which is a mega plus, because they offer local and international brews.

I've been seeing Dr. Gottlieb here for over a decade. Then I decided to switch to a dermatologist that was closer to my work. Then I moved 15 minutes away for SDG and made an appointment to see Dr. Gottlieb again.  I probably hadn't been in this office in about 6 years and not since he joined with Schweiger Dermatology Group. 

This was prior to the COVID restrictions being lifted and keep in mind the majority of this practice seems to be elderly people. For COVID, they installed iPads as a patient self check-in; you can imagine who that went over. And it was an EXTENSIVE questionnaire/process. No one was there to help. The people behind the desk just ignored the whole sh#t show, which is exactly what that part of the visit was. 

I was called back in short order and saw the doc to have some skin tags removed (which is an out of pocket procedure). And the place had gone down hill hard while I was away. The vinyl faux wood floor was beat and missing in places and the back office in general just didn't seem up-to-date or clean. The condition of things was . . . beat and dated. I wouldn't say  unsanitary they simply need to invest in the business and renovate. 

The 2 star rating is because the check-in was just SO bad. 1/2 the skin tags came back (which was a first for me). The needs to be updated. And their appointment cards are a photocopy sheet cut to the size of business cards. Given what I had just paid, I think they could afford to buy some proper cards. 

All in all - it wasn't the same and when I needed more tags addressed, I drove to the other place I'd been going to. SDG 15 minutes from my house. The other? Over 30. The fact I'd make that drive kinda says it all.

On Thursday, October 31, 2019, my daughter, had a 9:30am appointment in Dr. Sam Kadan's Doylestown office to fix a broken bracket. 

We arrived at the Doylestown office shortly before 9:30am. We checked in at the computer kiosk and took a seat in the waiting room. The staff was joyously adjusting their Halloween costumes and talking. We were not acknowledged by anyone.

At about 9:50, I approached the front desk and asked if we would be seen soon as my daughter needed to get back to school. I was dismissed and told that they "got us in" so we would have to wait. I was obviously annoyed by this and phoned my husband to verify the appointment time and if we were being "fit in" or if we had an actual appointment. He stated that when he made the appointment that it was a confirmed time. I expressed my displeasure for the situation to my husband during my phone call.

At 10:00am, Dr. Kadan came out to the waiting room and called us back. He was rather gruff and did not give us much directional guidance as to which way to go, considering that this was our first time in the Doylestown office. Once we entered the "arena-like" room with the dental chairs he stopped and started to berate me in front of my daughter and his entire staff.

He stated that he was offended by my conversation with my husband and I replied that I was offended by the way I was being treated by the office. I said I just wanted my daughter's braces fixed. He replied "No and said he would fix them when he decides, not me". He continued with his rant and I was given no chance but to leave as he was upsetting my daughter who already suffers from an anxiety disorder. I told him we were leaving and he replied, "Good, leave". By the time we reached the car, my daughter was crying hysterically and could not go to school the rest of the day.

Not only did Dr. Kadan care more about his ego than the welfare of my daughter and the discomfort resulting from the broken bracket, but no one from his office called to check on her or remedy the situation. My husband left 4 messages (one at each office) on Friday, November 1st for Dr. Kadan or an office manager. As of today, we have not received any return call.

One of the best restaurants in Bryn Mawr. The food is always good, as is the service. When inside dining is allowed again you will see a beautiful, unique interior. Even the outside tent is well done. Be sure to meet Steve, the manager (owner?) - he's super nice and will most likely remember you if you eat there again!

I usually don't give negative reviews, we walked by and decided to give them a try. The menu had some good options so we waited for someone to greet us, we stood there for a bit until a customer who was at the register with cashier asked us if we needed help. The young lady brought us the menu and told us somebody would be with us in a minute so We waited and waited....... until We actually just got up and left. No customer service what so ever. Hostess seems like she doesn't really enjoy her job.  Menu's where filthy, so glad we walked out.
Fantastic little breakfast place. We go there every time in Nola. Great atmosphere. Eggs bacon potatoes and bagettes taste great. Friendly customer service.

Seriously authentic. If you can, stop here. Every time I'm in Tucson I pick up a bag of the Anita's chips and red salsa, and a carne asada burro... Unless it's Tuesday when it's .99cent taco day. Some of the best red tamales in Tucson, and their tortillas can't be  beat. Support Anita's.

Was forced to stay here on government travel. The shuttle & the fresh salad at the in side store were the best part. The hotel is very dated versus the pictures on the web. My room was falling apart, layers of the cabinet in the bathroom were peeling, mold in the shower etc. They did not restock the in room coffee after the first day. The room was load & the neighbor did not turn off their tv until 2 AM. The bed squeaked every time you moved.Hotel not close to the convention center or many restaurants when the temp is 95 & 80% humidity. Will not stay here unless forced to. The hotel staff was pleasant.

I've wanted to try the Columbia for years but just never got around to it until recently.  It was my 38th birthday and my significant other and I decided to go there with my mother and grandmother who were visiting from Connecticut.  We had reservations and after a few minutes of confusion at the hostess stand (maybe they were having a shift change or something but everyone seemed very confused about what they were doing) we were taken to our table.  We were sat at a table basically located between the bar and the server bus station.  Since there were tons of open tables all over I was not sure why they put us at this table considering that the bar was very busy and people had to continuously squeeze behind my chair due to the bar being so crowded.  Anyway, the decor was nice and I appreciated the historic aspect of it.  There was a cool painting next to our table that said it was done using the Columbia's coffee instead of paint...very neat.  

The waiter came by pretty quickly and took our drink order.  I enjoyed their one draft beer, Estrella (from Spain).  We were asked if we wanted bread and we said yes so that was soon delivered.  That's when I noticed a card next to my bread plate...I opened it and it was a birthday card with the manager's business card inside.  I thought that was a nice touch...little did I know it would come in handy later on.  The menu was quite large but we had checked it out online beforehand so we were pretty much ready to order.  I had the combo plate with boliche (beef stuffed with chorizo), an empanada, and roast pork with rice and plantains.  First off, I lived in Miami for 3 years and know good Cuban food.  This was not good Cuban food.  The roast pork was the best thing on the plate but the empanada was super greasy...like it had been fried an hour ago and left on a plate with no paper towels for draining.  The boliche was just not good...didn't care for it at all.  The entire meal was super salty...and I love salt...so for me to say that means it was barely edible to most people.  Unfortunately that salty theme continued with my partner and mother having the stuffed shrimp.  They both agreed it was very salty and tasted frozen, dry, and overcooked.  My grandmother seemed to enjoy her stuffed grouper so I guess that was not as bad as the rest of our meals.  I felt so bad because I know we were all so excited to eat at the Columbia, and it was my idea, and the food was just terrible.  It's rough when you pay $25+ for a plate of food that you barely eat.  Ok so we decided to take our chances and get dessert.  I had the key lime pie...I'm not a big dessert guy but this is one thing I love.  I was really surprised when it came with no whipped cream on top.  I thought that was pretty standard and despite the pie tasting pretty good it was screaming for that whipped cream because it was so incredibly sour.  I didn't even eat it all and nobody else wanted it after having a bite ( I was trying to pawn it off).  The other desserts we got were the bread pudding which was good, the guava cheesecake which I thought was just ok, and the flan which was good.

Like I mentioned earlier, the bar area was very busy (it was a Tuesday around 5:30pm) so I don't recommend sitting near it if you don't have to.  I would've asked to move but they were practicing what I assume was flamenco dancing in the other dining room and it was so loud we could barely hear each other talk (and we weren't even in there!).  The waiter was like a robot, and not like Data from Star Trek who at least had a personality.  He was pretty efficient but seemed like he would rather have been anywhere but there.  I would think that knowing the bill was going to be pushing $200 he would have been trying to earn the best tip he could by at least being somewhat friendly.  Nope.  

Finally, while driving the hour back to my house we all started chatting about how we felt about the meal.  At the table I think we were all so surprised at how bad it was that we were at a loss for words.  Since it was supposed to be a birthday celebration dinner we just focused on family time rather than the food at that point.  But after saying how we really felt on the ride home my partner decided to use the manager's business card to email her and let her know about our experience.  After not hearing anything for about a week we assumed that was that.  But then we got a letter of apology in the mail along with a $175 gift card to the restaurant.  While I think that is a pretty nice gesture, and the reason for me bumping the rating from one star to two, I just wish the food had been better.  Quite frankly I don't even want to go back!  Perhaps I will try their Clearwater location instead...or I will just throw the gift card in a drawer and forget about it.  Who knows.

On any other day I would've given this place 5 stars because I am a regular and the food is delicious so this review is solely on my experience tonight. I placed my order over the phone per usual and they said it would take 30-45 mins for my food to arrive. It took about an hour and I live less than 5 mins from the location. When I received my food my fries were soggy and my sandwich was cold and my chocolate cake was practically melted. This never happens when I order from here my food is usually super fresh and arrives in a timely manner. I called the store to express my disappointment and to get the situation rectified. Danny proceeds to tell me that "no matter where I get my fries from I will always receive them cold or soggy". I told him that I beg to differ because I order food from them once maybe twice a week and my fries are always hot and crispy. He put me on hold several times during our phone call which only led to me becoming more frustrated because there was an easy solution to my problem and Danny made it seem like it was impossible to do. He finally agreed to remake my food but I still had to wait another 30-45 mins which I think is completely unacceptable. I hope in the future they will train their employees on proper phone etiquette and how to rectify situations such as this so that regular customers such as my self can continue to support the business.

This is an inexpensive, run of the mill, neighborhood bar. I walked here after reading all of the hype on Yelp and hearing from locals that it's a great place.

What did I miss?

Sure, 15 beers on draft is great, but when your best beer is Alaskan IPA, you're not setting the bar very high. In their defense, most of the patrons here were ordering tall cans of High Life and PBR. They had a great happy hour of $4 u-call-it's so I had a Belvedere rocks and took off.

While I was there, I glanced at the menu, consisting of fried foods, checked out the beer pong tables, and watched some guys play ping pong. OK.... give me something unique to prove that you're a good bar like everyone says. 

Overall, it's an ok place to drink if you live in the neighborhood, but this is by no means a great bar. The amateurs drinking here need to see a good bar.

I was excited to try poutine in Philly, but ended up disappointed. This review is for the poutine, which was the only item I ordered here. It was a very large box of fries (I shared with a friend. I initially wanted just a side order but they don't do any smaller than this and it was a full on meal on its own). The fries weren't heated all the way through and the gravy was a little too salty. The cheese curds were just fine, not melted even a little, didn't add much to the experience. The order was around $8 and I just wasn't satisfied with it. 

That being said, this stall is not known mainly for its poutine and the servers were very friendly. Next time I stop by Reading Terminal, I'll be sure to try a corn dog here.

Definitely a college bar/restaurant on the main University Drive.  More set up in a bar format with some limited outdoor seating.  Open late for food and drinks.  The music and people can be quite loud.  A young and casual crowd, it was kind of late and we just were getting ToGo food.  The young, trendy dressed staff seemed positive and outgoing, friendly and helpful, also there was not much of a crowd as it was late Sunday evening. 

Ordering at the bar and they offered a water while I waited.  The Las Vegas Roll was very thick and perfect.  The Philly Roll was thin and long, and again well done.  Miso was full of Tofu, which was nice.

I'm not into loud areas much, but I can see that on weekends the place probably rocks and can be busy.  But when we went it was no issues and a great midnight food spot.  And the alcohol situation looks plentiful.  I will probably bump my star rating up one more if it works out on a future visit.

This is the worst fastfood restaurant I have ever been to. They have messed up the last three orders so badly that it nearly boggles the mind that somthing this simple as a burger could be botched.

I could understand if there was a line out the door and they were understaffed maybe being slow or getting somthing wrong.  But everytime I'm here it's dead and they STILL cant get it right!

 This is why the minimum wage needs to be abolished. These fools need to be fighting ditches by hand.

We visited on Thursday, May 13, arriving at 8:45. There was brisk business at the bar and two tables of diners in the restaurant. It took over 10 minutes for a hostess to seat us.

After, service was incredibly, incredibly slow. We received our meal after 10 pm, which is what time the kitchen closed. The problem is my steak was well done and very dry. I ordered a medium (the waitress bragged their steaks don't need steak sauce, but this one was inedible) but got crispy. The mashed potatoes were also dry. My daughter's cheeseburger came out plain. No condiments, not lettuce, no tomato, nothing.

The problem is that when the server brought us our food at about 10 minutes after ten, that was it. She didn't come back to the table to check on us. Her return was to drop off the check. No opportunity to ask about the overly dry and stiff steak. No chance to ask for condiments or anything for the burger. Nothing. 

Terrible food and terribly slow service for an $86 ticket.

Best part? Their website has no method for complaining about a meal.
