I sent in an inquiry through their website and received an automated response that they would get back to me within 24 hours on 8/22.  It's 9/5 and I never heard back.  It's okay if my request wasn't something they could accommodate but no follow-up at all is kind of unprofessional.  Fortunately, this wasn't urgent.

The drinks are a bit expensive but delicious. I had the Tiger Lily & Pineapple Express. Both were great. It's fun sitting at the bar and watching the bartender make the drinks.

This bar is located in a basement. Very different from other bars in I've been to in Indy. Nice & relaxed. Went midweek so it wasn't crowded.

I used to love coming to this place after work for a beer. Decided to stop by on Thursday around 6 PM. Honestly didn't feel the vibe I did in the past. Maybe because I paid $7.15 + tip for a beer. Maybe I got tired of a decor. I think it's been the same since 2007 when I first came here with my gf. Maybe crowd somehow changed. Still a great spot if you are looking for a good overpriced beer. Saturdays are crowded so prepare to scream if you want to have a conversation.
First timer eating hot chicken a few weeks ago. Food was excellent, place was clean and service was good as well.
Looks fancy from the outside, but just your average pizza joint. Had a couple plain slices. Standard stuff and cheap. I would stop by again, if I wanted a slice.

Dottie's is a great addition to the Fishtown area. My boyfriend and I went this past weekend for brunch and both enjoyed our meals and the attentive service (although we were the only customers in the restaurant). I ordered a veggie omelette and was happy that they had no problem with swapping fresh fruit for me instead of their hash browns. My boyfriend had their chicken sandwich which he really enjoyed and thought that the bread was very good. I would love to see them add a few more options to the menu, but would definitely come back again.

Saved the day! I highly recommend! Very quick turnaround and reasonably priced. I lost everything and he was able to get it back for me. I appreciate the updates and information that was provided to me during the process.

As this is the real Princess Tiana's restaurant, I came in with elevated expectations. Dooky Chase's really embodies the soul of New Orleans. If you're looking for a place to eat representative of the city, I would definitely check it out. We all tried the buffet because it was about $20 per person while each entree on the menu was about $15. The brussel sprouts were amazing, which might be an oxymoron. The catfish was pretty good as well. However, my brother's was much more salty, which ruined his palatte. The fried chicken was well fried, but not well seasoned. Their gumbo was extremely disappointing, for it tasted like seasoned tomato soup sprinkled with rice and seafood. The service was mediocre - not great but not awful. Overall, I'd say it captures the heart of NOLA, and the food is good but not great. In addition, the restaurant is located in a ghetto area, so if you're from out of town, I recommend visiting during the day.

I  lunch at this location Friday. My lunch was the brisket plate with corn & fries.  Fries were great, however the meat was mush and the city was overcooked as well.

Brisket plates usually contained sliced brisket. I was served way overcooked chopped brisket. It was swimming in sauce and like I said...mush when you tried to eat.

The clerk did ask if I wanted something else as I reported the issue and threw away my barely touched plate. I was just ready to go.

Not going back

Stayed here 1 night, checked in on my Hilton app and had my digital key. We got in late around 2am and used the digital key to get in to the lobby. While I was trying to help my husband bring in the luggage and take care of my little kids, I kept the door open otherwise my husband could not get in. Well let me tell you that the lady at the front desk was so rude. She came to the door and told me to be care I don't break it. Then as I was trying to explain that I was a guest checking in, she totally ignored me and began speaking to my husband. She thought we were not together and asked him if he needed help or if he was checking in. I answered he was with me and that we were checking in. I also explained I had a digital key. She was extremely rude and this was the first time I've had a experience like this. I felt discriminated, when she said we have to close the door since its late at night and anyone can come in. I was simply holding the door for my husband who was bringing in the luggage and the weather was 20 degrees. Very rude and I was glad that I was already checked-in so I didn't have to deal with her. Other than that, the room was clean and very comfortable.

THE BOUY BURGER. THE BOUY BURGER. I just. I don't have words. Amazing. Best burger I've ever had? I mean... up there. Top 5 at least. Buoy's was the surprise standout of our trip. Between perfectly crafted fish tacos, THE BOUY BURGER, and a stellar view... I was hooked. (Pun?) 

In addition, the service was standout. Everyone was so kind, funny, and clearly loved their jobs. We will be back! You've gotta go!

Closed on a Friday at 4:45 pm. Girl in there sweeping and then left. 
We have been back or I should say ordered from there. Their Tuna is second to none. The music is so loud we won't eat in there, we have noticed this at other locations as well so it must be company wide. Their speedy delivery in Speedway is fair. Ut the should be safe driving first and foremost.

Compared to the other burgers in the city this is more McDonalds like but I love the idea that it's a Steven Starr restaurant in the middle of Franklin Square that is basically fast food and you can sit on a park bench and eat....it's served in a box And I hated the fries,food is below average but the atmosphere is awesome

Bun--McDonalds
Meat--salty like McDonald's
Toppings---nice selection

it's just a fun place to go and for around 7 bucks for a burger and fries that's fine

This place kind of sucks.

It usually smells overwhelmingly of sweat. Which is fitting for the title, but definitely not something the gym should be striving for. Many of their cardio machines are broken or make lots of clanking and other weird noises.

Nevertheless, I can usually get a pretty good workout here. Natasha's spin class and Brittany's yoga classes are the bomb.

If I could give 0 stars I would.  They rushed my pedi it was literally like 5 mins. And then the man that did my nails. Mind you I paid extra for gel. He did not let them dry The full 60 secs was more like 30 secs if even that and my nails are crooked have no shape and are just freaking terrible.  And I still tipped smh but I shouldn't have. Never going there again I'd advise people to spend their money elsewhere. I'd rather pay 50 for a full set and get exactly what I want.

The Little Gourmand in Berry Hill is the second Nashville location of this charming French couple's delightful gift to the city's culinary scene - and what a welcome addition it is! I encourage you to try it today if you know and enjoy truly authentic French food and especially if you've never had the pleasure of visiting France and experiencing their world-renowned pastries, croissants, baguette sandwiches, and desserts.

My daughter and I both enjoyed very tasty baguette sandwiches; she had the Parisian Deluxe (butter, ham, and French brie cheese on a baguette - all ingredients from France of course!) and I chose the Brittany (tuna, mayonnaise, capers, onions, tomato, and green leaves with salt and pepper). Of course they were both delicious but the presentation also warrants mentioning - each baguette is wrapped in light brown paper, tied in thin twine with a bow, sliced in thirds, and accompanied by an attached decorative card describing the sandwich itself. Paired with a variety of assorted macaroons, it made for quite the early Saturday afternoon meal!

A little side note, this location is exactly like a " patisserie " (pastry shop) in Paris. In fact, this location's sign reads " Little Gourmand Pâtisserie Francaise " and if you've been to one in France, you'll recognize the layout immediately. There is no seating but instead large glass displays where the day's delectable pastries and other creations are easily viewable. In France, the locals will swing by their pâtisserie and pick up a small treat or two (or three!) for themselves and then be on their way. It is a perfect place to drop in on your way to anywhere and add a little elegance to your day!

Hint: for the best selection, arrive earlier in the day. The pastry chef makes what he makes for that day only and when they're gone, they're gone!

Another Hint: if you prefer to be seated, the Green Hills shop offer this option. Please see my other Yelp review of this equally amazing location!

Finally, this review is from my first visit to this new location that just opened in June 2020 and I assure you that it as extraordinary as the one in Green Hills. The food is identical so whichever location you choose, it's an excellent decision. The only differences are that the Green Hills location is more of an " épicerie " (grocery store) with seating and a small yet exceptional market and Berry Hill is the perfect French pastry shop.

Alors, si vous parlez la langue, vous pouvez commander vos choix en français parce que les patrons dévient du Bretagne et quelques des employés sont aussi français. Cette un endroit amicalement avec les pâtisseries extraordinaires et on a la chance d'avoir les deux magasins ici en Nashville! (So, if you speak the language, you can order your choices in French because the owners are from Brittany and some of the employees are also French. This is a friendly place with extraordinary pastries and we are lucky to have the two stores here in Nashville!)

We've eaten here twice. 1st time I had steak kabobs that were so salty I could hardly eat them. Second time I had a ribeye. Under seasoned and kind of boring. Average to bad service both times. Long wait time to get entrees. Bands in bar are typically good, but so loud in the dining room you have to shout to talk to the person next to you,

Had a great (and affordable) lunch. We ate off the specials list and off menu - French dip sandwich and meatloaf. Both were awesome. Service was great and we got to eat upstairs in the dining room so we had a nice view of the streets of Lambertville! Try the blood mary - we had the fender bender (garnished with shrimp, pepper jack & olives) and the spicy bourbon bay. Delicious!

I moved to Nashville not too long ago and hadn't found a doctor yet, because I didn't need too.
I came down with a sore throat and horrible cough and decided not to self medicate with over the counter stuff so I walked into the closest urgent care clinic I could find.

I walked in at 5:00 pm on a Thursday evening and was told that there was an hour and half long wait. So I decided to put my name down and come back at 6:30. Fair enough, it was my own fault for not booking an appointment online.

I go back at 6:30 and they make me sit in the waiting area for 15 minutes. Nothing wrong yet.
Finally the nurse takes me in to check the regular stuff and says the doctor will be just in, in a few, he says.
45 minutes later I'm still in the room and the doctor isn't here.

They could've asked me to wait in the waiting area, I would've at least had the company of my boyfriend. I'm just sitting in this room enclosed between 4 very sanitized white walls!

I'm sure they are really good healthcare providers, when they actually get around to providing it!

I might just stick to self medicating with over the counter stuff for minor ailments next time!

Good food. Waitress was attentive to our needs. The layout is more open so it doesn't feel that crowded. The wait can be a little long, but the candied bacon is worth it and at a good price. 

Edit: second time I went here the burger was burnt. And the fries were over seasoned. Waitress slow to fill water

Spent New Years Eve here. Wasn't packed to the seams and really enjoyed that. Only complaint is they need more than the two TV's (it was really hard to see either of them from where we were sitting).

Live singer was great really liked the music selection (Johnny cash, Tom Petty, etc).

The first ever experience was shoe carnival was back in Pensacola Florida absolutely loved it they had of the vast array of different type of shoes sizes and the prices were very good,  fast-forward to the Tucson Arizona store can't say much, not too great of a variety of shoes in the quality doesn't seem as well either I did get it down because it was buy one get one half off but will I return not in the near future
Jessica gave the best deep tissue massage!  I visit this spa when I'm in NOLA and have never been disappointed!

I went with a small group for drinks while waiting for our dinner reservation next door. We sat outside on the second floor terrace and ordered mixed drinks and beer. There's not much of view since they fenced up the perimeter (for good reason), but it was nice to get some air. 

There's a $10 card minimum and you have to go back inside to order drinks at the bar since there's no waitstaff going around. This is only an issue when it comes to the cleanliness of the patio area. Empty tables and the edges of the terrace are filled with empty baskets of food and plastic cups from other people and a lack of trash cans. 

One of the cocktails we ordered was neon green and painfully sweet, reminiscent of rum and Mountain Dew. Though to be fair, it was listed under the "Trashy" section of the menu...

It seems like it would be a fun place for a night out with a young crowd, but definitely not a place recommended for casual drinks.

I should have read the reviews more carefully......I ordered the Pad Thai. It's my favorite Thai dish. Boy, was I disappointed. Their version of Pad Thai is unremarkable. Considering it is $11, it seems very over priced. 2 shrimps and a few small pieces of chicken - no peanuts, and one or two sprouts. No flavor. 
The pad thai get's one star - order something else!
The crab wontons were good.
I won't be back.

The mgt. DId send me $10 in the mail.  Why not take care of it at the time can't say.  Want to give credit for doing that though.  Has been a great place for a long time, and hopefully was just an isolated incident.

The food at Cafe Alessio is very tasty but we have been here many times over the years where the service is terrible. This weekend was another one of those times. My DH and I ordered salads with extra lettuce and he ordered his with bacon and dressing on side. None of these requests was honored. I wonder if it was because the server was "too good" to write down our order. Granted, there was an event in Doylestown which attracted a lot of people but they have been in business a long time and certainly could have planned for a larger crowd.

They remade DH's salad without the bacon and dressing on side. Then, when we repeated that we would like extra lettuce, they said they are almost out of lettuce and couldn't accomodate this request. Really? On a summer Sunday evening they had no more lettuce? There are plenty of supermarkets in Doylestown who would have been happy to sell boxes of organic lettuce to them.

Each time we've been here, there has been more of an issue with the servers than the kitchen, although this time it was a combination. It's too bad because it is one of the few restaurants in the area with pleasant outdoor seating and an extensive menu.

I've never eaten IN the restaurant - always get delivery (foodnow.com) - so I'm speaking only to the food, not the service.

Delivery is always prompt, and I've never received cold food.  I absolutely LOVE the lobster roll and get it with the sweet potato fries.  I also like the fried pickles and crab cake appetizers.  The burgers are very good with either tater tots or onion rings.

I don't care for the lobster mac and cheese ... just a personal preference, I think.  The mac & cheese is good by itself (on the kid's menu), and as I mentioned, I love the lobster roll, but somehow the two together don't work for me here.

I would have given 5 stars, but my sweet potato fries were burnt the last time I ordered.  I will definitely give them another chance since that was only once out of about a dozen orders I've placed.

I CANNOT rate this place with just 5 stars I would give 10 stars. I was a little hesitant as I always am with new salons but WOW. AMAZING!

The salon is soooo beautiful and very large so good for a group! They also follow COVID protocols so well and it is super socially distanced. 

I had a picture of what I wanted and wasn't sure if it was something my nail tech Emma would be comfortable with but she knocked it out of the park! 

The detail and time they took I felt so comfortable and confident that it would look amazing. Seriously the detail. They took their time to make it PERFECT! 

And finally the price was soooooo affordable. Even with a soak off and full set. I was expecting it to be a lot because of the professional quality and time spent paying close attention to detail but it did not break the bank! 

Once again I cannot begin to explain how happy I am with the service.

I have received great service here. I want to site marco who is a fine sax technician.
I play in New York summers, Tucson winters. I prefer the Chicago store over al others I've dealt with, in my 40 years as a professional.

Waaaaaaaaw. Guys if you smoke hookah you have to try a new brand that is new its called ALRAYAN TOBACCO OMG its heaven smooth, delicious and amazing. I love the flavors that they have. its almost like your smoking real fruits. Its a must to try. its better thank Alfaker and starbuzz. I am hooked.

I always enjoy shopping at Cost Plus, so this location is no exception. It's located right in the heart of downtown Santa Barbara. 

If you've never been in a Cost Plus, it's a store chock full of imports from all over the world. They sell a little bit of everything:  Jewelry; wine; food; furniture; toys; etc.  It's definitely a great store to purchase gifts - for yourself, or others, I suppose. 

They have a licensing agreement with Sanrio, so that's my main focus. The Hello Kitty butter cookie tin I got here is the best. So many buttery cookie delights with her either embossed on each cookie or in the shape of a bow.

There's always a good vibe in the store. 

The store is always clean. 

The employees are friendly and helpful.

Over all the food is pretty solid, for take out Chinese style.  My biggest complaint is with anything they have to fry.  More often then not they really need to change their oil.  

We've all but given up on eating any kind of egg roll provided to us with a combination platter because they just end up being soggy and taste of old oil.  On the rare occasion we do get a "dry" egg roll they are fairly tasty.  On the same note the "Chinese Doughnuts" consistently taste of won ton and grease as they fry everything in the same vat.

They're by no means bad, but they don't blow any of their dozens of competitors out of the water.

Feels like we just got home from vacation.  What a great restaurant. Terrific seafood. Great prices and wonderful energy. Service was top notch.  They allow you to just sit and enjoy the vacation feeling.  Only thing that was missing was the sunburn !!!!!    Highly recommend

Interesting little spot in mid city, decided to check out their offerings, specifically in the ramen department. I am looking for a nice el Dente pasta whereas this ramen was a little undercooked. Broth was decent. The lady ordered fried tofu with teriyaki which completely eclipsed my meal in every way. Ramen= underwhelming, jury is still out on the entree dishes tho

Alright - Veracrzana is probably the joint I've been to the most in Philadelphia.  It's the ultimate comfort food for me, and just around the corner so highly convenient.  I want to preface this by saying, I give the Taqueria 4 out of 5 stars for a Mexican Restaurant with fluorescent lighting on Washington Ave.  If you go not expecting anything more than what it is - you'll be delighted.  Don't compare this to James or something!  For what it is - it's hot!

The value aspect is kicking.  First gorge yourself on FREE, freshly fried chips and amazing salsa - then fill up on the amazing burritos or tacos for $7.  What more could you ask?  Coke in a glass bottle always goes down a treat, or pick up a six-pack from the dodgy bar on the corner of 10th and Washington.   Dinner for two under $15 and you'll leave incredibly full and content.  

The ONLY reason TV doesn't receive five stars is the service.  I know I said to expect only what it is blah, blah, blah - but recently the service has been slacking.  There was a WONDERFUL waitress that used to work there, who we have recently seen at Mixto.  (Moving on up?)  Since then, we are left with waitresses who go MIA, forget to bring waters, and peer at us with sullen expressions.  I don't know.  All I'm saying is that a smile goes a long way.  Come on Veracruzana - we all love you.  Just love us back.

First time here - terrible service, long wait to order and long wait for food. My order was wrong once I got it. Once we got the food it was below average. Bland bland bland.  Yuck.  And hours later, my stomach is queesy.

I think the staff was great.  We enjoyed our experience at the hotel bars and at RumFish grill.  The service was excellent.  I suspect this would b a great hotel if you have kids.  We paid up for the beach front rooms and found in the hopes of some tranquil sunsets.  We found the constant noise to be unbearable.   Between the bands and the parties at the fire pit on the gulf side, then the rowdy adults and kids on the east side we found it difficult to relax or sleep.  The noise continues well into the night.  Our room was full of mosquitos every night though we took care to close out door quickly when entering.  The gap between the door jam and door allowed the buggers in.   When we checked out the bill had more than $60 of long distance calls, though we never used the phone once while there.  While it was corrected, it made me wonder who was in our room and what else they may have used.  

I had a better experience in the Sirata beach front rooms up the beach some.
This Taco Bell is also a Pizza Hut as well.  Pizza Hut is served until 11 PM but they have a tendency to run out of Pizza Hut items well before 11 PM, so be aware!

More Poke in indianapolis is always a good thing! At Moar you fill out your custom order on a little sheet and turn it in at the counter.  I loved my passion fruit black tea with mango jellies. I ordered the rice bowl with spicy tuna, pickled radish, eel sauce, seaweed salad, and fried onions. My major issue was that the tuna was shredded instead of pieces like other poke I've had. The consistency was gooey. My other thought is that if you're new to poke and not sure what you like, this style of ordering could be intimidating and maybe result in a bad order. I wish they had a couple of house bowls that you could order and know the toppings went well together.

I feel like I maybe should give more stars because this place is really clean and has good ingredients and great staff, but just for my personal preference, if I'm in the mood for Thai food, this is not the place I'd prefer to go. For example, I love massaman curry, but Blossom puts puts pineapple in theirs and I don't like that. The summer rolls are good, but they put some sort of fresh mint or something in them that makes them just a little off for me (plus their peanut sauce is pretty much just ok). Not a bad place at all, just not my favorite when it comes to Thai.

I absolutely love Target for almost everything you could possibly need! they always have fantastic sales.. however; I was very displeased with my baby registry, especially @ the Gravois Bluffs location. They hardly had anything, and the stuff they did have was seasonal so if you registered for clothes or blankets, it wasn't there a week later! So i did not get a lot of stuff of my registry. Also, as things were being purchased, they were not being removed off the registry so i received 4 of the same blanket!!!!  Any they only do in store credit even though u have a receipt. It's a bunch of b.s if u ask me.

I had an extremely dissatisfactory experience here. Two technicians at the Genius Bar weren't actually able to help with the problem and one gave advice that turned out to be incorrect. Neither had very much knowledge and they seemed poorly trained. 

I pay the premium for Apple products because it's worth good service and support when things break. I've had great service experiences in the past, but my experience at the Philly Apple Store was very disappointing.

This place is a cool spot to hangout and grab a drink, catch a game and play some pool.  They have a bunch of seating so it's pretty easy to find a spot during happy hour.  They have half off pizza and drink specials during happy hour.  Last night my buddy and I sat at the bar...had 2 vodka tonics and had 2 guinnesses on draft.  For food he tried their Kobe burger - he said it was good and I believe him because he killed it - with a $17 price tag I would hope it would be amazing!!  I tried the Philly cheesesteak and wasn't as impressed unfortunately...I had onion rings and they were good but the cheese and meat and little bit of onions on my sammy were just no bueno :/ 

I definitely like this place and the staff is awesome...I'll be back but will stay away from that cheesesteak!

What a wonderful meal! We ordered the Turista menu (which you should definitely do) and found the portion sizes just right, and the variety of flavors quite memorable. Excellent atmosphere. Service was friendly and attentive without being annoying or overbearing. 

I'm giving 4 stars instead of 5 because even though I loved my meal, they only had one vegetarian option per section, and I would have loved to be able to choose. Sometimes, when you know there's only one thing you are able to eat at a place, even if it's something totally delicious, it makes you reluctant to go back there. 

(For the record, my fiance gives it 5 stars and loved the porchetta, the lasagna, and the sausage appetizer.) 

I heartily recommend Modo Mio for an excellent experience.

Even though it's chain, I love this place. The food is always awesome and they give you a more then generous portion. I've had almost everything on the menu and no complaints. Their house beer is great as well, I love OJs.

I heard a few neighbors talk about this place and took a chance by taking my wife there for our 10 year anniversary.

Bad decision.

Let's start with the appetizer.
I literally felt like I was being punked. The server deliverd a small single slice cucumber with pesto sauce drizzled on it with 3 (countable) sprigs of salad greens, and crumpled goat cheese (not a fan of goat cheese). I laughed out loud at it as it was slightly enough to feed Fival.

It took about an hour for our meal to arrive.

Me, I played it safe and ordered the chicken.

It was severely burned on the outside, and blood, raw on the inside. Yuck.

I informed our server (which took an additional 20 mins to flag down and another 20 mins to return) to which he took back and  the chicken returned dry. The same one??? Yes. The same one.

I was done by this time... you'd think if you're paying $20-$30 for a piece of chicken at an around the corner neighborhood restaraunt you have to make reservations for... you'd at least get a fresh fully cooked piece.

My final conclusion... not worth wasting your money. Try Chick Fil A 10 mins up the road and you'll have a better experience in the drive-thu.

My advice to the owners. Hire new chefs, get a new menu selection, and give customers quick/promp service for the amount of $$$ you charge.

#wannabeupscale #raisethequalityofserviceandfood

I am visiting from California and needed my shellac redone. The girl was very informative and taught me a lot about the best shellac products. I am very pleased and will definitely return when I come back to safety harbor!! A++ Polished!!

Stopped here (thanks yelpers!) for a chai latte (ended up getting it with a shot of expresso, which I don't recommend) and a quick breakfast/snack on my way through Nashville to Louisville. 
I was specifically looking for something with a drive-thru. Well, it has one, but you have to call in your order ahead of time. ;-)
It's a really cute coffee shop with exceptional customer service. However, I didn't like anything I bought there (muffins, oatmeal, cookie) - I guess I need to order something else next time.

Our group stayed here for a conference last week and we all enjoyed our stay. First of all Tori and Jose are amazing individuals and always there to assist. Our rooms were nice and clean with each room having a microwave and small fridge was a bonus. This hotel is newly built and is located a block from the Country Music Hall of Fame Museum and about 2 blocks from Broadway where all the action is. The breakfast was good and my only suggestion would be to have an omelette station since they already have a grill there. The happy hour menu was also decent and the 3 drinks were more than enough. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars because I ordered towels and it never showed up. Overall experience was good and I would definitely stay here again.
And I oop! I read the reviews and walked my happy self right on over there and I'm so glad I did! The oysters  Sooo good! I sat at the bar and was treated so well as I ate myself into a coma.

Well, well, well...cutest little shop in town, orange/clove water in the warm & inviting waiting room, & a chocolate on the treatment table.  Christa does her very best to make you feel comfortable & welcome & even cleans your jewelry during your treatment.  I went to Christa for brazilians, facials & a spray tan.  Her brazilians are amazing as long as she has all her goodies in stock...meaning her numbing gel & hard wax for the extra sensitive areas.  That part was kind of hit or miss which was a bummer as the hard wax makes a HUGE difference!  
The facial was the first facial I'd ever had & I have serious issues with people touching my face...that was so not the case with Christa.  She walked me through each & every step & continually checked- in to make sure I was good but also allowed me to RELAX.  Highly recommend a facial.  
The spray tan...not a reflection of Christa, however, I would not do another spray tan.  She was thorough & made me feel comfortable but the results were not favorable.  I may have had a slight reaction to the formula but everyone thought I was sunburned rather than sun- kissed.  Also, I had my tan done the night before leaving for vacation which was BAD timing as you don't want to rub up against anything or get wet for at least 4 hours.  In the end, it was not worth the time although her cost was more than fair.  
All in all, I loved my monthly dates with Christa & was sad to move out of state...I just hope she keeps up with the same quality of service that she provided during my first couple of visits.  :)  She's a colorful, fun-loving, down- to- earth, love!
P.S- She even texts you to remind you of your appt!  :)

You can tell this place is an institution from the loaded parking lot and smiling patrons waiting to chow down. The atmosphere is just like a great breakfast place should be: bright, busy, friendly and clean. The service is amazing and cheery and the food rocks! The list of omelets and goodies means we will be regulars to try every single item!

They ripped out the water fountain and they try to charge you 2.50 for a bottle of water

Oh, and the wifi seems to be busted now too

Reno as fuck!

Took my niece to a mani/pedi for a girls day and this was one of the few places I called that could fit us in last minute, granted the other places were true day spas while this is more of a nail place.  Pedicure was good but manicure was something I could have done better on my own.  I didn't get any extra massage or paraffin treatments as some other reviewers seems to have received.  At one point I asked if they could change the channel (football was on) and they treated me as if I asked them to turn water to wine.  They put on golf and that was that.   The worst part was that I wanted to spend some time with my niece and they didn't do our services together.  They did her mani/pedi first and then did mine; therefore, we were there for almost three hours.   Finally I had to remind them that, as per their own website, children under 18 get discounted services.  Otherwise they would have charged us the same price.   They do have WIFi so I was able to give my niece my ipad so she could entertain herself while I was getting my services.
So delicious! The menu changes every time I come in so I always get to try something new! Ask questions about the menu and be willing to try something different.

Dan and I stopped by Varga for a nice weekday lunch.  I knew they started a new "speed lunch" menu.  Also, I hadn't been here since summer.  During lunch, this spot is pretty slow.  Still, the city is in sore need of good bars that serve weekday lunch, so I'm really happy this is open.

The beer list, as always, in incredible.  They also standardized the beer list: 3.5 for half pints, 6 for goblets or pints, and $25 growler fill.  Between us we tried Bell's Hopslam, Great Divide St. Brigid's Porter, Slyfox Odyssey, Great Divide Old Ruffian barleywine, and St. Victorious Doppelbock.   They have so many choices that I really love the new offering of half pints.  Varga also keeps their beermenus page and twitter meticulously up to date.

Dan and I were both craving burger, so we skipped the lunch menu.  The burger was good; well topped with bacon, aged cheddar, lettuce and tomato.  The burger was also served with fries and house made ketchup and mustard.  I enjoyed my meal, but it's not my favorite burger in the city, and the $14 price tag is steep despite the Kobe label.

We also split a bowl of spicy gumbo with chirzo and shrimp.  This was a good idea because neither of us ate all of our food, and the bowl of soup was $8.  Considering the speed lunch includes a bowl of soup and half a sandwich for $9, you almost can't pass up this deal.

Bottom Line: I still like Varga a lot.  It's a great choice for good lunch and beer in the neighborhood.  When the weather gets nice and you can sit outside, that just another reason to love this place.

I've lived here for 8 months and recently the management and ownership group has changed and it seems like the people in the office are attempting to do everything they can to have the residence move out before their lease is over it seems like they do not want the residents to live in this apartment complex the new name of the place is Lakes at Palm Harbor don't be railroaded into a lease at this place because they will turn on you and become increasingly more difficult to deal withThe longer you are in your lease I figure they really just want your deposit a few months rent and then they want to kick you out and keep your deposit it has not been a pleasant experience at this apartment complex very antisocial not a good place to live I wouldn't be sigh I won't be signing another lease at this place it is just not home they don't treat people with respectthey also do not uphold their end of the lease agreement they are just plain rude I would give it zero stars or negative stars if that were an option

1.5 stars

SW is wrong. Bad customer service is THE standard.

I've been to Innisbrook before, and I attended meetings there. This time I was a so-called "guest."

I just left this property today, after spending five days there for a conference. Be warned: if you are considering other properties, take it from a former Tampa Bay resident of eight years, please go to those. There are far superior properties in the area (Saddlebrook, for starters). 

A few bullet points:

*Innisbrook is located off the extremely unattractive US 19. The actual grounds are lovely, but that's where it ends. It's an ugly drive in, and an ugly drive out.

*The resort is extremely dated. They put about $26 million dollars into the common areas - two restaurants and a lobby area. Yes, attractive. They should have put that into the lodges. The suites are something straight out of a Golden Girls episode. The rooms are a bit musty as well.

*Getting anywhere you need to go and requiring a shuttle to get there-very inconvenient. (However, this isn't a complaint as this is a resort and they are set up that way. Rather, it's a warning to those who don't want to have to rely on shuttles to get anywhere).

*The largest pool, The Loch Ness, was closed early on a weekend day for a private party. It was nice, definitely NOT quiet nor peaceful. Very child-friendly. So we went to a smaller pool because of the early closing.  Eh. It was ok. Small, indeed. The service was horrible at the cafe' located at the Loch Ness pool. I asked the guy for a smoothie and he looked at me liked I asked him to go to China for sushi. We had brought our towels with us from Loch Ness to the Copperhead pool and everyone asked us where we got them. Apparently they weren't providing towels at other pools.

*The one highlight of the entire resort is the Osprey restaurant and our server Daniel. Each night the meals were quite good and Daniel was excellent. Packard's, the steak restaurant was also good. A bit stuffier, but good all the same. There was an unfortunate incident with one of the meeting attendees having glass in her meal. 

*The overall service was extremely poor. Despite knowing our group was coming, the staff was completely unprepared. Approximately fifty of our attendees waited hours for their room keys - after normal check-in time and were offered no refreshments or words other than "it'll be a few minutes and housekeeping doesn't have your room ready yet." Another attendee got to his room and it hadn't even been made up. The bed linens were not even changed.

*The meeting space is ample, however, it's dank and musty. 

Overall the only reason you'd go there is to golf. And eat. And golf some more. So I suppose that for people who actually golf this place could work. But if you actually like good customer service and nice accommodations - go elsewhere.

To put it in one word-----Average.

Probably the biggest reason I return to this place is convenience.  So easy when staying at the hotel to run down and grab a seat at the bar for dinner.  Overall the place is usually pretty loud, especially once the band starts.  

The food is pretty good.  Nothing exceptional or pushing the limits of the culinary world, but just good decent bar food.  Good selection on the menu, and decent prices.

The beer I would say is good, I like the pale ale and the red, but for the rest of the beers, Ive had better.  Extremely disappointed with the IPA.

My biggest complaint, the bartenders.  They need to replace their entire bar staff.  These guys are rude, slow, arrogant, inattentive and just plain asses!  I realize this place gets busy, but they manage to be slow when there's only 4 or 5 people at the bar.  It's obvious they have their favorite customers which they do anything for, but for us regular joes, it's nothing but attitude.

This location is always too crowded and the customers in there are less than polite. Has a great selection in most departments, but l would make the additional stops to multiple stores to avoid going there any day!

I've been standing up for 1 hour waiting for a table to be cleaned so I can sit down and continue waiting for my 1 large pie I ordered over a hour ago 
And they won't allow me to clean it myself.
So so food great location.  However the manager Keith decided to discipline two employees right by our table.  Totally unprofessional

Do NOT stay here, they DO NOT WANT YOUR BUSINESS. 

1. Made specific request for late checkout. When I arrived I was told "they don't  do late checkout. Checkout is 11, but they can give me another 30 min." (My offsite meeting ended at 11, SO nice to be "given" an extra 30 min.)

2. At 11:23, I got an angry, rude call from the front desk demanding to know "WHO TOLD ME I COULD DO A LATE CHECKOUT THEY DON'T DO THAT."  I said it's on my reservation AND when I checked in they made a note. "WELL WHO TOLD YOU?? I want their name and WHAT TIME ARE YOU LEAVING? Our check out is at 11"

I said I had 7 more minutes and hung up the phone.

3. At 11:30 PRECISELY she called back wanting to know when the H I was getting out.

It's a 300 unit motel. No one's beating down the door to clean MY room.

4. Virtually NO wheelchair access even on ground floor units. 

5. Breakfast is great. If they lose their cook, there's REALLY no reason to stay here even if you're desperate. Rooms are standard BW.

6. Did I mention the on the spot demand for a deposit "because they don't know if I'll damage the room? I'll get it back maybe in 7 business days if I don't damage the room." At 1am, I was too tired too argue. Another day, another time, I'd have walked out.

DO NOT STAY HERE. THEY DO NOT WANT YOUR BUSINESS. And they make that very, very clear.

The Florida Aquarium is a fun afternoon activity, but unfortunately, not much else besides that.  It's great if you have kids to take, but I wouldn't suggest it for adults really looking for extensive exhibits.  The entire aquarium takes 2-3 hours to go through, and that's including the shows and demonstrations they have.  It gets pricey if you have a big group.  I'm not saying don't go... but it's a place you'd only need to experience once.

The aquarium itself isn't bad.  It's clean and well-kept so you don't feel depressed that there are animals being kept there.  The first floor is primarily the entrance and food court, but at certain times of day, they bring out a penguin for a show and tell.

The second floor has a hybrid open air/water pool exhibit with lots of fish and birds to look at.  Check the stairs to the next level because you will miss my favorite birds: burrowing owls in their little corner.  They are the cutest!!  The deepest area of the aquarium leads to a giant shark tank, with huge viewing glass.  I could spend an hour just sitting on the benches and watching the different fish swim by.

There's lots of animals to see, but the hallways get crowded.  There is a stingray touch pool, which is a neat experience for everyone of all ages, although you need to submerge your entire arm to get a good feel.  The third floor has an art gallery, which is a nice change of pace from the dark fish tanks and away from people.

Bring your swim gear because there is a splash pad that's included with your admission to the aquarium.  Parents can sit and let their kids run off their energy in the water area.

The parking situation is the most annoying part of the aquarium.  You park far away and have to walk, and it's easy to get lost along Channelside because most of the parking lots are for the cruises.  Parking costs $6 and you will have to fight to find a space because it's usually always full.

If you're looking for a place to kill an afternoon, go to The Florida Aquarium.  As an adult, I would only go back if I had a kid to take.

I walked in and instantly got an attitude from the guy behind the counter. 
Asked him for help and made 2 condescending remarks about my car and intelligence. Needless to say they will not be getting my business.

Came here on a Sunday with a group whilst in the area for beers and lunch. The front section of the bar was crowded but we were able to get a booth towards the back. I ordered the sesame chicken salad which was a generous size and flavorful. My husband had the French onion soup and the crab cake sandwich he really enjoyed. Our friend also liked their meals. This place is just a solid last minute decision kind of place, especially if you're with any picky eaters, as the menu is large and offers plenty of American standards like burgers and fries.

For years we met friends WEEKLY for our sushi/sashimi fix in the cozy small Japanese area of this restaurant. The fish was perfect, the chef Taka exemplary, Lam was getting there, the waiters courteous & friendly, the ambiance casual & relaxing. Then over the summer came the owner's catastrophic brainstorm to move the sushi area to the main room: gone the cozy, relaxing area, in the "sports bar atmosphere" where you have difficulty carrying a conversation due to the noise level. That's for the ambiance. The new chef Keith (who got trained back in May-short training for sushi chefs in this establishment!) is doing a good job at sushi but the sashimi part has a long way to go. I complained to our waiter who promptly took our "sushi/sashimi for two" back to Keith who piled on  a couple more pieces. Not appetizing. If this plate was supposed to satisfy two persons the chef should be skin & bones. Is the owner insisting on increasing revenues by reducing sizes? She has already lost four of her "regulars", that I know of, I hope she takes a serious look at her profits these days & adapts this larger room to a calmer ambiance, with adequate servings. And please, get rid of the plastic sushi plates at the entrance, they are NOT enticing!

Went there yesterday 2xs once for breakfast n dinner not only were my hash browns hard as a rock I was able to drop it in my table n here the bang I tryed to call but no one answers I finback at dinner bother my woooers were over cooked fries were stale onion rings oldso good to say yet again they got me for another $26 if imyou want BK I suggest taking a ride to any other but this one I have video I sent to the health department yesterday so they can see this place is ripping my ppl off by serving food made hours ago

My good friend and I just enjoyed a fantastic meal at The Prime. The meal began with thick cut BBQ bacon. This pork just melted in our mouths, sweet and smoky with a hint of spice.  
The salad consisted of firm ripe watermelon and sweet strawberries tossed with arugula and feta in a tangy vinaigrette. Very refreshing. 
The next course was our favorite...homeade ricotta filled, sweet corn, crispy shallot and asparagus topped, perfectly made ravioli. This dish was awesome! Buttery, sweet, salty and creamy, yet bright and light. 
The bone-in, dry aged cowboy rib eye that followed was just as good, in its own right. This steak was super juicy and tender. Cooked just to mid-rare, our steak was evenly marbled with exceptional flavor. The main course was accompanied by crispy fried smashed fingerling potatoes topped with a creamy worcestershire sauce. Fresh cut corn off the cob creamed with butter, fresh herbs and smoky bacon finished this epic course. Delicious!
Dessert was not to be missed. Perfectly  torched, amber colored sugar topped a room temperature orange and ginger creme brulee. Not to sweet, beautifully prepared. Along side an alomond sponge cake tart topped with creamy vanilla gelato. Difficult to describe,  impossible to resist. 
This meal was just so good I had to recall it! Well done!
So obsessed with their one slice pizza combo! Nice prices and humongous slices, just my style:) hospitality is always nice there too. Such a hidden gem

Good beer, high prices.  Worth it if there is service with a smile from the  bartender atleast...(wont say her name ) 
 Not today. Changes everything. Think I'll go to my not-so-fancy watering hole where I feel way more welcomed.

Booked and paid for a room in advance, stepson was performing at the Fillmore New Years eve. We called earlier in the day and they said late check in was no problem (as you would expect since the room was 
 prepaid). When we got there at about 10pm, they said they were overbooked and they could send us to another hotel. This was useless to us since our sons were staying at the Monaco.
So we ended up going home which at least saved us $400. Very disappointed in this hotel and I would not reccommend it.

Kevin Walsh

Love beer? You need to come to this place and merrily drink. Don't drink beer? Come and just try the Lost Abby's Red Poppy Ale and enjoy the great atmosphere. So I'm the latter. I didn't have bottles after bottles of beer, but I still had a great time here because this place was just... interesting. The beer bible--LOVE IT! I immediately instagramed that shiz. Matching glasses for every beer--They have you guessing what sort of goblet, thistle, or tulip glass comes with your next beer. More instagrams!
The mussels were very good... for $10, get it!
Next time a beer lover friend of mine visits Philly, I'll definite take them to Monks.

For all the idiots who are SHOCKED and APPALLED by the sweet sauce of this pizza... yes it has sweet sauce, as many, many, many reviewers mention. If you don't like sweet sauce pizza, don't eat here or post a terrible review based on your own crappy taste buds. However, I would love to be able to advocate for this location since it is by my house, but unfortunately I have to agree with the previous reviews regarding lack of consistency. Some days this pizza is delivered and it's fantastic and other days it's burned on the bottom, dried out, or cold. The last time this happened I complained and they did send me another pizza free of charge, but that was still the last straw. Hopefully they turn things around, but until then I will be driving to the Manayunk location and fighting traffic while searching for a parking spot for a decent pizza. They never disappoint.
Hands down the Steamboat Willie is the best French Dip that has ever graced these lips.

So good. 

I have to say that it's been a REALLY long time since I've been to a pizza joint that I really liked. And while this place has all sorts of options, the pizza choices are top-notch. 

I'm not a traditional pizza fan but I do enjoy gourmet + out of the box pizza. For the lunch special ($10 for personal pizza + salad), I got a shrimp, pesto, goat cheese pizza with arugula on top. HOLY CRAP. Just. so. good.

They also have a great selection of beers, although as it was lunch (and not friday...), I didn't imbibe.

This is a two part review!

The first part is the food. The food great when it comes out right, which it usually does. Food is always hot and seems fresh, as it should. This is the main reason we come here, healthiest food around when we don't feel like cooking.

The second part is the service, mostly the waitresses themselves. They are usually all rude and make you feel like an inconvenience. The waiter you have there is good, he is always happy it seems but the girls are not. You can here them bad mouthing people in the back while they put orders in, they only come by when you flag them down to get or they give you the check and lets hope their friends don't come eat while you are there. They will sit down with them and for some reason, their food comes out way before yours. 

Please keep up the great food but please fix them service. Only saw our waitress twice on this visit, once to take our order and then to give us our check.

When you come here - expect to wait and wait and wait and wait.  I had an 11:15 appointment and i wasn't seen until 11:50 - i literally had to go up to the nurse and be like "hi did you forget about me?" the time before when i had an apt for my annual i also had to wait 30 mins after my appointment.  

either way my doctor rules and she def helped me.  the staff is nice. i guess patience is a virtue.

Basic menu that has drink items on the menu that are no longer being served... Why still have it on the menu? Terrible servers.... Probably because there is one employee working both the bar and restaurant area. Also felt a little uneasy when I ordered a salad and the sever says "that salad is not looking too good tonight. Also my Crown and ginger ale was weak.

I was very excited coming to Hart Bakery & Gifts because it appeared to be a family owned small business. These usually are the best places to eat. I made sure I got there early so I could catch the donuts hot. When I walked in it gave me the flea market feel. They not only sell baked goods there's  a ton of flea market like items. Of course I didn't mind because I'm there for the donuts. The next thing I noticed that the donuts didn't have a barrier or in a case. I'm not a germaphobe but the donuts aren't too far from the door (See picture). It was too late to worry about that so I opted for the glazed yeast and maple cinnamon. 

Ok. 

Pros: 
Customer service was decent. Everyone was greeted when we walked in. 

Donuts are fresh. 

The employee counted back the change the right way. Lol. This is a lost art. 

Cons: 

No barrier. I'm not interested in fly or gnat donuts. 

There's not a large selection of donuts and no specialty. 

There's really no WOW to the donuts. They taste like your basic fried donut that you can get at any gas station. 

Price. I think I paid $2 for 2 donuts. These are easily .50-.65 donuts. 

I doubt I'd go back here. If I did it'll be to taste some of the other baked good.

No bueno. 

I was in town for a wedding and thought I'd drop by for a margarita and some chips and guac. I should have kept walking. I'm no booze-snob, but my margarita tasted like floor cleaner. The guac was brown, and my waitress seemed ticked off that we weren't ordering real food. I'm adding an additional star to my review for the patio. It was nice to get to sit outside.

Thanks, but no, thanks, Red Cantina.

Very rehearsed serving style, which is perfectly fine. However, it felt awkward.  The shrimp cocktail and crab cocktail were both overcooked and rubbery.  The steak was good and cooked perfectly.  The steak was comparable to St Elmos but 20 percent more expensive.  Let's be real.  We just spent 240 dollars on a dinner for two.  Don't try and sell me a membership card (which I know the staff is made to do and probably rated on performance reviews). It cheapens the experience.  A once great establishment taken over by a large corporation.

So on opening night it appears they are not ready to open. Small  things overlooked that I'm sure for the price tag they paid for this place was an oversight.  Stage is awkwardly placed,  second level has poor view of the acts. Handrails falling  apart  tables are simply  poor craftsmanship. Basically some laminate flooring as a table, very underimpressed. Knowing who backed this place I would expect way more,hopefully they put on the finishing touches over the next few weeks. First impression leaves something to be desired. On the bright side the staff they hired is super friendly,  very engaging bar tenders. They will probably do just fine once they work out some kinks

Love Cheekwood, but the Pineapple Room needs to up its game. Sunday Easter brunch may have been the worst buffet I have ever experienced. Cold and tasteless. I hate giving bad reviews, but I just can't get over how expensive it was and how absolutely horrible the food tasted. The service was just as bad. Our waitress would never refill our glasses of water after asking several times. Never cleared our plates from the table even after she brought the bill. Even acted annoyed when I asked her one final time for a glass of water and if she could remove my plate from the table.  Who wants to look at a dirty plate for 30 minutes after they have finished!? The one star is for the beautiful stroll thru Cheekwood with the amazing Spring tulips in full bloom. That was the saving grace of that awful experience.

I decided last minute that I wanted a small bouquet for my courthouse wedding. When I came into the shop I was pleasantly excited about all the options and the staff was super friendly. 

I really got excited about putting the perfect fall mix together, and Terra helped me pick out the perfect pieces! SO happy and the whole staff was really wonderful

Stopped in Sunday 9/28 while we were waiting for a prescription. We were just going to have a quick drink, and we were there for three hours. Tomato Bisque (delish). Pretzels and Cheddar (more delish) And the BEST Irish Stew I have ever eaten (sorry Grandma). We sat at the bar and everyone was wonderful; from the Manager to the Bartender. We will definitely go back!
The food is OK. The prices are OK.. Notice today how dirty dirty the table edge is. It's grossing me out

We were really looking forward to trying this new (to us) spot for breakfast, having heard good things about it.  When we got there at 10ish on a Friday they were busy but there were tables available.  However, since there was a sign asking us to wait to be seated, we did.  Although waitresses saw us standing there, no one acknowledged us and we ended up waiting 5-10 minutes before someone came over to us.  We were then seated and from then on the service was very good.  I was quite startled when I saw the prices on the menu, but I had seen pictures online of some very delicious looking food, so I assumed you would be getting quite a lot in your order.  Hubby ordered the sourdough pancakes and two eggs, and I ordered an omelette with cheese, bacon and onions.   I didn't realize that EVERYTHING on the menu is ala carte.  Translation, the prices are exorbitant for what you get.  The pancakes were two very thin and somewhat cold cakes...and my omelette looked very lonely all by itself on a large plate.  See pics.  We had ordered a scone to take home and there was no charge for it so that made up for it a bit...but if I'm paying $32.07 for breakfast...I expect more food than that.  On top of that...my omelette had ham instead of bacon in it...guess the cook doesn't know the difference.

Ordered takeout on a slower thursday night. We ordered the gnocchi and the manicotti. Girlfriend really enjoyed the gnocchi which had a lot of flavor. Unfortunately the manicotti is straight up terrible. We were literally served a bowl of sauce with two small strands of pasta with cheese. Highly recommend any other dish!!!!

The waitresses were very attentive and friendly, the service was speedy, and the food was great. Do not leave this place without trying one of their cheesecakes! Their Chocolate Tuxedo Cream Cheesecake really hit the spot - yum~

Great barbershop. Very welcoming staff. Easy to book online. Emily did a great buzz cut as well as was very nice. Very reasonably priced at $19.50. A lot of product and merch for sale. Free street parking. Accepts cards. Highly recommend.

My boyfriend brought me here for our 2 year Anniversary dinner. It was a pretty nice place for dinner and in downtown Santa Barbara. The food is overpriced. But, what we had was pretty good. I liked the Pretty in Pink & The Pro Roll was pretty good as well. The Miso Soup was kind of bland though. the service was great. Our server was very nice and polite. It's very crowded, I guess because it has a 4.5 star. But, the wait isn't that bad, at least it wasn't that bad for us. We just gave them our phone number and they called us when our table was ready. So, while we were waiting, we walked around downtown. The atmosphere was nice. The restaurant was 2 stories. We sat upstairs in a cute little corner. Most romantic table in the joint. It's way too busy downstairs and has very little walking room. The door was really crowded with people coming in and going out. The parking isn't that bad at all either. you get 75 minutes free if you decide to park in a lot. But after 75 minutes it's only $1.50 an hour. We ended up paying 1.50 more because we walked around after dinner. Overall, I would go back and recommend this place to friends who visit Santa Barbara.
Vibes are great ... concept is great but the cut was not good. I had to come home and recut my hair after I realized there were many uneven spots.

Cute spot for a winter night out! Definitely recommend this place for weeknights as the weekends can get crowded but with the additional room and backyard sections, it's way better than previous years! Drinks are pricey but good for a photo opp. We tried the seasonal cocktails- resting blitz face and the moose milk that comes with a cookie!
great breeze up here. ample seating outside or inside, but it is all open air-style. the tri color cocktail (one of the first two on their cocktail list) is a must

This place is great for the fact that they are super close to my home. My boyfriend eats here once a week and loves it. The prices are great and the sushi is fresh. So why the 3 stars..? I would like some more veg/vegan options for myself. The atmosphere and service were good though.

Great service, good food, nice ambience. Family style dining, great for loud kids too!  I'm a poke (poh-kay) lover, so I naturally wanted to try that dish. It's definitely a fusion dish that combines a salsa type design, mingled with small cubes of ahi, served on top of friend won ton. 
The only feedback I had for the team was how to properly pronounce poke...it's like poké, not the English word "poke"...as if it puncture something.

Hyped on Good Day Tampa Bay, I'd go anywhere Jen Epstein was! Very disappointed 1st time . Spent $10 on cold soggy thin cracker they called pizza. I'd only go back because I saw good reviews on other dishes.
One thing to add- the order was take out but I only live a mile away. The thin cardboard box probably doesn't insulate the thin cracker crust well enough.
False statements in the message they send that they help the small and not rich that's just a lie.

Soggy pizza and Greek salad had ham on it and so little lettuce I wasn't sure you could even call it a salad.  Not even worth taking a pic of to show online. At least the beer was cold. Waitress was friendly.

I want so much to love Founding Farmers - the menu has a great variety of options and the building is gorgeous, but we're struggling. 

My husband called to order food for pick-up and after a long hold was told we had to use Door Dash.  So, we hopped over to Door Dash and placed an order.  It was to be done at 6:40pm.  When we arrived we waited 10 minutes for our food.  Fast forward to us sitting in our kitchen, unpacking our food...our spicy chicken salad was a large piece of bony fried chicken with mashed potatoes and veggies.  On top of that, our medium burger was way more medium rare/rare than it should have been.  

Both my husband and I were incredibly disappointed- not only had we picked Founding Farmers specifically for the salad, but also the food just wasn't good. 

DoorDash refunded most of our money, but we both went to bed hungry.  

I will say that last year we had Mother's Day Brunch at Founding Farmers - it was a three / three and a half star experience.  The service staff was great, but the food was hit or miss.

I had a general reading for 55, Gina gave me some detailed information on my personality and current situation, and predictions of future. What she said about me was basically correct, and the future she saw was very nice. However, she said she felt a negative spirit attached to mine, and she suspected it's conjured by someone on my father's side who was jealous of him and didn't aprove the marriage, and she can do a psychic research for 185 to find out who it was. I was happy about the reading and decided to go for the research.
A week later after Gina introduced me her daughter Anna, who said she found it was a male spirit in slavery for my family years ago, who was very angry that he didn't have a family of his own, so after his death his spirit attached a family member and passed down for generations. I'm the fifth person attached, and this spirit is the reason why I feel tired often, have trouble sleeping, experience problems in relationships and finance, etc. The spirit feeds on me, my romantic relationships, it's obssessed with me and wants to take me over. Right now it is weak but I'll have a psychic attack when I'm happiest, I'll meet my soulmate, get engaged, then boom, everything lost. Anna Said I should sleep with an envelop with 12 pieces of big money (50 or 100, 200 is best) and some other stuff inside. They also told me not to tell anyone. I was shocked, kind of suspicious, but still believed the story mostly.
Two days after I got everything, they told me all the evil energy was trapped in the envelop, so they had to destroy it. Anna said she cast a spell on the spirit so it was paralyzed for 48 hours, and if I didn't do it now there would be nothing they can do later when bad things happen. The process will remove all the difficulties in my life caused by the curse, and my life will be totally different, I'll feel lighter and happier and the result is instant, and if I don't do it bad things will happen. After some struggle I decided to give it a try, Maybe it'll work?
After I got home I was very uncomfortable, I searched online and found curse removal was the MOST COMMON PSYCHIC SCAM! There are numourous people who went to similar experience as I did, and they spent tons of money and never got the promised result for sure. I also read that even some psychics are genuine they still rip people off.
At this point I just couldn't continue to believe Gina. I called and Anna said I can only see Gina on Monday, I agreed. Then Gina canceled the meeting last minute, rescheduled it to Tuesday, saying a family member was in hospital and she won't be back till late. I was worried if I'll ever see her again, and said I could meet her no matter how late, she said she was far away and can't meet me at 3am. She also said she destroyed the envelop already. But around 11pm that night I saw her walking away from her shop when I was showing my friend where it is, who would accompany me the next day to try to get my money back. She didn't see me, and my friend told me not to approach her, so we didn't talk.
On Tuesday when she saw I was with a guy and he knew what happened she was very upset at first. She defended herself, blamed me, but agreed to return the money, for it was only 600 compared to her '6000$ business' and she has a reputation. She then said to my friend:'She's dark energy.' (Meaning me of course), but soon she probably realized that my friend didn't buy any of that, and it only made her look bad, so she calmed down and started to say nicer things.
I asked her if she wasn't home till 2/3am on Monday night, she said she wasn't. So although I can't verify if she worked with the envolop, burned it and buried it in a graveyard as she said, I know that she's lying about where she was on Monday night.
Looking back, I think the reasons why I fell for this are: 1. I don't want to take full responsibility for my life, and would rather have another entity responsible for my own unhappiness and negative feelings. 2. There are misfortunes and unpleasant things one can't control or explain, and having a definite answer like a ancestral curse makes it much easier. 3. It's good to have a magic pill that takes care of everything for me, happily afterwards, once and for all.
I don't want to label Gina as a con artist, for in many ways she was only offering people what they want. I gave her two stars, for I do think she has some psychic ability, or at least ability to read people, and she returned my money without much trouble. She's very persuasive, experienced and shrewd in her business, that's my impression. But I won't really recommend anybody seeing her, unless you're prepared to end up paying hundreds more.
There are many psychics in old and center city. I don't think they can survive just by giving readings considering the rent. There have to be more expensive services to keep the business going. So my suggestion is to google psychics scams before you spend more money, before it's too late.

Not impressed. Went for dinner with a friend on a Friday evening. We passed on the $5 valet parking, paid $1 at the Ben T. Davis parking area. We waited about 10 minutes before our friendly server arrived. We ordered the jerk chicken sandwich and black and bleu burger to share. I opted to replace the french fries with Caribbean rice and was charged extra since it's a "premium" side. The rice was ok but nothing special. The chicken was dry and flavorless, the burger was mediocre and the tablespoon side dish of coleslaw was vinegary. I could have skipped that. 
The view is nice but that's about it. Overall, this is just another noisy, crowded restaurant with bland, overpriced food and drinks. We don't plan to return.

I would skip this hotel. The bathtub in the first room I checked into was filthy. It was as if there was a sewer back up and it was not cleaned. The front desk staff was sympathetic and gasped at the picture I took of the tub. They gave me a new room and offered a drink credit, which I did not need. My new room had dirt and lint literally hanging from the bathroom vent but I decided to just cringe and live with it as I did not want to be *that* person. The restaurant was understaffed and people were walking out without being served breakfast or even given a menu. Very pleasant staff, but appears to have poor management and attention to detail.

Flatbread is the one place where I know, for 100% fact, that I'm going to get a good meal and fabulous service each time I go, no matter when I go and no matter what I order. It's just that good, all the time. 

Food: You can't go wrong with their Pick 2 (Craft 2) lunch specials, from 11-4 daily. Whether it's a pizzetta, salad, half mac and cheese, sandwich or a soup, it's ALL GOOD. Our favorites are the quattro formaggi pizzetta, mac and cheese and chicken apple walnut salad. The new kale salad is also really good, and the nuts and seeds on top are delicious. My husband's favorite appetizer is the sea salt flatbread, and I love the bruchetta. Literally, it's ALL GOOD! If you're a mac and cheese lover, try the mac and cheese! One of the best parts is the cookies and milk for $1 after a meal. The perfect light topper to a great meal!

Drinks: Daily drink specials, great hot tea and coffee!

Atmosphere: The atmosphere is great. It's not a huge dining room so it gets very cozy and romantic when it's busy. You can also sit outside during the warmer months, and the patio is wonderful. Good people watching in the little park they have there! My favorite spot in the whole place is at the tables by the fire place. It's the warmest, coziest spot!

Service: Always fast and super friendly. Flatbread servers always seem like they really enjoy their jobs.

Happy Hour Offerings: Daily happy hours, with the best lunch pick 2 special around daily from 11-4

Healthy Hack: Very healthy salad options. I always order the half salads and they're plenty for one!

Tips: Ask for their ranch with pizza... it's a chipotle ranch and so tasty!

Two and a half stars.

Decor was horrible-- tables that small should be outlawed! Plus our table was super wobbly, our drinks were about to fall over.

Food was okay. Oysters weren't as fresh as I've had before; I wanted to stop halfway but my husband doesn't eat oysters so it would have been a total waste. I got the sesame crusted ahi tuna with some kind of pineapple/papaya sauce with sauteed snap peas and polenta cakes. The ahi tuna, although cooked medium rare, was dry. I don't know how that was possible, but the sauce definitely saved it. Snap peas were standard and polenta cakes were unexciting.

Whatever you do, don't go here if filling your stomach and value for the price are priorities.  $7.95 for a caramelo that was maybe three bites' worth, and wasn't even hot by the time I got it home?  No thanks.  Lame, overpriced Mexican food-come here to get ripped off!

had a weekday lunch there today. Ate in the dining room in the back (easy choice the front  near the door was freezing as the weather was 23F and windy).

Food good but not outstanding especially given the prices ($38+tip for two).

I had the tomato basil soup which was thick with tomato base and delicious. My friend had the lobster potato bisque which looked great as well.

I had the cobb salad as I heard their salads were good. It was fine. My friend has a lobster croissant which he enjoyed as well.  

Service adequate but not great (a 17ish girl who kept handing off as she wasn't allowed to pour our wine (BYOB). 

Food good; would've rated higher except for the value prop.

When I'm looking for a burger fix, Burgerhaus doesn't come to mind. I've only been once, but from that experience alone, I haven't felt the need to go again. My burger was dry and unseasoned. No salt, pepper, onion powder, herbs, nothing. I ordered it medium but it was served well done. The fries were decent, to their credit. Burgers are so simple to execute that I really don't give a lot of leeway to restaurants I order them from.

pho was good, flavorful. its a one size bowl, would be nice to have various sizes. honestly couldve had a 5 stars because broth was good and noodles were cooked perfect. only downfall was it took a while before someone came to take our orders, took awhile for soup to come out. but aside from that , good soup

excellent food and even better price. I was welcomed and love the personal touch they give to eat customer. we had a nice conversation while waiting on my order. I got the crab leg platter and I love the fresh garlic butter sauce. very nice! the shrimp add on is well worth it. they are huge and tasted great! I hope I have time to return here before leaving town.
This is a very pretty nail spa.  If you want to get a mani/pedi and not get your cuticles done and not know what you are going to pay when you walk out, this is the place for you.

My fiancé got my birthday cake from here. He called a few days in advance and needed a last minute cake. They advised that they didn't have any white cakes so he picked a strawberry (why??? I'm not faulting classic cakes for that choice) with buttercream icing. When he mentioned he was getting the cake on Tuesday for a Thursday birthday I was concerned the cake would be dry, but he said that he was assured it would be moist. He also requested that there be no red icing on the cake (it has a weird chemical taste to me). We were bad and had they cake a day early. The cake tasted STALE and was dense, it had orange icing on it with the chemical taste, and cream cheese frosting (not what was requested/ what was going to be on it). Overall, the flavor of the cake tasted like a strawberry box cake with some strawberries mixed in. I'm not normally a fan of strawberry cakes so that might just be how they taste but it wasn't too pleasant. The cake was decorated nicely minus the red icing based color. If you just care about cake appearance then this is probably the place for you for the price. If you are focused on having a delicious cake, try something else. This was a step above a Walmart cake but not much. Luckily my mom is saving the day with a Cake Bake cake (they just needed 48 hours notice). #motherknowsbest

We worked with Jen of Jennifer Designs for my daughter's wedding. The flowers were absolutely gorgeous!  They were truly breathtaking. We received so many many compliments on them. Jen worked with us on the colors and sizes and availability. Also was conscientious about our budget. Couldn't say enough great things about the design and beauty!  Also, she was very timely getting our flowers to the home, and to the church and yacht club which were in two different towns!

Just got back from an amazing tour.  Our guide Eric was very friendly, knowledgable and enthusiastic and the tour route really gave us a good feel for the city and it's history.  I really can't say anything but positive remarks for this company, we had a fantastic time.

I've only been here once, but had such a great time.  The food was fresh and hot, the service was outstanding and the people who frequent Fish Tales are warm and funny.  I highly recommend it!  On a warm sunny day, you can't beat the views or the tiki bar on the dock. :)
Customer service was horrible receptionist was incompetent and manager was rude. Disappointed because we've visited before but experience was enough me not to want come back and would not recommend.

Came here for lunch today. I had a chicken salad sandwich with side salad -both were very good.  The service was nice and prompt. I would definitely recommend this place for lunch if you are downtown for work or leisure.

This place RUINED my best friends bachelorette party. She had spoken with the manager THREE times the week prior to make sure they had our reservation for 20 on cinco de mayo. We went in to be seated at 5 p.m., which is when they said we should show up. They told us it would be 2 hours for a 2 top and were not apologetic at all. As someone in the serving industry I am appalled by this kind of treatment and blatant disregard for customer service. I will NEVER be back!

The plain cheese pizza is too doughy,not crispy at all and soggy although what I had was reheated leftover slice I will say I won't be in any rush to try this again. 

Limited pizza options out this way may have me circle back to trying another slice, but so far Marino's may be the more consistently good pie that delivers out here for now.

I've been wanting to try this place for a long time but their limited hours have made it really difficult. I finally ordered takeout today (because that is their only option). Good to know, because I almost invited an out-of-town friend to come with me because I thought you could sit down to dine. A few months ago in the fall they were open for in person dining. But I guess they aren't now. Kind of frustrating. I ordered the Buffalo Chicken potstickers, Stl Homerun potstickers, and the Traditional Potsticker with pork. An Apple Pie Potsticker for dessert. I wasn't as impressed as I thought I would be. Maybe because it's takeout? Their plating and garnishes look so good in the pictures! That's what I was hoping for. I'm sad to say I was disappointed. The food wasn't bad by any means, it just wasn't as good as I thought. Honestly, my favorite were the traditional potstickers which were very good. I felt like the other options were a little too "extra". However, the Apple pie potsticker was really yummy for dessert.  

I'm willing to give them another chance, however. Maybe when the weather is a little nicer they will allow patio seating.

Best al pastor in Santa Barbara. I have tried the tacos and burrito so far and both have been outstanding.

 Great flavor to the hot salsa at the salsa bar.

When I chose this place it had six 5 star reviews.  

They did a fabulous job at remodeling this two story building on south Virginia just north of Wells. During current 2019 road construction, you park and enter from the back off Wells. 

It is beautiful inside, yet not entirely what I was hoping for.  I had the time and was driving by, so walked in. They took me right away. It wasn't very clear that I should go upstairs. But I finally got up there. The manicure price doesn't include polish. That is an extra $15. That was rather pricey I thought. Even NYC only charged me $16 for a wonderful manicure with polish that lasted three weeks. I'm a hygienist, live in nitrile gloves all day for four days plus constant hand washing. I get it, my cuticles are trashed. The manicurist seemed to scold me about the condition of my hands. She did give me a couple of tips, but it seemed condescending.  

For others and the style of nail work they have done , this I probably a great place.  For me, I'm still searching.

This fence company does not stand behind their work or follow through with the things they have agreed to do.
