When we first requested a quote, our sales person, Jonathan attempted to use shady tactics to force us into putting a deposit down immediately by saying that if we didn't put the deposit down by the end of the week, prices would go up. However, he was not able to say what price would go up (materials, labor, etc). When I called the office to find out what the new quote would be, the office didn't know what he was talking about and said that they were unaware of any price increases taking effect that week. 
We paid the 50% down and then paid the remaining amount when they came to install it. During the installation, they severed our underground cable lines. We contacted Jonathan who apologized for not having the utilities marked beforehand and asked us to have Spectrum forward them the bill. When we contacted him to find out where to send the bill, he said that they weren't going to pay for it because they felt that Spectrum shouldn't have buried the lines there. (Now the fence company is an underground utility expert ). We purchased a $10,000 fence and they are not willing to pay for a $50 repair that they caused.
After the fence install we noticed that rivets were missing in a few places. Then during a storm, one of our $250 solar caps flew off. They too were not secured. When they came to secure them, they brought glue. The fence was wet from rain, so glue wasn't going to be a good option. We asked them to secure them with screws or something more permanent. We are still waiting for that to happen 3 weeks later. 
Save your money and purchase a fence from a company who stands behind their work and their word.

I forgot what a great happy hour Fleming's has and that they are part of Bloomin Brands. I went there on a Friday with a girlfriend for drinks. I got there before the rush and got a booth at the bar. Our server was super friendly and attentive even as she got busier.  The bar area really started to fill up around 5:30pm. 

They have wine, beer, cocktails, appetizers and a few entrees on their special menu until 7pm. The house wines, calamari and burgers seemed to be the best deals during this time.

I've also had dinner here several times with my family. The prime steaks are always delicious and cooked perfectly and we love sharing the family style sides. 4 stars only because it's still a chain and there are some other steakhouses that are more upscale.

If I could leave -2 stars I would. The employees were super rude and unprofessional. Besides the fact that it took over 15 minutes just to be seated and the food poisoning I experienced about an hour after leaving the restaurant, I would say the only good thing about that place was our server Josh who has the best attitude and was overall amazing.

I was tempted to go 4-stars here and I still may come around if I eat here again (something I'm willing to do). I had a Mcdougal Sandwich, which is basically a pita sandwich with both falafel and shawarma. My girlfriend got a basic falafel sandwich and we split an order of fries with garlic sauce.

I started out very happy with the my sandwich - the lamb shawarma meat surprised me with how flavorful and "real" it tasted. And by and large I enjoyed the sandwich. As I went on, though, I noted that the falafel was taking a serious backseat to the lamb and the lettuce/tomato - there were I think two balls in there, but their presence in the taste and texture was minimal. My girlfriend somewhat similarly felt that there was a disproportionate amount of lettuce and tomato (as well as disappointed that the place doesn't seem to offer cucumber with any dishes), somewhat at the expense of the falafel. 

The fries also elicited mixed feelings. Make no mistake - they taste exactly like I wanted them to. They were lightly seasoned and a perfect mix of outwardly crisp and internally billowy. But at $4.55, the portion was way too small - we would not have split them had we known how few you get...but we also would't have spent $9+ on two small bags of french fries, so damned if you do, damned if you don't there. The garlic sauce was a letdown, too - more than tasting garlicky, it was salty enough to choke a horse. It worked out because they tasted great alone and with ketchup, but the garlic sauce was disappointing. 

There's still a lot that looks great on the menu, and the quality of the lamb and fries suggests to me that, say, the chicken kabob plate, falafel plate, or kafta kebob sandwich might be absolutely delicious. But for this first experience we had with the restaurant, there were one too many "it was good but"s for me to have been totally thrilled with it.

Ugh.  Don't get me started on O.G. - and this one in particular is where 2 out of 3 of my DH's brothers had their wedding rehearsal dinners.

Thus, my (then future) FIL wanted US to have our rehearsal dinner there too.  NO WAY!!!  (We wound up going to Bensi's across the street).

Meanwhile - O.G is NOT Italian.  It's faux-talian-american.  And it's full of kids.

I am not a fan of the over cooked, over sauce formula-cooked pastas.
It's OK in a pinch but there are so many way better choices.

Not bad for a Tues night
Prob one of more packed places...
Has Nice outdoor seating

Waiter took our drunk order and forgot water (which i asked for) until I asked again...

Fish tacos: interesting... Different than what I'm used to... Marinated in a dark sauce and shredded

Chicken fajitas wrap: what is indeed a wrap but what was weird is that it had cheese in it. Normally he does don't have cheese. It wasn't bad at all just not what I expected. The fajitas came with either chips french fries or sweet potato fries but the fries were $2 extra.

I was however thrilled when I saw that they had rogue Chocolate Stout on tap...delicious!!!!!

I also had the sangria which was decent.

Overall not a bad place.   Prefer over the "branded" food restaurants out there.

The feel and look of the hotel was amazing, the internal functionality of the hotel did not match the price.  The food was under rated for the exuberant price, not single restaurant had more then three stars on yelp.  The hidden fees inflated the price of my stay, even when I was told at the front desk that self parking was included in the room rate.  There were not a lot of activities like there where a few summers ago when I stayed last.  The underpaid employees who begged for tips was off putting, as well as taking away my ability to use hotel service (luggage racks) on my own to force me to tip was upsetting.   

The rooms were beautiful and well maintained, the hotel look beautiful in all the gardens, and even though there was construction going on it was not inconvenient during the stay. 

It was an average stay, and I don't think we will make it a priority to come back. I am upset that the internal functionality and extra features like dining and services of the hotel does not match its visual ambiance.  Those changes would make this resort my go to summer retreat and would force me to recommend this hotel to everyone I meet.

i got there and the stupid workers wouldn't let me go in because of their beyond dumb curfew at 6 pm if you're under 18. then when i asked the worker if that meant we couldn't go see the movie he like can't speak or something and just couldn't answer a simple question. such a waste of time and money. they didn't even make it clear when buying tickets that the curfew is 6. like no wonder they're going out of business. most idiotic movie theater of my life. wish i could rate it 0 stars. don't go here if u care about urself.

We have had so far a great experience with this furniture store.  We purchased some lamps and a bar. I did not give it a five star due to we haven't received furniture yet because our home won't be built for a couple more months. Susie has been helping us and she has been wonderful. I will re-rate once furniture is delivered. So far it has been a pleasant experience.

I love this place. The service is always so good, drinks are strong, food is delicious. They have done an exceptional job of adapting to COVID-19 restrictions and it was so nice to "be back" and feel some sense of normal. There is not a bad seat in the house. 
Thank you for all you do! Don't skip this place when you are in town!

Charged me for ranch and refills.  Not the nickel and dime kind of person.  Now with that being said, the food, service and atmosphere were A1.  We came on a Saturday evening around 5:30p just making happy hour and were met with smiles and great people.  Food service was fast and quality was great.  POTATO SKINS were the best I've had in a while.  Big chuck of potato, not stingy with the cheese and bacon.  Great appetizer!  I had the blackened mahi fishermen's Reuben...great great sandwich.  Wings were great too.  Overall, nice place for the money.  Great atmosphere and local place.  Would recommend for anyone.  The place is a Steelers bar and I'm a Bengal fan so imagine that lol.

I was told it is a great place for Italian food.  So I take my friends advice. 
The Host was a teenager that sat us in a middle booth.  There was a very dark curtain to the right and the tall booth walls were above our line of sight. I asked if we could sit in another location, he said they had to sit by the availability of servers. When our server came by, I again asked for another location. The dark red curtain was blocking any light. He indicated the owner had a had a small private party behind it. The server said they should be clearing out any minute and we could look out the windows.Our current view was of the rusted ceiling panels and the curved wooden ceiling beams, We felt like captives being held in the cargo bay of a ship. This was between 5:30 and 6:00 pm on Sunday night. The place was only at about one/third capacity. there were seats in many other areas. The booths were very snug for four people. We were constantly stepping on each others toes and bumping elbows throughout the dinner. 
Oh Yes, the dinner.  Well I will not rely on my friends advice in the future. 
Of the four meals, three were just OK and only one was pretty good. When you spend over $200.00 for dinner on four people, there is a slight 
expectation that there should be some enjoyment at that price.

What is the true advantage to being a member? The Apple watch series 6 44mm is $40 more than on Walmart online. My payment was on auto renew but not anymore. Don't tell me you will price match why am I doing the work when I'm paying a membership.

I'm pretty mad at myself for not stopping here sooner because this is by far one of the best deals for buffet in the area. The outside doesn't look too appealing and the interior is pretty similar in that it's just plain !! However The food is nothing of the sort , I'm talking super flavorful juicy meats that have that fresh grilled flavor and all the sides you could ask for. I was frantically looking for a spot on Christmas to eat and this happened to be one of the few. 

The buffet was about 25$ per person and there was kibbeh, lamb and eggplant stew, grilled chicken skewers, chicken kibbeh, hummus, baklava ,pita , lentil rice, falafel, pita, dill rice, dolmeh, taboulei salad don't know if I spelled that right lol and so much more. Oh!! I forgot there is this white yogurt sauce that is a must for the kibbeh. Since there was so much I'm going to list my standouts everything was great except the falafel which was too dry , minimal flavors and definitely needed to be dipped in a sauce of some sorts to make it edible. 

Kibbeh these brown chunks of grilled meat are spiced and grilled to perfection, this is a must try and pro tip use the white yogurt sauce with this !! You will be in heaven 

Lamb eggplant stew , I wish I knew the name of this but I was starving at the time and wanted to stuff my face :) anyways this is a slightly acidic dish due to the tomatoes used and the lamb is tender and savory. The eggplant just soaks up the juices and gives a nice texture to the lamb . Very good 

The baklava was decent because it didn't have much crisp I like a flaky crunchy baklava but flavor wise this was pretty standard. I also noticed there wasn't too much butter flavor or maybe my tastebuds were exhausted from the 3 plates i ate lol. This is truly great food that I will be coming back for soon

Omg! The Spaghetti is amazing !!!! I definitely would recommend! I remembered this restaurant from  like 2yrs ago and it's definitely worth the money. Prices are  just right! I drove 30min just to eat lol that drive was most definitely worth the ride. I will be going back.

The last few times that I came here it was rather quick but today I had to wait about an hour but it wasn't bad I guess it could've been worse some people say they been here and waited hours and hours so I got all my business taken care of so I don't think I'll have to be coming anytime soon hopefully that is

The partner that just got off work ordered five drinks thus causing a already slow team to slow down even further. They first made my hot latte that I ordered cold. I placed my mobile order at 4:28 and I have been waiting til now for my mobile order (the time is 5:08.) They need to get a grip.

I originally gave 4 stars about 6 months ago, and now I'm upgrading it to 5!

As a local dog walker, I often find coffee shops near my walking routes where I can sit down, relax, rehydrate and have a few nibbles of a snack.  I usually stop by in the early afternoon and this is a popular coffee shop, so if you see a table, grab it!  

My favorite items to order here are the jasmine pearl iced green tea, which is very flowery.  I tried out their light roast coffee it was the most delicious tasting coffee but I  have a sensitivity to lighter roasts, which are sometimes more acidic, but it tasted delicious.  I really enjoy the hot cider and it's very flavorful with the biscotti.  I like the Spindrift sparkling waters, especially good over ice. 

As far as the food offerings, I like the zucchini bread and other breads.  I enjoy the bagels, especially with fig jam, I love Dottie's Donuts, the biscotti and the polenta cake are some of my favorites.

It's very relaxed in this cute coffee shop.  The music is usually not loud, which is nice.  It is a sweet coffee shop, and at times is quiet but it's very popular and gets busy often.  A lot of people come here to work and I often do work on my phone or read.

I have found the baristas to be very friendly, knowledgeable about the different beverage and food options.  It has a welcoming atmosphere, especially with the plants along the windows.

Definitely one of my favorite coffee shops in the area!

I ate here two mornings while visiting from out of town. The first morning was excellent, it was my first time. The menu was extensive, food was delicious, and covid precautions were well managed. There was a 45 minute wait time which was accurate and they texted me to let me know when my table was ready. Great server as well. The second day was not as great, we were seated on the patio directly under the heater. The temperature was set on hell. We had to ask the take out runner if she could adjust the temperature before I melted into the floor like a drippy egg. Food was still good. The waiter we had was ok, seemed frustrated and not as attentive as other staff. I chalk it up as a busy Sunday. This will become my go to spot when in cherry hill but I dont want to be seated on the patio again.....thats for sure

I can't really see myself coming back here. Maybe as a Japanese girl who grew up in San Francisco my ramen standards are high, but the ramen just didn't do it for me. The broth wasn't authentic, there was too much stuff in the bowl and not enough noodles, and my egg was far from a soft boil...the yolk was completely solid inside. To me, this is like a ramen Cardinal sin! If you're looking for ramen in the city, I've gotta recommend Noodle & Pie hands down. As far as other stuff on the menu, we ordered spring rolls which were just okay (and not worth $10 for how small they were) and my husband got the burger. He also left more unsatisfied than he planned and was already asking when we can go to Company Burger to make up his burger craving. You've gotta be more on point than this place to do "Asian fusion" and make it in this city that is full of delicious other food and actual authentic Asian food. I hope this place improves for their own sake, but they still have a long way to go.

Stay Away! I stayed in filth for the duration of my nights. Human hair balls all throughout my room. Complained to front desk (Zoti I believe her name was each time, brunette shoulder length hair approx 5'5") Three nights in a row was told no one could do anything at this time that would clean in the morning. Never the less when I got back in in the evening my Bed was made but hair balls still remained through out.  Front desk Joanna (blondish hair, 5'1") didn't care wouldn't help me in anyway upon check out, incompetence on many factors here. If you like dirty rooms this is the go to place for you.

Good place to watch sports, listen to live music, or dance. The food is great and the drinks are good. This place has 2 outside patios and a restaurant inside. Good spot to bring people from out of town or locals.
The black bean dip served with the chips was pretty good, that was about it. My food was flavorless, which was very disappointing for this type of restaurant.

I'm not too sure why people love this place so much.  Yes, the wait and the lack of seating can be annoying, but that's not the reason for the 3 star review.  I don't care about all that stuff - all I want is good pizza.

But the pizza itself simply isn't that good here.

I got half plain and half sausage and mushroom.  The pie was too salty and cheesy for me, the dough wasn't anything special, and Joe was pretty heavy-handed with the olive oil.  The house-made sausage, by the way, was really good.  Everything else was so-so.  Even the crust/dough was alright.  I was impressed that the bottom was crispy without making the dough to cracker-y, but there just wasn't enough chew to it.

Now, I think some of it is a matter of personal preference.  After all, the closest comparison I can make is to Di Fara's in Brooklyn, and that place is legendary.  Still, I don't think it's all about personal preference.  True, I didn't love Di Fara's that much, but I still thought, "This is good pizza" even while acknowledging that it wasn't my favorite.  Joe Beddia, however, seems to put a heavier emphasis on cheese than Dom Di Fara, and that makes the pizza a bit too... much.

Still, I don't know why people are complaining about the vibe/service.  I thought the guys were super friendly and welcoming.

Bottom line: there's better pizza here in Philly.  My favorite is still Pizzeria Nomad.  For straight-up American-style pizza, I think Tacconnelli's is much better than Pizzeria Beddia.  I see no reason why I would ever return.

Four stars because Mad Donna's is really trying, and the owners are super friendly. I, too, miss the Radio Cafe even though I only went there a couple of times. The past is past, though.

Mad Donna's has become quite a hotspot in East Nashville, located just across the street from Lipstick Lounge. Quite a few of my friends go there quite often. 

I would avoid the fish tacos. I still haven't found any in town as good as Baja's, but I will keep trying. The sweet potato enchiladas were good, though. They also have solid nachos and other basics.

I've been for brunch one time and had a really good omelet. However, service was pretty bad. It was maybe the second weekend they were open for brunch, so I'm sure they were still working the kinks out. They also had plastic sheeting up outside which is a good idea to keep the bugs away, but the solid plastic sheeting had a sort of greenhouse effect. 

The bar inside is pretty nice, and there's also a bar/lounge upstairs with a dance floor. I think they have done a good job renovating the space, and once they get everything ironed out, this will be a favorite neighborhood haunt (and I'll cross the river to go there every now and then). And, like I said, the owners definitely seem like they want to get things right which is a big plus. Oh, and thankfully they have removed the table we were seated at right by the door one cold cold March evening.

Just got back from a wonderful trip to Santa Barbara and the Orchid Inn. Friendly and helpful staff, great breakfast and coffee, well apportioned room and a beautiful setting. What more could you ask for ? We had a great stay and had the place to ourselves - the "off" season is the only way to go. We walked to the beach each day and had wonderful dining experiences on State Street. We will definitely be back.

I'm giving 2 stars as a baseline simply out of respect for how amazing their art work is. I've contacted the main page, and attempted making appointments with 2 different artists...to no avail. The first, I asked about pricing and I was ghosted. Maybe that's bad form and he got insulted, which could be entirely my bad...but I made it clear that this was to be my first tattoo and that I didn't really know what I was doing. The second...I have no idea. I got an initial reply...then ghostville, USA...population me. If I did something rude or if my design idea was impossible: That's okay...but tell me. I'm a newbie, and I made that clear. Just don't ghost me. I know this is a business run by emo people...but we've all been out of 8th grade for a while now. Again, based on what I can see of their work...they seem worth the effort of trying to get in. Ultimately, I found another place.

This is a good place to grab a couple drinks and apps. They can accommodate large group and have a sizable outdoor seating area which is covered. I have been here several times and have enjoyed the rotating draft list and some of their apps. Would probably for elsewhere for a full meal.
Don't go for the the whiskey cocktails. Decent whiskey selections at over retail price. Food was pretty good,

Now I don't have to feel so bad about always ordering the pizza at Pastaria!

Porano is a trendy fast casual Italian joint from STL chef Gerard Craft. I finally visited Porano today and I am definitely impressed (not sure what I was expecting given this is a Gerard Craft restaurant). 

On my visit I had a pasta bowl with the Smoky Sunday Sugo, meatballs, Parmesan, and sweet peppers. The sugo was fantastic with its tomato backbone bolstered by the taste of smoked pork. The pasta was also cooked to a perfect al dente. I also got a dark chocolate gelato pop which was a satisfying conclusion to the meal!

The space feels modern and urban to match its setting in downtown St. Louis. After visiting, I really wish I worked close enough to grab lunch here at least once a week (unfortunately my office is waaayyy out in chain restaurant laden Chesterfield). 

Overall, I would describe Porano as Pastaria lite. The pasta isn't quite as elevated as Pastaria's but that's to be expected at a fast casual restaurant. I look forward to coming back to try the Detroit style pizza!
Food is decent, lazy cooking. Got an omelette put the filling on top of the food. My hash browns were only cooked on one side

Overpriced food and drinks. Happy hour is the only time you should come. I tried the veggie burger...horrible.  Why is it so red?  A friend highly recommended it, but wasn't for me.  Fries were good. My friend thought the $5 Monday night burger was very average.  

Lovely atmosphere, great for a date.

This is a very nice place. Masks are required to enter and sanitizer is provided upon entry. We visited 2 days in a row while visiting Tampa for spring break. The waitstaff were excellent. The first day we had Pam and the next day we had Dericka. They were both very attentive and got both orders right! My son had the Mr and Mrs crab seasoning mild and I had the garlic butter seasoning hot. They were both really really good. Being from Texas, I appreciate the fact that we are able to get fresh crawfish. That was the only downside BUT the waitress did advise me that they were previously frozen before ordering. I took my chances and they were not very good. Nonetheless, everything else was seasoned to perfection and very filling. We will definitely be back when visiting Tampa again!
Pizza and bread sticks were great. I actually know what fresh veggies taste like on pizza now.

I really like this place, cool concept, very tasty.  Unfortunately there are so many drawbacks.  The line can get very long making your meal a very long night.  Service is hit and miss as the staff here seems to turnover weekly.  There are so many options in the same area, so make sure you have a good 45 to 60 minutes if you are coming here.

You disappoint me yelp. In no world should a restaurant that serves gumbo, jambalaya, and red beans and rice this delicious ever be rated 3.5 stars overall.

I got the sampler plate, which serves the three above mentioned dishes. It was the type of delicious, authentic Cajun food you dream of when thinking New Orleans. All incredible.  

It is a dive bar, and maybe that is the cause of some of the negative reviews? I've heard it can get a little rowdy late at night, and the seating is certainly limited. I don't care, I'll be back again and again for the delicious Cajun food.

I was cursed out by the drive through attendant when I joked about them not having any donuts available. I'd normally let that slide, but I had my kids in the car. So I responded in kind, and then was verbally assaulted by the attendant(s).

It's okay to not be ready for the job. It's certainly expected in places like this.

Seems like others have had great experiences at Walk-on, we did not.  Our experience was a disappointment - great location, close to the Super Dome, so parking is tricky, but we arrived later in the evening so did not have parking issues. 

The concept and history are compelling - two buddies that were "walk-ons" to the LSU basketball team got together to open the sports bar.  Concept is good - each booth has its own draft of three biers.

Apparently, Walk On conducted maintenance on the storeroom where they keep their kegs, as a result there was NO DRAFT BIER.  A sports bar with NO DRAFT BIER?  As for bottled bier, well, I thought that being in NOLA, they would have the local brew, Abita bier...nope.  ??????????  Also, being in NOLA, I wanted to eat some crawfish - nope, none on hand.  So I ordered an appropriate Louisiana meat, gator in a wrap...pretty good - tasted NOT like chicken, but more like beef.  The group that I was visited the establishment ordered battered fried onions and nachos - pretty good portions and taste.  

So food was decent, but no crawfish and NO DRAFT BIER nor ABITA bottles - disappointing.  May consider returning, but there are other options in NOLA that WHEN open, will have draft BIER and crawfish...

We went here on a whim on the last day of our trip. We were excited to find an affordable place that gets pretty good reviews. We were there mid-afternoon so most of us were in the mood for lunch foods as opposed to breakfast foods.

I just really wanted a salad. Their large romaine salad (which literally comes with lettuce, a few halved cherry tomatoes, a sprinkle of parmesan cheese and a side of toasted bread with pesto) is $8.00. Uhhh, that's a bit steep for a basic salad but I really wanted it. I actually added shrimp to it which cost another $6.50. Yikes. Okay $14 is steep but I figured it was going to be BIG and really good. Turns out it wasn't very big and it was SO BASIC!! I was so disappointed. My boyfriend ordered a reuben and seemed to enjoy his sandwich. Our side of sweet potato fries were barely warm, at best. 

Our service was also pretty poor. Our server was less than friendly, got one of my friend's order completely wrong, brought out my salad without the shrimp at first (didn't even notice that it was missing before he set it in front of me), disappeared for long periods of time, and we had to ask to have our waters refilled. Basic stuff here, folks. Nothing crazy.

I don't know. I guess everything we had in New Orleans was just so good...surpassing expectations is every sense. I was majorly disappointed by our experience at Stanley's. Looking through pictures, perhaps breakfast is where it's at. But I certainly won't be back, no will I recommend this place to friends.

Greetings,
I visited this office site three weeks ago. The worst mistake,  I arrived at 12pm and fell asleep in the chair. Then I woke a up from extreme pain I was in at 1:00pm. I told the medical assistant to cancel my visit and I will drive to the hospital, due to I couldn't wait any longer, she wasn't to pleasant about the cancellation. "Luckily I drove to hospital, I could of past away, they keey me.This place need to be professional "front desk staff"

Yum! I brought my parents here for their anniversary. I ordered the seafood boil with clams, shrimp, and mussels and the "juicy" seasoning. So good! Large shrimp, fresh tasting seafood. It came with a half corn on the con and potatoes. My dad ordered the fried oysters and my mom ordered the fried shrimp. Decent portions, not too greasy. Clean restaurant, attentive staff. I'm a fan!

Went here for breakfast on a Saturday; seemed to be a good mix of people eating breakfast or just drinking coffee and working. Decor was very cute and the space was clean and inviting.

Coffee was good and hot; it seems like this is their specialty. Many people were stopping by for to-go coffee. 

The food left a bit to be desired. I ordered the farmers omelet which was quite tasty. However I also ordered a side of house-made sausage; while the sausage had great taste, it came out cold. It seemed like it had been pulled out of the fridge and just barely seared on one side on the griddle. 

Overall our service was good, but I expect the server to come over soon after the food is delivered to ask how things are. By the time we flagged our server to the table it had been several minutes. He was gracious enough to put in a new order for sausage, but by the time it came out the omelet was cold. 

My girlfriend ordered the standard with bacon. The bacon was extraordinarily salty and unevenly cooked. Additionally while I like the idea of the haystack potatoes, something a little more absorptive like hash browns or home fries would be better to pair with fried eggs in my opinion.

In summary, great place to get a coffee (or tea, I presume). The service was team-based and generally pretty good. Food preparation and execution needs significant improvement; I hope it comes because this location has a lot of potential.
This is a "must do" every time we come to Gulfport.  We love their food and atmosphere.

I'm eating while I type from my iPhone all just to laud this new addition to rittenhouse! An update to come but be sure to stop by next weekend for a healthy oh soooooo yummy treat! The mere fact that I'm doing this as I am is testament to its goooooood!

It was the best of times it was the worst of times.  My seafood entree, buffalo shrimp and french onion soup were delicious.  Then came the rest. My chicken parm was overcooked and dry. The chicken in my BLT wrap was too dry.  Finally my chicken wings were hard as a rock and inedible.  It's almost as if two different chefs made my dinner.  I don't know if I would order from here again, but if I did, i will avoid the chicken.

Really like this place and especially that they take time and prepare food from scratch. I love their vegetarian platter, chicken schwarma ( prob misspelled it:)) sandwich or platter. It's clean, spacious, Lebanese music in the background and service friendly. One of my regular spots to eat in the area. Wish pita would be better.
Fantastic place to eat! We love coming here. Great service, great people, good times! Try the fried pickles it's my favorite app! The steaks are the cooked to perfection. Love it

Great, comfortable atmosphere. It's elegant, yet such an open and spacious building. It reminded me of restaurants I've visited while on vacation in Hawaii. Maybe a little bit like a Colorado steakhouse in the mountains. If that's confusing, I'll just say that it's not what I'd expect to see in Tampa or Florida for some reason. The atmosphere was very different and unique. It's nice, but not "stuffy." 

Foodwise: I got the Filet Mignon. The sides were asparagus, baked potato, and the bacon-wrapped scallops as an appetizer. The filet was amazing. I love the tenderness and texture....or absence of texture. It's like cutting through butter. It reminds me of what I have had at Flemings. They have a very nice selection of meat, including offerings of buffalo and some nice Kobe. The asparagus was really great, very crispy. The scallops were as well. All of the food was crisp and saturated with that iconic smokey flavor. I think that they cook everything together over an open flame. It all has the same flavor and crispness. 

Price wise: I think that Charley's is a great value. It's not too terribly expensive. (not cheap by any means) You could go there and eat on a budget....or you could go there and have an extravagant dinner. It's flexible, really.

Ordered a pizza for dinner. Ordering online was easy. the Pizza even arrived fast. Then the incompetent driver/delivery person showed up looking dirty and disheveled, wearing a sweaty dominos hat, and without a greeting he tells me the total of $25.78. Great customer service. Whatever, So I go to hand him a $50 and he asks me if I have anything smaller. I did not. he asked me how much do I want back. presuming a tip I imagine. not that big of a deal cause I figured drivers don't carry a lot of change and I planed to tip my usual $5. He tells me he only has $10, and its in his car. we live on the 8th floor. I even offered for him the $50 and bring back the change. He tells me he not going to go looking for change.  So long story short He left here with the food. and Im placing an online order at Papa Johns. Fingers crossed papa johns drivers have a little more than $10 to their name. Thanks for Nothing Dominos !!!

I don't normally complain but this was sneaky and underhanded. 

While ordering food we asked for water, the waitress says "is bottled ok?" Um, I guess so. (I'm now wondering what's wrong with the tap water) 

When the bill comes we realize that the three waters cost about $10! It's a bit much and complain to the waitress because she didn't mention the up-charge. Her response was that she has a quota, and the fee was on the menu. Surprisingly poor response, and it makes it clear that it's a planned scam on the tourists. Boo :-(

Food was good, the vibe is nice, great view. Too bad I won't come back here

If there's one thing that actually makes me want to go to work everyday, it's the fact that Trader Joe's is located right on the next intersection. In fact, I used to be able to see the store from my office window, until I switched to the other side of the building... The staff is always kind and helpful, even too helpful sometimes (if I ask something about an item, it doesn't always mean you have to drag me to the place where the item in question actually is)... It's the go-to place for a quick lunch bye for those who hardly ever get time for an actual lunch, as well as for some desert to bring to a dinner at someone's house, or even quick dinner buys for that emergency "hey, we're coming over for dinner" email that you get at 4PM... It does get a little crazy during lunch and 5PM rush-hour so maybe an extra "express" lane (only have one lane with 2 registers today) would help... Also, the parking lot is actually pretty small and people often have to pay for parking... Although I am not sure what, if anything, can be done...

I walked in and there are employees leaning and eating over the counter by the food holders, and dirty tables and counters. Felt like I was intruding on their party, they could barely acknowledge that I was there. Now I know not to go back.
There is one employee that's a complete "know-it-all". I came in the other day and she repeatedly interrupted my order. I think her name is Taylor. Cold Stone needs to get their employees in line.

Portions are huge but pricey; $12 for a large cookie.  I HIGHLY suggest ordering 1 item and splitting between 3-4 people; that's how big it is.  The hot chocolate tasted like powder chocolate mix and the dessert also tasted as though it was baked from a prepared mix.  I don't recommend this place.  I felt sick to my stomach afterwards and didn't have much.

More event than meal! 

Hanging out with friends one friday in Largo, we laughed about how there's a lack of interesting places around town to grab a bite. THEN, this showed up in our Yelp search. We were ecstatic since none of us had ever tried hot pot or conveyor belt sushi and especially not in the unexpected location of a Largo strip mall.  

Overall I'd say the experience is a MUST when it comes to entertainment level. If you're doing hot pots, you'll need a whole lot of time, creativity, and be with a group of open minded dining partners. You're essentially choosing a broth, noodle, meat and sides that will make up your "soup," and cooking it up in real time. There was boiling over, splashing, overcooking, slurping, egg cracking. Basically, a whole lot of mess for what seemed to be a mediocre meal. 

Overall, our server was very informative, the business was modern and clean, and we loved that they also had boba tea on the menu. But, because it's such a novel idea, I don't foresee us going back anytime soon.
Service bad and the food order was incomplete sent back twice and vegetables on the burgers not fresh. Unacceptable in my book

If your debating taking one of the voodoo, haunted, or vampire tours in New Orleans come here the night before.  Almost all of them stop here for a watering hole.  It will give you a chance to talk to the people on all the tours and pick one based on their observations.  The do make one of the best hurricanes in NOLA as well.  Lots of history to see in this town.  And one of the last cool spots on Bourbon Street.

I purchased balloons had to be blown up...had 2 stand n the long line...only 1- 3 employees in the WHOLE store wrkn always at any party city...lady before me was taken care of...phone customers were taking care of...THE (WHITE) LADY BEHIND me (had a online order)...meaning her order was ON THE TRUCK ( I'm still  here in line NXT.) "Team Leader"John (I think) NOT BALLOONS LEADER comes back proceeds to answer customers on the phone again & blowing more balloons still here about 15 mins now ...told him that wasn't my order wen he finished off the phone...he said those ppl were nxt in line but when he called them they was NOT NXT IN LINE- THEY WAS NOT EVEN THERE....I WAS!...so now I'm finally next he gets smug with his next comment busy busy he says... IM LATE 4 MY SONS BDAY DINNER. I will search all over antywhere else b4 I continue 2 let any party city facilities decor my functions again! Worst experience ever with them....O p.s. he didn't even blow up & give me all my balloons had to go back!

BARTENDERS WANTED! (Needed...)
Food: Great! 
Beer: greater! 

Bar service: non-existent.......

This is not a 1 time review, rather the culmination of 6-10 visits in the last 6+ months. 

Again, BEER AND FOOD AWESOME. 

However, the bar does not seem to have actual bartenders, rather bus boys and bar backs. Most of their time is spent making drinks for the restaurant (again is great) but pay little attention to bar patrons, both in getting getting tabs, and overall building very basic rapport. 

Competition is coming. Stay complacent, and it will be an unfortunate day for Iron Hill and Media. 

(Iron hiller since '04..WC)

The friendliest crew around. They always get our order right, unlike other DDs I have visited. Sometimes there is a wait, but never too bad. There are only a few tables to eat in, but it's usually manageable. It's conveniently located in Market East Station..
Updating because the Salon emailed and apologized for their mix-up.

I was really excited to try out the sister restaurant to the Fork. This restaurant has a ton of hype surrounding many saying one of the premier "Italian" destinations in Boise. It is definitely a trendy place; wasn't an empty table in the house you will need reservations in order to get a table most nights. The customer service was top notch!! My water glass was never more than half empty always being refilled. The waitress was on point the whole night; she knew the menu like the back of her hand. She was very knowledgeable of the menu and helpful in finding you the right dish. For me; the food is where it fell flat. I don't know I ordered a few different things to give me a sampling of their menu. The appetizers ordered I thought was a pan fried mozzarella cheese ball; more like a pan fried rice ball with marinara sauce. I wasn't a huge fan plus it didn't even really seem very Italian a pan fried rice ball with marinara sauce. The salad was very fresh; the house dressing the waitress raved about an Italian dressing they make in house didn't really taste much of it; it was very fresh yet lacked flavor in this case the taste of Italian dressing. Lastly I ordered the rigatoni with short rib. I am still scratching my head on the Italian classification of this restaurant. They make all their pastas in house that part I get. This dish has absolutely no flavor/no pop to it at all. It was just rigatoni and "shredded beef". It literally was just an overpriced bowl of noodles with shredded beef. For me, the flavor didn't pop at all in any of the 3 dishes I ordered. I found the food bland and not even close to Italian. I walked away impressed wit the ambiance and customer service yet the food isn't going to be be bringing me back anytime soon. Maybe I missed out on it somewhere to me a northwest spin on Italian is now just high priced house made noodles and flavor wise isn't anything close to what one would expect in an Italian restaurant.
This place is okay. not worth the price in my opinion.. BUT their ice cream sandwiches are too die for!

I don't think I've ever encountered a place quite like Thai to Go. I strongly suggest it to anyone in the Roxborough/Manayunk area. They have some of the best prices and flavor in town for their entrees, though I would stay clear of their disproportionately expensive appetizers.

I will probably no longer be going here. I say that because I do not like the vibe in the salon- they could be more welcoming and kind. Secondly, in my opinion it is unprofessional to have your children literally running around the salon. Thirdly, I am not happy with how my nails look a mere 1 wk after my full set. 3 stars bc at times my nails looked great, other times not so much.

I love this place. I've been there multiple times and always find it satisfies my cravings for good hearty homey meals but with a bit of a twist. First, their club sandwich is awesome, love it. I also have ordered their omelette breakfasts, pastrami sandwich, etc. My favorite though? They have killer cakes. I love their strawberry shortcake, so yummy. Pricey? A bit, but really, you are not eating a $5-6 dollar sandwich at Quizno's so I wasn't expecting those prices to begin with. I love the ambiance of their patio, I only sit on the patio. Service has always been great, and I was a restaurant mystery shopper for years at fine dining establishments. Overall, a great restaurant to fill those cravings and will always be a repeat customer. I've recommended friends and they've all enjoyed it as much as my husband and I do.
had the hush puppies here  they were great golden on the outside just right on the inside  it was a great treat

It only earned 2 stars because the wait staff was nice and the view was nice. The food was very disappointing. I try to go to a restaurant twice before I leave a review so this was my second time here. The first time was the same as the second time.  

The peanuts hummus tasted like peanut butter. I'd think for the price , the shrimp would be peeled. It was 14 dollars for about 10 shrimps but you had to fee then yourself. The Mac n cheese was runny. 

The lobster bake tasted like chorizo and overpowered the taste of the lobster. The pealla was oily and it seemed as though it was just fried rice with scallops  and shrimp sauteed separately and put on top of it. The shrimp on top was super tiny. Did not taste like the rice was prepared with the seafood like a traditional Paella. The fish and chips looked over cooked and was bland. The potato wedges were not cooked thoroughly. They need to redo the menu and change some of the recipes.

I've been a customer of Plato's Closet for many years, and I've defiantly seen ups and downs in the way of customer service, management, organization of clothes, selection, and what they will buy when you bring them clothes. 

Currently, the Fishers location is very well organized, with a good selection. However, I truly feel that they don't put the higher end items that they buy from people on the floor. But rather sell them somewhere online. There are NO real leather purses or jackets, NO high end brand names, and the jewelry looks like you could've gotten it @ Chuck-e-Cheese. I know this stuff gets taken in sometimes, so where does it go??? 

Also...I'm pretty certain that the "40%" of the selling price, that they say they give you, is a little on the low side. 

Attitudes of the employees have been at times (in past years)  to the point that I won't  shop there, but then the staff got pretty friendly. Have to say, at this point though....it's about 50/50. 

Overall though, the quality of the selection is at the highest I've ever seen it

TIP: Make reservations, then come in complete company or you won't be seated!
We waited for awhile, since the rest of us are parking their cars.

We're having brunch here today.

I ordered Lancaster Three Egg omelette and my wifey ordered Green Meadow Double Cheddar Burger. 

Order took awhile on a Sunday, busy kitchen? 

The entrees we ordered are freshly made. Ingredients are organic, cage free chicken eggs and seasoned beef patties. Its delicious. 

Serving staff are courteous and helpful. Serving are in moderate portions.

Clean, very tight neutral gender bathrooms, four of them. No changing table. 

One of the best farm to table restaurants in Philly. 

No ramp access for wheelchair person in need.
Recommend the nachos and the beef tacos, nice local place with good food and cool decor

We have been there twice for breakfast.
The wait staff is very friendly and the food is fresh  and very good.  Go early !!

Visited the area from our hometown of Dallas where we have a billion awesome restaurants. Driving down quaint Main Street to get a feel for the area, took a chance on this attractive looking patio. AMAZING experience! AWESOME service, food and experience. GO THERE!!!

This place does an excellent job of looking and feeling like one's living room, with just the right amount of personal and cozy touches -- the inviting couches, the wall shelves with small vases of dried flowers and books.  I didn't feel like the menu was terribly extensive but I liked what they did have.  The base prices are pretty inexpensive, like around $6 for Belgian waffles, but everything has an add-on cost, like if you want to add fruit or jam.  I got mine with a blueberry compote jam, which was very good.  I was a little wary about coming on New Year's Eve for fear of long lines but maybe precisely b/c it was New Year's Eve, the place was pretty deserted so we had most of it to ourselves.  The staff were all very friendly and welcoming, which is definitely a plus.  Overall, minus what I felt was a somewhat limited menu and a single restroom, I enjoyed my experience here.

The bartender smokes.

There was a group of 20-somethings vaporizing tobacco (or some such) and being generally boisterous, but you must keep in mind it IS a sports bar. Also explains the deafening NFL broadcast to a nearly empty pub.

Nice selection of beer, the polish dog I had was surprisingly OK for a mini mall pub.

I saw (bar) pool tables but did not opt to play.

I -think the place is OK, but nothing special. They need to be smoke-fee, though

Dream tea is a classic for bubble tea. There have been a lot of newer bubble tea places opening up in Edmonton but it's still pretty good. There is a large selection of flavors available; however, unlike other chains that have opened, the sugar, ice, etc is not as customizable. Dream tea offers food snacks that are fairly tasty but I find them to be a little expensive. This location has renovated and looks much more modern and cleaner than it has in the past.

Clean bathrooms on both levels & friendly store staff. Note that this location is where multi bus vendors depart- so be aware that certain times are busy with bus customers; And if a bus customer, they prefer u wait outdoors. I was there to catch a bus to Lewiston, & found it easy to move my bag thru the store as I picked up snacks for the bus ride. A definite recommend.

I like going to Walmart, but sometimes I avoid it because it does not have the same quality of service or cleanliness as Publix. When I came here I expected to find the same, but I was impressed with this Walmart. Usually when yo go to Walmart you always find things unorganized and on the floor, boxes in the isles. This Walmart had everything organized, and I found the things that I was looking for vey easily.

When I went to check out I was greeted with a smile, which is unusual for Walmart, but a very nice surprise either way. The cashier was very nice and even had a conversation with me while scanning my things, she also helped put the bags in the cart, and that kind of serve is always nice to receive. This Walmart is really nice, and I recommend.

Sorry, I just can't love this place as much as everyone else. I eat a lot of Thai food, and this place just doesn't quite measure up. The prices are higher than typical Thai restaurants. The restaurant is small, so the wait is long for dinner. The pad thai, with it's clear noodles and fish sauce base, is blander than what I'm used to with pad thai. I usually get the Khao Kai Ra-bert, which is minced pork with garlic with fried egg and rice. The egg is usually overdone and the garlic pieces are chopped too large. The flavors are strong in this dish, which is why I like to order it when I come here rather than the noodle dishes. Perhaps it's a favorite because it seems to have the reputation of being a more healthy Thai place. But for better flavors and cheaper prices, I'd go elsewhere.

Was told that i could wait at bar during my 20 minute table wait and have a drink by hostess.  I made my beer order and watched 4 tables get drink in front of me.  Then told that i can only get a drink when my table is assigned.  Ibwalked out!

Ordered the veal marsala. The veal was a little tough and the pasta overcooked. Called it in as a to go order and arrived 25 minutes later and still had to wait 15 minutes. I could see if it was packed but it was 8:00 on a Tuesday night.

If catering, this place is awesome, if buying food for just your household, it's overpriced and not as amazing as the catering (the opposite I feel is true for Tastebuds... catering, not so great buy reg food muy bueno).  I have been to two parties catered by Bacchus... always delicious and eclectic appetizers and dishes abound!  I went there over the summer, got a smoothie that was neither smooth nor tasty and on another occasion, tried an orzo that had very little flavor.  

Additionally, the cashiers I encountered never seemed happy to have to deal with customers (or people in general).  I agree with Ben D.: a little smile can go a long way!  I give this place 3 stars for great catering but not so great in-store food and peeps.
Place was closed when I arrived. Call me old fashioned, but businesses need to honor their posted hours! Please change your open hours on Google and Yelp!

Strong winds and snow accumulation couldn't keep these guys down. We called six different pizza restaurants during a recent snow storm. Skippack pizza was the only ones open. I was surprised because the whole town was without power. As I pulled up to the store, it was pitch black. I thought they were playing a prank on me. However, I mustered up some strength and walk into this pitch black building. To my surprise, they were taking orders and cooking by candlelight! I admire their dedication to serve our local community. Plus the pizza was delicious!

Smoothie was delicious, don't get me wrong. But their inconsistency with enforcing mask rules is why this place is getting 3 stars. There are 2 or 3 signs on the front door saying that masks are required to enter the establishment. Yet, a woman walked in with no mask on and no employees batted an eyelash or asked her to wear one, and she was promptly served. Everyone else in the store had a mask on. If you're going to place signs on your door requiring masks but don't enforce it, that is not fair to the patrons who are following your rule. Too bad the smoothie was so tasty.

Wow omg this was amazing! I tasted the peanut butter, the snickerdoodle, and the funfetti because they were all dairy free. All three were amazing but I went with the funfetti and ate it plain. They have toppings options but it was so good just by itself so I didn't add anything. I will definitely be back when I'm back in town!!

We thought it was really good! Love that you can sample the chicken salad before you order. LOVE the pimento cheese. The lemon basil was very tasty but the Dixie Chick is so good!! I thought the croissant could have had more salad on it. A little thin layer but very tasty. The little cookie melts in your mouth. A nice change from a burger place!!

Honestly, just okay. It's nothing to write home about. My family eats 90% vegetarian so this place used to be our regular joint. But recently, I tried the chicken from here and it was.. bad. It tasted like the freezer. It was funky and didn't taste fresh.

I would say if you're vegetarian, their potato enchiladas are yummy. But if you like the meats and appreciate freshly cooked meat, you may be in for a surprise.

The salsa is damn good though. The chips can be really hit or miss, sometimes great and other times oily and stale. 

I don't think I will be back, but I will miss the choriqueso. That shit is bomb.
delicious food; rich flavors; EXCELLENT service.  A bit on the pricey side, but whatever.  Ya gotta go.

Chef Rio has brought real sushi to Nashville.

I complained that there was no good sushi in Nashville, so my friend took me here for a true omakase experience. We called ahead because Rio only serves one group at a time in the restaurant (COVID-19 regulations). I was suspicious, since it's a 'mongolian bbq' place... but for those 3 hours, my mind was blown. It's a shame I didn't have a camera on me, because there were so many dishes and fish that I had never eaten before - and all were incredibly fresh and well-prepared. My friend who is a regular customer there mentioned that every time he's had the omakase, there has never been a repeat in the menu. Basically, Rio is a genius.

I will say that if you don't know your stuff, it'll be harder to appreciate. But if you want high quality sushi, get it from Rio. Order takeout platters for a big group, or if you want quality but don't have time/$$ for the omakase, the chirashi is BEAUTIFUL. He packs a ton of fish onto a special mushroom fried rice... you have to try it.

Tasty, delicious coffee is just a hop, skip, and jump away from the Camino Real Marketplace if you're looking for a good alternative to Starbucks. The French Press's space is clean, well-maintained, and well-lit, which should lift your spirits if you'll be working on something at the shop. It's also a great place to meet up with a friend or a co-worker if you're also on the hunt for a great drink.

I got a vanilla latte when I was there and at $4.50 for a small, it was the perfect size for me, an infrequent caffeine consumer. It's rich and not at all acidic, lacking the burnt taste of some coffee beans. Dune's beans are awesome, honestly. My friend's matcha latte was also a good option, lacking the abundance of sugary sweetness that some cafes add to their drinks. They also have some cool options, such as getting a whole pot of tea or their iced tres leches drinks.

Their baristas are also friendly and personable, and from what I could hear with their interactions with other customers, they really get to know their customers. Listening to patrons as they got to know the faces making their drinks was heartwarming. 

All The French Press is missing are outlets. On one hand, it promotes flow of traffic, so that people don't sit there for ages and hog all the seating. However, as a college student who wants to escape Isla Vistan coffee shops and the campus library for some high-quality coffee and wifi, it sucks to be booted the second my laptop dies. I'd suggest installing one or two, if only just to watch people fight to the death there.

You have Lavazza espresso for my latte?  YUM.  That is enough for me to give a 4 star review.  Lavazza make a fantastic coffee.  I would even say it is worth a visit just to drink the lattes.  The gelato was good as well.  I had the rum raisin gelato, which went really nicely with my latte.  The lady behind the counter was really nice as well.  I'm sure I'll be back soon.

:  We believe that your position regarding your cancellation policy is outrageous and heartless given the facts that1) the French Quarter Festival has been cancelled; 2) the Governor of Louisiana and the Mayor of New Orleans have declared states of emergency because of the Covid 19 virus, and 3) President Trump has also declared a state of emergency.  In light of these developments we believe you are arbitrarily hiding behind your cancellation policy.  An enlightened business would waive its cancellation policy or at least offer a future credit toward future stays as the cruise lines and airlines have done.

This place was SO good. They let us place a reservation 50 minutes in advance, plenty of parking spots. The decoration is amazing! Even though is indoors, you don't feel unsafe and tables are social distanced. Service was 10/10. Food was AMAZING. The pizza was my favorite part. 100% coming back. Love it

I can't figure out what everybody's raving about. I went there the other day and ordered three chicken strips. I added a side of sauce for $0.32. my total came to $4.54. I noticed the chicken was not greasy. I was surprised since most fried chicken is. I found the breading to be unseasoned and tasteless, as well as the chicken itself. I thought perhaps it was just the breast meat not having as much flavor as dark meat, but then I bit into my last tender, and it was total mush. I thought, some people will say this is tender, but I think this has been marinated, and way too long. Over marinating will break down meat and make it mushy. This does seem like food a child would enjoy. I have to say that was my first and last time eating at Cane's. I was so unimpressed with the mush and also a little put out that I had to pay for their weird sauce.
This place is really good.  Try the smoked salmon with that cheese that they pair it with (I don't remember right now).  It's great.
Came to Al's late on a Saturday night with my buddy who has also never been. Anthony was great and made us feel right at home. Ps. The urinal is awesome!

So sad.....the menu is the same, but the taste was awful. My shrimp was sour (not eaten). Hubby was ill after eating. They did take my meal off of the bill, but overall so disappointing. The appetizer special is no longer available. I do believe this is under different ownership.

Photo on left was what I asked for, photo on right is my own hand. SPOT ON! Nang understood exactly what I was looking for. She communicated with me the whole time to make sure we were getting my desired result. This is a French hombre dip manicure. I also had a pedicure which was fine. The place is clean and new, with a nice sound system playing pleasant music. No TV. A little pricier than others but totally worth it in my opinion. Nice location in the heart of Media if you have other errands to run in the area.

If you love Mexican food and seafood THIS IS YOUR PLACE! 

The way I measure the Mexican restaurants is by the chips and salsa.  To my surprise not only did I receive chips and salsa as an appetizer but also fish ceviche!  Fish Ceviche Basically pico de gallo with fish (tilapia) mixed in, very tasty! My favorite dish is the Large Red Snapper dish! This is no skimpy filet; we are talking the WHOLE fish eyes and all!  Every dish I have seen come out of the kitchen would fill any grown mans belly meaning they aren't stingy with the servings.  There are a number of items on the menu that are meant to be shared with the whole table.  One popular item is an order of the crab legs which comes out in a bowl the size of the Stanley Cup! lol If you come on the right night you will catch live music by talented local artists who will serenade you and your guest if you'd like!  This is still a hidden gem on the Westside in my opinion so be sure to experience it before the whole city finds out and you have to wait in line!  

The service is always served with a smile and I was even given a bit of a history/geography lesson on my last visit about El Puerto de San Blas and Nayarit, Mexico.  Nayarit, Mexico is located on the west side of Mexico and is one of the smaller states but has a large pacific ocean coastline great for fishing and is the inspiration for the restaurant.  There are some incredible photos and sculptures all around the restaurant that come directly from Nayarit, Mexico that you must see.  Make sure to greet the human sized frog sculpture at the front door!

Next time you are on West 38th street make sure you stop by!

The "to go" employee was not very nice. The customer service in that area is horrible. Also, I feel like the "to go" food is made poorly. The food when you dine in seems to be a low 4. But the the to go food is not very good at all. I had to call and speak to a manager just to get some steak sauce for my to go order.
Giant pizza is amazing here! Sandwiches and sides are also delicious. Have not dined in, only gotten take out on multiple occasions but food was always good. Online ordering is super simple

I don't expect much at 12:45am on a Monday morning. As a matter of fact, I expect the worst. Well, the Black Horse Tavern fell short of my expectations. 
I placed and order of wings at the same time, and in some cases, before my friends. I was by far the last to get my food. When it arrived, half of the oil from the deep fryer came with it. So much so, I placed my wings on a napkin to soak up the grease. 
Needless to say, I've been pissing out of my ass ever since. 
If I come back, it will be to eat some garlic Parmesan wings and then to drop an upper decker in the men's room roughly 15 minutes later.

Hands down my favorite breakfast sandwich EVER! Love the old school mom and pop feel of this place. We call ahead almost every time we order for fast pick-up and the price is right. Try the pepper, egg, and cheese on a long roll, it's to die for! I've also had their waterice many, many times...the super pineapple and super peach are amazing!

Pretty good, something different. I had the Cajun shrimp sandwich and my husband had the grilled beef sandwich. We shared shrimp spring rolls. I liked the jalapeño and cilantro in the sandwich as well as the pickled veggies. I would eat here again, don't know that I would go out of my way but if I was hungry and in the area I would :)

MVG gets a 5* for variety and selection. A 2* for value and a 0* for customer service.  They are rude and unhelpful.  In late February I stopped by and asked about the roses and was told rudely "They won't be available until the first weekend in April."  I tried to ask about a specific Rose I was interested in and the young man at the info counter ignored me and started talking to another customer.  In mid-April I stopped by to buy a planter.  The one I selected was quite heavy and one of the workers did help me move it to my cart but it didn't fit on the small cart I had so the worker told me I needed a flat cart.  The worker drove off in his cart so I thought he was leaving to get me a cart.  When he didn't return in 5 minutes it was clear that I was on my own and expected to go get my own cart which I did.  But I still needed help getting the large heavy pot onto the cart but there was no one to be seen.  I just left the dang pot on the ground where the worker had placed it and left.  Finally, today May 2 I stopped by to look for a butterfly Bush.  I told a worker what I was looking for and the worker asked, "What kind?"  I said I wasn't sure, what do you have.  Rather than take the time to help me select a plant she just said "they're over there past the cactus" and walked away.  That's it for me.  There are just too many nurseries in this city that treat their customers better.
Decent little place off the beaten track. It was hard to wrangle a waiter sometimes for a refill on water, but breakfast was good enough. I'd like to come back for the scotch eggs!

Not impressed. Mediocre at best.  Service was extremely slow even though there were only 3 tables in the entire restaurant.  Had to ask for salsa and more chips.  Wasn't offered another sangria until the bill was presented.  Had to ask for silverware.  Our quest for a good Mexican restaurant will continue.

Stopped by on the way to Frenchman st. As a tourist I asked my NOLA resident friend where I could find good coffee, and they point pointed me to Envie. 

The ambiance is total hipster/hippie. It seems like they have a good selection of lunch foods and snacks. I tried their mocha and it was great. Definitely better than the tourist spots, like Cafe du Monde and Cafe Beignet.
I ordered a large jerk chicken platter jerk and two juices and they charged my card $28.00 the menu says $14.00 for a large
food market.......has two tables for breakfast and coffee and usual hotdog table.........the cashier desk is square and surrounded by candies and cookies.

The fried chicken here is BOMB! Crispy fried on the outside and juicy on the inside. The mac and cheese side was okay - nothing special about it.

Came here during prime lunch hour on a Friday around 1 pm and the line was out the door. The wait would have been 40 mins and we didn't have enough time since we had a tour scheduled afterwards. We opted for takeout and got our food within 10 mins of ordering. This place is super secured. The security stand right by the door and carries a gun with him. Only reason I'm docking a star for this place is we were charged for 20% gratuity when we ordered takeout... there was little to no service other than placing out order ... Remember to review your receipt/order before leaving! We realized after we left and I left an additional $5 tip because I didn't know it was already added.

Cold Stone has always been one of my favorite places to go for ice cream. However, over the years with yogurt shops becoming more popular, I've seen more Cold Stone's around me closing than I have opening. This location is one of the last few around me- and I don't forsee it closing anytime because it is always crowded when I go. 

Just like most ice cream places they will let you try different flavors before buying- but I always have to stick with my favorite flavor: cake batter. 

It can be a little pricey- but you always get a good amount of ice cream. Be sure to look out for coupons or sign up for their e-mails to get a better deal.

Minus one star frankly 
Made Rez on open table 
Special request box - so I asked for a 'nice table' 
Last time I checked, this table is certainly not. We were bumped - I was bumped - the entire time as that was the superhighway  to the bathroom
Notwithstanding, food came out 
Blackened fish tacos were ice cold and certainly not black - pale white and cold - 
Even the taco shells were cold - it was gross.

Oh - and of only 2 wines by the glass of  Sauvignon Blanc  - Kim Crawford being one - "they didn't stock the bar" - so my wife waited 15 minutes for a glass...it's dec 23...what bar doesn't stock at the very minimum the wines by the glass at the bar...?
Place is shit - too bad as Palm Beach Gardens and West Palm provide a far superior experience.

They comped the meal but who really cares 
We'll never go back - ruined a holiday dinner -
Normally love this place. I ordered the AVOCADO, CORN & BLACK BEAN SALSA and literally got three tortilla chips to go with it. Not cool.

Good morning eats!  Small and busy but very friendly.  Had the chili cheese omelette with rye toast. Awesome. Kids had pancakes. My oldest couldn't get enough of the homemade coconut syrup. Mix of regulars and tourists. Coffee is hot but nothing to get excited about - it works and comes in big cups. One of your best bet for a good hot cheap breakfast in the area.

Food & drinks are really good. They have a large variety. I love their Caesars and bruschetta bread. And if you love chicken fingers, they are the best. Overall atmosphere is casual and relaxing. It is a nice place for a date.

Superb Service, Absolutely the best lobster bisque. My rib eye was cooked perfect and seasoned just right along with scumptious grilled asparagus. The bananas foster was delicious but there was just too much sauce that made the ice cream melt too fast for my liking which ended up  ice cream soup .

This is a fun bar/restaurant in historic Alton. I've had several different items on the menu and have never been disappointed as everything is delicious, especially the fried ravioli. The prices are very reasonable and I will definitely go back the next time I'm in the area.
Not impressed. I think it must be a good place if you are a regular.....

Kind of split down the middle when it came to my experience at EM. On the one hand their professionalism was exceptional. They were extremely polite. Probably the most polite attendant I've ever had. There was absolutely no excess pressure for other services that I usually get from other lube shops. But the most impressive thing was how quick they were. The visit had no line up and they had me out in 10-15 mins. Well, I'm not sure if that was the actual time that passed, but it felt like the Usain Bolt of lube shop service. In and out, just like a robbery!

That being said, 60$ for a simple oil change! That's either very pricey or my brain is trying to ignore it's age and the inflation that had accrued in that time frame.

Regardless, EM's has given me something to think about. Perhaps a cheaper option next time would provide a good comparison. If that speed continues then it could be a justified premium. As anyone who has studied corporate finance would tell you, time is money!

Wow... so first I go to the David dr location which states it closed at 10 and the guy was already counting the drawer when I pulled up at 9. So I called the clearview location where the guy tells me we close at 10. But not once did he mention until I walked in at 915 that the kitchen closed at 9. Won't be back.

I have been using this facility for my dogs for years.  I have had 3 different dogs boarded here from as little as 2 days to as many as 2 weeks and they are always taken care of.  The staff is kind, caring and always energetic when dealing with the animals from all of my experiences.  They havent let me down so I will continue to use them in my future boarding needs.

Not sure why it has such high reviews but it was not worth my walk there. Nothing close to an authentic croissant which should be flaky on the outside and Fluffy on the inside. Instead it was soft and oily. Ham and cheese one tasted like a homemade sandwich instead of one from a patisserie and we were so disappointed. The cafe au lait and latte was just bland. Stick to cafe Du Monde for the cafe au lait. It's one of the rare places that actually lives up to it's hype and reviews.

I know Chinese...this is NOT very good.

Yeas, the deliver aspect is very handy, but the food was...just...BAD. Hey, if a few people liked this place....good for them. But I can tell you this: There are WAY BETTER places in the area (aka: China Bistro). The issue is...you have to pick it up.

Hey, as a fellow Hoosier, I know that many of us have not experienced really good Chinese...but...ya know...whatever fills your belly.
Missy was amazing! She placed my party in a quiet little corner and hooked us up with our champagne!! Best bottomless mimosas for such a great price!

In all my years of eating at many Cracker Barrels, this was absolutely the WORST food I have ever encountered!  We were celebrating family and friends birthdays and I was so embarrassed to have chosen such a terrible restaurant!  I guess the fact that the restaurant wasn't crowded at the peak of dinner time should have clued me in, but I really wasn't expecting such terrible food!  Our server was new but nice, and he had a lot of trouble getting the right side dishes to us.  I ordered the signature cheeseburger platter with an extra side of fried okra - and when my plate came out, I received a horribly charred burger and okra - no fries or slaw. For the first time in my life, I actually sent the burger back - it was just inedible!  Then after waiting until everyone else had finished their meals, I finally received a new burger - the WRONG burger!  I got the nasty maple bacon one, which I could not stomach.  And the bun must have been recycled from the first burger because they had nuked it until it was so tough I couldn't even cut it with my knife!  I couldn't eat it at all.  THEN most of us ordered fried okra, because it has always been a favorite of ours, and it was practically inedible.  Mushy on top and tough on the bottom and had almost no flavor.  VERY disappointing!  So, overall I only choked my way through the okra and did not get any dinner.  THEN when my husband went to check out, he had to wait and wait at the cash register while waiters were getting their tips cashed out.  SO disrespectful of paying customers!  You can trust me on this, we will NEVER eat at this restaurant again - and if this is any indication of a corporate-wide problem, we will never eat at any Cracker Barrel again!

By and large the store employees are amazing people (5 star). Here's the big but:
The Hampton Bay cabinets are of substandard quality. I had numerous defects and went all through the procedures for rectifying the problems which included faulty drawer glides; however, the manager and the company and the cabinet rep did NOT stand by their products. I would also caution people against the Anderson 100 series windows the hardware for them are substandard as well. The higher-quality Anderson reputation is for the higher numbered windows.

After careful going through Xmas cards founds some that were 4.99.  On hour layer finding special cards I needed. I went to check out. They box of cards that said 4.99 on them rung up at 20.00 I showed the cashier which explained to another which the other just pulled the ticket off and said we are not selling them for the price that was on the box. I ask if she was going to honor the price that was on the box when I choice then she said she could not if people put stickets on the boxes. Which was very dis respectful. I had over 60.00 worth of cards to pay for but because I was so rudely spoken to I choose to leave everything and will not be back. I would advise to check the prices when they ring things up.
This place is a lot of fun. I just love that it's lit only with candles and they have a piano planning covers. Always a good time.

While the show is a little above average and the price reasonable, it is certainly not worth a 3+ hour wait. An important note is that once you reach the entrance to the grounds, you will be in line for at least three hours. So all of the time you spend on Keystone or Fall Creek just adds to that. Not to mention there's no way out once you turn into the grounds. It's almost offensive that the organizers set it up this way. Asking people to buy tickets in advance and giving them a scheduled window to show up would solve everything. Sure they may sell out and it would likely limit the crowd, but I can't imagine there is anyone who it OK with wasting hours upon hours sitting in a car just to see some decent Christmas lights for 15 minutes.

MUST READ: Planned a trip to NOLA & Read all these reviews on yelp, so me and my friends decided to go. Our waitress was an older lady who immediately showed us she was very upset. Took our order & after our terrible sour mix margaritas we got some pretty decent onion rings so we were hopeful for our meal.  We got gumbo,jambalaya and she insisted that we get the 4 piece chicken wings so we could share but again very DISAPPOINTED. She came to ask how things tasted and we said we didn't like the Jambalaya &  asked if we could exchange or just have it taken off SINCE WE DIDNT EAT ANY OF IT BUT A SMALL SPOON PORTION. She laughed and said no, " the food is going to go to waste we didn't even eat it" in exact words she said " get a to go box and give it to the dirty people on the street" UM OK?! at this point I can't believe she even suggested that.. after her being very disrespectful, we just ask for our check ask if we could split it & before we finished asking she yelled no "all cash or one card". We paid and told her to keep the 12 cent change. As we head to the door to walk out she very loudly said "F*** you" and told us to hurry and leave. We told her it was a tourist driven economy & she should be nicer and she kept insisting to get out and continued to yell at us and said she didn't like tourist like us and said their was a Denny's close by. We did tell her we had way better gumbo and jambalaya at the market. And she yelled "I doubt it" . PLEASE don't go unless you want shitty service and want to be treated like nothing. Ps Denny's servers are way better at their job then you lady!!!!
Awesome place! Great sushi! Terrific Service! Excellent prices!

Came in at 830pm for a reservation for my friends bachelore party. The server was not the problem. The only problem was the two hour wait for our food. Our party was a party of seven and did not order anything out of the ordinary. Just ordered what was on the menu. The sever was calm and collective and brought us the right drinks. It was not his fault. When they finally brought out my food 230 hours later they brought me the cat fish instead of the aligator sandwich. Come on guys, almost three hours for you guys to not know what the fuck you're doing! Don't come here l. Nothing special, server was not the problem. The problem is the establishment.

Absolutely our favorite place for Mexican fare.  Always good service, always great food and atmosphere.  Never, EVER get tired of this place!!!  Lunch prices are a great bargain and after lunch stroll a few doors down to the authentic Mexican bakery and have a drool fest there....  Las Palmas -- We LOVE YOU!!!
Great stay at the Roosevelt!  And the spa..... Amazing!  Dana and Cassie were personable and absolutely fantastic.

Still "A-OK" but on the brink of "Meh."

My friend really likes this place, so we came back. The food is about the same, but there were some minor things that detracted from the experience.
