There is so much on the menu, including advertisements, that it is difficult to read. Actually, their coffee mugs advertise for other businesses as well, which is interesting. Our waitress kept telling us and another table that our food would be out shortly. I didn't really notice that it was taking a long time, but I her commenting on it so many times was strange.

As we ate, the lady at the table next to us apparently didn't get what she ordered, and it took two servers to straighten the situation out. Although this didn't happen to me, the mistake and how it was dealt with did not seem very professional.

Both times that I have come, I have never had to wait for a table, which is nice. And it you can bring your dog if you sit outside, which is cool. However, there's still nothing special about this place. 

I am willing to try their second location in Wesley Chapel when it opens to see how it compares.

Can't say I even tried it. Went there thinking I could get a regular breakfast and when I looked at the menu, I found nothing I would want except ridiculously expensive chicken biscuits. No thanks, I'll go to chik fil A and get an amazing chicken biscuit for way cheaper. It's way too bougie for me. Vegetarian biscuits? Lmao

If I could give 0 stars I definitely would. I went in today to get my sinuses taken care of after I called telling them the symptoms I am having, the lady said I didn't need an appointment. When I walked in the lady sitting there said I needed an appointment after I had just gotten off the phone with a different lady. The lady inside then proceeded to try to argue with me about what I was literally just told something else. I am not happy at all! This place is a freaking joke!

Honestly there was nothing special about this store from my experience. Yeah, they have a lot of inventory but most of it isn't what I was looking for. The man at the counter was abrupt and not willing to help you find the gun you are looking for. Seemed to me like he was distracted with trying to help several other people at once and it led to a very unpleasant experience in general. Their prices are also $150 - $200 more expensive than other gun stores in the area. I was really hoping for more here but sadly, they didn't deliver. Overall an average gun store experience by today's standards.

Sad to see what this place has become.  I've been coming here since it opened but I hadn't been in about 6 months.  I've always loved FH but when I went last night (a Friday night) I was disappointed to find that this place is much more a night club now, minus the music, than a casual beer garden.  I used to love coming here and seeing the great mix of clientele.  Now, it's teaming with meat heads, the frat type and women in heels and skirts.  The scene reminded me of a bad bar in the Rittenhouse area, like Ladder 15.  It's too bad because the beer garden concept is fantastic and the beer/food here is good.  Unless you want to be surrounded by douche bags, I'd recommend avoiding this place on the weekends.

Photenthic enough to get by but let's get brutally honest. It's still pho getable, pho one thing, the noodles on the pho was way too thick. The egg roll was way to greasy, it appears as tho it was fried in oil that has passed its expiration date. It was a pho-aile... Ok that one was forced but, pho realz tho... Service was ok, pho broth was ok. But who wants to pay for anything that's just ok. So if your nearby and craving pho, I would take a hard pass.

I love ramen. I get so excited when I find new places to have ramen. So I was super excited when a friend told me that St Louis had some ramen shops and he dropped this name. When I arrived and saw the menu my first thought was, if you're going to have ramen in your name, how are you going to only offer 4 ramen choices?

No matter, I ordered the seafood and my wife ordered the Vista ramen (pork).

Let me say the atmosphere was nice and the service was great, my only complaints were the food.

Also ordered the empanada appetizer. Many different flavors that simply didn't mesh well together. It was an A for effort and a C or maybe even a C- for execution.

The seafood ramen was soy/salty....not savory, not delicious, not "Omg I can't wait to come back and eat this again!" and unfortunately not very flavorful, the smoked shrimp that it advertised are ground up and laid on top which makes them unrecognizable as shrimp of any kind and the shrimp cakes that were added were also not really flavorful either. I could have made better seafood ramen for $16 than what I was served. My number 1 pet peeve is going out for food that I could have cooked better and for less. 

My wife's broth was pork flavored but not in a good way, sadly.

I've eaten at some amazing ramen places and always get excited about tying new ones, some hit home runs others don't.

This is a ramen shop that I'll not return to as there's nothing left to try when 50% of the ramen menu falls as flat on your palette as this did.

Love this place! Great cocktails and atmosphere.
Modern Larger was our favorite cocktail!

This is a historic restaurant with an interesting menu. The view of the street was a great backdrop. The duck fat tots and dipping sauces were extremely tasty. The special was a brisket sandwich topped with homemade pickles (the best). The plate came loaded with sweet potato fries. Glad we stopped in!

Nice guy. Friendly, approachable. Followed COVID-19 protocol, but no call back. NOTHING! 

If someone doesn't want to perform work, that's fine. I completely understand that sometimes that happens with contractors. Sometimes you don't have the personnel or the time to take on certain projects. No issue there. 

However, to leave a family hanging is not very professional. This was an emergency in our eyes and we clearly communicated that. 

Andy came and left. 

No call. Nothing. 

Hate to see it.

Sorry, not great.  Ordered an overpriced salad and had a bunch of romain lettuce with a side of dressing.  Sure, the caesar dressing was vegan which is a rarity, but $7 could have bought me a lot of lettuce.  The pizza tastes fine.  I wouldn't give the flavor more than a "fine."  Speciatly pizzas are quite costly- $19.  I'm interested in knowing why Blackbird charges $3 for mushrooms on top of a pizza.  I suppose if you are a true vegan you go here for more than the taste or value/price point of their food.  That's gotta be why they've received such high ratings.

Root probably has the most amazing burger i have tried in a very long time. They are a little bit meat oriented and i am not a fan of the charcuterie boards but the burger here is amazing, the fries are crispy and the home-made ketchup like tomato sauce complements it perfectly.
Their other items such as appetizers are not as impressive. Instead of an appetizer you should order the chocolate dessert which has a weird yet quite intriguing presentation. (comes on a heavy plate with ice cream and other stuff. the portion is definitely not big though.)
The place is also so nice and cozy, if you live in NOLA a very good place to have a business lunch or a casual meeting or if you are visiting, you should not miss that spot that offers something way better and different than those tourist traps the french quarter is filled with.

We stopped in because my buddy wanted to buy me a powerful drink for my bachelor party. The drink set him back $18. Sure Absinthe is a specialty drink but we both felt that was a little steep.

Beyond the prices, the music they played was decent (but seriously, shouldn't they have LIVE music anywhere on Bourbon Street? I think that should be the rule going forward, who's on board?), the bartenders were pleasant, and the decor was pretty nice.

We really just stopped in to grab a few drinks and say we drank at the Old Absinthe House. Worth the visit but I would not say it's a place to frequent while in NOLA.

Cheers!

Our first time here!! Tacos were great! We tried all of them..
Salsa bar was good, clean and stocked
Had the Super Quinoa Salad. It is OK for an airport grab-n-go, but severely overpriced at $15. The checkout staff is not friendly at all too.

I was super excited about going to this salon and I made my appointment about a month ago. I told the scheduler that I wanted my hair colored and she gave me an appointment with Diana. I finally went to this salon last week. When I get there she educated me on Devacurl which was great, and I showed her a picture of the exact hair color I was looking for on someone with a hair texture almost identical to mine. She asked if she could take a before and after, to which I said okay to and she took several pictures. But when she started I noticed that she was giving me highlights instead of all over color. So I'm thinking okay cool, I'm going for a drastic change so she's taking me there slowly and I'll come back to get the color I want. At the end when she was done, I asked her how soon I could I come back to add more color to get to where I wanted to be. Diana informed me that it wasn't a good idea to keep adding more color. She did NOT take an "After" picture like she said she would. I left the salon mortified and cried when I got home because I was nowhere near where I wanted to be, my hair colors didn't blend seamlessly and I looked like a bumble bee because my natural hair color is very dark and my highlights are basically platinum blonde. I began to wonder if she didn't take an after photo because she knew how badly my hair looked. My mom and sister even pointed out that some of my hair towards the back of my head was a greenish blue color and I had my mom cut out several pieces to avoid looking ridiculous in public. I ended up calling the salon back the next morning because I was so upset with my hair and Diana was FAR from apologetic and I was made to feel as if I was wrong for telling her that I didn't get what I asked for and she was SURE that she gave me what I asked, but she told me to come in so she could look at my hair. Fast forward to the appointment..Diana still continued to say that she had given me what I had asked for and said that she would be able to give me more highlights but I would have to pay for more color! I was completely shocked by this and I refused to pay even MORE money when I was not originally given what I asked for. In the end, I was refunded but I still left defeated and unsatisfied. I didn't want my money back, I simply wanted what I asked for and now I'm stuck with bumble bee highlights until I can find someone TRUSTWORTHY to give me what I actually want.

We absolutely LOVE Amerigo! My wife and I had our 1st date at the Amerigo in Jackson MS 20 years ago, ate their regularly until moving to Chicago in 2003 and were so pleased to find Amerigo in Brentwood when we moved here three years ago. Their steaks are always excellent and are on par with any steak house in Nashville (no kidding). A special find is the chicken acuto, but I sometimes switch out and order salmon or tuna acuto instead. I suggest changing the green beans for asperagus and definitely get an extra artichoke. They are marinated and wood fire grilled. Awesome!!!  Their terimisu is also better than any we found in Italy. And the best waiter in Nashville is Ed Puckett, but please dont ask for him. I dont want to go and not be able to be seated in his section. We love this place. You will too!

Have been coming to Sir Pizza for years. Although we have been to the Madison Pike location, we usually go to the location an Charlotte Pike in Nash. Tonight the pizza was awful. The crust was so thin there was a hole in one if the pieces. There was verry little sauce and hardly any toppings. It was two for one night so I guess we got one pizza split between the two crust. Will not come back here!!!

We were very disappointed when our hostess took us to a brightly lit room which is not your typical melting pot experience. We asked to move to one of the darker rooms/booths but were told they prearranged all the reservations and couldn't move us. We were celebrating a special birthday so this was very unfortunate. This experience is not what we remember about this restaurant. We chose to leave because the manager was not pleasant in trying to work things out. We will not return.

alright look the food is awesome, just like every other Olive Garden. the location is ideal being right on the Boulevard and easy to get to. the staff however are lacking in a couple things. one of which being personality, or really showing any emotion at all other than anger. for some reason every single employee in this specific restaurant looks at you like you killed their little puppy and baby brother. god forbid you go to the bar, because then your looked at like a crazy alcoholic even though you asked for a Shirley Temple for your child. the wait staff is true to its name, since you're sitting there waiting for at least a half hour before they even come and ask you what to drink. then you have to wait another 20 minutes for those drinks to come back to your table, and then you get to order your food. fast forward 45 to 50 minutes you're finally evening depending on what you were there it may or may not be piping hot most likely not. If you come here you need to be really patient. I love their food, I love the atmosphere, however I'm not a patient person if I'm hungry I want my food. I'm thirsty give me my soda. I know two people who work here, both are very nice people and they're very quick outside of work so I don't know why so freaking slow when serving. Until they build a new and better Olive Garden in the Northeast I'm going to have to stick with this one and I'll have to just learn to be patient. if you're going to go here, you better have a good couple hours carved out of your day. just saying
It was fine but I prefer the middle eastern pita more than the Greek style. I prefer hilal guys overall.

No where to sit.... Website doesn't advise. We drove 45 min to sample the food. My daughter was so excited. 
Huge disappointment. Should update web site

We saw the reviews on our way down the coast and decided to stop.   Of course we tried the almond croissant it was heavenly.  We ordered the steak sandwich and chicken salad which were also good.   I only give four stars because of the service.  
It's obvious it is not child friendly too..
This place is the bomb!  We frequently get dinner there on the weekends, loved the half chicken roasted in honey sauce and the roasted artichoke! Everything has been great.

The husband and I decided to give them a try when we got to NOLA (this isn't our first trip). We've tried Acme previously and the line to get in was extremely long (almost to the corner of Bourbon) and we didn't feel like waiting, so we came here instead. There was a line, but we opted to sit/stand at the bar because we just wanted oysters. The oysters were amazing! They were fresh, HUGE, and had their own hint of saltiness to them. I love the fact that we had to make our own cocktail sauce. One down side is that the guy pre-shucked two dozen oysters (we only said we wanted oysters, not the amount) and we had to eat them off of the bar. We tried the calamari and it was ok; we had to send it back because they were lukewarm, but they weren't bad once they were heated. The only other thing was that the batter on them was kind of overwhelming. Also, there's a price difference of $2 on the oysters. The website and menu say that their oysters are $10.95/dozen, but the price inside of the establishment written on the mirror is $12.95/dozen. However, they're still some of the cheapest in the French Quarter. We'll definitely be back in the future!

I hate the customer service and management. Every time... Always the same HORRIBLE service. My last visit here I said I would never come again but today is a friend of a friend's birthday party... I'm here again. :-(
I think I have found my favorite nail salon. The service is great and I have loved my nails the times I have gone. Emily was great and overall great environment!

Vicious cycle is like eating a porterhouse after eating flank steak your whole life. It provides a sense of awakening. The studio is beautiful, the equipment is much nicer than I have seen at my usual spin studio, and the staff are beyond friendly and enthusiastic about what they do. It is a nice touch that they provide towels (which for me, made me feel right at home. One less thing to worry about.) Also, there is a cooler to refill your water bottles. To the left of the instructor's bike are the pre-moistened wipes to sanitized your bike after use.

Jennifer was so welcoming and took the time to help measure me for the bike and followed up with me after class to see how I felt and how I liked it. She also emailed me the settings for future reference. And when I returned I couldn't find the email on my phone but the instructor that day was able to help me find it on the tablet in the front. You check in by clicking on the tablet next to your name (of course). The equipment is much sturdier and better than average, the seat cushion is also larger than your standard spin bike which helps a little with comfort. 

Overall, Vicious Cycle is not your average spin studio and though it is a little out of the way for me I will continue to visit to add variation to my routine workout.

Great customer service. Paid $70 for tire balance and rotation and 1 quart of oil....way overpriced.  Should have done my research...average cost for this service is 25-50 bucks.  Would not be returning.

Sooo, I would probably give this place four or five stars if it wasn't for the creepy manager. 

I am a young woman and I love to travel alone. I also love Mexican food. This seemed like a hip place to go and I was really enjoying my Tajin-rimmed margarita and appetizers until a creepy guy sauntered behind the bar and tried to chat me up. 

I don't mind talking to the waitstaff--I had actually really enjoyed conversing with the bartenders and servers who I met--but this guy made me really uncomfortable. He kept trying to take the conversation in a "weird" direction, asking how long I'd be alone, where's my boyfriend, etc. 

In the end I closed out early and found another spot near the MetroLink. I felt so uncomfortable because of this manager that I cut my meal short. It's too bad, because otherwise I would recommend this place, but if you're a woman and by yourself...steer clear.

Whether it is or is not good pho, this is about a matter of appropriating asian food and culture. You can replicate dishes all you want and it will taste the same, but you cannot change a culture and something vietnamese people have been doing for generations. You cannot say whether their customs or wrong or not because it is NOT your place to say. A white person telling us how to eat foreign food is incredibly disrespectful. This is our people's food and should be eaten the way we like to eat it. If you're going to have a vietnamese restaurant, at least have the decency to keep vietnamese tradition and not disrespect their culture by saying you eat it wrong. 

There is nothing ever wrong with tweaking dishes, but you cannot ever change a culture that you did not grow up with. Cultural asian foods should not just be "trends". When you take away the authenticity away from pho, its not even pho. White entitlement never ceases to fail me.

Finally a breakfast spot near me and it's not too shabby. I ordered the triple 222 which costs 11.25 . For that price  I feel it could use a couple more slices of bacon . So value 7/10. Taste - first the bacon wasn't that crispy and the grits/eggs could've used a bit more salt . The pancakes were nothing special pretty standard so for this was a 6.5/10 for me. The service however was exceptional , the staff is quick on their feet and attentive to the customers. Cheers friends :)

I always order eggs Benedict when eating breakfast out, and this was THE BEST I've ever had, hands down. So
often the poached eggs are over cooked and hard. This was the runny, gooey egg that make eggs Benedict! I would have liked to tell the server how good it was, but she never returned except to drop off the check. None of the staff seemed happy to be working there -- from the gentleman (owner ??) handing out menus and seating us, to the server with no personality, to the checkout person. Not a single "how was everything?"  Thankfully the good food made up for the lackluster service.
The ER is death trap, a friend of mines waited 11 hours to be seen by a doctor after being admitted.

If you work in the food industry  or are a foodie you know re fried fries when you see and taste them. I was literally the only person in line what was the point of serving me old food? Hard and so crunchy you can't eat it the breading came right off my tenders again a fail  the creamy garlic amazing wish I had food to eat it on opened it and put in bag after tasting fries I will say even with this horrible food the lemonade omg to die so good for whoever the expo or food check quality person is you need a new one total $10.94

I experienced very good service here twice.  Chris is very professional, friendly, and honest.  They have great prices and seem to get the job done efficiently.  I would go here for any oil change, inspection, tire rotation, new tires, tire alignment, etc.  Excellent business and managed very well by Chris.

Hmmm... how do I judge this place?  Service... slow, casual, forgetful...  It wasn't a busy morning but our waiter still didn't manage to get my order right.  It wasn't an annoying custom add/subtract job either, just wanted a side of fruit and apparently they disagreed with that decision.

Food-wise it's solid breakfast fare.  I'd rate the food a 3 star.  Location wise it's right by my friend's condo, so that's good for a star.  Service subtracted a star so that brings us back to 3.

It's very reasonably priced, but I don't know if it's b/c I'm used to Chicago prices or it's actually reasonable.

Try it if you live nearby... otherwise, eh?

3.5 star review.

My fiance and I were excited to come back to Chago's after having attending a very successful elite event (shout out to Marcia M!) and loving their tacos.  It also happened to be Nashville's restaurant week when we visited, one of my very favorite times of the year.  We were excited to try the drink special ($5 mango margaritas, anyone?) and to order off of the R. Week menu.  We ended up with an order of queso, a chicken burrito with associated Mexican sides and a plantain stuffed with carnitas with same sides.  Our waitress told us that it was her first day, and aside from her seeming a little frazzled, she did a great job!  She remembered everything we ordered, charged us the correct prices for all of our specials, and brought us not one, not two, but three baskets of tortilla chips.  Win.  

Probably the most successful dish was the carnitas and plantain, mainly because the plantain was so smooth and delicious and really went nicely with the pork.  The black beans were tasty too.  The rice and the chicken in the burrito I found to be dry, but it was pretty nice when you got a bite that also had guac in it.  Looks like they've got a great happy hour too, so I'm sure at some point I'll be back for that.

We were 4 Vegetarians, two kids...many of the dishes are spicy so ask your server if that's challenge for you. We found the dishes inventive and quite good. Maybe a little too inventive. I found myself longing for something a litte more hardy, a little more filling and perhaps... Dare I say it, predictable? We sat at the Chef's counter which was fun and energizing. Our waiter was perfect and the ambiance of the place is just right.

I came here today with baby in tow to get some quick Chinese food and was surprised to see they now remodeled their restaurant into sit down only. I asked if I could order take out and they sat me at a chair by the register with a menu. The old concept you could get a meal with an entree and side for $6 which is all I need. Now a meal will cost you $9 but it does include soup, rice, crab Rangoon, and spring roll now. I got the orange chicken with fried rice. I was able to place my order quickly as there was only two people dining in the restaurant but stood at the register with my card out waiting to be rang up as the guy messed around with things on the counter saying nothing it was really strange. I got my food quickly and took it only to be disappointed in my meal. The orange chicken has a ton of breading and heavy sauce, the rice was chewy and didn't taste fresh, the spring roll and crab Rangoon were also lacking flavor. The only saving grace was the egg drop soup which was good. Out of all the cheap Chinese restaurant choices I will be choosing a different one next time unfortunately.

Short & quick: Good tasting burgers. But too small for the prices. I was shocked when I opened my package when I arrived back to work, and my veggie burger looked like a junior burger from the dollar menu.
The food was delicious as always but our server was outstanding!!! Bree (Breeann) was so sweet and efficient! Hope to get her again when we go back.

Great spot, just North of 101 and 5 minutes to beach.  Fresh authentic Mexican food.  Great selection, though a bit pricey.  2 fish tacos $12.99!!!! Carnitas plate $12.99.  Anyway, deserving of your business.

We did a remodel of a guest house on our property.  Our contractor used Desert West Cooling and Heating for the HVAC system.
In my opinion, the whole experience was extremely frustrating.  Husband and I did the demo on the inside of the house.  This included the duct work that had been put in by the previous owner and was a mess; if the furnace or AC was on, it was impossible to hear the TV. So we pulled out all duct work except for the run from the living room, through the kitchen and into a closet where the HVAC systems had been located.  We were adding a mechanical room and pulling all the HVAC out of the closet in the house.  The contractor told us to leave that part of the duct work and that if it needed to be removed, they would let us know.
After many, many delays (contractor would tell us Desert West would be out on a certain day, only to have them show up days, or in one case a week later) they came out to install new ducting.  The two employees told my husband that the remaining ducting had to be removed and then stood there, doing nothing, while my husband and I started removing it.  When I asked them if they could help, they said no. After an hour or so, they finally helped us with the last of the removal.  Then it was more delays, waiting for them to come back once with the mechanical room had been built. They would come back after we would be waiting for days, and tell us something else had to be "fixed". The contractor would "fix" the issue and then we would wait days for them to return.  This happened many times with Desert West and, in my opinion, delayed completion of the remodel and passing permit inspections.  
When I questioned the contractor about their lack of presence/follow through on the job site, she indicated they had used them before and had not had any problems with them.
The final product is good and the HVAC system is quiet,  but it was a definite hassle dealing with them.
I will not use Desert West Cooling and Heating again.

I've always had an amazing experiences at Olive Garden and one of our favorite places to stop on the go. Always a go to for us! The Streak Gorgonzola is AMAZING. The only thing is that the waitress this time never came back and refilled our drinks and I had to ask another waitress to fill up our drinks as she walked by and we had to ask for her through that same waitress. But will continue to go here!
Good food but loud. Be in the mood for lots of noise. Table side guacamole is excellent

This place was a quick stop to get my gel manicure done for the weekend.  It was $25 which wasn't too expensive.  I didn't have to wait long.  There were no boards or samples to look through to select colors; you have to pick from the actual bottles which for gel means unscrewing the bottle to see the color.  They work it like an assembly line, which means that I had multiple people working on my nails throughout the process.  They did a decent job with shaping and painting my nails but apologized that they had no hot towels so I missed out on part of the service and paid the same fee.  I was a little put off with being asked to pay for the service before it was complete when getting gel; I completely understand if it was regular polish but I have never been asked that for gel manicures before.  The plus is that you can add a tip to credit card purchase, which most places I have been to do not have that option.

Called about a Yorkie puppy......$4K!! Really?!
Whose paying that much for a puppy?? Probably a puppy mill puppy. 
I did not go into the store, so I can't judge for that, but that price was a real turn off.

As good as it get for a small town outside venue that's for sure 

WOOOHOOOO!!! KINGS OF LEON F@#$ ROCKED 

if you don't know (like I did before the Summer Roundup) Santa Barbara has a nice outdoors Venue which even though (like me) if you're in the nose bleed section the sight and sound is AMAZING!!! 

The sound resonates INCREDIBLY here soo if you are planning or have a concert in mind check out the website for a show and make your way down to Santa Barbara cus the SB Bowl is a must for ALL concert lovers (LIKE ME)
Fantastic! Beautiful modern atmosphere, delicious food. I highly recommend the dates and the avocado blt.

They are new in market, more like runned by ameture but though too pricy !!

We ordered for Prawns 65 and veg Manchurian for appetizers. Veg Manchurian was undercooked and prawns 65 was just fine in taste but really small portion to it's cost. Then we had to wait for almost half an hour for our briyanis, which very quiet dry. Chicken was too chew seem like it wasn't fresh. Later discovered that they had made our briyanis to go so we had to wait long for it's services. 

Service is too slow and protion are too small for it's price. Won't recommend this place at all.

Delicious food, great ambiance, and good service!  I took my boyfriend there to celebrate our new jobs.  I would highly recommend Nada for a special night out.  The prices are not bad--sadly they do not have them listed on their online website, but know that it will be around $30ish per person, if not more.  We shared an appetizer, had one drink and entree each, and shared dessert.  After tip, it was $70.  

Not bad for a special occasion, but I wouldn't go there all the time.  If that price tag doesn't phase you, be assured that you will get your monies worth!

First time back since the start of the pandemic and Oloroso did not disappoint. Love the updated tapas menu - don't sleep on the swordfish or the eggplant.     So. Damn. Good. Like most restaurants in the city, there were only a few servers to go around and they were HUSTLING. Docking a star for the outdoor set-up - we were on an old, shaky high top outside Irish pub and it was not the most comfortable. Glad to see this place is still thriving and already looking forward to coming back.

We went to open mic night on Thursday. They're really great about focusing on the musicians and don't allow excessive noise while they are performing. We didn't have any food but had some drinks. Service was good.

We came for lunch.  Nice vibe and Florida-esque.  We both had the southern fresh salad and it just wan't great.  There was too much dense romaine lettuce, hiding the flavor of the toppings like avocado and tomato.  The citrus vineagrette is too light and without flavor for the lettuce; romaine pairs better with a thicker dressing, or vinegrette is for spring mixes, etc. They forgot the chicken and when we asked, it arrived, cold, and literally with no taste or seasoning.  A first impression was not good.

DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY...THEY ARE RUDE AND WILL NOT UPHOLD WARRANTIES SHOULD YOU HAVE PROBLEMS.  This company has the worst customer service I've ever experienced.  We purchased a home that had a SunTec installed solar water heater system that was installed in 2012.  I called Suntec not long after we moved in to see if they could let me know about any maintenance I needed to perform and received zero help and a rude woman on the phone.  After about nine months in the house, we noticed water leaking down the roof from the solar collector.  As I didn't want to deal with the attitude from Suntec, I called another local company (who was fantastic) and was told that the solar collector was bad but should still be under warranty as there was a 10 year warranty on the part, but this other company didn't perform warranty work.  I called Suntec and explained the situation.  They told me that I was not covered under the warranty since we were not the original purchaser, even though I had called the manufacturer and distributor who both said we were, in fact, covered under the warranty.  This company is full of extremely rude people and I will never ever give them any of my money.  I called another company the distributor referred me to and got the problem fixed for a small labor charge in about 30 minutes.  SUNTEC CAN GO SCREW THEMSELVES.

My friend kept bragging about how good the sandwiches are here. I had my doubts as is recommendations are usually way off the mark. He has a knack of thinking that off the beaten path diners serve great food. However this time he really came through. One of the best deli's I have ever been too. I went with the classic turkey club. The sandwich was HUGE! Almost impossible to take down in one serving. The latkes were perfect as well. Just add a little sour cream to put it over the top. The other guys had brisket sandwiches and they looked great as well. They have a large display of desserts as well. I ordered a brownie and slice of German chocolate cake to go. They were both OK. I have had much better. I don't see how you would have room for dessert after those massive sandwiches anyway. I will be back for the turkey club!

I used to go to Pei Wei all the time and loved it, but my most recent trip was disappointing. Took a meal to-go and once I got home I realized some vital pieces of my meal were missing. I ordered Kung Pao with tofu and veggies. I had NO tofu and nearly no sauce on it (I ended up adding my own since it was so dry). I am very disappointed in the decline in quality here.
Employees at drive thru were rude and very argumentative. Waited in line over 5 min and no customers were served. Prices at this DD are higher than any other in the area.  Will not go back.
A tad crowded on weekends (obviously).  The cheese/crab fries were delicious and a nice beer selection.

Great way to burn calories and get in shape if you include  and follow a healthy diet ofcourse. Their classes are always fun and the instructors always make sure the classes don't get repetitive so it doesn't get boring. The owner Heath is a great friendly guy that really pushes you you can see that he really cares for his business and his clients

Going to a Shoney's was not a lifelong dream fulfilled. The truth was that Jamie W. and I had traveled most of the day from Memphis, made it to the Grand Ole Opry for the evening show, and found ourselves looking for dinner around 11PM after checking into the Radisson near the airport. When we asked the front desk where we could go for a late meal, he gave us two choices, Waffle House and Shoney's, each next to the other on busy Donelson Pike. I'm certain I would have had a more colorful review if we went to the Waffle House, but we just couldn't afford the calories that would come with that ill-advised decision no matter how decadently tasty it would have been.

So we walked to Shoney's which was a huge space with only one table of diners along with one guy at their fully stocked bar. I was surprised they had a bar since it struck me more as a Denny's-type restaurant, especially with their laminated pictorial menu. Apparently this was a pilot experiment according to our server, but the prospect of loud drunks hanging around kids in the dining area didn't seem well thought out to us. Anyway, finding healthy options turned out to be a tricky proposition because they offer way too many things to be good at any of them. Jamie and I both decided to focus on the poultry selections just to play it safe. The double decker burger was just not going to happen near midnight.

Or so we thought. Jamie ordered the $11.99 Grilled Chicken Breast with Cucumber and Cranberry Relish, which was granted an odd combination of flavors that didn't quite work (photo: http://bit.ly/2tjZ6nn). He extended on this strange anomaly by having a fruit bowl and heavily fried onion rings as his sides (photo). My order looked and tasted more like a TV dinner. It was the &10.99 Lemon Pepper Chicken topped with the more predictable Corn and Tomato Relish and accompanied by steamed vegetables and green beans (photo: http://bit.ly/2K8tJCS). No, I don't live in a retirement home...yet. One nice surprise was that we each had a free trip to the salad bar although the pickings were slim at that hour (photo: http://bit.ly/2tuwQOf). We also appreciated how the manager also checked up on us.

FOOD - 2.5 stars...middling to mediocre food but that shouldn't come as a surprise, presentation like a school cafeteria pile-up
AMBIANCE - 3 stars...spacious booths and where else do you see a wet bar and a salad bar next to each other?
SERVICE - 3.5 stars...server was attentive as was the manager
TOTAL - 3 stars... it's a big chain and they're open late, enough said
Dilworth Park is the baby of Paul Levy. Our dog Louie lived for a walk in Dilworth. There is now a stainless water fountain for dogs and their owners in memory of Louie.  Enjoy!
This is my new favorite restaurant in Saint Louis!! Great Italian  sandwiches with local flavors, and try it and you will love it! Or dont because you will want to go every day!

Great food, and great margaritas! Absolutely get their Carne Seca; it's a deliciously seasoned and cured beef. They'll let you try a sample before committing to it, so make sure to at least get a sampler.

My name is Elizabeth Dana, I bought a sectional from value city furniture in castleton, Indianapolis Indiana. July 2020  After one month, the cushions started breaking down.  Service came out August 2020 and he said he was ordering the replacement cushions.  With covid I was very understanding, but I got married 09/04/2021 so after 13 months and many many calls I had to go to joann fabric and add filling to the cushions myself.  Now it is October, I called the store for a refund of the service contract, 14 months later, and was told no, they will check on the cushions again, if you are not completely broke and you can afford a few dollars more.  Please save your sanity and don't buy value city furniture. Im had to spend over $200 for filler at Joanne fabric, but it doesn't look 10 years old any more...ps, we dont kids or pets that sit on it!

The employee female that was filling up the bowls today I asked her for half chicken half Barbacoa she always skimps on the meat this time she took her hands and pulled meat out of the bowl and threw it back into the pile I think it's pretty gross and unacceptable to reach in to someone's food bowl take the meat out and throw it back in 345pm 2/21
By the way if you try and call after you leave the number on the receipt 813-920-0992 it just hangs up on you as an invalid phone number

Pizza by the slice
With a full menu of choices
I had the opportunity to try this place 
Recently 
And here is my thoughts 

Customer service, okay... no issues
I sat inside since outside was on the cool side
Not to mention it was the day for cleaning trucks 
To make their appearance 

Had a nostalgic feel to it
Noticing the inside decor.
Only thing missing was the mobsters..

Food wise...
I'm going to look at this review with a different perspective 
We will incorporate price.
For the price that is offered,  3.50 per slice.
It's going to be hard to beat this place
If you are on a budget, looking for pizza
Without the extra amount...( a whole pie)
They do a good deal
You get up to three ingredients too

Considering taste..
It's pizza ladies and gentlemen.
I wasn't disappointed 
Nor did I find my taste buds sending signals to the moon
I simply sat in my chair enjoying the pics on the wall
Along with the grey Santa Barbara afternoon.
I got pepperoni, sausage and olives as my three choices



I didn't find the ingredients to be authentic 
The sausage was okay, ( better if browned by itself and added)
pepperoni standard, 
olives canned
But what they do have they utilize well 
I still have more items to try


The location is as prime as it gets...
Smack in the heart of downtown 

I will say,
While sitting down eating 
I noticed different populations of people 
Coming in to order
I payed attention to how they were treated 
And watched as they got their orders to sit or walk away 
Everyone was happy, 
Decompressing over the hustle and bustle 
rooted in the holiday season
While seated inside, we all ate as one family
Until the last bite..

I will be back.

Saw Kingsmen here on Valentine's Day. My bf hates all things 50 shades of Grey related and thankfully this theater was not showing it and had a decent availability of other movies available. 

We did not have to wait in line for tickets or concessions which was nice but when we entered our theater it was super small. There were seriously probably a max of 75 seats in there and we arrived about 25 minutes before the movie so we were expecting decent seats. We had to sit a lot closer to screen then we would have liked. The seats however were nice, very spacious and they reclined. 

Overall it was an okay experience but I suggest if you plan to have show times in the small theaters let your patrons pre select their seats.

This place is a blast, definitely coming back for it. Extremely entertaining especially for large groups. The zombie paintball shooting was fun but the 30 mins walk maze was the best. If you're looking on having fun next Halloween, buckle up and visit Terror of Valley.

HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

a.kitchen was very good this time around. The space is beautiful and the service is excellent. I came with a friend for restaurant week. This is one of the few restaurants in which restaurant week is a GOOD deal. Our dishes, if purchased on a "regular" night, would have cost upwards of $50 per person, plus alcohol. So the restaurant week price of $35 was excellent. The food was quite tasty - started with the shrimp, then the fluke, then the hen of the woods mushrooms, and finished with the olive oil cake. My friend did the shrimp, chorizo meatballs, roasted chicken, and olive oil cake. The flavors of all the dishes were very good, and everything was cooked perfectly. But I must say, I don't know if I would've been thrilled had I paid full price. As many others have said, it's expensive and the portions are small. The wines and cocktails are expensive (though I did learn last night that they are all organic). The only time I am okay with expensive and small is if the flavors are truly mindblowing, and a.kitchen's just weren't. I am excited to see the changes made after Eli Kulp gets his hands on the menu...given that High Street on Market is our favorite restaurant, I will definitely revisit a.kitchen after Chef Kulp makes modifications. This place has huge potential - gorgeous decor, excellent servers, and perfect location!

We are from NY and have a 45' Diesel Pusher.    The Lazydays RV resort is fantastic.    The pool area is really nice.  They have a spa and a small restaurant/bar attached to it so you can get something to eat or drink.   You can also bring your own cooler if you prefer.   The sites are nice and you will find many who are full timers here especially during the winter months.   The front desk is also very helpful and friendly.     There is also a RV washing service company that you can get your RV washed where you are parked.    The area is conveniently located to Lazydays so you can check out the new RVs or the RV accessory store.  They also have free classes there.   We have stayed at this park 3 times and would return for sure.

What do they need to get a 5  star?   
- faster internet service through-out the campground
- the bar/food area should be open for lunch (currently opens at 3pm)

Had the Etouffee and it was wonderful and I have eaten at least 20 versions of it.  The jambalaya was also very good, that I tasted off the plate of my boyfriend.  Saw the poorboy come out and asked the table next to us what they thought and they said excellent!  I will be ordering it next time.

Ordered a plain and a Sicilian pie and both came promptly. The Sicilian looked and ate better than the plain. The plain was average Neapolitan style pie, little on the greasy side but decent and edible. 
The Sicilian had a nice thickness, crispy solid crust and the sauce was a little sweet. The cheese was stringy and fresh. A decent average Sicilian. Could use a bit more flavor in the sauce, but I definitely didn't push any slices away. 
I would repeat a pizza order from here if closer options weren't better.
Overrated, overpriced and underwhelming.  Pass.

i find it terribly lame that folks are losing it so much about the rising rates...are we not in a bit of a shit economy right now? yeah..thought so.

for those going with zipcar--remember this--they will raise the rates once they push out the little guy (PCS).

We've been to other locations and always loved it.  Last night we ordered crab fries (first, with our drinks), a lobster roll and a lb of snow crab legs.  Lobster roll came out first- roll was totally burnt.  I smelled it as soon as the server put it down, but she dropped it and ran.  I asked for a new one (waitress tried to convince me it wasn't burnt, just toasted, but it was black all the way around!) when she came back by a few minutes later.  Next came the crab legs, which were done perfect.  About 5 minutes later my new lobster roll came out with the crab fries.  So much for an appetizer.  We didn't even eat half of the crab fries because we were trying to eat dinner before it got cold, and by then, the fries were cold.
Luckily we were paying with a gift card, so I wasn't too upset about wasted money.
Decent place. The owner was nice, very clean inside. Food was alright, but not extraordinary.

I love that Blind Onion is a basic, 'pizza and beer' joint.  The beer (Deschutes Fresh Squeezed IPA) was phenomenal and the pizza (Megan's? - red sauce, chicken breast, spinach) was to f*#@ing die for.  Great crust, abundant toppings and beautiful to behold.  Friendly staff and casual surroundings.  We'll be back again and again.

This place is mediocre, at best. I have been here twice and was honestly not impressed with the food either time. You might be drawn to this place purely by the decorations, if you are huge anime fan. I mean, they give you your receipt inside a volume of Naruto manga. That would totally tickle the fancy of certain demographics. 

All the drinks are named after Naruto characters and the ramen is decent for the price. You won't be blown away by it but you sort of get what you pay for. Their sushi rolls aren't anything amazing and not rolled perfectly. I've had the pork buns twice and once it came out watery. The sauce was watery and had spread all over the plate and made the bun soggy. Not very satisfying at all. 

There are cheaper and much better tasting food in Chinatown, PA. If you want good Japanese food, I would suggest you try your luck at Bubble Fish.

I liked this place for the food and thats it, service is not that good though. Once I went there, and the waitress was super slow, but we were the only people in the restaurant... That does not make sense for me! But besides that, the food is really nice, I would definitely recommend it to people if they like Arabic food.
Lukewarm-to-cold fries once more.  Forget this place.  I won't stop by there again.  Pass.

This place does not enforce their mask policy. I asked the waitress about all the people walking around without masks and she said "oh well". The Avenue is not sad and the food is mediocre at best. Find a better place on the strip for your friends and family. One star.

In a shopping center, -this place is a bit hard to find. This is a nice space inside when you enter... sorta rustic feel. Montreal style wood oven bagels are special. However when you taste one of these bagels, you'll find that they lack flavor....just plain bland. They are also rather thin.... about the size of ring-toss rings. The best thing they offer is the La Colombe coffee. 
Oh...  and, by the way, if you want some butter for your flavorless bagel, cough up another 75 cents.

Update: we recently went to Las Palmas 2 separate times and felt the experience was very different than all the other times before. Jorge used to be our waiter every time and in these recent two times he has not been there. He always remembered which booth we sat in and what we ordered previously making it a very personal experience, setting it apart from other restaurants in town.  This last visit, we were served directly by the owner (Jorge's father maybe?) and the experience was very different, and we felt we needed to revise our review and reduce it to 3 stars from 5. The food is still good, but we did not feel like the chips were as fresh. We ordered the chimi supreme and an ala carte chimi with cheese dip. They brought out a macho chimi and chimi supreme - when we questioned it he told us that we didn't order it al a carte even though we pointed to it on the menu while ordering and he explained that the chimi ala carte comes with red sauce and not cheese - which is why we ordered the cheese.  Overall, the last couple visits where not what we have come to expect.
Prior Review:  Service and food are always wonderful! Macho Chimichanga and Chimi Supreme are very good. The salsa and chips are also the best we have had and we love trying different restaurants around town - this restaurant is our favorite by far!

This place used to be so good but with time their middle eastern hospitality started going down:
- be aware of the big guy who stays behind the counter and scratches his balls time to time. Just imagine what happens in the kitchen!
- the girls at a pastry department are rude and full of attitude. You ask them for a tea or smoothie and they do it slowly as if you inconvenienced them with your request. They never say "please", "thank you", or "you're welcome", and they barely smile.
- if before they paid attention to how much they put meat on "Kafta Manakeesh" now they don't even care to spread the meat and the meat itself is raw and salty. 
- how difficult is to put a big bottle of ketchup instead of ketchup packets? They are annoying! And if you ask someone behind the counter to pour some ketchup into the cup they bluntly say they don't have it when it is right there. 
- the biggest complaint is their restroom! It is always dirty and smelly. You can't enter it without holding your breath. It is right next to the kitchen and they use these restrooms themselves. If they don't care how clean their restrooms do you think they care about the hygiene in the kitchen? Right across there is a Saad restaurant. I've been there couple times only, because I'm not a fan of their food but their restroom is crystal clean compared to Manakeesh.

I received the response from a manager and very glad that he reads reviews and is willing to work on the issues they have. So I give 3 stars this time.

I really hate to give these guys a bad rating. But I spent 10+ minutes in the phone with them not being able to understand anything they were saying. As well as one moment they would be on the phone talking to me and the next they would have set the phone down to talk to someone els without even letting me know they were gonna set the phone down in the middle of one of my sentences. And all of that just tonnage them tell me that they don't deliver to me, bc I'm to far away. But I'm sorry less then two miles away is really to far people?
Decent pizza at fair price. Hardly fast ever though. Either a line and 5 minute wait to even start building your pizza or employees chit chatting not paying attention.

I don't see why this place is so poorly rated. I wouldn't compare this place to the small mom and pop authentic Italian restaurants as it's a chain restaurant but the food is pretty good. Our first visit in December wasn't terrible but wasn't great either. Mainly service related issues. I filled out an online survey stated on the receipt about my experience and the manager contacted me to make it right (which is more than I can say about restaurants that actually really do suck really bad!). My husband and I returned last night for our anniversary. Everything was excellent. We had made reservations and were seated immediately. The restaurant was steady busy at 7 pm yet we did not have to wait on our server to come and greet us. He was prompt and friendly. His name is William and he is an excellent waiter. The manager, Jason, is also excellent. Greeting customers and making sure things are running smoothly. We didn't wait long at all for our appetizer, drinks, main courses and dessert to be served. Everything tasted great. I ordered the same appetizer and meal as the first visit and it was even better this time around. I recommend the Crispy Shrimp Napoli appetizer, the Petite Filet & Grilled Shrimp steak which is served on a romano crusted tomato and bed of lightly sauteed FRESH spinach (sooo goood) and their DELICIOUS cheesecake. The Rustica salad is really good also. My husband and I will be returning!

Food was delicious but terrible terrible service. Our server was a Hispanic lady. We waited half an hour for our order because the server put someone else orders in before ours. Terrible terrible! Never come back again!

A decent outlet mall with a couple of big anchors like Scheel's (a sporting goods and outdoors store) and an IMAX theater, as well as a number of smaller outlets lining a pleasant interior walkway with benches and fountains. There's parking galore, and if you're a fan of specific stores here, then it's worth a longer visit. Otherwise, it's a nice, quick stroll in the warmer months.

DON'T TAKE YOUR ANIMALS TO THIS VET.

For a few years, I had mediocre experience with Caudle Vet, but I kept going there in spite of my reservations. It is near where I live, and I think I just nursed a hope that I would eventually come to have a good experience there. (I will say, the techs and front office staff are really, really great.)

The final straw, though, was when I took my 19-year-old cat in because one of her eyes was extremely swollen. The doctor sent me away, saying I didn't have anything to worry about, and that her eye would probably look better in a couple of days.

It did not. 

So, I took her to a different vet who found that she had a few abscessed teeth, one of which had splintered off and migrated into her sinus cavity. I cannot imagine the pain she must have been feeling. I'm furious that Dr. Pandya doesn't have any more empathy for a poor innocent cat than to just send her away like she was an inconvenience. He barely even examined her. I get the feeling he just didn't want to treat her. He was always trying to sedate her - as if he couldn't bother with her unless she was heavily sedated. Our new vets have never tried to sedate her except for when she had dental extractions. They've taken blood and urine samples and never had to sedate her.

I highly recommend Five Points Animal Hospital. It's an ENORMOUS step up from the experience of Caudle Vet. I really regret that I trusted Caudle with my precious pets for so long. I should have gotten a second opinion a long time ago. I could have saved my cat a lot of pain.

Pros:
- Staff was very friendly. Doormen always greeted you, the front desk let us access the room earlier than we anticipated, and the cleaning woman pointed us to our room.
- Price was better than most hotels for the dates we stayed.
- Good location.

Cons: there is really only one - lack of cleanliness - but it's enough to dock 3 stars. We arrived in our room to find some blonde hair and some black hair on the bathroom floor, in the tub, and on the bathroom counter. When we were waiting for the elevators to take us down to the lobby, we noticed a thick layer of dust on the outside (see image). Then, to top it off, we returned to our room after a being gone the entire day only to find housekeeping had not come. We requested new towels which were plopped on the tv stand when we returned later in the night, but disappointingly there was not an effort made to clean up anything like the hairs in the bathroom.

I won't return to this location.

Had a most unpleasant experience...have been going to Harry's for over 20 years off and on...service not friendly, wine list is non-existent, food not good.  Last time had a tough steak, this time a tuna sandwich.....they go on their drinks, not food.  Amazed that they have such a loyal clientele.....used to be good old SB place, but no more.

Great, friendly service. The host/server made us feel like old friends. Located in Passe-A-Grille, a little piece of Key West in Tampa Bay, feels like a laid back, genuine beach village. The second floor location has an outstanding view of the Gulf and perfect for watching the sun set in the water. 

The blacked grouper sandwich was perfectly cooked. 
the interior could use an update, but otherwise a great nice eatery!

They do not guarantee their work. They came to fix the clog sink and next day it was the same way. I called them next day, they said they do not guarantee the clog fixing. My question is then what do  pluming company guarantee?
Nice clean place but no coffee choices.  Says coffee on the sign but just a carafe of regular coffee.

Honestly this is the best pizzeria in the area hands down. The people are kind and are always ready to assist in making you the best pie you can think of. Prosciutto and arugula? Check! Try their inside out. It's fabulous. You really can't go wrong here.

I've been to this restaurant twice previously and went today for brunch and am not sure what exactly has happened to this place. We got there about 40 mins after open and there were no other guests in our dining room so I can't even use them being busy for an excuse. For starters we got the FGT which we've had before and was so delicious, but instead of the normal plating it was one full tomato and then broken up bits of two others on a plate of lettuce and little piles of pimento cheese scattered around the plate. The very few pieces of pork belly we got were severely burnt. It genuinely looked like the scraps from last nights dinner service. For entrees we got the shrimp and grits and the smoked salmon Benedict. The shrimp and grits were so bland there wouldn't be enough salt in the world to enhance the flavor. The smoked salmon Benny which normally is covered in delicious hollandaise had a drop on each over poached egg and was extremely salty. The server was nice enough, but after bragging her menu knowledge the second she saw the plating of our appetizer, and the fact that we barely touched our food she should have at least acknowledged the fact that something clearly was going on in the kitchen. Hopefully it's just a one off but for how expensive this place is I except a lot more.
Wonderful food menu and beers in a welcoming venue.  Some excellent vegan and vegetarian options with interesting content including Korean kimchi fried rice

Most unfortunate experience....

Had a nice dinner at this establishment on a Wednesday....paid with my c.c. which DOES NOT have my name on it...but the name of an employee in Myrtle Beach.  The staff at Oceaniare happily took it for payment for a couple hundred bucks.

Moments after leaving..... the restaurant calls to tell me my "my date" had left her c.c at the restaurant.  I explained it was not my "date" and that she had not paid for the meal...that I had used my c.c. with another persons name  (I am responsible for the charges, and we have several c.c. with employee names on them)..which is perfectly acceptable according to my c.c. company.

Apparently the crack staff at Oceaniare....catering to a large contingent of corporate clientele.....that pay with corporate cards....had never heard of such a thing.

Keep in mind...I go to Oceanaire often enough that the valet always parks my car in front...often enough that they have my profile and phone number.

I called on Saturday to make a reservation for 6 people....and explained I would be picking up the c.c. at that time.    This meal ended up costing around $800.  Trying to leave...I asked for the c.c. to be returned to me....and they refused....stating that they could only give it to the person who's name was on the card....and of course this person is in Myrtle Beach....kinda makes that impossible.  Fortunately I had sufficient cash and an debit card.

It just amazes me...that a long time well know customer can be treated as a petty criminal that likely stole his "dates" c.c....and was perhaps attempting to use it pay for a a rather lavish dinner.

Why has common sense so often left the building ?

Pleasant atmosphere, okay food. It's just glorified bar food (nothing wrong with that), but it was not priced like bar food. The waiter was trying his hardest to upsell some stuff we didn't want. I understand, it was lunch and quite slow, but you will not create repeat customers by being pushy. Long time customers are what sustain businesses, of which we will not be. First and last time visiting this establishment. Sorry restaurant, just being honest.
Food was good, friendly staff, beers were tasty.

Came here for a work team dinner and enjoyed every minute of the experience.  The service is exactly what we've come to expect from Gerard Craft and his team.  Every single person in our group loved what they got.  The attention to detail was just great.  I had the Italian Ramen and it's exactly what you'd think it'd be -- a flavorful soup with noodles, an egg, chicken, herbs, and great parmesan.  You might be thinking that the broth was a tomato base -- not even close.  The broth was full of italian spices and flavors that it could stand on its own.

Most everyone in our group got pasta.  One person got the pizza and completely enjoyed it.  The next time I go, I'm getting the pizza...and the pasta...and the arancini...and whatever else I decide to mass-order offa the menu!!
I received excellence care from Dr. Marsh. He listened to my concerns and immediately ordered the appropriate tests. He also went out of his way to follow up. I highly recommend him and this facility

Not the best location to get in and out of as the business (while attached to the mall) is somewhat detached from the parking lot.

Arguably, I believe Qdoba has better burritos.  (It's all about the queso).  However, Chipotle has the whole burrito-making process down to a science and does it all at a slightly better price.

This particular location has ample seating but the wait line has a confusing configuration.  If you're entering from the main (not mall) entrance, your natural walking pattern would bring you to the end of the line, not the front.
Good burgers, pretty bad service. The hostess decided to ignore my date and I as we stood directly in front of her. Probably will not go back.

Pretty solid Mexican. They were very busy and still new but the managerjumoed behind the bar to help out. The chorizo cheese dip was pretty tasty. I might give them a few more weeks to get things figured out but definitely worth a trip.

First time ordering here. First I call at expected delivery time of 6pm asking where my food is and learn it could be an hour wait because they had one driver and am offered pickup instead. I ordered delivery so I would have to do that. Decided to wait and when the food was dropped off, it wasn't even my order!  As I'm writing this I've been trying to call them 4 separate times with 5-10 minutes of waiting each time. Going to hang up because it's pointless. Never ordering from here again.

First time eating here. Decided to go here for my wife's birthday based on Yelp reviews. Have walked by the place a dozen times but never stopped in before. The 5 star reviews were spot on. Food was excellent. I typically eat a lot of food and ended up having leftovers to take home. But still ended up ordering dessert because it was too good to pass up. Service was excellent as well. Very friendly and made great recommendations. Overall excellent experience and will absolutely go back.

Do you like making appointments for a wax or lashes and then watching numerous walk ins go ahead of you? Do you also enjoy a wax experience that leaves you with raw skin? Well then the Pampered Peach is the place for you! Jessica just changes her company name and reopens in a new location. First it was Plush lash and beauty lounge, then Karisma and now Pampered peach. Do yourself and your lady bits & lashes a favor and go else where. And her claim for a 5 minute wax is not true. Try 10/15.

Horizon just replaced my HVAC, and I would give five stars to the installation team. Unfortunately, the salesperson made so many misrepresentations that the experience was soured.  It remains to be seen how they will handle the warranty, etc. and I do have hopes that they will stand up to their claims on that. I can't,  however, believe any of the claims made by the person who sold the unit to me.
I would caution anyone who is buying something big like an HVAC system from Horizon to say "no" to the next-day installation (which they encourage). Wait and check any claims that the salesperson makes. Call the company to verify everything before you schedule the installation.  I was quite shocked to learn that my salesperson--who seemed extremely nice--just made stuff up, including telling me that the new thermostat would notify Horizon if the house's temperature varied out of range.  The company offers no such service. I was also told that the installation crew would receive a bonus two years from the installation date if there were no warranty claims... seemed like a smart policy to encourage good workmanship...except he made it up. No such policy exists. I won't fill this whole page with other untruths, but there were plenty. 
So just beware of the salespeople working for this company.  I do think that if you can get one of them to do an honest deal, you will get good workmanship and courteous behavior from the technicians.
I added a star to this review. The company did follow up with me. They seemed concerned that I was unhappy with the sales experience. I do think that they care about customer service.

The food was amazing. Atmosphere very hip as well. The only thing that turned us off was a very rude hostess. It set a bad tone for the whole experience that luckily our server and the food made up for but it was quite possibly the worst start to a dining experience we have ever had.

I am writing this review to review the Project IMA Fashion Show I attended this past weekend. I love the 100 acres woods but this was my first event at the IMA. They did a great job!

I had no idea what to expect before the show. Was this going to be fashion I could wear? Local boutiques? Fashion school designers? I had no idea so I really went in with no real expectation. 

It turns out it was not fashion of clothes I would wear but art. Clothes are art, as this really should be the way you go into the event. You are looking at art and not something you want to go to a date night in. Thinking of the fashion show in this way is what really made the event for me. It is amazing what talented artists can do with fabric (or material we don't consider fabric) and make it into a work of art. The IMA did a great job of showing me this.

I highly recommend this event or something like it to anyone looking for a night of fun art or a girls night (as I did, they have wine). Link below. Only reason I didn't give this 5 stars as it was a bit pricey for a 30 minute show. $20 dollars (but hey, you are supporting our local art museum.)

http://www.imamuseum.org/special-event/project-ima

Last evening we tried them out for dinner (along with enough other people in the 5:30 - 7:00 slot) allowing only bar seating.  Not bad, nosiy as usual for a bar.  BUT the food was ordinary. . . when I pay $47 for a Rib Eye steak, I expect it to be Prime meat, not Choice.  And they could lose those onion rings - too much miscellany covering up the steak itself.  
I feel $45 for a 7oz Filet and 3 Shrimp in the Scampi combo is a bit much too.  Again, not Prime meat and what is with the throwback bacon wrapped?  That is a OLD thing and not so healthy.  However, the Shrimp Scampi was THE BEST I have ever had!!  Garlic mashies fantastic also.
I checked OK as a dinner place. . . . I really think this will be a better 'nice lunch place' and will be taking some people there soon.  I am happy they are in Sparks, we need nice places!  The decor is lovely and I think sitting on the deck will be a real treat when it is not raining!!

I think that this place is absolutely amazing and I cannot wait to go back. The food was fresh, flavorful, cheap, and the portions were big. Everything on the table next to mine looked equally amazing as what I had ordered. The service was awesome. The waiter had great recommendations. I don't know what authentic Cambodian food is like, but either way this place is delish! Oh, and its BYOB.

Came with friends who recommended this place and I have no idea why. Smells like old grease and I have worked in the restaurant industry and I can tell. Asked for a lemonade twice and in an hour of being here it never showed up. I ended waking to a store up the street and bought myself a drink, came back and the lemonade still hadn't arrived. Even the people at the table next to us came over and complained about how HORRIBLE the service was. My hummus plate was to greasy to the touch it made me lose my appetite immediately. Absolutely terrible
My new favorite resturant, great atmosphere, wonderful service, amazing drinks, and the food was beyond delicious!

So i was always wondering if this place is affiliated with the great Lam Zhou in NY's chinatown, but i dont think so. Lam Zhou in NY is seriously good (and cheap). Nan Zhou's noodle soup is definitely excellent. On par with Lam Zhou in NY and that's saying a lot. Had the tendon noodle soup and beef brisket noodle soup. The tendon is seriously tender and tasty and so is the brisket. Noodle texture is chewy as it should be and tasty. broth is amazing. had the dumplings as well and they were just ok. Pricey at $5.75 for 8 pieces (compared to $2 for 8 in NYC's Lam zhou) and they were nowhere near the goodness of lam zhou in ny. 
Soup was $6.75 which i consider pricey being used to $4.50/$5 in ny. 

Service is horrible. No smiles, and when they messed up my order (i ordered beef brisket but they gave me tendon) they told me it would be $2 extra for the brisket. WTF?? i told them brisket originally but they wanted to charge me for their mistake? Server definitely need to be trained in customer service.

however, if i want noodle soup in philly, this would be the spot i would go.

Wow! Without a doubt, this is the best Mexican food I've ever had. The food is extremely fresh and tastes above and beyond other local Mexican restaurants. The grilled chicken burrito was huge and stuffed with amazing grilled chicken. The cheese dip has a great flavor as well. Also, the prices are very reasonable and cheaper than other Mexican restaurants. I'm extremely impressed and ready to go back again.

Not terrible.  Disappointing.  Our server was very attentive and nice.  The calamari appetizer was tasty.  I ordered the crab salad and could barely find the shredded crab in it.  The lobster bisque was tasty but salty.  My husband had the fried shrimp basket.  The sweet potato fries were ok and barely warm.  I tried a shrimp and all I tasted was the breading.

In a nutshell it was just OK and we won't be rushing back.  Bummed because the location is great!
Pearl Street has consistently good pizza, a good beer selection, and good service. It really stands out among the options in the Wholesale District for atmosphere and quality.

Karol Plawsky was our real estate agent and we couldn't be happier with the service that was provided to us. We were so pleased that we used her twice in the past year. She went above and beyond our expectations and was prompt in all her correspondence whether it came to emails, texts, or phone calls. She did many hours of "little things" behind the scenes to help our family with decisions that we were just unfamiliar with, due to being new to the process as well as to the area. When an "unsolvable" issue came up between our loan company and the selling agent, Karol went to bat for us and she stepped in for all parties and made sure that everything was taken care of. We didn't have to worry about things being done correctly because Karol is very experienced, professional, and paid attention to the details so that everything would go off without a hitch. If you need a real estate agent, I highly recommend Karol Plawsky. We won't use anyone else for our real estate needs because we have found the best! Thank you Karol.

Don't ever buy an appliance from Lowes with an extended warranty. Bought a whirlpool diswasher on 2/28/2018 with 5 year warranty. Called Lowes on 8/10/2020 and asked for service. Service came out in 1 and 1/2 weeks on 19 August.  Found needed "mother board" and not available until end of September. Then got call from Lowes saying the parts cost more than dishwasher and they were exercising their right to deny repair and they would pay the cost of my dishwasher. Called today the 25th to inquire about check. Jessica said it was just approved today the 25th and I would get a Lowes credit card in 2 weeks. Not acceptable send check. Then quoted extended warranty folder that said Lowes owed me $50 since product could not be repaired in 10 days. Jessica said since I had taken the buyout I did not qualify for the $50.  I had no choice, Lowes told me that they would not repair the dishwasher and I would be paid the costs.
Used to really like Lowes but no  longer. They do not abide by their warranties.  DON'T BUY APPLIANCES OR EXTENDED WARRANTIES FROM THIS COMPANY

Tried a few things from here and each was very underwhelming. The biggest disappointment was the falafel that left a terrible taste that I could not get out of my mouth the rest of the night. The gyro was average but I have had much better. Two thumbs down

Terra Nova did a awesome job with outside dining. Very enjoyable. However, the menu has been the same there for many years. Needs a make over on the menu. Nothing that really is different. Love the sushi
Rolls. Love the happy hour when we could have a happy hour.

I live closer to another Winn-Dixie, but come to this store for the customer service. The employees are nice, courteous, and helpful. I have never had a bad experience here. The parking lot is in severe need of repair and complete resurface. If not for the parking lot I would give this store 5 stars.

Sesame Chinese is in need of a lot of updating including dirty carpet, old wallpaper, cleaning wood rail and fresh paint.  Outside could also use a fresh coat of pain.  They have an "A" rating, but just not appetizing once you enter.  My water glass has a chip in it and I cut my lip on the first drink.

Service was very good probably because it was 3 p.m. and only a few people were in the restaurant.  Server was very attentive and free tea is served with the meal.   Tea was just barely warm and was cold by end of meal.  We started with soup.  I had the Wonton Soup.  It was just okay.  There was a big slice of meat in the dumpling where there should have been ground meat.  Soup was served very hot and had good flavor.  My husband had the Hot and Sour soup.  It was very thick and was more sweet than hot or sour.  We next had an appetizer of Pot Stickers.  They were served very fast with a dipping sauce on the side.  They had a good flavor and would order again.

Our main entree (which we shared) was Orange Beef.  How disappointed we were when it was served in huge thick slices.  Not what we are use to.  There was not much orange flavor.  If you like spicy you may have to ask for more peppers in it because it had no hot spice in the sauce.

It was a disappointing dinner.  Our good friends say they love it and it is the best.  We tried it but will not go back.
Cool ambiance. Good beer selection. Tacos were just okay, they were 2/$9 and could have used more filling.
Was in town for a few days and wanted somewhere to eat early morning.  The tacos and carnitas  were delicious.  I will definitely try this place again when I'm in the area

Meh. Nothing really stands out about this place. OK coffee. Dumb name. This place used to be a Saxby's Coffee. It looks like they simply rearranged the letters on the "Saxby's" sign to come up with "Abyss".  If you couldn't spend money for a new sign, what does that say about your product?

This my favorite new lunch spot! I learned about this place from my manager who told me her friend told her that they had amazing cupcakes. I decided to go grab lunch and a couple desserts.

 I walked in and was blown away by the "cupcake bar", the pastry display, and tasty menu. I decided to order the "you pick two combo" half teriyaki chicken on foccacia with chicken noodle soup. FYI you get a free treat whenever you order the you pick two combo. The combo was very reasonable around 7.25! 

The cupcake bar has about 9 different flavors mostly with a filling. I decided to get two chocolate with chocolate frosting but they do have carrot, black forest, red velvet, strawberry and a few others. 

When I made it home I couldn't wait to taste my chicken teriyaki. It was so good. My boyfriend kept trying to take bites and I kept yelling "get away"!!! You have a car...go get your own" )i'm not kidding).  I loved the foccacia bread, red onions, chicken, and lettuce. I saw the little treat on the side but thought nothing about it. Then i decided to taste it and it was AMAZING!!! I don' t know how to describe it...I guess it was a shortbread pastry cup filled with some bomb chocolate frosting or whatever that was. I wanted to go buy and buy 50 of those! 

I havn't tried my cupcake yet, but my boyfriend said its really good and he thought I bought both the cupcakes for him. I said NO one is for you and one is for ME! no need to be greedy. Needless to say we had a lot of arguements over Pastries of Denmark.

I WILL definitely be going back again. 

Check out the pics!

It's so nice to see local spots like this pop up in our city. The drive thru is quick and super convenient! The avocado toast is amazing! This place has great coffee & tons of other amazing breakfast foods to start your morning. Highly recommend this gem of a place!

The quality of their steak has really declined and it seems they never cook it to the right temperature. Shrimp was rubbery and way over seasoned. The green beans were disgusting. The loaded sweet potato was delicious and the service was excellent.
