I just love this pizza! The ingredients are always fresh. Everything is always delicious. Portions are definitely filling. And service is always good. 

It's also always super busy when I go. So parking can be a challenge and sometimes there is a wait to be seated. 

That's not necessarily a bad thing though, right? That means thatsa GOOD pizza pie!! 

I did use the Yelp wait list option on my last visit. That helped get us seated a bit faster, I think, as we were able to get on the list before arriving and were seated within 5 minutes. Coincidence? I *think* not. High five for Yelp wait list!

It was a great day outside so we decided to go to the outside bar at Marathon.   It was really packed at 5:00 and we had to stand on the stairs near the sidewalk....which was fine.  BUT apparently this place is a daycare as well.   There were so many kids running around while their parents hung out.  I have no problem with that other than the fact that they were climbing all over stuff and near our drinks.  We finally asked them to be please careful.   I guess that didn't settle well with the one kids and we got a talking to from one of the parents.  Not an apology for their kid climbing all of our things but for us saying something to them.  We left immediately and I'll never go back there.   When we walked passed the place around 8:30pm the kids were still there.

THIS IS AN OUTRAGE!!! I should've been going here FIRST for the past 15 years!!! 

My wife's 2012 MacBook Air needed a replacement trackpad. The Apple Store itself said they didn't have the part (and on a follow-up visit, did something that made the trackpad go from sorta working to dead). Other repair shops in the general area, including Murfreesboro and Cool Springs, said it'd take 3-5 days to get the part - understandable, but a pain in the keister. 

But ZMAC came through!
In my case they were able to make the repair same-day and at a reasonable price (certainly a LOT lower than replacing the MacBook)! From now on they will be the FIRST people I call for repairs on our Apple products. Their service is outrageously good!!!

Came in Easter Sunday for breakfast and got seated right away. The owner was greeting people at the door which was a pleasant surprise. I got the croissant French Toast and it was amazing. This place reminds me of a small town diner. Service was great will definitely be back again

This was the best Tampa surprise! Mathew helped entertaining us with the menu and did a wonderful job! We had shrimp toast and tuna with some mango sorbet to begin, then we had sea scallops and grouper: both incredible! The best part was desert, peanut chocolate bar and sugar cake with vanilla! OMG!! Simply loved the experience!

We dined on small plates and were quite happy that we did. 
Our server explained that the scrapple was nothing like the stuff found in Pennsylvania and vicinity - glad that she did, because it was great. 
Brussels sprouts were also a big highlight. Everything was tasty though maybe too much smokiness to the carrots. 
Nice setting, comfortable atmosphere, very friendly server.

This place was close to our hotel, rated 4 1/2 stars, so you can't go wrong, right? The food wasn't bad, it just wasn't very good either. We got Calamari as an appetizer, and I have had better at a Chinese restaurant. Not tender at all, but not quite eraser texture. For our meals, my g/f got crabs cakes and I got the Grouper. The crab cakes looked like small baked potatoes, which is not a good thing. Apparently the flavor was good, but we eat with our eyes first and it didn't look like crab cakes that I am used to eating. The Grouper was good, but the side items were edible, not good. I'm sure this place has been opened a long time, but the food hasn't changed since The ThunderBird Hotel opened in 1957. I was hoping for so much better based but n other reviews.

Although the DJ that performed the night of my visit played a lot of older, throwback songs, I noticed the crowd was much older too. We came here for a birthday party for a family friend (her daughter's 21st birthday).

We noticed it was super busy when we arrived; we got lucky and scored a spot as someone was leaving. Like I mentioned, we quickly found that the majority of the crowd was easily 40's+ (apparently, Friday nights are a younger crowd, I was told).

I wasn't really impressed; definitely one of those places that you need a good group of friends to make good! They do have tvs everywhere as well as pool tables (2) and some arcade games in the corner. We had some fun people watching but left after about an hour.

This is the worst bus service i have ever experianced. So unprofessional. DO NOT GIVE THEM BUISNESS. Im currently ready to take legal action if they do not start returning my calls and give me my deposit back. They screwed us over for a bus that never showed up. Unreal.

I was torn between giving Stephanie's a 3 or 4 star but ended up with 3...here's why:

Pros:
-VIP card: my husband and I have been VIP members since they started offering it. We love that this card will get us discounts on drinks and food plus other random perks.
-Great selection of beers...over 30 on tap plus many, many more bottled. I love their take-out/buy by the bottle shop too.
-Great location...downtown Doylestown!
-Pretty decent food. When ordering off the menu, the food has been good, but read below in the Con's section for another perspective on the food.

Cons:
-Inconsistent levels of service: over the years (yes, we've been going to Stephanie's for over five years), I have noticed a steady decline in the quality of servers at Stephanie's. When we first start going, the bartenders were knowledgeable about the beers and very friendly...my kinda place. Lately, they seem to be uninterested and do not know much at all about beer. A bummer for a place with suck variety.
-Bouncers are not friendly at all: I don't know why some bouncers feel they have to be rude and act all tough guy to do their jobs...REALLY not necessary for the friendly regular coming in for a beer or two.
-Late night food is awful: they started offering pizza late night so we gave it a try once...really awful. Don't waste your time with it if you're craving something greasy after a night of drinking...just go across the street to Baked on Main for late night pizza (that's actually good)
-Identity crisis: on the weekends, I never know what to think of Stephanie's. The bar I love is upstairs, but then there's a dance party/club going on downstairs in the basement. And now they even have a martini bar. I just feel like they are straying away from what their good at and what made them popular to begin with. 

Over the years, I've gone to Stephanie'[s less and less but what keeps me popping my head in here and there is their beer selection and decent food. If they could focus more on customer service and figure out what kind of bar they want to be, I'd become a regular again.
Neat place to stop for dessert after a nice meal. Dessert were fantastic and drinks very good. Very creative

I feel that my review can't be complete cause we only stopped in for a beer during the day to kill some time. Though it became two beers cause it was a happy hour promotion. But they have Old Style, in Nashville? Yes and a few craft selections.  The people there were friendly. We'd like to check it out at night. Don't sleep on this place, it's like an old school road house.

Fairly large thrift store. Lots of furniture and clothes although the arrangement of used media is a little odd. Records, tapes, and CDs are in the front while books are all the way in the back. If you're looking for a bargain, this store is worth a browse.

I went to this salon to get my hair and makeup done for a wedding I was in.  The price we were quoted and the price we were charged varied significantly.  We were told updos start at $50, and after my hair and makeup was done I was charged $145.  $100 for an updo is a bit much, especially considering I have extremely thin, straight, medium length hair.  Everyone had varying hair and we were still all chargd the same price.  I was happy with the end result, but I have been to other places that have charged much less, and more importantly that were upfront from the beginning about the cost!  To make matters worse, I heard them whispering about how much to charge us, so it just made me feel like we were being taken advantage of.
I tried tacos in several restaurants on the beach, and this was the best place for me. I loved salsa and chips too! Cute deco is definitely a plus. The service was quick and good.

This shop is super adorable. It smells phenomenal and is set up nicely. Immediately as I walked in I was greeted by a nice young man. I mentioned to him I was interested in hair products and he recommended I speak with the young lady that was very knowledgeable in their products. I appreciate the fact that he directed me to her instead of selling me on stuff that he didn't know much about and I really did appreciate that. The young lady named Lexi, she was very nice and of course very knowledgeable in the products for hair. I was even able to sample a couple products and eventually landed up buying a few. I found the employees to be very friendly, resourceful and comfortable to be around. When I return to New Orleans I will be sure to stopBy the shop to enjoy any new products and buy some of the ones that I had purchased already. In regards to the products I bought for my hair I have to say that I am very pleased and so is my hair. They smell great and my hair responded well to their luxurious products.
Great burgers. Terribly uncomfortable seats.

Was nice, clean and very professional, will recommend to others. They all
Seemed very nice and helpful
Even thou I didn't have a appointment

I buy a ton of stuff on-line through STP, so I was super stoked to get to visit an actual store.  They had a good selection of items, but I think there on-line promotions are cheaper than their in store prices.  Still fun to visit their store!

My husband got the Southern Breakfast, which he really enjoyed. He added the alligator sausage, which he said was nice and spicy. I got the Rose Benedict. It was a tad bit cold, but they put it in the oven for a bit. It was still good.
It was an ok experience.. vibes were nice but shame there's no outdoor space. Food was very tasty.. drinks were unique. Bit overpriced and service was just fine.

Came to Deanies because they were suppose to have good seafood. Lets start by saying the waiter was very friendly and i have no complaints on that end. The food was a different story. 

When we went they were out of the first 4 entree items i wanted to get, so settling on a 5th, normally were i would leave but since we were with a larger group i didn't complain. 

The potatoes they brought out were good but that were it ends. The shrimp appetizer was ok, I was hoping for more. My dinner was just meh! I eventually go sick from it which ruined my night. Kind of disappointed.  And don't think I will be coming back here again.

The food is good, however I only suggest going there to eat. The lady who " works the counter" does not answer the phone and IF She does you are lucky...... sometimes she will answer and then just hang up on you. Anyways she speaks English well and seems nice but they would make much more money if they answered the phone! Sometimes when I go in after calling 10 tens or being put on hold for 15 min I will see others inside saying the same thing ( they had to drive up there to order because she wouldn't answer the phone )
Being a new mother with an infant and working full time it's hard to always make dinner and I do like the food but they make it EXTREMELY difficult....just my honest thoughts on this...if the food wasn't so good I wouldn't ever go back here so that sucks you know... they are just COMPLETELY under staffed

We did takeout for Easter dinner.  We purchased Chicken Marsala which was very good.  The part I found disturbing was what they charged for a loaf of Italian break.  $7.50 is a little excessive. This was just a case of gouging.  That is why we rated it a 3.  If the restaurant didn't get greedy it probably would have been a 5.

Went  for lunch with my teenage daughter....both ordered pizza........we each agreed way to dry, ...I thought too thin....honestly reminded me of cardboard....have had better frozen pizza !....The crust was so bland, not enjoyable to eat....Do think Zona and Travolino do much better...will not come back...

My new favorite spot for a good beer and a bite to eat whenever I find myself getting off the MFL at 2nd St.

When you walk in you'll immediately see the bar area, but keep going and you'll find a sizable dining room. Dimly lit, cozy and sans TV, the dining room makes for a more intimate meal at this seemingly hole-in-the-wall establishment.

The beer list - both the size and selection - is impressive. I always take advantage of the unique craft beers on hand and try something new. 

The food... oh, the food. Heavy on the Southern flair and Creole seasoning, you're sure to find something fresh and extremely flavorful. The specials offer a few unique choices. I recommend the Sweet Potato soup - full of spice and creamy, yet not heavy on actual cream.

Did I mention they do brunch? No? Well they do and they do it well. This is where I have to rave about the best french toast I've ever eaten in my life - Bananas Foster French Toast. It's exactly what you'd expect - thick cut toast piled high with bananas and glazed with the most sickeningly sweet (and I say that as a compliment) liquored butter and brown sugar glaze. I should mention this was part of a special New Orleans brunch menu they had this particular week, so I'm not sure it's still there. This menu also included bacon... but not just any bacon... PRALINE BACON. I kid you not... and while my french toast was already sending me on the fast track to diabetes, I couldn't resist putting in an order of this wondrous sounding bacon. How was it? My boyfriend described it as, "The best thing I've ever eaten." I concur. 

The last menu item I will highlight is their bacon grease popcorn. It's absolutely delicious but I suggest only eating a few handfuls or else you're likely to feel sick later.

The service here is always friendly and attentive. If I'm not sure what beer to try they are quick to lend a suggestion or two.

I definitely recommend Khyber Pass for excellent food and beer no matter the time of day.

The best brunch we've had since moving to Nashville! Amazing cocktails and bubble service and the food did not disappoint. It is easy to tell that Chef Dale really puts his heart and soul into the food here. Definitely recommend it!

I like how when I scrolled over the two star mark, it said "Meh, I've experienced better".  That pretty much summed up my experience at Osaka.  For what they charge, both their service and their sushi should be better.  

But it wasn't.  It was good sushi, but for what they charge I expected better than good, or at least better than average.  Osaka is very average.

I went here last Friday excited to try the burger. Six of us ordered burgers, and honestly no one said they were very good and I had a better burger at my local pub. I ordered it medium rare with a side salad and it comes out well done with fries. They re-made the burger, but when it came out again it was more rare than medium rare and I wasn't going to send it back twice. It was mediocre at best and very greasy. Then, when I get my bill I see they accidentally put two burgers on my receipt. Mistakes happen, I get it. I've worked in the industry 10 years, but I would have expected them to at least take my iced tea off the bill or offer a free app or dessert my next visit back. Honestly, I'm not sure I'll be returning.

An authentic middle eastern restaurant. Great decor and ambiance, although I hoped they covered their kitchen/ working room properly (didn't look clean). I ordered garlic bread, kebabs for main course and fries. Although the food tasted great in the beginning, it started getting chewy after a while. Meat were not juicy enough. Service was really good. So give it a try.

Overall the food is tasty but somewhat expensive and the portions size are small.  It's located in the middle of a residential street so parking is hard to find at all times, so it is best to  walk.  The atmosphere is ok, nothing to write home about.  The presentation of the food is somewhat amateur (more like a cook in the kitchen versus a chef) so if you are all about presentation it is not the place.  However, as I mentioned the food is quite tasty  one of the best as far as taste in Chestnut Hill, just don't go there super hungry or you may have to order 2 meals!

I'm always skeptical of hype. But this place delivered. I walked in planning on ordering a tamale but saw a picture of an awesome looking taco and ordered it instead. The girl taking my order was friendly, and the food came was ready in about 2 minutes. Chips and salsa were awesome. The rice was awesome. And the taco was one of the best I've ever had. I was really impressed with the way they cooked the cheese onto the tortilla and crisped the outside. Steak was tender and flavorful.

They have one vegan entree. I've come to expect disaster when ordering from steak houses and they did not disappoint. The blend of acid and sweet dressing on the veggies missed the mark big time. My entree arrived 10 minutes after everyone else's. They took it off the bill and I went home for dinner. I just have to learn to say "no" more often when friends want to take me out to dinner. If your party includes people who don't eat meat, look elsewhere.

The vegan entree does not include any sides. Nada. After everyone else was served their salad and waited a few minutes uncomfortably for my food to come, someone asked "Aren't you going to eat anything? Didn't you order?". "I guess not." I replied, "Go ahead and eat.". Us vegans just love standing out in a crowd and being the center of attention (not!). Charlie is charging me the same as a steak for $2.50 worth of veggies and can't spring for a salad like every other entree on the menu? Come on. Step up to the table and serve us a salad like everyone else - we vegans love salads. Trust me on this one. You can even leave off the bacon bits and grated cheese and make my day.

Also a note to oblivious carnivores everywhere: anyone calling themselves a "vegan" should be warned that this establishment is a Steakhouse, not just a restaurant. It's sometimes difficult to get the idea across that the experience of watching people eating dead animals invokes emotions ranging from disgust to nausea, or worse. It's the gastronomic equivalent of second-hand smoke. Of course getting my food on time would have allowed me to focus on my own dish instead of everyone else's.
I opened the lid to my Sierra Mist I ordered and there was a ripped apart fly in it. I'm glad the straw they gave me was broken because I would've used it and ended up swallowing that.

You definitely get your money's worth here and what you are getting is absolutely delicious.  It's a little hectic when its busy but overall great food and friendly staff.  It may be obvious by the name but the hummus is the best I've had.

Went to the store shopping for a 20th anniversary engagement ring "upgrade."  Found a beautiful setting.  The diamond selection they showed me wasn't to my liking, so I told them I'd think about it.  Came back a few days later ready to purchase the ring, but the setting wasn't in the case and they couldn't find it for several minutes.  They didn't like that I had gone ahead and purchased my own diamond for the ring, and asked me to give them a chance to be competitive with the diamond I had bought (after saying that I hadn't told them my true budget when I came in initially, which was not true).  Since the diamond I had purchased hadn't arrived yet, I said sure.  

Came back this Saturday with my diamond, again ready to purchase.  Again, they couldn't find the setting, and after 5 minutes of looking for it, they showed me the comparable diamonds.  They were lovely, but they couldn't match what I had.  Then they "found" what they said was my setting -- only it was not the same one at all!  They were all set to order me the totally wrong ring!  We had to convince them we were right about it (we had written down all the information).  So they started searching again and I told them I'd come back.  At that point I had honestly had enough -- I don't like the idea that things disappear from a jewelry store -- are they so disorganized that they lose $2200+ items all the time?  Do they not care enough about customer service to keep a note to themselves about where they put things?  

I then went across the street to Bernie Robbins and had an excellent discussion with their salesperson Ellen.  While I was there I did get a voicemail from Jay Roberts saying they had found the setting....but too little too late.  I tried to call their store Saturday night to leave a message letting them know I'd purchased another ring -- but NONE of their phone numbers works properly.  Every number listed sends you to this automated message that won't allow you to leave a voicemail....it's not even a "You have reached Jay Roberts jewelers..." type message.  Well hopefully this review will find them and they will "get the message."

The menu looks good but most of what we ordered was mediocre at best. We did not order pizza and maybe that would have been the way to go. I ordered the mussels which turned out to be quite bland. The broth was nearly flavorless- the opposite of how mussels usually are done where the mussels themselves are simply an excuse to get a delicious broth. The accompanying bread dissolved when I tried to use it to soak up some broth. Ambiance-wise the room is very loud. Everywhere you look there are TVs. Not our kind of place at all so definitely will not return.
Beautiful salon and the staff is friendly and attentive. I've been there twice since I  recently moved to the area. I always have walked away pleased by their services.

I had sandwiches a couple of times. Kebab was good, but I didn't like so much falafel sandwich, unfortunately. It's just my personal taste. I didn't have any plate here, like what other reviewrs ordered, they look nice. I would like to try plate ones next time. Fried potato was good though. The waitress was smilley and seemed friendly.
Litterally has the nicest employees. They do an amazing job doing acrylics and waxing! Will definitely be back!!

Tonight was my very first time going to see music at The Basement East!!! I don't know why it has taken me so long to check out a band here...I was the lucky winner of 2 free tickets to see Austin Meade (opening act) & the Read Southall Band - THANK YOU @NowPlayingNashville

The music was great and the venue is simple and easy to navigate.  I highly recommended coming early to grab a seat at the high bar tables... (most of the venue is standing room only). There is a free parking lot and nearby street parking.  I'm not sure If everyone was at LOTG but it wasn't that busy tonight.  I'm not sure how the venue picks the lineup but I thought both bands were really good! Austin is from Texas - favorite song was Savannah! Read Southall is from Oklahoma and describes themselves as Southern Rock & Crunchy Country . My favorite song was "Why?" - Loved belting out the lyrics!  The drinks were great and the bartender was really nice.

I honestly feel the restaurants could be much better for how nice this place is. But wow Boise needed this. Such a cool environment. A lot of stores I would probably never go to. But great fountain. Love that my gym set up a location here. Overall 4/5 stars

We actually frequent Ocharleys and my daughter picked tonight on her birthday to have our family dinner.  We called several hours ahead to let them know we had 9-10 people coming so they could be prepared.  When we walked in they had no idea we were coming and we had to wait additional time so they could set up before we were seated.  We waited another 20-30 to get our drink order  and after an hour of being there finally our food order was taken.  We were there over 2 hours before half our table received their food, not to mention the filets were completely raw.  I would have never allowed our dinner to be here if they would have just said ahead of time they could not take care of us.  It was terrible and stressful on what should have been a happy occasion.  So sorry most likely won't be back!

I had to choose three stars, because it's A-OK, right?
But truly, it does not deserve more, possibly less. 
So the concept is that they use a Japanese-style charcoal grill to prepare almost every dish, and it gives this special taste (and smell to the restaurant, and quite possibly your clothes). 
It is very pricey (small portions of meats, with no sides included), and not that great: the wagyu was OK, but not melt-in-mouth amazing, the chicken was good (with a spicy BBQ sauce). The side of potatoes (extra $8) was so rich, it was impossible to eat more than 3 bites without feeling overwhelmed by the butter and white creamy sauce (mayo? triple cream? liquid lardo?) on top. The bread was OK, but nothing spectacular (Dandelion and Le Parc both have better breads, and theirs are free) and the 'house-made' butter was really really weird. Apparently it's the live culture that creates a funky/cheesy smell and taste. It's definitely not for everyone (personally, I thought it tasted rancid). And the bread+butter are $5...
The grilled cornmeal cake with ice cream was very so-so. Cornmeal cakes are heavy, and grilling it does not make it better at all. They were trying to carry the grilling concept over to the dessert menu, but it's just a silly idea. And the ice cream had virtually no taste.
Final issue, my wife couldn't find any red wine she liked. And wines by the glass are expensive: hers was $15 and wasn't special.
In that price range, we have many better options nearby. Not coming back.

I read reviews on here before I actually went to Boobies.  I didn't heed the warning, and my friends and I payed the ultimate price-- having to spend one dime there.  Everything about the food at Boobies is gross.  I feel really bad about it because the people who work there were very nice but honestly they should think of a different franchise.   Like many of the other reviews say: no grill, no fries, and no beer, and cheap quality everything.  My friends ordered the burger and I the hot dog (how can you mess up a hot dog!?.)  We couldn't even eat it, the meat looked inedible (we're unsure how long it had been freezing before it was presented to us.) The con-queso dip looked like brown goo, and tasted like marinara with chili powder.  I took one bite of my hot dog and had to spit it out, the hot dog was purple on the outside and white on the inside.  It was unbelievably sketchy, and although there's ski ball and the people are very nice it's not  worth eating at a place called "boobies" (the novelty wears off as soon as you see the food.)  

We felt awful but we paid, left, and threw our food away outside.  The place had every potential in the world.  The idea was clever but honestly to make it work you need good food and they are lacking that in spades.  Heed this warning and go to Red Robin.

Unfortunately, not a great experience. I ordered a hot pressed Cuban (as advertised) only to receive a cold not pressed Cuban. I did get a free "hot" sandwich because I complained. The gentleman who dealt with me seemed in charge and was kind but argumentative about what the word "pressed" means. He seemed like the only person who was working hard. The place was packed and the staff had no sense of urgency. After all was said and done the hot not pressed Cubano I ate was decent. I will not go here again. All they have going for them is the historical value of the company. I would recommend the ownership to start a press station running 6 presses with two employees working the station. I am from Tampa and have had a real pressed Cuban sandwich and this place did not deliver.

Ordered some Chicken with FRIED RICE, these people offered my Pork fried rice and I told them I want fried rice.CHEAP customer service gave me 4 wings and WHITE RICE which was disgusting. Bad customer service and the HOMEMADE ICED TEA TASTED HORRIBLE. Wouldn't recommend

They are great, very affordable,  honest,  and have been very accommodating.  They have no problem coming back out to do the inside if your not available on their scheduled day, but they still come out and spray the outside. Definitely recommend over the big name guys

I went to the Planet Sub in Maplewood Commons a few weeks back (because my friend had a Groupon :)). I was pleasantly surprised at how many vegetarian options they have. Most sub places just have one veggie sub - these guys also had protein (tempeh)! 
I ordered the Italian veggie (like a meatball marinara with tempeh as meat substitute). I added jalapenos and got extra sauce on the side. With the additions it was a great sub! Can't wait to go back and try other stuff on the menu.

We stayed at the days Inn next door and wanted something to drink and eat so we took a stroll over. It seems like it calls itself an irish pub but i just don't see it. It has a corn beef sandwich but that is about as close as it gets to it. My syburger (medium rare) was done perfectly. I liked the sauce a little spicy but not to hot. The homechips were a bit greasy but that's the way I personally like em. It was a slow Tuesday night so the wait staff were just hanging out. None of this bothered me, they were the perfect medium of attentive without bothering you while you ate and drink. The bar itself is huge with An area for a dj and space. Darts,photobooth and games in the back. They have a separate dining area so if you just want food you don't have to be bothered by the bar and music.

Another great experience at Iron Hill Brewery. We had a great waitress, Leah, who was professional, friendly anAnd attentive. She made informed, insightful menu recommendations and despite having working a busy Friday night checked in on us regularly. I got the cheese plate for an appetizer, which was unique and delicious,  and the loaded nachos for dinner. I managed to fit in a slice of 5 layer chocolate cake which was INCREDIBLE. 

The food, service, and environment at Iron Hill is always wonderful. I heartily recommend this spot!

Great Malaysian restaurant. Came here with a few friends and enjoyed most of the dishes.
Tropika home style (fried) chicken was tasty, the meat has subtle sweetness flavor. 
Sambal bunchies - greenbeans with chili was great. Not too spicy so most people should be able to tolerate it.
Kari lembu - essentially need curry. Good and tasty. The meats are small cubed and tender, and the curry flavors are all soaked in.
Fried hokkien mee was bland, avoid if you can (and know what it should've tasted like).
Service was hit and miss, but we were able to get what we wanted when needed.

Maybe be I'm just a straight up meat eater... I had some time to kill around noon today, so I figured that I'd head to this place after work to see what has everyone so excited about the tofu hoagie.... I eat a lot of Vietnamese food each month. It's something that I just tried about two years ago and love it. But have never gone with the tofu hoagie...It was pretty good and tasty.. It's something that I'd have again.. The store itself is a store that I wish that I had in my neighborhood.. They had a variety of drinks and snacks that you just don't find at your local convenience store.. The service made a good first impression... It's a nice change from the convenience store norm these days, which has no  problem having the register counter full of blunts and wrappers, legal pipes, condoms, and "energy" (stamina) pills... All of the shit that most people don't want to have in reach of their kids, when there should be candy and gum there for them to reach for.... Hell, even Wawa has stooped to selling blunts....
Good job Fu-Wah... If I didn't live across the bridge, I'd be a customer of yours daily...

The absolute worst service from waitstaff, kitchen and management. Our waiters were incredibly rude and confrontational over a mistake on his part when taking my order. After the waiter told me he would place the order in which he initially forgot to inform the kitchen of, I waited 30 minutes for the rice only to be told by another waiter that the rice I ordered does not exist and that I would not be receiving it. Confused by this, I asked to speak to the manager who was dismissive of the entire ordeal. The food is not very good either and the restaurant is not very clean.

1-Star for the Cactus and Chorizo taco
1-Star for the perfect double corn tortillas
1-Star for the the quinoa taco, totally fuckin awesome!
1-star for the perfectly seasoned and flavored components of all the tacos 
1-star for the Chile mango cantaloupe and the awesome juke box!

I am so happy I live in the 37206, for many reasons and Mas Tacos is now high on the list.  Its perfect food, fair prices, and did I mention tasty!

It's the perfect unsuspecting hole in the wall, next visit the avocado taco and the grilled corn.  Oh year the juke box is sweet, 50 cents for 4 songs!

*cash only*

I've been here a handful of times with colleagues over the lunch hour. The service is generally friendly and prompt which is great when you have limited time to eat and get back to work. This place is pretty big and appears to have a stage area that maybe has live music in the evenings or on weekends. Outside seating is generous and clean with a great overhang for shade. 

I have tried several menu items and have been pretty satisfied with everything -- but nothing to really write home about. The macaroni and cheese with chicken is tasty, but I've had better at places like Nada downtown. I will say that the Ceasar salad with grilled chicken is MASSIVE. Even after mowing down on it for 30 minutes I still had an entire lunch salad left. For only $10, you definitely get a lot for your money. 

My colleagues say these are the best wings in town and get them every single time we come. These guys have put away lots of wings from lots of different places so I believe them when they say these are the best. 

I plan on trying the deep dish pizza when I have more time since it takes 40 minutes to bake. I'll update my review when that happens!

I have ordered the wings, drumsticks and strips from here and they were all horrible. The wings and drumsticks are very greasy and there is barely any meat on them. They made my stomach hurt right after I ate them because of the amount of grease. I know fried chicken is greasy but this is korean fried chicken and I have had others that were not as greasy at all. The strips are cut into the smallest portions and they barely have any meat. Some were cold, a couple were not cooked all the way and I tasted the dough inside the chicken. Some just had fried batter and sauce without any chicken in it. Overall the wings were all cold and the size were about 2-3inches. They were once good but the service and quality have gotten so bad.

I have been going to the lovely Benefit Brow Bar for a few years.  I moved to SB just over 5 years ago and was going insane trying to find a good place to go for brow shaping.  I tried 3 different places in town and finally found Ashley at the Brow Bar.  I wouldn't trust my brows to anyone else now!  She always does an amazing job, and is very much a perfectionist.  She really listened to how I wanted my brows shaped and does exactly what I want, every time.   (I have been getting them done for over 15 years and am quite particular about them.)   I have seen her do brow shaping on clients who have never had it done before, and she has a great eye for what looks perfect on every face shape.  She also does cover up for any redness and fills in my brows expertly.  Also, the entire team at Benefit is wonderful and their products are awesome.  I always look forward to my brow appointments and to check out the make up and skincare at Benefit.  I definitely recommend going to see Ashley for brow shaping and to Benefit in general for their great service and products!

Awful just awful!!! Do not have any work finished by this place!  I went with my 12 year old daughter to get a rug pillow finished.  Since it was hers and her money we asked how much it would be ... around 50 dollars. Which was high for a rug craft but she wanted it ...so we said okay. Today I picked it up, when we signed the ticket I thought it said 60 dollars . It was not until we were in the car .... $160.00 yep 320 % More than a quote not 20% higher. She called and was told the person should not have quoted her a price !

Came here on New Years Eve around 4 pm and they were able to fit me in for a manicure and pedicure with no wait. Not the fanciest salon, but I got 2 pedicures and 1 manicure for $47 - can't really complain. The woman doing my nails was polite and kind. She did a great job shaping my nails which is my biggest concern (polish is polish - it will eventually chip). The salon is clean and actually pretty spacious as it goes back pretty far with 4 pedicure chairs in the back and about 5 manicure stations in the front. They also do massages upstairs and have waxing available. Would come back here if in need of a manicure or pedicure in a hurry.

With beer such a big part of you experience it is disappointing that no attention is spent how the draft beer  is being served take a look at your draft beer no carbonation or foam beer is totally flat either the soap detergent you are using to clean your beer glasses is affecting the carbonation or your co2 air handler is effecting the beer. Get with any of your beer suppliers and they can assist thanks for having Stormbringer what a great band

I had looked up restaurants before my first time road trip to NOLA. This was one of many places that listed oyster Po Boys (I had made a list of foods I wanted to eat that aren't prevalent or good in my area). We had a group of 10, and I and a friend of ours that had been a local here for years, both sold this place to our group. Then we got there. Immediately 6 of our group wanted to go elsewhere (of course only AFTER I told the staff we were seating 10 and they moved tables all around). just from it's appearance. I myself was a little concerned, as what others might describe as decor I would call old and run down. To me, this place is the literal hole in the wall.

So 4 of us sat while more than half our group went across town. I ordered a cup of gumbo and a fried oyster "LOAF". The gumbo was ok. I can't speak to it's "authenticity", which is a word that seems to continually come up in any group when dealing with cajun food, but it was just good. Now for the po boy, it's not a po boy, at least not in my opinion. I did not like that it came in slices of plain white bread. The wording of loaf on the menu didn't really sink in until after it came to the table. Well it was stacked inside with fried oysters, so much that a few had fallen out onto the plate. So I picked one up and ate it direct. WOW. The best way I can put this is I've never had a fried oyster that tasted this good, NOR WILL I EVER. Out of a week stay in NOLA, this was the one memorable food I had there. I could have eaten 2 plates of this, gone home, rested, and came back to order another 2 plates. It is very hard to impress me, as the rest of my stay in NOLA is a testament to. The second half of the loaf, I just removed the bread and picked at the oysters.

My friends had raw oysters on shells, they were large and looked good to me, but they seemed unimpressed. They possibly weren't big fans to begin with. One in the group had fried soft shell crab, and he seemed pleased with it.

From this point forward, whenever I think of fried oysters, I will immediately think Casamentos.

17 Souls ...

     Where were you when you learned of Nine Eleven? 

     Some things we can never forget.  Nor should we ever want to. 

     The Garden of Reflection is a work of love, loss and remembrance.  The families of those who perished in that horrific attack, along with some very talented and dedicated people, came together to create a one of a kind setting for the somber, yet uplifting, task of bringing us together to Reflect on and Remember what happened on that day. 

      We feel a shared emptiness in our hearts from the loss of loved ones many of us knew.  We appreciate the powerful yet gentle flow of the park-like design that guides us along its paths ever closer to its center, the twin fountains in a pool made of concrete, stone and reflections of trees and sky.  We count the trees planted around the low walls of concrete and glass with so many names...

     This is a profoundly beautiful and haunting place. It echoes within, and calls out for love, peace, understanding, and never forget determination.

9.11.2013 - The Memorial Ceremony held here today was poignant and sad.  Finding and giving comfort was the message of the day from all the spiritual leaders asked to speak, pray, play music, and stand silently.  From the size of the crowd it is clear that we will always remember the national and personal losses we suffered that day 12 years ago. 

Teach your children well.

It smelled fantastic in Steadfast the day of my first visit, like a savory onion soup. Having arrived in the morning, I chose a hot butterscotch latte, served in a squat glass. The price is comparable to other trendy coffee shops like Crema and Barista Parlor, though I found the team at Steadfast far friendlier than Barista Parlor, of which I'm not much of a fan (of their coffee or ordering setup).

It's clear how the process works at Steadfast: You're pointed to the counter with an iPad, and they use Square for payments. The tables form a U-shaped space, and I started my stay on the outdoor patio, the Friday crowd at 10 a.m. filling up the tables indoors.

Once there was space inside, I moved to a table in the corner and plugged in my laptop for a stay. Though I'd since finished my drink, the Steadfast team never rushed me or suggested I order anything else to validate my taking up a table (though my own guilt had me a bit concerned I was being rude). However, by 11:30 a.m., enough tables had cleared up to ease my worry over taking up a customer's space.

I'm really happy with the quality of their coffee and found the fellow who helped me order, delivered my drink, picked it up once I finished and ushered me to an open table indoors kind and in good spirits. Other than limited table space and parking difficulty -- the lot is exceptionally narrow and with only eight or so spaces, so street parking along Taylor is an inevitability -- I've no qualms with this spot. I'm looking forward to returning, which is always the measure of success.

This is my go-to coffee shop in Headhouse Square. My friends and I usually come here about 2-3 times per week. They have fun drinks (like the lavender latte) and good staples, like iced coffee. They also recently started selling ice cream. The staff here are very fun and friendly, too. Especially Bean, with the cool hair. Would definitely recommend this place for a simple but lovely coffee shop.
Great coffee. Went for a walk and decided to get a coffee. It was perfect. Sandwiches looked very good and so did the bakery area. Nice little place.

Wow, I didn't even realize this was a chain! I guess it kind of has that look to it, especially from the outside, but it seems nicer than most chains to me. It has a pretty unromantic location--right by the Kroger, pretty much in a strip mall--but the food is good, and pitchers of beer are relatively cheap. The decor is a little nicer than the standard Mexican restaurant pictures of girls in 19th-century-looking traditional dresses baking bread or something.

I recommend either not ordering an appetizer, or sharing an entree with your dinner companion, because the portions are really big. Any kind of burrito smothered in sauce is a good bet. The only thing I really can't recommend ordering is the quesadilla with meat added--when mine arrived, it was almost all meat and very little cheese. Hardly a quesadilla in my book!

Another thing I really like about this restaurant is that there are two "sides" to it. When you walk in, you can either go to the right or to the left. To the right is a dining area which is mercifully free of TVs, and to the left is bar area (with tables) that does have a TV. I really appreciate the choices there, since TVs that can't be escaped are one of my biggest restaurant pet peeves!

I was super excited to try this place and was severely let down. This was my first time and I ordered a double char, but well done and with no mayo and no tomato. I got a soggy burger with more cheese on the paper than on the burger that was not well done, and had mayo and tomato. Super disappointing. This place seems like it could've been awesome, but was a huge let down.

Everyone has a hobby, and my hobby happens to be speakeasies and tiki bars. I quite literally travel the nation hunting them down everywhere I go. Of the 213 speakeasy/tiki bar I have patronized thus far...,  THIS is the one that started my obsession about 10 years ago.  I have returned here time and time again, both alone and with guests.  Although the entry into the bar is not particularly creative (by modern speakeasy standards), it still maintains a great secret bar feel. The overall vibe and ambience once you're inside more than makes up for the lack of a flashy hidden entrance.  As to the magic of the mixology itself, they offer a good selection of craft cocktails with minimal trendy hype. If you are looking for a classy cocktail experience in the heart of Philly, take the time and effort to track down this gem. You will not regret it.

Our first stop in Nola when we got into town and our last dinner destination before we leave tomorrow.
Loved everything we've eaten.... and Loved our servers and bartenders, too

This isn't your cup o' noodles ramen!  I've not been to a ramen shop until we visited Rai Rai Ramen.  We've gone back several times since they opened.  The staff is super friendly and patient when we ask for details on the different dishes.  
We've tried several and have yet to be disappointed.

I went here many many years ago as a little kid and went back for the first time the other week. My intention was to see the mum mountain for fall. The display was beautiful and inside the store they had plenty of pumpkins for fall and tons of plants - like tons! You can get lost in here. The prices seemed reasonable and they have an extensive variety. 
This is definitely a unique spot in Montco.

This is for the tire center. They rarely answer the phone when you call and when I finally did get through I received the run around and bogus info from someone who was either new or poorly trained. I don't want to pay $200 more at Les Schwab but I'm willing to just for their customer service alone

Poorest  service  i ever had anywhere. 14 mins for waiter another 17 mins for our drinks. 19:39 for our food to come out. Completely lost my appetite for this place.  Heard such great things about this place. The food was good.  The service well that was something to be left to the imagination.

Went out for an impromptu girls night to Avenue Kitchen. Was very impressed by the beautiful, soothing interior design - upscale yet still casual enough not to feel stodgy and overly "mainline" (I'm from Berwyn and live in Bryn Mawr, I can say that.)  Anyway, service was excellent, I'm giving 4 stars because they did mess up my girlfriend's order after she specifically told them about her dairy allergy - but they were very accommodating about it. We had the baked goat cheese - warm, savory, and delicious, served with toasted/buttered garlic bread. Also ordered the hummus platter app, very tasty as well. I ordered the blackened fish tacos which were perfect - on the spicy side but very fresh and perfect with the Durango sauv blanc. Overall thumbs up!

So, yeah, actually my nails look pretty great (dip manicure). But this place just seems like a hazard? It's so small and there are so many people inside. And I'm not sure it's ventilated properly, because the smell of acetone is a killer in there. I'd say the prices were average for Nashville - even at places that seem cleaner and better ventilated. I was in a pinch, and Top Line Nail could fit me in (they were the fifth salon I tried on a Wednesday around 3 pm). But it's definitely not going to go into my normal rotation of places.
Always get excellent service at this location. Joanie the server is excellent. She has amazing customer service skills and is just awesome. Kathleen the manager is also great.
Poor service after taking 15 minutes for a milkshake. Friend next to me just ordered a small fry and that took 10 minutes as well. At least the food tasted semi decent.

I came here tonight with my best friend and we had a great girls night out! The happy hour menu is amazing here. We each had a drink and we split 3 of the happy hour starters and it was plenty for our meal! We split the bill and all that, with tax and tip was only $16! It was a steal!

I had their draft cider, which was Angry Orchard cider and on happy hour for $4. I love Angry Orchard and it was great on a Fall night! We split the Mac and Cheese bites, margherita flatbread, and chicken tacos. I loved the Mac and Cheese bites! The sauce they came with was a little spicy and so addicting! We ended up pouring some of it on our chicken tacos because we couldn't get enough of it! The chicken tacos were great as well. I thought the margherita flatbread was just ok. The other two were definitely better and next time, I would probably try something other than the flatbread.

Overall, I definitely recommend this restaurant for happy hour! Their deals are amazing and the food and drinks are great as well.

We had a room upstairs which was great. Service was outstanding as well. Started with pork belly boudin balls and crawfish quesadillas. Really tasty and flavors were wonderful.  I love Drum and this was a nice surprise!  Crusted with a wonderful breading over potatoes with a burre blanc sauce.  Will be back in a month!!!

This was my go to restaurant at least once a week.  Since they moved, the service is AWFUL!  A friend actually walked out of the restaurant because the waitress never came back to the table.  I don't understand because everytime I was in there servers were sitting in booths talking to each other.  The owner or his daughters were always in the restaurant before they moved and now nobody is there.  Lack of management.

This place is a dump and a rip off, plain and simple.  I have NO idea how this became such a destination spot, but good lord.. there are MANY better places than this.  The rooms are shoddy and stink like roach spray.  The bathrooms have institution style toilets with 1960s tile work.. and all this with a backdrop of puking spring breakers running semi-nude throughout the place.  AVOID.

This is an honest, impartial review.  I have no bones to pick with them.. but someone needs to leave a fair review and call it like it is.

I use another bank/credit union someplace else, but learned of this bank through my spouse.
I have never had an issue with this credit union whether I'm going through the drive-through or coming inside.
 The people are usually helpful and if you have banking problems with your ATM card or anything they're quick to get in there and help you with that.
 I loved the rewards program up until this year when they stopped giving reward points towards the debit cards which I think is unfair...but I guess that is business.

Went here the first time the other day, I'll probably give it another try in the future.  It's in a convenient location, open 24/7 and has a nice diner feel too the place.  The decor looks delightfully 70's and is in remarkable condition considering it's age.  I like that the menu is a manageable size. Diners that have huge menus worry me about the freshness of their ingredients.  I had coffee, a slice of apple pie and some water.  We were served quickly, but the apple pie was bland so I only took a few bites. I also had to hunt down the waitress for refills on more than one occasion.  Next time I go, I'll order real food and will update my review accordingly.

Agree with most of the comments here.  This place  is shady AF and the people are extremely rude.  Lady called me to let me know I won a gun.  I asked how long I had to pick it up.  She immediately put another man on the phone who immediately yelled "what's your problem, I'm going to charge your card  and you can come pick the item up".  Before I could get a word in, the guy hung up on me.  However, the terms of sale clearly state that the sellers premium is determined based on cash or credit sale and they didn't give me an option, they just charged my card for the 15% rather than the 12%.

Crooks!  Don't buy or sell ANYTHING here!
Excellent food and service. Have been there a few times and we live the exotic types of pizzas and appetizers. Just need to work on making that French press coffee hotter.

I was on vacation visiting family, and this place was right across the street from my hotel. My dad and I met his friend there for lunch the last day we were in St. Louis, and the atmosphere was wonderful and the service was great. We were seated right away, and our food came out quick. I ordered the chili and a dinner salad because I wasn't that hungry, and it fit the spot. The chili was a little spicy, but it made me happy.

Coming from CA, where the service at restaurants always seem to be below par, this place was wonderful!!

If you are looking for pill pushing doctors who are not concerned about getting you better and don't mind an extremely rude staff, then this place is for you. The office manager thinks she owns the place and that the Dr's work for her.

Haircut was fine, but if you are pressed for time go somewhere else. Despite having called in the morning to make an appointment, once there I was just mixed in with all the walk ins and was taken 25 minutes after scheduled appointment time. I was 5 minutes earlier than my appointment when I arrived.

Love chipotle but this location really didn't set well with me tonight. I live less then a half of mile from the place so I do go there a lot. Now this is the first time I've had a problem here. I for sure blame the particular staff that were working at the time. I waited in line over 15 minutes with only six people in front of me. No one had a clue what they were doing even down at the register the girl gave me two dollars back more then she was supposed to. 

Now I do work in the restaurant business so I do understand that some employees are better then others but come on. There needs to be at least one person every shift that knows what's going on. It's like they put all the rookies on at the same time no supervision. I will be going back only cause it's a half of mile from my house but if you can find a different location I would recommend doing so.

DO NOT EXPECT Mattress Firm delivery will reach out to you when they arrive if you live in an apartment building!

I'm so disappointed with​ their delivery service. I was waiting at home all the time after I got their automatic voice mail. When it was approaching​ the last minute of their delivery window, I called the retail store. The only thing that the manager repeated was THIS IS YOUR RESPONSIBILITY. He said he told me at the store they wouldn't call, which I DIDN'T KNOW AT ALL. He said the delivery man called my apartment, which I DIDN'T RECEIVE EITHER. He said my apartment building lobby was locked and they took a picture. Of course,​ it would be locked because of this late delivery time on Sunday and the property management office was closed then (5:10pm according to the manager), but what's the picture for, to prove I'm not waving a ​hand at them at lobby??

I was so angry at the manager repeatedly saying or indicating this was my responsibility, and not answering my question on how to solve this problem. The manager also kept repeating that the delivery team wouldn't call me because it is against law. I'm not an expert at the law and don't want to argue if it is against law, but I strongly recommend Mattress Firm to improve the delivery service. Instead of a ​phone call, you can text me or let me choose if I would like to be called at. I live in a large apartment building, and we have a loading area at the back of the building. If you don't call me and use a third-party vendor which you can't control at all, how can I tell them they need to go to the loading area instead of the ​lobby? I understand not every company could have their local delivery team like IKEA who is familiar with the local building, but please try to reach out to your customer rather than just wait there, took a picture and blamed your customer not showing up.

I decided to cancel this order after I heard from the manager that they would process my order and begin to reschedule as early as Tuesday. My second suggestion here for the miss delivery is, please try your best to help your customer get their mattress on the second day when you don't make it for the first time. I don't know how many people would need their mattress as soon as possible due to relocation like me, but this slow process indeed pushed me even further to cancel this order and turn to other retailers.  The disappointment, the blame and more waiting days, all of them are too much for your customer after they sweep the floor and make everything ready for the new mattress with an exciting mood.

One of the worst dining experiences ever. From waiting roughly an hour to get just appetizers, reminding the server about our missing miso soups, and trying to get some chopsticks because there wasn't a pair set at the table. 

Sushi was not great. The long wait became dreadful and disappointing to say the least. The sushi was loaded with more than the average amount of rice. Almost two hours have passed and my table is still waiting for some sushi from the original first order. 

The only thing that seems good is the water refill service, but that could just be a tactic to fill us up with water before even eating any sushi...

I ordered a $100 tray of hoagies to be delivered for the holidays to my local doggy daycare. I was very specific about address, time and card. The delivery person called me 30 minutes after it was to be delivered and was lost.  He never called back but I got him an hour later and he told me he made it. Weeks later when I didn't hear anything from daycare I asked and they said they received with note or card. So they had no idea who they were from. I called primo to let them
Know of error and basically got told well I wasn't here that day.  I will think again before using them for tray delivery.  When I spring money for a service especially around the holidays I would hope for the company to get it right.

Me and my wife have eaten here at least 7 times since July. I really like the atmosphere inside the restaurant and they have a great beer selection. We kept returning because we liked the food and the service was not horrible.

I don't know what happened, but the last two times we ate here the food preparation was embarrassingly bad. For 2 people, for dinner and a couple beers, we are spending like $50, so it was really disappointing to be served food that was almost not eatable during our past two visits. I ordered chicken tenders and they were soooo over cooked . . . hard as rocks. My wife got a burger that was haphazardly thrown together such that it looked really unappealing, for example, like how a burger would look after it was squished inside a McDonalds wrapper. We also got side salads and the lettuce was starting to go bad and slimy on the edges. 

As I mentioned above, we have eaten here several times over the past 6 months, and during our first visits we were satisfied, but it seems like there has been a change for the worse at Brooklyn Burgers. My guess would be that there is some kind of problem with the cook/kitchen. Given that we have had two bad experiences in a row, we have decided to not return for a while in hopes that Brooklyn will fix whatever issues they are going through. This is really disappointing because I used to like this restaurant, but given the poor quality of food that we were served in two occasions, I cannot recommend that anyone spend their money here on anything beside the beer. Please see why you are having issues with the kitchen Brooklyn Burgers and Beer!

I was hesitant to write a review since I only had brunch but that meal should be pretty easy to do and impress. 
Cute interior- pleasant service. We walked in around 12:40am with no reservations and there were plenty of tables.  
Cornmeal pancakes good-but not really special for 12/$. The spiced maple syrup was a nice touch. 
Frittata was horrible. Cute presentation in mini cast iorn skillet but the frittata ( wild mushrooms and gruyere) was completely flavorless--I  think they forgot the gruyere. Potatoes on the top of fritatta were soggy but maybe that is how they serve them.  
Had to ask for salt and pepper-no tables had salt/pepper on them--so either they forgot to set out or they don't do salt and pepper implying the kitchen will season everything to perfection. Since my fritatta had no flavor I needed pepper which the waitress had to serve via peppermill. Nice touch but  I would have preferred salt and pepper on table.
I was excited to eat here but for brunch there are too many other places to go. Maybe not with as sleek a finish out but oh well.

The ambiance is nice. Enjoy the chess images and the look of the chairs as well. The food was ok, I had never been here before, qualify of meal made me think the restaurant was new. The pancakes are really big and the cheese eggs didn't have the cheese blended in, it was laid on top as if they almost forgot cheese was included. I tried the BLTA it was a different taste, bland for my liking. Service was nice, ambiance enjoyable and food was mediocre.

Newsflash, McDonalds. You have other customers than just the drive-through window! I went there yesterday for a simple breakfast; something to eat, a cup of coffee, sit down at a table and read the morning paper. There weren't many people inside, but the drive-through was wrapped around the building. After being first in line, and waiting several minutes, a staff member came over from the drive-thru window, and took my order. She gave me the price, and as I began to hand her my money, she walked back to the window. There I stood, cash in hand, the cash register flashing away, and nobody to complete the order. Frustrated, I looked around to see another person in line behind me, just shaking his head, another waiting for his order, who was waiting when I came in, and not a staff member to help. I finally gave up, pocketed my money, and went down the street for a nice, relaxed breakfast.
Great lunch spot! And, solid management. A slight mix up on my order and Kevin went out of his way to make it right, and then some! Thanks!

I was in Tampa for the day with a friend and we decided to go here for a drink and some appetizers before we left home for Orlando.  We were seated in the bar area immediately and the waitress brought us some menus immediately and took our drink order.  We told her we were waiting on 3 others to join before we ordered any food.  That was a mistake because we could not get her to come to our table again until she saw the rest of our party arrive.  I wanted to order another drink in the meantime and an appetizer but that was difficult since we could not find her anywhere for at least 20 minutes.  The service wasn't very good.  We also asked her if there were any drink specials on beer and she said no.  When in fact we later discovered all over the menu it stated $1.99 select beer specials all day, every day which we later had to ask her about and then she finally revealed the beer specials and prices to us. 

My friend and I ordered cheese fries and boneless buffalo wings to share.  They were both pretty good and hit the spot.  I would try this place again when I'm in the area and would hope service is better next time.

This used to be my breakfast green smoothie home. Last school year I'd call almost every morning. They knew me by name and my order. Always had it ready to go on time. Never had any problems using my rewards. The 3 stars are for the great service I received in times past. 

Now it's a mess. They have new staff and more of them but somehow service has gotten worst. On my birthday I was so disappointed. First of all I tried to order using the app like I've done several times in the past however the app for some reason wasn't working so I called. The woman who answered the phone was rude. I explained to her that the app wasn't allowing me to order. She then told me she do it over the phone one time as a courtesy but next time I need to use the app. As if I can control the app not working. I walk to the counter and I go to get my birthday reward and the person at the register has no idea what order I'm talking about or how to redeem my birthday reward. Then to top it off my drink isn't even made! 

I wasn't the only person having a bad day there. This other woman was asking for her food to be prepared for a third time and requested that one of the girls not touch her food. I'm assuming it was because of something has happened before I entered.

Visitors were not wearing masks in the store.This is my first time being in Target since Covid-19.I asked the girl at the checkout if everyone was suppose to be wearing masks when they come in and she said yes.I said there is a whole family with kids with no masks.I asked her how they were allowed in the store and she told me,when she works the door some people will not take a free mask.If that is so,they should be turned away from the store.Does Target need their money so bad that they let them in anyway.ASHAMED of you Target!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

MAKE A RESERVATION FIRST.  If you don't you're probably looking at least at an hour wait for a hibachi table.  I've been to my share of Benihana's and this one ranks in the lower half of those experiences.  The #1 reason is the service.  The Benihana in Orlando knew how to treat a family with young kids.  Its their specialty and they've pretty much set the bar so high that most other locations simply can't match the performance.  The staff here is nice, but you can tell that something is missing.  Could be that we're in the middle of winter and people from Philly in the winter generally don't have much to be happy about when compared to Orlando, but its something to note.  The food is comfortably similar (which is why we go) and while not spectacular I don't feel like I'm taking a risk when eating here. I did see that they have an "Endless Sushi" special for $29.95 Monday thru Thursday so if you're hungry on a school night it might be worth trying.

We visit Scape about 6-8 times a year. Up until this visit I went to lengths to extoll the positives about each and every visit. Each and every is pretty darn good in my book! Four of us dined there last night and were somewhat startled at the first piece of information provided by our server, Phillip; Chef Kelly left a few months ago. Wow, that's huge! 
The evening just didn't click this time. I'm trying not to blame in on Kelly's departure but some restaurants, especially the really good ones, are affected by a principal players departure. The Caprese salad and Gazpacho were spot on. My friends fish and my wife's items also hit their mark but my ribeye was terrible, both in quality and preparation. The menu states Cowboy, i.e., bone-in but it was boneless. Half went back to the kitchen as gristle and fat. It was medium well instead of medium rare, unforgivable. ($39) In all fairness, Phillip offered to take it back as he did our guests pasta (cold) but who wants to hold up a dinner for a steak to be reworked. Desserts were compted because of "the previous issues". As a final note, they took their signature banana dessert off the menu? I recall Kelly telling me once that it was their most popular dessert. Gosh, I hope this was just a glitch!

Incredible service!  Had been going to Fountain Tire and got completely fed up with the lack of friendly timely service.  I decided to try Big Kev's and couldn't believe how Friendly the service was and the amazing quality of work.  Bonny provides service the good ol fashioned way and goes the extra mile - said he'd check in to something and call us right back and he did!  You feel they appreciate your business and want to make you happy.

Only there for a nightcap, and it was worth it. The place itself is really cool - laid back, dark (in a fun way), and just the place to go for a quiet conversation with friends. The drink menu was intriguing - not your usual drinks - and the waiter was kind enough to guide my friend through the selections to ensure the choice was just right. I had a draft beer - which was really all I wanted, and it was perfect. We didn't have any of the food offerings, but if the food was anything like the drinks I'm sure it had to be eclectic and very good! Highly recommended for a good drink in an atmosphere conducive to reasonable conversation.

The place was okay. The fried rice could be better, but I definitely enjoyed the chicken. It is a good price for a large portioned meal. The line wasn't bad and I got my food quick enough for my 30 minute lunch break. I would probably eat there again. Definitely better than panda express, but still isn't anywhere close to PF Chang's. Good quick cheap Chinese food.

Excellent food!! And service!!!
Just stopped in here for dinner after a train ride from NYC and I must say I'm very happy I waited to eat here. It's such a nice place with nice staff. I wish I lived close by so I could come here often. Although, now that I found this little gem of a restaurant, I will be making the hour+ long drive to come back. Owners have a lot to be proud of. The food was delicious. I had the roasted pork and my daughter their Churasco steak and both were super delicious! Prices reasonable but the family atmosphere was also very welcoming.

I previously enjoyed going to this restaurant prior to the pandemic. Today I tried to introduce my friend to a new restaurant. There was a short wait, but we were seated pretty quickly. We didn't see our server for at least 10 minutes, and before we even had water, we had our order ready. We ordered a salad and a sushi boat, which we expected to take a little longer. But we sat watching our boat in the window for a good 5 minutes. By the time we received our sushi, we needed a refill of water. We had to ask 3 times before getting a refill. I must say, the food was great, but my friend was quite upset because she was dehydrated and sushi is salty, so I had to wait to eat until she got her water. We ate every single bite, despite not seeing our original server until we walked to the host stand to pay 25 minutes after our check was dropped by another server. The were no acts of good faith and we didn't see a manger, not that we asked. I don't usually Yelp restaurants because I've worked in the service industry, but this was an exception.

Edit: I just realized they never brought our salad or rice that was supposed to come with our boat!

I've eaten here MANY times with no issues. We celebrated my daughters bday here on Friday night. They were busy but not packed full. We ordered drinks, appetizers, sushi,  and then our meals. When our food started arriving it was literally just thrown on our table. We got our entrees before our apps and before our drinks. Then the appetizers arrived and again were
Thrown on the table. Drinks came last. I was so overwhelmed that I did not enjoy this meal what so ever. Sushi was the last thing brought and by this time we had so much food piled up and were so rushed we didn't even eat it. We were short silverware and when we asked the guy throwing food on the table for and extra set before I even got my sentence out he said ask your server. My daughter never got her miso soup or her spring rolls, but they were on our bill. I left pretty pissed off about this whole experience. 45 min and we were done with the meal. Vert disappointed in how they are running things right now. I didn't even get pictures

Expectation vs Reality...  the new Banana Pudding.  What a joke.  It shows it in a mason jar and all delicious.  Then I get something that looks like it was made by a 5 year old.

The food is average at most Cracker Barrel restaurants, but it's the closest thing to southern cooking a lot of times, so we came here.  I'm so disappointed by the new banana pudding.  My chicken fried chicken was ok, but this dessert is an absolute joke.  And we ordered 2 of them....  next time, I'll find dessert somewhere else.

Just tried this place out for the first time. I'm always skeptical of making last minute appointments and whether a salon will honor calling ahead but even though this place was super busy they were true to their word and got my nails wrapped almost immediately. V did my nails and he was so thorough and polite too, making sure he wasn't hurting me and explaining what he was doing. He did an excellent job and had me out of there in under an hour without cutting any corners! Great interior, very clean and the staff are all super friendly. I'd certainly recommend this place.

Bartender was only saving grace.  The whole restaurant is a patio.  It was cold.  Took 30 minutes to get a working heater.  It was 5 min till happy hour and server refused to put in the order early.  We had to wait to get our happy hour drink!!  But The Bartender helped us out and hooked is up though.  Could have been an off day at Sandbar, but not impressed!

It was a 30% off sale that brought me in, but the staff and events keep me coming back. The staff here is incredibly knowledgable when it comes to wine and the stock they carry. They were the only place I could find in Edmonton that carried a brand from Santorini, Greece that I fell in love with years ago. Ever staff member I have run into there has been helpful and kind. 

The wine testers they bring in are always friendly and have given me fantastic suggestions, even when it isn't their brand. They also carry some of the best stock of beer from Alberta breweries such as Hell's Basement, Blindman, and Citizen. If there's something unique you are looking for make sure this is your first stop!

Probably the worst experience I have had at a restaurant in Boise. Went with a group of friends and it was 25 minutes of waiting and still did not receive our first round of drinks. They were out of multiple items. There was blasting rap music distorting the speakers. And when we went inside to look for a server they were on FaceTime making plans for the night.  So we left and spent our $300 elsewhere. We won't ever be back.

Will not go back

I am a Mexican food connoisseur, have eaten at at least 100+ different restaurants across 10+ states.  Grew up on Californian Mexican food.

Ordered (Saturday Night 8/24/2019)

Red Tamales (Pork) 2 of 5 stars
- the pork was good the enchilada sauce was horrible
- It is probably a preference, but the red sauce was too chalky and needed a little sweetness.  they could easily fix this, but probably grandma's recipe

Green Tamales (Spinach) 4.5 of 5 Stars
- the creamy green sauce is excellent, a little sweet for me but makes for a great tamale.  My wife loved it!!!

Chile Verde a la cart with soft tortillas on the side 2 of 5 Stars
- Something was just off with this, it seemed to have a thickener in the sauce which gave it a weird texture
- had an awkward flavor too it, with the balance of sweet to spicy

Chips (have to pay extra) 4 of 5 Stars

Salsa Bar (included) 4 of 5 Stars
- have 5-6 flavors, all fairly good, did not have extra pico de gallo, but maybe a fluke night
-the main mild salsa again had a slight off sweetness that kept me from liking it all the way (Drinkable is a 5 star salsa) so not the best

Staff was fine, nothing good or bad!

About 12 years ago we stayed here while looking for a home to buy. It's definitely not the same place that it was then. The good:  no bugs, roof didn't leak, quiet, surface clean (except for the tub.. The "interesting" features:  several holes in the walkway ceilings (some covered with black plastic and duct tape), enough mildew in the sauna tub to worry me that something might crawl out of one of the jets while I was using the tub, garbage left on the private balcony that we had to clean up, and the  blackout liner behind the curtains in the bedroom actually had several holes. And this was one of the presidential suites. We would not have stayed here had we not gotten in so late and been so dog tired that we didn't notice much other than the mildew in the tub. I would have given it only one star except we did stay in a worse BW years ago. All of our other BW stays have been very good experiences.
Very good. Really enjoyed the Carna Seca Chipotle Burro. Awesome guacamole! Very fast service.
Decent food, good service. live music. Prices arent bad. SPinach salad with warm bacon dressing is this bomb. Good neighborhood spot, especially for the older folks at night.

If I could give negative stars, I would. I strongly urge everyone to use any other tailor. 

I took a bridesmaid dress to Maria to be tailored and she absolutely butchered it. She made it too small. There were threads hanging out of the top of the shoulder straps. She cut material out of the side seam after I specifically asked her not to. I asked for side ruching and she added horrible side pleats. 

She also took way too long to complete the work. She had the dress for a month. We arranged for fittings in advance and the dress was never ready at the agreed time. I asked for the dress to be done 1 week before the event and she finally gave it to me two days before the wedding I needed it for. At that time, I was able to see the horrible quality of her work. I rushed it to another tailor who was unable to fix the dress before the event because the dress was so terribly altered by Maria. 

In fact, I'm not sure it can be saved at all. When all was said and done, I had wasted about $200 on a dress that I could not even wear. I didn't match the other women at the wedding. It was embarrassing and upsetting!

This place is so disorganized. The service was terrible. I ordered online and showed up at the promised time (30 mins after I ordered). I actually felt bad for the young lady at the front. She was taking phone calls, handling to-go orders and hosting on a busy Friday night. I waited for 25 mins for her to finally realize that she didn't even have my order entered. Apparently when you order online they still have to manually enter the order and she missed it. So it's been an hour since I originally placed my order and am waiting another 20-30 mins for our dinner. This is our first time coming here and I'm sorely disappointed. I really wanted to love this place. We're not eating dinner until after 9pm now
