My wife and I went Seorabul for the first time to check out Korean BBQ. We arrived there around 6pm on Saturday.  The place was crowded and that was good sign of a good restaurant. 

First, the service/waitress was a bit slow on our table compare to the other table who arrived 5 minutes later than us. Because the other table already got there side dishes before us. Second, Bulgoghi tasted just or just below average. It had very little flavor. Same with the spicy chicken: neither it was spicy or flavorful. Lastly, the portion was a bit much for the bbq. Each order would be sufficient for two average adults in my opinion. The restaurant required customers to order minimum of two portions. 

We didn't try other dishes, so my opinion is just on beef and chicken bbq we had for dinner. In my opinion, this place is just an average and I do not get all the hypes from others.

I am in shock that the average for Pappy's as of today is 4.5 stars, perhaps my experience is biased because we just ordered takeout.

Sweet potato fries were the absolute worst I've ever had in my life. Not only were they soggy, but they weren't even fully cooked. It was consistently recommended by folks on Yelp ... is that because the Lou doesn't have lots of sweet potato fry offerings?

Potato salad was just OK. I've never seen carrots in potato salad, and am not sure what they added.

Baked beans were only marginally better than what you can get in a can.

Pulled pork looked a lot like chicken to me. Order taking fail? Probably -- since we ordered 2 baked beans, 1 sweet potato fries, and 1 potato salad and got 2 baked beans, 3 sweet potato fries, and 2 potato salads. Usually, I'm grateful for an overload, but they also overcharged us and well, the food was not good.

Only decent offering we had was the brisket. Good for them to serve sauce on the side. That said, I wouldn't go back. Brisket was good, but not good enough to counter all the bad with this place. There's gotta be better BBQ in the Lou.

I will never order from here again. Placed an order around 8:00 on a Monday night, called around 9:00 they said my order had already left about 10 minutes before I called. 9:40 I called again only to be told that they didn't have an order for me, they couldn't find my order In their system. So I asked why was I told my order left in the first place when they never saved my order to be placed. Ridiculous, I've ordered plenty of times and occasionally have small issues but this time they have lost my business for good!

I was in the mood for a really good slice of pizza so I did some research for places that's didn't serve St. Louis style pizza because that was not what I was looking for. I came in to Racanelli's and they did not disappoint! I loved everything about the pizza. It had the perfect amount of cheese, the perfect amount of sauce, the crust was the perfect doneness. I have no complaints about the pizza what so ever. Some of the best New York style pizza I've had in St. Louis. I loved my visit so much I went back later that week!

It's been said many times before and I'm here to say it again -- this place deserves zero stars. I came in to rent a truck to move a piece of furniture from SB to Goleta (24 miles total). I was initially given a quote of $19.99 plus mileage. When I returned the truck an hour later the final price was $52. The man who worked there mumbled the final price to me and I had to ask for a breakdown which was a major grievance to him. He sighed his way through the up-charges, the math didn't check out. I questioned him about it and he gave me $3 off the final price as if he was saving the day. It was rude. It was a ripoff. I should have done more research on renting a truck for an hour, but this place is conveniently located near my office. However, dear reader, learn from my mistakes. Do not go to Goleta Motors for vehicle rentals or buying used cars or whatever else they do there. Avoid at all costs and literally go anywhere else.
Busy, but comfortable place for late lunch on a Saturday. Nice atmosphere and good nachos and margaritas.  Staff was accommodating and friendly!

The current manager here, a young girl named Brittany, is an extremely inappropriate nuisance and is downright harassing.  She calls approximately 3 days in advance to confirm scheduled appointments. Completely reasonable and understandable. We always politely confirm.  She then proceeds to call several times a day to re-confirm, including leaving multiple nasty voicemails telling her you NEED to call her back and threatening to cancel your appointment if you do not call her back and confirm AGAIN. I received 3 of these voicemails in a 2-day time span, which I have saved on my phone. The exact same thing happened either last February with my other daughter's photo shoot, or last October for this daughter's last shoot. Brittany called me about 4 or 5 times, asking for re-confirmation each time. I finally called the 1-800-345-6019 to describe again this girl's behavior and request to have the district manager call me so I can relay this information to him.  He/she has yet to call me back. We have been customers for years, coming twice a year for both of our daughters' annual birthday photo shoots, and we have never once been a no-show.  Nick was a wonderful manager who never treated customers this way. This is just crazy, unprofessional behavior and needs to be stopped immediately, as it has been going on for months now.

I really don't even want to give this place a 1 how about a 0.
 
 Ok lets go to the fact that this was the worst $14 I have ever spent on food.

 The lunch special was:

A salad that came out in big dinner bowl but was only the size of a balled up fist with about 2 baby teaspoons of dressing. The lettuce was mostly the heart of lettuce.  A total disgrace!!! 

Pizza was the horrible. I've tasted better from a $.79 totinos pizza from the grocery's freezer... Come on $14 for something as thin as a fried 9 inch tortilla with about .25 oz. of cheese and about 3 nibbles of chicken that would make a mouse say damn yall cheap as hell.... take this back !!!

2, 3 4 or 5 stars?!!?.... Me thinks not, unless you like to waste money on overpriced nothingness!!!

Okay... Grumpy's was alright... I believe there's better spots for burgers though. I had the open face burger, but instead of bread I opted for lettuce. Let's just say, I woke up last night feeling like I ate a pound of msg haha. I think it was the green chili that they served. I mean, really? 

Anyways, we came here on a Monday night. It was slow, only one table. We sat down and ordered. What felt like 30 minutes pass before we get our food, and mind you, it's not like the food was very difficult to prepare. Burgers mostly. Nothing too crazy.

As I said previously, I ordered Grumpy's open faced burger. It was umm not as good as I thought it could be. I felt as if the chili came out of a can. And it just wasn't satisfying. This place is rated as 4 stars and maybe I just ordered the wrong thing, but I'm sure there are better things around.

I probably won't come back here, even if I was visiting. It didn't last as a lasting impression on me, but hey, try it out and if you disagree let me know what you got!

Went to see if they could install a brake light.  They gladly took my car in and realized they didn't have the bulb in stock.  They said the parts truck would be here in 20 minutes and they would install it and provide the bulb for free.  I told them I could go buy the bulb and they insisted on me waiting and they would rather me drive safely then spend 20 minutes going to find a bulb.  Can't ask for better customer service than that.  Thank you!

Tried this new Mexican restaurant today for lunch. There were only about a dozen tables of diners in the place so we didn't have to wait for a table. The service was good, but the food arrived lukewarm. We both had the "Texas Fajitas" and they were good and would have been excellent had they been hot. The tortillas were wrapped in foil & were cold. When we checked out there were a couple of issues. I was billed for a drink that I didn't have and my friend had a problem using a gift certificate. It took an excessive amount of time & the man handling the register seemed irritated with us. I doubt I'll return with all of the other options available in our area.

Be sure to check their cancellation policy very carefully before giving them your credit card!  We had to cancel a reservation 10 days prior to arrival and were charged a $25.00 cancellation fee.  In the future, we will plan our stays at RV parks with more flexible customer service-oriented cancellation policies.

This place is a national treasure.

One of the great things about living on the West Siiiiiiiiiiiiiiide of Nashville is the proximity to the amazing Warner Parks (3000+ acres of trails, a nature center, picnic areas, and a flying monkey habitat), and the fantastic Cheekwood Botanical Gardens.  

Hidden away at the tail end of West Meade, Cheekwood is an expansive estate loaded with character and beauty.  You can roam the grounds and see some amazing sculpted gardens of all sorts of styles.  A fantastic place to take kids or for a romantic picnic during the day or evening (if they are having an evening event).  

They also have a great restaurant on-site, but it can sometimes help to  get reservations as they get overwhelmed quickly.  Last summer's Chihuly exhibit was amazing, especially at night.  All over the grounds there were beautiful glass sculptures that were lit up and placed tastefully into the gardens.  This summer it looks like the big draw is the "Trains!" exhibit, which consists of a display of around 8 'G' -scale trains set up with wood carvings of famous architecture from all over the state of Tennessee.  Great place to take kids, but even after you've seen that, you can spend hours exploring the grounds.  The main house is a cool place to check out as well, chock full of beautiful art in a beautiful manse that was built off the coffee trade.

They do a lot of special events here, such as "Highballs and Hydrangeas" (a cocktail social), concerts out on the grass, etc.  You just can't go wrong here.  I highly recommend becoming a member, it pays for itself pretty quickly.  This is also a great place to take guests visiting from out of town.

Came here Sunday morning.
Service was friendly. I ordered a cappuccino, which was... fine. Nothing special, and not very well made, but it was caffeine so I can't complain too much!

I used to go here years ago for sushi and the kimchi fries. Honestly this past time I will refuse to go ever again. The curry was sad, the sushi was so ugly and poorly made. There was no direction for how to order, when you scan on the table QR code it sends you to the mobile ordering website. Apparently you had to just look through the menu for what you want and then get someones attention to order what you want. 

The place forces you to use hand sanitizer when you come in then sits you so ridiculously close to the next tables all in on side of the small restaurant, separated by these hanging plastic screens that don't do anything for covid.

Yet the majority of the staff were not wearing masks and definitely not wearing a mask when getting so close to me especially the hispanic guy, and I'm pretty sure when I was paying he selected a higher tip FOR ME right before letting me sign on his mobile payment terminal, which was really ridiculous, but I didn't say anything. I'm just never going to come here again.  

Bathroom is disgusting. And honestly the covid precautions here are just hypocritical once you don't have the staff wearing masks, and the way the service and food is, it's just honestly really sad. I'm very disappointed I showed my friend here that day.

I really like End Of The Line.  I've always had good food and good service.  They have amazing outdoor spaces (the roof and the front walk in the summer) that are great for city viewing or people watching.  I always go in for the mac and cheese, which is baked in a little dish and served creamy.  So good!  I've tried several main dishes, and each one has been very good.  I love coming here for weekend brunch.  The breakfast burrito is amazing!

After my last visit (and review), I wanted to give A Sense of Serenity one more shot. I visited around 2pm on a weekday and the store was empty, leaving me ample time to browse. 

The shop, once again, had me feeling uneasy, and the gentleman behind the counter had an off-putting vibe about him, which didn't help.

They offer a wide selection of Angel and Tarot cards, many of the decks being at least $5 more than the same decks purchased at a similar store downtown. 

While I can't speak to their other book and  gift prices, I can speak confidently about their stone collection.

Since I frequent many shops for stones every few weeks, I am able to gauge whether certain crystals and gems are in line with pricing around town. I couldn't begin to fathom the exorbitantly high prices listed for the stones in ASoS. Most stones were AT LEAST $5 - $10 more than you could purchase them anywhere else and when I found pieces of optical calcite (comparable to a specimen I purchased for $4 at another store the previous week) labeled with  $80.00 tags, I made up my mind. I will NEVER shop at a store that so blatantly takes advantage of it's clientele. It's places like A Sense of Serenity that give new age/metaphysical shops a bad rap. 

I ask ANYONE looking for stones and crystals to go to ANY other shop in town. You will find friendlier, more knowledgable staff, pleasant vibes, and fair prices.
Ummm was brought to my job by a vendor. Ice cream had no taste and toppings were stale and not much selection.

We've eaten here occasionally since they opened in Bordentown.  They have an extensive draft been list.  Today I ordered from it and after a few minutes the waitress came back to tell me they had just run out of that.  I ordered something else and again she came back, saying they were having a problem figuring out which beers were which and couldn't guarantee that I'd get that.  So I tried a third choice and sure enough she came back, saying they shouldn't be using that list because they didn't have that one either.  She told me one draft they did have and I ordered it (Summer Sam Adams.)  We often get the house salad and the mussels with red sauce.  The sauce has changed and not for the better.  It was very heavy on oregano, very salty and very thin with a bitter taste.  The mussels were overcooked.  Overall, not a very good lunch.  We overheard the table behind us returning their bread and the waitress saying, "yes, I guess it is pretty dried up."  We won't be back for a few years.
Had the most upsetting experience with A. Meger today. She is the rudest individual I have ever dealt with in the public spectrum!!
Its ok. Burgers are huge and a good price don't get the fried mushrooms way to greasy

This place is the best deal in town, plus they do a great job!!! Took my daughters phone and he fixed it in minutes and it works perfectly. Today we went for another phone that my son had attempted to fix himself and it's harder than he expected , the guy saved the day! 
Definitely recommend them!

Never been to a Lenny's before. I honestly had no idea how big the subs were!! The large sub is literally a foot long (not like Subway's where it's not filled with much and just a skinny skimpy sub). Lenny's packs on the meat and whatever you want on it. 

I had the roast beef sub.... I've had better roast beef. meh.

Service was good- the guy who took my order was nice and so was the girl at the register.

Leave your stuff at the table when you're done- they clean it up for you! (that's what the sign on our table said!)

Yummy food
Went on a sat no line maybe because it
Was after lunch Went here around 3pm Oysters were so good Poboy is Amazing Soft Shell crab is amazeballs service was excellent
Food came fast. We had gumbo 
Chargrilled oysters and a soft shell crab poboy

Not sure what's going on over there most of the food is always pretty good. The fries have been mushy and brown lately. We actually called the managerThey said they have an issue with their oil. But two weeks later same issue. Their fries have gone downhill so much is hard to get excited about going there when there are so many other options
Their fries used to be amazing a while back until they change them. And now they're not even cooked properly.
We miss the old wide square  fries. 
Not sure if it is just this location

It's been almost nine years since I last wrote a review for Shere-E-Punjab and since I visit this restaurant twice a week, it's time for an update. My husband and I came here for their New Year's Eve buffet and it was SPECTACULAR! All of my favorite dishes were made: goat curry, lamb, chicken bhuna, vegetable korma, three kinds of tandoori, their marvelous basmati rice (better than any other Indian restaurant), okra, cauliflower, vegetable pakora, saag paneer, a mixture of different naans, salads, fruits, condiments, and a large assortment of desserts. Everything was so delicious (I went up twice and for my husband, it was three times). The lights were dimmed which made it more relaxing. There wasn't a large crowd and it was actually pretty quiet. They have some incredible chef in the kitchen. The servers were very attentive by coming by to pour more water in our glasses and to take the finished plates away. More people should come here to savor their wonderful food. They have a lunch buffet every day including the weekends. The food is replenished promptly and for sure, you will not walk out hungry. During the week, Diptesh keeps the place running quite well. He is attentive, respectful, and will answer any question you have about the food, especially all of the ingredients. Diptesh did a great job on New Year's Eve, too, along with the two other servers. So, if you have an urge to eat Indian food that is of high quality, stop in and enjoy yourself!

Salem was the worst! He did nothing to fix my computer and threatened to call the cops when I called him out on it back in 2013.

DO NOT COME HERE! Save your money and go someplace better. NEVER HERE!

Whatcha wanna be Five Bistro?

I live in Los Angeles. We research areas before traveling checking out restaurants. I love the hill and St Louis restaurants, but this is not a 5 star restaurant.

Here's my short take on the night:

We had a 7:30 reservation which was confirmed a few hours before.  I love getting a confirmation as it shows resepect for the guests. We (4 of us ) are seated at the first table in the middle of the first room.  So here's how the dialog went:

Me - I'd like a different table I don't want to be in the middle of the room.

Crabby Pants Seating Man - (who I later found out was the chef's Dad) -
You don't want to sit here.???..silence...Well go look in there then (bar area) and points to the left. Which we did ( by ourselves) and picked our own nice table between the bar and the windows.

The bar area room is so much nicer than the room you enter.  It's almost like two different restaurants with the wall taken out.  It looks very unsophisticated. 

Water was brought to the table immediately along with the wine list and menus. We noticed that there was bread on other tables and decided to wait to see how long it would be before we were served bread.  We finally had to ask for it. 

The waitress came over asking if there were any questions about the menu. She started telling us about EVERYTHING in detail on the menu until our heads were spinning.  My sis had to tell her to stop so we could disgest the info.  The menu isn't that big, but I guess they feel a need to tell you in detail every item on the menu. 

Five Bistro puts a big push on local farm food and grass fed beef.  It always annoys me when the staff thinks nobody has ever eaten grass fed meat or local farm food. Surprise..we don't need a big explanation.

The wine list is exceptional. We were impressed being from California.   There are lots of varietal choices that go well with the menu.  We chose a nice Villa Creek (we are familiar with the winery) and a Mendocino, CA syrah. Both delicious.

The pork and salmon entrees were very tasty. The pot roast (which two of us had) was okay.  The meat was dry and bland.  When I told the waitress she said well it's grass fed and it's lean and tastes different.  I wanted to say, I eat grass fed beef and I've never had dry cooked tasteless meat before.   She told us she would let the chef know.

 In other restaurants when you complain about the main dish the chef always comes out. We were just ignored. Especially, since I had taken only one bite of meat. I was really surprised.  I didn't eat it.  The squash/gnocchi hash under the meat was not tasty. It needed a carrot, potato and some seasoning.

We ordered the cheese plate letting the chef pick the cheeses.  Very tasty, but again we had to ask for bread to eat the cheeses. The dipping olive oil was very good. 

The jamoca ice cream desert and sweet potato torte were  good. The pear tart was burnt on the top and bottom and tasted burnt. The sorbet plate had weird consistentcy and wasn't very tasty. 

One last thing...what's with wooden chairs in restaurants lately.  Your behind gets numb and achy.  Can't we just have a comfortable chair for a 2.5 hour meal?

Overall, the restaurant needs to define itself.

To start off the place has a great atmosphere and location, along with that it seems that they have dedicated customers. I came here with two of my friends. When we walked in we weren't even greeted by the rude hostess. Upon sitting down we put in some appetizers to start off. The oysters casino were very good. The stone crab seemed to be inconsistent as some claws were really good, but others were dry and lacked flavor. The conch fritters themselves were good but the sauce they came with was berry syrup warmed up which was way too sweet. We also finally received our bread well after we were done with our appetizers. After we got done with the appetizers, we placed our order for dinner. The food took over 40 minutes to come. When our entrees finally came, the mahi was extremely dry and the two grouper dishes were just alright. All lacked flavor. The collard greens although were very good. All in all, the three of us were really not satisfied with both the food and service. Would not recommend!
Granted I got the cheap carwash ($7.) but I expected my vehicle to at least look like I washed it.  It had places all around it that didn't get touched, so was a WASTE OF MONEY .

Three dishes here are five stars, and the rest are just three. I'm a little surprised this was rated the #1 restaurant by Philly mag, but I will say I'd definitely come back for these three dishes only:

- the babganoush: creamy, perfectly balanced in smokiness and sweetness, sprinkled with pomegranates, divine on the hot pita bread, it lives up to the raves

- the chai latte: creamy, foamy, sprinkled with rose petals, totally divine, order this the moment you sit down 

- the cheese-rose water dessert: I forget the exact name but this blew me away with its crispy, toasted edges, cheesy and warm interior, and pistachio-topped crust, impeccably made

The rest of the dishes were disappointing. That Fatteh dish everyone raves about? Yeesh! Such a bizarre, crunchy concoction with a confusing consistency and too-sweet taste. 

The lamb dish with the cinnamon was way too spiced and off-putting, and the hummus was forgettable. 

Also wouldn't recommend the tahini hot chocolate. The best part is the cotton candy they top it with (delicious!), but once that's eaten, you're stuck with an expensive Nestle-tasting drink.

Just stick with those three dishes and you'll have a five-star meal.

The food here is very good and plentiful. The wings with the Asian sauce (can't remember the name) is amazing!! The drinks were full of syrup and had to be remade per managers request. But the overall experience was nice. Definitely would go back.

Breaux bridge Benedict & corn beef hash , exellent! With a mimosa and a Stanley screamer that will have you slurring the rest of the afternoon ! Food and drinks came out fast and it's continuously moving .

It always seems like it's a bother to order or to dine in. Today my coworkers and I went to have lunch and they had applied tip without telling us. The food is expensive and not very good. We just wanted to go somewhere that would be quick and close but I definitely wouldn't go again

I went twice: lunch with a friend and dinner with a group. Both were excellent. Food, drinks and service didn't disappoint. The dishes were unique and delicious. Some places put all their effort into serving aesthetically pleasing dishes but the taste is mediocre at best. Not here!

Just hope you get Chad as your server. 
Amazeballs meatloaf tower and plantains...what more could you want. Also, their deals are BOMB! 2 for 1 rum drinks?! HELLO MAI TAIS!

Pros:
1. The waitress was very friendly, we were seated quickly and our drinks were never empty. 
2. The menu and the specials were extensive, I believe there is something for everyone to be found here (unless you are vegetarian of course!)
3. Their white bread given as an appetizer was very good, it was served hot with a garlic/ Italian butter which was delicious. 

Cons: 
1. The restaurant looks nice from the outside and the decor is clean, however my silverware was incredibly dirty with old  food stuck to one of the forks. The bathroom was also very dirty, both toilets were covered in old used paper. 
2. My house salad looked at  least two days old. Most of the ice burg lettuce had turned brown and was inedible, I am not sure why they would have even served it to a customer. Lettuce is one of the cheapest produces available, so this was not a good start to my meal. 
3. I ordered their special 9 oz sirloin steak, and when it came out I swear there couldn't have been more than 4 oz of steak on my plate. Two days prior I had a 6 oz steak at Outback that looked twice as big as the one presented to me. They did however manage to cook it to a nice medium, which was okay for me even though I had asked for medium rare. I think it was the size of the steak that made impossible to get a nice evenness throughout. A large part of my extremely small steak was also comprised of gristle and fat, so in the end I didn't manage to eat much of it. 

Overall I was not impressed with my experience here, for the price I paid I will throw in a few more bucks and go to a different steakhouse.
This was the most flavorless and tasteless chicken I have ever eaten. I asked for original recipe. Perhaps that was my mistake.
Great place for drinks and food. I visit here when traveling for work and they have the best happy hour special. Food is good too and so is the service.

This place is a crappy overpriced tourist trap. The restaurant was empty and the hostess was rude. One of our party of 5 was running late but the hostess wouldn't seat us at a table for 5 until everyone was there. I can understand this policy if the restaurant was full but it was empty. The food took forever to get to our table and the quality was mediocre at best. Our total bill for 5 came to $146 without any alcohol. 

The worst part was the vile stench in the men's room. It was so bad that I couldn't even stay in there long enough to pee.  

This place is horrible, don't waste your time or your money.

Y'all, I gotta be honest. This is the tourist version of hot chicken. Don't get me wrong, it's pretty tasty and they have a variety of sides that are yummy too. However it is not the authentic, soul food that Nashville hot chicken was created to be. 

Pro-tip: if you want REAL hot chicken, try a spot in East Nashville.

The first time I saw BJ's BBQ I was driving down the street and I thought I saw a car fire up ahead due to all of the smoke.  Nope, it was the sweet smelling smoker of BJ's.  Unfortunately, they had run out of BBQ due to it being a football game day, so I had to plan a return visit.  This weekend, my son and I were in the area at lunchtime, so we stopped in a BJ's.  I had the two meat plate, beef and pork, and he had the special, which was a pork sandwich.  The meats were good, but I prefer brisket and pulled pork to be moister.  Instead, BJ's chops their meats and although my plate was flavorful, it was a bit on the dry side.  As for the side dishes, we both had the baked beans, which were good, and I had the cole slaw, which was ok.  Overall, BJ's is an authentic BBQ joint as can be attested to by the hard working smoker right there on the street, it's just not the style of BBQ that I prefer.

The new buzzfeed spot to crown the king of cheesesteaks. I've been going here for years and always enjoyed the quality of cheesesteak. The quality is still there, but it's just not worth going anymore. 

Go at any time and the small interior is packed to the fullest. Just recently I ordered 4 steaks over the phone so I did not have to wait in the crowded space. Upon entering they told me my order was coming right up and made me wait, in the packed space, for another 40 minutes. Upon confronting them about my forgotten order, they realized their mistake and offered to make the steaks for me right then. But, it would take another 30 minutes. No thanks. Terrible experience. 

Do yourself a huge favor and go across the street to Chubbies.

Great place to get hammered but I went there for oilers game and food and they had reserved signs on all the decent tables.  By reserved I mean I'm there at 7pm and the tables were reserved for 8:30... leaving tables unoccupied for hours is a great business strategy.  ;)

This store is dirty and gross. Employees are rude and I even saw water leaking from their ceiling onto their coffee machines for hours. Dirty!! Onto their drinks. 

AVOID. Go local!!! So many other cheaper and better local coffee places within the same block.

Pretty standard food and drink for the nicer Philly bars.  Above-average food and a great beer selection.  Where Interstate really stood out was the service and the prices.  Compared to similar bars around Fishtown/NoLibs and beyond the prices were pretty low.  Two people could easily have a couple drinks with an app and meals for under $30.  The service was good too.  Some of the hipsters that usually work in places like this are pretty apathetic and not great at their job.  That was not the case at all with Interstate.  Definitely will be going back.

I've never written a yelp review before but I'm so fed up with this place I had to. every time I've ever been to this store its the biggest waste of time. today was probably my fifth time going there, gave them roughly $300 worth of nice clothes and shoes that are current trends and in season, two hours later I get a text saying they "can't make an offer" and to respond and let them know if I want to donate them. I don't believe that for one second. Also, when I was there the girl couldn't even be bothered to look me in the eye and was very short with me. Every other time I've been there I've gotten $20 MAX for massive loads of nice clothes its ridiculous. This city needs a Buffalo Exchange desperately. Never wasting my energy with Plato's Closet ever again. Not sure if its just this specific location or not but either way I've had it with this company.

I keep going back in hopes I'll have a better experience than last...  my pedicures are never enjoyable, nails are always sloppy or uneven (unless done by fish). When I did my acrylics there they never had gems or rhinestones, always out of colors on their gel color wheel. They charged me extra to use razor when I purchased most expensive pedicure... I tip 20$ every time no matter what but this salon is mediocre at best. The staff are great. Very friendly..

Excellent, authentic Chinese food in suburban Pennsylvania!  I was here for lunch last week.  I loved the food.  I ordered the vegetarian mapo tofu lunch special plus an order of bok choy.  Both were delicious and generously portioned.  The mapo tofu was super spicy just how I like it.  I didn't even need to add any hot sauce.  The bok choy was fresh and perfectly cooked. I ate half of my meal and had enough leftover for a full and very enjoyable dinner.  The restaurant was clean and the service was friendly.  

I would have given 5 stars but I have a major pet peeve with restaurants that serve water out of plastic cups and hot soup out of plastic bowls with plastic spoons.  Once plastic items are washed in hot water or in a dishwasher, phthalates and chemicals such as BPA in the item leech out.  So if you drink water from a washed plastic cup or eat soup out of a plastic bowl, endocrine-disrupting (hormone altering) chemicals will leech into your food or drink - and you can ingest it.  Yuck.  If you don't believe me, google "BPA and washing plastic."  Hopefully this restaurant and others will stop serving food and drink in plastic cups and bowls!!!

A real experience... I'm glad we went. That being said, I won't bear that line another time. 

Pros:
- The experience! (Especially when they're filming. It's fun to see the show from the inside).
- The food! So good.... SUPER rich. You'll have trouble walking to your car.

Cons:
- The line. We waited hours.
- The service. They're grouchy and will shout at you. (I know, I know, it's part of the experience...)

Lack of food service. I was so excited to check out the soda floats made with Fitz's own sodas. My friend was in town from Chicago and we went here around 9pm only to be told that the kitchen was closed and only the bar was open. Considering there are few places offering handcrafted soda floats but many serving mediocre drinks on the Loop, you would think Fitz would give a better effort to please customers. If I want to go out for just a drink, there are dozens of better establishments within walking distance of Fitz.

The best person I interacted with was their internet / intern manager Daniel. Great guy. He was very professional, knowledgeable. I visited 5 Honda dealers on the day of my visit looking at used cars (great experience everywhere except here) and had a bad experience with the used car manager at this location. He was a bit abrasive overall, following me out of the place telling me how his price was final on the car and they would not take any money off their internet price and how their prices were better than anyone else around. 1st, I never tried to or intended to start a discussion about price on this visit, that was ALL HIM.  2nd, their prices were not all that great in my research and you really need to look out for the dealer fees / reconditioning fees on used cars in general. I ended up deciding to buy a new Honda, I got my 3 Truecar prices (Tampa Honda was not one of them) and even then called around some more and had 3 dealers beat the Truecar price. I decided since Tampa Honda was my local dealer, I'd give them a shot at my business. Even with all the great quotes I had from around town (I emailed them a few of them) , they came back with a price on a brand new 2018 Accord, Get this, at least over $2,000 higher than everyone else; they said that was the best they could do. I ended up buying a car from Kuhn Honda the next day. (Top notch dealer, look for my next review on Yelp about them).  So if they don't need a sale that bad, fine, but to stick to a quote $2000 higher than 6 other dealers in the Tampa area, that's crazy in my book. Coupled with the brush in with their used car manager, probably will never bother with them again.

I love Uniqlo and their understanding of my need for many pairs of affordable leggings that can pass as work pants. And free next day pants hemming is rad-and essential, since their non leggings are about a foot too long for my average heightness.

I purchased a Jeep Wrangler from them in mid-June and did a trade-in with the transaction. Come July, the payment was still due in our name on the trade in. It took a week of chasing to finally hear back from someone that the car was going to be paid off. It was still paid off past the due date, obviously no concern for the potential impact on your credit. Then the temp registration expired, and no registration cards in the mail. After calling several times to get someone, we found out they still didn't have the title and cards for us a month later.  And don't bother trying the finance department, they can't be bothered to return a phone call. I feel dirty that I bought a car from them. Seek cars elsewhere, you can do better price and people wise.
Average Haircut at a Salon that charges above average prices.  Next hair cut, I'll be looking for another Salon

This review will not matter since people are going to flood this place regardless.  Maybe an off night but the ribs are very dry with rub applied after smoking.   The atmosphere is nice and the sides were good.

Well unlike most Knights Inn these rooms were clean, hence the 2 stars. BUT THEY ARE CLEARLY RUNNING A PROSTITUTION RING OUT OF THE HOTEL. The man who's girl friend is the hooker hides by the pool when the pimp in the silver truck pulls up or the pimp won't pay for the girlfriends hotel. And the front desk is clearly in on it. Left the hotel because it was not very kid friendly with the rednecks drinking by the pool. And the husband swearing at his wife in the parking lot. Was the hotel cheap? Yes. But when I left i felt violated lol.

Used to be my "go to" breakfast place. New ownership, noticed that they altered the once superb pancake recipe. The Head Chef who was a family member was great. New chefs now. I couldn't eat the pancakes, haven't been back since, and I notice the once full parking lot on weekends is sparsely populated.

This place is terrible.  Absolutely terrible.  I only eat Chinese food about once a year, and it's always because my other half insists on it.  If I had it my way, I'd never eat the stuff.  Of all the places she's forced me to eat, this one was the worst.  The Bourbon chicken was super salty, and no flavor.  Unless salty is their flavor.  Who knows?  Never going back though.

I had 15 Who Dat flavored wings fried well done and they were absolutely delicious and the Wings were nice medium to big sized wings unlike Wing Snacks little a#% wings they pawn off! My wife and I were very pleased with this new establishment. We also had the Grilled Shrimp salad and that was pretty good too. The man who took my order was an Arabic guy and he was very friendly. He let me taste the sauce before ordering it. The food took a long time though like 25 minutes but it seemed like they were slammed with GRUB HUB and in store orders. 

I don't want to sound like the Terminator but I WILL BE BACK. Yes all the way from Belle Chasse I will be back!

Went for lunch. 
High ceilings, large layout space. Service was a little slow, but friendly.
Menu has the typical bar food, with some pizzazz. 
The desserts looked pretty awesome.
I ordered the "I Dare You" hot wings. Very spicy & delish. 
My mind wasn't blown, but I definitely liked this place. I will go back for the wings.

This place has a great atomsphere. Helps being right on the lake. I have eaten here a few times and have found a couple great choices. Bavarian Turkey sandwhich is great. Today I ordered some grilled wings and was told since they were grilled it would take longer so I also ordered chips and queso so I could snack on something. The server forgot about them so I had to ask for them about 30 minutes after ordering and once I got them you could tell they have been sitting out. Her attention to detail and customer services were below average.  Worst part is she only had a couple tables. I hope next time the service is better.

Been here twice now and if you are looking for a place that isn't very impressive, low quality food, a bit dirty and staff that's English is poor- this is your spot!  The buffet is $8.95, but let me warn you- the food sits out for long periods of time and because this place isn't very busy- the staff will not cook fresh buffet items. They will tell you to order off the menu (Beef dish $15-18, Chicken $10-16, etc). I decided to order off the menu and my boyfriend got the buffet. 

When ordering- staff had very hard time understanding me. I asked for carrots and they had no idea what it was until I showed them a picture online, and yet when my dish came out- it had brocolli, no carrots. I was fine with waiting for it to be corrected. My boyfriend had a little bit of everything on the buffet line- although it looked old, dried out and questionable, it tasted pretty good (Minus pulling out chunks of chewy fat here and there). When my dish came out, the chicken was steamed and presented 2 different colors in the meat. Not sure if this is safe to eat of not, but I ended up cuting and throwing away any pieces of chicken that looked questionable. 

The portion was average, sauce was very good. Server was very friendly- I felt bad that he was having difficulty (as well as all the staff there) with English. They seemed to all be working hard and overwhelmed at a small crowd. We had to ask 2x for water and we were given a gross lemony water in dirty glasses- so I stuck with drinking hot tea. 

Can't say I would go back....better Chinese in the area. Bathroom was discusting, parking was a little tight. Restaurant needs some repairs, but overall- food was a C, atmosphere B, service B, cleanliness D.

I will start out by saying I haven't used this practice in 3-4 years being as though I was obtaining a secondary bachelors degree at the time, and with a busy lifestyle had no time to visit the dr's office. After a turn of events at the ED, I decided to resume using this practice at the recommendation of the ED physician. I called and explained I would need a physical and a follow-up from my ED visit. The receptionist transferred me to a nurse named Linda who was extremely rude and not patient-oriented. I suppose she was ready to go home being as though I called closer to 5PM. She could not answer any of my questions and continuously stated ....call back tomorrow. She kept stating well you haven't been here since this date. I'm not sure what that has to do with me resuming my medical care at this practice? She continued to raise her tone and attitude with each question I asked. The sad part is I am an RN-BSN myself and would never treat a patient in such a manner. I cannot speak for every nurse at this practice, but she certainly left a horrible impression. I will be using a different physician whose office employs more caring, knowledgeable and compassionate health care staff.

I am not one to posted on social media every time I have a poor experience with a company or a service l...but this has really ticked me off!
Prior it Christmas I purchased a Walmart gift card at the Kirkwood Walmart for a gift to my parents. My Dad called me this weekend and told me that when he went to check out at his local Walmart the gift card was not activated. He had to explain that the card was a gift and even then the store told him at first they wanted to take the card from him. He eventually was able to keep the card and the receipt showing the error message that the card was not activated.
Since this, I have searched for my original receipt but have not been able to locate it. I called the Kirkwood Walmart and was told there was no way I could have obtained a store gift card without purchasing it. After explaining several times the entire story I was told that Walmart can not look up receipts with credit card numbers and they do not review video camera for items under $250.
Today I called the Walmart nationwide customer service line. I was transferred twice and eventually received an automated response stating the hold time was 32 mins. I then hung up and called the Kirkwood Walmart again. After being transferred 4 times and being on hold 10 mins I spoke with a manager and was advised that they could in fact look up sales receipts with a credit card number as long as you have the exact date and time of your visit and the exact dollar amount. The manager then stated I would need to speak to customer service and transferred me to that department.
Now after speaking with 2 managers and 3 sales reps I finally thought I would get to the bottom of this. While speaking to the customer service department at the Kirkwood Walmart I heard a voice in the background say $50 was not worth the time and effort that it takes to look up receipts and then I was hung up on!!

I had a cheese omelet here for breakfast a few weeks ago.  Well cooked egg with kinda cheapish cheese.  Home fries were very good toast was fine, coffee was weak  but I like strong coffee...

Service was fine, price was just under 10 bucks and a 2 buck tip.

Nice homey place, not outstanding food but it's fine.

This place was horrible. First they bring out our drinks and the glasses were filthy. Then for drinks they charge you $1.75 for a 12oz bottle drink and if you want a refill it's another 1.75 because they don't have fountain drinks.The salad was like cold slaw and tasted horrible and the sushi was fair. And to top it off they do not offer any type of menu for a child. This place needs to step it up.

Terrible terrible customer service! I will never shop here again. I just moved to the area from the west coast and decided to stop in to the Fresh Market to see what it was like and buy a few items on my way home. I stopped at the cheese department and noticed they had pre-packaged cheese but in serving sizes that were larger than I preferred. I stood at the counter waiting and waiting and waiting while the girl behind the counter pretended I did not exist. That's a really quick way to turn me away from a business -make me feel invisible. Finally I asked the girl if her department was open and she said yes just a minute let me finish cleaning this machine. Really? Then when I placed my order she said "well, isn't that a grab and go item? ".  I explained that the grab and go items were larger than I preferred to purchase and I would like to purchase half that amount. I told her if she would like she could open one of those "grab and go" packages and give me half of it. She was quite happy knowing that she wouldn't have to exert herself. Then she placed a small piece of paper on a filthy scale to weigh the cheese- I'm not exaggerating-the scale was covered with old dried up food bits of all sorts - it was disgusting. I asked her to just give me the other half of the order that she didn't place on the scale. I could tell she was not very happy and she hastily gave me my order. I said thank you and she did not reply. I had such high hopes. Next.

Ok so Tony Luke's is pretty legendary no doubt. But this location needs to get its act together before it tarnishes the legendary brand. 
Very simple order, cheesesteak American light fried O and hot pepper side with a bottle water. Pretty easy,  right?  Nope; along with my order were fries I didn't order but paid for and a kinda sorry ass cheesesteak. What happened to the cheesesteak?  Well the light FO was located on a very small portion of the sandwich and it was the "knots" of the onion.  Chunks of onion that no one in there right mind would want on their sandwich, but here they were on mine. So...I had to remove the crap onions and try and spread out what remained with my fingers as I did not grab utensils as I thought I would not need them. The meat also was not leveled properly on the roll. C'mon man you gotta do better than this!  
So...to the folks actually making the sandwiches at this particular time and day...pay attention to what you are doing because you are ruining the legendary brand. Simply not up to standard.
Wonderful Jewish fare, open early too.

I visited 7th + Grove for curbside to go.  Got a beer while a waited but the food was ready before I finished my beer.  I order the Rock The Boat which is fried catfish, gouda grits, and collards.  It was so so good.  I thought I had already had the best catfish possible but this might just be better.  Can't wait for restaurants reopen completely to enjoy this meal fresh out of the kitchen.  

I will definitely return to 7th + Grove in the future.

American Arborist came through again!!!  Early 2021 they came out and removed trees and scrubs which were either too close to electrical utilities or were over grown and not well planned from over 20 years.
Today Kody and Ethan returned once again professionally removing the remainder of a dead infected tree and bushes.
They introduced themselves, we walked around and discussed the work plan and once I approved everything they began the job, working fast until the job was done.  Afterwards everything was spotless and needless to say we are very pleased with the outcome.

Milly is amazing! My appointment was from 12:30 to 2:45 but, it only felt like a half hour. She genuinely cares about her customers, and ensures that she provides the most  excellent service. She is knowledgeable in what she's doing, she's customer oriented and I cannot say enough great things about her. I have no regrets and I am beyond grateful that I chose to see her. I will definitely be back!

At least they were open on New Years Day!  It was cold and miserable and its' surprising that so many places are closed on New Years Day with all the tourists in town.  So at least I'm thankful that Gumbo Shop is open. 

Service was fast and OK.

We had the Blackened Catfish and Crab Cake (special, not on menu) for appetizer and Catfish St Peter and BBQ Shrimp Pasta (both special) for entree.  The two fish dishes were both excellent while the BBQ Shrimp Pasta had a certain flavor from the spices that we did not enjoy.  The crab cake in both the appetizer and Catfish St Peter fell flat.  Although to be honest I have never had good crab cake, ever.  I think I just don't agree with the flavor and much rather just have crabs.  The pasta is also unacceptable as it seems it was over boiled and fell into tiny pieces.

3-stars for being open for business and the fish dishes.

Ultra modern design, food was meh and very overpriced.  The customer service was second to none. The staff here is awesome.

Although there was this ogre manager that kept eyeing me. I seriously think dude thought I was an employee goofing off.

I understand times are rough. Staff is low because of unemployment. And even then my cashier was incredibly nice. And I don't blame the cashier for this... but... once I got to the bottom of my popcorn the other night, there was mold on the bottom pieces. I've had this happen before when I worked at a restaurant where the bottom of the ice maker never got changed out and had mold... please make sure the uneaten popcorn gets cleaned out every night. I don't want anyone to get sick, and I'm glad I noticed it before I ate any of the moldy pieces. Again. I understand the staff is low due to COVID. Just wanted to send in a reminder.

I had the two taco meal which wasn't bad.  It came with two hard tacos, chips, guacamole, and a drink.  Plenty of chips to share with my daughter.  My daughter had the burrito special for Monday.  It's one of the better places to eat when looking for fast food.  The one in West Chester area is the nicest one we have been too.  But, this place was nice.

A truly awful experience! I would avoid renting from them. In Tampa for my daughter's weddin on 8/19-8/25. Waited 40 minutes to get picked up by airport shuttle. Got to the agency, where there were a number of angry folks at the rental counter. All of the customers had raised voices. I stood in line more than 30 minutes waiting for my turn. Mine was a classic bait-and switch experience. First there was the AGGRESSIVE up-sell. Then there were the FEAR tactics. Finally, I was told that even though I am a Perks member, and belong to a Payless Frequent Renter Program at another airport, I might -- MIGHT -- be able to get the car that I reserved if one were returned in the next hour or so.

I left without renting from them. In fact, a number of other customers left out of frustration, as well.

Shuttle driver hollered and swore at the passengers as we returned to airport to try and rent from other companies. Rented from National and it was great.

I contacted Payless, and I received one of those lame corporate messages, "We are always striving to improve. We have shared your concerns..." There was no mention of apology or how they would try to regain my business.

AVOID AVOID AVOID

I moved from Ohio and the Nissan dealership in Ohio always did a great job servicing my car. My dealership in Ohio was 1 hour away from where I stayed so every time I had to take my vehicle in for any service they offered me a loaner. Unfortunately, Ed Martin wasn't generous enough to offer me that service here. My work is 1 hour away and it's hard for me to take time off to get car serviced and a loaner would've been appreciative. Anyways, I took my car in for an oil change and the employees in the service area were extremely rude. As soon as I went to the desk to pay the first thing the girl said was to buy new tires. I asked to her explain more and she said "it's in front of you read the paper you need new tires...nope never going there again!
Not bad bbq. Wasn't the best I've ever had but it was good. Fried pickles were and corn fritters were really good. I'd go back.

Not really a bad spot for a lad to get a cut, in a pinch, but much safer if you're a lass. I'm one of those people who is a magnet for bad haircuts, so I'll just suggest some folk you should probably take a pass on if you find yourself to be a similar case: 

Donald- Nice enough fellow, but he just doesn't have the mastery that my misshapen head requires 

Salomz- Displayed much zeal, but that unfortunately left my cut lines crooked and unsymmetrical (in a very bad way), with more than one divot of a balding spot, as well as a quaff that was much shorter than I'd hoped for. After two separate self corrections (and the hair growth that happened in between) I finally felt confident enough to go outside hat-less. Also, he tried to trim my eyebrows without asking (only got one before I freaked; I thought he was just shielding my eyes as he cut the front)... no way that flies in my book 

Generally the ladies here hook it up, though I have been weed whacked by one or two of them in the past. Remember though, it's just a hair cuttery, the chain restaurant of the hair styling industry (but still worlds better than Great Clips)

I just tried to return some things that I had bought for various projects and either not used all of or didn't use. I was denied the return because I didn't have a receipt. I am very frustrated as I did a lot of business there over the years. It frustrates me because they simply give you a little piece of the receipt from the register and say I can't help you. I looked it up on line and it makes it seem like I am stealing things and then trying to return them. This is what happens when companies get so large that they loose touch with the little guy and don't give a crap about them any more.
Not a fan. Ordered 1/2 pulled pork 1/2 brisket sandwich, which is a menu item, and it was OK. It was nearly 30 minutes from time of order to table and only a few other patrons in restaurant

I have been going to Lucky Dill since it was a much smaller shop over on Alt 19. Maybe 15 or 20 years? Back then, there was never a bad meal. Now, not so much. My last three meals have either been awful or just plain disappointing. 

One of those meals was breakfast. I ordered the blueberry pancakes. They were not served as promised on the menu (citrus butter, fresh blueberries and blueberry compote). There was a blueberry syrup but the pancakes were inedible. Worse than cardboard. For the very first time in my life, I returned the food. The manager came over after about 10 minutes and offered to remove them from my check. Well, I am glad she 'generously' offered because there was no way in hell I was paying for that.

We recently went back for dinner. After two disappointing meals, I wanted to give them another chance since I've been coming here for YEARS. I ordered a steak sandwich. There was so much fat that I ate maybe 1/3 of it. 

Their only salvation is the free cheesecake. Best thing on the menu and the only reason they didn't get one star instead of two.

Sad but this place is now on my 'never again' list.

First time for me, very nice decor really well done. Food is very good and staff was knowledgeable and attentive, place was clean and vibe was nice. Had the chopped salad w tuna, cheesesteak egg rolls, edamame and the fries. Everything was very much on point, quality was above average and prices were reasonable.
Looking forward to my next visit and exploring the drink menu..

I'm a New York transplant to the Philadelphia area and I've had the privilege of working with some of the finest trainers and attending the best boutique exercise classes while living in NYC. Due to personal circumstances when first moving to Philly, several months went by before I began to think about about exercising again. At this point I felt like I had lost much of my muscle definition and, honestly, I wasn't feeling good about myself.  I set out on a mission to find high-quality classes that would inspire me. From experience, I knew what I wanted and have always had very high expectations. 
And then I found RIPPEDPHL! If you want a serious "kick ass" workout and want to see fast results, then this is your place. Treadmill and HIIT kind of says it all. How can you not get a good workout? 
I am constantly amazed at the different types of exercises that are given in the classes. They are by far the most innovative and creative exercises that I've ever seen. The instructors circulate through the class, reminding you to engage your core muscles, and correcting your form if you're not doing something properly. 
I also had the opportunity of taking some private one-on-one personal training sessions. Again, off the charts amazing fast results and again, some of the most innovative exercises that I've ever done.   
 Katie and Charbal, the owners, are the most down to earth, authentic, and enthusiastic people that you will ever meet. This place has a really great energy.

Our first thought for this brewery is it definitely needs a 2nd visit (thank goodness for the new Tampa Bay Ale Trail book) because when we were there, sadly they hardly had anything available we wanted to try due to the Brew Master being at the Craft Brewers Conference and they ran out of alot... Which is KINDA understandable but as a business, you need to ensure you have enough supplies while you are away.

2nd visit was not during the conference and we didn't have enough options to make a flight here that was actually their beer vs guest taps. We do not go to breweries to have someone else's beer. 

As we do participate in the TBAT, we have to go again each year, hopefully things start to improve with their beer selection as it is a huge hike over to Valrico from Palm Harbor to be continually disappointed.

I've been eating here for years, love the food. We got takeout yesterday with ubereats. The Huevos Rancheros (vegetarian, over medium eggs) were delicious and a real hit with everyone in the family! The Stuffed Challah French Toast was really good too. I would order it again. The Cannoli French Toast was kind of a let down.. the "cannoli cream" part just tasted like a super bland cream cheese with some chocolate chips in it, which was disappointing since that is what makes it Cannoli French Toast. If that was improved it would boost the dish a lot. Overall great takeout experience, would order takeout from here again for sure.

If you are an outdoor enthusiast be careful when walking into Cumberland Transit. You might think you have died and gone to heaven. They have it all, from high end bikes to camping gear. If you need something to make your outdoor adventure complete, this is where you need to go. 

The store reminds me of a more unique locally owned REI. The staff is extremely nice and always willing to help you make a decision if you cant decide on your own. Or if you just to talk to them about cool day trips that your planning, they always have great suggestions. 

The store also plans various different trips. From bike riding excursions and day hikes, to week long trips to Coast Rica, they really know what they are doing.  

I really wish that I had endless amounts of money, because it all would go to Cumberland Transit. You guys rock!
Small venue, best for takeout or a quick lunch.  Many options with create your own bowl. Little pricey for the small. Not very filling, but the large looked like a better option.

We will never do call-ahead seating again!  My husband and I called the Palm Harbor, Fl Carrabbas at 6:00 PM for a 6:30 PM call-ahead.  When we arrived at 6:30, we were told that it would be  25 minutes wait.  We told the young man at the front desk that we would be at the bar.  I checked several times, but after waiting an HOUR AND TEN MINUTES, our table was finally ready.  The young man said that "he couldn't find us."  We did notice that several tables needed to be bussed, so maybe that was part of the problem.  I don't think that we will be going to that particular Carrabbas again, and it wasn't even very busy.  We probably would have waited less time without call-ahead.  Never again.

They seated us together as close as possible to other patrons at the front of the restaurant where the waitress would not have to walk far I suppose. I thought maybe they might distance us from other folks...

Oh so NOT appetizing. Let's start with the "Salsa". It tasted like tomato purée. No spice, no flavor. I ordered the hot salsa, it was greener...Still watery with an unsavory heat flavor without being hot at all. I ordered the Beef Monster burrito, and beef nachos.

The burrito arrived like it had cheese on top at some point, but it melted into the bottom where it turned into a watery greasy mess. I opened it up, and greasy hamburger ran out. No spice, no cheese just a greasy, soggy mess.

The Nachos arrived with the worst presentation possible. Fried out super greasy hamburger on top of chips that used to have white cheese on them, but now resemble just hamburger, and not real good hamburger. Cheese was terrible. It melted into a greasy mess on the bottom of the plate.

Sorry folks, you guys need to taste your own dishes.

I asked for pickled jalapeños, and got what tasted like a pickled sweet pepper. Beans and rice were utterly a disappointment!!

I won't try this restaurant again, and I DO NOT recommend it to anyone.

I am not sure why this dairy queen only hires teen girls. kind of giving me a bad vibe. they did start selling the dairy free silly bar which is good, but usually the workers kinda give off racist vibes. overall 2/5. only a 2 because of the dairy free bars

I've never dealt with a business quite like this. The attitude that permeates this entire company is that you, the customer, are lucky they've decided to give you the time or day. If emails and phone calls go unanswered- oh well. If you have an appointment and Overhead is a no show - oh well. If Overhead is hours and hours late? Oh well, you are lucky they even considered working with you. This isn't the attitude I am going to deal with when thousands of my dollars are on the line.
Love this place! The live music is amazing. Too bad I wasn't here when Blake Shelton paid a visit. They do have a few vegan options, like the a impossible burger, salad, and a mushroom sandwich.

The staff was very nice. The food was average. Main problem, the food was not hot! I ordered from the breakfast menu. Eggs and cheese grits need to be piping hot. They were just warm. The butter was soft but didn't melt on the biscuit.
Really enjoyed happy hour. Specially the sweet potatoes fries and the mini rib burger. Oh and the sangria was the best, after four of them I guess I liked them!! It's fun, nice and clean. So go!
Went several months ago, and was super disappointed. Food was cold, staff didn't want us there as it was 20 mins til closing time.  Won't be back.

I came in to use the coupons that were mailed to me. It's a tight squeeze in the store. Some old lady even knocked something over and broke it by accident with her purse because it's so crammed. The lines were long but they had all 4 registers open and it's the holidays so I expected it. Multiple workers checked on me while I was shoppi

We have lived in the manor at Chestnut Hill Village Apartments for one month and are loving it.  The amenities are great and the maintenance staff has been very responsive and professional.  The location is outstanding. It is a two minute walk to the train station and less than ten minute walk to popular Germantown Avenue with multiple unique restaurants and shops. We highly recommend CHVA to anyone looking to stay within city limits but without the urban environment.

Sunday brunch amazing. Will be back. Must make reservations. Variety. Fresh. Healthy choices. Drinks included. Beautiful view. Crowded. Get there before 11am first come patio only. Service was ok, not good. Will go for Special occasions. Expen$ive.

Stay away. First not what is advertised.  Their data is 20 times slower than Verizon.  You don't get the normal Verizon speed but much slower throttled down speed.  Also they say it is monthly but it is based on 30 days so when you cancel they hit you for an extra month.  I thought it was Walmart but found out the company is in the Philippines.  Go with Verizon and stay away from Straight talk.  Verizon prepaid is the same price with better service. Would like to give them a ZERO!

I gave a rating of two because the collard greens and potato salad reminded me of my auntie.  The chicken however was greasy and fried really, did I mention really really hard with a lot of salt.  The concept is great, but the space is. It large enough to accommodate the wait.  As a person with handicapped needs, I literally had to stand 23 minutes for friend chicken legs - don't feel as bad as the woman in front of me who had already waited 30 minutes and was told it would take 7 minutes.  

I guess you live and learn.  Having read reviews and since I was staying next door in the hotel didn't want to walk too far tried this place.  I think I did make a mistake when I walked past the BBQ spot to find this place.  The mac and cheese was cheesy, but unseasoned if you could picture that. By the time they got the chicken out and I waked back next door to the Hilton the Mac and cheese was I iced cold telling me it had been sitting a while.  $19 later, a little disappointed.

Review is for food only, no music performance while we were there. Completely underwhelming entrees. Both the steak frites and the shrimp and grits lacked flavor. Steak was a puny thin cut, not worth the over $30 price. 5 small shrimp in the blah sauce. Service was ok, with a very friendly and helpful waitress at first who couldn't be found for 10 mins after we finished. Also out entrees were brought out while we were still eating our appetizer. The one  shining star of the meal was the poke appetizer, which is huge in size and comes with tons of veggies in a tasty sauce. If you end up here for a music night suggest sticking to apps and small plates.
worst coffee in town must have brewed same grain like 4 times (I've had it more than once) food was okay but id rather be at moons kitchen

This is an interesting score for me, I usually try and visit an establishment a few times to get a baseline reading so I know what to expect. Every single one has been so widely different than the others it's really hard for me to give a 100% accurate score. Im stuck between 3/4 and giving the benefit of doubt by listing as a 4. 

Overall the food quality has been swingy. The food at times is literally the best sandwich I've had in a long time. Other times the meat is dry and bland. I Personally order the cheesesteak so my opinion of food is limited to that one sandwich for now. Every time I order I hope that I get to experience the great tastes, but it's hit and miss.

The staff is mostly pleasant. I've witnessed a few interactions with other customers that I would have corrected if I was a manager on staff. There are ways to be compassionate and still express your point. 

The App is good for ordering but the single time I used it for delivery it took over an hour to get delivered and my sides were not included. The driver (who I believe is doordash) said something I couldn't comprehend about not having everything, but I was never notified prior from Jersey Mike's. Maybe they could have called me and asked for a substitute item rather than leaving me with something I didn't ask for a s still charging me.

I'd like to say I'll go here again, but I'd like to see some stability.

Please be careful with this contractor. I hired them to do my fence and they made me pay more then 60 percent up front then a week later asked for more
Money because they didn't have any. I recently learned that it is against the law for a contractor to ask for more than 50 percent of the job before they comp the work.  So once I gave them the money then I had to chase them over and over again to complete the job. I am now done chasing and will be taking them to court as they literally stole my money because I now have to pay someone else to finish what they took my money to do. This contractor is very unprofessional and dishonest. I would not recommend them at all.

Still the best cheesesteaks in Philly. It's been a year since we have been back and it's still great. The whiz is not that gloppy canned stuff and perfectly melds with the meat and onions. Get the whiz with your fries as an extra treat. The sodas are like an old fashioned soda jerk style and you do get one refill. Grab a great hand blended shake on the way out for the perfect ending. It's not that far from Center City, give it a shot

I checked out this establishment today for a late late lunch based on the reviews. In addition, they have additional options for vegetarians. 

The space is cozy with seating both outside and inside. The atmosphere is definitely eclectic. 

The service was good/average. However, I will note that they we were brought the wrong pizza. However, a few minutes later our server quickly realized the kitchen made a mistake. Our server was apologetic and quickly got another pizza made/served. 

However, unlike other yelpers loving the pizza. I did not find the flavors of the pizza tantalizing. The ingredients were fresh, but I felt though the pizza was average, nothing special.

Ate buffet here with friends on a weeknight and the food was fantastic! Really delicious standards like butter chicken and daal, but also great pumpkin curry and goat curry. Good service, nice place to sit and catch up with friends.

Umm... HELLO??? Does anyone know what they're eating...? How this place receives any good reviews for food or service is beyond me. Ca' Dario, you're doing EVERYTHING wrong.

The food was so bad that it became a joke among our table, and our snobby waitress didn't help our experience either. I'm going to use quotes from the night as my review so you get everyone's perspective. I was dining with three others, so let's call them T, K, and M.

M (eggplant parm): "This is awful... The eggplant is undercooked and the dish doesn't have any flavor. This is just awful."

T (salmon penne): "This tastes like airplane food."

K (ravioli): "That's a lot of butter... I don't think I want to eat this." (There was so much butter it might as well have been ravioli in butter soup. Raviolis swimming in butter with a dollop of tomato sauce in the middle -- the whole table had a good laugh when we saw it.)

B (ravioli): "This tastes like Trader Joe's ravioli that was burned in a frying pan."

We sent back the butter soup and ordered the ossobuco since we were afraid of the pastas. The ossobuco had a bone so small in it that I had to dig through the mountains of tomato sauce to find it. Come on, Ca' Dario, I order ossobuco and that's what you give me? To top it off, the risotto was so undercooked that I think it's safe for me to say that it was raw. No, it was not al dente -- I know the difference, and I'm sticking to the risotto being raw.

We received the bill along with some candy, and T tried one.

T (candy): "Even the candy is bad..."

That's it -- we were done. I literally have never had such a bad meal in my life, and I'm forever scarred from this experience. After this awful and unfulfilling dinner, I snuck over to In-N-Out which was much more fulfilling than whatever this pretend Italian joint's food is pretending to be.

Vary noisy!  Too close to the bar.  The food was okay but I would never go back.  The place was empty on a Sat. night.  Certainly not a date night  kind of place.  Found it rather confusing.  It looked great. White tablecloths and all.  It faces the big screen tv in the bar.  If there is a game going on, then that is the last place you want to be.  You should just go to the bar.  They might make it if they get out of that place.

I would love to give them an extra half a star. They only lack in service, but it might just because of my appearance. 
I've eaten here many times and every dish I have had has always been excellent. 
Dim sum, plenty of variety that include some of my favorites. Always fresh and hot.

I started writing this review prior to my girlfriend coming back to Philadelphia. Now that she is here and we frequent this establishment more than twice a week I have some added thoughts.

Treatment is much different when you are with an asian person. When I eat with my white buddy, they ignore us and give us the :"gweilo treatment". When I'm with my girlfriend they cater to almost every need and pay extra care to us. 

The food is great and I've come to enjoy more dishes than I would normally order under the "american chinese" type of foods. This is my new favorite spot in the NorthEast.

Northern Liberties hot spot - get there while you can still get a table!

I had dinner there Thursday with two friends.  We ordered and shared:
- Octopus
- Caesar salad (lightly grilled)
- Brussel sprouts in bacon jam
- Salmon
- Sirloin
- Monkfish

The food was amazing - the sauces and accompaniments brought out unique flavors that made each dish come alive. The place was hopping.  Get there while you can still get a table.  

Tony and his team have put together a delightful creation!
Great craft cocktails. Food was meh   Service was indifferent (except at the bar). Seems like place tried to scale back to deal with covid times probably pass on food

This was probably one of the most disgusting meals I have ever had in my existence. Service was garbage, food was garbage, all in all... nasty. Food was cold, "clam sauce " was a jelly chowder .... literally horrific.

I LOVE LOVE LOVE South Philly Community Acupuncture! From the knowledgeable , personable & warm staff, to the relaxing scents  -- the entire space is so Zen.

The best part (besides the budget-friendly pricing) is the way their acupuncture  treatments REALLY helped my pulled tendon after many VERY expensive physical therapy visits (which  only caused more pain).

I'm happy to be back in action! 
Thank you,
*´¨) 
¸.· ´¸.·*´¨) ¸.·*¨) 
(¸.·´ (¸.·'*~ Lee*

The brews were good as I had expected. The quesadilla with shrimp was good but over priced. It cost  $14 and only  had TWO small shrimp spread among the entire dish. They even included one of the tails. I also want to mention that indoor space  was really, really loud so we oped for the outside seating.
The food is very good.  Fun atmosphere but the music is too way to loud.  You have to yell at your table to have conversation.   Drinks are great.  A lot going on for a true date night.
They took such great care of my cat!  I got in quickly without an appointment and the visit was dirt cheap. I will definitely recommend to anyone in need of a new vet.
My cousin and I are here on our way out of NOLA and loved it! The poboys and gravy/cheese fries were delicious! Will definitely come back next time I'm in town!

If Escape the Rooms are meant to be hard, then Escape the Room Philly gets 5 stars automatically. If they are meant to be overly exciting then, meh. 

Keep in mind this is a review of my personal experience, but I found the clues too challenging to be able to enjoy myself. Thankfully some members of my group were better problem solvers so that the rest of us could stand around getting in the way. 

The room itself was pretty cool. We did "the Agency" and I felt like I was in a real room, versus some fake staging set up. I also appreciated the clues given by the employees monitoring our game. 

I'd probably never go back, but if this is your thing, you'll definitely get your money's worth of deep thought and frustration.

If you are turning in your lease vehicle and is not purchasing a new vehicle  make to tell them that you want to sell them your vehicle, so your able to get money back based off of the Blue book value and after they pay off the remaining cost of your lease. If you do not say you are selling your car to them they will take advantage of that and ground your vehicle and your basically giving them your car for free while they're able to resell your vehicle and make a big profit off it. Michael Smith and his staff is very conniving and they will take advantage of customers lack of knowledge. Michael told me it's their job to not mention anything so they're able to get your cat for as low as possible so they can make more profit.. so they are okay with scamming people out of money that would be owed to them

Don't come here expecting a host/hostess greeting you and showing you where to sit. Mediocre service is why this joint didn't get a full 5 stars. If your drink is half full and you don't think you need anything when the waiter stops by, ask for a refill. On the topic of food, however, they hit a home run. The burgers are amazing and like others have said, go for the build your own. The bacon on these burgers is the best I've had at any burger joint. The fries are also great but they are small and get cold quick.

Sunday night has limited beers (all out??) and limited menu. Fried and more fried were our choices. Music was OK but the cougar crowd affected the atmosphere. Too many good restaurants to try this one again.

PODS is a great idea when executed well.  Unfortunately, the Harahan, Louisiana location at 1301 Sams Avenue does not conduct their business well--very poorly in fact.  They did not pick up our rental unit at the agreed date/time.  Their stated excuse was they could not find the pick up location; however, they had no trouble delivering the unit to our location and charging our credit card every month.  Our street address was in the contract and is easily located via GPS.  UPS, FedEx and several freight companies find our location on a regular basis.  In most instances, an extra day wouldn't bother me, but in this instance I had to reschedule delivery of some construction materials for my home costing me time and money.  If you need to do business with PODS, don't use this location--or better yet, find an alternative.  You've been warned about their poor performance.
The service was very good, as was the coffee. The breakfast sandwich and home fries were extremely greasy. Didn't eat the potatoes at all. You could've lubed a car with the grease.
