The cruvinet is awesome. With 125 choices by the glass there is something for you to enjoy or explore.  Flights are put together well. 
I admit to being wary of eating there due to many poor reviews, however on one evening at dinner the plates looked good. I'll eat there next time.  
It may have helped me to order food on the last visit though.  We were met with tons of attitude when four of us came in and said that we were only there for a few glasses of wine - weren't even allowed into the bar until the "hostess" took her time in finding out if there was room.  Could we have stood at the bar, or taken a couple of high tops? Sure.  Was that offered? No.  But we did get plenty of that attitude.  
Hey Panorama, I've enjoyed many glasses of wine at your place, and even the crappy gnocchi that you used to serve.  Train your front of the house staff properly and it'll get you another star from me.

Thank you to Brandon Ford and Dave Scott for a perfect car purchase experience.  My previous car was totaled in a traffic accident and you guys stepped up big time.  Dave Scott was a great Sales Consultant and I highly recommend everyone visiting Dave and Brandon Ford.  I love my new Ford vehicle, thank you!!!!

Upstairs bar was cool. Good service. Good prices.

Downstairs looked so fancy we were afraid to attend in our jeans & t-shirt ensembles. After receiving our meals, we realized we were overdressed. Should have dressed like raccoons to match our garbage meals. Also drink prices surged. Just wanted a good piece of chicken. Not here. 2 stars are for upstairs bar & for warm bread.

This is fun dessert option in Chinatown, particularly if you're looking for something a little different. On my first visit I had a Crystal Sunrise mango bowl. that I really enjoyed. It was a good mix of textures balancing out the sweetness of the fruit. On another I tried the Peanut Butter Snowdae of shaved ice cream, which wasn't quite as good, probably because it's not in their wheelhouse of fruit flavors.

Staying at the Chase for the A list party. Decided to have a drink and app before.  Service was actually worse than an Applebee's . Ordered a bottle of Rose' and the waitress tried to open a bottle of Chianti.   Just the worst!

Wonderful dinner! High points- really delicious Eggplant parmigiana, very big portion- enough for 2.  Delicious garlic bread, great Caesar salad.. And the most amazing cheesecake ever.  Service was a little slow.. And unfortunately the pace of the service wasn't great.. But the food was very good.

If I could give this restaurant no stars I would. I ordered the crab legs and gizzards.. here's my horror story:

1. The gizzards were cooked in the oil with the other seafood and was not breaded/floured correctly; not good at all!

2. OMG. The crab legs... how can you get crab legs wrong? Keep reading.. the crab legs smelled like a dead body they had a severe disgusting and foul odor. I actually threw them out of the window because the smell was on my hands and I couldn't eat them.

I was visiting and I must say I will never..ever visit Tampa again. It's so hard to find seafood here.. go figure, and the seafood was extremely disgusting, never again.

Thanks, but no thanks.

Some of the better bread I've had from a sub shop! I got the Italian sub and it was average, not a whole lot of flavor. My husband got the chipotle steak and his was pretty darn good, so I have failed.

Generally this restaurant would get four stars but today's Ita hard to even give it three. The restaurant is totally understaffed but I guess their attitude is - "you've got no where else to go tonight as all the other restaurants in town are closed for Christmas so just be thankful you don't have to cook". The service is totally lacking tonight as we ordered edamame and it never came out. The food did not come out together which means one person got their food at a time. Suggestion - avoid this place on Christmas
Good BBQ! one of the only BBQ joints on state st. $2 for 2 sliders on tuesday are my favorite. oh and the mac and cheese balls! So glad to see this place is doing well!

I hate to be the negative review guy but wow what a waste if money! $24 to do what I could have done for $3 at the DIY spray wash with vacuum. They didn't clean the rearview mirror (never do), did not take up the floor mats and didnt even wipe down internal surfaces AS PROMISED. just a bit of light vacuuming, wiped the windows Nd sent through an auto wash. Avoid this place!! Total rip off.

Always make a trip here when I go visit my brother! I'm a huge donut fan and this place has never disappointed. Their apple fritter and boston cream donut is to die for! They have a huge assortment of bagels and breakfast items as well. Highly recommend to all donut lovers like me :)

The food here was pretty good. I tried a soup with scallops,shrimp,crab and a lot of other stuff in it. I can't remember what it's called but the flavor was delicious. I also ordered the Jambalaya and it was average. The Customer Service on the other hand was horrible. The waitress would walk around ignoring you when you need something. I would come back for the food but took off stars for the horrible customer service.

I ordered the chicken cheesesteak. it was bland and honestly, very overpriced for what it was--a slab of chicken with melted cheese. next time (if there is one) i will try their regular cheesesteaks, but DO NOT get the chicken or you will be disappointed.
I went to get my nipples pierced about 2 months ago and it went great. Josh was very nice and put me completely at ease. The whole process took about 30 minutes and I'm very happy with my piercings.

Went in for an issue that I had with an install from a different place. Should have went here from the start. They fixed my engine whine, gave me great speakers in the front and adjusted everything. My radio sounds great now! Anthony and the rest were great. I will definitely come here again for my next install.

I've been dragged here a couple times now, mostly because it is a refreshing change from all the Italian restaurants in the area. I've tried a couple different menu items but each one had been incredibly unimpressive.

We were driving from Baton Rouge to pick up my wife at the NOLA airport so we stopped at N&O for lunch. Unfortunately- the owners had decided to randomly close the restaurant during a time that they advertised to be open. In fact - their yelp entry STILL says "Open until 9 pm". 

They are not.

We will NOT go back.

I am so loving this place! Frothy Monkey serves fair trade and organic coffee, kombucha, juices, teas, and tasty sandwiches and baked goods from a local bakery. Throw in free wi-fi and you've got one sweet place to hang out in 12th South! The atmosphere in this converted house is very comfortable and mellow, with a mix of booth, table, and bar seating, plus an awesome outdoor patio and a glassed-in porch room. I think I want to get a job there just so I'm allowed to go up the spiral staircase.

Awesomeness in a box. That's what they sell. Seriously, their prices are very fair for a variety of things you may need. 

Their prices on pool chemicals are the best in the area.

I won't be coming back here. The vegetable lo mein is flavorless. I'll give them credit that their sesame chicken tastes good, but that's mainly due to the sugary sauce they put on it. 

When I asked the woman at the counter what vegetables were in the vegetable lo mein, she said "oh lots of different vegetables," which did not answer my question. 

I had a pretty low bar for the food I was trying to snag before work, but this lo mein is below that bar in terms of quality. It makes me sad to eat it.

Where I come from in Wisconsin, there are no places close by that sell french macarons, so when my family and I came to New Orleans we decided to check this place out. I ordered the 15pc set for around $30, which from other reviews, seems like the price went up? They were a nice size and tasted good. If I were to compare them to other macarons I've tried though, they are not the best. still pretty good though! 

The place is very cute inside and seems to have friendly staff working there. They guy my mom and I were ordering from though seemed annoyed and wasn't very nice, but we just dealt with it. 

We didn't order anything else from there, but they do have other tasty looking treats available!

Food was OK....very plain, no seasonings. Not bad but not great.
 Customer Service is great and delivery is on time but could do better with flavor but thats preference

Went in an evening for couple dozen oysters and clam chowder... GREAT food as we have always have!! 
(Yes, this 'fresh' seafood is a little pricey, but look around folks, unless you are seeing a mirage of the ocean, I believe we are in landlocked, heart of the midwest, Indiana! SO the cost of shipping that 'fresh' meal in has to add up somewhere!)

Service was a bit better than our past couple experiences. A manager (I suppose) chatted with us (my young daughter) most every time she walked through - calling her by name. And had a very attentive bus boy keeping my table clean and water filled (he was also quite sweet and silly with our daughter). And our server checked in enough that I didn't feel abandoned. Hope this service improvement continues. Thank you!
First time in Philly and ended a business meeting early. Ran into PHS and a fantastic setting.

This place's food was awful. The burrito was cold (the cheese inside wasn't even melted) and the margarita tasted like there was soap in it. Strange, I know, but it was really gross.

And the waiter was a little rude (I know everyone has bad days, so this is not the primary reason for leaving a bad review.)

The one star is for attitude, and as they say, "Attitude is everything."

I made a reservation a few days in advance for Saturday at 9:30pm for my first anniversary dinner with my beautiful wife. We are early birds so a 9:30 dinner is tough for us, but I had heard great things about the restaurant and thought it would be a great place to treat my wife during our visit to New Orleans.

When I made the reservation I ask the gal if there was any chance we could move it to earlier. She said I should call Saturday morning to see if there are any cancellations and that they would gladly move us earlier. 

I called Saturday morning and the reservation gal suggested I call around 3:30pm after re-confirmation calls for reservations have gone out. 

When I called back at 3:30 I got a rude reservation guy who said they won't be moving anyone around, EVEN THOUGH HE CONFIRMED THERE IN FACT WERE CANCELLATIONS. When I asked him to try his best to help me he pretty much snapped at me and was short with me. I politely thanked him and said my goodbyes. 

I then called back and hour later and calmly and politely explained to him that this was my anniversary dinner and since according to him there were some cancellations if he could please ask the manager if he could make an exception and allow me and my wife to take someone else's cancelled reservation time. 

It was like I was talking to a brick wall, or as if I was stupid for not understanding their rule that they just had made up that day that they won't refill a cancellation from someone who already has a reservation when every other person I had talked to prior to that said it wouldn't be a problem. 

The guy could have lied to me and told me no one cancelled. The guy could have put me on hold and pretended to talk to the manager to try to convince him to give me a better time. Or the guy could have sincerely tried to help me out on my wedding anniversary. 

Nope. Instead he decided to act like a [pick your word]. Good thing this place is not the only restaurant in town....

Do not use this DR. He prescribes addictive medicines then cannot get the prescriptions right and the prescriptions run out leaving a patient in withdrawal. They do not respond to urgent voicemails, ignore faxes and calls from the pharmacy, and this happens every cycle. He requires a visit every 60 days, but they schedule past the 60 days. I believe this practice of ignoring patients who are having to stop taking medicines that warnings say DO NOT suddenly stop taking medicine is malpractice and a call to our lawyer is next after writing reviews to let people know to never ever use this DR. He is bad news, period. Or his office staff, but the buck stops at the top, and hard to blame a horrible team if the DR does not care. The past few years with Dr. Ashtok Patel has been a repeating nightmare from day one.
We ordered  boneless BBQ wings , salmon and fried chicken. I ordered the salmon medium and it was dry. Same with the chicken wings. Flatbread was okay. Not very good. Service was  excellent.
Awesome staff!  Awesome massage therapists!  They run on time and you get a full 60 minutes of massage rather than the 50 minutes that other studios do to you.

This hotel is not new and definitely could stand an update / overhaul etc.  However here's the pros & cons. 

Pros:  
*Close to bishops gate business district. Super convenient. 
*Has a great hot breakfast selection & super happy hours.  
*Very friendly staff at the front desk at all times.  Primo customer service. 
*wifi is strong.  TVs / cable offerings are above average. 

Cons:
*cleanliness is not their strong suit.  *Hallways have strong gross odors.  *Carpets need cleanings.  
*Bathrooms are overlooked when it comes to detail cleaning 
*Beds are half ass made - even worse if you stay over 3-5 nights. 
*elevators need cleaning 

Ultimately I stay here bc it's close and it's cheap. But you do get what you pay for.  That's for certain.  This time my fun discovery was this gross thing on the bed skirt.  Feces? Mud? Not sure and I'm not finding out.  Left a note for cleaning crew to replace, totally ignored.

I'm looking for some real island food and this is more of a quick bite. Teriyaki noodles are so bland. Just plain noodles mixed with teriyaki sauce. Hardly any taste to it. Teriyaki chicken was ok except that on the photo the chicken meat looks like a thigh (which is normally used in real teriyaki chicken).. but they served sliced chicken breast instead. I say if you're looking for something close to Hawaiian food to go to CK Hawaiian BBQ.  

I will give this place a second shot and try something different.

Dr. Speyer is awesome! I went in for severe allergy's and horrid nose congestion. He very clearly explained everything and quickly got me setup for a procedure that immediately improved my comfort of living. 

And the allergy department is the sweetest group of ladies, and I actually look forward to seeing them every week for my allergy shot, which has helped tremendously with my day to day allergy symptoms.

We stumbled upon this place right after arriving in NOLA. It was a little confusing to find as we didn't realize it was inside a different building in a food court setting, so pay attention to the address number. We were both happy with our food and it was quite tasty. Great way to kick off the trip! Also, my boyfriend has celiac and didn't have any issues.
This is a chain and the food is of that type of quality.   I would go again and have been here a few times but I don't think it compares to some of the full service restaurants like dinosaur bbq.

I was very disappointed to find out there is a $40 additional fee to have lenses made here if you do not buy their frames! They are going to get their money out of you one way or the other I suppose. Even if you want to use your existing frames like I did. 
I guess the profit on the up-selling of the lenses alone are not enough. They have to penalize you for not buying a frame from them. I have bought 5 prior pairs of glasses for myself and my child including very expensive custom made prescription sunglasses here. 
This "nickel and dime" treatment lost them two customers and we will be spreading the word. 
Why MUST I buy new frames from you if my own very expensive frames are in great shape and I don't need new ones? So charge people a fee? That's so low.
I loved it. Steak fajitas were delicious. We got there close to closing and service was great. Looking forward to my next time there.

Great location just off Bourbon street across from the Dauphine Orleans Hotel.  24 hour service.  We had gator balls, the shrimp po-boy and a pulled pork po-boy, red beans and rice with spicy sausage and the sweet potato andouille sausage bisque.  Hands down the favorite was the red beans & rice with spicy sausage.  We also had bloody mary's, which were really good.  Yes, the staff was a bit surly and it is a dive, but if you go in knowing to expect that, you probably won't be disappointed in your experience there.

I was really excited to try LaScala after hearing about them and seeing all the amazing pictures on Instagram! The inside is absolutely gorgeous with light colors and black and white vintage photographs. The dining room overlooks the water and there is a nice outdoor space. 

The menu is not huge which is nice. They have a great selection of different pizzas, appetizers, and pasta dishes. The ricotta appetizer with honey was interesting and tasty! Extra bread was definitely needed for the portion size. The muscles were very delicious and served with herbed bread. 

The mac & cheese pizza was a little bit dry and under seasoned. I asked for salt and pepper but would've loved pepper flakes or even some old Bay. The burrata  pizza was great! There were generous portions of burrata and the arugula really took it to the next level!

Overall a great experience! Happy that they have a location in Philly as well!

I love this little place. I lived in Sierra Vista and would drive all the way to Tuscon just to visit. My husband and I loved the hookah and we especially enjoyed their house flavors (Ice Flower being my personal favorite). They don't offer fruit bowls or the ice hoses but honestly the shisha options they have more than make up for that. The staff was very attentive even when it was busy and would frequently check in and bring fresh coals. It's also very affordable. 
The bar had great drinks and the whole atmosphere was just overall pleasant. On weekends they have themed parties, karaoke, or a DJ so it's very lively. During the day the outside patio is also a nice place to relax and smoke with a coffee. 
We just moved to North Carolina and we have yet to find another hookah spot that even holds a candle to this cafe. We miss this place a lot so if you have the opportunity to go I highly suggest it!

When I flew into Philadelphia and found out that my plane was coming into terminal F I was excited because I had been wanting to try the airport version of my favorite cheesesteak place: Tony Luke's! 

I hurried down and found it in the main food court area next to Chipotle. There were already a few people in line, but it took no time for me to reach the register and place my order. (Traditional cheesesteak with whiz and onions.) I opted for the 9 inch and would have gotten bigger if they had it available. The wait for a cheesesteak was approximately 8 minutes which I was told at the register. No problem.

I sat down and opened my cheesesteak and the steam and delicious smell poured out of the cheese paper! I was a bit disappointed to find my roll had been charred on top, but I dove in. The sandwich was almost as delicious as I remember from downtown. The steak was well seasoned and wonderful! The whiz and onions were on point! I give the Luke's downtown a 5/5 and for now this one is a 4/5. The guy next to me did not have a charred roll so I'm sure it was an accident. I'll try again in a few days!

Positive: This used to be Vesuvio, but they had to change the vibe after complaints from neighbors (used to be sporty/clubby ... now they're gonna start hosting jazz nights ... which is all good).  Owner is a cool cat.  Hummus was fairly generous portion (though not cheap).

Negative: On a single visit they intentional mislead my party into ordering something other than what we intended at a higher price TWICE (though to our credit we caught it both times).  

Example: 
Q: the beer and shot special, does that mean $6 for a beer AND shot?  any beer? 
A: Yes
Q: I'd like a xxx beer and the shot
A: That will be $10

Q: Could I order the hummus
A: Sure no problem ... OK hummus and bruschetta coming right up
Q: I know that some toast comes with the humuus, but you're talking about a whole seperate bruschetta order?
A: Yes

First I would like to say only consider this club if you  LOVE techno/electronic. Secondly this space had so much potential  but you have to pay money just to sit down? I mean have a few VIP spaces but not a single chair in sight unless you want to pay $300?  And Last but not least the DJ kept "sampling" according to my husband  I call it switching songs every 30 secs which was really bad if you were trying to dance.

We had our Rehearsal Dinner catered at Houston Station. B and C set up a beautiful spread for 110 folks. The servers and their table decor were lovely. The food was hot and delicious. Guests will be talking about the Beef Brisket for months! We had a cupcake bar that was a big hit! Sara was extremely professional and confided with us for everything! We were extremely happy!

So, I try to never give lower than a 3 star review but my experience at Ava, was barely a 2 star. The decor is great, modern but still has a homey feel. Servers wear plaid, each one different, which didn't really feel like it meshed with it being an Italian spot. Atmosphere was ok, but not very lively. The food was pretty good and the pasta is definitely fresh, the server didn't write anything down and of course she forgot to ring in my steak. I had my son with me, I was with 7 family members, they have no kids menu, they had to run to 717 to get me a booster seat (which I was very grateful for) and they have 0 changing tables... 0. I had to put paper towels on the floor to change my son. If I had driven, I would have left. I get not being "Applebee's" but at minimum a changing table, in case a young married couple with a kid come in would be nice. Cool decor, mediocre food/service, not only not kid friendly but blatant disregard for young children and families with young children.

I walked in after a long flight from Cali.  They were soon to close in an hour but did not appear rushed to take my order and chat it up over IND vs NY, who was dominating.  Only one real concern, the wasabi sauce was sweet and I expect with the description that I would get a mild punch in the back of my head.  The standard test for me is can you make a Spicy Scallop Hand Roll, it was spicy, fresh and met the challenge.  I plan to return on another of my infrequent visits to the Hoosier land !

My first medical supply company suggested I use them because they wouldn't' be carrying as many varieties of C-Pap supplies in the future. You want to know why our insurance is so high. This new company billed my insurance company almost 10 times what my C-Pap supplies would  cost if I bought them myself from Amazon. My insurance company paid 1/10 of the cost and then I was billed about the same amount for the same supplies. So these supplies were paid for twice. I'll buy the supplies and send the receipts into my insurance company and they can refund me 50%. still would cost both of us less. My insurance company paid almost 3 times what the C-Pap machine could be purchased for. Watch these medical supply companies they will rip you off every time.

Sorry, not a fan.  We reached out to the owner first, but got no response.
The chicken was so oily and under seasoned.  I watched kitchen staff eat on the line and sneeze on food. (The cook wore a LA Dodgers hat if anyone cares)
We ordered the exact same meals and received two different orders.  I received two thighs and a leg but my wife got two legs and a thigh.That is a big difference in protein.
The mashed potatoes were stone cold.  We saw that an adjacent table received rolls and butter but it was not offered to us. Apparently it was factored into the price of the meal but not offered to all.  The collard greens  had no flavor.  The waitress told us that they quit offering bread because people didn't want it.  The amount of gravy was presented in a paper cup the size of a fast food ketchup portion.   For the price of our half eaten meals, we could have enjoyed much more elsewhere.  Our dinners were disgusting..bland, unseasoned and just a waste of money.  So disgusted that we spent hard earned money on this:

This could potentially have been my favorite place. The location is great. Right in the middle of the action. We had so much fun watching the party buses fly by and the people watching. 

The band was amazing. We loved singing along. The waitress was so much fun and friendly. We loved her!! 

I enjoyed the specialty drinks but the food was the worst of my trip. I ordered the crawfish po-boy. Way too much bread. 95% bread, 5% crawfish. I had to open it up to make sure they even remembered to put the crawfish in it. So disappointing!

I'm all about the hot sauce at Buffalo Wild Wings because I can actually get the high level of heat that I desire.  The food itself is never anything special, but it's tasty fried junk food that occasionally I want to indulge in. I've visited this particular location on three occasions and unfortunately, all of them were a bit of a train wreck.

The first time was during the grand opening week.  My husband and I ordered the chicken tenders and fries.  After an hour we still didn't have any food.  Finally a manager comes out and explains that there was a disaster in the kitchen of epic proportions, and all the food was ruined.  Therefore, they would have to remake everyone's orders, and it would take a while.  We'd already waited an hour, so we decided to wait.  It took another half hour.  The food was warm, but not piping hot when it finally arrived.  We were given a coupon for a few free wings on a return visit.  My day was shot though because the places I needed to shop at in the area were closing soon by the time we go out of there.

The second time they just took forever again, but the food was okay.  I decided to give them a third chance the other day after a lot of time had passed thinking that the big disaster was during opening week, so they were probably still working the kinks out.  The third time when we arrived we were asked if we wanted to sit at the high top tables or the low regular ones.  I wanted the low ones because it's more comfortable to have a seat with a back.  The server walks us over to the high tops and tries to seat us there.  Then seeing the confused look on our faces, she asks where we wanted to sit again and moved us.  No biggie.  

Then we ordered the same thing as last time.  I had the hot sauce with mine and salt and vinegar on the fries.  The food took a reasonable amount of time.  When it came, they didn't bring any sauce and just had a big thing of the salt and vinegar powder on the side.  We told the server who soon brought out our sauces.  Then I start eating and turn over a chicken strip to notice it's all weird and wrinkly.  I look at the inside and sure enough, it is still raw inside.  Yuck!  I send it back, and soon I got a new cooked one. My husband's breading on one of his strips was undercooked as well on one side, but not totally raw.  I feel like this is unacceptable considering all they have to do is throw frozen chicken strips in the fryalator.  I guess they were throwing them in in one big frozen chunk and not separating them, so they don't get done on all sides.  This is just sad.

The servers were nice and tried to rectify the situation, and it's not their fault the kitchen is a wreck, so I'm giving two stars.  If you have a hankering for BDubs, I'd recommend going to another location. This one is not worth the trouble.  Even though it's a bit of a haul, I recommend the location on Limestone Road in Wilmington, Delaware.

There was some misunderstand about the bill initially but Eddie the manager was very helpful in getting the matter resolved. Most clubs wouldn't care about the issue I had with the bill but Eddie was very professional and understanding and made things right. He really cares about the club but more importantly about the guests.

University City has changed a lot over the past decade.  With both Penn and Drexel doing a lot to upgrade the surrounding area, I am glad to say that Penn has built a set of buildings on Walnut which include this watering hole.  

Tall ceilings, tons of beer choices (on draft) and a rooftop bar area all make this a great spot to have a beverage.  Although I tried a beer that was a bit too sour for me, it was still a good experience.  I've been here a few times and the food has been good.  They have fire pits so the outdoor area is still a good spot in the winter.  

Important to keep in mind though, this place is not priced like a college bar - so expect the crowd to be late-college to young-adult.

My boyfriend have been here several times before and the service was usually decent. We decided to visit again for Halloween with our friend, where we ordered several drinks, and my boyfriend started a tab under my name. When it was time to collect my card, a bartender donning a zipper mask asked my boyfriend extremely rudely what the card name was under, then yelled at us, then overcharged us my drink (it was 7 but she charged us 10). Obviously, she didn't get a tip, and we were both disgusted that we were abused by someone whose job is to give us respectable service.

Ok I know I going to be in for bad times when I ordered the bun (bOOn) with the vietnamese pronunciation and the lady taking the order paused and then said oh you want the bun (BUHN) as if she was talking about the bread that you put a hot dog into. It was bar none the worst bun I have ever had burnt tofu under cooked noodles and a sauce that should have been thrown away.  My special lady friend ordered Laksa, I knew she was in for a horrific time when the lady said oh you want LASka. Putting S&B curry powder into a shameful Pho  and calling it laksa is like pulling out the inside of a mcrib and calling them spare ribs. I also like how the soup came with a side of soup seriously I dont know.

The very guero friendly american chinese dishes are well executed 

I cant believe I am saying this but,
If they dont have it on a panda express menu dont order it here. Stick to things like cashew chicken, orange chicken, and beef and broccoli. Or you will be sad, I know I was.
 
Or better yet come with me to OLD PEKING the only place in town for chinese food. or the Chinese BBQ guy in the front of lee lee oriental market.

I've had local taco before via uber eats and always enjoyed it. However, going there in person was another story. Waited 4 minutes to be sat, even though multiple employees walked by, and then sat for 18 minutes without being asked for drinks or anything. This was after a server came up, asked to wipe our table off, then said he'd be back. He proceeded to clean a large table off while another server waited 2 tables ON EITHER SIDE OF US!!! We ended up walking out.

Bravo, Furniture Liquidators!  You have made a glorious viral debut with your instant commercial classic,  "Lamps Everywhere." I see you're only open Friday & Saturday to the public; therefore, I'll be twerking my rump your direction soon to check the merchandise out. BONUS - a free armour with purchase?! CH-CHING! 

This review if for the commercial only, which has so many colorful elements to behold, I can only think of one term to sum it up: sursplendent!

This was my first time at a Smashburger, and it was just ok. You walk in and place your order and they give you a number and they will bring your food out to you. The place was very clean and well kept. 

My reason for the 3 stars is that my burger was super greasy ( hubby said his wasn't). I did like you could create your own burger with your choice of cheese snd toppings, however premium toppings like bacon or avocado were extra. They did have a decent variety of fries and sides to accompany your burger. I ordered the garlic smashfries & they were delicious.  

I personally thought they were a little expensive for what you were getting. I really was not impressed and doubt I will be back.

I have driven past this place a million times and never knew what it was. Maybe a place that's really into hot pockets? Pita pockets? Ahhhh.... Pool table pockets. It's all coming clear. The outside doesn't really give you a hint of what could be inside, but maybe that's just my opinion... Or ignorance?

I had never been inside a pool bar, so I didn't know what to expect, but I was very pleasantly surprised. Everything was clean, drinks were cold and cheap, and service was quick. At 3 in the afternoon pool cost $8 a table per hour and I think at night it's $10. Yet again, nothing to compare that's price to other than the pool tables that takes a dollar worth of quarters per game. I'm no pool expert, but I know that they were very high quality pool tables and it was a nice place in general, so I think it was worth it. I was surprised how busy it was in the afternoon, but it was packed with men. I was the only female in there other than the bartender. I guess if anyone is looking for a man who likes pool, this is the place to go! Ha. The only complaint I had was there was no music there. They have football on the TVs, but I would have really liked some music too. I mean that's a minuscule detail, and it's not make or break. I imagine at night there is music and I bet this place is packed. All in all, a great place to play some pool, even for a rookie like myself.

Very impressed! Ordered a watermelon mojito, it was very fresh and delicious. I had the stuffed pork chop and it was great. The fried roast beef poboy is incredible. Also the best fried chicken Ive had in a while. Cool atmosphere.

Three stars. 

My first delivery order was prompt and still hot. Exactly as ordered. 

Second order for delivery was average. I SPECIFICALLY asked for extra spicy for all orders. None were made with extra heat. 

Please make orders as requested!

Wingstop has good tasty wings. I tried the 10 boneless combo. Not a bad price for 10 wings,fries and a drink. I tried the garlic Parmesan and hickory smoke bbq boneless wings. Both were really good but the only negative that I had was only one customer ahead of me (we were the only customers in the store) and it took 25 minutes for my food to get done.

Front desk staff is rude, specifically LeRon.

I am currently on tour with my job and we are staying here. I got stuck in one of their elevators for 10 minutes, where it completely shut off on me and was unresponsive to anything I did. I luckily had my phone with my and connected to the wifi to call my coworker to call the front desk. I was let out eventually, but very shaken. I approached this LeRon and his handling of the situation was 100% percent unapologetic and brusque. His response verbatim? "Well what do you want from us?" and to contact my company manager instead of going to the front desk. 

So instead of putting on the lens of an actual customer service worker, I get "too bad." No solution, no reparation, no sincerity. Not sure about any of you reading this, but do you think it's ok to be stuck in an elevator at a place you are visiting, to be met with a "Well what do you want from us?"

Rude staff. I paid for my services with a credit card, as most people do these days, and was confused because there was no place to tip. The receptionist saw my confusion and said "we don't accept tips on credit cards. Cash is better for our employees. There's an ATM over there." Of course, the ATM charges fees. I don't carry cash so I said I'd be back the next day, and when I returned, I suggested that they should tell new customers about this policy since it is so outside the norm of what is expected. I was told, "well, I always do that, but some people think it's rude." No, it is rude to make customers pay extra fees or come back again to tip someone. I suspect some people just don't tip at all, but having worked in the service industry in the past, I wouldn't do that.

Inform your customers. Simple request.

I've been to Dr. BBQ on three occasions. The food is always pretty good. On the last occasion I visited it was Father's Day and we took our family out for dinner. After waiting much too long for a server to greet us, we were informed that they were out of ribs, beef ribs, and pastrami. I eat barbecue fairly infrequently, but when I do go I want ribs so that was disappointing.
The music was turned up way too loud and not good.  We could barely hear ourselves speak.
The restaurant was not very busy but our server appeared over extended. The service was sporadic and she actually gave us a check before clearing our table our asking us if we wanted anything else.  Feels like management might be slipping a bit. 
Also there is now a charge to self park.  We arrived in a drizzling rain and walked up to the restaurant. The valet attendants were nice and informed us that we either had to bring back the car to be valet parked or walk all the way to the other end of the block and pay for self parking. We did not want to valet so we had to walk in the rain to pay for parking. At a minimum they should move the self parking pay station closer to the restaurant. I understand it might not be the restaurant's parking lot, but they need to figure it out.
ok this looks place looks like an oldd italin resturant . you know the ones you see on the godfather. love this look you dont find many like that anymore. the pizza is to die for love the vibe.

A BBQ joint in the making....give them some time, and I'm sure they'll blow you away. 

I ventured into the modest establishment desiring to sample their best wares, and let's say some I was thoroughly disappointed in, and in others made me want to come back for a second take.   As a new resident of St. Charles, Mo part of what plays into me returning to a place is distance, and if the food + service is that compelling then I'll waste a half of tank of gas to enjoy the experience.   Let's just say it will be a minute before I make my way back down to Vernon's. 

I had a combo plate where I got ribs, wings, and pulled pork which include one side, which I ended up paying to get a second for balance, and honestly was extremely disappointed.   While waiting for my food, the service was warm and friendly, and so I mozied on to the sauce counter and attempted to sample them all.  I will tell you the THAI BBQ is out of this world, as there is nothing on this planet that taste like it.   What you will find it is simply either you love it or hate it....and the owner even stated that was the general consensus of what people thought. So onto my meal:

The ribs were cooked perfectly with good flavor, and chew.  They didn't disintegrate off of the bone, which was bonus, that the smoke master knew what they were doing.   The Chicken wing while it was only one, was a pleasant surprise, because they used mulberry wood it doesn't darken the skin at all, so I was expecting to have an undercooked tasteless piece of bird, and let me tell you it was cooked perfectly and this bird soared....my only complaint was that it was only 1 whole wing...which I personally think two should've been included. The pulled pork was moist, while not soaked in water or broth, which is a sign it came out of it's own juices, and needed a hint of salt, but aside from that was tasty.  

NOW THE SIDES....the original sides I ordered was the Brisket Beans and the Potato Salad, and I am sad to say when the owner came out to make his rounds a round the dining room I had already surrendered eating another bite.   The beans in the BBQ BRISKET BEANS were GYnormous and the sauce was less than palpable.   The Horseradish Potato salad isn't a mustard or mayo based....it's actually sour cream...and while this is out of the box for a BBQ joint, I would suggest salting it so that it popped, or used one of their great rubs....as it presented as extremely bland.  Upon conveying this to the owner, he saved the day...with bringing out literally every side they make.   So in this order these were the BOMB.....Tequilla Lime green beans....do it...do it.....do it..trust me!  The Coleslaw....the healthiest, Tastiest out of the box creamiest cole slaw you'll EVER HAVE! The Brisket Chili was delicious.   The candied apples were so good, my complaint was that they didn't put them in a pie and serve them up that way.   The cheesy scalloped potatoes were not bad, just nothing fancy.  

If you live anywhere near Vernon's please give them a try, they are worth you visit.  Next time I plan on attempting to tackle on of their mammoth sandwiches. I gave them 3 stars on the food really because of the waste I felt on the sides before the owner knocked me out with service recovery.  So please know that if I hadn't chosen those initial 2 sides, this would be a 4-4.5 because of the food and service.  

Vernon's I'm rooting for you all!

I'm a bit picky when it comes to bagels but honestly Chestnut Street bagels is no different then like getting a bagel at Dunkin' Donuts... but more expensive. I'm of the mind that if you are going to specialize in the bagel sandwich, do it right, cook your eggs/meats on a griddle. Microwaved eggs are a deal breaker for me. 

That said, they have a wide variety of bagel options, yes- even inclusive of the rainbow bagel! I opted for a sun dried tomato bagel because I had never seen one before and thought I would enjoy it on the veggie bagel sandwich. It was ok, the bagel was not as soft as I anticipated and the sun dried tomatoes made it pretty chewy. I did like all the veggies included on the sandwich! 

My boyfriend got and everything bagel with bacon and egg and a nitro cold brew coffee! He enjoyed his bagel but is not anxious to go back. 

Pro tip, the store is relatively small with no room to sit, but Pocket Park is a nice spot to enjoy your bagels on a pretty day!

I was initially very nervous to move. I only had about three weeks to prepare and being a single woman I couldn't lift all the furniture or totes of things. I checked out Yelp and saw that these great guys were near my house so I called to get a quote. Paul took the phone and was very patient while I scrambled and took forever to give him the new address and everything. An hour before the move the guys called to say they were on the way. When they arrived they were very easy going yet professional and careful with all of my fragile boxes. They even quickly helped me pack up the odds and ends! The guys were great and broke down my extremely complicated ikea bed and put it back together at the new place! They moved my 2 bedroom in less than an hour which is incredible! After unpacking today not a single item was broken so I'm very impressed! Rates are $100 initial booking fee and $100 an hour for two guys with a 3 hour minimum. I will recommend these guys to anyone!
Best BBQ in town! The mother load is the best value. I can make 3 extra meals for a family of 4 after we eat. The meat is moist and the flavor is exceptional. I definitely will be back.

I had order three time in the past two days and they are unable to make the order correctly. They like to rip people off on the cinnamon delights. I have ordered two sets of 2. And I get nothing or only one set. I make the special request to hold or remove items. They do not read. My request was not done. I requested NO SOUR CREAM, because I can not handle dairy, well guess what I got SOUR CREAM, eat some and got sick, so just wasted moneys on food that I can not eat, plus got sick off of it do to lazy workers that can not remove one bloody item.
DO NOT ORDER FOOD FROM THIS LAZY, INCOMPETENT, AND THIEVES.

First off, let me say the 2 star rating has nothing to do with the food. The food was fantastic. The seafood is to die for. The sea bass is fantastic and I had the monk fish special which was fantastic. The 2 starts is for the service. My wife and I went there for our 6 year anniversary and also devour Indy. Our server was slow and inattentive. He gave my wife the devour Indy portion of the sea bass and not the dinner sea bass and we still charged for the dinner portion. My wife ordered the dinner portion but received the Devour Indy portion. How do I know this, our couple friends ordered from the devour Indy menu and the sea bass was the same portion even though the waiter stated it was smaller. When we addressed the server about it, he acknowledged it but did nothing about it. We went to Salt 2 years ago and the food and service was fantastic. I'm not sure what changed since then or if we had a bad experience last night, but the service was definitely not worth the price of the meal.

Kitchen sink clog sent tons of water overflowing tiny sink in carpeted lower level. Patrick came out (2pm on a Saturday),and was VERY informative. After being told of the hourly rate was relieved that he worked quickly but thoroughly and professionally. Patrick advised us how to avoid further house calls and where to get a coupon for future services. 

A true professional and a great job. Hope we don't need it again, but will call Roto-Rooter and ask for Patrick if so. Thanks for a great job!

This was the worst experience I have had anywhere. I went to the TGIF's on Hillsborough Ave in Tampa about 11pm on Feb. 6th 2021. I was seated and then had to wait 20 minutes for a server. Finally when he came over he informed us that we had 15 minutes to order. Really, I sat there waiting 20 minutes on him. I asked about the Endless Appetizers he said , "We don't do that here". Really??? It's advertised that it is permanent. False advertising. So I order potato skins and wings for $26. Never received silverware or the extra sour cream and ranch. Then to top it off I paid my $26 bill will a twenty and a ten. Guess what, never got the change. Hmmm....what is that considered? STAY AWAY FROM THIS LOCATION.

Ok so stuck in a hotel... needing real food as opposed to Wawa, I saw this as a nearby joint for an Italian pizzeria and restaurant. Im from South Philly and the name attracted me because of my brothers are the same name. Looking over the menu seemed promising. Peppers and eggs were a staple in my house growing up and I haven't seen that on a menu is years so I ordered. How can you mess that up?! Lol anyway, as you can see in the pic I posted, it turned out to be a roasted pepper sandwich with egg as a condiment. Literally all peppers I mean, it tasted good but come one, eggs aren't that scarce or expensive that you should skimp on the egg. Just saying. Makes you wonder... You sure your Italian?!

This place is just ok. I do like the outdoor covered patio. But the weekend menu is super limited and I was very disappointed.  I have never been to a restaurant that has a weekend that is so limited. The waiter was adorable and very good. He allowed me to order off the menu. Not rushing to go back, that's for sure.

The hotel is nice and the staff is friendly.  The room are a nice size and clean. I wish that they offered free breakfast for the price you pay to stay here. My only problem was 3 nights in I got to the bathroom in the middle of the night and find a ROACH  hiding in the towels I had on the floor NASTY!! I was so shock ! That as nice as this hotel was it has pest problems. I had ANOTHER  pinched tailed bug on the sink!!!  WHERE AM I A CHEAP MOTEL ???  I made sure I kept my bags and luggage zip tight. That was the only day I saw bugs but it was traumatizing none the less.
Great little record shop in the French quarter. I found several things that had been on my list for a while. The owner, Scott, is very knowledgeable and friendly.

Came here cause the carnitas looked good when Guy visited this place foe Triple D. However ordered a breakfast burrito and a Louisiana hot link breakfast plate, both were good. The Louisiana hot link tasted yummy with a good amount of spiciness to it. The breakfast burrito (chorizo, eggs, potato, and cheese) tasted yummy and moist. I have to come back to try their carnitas still. Also love the fact that they open at 6:30 am.

This place was honestly NASTY. We heard mixed reviews so we figured we'd try it out for ourself and boy was that a mistake on all counts. I'll break it down for you.

Ambiance: they were BLASTING early 2000s rap like it was so loud I felt like I was in a dive bar right out of high school again and I kept thinking it would end but it never did, bubba sparks, young joc, on and on.

Cleanliness: firstly our table was sticky like I peeled my forearms from the side of the table. I ordered a glass of wine and the glass literally had like a sticky splat on it and it was far from polished. Also I know this is a lot of restaurants but the water tasted like straight pool water.

Food and drink: so my fiancé ordered a modelo and it came completely flat, switched for a Pacifico and better... but I guess better is the standard lol. So we ordered cheese fries, wood fired cauliflower and wings (garlic bbq flavor). We were nervous right off the bat when the cheese fries came out like 2 min after we ordered. They were ehhh def fake cheese but whatever at least there was good ranch. I didn't know what to expect from garlic bbq but this sweet honey chemical taste was not it, so gross. And the cauliflower, dude this was not wood fired lol this was raw blanched for like 3 seconds in some weird oil and thrown in with the croutons you use for stuffing.

The waiter came by and asked how everything was and I was like "honestly it was gross but it's whatever" and he was just like "ok I'll get your check." Kinda acted like it happened all the time. Didn't offer to fix anything, didn't take anything off the check, didn't even offer a box for all our untouched food just "ok, I'll get your check." Needless to say we will NOT be going back.

When I arrived I was told I was not in the system for 8/31 but for 9/14. This was frustrating because I received a call last week confirming the appointment. I also have on my refrigerator the business card given to me by the individual who scheduled the appointment which has 8/31 at 8:50am on it. I unfortunately did not bring the card with me to todays appointment because I did not think I needed it. The staff was not very helpful and I felt I was inconveniencing them and that they did not believe me. I was looking forward to this appointment to see the healing progression of my shoulder and the Doctor told me at the last appointment that he would be starting me on some sort of physical therapy at my 8/31 appointment. Now I have to wait until 9/14 for my appointment and my back to work date was set for 9/24/20. I am extremely frustrated with the level of service I received today and I was also supposed to follow up with my workmen's comp nurse and inform her of my progress and now I can't. Not to mention the hour and a half of my time Rothman wasted this morning.

Went for my boys birthday lunches. It took us over an hour And a half to get our food. Our ticket was started at 12:28 according to the receipt. We got there at 12. We were there until 2. We didnt have time for them to get their birthday desserts because I had to go to work. They were not busy at all, I'm talking 3 tables in the whole place. My son asked for onion petals on his steak and she charged us 4.99 for a bowl of cold petals.

If you want to charge me $15 for a burger, I'm sorry, but there better be some meat on it. Especially, when we're talking about ground chuck. 

We had a nice time due to great service by Nakita. She was attentive, thoughtful, polite and knowledgeable. I was so happy to finally good service in a hipster area. She really knows whats up. I'm actually sorry that the food was so unjustifiably average for me... this pretty much guarantees no return visits.

The bragged about Brioche bun was too buttered and toasted for me to enjoy it as part of my burger.

All of the choices looked great, so we had difficulty deciding. We chose to have three and share. Nakita brought the first, the portabella mushroom burger, as an appetizer. 
My first instinct-- tiny, second, delicious. 

Next, we had the Monster Burger and Lobster Burger. The Monster Burger was piled at least 12 inches high. After taking the incredibly soggy, oil drenched onion rings off, the patties seemed to be 5 oz. I could not believe how tiny they were.  It was wildly disappointing that the big "brioche bun" and onion rings made this burger look huge but when you really get down to it, there is nothing there. 

The same, if not worse, goes for the Lobster Burger. I was not expecting a lobster tail. in fact, the lobster meat on the burger was fine. It was the burger again that was disappointing. Tasty due to an excess of salt, yes.

It was just too tiny for me to get excited about.

View is 5 stars!

Food, drinks, service, atmosphere, is A-OK. 

It's the Westin hotel lobby bar and restaurant so everything is expensive. 
The shiny ultra clean floor makes rubber sneaker type of footwear squeak as both servers and patrons walk around. Kind of makes me cringe as my ears are just a little sensitive.

We defiantly enjoyed our time watching the tall ships coming into the harbor from the gulf, truly one of the best views of the mighty Mississippi. 

 4/2018

This place would have gotten a 5 Star if they had something of their own that I would have ordered to drink but sadly the only thing was a guest tap. 

I had heard about this place a while ago when I use to work this region but when my region was rearranged to Pinellas Co, it took me away from being out there often. I was glad to see that this year they were in the Tampa Bay Beer Trail so that we could get them in with the other Brandon area locations. 

We planned our visit to the region to be at Three Bulls around the time we would be hungry as I also heard their food was noteworthy. Nothing better than a brewery that also produces great food. 

When we arrived, if I didn't know better, I would have just thought this was another restaurant with the feel of the place when you walk in but it is very much a brewery. The design is done very well, with the perfect split between restaurant and brewery that would invite people who are just looking for a well crafted beer or those looking for a well executed meal. But lucky you, both are available!

We started out with the ribeye french dip appetizer to be followed by their staple burger and the reuben. Everything was great, the mini french dips were surprisingly good and we will get those again. The burger had a mixture of different meats in the patty, making it unique and according to Ben, you just can't go wrong with their reuben. 

The beer staff was very friendly and even joked around with us which we loved. We will be back and in Ben's words, this was a win!
Longbar kicks butt. Super chill to hang out with co workers after work to have a couple beers and play some pool. wonderful service and friendly people. Open late. Perfect.
I got a good cleaning for a pretty cheap introductory price. The scheduling was a bit weird--had to make one for checkup and one for cleaning but everything else went well.

I asked if they have provolone and he mumbled a yes and wrote down my preference for provolone on my sandwich. I wasn't even sure if I wanted provolone, but I wanted to know my options other than American or whiz. (I didn't order provolone, I only asked if it was an option.) 

They guy continues to have a conversation with the cook making me feel very not welcome. I didn't notice fries on the menu but saw bagged potato chips on a display and I asked if they had fries and he writes that down on my order. (Here too, I wasn't sure if I wanted fries, but wanted to know my options. And here too, I didn't order fires, I only asked if they were an option.)

At this point, I feel like the guy could give 2 sh+ts if I am there so I canceled my order. He did nicely ask if I was sure I no longer wanted to place an order and I said yes as I didn't feel I was getting the attention a customer deserves. And the funny thing is, the guy seemed plenty nice. He wasn't a d+ck or anything, but he wasn't in a 'good customer service mood' with me. He needs a 2 hour class though in good customer service because he is lacking knowledge in that department. 

I took my money and ate somewhere else today. 

The Prince's loss here, not mine.

Quite possibly the most liberal nightmare employees of ever met at any Apple store or for that matter any place else do not go here if there's a possibility of going somewhere else the people in here will make you sick

Of all of the Warby Parker stores that I have been to (including the flagship stores in NYC), this is by far my favorite location. The staff is super helpful and I never feel rushed.

During COVID, I came with near-broken glasses due to a fall (thanks Philly sidewalks), and after a back and forth with the online customer service agents about my options, I came to the store to see if they could help. The staff performed a miracle and were able to get me on my way within 15 minutes. Excited to come back and browse this location again once COVID is behind us.

We stopped by after visiting Winterthur and ordered an assortment of pastries and cookies.  Everything was pretty good, but nothing special.  The employees should learn how to smile.  The prices were a little on the high side.  Next!

The El Bar is hang to some of the most diverse neighborhood folk. The prices are great, the bartenders uber cool, the outdoor patio the place for lively conversation, and they offer ramen - what more could you want?

now THIS is a hometown favorite. Situated right in the heart of  the Jefferson's street area, this place is a family favorite for all
Nashvillians. The soul food here is quality. It's cafeteria style with open seating. I usually get two thighs with sweet potatoes (to die for btw), Mac n cheese, and the turnip greens. The corn bread here is also pretty amazing. I don't give many people 5 stars but If you're looking for actual homemade southern style soul food that's got good portions and great staff, I would highly recommend this place. A top 5 Nashville restaurant for sure.

As I get prepared for yet another Tenderness Tour event in March, I'm finding myself hitting the Indy trail system more often including this particular trail, Fall Creek Greenway, which is actually the closest trail system to my home and one of Indy's more underrated trails. 

The Fall Creek Trail/Greenway starts at the Skiles Test Nature Park trailhead, a small park that is also one of my favorite spots in Indy. As is true of most of Indy's trails, The Fall Creek Greenway has some fun detour options, ranging from a 1.5 mile limestone loop trail that is a great spot for jogging as it passes the Fall Creek overflow pond to a 10.5 mile pedestrian trail that follows the George Kessler Boulevard plan that travels along a waterway and some really awesome bridges. 

While the Fall Creek Greenway "feels" less accessible than the far more popular Monon Trail, the truth is that the Fall Creek Greenway is easily accessible at several points along the trail and offers several parking options in safe areas. The Fall Creek Greenway has, at times, had a reputation for being a less populated, less safe option but if you check out the stats you'll learn there's actually more crime along the Monon Trail and it's for the most part considered to be a safe trail. I'm a disabled wheelchair user who typically wheels alone and I've never had any trouble on either trail. That said, I'm a big believer in practicing trail safety.

The Fall Creek Greenway has some delicate wetlands along the north side of the trail, though you should probably take note that these areas can also flood during times of excessive rains. While I've never seen the trail not able to be traveled, there are times you should definitely plan for a wetter trail. The south section of the trail ties into the Monon Trail, while you can also catch the White River Wapahani Trail directly off the Fall Creek. 

The majority of the Fall Creek Greenway is nicely paved and easily wheelchair friendly. The upkeep on the trail seems to be a notch below that of the more populated Monon - I've seen storm damage take a few days to clear and the trail can occasionally get a little muddy in spots from the rains. Personally? I like the less tailored feeling of the Fall Creek Greenway along with the fact that it offers more natural sights and sounds than the Monon Trail. You get to walk/run/bike/wheel along water quite a bit including a stretch near Fall Creek Parkway that  is truly right next to it much like a similar trail up on South Bend. If you're a wheelchair user who is prone to spasticity or you have difficulty steering, you should be aware that the waterside sections of the trail do not offer railing and you should plan to ensure your safety. 

The Fall Creek Greenway Trail has the usual educational stations along the route, especially near the Fort Harrison State Park area but they exist throughout the trail. The trail is mostly flat with some minor gradual inclines that, once again, are more frequent around Fort Harrison. It's relatively easy to spy fish, birds, deer and other animals along the trail and in the small but beautiful Skiles Test Nature Park. 

If you're needing some time alone with nature, Fall Creek Greenway is likely a better option than Monon Trail. While people certainly utilize this trail, it's an awful lot easier to use the trail without the forced social interactions and every two minute exclamations of "on your left" that are part of life on the Monon Trail. However, if you love the social experience of the trails then this one likely won't satisfy you. 

I'm knocking off a star primarily due to what I'd consider to be slightly lesser upkeep than is offered on the Monon and those occasional days when the north section is a little more challenging to use due to flooding and weather factors. However, these really are minor considerations. Indy's trail system is awesome and disability friendly and Fall Creek Greenway is a terrific trail for walkers, runners, bikers, wheelers and those who have disabilities that include sensory issues (though if these individuals gravitate toward water, I'd exercise caution). While the Indy Parks website doesn't talk about it much, there's also a section that goes north into the Geist area. Again, it offers far more nature and beauty than the Monon with a few extra inclines.

If you are looking for a farmers market, look elsewhere -- this place is something, alright, but a farmers market it is not. Just a bunch of overpriced "artisan" food vendors. If you buy into the whole main-line elitist attitude, you might enjoy paying the premium and eating a fillet of beef from a cow that was raised on ancient grains hand-grown by the Dalai Lama. However, if you want fresh produce, there are a few REAL farmers markets around. Or if you want quality food without the jacked-up cost or main-line attitude, you need only look across the parking lot

Food was ok, drinks were not good and I had a margarita and had little to no alchohol in the drink and when asked about it they wanted to charge me for an extra shot. Good view is about all this place has going for it. Less than impressed.

Got two vegan pizzas to take to Bonesaw Brewery:  pepperoni and sausage and pepper.  Pepperoni was great.  Sausage and pepper too spicy for me and it had no red sauce.  The vegan sausage is good.  The owner of the Fishtown location said she the vegan sausage crumbles are made by Dibruno's.  Not sure if this location gets them from there.  Nice selection of vegan choices.

Had takeout tonight and ordered fish and chips and char grilled salmon, and crab cakes. The salmon was very bland and over cooked. I was very disappointed! The fish and chips were ok. The chips were your typical steak fries with absolutely no seasoning. Fish was ok though if you were wanting fish and chips.  Crab cakes, again, no seasoning on anything. Over priced. Overall just so so, probably wouldn't be a first choice.

My husband took me out for my birthday before he took me to the Grand Ole Opry.  Since we have never eaten at a Claim Jumper I was pretty excited to try a new restaurant.   Service was great we sat down ordered a drink I took two sips of my drink when I went to pick it up again the glass busted in my hand.  My hand is bleeding and the waitress says can I get you a Band-Aid that was it.   Once the food arrived my husband was so full of Gristle he could not eat one single bite of it.  I ate  maybe five bites it appears that 3/4 of the steak had so much salt added it was Inedible.  Manager came over and told my husband I'll take care of your food he said  said thank you after what happened earlier.  The manager had no idea that one of his glasses has busted in a patrons hands.  He said I'm sorry we paid for our bill walked out.   We will never recommend this location.   I wish I had better things to say but the food was horrible and once the glass busted in my hand the service was horrible.   Thanks for a horrible birthday dinner claim jumper.

I've been going here for about 5 years! Two happy pregnancies and babies later! I originally wanted a female dr but Dr.Garol was so awesome and same for Ruby his Medical Assistant. They know exactly who I am, ask about my kids, job etc. One time I showed up 2 hrs late for my appt thinking it was later than actually scheduled. I was told that if I wanted to be seen that day I would have to see a RN or reschedule with Dr. garol. I said RN was okay and apologized for my unintentional tardiness. And who shows up in my room? Ruby and Dr. Garol! Love love love them. Yes it's a busy office. But I'm not complaining about taking the rest of the day off.

They serve simple and delicious Mexican style street tacos out of their little truck. The service is pleasant and efficient. The salsa verde is perfectly spicy. The pork is tender and delicious. The pupusas are our of this world. I've never tried the burrito, but I'm sure it's also top notch. And oy, what a deal.

I used to go here often , actually a couple times a week & in the past given 5 stars however today I ordered take out. Stood 5 min at 1 end of bar people blew me off then bartender walked by me as if I was in her way. Then I went to other end of bar where customer told me to go not the 3 servers I asked. Stood there for another 5 min & waited.  Bartender finally acknowledged me at this time my food was cold & don't forget I still had to drive home. When home the quesadilla were completely saturated in tomato juice , spicy fingers were dried up & they didn't even give me the amount I ordered. sMh the chicken in quesadilla was hard & crunchy mind you it is grilled chicken. I will not be returning & also I still tipped 20% for dealing with a rude lady. Smh they need a new bartender she needs to remember she's there to provide customer service.

My name is Jerome and my friend name is Yvonne   We make a priority to stop by different pizza stores. We found out that this is a hidden gem in  South Philly  if you're looking for the best  look no further  you found the best .please check it out and try the pizza is great and the lamb is excellent .Bon appétit.
Started my day with their delicious keto friendly bulletproof coffee (hot). The iced is pretty darn good too! Great service and atmosphere.

As a Loco Pez regular, I enjoyed watching the slow construction of Cedar Point over the course of the last year. It was with no lack of excitement that I visited the restaurant a week after it's grand opening. The best way I know to describe the experience was that despite high levels of promise, what resulted was very mediocre execution. The food is OK at best and undoubtedly overpriced. Service is very lacking. It's clear that they hired folks without service backgrounds, especially at the bar. One time at the bar I watched the bartender spill part of my (expensive) cocktail then proceed to top of the drink with water. Very disappointing. Interior decor is very "meh", especially if you get stuck in the back section away from the windows/deck.

I still go to Cedar Point occasionally for drinks on their sweet outdoor deck, but only due to the fact that I live a few blocks away. Otherwise, I'd not bother making the trip.

Can't get much closer to the convention center than this!  Right on the same block as Reading Terminal, if you aren't planning on doing any driving and just want a central place downtown, this works great.   The hotel was very clean, staff friendly, and the gym is small but good.  My biggest complaint was the pool...not terribly big (about 8 strokes long)...the first night I was there it was way too cold (and I swim in lakes), then after that it was like bathwater...ug.

2/24/18. Celebrating my birthday with a dinner date with the my better half. Wait time 1.5 hours. Seating in the front in a small section. Large party was extremely loud and obnoxious and made two other s and our table very uncomfortable. The food was out in a timely manner but lacked proper seasoning. A little bland. The mojito was not properly muddled and vibrant. To say the least we did not enjoy our experience. We were very disappointed. Quick tip, make a reservation. ‍

Food is consistently good.  Good draft beer options.  Service is consistently horrible though!  Need new management to come in and change the attitudes of the bar and wait staff.  3 stars awarded for food only.  Don't plan to return.

I really enjoy this bar. The bartenders are always friendly and they have a nice assortment of beers. However, I have one complaint. I wish they would list the alcohol % on their beer list. I was drinking beer all night with a 10% alcohol percentage and had no idea until I looked it up. As some one who appreciates a large variety of beers, I like to know what abv I am drinking because it makes a big difference in the number of beers I purchase. This is a bigger concern for someone who plans to be a designated driver. Obviously buying one beer with a 5.4 abv is much different than a 10 %. But overall, a nice experience.
Typical Dunkin' Donuts. Nothing amazing about it. Conveniently located near the pier.

So disrespectful. The security guards are racist. They shouted at us in Spanish , assuming we were Hispanic and were derogatory towards our group. We spent a lot of money there and their security told us to "eat shit". So bad. Do not waste your money. This is a racist and bigot venue. Please do not support a venue like this.

Agree with everyone that gave 1 star. Absolutely NOT worth the cost of a "cleaning". They did nothing! I wouldn't even give 1 star if I could skip the star rating and just post a review only. 
Terrible!!!
Great place for the price.  A little run down, but it is definitely a well run family establishment.  Pho is good, and the lunch specials are reasonably priced and yummy.

I would normally give this hotel a 4 star rating from the look of it, but there are the things that were bad. If the room next door or above turn the shower on, it's louder than as if in the same room with a piercing sound. Do not let them try to put you in a room next to an ice maker machine, that's almost just as loud. The staff is snobby and has attitude. It's supposed to be a nice hotel, but the staff member checking us in not only had major attitude but had a huge tattoo on her neck of a tongue. I mean, cover that up, it doesn't fit into the hotels image in my opinion. The lobby is nice and chic, great for pictures. Again, the noise of the neighboring rooms showers are unbearable.

I've always received excellent service here. I usually get the Peanut Butter Cup and Sunny Day smoothies and occasionally the Peanut Butter Banana Crunch flatbread. The flatbread is so delicious! It has bananas, honey and peanut butter heated up within the flatbread. Smoothies are excellent, can't stress that enough!
Best sliders I've ever eaten...seriously!! Fries were way over salted or else this would be a five star review.

Solo coman anticucho aqui!
La parihuela es el peor que he probado en toda mi vida!
El seco super salado!
This review is for the paddy on welsh rd. The bartender girl is more preoccupied with her texts ( she looks like a heroin addict too). Pls give a shit. Food: garlic parm wings is good though

And so we've come / to the end of the Reeeno / I can totally let go / It's unnatural / to pay $120 / for a Motel 6 roooom (Boyz II Men, sorta)

Officially moved the last of my stuff out of storage here and severed all ties. Give me a call if you ever figure out how to do symmetrical growth that lifts all boats. Or if the casinos stop being greedy as hell and maybe put some effort into proper renovations.

Looked online for their hours, went there when they opened and was told that the website was not correct and they don't open for another 35 minutes so I had to wait in the parking lot. Website said they had an arcade with 30 games but when I got there the arcade is completely gone. my son went skating and they never played music, he was skating in complete silence.  they also served us cheese fries with cheese that was chunky and disgusting and had to be returned. The woman said yeah I didn't think it looked right either it was the bottom of the bag I'll give you a fresh one so my question was why would you even serve us something you knew was expired and not right? Not to mention they clean the tables with a dirty rag then went behind the counter to make food and did not wash their hands.  people at the front desk were very rude. I won't be coming back,  was completely disappointed and disgusted.

A little hidden gem on a shopping plaza.  Excellent Indonesia food.  I had the fried fish with coconut rice.  The fish was tender and crispy with some spicy sauce.  Excellent choice!  Try it out you won't regret

I use this park as part of my outdoor fitness rotation (mostly for jogging). Cooper River  runs through Pennsauken, Cherry Hill, Collingswood and Haddon Township. The park is bounded by North and South Park Drives, Route 130 and Grove Street. 

Fellow Yelpers (Christopher R) review on  8/31/2010 sums up the park very well. 

I have noticed additional porta-potties throughout the park. A centralized bathroom is also available at the Jack Curtis Stadium (next to the cinder track). 

I appreciate the monuments located at the  Memorial Grove  (Medal of Honor, Fallen Officer, etc.)

As a note, if you cross over the intersection/corner  of Cuthbert Blvd and N. Park Dr. (where  Parkway Miniature golf is located), you will find a less traveled section of the  park. (this is where I usually go).  Pass the Parkway Miniature golf on the right & continue down to the far end of North Park drive; you will come to a dog park ('Pooch Park'). Continue on the dirt path and you will see the back of the Cherry Hill National Guard Armory. (area is a  little sketchy late at night, be careful).  Continue on the paved path and then cross over Grove Street. You will then enter a long track of woods that has cool trails for trail running. (goes all the way to Kings Highway, connects to Maria Barnaby Greenwald Memorial Park and Hopkins Pond).


Overall, I like this park. Camden County just announced a proposal for a $23 million dollar upgrade to the park. Here is the architectural vision plan 
http://www.camdencounty.com/sites/default/files/files/1132-cooper%20river%20booklet.pdf.  for the park. (.pdf file).  It is actually a pretty cool read. 

Hopefully this upgrade  comes to fruition and helps to better the park. We shall see..........

ps: Don't forget to visit Moore Brothers Wine Company on Park Drive http://www.yelp.com/biz/moore-brothers-wine-company-pennsauken

Terrible customer service, just left, 5 girls cutting hair and only one person in front of me, waited 40 minutes and still never got back. 2 girls finished 2 gentlemen and walked to the back for 10-15 minutes and never helped the next guest. After that the women literally took over 10 minutes to sweep her area, then walked to the back for a POW wow again.... this was when I left. Get some people who want to make your new establishment some money...

One of the best restaurants in Brentwood TN by far. This place is awesome in every way, a bit pricey would be my only complaint but I am cheap. The food quality here is top notch, awesome atmosphere, great decor the restaurant is the best. Very friendly wait staff, they make you feel at home. The food is American standard fare but high class at the same time. I would recommend this place if you are in business in Brentwood its in the business distinct part of town and they cater to upper middle class to wealthy clients. I would recommend you try this restaurant out.

I expected a bad experience based on the reviews, I was wrong. We were seated quickly, our server was pleasant, food came out very fast, and the place was clean. 

The bad reviews are very misleading.

Not much of a country fan, BUT the show was good! Alan Jackson and Miranda Lambert played tonight, so it made for a good show. The venue was smaller than I imagined and the show seemed short, but overall I had a good time. My country loving Grandma would be proud!

It's time for an update.  We went to the new location to check out just how well they did in the move.  We hit them right in the middle of a 12:30 lunch rush.  Michael T. was coming to join us at about 12:45 so we wanted to get there a little early to get our names in for the bar.  It didn't really help much, they only have like 8-10 seats at the bar.  I haven't heard of a restaurant reducing their size on a move to another location.  But these guys did just that.  Probably a reduction of up to 5 seats.  

So we walked in just as a group was being sat.  When we finally were seated...we sat in their seats!  Yep the bar is that small.  But that was ok...we didn't have any push for time.  When we sat, Michael T is well known in these parts and the Chef remembered my name too, we were started on a quick regimen of goodness.  And my goodness it was good.  Usually we focus on the raw because that is just what we like...but the new Menu has so much new baked stuff and cooked stuff we had to try as much as we could.  

The baked section was good, but these rolls might be harder to share for groups that aren't super comfortable.  The rolls are cooked and the stick together and fall apart.  This is NOT a complaint, it's just a warning.  They were all very good.  I especially liked the Pizza roll.  The best thing I had this time was the yooki, yuki, uke, I have no idea how he spelled it.  But it was salmon with some ginger and some sort of sauce on top.  Just wowfreakingawesomeamazing for my mouth.  I could just eat a whole batch of these.

I was able to get a nice bottle of cold unfiltered Sake.  Exactly what I needed and it hit the sport perfectly.

We walked out full and happy. 

So a few notes...
Don't go right at peak time and expect them to sit you at the bar.
We didn't sit at a table so we don't know how well the tables are served.
If you can wait til 2ish you can probably get in pretty easily.
When you do go...you will probably see 20 people you know.  We did.
Parking sucks compared to their old place.  This mall probably has the worst parking lot in the city.
Still easily top 3 sushi places.
