See you there!  Yes, that's me sitting there waiting for the bar.

We came here early Sunday morning and arrived just before 8. We sat outside until 8:15 when they finally decided to open (they were supposed to open at 8) and their staff was rude and took the orders of people who had literally just walked up before those who had been waiting for 15+ minutes. Very poor experience, haven't seen such poor service and lack of attention in a very long time. Disappointed.

Although the times I have been to this facility have been good in terms of being seen quickly and generally received good care, (with the exception of Dr. Straw who has zero bedside manner) they are attempting to bill me twice for visits made there.  I paid a copay when I was there for two separate visits and they are now attempting to double bill me with a copays I've already paid.  I've contacted my insurance who says that they're aware of this facility and what's been going on over there and they will have my claim adjusted as I definitely do not owe two copays.  

Look at the BBB reviews for this group and you will see numerous claims against them for doing the same thing to others.  In each case they admitted they were wrong and dropped the claim, but how many people just pay it because they don't know how to contest it.  These people still owe me a $15 refund that I was promised in January and it's now September.

Although I like the staff there and the very short waiting times, I'm going to another facility in the future due to their unethical business practices.

Worst place I tried in New Orleans,  including the free breakfast at the hotel (which was far superior). To start with the positive part,  they have a nice beer selection,  so if it were just based on the beer selection it might be a 3. 

Unfortunately,  I ate the food.  In short it was the worst stuff I had in the city.  I started with lamb spring rolls that they burned to a crisp.  See photo below and judge for yourself.  It was shocking to me that they let such an error get through to a customer. I tried one thinking it might be a Cajun seasoned skin,  but it was just burnt. Next, the half and half Po'boy was was virtually inedible because the bread they used was so stale, I literally aspirated some crumbs and am still trying to clear my throat.  Mercifully,  the oysters and shrimp were reasonable.  

Sad to say this is one of the few Yelp misses I have experienced.  After a bad experience giving a bad review in the past,  I told my wife I would refrain from giving reviews below a 3, but the fact that this place is a 4 is simply offensive to me.  I would have gone lower, but the places overall decor and atmosphere were fine.  

In short,  go here for a drink and stay away from the food.

Worst. Steak. Ever. The service was very good (I sat at the bar). The potato was served cold. The ribeye was bland and VERY chewy. I won't eat at Outback again. I strongly suspect they sell fake meat made with meat glue. (There's a video on YouTube about fake meat. Apparently restaurants are the number one offenders because they aren't required to tell their customers that they use manufactured steaks, unlike grocery stores. The most commonly created steaks are filets and ribeyes.) 

The manager did comp my "meal" (I only ate half of that disgusting garbage) but I ended up spending just as much because I gave the bartender a $15 tip for two beers since it's not her fault her employer sells inedible garbage. 

If you want a cold beer, or something other than a steak, it's pretty good. If you want a steak, I'd suggest going elsewhere or cooking at home (check out "how to cook the perfect steak, flip the script"  on YouTube. I've used this method for awhile now and my steaks are perfect every time.)

Goody Goody Diner is what all other diners strive to be.
We heard the buzz of the Chicken and Waffles, and we just had to experience for ourselves. This weekend with a out-of-town guest, it was the perfect time. We arrived at 8 AM., and within an hour all tables were filled and the diner was bustling. 
My other half and friend had the Chicken and Waffles Supreme which came with two eggs and grits. I got a smaller version; 4 wings and a half waffle with a side of Hash Browns with grilled onions.  Once our food was delivered our table fell silent, the food was so good there was no need for good conversation. 
We all agreed the fried chicken was some of the best we'd experienced and the waffles in my opinion are better then some I have had at high end Sunday Brunches.  The grits received two thumbs up and my hash browns were good, but they weren't true hash browns they were more American Fries. 
Staff was friendly and chivalry is not dead at Goody Goody. When food was ready, one of the men would carry out the large tray of food for the ladies to serve - good team work.

Tough steak & the best part of it was the bread.  I know it's a tourist joint but come on I was at least expecting decent food.  Not impressed.  Pat's was better.  Don't let the lights, glitz and glam fool you.  But I encourage you to play in the rivalry and do try both.

The lobby is beautiful!   The room had a great view. But we discovered after a late night that the carpet was soaking wet--cleaned but not dried?  The room and hallways are tired--peeling wallpaper, damaged decor, etc...the hotel  really needs renovations for what is expected of an Omni.  The bed was really comfortable and the room was quiet. In the morning, we discovered the valet had left our car unlocked all night.  Overall, meh.  Not a typical Omni experience.

Ordered bbq bowl, it came out cold. Sesame cold noodles not as expected.  I think the chef was not the same as my first terrific experience. Server was great and ambiance fabulous, but I won't be back anytime soon.

Having been away from Philly for awhile, I don't know all the good local places, so my family picked Bistro Romano for my birthday. I'm glad they did. I can't imagine how there are any bad reviews of this place- to start, its old city charm and coziness perfectly match the charm of the surrounding neighborhood. We had outstanding service- not rushed but attentive. The artichoke appetizer was excellent, and we all enjoyed the tableside-prepared Caesar salad. The table had a mix of veal and pasta for entrees and everyone enjoyed their dinners. My veal saltimbocca wasn't the best veal i've ever tasted, but it was very good- simple but flavorful and well cooked. We also enjoyed our desserts, and it's always nice when things are made in house. I would give Bistro Romano an extra half star if I could. It was classically good fresh food in a nice atmosphere with good service. I'd definitely go back if I were in town again.

Once you've been to one Denny's you've practically been to them all. This one is no exception. Your typical 24 hour diner style franchise right here. 

Me and my wife went in after a movie screening and we were seated right away. That's about where the fast service ended though. There were only a few other parties there before us but fifteen to twenty minutes after being seated, those other groups were being served. It just took a really long time (almost 30 minutes) for them to make a breakfast sandwich and pancake special with hash browns. 

Don't get me wrong, the food was hot when it came out and it tasted good. The hash browns had a crunchy outside and soft inner layer that mixed well with the sausage and bacon. Everything mixed well with the fluff pancakes.

If you're looking for that diner style breakfast you can't go wrong with Denny's and if you don't mind the wait you can check this one out.

Not terrible, but not nearly as good as the reviews. The salmon was cooked to oblivion, the rest was just over cooked, then served submerged in a water, butter and zataran's mixture. I have never seen seafood served like this. 
They told us we were lucky cause our food would only take 30-45 mins. Some people wait up to 2 hours. Not a chance! Hopefully they can figure out how to plan ahead and cut down their wait, but it isn't worth waiting in my opinion. 
Staff was very nice, but I go to a restaurant for the food.

Awesome customer service, delicious food, amazing everything!
It's simply the BEST place to have lunch around downtown Indianapolis!!!

Great location on the main street that has a variety of desserts, chocolates, and ice cream. I liked that they have a variety of ice cream flavors that are unusual than most ice cream parlours. We had the following Turtle, Sea Salt, Dolce de Leche, Lemon Sorbetto.

Maybe a bit over priced for my liking tho.

I'm the boyfriend Christine F. referenced in her 1 star review of this place.  Clearly there are a lot of strong opinions about her review.  

How about this, if you were at Rhine Haus the night she's referencing then you can debate about whether or not the floor was too sticky or if the bar tender was frazzled/rude.  If you were not, why not write your review according to your own experiences.  

Since I was the sober driver that night, I can with 100% certainty conclude that everything Christine F. said did in fact happen and she was quite accurate in her description.  I would like to note that she hadn't eaten anything that day for roughly 12 hours prior  to eating the burgers/fries from Rhine Haus.  I mention that to highlight she may have thought the burgers were a bit more tasty than I.  I will however note that the fries were very good.  

Here's what I liked:
 - Location to my apartment
 - Fries
 - Bar games (shuffle board, bags, touch-screen games, etc)

Here's what I did NOT like:
 - Horribly unprofessional manager/owner named Kirk or Kurt
 - Service & a very bitchy bartender (by now she may not still work here)
 - Deceiving business practices (ie. Specials not owned up to)
 - Very sticky floors (yes, it was true that evening)
 - Creepy DJ who hit on both Christine and Christina and tried to throw a $20 at me while discussing my bill with management for a taxi ride home.  (This guy almost got hit for that one).


PS.
As for Phil,
I think your review has already been filtered, however, please use spell check or take an English course before writing your next review.  You've been in Germany too long buddy.

Ok, so the price of happy hour martinis have gone up...  But they're still super strong!  Make sure you check the speciality drinks menu, as the martinis change on what seems to be a seasonal basis.  When the weather is warm, try to sit outside in the courtyard!  It's a beautiful little patio decked out with plants, water features, and cool outdoor objet d'art.  The staff is fun and cheerful.  And the customers are a mix of interesting characters!  Go early and go often!  It's lots of fun!

We are 4 families visiting from Hawaii and Virginia. my five grand children loves fishes so I decided to stop in for lunch with them.  The host, waiter and ambiance is awesome. 

The aquarium itself is breath taking and the children's menu was well priced $7.99 per meal ......however, the food was awful. The broccoli was soggy, the grill cheese was salty and dry, the mini cheeseburgers were also dry and my grandson who loves cheese burgers did not want it. I spoke to the waiter and he said he would get it corrected in the kitchen but that was all of their food. And I don't know what happens in a kitchen when food is sent back. My grand daughter who loved fruits said the cantaloupe did not taste like cantaloupe. I ordered the castaway combo and agree that the food was not good at all.

Tate's was good about 15 years ago. Now, the service is absolutely terrible. Very slow, and they don't even give you menus when you sit down. 
The food itself is no better. God forbid you order a fried egg. I guarantee the yoke will come out broken. The home fries Tate's was once famous for are now greasy as hell!
I refuse to eat breakfast there, but I decided to give lunch a try today... Same bad service along with a very dry, very bland cheesesteak. 
I'll never go back

I've given them more than one chance and it doesn't seem to matter.. They will not fix what they do wrong without an attitude.. I got all the way home all my carne asada frie meals were made with chips instead of french fries and the sour cream I paid for it was missing.. My favorite thing to get is a chile relleno I make them at home so I know how they're made but here they leave the seeds and they don't even clean the peppers.. My husband went back to get our food fixed and they were extremely rude and still left out the sour cream so all my food was cold while waiting to eat with my family and I didn't even get to enjoy it..

I love the Irish 31 location in South Tampa so it is nice that there is now a location much closer to my home!  This Irish 31 is MUCH larger that the Hyde Park location.  I went on a Sunday and there was a $2 Miller Lite special.  I didn't eat any food but I am sure that I will return soon enough.  Another great addition to the Westchase area!

Great food, service and atmosphere.  Stopped for lunch on a July Friday and was blown away.  The drinks were excellent.  For food, my wife and I split the heirloom carrots, smashed potatoes with goat cheese, gnocchi and cod sandwich.  All were great.  The Cod was a big surprise and was perfectly balanced.  For desert we split the lemon bar on the suggestion of our waitress.  It was perfect.  Highly recommend and we will return often

Just had an amazing breakfast sandwich. Scrambled eggs, bacon and cream cheese on bagel. Side of fruit and mug of tea. All under $10 and friendly staff. It's probably dangerous that I'm within walking distance.
Dirty bar, and expensive drinks assembled with little care. There is absolutely nothing unique about this place whatsoever.

Used to be fantastic, but Blaze in Brentwood has really declined in the last couple of months. No longer offering curbside.  Order accuracy has been off on our last three visits.  And the quality of the pizza is gone. Tonight's order included what was intended to be a Red Vine, but looked and tasted more like a hardened tomato and cheese frisbee.  Too bad.  We will move on to other options.
Consistently good food and service at least on weeknights when we go. I'd give 3.5 stars if it was an option. Not quite 4 stars because it is still a chain after all

Friday evening in New Orleans.

Great venue. Seeing Auntie Mame with Ricky Graham and the consummate Varla Jean Merman. Those of you familiar with New Orleans will recognize both from many venues. They are doing a marvelous 'drag reading' of this classic. Varla is putting both Rosalind and Lucy to shame!

To be honest, I only stopped in here because my husband was at the record shopping but I'm glad I did. It filled a craving I didn't even know I had! Although the Thai milk tea was a tad on the sweet side it was still pretty good.

Food was delicious, service was ok. Server kept disappearing to the back. When she my card, I didn't see her for 10 mins. I had to go ask for my card back. I would definitely return for the food, but ask for a different server.
Tuna was good.  Filet was ok, needed zip sauce. Kids tenders were  good. Waiter was friendly but did go missing for a little bit but very friendly.
Amazing service, quick, and I am content with my belly being full with deliciousness  would love a stand like this every corner of Philly or in my backyard.  Either or
More of a bar than a restaurant. Food was OK. Service was meh. Crab cake appetizer is pictured- pretty good.

Every time I've come here, I've had to wait an addition hour for my dog to be seen. Doesn't matter if it's busy or empty, they will not see you on time. I understand a vet can't see you right on your appointment and it may take an additional 10-20 mins but they go far beyond that. Aside from that the majority of the stuff has an attitude problem, not very friendly.

Very nice and clean place, just needs better management.

Not pleased at all with the curb side service for 2 weeks in a roll I have called multiple times with no one answering the phone.  Called Smyrna location they always answer right away and will continue to get my service.

No backbone. You can't move tables for families to sit together? Since when did we become scared of our government and suffer for it?

Food was ok.
Help america find a backbone.

Sushi is good but they have the weirdest hours. Some nights they just unexpectedly close early. I have been there three times when they decided to close early and were no longer seating anyone. Kinda frustrating.

One of the worst experiences I've ever had. We were told we would get special prices on the rooms because we were on the American steamship Cruise Line. That was Lie number one, and the front desk clerk  refused  to tell us what the prices where we were supposed to get . Is she booked us for one day  with a river view  and said that we have to move to a different room the next day .  We said we didn't want to  pay the extra $35 to stay in the same room  and  she then tells us  that the less expensive room  is the exact same price  as what we're paying for the better room. To top it off we had to ask her to repeat everything multiple times because she never spoke loud enough that we could hear over the background noise.
 We leave the room at 10 a.m. in the morning and come back at 4 and the room still hasn't been serviced. At the price they're getting for these rooms the service should be a hell of a lot better than it is. My wife went to the concierge desk for some help and before she could even start talking the concierge says let me interrupt you first and then talk to us in a condescending voice about what we have to do since we're on the cruise. Overall one of the worst experiences I've had in a hotel and based on this it's most likely I'll never stay at a Hilton again.

Super cute bar/restaurant right near my AirBnB that some of the Philly-area Murderinos met up at before the show in January. The staff, apparently unsuspecting of the 100ish people about to show up, were friendly enough and got our drinks to us in record time. I wasn't super happy with the hush puppies or the chips (wayyyyy too much Old Bay) but the drink options were plentiful, varied, and affordable. The taproom itself is dark and sets an intimate mood. I don't know if I'd return, but we liked it for the event.
Wow! This is my first time here and I'm blown away with how good this food is. I had the veggie and tofu pho. I can't wait to come back and try more delicious food.

Went on a Sunday afternoon & the place was empty. Atmosphere is nice. Nice outside seating. Food was ok. I had the chicken club which was very bland, nothing like the description in menu. Husband had a burger and Mac and cheese. He loved the Mac & cheese. Service was not good at all. Thinking maybe she was new. We had a 1.5 year old and try to get in & out. Waited forever to place our order and even longer to get the check. So long I had to take the baby to the car because he was getting cranky. Def wouldn't go back for dinner but maybe for drinks & apps.

Wow. We just asked the server if she could bring us drinks. Direct quote "I'm sorry, I don't have time. I have 5 people waiting at the bar". I get it - you are busy - but you don't EVER tell a customer you don't have time for them. Especially when you just brought drinks to the table next to us. #worstserviceever #wontcomeback

This was my first time to Philadelphia, and of course had to try a cheesesteak sandwich.  I thought the food was excellent and the prices very reasonable.  Although it was hectic when we first arrived, everyone was working hard to fill all the orders and did so very professionally.

Thanks to Carmen personally for being so nice to talk to (and the souvenir picture with him) and his staff for the great customer service when we asked for the remainder of one of our sandwiches to be rapped to go.
I love the big rink, friendly knowledgeable and always helpful staff.. Great place to skate

The service was awesome, however we sat in the bar area in a booth and could see the open line kitchen.  A waitress walked by the prepared dishes and grabbed a French fri off one of the prepares dishes- so gross.

Parked my vehicle there for my upcoming trip and the customer service was excellent. Very quick, friendly and professional. Definitely feel comfortable with my vehicle parked there and will use their services again in the future. Definitely recommend for anyone traveling.

I am new to town and stopped in here for lunch since it is near my work. What a lovely, eclectic place. It has such a modern and clean vibe to it, not a romantic place but a great place for lunch and to hang out with coworkers.

I had the grilled cheese sammie and I much say that it was amazing, perfectly cooked cripsy on the outside and gooey warm on the inside.

Gotta love the mural on the inside to, graphically clean. crisp and captivating stark in it's design.

They were great when I visited to get some snorkeling gear but the other two times I tried to come they were closed 15 mins early, came back the next morning 25 mins after opening and there was a note saying "be right back".  My suggestion is to call first to make sure you aren't interfering with their personal schedules.

I was really looking forward to bodega. The reviews were so positive! Well my trip was a mixed bag. I had the tofu plate and it was amazing. I would heartily recommend it for any vegetarians. But I got a tempeh sandwich to go and it was terrible. The tempeh was tough and flavorless. The slaw had no pizazz.  If I'd known I might have ordered tofu on the sandwich. Be warned! Order the tofu plate! Ambiance at the restaurant is great, tables on the sidewalk, public art and good people watching.

WORST BRUNCH EVER! Visited this place for an event during brunch and the food and most of the waitstaff were a disaster. They were pushing and cursing at the party goers. Although the event was probably at the building capacity limit the staff wasn't able to handle the sheer volume for a private event. 

The food was disgusting, ordered the avocado toast which came with a salad and an unknown white spread. After trying it the avocado toast couldn't have been real and that mystery white spread was as actually fish (surprise!). I returned it after two bites and was never refunded although I was told I was. We FINALLY got a decent server but he came so close to the end of the service we weren't sure he would get the tip. Overall WORST food ever!

I want to still like this place, I really do. But it's gone rapidly downhill the past few years and seems to only get worse. I took my car to get vacuumed and cleaned (gold level) this week and they did such a half-assed job. Dirt was still on the inside of the doors ( I wiped it off easily with a towel, which tells me they didn't even bother to wipe down the doors), I had a huge sap mark still on my windshield, and my trunk was still full of sand! I don't think they even bothered to vacuum my trunk at all. I honestly don't think they care about their job at all. Waste of time and money. Such a bummer, as this place has been my go-to since I got my first car many many years ago!

I'm giving two stars instead of one because the guy directing traffic was working really hard and the girl at the counter was very nice.

We were walking with our stroller, our 2 year old was asleep so we decided to stop in for a meal. My wife went in and asked the manager if it would be ok for us to come in with the stroller. He was very nice, said it would be no problem and even helped us in. He brought us to a table and showed us where to park the stroller so we wouldn't be in anyone's way. As we were reading the menu the hostess caught sight of us, stared at the stroller and at my wife then stormed off to the back of the restaurant. She returned in a minute and very rudely (and loudly) instructed us to move the stroller so we wouldn't be in the way. My wife told her that the manager had sat us down and told us where to put the stroller and we would prefer not to move and risk waking up our child. She again, rudely and loudly, told us we had to move as the stroller was "a hazard". With this we got up, said thank you, and told her we would dine somewhere else. No apology, just "OK, goodbye." It's a shame, food looked good but needless to say we won't be returning.

I have never had such a poor level of customer service from any groupon partner as I experienced from this company.  I left five very nice and kind voicemail messages during this past week asking for a returned call so I could schedule a very nice day of horseback riding with my young daughter on her birthday because she was off from school.  This company never made even one attempt to answer their business phone or return any of my five calls.  How rude is that?  If they didn't want my business, weren't going to be open, not doing trail rides that day, didn't want to accept a groupon certificate or any other reason, all they had to do was return my call.  Wqas that too much to expect

I concur with the other 1 star reviews. They've been out of fries before and were unapologetic. They didn't bother to tell me until I waited for 10 minutes in the drive thru.Terrible Customer Service. When I then walked in to see what was going on after hearing people shouting about my order the employees are having a party while ignoring the customers. When I spoke to the manager I got the sense he was going to speak negatively about me to his employees when I left. Zero resolve or concern. He said he was yelling to them about my order taking so long, but it came across as aggressive either way. Tighten up.

This place has been around for years and they are consistent.  I like their salsa and pico de gallo that they serve with the chips.  For a late Tuesday night their service isn't bad at all.  When we were there at around 9PM it appeared that the bar was a good place to get drunk and loud as well.  The seating is comfortable in the booths.  The taco salad is presented very artistically and is delicious, so I recommend it.  I personally like their taco menu - you can mix and match tacos and traditional tacos with all kinds of different meat - good stuff!  I think my only gripe about this place is that they load their rice and beans with way too much salt.  Murrieta's, please reduce the salt in your rice and beans.  Thanks!

So I have to say it's not as amazing as everyone says. Yes, they have a great bottled beer selection but their tap beer selection isn't amazing. I did enjoy a few beers I wouldn't have tried otherwise but I wasn't horrible impressed. I was amused to find out that a SEPTA bus crashed into it only in August which explained the rather unfinished look to the hallway leading into the restaurant. I might go back for a beer but I don't think I'd eat here since I wasn't digging the atmosphere.

The smash burgers here was so burnt... it made me want to cry. I loved the chicken and waffles. Please be careful going here if you are hungry... Stay away from the burgers... You have been warn! Other than that the host was nice and so was the girl that I guess was in training

We were visiting Reno for a few days and wanted something other than casino food. On a recommendation from our hotel, we decided to give Sabrina's a try. We were thrilled with the quality of the food and the service. We liked it so much we went back the next night. This restaurant is must try!
There was a lot of hype and I was pretty disappointed. It was just ok. I won't be back. The Loveless has much better biscuits and they aren't $4 a piece
Excellent Mexican food. Our waiter, Henry was outstanding, quick, and polite. I recommend the chicken poblano and the watermelon jalapeño margarita. One of my favorite restaurants in town.

It's the cheapest place to get alcohol in Deleware. It gets two stars knocked off for the fact that you never know what kind of mood the employee's will be in the day you go. I've received stellar service one day and absolutley rude service the next.  The other star gets knocked off for the horrible and I mean HORRIBLE parking lot. It's a constant accident waiting to happen.
I like the food, and it was good, but the counter for drinks was sort of wet and dirty, and they didn't have the bread my dad wanted and the soup my mom wanted

Yelp has instructed me to write a review of Dunkin Donuts. Mmkay. Well. Dunkin Donuts has donuts and like coffee and breakfast sandwiches and stuff. It doesn't taste better or even worse than McDonald's, just different. Except for donuts, because McDonald's doesn't have donuts! You can eat here and get fat easily because the flavors/textures won't slow you down too much, and the coffee doesn't have that crAaaAAazy Intelligentsia caffeine that will stifle your appetite and make you enjoy lowly retail underemployment more than you ever ever should. So if that is your MO then you are at the right spot! Also if you use Yelp for sensory guidance you probably won't be swayed either way by this so it doesn't matter what I akljaejpaoiwj;alskdfjas asldkf;adklfa sdfasdf,

What a huge disappointment. The decor is beautiful but the food is so underwhelming that I find it hard to believe the prices they charge. 
The ceviche Classico was ok but way to many onions that overwhelmed the dish. I like onions but I want to able to taste the other ingredients. Causa. Con pulpa de cangrejo was just ok. Not a very flavorful dish.
We were told the Camarones A Ajillo was one of their best dishes. I'd say it was average at best.  The Arros Chaufa DE. Camarones was absolutely uneatable. The fish was like rubber. The sauce was tasteless. And once again, too many onions.
I have been back several times and am still in love. The food is consistently delicious and well made. Service is better each time I go. Highly recommend this place to all!

I used to come here all the time for tires but the place has changed in a negative way. I came to get a run flat tire repaired or replaced if needed. Well this guy told me that the state of MO doesn't allow these to be repaired and it has to be replaced (odd b/c Dobbs repairs them) which was the first red flag to avoid. Then he started saying oh depending on treadwear on the others they may have to replace all 4....like wait what? I've never heard that before, ever and decided to never come here again. Go elsewhere, avoid here.

The only difference between Old Eagle Tavern and that sports bar in Wichita, Kansas that I went to is that the Old Eagle Tavern is in Roxborough...

...and you can get a 6-pack.

...and risk getting your car towed because there's no parking. Anywhere.

So unless you love the Philly's so damn much that you'd be willing to pay more for your Miller Light and risk a parking fine that would cost as much as your airline ticket to that sports bar in Wichita, I suggest you'd head elsewhere.

I'm not sure, particularly in Philadelphia where there are so many better choices, how this place gets more than 2 stars. Ordered a cheesesteak with whiz and onions; cheese is on first, which doesn't allow anywhere for all the grease from the meat to go, except to pool on top then drip all over everything. Minimal meat, not great quality, and pretty much would go anywhere else for a better cheesesteak.

Found them on yelp when on our way to other coast, was in a rush but did not want a drive in so we dropped in here and will be back to try lunch next time , had breakfast and was good but lunch will be a better judge of the food,place is very clean and The decor is set up as a New York St., very clever.

We placed our food order for pickup at 6:40pm through Uber Eats. The app said it would be ready at 7:35pm, so we drove to restaurant and arrived at that time. Upon arriving, the hostess told me that it wasn't ready, even though they had sent a notification that it was and the app closed the ticket. She told me to just "go wait in my car" with no specification of timing. So I waited in my car for 15 minutes and then went back inside. By 8pm, the order was still not complete. She told me "they were working on it". So I sat patiently, 8:10, 8:20, still nothing. Then my wife finally came inside after sitting in the car for 45 minutes wondering what was going on. They finally brought out our order at 8:25 pm. So it took nearly 2 hours to pickup a couple of hand rolls, seaweed salad, and two long rolls. The food was ok, but not at all worth the hassle and inconvenience. Also, this place doesn't appear to be encouraging social distancing inside. I would not recommend at all.
Wonderful hotel & the best complimentary breakfast I've ever had. Thank you for treating us great!

First time on bourbon street and we decided to stop here to eat and use the restroom. I recommend the alligator po boy and red beans and rice. I do not eat seafood so We had the alligator po boy and the hot sausage po boy. The alligator po boy was delicious! The meat was well seasoned and in fried nugget form. I was not a fan of the sausage po boy. I expected it to be links but it came out in thin patties and it gave me really bad gas and indigestion. I also had a margarita that was made well. And I had a side order of red beans and rice, which was delicious and well seasoned!

Their meat is soooo good. But I'm not sure it justified my FOURTY-FIVE minutr wait in line. I can eat faster at a sit down restaurant  and the line was not out the door. I seriously don't get how it took that long.

We love hanging out at Miss Vicki's on the weekends. They have a beautiful view and great entertainment. A fun local secret to bring friends when they come to town! Seating is limited, so we have to go early to get parking and a table.

Delicious spot for the vegan foodie. Exquisite secret sauce and falafel. 

The staff could have been more nice. 

Other than that, happy to have gone here.
Wow the older gentlemen who may be the owner is awesome.  They got my order wrong but he corrected it no prob.  Meat quality delicious.   And the food is dope.

Strong selection of beers and one of the best burgers you'll ever have. Enjoyed the atmosphere, the great staff and wonderful, inspiring backstory. Kudos to Mark Hinkle and the team. Well done. Webster Groves is lucky to have O+O here!

Waited ten minutes for electrical guy to help me find a specially bulb. While waiting I asked customer service for help and they obviously had no interest in helping so I asked them to call electrical guy again. He finally came, but before finding out that the bulb I found was the right one, he gave me two  excuses why what I wanted was "rare" and "didn't exist"...

Tony Lukes has a fairly good cheesesteak, and I usually stop here for one when driving home from the airport.  Their roast pork with brocoli rabe is also very good.  I was born a raised in Philly, and never had cheese wiz on a  cheesesteak, most of the local places used american or provolone.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS. This place is a joke. Tried to eat here on Memorial Day, which I understand is a holiday, but service, food and environment were all horrible. Server forgot our order and we waited over an hour for food that at best was edible. Had to send back onion rings because they were halfway cooked. I usually try to be positive on Yelp but this place was one of the worst dining experiences I've had in a long time. Also, flies were all over this place and kept landing on us while we were sitting in side.

Why I will never return to the Sage Diner in Mount Laurel, NJ:

While having breakfast this morning, a friend stopped by to show me a proof of a marketing piece. He told the waiter he would not be eating and would simply like a glass of iced tea. The waiter politely informed him there was a $2.50 minimum for him to sit in the booth whether he purchased anything or not. My friend and I were both... embarassed and humiliated ... and instead of causing a stir ... he simply left.

When checking out, I "attempted" to explain to the cashier what had happened and why I would never return. She was determined to justify the policy ... which I could literally care less why they had such a policy ... and simply wanted to explain why I would never return ... and let everyone I know why I would never return.

So ... if you ever go to Sage Diner ... understand there is a $2.50 minimum PER PERSON, whether you order anything or not, if you choose to sit in one of their booths.

I understand this is their business and their right to have any policy they choose. It is also my right to refuse to enter their business again.
Extremely long wait, service was okay and food didn't meet my expectations. Doubt I ever go back.

Let me start by saying that my husband and I loved The Garden on Grand, but something has changed lately. Normally I would give this place top marks, but the recent changes have made me suspect the place. 

The change in hours is one thing--no more weekend breakfast. But gone are the friendly guys we were welcomed by during our first five visits before. The two times I've been there recently, they weren't open but the front door was standing open. The music has changed and flashing disco lights have been added. 

I will probably give them one more chance, but the Garden feels like a different place these days.

Looking for an ice cream. .. daughter needed restroom. . We could not use it ... not for public use... my daughter is 7.  Did not stay for ice cream.   Can't wash our hands ..  can't eat ..  I thought all resturants had to have restroom. .

Come here for something different! I like the Brazilian twist of their dishes. I ordered the grilled chicken with rice and beans. It came with fried banana, which was effectively a mini dessert! The bottomless iced tea is a nice option, the interior and live music held true to the "arts" in the name, and the smoothies looked like they'd be good too.

I need to dock one star because their Axxess deal, which convinced us to choose this restaurant, is poor and misleading as it is advertised as a "buy one, get one," but it only works for up to $10 on mains, nearly all of which cost well more than that. I think that the prices are a little high. The service could also be a little better.
Please share a great comedian that we seen but we had to see the jerks before he came on this was still worth it thank you goodbye

I had lunch here recently, and this sits squarely at 3.5 stars.  The front patio is great, right on St Charles and close to other neighborhood attractions.  The inside bar is nice as well.  There is a small, but good beer selection on tap.  Several NOLA offerings, always good.

The service is friendly, if a bit slow.  Though not a problem.  The food ranges from small plates and po boys to fillets.  I had the Shrimp and pasta plate.  Good flavor, not to garlicy, with some kick from the peppers.  The angle hair pasta was cooked well.  All in all a good lunch.  A place I will visit again if I get a chance, maybe for happy hour and try some more of the menu.

This food truck place has the cutest set up ever and was very much worth of a trip to get some drinks. I got the Return of the King, Mango Tango and Sticky Sticky Mango. Return of the King has a slight durian taste but it wasn't overwhelming however the smell is still there so try not to get this if you're going to talk to somebody later lol. Overall, it was a nice drink to try once in a while. The Mango Tango was EXTREMELY sweet and I literally thought I was drinking straight up sugar. Even though, it looked appealing, I would probably not get it again due to the extreme sugariness. The Sticky Sticky Mango does not look like the picture on the stand they have and it definitely was not worth the price it was. I was disappointed to see how measly the portion was and how hot it was. Since it's a dessert, I expected it to be cold but that's just my expectations.
Perfect until I went back for another at 7:25pm and they were closed. Yelp says they're open until 8! I go back to California tomorrow. I'm sooo sad!!
Absolutely delicious food. We enjoyed every bite. Definitely expensive but if you're looking for good, creative pizza and pasta, you can't go wrong.

The food and service was terrible today.  Place nearly empty, but took 45 minutes to get our food.  Sweet balsamic vinegar further ruined a bad Margherita Flatbread.   Husband's fish and chips was mostly breading.
Very judgmental and unhelpful. Don't bother if you're in pain because they think everyone is drug seeking. As a medical professional myself, I would not recommend MHM to anyone.

Was looking forward to trying but waited to be seated at the hostess stand for 5 mins, had three employees look at me and ignored us.

Left and went to Peggy's

I went to this place since everyone was writing about how amazing Alex is. I got a pedi and a mani from her. The pedi lasted me 3 days!!!! Just 3! And when she gave me a gel mani, she did not even cap the nails! And asked 8 dollars for french! What?!! It is a waste of money. Go to a normal salon! Never coming back again!

I truly enjoyed my stay here.  The location was in walking distance to many attractions.  Parking was a bit if a nightmare, but the friendly staff were very helpful in this regard.  The rooms had a modern feel but yet remained in keeping with the historical aesthetic.

I would like to come back to NOLA and would stay here again.

We stopped in after an air boat tour.  Everyone was friendly, service was quick and efficient.  Gumbo was good but a tad salty.  Shrimp and oyster po boys were really good!!!  Red beans and rice were good too.  Cheddar cheese jalapeño grits were very good.  The only drawback was the restaurant was a bit chilly.

Friday lunchtime at Christmas/Holiday time. I arrive and there is a podium like piece of furniture with menus on it. Waited and no one came so I waved to the one server I saw and he grabbed a menu and sat me.
One thing to ruin a meal is slow service. Another is your entree arriving lukewarm instead of hot. Both happened today. 
In busy Media during the holidays they had one server on handling about 10 tables, most with over 2 people per table so he did his best to keep up but as my entree was lukewarm it obviously had sat for awhile before being delivered. It also took over 10 minutes to get my check after I asked because he was busy seating people, taking and delivering orders. 
 Very disappointed in a restaurant that for years has been a Media staple but even though the entree was lukewarm it was good. I hope to return soon and have a better experience

This place is a joke. The staff never returns calls, the doctor asks you questions like they don't know their own profession, and you wait 30-45 min once you get taken back by the nurse. They tell you that you owe money and then a week later say never mind we messed up... Who is in charge of billing!? The receptionist must only work 15 minutes a day because the chance of her answering is about 3% on a good day. Please save your money and find a different office.
Beers were good, they had 10 of there own on tap. Enjoyed the Brown especially (hickory smoke was strong!) good outdoor seating with cornhole and other games. Buff Chicken pizza hit the spot.
It's just great, when you are lost, cold, and hungry. Truckee bagel is the place to go

First time with Ethiopian food, excellent option for vegetarians! Tasted familiar (lots of similar Indian spices) but different. Very nice way to make collards for sure, no bitterness and lots of tart flavors.

Well 5 months later i tried them again.  I ordered my lunch take out with no onions or garlic as i am alergic.  I get back to work and of course it was in there.  When i looked at the receipt to see if it was atleast written and perhaps just a chef mistake, but it was not.  So the woman owner who took my order chose to ignore my diet request and in turn i pay her and not eat lunch  if the owner does not care to do the right thing the most important part the order, then why would anybody want to patronize such a place.  There are many places in the city who care about their customers.

If only a 0 star rating was available this place would deserve it!

The price seemed reasonable until I saw how bad the product was I got for that price.

1:30 on a Sunday afternoon with less than 20 people in the restaurant and the pasta and pizza was empty on the buffet.  The few slices that were on the buffet were burned, flavorless and stiff.  Even the salad was bad- wilted lettuce, the past salad was just mush covered in oil.  

I won't be going back and if you're reading this you shouldn't either.

It's tasty but it's tourist tasty. They have good food but it's not mind blowing. I had the chicken fries and it was really good. The wait was 30 minutes but this may have been a little different because the cast of a tv show was there.

I brought my car here for an oil change. At my next oil change (at a different place) I learn the bolt that holds the oil pan is stripped and so worn its made the hole in the pan bigger. Instead of telling me about the stripped bolt, which was probably normal wear because my car is old, Sears put a janky metal coil and silicone putty around the bolt to hold it in place. I'll never come here again.

I would like to start by saying, I am not a beer drinking. That being said, the food and the atmosphere are fantastic. They actually have a beer sampler tray that I tried when I first visited to see if their craft beer could change my opinion on the whole beer genre, but alas, it is not for me. Luckily, they have a full bar and the drinks are decent. It is great to have the option to sit outside when the weather is nice. I have tried a good portion of the menu and have yet to discover anything below par. The pizza is outstanding. The wings are delicious. The fried shrimp are crazy good. 

If you are not a beer drinker and you are looking for a place to have a few drinks, then this might not be the place for you; however, if you are simply looking for some great food with friendly service, this is the place.

If you enjoy your coffee and a good coffee experience, this place deserves a visit.  The staff are great, each cup is freshly brewed and it has a great environment.  

This place is worth the drive.  Even for the die-hard city lovers like myself.

Second visit to the Parish this evening...and well worth it!    Our first visit was at lunch...which we enjoyed...and our dinner today measured up.   My wife had the Drunken Angel and said it was excellent...she'd give it a 5.   I had the mussels and they were very good.   We'd order both again!
Service was very good and atmosphere is very comfortable and lively.
The reason I didn't give it a 5 is that I've only been there twice.   I feel I'd need to have a couple more experiences at exceptional levels to confirm a 5.
We'll definitely be back...

7venth Sun is a great place to visit! They have a huge inside and outside area, great food trucks and they are even dog friendly! Only reason they don't get 5 stars for me is the beer quality. It is definitely better than most, but I think it could be better. Large selection none the less!

In today's fickle dining and drinking world, I am leading the charge in this one small example.  When Franklin hit the scene, it was heralded as a cut above others for its inventiveness and its staunch refusal to serve vodka.  

But on this day, Franklin's luster was tarnished by several missteps, and I fear that these missteps are indicative of a trend at this place.

I have complained in the past about the forced wait at the entrance.  Pretentious, unless it actually is crowded inside.  The doorman wants to create this impression that the bar is so hot that you have to wait in line.  Or is it because it takes 5 minutes to make each drink?  Hmm.  So tonight we start the line, we are the first in line at 7pm.  We are escorted in 15 minutes later to find four empty tables.  Strike one.

So it's not full, not real busy, and the waitress gives us our water and menus.  Comes back 3 minutes later to take order.  Then we wait.  And wait. 15 minutes after we sit down we get our drinks.  30 minutes from arriving at the bar to the time we get our drinks.  Strike two.

All three of us have been to Franklin in the past and loved it.  I was here about a month ago, and they have changed their drink menu since then.

The Death on Two Legs sounded cool, especially the allspice dram.  The drink was imbalanced, too sweet, too much maraschino, not enough allspice.  It tasted like a bad Manhattan.

The Sinister Side tasted like someone poured random things in a shaker and mixed them together.  It tasted like when the person cleaning up after a big party walks around at the end and collects all the half-empty glasses and pours them into one pitcher, and then the really wasted dude who hasn't left yet decides to drink what's in that pitcher.  Yeah, it was bad.

The third drink I can't remember, it was passable but my friend didn't like it.  Strike three.

Hey Franklin, other bars making sexy beverages have caught up to you and passed you. It seems as if Franklin, in its desperation to be at the front of the curve, is running out of good recipes.  

For example, people are experimenting with stuff like milk fermented in wine casks.  Now THAT is cutting edge.
the food is still good. menu getting old but why is the service gone to shit..? the staff stands around and looks right through your or doesn't acknowledge you at all.

I really don't like to write negative reviews, but after many years of loving the in-town Mi Lah, this latest incarnation is a poor shadow to us and not worth the drive when there are such better options. We went for brunch this past Sunday, hoping to enjoy our favorite vegan omelettes that we ALWAYS got at the old Mi Lah. When they moved, I remember seeing that they were going to start using eggs, but that you could still request the vegan omelette. Unfortunately, that is not the case. Not only did the waiter tell us it was not available, he simply could not have cared less. His demeanor was sullen and very unapologetic. My husband was so upset, that he actually told him that we were long-time customers and that the vegan omelettes were the reason why we came to Mi Lah and we would not be returning. One would thing if someone said something like that to a server, they would apologize, get the manager to come talk to us... nothing!!! We were disappointed and disheartened. It was great while it lasted.

Nice wedge salad- wish there were blue cheese crumbles though. Huge grilled cheese sandwiches that are ridiculously delicious! It's a food order to be shared. Service was a little slower probably due to the cashier, waiter, and chef were 1 person. That being said: when he becomes the chef he might want to rethink not wearing gloves while food prepping. Especially since the kitchen is open to patrons eyes. Beer will have to be tested at another date.

Pi Indy is hands down the best pizza that I've had in the Indianapolis Area. They feature so many unique combinations. My personal favorite is the Pineapple Express. And guess what? It doesn't have marinara. They have options even on the sauces. A regular size specialty pizza is roughly $12. The crust is hand tossed but closer to the thin side. They toss it into the wood-fire oven and soon your toasty but still soft pizza is on it's way to you. 

They had an issue with the online ordering not going through to the printer this last time I went in, but they quickly got my order into the oven and brought it out to me as I waited at a table. They have always had great service whenever I have stopped by, which is at least twice a month.

I have so many great things to say regarding our experience here that I am simply going to list them:

-We love the landscaping. It's tropical and yields a resort style feel.
-The leasing office and internet cafe is beautifully decorated. We were impressed. Very contemporary, trendy, and clean feeling.
-Office staff is very friendly. Not only were they professional in their dealings with us, but they were dedicated. They didn't make promises they couldn't keep. They were forthcoming and also mindful of our requests.
-We were initially concerned that our apartment wouldn't be as nice as the model. We were wrong. We requested an end, corner unit and they were able to accommodate us. It's just as gorgeous as the model.
-The light fixtures and ceiling fans were a big plus for us. They're sleek and classy in feel, quality in function.
-The bathroom is split so the toilet and shower are separated with a door from the vanity style sink/counter. The lighting is great.
-The bedroom is surprisingly large (we have a king) and that's not including the spacious, walk-in closet. Also, another thing we've been appreciating are the full-slide hanger rods that enable you to slide clothes across them without getting caught on any dividing pieces of plastic!
-Our kitchen is very modern and came with new appliances and gorgeous granite-looking counter tops. No icemaker or garage disposal, but there is a dishwasher!
-The layout feels like that of a home, in my opinion, as opposed to an apartment. And we are only in a one bedroom!
-Our back patio sectionals were made with new wood. And the light automatically comes on, as does everyone's in the evening, providing a feeling of safety and awareness as to what's going on outside our "home." 
-We received our first community newsletter that was kindly written and included a calendar of current community events, informative info (ie parking repairs coming up) and even a recipe of the month. 
-They really provide value here to you. The gym is updated. Equipment is new. It's inviting. It's clean. I *want* to workout there, and do! There are 3 pools on the property that are clean, and decorated nicely. Furniture is all new-looking and fully matching. We love it here!

In summary, I would absolutely recommend this community and these apartments to move in to. Despite other reviews, I can tell they really care here. I pay attention to detail, I am a photographer by trade. It's picturesque here, and as my mom and grandma noted, "It feels like a resort or spa." It's relaxing, clean and a place I am proud to invite my friends/family over to, hang out at and, most importantly, live in. As a small business owner, I know that testimonials & reviews can make or break ya and I hope this heartfelt summary of how I feel is well received. Cheers :)

brought friends from outta town, i use to eat there 8 years ago.  i was told then try the  blackened grouper sandwich,  i brought my friends there  present day and ordered the same.  wow fantastic still.  i moved away 40 miles, so sorry i dont get the chance more often.  my friends loved it and still talk about that great meal!

The food is okay and only acceptable on Wednesdays (half priced burgers). Sometimes it makes me sick, not gonna lie (especially if I try to work out within 24 hours of eating it).
Try to eat outside if its nice. Sometimes if its busy you get shoved into the basement with no windows and the servers forget you're down there so you pretty much run out of water. 
I've had the nachos, they were pretty average. The buffalo burger was pretty good though.

Since they moved to the new location, the service is subpar for sure. Granted, they only have been open at the Glendale location a couple weeks, but the still have not got it figured out. Very slow service, cold food etc. The new location is nice, just give them a month or two to learn how to run a new, busy restaurant.

Overrated dump.   Prices are ridiculous for a Dive bar and the crowd is so cliquey you will feel like a shadow.      I go there a few times a month to see friends but hate every second there.   Plenty of more friendly, entertaining spots in town.

The dining area is in the back, so there is more seating than the stools. We didn't know and ordered our pizza togo, then found the bar to wait on it. It was pretty busy for 11pm. Nice decor, nice vibe. We had some AMAZING garlic knots at the bar, along with what is the best hard cider on tap that I've ever had. I think it was called Bold Rock. Pizza was a little soggy when we got to it, the crust honestly tasted a little undercooked. We got an Old World and loved everything but the sausage we added to it. The fresh basil had the perfect amount of spice to it, it is put on the side, not baked in. It was definitely not good the next morning (as pizza usually is.) A pretty mediocre but not terrible pizza experience, but I'd probably give something else a try next time.

Five of us dined at Bernie's this evening. The food was fine. The service was slow and inattentive. The drinks took forever to arrive. The food was delivered but there were no flatware settings. We had to go to the hostess to get silverware. The bill was delivered but, again, took forever to collect and return. The place seems to be missing management and efficient processes.

I have to say - I drive OUT OF MY WAY to come here.  I see some of these negative comments below and I can't believe what people are saying.  The only negative I can say about this place is someone bought ALL the everything bagels this morning!  I was not happy!

This place has always been great and their bagels are AMAZING.  It takes a long minute to get a sandwich but they are MADE TO ORDER.  If you expect something to be fresh, you have to WAIT.  

Impatience and pickiness doesn't warrant a poor review.  I am a bagel expert and I'm telling you, this place is AWESOME.

When in a pinch and not in the mood or don't have the time to run to ShopRite, Murphy's Fresh Markets in Medford delivers. Although at times their prices are higher, there are products that Murphy's carries that I cannot find at the local Medford ShopRite. I always like to support local stores or smaller local chains and find I am there more often than not.

The only negative is I wish they would keep their herbs stocked more. I cannot rely on Murphys for herbs and fresh herbs to purchase and am stuck with going to ShopRite for my fresh herbs. 

Otherwise, I am happy that Murphys is located between Medford Lakes and Medford as I am always stopping there 2-3 times per week.

I regularly visit this Jack in the Box store & I have to say that I am absolutely impressed each and every time. I have only used the drive thru. Every time I pull up to the speaker, the worker greets me in a really nice manner, not over the top animated where you feel like they are faking it. They seem a bit genuine. 
The drive thru is usually pretty busy, but the employees are always moving fast to make sure you aren't stuck in the drive thru for days. 
Each and every time I have visited this JITB, my food has been fresh, hot, & cooked according to my specifications.

Meh. 

I've been wanting to try this place for awhile, given its positive reviews. I would be hard-pressed to return. Paid a visit during lunch hour - The place was almost empty, but was stifling hot inside (not something you want when you're going into an establishment that serves raw fish). It also smelled odd inside (not a "fresh fish" smell, either). There were also several flies buzzing around the restaurant - Definitely a deterrent when the food is all sitting out and exposed behind the counter.

Since it was my first visit, I ordered one of their signature bowls (spicy salmon), and I didn't want to inconvenience the workers by standing there trying to pick out a combination of ingredients. I should've just made my own, because the worker who made my order was clearly brand new, and knew even less about what went into the spicy salmon bowl than I did. He kept referring to lists that are kept behind the counter, and asking one of the other workers, who seemed to think I was getting a spicy tuna bowl, and kept telling him the wrong things to put in it. I think I ended up with a weird combination of both bowls. 

The spicy salmon that the worker scooped out of a container was absolutely smothered and dripping in spicy mayo - Not at all like the pictures on their menu boards. If I'm getting poke, I want something light and fresh - Not dripping in mayo. Not to mention the worker squeezed even more spicy mayo on top of the bowl before I could say anything.

It was only after the bowl was assembled (base and fish), that the person making my order told me they were out of mango. It's a major component of many of the bowls - It would have been appreciated to have been told this when I placed my order. Not to sound snobby, but given my experience thus far, I almost walked out at this point. But I didn't want to be wasteful, since the fish was already in the bowl. So I soldiered on.

I got zucchini noodles as a base (which is awesome - so nice to have a choice aside from rice or lettuce), but they weren't properly drained and I couldn't finish the bottom part of my bowl because it was sopping wet with water.

Also if you're eating in, be aware that they only have four drink options in a small drink cooler - One of which is a specialty soda I've never heard of, and the other of which is a twee bottle of Pellegrino for $2.50+Tax.

Overall - Meh. There are plenty of other better options out there in the city that I would happily drop $15+ on before coming back to this spot.

Pizza options, price, service, beer selection and decor/vibe were very good.  However, pizza had very little sauce and the crust was too doughy. Mid-range for pizza in NOLA; would not make it in Chicago.

EDIT - while I still like Sidecar, the strength of the Philly food and beer competition has really ramped up and I just don't feel like Sidecar is as good in comparison. I mean, a block away is Resurrection, which I think is a much better bar and has better food.... But I do still like Sidecar, just not 4 stars worth.

Sidecar is a really fun place to grab a meal, a beer (several beers), or both. All the food that I have had here has been very good, and the beer selection is a quite broad. Prices can be a bit high, but they have a lot of good deals. I recommend going on a Sunday night - if my memory serves me they have half off all pork dishes and a pretty decent beer deal? 

All in all, it's a reliably good time. It looks like it's in the middle of nowhere (even though it isn't...), which actually seems to be a good thing.... It is just far enough out of Center City that it's not a place people tend to wander into. You hear from someone that Sidecar is great, so you venture down there to check it out. Then you do it again and again, because you love it. If you haven't been, I highly recommend it. The Beer selection is similar to the relatively close Ten Stone, but the food is far superior, and the ambiance is decidedly less I'm-still-wishing-I-was-in-undergrad! 

Put Sidecar on your to-visit list.

Food is ok but service is Sooo Slow.
Took way too long for food to come out of kitchen

Probably the worst Walmart in the Bay Area.  Dont even get me started on the mean employees in the pharmacy.  The Deli that is stuck in slow motion...if the fryers caught on fire they surely wouldn't make it out in time.  NEVER anyone in Electronics, home, auto.  One time actually had to call the main number from my cell phone to get someone to call someone to come to the paint department after waiting 15 minutes.  Never have propane or anyone willing to go out and get it.  Customer service desk in understaffed and always long lines...God forbid someone has to send a Moneygram.  Go to the one on waters, the neighborhood store on Gunn or go up to Dale Mabry...do yourself a favor drive by without stopping

Donna, the person at the checkout desk was very rude to me and my girlfriend. We spent about $100 on items, and when we go and checkout, she asked us about the cart we used to put out items in. I told her that I found it in the middle aisle, and I would gladly replace all the stuff that was on it which was like 3-4 items. She didn't even want to check out my girlfriend, so she went on another cashier which was way nicer. I never experienced something like that at any automotive shop.  Never going back if she is there. Auto one down the street are awesome and actually help you out and say hi when you walk through the door.
A nice place to try out.  The highlight is the table-side salsa.  The food is good, though I feel I can get similar food at other places a little cheaper.

Food was decent overall. There's a huge sign outside that advertises a $3.99 breakfast, so we were disappointed to see that breakfast was only served until 11 a.m. Not many people in there when we went. Service was ok, but I ended up getting something slightly different than I ordered, which also cost more. The waitress said I wouldn't be charged, however I was since she'd get in trouble for giving away free food it was posted on the receipt. And I was charged. The price didn't matter, however it rubbed me the wrong way that I had to pay for the restaurant's screwup. Probably wouldn't go back there as there are better restaurants in the area.

Food was edible but took 30 minutes (from order placed to table delivery)  for an order of pancakes and an egg/cheese bagel. Just a handful of other tables in the whole restaurant. Hey corporate Steak n Shake, read your reviews! It's your responsibility to fix your underperformers! I'm in the parking lot and want to warn anyone else going inside. Don't do it!

Last time going here! For several years, u was a regular. There were hiccups from time to time, but they were consistently good for the price. About a year ago, I started going to The Nail Bar down the street because it was a less hectic and more relaxing environment. Occasionally I would still pop in here if I needed something on the fly or I wanted to take my daughter (as TNB does not have children's rates). This morning I came in with my daughter. She was getting a mani/pedi and I was getting a pedi. They set us up in the back with the butterfly chair for her. They barely got her painted when they had her move because another family came in with a preteen that wanted that chair. (Nice seeing a thirteen year ok'd throwing a fit over my five year olds butterfly chair. BTW, they didn't sanitize or clean it in between.) The staff wanted my five year old to move to the drying station on the other side of the facility where I would not be able to see her. Nope! I asked if she could sit in the empty chair beside me....one of six empty ones in a row. I got lots of attitude back and was told she would have to move if anyone came in. The place was half empty and I could count fifteen empties from where I sat and there were more empties on the other side. "Ok, she'll move if you need the chair." Five minutes later, they wanted to move her again for a party of four. The party did not want to sit there and moved before they even sat down. My daughter stood next to my chair (not in the aisle or walkway) playing on my phone while they finished painting me. Again, here they come telling me she'll need to move to the other side of the building to dry. Again, nope. Still counting 14 visible open chairs including the six next to me, she's not in the aisle, she's quiet, standing still, playing a game on a silent phone so she's not bothering anyone or in the way. We quickly paid and left but as I mentioned, that's our last time there. If she had not received a service and was in the way or causing a disturbance to other guests, that's one thing. This was not that situation.

The place is known for the lobster roll but it lacked flavor in my opinion. Fries and onion rings seemed to have been cooked in old oil. The food and price isn't worth the wait, the dated environment, or the plastic utensils / plates. Really wanted to love it based on the feedback from others but it's average at best.

I told myself I'd only eat half the container...

20 minutes later, as I was scraping the last bits of rice and delicious curry sauce from the corners, I had zero regrets.

Tonight I swung by the Rosemont Farmer's Market for dinner when I was feeling especially indecisive. After a walk about scoping out the wares, I decided to finally try the Jerk Pit. Went with a regular-size combo of curry goat with rice & beans and plantains as my sides (per the suggestion of the very lovely and friendly woman behind the counter), plus a beef pastry that looked too flaky and tempting to pass up. 

Hot mama, it was all amazing! The goat was fall off the bone tender, the curry sauce was spicy and complex, and the portion was ample. Rice and beans packed a signature Jamaican allspice punch and the fried plantains were perfectly ripe and sweet. The beef pastry was also fantastic and I can't wait to go back and try more items. Don't skip this place.

Also, don't skip the Rosemont Farmer's Market. This place is a serious gem, with ample ready-made foods, meats, seafood, cheese, pastries, and almost anything else your heart could desire.

Very high quality stuff, but usually out of budget range, even for this devoted foodie.  Very source-conscious, which I appreciate.  Sandwich prep a little slow but friendly.  Good place to survey passers-by on Baltimore.

Let me start off by saying I came in here with a recommendation from someone for a shampoo Bar. I have my kids with me so I plan on being in and out. I entered and I was greeted and went about my way but went back up to saleswoman and asked for some assistance. Cassidy took me over to the area of what I was looking for and I told her a little bit about my situation of my hair I asked her what she recommended before telling her what was recommended to me and she explained the differences thoroughly she was patient and helpful and suggested honestly. After I chose what I wanted she recommended a sample of something that I can try and I really appreciated that as I did not want to just try something and absolutely not like it  
I went to pay and she made up my samples and on the way out The kid spotted a fun area of play fun which I never knew about its use for bathtime but can also use dry so we spent 10 minutes at the table playing with it and Taylor came over to introduce us to the product and was so kind as she listen to me talk about my experience with Cassidy and then I asked her about some skin care suggestions for my next time I visit at that point she went over and made me some samples and told me to try them so I know what I'm looking for for next time .The Energy both Cassidy and Taylor put into making sure that I had something that would fit my needs  and I never looked forward to taking a shower as much as I did tha lt night. I used my shampoo bar and my hair feels literally squeaky clean I haven't tried any sample products yet but either way my experience at the store and they're honest opinions and the time they took would make me go back I'm so glad I chose to go in there rather than order online thank you for giving me a great experience
My experience was great. I did the deluxe mani and pedicure and my nails feel and look great!

We live in the 'hood, on Annunciation Street and eat out frequently.
This is the most disappointing place I have tried in a while.  I watched them unwrap pre-cooked packing house roast beef for my po-boy, then douse it with canned gravy and serve it.  Now, I stood right there and watched them do this; not hear-say.  It is so; I WATCHED FROM UP CLOSE !
Fried oyster po-boy isn't much better.  This place is surrounded by lots of better po-boy shops.
To give the Devil his due; they do have cold, cold canned beer; which I MUCH PREFER to those damned beer bottles, for many reasons.  It is hard to find a stop with canned beer around here.
Instead, try (The Original) Parasol's on Constance @ Third Streets; much, much better food.

I came here a couple times for a special catered dinner talk in the back room.  They usually gave us a small wine flight.  I guess they've opened it as a full restaurant now.
Also came a few times to buy wine and they have been helpful.  Alternately, you can try the wine merchant.

We have been regulars at Le Bus for years, and couldn't wait to try this location.   We were among the first customers to arrive for brunch today at 9:15.  When we walked in, the hostess asked us if we had reservations. RESERVATIONS???  I didn't think ANY brunch spots took reservations.  We were accustomed to really long waits at the popular hipster spots, which is why we arrived so early this morning. Anyway.... this was good to know.

I ordered the stuffed french toast rolls and my fiance' ordered the vegan pancakes.  He loved his pancakes, and I thought my french toast rolls were ok.  I think I would prefer regular french toast to the "rolls", but they weren't bad.   

Anyway... I love the decor at this Le Bus. It was pretty cozy. Loved the ambience, as well. I hate a noisy restaurant first thing in the morning, and none of the patrons were being overly loud, as the place filled up.

Service was good, just like the other Le Bus location.

We came here for dinner last night. The appetizer was crab lettuce wraps. We only received two. They were good but why not give us three? I ordered a lettuce wrapped California burger with a California salad. The salad was great but I wasn't impressed with the burger. The burger patty tasted dry and bland, so I didn't like it. The only parts of the burger I really liked were the bacon, cheese and avocado- the good fatty stuff. This was my first time ever eatingg to Milestone and I wouldn't mind coming back to at least try the brunch menu. But I'll never eat another California burger here again.
Great tacos! The flight of beer was delicious and the corn yum yum! The employees were very friendly and informative. We will definitely be back and highly recommend.

It was just okay. We were looking for something different after nearly a week of cajun and seafood. Definitely overpriced $12 salad. The dressing was delicious but it was the size of a little side salad.  Lasagna was served in a tiny cast iron pot.  I found it odd, as well as extremely light on noodles and absent of the meat sauce the menu promised.

$21.00 for steak platter. What a waste of my money. Main dishes were bland. Steak was chewy. Was a good restaurant at one time. Food trucks produce better food then this place. I strongly recommend to eat somewhere else.

I had a wonderful experience with Maid to Glow! They helped me with a last minute clean due to another  scheduled clean being cancelled! I would definitely hire them again and most likely will. I got the 30 minute call letting me know she was on the way and she did a very thorough clean and was extremely personable! I highly recommend this company and will be a customer again!! Also can't beat their prices!
Boring. No true history. Boring. Did the city/ Katrina tour.  Never again.

We booked a 2 hour city tour with VIP.  The driver was friendly and informative and even worked in personal stories into his tour. Overall an interesting tour especially the Katrina and cemetery portions.  We took the 4pm, but this time of year it gets dark so its hard to see the spots at the end of the tour.  So keep that in mind when choosing your time.  It's a great way to get an overview of the city and hear some history behind it, you even get to see a couple of celebrity homes.

Honestly, it's a shame that we don't have a better yarn shop on the NW side. LYS are supposed to be friendly and inviting, but the customer service at this place is sorely lacking. Usually it seems to be that the staff are extremely friendly to... their friends, and anyone who buys a ton of overpriced yarn. As I usually come in to get one or two skeins, I'm not in their clique by any means. Would be nice to have a reasonable room temperature in there - as they also charge for water, it seems nasty to keep the shop so hot. 
I hate going there and will only do so when I can't get what I need anywhere else. As soon as I walk in, there's a chill if the owners/staff don't know you, like you walked into their living room. Not a great way to run a business.

I have to say that I went there with a little bit hype, but although I was a little bit drunk I don't really enjoy the food haha.

The service was amazing instead, and we had a great time. The pecan waffle was disappointment but the biscuit and gravy wasn't that bad.

Definitely a place you have to try at least once!

Located inside a casino, you expect the quality to be a bit worse, and the price to be a bit higher.  However, I am still rating this establishment compared to what it should be.  This restaurant is your standard asian restaurant, but everything is considerably marked up.  In addition, while the food is usually tasty, all the dishes are extremely salty.  Service is adequate.

I've been to this place since it opened and never reviewed it but if I had I would have given it 5 stars. I went tonight and the experience was just horrible. The waitress guided us to our table without even a hint of a smile, visibly annoyed by her job. Another waitress (that one at least smiled) brought us warm water ... supposedly what used to be the tea (it used to be amazing). When the food arrived it was tasteless. The rolls looked and tasted terrible (the tuna looked like left over pieces, the low quality smoked salmon was uber salty and the cream cheese was not cold). The soft shell crabs were not properly cleaned. The pad Thai had the most horrible unappetizing smell and was completely bland and piled in the plate without any effort of making it look plated. My date only ate half of it. They never asked us if we wanted dessert and just brought the check. What happened to this place? I never had a bad dinner there before. I used to go weekly but had not gone for a couple months as I had a busy summer. It seems that their succès finally got to their head and they are not willing to make any effort to keep their clients happy and live on their good reviews. It's a shame ... I have to find another place. I cannot believe how this place went from 5 stars to this ... I'm so disappointed with the whole experience.

My wife and I recently traveled to New Orleans for Mardi Gras.  The first place we went after getting off the plane and the last place we went before returning was Kenner Seafood.  The food and service was great.  I'm going to miss the crawfish.

Don't waste your time!! I went to the drive-thru and ordered a whole Apple Pecan Salad however, I get home and I have a half salad and the apples are rotten and soft. I had to get back in my car and drive back to Wendy's. The counter person, Edward was the best part of this whole ordeal. He promptly took the salad with rotten apples and had another salad made fresh. He was polite and handled my issue well. The biggest problem is the fresh made salad also had ROTTEN APPLES (pictures). I will not waste any more of my hard earned money on WENDY'S at this location.

If ONLY I could give NO stars!! I'm EXTREMELY discussed and offended on the way my mother was treated by this funeral home. I was denied any information on arrangements that were started without me after clearly stated that I was legally named the "Executor" by my mother. My mother's creamation was delayed for a month and then given to me in a "ZIPLOC " bag. I was NEVER given the option of buying an Urn. This was disrespectful and insulting after tragically losing my mom to covid. I would NEVER recommend this funeral home or any affiliation to anyone!!!
