I have since hired another reputable,PROFESSIONAL funeral home to hold a "Celebration of life" service for my mother. She deserves to be honored and remembered the right way!

Large place, well located. My clothes came out very clean, though I wish there were lint traps in the dryers...I have a dog and still have fur on my clothes, even after an hour in the dryer! I'm happy with the experience overall, though, and will be back here.
Just excellent pizza. The selection extends beyond pizza which is nice. The Buffalo chicken pizza was simply fantastic and the bourbon wings were on point. I'd happily return!

Wow,

   Most horrible retail experience in 61 years Dropped lawn mower off for repair of a broken starter rope at Lentine mower center/langhorne and made the mistake of requesting a tuneup.  After a full month of waiting completion, i took mower home and listened to it backfire/smoke and stallout for the first time ever. Not wanting to lose the mower for another month during the growing season, i fought with the mower all summer until it finally died.  Receipt in hand, i spoke to the counter person who lectured me for not dropping it off sooner, which at this point was very clear to me. After 2 minutes of receiving his lecture and realizing he wasnt going to let me explain the mechanical problem, i knew this place had zero experience in customer satisfaction.  An hour later i got a voicemail the mower was done. I called and asked what the problem was... and i heard "CLICK". I arrived at the shop with a bad attitude, and it got worse when counter guy claimed he didnt hang up on me, the phone system disconnects people all the time.... ugh....   counter guy says the newly installed spark plug was defective(yeah right).  I was foolish enough to think they would start it up after the tuneup, if they had, they would have heard the backfire in seconds.  Now its time to go home when the owner come into the shop and yells who's the customer bitching?  Thats when my bad attitude got worse!  P.s.    lawnmower running like before now that the correct spark plug was installed.  Do you think i needed them to tell me to never come back

Working with Scott was the most surreal experience I've ever had with a contractor.  We asked for a simple design for 4 separate areas on our property and we were quite explicit.  We had done to-scale drawings of each area and put together a plant list.  He came back about 2 weeks later to show us his design.  There couldn't have been more of a disconnect.  Where we wanted 2 straight block wall fence sections, he designed a curved one.  Where we just wanted some stone and about half a dozen plants, he designed 8 flower and vegetable containers along with a host of other plants.  He had plans for areas well outside of our defined area.  His design didn't take into consideration existing plants that we wanted to save, and the plans looked very poorly conceived.  We have a modest house, but his cost estimate was over $50,000.  I was very encouraged reading all the great reviews this company got on Yelp, but that was not our experience at all.  Very disappointing.

So we were in the area and wanted to get something fairly quick.  Have not been to a BWW in forever but hey buffalo chicken that sounds good right?  Took a seat in the bar area mostly because I saw a waitress working around there.  Well she disappeared and no one came over for about ten minutes.  I seriously think this waitress was high, like she talked crazy fast and wouldn't look me in the eye.  Our order then proceeded to take forever to come out.  The wings were pretty good actually but I was practically starving by this point.  The real reason for the two starts though is that my wife and I both felt pretty badly after.  I wasn't able to be too far from a toilet the whole next day.

I've become a pizza snob over the years. I've studied and learned how to make amazing pizza and most importantly pizza dough. So, when we received a coupon for a free pizza "no strings attached" from a new pizza shop in the area, I thought to myself, "That takes balls. You better have a damn good pizza to be giving it away for free."

We ordered some garlic knots in addition to our cheese and onion pizza. The knots were buttery, garlicky, toasty and fucking delicious. Good sign.

However, the pizza itself was underwhelming. First off, they put chopped red onion on the pizza. I probably should have asked when I ordered, but red onion on a pizza is gross. It caramelizes with a much sweeter taste than yellow or white onions creating a sugary flavor. Combine that with salty cheese and tomato sauce and you have a barf sandwich, not a pizza. Also, I like onions added in thin rings not chopped chunks. The rings roast better in the oven and the chunky pieces just have a strange slimy texture.

The dough was puffy and chewy, which tells me they used too much yeast. Pizza dough shouldn't have a lot of yeast in it. We're not baking muffins here. It's pizza. The crust should be toasty and crispy. If I had to guess, the dough comes to the shop pre-made from some manufacturer and the underpaid grumpy employees who have no self esteem to get a better job anywhere else roll it out from there. The pizza crust should be a part of the eating experience, not bread to hold the ingredients in place. In my opinion, if you would rather feed the crust to the dog than eat it, it is a lousy made crust. Our dog ate well that night.

So, would I order from these guys again? Yes, but only if I had another free pizza coupon (Hey man, Free food is awesome!). I certainly wouldn't pay twenty bucks for this pie, though. 

If you want a really great local pizza, try West Bay Pizza or Queen's. Otherwise, this place would be a great place to stop and get a beer after work with an order of garlic knots on the side, while you contemplate cheating on your wife.

Nice quiet little park.  I took my toddler on a Saturday late morning and there was only one other family there.  I would say that the playground equipment would be best for preschool age but my 8 year old would have enjoyed the monkey bars.  The park does have wear and tear on the equipment and a few items were either broken or missing.  Don't let that deter you from the park though.  It was peaceful and quiet even on a Saturday morning.  It would be a great place to meet up with other families and have a picnic.  Lots of shade (though not on the playground), lots of nice shaded picnic tables and really quiet.   It would have been nice to have some information, as other Yelpers stated, about the "heritage" portion of the park.  I'm assuming that it was a former site of the water company since the current one is next to the park.  

It's a nice little gem of a park.  A little paint, a few repairs and some fresh mulch would go a long way.

Just tried Tommy's for the 1st time:
First I will say I will not be returning 
Looked at prices on line was stated it was 10.00 which was not the case it was 10.70 so that was the first issue. Then I was met with a cashier that did not have a mask on when we are under a mandatory order.  No Employees had masks. No cloths are provided to dry your car which are done at the other car washes around. Lastly you are in the sun when you are try to vacuum 
Which don't work very well... it wasn't due to a large amount of vacuums being used I will go back to Home Team...... Across the street

Great experience at this shop for myself and my daughter.  We were pierced by Dan (nose and navel) who was very polite and thorough while explaining the aftercare process.  Overall, shop is clean and well lit.  We will be back.

The lunch buffet is just really bland and lackluster. The saag (spinach) was watery and the basmati was overcooked. Luckily it was only 8.95 or I'd be complaining. Seem like nice folks; not trying to knock them. Just being honest. Could get some basmati and paneer pouches from the grocery store, microwave them and get the same effect.  Meh.

I really enjoyed the museum. Many of the other reviews made it seem as though you need the whole day to really experience it. I did it in a couple hours. My one complaint is how they brushed over the holocaust and both atom bombs. I did not pay for the extras so maybe they were mentioned more there. But a brief (5 minute) video about each does not seem like the best way to represent these tragedies.

This is a review ONLY for the their LUNCH time menu as I have never been there for Dinner.

I have been to this restaurant for lunch on several occasions, both on my own and with collegues. The food has always been excellent and the service very good. Their lunch time menu is well priced and the taste is as authentic as you can get.

Gave me food poisoning never eat there again  it was good at first but bad in the end don't know why they are still open need to be shut down and they have dogs on the inside of the business so I don't trust them at all bad for business if I must add but go and eat what they have to offer and see you in the hospital

We had a Groupon for Eats because we've eaten here before and the food was good.  The restaurant is not necessarily around the block from us and the reason for the 1 star is before even walking into the restaurant there is a note on the door saying that their contract with Groupon has expired and they do Not accept previously purchased Groupings.  
It's a shame because it turned us off to even going inside and we choose to eat elsewhere. As a business decision to not work with Groupon we have no problem. However, the business should have honored already purchased deals.

One of my new fav spots in the Gulch (North Gulch tbh, but right by Chauhan) and is a great cocktail spot, Duckpin Bowling attraction, and retro game aficionado bar. What more can you ask?! Also, it's dog friendly and they rotate food trucks daily that you can check on their app. This is a killer spot and will be a staple of mine in Nash. Not to mention, their Maple Nut Old Fashioned is one of my favs on Nash.

Can I give Zahav 10 stars!?!
My husband and I are BIG foodies from NYC. We heard about Zahav and planned a road trip to Philadelphia just to dine there. It was by far the  most exquisite dining experience-
We basically tasted 80% of the menu by the recommendations of our beautiful waitress, Alena.  We personally met Chef Michael by the counter- he was so cool and welcoming. He even chatted with us before we left. We were treated like guests in his own home! 
Our top favorite dishes and drinks were:
-Za'atar cocktail 
-Rye & herb cocktail
-Lamb tartar (unbelievable flavor!)
-Lamb shoulder
-Fried cauliflower
-Sweetbreads
I also bought his amazing cookbook which is like a narrative. I enjoyed reading it so much! Get it!!!

I had the Tuna Salad from their new seafood menu. Very disappointed. The asparagus was bad & the salad was mostly willted & sone brown to black. Won't be going back anytime.  At least the spinach bowtie paata was good.

The service at Franzone's is really what made me give it 2 two stars. The last time I went was on a Friday night, and it looked like it was run by a bunch of teenagers that had absolutely no concept of customer service! It was just a bunch of people standing around doing nothing while I waited at the counter to be served my pick up order. And when I was waited on, I got little to no acknowledgement.
The chicken wings are pretty good but I've gotten a cheese steak there and it was really dry and lacked the toppings I asked for (mushrooms and onions). The beer is over priced, and even though many people rave about the sweet sauce on the pizza, I really don't think it's that special. 
I don't think I'll come back here anytime soon due to the poor service.

Update 10/25/2013
Got a pizza the other night, the toppings were good and plentiful, however the dough is too soft almost like they don't cook the pie all the way. My main complaint this time is while eating my pizza I found a few (not one, a few) short hairs on my pizza slice. I called to inform them and the guy who answered the phone was very friendly and offered me a free pizza for next time. Beware, if there is hair in the food, I just wonder if they even wash their hands or follow general health and safety guidelines.

My wife and I stopped in and had an early dinner. Dockside Dave's has a large selection of seafood. I had fish and chips and my wife had an fried oyster platter. The food was ok. The server was very good with fast service. The place was packed. We did not experience a happy hour. Drinks were reasonably priced. Dave's offers grilled and blackened fish...mahi and other selections. Nothing wrong with the food. Onion rings and fries were very good. This is a good restaurant for bar food.

I've now visited this location twice since they've opened. They have only been open a couple of weeks I believe and I have to say, I was impressed. I've been to the cherry hill location a few times as well but I found the food to have more flavor at Marlton and the service to be faster and friendlier as well. That's definitely impressive for having just opened. While I was sitting and eating, Michael walked around checking on customers including myself. Not something you typically expect from a fast food place, so that was really appreciated as well. I also love that they have gluten free and vegan or vegetarian options. Something for everyone! . I will definitely be back.

Thai Chef & Noodle Fusion is located on a dreary strip of Rt. 1 (Lincoln Highway) in Penndel.  The effort to bring new life is taking shape with a few new restaurants in a two block area.  This place is one of its anchors, and is a pioneer there over several years.  

The shop has a casual air, much more laid back and cozy than it appears from outside.  The tables on one side are separated by privacy screens made up of thin branches and curtains.  The outer wall has carved wooden folding screens that can be opened to extend for more separation.  Nice touch.  The staff is friendly, informative and prompt.  This is primarily a Thai house, although the name and menu offer alternatives in Japanese and Chinese cuisine.  I join the other reviewers in skepticism that "fusion" is a reality as opposed to a marketing gimmick.  I have only tried the Thai dishes as the owners and chef are Thai, and they come by this style honestly - they live it. 

I had a lunch special which is a combination of a choice of cup of soup, app, and entre.  I chose the Tum Yom Soup, Moon Dumplings, and Drunken Noodles with Chicken.  The soup had the strong taste of lemongrass and mild chili with ample slices of chicken and veggies.  I enjoyed it.  The Moon Dumplings were a pleasant surprise.  While small and lightly fried, they were loaded with ground mushrooms and possessed a nice earthy flavor, and absorbed the hot chili oil/sauce well.  The Drunken Noodles were very good, with the right ratio of broad noodles to veggies and chicken and a decent sauce.  It was a little bland so I asked for chili flakes to liven it up.  I was brought a shaker of finely ground orange pepper that was a perfect complement to the dish and brought it to the heat level I wanted.  Some joints don't have the right spice and offer only Sriracha, which, while hot, does not blend well and distribute evenly over most foods.  Thai Chef had what was needed.  

I can't comment on the "fusion" aspects, but I was very satisfied with my Thai lunch here.  I know that I look forward to more visits, and that makes me a fan.

August 10, 2014 - I have had dinner here a couple of times recently.  If anything, the dinners are superior to the lunch deals.  Last night I sampled their "Jungle King" and "Cashew".  The names themselves were unique. Both were nice portions of fresh veggies, tofu, chicken, with a nice mix of spices, including ginger and a sweetish curry, and large cashews on one.  An order of House Fried Rice rounded out the meal, while leaving enough to take home for today's lunch.  

The vibe continues to relax and encourage lingering.  The fact that the owners are Thai helps bring the authenticity home.

Don't buy gift cards from this store someone me is stealing the money off the cards before they can be redeemed  I bought two $100 eBay gift cards and one was used before I could get to my car to redeem it  I called eBay they said it was used one minute after purchase  I'm on crutches it takes me more than a minute to get out of the store so now I'm out of $100 I can't get it back
Love. Keep my shoes/boots for years. Wonderful service, shine & repair. 2-3 week wait time but well worth it & drive quite a distance to get to family owned business. Very fair cost.

The store on Tchoupitoulas St. is large and in a large shopping mall. So there is always parking. The people who work here on my occasional visits have been more than helpful and friendly. And the prices are not bad. For example in running around today I purchased cat litter about four dollars less than what the same product cost at my friendly neighborhood supermarket. Worth the extra stop at Petco.

And when I returned home with cat grass as a treat for my boy, he too was happy with Petco.

This is the best food in downtown Clearwater. It's always delicious and is far healthier than anything else in the area. The staff are consistently friendly. Prices are in line with quality food. Personally, I love the wild-caught salmon and the carrot-raisin muffins. The salmon is always crisped just a bit on the outside and slightly rare on the inside unless you ask them to cook it a little more for you. Take their chocolate pudding and truffles home with you.

For those of you interested in how NOT to make a breakfast burrito....pay close attention and take notes. Get on a plane, go to Reno, check in at the Grand Sierra Resort, drink too much at the escalator bar, get a hangover and stumble into the 2nd St Express and order their "Super" breakfast burrito! 

So I'm genuinely excited that there's a breakfast burro here at the hotel...yup...I'm simple like that! I order it up and the lady behind the counter asks if I want "everything" in it...I usually just say "yup"...but she had this utterly confused look on her face so I kindly ask "what's everything?" I am not kidding this look of terror comes over her face like I just told her I slaughtered her kittens with a hacksaw...and she nervously mumbles...."eggs, lettuce, pickles, sour cream..." WTF!? Where in God's name am I?! I try and make it easy for her and just say bacon, eggs, potatoes, cheese and salsa please. 

This mini $4 monstrosity in a cold tortilla had overabundant undercooked scrambled eggs, NOT a single potato or hash brown, a lump of cheddar hidden in the corner, too much cilantro AND NO BACON! (they accidentally put sausage in there) A sin! http://static.px.yelp.com/bphoto/eHubGFLLur8dG4OWoHsKog/l 

I was so hungry I ate the first half without breathing or thinking and then realized how bad it sucked. I found some hot sauce packets and drenched the other half in it....Lindy's Jalapeno Picante Salsa! this stuff is tasty! I think I took another bite or two of the burrito then tossed it in the garbage where it rightfully belonged. Thank God there was another packet of hot sauce in there....I sucked on that thing like Al Bundy eating ketchup packets during a recession! Extra star for Lindy's Jalapeno Picante Salsa! http://static.px.yelp.com/bphoto/KHXHwHKPMdks4Cw64tTa1A/l 

Thank you Lindy! You saved the day!

Great pizza! Great people! Food was nice and hot....looking delicious as it hit the table. We were pleased. It was delicious. ...pepperoni pizza! 
The service was great too...always a plus at a restaurant :)

Will not come back to this location. Who does the hiring and training? Some of the staff are like "duh".. also the layout of the gym is jacked up.." hey lets put the swimming pool on the other side of the gym and the only way in is by walking through the middle of the gym". Also totally NOT kid-friendly pool. Just 3 swimming lanes and that's it!
Love this place and it's halal! The staff and owner are really sweet people and the food is great! I go all the time when I am back home and even had a wedding dinner there with friends!

Back again with my 2 year old to give Wild Eggs a second chance and this visit was a major upgrade over the first.  We were accurately quoted a 10-15 minute wait and when we were seated the hostess took care of my son offering to open his crayons and making sure his high chair was pushed close enough to the table. Very sweet and welcoming. Our server promptly took our order of a kids meal and the Surfer Girl omelette...yeah I ordered the Surfer Girl and I am glad I did!  It was fabulous though I did subtract the cream cheese and sour cream and subbed in fruit instead of toast and potatoes.  My little guy polished off most of his fruit, toast and sausage links and seemed very satisfied.  The coffee was hot and fresh too. The service was friendly and efficient and I will definitely be back. It should be noted that Management reached out to me after my last visit and graciously offered a credit towards a return visit. I did not take them up on the offer and gladly paid full price for this breakfast experience with my son.

It would have been two stars except I did enjoy one of the five things we ordered, the jeweled beef with rice noodles. The hoisin ribs were my least favorite, the walnut shrimp was ok, the sizzling chicken and pork was tasty. The lettuce wraps we're ok, strong gingery lemony flavor, I liked it enough, except it came with a measly 4 pieces of lettuce. Yikes. 
The bar was noisy but the energy level was good. It could be a good family restaurant and a great hang out for watching a game. The wait help we're very friendly and I felt welcome here!
Not very clean. I wish that they would restock the vegetables and throw away the rotten ones.  This is a very big Walmart though.

Not what I expected, at all.

I ordered hot tea... it was flavored? Mango or peach, not sure, but I was expecting plain green tea. I wanted plain green tea, they should have let me know it was flavored.

I ordered Tom yum soup with chicken, should be pretty basic but the chicken chunks were so large I had to fish them out and chop them into bite size pieces. I was not expecting a some assembly required meal.

I ordered the chicken pad thai with egg fried rice. Both were cold. Like not even the center was warm... been sitting out for quite some time cold. Since I was one of the first ordered, I was not expecting the food to be cold.

 Also the pad thai was orange? I've never seen orange pad thai before. It left an orange film on the plate, utensils, my mouth and napkin. Pretty disgusting really.

I feel like I had a pretty good sampling of their foods and I'm not impressed. Will avoid in the future.

This used to be our favorite place to go in Tucson. Great happy hour, great food and great service (Usually from Mia- she's great!). They have a fantastic wine assortment and some great appetizers. Sadly, after losing their head chef to Texas within the last year...It's been a downward spiral. Overdressed salads (The spinach salad used to be incredible), poorly cooked foods and just uninspired entrees. The manager has pulled a few shady things with us as well. When we've complained about things, he's lied and tried to cover it up, rather than owning it and apologizing for it. Don't get me wrong... We've enjoyed amazing happy hours, wine dinners and family dinners here... But after 4 or 5 bad experiences in a row, we've thrown in the towel. Not sure this restaurant will survive if it keeps this up.
The staff is very friendly. The food and drinks are unique. Has a very relaxed and yet sophisticated ambiance. We did a small 7 person gathering there for brunch and it was great.

If I could give zero stars I would. My boyfriend purchased a vehicle from Mall Chevy in 2014, and when he went to trade in for a newer model the manager told him "if I give you $400/ month payments you're buying the car today" so naturally he walked out. Then I went in to purchase a vehicle I was approached on the lot by a very helpful gentleman, but then was transferred to a different salesman when I got inside. I was told that I would receive no money for my trade in but they would take my car anyways. Then when I was filling out the credit application, the salesman left me sitting for 45 minutes because it took a few phone calls to get some of the information on the application. When I told another salesman I was ready, I was still left sitting for another 20 minutes! The salesman supposed to be helping me was sitting at the front desk looking and pointing at me while chatting with the people behind the desk. Needless to say I walked out and as I was leaving the salesman looked at me like I was worthless trash. A couple days later I received a voicemail. I will NEVER go back to Mall Chevy again, and if you're smart you won't give them any business.

This no-frills Brazilian restaurant does not disappoint. Buffet is expansive and the meats are  just right (at least whenever my friends and I have gone). Have only gone for lunch and paid by weight, but I'm sure the buffet is well worth it, too.

I've found my new favourite coffee shop in the city, I think. Just maybe.

Swung by last weekend for a morning date with Sarah J. Now, after reading this review, you can't say I didn't warn you when I tell you that it might be awfully tricky to find a seat here on a Saturday morning. Or Saturday afternoon. Or anytime on a Sunday, at all. Busy busy busy!

My muffin was fresh and delicious and full of berries. My coffee was fresh and hot and just what I needed!

We snagged a seat the bar by the window, luckily. Even though the joint was hopping, it never felt too loud, or like we couldn't have a conversation. I really like that about this place!

When I really feel like splurging, I'll try one of their infamous cinnamon buns. And when I have a Friday night with nothing to do, I'm going to check out their live music scene - NO COVER!

This establishment is a nice and cozy little place and tastefully decorated. Like many other restaurants in Reno, it used to be a house for people to live in. We started off with shrimp scampi. There were 5 shrimps and they were huge. The shrimps were firm and tender, and full of flavor. The scampi sauce was different from what I had in other Italian places, it was less garlicky and more acidic, citricy. But the scampi was only half a dish without bread to dip in. We also had to ask for bread, but I think it was more of a "bread upon request" thing,  since there are so many carb-free and gluten-free people now, and they don't want any good bread wasted. The overall quality of food was great, the short rib rigatoni was a must have and the dessert was good too!  But be aware that the restaurant is a "slow dining" restaurant meaning it takes quite a while for the food to get to your table, especially when the head chef likes to do everything himself. And the dining area is cozy, like, real cozy. It has a capacity for only 40 people, so if you plan to go there with a big party, it would be a good idea to make reservations.

Only went because my group wanted to go. I didn't appreciate the "sarcastic humor" because it wasn't humor, just rudeness. Please don't go here for the food because it was terrible! As was the service! Even our hats they made for us were lame! If you have to eat lunch room quality food, I at least want good entertainment!

I haven't been here in a while so my husband and I decided to get an early dinner. Upon walking in we were greeted and taken to our table. The waitress came to the table took our drink and appetizer order. She seemed to be a little pushy when taking our orders as if she was rushing to get something else done. My husband and I enjoyed the appetizer which was zucchini balls. We ended up ordering the same entree without realizing it. The pasta was super blah. No seasoning or taste just greasy pasta. Sliced red bell peppers, a few shrimp and chicken tenders. We were hungry so we finished our meal packed the rest to-go, paid our tab and left. 
There wasn't anything special about this location just the typical Olive Garden. This is the first time that I've been and the food was bad. Maybe it was just that particular dish however I can't say if I'll visit this location again. 
I've given three stars because of the zucchini balls they were delicious and reminded me of fried green tomatoes.

Went here for the first time with a group of about 8 people. The drinks were good, although the bartender switched my drink with my boyfriend's so I took a half sip of a very bitter tasting beer. We ordered the cheesy bread appetizer, a burger and a pizza. Luckily, the cheesy bread was delicious because that ended up being my meal. I waited about 15-20 minutes after everyone else got their food before inquiring about the status of the pizza. The person who took our order forgot to put the pizza in so I told her to cancel it. Overall good food, but service is hit or miss. Would consider giving it another try.

One thing that's cool here is the water in the shower. Ouch!!!!!  It took so long to warm up that I wasn't sure it ever would.  The dark marble in the bathroom was very pretty and I had lots of time to admire it as I prayed for warm water and made myself coffee.  The coffee maker, microwave and refrigerator in the room make it cozy and welcoming. Back to the shower, somebody should have given such attention to basics as they did to pretty marble.

Things that were less cool included the shabby housekeeping.  There was a half-eaten brownie in the refrigerator, a pair of old socks on the floor, an empty food wrapper under the sink, and a medium-size toy under the bed.  My young child was grateful for the toy and I was grateful it wasn't something harmful.  Maybe I should check under the bed myself now, in case there's something more?  Ugh, never mind!

The spa, arcade, movie theater, and shops in the basement are great.  They made the visit worthwhile.  There weren't too many people around, which probably explains why some locales were empty and some were in the midst of close out sales.  Too bad.

We came here because we were given free rooms and that's probably the only way I would come back.  My wife liked it more and says she'd return.

First visit, awesome brows! Second visit, crooked brows! 

I gave them two stars because my first visit with Khuki gave me hope that I could have beautiful brows after a year of leaving them alone and letting the hair grow in.  However that hope was dashed today after another lady reduced my brows to near pencil thin wisps and made one higher than the other. I thought they looked ok when I left but when I examined them further at home I was pissed.

Guess I'll go back to growing them out again for another year and then find Khuki.

Positives: clean, quiet, available machines (washing: -+6 small, 3 med ($4.50 +), 2 lg ($5.00 -$7.00); drying: 8 $.25/6min)
Tennis balls for dryer ( or play catch while u wait).
Amazon Lockers!
Customer service promptly addresses issue - ...see below 

Negatives: just ONE power outlet by the dryers (which was broken); can't charge devices if waiting for laundry.

A positive experience.... 4 stars!
Yes, the change machine worked however it's always best to BRING QUARTERS!!
I used to bring my laundry to this facility over 20 years ago when I lived at 10th and Spruce. It was no big deal then like many self-service laundromats it had its share of problems but being less than a hundred yards from the front door it was the only option in the neighborhood at the time and pretty much still is. Recently, I needed to bring some items in to be cleaned and prior to arrival read the reviews on Yelp and other websites to see if it was the same old place that I remembered. I saw a few bad reviews regarding customer service and some amenities that were lacking but again with very few options in the immediate area, decided to get give it a try. I believe the prices quoted in earlier posts were pretty accurate. I needed the medium sized washing machine which starts at $4.50 and increases depending on whether or not you need an extra rinse or if your clothes are more soiled or even if you need to use hot water. The increase was roughly $.25-$.50 depending on which option you chose.  
The issue came with the drawing. One of the dryers did not heat up and despite only having one item in the dryer, I repeatedly got the E15 error which meant the vent was being blocked. I adjusted the item and started to cycle again. Unfortunately, the dryer never got hot. I checked the settings several times repeatedly got the E15 error but continue to dry the item, since I had already put $1.50 into the machine. There is a sign posted what the E15 error meant and suggestions to remedy the issue. I ended up throwing about 4 tennis balls into the cycle with the hope that I would no longer get the error this was not the case. There is a sign posted on the wall for contact should there be any issues and I sent an email with a picture indicating which dryer was causing the problem. To my surprise later that evening I received the email with an apology and then offer to refund my money and said the dryer would be repaired. I thought this was very good customer service.

Love costco but do they seriously need there maintenance and landscape crews working at 10pm? They are trimming bushes and making so much noise it's all I can hear when trying to go to sleep. What is prohibiting them from having those crews work hours that don't violate the noise ordinance law? Having a hard time wanting to keep spending money at this rate.

I would never recommend this company i had nothing but issues getting our pool finished they told us 3 months it took 6 months .They kept saying i'm the start is was issues with getting the permit but yet they were to lazy to go to the permit office to talk to someone.We started construction on pool may 7 2018 we had a inspection on june 15 but couldn't move any further because they didn't order pavers in time. When i would call Iris I would get  bs story on what was going on or tell me things were going to happen this day and nothing would happen. There communication is so bad i would call and get send to voicemail most of the time. When you pay this much for something you don't expect for it to be such a headache.

It's pretty disgusting to see your workers handling food and going to use a bathroom that has no hand soap come back out to continue handling customers food/orders. I went to use the bathroom and came back out and told the person behind the register about it and was given a blank stare. I guess this is the norm in Franklin now?  Let me remind you we are still under a COVID-19 pandemic and your restaurant is risking every patrons life. Check your bathrooms!  Make sure there is soap to wash with!! Filthy! Stop placing everyone's health at risk!!  Now I understand why the health department have issued you violations in the past.

YUMMMMMMM!

This place is amazing; a little short on ambiance, but boy do they make up for it with food!

We started with the Vegetable Samosas.  It's described as "Crispy stuffed vegetable pyramid filled with potatoes and mild spices."  I say these are incredible little potato pillows and I want MORE.  Best part of the meal, for sure, but that's not to say the other dishes were lacking.  This was just THAT GOOD.

I had the Saag Shrimps (Delicious shrimps Masala cooked with spinach and fresh spices for added flavor) as my entree along with the Jalapeno Cilantro Naan.  This was super green and dark, loaded with shrimp and Indian/Pakistani spices, and absolutely wonderful (although still not as good as the Samosas.  Oh man!).  New to Indian food, so I'm not even sure how to describe it, except to say it was scrumptious.

My friend ordered the Smoked Chicken Masala, a chef's special.  Ok, this was really close to the Samosas for being tasty; not as exotic tasting as the Saag, but super delicious and with a little creamy aspect the Saag was lacking.  The Garlic Naan on the side really complimented the dish.

Seriously, I cannot WAIT to go back to get some of this food!

The draft beer choices are actually pretty good here. Of course I am biased cause they actually have Yuengling on draft here and I'm a huge Yuengling fan. The food was really not that good at all. My friends mahi mahi that she had was really fishy and tasted disgusting, I hope she doesn't get sick tonight. The BBQ duck pizza I had was kind of bland and had no flare to it. Very disappointed cause this was the first place I've been to eat in Tampa. If you can't do good food at a pub/bar then don't do it at all!
Easily the worst iHOP I've ever been to. What a terrible experience. It took them an hour to split the check. And it was still wrong. Never coming back. Would give zero stars if it was possible.

We found this place through recommendation of my fiances coworker because we were planning to go out to dinner and wanted to try something new. The food is amazing and the staff is so warm and welcoming. I feel like the menu is small, but everything they offer is amazing no complaints here.

If I'm not sure what to order I always ask the staff what their favorite is and our server the first time in we were recommended the Tostones Rellenos De Carne and we wound up loving them and have ordered this every time since. We usually get a split order half ground and half shredded beef.

My favorite thing I have ordered is the Cuban Tamale which i've ordered almost every time we went. It is so good and the serving is fairly large. The last couple time we went in they were sold out though,  so I ordered the Fricase De Puerco because it has the same filling and is also delicious, but the tamales are still my favorite.

Second visit wasn't so impressive as the first. Cheese ravioli was served lukewarm.  My husband's spaghetti and meatballs, on the other hand, was piping hot. I gestured to our waiter, who ignored me. Luckily, a nice lady from the kitchen saw me and, when I told her the problem, she had the kitchen prepare a new plate. This time it was hot. We bought a wonderful loaf of bread before we left. It turned out to be the highlight of our visit!
I've worked by the location for a decade. Started getting haircuts within the last year.  Sherri is outstanding.
The food is pretty good. I ordered the dry rice and fried fish. I've never had African (Liberian) cuisine before and I thoroughly enjoyed myself. The portions are quite generous.

This was my "go to" location for decades - even way before it was located at this spot (when it used to be a HoJo's! ) and P.B. was in Abington, PA in the 1980s! It's now just simply pathetic. They redesigned this store recently - for some reason. It's now a VERY cramped area with limited supplies - which are never in stock no matter what the website states. 

About 70% of the inside space is now tires...rows and rows of tires alost reaching to the ceiling! The help is rarely helpful and downright rude. I did my research on spark plugs for my car before hand, I asked this guy for these spark plugs and he simply said, these fit your car right?Because if they dont your car is screwed and these are non returnable. Ok..fine I get that. 

He cuts me off and asks what year, model is the car..(like this was a big chore to enter a few entries on the computer) The plugs DID match and process to slide them across the desk and walk away. 

I rather drive another 8-10 miles out of the way to the Warminster or Montgomeryville location. I have MUCH better luck finding what I need there.

Upon entering I noticed that the service was Extremely slow, and no there wasn't a lot of customers at this particular time. When I was next up in line, the server stood away from the stem table and acted as if she wasn't interested in serving me. It took her nearly 3 to 4 minutes before she stepped up and asked what I'd like to eat.  The older lady then properly ate my food, but I had to wait a few minutes for the corn bread to be cooked, as well as the customers before me. 

When I returned to work and began eating my lunch, I found my food to be EXTREMELY salty. I even allowed a co-worker to taste it and they agreed the food was salty. 

This is the 1st time I've ever found the service and the food to be subpar.

A fight broke out!!  Our children were so scared!!


We have been frequenting PK's since it opened. My husband and I just brought our daughter 10, niece 5 and nephew 7 there and had to get a refund immediately due to a fight breaking out between a man and a woman!  This place is going downhill quickly. They are not equipped with a staff that can handle fights between customers. It was so terrible. 

My 10 year old and 5 year old are like totally scarred. Crying and just feeling unsafe in general. We had to wait until the manager on duty was done breaking up the fight (which we had a front row show to) because it was right near the front door and cash register where we were. The girls that were at the front desk said they had no authority to refund us. This was after they told us there were no refunds. Ummm, we were absolutely getting a refund. We hadn't even played one game yet and hadn't even left the front desk yet. 

Point being we had to wait for the manager to get done "handling" the fight and clean up before we could leave.  

I'm not sure how PK's can rectify this for us. It seems like it's becoming an unsafe and seedy place to bring children. I'm very disappointed as we used to really like it here.

If I could give a -10 I absolutely would. Do not bring your car here.  I initially bought it in for a minor accident. Jeff assured me the first time I went to pick up my car that he personally test drive it and it was good to go. I get a few blocks and my front sensor is not working.  I look under the hood and some type of rodent has chewed my wiring. They get me a rental and promised to fix this new problem.  I get my car back and what'll you know it's still not working.  Jeff prints out this incredulous paper basically stating the rodent issue happened before the accident but still nice they can't prove otherwise WTF. I bought a brand new 2017 Jetta with no issues until the accident and I just had a oil change at a VW dealership in December 2wks prior sooooo what the freak are you talking about.  Jeff berated my character to the Enterprise rep in my presence for a mistake his dimwit of a receptionist made in billing.

Great place to call in Indian food in green hills! Free delivery if close and they have a pretty good chicken tikka masala, where you can choose your spice level, which is nice! Good portions, bite a little pricey. Over all, a yummy treat!

TJ Maxx's can be hit or miss.  Yes, there are some great bargains, but sometimes you'll need to put away your OCDness for a hot minute and try not to be distracted by how messy it can be. And it's not always the store's fault - shoppers are damn lazy and will pick up merchandise in one area and then get rid of it by dumping it in another section if they change their mind.

I like that that they have different sizes for women available, and I once lucked out and found a bunch of their cardboard cat scratchers for $6.99 that are usually closer to $10 each, so I grabbed a few of those.

As always for a store of this kind, grab what you like now as more likely than not, it will not be there three days later.

In town for a volleyball tournament and this location was the closest to the venue. Our team coordinator called 3 times to get a group rate, none of which were returned.  Strike 1.

We got to the hotel the night before to find a football clinic being held for high school football. The lobby/bar are was full of men drinking, to the point that conversations got so lively that the local police & sheriffs were called in later that night to break it up...in the lobby. Get to the room & find 1/2 a 2L of Dr Pepper in the room fridge...not mine.  Strike 2.

While sitting in the chair putting my shoes for dinner, I happen to look over and see a BOOGER on the wall!!! What!?

With all the other options in the area, I will NOT be back!!

Came here for the Taste Philly food event. I was unimpressed with the venue. The atmosphere feels designed for a rodeo or tractor pull. Very little effort was placed into the aesthetic. Exposed rafters and life sucking beige paint bore the eyes and dampen the spirit. Not surprisingly, the clientele closely mirrored their environ. Valley Forge Casino feels a bit like a mixture of a cheap Vegas hotel on the old strip, Walmart, and a grease-shone Hardee's restaurant from 1993.

Purchased a Groupon for an hour long couple's massage. Neither of us had ever had a massage so we did not know what to expect. We arrived a couple minutes late and were directed to fill out at least 10 minutes of paperwork (there was only one iPad, so we had to take turns). We finally got into the room and started around 4:20. I will say, the massages were great. Our masseuses were friendly and made us very comfortable. However, when we looked at the clock after they had finished and left the room if was only 4:55. Now, I totally understand that we were 5 minutes late ourselves and that time would be taken out of our massage, but when scheduling the appointment, I was not told to expect 10+ minutes of paperwork to take up my massage time (paperwork that easily could have been completed after the massage). And if it is an hour long massage, why are we ending 5 minutes before 5pm? It just would have been nice if the receptionist mentioned on the phone to arrive plenty early, and even then, there did not seem to be any sense of urgency to get us started. Seems like a sneaky business practice, and I won't be back.

I think tea has great benefits. I have been drinking it daily for years... I made a purchase here and I'm happy with it. Would I do it again? Probably not.... 2 oz. of tea for $20 something is a little much. The Republic of Tea, which you can get at World Market, Whole Foods, or some other specialty stores makes excellent tea at half  the price. I don't doubt the benefits and quality of the tea here, just seems unnecessarily expensive.  Cute teapots and accessories.

Nice cafe in Old City, good for working as they have free wifi and it is not too loud besides the lunch rush. I had an apple brie sandwich which came out toasted. The sandwich was a good blend of sweet and crunchy from the apples and cheesy from the brie.

It's sad that they never always have what you are shopping for. Great Value diet sweet tea is never on the shelves...sold out!  However today I'm told they are not carrying it anymore. What's the deal!  Make your customers happy!

Learn more about antebellum Louisiana, and after the Civil War. The building itself is significant as this was where France turned Louisiana over to the United States after the Treaty was signed in Paris. Want to know the difference between Creole and Cajun? Obtain a better understanding of the early settlers and Louisiana before and after Reconstruction?  It's this building. 

If you plan to go to the Presbytere two doors down, you can save 20% on both admission tickets.

The day we were there, the security alarm went off a number of times.  We had to vacate the building initially.  When it went off again and again, we ignored it.

We took the stairs up three flights but there's an elevator.  Go into the courtyard to locate it.

It's so nice to have a fabric store in Germantown!  Before a random bus ride, where I just couldn't stand to read my book I was going all the way to fabric row to get fabric.

This place is right near school house lane on the ave., and has a great selection of good quality fabrics.  Prices are reasonable.  This place is huge, so go with someone who can appreciate fabric or you're bound to lose your friend...

Big drawback (to me anyway) is that they don't sell patterns...booooo.

I liked the doctors, but I am not very happy with the services of the optical store. My prescription was correct, but the glasses were not. I was uncomfortable with my vision through the glasses. Talking to a friend, she told me it happened the same to her and her son.
Great place to eat! Good variety, Fresh food, kind staff, and speedy checkout! Love this place!

This is a bench jewel.  Wood fired pizza, pasta and sandwiches that are creative and delish.  They have great beer and wine options, and service is friendly and attentive.  Their lunch special is the best deal, 8" pizza and a salad for $10. The only downside is the parking lot.

maybe my expectations were too high but i wasn't that impressed. our service was friendly and helpful and the atmosphere was average but it was my meal that was just kinda good but not great.

my lamb chops were very average. a little tough with a sauce that just your basic brown gravy. my mashed potatoes didn't taste fresh either. i'm guessing they plated my mashed well before my chops arrived. just guessing... the little bit of spinach that was draped over the chops was really good. maybe even the best part of the dinner.

i like the healthy theme of this place and i'm rooting for them, but they've got some room to improve.

My boyfriend found this deal for 2 free nights on slickdeal from November-December. How can someone who loves snowboarding as much as I do pass up o a deal like this?! Well I didnt, so we booked a stay here from Monday-Wednesday. I remember this use to be the Hilton, but have changed ownership in recent years. This is tucked away from downtown Reno, so it's less crowded which is great! They have their own bowling alley here! How cool is that?! I spotted a Starbucks in the lobby as we walked towards the elevators, and thought perfect!! Just what I need in the morning:) my regular cup of joe. 

The room we got is a bit older compared to the other rooms here. Since it's free, I can't complain. Although the room did smelled like an old room when we first got here, it's not unbearable and not something a little turning on the fan won't fix. The decorations here are definitely a bit older with floral wallpapers. The hallway did looked a bit creepy to me, gives me a feeling like someone crazy is going to chase after me! Haha (I know, what an imagination I have right?) instead of an updated flat screen, we're presented with a tube television. Overall, the room is really clean. I "would" walk on the carpets barefooted if I didn't bring my flip flops, that's how clean it is coming from a person whom is germ-phobic. Haha :p

I'd heard so many good things about this place that I had to try it not to long ago. I have to say I was very disappointed in the food itseslf. The atmosphere is nice and family friendly, and the service is good too. The food however reminded me of my grade school days. It was was certainly not homemade and fresh, it was instant and pre-breaded staight out of the box. No not impressed

We stayed there for 3 nights and the hotel is a short walking distance from Bourbon Street and other attractions in New Orleans. I enjoyed the small breakfast selection including eggs, sausage, bread, and cereal. What I didn't like about the hotel was parking; $44.07 a night including tax. The bed sheets and blanket had stains and the carpet was old and not vacuumed properly.

Had this in NYC and was surprised to find it here. Food was delicious and service was pretty good. Even though they were short staffed and had a lot of people, the service was still up to par. Great location as it's close to hotels in Logan square.

Solid mediocre in terms of food. Their salsa tasted like watery tomatoes (no flavor or spice) and I got a chicken quesadilla and wasn't super impressed. The chicken was dry, which is strange because typically the way the shredded chicken is prepared seals in moisture. 3 stars for OK food, but really more for the cool modern touch on western interior decor- galvanized metal meets bright colors and wood. Also, their bar is HUGE and well decorated to look inviting. I'd come back for a few drinks here, but I'd skip the food.

Outstanding local game store -- professional, clean, and very helpful. The staff will help you find what you are looking for, make helpful suggestions, introduce you to new games, or order what they don't already carry in stock. 

They carry a large selection of board games, and their stock of miniature games is growing as interest presents itself. You can find groups here regularly playing Dust, Bolt Action, Flames of War, X-Wing, SAGA, Wings of Glory, and Sails of Glory.

They SUCK!!! If I could give 0 stars I would!

The first cleaning was OK.

The second cleaning they didn't move any furniture at ALL...no small table like bedside tables. They didn't clean under anything like the beds, the tables, the bedside tables, the corner cabinets etc. They didn't vacuum the edges of any room and didn't clean the kitchen floor. 

To make matters worse. when I called and spoke to the manager, she had excuses...maybe the vacuum broke!!! NOT!!! Then, I emailed her with pictures of the poor cleaning and haven't gotten a response since!

DO NOT USE THEM!!! I think they may have stolen my iPod as well

You're in Nashville, you go to Pharmacy, please! 
Decent price, BIGGG portion, awesome beer selections, quick service (regardless of how busy it is, all the time!), great burgers and sides! 
I didn't go with their famed sweet potato fries, but their normal fries were amazing! The Mission City was the most interesting and different burger I've ever tried!

Came here for a post wedding brunch a year ago and my nieces 21st bday last night.  This is a Russian restaurant with a live singing band. The food is really good and it gets better depending on what menu you pre order.  I enjoyed the service, the food and good atmosphere. Its also a byob and for Russians this means bottles of Vodka or tequila.  Great place for a Russian person to have a birthday party.

Ok so where's the "country" in this country bar?  - there's a bull and maybe one or two songs every 10 min intermixed with rap and pop songs which were really poorly mixed. Just your everyday mediocre dance club ala 1991 ... Had an ok time when we accepted the place for what it was but just know it's just a dance club / pick up joint.

Excellent quiet and peaceful meal. Byob which I enjoy to do. The fried oyster and deviled egg appetizer was unique and enjoyable. The Delmonico steak and rack of lamb were cooked perfectly. I give it a thumbs up restaurant.

I will say this first. The food doesn't really change here at all... You know what you are coming for. But the service is much better and the manager currently working is on top of things! I believe his name is Materson but he is doing a great job. I apologize if in spelled his name wrong.

We enjoyed the view, but your menu selection is extremely limited.  This was my birthday celebration and I have to say I was rather disappointed.  There were only 7 other people in the entire restaurant the whole evening and that in itself is not a problem but the extremely slow service, the quality of the meals was very disappointing.  Probably won't be back

Food was great! 
Service was very poor. We waited over 5 minutes for someone to come to our table to ask if we wanted drinks. No apologies for our wait. It was not busy. Many tables open. It was 2pm. 
After our food came, our server never came back to ask how the food was or if we needed anything. 
I will look elsewhere in the area for better service before I come back here. Hopefully after the reviews I read on here how poor the service is-- they will take notice and improve!

Ordered from doordash so I should start with that caveat; however when "toastpoints" are essentially a slice of baguette thrown in the oven for 20 secs and slicing the bangers and cooking them the way you would cook freshly expired meat are your best first impression it leaves me saying "meh". With $20 spent between a side salad and the entree the pricing is not crazy, but don't be fooled by flowery language in the menu; the cooks have the same skill and passion as your local fast-food joint.

I booked and paid for my car online, went to the ticket counter to pick up the car, declined the additional coverage when asked and initialed the boxes that I was instructed to initial. I was unaware that the agent had added the coverage that I had just verbally declined when I initialed the agreement. This practice is deceptive and immoral. Calls to Budget customer service fell on deaf ears. They just don't care. They added $211 on my $207 car rental. 

The first car they put me in had a flat tire. The second car didn't until later. Had to air it up before returning it. 

Stay away, stay very far away from Budget Rent a Car

As far as gyms go it really isn't that bad. There are a lot of treadmills and a variety of other cardio machines. You can't beat 24 hour a day access. They have a lot of equipment which provides a quality work out. 

Now on to my challenges. The individual work out station equipment is so close together that it can be difficult to work out when the gym is busy. The equipment is in good shape for the most part. It's the people that go to the gym that are the problem. Rarely do I ever see anyone wipe the equipment off with the sanitary wipes when they are finished with the machine. Several put their gym towel down on the equipment before they use it and when they get up they wipe the machine off with their sweaty towel. There have been several occasions when I have been working out and there were no sanitizer wipes in any of the dispensers. Also, there is minimal space so when the trainers have people doing lunges in the main walkway, it definitely can create a problem.
Restaurant is clean, bathroom is clean, food is hot and delicious.  Service was fast and pleasant.

Worst pizza ever. Had a sad excuse for a Margherita pizza- it was dry, not saucy and had old globs of mozzarella. Service was slow. Waitress was nice but took way too long for just about everything- the order, the grub, and the check. Won't be coming back.

The city tap house is my favorite restaurant in university city. I love the burger but the service was even better. Deja served us and she was very quick at getting us what we needed while being very friendly.

What can I say about Camelot except for exceptional service, cleanliness, and general homey feeling. Nick, Wendy, and Bob, where hospitable and friendly. Our room which is more like an apartment, was large and comfortably fit seven people plus my 17 pound dog, which also enjoyed the stay. I accidentally stumbled across the Camelot while looking for a pet friendly place to stay on the West Coast. I was pleasantly surprised as was the other family I was traveling with. I will definitely be back. There are so many good things to say about this hotel. The pool area is immaculate with toys, floats, beach chairs, and all the beachtowels you can Ask for. The beach was literally across the street. There's always something to do on this beach. Oh I forgot to mention The rooftop barbecue area which overlooks the beach which I did not get to use this time, but definitely will next time. We will definitely be back!

So to start off we were celebrating my mother-in-law being cancer free, yay her, cancer sucks. Now on to the review I love little places like this, upscale and tight decor. 
   Let's start with the service because that is how I believe a restaurant can keep their customers even if the food is bad (which is was not but more on that in a moment). I don't happen to remember our servers name but she was a doll very helpful and wanted to make sure everything was up to snuff. My only downside was the kitchen seemed to be in the weeds not sure how since they weren't that busy, but she apologized for our food not coming all together and made sure we were all taken care of, way to go!
  Now for the wine and beer selection. It was nice to see a few local places on there however there could have been a few more local wines and beers, I also thought the prices were a bit high, but with a wife that works in the industry I have a bit of an unreasonable expectation since I don't ever pay full price for wine. 
   Now in the the goods, the food, 2 orders of chicken piccata ,one order of veal,  one fish and chips, and one burger. The chicken was super tasty not to much lemon and the right amount of sauce, the pasta was a perfect al dente' and the veg was good too. Although my wife hates bell pepper but I love it, also it had squash and eggplant, tomato, and red onion cooked the same. The veal tasted ok not to much breading and the sauce was tasty, while I do love mushrooms it was a bit much the veal needs to be the star and I can see why it was a bit chewy aka over cooked. My father in law is from England so his expectation for fish and chips is pretty high and he liked it. My daughters burger was good bit needed seasoning and the fried were kinda limp.

The dessert was pretty good the lemon tart was super tasty and the coconut cake was ok the sponge was super dry and the berblanc was rich and didn't make up for the dry cake

So all in all we'll be back I wanna try some other food and see if there is a difference

I really wanted to give this more than 2 stars. I ordered through a delivery app, so I could not send food back. Just do you know. I ordered  hot chicken and waffles, with the spiked honey sauce. So I love waffles and I immediately ate those first. The waffles were wet and soggy but I still at them. I even tried adding the butter and the maple which wasn't maple syrup. That did not help. So then it was time to eat the chicken. I always break my wings apart, something we were taught growing up. And as I broke the first wing open a I saw was pink, and I was disgusted. I then opened the second wing and I saw more pink and I was very disappointed. I said ok third times a charm and yup it was more pink chicken. This meal overall was a disappointment. I never got a chance to even taste the chicken. Until you get your Temps right with cooking your chicken since this seems to be a common complaint I will not be back. I was looking forward to some hot and tasty chicken and this was not it.
Great Place! Make A Reservation Before Hand! The wait can get up to 2 hours! Luckily we Made One!

I have been coming here are years & after today's visit this place is slowly on the decline. I have never had a problem here but the floors were wet & dirty today, 3 seats/booths are started to fall apart, the walls are in bad shape, this place needs a do-over bad. I got the KFC $5 pack today, skins were good but the chicken sliders I got had (I think) stale bread because most of the bread was hard. I like this location because it is in front of the AMC I always go too but it needs help.

Came here for happy hour on a Friday afternoon! Cool place with a chill vibe! We ordered their $5 slice and beer special. The pizza that comes with the special is cheese, but you can upgrade to one of their specialties for $1. So I got the Buffalo chicken pizzas it was pretty good, only complaint was that it was a tad dry. I think it had been sitting under the heat lamp for a bit too long. But was still tasty and I would order it again as a full pizza instead! Great place for happy hour! Definitely would go back!

I can't help but express confusion whilst dining at the Cheesecake Factory.  There's a million items on the menu, making it extremely difficult to narrow your options.  And what the heck is with the decor?!  It's like Candyland, meets Egyptian goddess, meets Italian styled ceiling art?  However, I have been twice and have left pleasantly plump and happy both times.

If you are a dieter...forget it.  Nothing on the menu is calorie friendly.  Even the "Weight Management Salads" are packed with 590 calories!  The Cheesecake Factory is meant for everyone to forget about the fat/calorie intake, and splurge a little. 

I have indulged in the Asian Chicken Salad, which was fantastic.  I've tried the herb crusted salmon as well, which was also fabulous.  However, I do have to say the Red Velvet Cheesecake is worth writing home about times 10!!  YUM YUM YUM.

1 star for the atmosphere(not the people just the decorations and music) and the other for the variety and cleverness in the drink menu...

soo... they act like this is some top notch place that everyone is just dying to get into. the dude that checks your id at the door acts like you trying to get into the White House or something. After you get past his ass, you can sit where ever you can. Fridays are pretty cool..complimentary happy hr snacks, tacos, chips etc..other than that the food sucks.. (if your lays chips are better than your menu items i think we have an issue).

yes there are a lot of drinks, but honestly after awhile you cannot tell the difference and all you feel is a stomach ache. service is eh' nothin special either.

all in all i feel like this place is somewhere you can gon once try the damn scorpion and go on with your life.

I was seated right away, but I arrived at 8:55pm and I think I got the only available table.  I had the regular Pho dac biet with beef, tripe and meatballs. The broth was super tasty. The greens were plentiful, but a little old-looking. I also ordered some egg rolls, but they came out burned (the ends were both BLACK!). Next time I'll give 5 stars if they don't burn the egg rolls and have some fresher-looking greens.

So disappointed!!! They boasted how Their Italian hoagie was "The Best" we just ordered 2 on Doordash from them, we each asked for extras , pickles, sweet peppers, ect.. They Obviously did NOT read the notes! And the actual hoagie was no better than a ham and cheese sandwich, there was a piece of salami, that's it!  I was the one who said, they are the best when we were looking for a place to order from... well that was a while ago I guess because I would never order there again or recommend them at this point!
These cookies are delicious, but pretty expensive.  I loved the chocolate chip pecan ones.  They're sold by the pound, but you can still buy just one at a time.

I had a leak into the basement from my bathtub. I called my home warranty and they assigned my claim to PK Plumbing. The "plumber" Steve, came out and took a five minute look at it and said it can only be repaired by ripping out the wall and the warranty will not cover any of the tile work to replace the wall once the leak is fixed. The plumber said he'll fix the leak but leave the hole in my wall for me to have repaired. I was dumbfounded and not sure how he could come to this conclusion without touching or looking at anything at all so I posted on a handyman Facebook group with many plumbers who all agreed this didn't sound correct with the video I uploaded. They all believed the leak to be coming from the drain. I had a different plumber come out and give me a second opinion and he actually fixed my leak in 5 minutes!! No need to rip anything out as it was just a seal around the tub drain. PK Plumbing is shady and Steve is dishonest and out to rip off the warranty company (and the customers along the way). My warranty company agreed to waive the service call fee and will be looking into the shady practices of this company. I would never allow them to step foot into my home again.

This place wasn't the cleanest, we stayed for two nights and all ended up with bug bites. 
No matter how many times we asked for towels, we would never get them. We ended up going to the dollar store to buy ourselves towels so we could shower. 
They also weren't about to give us the Wi-Fi password, and we had to ask several different people for it. 
Finally, we went to breakfast, and it was basic and good, but they informed us that I couldn't finish my bagel as I was walking out the door. No, I had to sit at the table and finish it, much like a small child. 

I hardly ever say a place is awful, but I'll pay more and stay at a comfort inn next time.

This is your standard Qdoba but during the school year you'll find college students dominating this location.  That's a good thing mostly.  I like people watching and it doesn't get much better than here in terms of the "bouncy" co-ed factor.
Lobster mashed potatoes are so yummy and so are the fish tacos! Very nice atmosphere and the waitress was great!!

No masks required by Home Depot for their employees, be aware!
St Pete will require tomorrow, because the Mayor demanded it.  
No masks?  Beware

Ive stayed here several times I've never had a bad experience although this time they put me in a different building normally I stay in the middle building in the 10th floor or up the rooms that are on the sides and not in the middle building are older and they're not as up to date as the ones in the middle building on the higher floors so I'm a bit disappointed with that other than that staff is very nice and the food is good

I knew when I bought my 70 year old house that the sewer line was ... sensitive. That compromises would have to be made for pipes well past their expiration date. But this year it became clear that it was time to call in the professionals to take care of a problem I had hoped to avoid - a complete replacement of the sewer line.

I was referred to A1 Plumbing, and from start to finish, they couldn't have been better to deal with. Their estimate was more than fair (and less than I had been dreading). The team arrived on time (a rarity these days). They excavated four deep holes with a care for the existing landscaping. They did the pipe bursting and connections (fascinating!). And then they put everything back together so well that it's hard to tell at first glance that they were even there. All of this they did in two days with me watching over their shoulders, asking questions about the process and taking pictures. They turned what could have been nothing more than a depressing necessity into a fascinating demonstration of skill and knowledge.

No one wants to have to replace a sewer line. But if you find yourself in that position, know that the people at A1 Plumbing are the ones to call, full stop.
Price gouging gas prices in a pandemic...its a fact and a shame...Stop it!!!!!!!!!!! You're a gas station that's why??????????????

Came in for a dip manicure. Lasted about one hour so it was quick but Sophia did a good job! Definitely on the pricier side, with tip extensions, French nails, and special shape plus tip it was almost $100. What was a little frustrating was they used a communal dip pot and for that much I would definitely expect a nice salon like this to be using individual powder. I love the manicure, it turned out great! Don't know if I would return based on the fact that it didn't feel totally hygienic, especially for what I was paying. 

Pros: beautiful nails, nice atmosphere 
Cons: pricey, unhygienic dip

This place was pretty alright. Unfortunately, there's a lot of construction going on in the area at this time, but if you pay close attention, you'll make it back there to the right spot.
Service was o-kay. When we walked in, I said "hi" to 2 different servers and got looks instead of hello's. You seat yourself, but it was a bit of time before someone acknowledged our table. Then we sat for a while before we had to ask our waitress for glasses of water. She did get the basics of our orders down though, and the food came out correct, for the most part.
I will say, the food was pretty good. I stuck with a safe selection - hot wings. Your typical buffalo wing sauce, but they fried them up nice and crispy, and that made them more delicious. My friend got their Loaded Rocket-Tots, and they were GOOD! So glad she shared. I don't even like tater tots (it's a texture issue, lol), but these were delicious (texture aside)! Yummy jalapeño potatoes, cheese, egg... my goodness! You need a bite with everything together to understand the deliciousness! 
The decor was cool and beautiful at the same time. 
I would definitely return. Nice pub atmosphere. 
Still want to try their pretzels...

A new found favorite of mine. Not many ice cream shops makes malts anymore for whatever reason. I googled malt shops and Creole Creamery came up with great reviews so I gave them a try. Reviews were true with no exaggerations. My chocolate malt was thick, creamy and very delicious.

When my boss comes into town, I groan with the anticipation of a team dinner at McMahon's.  

For the record, I travel, a lot, and have eaten at many fine steakhouses (and I will say that Ruth Chris is way over rated), yet without a doubt the food at McMahon's is really sub par.

My last trip, I ordered the ribeye.  How hard do you have to work to screw up a dry aged ribeye?  Naturally, I ordered it medium rare (which is cool red center) and what came looked to be a NY Strip, poorly marbled, and as tough as shoe leather.  At least my dogs enjoyed it as part of their morning meal the next day.

The only redeeming feature is if you can get though the plainly mediocre meat, the fried strawberries are excellent.  Sadly, they can't lift the rating beyond one star.

If you want a decent steak in Tucson, there is a Flemmings, and a Sullivan's, both who have that "steakhouse" atmosphere, and, more importantly, great cuts of beef, cooked to perfection.

BTW, the best steak I ever had:  Metropolitan Grill, Seattle.

This place has so much potential, but fails to deliver.  First, let me start by asking why does one have to pay $5 to valet when most other restaurants in this area offer complimentary valet service.  I mean it's one thing if they actually had a parking lot and you were too lazy to want to park your vehicle yourself, but the lack of parking should be reason one as to offer complimentary service.  The décor is outstanding and the outdoor area, bar area, and he dining area have been designed very nicely and give this place a very upscale, elegant feeling.  

I had called two days prior to our visit to make a reservation for 6 people in our party.  I was told by the hostess that she would have to check with the manager first to see if a reservation could be secured, which I thought was odd given that it's usually in a hostesses job duties to make and manage reservations when customers call in.  I called back a few hours later as I never received a call back to confirm if they could or could not accommodate my reservation and was told by the same hostess that she was still waiting on the manager to authorize it.  Again, odd but ok whatever.  I finally received a call a few hours after that to tell me they could make the reservation, but it would be in the bar area and not the dining area.  Not ideal, but we really wanted to try this place out based on recommendations we had received from others.  

We showed up for our 8pm reservation and were told that the table we were to be seated at was finishing up so there would be a slight wait.  Not a big deal and we made our way to the bar to get drinks then sat outside to enjoy the outdoor area.  After numerous trips to the hostess stand and being told the table was still not ready, they scrambled to put two tables together in the main dining area that was half empty to accommodate our party.  Our 8pm reservation turned into being seated at 8:45pm.   Our server was quick to take drink orders and I must say their cocktail menu was varied and the Tea that I had was great.  Probably the highlight of my experience.  We all put in our orders and waited quite a bit for our food to finally arrive.  Two people in our party had to ask for plates as they were not able to eat the food the way it was presented without getting it all over the table.  I ordered the Shrimp Skillet, which I did not know meant shrimp that was still in the shell.  I spent more time trying to get my food to be edible than actually eating.  The portions and food for the price were disappointing.  

Overall, this place seems to be trying to have that Bocaesque feel and image but fails miserably.  I don't know if they are still working out kinks since they are new or just don't have a clue, but I fear for how long the novelty of this place will take it.

I just called the exotic plumeria regarding two plants that died. They were actually cuttings and not rooted plants. This was not disclosed to me. The other two plants I purchased were rooted, so they are doing well. I lot $90 on my purchase of the two large cuttings that died. Make sure you ask the if the plant is rooted, even if it is very large. If the fact that they were cuttings had been disclosed, they would not be dead. You have to care for them differently. I asked if they would at least replace one plant, and they would not.
Everytime I come here they take a hour to take my order and prepare my food. They also give me wrong food most the times

If I could put a 0 I would. We were just there today at 6:30pm drive through. Girl came on said give
Me a minute we waited and nothing for 5 minutes and when she does come back it's to the lane with a lady that pulled in after us. I wanted my husband to pull up to window and place our order first but he would not. I said leave and we did good thing because the lady was still giving her order and when we pulled around they had 4 cars waiting in line. Will never go back to this MacDonald's it sucks . Gave them another chance this time and they blew it. Get some competent people to work for you owner.

The view is great and our server Vinnie was awesome.  The food is very underwhelming.  Fish fingers are basically frozen fish sticks.   The fish tacos might have been ok except for the one tiny piece of fish and the rest was lettuce.  No sauce, no nothing else on them so they were really bland.  The chips and queso and chips and salsa were hardly a serving and came out of  a jar for sure.   No one in the group hated their food, but no one was really wowed by it either.  I did not have an adult beverage but those in our group said the same about the drinks... ok but nothing that stood out.

My project has been left incomplete for about 3 weeks now. Almost a month since it has been started. I have contacted this company 3 times and no one has responded. My house is missing shutters on one window, but the windows that do have shutters are too small. The SECOND project manager said this will be taken care of. 

I thought we were supposed to sign documents stating that the owners of the house approved the final outcome? Neither my husband, nor I have signed any documents.

I have to reach out to the BBB because this is unprofessional.


UPDATE - I finally spoke with a rep, and the custom shutters have just been ordered. Why weren't they ordered the day of, or following business day when I requested that they be fixed? It will now take an additional three weeks for my project to be completed. I will have to wait until then for my house numbers to be put on and my yard officially sweeped of all nails. I have found dozens of nails in my backyard where my dog plays.

I will not recommend this company to anybody.
