Right now just left the Wawa in Belmont Avenue, the biggest heavyset dark skinned it woman approached me saying I had to leave because I had so many candy bars in my hand, she called me a racial slur told me to get my white ass out of there it is 220 in the morning where of this place full of weirdos I watch the people who work there takes the rest when I leave without you noticing packs of them behind the register also I said to her what are you talking about I didn't take anything it was packed in there I said are you kidding me she said yeah I am I am she said oh your white ass out of here I will be contacting the yellow his company as he is good friends with my corporal detective up this area over the bridge my girl
If you love tacos, this is the spot. Almost 10 different meats to choose from plus fish.

I'm sorry to say we didn't have a very enjoyable evening. We walked in and it's a cute place the hostess was very friendly she said sit wherever you want. We went down and the very center table had a reserved tag on it. We sat down and ordered wine. My husband said it was very good. They brought bread and oil that to was very good. We're off to a great start. We order appetizers Caesar salad for me pasta fagioli for my husband. Meanwhile the reserved table guests come in. At this point the waitress starts spending more time at that table. We've waited a good 10-15 min for our appetizers.  Why such a long wait?  Mine came out of the frig because the bowl was cold and the croutons were soggy from sitting in a salad made previously the soup out of a pot. The soup instead of elbow past is made with spaghetti noodles broken up. It also looked green instead of red. We've had this soup in Boston and down here in Clearwater both places elbow pasta and red.  At this point the reserved table gets their food on their table clothed covered  table we're sitting at our sticky table still waiting for our dinner. Finally our food comes. It was so dim that we couldn't see what our meal looked like. My sauce was with olives and artichokes. There were 3 olives and 1 artichoke broken up. It was hot and was very good. The waitress keeps going over to the reserved table never asks us how our meal is. My husbands meal is usually made with spinach this was served with broccoli.  The reserved table got their check right away and left (hostess did talk with them as they left).   We wait for our check then wait for it to be picked up. We got to the restaurant at 535 1 couple was finishing their meal. We start the process and reserved table cones in and 1 other table soon after. I'm not sure who the reserved table was but it's pretty bad when such special treatment is so obvious to other diners. Once they left the tablecloth came off. we had a Groupon so the evening only cost $24 instead of $54
The seats were disgusting. Bring a tarp or some blankets to cover them and you will be fine.

The BBQ ribs and brisket were delicious. Sides are also very tasty. Cornbread is sweet, little crunch and moist.
Dining area is clean and atmosphere was pleasant. 
Will come back when I'm town.

Over rated.I you are in the immediate vicinity and have to have mexican food (tex-mex) sure thing, go there; but, I would under no conditions exert effort to go here again. Kinda blah, kinda the same as so many other places.

I used to eat at Niko's Place once or twice a week.... Not any more!
a. John (owner) removed the soup and salad bar, claiming it cost him too much to maintain.
    The soup and salad bar was my main reason for eating there.
b. Now they give you a medium 'Mediterranean style' salad bowl.
    The first time I had it it was very good. On my second visit the Mediterranean salad
    was 'mushy' (sat in the dressing for far too long.  VERY disappointed.
c. Also on my second visit, I ordered a bourbon steak from the $9.99 menu.
    When the steak with french fries arrived it was warm at best, (not hot of the grill).
So I don't frequent Niko's anymore. Also while I was there, many folks noticed that the salad bar was removed and left without being seated. Wonder how long it will remain open.

So so. Nothing special. Interesting that everyone that works at an asian place is not asian but Mexican. 
Quality of all you can eat is always a joke so can't expect a lot.  Staff/ manager strange group. Spanish being talked the entire time on floor and from the cooks. 
Bad atmosphere.

I have been enjoying meals at The Rose since the eighties. It's one of my favorite spots. The enchiladas verdes is one of their most delicious dishes and should not be missed. This restaurant is one of the best in Santa Barbara. The decor, the staff, the food, all of it, every bit, wonderful!!

Attended an impromptu wedding after-after party here within the Marriott and food and beverages were typical. Service was friendly and quick as we were the only ones posted up at the tables. Stayed open relatively late (for Florida on the weekend).

Oh, Sidney Street Caf! How I love you! Soulard has a ton of good places to eat, but most of them are just traditional American restaurants without a whole lot of interesting or innovative menu choices. I guess the people over at Sidney Street Caf decided they wanted to fill that void. The menu at Sidney Street Caf changes all the time, reflecting what kind of quality protein is available on any given day, so trying the specials is a must. I love the wasabi steak and oyster poorboys. Sidney Street Caf is dinner only and kind of pricey, so keep that in mind before you dine.

Amazing ! You can taste the freshness of the veggies , the broth of the Pho is perfection . The portions are super generous and just about everything I've tried on the menu was delicious. The staff is so friendly and the owner has such a beautiful spirit ,he literally smiled the  entire time we talked and it was so genuine.

Hotel was overbooked and didn't have the type of room we reserved (king with sofa bed). We were four people expected to sleep on one king bed? They offered to move us to another hotel.

No star for the management. One star for the gentleman from housekeeping who cobbled together a workable solution. Management would have just ignored our reservation and assumed that the inconvenience of being moved was no big deal (we were there for a family event and other family members were at the same hotel). 

The hotel was full, but I had "checked in online" to make sure my room was ready.  What's the point of having a reservation if it's not kept?

We've never had this kind of treatment at any other Marriott hotel. My next review is going to be to the corporate office.

This could very possibly have gotten four stars. But the elevators, man. The elevators. 

On the first day it took me 20 minutes to get up to my room. Second day i waited ten minutes for the elevator so I resorted to walking down the 18 floors of winding stairs.
On the third day I met some guys from a baseball team on my floor waiting for an elevator. They had these huge bags, and said they'd been waiting for 15 minutes. I was not doing that, so I walked down. They were right behind me, dragging down their huge bags. On the seventh floor I realized I forgot something in my room, and had to go back up again. That's ELEVEN FLOORS my friends. Not that I'm counting. 
Then again. I came back in at 11am, and no one had come to fix the elevators so I had to walk up the eighteen floors. That my dear Yelpers, is 29 floors climbed in one day and it's still not even noon. Can you believe I am so lucky that I have to pay money to climb these stairs? 
Should you still choose to stay here, the rooms are huge and the beds are comfortable. If you ever get to them.

I honestly don't understand what the hype about this place is. It feels like a glorified Boston Market in my opinion.

I went on a Friday night around 7:45 PM with a party of three. The place was packed, just like any restaurant on the weekend. 

When you first enter the restaurant, you're greeted by an overwhelming display of decor. It basically looks like an up-scale Cracker Barrel shop. I can understand the aesthetic appeal, but I don't understand why they wasted so much space with towering displays when they could barely accommodate customers. You weave back a short ways until you arrive at the counter.

The menu appears rather confusing at first, mostly because you are rushed to order due to the proximity to the staff. I'm sure it's easier to understand if you have a good chance to look it over. Fortunately, a member of my party had been to Chef's Market multiple times and was familiar with the system. 

There are two, large, glass cases, much akin to a cafeteria. One of the cases is for hot foods while the other is for cold. The cold case contains numerous fresh salads and options ranging from coleslaw to chicken salad. My party didn't spend much time there, so I can't exactly tell you about the quality of anything within it. 

The hot case contained many delicious looking foods. There were pork chops, some English pie, garlic mashed potatoes, green beans, vegetable casserole, sweet potatoes with brown sugar and pecans, and beef masala. In the end, I settled for Jerusalem chicken, macaroni and cheese, and wild rice pilaf. 

Your plate is placed onto a tray along with your drink and your bill, which you pay after eating. After seating ourselves, I glanced at the bill to find that my total was $13.50! It was an insanely high cost considering the quality and amount of food I received, plus the fact that I hadn't even ordered a drink. 

The food itself was mostly delicious. The macaroni was my favorite because of its creamy texture. The chicken was fairly good, but was dry in the center and had an surprising sweetness to it that some might enjoy. The rice pilaf tasted like it was straight out of an Uncle Ben's package. Another member of my party got hot chicken salad, which I found delicious. Additionally, their sweet teas are fantastic. 

After eating, we left our trays on the table, collected our receipts, and headed to a counter by the door to pay. The service was very fast. It only took us about three minutes to get our food and another three minutes to pay on the busiest night of the week. 

Overall, Chef's Market is not worth it at its price point. For $13.50 (which was the lowest bill in my party with others around $15.50) you could go to Long Horn and grab a steak. By doing so, you'd receive higher quality food and a more relaxing and professional atmosphere. Although the food was tasty at Chef's Market, the price was simply overwhelmingly high for the quality and quantity provided.

Some of the best Mexican food I've found since I've been in Tampa! I don't particularly like TexMex, I'm not a big fan of cheese and sour cream covering everything, but once I got past that it was delicious!!! I will be back

Should have listened to others. I don't recommend. Long wait. I got the jambalaya pasta. Big potion, no flavor tasted watered down. Cashier was friendly. Would not recommend but aren't many other options in this terminal. Biscuits were good.

I am so glad they are building a new car wash on 22nd Ave.  Mariner carwash need some competition.  Mariner car wash does a terrible job.  They have terrible customer service and because they have no competition they don't aim to please they just want your money.  You're better off driving through the gas station car wash then spending your money at mariner!

The best bang for your buck for apartments in Germantown. The apt is seasonally priced and the apts seem to be bigger than the others in the area. There is plenty of parking for guests in the garage or on the street. You get the benefit of being in Germantown and 1.5 miles from downtown but feel like you're in a great neighborhood. Better than being in the congestion closer to Jefferson.

Pizza Hut has gone up beyond my wildest expectations in quality. The stuffed crusted has become so wonderful. It doesn't stop there either. The pizza is just so good. Even trying to think while typing this gets my mouth watering. It is worth what you will spend. I gained about 20lbs during  winter living off it. Sure it's work to get off that weight later but oh em gee was it worth it. I encourage everyone to give this place a try.
A good cheesesteak sandwich; great bread, decent meat, decent fries.  Despite the superior bread though, I'd go for Pat's instead next time I have the chance...

Amazingly organized! The Santa Barbara Botanical Garden is a great way to spend an hour and a half. The garden isn't huge but pretty decently sized and great for kids. The selection of plants is comprised of California native plants, their Redwood section is amazing. If there's one section to visit it's that one. Admission is $16 and if you are a student, don't forget your student ID because you can get in for $10! There are two sides to the garden so you can make it a nice easy hike. Also be sure to bring close toed shoes as you should on any hike.
The place is clean and spacious. Some of the employees have been really helpful. For a post office, it's convenient. I find the wait times better than most post offices.

I had back surgery and my dr. was great but the nursing staff at this hospital is the worst I laid in the bed and could not get help and when they did come they were rude and did the lest possible. I could not get my pain meds. and when I ask for water you would think I was asking for gold. One nurse named Grace was awful she let me know I got my pain meds when she decided not when they were due. If at all possible do not go to this hospital. My surgery was July 2013 and I still have bitter felling about the treatment I was given there.

The pizza is ok. Nothing spectacular. Nice staff. Pizza has a little too much bread and grease for my taste. Not NY in my option. Will definitely scratch the itch if you want a slice. Out of a ten, 6.1.

Margarita Tuesday!!! It was a coworkers birthday and we chose to go there after work to party. The atmosphere was boring and no juke box to play music.   There was only one bartender so I didn't expect service to be there. When the bill came and I read my receipt. She charged me full price on margarita that are half off and add a few more drinks to my tab.   I called her out on the mistake so she can fix it. Instead of just fixing her mistake and call the manager.  She said she can't fix the mistake and would give me free drinks.  I have to drive.  I can't drink additional 4 drinks and drive home.  I can't afford dui and it was snowing.   I honestly don't think it's free drinks if I'm being over charged from the beginning.   I ordered 4 more drinks and I tipped her.  Instead of saying thank you and apologizing, she took the money and walked away as if she deserved it.  I'm never coming again and I want everyone to check their receipt.

After many times visiting Founding Father's for food and drinks, I decided to switch it up and try their ahi tuna tacos. This is more than anything a cautionary tale for others. It's the only thing I've eaten there I haven't liked.

The tacos were served on cold flour tortillas (WHAT). The ahi tuna was dry and over seared contrary to the menu's "lightly seared" promise.

I didn't finish them and kicked myself from straying from my favorite, Cajun Chicken Sandwich.

Go to Founding Father's, order a beer off their awesome draft list, and order literally any other entree on the menu but the ahi tuna tacos.

How can UA have no reviews?!

...oh wait! There are a few locations listed with no differentation here.

Ah well.

Love this institution! I have earned two degrees already and am working on my third. The institution has some great museums and arts performances a range of athletic events and some decent restaurants (try Redington at the Union).

So many of my faculty have been good supporters in my development as a student and professional (Go College of Ed and J-school!)
Surprisingly, it has very good quality of cuisine! Menu even for vegans. Nice service! Bravo!

I wanted to like this place more. It was my first visit. My friend's Caesar salad looked great -  full plate with plump shrimp. My Smashed Meatball sandwich was less impressive. The meatballs, cheese, and sauce on the sandwich were skimpy for $12. This could have been much more generous given the price. I will probably try again, but am not eager to.

Their food is Delicious. Service is fast.
They aren't the friendliest, but hey I come here to eat not socialize with staff.
There's a reason for the 5 star average.  Everything we had was fresh and legit,  all homemade. Lamb is standout,  as chops or on a salad. Ate there twice while in town.

My experiences at Oscar's have been consistently good, full of attentive-enough service, cheap drinks, and the traditional dive bar energy you need in your life. 

Visits to Oscar's are often after getting food nearby, or before going out dancing for an evening. It's typically more a pit stop for me than a destination which I think is unfair to Oscar's. I can always rely on getting a table, and love the outdoor space they've set up. The family that runs this establishment is welcoming and will even bring out a water bowl for your pup, if you're drinking outside. 

I typically avoid cocktails at dive bars, but I am a huge fan of Oscar's Mango Martini. Worth every penny of the $9 they charge - not too sweet, not too strong but just two of them will do their job.

Minus one star because my first experience here wasn't great, but that was because of our server. After taking an order for our third, maybe fourth round... she let us know that if we wanted to order additional drinks that we would all have to order at once. The only time we didn't do that was when someone else joined our party and asked for a drink in between rounds... who knew that was such a crime? I have not seen her since, so I take that as a sign that she no longer works there and that is for the best.

Call ahead and place your order before they sell out for the day.  We tried ordering on a Friday around 7 and boom sold out.  The next day we called around noon for the happy hour special and our order was ready in about 45 minutes.  The wait is no big deal if you plan accordingly.  Happy hour is daily from 12 to 3 and costs $8.50 for a wing, thing, breast, and leg.  I'd call that a steal.  I was quite full from the 4 pieces and didn't even order sides.  We got an order of the original and sweet chili.  The skin was very crispy.  The meat was fresh and juicy.  The sweet chili is a must order flavor.

So this would be excellent as strictly a bar. Back to back nights of walking into the establishment and there's absolutely no seating in the main area. Which this wouldn't be an issue, but we were told they only served the full menu of food on the first floor. Everyone at the tables were just drinking. No hostess. The setup is just bad. Like I said, if it was a bar it would be excellent, but the "amazing steaks" can't even be eaten.

I don't think it would be a huge deal but we went in back to back days and absolutely zero room to sit down and enjoy a meal. Don't serve food if you can't even serve people. There's a standing room at the top if people want to enjoy a drink. I actually saw no one eating because drinkers were occupying all tables.

Cute place. Sadly, the potatoes were terrible and found bacon in them which is not ideal for a vegetarian. The waitress did not check in at all so i was not able to even get new potatoes. Not air conditioned, lots of flies. Also saw a cake from giant in the bakery case ?

I looooove ME Sum Blenders!!!

I am headed there now so here's my update! 

As far as options, they have added quite a few over the years plus have seasonal faves...

Definitely check their side poster board menu of new smoothie/drink options... 

Some cool healthy ones with coconut water mark a different blends called antioxidant recovery trim fit etc. also Acai bowls too! Take a look! Make sure to say GO SOY if you don't want dairy. They have a frozen soy blend...it's great! Wish they would do coconut though as I'm tech a non soy also person but I suffer it gladly to drink my mmmmmmm smoothies!!! 

Nice options for those of us who have had it since Art and the gang opened it! I've been addicted since...I literally get 2-3 most visits...I like them when I don't have the right smoothie etc ingredients I need at home or the blending power on the road...

Definitely worth loading a card with $30 or more to get a benefit if you visit 10 or more times in a month or just to save 10% of the overall... 
Convenient and it's a good idea for us fully or even semi frequent buyers!
Make sure you reload the card before you buy your smoothie. It can seem tedious, but clearly ask to transact that first then order the drinks, it's the only way it works well for the savings!!! :)
Please recycle your card...save them when they're empty and fill them when you can..

And..hooray, the cups are back to be reused after a long wait during covid to not use them!!! I've had mine clean and waiting so buy one and bring it each time for a savings and eco reasons! Cheers! 

Off to get my fave smoothies! 
Ask to see the secret menu too...it's fun to change it up!

This place is a good old fashioned Italian restaurant.  Order up some wine and some mussels, sit back and enjoy the nice and cozy atmosphere.  For my entree, i went with Rosemary Chicken Lasagna,  bellissimo!

This was a COVID visit and they handled it very well. 

You call your order in advance, then when you arrive you call again to let them know you've arrived. When I arrived, the young lady running orders out was already making deliveries to other patrons and just stopped by car to ask which order was mine. When she returned with my order, she used the passenger side, so we did not have to touch each other passing the bag of food. The lady was friendly, detailed and was hustling to take care of all the cars waiting for carry-out. 

Being new to the neighborhood, this was my first visit. I ordered, an Egg Roll, Crab Rangoon and General Chicken with Fried Rice. I was not impressed by any of the items. My Egg Roll was over cooked. The pockets on the Crab Rangoon's were small and contained a very small portion of crab. My entree, General Chicken was very dry and was more breading then chicken. The Fried Rice was, just nasty. 

I'm still searching for a good Chinese restaurant in my new hometown. I may circle back to China Wok, to give them a second chance, but until then, I'll continue my search. 

When: December 14, 2020
Why: Dinner - Curbside

Nice big place with high roofs and 2 bars. It is a great place for a big group to hang out. You can also go up to the second floor bar and watch a game when it is on (I went there to watch Usain Bolt win his 6th gold medal)

I hate my boss. I mean, I hate him.  Not for the ordinary reasons that people hate their bosses; in fact, he's one of my favorite people out there (I love you KSB). No, I hate my boss because, in addition to the other benefits of being a super badass who has earned enough to enjoy the finer things in life as a matter of course, more often than not on any given Friday afternoon, he can be found at Ruth's.  I have had the good fortune not only to have the opportunity to learn from the master, but to have been  taken to his famous Friday afternoons at Ruth's a few times.

Ruth's cuts the best steak you can eat at a restaurant.  They cook it precisely to order (even obeying my admonition: "take off the bell, wipe her ass, and walk her through a warm room"), lavish it in garlicy buttery ooey gooey goodness, and serve it up sans accompaniment.  I love that they prize their steaks enough to serve them solo - that volumes.  Their side dishes can stand up to the steaks, though - I ADORE the creamed spinach, the mashed potatoes, and the stuffed mushrooms.

The service is stellar, the windows are blocked out (very convenient for day drinking with the boss), and the whole ambiance is pure elegance.  I would recommend Ruth's to anyone.

This has to be my favorite local bar for good drinks, great music and memorable nights. The bartenders are amazing, especially Tyelor! But unfortunately I recently had an undesirable experience with one of the bartenders named Nick. During this particular visit, he was unprofessional, rude and unpleasant. Although the matter at hand, which should have not been dealt with so much negativity, was handled and resolved in a professional manner by Tyelor. This experience has placed a hindered on my visits to your establishment as I do not want to experience such behavior again. The reason I still gave three stars is because this was one infelicitous experiences versus the many enjoyable experiences I have had at Bradley's.
Thank you Towner for literally catering to our particular taste with the Charbroiled Oysters!! The atmosphere was quiet and enjoyable, but the food was AMAZING! and so was the service!

Had a blueberry/walnut/goat cheese/greens salad..it was very tasty but really small for the price. I wanted an espresso but their machine was broken :( I would go here again with a large group so we could try all different kinds of pizza..I think the pizza is a better value than the salad. Overall great vibes, good location across from St. Joes bar and next to Whole Foods, so you can get groceries after lunch like I did :) Next time I also want to try the cinnamon bread pudding!

Crispy's was a pleasant surprise.  I am a big fan of fish and chips and this is the best I have found so far in the area.

This place is nothing fancy at all.  The dining furniture consists of plastic picnic table/benches all in ones.  The menu is very basic and they have fish, chicken, wings, and shrimp.  The sides are fries, coleslaw, and potato salad and a few others.  The drinks are all canned sodas.

When we arrived most of the tables were occupied with people waiting for to go orders and was fairly busy.  Busy usually means good and with the prior reviews we had high hopes.  We were not disappointed.  

I went with the four piece cod combo that had fish, fries, coleslaw, and a canned soda.  My wife went with the three piece chicken combo.  We split an order of hush puppies that was served in a basket that had several in it (largest portion we have ever seen).  The food is cooked to order so it tastes fresh.  Even the fries were a winner here.  It might take a little bit longer but it's worth the wait.  As I watched my food prepared I could see they were doing a little extra in the preparation of it and they were taking pride in what they were doing.  

The cost was 20 bucks for two and we were full, satisfied, and totally enjoyed our meal.  The owner took the time to ask us how we liked it.  I would rate this as the second best fish and chips I have had in the state of Arizona and the best in Tucson.
  
We will eat here again.  As the saying goes don't judge a book by its cover.  Well don't go here looking for a fine dining restaurant.  The food is really good and if you want a great tasting fish and chips this is the place!

3.5
Stopped in today with a group of co-workers to order take out. Pretty excited there is a gyro place right around the corner now! You order at the counter then sit down and they bring you your order. Lots of seating but the largest seating area was a 4 top..I'm sure it wouldn't be a problem to push together additional tables. 

Looking at the menu my first thought was...hm no lamb gyro? So I stuck with the lamb souvlaki sandwich. Second thought was that the service was slow...it wasn't terrible, as I realize we were waiting for 6 individual dishes..The longer I waited the more excited I got. We were standing near the kitchen while waitresses walked by us with STUFFED sandwiches, zucchini chips (which I will definitely try next time). 

Upon returning to the office, everyone was surprised at how small the sandwiches were.  Ours were definitely not loaded like the people who dined in..this was surely disappointing. I was happy that my special request was granted for no onions and extra tzatziki however let down when the last quarter of my sandwich only contained tomatoes and frozen waffle fries. 

Overall it is reasonably priced, the space is clean and bright, service was friendly. I'd definitely go back but I think I'll dine in to see if the experience is any better.

To my lactose intolerant peeps, I don't know if this is just a me thing, but the options I have for milk teas do me dirty every. single. time.

I ordered a thai milk tea with coconut creamer (I've tried the soy and almond milk before and they just make the tea super watered down and lacking in flavor) but this was even worse than the other two. It turned the tea this sickly dark orange color, and the creamer powder wasn't even mixed properly, leaving a grainy residue on the sides of the cup as well as in my mouth. I specifically asked for 50% sugar, but it still was so overpoweringly sweet I could barely manage a few sips before I gave up. Paying almost a whole dollar extra for badly mixed creamer powder in an overly sweet drink was so not worth it.

Now onto the service: I don't expect much from college towns, we all have better things to do and other places to be, but the guy taking my order couldn't even look me in the eye. I had to repeat myself many times before he registered what I was saying. It's just common courtesy, bro. 


I was really glad when they opened a boba place in iv, but over the past year, I've noticed a significant decline in customer service and quality of the products. It's really sad to see a business with so much potential losing the spark that drew people to it in the first place.

New place but truly not worth the time and money.. waiter extremely rude.. they made such a big deal because my husband was wearing sandals and a dressy shirt... this is florida!! It's hot!! You're in a side walk.. but at another table 3 girls wearing shorts and bikini tops and sandals were ok??...when I asked the host he said.. they were here since brunch, mind you we came for dinner at 5pm... I would never come back here..

I very much dislike posting unfavorable reviews.  So, Joey K's, this is going to hurt me more than it hurts you.

Very unremarkable overall. Dim lighting and kitschy signs all by the same dude and perfectly straightened with a level lead to a surprisingly sterile and vaguely unsettling environment.  My fries were fresh, but my shrimp platter tasted thawed and/or reheated. Clientele is cops, old folks, people who tuck their tshirts into their jeans, and sad looking couples. My most concise description would be New Orleans' answer to Bob Evans.  I see literally no compelling reason to come here regularly.
Always get a bean and cheese burrito with some of their organic Root beer!! Never disappoints. One of my favorites for sure!!!
Only good thing that came outta Sal's was the $10 all you can drink Friday nights...

Finally a legit taco joint that makes some seriously bomb mexican food.
 I am so over the traditional mexican restaurant that serves the same bland red rice with a ladle full of canned refried beans and cheese and a chefs tostada. Don't get me wrong its good but its not really authentic. My Dutch in laws could make the same meal that most mexican restaurants put out. These joints are easy to find and are everywhere.

La Super-Rica is not one of these joints.  Its actually pretty hard to find come to think of it. You can easily drive by it and miss it if it wasn't for the long line crowding the sidewalk that flags you down.  I spoke with the owner of the place while he was taking my order. You know a mexican food place is going to be good when you see an old mexican man who is the owner actually taking your order and over seeing the whole kitchen. He told me how he moved here from Jalisco Mexico and started this restaurant using all his mothers recipes as well as using some of the local flavors to mix it up a little.

Enough small talk, lets talk food.......I always hate when vegans review restaurants like this place and give it 3 stars but never order anything with meat on it. Your reviews are ridiculous. Uuuugghh this bugs me. Anyways we ordered about 6 different dishes and all of them were amazing in their own way. Take the #7 for example. Grilled Tri-Tip sliced with sautéed onions and bell peppers all mixed together with crispy pieces of bacon!!!!! Are you kidding me???? So good. This entree came with three tortillas. By the way all of their tortillas are fresh and made to order right in front of you. Another delicious treat was the #16 which was by far our favorite. Seasoned pork shredded and mixed together with grilled chili relleno peppers covered with cheese and placed on top of two tortillas. Along with these we ordered a side of guac, pork tacos and an unforgettable tamale stuffed with veggies and covered with melted queso. I can't say enough about this place. I truly believe they serve the best mexican food in the whole state of CA and I put there tacos second to none in flavor and authenticity. 
Cant wait to go back.
Food was okay at best. Didn't live up to hype. Girlfriend said the place also needed a bath.

The Drake is known for their burgers and they are all good. This is a medium sized pub style restaurant located on a tight corner with a small parking lot. Worth a visit for a weekday lunch or really anytime the burger and beer craving hits. Lots of drink specials daily. Visited here several times with my family members and our advice is stick to the pub style fare with the wide selection of burgers and sandwiches.

I've been going to Amethyst salon for two years. This place is amazing. It's like my own hidden salon. 

The service that I receive is waxing. My services each time are phenomenal. I leave here happy and satisfied. After each appointment I plan to continue and go back. 

The staff, even though I have only worked with Mary, the other staff the greets me every time I walk through the door. They have all been courteous and nice. As for Mary, she is a wonderful person who is amazing. She has a passion for her job and her clients. She is the reason I continue to make appointments. 

The environment is comforting and bright. The waiting area is in with the single hair station in the salon. 

What they offer at amethyst salon is nails, pedicures, waxing and the newest hair. They have other products there like medusa makeup. 

The price for waxing is very reasonable. I have not found a place that matches the price of Amethyst Salon!!

I would recommend people to try this place! I for sure will go back!

Picked up (half trays of)  chicken 65 and Chicken Biryani over the long weekend! Both of them were excellent - right amount of spiciness and flavor. The food was hot and was ready to pick up on time. Have not tried their lunch buffet. Will do so soon..

This place is delicious but I give it a 3 because it is way overpriced! I spent 20$ on breakfast here. I understand it's not going to be 6$ like other cheap breakfast places because the quality is considered better, but 20$?! I've been to high end eateries in Chicago and NY that were still way cheaper than that! Lower the prices and I'll happily give it 5 stars.

OMG!! Great food bursting with flavor and texture and  presentation. Appetizer and Entree were beautiful! Outstanding service from Dustin.  Luscious libations!! Ahi tuna was succulent with subtle flavors that compliment the food, lamb shank was tender with rich, bold flavors.... Yummy!!
I absolutely love this wing spot! The food is always fresh and delicious ! I must have whenever I'm in town

This is a block from my job so it's very convenient. I usually get the 2 for $20 with co-workers. I have tried to call ahead to save time to arrive and someone has lost our order. So you have to order then sit there. I've eaten there a few times at lunch. It is always empty so we're quickly seated. One time the service was horrible and another superb. It's so inconsistent, hit or miss. The one time that my order was not forgotten over the phone I went directly to the to go side to pay for my order. Someone that looked like a manager (not wearing the usually apron or shirt servers wear but black pants and shirt) was on the phone on a personal call. Didn't stop his call and totally ignored me. One of the servers took pity on me and checked me out but whispered to not disturb the call. WTH!
Always a great place for tire service!!! Would not take my car anywhere else!  Thank you boys, you are greatly appreciated!

Great Balls...I dont like to give out 1 star reviews but this place was pretty awful, almost everything we had was nasty. The only thing they didnt make totally awful was some broccoli in general tsaos sauce so maybe thats ok here. The fried rice also was manageable but its tough to mess up.

The soup was a watery oily mess, the mongolian beef was tough and stringy and the lo mein had no seasoning. 

I also wasnt feeling too good after I ate all this after a bad decision to get lunch here post hangover. Stick to the other chinese food place thats in close vicinity on canal street.

Came here again this week for lunch. Got the gazpacho and lechon asado. Good, but not as good as the Cuban sandwich I had last week. 

Game to try their other dishes really soon too.

Had brisket, chicken, and ribs, with sides of mac & cheese and baked potato casserole.  The brisket was very good.  However, the chicken was too dry, the ribs were dry and tasted very salty.  Mac & cheese way way way too much cheese  (could be good for children??) .  Would not return.  However, the servers are very nice, service was good, and the place is small but cute.
Good place for beer and bite to eat. The staff is friendlier than most. The food is good, nothing to brag about that I've had other than the chili. The chili is great!

When I read the reviews I thought this place was going to be delicious honestly it was just ok I went there the first time and it was a 45minute wait outside ...there was still a line inside. The next morning I came back and got breakfast. I'm glad I didn't stay in that long line there were 3 pieces of shrimp in my omelet way to expensive for that out the service was great

If the actual service could match the food then it would be a 10/10 but sadly our experience was poor. 

As soon as we walked in we kind of just stood there because there was no host stand or host! So it felt odd just kinda standing in the middle of the walkway waiting for someone to attend us. When they finally ask us how many we are and if we had a reservation, we said no. They kinda looked around and made it seem like there wasn't enough tables, when funny enough there was only 3 tables seated outside and only one inside..

Anyways we get seated and about 5 minutes later the waitress finally comes and just offers water, doesn't ask to take a drink order just asked if still, sparkling or house water would be fine and walked away. Ok? When she finally returns to take our drink order I tell her that we are also ready to order our food, she walks away again this time not saying anything? I thought made she just needed to grab something but no she walks away and starts filling up water jugs and even goes outside. She returns about 5 minutes later and acts like nothing. I understand how it is to work in a restaurant, I've been a hostess, food expo, and a server. When I see the place is busy or people are flagging the server down, I get it! But when you keep walking away as we are trying to order that I don't understand. 

We get our food and before any of us even touch it she comes to ask how's it tasting. I let her know it literally just touched the table and we can use some ketchup. Also my boyfriends fork was dirty and covered with chocolate we had to ask for some different silverware. She never came back to ask how everything was tasting once we actually started eating. Never came back to ask if any of us wanted another Bellini or a different type of drink. Me as a server I would be trying to sell you as many drinks as possible or anything else to make that bill go up because I know that will most likely mean a bigger tip. I guess nowadays people assume just because they are waiting on someone the service doesn't matter and they will still be getting a tip. Sorry but that's not how a view it. You work for what you want and that is also a damn good tip! 

I will be back just because the food was so good. But maybe next time I can add a few stars, that is of course if the service gets any better.

Had a pretty good lunch here today, they have an extensive list of lunch specials on the back of the menu where you can get a platter and choice of soup for around $6.

I had the Three Pepper Chicken, which (like all of their dishes) is served with a large side of white rice, just enough to balance the heat of the spicy and salty soy sauce marinade. Each piece of chicken was stir-fried to a reasonable crispiness and the peppers (the milder, edible ones) had a nice char to them. Mixed with wilted green onions, charred garlic slices, and small bits of ginger (I think), I would order this dish again. With a starter of soup, it's an offer you can't refuse.

Only problem was, my dining mates each ordered the General Tso's Tofu and their dishes came out 20 minutes after my dish. I sat there, letting my plate reach room temperature before I began to fully enjoy it. Okay, so the food was pretty good all around, but when you're serving a table of 3, who are obviously not interested in "family style" dining at lunch, make sure you bring the food at the same time - heck even within 8 minutes would have been acceptable!

I was encouraged by a friend to visit and try both the shrimp cocktail and a ribeye. The homemade cocktail sauce was fabulous in that it reacted with both by spence of taste and smell and a little sight as my eyes watered just a tad.  The ribeye was perfect and melted in my mouth.  As a single dinner, I at at the bar and the staff was fantastic.  A yesteryear dinning experience highlighted by the staff and deco.   I had enjoyed an old fashion for my drink and it was smooth.

We walked in and were greeted in a brisque manner by the hostess. We opted for the bar; the bartender waited 5 minutes to greet us despite the fact that there was only one other person in the entire restaurant. I asked for a bloody mary, and the bartender just shook her head no. Apparently, my request didn't dignify a response. Needless to say, we did not stay. The restaurant looked great inside, and I am sure the food is excellent, but I prefer to be treated with more courtesy when I go out to eat.

For the people that have posted bad comments about pet palace are wrong they are excellent people with great customer service I have been taking my dog for 3 years now and they have never done me wrong the only way a place does your wrong if you go in with a sour attitude then your service is gonna be sour and that is any where that you go they are great people

The anti pasta was complimentary and was right up my alley. I would go back just for it and some drinks. The drinks were great too. The service was super fast and my Manhattan was on point.

They really do feed you into submission in this joint but If when I go back I will probably stick with the pasta dishes since our main courses which were specials that night were OK even when everything else was terrific.

BTW - this place was packed on a Friday night and it was only about 6:30. I have not been to Penns Grove often but I bet there is not much else around. Its like a diamond in the rough!

Oh my goodness what a fun place. From the moment I step into Need 2 Speed it felt fun. Was greeted by a smiling and joyful employee to see how she could help. I was there with my girl friend snd her too young grand sons. We started off with racing there electric go carts around a track for two miles. The employees attention to safety and explanation of the carts to all three of us, myself and the two grandsons were in the first race. Not sure how many times around the track is fir the two miles but it goes on snd on. Just a whole lot of fun, the carts were quick and responsive.  I did sign up the boys up for a virtual video game they had, which they both loved. Need 2 Speed offers the usual swag, so we pick up a couple t shirts for them to remember the day. They went back out for another race and another go at the virtual video game as well. Just one heck of a fun afternoon. I would definitely recommend Need 2 Speed, great afternoon

This is my go to place for coffee and snack 3-4 times per week for over two years. The staff in the bakery are each as good as they come.  And...they never forget a name. We have catered events through AJs, bought lots of flower arrangements, unusual gifts, enjoyed sushi, used the back wine room and more.  Love this place!

My husband and I eat here frequently. It is close to our house, they serve a good variety of tasty food, and have good service. They are also one of the few sit down restaurants in Spanish Springs. 
They have quite a few staff members who have been there awhile, and are great at remembering the regulars. The new staff also seems to work hard at remembering the clientele that frequent the restaurant.
The menu has something for everyone...several types of burgers, salads, sandwiches (hot or cold), seafood, steak, and Mexican food. They serve breakfast also (until 2:00pm). The restaurant portion is not very big, but we never have trouble finding a seat (you can also eat in the bar area). Rarely have I had a disappointing meal here. Only two times come to mind...once the burger was way overcooked and another time the Caesar salad was drowned in dressing. The staff is quick to refill drinks and check on you. 
My husband and I don't drink so I can't say much about the bar, but patrons seem to enjoy themselves. They are often playing sports games on the TVs, they have slots at the bar, and I enjoy the music they play. I have also ordered take out food from the bar. The service is always friendly, fast, and they always include condiments (ranch dressing, mayo, salt, pepper, etc.) as well as silverware with your order. This is impressive to me as I can never seem to get a fork with my salads from fast food restaurants! 
There is also a small game arcade in the back corner for kids. 
Overall this is a great place to eat with reasonable prices. They offer a senior discount as well. The restaurant and restrooms are also consistently clean.

I was very excited to try this place out. We happened to be at the Indian grocery store next door and my husband and I had some time on hand, so decided to try it for dinner.
They had decent lineup of veg and non-veg dishes for the dinner buffet. We both filled up our plates and sat to enjoy the food. But the food was literally cold. The naan were stale - my husband even joked saying that he wonders if these are still from the lunch buffet. We both really are not picky and I would have still given them 5 star if they handled the situation decently. But unfortunately they did not. For me customer service comes before the food. When my husband complained that the food is not warm, I believe the owner said they we didn't stir it. And then when I said that the naan taste cold and stale, his answer was, "I can't do anything about that, but I can bring new naan for you". Really? You couldn't do anything about that??? How about a simple apology and a better attitude of handling such situations? They did bring us fresh naan (after waiting for 5 minutes), increased the flame on their chafing dishes, stirred the dishes, but left a sour taste in our mouths for their customer service.
I am not going to say that we won't be going back (although we really might not)....we might give them another try (maybe), but I would like them to improve on their customer service. When a customer complains, handle it well, please.

Group of us went last Saturday night and really enjoyed the experience.  They were having a crawfish festival with huge boils and inventive crawfish specials.  There was a huge crowd, but the service handled the situation.  We had a great time, and we will be going back.
I like the grandmother's special dumplings soup,  pho is fine,  good noodle bowls. The pho at the new Pho Viet Hoa a mile away is even better though.

so my friend IMs me and says "hmmmm pizza.  yuuummmm Woodstock's"  That was a few weeks ago and so last weekend when I felt bad b/c I had to leave on a sunday night for work we had pizza for lunch on Sat.  

We got a medium, with one soda it was about $24.  We ordered half Mediterranean and half Pesto on wheat.  Both were yummy, but I thought the Mediterranean was on the salty side.  

We were there at 11:30 and I was surprised to see kids already there watching the game on TV.  I guess it's that sortof place, eat, hang out, watch the game.  not bad.

Let me start by saying I've been here twice- the first massage was meh and the second was even worse (both massages I did not get my full time that I paid for ) I don't really like that especially when I'm paying for it by the minute- (also I took my husband with me to the second time and he said his massage was also meh)
I'm extremely picky with my massages so I guess I'll just stick with professional massages (they say you pay for what you get) and in this aspect I blame the school because maybe their students are not ready to be out in the clinic yet as I've been to other massage schools and have never had a bad massage. I gave this review 3 stars because it's a school and I wanted to be kind in my rating.

Its very clean in the inside. They have great customer service. The chef Nafice wore gloves while preparing everyones food. Which is very important to me. He also checked ID's when paying with a credit card. To prevent FRAUD.  Which also important to me. I really love this place. My food was hot. And my order was correct. I appreciate the service.

While the french windows and tiny tables give the ambiance of being in Paris (?), the service was super slow and I was starving by the time my food arrived. How long does it take to make french fries and scoop soup du jour into a bowl? The food is good but I do think it's over priced. The waiters were nice but super slow.

When this location opened, I was super excited! I can always get hot coffee but they run out of breakfast sandwiches very quick and their night staff is limited so you wait almost 25 minutes for a simple sandwich. Last night they didn't even make my sandwich and about 30 minutes after I placed my order I had to ask. And while they apologize for the slowness, it's not their fault. It's the managers fault. You can't staff a wawa so poorly at night in a area full of bars and drunk people who want good food late at night.  The employees do the best they can but when there is one person behind the deli at midnight, bad things are going to happen and it's not fair to them or the customers!  This is the worst wawa I've ever been too. One cashier and a line of people out the door.  Half the doors are locked so you have to walk around to get in.
We enjoyed our Reubens-  top quality ingredients. Their homemade chips are wonderful. We really enjoyed their breakfast choices too. Great biscuits!! Strongly recommend this place.

Went here because the location was ideal for meeting up with friends. 

The salad bar was the best part. The prime rib was terrible. 

Go for the salad - not for the steak.

my dream of finding a fresh and fast delivery sushi restaurant in mayfair has finally come true.  Sushi is delicious, special rolls are really good and its convieniant.   Definitely a good find and our new go to sushi place.
Don't bother making an appointment; it isn't any faster. Just show up as a walk-in if you have to go.

We ordered delivery. We were told it would take 45 minutes to an hour. It took an hour and 25 minutes.  I called after an hour to inquire, but the woman who answered didn't seem to care. Said it's on the road somewhere.  When the food finally arrived, it was cold and did not taste good. Very unhappy with service and quality of food. Wont be ordering again.

I've used three different tailors in Philadelphia; the first two were decent, but their quality declined over time.  I've been going to Sarah for the past five years and she's hands down the best, and by far the most consistent.  Sarah's honest and she doesn't mince words.  If she says she won't alter something, it's because the alteration will ruin the garment.  Other tailors haven't been as honest with me and the results were unwearable.  Sara was educated as a fashion designer so she's a great seamstress and she knows how clothing will drape and fit if alterations are made.  She's altered shirts, pants, and suits for me and the results have always been as good or better than I'd hoped for.  There's no better tailor in Philadelphia.

I decided to try Signature because my manager had always have a good experience here. When I went in, I said I wanted to try dip powder nails with tips, and the woman ushered me after her and sat me down at a station. A few minutes later, another lady came over and asked if I was being helped, but I hadn't actually been told anything, so I assumed she was my manicurist. At first, they were friendly and offered me a drink, and the initial experience was quite nice. However, exactly one week later, I was pulling tissue paper out of a shoe, and one of my nails bent in half. All of my friends have dip powder and assured me that should not have happened. I immediately called Signature, told them what happened, and made an appointment for the following day at 10am to get it fixed. 
When I walked in, I explained that I needed a nail fixed and that I had made an appointment the night before, to which they said that they didn't have my appointment, and that they apparently don't make appointments for one nail. So who took my appointment?! Then they said that it would be $10 to fix it, but I said it shouldn't have happened and that I shouldn't have to pay to fix a poor job done by the salon. They responded that a broken nail was my fault and that it wasn't a chipped polish or whatnot (dip powder isn't polish!). They then said that there was no one available (so who would have taken me before?), and that I'd have to wait for the manager, who wouldn't be in for an hour. So I decided to go sit in my car for an hour for the manager to get there. 
When I went back, the lady at the front desk immediately started talking to her coworkers in another language, but I don't have to speak it to know she was talking smack about me. They then ignored me for five minutes before someone finally told me she would let the manager know I was there and that I could sit and wait for him. Instead, I stood off to the side of the counter and watched them seat person after person as I waited. After 20 minutes, I asked her to remind the manager that I was waiting, at which point she said he wasn't even there and that I would have to wait for him. Another guy came over, and I asked if he could get the manager on the phone and get an eta, but he said the mgr wasn't available and that I would just have to wait. I asked if there was anyone else who could deal with this situation, and he said no and blamed my attitude!! Well, you've given me the run-around for an hour and a half, so I wonder why?! They were so rude to me that I burst into tears as soon as I got in my car. 
I ended up having to go to a different salon, who were able to get a near match, so now I have a slightly different "party nail" on my middle finger, which is exactly what I want to give to Signature. 
I work in a service-centered retail environment, and never before have I been treated, nor have I treated anyone I was helping as poorly as I was treated here. They blamed me for the poor job they did, then then tried to charge me extra to fix their error. That is not how a business should treat its customers, and I will never go back.
Pad thai here is awesome!  But, the service was somewhat lacking and so I can't give more than three stars.  I might come here again but that would only be if Penang was totally packed.
Decent dinner food but nothing over the top. My wife had the vegetarian skillet and liked it. Good for kids, service was good too.

Came during lunch time and it was busy, I tried using my debit card the cashier ran it 3 times and said it wasn't working (even though I had funds in the account) I had cash so I paid her cash instead and went on with my day. Later I checked my account and she did charge me! And to top it off she didn't give me a receipt even though she's suppose to give it to me. Really annoyed and disappointed in the service won't be coming back to eat here.

2 to 2.5 stars at best.

What a disappointment.
A close friend and I had been planning dinner here for over 2 months.
We finally got to go and the food just isn't up to par (or the high prices - $22 - $45 for entrees!).

We started our meal with delicious fresh bread and garlic-infused oil.  (That and the after-dinner coffee were the best bites of the night!)

I love a good caprese salad and was thrilled when "Fresh tomato and buffalo mozz" was a featured special ($12).  My friend and I split it - and the tomatoes were HARD - deli-quality slicing tomatoes.  This - in the middle of August when tomatoes are plentiful - is a sacrilege!  Also - who serves this over iceberg lettuce?  ICEBERG?  On a Caprese?

Blah!!!

Our server Leo tried to please us - but the entrees fell flat.
My entree was OK (albeit very over-salted) - al dente linguine with about a doze steamed littlenecks in a garlicky, salt-bath of oil.

My friends "veal saute" was a joke.  While the veal was tender, the sauce was a bland concoction of greasy-oily floury blandness.  It was supposed to be a "light white wine sauce" and no wine or flavor was detectable.

We skipped dessert and had 2 very nice cups of $3 coffee.

I would not go back here.
"Founded in 1990" - looks like they have not renovated since 2002.

FAIL!

Such a shame.

This place ripped me off big time. Peter Nasr the owner is a thief and liar. He is a man of no honor and hides behind his liar salesman Terry.  Run from place. They sold me a Mercedes Benz SL 55 AMG and promised to fix all the issues it had and then renigged. What a bunch of snakes.. It cost me $6000 to fix. These guys will tell you anything they have to in order to screw you. Run away from them. .
My main complaint is the dishes are not the healthiest.  Should be at least a few healthy choices without the rich sauces.

It's the inconsistency for me..would've been a 5

The food was Delicious!!

- Philly cheesesteak egg rolls are huge. They were crunchy cheesy flavorful logs of goodness. I typically don't enjoy sweet dishes for dinner but they added the right amount of Thai chili sauce. 

- Loaded Spinach Queso netted a foodgasm. Sooo good and the lobster pieces were abundant. The lightly salted chips help cut through the richness of the cheese

- Smoked wings weren't bad but they definitely were overshadowed but the other appetizers

I've ordered the salmon in the past, it was just as yummy as before. 

Our server was friendly, prompt, and knowledgeable about the menu. She answered our questions honestly which is always appreciated. She also offered us bread..what!!! This is the first time that's happened. In the past, we asked if we could have some. Was it an oversight?

It was also a sweat fest in there. Sometimes the temperature is comfortable. On this occasion I was uncomfortably hot, would've taken off my sweater if I could've dined topless 

Firebirds is a winner... looking forward to trying other items in the menu

I was over on the east side of town with a friend and wanted to find a place for dinner.  She is a vegetarian so I opened up my trusty Yelp app and let it do the work.  We ended up picking New Delhi Palace because it was near had vegetarian options, and because I love Indian cuisine.  I was also sold by the $10 for $20 deal they had available on Yelp.

My experience here from the service to the food was great.  The waiter was nice and attentive, while not being overbearing.  They took the code for my Yelp deal right off my phone, which I liked and made my first experience using one of these online deals very simple.

I think that their idea of sampling a different region of India on their specials menu every month is wonderful.  It really gives them a chance to highlight a lot of subtle differences between dishes all over the enormous country that is India.  It also made me very interested to come back next month and see what region they pick next.

I ordered the lamb tikka masala, spicy, and it was quite tasty.  Their garlic naan was also done very well, it wasn't over seasoned with garlic, and was also not too crispy which I don't like.  My lamb was a little overcooked and tough, which can easily happen when you cook lamb in cubes, but the flavors of the sauce and spices was perfect.  The big thing I did not like and do not like with any restaurant is the fact that I had to order rice separate from my main dish.  For the price of my entree I expected it to come with sides.  

Overall a good dinner, and while this place is on the wrong side of town for me, if I'm ever in the neighborhood again I could see myself stopping by.

I'm so surprised this place has such a high rating for how flavorless the dishes were . If you're familiar with true Cuban dishes and how dynamic the spices and flavor is this is a total miss. My black bean soup had olive oil poured over top & onion sloppily cut thrown into it, the yuca was actually cold when they served it and hard and not seasoned at all , a few of our other dishes were cold and you could tell they had been sitting out, the croquetas are mostly flour, the Maduros were not fried enough and the tostones were stale and cold actually stale as in thick and tough , who gets tostones wrong! The empanadas are chicken or beef, but the chicken was spiced so Terribly everything tasted so cheap :( After a fun day at the beach almost anything is delicious we were so excited to eat here, I think what saves this place is the location it's in & that maybe it's mostly people who aren't Latino and haven't had a good dish of food , but please don't waste your money here if you want to eat well :(. The service good but that was it!

This place just opened 12/14/18. Service was friendly and accurate. Menu is mostly typical of standard American-type Mexican restaurant fare, with a few more authentic dishes than most. There is a nice selection of seafood and vegetarian dishes as well. I was there for lunch and had the steak and shrimp fajitas, which was good, but also pretty standard. I understand that they have $1 tacos for Taco Tuesday, drink specials and live music at night. Definitely worth a return trip to try more menu items.

I came here for brunch on a Sunday with 3 girlfriends... it's super small and quaint inside. Our waitress was very attentive (almost too attentive.. one person of our group was running late and when she arrived the waitress immediately asked if we were ready to order when we obviously weren't)  anyway.. 

The coffee wasn't that hot.. it was mehh. My frittata was actually VERY good.. came with a salad on top. I would say give it a try if you want something different, but I wasn't blown away.
Not bad, kinda pricey for your basic burger and fries in a paper bag but it's pretty good. Three stars

We really love this Smoke Shop, the employees are usually very friendly and easy to talk to. Great products. However Just today, I went to purchase some products and was greeted with a terrible attitude from a girl working there. She had green/blue hair and green/blue eyebrows. There's another girl who works there with blue hair who is always very nice, but the one with the green eyebrows gave me a bunch of attitude for no reason today. I'm always very courteous and kind to everyone I meet, and would hope I would get the same treatment. I really enjoy going here, and have been a loyal customer for 11 years, however the negative energy from today's experience has made me question my loyalty to this business.

Why 4 stars? Because these were the fanciest arepas ever!

   ---   ---   ---

Prior to my visit here, my arepa experience was usually a bit grungier - maybe arepas from a food truck, or a hole-in-the-wall spot. By Mais Arepas is a really nice and trendy restaurant in the heart of New Orleans. As I mentioned above, everything looked super fancy.

The only bad thing about this, is that there was virtually no difference in the taste of these arepas, but the total bill probably ended up being a few notches above what I'd normally pay.

But we did enjoy the experience. The maiz de la rueda is basically corn on the cob with all the fixins', and basically required eating anytime I see something like it on a menu. 

As for my arepa, I went with the mechada, was is skirt steak, maduros, sofrito and mozzarella, which ended up being solid. Solid is my typical word of choice for - "good, but not amazing."

We shared a pitcher of sangria, which was some of the better sangria that I've ever had. I'm not one to typically order sangria, but our local friends we were dining with, said it was wonderful, and they were not incorrect.

We had a bit of a wait for a table here, as it's a small restaurant, that's apparently really popular. The NoWait app would have definitely come in handy here, so if the owners of Mais Arepas are reading this, I hope you look into it :)

Seriously disappointed, especially after being a huge fan of Amada. Had a 9:15 reservation, but were told the table was not ready, and would be in 15 minutes-ok fine. We weren't seated until 10. On top of being inappropriate to not have the table ready for nearly an hour, being a very visible 9 months pregnant, standing in a foyer for 45 minutes was extremely uncomfortable. Once seated, service was extremely slow. It took asking 4 separate times & getting to our third course just to get bread. The wait time in between courses was ridiculous- at least 40 minutes between some. We didn't get out of there until after 1 AM, which I find totally unacceptable for a 9:15 reservation. I understand it was NYE, so things were more  hectic than normal, but they were beyond ill-equipped and too poorly managed to handle the evening. I definitelyexpect more out of a Garces restaurant. The only save was that the food was great... However, after being tired and annoyed, we couldn't even enjoy it that much.

first time i rented from hertz... and the last..
prepaid online, selected what i needed, showed up a tthe airport to pick up my rental, waited 15 minutes since no one was around...

declined all insruance ripoffs... was late... ran to my meeting, ran back., dropped car off., sitting in plane doing my expense report.. noticed an additional $19.99 damage insruance... i take the blame for not reading the 6 page agreement...

i used an amex... which gives me that protection for free...

called hertz., they wanted to give me back HALF the charge...; not the full amount..

called amex they couldnt do anything....

still with enterprise... read ur 1 sheet contract... dont get ripped off

This Panera location is decent and employees are always nice but they always mess up orders. I occasionally order breakfast from the app to go pick up in store and they always leave out ingredients. Is there not a guide on how to assemble breakfast sandwiches or are they just winging it every time? How do you miss the sausage in a SAUSAGE, Egg & Cheese sandwich? Lol. If you're gonna order form here make sure to check your order!
I ordered a large cheesesteak Stromboli and the chicken sampler.  I wasn't pleased at all. I wasted my $25 bucks! Live and learn

I have to say this is not the worst hotel I've stayed at but it still needs a lot of help it seems clean the walls are thin you can here the people next to you watch TV the bathroom lights don't work so I have to take a shower with the door open but don't take a hot shower or the smoke detector will go off the room doors you have to slam them to shut and you here ever time a person leaves or inters rooms around you and if you plan on using the WIFI forget it to never works well yet another week goes by and I stand corrected this place is not worth you time do not say here I've had to go to the front desk just about everyday for issues like card key don't work the trash cans didn't get emptied and today well finally I got bathroom light guess they got tired of the smoke detector going off but today I get here and there's no shower curtain that's after I stand in line for 15 min to get my room key reactivated I find out there's no curtains they told me they'll get me one as soon as possible well now they need a mop and I'll be switching hotels DO NOT STAY HERE there's plenty of hotels in the area and today I leave nothing but a whore hotel we do yourself a favor sleep in the mc Donald's parking lot don't give this hotel  your business DONT STAY HERE
Awesome beer, awesome service, Chris is the man. We are planning to move out this way and definitely will be visiting Revisions again.

Great ambiance and decor. Large selection of food. Maybe too large. I think they are trying to be everything and not focusing on maybe their best. I tried the fish and chips which was ok but fries came out cold. Bread was average. I guess I'm saying the food didn't live up to the atmosphere.

Looking for great sushi? You will find it here my yelp friends. We got 4 different roles and all of them taste very fresh. Definitely some of the best I have had in awhile. Also we are not from the area so we just went with this one do to the other reviews and ratings and we definitely didn't get let down. Also we got the chicken pad Thai it as well is very tasteful a little on the not so spicy side for me. I will definitely be back for more.
Excellent little dive restaurant. Not a lot of seating but it's a cool vibe and worth the wait. Food was excellent. Service was a bit slow.

I struggle with whether the Phily dinner should get a 3 star rating or a 2 star rating. If there was 2.5 that's what it would be, but I'm rounding down here.

Our waitress was tremendously slow, especially since it was lunch time and most people are on their lunch breaks from work, as was the case with me. Needless to say I was late getting back to work. In fact, we waited 10 minutes before she even acknowledged that we were there. We almost thought we didn't have a waitress.

So I ordered an over priced grilled Reuben that I had to ask for Russian dressing for, ate it, and went on my merry way. This sandwich was pretty much the same as everywhere else I've eaten it. The decor was supposed to be of a retro theme I think, but it just ended up looking a little obnoxious. If I recall correctly, the booths were orange and blue. Ick.

So in conclusion, it's in an area I never go and it's outta my way for lunch during work, so I probably will not eat here again.
Do not Recommend!!!!!!! STAY AWAY! I love Chinese food and this dose not come close. It was over priced and not very clean. If I could give it zero stars I would. I say again STAY AWAY!!!!!!!

Super charming; this is a gem that's worth revisiting. Pastries are homemade and top-notch. The owners are wonderfully attentive. Your coffee cup will never empty. The interior is petite and European. Prices are far less expensive than a plane ticket to France.
Family restaurant. Popular. Basic. Large portions. They don't ask about allergies-let them know. Family style sides. I would go to boston market next time for Thanksgiving. Shrimp cocktail good.

Lesson learned, Yelpers, always read the reviews first. My hubby and I were out tooling around after Christmas shopping and thought, "ooh, a new sushi joint, let's go!" Now, I'm not a sushi aficionado, my exposure to sushi is limited to the Mango Tango and Playboy Roll at Naked Tchopstix - and for good reason - but I have had fantastic Japanese food, so I know what's out there. 

We were the only couple in the restaurant (let that be your warning) and the waitress was very young and polite, a star for that, at least. We started off with Shrimp Tempura (how can you screw up fried food?) only to find the sweet potato and onions still raw, though we were so hungry we ate them anyway. The shrimp still had clumps of cornstarch inside the fried portion rendering it indelible - unless you're into eating powdered cornstarch raw, which we aren't. 

I ordered Yakisoba noodles with veggies, chicken and a side of fried rice - you know, in case the noodles sucked I'd at least have a fallback, right? 

Not only were the noodles spiced with cinnamon and cumin (not so Japanese), but they were sitting in a bed of oil, though that wasn't the worst part. 

Have you seen those news stories about delivery trucks with refrigeration units being stopped by police only to find the meat inside rotting? I suspect the chicken that isushi serves came from one of those trucks - it was rotten, both in taste and smell. I'm no chef, but I know what rotten chicken smells like.

The rice, like a previous reviewer mentioned, was oversalted and tasted like burnt wok. Two bites in and I had to set the chopsticks aside in sheer defeat. 

My hubby's meal (the Volcano Roll and the Southern California Roll) was so bad he had to stop eating and walk away from the table. The Southern California Roll was the standard faire Cali roll with a heaping tablespoon of fish roe (way to get creative). Surely the Volcano Roll would be better, right? 

We stared at the beautiful roll (filled only with cream cheese) and topped with a crab cake, of sorts, melted cheese, black fish roe and a splash of a red sauce. "Just wing it," I said. 

The reaction that came from his face was so priceless that, had I not been begging for water, I would have snapped a picture. That innocent looking red sauce was likely made from the ominous 'ghost pepper', one of the hottest known to man, because he started to shed tears and yell. 

In the likelihood that he was being dramatic, I tried a dot of the sauce to confirm his reaction necessary. Be warned, folks, this is called a Volcano Roll because the thing is FLAMING, and not in the oh-so-delicious way that the Playboy Roll is delivered in blue flames - we are talking straight liquid edible (er, inedible) fire. 

I rest my case. Save your money and a potentially nauseating experience and go to Ichiban, Sakura, Sushi Club or Naked Tchopsticks where they do it right. I'm sure there are better items on the menu, but we tried five dishes on the menu and all were colossal failures.

Do not waste your money getting a car wash here. Paid $13 to have the machine spray water and minimal suds on my vehicle. Go down the road to Mister Carwash, for the $13, it will get washed. What a rip off....
Friendly service and pretty good food. Great salsa!
The shrimp tacos were A+++ and my whole family LOVED the tortillas. Try this place when you're in Tampa.

Were told that there was a 40 minute wait. We told the host we'd sit at the bar adjacent to the restaurant. He said "Fine" and took our phone number. 50 minutes later, we walk back to the host stand and were told that they had given away our table. When asked why we weren't called at the number we left, we were told that they aren't allowed to make long distance calls. Come on. #1 - You only have local patrons? #2 - No one has a cell phone? Manager tells us that he's doing all he can to seat us. That meant that he was waiting for another table to finish....that's not doing everything to seat us. Doing everything means setting up a table and seating us. I talked to the hotel manager and was told that the restaurant is a separate entity, gave me the General Manager's card, apologized and said that I was the third complaint about this today.  Food was fine, our server was nice, but really, I won't be bringing guests back again.

The management did give us 25% off of our bill, but to make matters worse, because we were delayed in the first place, we ended up getting caught up in the torrential downpour on our way to the car.

Attractive setting for what is essentially fast food. However, the food is prepared fresh to order, and it was quite good. But don't think that because it's a fast food-type setting that the food will be inexpensive -- expect to spend $12-16 per person depending on what you order.  And yeah, the soft drink machine is pretty cool.
No complaints. Great price! Great staff! Great service! I'm seeing 20/15.. Pretty dope.

I typically don't care about pleasantries; however, when it gets in the way of doing your job, it's problematic in the workplace. The gentlemen, and I use that term loosely, that works there is incredibly rude. He holds conversation with people while making others wait as if people don't come in for a quick in and out. Furthermore, he has problems accepting Amex. If you don't want to pay the fee, don't accept Amex. 

Lastly, I get people have bad days. That being said, employees are there to render services and be somewhat pleasant, that is it. Leave the attitude for personal life... Probably will never use this location again.  The guy has such an attitude that I'm not sure if my package will even reach its destination.

I would give it a 2 for food but I'm giving it a 3 because I appreciated our server. I ordered 6 rolls for 2 people and she suggested that 2 per person would suffice. I don't like to have leftover sushi so I particularly appreciated this. The spciy scallop roll was ok, but the other 3 rolls all became a blur. Each had different ingredients (salmon, eel, shrimp), but because the sauces they used were similar, and many of the rolls have crunchy fried components they all ended up tasting the same. I think that more than anything else, their rolls hide the seafood rather than highlighting it.
Great place, best deli in south market district hands down. Everything on the menu is house made and fresh.
The fries are good but that's about the only redeeming quality. Typical cheese steak place. Good enough to eat.

Great place just west of Saint Louis by Olivette. Very casual, amusing service from behind the counter and very good BBQ. When we arrived, the line was almost out to the door, but we were eating within 10 minutes. Good pulled pork, good sides and great sauces. A great place for a BBQ fix if you are near St. Louis.

Both of us felt this was a waste of $100. Their brochure shows a guided tour of St. Louis Cemetery #3. Not what happened on our tour. Our driver pulled up and dropped us off with him just staying on the bus. We were told to just get off and walk around then he would honk the horn when we needed to return to the bus. We were given maybe 10 to 15 minutes before he honked the horn. Additionally we were told not to get very far from the entrance so we would hear the horn when he wanted us back. 
Next, no matter how much sympathy you have for the victims of Hurricane Katrina, you can only look at so many abandoned homes in the 9th Ward. Our tour spent at least a third of the time driving through the 9th Ward looking at abandoned homes and high water marks. Extremely boring after the first 10 minutes. 
At $50 per person, this was a pretty expensive 2 hour bus ride. 
Would not recommend.

Ugh! 
Food... nah!
Service... good!
Overall... probably won't be back!
