So apparently they cook their wings for 10 minutes and that's their standard. 10 minutes wasn't long enough because our wings were barely done. We asked to speak to a manager because we just couldn't eat them. She adjusted our bills, which we appreciated. 

The drinks were good and our 1st server, Brittany, was great! Our 2nd server, Skyler (because Brittany got off work), was fine. 

With so many places that are now doing wings right, the wings here just weren't good enough for me to want to return.

WARNING. I took my vehicle here for an oil change and was told my axle half shaft was damaged because of a torn CV boot and the cost to replace the axle would be just under $1600.00. They were charging $1400.00 for the part alone! They also said my power steering fluid was due for "factory scheduled maintenance" for an additional $69.00. THANK GOD I took my vehicle to my car dealer instead. They said the boot had a "small cut" in it and all I needed was a boot kit. The axle was fine and my repair only cost me $303.00. The new axle (I didn't need) retails for $617 on Kiapartsnow.com  (not $1400) and the dealer said it would have cost $980 if they had to replace it which is less that what Midas wanted for the part alone. Royal Kia also said there is no "scheduled maintenance" for power steering fluid and determined I wont need this for another 15,000 miles. This was the first and last time I will ever visit M.I.D.A.S.  Masters In Deception And Scams

I want to like this place, but after a recent experience there, I hardly have a desire to go back.  First off, their menu rarely changes, and I really don't think it's that great.  I struggle to find something to order every time.  Second, we went for my roommate's birthday and brought a cake with us.  Our server informed us that the owners would not let us eat the cake.  The explanation was something to the extent of, "We are too proud of our dessert menu to allow you to bring outside food."  Seriously?  That is arrogant.  We were all a little stunned at that.  Will probably only go back if someone else suggests it.

There's a fine line between dumpster chic and dumpster. Unfortunately, this place falls on the wrong side of it. Popped in to work for a few hours based on positive yelp reviews, but was completely underwhelmed. My iced chai latte tasted like a strong black tea with some soy milk poured in. I'm not sure where the spice was. It looked like they had a decent selection of vegetarian fare, but after seeing the absolutely disgusting bathroom (presumably used by employees), I wasn't willing to touch the food.

Not a great spot to read or get work done, owing to the general dark and gloomy atmosphere. Also, cash only, so if you do stop in make sure you have some on you.

Okay, first of all, I'm an engineer , with a college degree. So I went into Cody's in Palm Harbor, I had one drink and my buddy accidentally spilled it. We ordered dinner and ate our dinner, and then the manager came over and said we were not allowed to have another drink. No idea why, I'm not allowed to have one drink at Cody's I guess. I would not recommend this place to anybody. And the manager was a probably 22 year old. I'd like to see her college degree, and treat me like that. Stay away from this place!
The bloody Mary was good. The breakfast, pulled pork benedict was bland, no flavor and very dry. Will not be going back.

I'm so happy to have this restaurant in our neighbourhood. I've been several times now, the food is always great. The chef has taken time to come out & talk about various dishes. Prices are reasonable & good sized portions. Great naan! Highly recommended.

They say they would reserve a place for my friends birthday party for a group of 20. When we arrived they said they had to many customers and canceled it.  Then they told us to get out.  Disappointed in how they handled the situation.

Richard A.
5-9-15
Service is non existing. I called on April 9th do to an over whelming amount of weeds and was told they would send out a specialist by May 3rd it never happened. I called on May 6th and I was  told a specialist would be out by May 8th it never happened. I was also informed that everyone wants to be fist and I need to wait my turn. On May 9th I cancelled my service with the Warminster Trugreen office.

Great service, knowledgeable staff (when they are available because this store is so busy)....  They carry Blue Buffalo wilderness, which is all I feed my pets.  They offer a special:  buy 10 bags of food and get the 11th one free- not bad!
Terrible food. Bad waiting area. Don't seat for movies when the board says they will. And bring appetizers out at the same time as your entree. Trashy.

My partner and I love tacos and rarely ever miss a taco Tuesday. This was the first time dining here for both of us. We were underwhelmed. 

The vibe is cool, LOVE the music they were playing. We are pescatarian so we ordered the spinach empanadas, the only fish option, grilled salmon, and the cauliflower and mushroom tacos. The salmon was very bland, the salsa on top was good but I wish the salmon was seasoned better! The mushroom was just a no for me. It was smoky and just not good. The cauliflower tacos were both of our favorite! Could do without the spinach empanadas. Nothing special. 

We were trying to hook our tacos up with hot sauce and they have none!!! The wall with all the decorative peppers and spices led me to believe there would be some great sauces to try. There were 3 - none were good. The guy who was working actually gave us a hot sauce that he created and it had a great kick. They should put that on the menu!

Probably won't go back, unfortunately.

Came here based on a tip.  The building is located on its own, slightly away but still part of a strip mall.

Parking is quite plentiful here.

The interior is Irish themed for sure, but contains a lot of sports memorabilia (especially Oilers stuff).  There is a large (oldschool) projector showing sports at the back of one wall.  The bar area looks pretty cool too, with lots of dark wood.

The staff are all showing their midriffs.  This must be their claim to fame.

One food item that stuck out was the dill pickle wings.  I can't say I've seen this anywhere else.  They're a bit pricey at $10 for just 7(!?), but the wings are bit larger than other places, and the flavouring is quite good.

I also had their 16oz T-bone steak, which is actually quite decent.  You can't beat the price at $24.

My one biggest complaint though are the flies.  There were several buzzing around my table throughout the entire night.

Overall it was a good time.

i've been going here to get my nails done for a while and it started out great and my nails were always done right and a fill with gel was only $28... after going today my
usual man who does my nails was rushed by another customer and he left my nails dirty, uneven, poorly shaped and with patchy color. keep in mind i've been coming here for a year. he then proceeded to charge me $42 for a fill with gel. looking for a new nail place.

I LOVE this place. Hubby and I used to come a few times a week for lunch or dinner but due to the pandemic we've been getting takeout. We were surprised at how fresh and consistent pho 20 is and everything is just as good as before. My go to is P3 or P9 the pho with everything or pho with brisket and flank. I also love the vermicelli and banh mi (banh mis are only $5!). The drinks I really enjoyed their coconut drink, strawberry smoothie, and milk tea. The milk tea has a slight mocha chocolate flavor to me but my friends disagree haha

If I could give them 10 stars I would! Ok, let me start of by saying that in all the years I've been living in Florida, I have never been to a family owned spot where 1. The owner is in house working and 2. Is mingling with his guests both regulars and new guests. Jimmy is a humble man who greatly appreciates his customers. It wasn't until the end of our meal that Jimmy came to our table and asked how our meal was and then told the bartender Sake for us on the house. We enjoyed our first round of it as Tina and I giggled and chatted. Jimmy came around again and sat with us to take photos and got is a second round! This man, is wonderful! This I what I've missed since I moved to Florida. I used to work in tight knit small mom and pop places in Richmond. The owners were always in house working and mingled with their guests. Making them feel like family and welcomed. This is what I miss and what I wish I would see more by small businesses here. Thank you for your amazing hospitality this evening! Your staff is so friendly and our waitress Danika is so adorable and funny!

We orders a bunch of different sushi and shared together. I ordered the Volcano roll & Spicy Tuna , Tina ordered the Arizona roll and Tuna Tataki. Everything we ordered was fulfilling to our bellies and tastebuds. Volcano roll was pulled with krab and conch in a tasty sauce. Note: all krab is imitation. The spicy tuna was different than any other I've tasted. Everything was fresh and full flavored. I couldn't stop! I could've ordered another roll, but decided to hold back. I love the interior. It's unlike any other place I've been. This sushi bar experience really made me feel like I was in Tokyo. From the adorable table tops with the anime sushi chef smiling back at us, to the decor and the feel of the place. So many of the regular were all saying "Jimmy in a legend....he's a rockstar". The guests here show their love for this guy and he is so warm and welcoming to everyone. Thank you again for such a great experience! Looking forward to coming back for more sushi with my husband and our friends and to be regulars for years to come!

The cashiers usually charge me wrong and apparently they have no clue how the rewards cards work. If it weren't for hour breaks and their convenient location I would go somewhere else. Seriously canyon in better.

I had a great experience with We Move for my move. I hired them through UHaul and was completely blown away by the professionalism of Andrew and Ryan. 

They worked very efficiently and were able to pack my old apartment, transport and unload the truck at my new apartment within the three hours I had hired them for. They were great to deal with, very personable and could not have asked for a better team to work with.

I had a much better buying experience here than my last car purchase from another local dealer. Chance B. helped me out and even took in my old VW Tiguan without even looking at it. They gave me a fair deal on that and the price of my new Hyundai Sonata!

This was my favorite place for so long. I used to work right on the same street. Everyone there was so friendly to me. They new my name and they would ask me if I want to usual. Which is always kind of cool just because. I moved to a different area but I miss the employees. They were really so sweet all the time. I loved going there on my little lunch break. I would recommend this to anyone who likes a friendly rapport.

Went there with some colleagues a couple months back and had an excellent time. Staff is very friendly and accommodating. Great instruction on how it all works, the best way to make the axe stick. 
Overall a real good time. Highly recommended for some good fun.

Please read the whole thing, you'll be glad you did.
This place was a very interesting experiance to say the least. We couldn't come to terms and to be honest I wasn't happy with the value they were giving me for my trade. 
Who ever is, lol.
Anyways within a few minutes of me leaving the dealership the sales person Tiffany is texting me telling me saying she can get the manager to give me $400 more for my trade and that if I came back she would give me $500.00 to put down as a downpayment....I told her to keep her money because (a). Its against the amvic rules for her to even offer that and (b.) Who in there right mind would make such a offer???
I gave tiffinay my terms and told her I'd be back only if they would accept them. 
She got back to me and and told me she needed a deposit to show her boss I was serrious. I laughed and said sure why not. I gave the $500 via my visa and Tiffany sent me a picture text with the receipt for not only my $500 but another one she says is her $500 to put towards my purchase.....now this seems completely insane to me but whatever right.
Next message from her told me that her manager accepted my offer. Perfect right?
I gave my info to the finance office and
the next day She sends me a image of the bill of sale from the finance office as my wife was aproved.  I picked up right away that they had built $500 into the deal and you guessed it they put
$500.00 in the dealerships favor by lowering the expected payout on my trade by exactly $500......so as I suspected her $500 although not expected and not asked for was nothing more than some sort of numbers game used to trick people into thinking they are getting something they are not. 
I know this sounds crazy and I wouldn't be surprised if you don't believe me, but msg me and I'll send you the texts and a pic of her card  so you can see the phone number and the texts belong to Tiffany at Go Kia South
 Don't deal with people who lie, don't deal with Kia on the south side. I called the dealership back and spoke with a manager, I forwarded the messages and proof of the fraud to him and he seemed disappointed but ultimately never called me back within 24 hours as he promised. Baised on that action I would assume this is normal practice for them and they don't really care, if they cared they would of called back.
I'll be going to AMVIC with this as I think they should be made aware of this fraudulent activity.

The first time I tried to eat here they had a ventilation issue and could only serve cold food. We went back and had dinner and it was delicious. I had the adult mac and cheese and it was very good. Nice big place, great atmosphere. Casual, everyone was wearing jeans. Seems like a nice place to grab dinner or a drink after work. The hardest part was picking something off the menu! The menu is huge and everything sounded so darn good. Excellent service and a server who was knowledgeable of the menu and made recommendations when I was torn on what to order. We will be back!

I am completely shocked by the lack of professionalism displayed here.  I went to Adrienne for a double process highlights and haircut.  I came in telling her that I was happy with my current golden blonde hair.  Three hours later as my hair was being styled--I commented that my hair looked very ashy and I definitely didn't want that.  I was told that it was the lighting in the salon and the orange brick walls.  I knew this didn't sound quite right, but attempted to give her the benefit of the doubt.  Sure enough. when I arrived home, my friend instantly commented that my hair appeared an ashy gray and that I was now a mousy brown brunette.  I cried the entire night and contacted Adrienne early in the morning about how upset that I was.  Adrienne stated that she was willing to fix my hair around 7 pm after an appointment and after the salon was closed.  So, I planned my day accordingly.   Two and half hours later, I get a text telling me to come into the salon at 4:30 with my hair washed and dried.  Huh?! I was already well into my day and no where near my apartment to do this with such short notice, especially after already deciding on a time.  I wrote back that I would not be able to make that time.  I called the salon and spoke with Mary, one of the owners.  When I told her of the situation, she actually had the audacity to comment to me that "now she is going to have to cancel her plans too".  Is that my problem?! She got back on the phone and stated that it would be impossible for me to come into the salon at 7.  Apparently, she wasn't concerned enough with my satisfaction to actually cancel her plans, despite me planning my whole day and Saturday night around this 7 pm appointment. Mary then went on to turn things on me and comment that 7 pm is a crazy time for an appointment and that I should have talked to her earlier.  As if it was my idea to make the appointment at this time and spend my day hiding under a baseball cap in the first place!!!  So now I am left with a head of embarrassingly ugly hair, in complete shock of the poor costumer service.  
DO NOT go to this salon, unless you can afford to deal with such irresponsible amateurs.
Average at best. Fish didn't taste that fresh. Everything is pre sliced for rapid service. Albacore was fishy. Seared ahi was salty. Rolls were decent. Service was very good.
You're going to get full on this food because it's loaded with butter oil and bread. It's a diner don't expect anything else.
it is a great place to get waxed in this city.. however, it is SO expensive.  you can get a Brazilian in California for $42 at European Wax Center.

Great place for a romantic dinner. Beautiful indoors and outdoor garden area. Always have vegan options clearly marked on the menu. Full bar with great drink specials. Pro tip: make a reservation as they fill up quickly.

The food we eat has some really great flavors, but the execution leaves a lot to be desired. Lobster and Crab Bisque, tasted fantastic, but having to pull pieces of shell from our mouths, not so good.
Then my wife ordered the Crab Cakes, and the same thing happened. Being a chef myself I was thinking this is a fluke. But when we told the manager about he issue, she commented that she loves the soup but gets tired of having to pull the shells out herself. Are you waiting for a lawsuit before you take the time to thoroughly go through the crab meat and picking out the shells? 
Conner's should get a higher rating, but in good conscience, I can't.

I've been here a couple of times with my parents and the food here is ok but not great.  It is located on 309 right by the Honda dealership in Montgomeryville.  It can be a little tricky to get in if you are traveling southbound so your best bet is to enter the place going northbound on 309.  Exiting the place will only get you heading northbound on 309 and it can be a little tricky to check to see if the traffic is clear to get back on 309.

As for the food, it's better than some of the other chinese buffets that are in the area but it's not great.  The sushi was ok but I have seen more variety at other buffets.  The hibachi station was also ok but nothing like a proper hibachi restaurant.  There are quite a few food stations but not as much variety as the size of the place would suggest.

There are plenty of seats available for dining.  There is even an area where it seems that you can have a private party but might just serve as an overflow area for customers.  Being that the place is still fairly new, it's still clean.  Hopefully it stays that way as the place stays open.  And yes, this place is wheelchair accessible.

Prices do vary between lunch and dinner and are reasonable.  Overall, it's not a bad place; however, if I had a craving for really good sushi or hibachi, I would go to a proper place.

Christmas of 2020 was one of social distancing with no hugs.  Village Green Florist came through for me with flying colors!  All arrangements made by this florist delivered to my Dad, my Aunt Gail, Uncle Jack & Rita and my sister, Nina provided much warmth and seasonal joy to my beloved family.  I am most grateful to Village Green florist!

This is a new bakery/café near MD Oriental Market that is on the border of Brandon/Tampa. A great location that is just off the Brandon exit ramp from I-75, and close to the Selmon Expressway. I have seen the "Coming Soon" sign for months and it's finally open for dine-in and takeout. It appears to be a family run business, but I wasn't able to confirm that. All I know is that everyone worked well together from the counter to the kitchen. The staff seemed very in sync and helped each other out.

What I love is that it's not only an Asian bakery, but it's also a café. They serve specialty boba tea drinks, coffee, snacks, and sandwiches. Their stock is limited at this time, but I was told they will be expanding on the baked goods eventually. Do go early if you want to see everything they have to offer. They were open this past Saturday at 10 am and had full cases of all kinds of buns, mille crêpes, muffins, cake slices, cookies, cream puffs and more. There's an extra case that is empty right now but that will be part of the expansion. They also have a very good lunch special that includes their awesome fried chicken. I didn't get a sandwich, but I did get the basil popcorn chicken which was amazing. I could snack on it all day. Seriously, it was crispy so you know it was double fried. The flavor was very Taiwanese with the 5 spice seasoning and the Thai basil leaves were fried as well. So full of flavor, and so much so that I know I'll be going back for many more visits just for that. They have many more snacks like spicy edamame, spring rolls, mozzarella sticks, and fries... but that popcorn chicken would be too difficult to pass up.

The boba drinks are also pretty fantastic. I know that Green Island is nearby but I feel that Zimó's specialty drinks and toppings are unique enough to set them apart. I had the taro milk tea which doesn't seem unique by itself but I love that you can add cheese foam or caramel brûlée to any drink for just a $1. I got the brûlée topping which added a rich texture and flavor. They even torched the top of it so that you can have some of the bits of caramelized sugar pieces at the end of the drink, or take it off the top (whichever you prefer). Yep, it was my first experience with that topping so don't judge me. I also got to taste durian fruit for the first time. I got one of the mille crêpe cake slices in that flavor and it was a cool experience. I remember seeing it on the Food Network, so I knew going in that it was a custard like fruit that doesn't have a pleasant smell. Think savory, and not sweet & fruity. It had a very slight flavor of green onions that were caramelized. If this doesn't appeal to you, then I'd get the mango or matcha flavors. I'm all about trying something new so this was a great experience. I love that the cake was so light and creamy. I'm looking forward to trying it in other flavors.

The cream puffs were also great. They had a chocolate and a matcha green tea flavor. The flavors were all there and the cream was light. I'd get this again too. I'm no expert when it comes to Asian baked goods. I've never been to Sweet Buns so I have nothing to compare it to. I'm just so excited that they are finally open and they are close to home. They get 5 stars because of service, taste, and warm & inviting atmosphere alone. Okay, and that delicious popcorn chicken! I can't wait to go back and try the Mango Pomelo Sago and Super Fruit tea as well.

Mom wanted to eat here. She likes it here. A lot. So I took her here.

She really enjoyed it. A lot. Three or four trips for different foods each time. I'm really happy she enjoyed it so much. The food I had was ok.

I'll even admit that the dishes, silverware, floor, tables, etc were clean. Our 'waitress' kept our drinks full and removed our dirty dishes quickly. She was really friendly too.

It's still not my choice for eating out unless my mom wants to eat there.

Breakfast was a waste of time and money. The food was practically plastic. 

Gorgeous view from a few tables. 

I wouldn't go again.

I want to start out by saying that my massage was fine - by no means the worst I've ever had - but I likely won't go back for a few reasons.

1) Their technique for a Swedish Massage was not what I'm used to.  Rather than a fluid, slow, soothing experience with good pressure, my therapist moved VERY quickly, so fast that I felt I didn't have time to absorb the experience before she moved on to a different area.  I also felt more prodded rather than kneaded.  My therapist also didn't use any bolster pillows at all under my ankles or knees and I noticed their absence compared to past massages I have received.
2) I'm 30 and wouldn't consider myself especially shy, but I was a little taken aback by the lack of attention shown to keeping me covered up.  We started with me face down, and my therapist walked in the room and threw back the covers so that a good 3/4 of my rear was exposed.  In addition, when she worked on my legs, I expected that she would tuck the blankets to keep me covered up down below, but this was not done so I felt a little uncomfortable.  Same when I was turned to lay on my back.  My chest was barely covered and the same inattention was shown to covering me up when working on my legs.
3) My therapist tried to sell me on facials during my session.  I was there to relax, and the last thing I wanted to do was fend off a salesperson.  After I just stopped responding, she eventually dropped the subject, but it took me a while to get over the annoyance which of course impacted the relaxation I was hoping to achieve when I booked the massage in the first place.

Bottom line, if a friend told me they were going to Day Spa by Zsuzsanna, I wouldn't tell them not to go, but I would definitely warn them about my experience.
Been here 3 consecutive days for breakfast food coma and food-gasm guaranteed! Thank god they don't serve alcohol people would never leave! Highest possible recommendation

I absolutely love Kaia Fit.  I have been going for 3 months now.  I have already lost 25 pounds.  It is still hard to make myself get out of bed in the morning but with two young children, I tell myself that this is ME time and I have to go.  (If you don't they will call you to see where you are) This has been the only program that has worked for me because it also incorporates nutrition.  I like that the ladies there are so encouraging and motivational.  It has been the best investment that I've made in myself and my health.  I would highly recommend it!!!!
Wasn't a fan of the meatball sub but the chicken Parmesan was very tasty and my wife got a sandwich with kielbasa which was good. Was a little pricey though

Went 3 days ago for a standard manicure, it's fairly chipped now (and my home manicures tend to last at least a week). I thought the service was spotty; some of the nail techs were very attentive, and others were definitely not. All in all, it's about what I expected for a $15 manicure, but I wish the service had been a little better.

Peterson's Tree Care is AWESOME!! We hired them to trim these huge, old, and overgrown avocado trees on our property. The woman in the office (Nancy) was so helpful and nice to talk with. In the next couple days the owner (Peter) came out to assess our tree issue, he also was so nice and had great advice. The crew showed up on time and on the scheduled date. They did a great job and our trees look so much more healthier and happier. The crew was very professional and they cleaned up so nicely, you didn't even know they were here!!
I highly recommend Peterson's Tree Care for any of your arbor needs!

There is clearly no management oversight of this restaurant. After being served soggy and greasy tasting calamari's, which we sent back after saying that we were not happy with, We were never there still charged for them. Then I had a Reuben sandwich with a Caesar salad on the side that was served dripping in the oil and soggy wet which I also told the waitress about his condition and to relay that to the kitchen but apparently there is a lack of communication between the servers in the kitchen where she was afraid to say that to them. Because there is never any apology but how bad the food was. I will not be here for a long time.

This restaurant was amazing!  I was home visiting my parents in StL and since I spent a summer living in Nepal they took me to this restaurant.  It really was authentic.  We all ordered from the Nepali/Tibetan part of the menu and enjoyed every bite.  Dal bhat is the most traditional item on the menu (the Nepali family I lived with ate it every day for bfast and lunch).  We also tried to appetizer sampler which gives you a great taste of several dishes (my favorite were the momos).  I would highly recommend this restaurant to someone who was looking for something adventurous and a little more exotic to try.

Very disappointed in their lack of attention to detail. The toe stitching on one of my expensive ankle boots had come out. Lady at the counter said they would probably need to glue it and then run stitching over it. A week later, I picked my boot up and to my dismay there was glue all around the leather and the stitching was the wrong color and wrong gauge. I only hope I can find someone else to fix this because I'm certainly not trusting Joe's to touch these boots again.

Amazing brunch/breakfast spot in the heart of Center City. We started with an excellent bloody mary, and moved on to our delicious entrees.  Avocado toast with perfectly cooked eggs and Creole shrimp over cheesy grits with poached eggs.  The food was terrific, and the service could not have been any friendlier and more professional. Beware, the waits can be long on the weekends, and they only take cash.  But, this place is well worth the wait and the cash only hassle.

I had been going to this dentist for years and they were all very, very nice. I had been going in for regular six month check-ups a few years back and had my teeth cleaned and xrays done. They told me everything was fine. But my teeth looked bad and I went right away to another dentist for a second opinion. That dentist took xrays and told me I had 11 cavities!!!! I went back to Premier Dental and met with the receptionist. She told me that they knew I had dental problems but they knew I could not afford to get more dental work done, so they didn't tell me about them. Yes! That was their story!!! I cannot believe it!!!! I spent the last year and a half going to a different dentist getting all the work done that was overlooked by Dr. Williams at Premier Dental. I do not recommend them AT ALL!!!

The location is supposed to be open at 11am. I ordered in the application and even got a notification my order was ready. When I arrived there was no answer and doors were locked. All the pizza huts in the area have been horrible with customer service.
Food was ok but ambiance is perfect for the beach! Great spot overall!!! Definitely recommend

Came here with my cousins. The waiters don't seem very attentive. Maybe the soup dumplings are worth coming for, but the other entrees are utter bull and not worth it at all. Too expensive and you won't be getting much bang for the buck. One of us ordered a noodle dish and got a small plate of 90% noodle and  a small piece of burnt meat?

4/13/2019 (evening service). This was my first time sitting in this restaurant. I may have ordered car side before, but will no longer. This is the worst Chili's I have EVER been too. Quite frankly it's the worst looking restaurant I've been to in a while. It is so dirty looking, dirty windows and it even has an old smell. There were dishes everywhere on tables and the staff took their time taking care of it. Someone actually sat dishes on the median separating my table from another and it took them forever to notice this and remove it. I immediately wanted to walk out, but our kid wanted to eat there for their birthday. Then they actually forgot what I ordered and when it was received the fries were lukewarm and the skillet queso was watery. Quite shameful. Also the people bussing tables preferred to stand around and talk instead of clean. I will never be back to this location in any capacity. I sure wish I had stuck to Applebee's this evening. Go there, the food is better and it's clean.

Naf Naf Grill is less than a block away from where I now work.  Add to that its fast-causal approach, and it should be in line to becoming my one of my regular go to spots.  Based on my visits thus far, it has not earned this kind of 'tier 1' status just yet.

Their menu offers pita sandwiches, rice, couscous, or salad bowls, and also a hummus bowl.  All can be made with chicken, steak, or falafel.  They also offer some extensive veggie options, but I haven't had a chance to try any of them just yet.

I found my chicken pita sandwich to be A-Ok.  It was made to order and quick to deliver.  My steak couscous bowl wasn't quite what I had hoped for.  The portion just didn't seem to add up and the steak didn't have much flavor.  However, it too was made to order.

Net - I'm not writing Naf Naf off just yet, but I am looking at other options instead of just making it my regular spot.  It has location potential at least...

Went in with my friend and was happy to see that they had a two for twenty dollars meal and that entitles two full size entrees and one appetizer to share. They have a good size menu for this meal and I chose to get six oz sirloin while my friend got the grilled chicken fajitas and we shared the half order of texas cheese fries and these were delicious! I was happy with my meal because it included the sirloin, mashed potatoes and steamed broccoli it was a good and filling combination. This is the place to go if you want quality food at a quality price and good/speedy customer service.

Classy Italian BYOB with a very professional / friendly staff and great food. Also perfect for group celebrations. 

I've organized multiple large group dinners here (anywhere from 8 people to 40 people), and Melograno has delivered time after time with an easy pre-fixe menu that's very reasonably priced for the quality of the food offered.

One of my favorite establishments in center city. So much so I celebrated my birthday here last year!

I had been having problems with my Galaxy J7 phone.  Yes, I know its an older phone.  An employee at T-Mobile at Clearwater Mall recommend CPR to see if my phone could be fixed or if it needed to be replaced.

So today hours before going to buy a new phone I took my phone to CPR.   Within about 10 minutes they fixed my phone and it's working like a charm. 

I highly recommend CPR.
Stop by earlier for lunch and found a cockroach in my plate!!!! Will never eat here again

Cool hipster vibe. Loved the decor, the Meze Original was really good, and the iced tea was brewed to perfection. We were there on a Sunday so the brunch menu was our only option to order from.  We were slightly disappointed that their hummus was not available for brunch, especially since they have the award for the best hummus. We will definitely be back when we are in the St Pete area again.

This place is absolutely amazing.  I had the Lunch Special (try any 3 items) and it's really good.  Gumbo was very tasty and rich with flavor.  The dirty rice had some great tasting livers and gizzards chopped in it and the chicken pasta was good.  The staff is very friendly and I'll definitely be back for more gumbo!

I've been to both locations, here and Center City. The only difference I noticed between the two was the music at this location was obnoxiously loud, but the important thing was the food was consistent. Below is my review of the Center City location, really don't have anything different to say:

"Very hip vibe. You order either a salad or stir fry meal via a touch screen monitor (just like at wawa, for those familiar). I like to build my own, but they have several standard options to pick as well. 

My only gripe is it was a bit light on meat. You can add more meat when ordering, but I would have liked a bit more with the regular order. Speaking of which, be careful when adding veggies on the build your own, after 3 you incur a $0.35 charge. Not a huge cost, but it can add up if you don't pay attention. 

All the produce is local, which is great, but wouldn't necessarily bring me into or keep me away from a place. I just like that it is good, fresh, healthy food. Will definitely be back."

I have to give it a 3 star only because of the portion. Stopped in for lunch today. the service was great. Ambiance a plus and the time in preparing the food was also great being that I only get an hour for lunch. I ordered the jumbo shrimp with garlic sauce. When I got my food, I couldn't understand where the jumbo part came into play. I also couldn't understand why I was only given four (4) shrimp. If I had know I was going to get four (4), I could of stayed at the office and paid less money ordering from a delivery restaurant that would have given me more shrimp for less money. Very disported. 

Although I will say that I have had their sushi before and it is amazing.

If you have no other option of another restaurant and you go to this La Guanajuato, I recommend that if you get a waitress named TERESA, go running, you will lose your money. la verdad este lugar es bueno por la comida pero el servicio de las meseras es pésimo maneje 25 minutos para tener una cena con mi hija y la mesera fue  muy grosería  a ella no le importo que hubiera una niña menor con migo su actitud era pésima como si le estuviera pidiendo comida gratis  he estado en otros restaurantes y  los meseros son muy atentos y la verdad no recomendaría este lugar por que  la cajera y los meseros tratan mal a la gente piensan que estamos alli por comida gratis. si no tienes otra opción de otro restaurante  y vas en este La Guanajuato te recomiendo que si te dan una mesera llamada  TERESA sal corriendo perderás tu dinero.

This place is a hidden gem, tucked at the end of a dead-end street on the Hill in St. Louis city. It has a beautiful outdoor space that would be lovely to sit at during the summer. They host wine down Wednesdays which would be great to come to in the future. I ordered the salmon BLT which was delicious and came with sweet potato fries and a deliciously brined pickle. It's next to a really cute vintage market where I bought 4 vinyl records.

I went here with my mom and got a gel manicure. It didn't last very long but maybe that's because I am a picker when it comes to nails and picked it off. But she wasn't very gentle and ended up giving me a scrape on one of my fingers. I'm not going to exaggerate I didn't notice till later and it wasn't bleeding but it was defiantly from this nail salon!

Night one... Get to the check in counter, lady pulls up my name and tells me she needs a card for payment, I explain I prepaid on hotels.com and pull up the transaction on my account, she tells me to call them because her computer says collect money. I proceed to call hotels.com customer service who also tells me the trip is paid for, the lady at the desk interrupts me and says "I have you checked in, I told you to give me a minute and I would figure it out," everyone in my party heard her tell me to call them...
Night two... Get back to the hotel at 10:30PM and there's music blasting in the lobby, we're on the second floor and when we get to the room the music sounds like it's IN our room and the floor is thumping. Call the front desk to inquire because we're tired and ready to go to bed, the response we get is "they're paying guests," to which we reply "so are we," then were told that they have until 11:00PM to cut the music down. This is just ridiculous at a hotel, there's no nightclub downstairs, but we have to deal with this.. awful professionalism.

When I go here, it's honestly difficult for me to get anything but tots (the brekky bowls). But I have a soft spot for the challah French toast, and anytime they have pancake specials they have never disappointed! The blueberry lemon pancakes, the peach cobbler pancakes, and the caramel apple pancakes have been A+! 

Giving 4 stars instead of 5 due to it being a little cramped and sometimes the service isn't great. But the food is worth it.
Might be the greatest pizza I have ever had. Perfectly cooked and delicious. Prices are great. The staff was so friendly and nice. Wonderful experience. Go here.
Update. New owner again. Seems. More expensive and less on the menu. One pastrami and a bag of chips was 8.95. Food doesn't taste bad.

Amazing! Super knowledgeable staff. We were first timers and we had a blast. Maria was patient and super nice and they roll the cigars in house! I highly recommend for novices and it seamed like the regulars were having a good time too.

This is a really cool store, however the are supplies are very over priced. You can get them at like a Hobby Lobby or online for a lot cheaper. Other than that they do have a good selection. The store is okay, they always seem to have shopping carts of broken items and trash in random places in the store. The staff was less than nice, the cashier was very slow and didn't seem to care about anything. 

My wife and I like water color, so we got a small water color set, it said $12 on the self that it was on, (I checked the barcode) and when she scanned it it pulled up at $34.99. We said that's not right, she then proceeded to say "It is pulling up as $39.99, theres nothing i can do" Very rude! So we didn't get it, but we did get 2 other paint brushes. I do not think I will be back.
My favorite place for brunch! Worth the wait on weekends but easy on the week days.

I was always curious to see how the food was because I work near by. I can actually say I'm not impressed for the crowds I see heading out for brunch with family/friends every weekend . I ordered the farmers platter, don't get me wrong, for me to have barely finished my meal I was disappointed at the quality of the food. Ordered my food a certain way and it wasn't received as such, but while being at work and unable to to leave I ordered for delivery. The menu doesn't have a nice variety selection either. I will not dine in, I think I'm sold with my first experience....very nice establishment for both locations !

I'll start by saying if you are a bargain Hunter this is for you. If you are looking for anything specific then don't bother wasting your time. 
PROS: 
I have found a good air fryer about 2years ago which was only $35 
I found a robot vacuum for $30 
 Sometimes you can find 1 or 2 things in the $5 bins but that's only if you don't go often. 

I use to get diapers and wipes here for half of the store price but they don't seem to have much of those anymore.
CONS:
Most of the stuff there is junk or returned items that don't work. 
They tell you not to open things but when you get to the register PLEASE check your items to verify they work. I checked my item to make sure no pieces were broken or missing and it wasn't BUT I didn't check to see if worked! I assumed it would because it came neat in a brand new box!
 I got it for someone as a gift wrapped it and gave it to them. When they went to use it BOOM it didn't even have a flash of life almost as if it was a toy! Since this was a gift I wasn't aware it didn't work until 2 weeks after purchase. Unable to return, get a replacement, or even store credit even though I still have the receipt. Now I am stuck with a product I can't use and not to mention embarrassed I gave someone a faulty gift. 

The staff treats you as if you need them and not the other way around.

Poor customer service eating at the register. I had to wait until she finished chewing before I was checked out.
I am not sure if they are under new management but the bad outweighs the good so I won't think twice about going back.

The Skincare Lounge Spa is absolutely wonderful -- I keep going back month after month for a facial! Victoria is excellent -- she is professional, thorough and gentle. She takes her time to analyze your skin and gives you pointers to keep your complexion looking healthy and radiant. The prices here are very reasonable (not overpriced like other establishments) and well worth the monthly visit. I would highly recommend this Spa for anyone in the Philly area looking for a go-to spot for all their skincare and grooming needs!

Stop at the little town Verdi right before Boomtown and try this place out. Got the Grill burger for $10.50 with a ministroni soup.  The soup tasted homemade and was full of a lot of veggies and noodles.  The burger had the initials marked into the bun and the cheese skirt was great!  The onion rings were more of a tempura style and were perfectly crispy.  Everything had a lot of flavor and the service was nice and friendly.  I will be back next time!

Went here on a Saturday evening as a date night in the city. Everything was amazing. For drinks, I had the suave mojito, which was perfectly in-between refreshing and sweet. Hubs had the Ricky Ricardo which was also delicious. 

To start, we shared the smoked marlin tacos. They were bite sized perfection, packed with a good amount of heat and lots of flavor. The crispy dinner biscuits with whatever butter/olive/Horseradish (I think) sauce they brought to the table were unique and really, really delicious. 

For my entree, I ordered the Bronzino which came with Peruvian potatoes and a caper/tomato sauce. The fish was crispy and had a great chargrilled flavor and the sauce went great with it. Loved every bite of it. Hubs ordered the skirt steak which came with rice, black beans and a tomato-avocado salad. The bites I had of his were also incredible.

I knew we were going to splurge  on dessert (even though we were both stuffed) as soon as I saw the coconut tapioca pudding. I don't even words as to how good it was. He ordered a regular coffee, I ordered the cafè con leche and it was a great way to end the night. 

I loved the beautiful decor and architecture, the romantic atmosphere and most importantly the food. I'm going to have to try to the rainbow ceviche and the paella sooner rather than later.

Absolutely love this place! The salsa is delicious and the tacos al pastor is my absolute favorite thing! I have loved every single thing I have ever ordered and the staff is always so nice. My toddler who is a picky eater loves it too!

Slice is awesome. Their food is great, and they're super nice. My order from Grub Hub  came earlier than expected (probably my fault) so I wasn't home yet. They were so understanding, made me a new pizza, and delivered it once I was home.

I have eaten here several times and enjoy the flat burgers. The burgers are always fresh and hot. The wait staff is not the friendly type all business but very effective. The price is good for the quality of the sandwich. Will be back when I have a craving for a good burger.

I was at Tonic this past weekend for a bachlorette party. The night started great and we had a blast....AT FIRST! With that said I was wearing sandles that have a thick wooden bottom and a strap on the back, basically a nice supportive pair of sandles not your every day flip flop. I was on the dancefloor dancing when i felt an extreme pain in my foot. When i looked down I noticed blood gushing out of my foot. I ran over to our booth to realized that a large piece of glass had pierced through my shoe and up into my foot. At this point i got the attention of the bouncers to help clean up the blood and my foot. The bouncers were extremely rude and declined to give me a free drink because in their eyes it was MY FAULT for stepping on the glass. They kept claiming i had no shoes on so they were not liable for it, when i did IN FACT have my shoes on the glass was just large and sharp enough to pierce through my shoe and into my foot. At this point i had had enough. I declined signing any waver because i thought how they handled the situation was extremely rude, ridiculous, and inconsiderate. All I wanted at that point was to get the hell out of that place.

Really good delivery. We went back the next night just because we were craving more. The dumplings were amazing and the fried rice isn't filled with veggies (which i prefer). Sushi looked really good, I didn't order any and all the food was still steaming hot once it got to our house. I'm decibels going to try some different things next time because I was happy with everything I've had so far.

Not a bad po'boy, but not worth all hype it seems to get.  Tried the sloppy roast beef, and the beef just seemed a little bit tough for all the props it gets.  I am not a huge shrimp fan, so i doubt that I will be going to try that anytime soon, but will soon be back to try the other po'boys and sides they offer.  I tried this one since they said it was their most popular, and the cashiers favorite.  

The bread was good, but not exactly the same as what I've had in New Orleans.  The prices were good, and worth the money for how much you get.  Hopefully after my next visit, I will be able to give them another star, as it was just the texture of the beef that knocked them down from a 4 star.

This place is definitely cute and it fits into the entire state street environment and vibe. I came in here with my boyfriend because he wanted an afternoon snack. I looked through the displays and everything looked good. He ended up getting a basic chocolate cream filled eclair and loved it. I thought it was okay and tasted like any other eclair I've ever had so I didnt care for it. The service here was definitely good though. Maybe I should have ordered a different items and tried something else.

Ordered take-out yesterday because what better way to commemorate our servicemen and women's sacrifice for our country's safety than to pay for meat cooked by someone else. ~*tHe AmErIcAn DrEaM*~

I'll start by saying not all the staff members working the food pick-up station were wearing masks, and that's honestly unacceptable. They were very nice though, which I guess makes up for potentially breathing Corona all over me and my food.

My assessments of the meats vary widely. The brisket had bites of greatness: juicy and tender and packed with flavor. But those bites were surrounded by tough, charred, dry bits that made it overall hard to enjoy. I'm gonna chalk up any problems to it not being consumed immediately as they had to weather my [albeit short] drive home. The pulled pork sandwich was raved about by the fam, though notably did not come with the side of bbq sauce I had asked for over the phone when I ordered. The pork ribs were the best thing we ordered in my opinion, quite delicious. The chicken was moist but relatively flavorless and the skin was as tough as a thin sheet of leather. 

Sides: fries were bomb! loved them. Would happily eat again. Brussels sprouts were just sorta doused in warm, seasoned grease. Potato salad was great, with bits of bacon every couple of bites.

I honestly I don't recall my food experiences here in the past, but I know that every single time I come, they are out of biscuits... maybe one day I'll get to try these revered buns!

Also, obviously the milkshakes are awesome, and very instagrammable. 

Food: 3.5/5
Service: 4/5
Ambiance: 3.5/5
Safety: 3/5

Not gonna lie! I was iffy on the details of the history. How does a family forget something like a distillery business? Apparently this was common for boozers back in the day. Cool enough. 

Once I got over my skepticism, the history portion of the tour was over and I was able to enjoy the distilling process. If you're into alcohol creation or just manufacturing processes, this tour will be a highly of your visit. 

Finally we got to the money, a tour of the booze. Weeeww boy, that first taste blew my palette out of the building. After that O was just reaching for a hint of sugar or caramel. Not at all complaining about fearing a blind future! The Tennessee whiskey put at least one hair in my chest, and I will definitely be on the lookout for it in my neighborhood liquor store!

Came here for breakfast.  Service is fast, and the food is awesome, but I seem to keep ordering the same generic egg dish.  The place is big and every time I've been, we always seem to get seated immediately.
If it weren't for Old Man Surly, this place would be the best ever. But no, he had to interrupt our game of pool by shoving all the balls into the pockets midgame. One word: nagl.

It has a HUGE selection, but the prices aren't spectacular. Furniture is about the same price you could get at Walmart. They have an assortment of Knick knacks/books/DVDs that are really cheap. There can be a really long line sometimes as there are typically only 2 cashiers at a time. Overall definitely worth a stop but pay close attention to price tags- don't assume the price is good just because it's a thrift store.

The service was great. Food was good, not great. Limited menu. We were seated in the atrium, crowded atmosphere, which might feel better on a sunny day and the plastic walls are up.  When a place has a strict dress code and a good reputation one would expect more.

I just don't get it!  I travel all over the lower 48 states and have sampled Thai hundreds of times. I was given a recommendation to a local tavern, but Thai Aroma caught my eye on the trip to my original destination. To put it bluntly....I am a Thai snob!  The part that escapes me is the 3 star average of this establishment. The ambiance is far above average. The service is prompt and very friendly. AND THE FOOD....was really well prepared and authentic.  It just escapes me what more people expected from this restaurant??!!  I would definitely eat here again and I have no reservations recommending Thai Aroma, period!
nice waiters and waitresses, pasta and salads are great, Tiramasu is perfect, and good food for a good price

We ordered the Rockefeller and the 801 oysters, they were ok... the broadway oyster bar does them way better, 3/5. The scallop potatoes were sooo good 5/5. My filet mignon was cooked to perfection one of the best I've had. The grand marnier soufflé was mediocre. The service was exceptional. If you go I'd say stay away from the seafood and dessert. Stick to the entrees.

Wow, so not a friendly welcoming feeling. She was so busy on her phone. We were not welcomed when we walked in or told about anything like specials. My wife asked what flavor were the cakes, no answer. I had to ask for flavors specials. Would I Go back, NO. I get better costumer service at circle k. If I could give a minus star, I would.

The very best in the Tucson area. I recently moved an hour away but I still drive back to Mission. They have taken care of my pets from simple care like shots to the loss of a pet to recent cancer surgery for my dog. No sales push here. No fancy waiting room. They work their butts off every day. I'm retired and they gave me an affordable price for my dogs cancer surgery. The fancy vets were way out of reach.

Worst counter clerk lady ever. She seems to take pride in making things more difficult. It would be great if she would be removed from dealing with the public as she seems to despise people. Don't try to reason with her, it doesn't work. The sad part is that she was training someone new. Apparently job performance is not something you worry about in this position, at least not here. I live close by, but after 4 frustrating experiences I will make sure to go anywhere else, or hand deliver my mail.
I was there with a group. Most people liked their entrees, which however were a bit small. Great fish and chips. Love the local beer selection. No one liked the olive oil ice cream.

The absolute BEST smoke shop in the city. Knowledge staff, amazing selection, and good prices. The store is MUCH larger than it seems from the outside (it's like a TARDIS). They have a TON of work by local artists, and sometimes you can even catch the artist at the shop! There are plenty of people to help you find what you need! Make sure you don't say anything that can make you be asked to leave or refuse a sale (and if you don't know, point and grunt). Definitely go here for all your smoking needs!

Side note *there are no cigarette sales at this location, but nicotine vapes are available*

Walking in here truly makes me feel like I'm at the Liquor Barn in Kentucky -Perhaps it's because they're owned by the same parent company!

That said, Wine and Beyond offers an amazingly large selection of anything you could possibly want to drink. Prices are comparable to other liquor stores, but the selection usually makes it a one-stop shopping experience. 

Spend hours browsing the shelves, or even enjoy some taste-testing.
 If you're looking for something specific, you may want to ask for help, however. The store layout has Sangria appearing in multiple locations, and with so many options, it may be difficult to locate exactly what you're looking for!

Very disappointing. Really bad meal. Steak frites should be great, right? Tasted rewarmed. Overdone. Ordered pink. Gave a taste to my friend who spit it out. Her $28 chicken was cooked to a frazzle sloshed with gravy. Her salad was soaked with dressing and who ever heard of a caesar salad filled with olives and tomatoes?? She couldn't finish it. I left mine. Frites were soggy and luke warm. At least we laughed a lot.

I have had my eye on this restaurant for quite some time so I was excited when my friend recommended we eat here. We walked in and the place was small and simple looking. It was pretty empty around 5:30 on a Saturday. By 6:30 it got pretty full. It was uncomfortably quiet in there. We were seated right next to the only other family in there even though the entire restaurant was empty. I actually started to whisper because I feel like the other customers could hear my entire conversation. Perhaps they could have music on?

 I ordered the chicken pad Thai. Thinking back the lady never asked what spice level I wanted which I thought was pretty standard at most Thai restaurants. The food came out really really quick so I assume they had it pre-made and just warmed it up. It was average in my opinion. In fact, it was a little on the sweet side. I probably should have asked for a spice tray to jazz it up a bit. Service was average. The waitress was not overly friendly but she did refill drinks. I want to say my dish was between $10-12.

Ok that is the best way to state what this chain is. They are overpriced, trying to be fine dining , food taste good like a Big Mac from McDonald's is good. Not worth the time or money, service was also very slow for food I am sure is prepped or frozen , If the food is made to order then is just ok at best not worth the price tag.
Would not go back and would tell everyone to go someplace else , plenty of better chains and family restaurants with better food and way better prices.

Food was excellent!  But my waiter had an attitude and a half!  He was so rude, couldn't make any good drink suggestions, and sent over a different waiter to deal with my table when I found a hair in my margarita.  GROSS.

Ididilydo, I love you!  I tried this place while visiting friends.  My friend and I have had an affection for all things sweet for years and often dreamed of opening our own cupcake place.  She'd been telling me about Whodidily for a while now and upon my trip down south we went.  I'll preface this by saying, these are "All American" cupcakes -- meaning they are sugary sweet BUT let me tell you, they are GOOD.  I happen to like this kind of cupcake more than a less sweet, posh cupcake.  We bought 1 dozen so we could sample the flavors.  Extravagant yes, yummy yes.  My favorite was the Lazy Days - vanilla cupcake, while choc chips, vanilla frosting but ALL were good.  Not one dry cupcake and the combinations were great.  This place has it down.  The only reason I didn't give 5 stars is that I am still waiting for the place that blows me away.  Who knows, if I don't find it, it may just be that I need to give Whodidily 5 stars.  I can't wait to go back.  I think about the cupcakes often...and by the way, just for comparison, I stopped at Crush Cakes on my way back up North and have to say, I like Whodidily better.  Crush Cakes are good too but the combinations and presentation at Whodidily top it.  Hip-Hip-Whodidilyday!

Just moved to area in July. Went alone and my wife will be glad I did. Now the service was exceptional, the food was your average breakfast, nothing special but fine. Coffee was good and was always fresh.MY issue here.. The table across from me was the typical "Snowbirds" from NY and with the restaurant not too full, the rude chick demonstrated such a Sailor's Mouth and was SO loud! What's sad is that All waitresses heard her mouth and didn't say A Word!!! A definite locals place where things like that slide, unacceptable and won't be back.. Too many other places around to try...

The Midtown Diner is reminiscent of all of the terrible times that Philadelphia went through in the 70s and 80s. As you're inside, eating their frozen food and staring at the extremely dated, but not charming decor, it's hard not to imagine driving away in your 1980 Ford LTD while passing by Philadelphia's Rizzo-era hookers, abandoned cars and drug addicts. There are restaurants in our city that have aged well, keeping mostly the same, with the same great food. The Midtown Diner isn't one of them. Last night I had the pleasure of having their chicken tenders, only to find out they decided not to fully cook them. After showing the waitress my raw chicken, she took the chicken back to the kitchen and subsequently brought the same (previously raw) chicken tenders back out on the same plate. She was clearly confused at my disgust, as if this were a regular occurrence at the Midtown and did not taken the chicken off the bill. As Philadelphians, we should shun Center City's sketchy 70s and 80s, embrace our new restaurants, enjoy our newly gentrified city and finally let the Midtown go.

There was a distinct odor that seemed to permeate throughout the entire main entrance as well as the left dining room.  I couldn't seem to place it at first until we placed our orders... it was coming from the server/chef?  A rather taller/large man who, upon approaching our table, smelled so foul (from sweat, which is why I assume he was the chef) that I could not tolerate to look in his direction.  It honestly just smelled like a locker room / bad body odor.

Once he left, the smell was not as strong and I continued my night, as my nostrils and senses had become used to the smell.

No complaints about the food.  It was actually quite yummy.. once I was alone.  Portions are rather large, and they were served within a timely fashion.
We love the Fat Tomato!   My favorites are this anti pasta salad and the blackened salad.  My kids love their pizza, burger and meatball sub.   Food is fresh, always prepared well and delicious

Takaya New Asian is one of the latest restaurants to open Downtown on Washington Avenue.  Located in the Mercantile Exchange District near the Convention Center, it's the perfect spot for pre or post-movie dining with the new theater that's opening next door.  I visited on a Friday night during their "soft opening" and had an excellent experience overall.

We were greeted by the friendly staff who gave us a choice of a booth or table.  We chose the booth which was an enclosed cube shape space with seating on three sides and a view right out to the entry and street; it was the perfect secluded spot for the three of us.  The struggles of a new restaurant were somewhat apparent, but overall our waitress was very knowledgeable and familiar with the menu.  

I decided to try the drink of the night which was some sort of Saki-martini concoction.  Made with cranberry red bull, citrus pearl vodka, pineapple juice and sweet sake, it was a smooth, subtle and refreshing drink without all the sweetness.

The menu was divided into Small Plates, Specialty Small Plates, Sushi/Sashimi, Maki Rolls and Fried Rice, and of course we had to try a bit of almost everything!  

For the small plates, we tried three different items.  The crispy calamari for $7 was the best of the three, and had a lightly breaded and nicely spiced coating which went well with the wasabi aioli sauce. The gyoza or pan seared dumplings with shrimp for $6 was served with a house-made Japanese red pepper sauce.  They weren't bad, but I found most of the other dishes more exciting.  And of course we had an order of the obligatory edamame for $4.  

The Specialty Small Plate kicked things up another notch with more interesting dishes.  We made three different selections for this section of the menu, and all were great. The creamy rock shrimp $9 was a particular highlight for me.  The tempura shrimp was tossed in a sriracha mayo type sauce.  It reminded me of chicken wings tossed in a hot sauce, but this was an amazing shrimplicious Asian version that was delicious!  The Korean style short ribs, marinated in a sweet sauce, were also a favorite at our table. The meat was lacking the saucy thick coating that you often see with Korean ribs, but the subtle seasoning worked well with the perfectly grilled strips of meat which was piled up in a healthy portion.  The Mozzarella Tempura dish for $7 was quite intriguing and turned out to be tasty. With a spear of asparagus jutting through the chunk of cheese, this creation was tempura battered, flash fried, then topped with a wasabi sauce which really brought the ingredients together to make a successful dish.

For sushi, we ordered three rolls.  The special sushi roll of the night was the crystal roll.  Wrapped in rice paper, but without the rice, this roll consisted of yellow fin tuna, avocado, cucumber, jalapeño, sprouts, lettuce and sweet chili sauce.  I've tried a variety of non-rice sushi rolls which often turn out bad, but this one was light, refreshing and tasty.  It somewhat reminded me of a sushi-Vietnamese spring roll fusion.  The Jurassic Park roll for $11, was visually impressive and equally tasty.  It included shrimp tempura, crab, cream cheese, eel, avocado, tempura shavings and masago.  Finally, the Sex and the City Roll for $11, included scallop, crab, tempura shavings, avocado sauce and duo of a hot red and green sauce.  I would describe this one more like Miranda; kind of ordinary looking, but with a hidden spicy side.

Overall, we had a great experience at Takaya's soft opening and plan to return soon.  While a few items were 3 to 4 star, there were some knockout 5 star items that I really liked, so I'm going with 4 stars for now.
Meh. Coffee was fine. Pastries were dry, decent taste. Donuts were great. Music was screamy death metal shit.

I am floored to see the abysmal reviews considering I have found this location quite decent. I had sworn off Dunkin' about a year ago when I started to see various locations continuously get my orders wrong. This one was different. Today I went to this location, and they got the order correct.

I ordered a egg white croissant sandwich, with maple bacon and white cheddar, and a chai latte; the sandwich was on-point. The chai latte was almost on-point had the hot chai latte had been hotter than warm; If the drink isn't almost burning my esophagus at first sip, it isn't hot enough. By the time I was done, it was room temperature--not good. I deducted a star for that, but I'm sure it was a one-time fluke.

Service is prompt and efficient, if a tad cold and indifferent. It is very obvious that they (Dunkin') are not as picky about the types they hire as Starbucks, Dutch Bros, and the like--hence the lower price and lesser perceived overall quality.

I would like to say that Dr. Maxwell does a good job, but I can't because I never met her.  I made an appointment to address a specific issue, but ther office does not have the capability to address said issue.  I had communicated clearly with the office over the phone and e-mail; They had no qualms about wasting my time with an appointment for no reason.

Also, the appointment turned out to be with a technician only, rather than with the doctor followed by the technician as I was initially told.  The technician was not able to go over other questions that I had.

I would give this place a higher mark but why is it freakin cold in here??? 

Experience ruined by the estimated 65 degree temp!
Had the chance to visit this place during our night and thought it was okay. It's kind of a smaller night market with a lot of art.

My visiting daughter and family wanted to try some "famous cheesesteaks", so off we went to the two big rivals, right across the street from each other.  The family was all waiting for my daughter and I at Pat's, while we were at Geno's. 

I'll start with first impressions: My daughter desperately needed to use the rest room and Pat's told her to go over to the park adjacent from them!  We did and there was no rest room!  Geno's was right there, so we asked them and they slipped her into the back and left her piddle.

Now we get to friendliness, cleanliness and decor.  Pat's is old and dirty and has not spent one dime of their profits to stand out in these three fields.  Geno's on the other hand has put a lot into these areas.

Now for the cheesesteaks:  Even though we were all headed to Pat's, my daughter and I were so impressed with Geno's that we ordered our steak and fries there. The other 5 in our party stayed at Pat's. 

My daughter and I took our order from Geno's over to Pat's to eat with the others ( tacky for sure and I would have rather ate at Geno's because of the cleanliness and decor). 

First of all, to order a sandwich and fries at Geno's, you must order them and pay for them separately at two different windows,  I can see where this works for them at busy times, but we were the only ones there.  

The meat at Geno's is much, much better than at Pat's.  Everyone, but my stubborn son in law agreed.  The bread is better at Pat's.  The onions are raw at Geno's and sauteed at Pat's.  With a grilled sandwich I would have preferred the sauteed, but I did order a cheesesteak hoagie, so that may be the reason. 

Pat's uses more cheese.  I had provolone. 

All in all, if you add it all up, Geno's definitely has the better of the two cheesesteaks and wins hands down for decor, cleanliness and friendliness.

I ate here several weeks ago and was not too impressed.  We ordered sandwiches and it wasn't bad but it wasn't good so it definitely wasn't great.  I will be honest I feel a bit jaded though because I was turned off by the customer service.  When I walked in there I picked up the menu and ordered my food.  When my food was ready I paid for it and the amount seemed so much higher than the prices I recalled on the menu.  I am not cheap but I am talking like a $4-$5 jump in what should have been a best calculation.  So I looked at the menu again and called the guy out and said I believe I was overcharged, he informed me the prices recently changed.  Now here is my issue - if I were ordering from a old menu at home I understand the whole statement "Prices are subject to change without notice" but if I am walking into your store and pick up a menu those prices should always be accurate as they are the menus you  have physically in your location.  When you change prices you should update your menu immediately and not have menus in your establishment with old prices (this is bad practice).  To make it even worse when I said we what are the new prices for some of these items I ordered he thought about it long and hard like he was just going with the flow and making them up and with the prices that he stated to me they didn't even match up to what he charged me.  I just found it to be odd that he couldn't give me a straight answer, he apologized and gave me some of my money back but it turned me off - I am a person of principle.

The only reason I dont give this a 5 is price. Was the food delicious ? yes. I got the Bill Neal and the shrimp and grits with bacon was really good. Here is the thing ... it wasnt a big plate it was a little bowl. Yeah Yeah quality over quantity. I get that and its not a matter of affording it. Its just not much... you want to be full after you eat. Anyway, the staff is friendly and the place is nice so no complaints besides the one.
Good place!  Take your time and browse, I picked up some christmas gifts and treats for my dog!

As a nurse I can provide a healthcare sided review to this office. The office is run by Dr. Parag Patel and Dr. Shah.  While the physicians are average skilled in assessment and treatment their bedside manner is unprofessional and demeaning.  The staff will "forget" or "lose" your referral numerous times yet continuously blame your insurance provider.  If you are fortunate your call will be answered by a respectable administrative assistant at best 10% of the time.  This office takes on PA students (docs receive a paycheck from the college/university) who herd in groups and yell your PHI so loud Dunkin Donuts employees can hear.  Finally the physicians are purely financially driven in your care, well truly lack of CARE. Worrying about CODING your visit for optimal reimbursement, and speaking about this in front of you (the patient). Then telling the staff that you will need an additional separate appointment to address your health complaints because they can't code and receive money for addressing it at this visit.  I understand medical coding and reimbursement, as a consumer you need to be observant and question how this deceptive office bills/codes you and your insurance. Easier to drive to Marlton, Voorhess or Medford! Convenience of local location isn't worth the trouble.

Came across this coffee stop while stopping by a friend's house- nice area; clean facility; friendly courteous staff; restrooms available; outdoor seating; free parking; coffee was piping hot and strong just how I like it. Bakery items offered. Some kitchen items available- after seeing someone else's order of scrumptious avocado toast, I regret not ordering any. Reasonable prices.

Food was great and our server was very attentive and helpful. The only reason I'm only leaving a three star review is because one of the waiters took the broom used to sweep the floor to sweep out the warmer where the keep the chips that someone had spilled. Not cool at all.

I visited The Loft for Sunday brunch and it was excellent. The food was very tasty.  I had the traditional eggs benny with coffee. Someone at my table ordered the bloody mary which was very good, but a bit on the pricey side at $9. 

Wait staff are young and friendly.  They can be a little slow at times, but that is the expected when you are in the country. This restaurant is all about slowing down, enjoying beauty and eating a solid meal.

Waste of time. 

I was told by a T-Mobile rep I could get a new phone. I get to the store and they have no idea. I even have the conversation from their official app saved and show it to them. No good. 

T-Mobile's reps and corporate don't know what's going on.

We were just looking for dessert on a weeknight at 10pm, not specifically vegan, and this was the only place nearby that was still open. The ice creams were delicious. I had brownie and strawberry shortcake. My boyfriend had cookies & cream and pistachio. I have never liked pistachio ice cream but I tasted his and was thoroughly jealous. It was perfectly nutty, sweet, and tangy. Two scoops was way to much for me, and as it warmed up, I began to taste the coconut milk it was made from more. I'm not a huge fan of coconut, so my man finished mine. The staff was friendly. It was a great value and a chill place to have dessert on date night.

This place is terrible with communication and service. I placed an order in April, they showed up at my door in August with no  call, text, email or anything regarding their delivering on that date. I was not home and they left an oven in my Livingroom with my 13 year old daughter! The service included installation and removal of the old oven, well to this day the oven is still sitting in the Livingroom and no one will contact me about returning to install or refunding my payment for that. 
You are on hold for an hour waiting for someone that brushes you off and does nothing to resolve your issue! I would recommend it your in the market for appliances GO SOMEWHERE ELSE!

One amazing experience.  After a 3 day road trip to Eagle we stopped in for a massage.  I honestly have never had a massage like this one!!!  The bad thing is, no other masseuse will ever be able to live up to my new expectations.  Kristy was beyond amazing and she was so sweet.  That night I slept like a baby.  Thank you Kristy and Eagle Day Spa.  My new favorite place.  

I also had a facial.  My first one!  Loved, loved, loved the experience.  Dianne was so kind and gave me great advice for my skin.

The most relaxing tiny bookshop café ever. The book collection is fantastic, and they also carry some fantastic stuff. This was my third visit, and the barista was really pleasant and kind. 

They have a wonderful selection of lattes, including one named "Kobe"! I wish I lived in the area so I could make it my regular hangout, but I'll definitely be back. The atmosphere is fantastic. There is a decent assortment of literature for all ages. In my view, this is one of the most friendly spaces in Philadelphia! 

The size of the space isn't awful, but I wish it were bigger for one reason: so that more people could experience the positive mood and inspiring atmosphere.

Food was definitely average.  My quinoa burger was rubber, the onion rings were great.  Service was slow and our server referred us to a QRC for the list of beers of the day.  A server should have a printed list or be able to recite the beers of the day. 

But the worst problem was the incredible noise on an early Saturday night:  other customers making lots of noise.  Could not hear people sitting next to me.

Staff is nice, but can not fix a cheese pizza with half cheese and half supreme.   Does not have thousand island dressing for salad.   They can not fix what I want so why would I pay them for something I don't want.  They are trying to dictate what they think I like and no ajustments at all.

I had to get a sonogram done, and the first available appointment was 2 weeks out. When I arrived at 7:30am, I was asked if I ate or drank anything, and I said a glass of water. Well, what do you know, the person that made my appointment never told me that!  And yes, when I stated that, I was told I should have been informed. So I had to reschedule, and next appointment available was another week out!  Not happy, and it is 3 days before my doctor's appointment, and it takes 2 to 3 days for my doctor to receive the results.

I tried to stop there this morning at 10:15 AM and they were closed.   The sign on the door, as well as the voicemail message indicated that they open at 10 AM.  I had my bike on the back of my car which I cannot take to work with me and have nowhere to store while at work, so I was dependent on them being there when I arrived.  If your sign says that you open at 10 AM, please open at 10 AM!  I am now late for an appointment because I had to go to Montgomeryville Bike Line to drop the bike off.   With the upcoming MS 150 city too short bike ride this weekend, you would think that you would want to make yourself available for potential customers.   Don't be a participating bike shop if you're not going to be available to inspect the bikes for the MS 150 bike ride.

I love this place! Their Tikka masala is to die for! The spicy will require you to have tissue on hand, but this is a good thing! I love their samosas for an appetizer, the chutney is for spicy lovers only though! the naan bread is so soft and flavorful. The service is amazing to the point where we felt spoiled! I do not understand why it never seems to be busy with how delicious their food is. Their serving pots are beautiful as well. The prices are reasonable! Wish I could eat here every week!
