Buffalo wild wings, a new addition to the King of Prussia Mall. Some of my friends have told me that they take their kids to this establishment a lot. I had only been once in the oaks location and wasn't "wowed". All of my people were craving different football watching food so we decided to venture out past our norms to Buffalo wild wings. We order door Dash take out and it arrived swiftly and warm. My kids absolutely loved the double bacon burgers. I enjoyed a few wings with orange sauce like A Chinese restaurant. They have a great ordering system where you can order flats only which is my dream. My husband liked his Cobb salad, looks pretty good for a wings place.
I gave Buffalo wild wings 4 stars because they don't have breaded chicken wings as an option. I crave them! The food was good and I would recommend ordering from them. We liked their double burger and the wings pretty darn decent.
i mean, it's just not my style.  i like danceable music and mixed drinks, but i try to remain objective.  if you like college crowds, live bands, and beer, this is a great place for you.

I have been on this tour 2 times after dark and one time during the day (today 5/16/16). I absolutely LOVE doing the tour with Carl!!! He is awesome Oh and btw he has a book that he wrote about his experiences that you can buy here as well. Sorry off topic. Our tour guide today was Betty, she really wasn't my cup of tea but that may be because I am used to the way Carl walks you through. Both are very informative and of course the building itself has a rich and somewhat torrid history. 
We have had our fair share of experiences here as well. From my arm being grabbed and my hair played with all nicely, to banging on the walls so loud during a night tour that Carl rushed us off to another part of the top floor. I highly recommend that you visit here.

Crab Rangoon 3 for $2.95
Very thick and fat with filling, heavy with green onions.
1/2 order $6.95 plus $1 for white meat.  Sweet and sour chicken. Light tempura, 5 pieces of fresh green pepper, some sliced carrots and cold pineapple. Underwhelming, but decent.
Brown rice was fluffy and had good texture. Their chopsticks suck! Bulky plastic things!

Fantastic stay, even for just a night.  Room is wonderfully laid out and furnished, down to in room snacks, drinks and spirits.  The entire place was clean and staff was over the top friendly and ready to serve from the valet to the front desk to wait staff during our conference.

Conference facilities were top shelf as well.  No complaints, suggestions or ideas on how to improve.  Just keep doing what you're doing!  I'll be back!

Service Department: Scheduled an oil change for my Jeep Wrangler, when I arrived I was told about 90 minutes, Jeep was completed in about 45 minutes which included the exterior wash. Second time visiting this dealer and was satisfied both times.

This small pizzeria has a whole lot of heart, and it really helped carry them through this insane year. 

Kudos to Walter, Judy and their team for pushing through 2020 and keeping us well fed with their delicious pizza. Operating a restaurant is already difficult enough, throw in a pandemic that forces businesses to reevaluate and Re formulate their entire operation and you've got a real problem. Watching Smiling With Hope adapt with grace was beautiful. 

Walter and Judy are closing their doors for the next month as Covid cases are on the rise to keep themselves and their staff safe. I can't blame em. They deserve it after this year. 

You'll be missed. Enjoy the break.

My husband and I went to dinner there Sunday, September 12, 2021.   Were seated in a booth.  As we were finishing our meal, a mouse appeared along the top of the booth.  I let out a scream!  The waitress came over and we told her what we saw.  She didn't seem too concerned but went into the kitchen.  We thought someone from management would have come over and apologized.  No one ever did.  We informed the waitress that we were DONE and she dropped our $100. check for full payment.  As we left, we told her to inform management that we will never be back. She said ok.

My husband emailed the restaurant when we returned home, we haven't received any response as of now.

Such a disgusting experience!  Even worse, no one even cared about our disgusting experience!!

The Board of Health will be notified!

We ordered veggie burgers being vegetarians like we have done many times. What we received was burgers with bacon in them and no bun!!!! Can't believe they messed up this bad. Don't think we'll be ordering ever again!

Unequivocally, the finest fast-food company on the planet. Best service, quality of food and taste. This store is no exception with outstanding service and consistently excellent food. Why not 5 stars? It is fast-food, after all.

Too bad there is another really good vietnamese restaurant in the area.  The food is good here, a little westernized then I would have liked.  But still good.  If you are in the area, stop by, i would recommend the broken rice dishes, as the Pho here is not the greatest.

Cool hipster work spot. Great vibe and excellent tea.

I wish they had more food options other than pastries. I had a meeting there, but we ended up leaving and continuing at another location with lunch food because we were hungry.

eh, it's alright. 

Nothing is terrible, nothing is great except for their butter cake dessert. 

The pizzas are ok. Some better than others. I would avoid the Greek pizza, and get the Jerk. Don't get the thin crust as you could probably eat two of them in one sitting and still be hungry. 

It's also a little pricey. Not my favorite, but certainly don't hate it.

This place apparently gets a lot of bad reviews, but my experience was pretty good. I went in to get a new phone last weekend as mine was just acting up. My associate was Eric, and he was really helpful. He showed me which phones I could get for free, before he tried to sell me the more expensive phones. I had some questions on which case to get, and he was knowledgeable on which case would work for me. He put my screen protector on my phone and it was perfect, not a single air bubble. He is even helped out my father as what he had done the day before was a worse deal then what he could get that day. My only issue was that there was a lot of up selling, which I guess one should come to expect in a place like this. If I have any other issues I will be going back to this location over my last experience 3 years ago.

First time eating here or I might even give it 5 stars. I will return and update this rating.

Very good diner style food with their own homemade recipes. They have a number of specialities especially on the breakfast menu which they serve until closing at 2pm. I had a Wild West scramble with their own homemade salsa and a side of hot cakes. Everything was terrific. The staff is friendly with very attentive service. 

Been at least a dozen times now for breakfast. All their scrambles are good. Huevos Rancheros are excellent. And yes, if you like hotcakes, not pancakes, they are terrific

So I have been to the Ruby Slipper a couple of times and was not disappointed with the food and service, however TODAY'S service from the kitchen SUCKED.  I was seated at the bar at approx. 1045am and my food did not arrive until a full 45 minutes later and it was ICE COLD...unacceptable....returned the food and had to wait another 10 mins.  I have been with a much larger party and didn't wait that long.  Thanks to Jessica our server who was very kind and very apologetic for the issues in the kitchen.  I'm not sure who was over the kitchen staff on December 27, 2019 at 1030 am but something needs to change. I was ranting about the Ruby Slipper to a guest from out of town and this happened.  To say the least I was very disappointed that's the reason for the 1 star.

They have done a great job with this location. We have been to the one in Las Vegas and this is comparable. Our visit was on a rainy Saturday but the food was great. The atmosphere was nice. We will be back.
Their boiled crawfish is so perfectly seasoned.  It was so tasty that I had wished that we had ordered more.

Stopped in for breakfast this morning while waiting on my flight. The staff is very friendly and as the hostess was seating me she asked what time my flight was, I assume so that they will know rather or not I would have time to sit and eat. 

The restaurant is very nice and clean. There is even an 'outside' eating area where you can sit to people gaze as I like to call it. The music they were playing was sooo 80s and I loved it. I was able to sing while I waited for my food, which made the time breeze by. They have a nice bar and about 5 TVs to watch around the restaurant. 

Now the food no had the Perfect Breakfast which was a steak and eggs and breakfast potatoes. The eggs were ok, and the potatoes were good. They were like wedges but cooked great. Now the steak on the other hand, was a huge let down. I paid $20 for this meal and the steak could have been a better quality. I ordered it medium well and it was a bit cold but was as if they have them pre-cooked and warm them as they are ordered. I also had a cup of coffee and let me tell you, it was strong but it was good.  

I give Shula's 3 stars because I think their quality of food should be better. They are a very popular steak house in the Tampa area and if they want to drive longer layover guests to their restaurants, I think they need to do a bit better at this one in the airport.

I went there for the first time today and it was awful. We arrived at 6:20 PM on a Friday night. It was not busy at all, just like normal. We ordered and recieved our food by 7:40 PM. An hours wait. It did start to get busy at 7:00 PM. The waiter then brought us ANOTHER pitcher of margaritas of which we joking said, "we will take another IF it's free since it's taking the food so long."
In the long run, all the food was wrong. We asked for chicken enchiladas and burritos and they brought us beef. When we told them they said they can not make chicken. (I about lost my mind there) Then the manager said they only had 2 chefs in the kitchen. On a Friday night that is just ridiculous and he did not even offer to fix anything and told us we had to pay for the extra pitcher. Which by the way was untouched and we never drank. 

Just down right horrible management which makes for horrible servers. 

On the upside, maybe on a slow day when they don't have to do much work may be better, because the food was good. They just need to work on their customer service.

Truly a great place. 10/10 would recommend. I went there today with a group of friends and we all loved the place. The staff were all very friendly. The food was extremely delicious and worth the price. Comfortable seats and clean tables and floor. Defiantly coming back here in the future.

I'm going to put a disclaimer: 

I would try again, maybe I got the wrong dishes. The mom and pop feel and treatment is good enough for me to go one more time. 

I like going to mom and pop. The food was average, and I even questioned if maybe they put velveta in the dish I had. The person I went with had a better dish, but overall I thought it was so so.

If you cook at home a lot, then a lot of the dishes are easily replicable, I think that is why I may have thought it was so so.

The wait staff was very nice. It's worth a try if you have not had Peruvian food.

This place can only be ruined by people who act as if they've never been to a show. (Looking at you, kids, who think you can shove your way forward to a pit 30 minutes into a set.)

The sound is iffy and, yes, the line upstairs to the bar can be slow. But that's not a big deal. The sound is not the worst around, the size is great, and they pull in great acts. For every crappy security guard everyone mentions, there are at least 2 great ones. Bartenders are friendly and prompt, beers are cold, and bands are good. Cant ask for anything more from such a large venue.

Major pluses: free parking at the Avenues that T to the beach (arrive early), beach showers at every Avenue.

Major minuses: a significant percentage of the beach is almost uncomfortably narrow due to erosion, free parking area has a significant number of spots reserved for residents most of which are usually empty.

Overall a nice beach. Every Avenue access point has a beach shower, a couple of park benches, paved up to the start of the beach, and has nice landscaping including some shade in the bench area. (Take THAT Treasure Island!)

The sand is soft, clean, but is hard packed enough near the water to make walking easy. Many people use the beach for their walking exercise.

Is not exceptionally crowded for several reasons. There is not a huge amount of parking, the island is very narrow so there are not a lot of residents to walk to the beach, and the motels tend to be small.

Don't expect to be able to toss a Frisbee for football around is there really isn't enough room the beach. A great place for sunning, splashing in the water, walking, and just relaxing. Not much of a party spot.

Given the facilities at the Avenues and a bit of free parking, this beach is likely to become more popular due to Treasure Islands move to charge for parking even at cramped unpaved streets that don't even have a beach shower.

Gametime is nothing but a wanna be Dave and Busters. OK if your looking for something to do when there is nothing to do.  Personally I could find better ways to pass my time.  If you have kids it might be a bonding experience, however.  The food is not the best and the soda was flat...at least what they served me.  I was not impressed and have no desire to go back.

Mr 100 from Hell's Kitchen cooked the BEST BBQ we've ever had. My husband loves BBQ and we've tried so many places. This was the best! Outside patio watching the sunset, live music inside that wasn't too loud. Friendly people all over. The beans and slaw was SO good. Perfectly prepared. And the ribs were smoked to perfection. The place was along the river and we were somewhat skeptical but I turns out to be the best hole in the wall BBQ we've ever had!! Y'all must try this place. Oh and the mac n cheese is to die for!

Slowest service ever. 

It took forever to get water that we had to ask for, let alone side dishes to snack on. They didn't replenish them either (unless they only give you the initial serving?). She didn't mention the utensils were in a drawer on the side of the table so I sat waiting for a few minutes despite her rushing passed our table without a glance so my food was cold by the time I was able to eat it. It seems as though there's only 1 server why the service was lackluster but it also wasn't super busy either. 

Food was decent. 

Overall, won't be going back here.

I always forget why I don't like ordering from this place. My most recent delivery reminded me why. 

The wonton soup that I ordered had some veggies in it. In the first bite into the bok choy, I bit into a rock! Owie! A simple rinse somewhere along the way would have gotten the dirt out of the veggies. *

I also ordered the mixed vegetables with seafood fried rice. The seafood fried rice is just a few pieces of shrimp mixed in with rice and soy sauce. The mixed veggies had a good assortment of veggies, but drowned in a brown sauce. ick! 

Another disappointment? In addition to the food being pricey, they charged me twice (total of $4) for delivery. 

* wash your veggies. Farms usually don't rinse the veggies because the shelf life of rinsed veggies is shortened.

Bulrush STL is a magical experience that must be experienced. This is one of those restaurants that is worth a special trip, even if that means you come into St. Louis for the day just to eat here. 

The restaurant has a wonder mirrored layout to it where the front bar mirrors the chefs counter in the back. Very comfortable place that makes you feel at home. 

The service is top notch from front to back, but the true delight of the service is chef Connelly and his friendly and Inviting personality. He is very engaging through the whole dinner service. He really takes his time and explains every meal in depth and gives you a true understand of where the meal came from. 

With tasting menus I always get concerned that I won't like some of the meals throughout the dinner. With Bulrush this was not the case at all as every plate was packed with flavors that worked so well together. The turkey thigh with corn purée and house made barrel aged hot sauce was definitely the highlight  of the meal with the pumpkin pie(not a dessert) a true honorable mention. In the end though everything was great, even the squash was delicious and that's not something I typically find myself liking. 

Bulrush STL comes with my highest recommendation, and can't wait for my next trip to St. Louis to dine with Chef Connelly and his team.

This place knows sushi. It's a craft. They serve it with care in the way that real sushi is made and served. They make their own mackerel pickled in house and they use the spanish mackerel. The lunch special is a freaking steal and the white tune is amazing. I can't stop stuffing my face so I finish it off with a handroll (usually spicy scallop) and walk away happy. Really lucky to have this high quality place in St. Louis. It is everything the opposite of Drunken Fish (this is a good thing).

They never returned my calls when I called them about cracking.

They did a complete pool and deck remodel.   I don't recommend them.

Food was GREAT.  Had the bistec, the octopus, and the pork belly and all three were genuinely great.  Waiter also did a good job directing us on how much food to order.  Cool ambiance in a small place, it's BYOB, and the neighborhood it's in is adorable.  

But, right as we were finishing ordering, the server asks us if we would like a side of rice.  My husband, who is always hungry, nods and says he thinks that would be a good idea, so we say yes.  Turns out the "side of rice" was actually just us ordering an $18 bone marrow dish that came with rice - we felt like we had been totally upsold and our bill was obviously higher than we anticipated.  When the waitress brought it out, I told her we hadn't ordered the bone marrow, but she said "this is your side of rice, and the marrow is just a plus!" so we thought maybe they were being kind and comping it.  Not a huge deal, but we will certainly be being more explicit in ordering the next time.  

Overall the take away is that the food was amazing, but we were frustrated with this one aspect.

I'm so torn on the rating of this place so I'm going with 3 for now. If I was judging on Pupusas I would give it a five, but yesterday for my main dish I tried to go with a meal that looked straight out of a Nicaraguan restaurant, but clearly I made a big mistake after I took my first bite. 
From the outside they advertise a Nica flag and even have some decor in the inside, but I don't think anything on the menu is Nicaraguan whether it appears to be or not. 
Nothing on the plate had any flavor to it. I would not recommend the carne Asada to anyone, you will be disappointed! 
Everything on the plate tasted the same. No distinct flavors, just grill and salt. It was bad, and the cabbage salad that accompanies this meal tasted like it had lemon instead of lime, and not fresh lemon, the one out of the bottle, which I hate because it has a soap detergent taste. 
That was the first time ever I didn't like the Queso frito, it was slightly rubbery like, so salty and tasted like I said, the grill. Thumbs down. 
Now, we also ordered pupusas. The chicken&cheese, and the pork. Both awesome ! Loved them. Highly recommend! Passion Fruit Juice is house made and FANTASTIC! 
Now when I come here again, I will just order more from the food items made out of corn because they clearly excel in that area. The tamales, tacos dorados, pupusa loca I want to try because it's a mix of all the meats, enchiladas, pollo con tajadas looked good. 
The people next to me ordered that, and looked like they were really enjoying it. I wondered how it tasted. Plantains w/crema also looked great as a side, and I've read a lot of good things about the soup. Combinacion guanaca hmmm maybe. Probably not. 
This place was packed when I walked in so I know it's good, I may have just had bad luck the first time, and I'm not giving up on this place yet. I was happy about the fact that I did find Bags of these little Nicaraguan corn treats called rosaquillas. They are not for everyone, but I love them
We were seated upstairs at rhe barsavona right away, the service was great and the food was fantastic.  It's a bit pricey but very good.  Portions were just right.

We had reservations but got there early. The place was empty. We were seated and ordered drinks. Drinks were good. Then apps. A dozen raw oysters. While they were good the tray they were sitting in was full of water, not ice, but gave them the benefit of the doubt because it's so hot. Also ordered boudin balls and crab beignets they were good. My daughter ordered the tuna sandwich but got a burger. She was ok with it. I'm not sure if we were charged for tuna or a burger. My son also ordered a burger. Both liked them. My husband ordered something shrimp, sausage and rice. He said the sausage was cold and the shrimp tasted off. We did get a side of Brussel sprouts which were really good and Lima beans & rice which I didn't eat. I ordered soft shell crab. The crawfish sauce on top was good, but the crab was off. It tasted bad, like it shouldn't have been served. It sat on a bed of maque choux but I really think it was just frozen corn. 

Getting there and being the first customers of the evening you would think there would be toilet paper in the bathroom - no. At the end of dinner there still wasn't any. One of the kitchen staff was in the other bathroom and when she came out I tried to tell her there was no toilet paper but she just ignored me. 

Go for drinks, not for food.

I just want to start this off by saying how wonderful the staff is at Dory's. I called in a special order (my boyfriend has Celiacs and gluten makes him sick) and the woman who answered the phone was knowledgeable, patient, and willing to help + listen. Big positive. I also want to say that unlike some other places with "Gluten Free food" my guy did NOT get sick after eating here, which tells me they are very very careful. Which is a huge positive.

On how careful they are: I received a call from a very apologetic person telling me that the cook couldn't guarantee one of our items were gluten free because of how busy they were. She offered me alternatives. The fact that she called up and didn't just give us the food speaks VOLUMES about the care they have for the customer.

Now onto the food: it's amazing.

The chicken was moist and not dry. The sauces good - especially the schwarma. The Hummus was probably the most flavorful Hummus I've ever eaten. The homemade cinnamon sugar pita chips + chocolate Hummus was so good. I wasn't feeling their pita bread much at first but honestly it tasted so good the next day - weird huh? But I'm not going to complain! It held up well and they give you so much of it to go with all their delicious dishes.

All in all, I'd definitely recommend Dory's, especially if you have a food allergy because they take such good care of you. And their prices are so reasonable. We love Mediterranian in my house and we ordered from another place - so expensive - Dory's gives not only quality but quantity for their price. I will be coming here again.

I know the owner reads this so I just want to say thank you for wonderful service and food. You made our last date night special. :)

Do not get the fried rice. The rice is full of pepper, no egg or vegetables. It tasted like uncle bens rice. It was the most disgusting meal I've had in a while. We went on a Saturday night and there may have been 4 other tables being waited on. You'd think it would have been busier.
excellent service. kudos to our server Pat. He was great. Tuna Ahi app was delicious. and the prices were pretty good too.
Hannah was awesome. Great conversation and great prices. Also, the cheese fries were AMAZING. I will definitely be back! Hannah made the experience the best.
Service sucks. Never again.  I've tried this place several times and each time it's been horrible.  I'm done!

The food and service were great, so we'll go back again.  Our problem was that even though their website stated the early dining specials were from 4-5:30, when we arrived at 5:03, our server told us that early dining stopped at 5.  So we had to order from the regular menu.  At 5:15, a group of four came in, they were given early dining menus and ordered from them -- they had a different waiter.  We complained about the inconsistency, but by that time our meals had come. We were given pieces of key lime pie on the house.  They just need to get their policy straight.

Took my mom here for lunch, it was her 67th birthday. We arrived and ordered quickly, hoping to catch a movie after. It took 45 mins (if not a little longer) for us to receive our food and we mostly ordered off the appetizer section, just three small things. They weren't busy inside and the people around us had their food in front of them. The waiter was kind and said the long wait was because of all the to-go orders placed. I knew it wasn't his fault by any means but it was annoying that we came in to eat and were being shafted by orders going out. By the time the food arrived we only had 10 mins to eat before we had to leave. I've worked in restaurants for 15 years and we always made the sit ins our priority while managing the carry outs as they came. Maybe they were short staff, but it would have been nice had they given us a discount or something instead of just telling us 30 minutes in our food was delayed. I felt trapped and pissed off. Better yet it would have been nice to have known the wait before we were seated. My mother was growing anxious and I felt like her birthday lunch was a let down, which sucks because she's always raving about this restaurant (but mostly the one in Cool Springs). Just feels like there was a breakdown in communication at this location. Won't be back, and wouldn't recommend anyone going here unless you have *a lot* of time to kill.

I've been to this spa several times with the service and quality severely diminished over the last year. I kept going back thinking "it's not that bad" but have finally decided I am done. Service has been terrible with little communication of when the manicure/pedicure is even done. And, they've recently raised prices. I would say skip this place, as there are many others on the same street anyway.
no bueno...annoyed I couldn't get any service and the prices are a joke.

I've been here many times, and I just can't believe I haven't reviewed it yet, as It's one of the best Steakhouse in the city. 
Vegetarians, you'll have your day. But this experience for all of you MEATatarians out there:
You'll be offered many, many and many meat by the many servers every minute as long as your green coaster is facing up. You'll feel as if the Butcher himself is proffering his choicest cuts right at your table: Sirloin cuts, Beef Tenderloin, Rib Eye, Chicken, Lamb and even Pork if you eat it. So hail to the all the meat.

Honorable mention to the amazing side dishes Fogo de Chão offers, such as the crispy polenta, warm Pão de queijo "cheese bread", mashed potatoes, and caramelized banana that's served throughout the meal. Plus, the exotic and fresh salad bar including Feijoada (black bean stew & rice), seasonal soup and more. Aslo, Their New York Cheesecake isn't bad.
 
P.S: Remember to turn your coaster to the red side, after you are done stuffing your face.
P.P.S: Try to score a table during Spring Restaurant Week. 
P.P.P.S: Male sure to come Hungry.

Four and Half Yelps.

I went to Campo during the first week of opening with my boyfriend and two friends.  I have been anxiously awaiting its opening for months now, so I pleased when we got a reservation on a Friday night opening week.  However, we were sat in the very back by the kitchen and wine cabinet, whereas, walk-ins were seated in better tables.

Our table got the salumi plate for an appetizer.  The meats were very delicious, and it came with a nice mustard for your dipping pleasure.  We ordered two bottles of zin wine, and the wine was very nice.  Two people at our table ordered the campo pizza.  I had a bite of it and it was amazing.  I loved the sausage, salami and pancetta on it, though I would prefer a bit more of those meats on it.  

Now my biggest gripe about Campo...I ordered the wild boar bolognese, and my other friend ordered the rigatoni with duck sugo.  I could not believe my eyes when our waitress brought the pasta dishes out, which came out at least 5 to 10 minutes after the pizzas did, but that wasn't too big of a problem.  It was the size of the dish.  We were served delicious pasta dishes, but the portion was the size of a kids dish.  I paid $16 for pasta that left me hungry.  And not just a little hungry, but I could have ate another bowl or two.  They did not serve a salad or bread with it either, which would have been fine if the portions were doubled the size.  Either bring the price points down for pasta, or offer two portion sizes like Mark's other restaurant, Moody's.

My boyfriend and I decided we must try the gelato since we love gelato.  We ordered the pistachio and chocolate.  It came with a nice biscotti which I did enjoy, but the gelato wasn't all that impressive.  it was grainy and not very creamy at all.  The flavors of the pistachio were good, but it didn't fulfill my craving.

I wish them the best and hope they take the reviews into consideration because I did enjoy the food, but would more likely go during lunch or avoid getting the pasta dishes until they have more generous portions.
Fast service but I'm sorely disappointed in the food. I ordered vegetarian spring rolls and Oad Thai.. Both were lacking flavour and the Pad Thai had a weird smell. Also, really expensive imo

I love this place! It's cute and quaint. I love the hanging aprons on one side..the whole vibe is so light and happy. The pies are pretty darn good too. Their most popular is the butterscotch bourbon which lives up to the hype. I recommend pairing it with the African nectar tea (if you're a tea person you'll love this one). Overall a light and airy affair! Will definitely drop in again next time I'm in the neighborhood.

I am by no means cheap, and if the experience was good I wouldn't mind paying $3+ for a bagel with cream cheese. So let me start by saying the actual bagels are wonderful. However, we ordered three bagels and asked for them toasted and one of them cut in half. None of the bagels were toasted, two of them were actually cold, and the bagel we wanted cut wasn't. So two stars merely for the fact the bagels taste good baseline, but otherwise these employees need to drink coffee before they go into work.

I was wandering the FQ last weekend and happened into this shoe store since I was trying to find information on a closed store across the street. The staff person couldn't help me with that and since that was my initial question, she didn't really seem interested in helping me in the store. Perhaps we just got off on the wrong foot. (How could I resist?)

The shoes are off beat and aren't terribly expensive and they do carry a variety of styles with heels and flats and spectators, etc. The store is really spacious and the decor adds to the funky vibe.

I was in need of Pho so in a search this place showed up.  

1) Place was cleaned and welcoming.

2) Waiter was Friendly and attentive.

3) Pho was very delicious tasting. There appear to be only one size. It is smaller than most places I have been to. Pork sandwich was good.

My Summer Rolls were too moist and one broke apart right away and the other came broken from kitchen. I would not have served it that way. Peanut sauce was okay.

Overall this place was good l. I recommend you give it s try.

Second trip with my portable critic. As expected, the perky staff was friendly and efficient. Had the smoked pulled pork burrito on the recommendation of the girl at the counter. It was nice, fresh, large. good mix of flavors without anything stepping on the others. Portable has the kids menu chick tacos and ate to her fill. Fresh mango juices was a hit. Churros for a nice desert. Overall, fast fresh and friendly. Great casual decor without going "fast food". Just a well executed operation.

So what can I say about this place. 
I have had lunch and breakfast here a few times. The burgers are pretty good and breakfast is very good. Service is a little slow but they get super crowded. Quite possibly the worst coffee I have ever had in the city, but is a big deal for me in a breakfast spot. I would recommend bring your own coffee when going for breakfast
Been here twice and I have not been disappointed.  The live music is always on point and it's a hidden gem within NOLA. I definitely would recommend coming here with all my friends.

Ordered the grilled pork chop last night for dinner. It was so over cooked I had a hard time even getting through one half of the chop. I should have sent it back. When the waiter came by and asked if everything was okay, I told him the chop was super dry. He offered additional sauce. That would not have made the meat less overlooked!  I'm visiting friends in Florida and was looking forward to trying a new restaurant. I live in Monterey area of California and we do not have a Bonefish restaurant nearby.

I have been coming to Greek city since they opened 6 years ago. I have never had a bad meal.  There falafel is best I have ever had.  Feta fries are amazing. Katy the manager is a great gal. One of the few great restaurants Spring Hill has to offer.

Yikes! Defiantly a miss for us tonight from start to finish. The stuffed mushrooms had a very odd flavor. We didn't eat them because it tasted chemical, not natural. The server said they pour white wine over them. I don't think so. The four of us each got a different entree and none of us finished ours. The portions were huge but very low quality. The poboy was watery and ended up a flavorless mess. The pizza had a very thin, burned crust with too much cheap cheese and not enough real toppings. The muffalatta was so much bread it was hard to find the meager filling inside. Maybe it's good sometimes, but not tonight.

I like IBUs.  I seek them out like some... deranged IBU-seeking wierdo.  Their menu had some serious IBUs dripping from it.

Dub is a good beer.  Drink it slow.  The Swill is also good (no IBUs here...), and daring, I think.

The open interior flows easily into the outside drinking areas, and always affords a view of the sparkling vats.  The servers were friendly and informative.  This will definitely be a spot to visit every time I get to Boise.  Just get there early... or get there late.

The staff was super slow and distracting during the entire movie!  Took them half an hour to bring soda and popcorn.  Then we had to request butter, which they said they would bring to us without even taking the popcorn bucket- so I have no idea if they were thinking they'd bring it in a dish or something???  Anyhow, they never even came back to answer that request.  Then we had to additionally request napkins. I have never been to a theater where you order popcorn and they don't ask if you want butter!!!  And why would you bring food (we also ordered pizza) and popcorn without bringing any napkins?!?  The staff did this to everyone in the theater so they were in and out constantly and talking to people during the entire movie!  It was horrible!  Plus the theater advertises on their website that this location has power reclining seats- which is does NOT and the seats that they do have were very uncomfortable.  Save yourself the hassle (and a lot of money) and go elsewhere to see a movie!

Since it was our first visit here I opted for a straightforward option, the standard "Farm Burger". Grass fed beef cooked medium well and it was VERY tasty, juicy, well cooked and flavorful. The carmelized onions and bun were great, the cheese perfectly melted. I thought the standard burger came with bacon but if so I saw no sign of it--however, it was so good I didn't miss anything really. It's a well sized burger too, not too big nor tiny by any means. The real selling point--as if the burger wasn't enough--were the fries. I find that most places who specialize in great/gourmet burgers often slack on the fries for some reason. Not this place--super crispy and perfectly seasoned. My wife went with the regular fries and I gave the Farm Burger fries a shot--delicious parmesan and caramelized onion going on, really good. My wife went with the chicken burger and it was pretty enormous, about twice the size of the beef patty. It was also SUPER spicy, like hot chicken level 3-4 spicy, which is more than the average spicy chicken sandwich. She really enjoyed it however.
I'd definitely come back to try the veggie burger, the pork burger, the milkshakes, and some of those craft beers on tap.
Other pros: plenty of lot parking, plenty of seating, clean restrooms, friendly staff, and though pricier than fast food for sure (our meal was about $33) it's not exorbitantly priced.

No Remedy for this Doomed Restaurant!

Il Remedio opened at the site of the former Rx on 45th & Spruce.  So much going for the place; so little coming out.  It's hard to fail in this neighborhood:  Penn students tend to flock toward novelty; the regular residents like to support neighborhood businesses; and there's quite some foot traffic.  So all a new restaurant has to do is provide reasonable food at  reasonable prices with vaguely competent service, right?  Wrong.  Il Remedio gets it wrong from the beginning, and there was literally nothing it did to remedy (ha!) itself or to suggest it cares to do so throughout one incomprehensibly bad meal.  

Let's start with the decor:  white tablecloths in a neighborhood restaurant--  with ill-cut curtains that sort of crunch over at the bottom of the windowseats-- fine if you're going for the grungy down-market dorm look, but somehow the two just create grotesquely contradictory curb appeal ("we want to be fancy [tablecloths] but we're lazy and sloppy and couldn't get our curtains properly sized at Ikea").

But who goes for the drapery:  let's get to the food.  I brought 7 friends in on a recent Tuesday.  We had plenty of dining options:  I dragged them here to support my neighborhood.  We ordered all the items on the appetizer list and were uniformly disappointed.  Cockles w/sausages sound great if they had something other than shells; the sausage in that dish seemed like it could learn a thing or two from Hebrew National (you know, taste, texture, complexity).  The radish salad had a few industrial looking pieces of white something and we're still debating if it was cheese, filler, or what.  The scallops with beans and "bacon foam" would have been nice if someone had bothered to add beans and to drop the foam (a few driblets).  And the "greens" on the dishes had that same industrially grown and processed feel-- heck, Trader J's might provide better produce (and no, that's NOT a compliment).

The housemade pasta was what I was looking for and it arrived lumpy, with the linguini actually angel hair. When the error was pointed out to the  waitress, she first berated the cook ("I told him to put linguini!") then came back and insisted that their "house made" linguini actually resembled that angel hair on the plate.  Our party dissed the risotto as well, and the pork on the menu (or was it lamb?) was tough and disappointing. 

But wait, there's dessert!  Aah:  lumps of more industrially prepared and cut gelatin appeared ("berry terrine").  The wafer had been sitting in the cream so long it was a soggy fan; the cheese platter ("we don't know the cheeses since we buy them the day of serving" informed our helpfully non-compos waitress)-- well, it was more of the industrial stuff that again TJ's could do better.  The profiteroles looked like they came out of a plastic can and did not  taste much better.

All the growing pains of a recently-opened restaurant, you say?  Maybe.  But in this very forgiving neighborhood, a lot might be overlooked for a competent, intelligent waitstaff.  Our party was one of 2 in the restaurant that evening, and our incompetent, simple-minded, and inept waitress seemed ignorant of every single aspect of her job.  At one point, we actually had to phone the restaurant to get her attention ("sorry, you're missing half our order, and could we please have that corkscrew you promised about 40 minutes ago.")

So, friends, support your neighborhood.  Eat at Honest Tom's down the street for a fraction of the price, or head to Kanella (10th & Spruce) owned by a W. Philly resident for superlative food and service.  But caveat emptor on this one:  there's simply no remedy for the horrors you'll find at Il Remedio!

Let me start by stating that I am really giving Evangeline's 3.5 stars.  We went for Valentine's Day dinner.  Before even entering, we were greeted by the sounds of a live jazz band that really set the mood for a fun and lively atmosphere.  I understand live music is a staple of Evangeline's, and I hope they continue with this feature because it really does create a special dining experience.  The interior is warm and well decorated.  We had a great table in the dining area next to the window.  The bar area is really nice as well.  Our server was friendly and accommodating.  It did take quite a while to receive our drinks, but it was Valentine's Day, so they may have just been a little busier than usual.  Other than the drinks, the rest of the service was fast. 
 
The reason I'm giving 3.5 stars instead of 4 or 5 is because of the food itself.  Overall it was good... it just wasn't exceptional.  We started with the Blue Crab Cakes as an appetizer.  They were crispy on the outside, and the Jalapeno Aioli was tasty.  I wouldn't say they blew either one of us away, but they were good.  For my entrée, I ordered the Seared Scallops with Champagne Risotto.  Unfortunately, this dish did not meet my expectations.  The scallops were very small, and I was only given 5 or 6 of them.  They were dry and chewy.  The risotto was quite bland.  The only flavor that I detected from it was onion.  And that was the entire entrée!  Halfway through, my husband and I decided to swap plates.  He had the Louisiana Crawfish Etouffee.  Now this was good!  It had an abundance of tender crawfish tails, and the sauce was flavorful with a nice spicy kick.  Since Evangeline's is a Cajun/Creole establishment, it's a good thing they do the Crawfish Etouffee well, or they'd be in trouble.  For dessert we shared the Bourbon Pecan Pie.  I'm not too much of a dessert person, but this was my favorite item of the night!  You can really taste the Bourbon in the house-maid Caramel Sauce (in a good way!).  It's devine!
 
Regardless of my entrée bust, we will very likely give Evangeline's another try, and next time I will be sure to order something from the Classics section! Overall, it's a good experience that everyone in STL should try.

We visited this location at the peak of dinner hour, so naturally there was a bit of a wait for our table.  I must say, however, that the young man seating folks was on the ball and worked hard to accommodate the large crowd as quickly and efficiently as he could.  We had a special request for an outside table because we had to make room for a walker.  He checked on us frequently so we could see that he hadn't forgotten us.  

The food is typical for a buffet diner.  There are real hits on some menu items and a few less than successful items too.  You can't please everyone with every single item.  I was mostly surprised at the quality of the fried chicken.  It is a favorite of mine but is usually way too dry in places like this.  My chicken was moist and extra crispy with plenty of great seasoning.  Bravo.  Anyone who complains about the variety is in the wrong place.  There is something for every tastes and that is why we go.  There are 4 of us in our little family group and no two of us has a similar taste for food.  Going to Golden Corral always leaves all 4 of us satisfied.  The price is more than fair considering you are only limited by your appetite.

Minus the muddy park, this is one of the better festivals I've been.  They have multiple stages with variety of music genres.  Really solid lineup of artist throughout the day.  The later into the evening the worst the lines get it overall they move along fast. A very eclectic selection of foods, mini haunted experience and carnival rides. 

Best to Uber to the festival.  Worth a visit but get tickets early.

Wow. What a pleasant surprise on a Sunday evening after a movie. I love authentic Chinese food and this place is a legitimate Szechuan restaurant.

First, service was fantastic. Servers were extremely nice and patient. One even had her daughter with her who helped drop off some tea for us! (Service 5/5)

Second, after we ate, we spoke to the waitress who told us the head chef is from Szechuan. The food certainly tasted like it.

The Hot and Sour Soup (5/5) was actually spicy unlike some other places that just make it very mild afraid that the average customer couldn't handle it. A pleasant surprise.

We also ordered Cold Sesame Noodles (5/5), a Beef with Pepper Dish (the best dish of the evening based on the suggestion of the waitress 5/5), and Ma Po Tofu, which had great Szechuan spiece to it. Three bites in and I was sweating already (5/5).

The place is BYOB which I also love. 

Overall, a great evening (5/5)!

I've been here maybe a dozen times over the past two years, which is pretty bare minimum considering I'm vegetarian (and eat vegan as much as possible) and this place is less than a block from where we live. The food, desserts, and matcha are very good, and I'd probably spend half my paycheck there every week, but I absolutely can't stand going here because the employees are absolutely awful. Every. Single. Time. 

I've been greeted a total of one time when I've gone in there, even when there's no other customers. And that single time was by a new employee, so maybe she hadn't been trained to be rude and ignore customers yet, who knows. Every other time I've gone there or walked by, it's the other two main employees (the girl with the dark long hair and RBF, and the guy with the long dreads) that are the absolute worst. It's very clear that they're dating since they spend most of their time cuddling up together inside the restaurant or out front of it. The one time I walked in and she was sitting in his lap in a chair at the two-top table near the stairs and bakery case and gave me the rudest look like I was annoying her for coming in and interrupting their personal time. 

Regardless of how much I like matcha, it's not worth me having to be treated like dirt by the employees here for 10 minutes (they take forever to make a simple powdered iced matcha even when no one else is in there).

It's funny how so many people state their bagel bona fides by proximity to New York City. You know, "I'm from the City, so I know bagels..." 

Sorry, I was born and raised there myself (Washington Heights), and I've had some great bagels in all 5 boroughs, but also sampled pretty bad ones in all corners of the Apple; often in the same neighborhood. True, soft water from old wooden water mains helps, but nothing takes the place of a great recipe and patience. So, the truth is there are awesome bagels to be had almost everywhere the proper ingredients and skills are present. That includes just about anywhere in the Eastern USA, and any bakery close to sea level. 

So, not sorry.

The Bagel Cafe in Delran, NJ, is but one example. It looks innocuous enough in a bland Rt.130 North shopping center in the middle of Burlington County. But, it would be a mistake to pass on it out of a mistaken assumption that nothing exceptional could come out of an oven there.

 Au contraire, Mon Frere. 

That Onion Bagel overlain with Veggie Cream Cheese was a true delight. Its texture was perfect - firm crunch on the outside; airy light texture within. Mild sour flavor perhaps from the brief bath in boiling water with brine or lye to toughen its skin, yet not too much kneading to harden its tender heart. Piquant spread to pull the varied elements together. 

True, I think I detected a soft Russian accent from the women behind the counter. That would give them an edge in understanding the cultural imperatives of a true bagel, but whatever their secret, their creation had me imagining old country roots in the villages outside Kiev. 

Outstanding work, my friends!

Not worth my time to contact the franchise owner of this DQ, but this is the 3rd time I have been and the same employee has treated us disrespectful. She said she didn't care if a complaint was filed on her and said her name was kaitlyn (not sure on spelling- she spelled it out for us,  I didnt write it down.) She said that we didnt matter as a customer. Saddened to see that this is the type of employee DQ supports. Most people aren't spending money in this economy right now. Employees should treat every customer like they matter, as we are the ones keeping them open. I hope her day gets better and other customers weren't treated like we were our last three visits.

Stopped into Real Food Eatery for lunch today and am absolutely obsessed! As a health coach, I couldn't be more thrilled to see a menu that was so well thought-out and full of delicious and healthy options that can cater to practically any dietary need. After my meal I also got a chance to talk with John, one of the founders, and loved hearing about his personal passion for changing lives through better nutrition as well as their mission to make nutritious REAL food quick, easy, affordable, and accessible to the public. Truly nothing better than great food created by people with incredible integrity and passion for making the world a healthier place! Will be back ASAP.

Stumbled into Cunetto today as I was exploring The Hill neighborhood. 

Pros: 
Amazingly soft fresh bread from Vitale's Bakery. 
Delightful Italian server who was brimming with recommendations. 
The lunch portions are HUGE. I saved half for dinner. 
The eggplant spagetti is a flavorful treat with a hearty helping of sauce.  


Cons: 
Sadly, there are not many vegetarian lunch options. 
Salads are served with iceberg lettuce.  Meh. 
Trys to be upscale but doesn't really succeed. 

Since there are so many other places to explore on The Hill, I don't know that I'll be back any time soon.

Very unhappy. Ordered a turtle and they made it a concrete. Made a caramel sundae a concrete. Bagged it and the cups were turns on their sides. What a mess! ! Called to speak to the manager and he could have cared less! !

The service in this location is very poor. The person who identified himself as a "Manager" by the name of Damond or Dane Davillier is very disrespectful. When I called the store to speak to a Manager he refused to talk to me for being a woman and stated he would only speak to a man, regardless that the receipt of the purchase was under my name. Him and other agents that answered the phone are very rude and do not know how to provide customer service. This place is dirty and disorganized and it explains the type of people they have working on this location. Very disappointed.

I will start with the good. I tried the lobster sandwich and the strawberry poppy seed salad. Hi really enjoyed the salad. I could have eaten that alone. It was fresh and flavorful; not overpowering at all. The lobster sandwich was decent. The negative was the service from the cashier. She was not kind in any way. The cashier next to her was better than she with speaking to me. However, it's not going to keep me from returning.

So very disappointed in our last visit to Miguel's. We went in for a late lunch. We ordered a margarita. They tasted watered down. When we received our food it did not seem fresh. It tasted like it had been warmed in the microwave.  Not sure we will be going back here.
Great environment and atmosphere. Pretzel dogs were amazing. Could probably eat triple digit of them in one sitting.

Unfortunately I have to give a mixed review on this one.  I went with a friend and we split two dishes.  I had bacon wrapped pork tenderloin which was the bomb.  Perfectly cooked, the sauce was incredible and the presentation was spot on.  My risotto on the other hand was a bit to be desired.  It seemed a bit undercooked and crunchy.  Now, if that's the way it was supposed to be please accept my apologies.  My friend had the ribeye which, on the first try, was over-cooked and charred.  The server said that was the dry rub that supposedly contained sugar and created a charcoal like flavor (not sure what that was all about but it wasn't so good).  When they brought it out a second time it was cooked to our liking and without a charcoal crust.  

We topped it all off with some gooey butter cake which was incredible (supposedly grandma's recipe).  Perfectly crunchy crust and soft, gooey insides.  I couldn't have asked for a better dessert.  All in all I was pleased and will likely try this place again.  I'm wondering if the quality of food may have been related to our reservation time...11:00 PM...after the show.  We were 1 of 3 tables in the restaurant and I'm guessing they just wanted to leave.

After hearing great things about Martins BBQ I was very disappointed!  We waited in line for an hour only to be surprised with cold food and cafeteria like ordering. Roof leaks and slippery conditions contributed to this review. Great band on stage was the only thing that prevented us from leaving.  Poor service I will not be back.

Pretty good! I think this will be my new go-to spot. I ordered a chicken salad and an iced coffee. Coffee was good and they have plenty of non dairy options. I have to take one star off bc although the salad was made quickly on the spot, it wasn't a tossed or chopped salad; it really just reminded me of a prepared salad you'd buy at a supermarket. It just didn't really taste incredibly fresh and made on the spot. I really want to go back to try their sandwiches. The ambiance also seems perfect for studying!

I have not dined off of the full menu, just in the bar upstairs (Black Duck Bar) from the bar menu. The food is straightforward but good quality. The drinks.... oh the drinks! I love a good craft cocktail and they got it right. The menu has some creative cocktails and also some of the classics- with a twist, as it is a rum bar so most of their cocktails are made with rum. I visited the Black Duck twice in a long weekend because as you can imagine so many of the alcoholic beverages you will find in the area are premixed and very sugary. This was the complete opposite. The Black Duck Down has a dehydrated orange sugar rim that they make in house and let me tell you, I may have to try it at home it was so delicious. The staff was great and happy hour is very popular. My only complaint is that not all of the cocktails were on happy hour special, but they were worth paying full price, so what can you say?

Great little "Irish" dive bar in the neighborhood that I return to for the great bartenders, staff and patron companionship. 

HOWEVER.............

Recently the owner has been making significant cut backs - kitchen doesn't open til 5 pm, several good menu items dropped, etc. I've seen several customers walk in and right back out after hearing of the new changes. I love this place and it is saddening to see how the owners are driving off customers/employees and running it to the ground.

If you do stop by make sure to ask for the curly-haired tatted bartender, Alex. He's the best! Hopefully they don't drive him away....

I hate leaving one star reviews but the ramen here is INEDIBLE. I enjoy Dosunco and Ichicoro for their flavorful broth which is why I was heartbroken when I first tasted the tonkotsu ramen. The broth was like lukewarm milk. Not like a cloudy broth but straight up milk. There was ZERO flavor so the add ins had no flavor either. My son had the spicy ramen and it was the same except...spicy lol. Neither of us ate more than a quarter of our bowls and for $15 a piece that's really upsetting. They should stick to beverages and phase out Ramen because they clearly don't care about the quality of their food. Yuck!

Not as good as we expected.  My fiance and I went to celebrate an anniversary.  We had such high expectations due to all the reveiws we read on yelp, but left very disappointed.  It was a hot summer weekend, and this tiny restaurant was packed with people.  The first thing we noticed was how uncomfortably warm it was, and I am rarely warm.  Also, the noise level was the loudest I have experienced and we have been to plenty of other intimate dining experiences to compare.  It was not a pleasant noise level, I could barely hear my fiance, and we pretty much stopped talking, because it was very frustrating, Understandably, that is not quite the restaurants fault but, the temperature is. Please upgrade your air conditioning system.  On to the food, My fiance ordered the scallop with pork belly appetizer, which was great. I ordered the crab and shrimp salad which was pretty good, but flavors got boring after awhile. Then for our entrees he had the King Salmon, which he said was nothing special.  I had the duck breast with foie gras, lets just say I honestly feel as though the forgot to season my plate.  The foie was delicious, but the duck breast, which is a staple menu choice for me, was extremely bland.  We did not bother staying for dessert, because we were so uncomfortably warm, we could not wait to get outside.  There are so many restaurants to experience if one does not stand out we will not go back.  I think it is safe to say we will not be back here.

Worst experience ever. Rude. Had a reservation and changed because of illness in South America. Lisa said she changed the reservation. Arrive at hotel tonight, and Joyce says, "no room for you. Your reservation cancelled, and not changed. No room here tonight or in the city". Very rude, went on to help other customer.

I am borderline vegetarian and rarely eat red meat; however, the burgers here are delicious and I can almost NEVER turn them down when someone suggests we go here. These are the best burgers I have ever had. The superior quality is consistent and their customer service is unmatched. Please, do yourself a favor and try this place. You will NOT be disappointed.

had a nice meal of fresh seafood right on the boardwalk!

we ordered oysters, ceviche, uni-topped scallops and a crab sandwich.  everything tasted fresh.

So we sat there for 51 minutes waiting for our burgers and they were ok, not the gourmet they promised by any stretch of the imagination and o asked for crispy fries and the waitress had to put in another order because the ones I was brought out were not even close. The nicest thing I can say about this place was the waitress was good. Moral of the story: I don't feel like the burgers are worth that price at this establishment and the impossible burger while good is not worth 3 dollars and 50 cents more.

I came here during an event hosted by a radio station of Philly. I was on a guest list so admission was free and I was able to bring a guest in for free. Upon entering, it looked like a very decent bar. The lounge area was pretty cool, with private tables located on the side of the lounge. The dance floor wasn't that big, and the place in general was kind of small. It's classy, but there's nothing more. The drinks they serve are very general, and they are quite expensive.

One thing that really stood out of the ordinary was that they consider the venue a skybar. I generally thought of skybars as lounges that are on top of buildings overlooking a city skyline. This skybar was by far, a huge let down. It's on top of a building, but it's only 3 stories high. It has a view of the Philly skyline, but it's like a view you would have if you were on the street. They have tables, or I should say beds since they were like huge mattresses, chairs, and private areas on the roof, and a very small space that I guessed was the dance floor. Everything was wet, though. I believe it was the rain from earlier, but there were coverings above them so I didn't understand why they would be wet.

One more thing I didn't like was how anal the policies are. I went to the skybar area when I first entered, since it was empty. When I tried to go back later in the night, I wasn't allowed to because I didn't have a drink. The bouncer said I must buy a drink downstairs to be allowed upstairs, which I thought was stupid. I ended up not going back up to the roof.
Authentic, fresh, great service and reasonable pricing. Highly recommend this new hidden gem!
The best Thai food around!! Love this place and we eat the curry and noodles and soups at least once every single week. Super nice staff as well! Highly recommended!!!

Meh! Not sure what all the fuss is about. Ordered the All that Jazz with sauce. Kinda a huge sloppy mushroom and cheese sandwich on a limp roll with a couple of shrimp. The Mrs had a catfish po boy which was far better but still wtf its a sandwich. If you are a visitor from another galaxy you may be amused but honestly... Its a sandwich. They need a better bun with a crust.

Nice new place. Good stage for live music. Got a table right away but the service was lacking. Chicken was decent but not $22/chicken. Out of very common bottled beers .  PA is could come down 15db . Mix for the room not the packed house it was not.

In my search for a good haircut, I had been frustrated that I had to travel all the way from Hermitage into Nashville to get a somewhere trustworthy.  That has changed, since Next Quality is in Mount Juliet.

My very first review was a complaint against the one of those standard unisex salons with a name like "style," "master," "clips" and "cuts."  These places are trained and priced for women's cuts.  Even the "sport" cuttery seems to have no knowledge of cutting men's hair.  I have long since given up on "stylists."  I want a barber!

This is the opposite: I am very satisfied with the cuts.  It's not a mill where they get you out of their chair as quickly as possible.  My barbers have spent as much time with the shears as they have with the electric clippers, in order to get the job done as well as possible.

And the price for the best quality cut I can find in middle Tennessee: $12 as of May 2012.  That's much lower than anywhere I know of.  I cannot recommend this place enough, and Mike and Brian deserve to have men and boys lining up out the door for the value and quality they offer.

I like this place. I want to like it more. The food is very good and the menu has variety yet traditional offerings.
The ambience is dour. A scratchy radio plays raucous music that emanates from the kitchen.  Am I being subjected to what the kitchen listens to or is this really a conscious  music choice?  The bright spot lights kill any dining mood and create a non appetizing glare and the cheap furniture and silverware could be softened by some plants or dimmer lights or candles. 
The waitstaff are friendly though not as professional as other places. It's one of those all too common places where the meals are not timed and when plates are cleared they leave a fry on the table just for good measure.  We were told a live band would be playing on the weekend.
So I have to ask what kind of place this wants to be?  A Chef that prepares above average meals (the dip was the best one I've had in years) and touts wine pairings has a poorly lit cheaply furnished environment with wafting radio sounds of metallic rock and will now offer live music of the same on weekends. Then just be a dive rather than having this good food. I want the good food but relaxing music and relaxing lighting and relaxing vistas. If I wanted scratchy music and harsh lighting and a live band I would enjoy the dip sandwich for sure but not bother with main dishes or wine pairings. 
What do you want to be?  A nice restaurant or a bar?  Vero amore has found a way to be both though they have more expensive interior and outdoor seating that plays tummy friendly jazz. 
I want this place to succeed. I would ask the owner to get out of the kitchen environment and dine in his own place.  It needs a customer-centered make over.

I was super stoked to try this place and be able to get out for my sister's birthday, but it was extremely underwhelming. The food wasn't bad by any means, but the whole experience was just odd (nothing related to COVID). 

The soup special, bisque, was not explained well at all and it included jalapeños. I have never had a bisque with jalapeños in it, while I loved it, my dad ordered it and he does not like spicy at all. Typically, spicy is key to note for all patrons. 

The main course took at least 45 minutes or longer to arrive to the table since the time we ordered. Not once were we told why or apologized, but the food came out less then room temperature. While the food was not bad at all, this experience was hard to overlook since it is ridiculous priced for portion and customer service. 

The house cab and zucchini side was the main reason for the extra star :-) 

Full circle, they never once wished my sister happy birthday or brought anything, etc...maybe it was just an off night, but for price I would not recommend.
It's I little hole in the wall place. Take out only.  I had the Philly cheese stake.  Not bad. It's not going to stand up the the big boys. But still good.

Terrible, do not come here! We only ended up here because St. Charles was sold out! Dirty, inside and out,  chairs sticky, service was awful!  Wait staff, loud during the movie, served drinks without kids, where do they do that at? Would have gave a negative but it's not an option!

The food was ok, definitely would not come back. The fries......soggy. The bread........hard and stale. The burger was ok. Service was ok, too. Best word to explain everything: "meh." Went to leave and the bartender went missing for at least 20 minutes. Great place to sit at a bar and be depressed about your life.

This place introduced my wife to young coconut juice outside of Taiwan.  We have since found the stuff (you need to get the frozen stuff... Seafood City has it...) we have had little reason to come back here.

I mean c'mon.  It's expensive and the food is just ok.  I don't remember how much they charge for the pork chop with sunny side up egg thing but my mouth dropped at the sight of it.  There are plenty of other places with far better price points and, in my opinion, far better food.  The wife made the mistake of getting some Living Socials to this place because she totally forgot about this place (the young coconut thing was from like 2007...) so we came here twice just to be disappointed.  

Now I'm not saying that the food is bad.  The service is pretty good too.  I'm just saying if you don't mind driving to S. Grand or to Olive you might as well head in that direction.  This place serves as a good option if you live in the CWE, but I definitely wouldn't drive over here just for this place.

We're from out of town and was looking for so quick food. This place was not to shabby. We ate here Two days in a row. The sushi was OK the first time. We had habachi the next and it was pretty good. The staff are very friendly and good.

9/28/21

Quality/Quantity - Ice Cream needs to be colder? We prefer our ice cream firm.

Atmosphere - Maybe we came too late, looked like they were ready to close, but the sign says open till 8pm. It was not even 7pm yet.

Service - The team member complied with everything we asked for, but we felt like we were interrupting her close.

Price - $3.50 for a single, seems reasonable, but we were not impressed with the quality.

Owner Suggestion - Not sure how much time you spend here, but I am sure it is very well run when you are here...?

Our usual butcher was full during hunting season so I frantically called around looking for anyone to take the two deer in the bed of our truck on a warm day. I found The Meat Shop had openings. I should have trusted my instincts, gross. But we were out of luck. I requested the ground have no added fat of any kind, since I personally only eat venison. Immediately after opening a MASSIVE portion of ground, I see added fat. I started to make into patties for my family, since I won't eat it, I see what looks like cartilage and tendons. Extremely hard pieces, that do not crumble with hard force. NOT JUST BEEF/PORK FAT. So all the ground is garbage!!! Now today after thawing a "roast" I uncovered a piece of meat I feel bad feeding to my dog. Absolutely inedible. Covered in hair, dirt, thick layer of fat and what looks like hide. It literally looks like a pair of tar filled lungs. A roast shouldn't be able to unfold. Disgusting! An entire year of meat for my family is trash. Out the money we paid to have it butchered and now we have to buy meat from the store AND EXTREMELY ANGRY I won't be able to eat meat until next fall. BEWARE!
Food is good, but the cafe itself is extremely dirty. I will probably not return unless they clean the place up.

I'm really shocked to see some of the rave reviews, or maybe I came on a bad night? Went for dinner one night and I had fried shrimp and my friend had turkey, both were just o.k. but the side dishes that came with them were really my issue, yuck! Maybe breakfast is better! Seeing the crowds and the lines at this place made me think it was something really special, so disappointing, I guess a diner is a diner after all.  Maybe I'll try coming for breakfast sometime.
Wings, Nachos, French Fries and Hamburger was delicious..the waitress was exceptional..nice clean place watch sports definitely recommend !!!!

Review #102 - 2020

I had this spot bookmarked prior to our mini getaway because it was new and had a few good reviews, some of which were a little too good to be true so I should've seen the sign that it was probably written by friends or family of the owners.

The 7 of us showed up at around 6:20 PM on Saturday, June 13th.

The place is very cute, but that's probably about all there is.

We each ordered our drinks and I specifically asked if any of their drinks are made with fresh fruits and the guy told me that all their fruit drinks are made with fresh fruit, which was awesome! 

Much to my surprise, my avocado smoothie and my daughter's mango smoothie had a very strong powder taste so I told them that neither drinks tasted like fresh fruits & the lady, whom must have been the owner, told me that only their drinks on the smoothie list were made with fresh fruits.  Thanks for the clarification!

She told me if I wanted, I can tell her what I wanted the next time I come back, which will be a never-- since I only took two sips of my drink and tossed it at a gas station.  

My daughter hated her mango smoothie & refused to take a second sip of it so hers went in the trash as well!

My son got the house milk tea, which is basically a Thai iced tea, and it was ok.  You really can't mess up something that simple.

My 11-year old niece got an Okinawa milk tea, which she also said tasted like Thai iced tea, which was very odd.

My 15-year old niece got the cheese foam green tea & she said it was ok, but she wouldn't order it again.

My two nephews got two different types of lemonade drinks, and they were the only two who said their drinks tasted like what they ordered.

So, just as a mean joke, the next day before we left Reno, I asked the kids if anybody wanted a drink from Num Num for our drive back home and they all yelled, "Please no!  We're good with bottled water!"

Never coming back to this place!

I feel sorry for this place because the owner is a hot head and felt the need to degrade me for returning a purchase. Saying "never return here" "it cost me money to return your items" . Clearly sexist and racist owner. If you are unhappy with your return Policy then you should change it, not yell and disrespect your costumers for simply following your policy. Sad.

Good experience, staff was hospitable and helpful, the food was good but in comparison to reviews we had seen we expected better. My Pork Chop was good and my wife's burger were both good but nothing to write home about. The sides left quite a lot to be desired, my wife's tater tot casserole I think it was called was way overloaded with sour cream which overpowered everything else in the dish. My asparagus was excessively covered in butter reduction along with ham and egg? Also the au gratin that came with my pork chop seemed overloaded with butter as well. Staying simpler with the dishes instead of going overboard trying to be different/upscale/fancy in terms of the food would be an improvement here. A simple olive oil, salt and pepper prep for the asparagus would be tons better...maybe do some blind question asking to patrons what they think about the massive butter content of menu items and if they could improve would be a step in the right direction.

Going to Mockingbird salon was the best decision. I didn't want to spend a lot but I knew I really wanted to walk out feeling good. This place was cool and laidback they were friendly and professional. I had Joanna who was amazing! She listened to what I wanted and gave me suggestions based on my look and what she thought would look good and I trusted her. It totally paid off!! I walked out looking and feeling amazing and she showed me how to achieve the look at home too. So grateful. I will 1000% be back for my next cut and a color! Thanks mockingbird!

Been going to Foxys for years. A must eat is their wings. Oh man they are amazing. They are not battered and they are crispy, simply yummy. Also their curly fries are great and my daughter's favorite, their garlic bread. But its not your usual garlic bread. It is basically white pizza with just cheese and garlic. There are 4 slices to an order and if you order 2 orders of it you get a pizza pie. The crust is fantastic. Always stop by there when we are at the beach.

If I could give it zero stars, I would. Sat and waited 30 minutes and eventually just drove away. After getting out of the car and walking up to the door to see what the deal was, they assured me my food was "on its way" as they proceeded to type it in right in front of me. Again, this was after 30 minutes. There were probably 4 other cars there, so "we're super busy" wasn't a viable excuse. Don't even waste your time. Even if the food is fine, I wouldn't know.

We hired Danny to install a surround sound system and mount a projector in our flex space. He came last Friday to do the install, which went great. However, he wasn't able to complete the work, which included adding the subwoofer, connecting the power to the projector, dropping a 2nd HDMi line as well a punching a hole in the wall for wires. He promised that he would come back the following Tuesday to come finish up the work. He didn't show and didn't bother informing us that he wouldn't be able to come that day. After following up with him on Tuesday, he finally got back to us on Tuesday late afternoon that he would come on Friday to complete the work. Well Friday came around and went and still no updates. He's stopped responding to our text messages and at this point we have yet to hear back from him. I've attached proof of our text conversations in the review for reference. We had several more projects lined up for him, but I don't expect to hear back from him anytime soon.

Edit: Well long story short Danny has not bothered to come back and finish the job either nor has he gotten in touch with us. If you're a customer reading this review, see what he just wrote in response - "if there's money on the table".

It's such a great break from being on the beach without leaving the beach. You can still look at the wonderful scenic views and enjoy drinks and food in their outdoor seating area! People are allowed to walk in and out in their bathing suits. 

Our server was very friendly but seemed to be very busy so was missing a good part of the meal. 

We did observe a table filled with 4-5 servers who sat right next to the customers talking loudly with scowls on their faces.

It was packed but we were seated quickly. Everyone enjoyed their food. I had their mushroom wrap with fries. It was slightly over-dressed because the balsamic vinaigrette was leaking all over the place so definitely messing eating. 

We had to remind another server (our main server didn't bring out food) that we needed utensils and ketchup.

Dancers on the bar on saturday
Cover bands or live music

Everything in the store, all makeup, all products are CRUELTY-FREE!!!!!! YAY! Love that about this place! If it's true and he wasn't trying to shut me up... More on that later...

One thing that really irritated me though, was NOTHING is labeled for the price, and the man at the register in the store side where all the bath items were, was more preoccupied in what he was doing, the man in the coffee side was more helpful.  I had to ask about the candles price and was unsure of what I heard in his unintelligible mumblings, so I said, "$60 or $16?" genuinely confused, and he was like "..sixty..". We continued to browse some more, me having to ask several more times the prices on all the unmarked items (pet peeve for my husband, when not marked, so he just followed me around).  I scoped out the clearance rack for items that were marked down to 75% off, and we ended up getting a few bowls that used to be around $11 and then rang up to $2.50 each, and we got some Key Lime chapstick by the register for like $5... and once my husband opened his wallet to pull out one of the 100 dollar bills, he perked right up, and had a much better attitude towards us.  It was actually kind of ridiculous, crap you'd see pulled in a movie.  That irritated me.

I give them positive stars for being a quaint little shop containing ALL cruelty-free products, as said the grumpy-turned helpful employee, despite many products not being labeled with the little bunny that signals cruelty-free/no animal testing labels.  So I hope he wasn't just saying that everything was cruelty-free just to placate me and shut me up from asking about every item I was interested in's:  
-price
-cruelty free brand or not.

They did have an amazing Lavender fragrance that was divine, I might want that for lowering stress/anxiety when traveling, it's good to have.  Maybe I will be back to scope it out and hope there is a different employee, but realistically we will probably only come back in here if we are already in the Blind Tiger that this place shares an open wall with.

Great little motel on north State Street. Very clean,,comfortable bed, good lighting and a nice flat screen tv on the wall. The owner is on property and very accommodating. Parking is provided right in front of your room. Great value !

This coffee shop is in the lobby of the building I work in, and it's a pretty awesome little shop!  Chris - the best barista in all of Philly - runs the shop during the week.
