They have daily latte specials - I recommend Thursday's C-3PO latte!  They also have a good assortment of muffins, cookies, and pretzels.

My friends and I had a very pleasing dinner here tonight! My husband and I are not from Tampa but we do visit every once in a while, and we've been trying to come here for quite some time but the timing never worked out until tonight, thankfully! During the week, they're open for lunch, close for a few hours, and then open back up for dinner. 

We were told it'd be a 10 minute wait and gave us a pager. We were excited to have made it in time for happy hour! We waited right outside the door. Watched a few parties go in who came after us.. Weird? So my friend checked and they said they were trying to buzz us in but we never came. So I suppose our buzzer wasn't working properly as other people father away from the place were working. We ended up waiting maybe 30 mins.. surely didn't make it in time for happy hour. 

We ordered a lot of items and shared most of it and we were pretty happy with most everything. In my opinion, the other food aside from the skewers, tasted better and had higher quality than the skewers themselves. We ordered a lot! So I'll break it all down~

We started withhhhh
Edamame: I especially loved these because they stayed warm for a long time. Ive had edamame several other places that go cold quickly. They also were cooked perfectly, just wish they had more salt!
Chicken karaage: Awesome. Crispy and flavorful. 
Mentaiko fries: An absolute winner. A favorite amongst us all. Fries were a great portion. Exceedingly crispy and never went soggy. The spicy mayo sauce over it was fantastic and the seaweed topping made it feel like sushi fries. We loved them. 
Gyoza: I didn't try these but my husband did. He said they were flavorful and didn't leave that oily aftertaste you sometimes get. 
Shiso rangoons: My friend ordered this not really knowing what to expect. He thought they'd just be regular crab rangoons but omg these were so much better than any crab rangoon that I'll ever have.. now or in the future. I LOVED these. They were light and crispy and all bites, with and without the cheese filling, were just absolutely delectable. I would come back just for these. I just wish they had a little more cheese filling. 

Sushi time! 
Yellowtail roll was very fresh. Big piece of yellowtail! 
My friends got different rolls. 
One rainbow roll- which was a huge roll!! The fish on top was bright and beautiful. 
The spider roll- I never got feedback from this but he ate it all so fast so I'm sure it was great lol 
The dancing crab roll- Basically everything I would enjoy in sushi in one roll. It was super rich and amazingly delicious. 

The skewers were okay in my opinion. I had a couple chicken thigh skewers and my husband went for chicken breast and pork belly. They tasted decent but I felt portions of chicken breast and pork belly could have been bigger. Like bigger cuts. 

Noodles-
My friend ordered tonkatsu ramen. Another one that I didn't get feedback from but was eaten so fast, leaving nothing behind. 
Kimchi cream udon noodles were soooo enjoyable. Shrimp was cooked well and sauce for the udon noodles was very satisfying and had a light kick but not too spicy. 

Overall we enjoyed most everything. We certainly plan to come back next time were in town!

Had breakfast here twice on my weekend visit to Nashville. Had to go back for another Bruleed Latte. What a sweet treat, especially the Bruleed Meringa which tasted like a toasted marshmallow and melted oh-so-nicely into the latte. One day I had the savory porridge which consisted of polenta with mushrooms, ricotta, and kale, and I ordered a poached egg on it just to make things a bit more perfect. The next day I had the #2 breakfast sandwich which was simple but satisfying. I also tried the espresso flip which wasn't as good as the Bruleed but it was still a treat. Very friendly servers and comfortable atmosphere.

Had a wonderful dinner at this recently reopened BBQ place in Unionville.  The restaurant is updated  with a nice bar area and the food was outstanding.  Definitely best BBQ around and worth the ride. The ride down 82 is beautiful and you will enjoy the ribs, brisket and all the BBQ at Hood's.  Service was great and staff was very friendly , too.  We will be back!

Honestly super frustrated by the service I received here. I took a bridesmaid dress in to be hemmed and taken in, and they charged $120. Seemed super expensive, but she said she would have to redo the zipper to take it in, so I agreed.

The result was a dress that's half an inch too short (and since I'm wearing flats, it's noticeable) and they only took in the top half of the dress, so it makes me look pear shaped and puffs out at the bottom when the original silhouette of the dress looked perfect on me - just needed to be taken in a little all over.

They also say they take cash or check, but make sure your check says St. Louis on it!!! I just moved, and my checks aren't new, so I had to go down the road to get cash and come back. Probably not a problem for most people but it was annoying that they didn't state this up front.

I don't have time to take the dress anywhere else before the wedding but I am frustrated that the dress looked better before the alterations than it does now. Waste of $120.

Food was ok...too Smokey no air circulation. We got the 7 course meal for 2 pricey and you don't get much food. It came out very very slow we had to leave bc other half had irritated asthma bc of lack of any air in there. They still charged us for the whole 7 course...smh which we didn't get. Really had it came out faster instead of a 25 min wait in between smal courses well we probably would of made it all the way through. The appetizer was awful...salad was delicious and so was the bread. Chicken not cooked enough for my liking. I will not return here....yuk

Good for the $4 Nachos at Happy Hour.
Tacos are small; chilorio & fish were ok, carne asada & al pastor were bland. The tableside sauces were also bland.

Service was weird. Guy almost walked off with my food before realizing I wasn't done and he didn't bother asking, just assumed. Another thing, I was shorted change. Just a few cents but no explanation. I was so confused and shocked considering that it's a cash only place.
I'm not giving two stars bc of the service that was good it's the food that was horrible..yellow hard big pieces of lettuce it was bitter and wet 10$ crazy the customer service was fine
First time here and it was awesome! We ordered the Carnivore with extra cheese and it was delicious! So fresh and tasty. We will definitely be back.

The online ordering process was a bit strange, you cannot order anything separately. We wanted to order an extra side and sauce and there was no way to do that. You could only choose one sauce per order. The sides were delicious, but I have to say the actual BBQ was not. The pulled pork was okay, but the sliced brisket was very dry.

Want to rent a loft at that the Exchange Lofts downtown managed by Village Property Management? 

If you'd like to wait three weeks for them to replace a light fixture that hasn't worked since you moved in (even though they say they check everything before you move in),  go for it. 

if you'd like to wait an additional two weeks to have one of your light bulbs replaced from a fixture that hangs from your 15' ceilings that the agent who rented you the property said would be the responsibility of Village Real Estate/Property Management, but are now being told that in order to have them replaced you'll have to pay for the management team's time, this is the company for you.

You'll get nothing but the run-around with every service request. We have stained concrete flooring where the sealing is coming up from because we believe there might have been water damage prior to our time here. When we alerted the property management team, thinking, "hey, this might be something you want to check out before the situation deteriorates and costs you more money," they just gave us the run around, then stopped responding to the request completely.

When tiles are falling out within our shower stall leaving behind rotting material that gets worse by being drenched every shower, and we alert the property management team, nothing. We get nothing back.

But, they really did check everything out before we moved in just a few months ago, right? 

So yes, if you want to live in the Exchange Lofts, with its paper-thin walls, minimal natural light and weed-smoke smelling hallways, go right ahead. Just don't expect to get any service while paying upwards of $1700 per month to live there. These people are ridiculous.


ONE LAST THING: When your water heat breaks while you are out of town, and you return to your entire office and bedroom under an inch of water, you'll find that they have no after-hour emergency number readily available, and will have to stalk them by individual number via numerous phone calls and text messages for about 2.5 hours until you finally get a hold of someone. When you do speak to the person in charge, you won't get an apology and will instead seem annoyed that you'd want this taken care of tonight.

Then, you'll be without water for more than 24 hours because they can't seem to get a plumber over. When the plumber comes, they'll tell you they don't have permission to get a new water heater, so they'll have to come back tomorrow.

All the while, you'll be banging your head against the wall wishing you had never signed a lease with them in the first place. No real emergency number....really??

I had sent my faulty drive and backup drive to this company. They gave me a free shipping label and I sent the two drives through that. The manager in the company acknowledged through phone that they received both the backup drive and faulty drive. Later I wanted them to send both the drives back as I did not want to go through their service. They sent me the faulty drive and did not sent me the backup drive. This three months since that incident and I am following up with them. They are now saying that they do not have evidence of getting my backup drive. Finally I spoke to them and said you guys are liars.

Please do not go through this company. I have the jobID and all the mail conversations with them. They stole my backup drive ( 1TB brand new Samsung EVO drive)

This company is a cheater company.

The atmosphere is ok, but the food is not the best. I order a crab cake sandwich and when I cut it in half, i realized the crab cake was Nothing but bread inside. It had a few pieces of crab meat in between, but for the most part, you're eating a bread on bread sandwich. (Not cool). The wings are fairly good just really really tiny. So over all of ur looking for good food this is not the place, drinks are ok.

I will always be a fan of the Zoes Kitchen chain. I used to eat at the one in Gainesville all the time. I love how this fast food service has a lot of healthy and delicious options! I came here on a Wednesday night - not too busy. The service was quick and helpful. Unfortunately, with the amount I paid, I was not impressed with the portion size. Not enough rice in my opinion. I got the shrimp kabob - the seasoning was a tad bit spicy. Next time, I will definitely go for the bowls for sure!

I had a terrible experience with these people Tom being in charge, his son being involved as movers, move my things in March and my Michael Jordan jersey got stolen and they were responsible for that and I hold them responsible for and therefore they're not good people for doing that to me stealing my jersey so I'm out very valuable memorabilia and never got a callback or any responsibility for anything from these people!

Many other things were shipped the wrong place clearly marked where they were supposed to go so just careless and just a bad experience but I want to forget about

Came here for a Wedding :) So this review will mainly focus on the place as an event location. We did get to try their Vodka, and whiskey. Both very good. Inside is a nice full bar with a couple of medium size rooms(our Buffett was set up in one) but the main focus is the huge patio. The crew setup a seating section for the ceremony and in maybe ten minutes after the ceremony was done they took it down and set up the dinning area on the same patio very efficient. My only complaint would be we actually couldn't hear the ceremony, not sure if they didn't provide a mic or the brides didn't want one but because of the traffic noise we couldn't hear the ceremony :( if I go back I may up my review because it's really a wonderful venue

I love to eat out for breakfast.  I highly recommend something from the staff favorites menu - unique blends of foods that end up being great.  They have done a good job of fusing your basic diner food with a twist.  It is for sure a local fav.

JACKPOT!

777! 777! 777! Yelp Review 777 for me! Everyone playing the slots would love to see Lucky 7-7-7 for BIG MONEY!

While I was in Tampa, I decided to check out the Seminole Hard Rock Casino. The new South Parking Garage had just opened when I was here. I didn't stay at the Hotel, so this review is based on the Casino.

Oh man! The floor was very busy and MOBBED with a sea of old folks, with most slot machines in this big casino being played. I felt very young, like I was walking through a huge and glitzy senior citizen recreation center. I think to myself: As a current working class taxpayer, I really hope those folks are financially stable and not gambling away their Social Security and Medicare checks. (sigh)

The Casino also has poker tables, Texas Hold Em tournaments, bars, nightclub, restaurants, food court, and the Hard Rock Retail Store. I saw the food here was on the pricey side and it's good that I ate lunch before stopping here. I did some shopping at the Retail Store and picked up some cool Hard Rock pins and t-shirts. This store does not accept the Hard Rock Cafe All Access Card (points program), but they gave me a 10 percent discount for showing the card. 

Overall, it's not Vegas, but it's a decent place to play the slots in the Tampa Bay area with very easy highway access to/from I-4.
HUGE portions! We had to have every course boxed but it was so worth it. Absolutely delicious and wonderful attentive service! Bravo!
Way over priced for mediocre ice cream. I wouldn't be so critical if the worker that was in that day wasn't so rude. I'm giving 2 stars because she did offer me a free sundae. But I don't recommend.
Very creative dishes! Best veggie restaurant in town.

After eating half of a chicken burrito, I finally gave up and immediately signed up for a yelp account just so I could vent to SOMEONE about how awful my food was. It was by far the worst burrito I've ever had. Ever. And I've eat A LOT of Mexican food. First of all, the burrito was falling apart. Whoever rolled it had no idea what they were doing. Second of all, it was just plain nasty. I also ordered horchata. HORRIBLE. Up until today, I didn't think that there was any horchata in existence that I couldn't drink. I absolutely love that stuff. But theirs was chalky both in taste and texture. The only reason I'm giving them two stars is because I've been there once before for breakfast and that was tasty. The breakfast service was pleasant but extremely slow, which struck me as odd considering there weren't that many other customers. I probably won't come back here, unless there's a zombie apocalypse and Panchita's is the only place left to offer food and shelter.

Might be a good spot if you're already drunk, but don't start your night out here. The food is just bar food and the pirogies we had were cold in the center. Food came out relatively quick though. Young and loud crowd.

Stayed at hotel for conference. Customer service is really lacking. Waited in a long line to check in and only 2 people registering guests. Clerk was rather rude about credit card info was not faxed even though room was already paid for. Signage for conference was minimal and difficult to follow. When I came back to my room at lunch break there were men were in my room. I was told my room was vacant and I must be at the wrong room. When I produced my key and pointed to all of my belongings then they finally left. My room was not cleaned by 5pm and when the maid arrived he asked if I wanted towels. I guess they don't clean the rooms everyday. The hotel is beautiful. Food is marginal at best.

I ate at Divas Kitchen for the 1st time on Saturday and really enjoyed the experience. The wait staff was very attentive and courteous but the food was the star of the show. I ordered the chicken wing platter and the fried fish platter. The chicken and fish were well seasoned and fried to a nice crispy textured and were accompanied by adequately prepared side dishes. If you are in the mood for a home cooked meal without having to do it yourself Divas is the place for you.

A couple issues.  The signage at the car wash is confusing.  Typically, with these franchised car washes, when you pull in there is a sign that tells you when to put your car in neutral from drive so that the automatic track pushes your car forward.  I didn't see any marker or sign anywhere letting me know when i should put in neutral.  Similarly, on the way out, there is a sign that says 'stop' and 'go' to indicate when to go back into drive.  But, the sign is very stupidly designed and both stop and go flash back and forth.

Not so sure I understand the buzz of this place :(. They always have a wait which does go pretty fast so that's not terrible.  This morning though I ordered a breakfast burrito and asked if possible they could put some pinto beans in it.. I do like their pintos .. the waitress came back to the table to let us know she asked the supervisor about the beans and they cannot put them IN the burrito... only on the side??? Doesn't make a lot of sense to me , seems a bit lazy

Long-time fan of Ralph's here.  I adore sweetbreads, which are not on every Italian restaurant's menu.  So i go to Ralph's for sweetbreads.  I think the mushrooms in the dish are canned, but the sweetbreads are perfect, with a light sauce and are a huge portion.  I'm also crazy about their Fettucini Alfredo, which is the real thing, made with pasta, butter, parm and cream and nothing else.  My partner always orders spaghetti and meatballs - he's not a foodie and that's what he likes.  Since I order 2 entrees (so I'll have leftovers tomorrow), we don't get out of there cheaply, but I love the place.  And yes, it's a good place to bring visiting firemen - old-time ambience, attentive service, and Sarcone's bread.

Good but not great barbeque. The bar is set a bit higher in one of the top barbeque heartlands right up there with Carolinas, Texas, Kansas City and so on. Tennessee is especially known for its pork shoulder with a medium hot sauce with vinegar undertones.

Though they only started the business since 1976, they had all the trappings to appear like they had been there for ever. One brick wall, lots of photos and memorabilia plastered all over and simple cafeteria line. Clientele in this touristy area leaned heavily towards visitors over locals. Line was out the front door onto the street. (It moves very fast and there is plenty of seating upstairs.)

Portions are huge. Great that they have like six different barbeque sauces to chose from to get a well rounded education. Cole slaw was lighter vinegar-based instead of mayo. Baked beans flavorful but a bit runny. Green beans looked cooked beyond recognition. Corn bread was distinctive and moist. I wished they had more inspired sides like fried okra and sweet potatoes.

Now on to the meat of the discussion. The pork shoulder was fabulous. Extremely tender and flavorful. Ribs were good, falling off the bone, but not great. My guess is that they could have left it the smoker longer. Texas smoked links were uninspired.

If you want to find a place that allow you to commune with the heart and soul of barbeque, go elsewhere. If you want a good barbeque to stop off at before hitting the honky-tonks in the neighborhood, its a sure thing.

The most amazing boba joint in reno! I definitely recommend the monster boba latte and the signature milk tea. The drinks are so delicious and refreshing. I'm never going to another boba place in reno.

Very happy with this place, best boba in town as far as I know. Good selection and decent prices, friendly service and free wifi. Hope the quality holds up, seems too good to be true, and I'll be back often.
Having been to Taiwan a number of times, 101 Taiwanese cuisine exceeded my expectations and never disappoints me. It's authentic! Try the beef noodle soup. It's the best dish on the menu.

Probably the worst service I have ever had.  Each time we needed anything I got up and walked from my table to the bar to ask for it because the waitress NEVER ONCE stopped by or looked our way, other than when she seated us and brought water.  The sushi itself was so-so, very small rolls.  Second time there but never going back.

This wonderful restaurant feels like home. The food is made with love, and is so delicious...I can't stay away! I love their (always changing) soups, and gluten-free paninis & desserts (you won't miss the gluten!) The service is warm (and supremely accomodating for dietary restrictions) And the atmosphere is cozy & comfortable.  You will feel better about yourself and life when you walk out of Adama :)

Had the salmon stuffed with crab today which came with a salad, bread and two sides. The salad, as always, was great. The salmon which was described as "broiled to perfection" was broiled to over-done-ness. The crab inside was more like a stuffing but it made the dry salmon better. I also chose the broccoli as one of my sides, again terribly over cooked. It was hardly even green anymore.

A little greasy but full of flavor!  Jewel's noodle kitchen is a unique Chinese restaurant in Tucson.  They have a very small menu and there is not much to choose from when it comes to veggies.  The chef makes all their noodles from scratch, but there are only 3 to 4 noodle dishes on the menu.  It seems odd to have such a small noodle selection for a noodle house.  For 60 dollars we got noodles with pork bean sauce, beef with scallion pie, triple dumplings, 2 Chinese pork buns, 1 Chinese beef bun, and 1 green onion pancake delivered to my home.  I didn't know how big their portions were.  Lesson learned, this was way too much food for 2 people. My favorite dish was the beef scallion pie.  The buns and green onion pancake are unique to Tucson, normally you would have to travel overseas to eat something similar.  You will need a nice beer or red wine to pair with this greasy food.  Cheers!

I am beyond excited for this place to be open! It is so airy and welcoming. The staff is friendly and knowledgeable and more than happy to steer the lost customer in the right direction. The coffee is on point and thankfully not that weak sauce burnt bean water that has become so popular as a quick cash grab over the years. I'm so so excited that I have a go to place to stop before work I've been missing a close to home local joint ever since we moved here five years ago. I am so excited to watch this place grow and I will absolutely make it a staple in my life. Thank you for opening!!!
Mediocre. Wasn't as great as i had expexted. Thai tea came as tea bag in hot water. Would expect more since i heard it was supposed to be all authentic. Food wasn't that great

There are places nearby that are much more crowded and yet I like this place more. I had the lasagna and a glass of red. It came with a salad and bread. Service was friendly and fast. 

The salad was big and a perfect way to start a meal.  The lasagna was delicious!!! It came bubbly hot, cheesy, and meaty with a tasty tomato sauce. Yum!
Richies is the best, and Richie is the man!!! Awesome dude!!! Eat here all the time. Great bang for your buck. Also, amazing iced coffee!!!!

Boyfriend and I came here for brunch on the Friday of Mardi Gras weekend. The ambiance is great, very casual, service is awesome, easy parking, and with the Yelp option to put yourself on the waitlist, we skipped a 30 minute wait and were seated immediately.

We also took advantage of the buy one get one free mimosa deal from Yelp. Mimosas were a 6/10 at best. The juice and champagne weren't the best quality, but not bad for a BOGO deal. Probably wouldn't order them myself though at the regular price of 10 dollars. 

The food, however, we were pretty disappointed by. We were really excited given the Yelp reviews, but the food was lukewarm when served, the Costa Rican Breakfast was extremely poorly seasoned aka not seasoned at all, and the Shrimp Boogaloo was meh. 

Perhaps the reason for our disappointing experience was not getting what other Yelpers recommended. Maybe if we got the Eggs Cochon or french toast special we would have a more positive review. Definitely take into account that the entire menu is not as good as some dishes may be.

My boyfriend and I just spent a night at the Bacara, and we loved it. Our room was fabulous and beautifully decorated. We were warmly welcomed by the staff and well taken care of. The complimentary glass of sparkling wine was the perfect way to watch the sun set over the beach. Cody, one of the managers, went out of his way to ensure that we enjoyed our quiet weekend away from home. Definitely recommended.

I've been to Calhoun's twice now, but I can't really think of anything that stands out for me.

The food is fine, and I am rather fond of the way they make their Coleslaw.

The Prime Rib is just average. It comes with Mashed Potatoes, and its extra for a Baked Potato.

For the price point its worth it, but if I really want Prime Rib I don't feel myself drawn here.  I haven't had the Ribs so maybe I'm missing the main reason to come here.

Ugh! Nice space, pleasant people, but terrible almond latte. Felt like I was drinking the coffee grounds, but with an unpleasant coffee taste. No balance, not smooth, not good. 

Cannot speak to anything else served there.

This place has potential but very over priced for the quality. Just your typical salon with an extra $50 tagged on and not for the friendly service. Ashley is fresh out of beauty school and any of my friends could have done even better than her. If you go don't go to her!! Looked like I didn't even get my hair done for a very expensive price tag. I would recommend  looking elsewhere.

I came here to grab a local bite with family.

It's a cozy restaurant, not busy during dinner time.  Very fast service.  We ordered duck, birds nest, some other protein, some seafood stuff.  Its food , no complaints.

Definitely I found the food came out hot, the service ass fast, the lighting was good.

Dessert is self serve in pots near where you pay.  Today they served sweet pumpkin (like coconut milk base) and red bean.

Location takes credit card.
Service is great.  Drinks are good.  I had the fried mozerella and it was amazing.  I was excited about th

Well they were out of most food options when we went it was a Saturday night. 
Staff was friendly. 
No mashed no Mac 
Almost no chicken

Wedge potatoes were cold and dry

Better to go to the bob Evans down the street

Keep your expectations LOW if you decide to dine here.

First of all, it's in a casino. If you are stuck here for some reason - like we were - and want to escape the smoke-laden main casino and buffet dining areas, this is the best alternative but dont expect much. With that in mind.....

There was a bit of a wait to be seated, even though there were several spots open. The green tea was made from a bag of Bigelow. The seaweed salad was so-so but rather chewy. 

Nearly ALL of the roll offerings contain something fried, or the entire roll is fried. They douse every roll with copious amounts of sauces (probably to mask the low quality of the ingredients). 

Everything is made with way too much rice - rice that was undercooked and not properly prepared or seasoned. You must eat all of the rice if you are doing all-you-can-eat and your server will point this out to you if you try to leave any behind on your plate.

The fish is NOT fresh high-quality sashimi grade one should expect from a sushi restaurant. The maguro/tuna was quite dry and tasteless, the hamachi/yellowtail lacked the usual flavor punch you get when it's truly fresh, same for the sake/salmon. The fire and ice hand roll (salmon and scallops) was the best offering IMO but again, not as fresh or flavorful as it should have been. The tobiko/fish roe was like eating large grains of dry fishy sand. 

The sushi chefs all appeared to be Mexican line cooks. This would not be a problem except that these so-called sushi chefs had absolutely no idea what omakaze meant. WTF??? That's like a chef in a steak house not knowing the difference between rare and medium. It may have been just as well considering nothing was of very high quality.

The portions are generous, but what's the point if doesn't taste that great? All-you-can-eat is not a good value when the quality is sub-par. I'd much rather pay double and get the highest quality fresh fish that is prepared by skilled sushi chefs. 

Oh, and you cannot sit at the sushi bar unless you pay for the all-you-can-eat option.

Definitely NOT a place for the true sushi connoissuer.

I'm not in the Sparks area often, but when I am I like to grab a quick bite at Super Burrito. This is a decent hole in the wall Mexican food joint. I rate them a solid 3 1/2. 

Their food and prices are good. The nachos are my favorite by far. The place is easy to find and has ample parking. Inside dining has plenty of tables, along with a TV. I find the staff to be friendly and accommodating. 

If you're looking for something quick and decent, give them a try!

Awesome sushi place in this neighborhood. Stylish interior and friendly service. The sushi I got had some of the prettiest presentation I've ever seen. They have standard rolls and the crazy combo types as well, and the quality is very good. The chef also sent out a complimentary spring roll and fries appetizer which was delicious.

Okay Fries we're great!  We had two different burgers and we both thought something was off with the taste of the burger. We also both had onion skin included in our sautéed onions, not good! Won't be back.

WOW. And not in a good way.

So we were out for a power supply at Home Depot and I wanted ice-cream (or reasonable stand in). We called to make sure they were open before driving from Home Depot as we had tried to stop here 3 weeks prior and some young guy simply barked, "We are closed." I think it was a Saturday at about 11am so maybe they weren't open that day or something but rather than lock the door...He told the couple that entered after us the exact same thing. And I had thought to myself 'what a jerk and who would run a business this way???'

Back to today: We enter the shop and some young girl is messing with the yogurt machines on her cell phone. The place looks under kempt like maybe the toppings had just sat there all day. Three machines had zero yogurt and signage meaning that they simply do not offer variety at all. There was a great looking pomegranate that did nothing when you pulled the handle. I didn't try the other flavor because it was vanilla. This left us with the AMAZING variety of cheesecake, vanilla, chocolate, strawberry and the birthday cake that came out like the runs. But we wanted a treat and by the Gods we were gonna get one!

Why oh why did we do this? I have NO IDEA! I asked the girl who was leaning against the register on her cell phone still (obviously bustling to fix this crap shoot) if the runs were the standard with the birthday cake as it was the only thing that really had the look of something I might truly like. She replied that there was something wrong with the machine. I asked if they normally had this little variety of frozen yogurt and got a grunt if I recall.

We left with some very plain frogurt that I will not recall eating 2 minutes from now and could have been elevated by any means one can think of. So as I am limited by Yelp to a positive star paradigm I will tell you what I think of this place. 

-1 star from zero for wasting my time on a Saturday when a place should be open.
-1 star for lacking any kind of service. From the rude guy who failed to lock the door or maybe decided not to want to work that day...to the chick on the phone who should be in crisis mode trying to fix this donkey flop! 
-1 star for ZERO VARIETY. I can get better at the firkin 7-11 in a paper carton.
-1 star for the dry sour pineapple to top off my boring yogurt. And for the unkempt nature of the place.
-1 star for the firkin price for this rabble. $37 (15% tip I should have skipped but I figured maybe it would keep her from working the streets) for 2 larges. Yes I needed some sugar in my life. I expected it to be a bit pricey but REALLY? FOR THIS???

That wraps us up with an official -5 stars which means not only should we all avoid this place but perhaps the Owners of this chain should issue a former apology to the public for taking too long a sabbatical and not tending their business. This place should close immediately so that maybe we could get an ice-cream truck to park here. They might want to sell some ice-cream.

I used to have good shopping experiences here. It is definitely not the dollar store you want to go to EVER, especially if you are in a hurry. 

Aisles were messy; organization of items are not very intuitive. There were unpacked boxes in the middle of this one crafts aisle -- read: also known as tripping hazards; the aisles are narrow as-is as I have had a few other customers bumping into me! Worse, customers kept stubbing into the unpacked boxes. It would have been a different story had the boxes been neatly stacked, but the mixture of half sorted & unopened boxes, alongside with a dangerously placed ladder, reminded me of a nightmare from any teenager's messy room.

Staff were not incredibly helpful either if you are looking for something very specific -- something you know that other Dollarama stores have got in stock. Different staff kept directing me to different aisles. It was very frustrating. I left the store without buying anything to go to a different Dollarama location instead.

I have used this nail spa's service a few times. The last time I went will be my last. I was not happy with the service I received. I sat down for my pedicure and was immediately told over  and over that my nails were not that great because I used too many harsh chemicals! Uh...really? I use the same nail products that THEY use! SO I guess it is their products that are causing my nails to seem non healthy! The girl that was doing my toes was not very gentle. I have a partial nail on one toe that I specifically said do not touch as it hurts. I was ignored and she went to town on it. I yelled at her to stop and I was just given a look. Then my manicure was just as awful. I was told again how awful my nails look. I am sorry....I am coming for you to make them pretty...no need to tell me how awful my nails look. Just rude. And my polish wasn't evenly done and came off within a few days. Will not go back.
Really good food at great prices. Nothing fancy but all around solid experience time and again.

I was reluctant to review this place after my first time eating here but I've given it several tries now (breakfasts, lunches) and the verdict is in: PB's is out. The food (eggs, potatoes, toast coffee; pancakes and coffee; burgers, fries, and beers) always manages to be underdone and overdone all at once! Also, the food was stone cold on every visit. The servers we had were gruff and it took a painfully long time to get our orders or to get water or meal accompaniments like hot sauce, or even the check after we were finished.  I understand slow service during busy times but when staff far outnumber customers there's a problem.  Last, everything felt sticky, as though our table wasn't properly wiped down. We really try to visit local businesses but the bad has outnumbered the good every time we've eaten here, so unfortunately, we won't be back again.
This place is very nice inside. They disposable plastic liners in the foot bath. So much cleaner. Beautiful chandeliers form the ceiling and always had a good job done.

After 15 minutes in the service line I went to ask a manger to send over help. Unfortunately this would have interrupted his important responsibilities of handing out business cards. So nothing happened. It has now been 45 minutes since I got on line and I am waiting while they try to find my now lost computer.  

If this wasn't my 10th time with a similar experience, I would think they were having a bad day.  But no, they are inept.

Beer selection is magnificent, food was forgettable. Ordered a burger medium, came out well done. Mussels we ordered were cooked well, but the broth they were served in tasted like the wine hadnt been cooked at all. The fries were incredible though. Loved how crunch and flavorful they were. Dipping sauces were bangin as well. Come for the beer and snack on the fries, eat your meal somewhere else though.

I was by the new chainmall area in Carrollton and decided to try Pei Wei for the first time. I went with the generic Pei Wei Spicy Chicken. It was surprisingly very good as well as the brown rice. There are not many items on the menu I am willing to try but this was a very good start, I will be back.

Went here for lunch at noon on a Wednesday. Pretty busy and we expected it. We were seated quickly, but then forgotten about for nearly 10 minutes until we purposefully made ourselves seen. That said, server was very apologetic and friendly.
Service was otherwise very nice and the food was great for fast food. 
I had the triple burger and fries, both were delicious and the special burger seasoning they have on the table is amazing. Seriously, do it.
Haven't had a Shake yet, but that will be accomplished soon enough.....
It's all about getting the right waitress. Betty is fantastic. The food is great and the wait moves quick.

I went to DMV to get a new DL after a divorce and thought my SS card would be sufficient for the name change. I noticed when I got there that I needed additional documentation. Rather than leaving I checked in anyway and the girl at the reception desk told my what I needed for a Real ID. I asked how much notification I would get on my phone bfore I had to be back and she said usually about 20 minutes. I went home and got the required documentation and headed back to DMV. I was able to check the status of the queue from home which made it easy to see approximately how long I had. I got back to DMV and only waited about 15 imnutes before my number was called. The whole process took less than 2 hours and I was able to come back with everything I needed without having to start all over in line again. 
I have heard a lot of complaints about the new process but it definitely worked for me.
Eggs Benedict eggs were hard poached and there was hardly any hollandaise sauce. Service was good so I add star.

Do not bother donating to this place. It's a waste of your time. All they ask before coming to pick up donated items is "are they in good condition ". Yes my stuff was in good condition, but it had some scratches and blemishes. Normal wear. They wouldn't take anything I had. It seemed as though they didn't feel like working too hard that day. I will never have anything to do with Habit again!!
I almost don't want to review this for fear that it'll grow busy with people. This bar is quiet, warm on a cold night, and perfect fit an after dinner cocktail.

Bought a dozen roses for my wife for our anniversary, looked beautiful, smelled great, at least through a mask in the market. Got them home and wife said they stunk of fish. Sure enough when we opened them to put them in a vase they reeked of fish, and some sort of funk/mold was all over the bottom of them. I'm absolutely mortified. Not only did I waste money on a dozen roses, my wife had to throw away the flowers I bought her. Horrible horrible horrible. Will never shop here again.

I've been a regular here for 13 years. The owner Tom has put a lot of work into the place in that time - doubling the size, adding new services, increasing technicians to 14 and installing the best chairs. Everyone is friendly and eager to please. It's very clean. I'm careful not to go when high traffic is to be expected like mid-day on Saturday before everyone goes out. If I need to go during a potentially busy time, I just make an appointment in advance. Nice they have Sunday hours now too! I never go anywhere else!
Italian as pizza (not very).  Fernando - 9 bowls, beeeyatch!!!!!!!!!

I'm rating this solely on the donation experience. After consulting the website, etc., I loaded up some furniture items and drove into their parking lot. After parking, an employee happened to come out of the building and told me they don't accept furniture donations and that any non-furniture items needed to be unloaded in the parking lot across the street. Of course, no signage indicated any of this. Mildly irritating, but, really, they could do so much better for people who want to donate items. 

Next time, I'll head directly to the Goodwill donation location on Ridge Avenue in Manayunk.

Cheap.  Filling.  Good.  Me likey.

I'm so glad I don't live near one of these.  I'd be in deep trouble (so would my arteries).  But if I'm going to splurge on comfort food, I would choose to come here.  

I'm not going to reiterate what everybody already knows - the double double is one of those "Hubba bubba, come to mama" burgers. I like that it's juicy, messy, and oh so good.  The milkshakes?  Yummy yummy in my tummy.  French fries - I'm not a fan of French fries in general, but I like to watch them being made here.  I'm a geek that way.

And what is it about the 17 cars in the drive-thru when the service inside is so fast?  Come on, people, get out of your vehicle and WALK 25 steps to the front door.  Really.

PS.  My sister from NJ was in town and had her first INOB experience.  She whispered, "Do they give the employees happy drugs? Why are they so happy to work here?"

Has anyone had their card compromised at this store?
It's the only place I've used the card recently so it only makes sense that it happened at this store.
I'll definitely be dealing with the manager on Tuesday when I pick up my order!!

Sad but true..this place certainly didn't pull through.  The service was crappy, and kept giving us the wrong rolls.  They ran out of high chairs.  They argued us when we said what we ordered.. They need to learn all about service and pleasing custumers. Won't be back here again!!

Great veggie burger even though I really meant to get the black bean veggie burger they had no full menu? Which is how I ended up with a normal veggie burger because I had no idea they had more than 1. ... Needless to say it was delish ... My friends say I shouldn't rate food anymore unless it's something sweet because I put mustard on everything .. I disagree ... Obviously .. Suffice it to say, I'll be back for that black bean burger!
Quick, so so, pizza. Staff of 4, 3 customers, but a dirty dinning room. Probably won't last long on this very competitive, upscale community.

Came through Chinatown Philly and since this seems to be a gathering of all Asian cuisines we thought we would give the ramen a try. Highly rated in Yelp.... BUT we had to look carefully because highly rated in PA doesn't mean anything for New Yorkers. Lol.... Sorry PA people... Just a fact and you know it.

Anyways... We came in for a quick bowl of ramen. In Japan this is typical street food and people go in and out like a bank. We ordered the Mayu and Shoyu bowls. The Mayu ramen came with thin noodles and the Shoyu ramen came with thicker more traditional ramen noodles. Both had aldente noodles, but the Mayu had a much richer flavor due to the garlic oil. So depending if you are feeling like a clear broth or a thicker broth both are quite good.

Very efficiently run establishment. Waitresses are loud but it's for efficiency. Even though the restaurant was full things came out quickly and we were off to the next stop.. nice job!

Some friends of ours took us here while we were in town for a visit, and I'm a fan.  I think the concept is pretty cool.  I like the idea of getting to cook your own items.  With all the different mix-ins they offer and the multiple pancake batters, you can end up with a lot of combinations.  If the idea of cooking your food is that unappealing to you (see negative reviews) then this place is not for you.

So from the beginning, their parking lot is pretty small, especially for the crowd they seem to attract, but luckily there are parknig lots around for other building nearby that don't seem to mind being taken over.  There was definitely a wait, but not ridiculous and I see that as a sign that the place is good.  They are very attentive to gluten allergies, which I don't suffer from, but am impressed with all the special steps they take.  The inside was WARM with all those griddles (plus it was a chilly day so I'm sure they had the heat on). I had to crack a window open that was next to me. There is a slightly overwhelming Pam smell (you know the spray grease) due to it being used on all the griddles.

To the good stuff, the food was awesome! I didn't try the pancakes (although not for lack of my husband trying) but they looked delicious and I love how many mix-ins they offer.  I did have the biscuits and gravy, which were delicious.  My friend that took us said she's a big fan of their white chili too.

Overall I really liked this place and I think it would be a great option for a special brunch or something fun when you have visitors.  The price was also pretty awesome!

I'm so frustrated!  I normally would not shop at Dierbergs b/c they are so expensive.  However, I noticed that they had more to choose from during this Covid situation, so I decided to try an online order.  One problem is that, while shopping online, they only provide ingredient information for half of their items.  This information is extremely important to see for people with food allergies.  The next problem is that, after you spend an hour and half doing your 2 week online shopping AND input your credit card information, your order is not allowed to go through b/c delivery nor pick up is available.  

I completely understand that this is a time of unusual circumstances.  I would've been happy to have been placed in a cue, or able to complete my order and then have them phone me with the upcoming availability.  I would even be understanding about having to wait a few days.  I have no problem shopping ahead of time.  But none of these were options.  My shopping cart, that I worked so hard on, was in limbo.  I used the contact support link and was taken to a chat with Shipt, the service they use.  After a complete 2 hours of waiting for an assistance to attend me, I gave up.  It continually gave the messages: "Hello, we're experiencing a high volume of chats, with delays up to 40 minutes. Thanks for your patience.  Connection Lost Waiting for agent..."  

I was trying to do the right thing by obeying recommendations and staying out of the stores.  Oh, well.  I guess I'll go to Schnucks now.
The food was delicious!! The bowwow fruit punch was on hit!! The chicken was outstanding!! Old school like bigmama just salt and pepper.

It's a chain.  You kinda know what you're getting into when you go, so no witty observations here.  Just the facts. 

Our waiter told us he had been at Outback for 5 years, but didn't have the confidence to do ANYTHING without asking our permission, including taking the bread plate after we'd told him 3 different times that we did NOT want any more bread.  I understand not wanting to take anything for granted and piss a customer off, but my husband and I both agreed that he was like a puppy dog waiting for approval.  The caesar salad tasted like it was soaked in a fishtank with anchovies and then had black pepper poured on it to remove some of the moisture.

We heard quite a few servers, including ours, describing in detail what to expect for each steak order.  My husband got the prime rib and I ordered the filet, at the suggestion of the waiter who stated that the difference between the Outback Special and the Filet was definitely noticeable.  After all, it was our 8 month wedding anniversary!  My husband's prime rib was fine, but had NO rub or spices around the outside, which I think anyone who has had prime rib can attest is the best part.  My medium-well steak that I was told would have a strip of pink through the middle was almost charred and had one tiny bit of pink right in the middle if you searched really hard.  And by the middle I mean the COMPLETE middle, not the middle of each strip that I cut.

In all, it was alright.  I'm not saying I'd never go there again.  But it left a lot to be desired.

We tried this place after seeing it on a Tucson Foodie piece on a recent United flight. We went on a Sunday, which is all-day happy hour and the HH prices weren't bad. I got the beef rolled tacos with rojo sauce. It comes topped with pickled onions and cabbage. My husband got a burro, cochi style. It was pretty big and again topped with pickled onions and cabbage. Overall, the food was good. It was nothing super special and a little greasy.  They provide you options for salsas, which is nice. Their queso was tasty. The outdoor patio was clean and it was a nice temperature.  It was good, but not sure I'd drive back across town just to eat there regularly.

I visited Nom Wah because I enjoy dim sum and have visited numerous dim sum places in Chinatown for the many years I've lived here. Nom Wah was a place I've heard about through reviews and it has been well praised, but have never visited, so I suppose I should give it a try and see what the hype is all about. 

Overall, I was not impressed. We ordered the typical round of dim sum dishes as a means of comparison. We found the food to be mediocre. It wasn't bad, but the dishes lacked the zing, didn't meet expectations, and was not better than some of the spots in the heart of Chinatown. What you get for the price was certainly not worth it. 

Service was ok at best, nothing worthy of a note - we did have to ask twice about not receiving a dish we had ordered... The restaurant did have a nice change of ambience compared to the hustle and bustle of typical dim sum places where you're bombarded by a battalion of food carts. The restaurant has a full bar, which is also a nice addition. 

Overall, I prefer other dim sum places - better food and better bang for your buck. Nom Wah isn't a place I would consider visiting as a first choice.

This is one of the places in the Santa Barbara area whose clientele skews older, but the food is dependably good. I always get breakfast and love that they offer multi-grain pancakes. My coffee cup is constantly refilled and the service is relatively speedy. Not the best ambiance for breakfast in SB (weird paintings on the wall and silk plants), but good for early afternoon breakfast cravings.

Good food,  poor service.  They started out fine but suddenly declined rapidly.  Our food kept arriving later with each order. Last week an hour after placing a delivery order they called to say they couldn't deliver we'd  have to pickup up. Today I placed an order online at 11, called at 1215, was told it would be another 40 minutes.  I told them be there at 1230 or I'm canceling the order.  Then they said it would come but the car was having problems.  At 1250 we called again and got nothing but rudeness and promises that the food was on the way, it was busy,  there were other deliveries.  Mind you we are 5 minutes away from them.  We finally recieved our food 2 hours later, no discount,  no apologies,  nothing.  It's sad because the food was good but they have lost our business.

The shop was literally right across the street from our hotel so I was excited to eat there for breakfast one morning. I even knew what I wanted it, an almond croissant. I ordered one with a bottle of apple juice. It was okay, really nothing special. I managed to eat it all. My parents ordered bacon, eggs, and a croissant. I tried my mom's bacon and it was way too smokey tasting. I wanted to try something else and I picked out a linzer tart because it was cute..haha.. The jam inside the two cookies was basically leather! I've never had one before so I don't know if it's supposed to be like that or if it was dried out. If I ever went there again I guess I'd have to keep trying stuff until I found something I liked. There was a pretty good looking cheesecake but I thought it was a little early for dessert lol.

Above average, but not by much. I had the Diablo burger, and while it brought the heat, it didn't have much flavor otherwise. I guess I expected better. Not bad, just not as good as it sounded or could have been. I feel a better cheese or flavorful beef could have added to the burger. Service was fine, again, above average but nothing extraordinary.

I had been waiting to go here for quite a while. While the service was awesome and everyone was super friendly, the only thing I liked was the slaw. I got a gourmet grilled cheese and it was ok. Not great. My aunts hamburger had a long hair it as well. They were very accommodating and made my aunt a new order. Although she felt a little embarrassed when everybody turned around to look at her. My mother did enjoy her hamburger. And the fries were ok. Tonight I give it two stars. I will go back and have something else next time and can possibly raise my stars. I'll give it another chance. We will just have to see.
I have been to this place two times and it was okay.  For a local spot, it's pretty decent...the drinks are questionable...but try the organic sockie.
Plush is now closed / replaced by http://yelp.com/biz/osteria-calabria-glenside / http://OsteriaCalabria.com - which is now closed. :-(

It's a great concept, but it's membership based, and the more members the less availability.  I thought it was going to be a fun and different way to meet people and experience interesting food but that has not been the case. 

How it works is that you pay an initial membership fee, and that fee allows you to pay for "events" that are typically a few weeks later.  Unfortunately, the "events" sell out within a few minutes after they are posted (on Wednesdays), so not only do you need to know your schedule a few weeks out, but you need to act quickly...within minutes sometimes...or the event will be sold out.  I've tried to book two hours after an event was posted and it was already sold out!

I expressed my concerns with the people running the events, but there was little they could do.  They offered to get me a reservation for the event I wanted, but in the end that would only solve my issue once, and not provide relief for everyone else that I am sure are experiencing the same issue.  They seem to be growing too fast and don't have the capacity to offer reservations for all their members.  

I thought it was going to be a fun and relaxing way to experience food, but so far it has not been...just one more thing I need to stress about.  I won't renew my membership.  There are other dinner events that are available in the city and I think I will give those a shot.  If you know of any give good ones give me a holler.

This was my second time here and it was no better than the first. 

The first time I ordered the paella that was mediocre with very little meat. 

This time around, I ordered the mango frozen margarita that wasn't to par and I didn't even drink half of it. 

My friends and I also ordered don Julio shots. The waitress came back with them and we took the shots. We realized quickly that it was definitely not don Julio. After talking to our waitress she said she gave us another brand b/c Don Julio was out, but tried telling us their both the same price. 

Pretty disappointed we weren't told of this switch up and wish it would've been comped for. 

And you get cut off at 4 drinks... Not even buzzed!

They also forgot my friends burro and he had to wait an extra 15 minutes after all of our dishes came out.

This place looks awesome, service is just not there.

Save yourself the trip and go to crimson crab. Sauce is runny and little flavor. Prices were high for what you get. Service was awful. Tripped ahead of time at the counter and no one spoke to us once. I definitely won't be back.

Got green tea rolled ice cream. Too hard and crumbly. Not worth $7. Can get better tasting rolled ice cream for cheaper at Chinatown. 

The cashier doesnt know anything and quite frankly kind of rude.

I'm am not going give a long review here because I only tried the ribs. 
Appearance: they looked great
Taste: left a after taste that stayed with me all night..and not a good after-taste. Ribs were very fatty and by no means even close to ribs I do at home.. I would not repeat.

If u aren't used to good BBQ, it will probably be fair to you. Sorry Jimmy's, major miss on the ribs!!

Went to the new location in elmwood today! Had high hopes they had improved since moving...wrong! Ordered the Caesar salad with salmon and cup of tomato basil soup.  The soup is delicious... the salad had way too much dressing and (I know I should have ordered it on the side) but the salmon was either from a package or a can... I thought it would have been a piece of salmon... husband ordered French onion soup and French dip. His soup was not even warm... the cheese could not melt... he took it back to the counter and the girl said she could not do anything about it and to take it back to his seat! He then found a manager and she did get him a hot soup... but his French dip came out cold...and the employee who brought it out had pants on that were hanging down below his ass! Not a professional look! Because I was not thrilled with my salad I went and grabbed a slice of bread and a butter... the butter was rock hard cold... so that also disappointed me...I gave 3 stars because the new place is beautiful and the manager did bring a hot soup to hubby as well as offers us free desert... but we were so disappointed we declined and left... don't think we will be back

Very disappointing experience. It was about 4:45pm so, not very busy. Service was pretty slow. I ordered the Roasted Garlic Three Cheeseburger. When my burger came out, there were 3 whole garlic cloves under the cheese. Something I've never seen before in a restaurant. To add on, the Michelob Ultra is listed for $4 on the menu, but rang up as $6 on the check. When I asked why, the server came back and told me it was because they raised the prices but hadn't had the chance to change the menu. No offer to ring it up at the menu price. Although the food had decent flavor, I would not recommend this location.

Love love Do Or Dye Salon! April is the best stylist in Tampa hands down! She takes the time to listen to what you want, gives you realistic advice on your hair and helps you find what products are best for your hair. I always leave feeling like a Queen!

The company I rent from, Invitation Homes, uses this company to maintain the pool once a week and in between I clean and maintain the pool. My pool is always green. There was a time period between a few months where my pool stayed clear because they hired a young man who did a good job with my pool but after he left the company, my pool is in bad shape again. I do not recommend this company. I have called many times but nothing is ever accomplished. Have had problems for almost 6 months straight. I would love to change pool company's but it's not my choice because I pay and extra $100 a month in my rent to have pool maintained and the company I rent from gets to choose the company. Ordered the Pad Thai. Food was good but for the price,  I expected more.  It's 2/3 as much food as a competitor and costs more.  Probably won't go back until the prob sizes increase.  Otherwise it tastes great with the right amount of spice.
